[E] FIXED CONSTRUCTIONS -- The CPC section E groups inventions concerning permanent structures that are built in situ, including buildings, bridges, tunnels and other immovable works. It covers construction methods, foundation techniques, structural components, and related equipment used in assembling, reinforcing or maintaining such fixed constructions.
[E01] BUILDING -- E01 covers inventions dealing with the construction of fixed structures that serve as buildings, such as houses, offices, warehouses, and other permanent shelters. It includes the overall architecture, structural systems, foundations, and methods for erecting and assembling these edifices. The class is used for patents describing novel building designs, construction techniques, or improvements to building components.
[E01B] PERMANENT WAY -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Structure of stationary tracks on which propelled vehicles run for the transportation of passengers or freights;  Tools and machines specially designed for making, maintaining, renewing or taking-up the track.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Model railways -> A63H19/00 - Wetting or lubricating rails -> B61K3/00 - Track brakes fixed to permanent way -> B61K7/00 - Devices along the track for guiding or ensuring the safety of railway traffic -> B61L - Foundation for paving -> E01C3/00 - Bridges in general -> E01D - Removing undesirable matter from the permanent way (cleaning) -> E01H8/00 - Foundations in general -> E02D - Making tunnels -> E21D - Cable through -> H02G9/04
[E01B 1/00] Structure of the permanent way for railways or tramways -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Continuous load bearing bedding for supporting the sleepers or the track, e.g. ballast or concrete slab;  Drainage of the ballastway or the ballastless bedding  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Slabs as such for supporting the rails -> E01B3/14 , E01B3/24 , E01B3/40 - Draining of tramway rails -> E01B21/02 - Placing, working or taking-up the ballast -> E01B27/00 - Making of concrete parts of the track in situ -> E01B29/005 - Means for temporarily supporting the sleeper or the track -> E01B29/40 - Fastening railway sleepers to bridges -> E01D19/12 - Draining the sub-base of railways by trenches, culverts or conduits -> E01F5/00
[E01B 1/001] Track with ballast -- E01B 1/001 covers railway or tramway tracks laid on a ballast foundation, describing the arrangement of rails, sleepers and the supporting ballast layer that distributes loads to the sub-grade. It pertains to the structure of the permanent way in fixed construction.
[E01B 1/002] Ballastless track, e.g. concrete slab trackway, or with asphalt layers -- The E01B 1/002 entry covers permanent-way structures for railways or tramways that are built without ballast, such as concrete slab tracks or trackways laid on asphalt layers. It falls under Fixed Constructions → Building → Permanent Way and specifies the design and construction of these ballastless track systems.
[E01B 1/004] with prefabricated elements embedded in fresh concrete or asphalt -- E01B 1/004 covers railway or tramway permanent-way structures where prefabricated components are positioned in fresh concrete or asphalt, becoming integral parts of the track foundation. This sub-class deals with embedding such elements during the initial laying of the concrete or asphalt layer.
[E01B 1/005] with sleeper shoes -- E01B 1/005 classifies permanent-way structures for railways or tramways in which the sleepers are fitted with shoes-metal or composite plates attached to the sleeper to improve rail support, adjust gauge or simplify fastening of the rails.
[E01B 1/007] with interlocking means to withstand horizontal forces -- This subclass covers permanent-way structures for railways or tramways that incorporate interlocking components designed to resist horizontal forces such as those generated by train loads or lateral earth pressure.
[E01B 1/008] Drainage of track -- The code E01B 1/008 relates to the drainage of railway or tramway tracks, covering structures and methods that remove water from the track formation to maintain stability and safety. It includes ditches, culverts, sub-grade drainage layers and other measures designed to control moisture in the permanent way.
[E01B 11/00] Rail joints -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Dismountable or non-dismountable rail joints, e.g. by using fishplates or made by welding.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Undetachably joining in or on the track, e.g. welding -> E01B29/42 - Electrical connections of the rail -> B60M5/00
[E01B 11/02] Dismountable rail joints -- Dismountable rail joints are fittings that connect rail sections yet can be detached for inspection, repair or replacement, typically using fishplates, bolts or clamps to transfer loads while preserving alignment. They facilitate quick removal and re-assembly of track components without dismantling the surrounding permanent way structure.
[E01B 11/04] Flat fishplates -- Flat fishplates are metal plates that join the ends of rail sections in permanent way constructions, ensuring a smooth, continuous running surface and transferring loads between adjoining rails. They are a specific type of rail joint component used in building fixed rail infrastructure.
[E01B 11/06] with keys or pins -- E01B 11/06 refers to permanent railway track constructions where the joints between rails are secured using metal keys or pins. The classification covers methods and devices that lock the rail ends together, ensuring alignment and continuity of the track. This falls under the broader categories of fixed constructions, building, and permanent way, specifically addressing rail joints.
[E01B 11/08] Angle fishplates -- Angle fishplates are metal components designed to join the ends of railway rails at an angle, providing reinforcement and maintaining alignment where tracks diverge or change direction. They are employed in permanent way constructions to secure rail joints in fixed building structures, ensuring safe and stable track geometry.
[E01B 11/10] Fishplates with parts supporting or surrounding the rail foot -- Fishplates that include additional elements designed to support, brace or encircle the foot of a rail, enhancing its stability and load-bearing capacity, are classified under E01B 11/10. This subgroup covers fastening components that extend beyond simple rail connectors to provide structural reinforcement at the rail base.
[E01B 11/12] Fishplates engaging only the rail foot -- E01B 11/12 covers fishplates designed to clamp onto the foot of a rail rather than the head or web, providing a simple method of joining adjacent rail sections in permanent track installations. These components are used where only the lower surface of the rail is accessible or where a low-profile joint is required.
[E01B 11/14] Rail foot flange clips in one piece -- The subclass E01B 11/14 covers single-piece clips that attach to the foot of a rail to secure its flange in permanent-way construction. These devices are used at rail joints to provide fixed, integral fastening of the rail foot without separate components. They are classified under Fixed Constructions → Building → Permanent Way → Rail joints.
[E01B 11/16] Fishplates for joining rails of different sections -- This subclass covers fishplates, also called rail joints, used to connect rail sections that have different profiles or dimensions. It includes devices that clamp the ends of adjoining rails together, providing alignment, load transfer and stability in permanent way construction.
[E01B 11/18] Fishplates for temporarily repairing broken rails -- Fishplates designed for temporary repair of broken rails are metal plates that join the ends of rail sections, restoring alignment and load-bearing capacity until permanent replacement. They are used in the permanent way of railway infrastructure, specifically within the rail-joint subcategory of fixed constructions. This entry falls under CPC code E01B 11/18.
[E01B 11/20] with gap-bridging -- This subclass covers rail-joint arrangements that incorporate gap-bridging elements such as pads, clips or elastic inserts to maintain continuity and load transfer between rail ends. It applies to permanent-way construction where joints are designed to bridge the interval between rails, reducing impact and wear.
[E01B 11/22] by parts of the rails -- The subclass E01B 11/22 covers rail joints that are formed by separate rail components, such as fishplates, bolts, welds or other fastening devices used to connect individual rails into a continuous track. It includes the design, installation and securing methods for these parts that join rail ends together.
[E01B 11/24] with oblique or overlapping rail ends -- The E01B 11/24 class covers rail-joint designs in which the rail ends are cut at an angle or arranged to overlap one another, a method used in permanent way construction for fixed railway tracks. It falls under the hierarchy of Fixed Constructions → Building → Permanent Way → Rail Joints.
[E01B 11/26] with interlocking rail ends -- E01B 11/26 covers permanent way constructions that use rail joints with interlocking rail ends, such as fishplates or welded connections, providing secure alignment and load transfer in railway tracks.
[E01B 11/28] by parts of the joining members -- E01B 11/28 covers railway track joints whose design is defined by the individual parts of the joining members, such as fishplates, bolts, clips or other components that connect rail sections. It falls under the hierarchy Fixed Constructions - Building - Permanent Way - Rail joints and pertains to ways of joining rails by separating the joint into distinct elements.
[E01B 11/30] Fishplates with integral tread parts lying in the cross-section of the rail head -- E01B 11/30 covers fishplates that incorporate built-in tread sections positioned within the cross-section of the rail head, enabling direct joining of adjacent rails while preserving the head profile and distributing loads evenly.
[E01B 11/32] by separate parts, Inserts bridging both rail heads -- A rail joint formed from separate parts in which an insert spans and bridges the two rail heads, providing a continuous connection in permanent way track structures. This type of fixed construction is used to join rails while maintaining alignment and load transfer across the joint.
[E01B 11/34] Auxiliary rail beside gap -- E01B 11/34 relates to auxiliary rails positioned next to a gap in a permanent way track, such as a rail joint, to provide additional support, guidance or reinforcement while permitting relative movement of the main rails.
[E01B 11/36] Fastening means for fishplates -- E01B 11/36 covers the fastening devices that secure fishplates to rail ends in permanent way construction. It includes bolts, screws, clamps, welding fixtures and other means that lock the fishplate to the rail web or head, ensuring a rigid joint for rail tracks.
[E01B 11/38] Locking arrangements for fastening means -- E01B 11/38 covers locking arrangements used in rail-joint fastening systems, detailing devices such as clips, bolts, wedges or other mechanisms that securely lock rails to each other or to the underlying support. The classification addresses the design, geometry and operation of these locking means to ensure reliable, permanent connections in railway track structures.
[E01B 11/40] Dismountable rail joints combined with welded parts -- E01B 11/40 relates to dismountable rail joints that incorporate welded elements. It covers railway track connections that can be taken apart for repair or replacement but use welding of plates, bolts or other parts to secure the rails while allowing re-assembly. The subclass falls under fixed constructions for permanent ways.
[E01B 11/42] Joint constructions for relatively movable rails, e.g. rails on turntables, traversers, or swing bridges -- The classification E01B 11/42 covers joint constructions that allow rails to move relative to one another, such as those used on turntables, traversers or swing bridges, enabling sections of track to be repositioned while maintaining alignment and load-bearing capability. It falls under Fixed Constructions → Building → Permanent Way → Rail Joints.
[E01B 11/44] Non-dismountable rail joints -- E01B 11/44 designates non-dismountable rail joints, a type of permanent rail connection that cannot be removed without cutting or replacing the section. These joints are used in fixed railway constructions where continuous, unbreakable track continuity is required.
[E01B 11/46] General methods for making gapless tracks -- The E01B 11/46 classification covers techniques for constructing continuous railway tracks without joints, focusing on methods that eliminate gaps between rail sections. It includes procedures for welding, bolting, or otherwise joining rails to create a seamless running surface, improving ride smoothness and reducing maintenance. This falls under the broader categories of fixed constructions, building, and permanent ways in the CPC system.
[E01B 11/48] Joints made by flame welding -- The CPC subclass E01B 11/48 covers permanent-way rail joints that are formed by flame welding, a process that melts the metal edges of adjoining rails and fuses them together using a high-temperature flame. It applies to fixed railway constructions where a welded joint replaces mechanical fasteners.
[E01B 11/50] Joints made by electric welding -- E01B 11/50 covers rail joints that are formed by electric welding, including the use of arc, resistance or other welding processes to fuse rail ends or fittings. The classification applies to permanent-way constructions where the joint is created by electrical energy rather than mechanical fastening, encompassing the preparation, execution and inspection of welded rail connections.
[E01B 11/52] Joints made by alumino-thermal welding -- The code E01B 11/52 covers rail joints that are produced by alumino-thermal welding, a process that uses an electric arc to melt an aluminium-based filler and fuse the rail sections together. This method creates a permanent, continuous rail link without the need for mechanical fasteners, providing a smooth, high-strength connection for fixed railway track constructions.
[E01B 11/54] Electrically-insulating rail joints -- E01B 11/54 pertains to rail joints that incorporate electrical insulation, preventing stray currents from flowing between rail sections and into the ground. Such joints are used in permanent way construction to protect signalling, signalling equipment and nearby structures from corrosion and interference.
[E01B 11/56] Special arrangements for supporting rail ends -- E01B 11/56 covers devices or methods that provide special support for the ends of rails, such as anchors, sleeves, brackets or other structures that maintain alignment and load transfer at rail terminations, typically used in permanent-way construction.
[E01B 11/58] Bridge plates -- Bridge plates are metal or composite elements fitted over rail joints to bridge the gap between adjacent rails, ensuring continuity of the running surface and distributing wheel loads. They are used in permanent way construction to maintain alignment, reduce impacts, and provide a smooth transition for rolling stock.
[E01B 11/60] with wedges -- E01B 11/60 covers rail joints in permanent way construction that are secured with wedges. It pertains to fixed-construction building methods for railway tracks where the joint includes wedge components to maintain alignment and load transfer. This subclass falls under the broader categories of Fixed Constructions, Building, and Permanent Way.
[E01B 11/62] Bridge chairs -- Bridge chairs are cast or welded supports that hold railway rails on bridge decks, transferring wheel loads from the rails to the bridge structure and maintaining proper gauge. In the CPC they fall under E01B 11/62 within Fixed Constructions - Building - Permanent Way - Rail joints.
[E01B 13/00] Arrangements preventing shifting of the track -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Arrangements, e.g. rail anchors, preventing shifting such as longitudinal rail movement, which typically occurs during acceleration or deceleration of vehicles, or during expansion and contraction due to temperature change.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Machines or devices for shifting tracks -> E01B33/00
[E01B 13/02] Rail anchors -- Rail anchors are components that attach rails to sleepers or the sub-structure to stop longitudinal movement and maintain track geometry. They are used in permanent way constructions where preventing track shift is essential, and include devices such as clips, bolts, base plates and wedge systems.
[E01B 15/00] Guards for preventing a person's foot being trapped in grooved rails -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Arrangements to avoid a person's foot to get stuck in the gap formed along a rail by grooved rails.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Safety means (in general) for rail/road crossing traffic -> B61L29/00
[E01B 17/00] Cattle guards connected to the permanent way -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Arrangements connected to the installations of the railroad preventing cattle or cattle-like animals (whether domesticated or not), such as horses, sheep or deer, from straying on or walking along railways, or preventing intrusion in tunnels.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Grids in general for preventing for preventing cattle from straying -> A01K3/00 - Guards or obstacles for preventing access to the route -> B61L29/02
[E01B 19/00] Protection of permanent way against development of dust or against the effect of wind, sun, frost, or corrosion -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Protective means of the permanent way against weather influences incorporated in the track;  Means for protecting the underground against spillage;  Means to reduce noise incorporated in the track  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Snow fences -> E01F7/02 - Snow ploughs -> E01H8/02 - Control of undesirable vegetation on permanent ways of railways -> E01H11/00
[E01B 19/003] Means for reducing the development or propagation of noise -- E01B 19/003 covers devices and methods incorporated in permanent way structures that limit the generation or transmission of noise. This includes sound-absorbing materials, acoustic barriers, resilient fastenings, vibration dampers and other constructions that reduce noise propagation along railway tracks. The subclass falls under fixed constructions for buildings and permanent way, aimed at protecting the railway environment from acoustic disturbance.
[E01B 19/006] Means for protecting the underground against spillage -- This subclass covers devices or systems installed in rail or road permanent ways to prevent liquids, chemicals or other contaminants from leaking into the ground, such as containment liners, sealing structures and drainage traps. It falls under fixed constructions that protect the infrastructure from dust, wind, sun, frost and corrosion.
[E01B 2/00] General structure of permanent way -- **Definition:** This place covers:  General structure of the track, e.g. on bridges or in tunnels, foundations for the track.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Track superstructure adapted for tramways in paved streets -> E01B21/00 - Tracks for special kinds of railways -> E01B25/00 - General arrangements of stations, e.g. platforms -> B61B1/00
[E01B 2/003] Arrangement of tracks on bridges or in tunnels -- This classification covers the geometric and functional layout of railway tracks when they are placed on bridge structures or within tunnels, including alignment, spacing, and support methods to ensure safe operation and structural integrity.
[E01B 2/006] Deep foundation of tracks -- The entry E01B 2/006 covers the design and construction of deep foundations that support railway tracks, including piles, caissons, footings and similar sub-surface elements used to transmit track loads to stable ground. It addresses the methods for installing these foundations, their structural characteristics and the way they integrate with the permanent way to ensure adequate load distribution and settlement control.
[E01B 21/00] Track superstructure adapted for tramways in paved streets -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Embedded rail systems, where the traffic area of rail vehicles needs to be shared with road users of private transport, such as cars, trucks or bikes and pedestrians.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Paving for railroad level crossings -> E01C9/04 - Paving adjacent tramway rails -> E01C9/06
[E01B 21/02] Special supporting means -- The code E01B 21/02 covers special supporting means for tramway track superstructures that run within paved streets. It includes devices such as brackets, pads, foundation elements or other fixtures that secure and stabilize rails embedded in road surfaces, ensuring proper alignment and load transfer.
[E01B 21/04] Special fastenings, joint constructions, or tie-rods -- E01B 21/04 classifies special fastenings, joint constructions and tie-rods employed in permanent track superstructures for tramways laid in paved streets, covering devices that secure rails, sleepers or supporting elements to ensure stability and accommodate movement.
[E01B 2201/00] Fastening or restraining methods -- E01B 2201/00 covers techniques used to securely attach or hold together structural elements in permanent constructions such as buildings, bridges or other fixed works. It includes mechanical, chemical or other means of fastening that ensure long-term stability of the assembled components.
[E01B 2201/02] by wedging action -- Methods that fasten or restrain components by inserting a wedge that creates friction or a mechanical lock, used to secure structural or railway elements in permanent ways such as track fastening or building joints.
[E01B 2201/04] by bolting, nailing or the like -- E01B 2201/04 covers fastening or restraining methods for permanent-way structures that employ bolts, nails or similar mechanical fasteners. It falls under Fixed Constructions → Building → Permanent Way → Fastening or restraining methods, describing how components such as rails, sleepers or track elements are secured by bolting, nailing or comparable techniques.
[E01B 2201/06] by eccentric or levering action -- E01B 2201/06 classifies fastening or restraining methods used in permanent-way construction that operate by an eccentric or levering action, such as lever-operated clamps or devices that apply offset force to secure rails, sleepers or other structural elements. It falls under Fixed Constructions → Building → Permanent Way → Fastening or restraining methods.
[E01B 2201/08] by plastic or elastic deformation of fastener -- The CPC subclass E01B 2201/08 covers fastening or restraining techniques used in permanent way constructions where the fastener is secured by inducing plastic or elastic deformation. It applies to methods that rely on reshaping the fastener material rather than mechanical interlocking or adhesives.
[E01B 2201/10] in alternative ways, e.g. glueing, welding, form-fits -- E01B 2201/10 pertains to fastening or restraining techniques used in permanent way construction that employ alternative methods such as adhesive bonding, welding or interlocking form-fit connections rather than conventional mechanical fasteners.
[E01B 2202/00] Making, maintaining, renewing, or taking-up the ballastway or the track -- The code E01B 2202/00 covers methods and devices for constructing, maintaining, renewing, or removing the ballastway and track of permanent railways or similar fixed transport routes, including activities such as ballast placement, track alignment, and component replacement. It is part of the broader fixed-construction and building categories for permanent ways.
[E01B 2202/02] Nature of the movement -- The subclass E01B 2202/02 groups patents that describe the type of movement involved in installing, maintaining, renewing or taking-up railway ballast or track, such as vertical lifting, horizontal sliding, rotational or combined motions of the track components. It is used to differentiate inventions based on how the track or ballastway is displaced during construction or upkeep.
[E01B 2202/021] Turning or tilting or elastically bending -- E01B 2202/021 relates to devices or methods that cause a track or its supporting elements to turn, tilt, or undergo elastic bending, enabling adjustment of alignment, gauge or level of permanent way structures. It covers mechanisms for rotating rails, tilting sleepers, or applying controlled elastic deformation to ballast or track components during construction, maintenance or renewal.
[E01B 2202/022] about horizontal axis -- E01B 2202/022 covers methods and equipment for constructing or maintaining the ballastway or track where the work is performed about a horizontal axis, such as leveling, tamping or adjusting rail support using tools that move laterally or rotate in a horizontal plane. It falls under fixed constructions for permanent way.
[E01B 2202/024] about vertical axis -- The CPC subclass E01B 2202/024 concerns work on the vertical axis of permanent way structures, covering techniques and equipment for adjusting, maintaining, renewing or taking-up the ballastway or track in order to control the vertical alignment of rails, sleepers and related components. It is used for patents dealing with vertical positioning, leveling and support of track elements during construction or repair.
[E01B 2202/025] Pure translation
[E01B 2202/027] Including a component perpendicular to the plane of the rails -- The CPC subclass E01B 2202/027 covers permanent-way elements that are installed perpendicular to the plane of the rails, such as vertical supports, brackets or components that project outward from the track structure. It relates to the construction, maintenance or renewal of ballastways or tracks where a component is positioned at right angles to the rail surface.
[E01B 2202/028] Movement along an inclined surface -- E01B 2202/028 covers methods, devices or vehicles used to move materials, equipment or personnel along an inclined surface of a permanent railway way, such as a ballastway or track, during construction, maintenance, renewal or dismantling operations. It pertains to the handling of loads on sloping track sections.
[E01B 2202/04] Nature of the support or bearing -- The CPC subclass E01B 2202/04 covers inventions that concern the type of support or bearing used for permanent railway ways or ballastways. It includes details on how the track or its foundation is fixed, maintained, renewed or taken up, focusing on the materials, geometry or structural principles of the underlying support system.
[E01B 2202/042] Sliding -- The CPC entry E01B 2202/042 covers sliding methods and devices used in the construction, maintenance, renewal or removal of the ballastway or track of a permanent way. It relates to techniques for moving track components, sleepers or ballast sections by sliding rather than lifting, facilitating efficient installation or repair.
[E01B 2202/044] Rolling -- E01B 2202/044 Rolling covers the processes of moving and positioning track components-such as ballast, rails, sleepers or other elements-during the construction, renewal or maintenance of permanent railway ways, including the use of machines or equipment that roll or shift these parts into place.
[E01B 2202/046] with rolls on fixed part -- This CPC subclass relates to permanent-way work that uses rolls mounted on a fixed component of the track or ballastway, such as rollers on sleepers or fixed rails for alignment, support or maintenance. It covers making, maintaining, renewing or taking up the ballastway or track where such rolls are employed.
[E01B 2202/048] with rolls on moving part -- This CPC subclass covers permanent-way work in which rollers are fitted to moving parts of equipment used for laying, maintaining or renewing ballast or track, such as tampers, ballast regulators or track-laying machines. It specifies the use of rolling elements on the components that travel along the rail or ballastway.
[E01B 2202/06] Use of friction-reducing surfaces -- The code E01B 2202/06 covers the application of friction-reducing surfaces on permanent way components such as rails, sleepers, or ballastways. It includes methods for installing, maintaining, or renewing these low-friction treatments to improve track stability and reduce wear.
[E01B 2202/08] Locking devices or mechanisms for inhibiting movement -- Class E01B 2202/08 covers devices that lock or secure railway track components, ballastway structures, or other fixed constructions to prevent unintended movement, including mechanisms that hold switches, fasteners or track elements in place during service or maintenance.
[E01B 2203/00] Devices for working the railway-superstructure -- E01B 2203/00 covers equipment and machinery used to construct, maintain, or repair the permanent components of railways, such as track, sleepers, ballast, and related infrastructure. This includes tamping machines, rail gins, ballast regulators, and other devices that shape or adjust the railway superstructure.
[E01B 2203/01] with track
[E01B 2203/012] present, i.e. in its normal position -- E01B 2203/012 covers fixed devices that remain installed in their normal operating position for working the railway superstructure, such as permanently mounted tools or equipment integrated into the track structure.
[E01B 2203/015] present but lifted -- E01B 2203/015 covers fixed railway-way devices that remain in place but are lifted or raised during maintenance, such as rail fastenings or track components that are detached from the rail but left in situ to permit work without complete removal. This subclass falls under devices for working the railway-superstructure.
[E01B 2203/017] removed or not present yet
[E01B 2203/02] Removing or re-contouring ballast -- The subclass E01B 2203/02 covers methods and devices for removing, cleaning, reshaping or re-contouring railway ballast, the stone material that supports tracks, including machines that lift, spread, grade or replace ballast to restore proper track geometry.
[E01B 2203/021] Blowing -- The CPC code E01B 2203/021 classifies devices that generate a directed flow of air, gas or steam for use on permanent railway superstructures. It covers blowing apparatus employed to clear, dry or clean tracks, ballast, or other railway components as part of fixed construction equipment.
[E01B 2203/022] Horizontal chain -- E01B 2203/022 covers horizontal chains used in railway construction and maintenance, such as those that pull or guide track elements along the permanent way. The chains are employed in devices that move rails, sleepers or other components laterally during alignment or positioning tasks.
[E01B 2203/024] Inclined chain -- E01B 2203/024 covers inclined chains used in railway-superstructure work, such as mechanisms that pull or raise tools along a sloped path for track maintenance or construction. The chain is positioned at an angle to the horizontal to transmit force in devices that lift, draw, or tension components of the permanent way.
[E01B 2203/025] Suction -- Suction devices classified under CPC E01B 2203/025 are tools used in permanent-way construction to create a vacuum that temporarily holds railway components such as sleepers, rails or ballast during placement or alignment. They facilitate precise positioning and can reduce the need for mechanical clamps, improving efficiency in building and maintaining the railway superstructure.
[E01B 2203/027] Sweeping -- E01B 2203/027 covers apparatus and methods for sweeping railway-superstructure elements such as tracks, ties and ballast. It includes devices that clean away leaves, litter, snow or other debris to maintain safe and efficient train operation, often mounted on maintenance vehicles or self-propelled units.
[E01B 2203/028] Alternative ways -- E01B 2203/028 covers alternative methods and apparatuses for maintaining or constructing railway superstructures that differ from conventional techniques. It includes devices that provide non-standard ways of working the permanent way, such as novel fastening, adjustment or positioning systems.
[E01B 2203/03] Displacing or storing ballast -- E01B 2203/03 covers equipment that lifts, transports, or temporarily stores track ballast during construction or maintenance of permanent railway ways. It includes devices such as conveyors, hoppers and shovels designed to displace ballast for proper alignment and support of rails. The class falls under fixed constructions for building railway superstructures.
[E01B 2203/032] with special use or configuration of conveyor belts -- E01B 2203/032 covers railway-superstructure equipment that employs conveyor belts in a special manner or with a distinct configuration. It includes systems where belts are angled, adjustable, folded or otherwise adapted to transport ballast, sleepers or other materials during permanent-way construction.
[E01B 2203/034] using storing containers -- The code E01B 2203/34 covers railway-superstructure equipment that employs storage containers for handling, transporting or delivering materials and components needed in permanent-way construction or maintenance. It includes devices that integrate reusable containers to streamline the supply chain for fixed railway works.
[E01B 2203/036] fixed to vehicle -- E01B 2203/036 designates railway-way devices that are permanently mounted on a vehicle, such as tamping or ballast-regulating equipment, forming part of the fixed construction of the permanent way.
[E01B 2203/038] detachable from the vehicle -- Devices that are mounted on railway-maintenance vehicles but can be removed when not needed, such as temporary platforms, scaffolding, or tool carriers used for work on the track superstructure. The classification covers mechanisms that allow quick attachment and detachment, enabling flexible use of the vehicle for different fixed-construction tasks.
[E01B 2203/04] Cleaning or reconditioning ballast or ground beneath -- E01B 2203/04 covers methods and apparatus for cleaning or reconditioning the ballast or ground beneath railway tracks. It includes devices that remove debris, restore proper drainage, and maintain track geometry, often using suction, vibration, or abrasive techniques. The classification falls under fixed constructions, building, permanent way, and devices for working the railway superstructure.
[E01B 2203/042] in situ , e.g. vacuum-cleaners -- The CPC entry E01B 2203/042 relates to devices that clean railway superstructure in place, for example vacuum-cleaners used during permanent-way maintenance. It falls under fixed constructions, building, permanent way, and specifically devices for working the railway-superstructure.
[E01B 2203/045] the ballast having been taken up -- E01B 2203/045 covers equipment and processes used to manage railway ballast after it has been excavated, including its collection, transport, storage, and reuse or disposal. It falls under devices for working the railway-superstructure in the permanent way building sector of fixed constructions.
[E01B 2203/047] Adding material, e.g. tar, glue, protective layers -- This subgroup covers methods and devices that apply additional substances-such as tar, glue, sealants or protective coatings-to railway-superstructure components during construction or maintenance. It includes equipment for spreading, spraying, brushing or otherwise depositing these materials to improve durability, waterproofing or adhesion.
[E01B 2203/06] Placing ballast -- The code E01B 2203/06 pertains to equipment and processes used to place ballast on railway permanent way structures. It covers machines that distribute, spread or lay ballast along the track bed, as well as associated handling and delivery systems. This falls within the CPC hierarchy of fixed constructions, building, permanent way, devices for working the railway-superstructure.
[E01B 2203/062] New ballast -- The CPC entry E01B 2203/062 - New ballast covers inventions concerning novel railway ballast materials, compositions or methods for supplying, placing, and stabilising the track foundation in permanent-way construction. It is classified under devices for working the railway-superstructure within the building of permanent ways.
[E01B 2203/065] Re-use of ballast -- The entry covers methods, apparatus and procedures for recovering, cleaning, sorting and re-using railway ballast in the permanent way. It includes techniques for extracting used ballast, removing contaminants, restoring its mechanical properties and redepositing it to support track stability.
[E01B 2203/067] Special methods for posing or quantifying ballast -- This CPC subclass deals with special techniques for placing or measuring railway ballast, covering methods to distribute, compact, or evaluate ballast performance in permanent way construction. It falls under devices for working the railway superstructure within building permanent way.
[E01B 2203/08] Levelling ballast or ground beneath -- E01B 2203/08 covers equipment and techniques used to level ballast or the underlying ground beneath railway tracks, ensuring proper support and alignment of the superstructure. It includes devices that spread, compact or adjust ballast and subgrade to achieve uniform track geometry. This subclass falls under Fixed Constructions, Building, Permanent Way, Devices for working the railway-superstructure.
[E01B 2203/083] Ploughs -- The CPC subclass E01B 2203/083 covers plough-type machines employed in railway-superstructure work, such as ballast or ground-leveling ploughs used to shape, spread or compact the trackbed during permanent-way construction and maintenance. It falls under Fixed Constructions → Building → Permanent Way → Devices for working the railway-superstructure.
[E01B 2203/086] Rotating brooms -- The CPC entry E01B 2203/086 designates rotating brooms used in the maintenance of railway-superstructure fixed constructions such as permanent way. It falls under the hierarchy of Building → Permanent Way → Devices for Working the Railway-Superstructure. This subclass covers equipment that sweeps or clears tracks by means of a rotating brush mechanism.
[E01B 2203/10] Track-lifting or-lining devices or methods -- This class covers equipment and methods used to raise, support or adjust railway track components during construction, maintenance or repair, including hydraulic jacks, mechanical lifts and alignment guides that facilitate track lifting or lining.
[E01B 2203/12] Tamping devices -- The CPC subclass E01B 2203/12 covers tamping devices, which are machines used to compact and level track ballast beneath railway tracks to ensure proper alignment and stability of the permanent way. These devices are part of fixed constructions for building and maintaining railway superstructures.
[E01B 2203/122] for straight track -- This CPC subgroup covers devices employed in the construction, maintenance or inspection of straight sections of railway track, including fixed constructions such as rails, sleepers, fastenings, ballast and related permanent-way structures.
[E01B 2203/125] adapted for switches or crossings -- E01B 2203/125 designates devices for working the railway-superstructure that are specifically adapted for use at switches or crossings, falling within the fixed-construction group for permanent-way building.
[E01B 2203/127] vibrating the track surface -- E01B 2203/127 relates to methods and apparatus that apply vibration to a railway track surface, for purposes such as improving track geometry, compacting ballast, or enhancing wheel-rail adhesion. It is classed under fixed constructions for building permanent way, specifically devices for working the railway-superstructure.
[E01B 2203/14] Way of locomotion or support -- E01B 2203/14 covers devices that provide or influence the means of moving along or supporting a railway track, such as locomotion mechanisms, support structures, and related equipment used in the construction, maintenance, or inspection of permanent way systems. It pertains to fixed constructions for railway super-structures, encompassing any apparatus that enables vehicles or tools to travel on or be held by the track.
[E01B 2203/141] on the track to be treated -- E01B 2203/141 covers devices that operate directly on the railway track being treated, such as machines for grinding, cleaning, repairing, or otherwise processing the rail, sleeper, or ballast while the track remains in place. These tools are designed to work on the permanent way without requiring removal of the superstructure.
[E01B 2203/143] on a side-track -- E01B 2203/143 classifies devices and apparatus used on a side-track for constructing, maintaining or operating the railway superstructure, such as turnouts, switches and auxiliary equipment that facilitate work on tracks adjacent to the main line. It falls under the broader category of fixed constructions for buildings and permanent ways.
[E01B 2203/145] on the ballast -- The subclass E01B 2203/145 covers devices, machines and methods that act on the railway ballast, including equipment for distributing, cleaning, leveling, compacting or removing ballast to maintain or restore the track bed and its geometry. It relates to permanent-way construction and upkeep.
[E01B 2203/146] using other means than driven wheels as propulsion means -- E01B 2203/146 covers railway-propulsion solutions that do not employ conventional driven wheels, such as cable haulage, linear motor drives, magnetic-levitation or other non-wheel traction methods used on permanent-way structures. The code is filed under Fixed Constructions → Building → Permanent Way → Devices for working the railway-superstructure in the CPC system.
[E01B 2203/148] having wheelsets that can be displaced horizontally or vertically -- The code E01B 2203/148 pertains to railway-maintenance devices whose wheelsets can be shifted sideways or lifted up and down, enabling the equipment to adapt to track irregularities, provide better stability, and facilitate precise work on the permanent way.
[E01B 2203/15] Reducing impact of devices on environment -- E01B 2203/15 relates to techniques and designs that lessen the environmental impact of equipment used on permanent railway superstructures, encompassing noise and vibration reduction, dust control and other ecological mitigation measures applied to devices that work on the railway track.
[E01B 2203/16] Guiding or measuring means, e.g. for alignment, canting, stepwise propagation -- E01B 2203/16 classifies tools and instruments that guide or measure the railway superstructure, including devices for checking alignment, determining cant and facilitating stepwise propagation of track components. It covers equipment used to ensure precise positioning and geometry of permanent way structures.
[E01B 2203/17] continually advancing -- A continually advancing device is a railway-superstructure tool that moves forward in a non-stop manner while performing its function, such as laying, tamping or repairing track components. The mechanism typically integrates propulsion or gravity-driven means to maintain steady progress without the need for repeated repositioning.
[E01B 2204/00] Characteristics of the track and its foundations -- E01B 2204/00 deals with the design and properties of railway or tram tracks and the supporting structures that bear them, including track geometry, material choices, and foundation types that ensure stability and load distribution. It belongs to the Fixed Constructions - Building - Permanent Way group of the Cooperative Patent Classification.
[E01B 2204/01] Elastic layers other than rail-pads, e.g. sleeper-shoes, bituconcrete -- E01B 2204/01 covers elastic layers placed beneath railway track that are not rail-pads, such as sleeper-shoes or bituconcrete mats, which provide cushioning and vibration damping while supporting the permanent way. These components are part of fixed construction in the building of railway foundations.
[E01B 2204/02] Ballast bags -- Ballast bags are flexible containers placed under railway track to retain ballast and provide a stable, uniform foundation for the rails, helping to distribute loads, reduce settlement and limit vibration. They are used in permanent-way construction where conventional ballast layers are impractical, such as on weak soils, tunnels or bridge decks.
[E01B 2204/03] Injecting, mixing or spraying additives into or onto ballast or underground -- The code E01B 2204/03 covers railway track construction methods that inject, mix or spray chemical or other additives into ballast or the underlying ground to enhance stability, drainage, dust control or other performance characteristics of the permanent way. It is classified under Fixed Constructions, Building, Permanent Way, Characteristics of the track and its foundations.
[E01B 2204/04] Direct mechanical or chemical fixing of sleepers onto underground -- The CPC subclass E01B 2204/04 covers techniques for attaching railway sleepers directly to the underlying ground or foundation, using mechanical fasteners such as bolts, clips or anchors, or chemical means such as adhesives, rather than relying on ballast or separate support structures.
[E01B 2204/05] Use of geotextiles -- The CPC subclass E01B 2204/05 covers the application of geotextile materials in permanent railway track structures, including foundations, sub-grade and track-bed layers. It addresses the use of woven or non-woven fabrics for separation, reinforcement, filtration or drainage to improve stability and longevity of fixed constructions.
[E01B 2204/06] Height or lateral adjustment means or positioning means for slabs, sleepers or rails -- E01B 2204/06 relates to devices that adjust or position the vertical or sideways location of track components such as slabs, sleepers or rails. It includes mechanisms like adjustable supports, shims, brackets or hydraulic jacks used during construction or maintenance to set the correct height or alignment. The subclass falls under fixed constructions, building, permanent way in railway infrastructure.
[E01B 2204/07] Drainage -- E01B 2204/07 falls under Fixed Constructions for Buildings on Permanent Way and covers drainage systems that manage water flow from or within track foundations, including drains, ditches, and related infrastructure designed to protect the stability of railway structures.
[E01B 2204/08] Deep or vertical foundation -- The code E01B 2204/08 covers deep or vertical foundations used to support permanent railway or tramway structures. It applies to foundation types that extend below the surface to reach stable soil or rock, such as piles, drilled shafts, or caissons, and includes their design and construction methods. This subclass falls under Fixed Constructions → Building → Permanent Way → Characteristics of the track and its foundations.
[E01B 2204/09] Ballastless systems -- E01B 2204/09 covers railway track constructions that are built without traditional ballast, using solid foundations such as concrete slabs, steel decks or other rigid structures to support the rails directly. The classification addresses the design, materials, and engineering of these ballastless systems, including methods for load transfer, fastening, and alignment within permanent way installations.
[E01B 2204/10] Making longitudinal or transverse sleepers or slabs -- The class covers the production of railway track support elements, specifically the making of longitudinal or transverse sleepers and slab sections used in permanent way construction. It includes methods and processes for shaping, forming, treating and assembling these components before they are installed on the track foundation.
[E01B 2204/11] Embedded tracks, using prefab elements or injecting or pouring a curable material -- E01B 2204/11 covers railway or tram tracks that are permanently installed within the ground by embedding them, for example using prefabricated track sections or by injecting or pouring a curable material such as concrete around the rails. The code applies to fixed constructions of permanent ways in building projects.
[E01B 2204/12] Floating rails or sleepers -- The CPC entry E01B 2204/12 covers railway track components that are supported on floating systems, where rails or sleepers rest on a foundation that distributes loads without direct bearing on the ground. It includes designs such as slab track, elastomeric pads, or other resilient supports that allow the track to "float" over the sub-structure, improving stability and reducing vibration. This falls under Fixed constructions → Building → Permanent way → Characteristics of the track and its foundations.
[E01B 2204/13] Dowels for slabs, sleepers or rail-fixings -- The CPC subclass E01B 2204/13 relates to dowels used in concrete slabs, railway sleepers or other rail-fixing components, covering their design, material properties and installation within permanent track structures.
[E01B 2204/14] Vegetation on or around railway-tracks -- The code E01B 2204/14 covers vegetation that grows on or adjacent to railway tracks, including grasses, bushes, trees and other plant cover, and the methods for its control, removal or use in relation to track stability, drainage and safety. It addresses how such vegetation affects the permanent way and its foundations, and the measures required to maintain the track's performance.
[E01B 2204/15] Layout or geometry of the track -- E01B 2204/15 covers the design of a railway's permanent way, specifically the layout and geometry of the track. It includes the planning of alignment, curvature, superelevation, track spacing and other spatial characteristics that determine how the rails are arranged on their foundation. This subclass falls within the hierarchy of Fixed constructions → Building → Permanent way → Characteristics of the track and its foundations.
[E01B 2204/16] Specific granulometry of the ballast -- The code E01B 2204/16 covers the selection and specification of ballast grain-size distribution for permanent railway tracks. It relates to fixed constructions in the building of the way, detailing how particular granulometries affect track stability, drainage and load bearing.
[E01B 2205/00] Electrical insulation of railway track parts -- E01B 2205/00 pertains to the electrical insulation of railway track parts, covering methods, materials and constructions that isolate rails, sleepers, fastenings and related components from stray currents. The classification addresses safety, signalling reliability and corrosion prevention by describing insulating coatings, pads, sleeves and other devices applied to permanent way elements. It falls under Fixed constructions → Building → Permanent way.
[E01B 23/00] Easily dismountable or movable tracks, e.g. temporary railways -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Light railways such as field, colliery or mine use, temporary railways, as well as shiftable tracks for heavy loads.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Model or toy railways -> A63H19/00 , A63H21/00 - Runway or tracks for trolleys or cranes -> B66C7/00 - Rails of supporting structures for mobile scaffolds provided on roofs -> E04G3/34
[E01B 23/02] Tracks for light railways, e.g. for field, colliery, or mine use -- A type of temporary or easily removable rail track designed for light-weight railway systems such as those used in agricultural fields, collieries, or mines. It is classified under fixed constructions for permanent way and covers portable or dismountable track structures that facilitate temporary transport or material handling in these settings.
[E01B 23/04] Fastening or joining means -- E01B 23/04 covers fastening or joining means for easily dismountable or movable track systems such as temporary railways. It includes clamps, bolts, pins and other connectors that secure rails, sleepers and supporting structures, enabling quick assembly and removal in construction or military applications.
[E01B 23/06] Switches -- The CPC entry E01B 23/06 covers switches used on easily dismountable or movable track systems, such as temporary railways employed in construction, maintenance or military contexts. It belongs to the Fixed Constructions-Building-Permanent Way hierarchy and addresses the design and arrangement of turnouts that can be rapidly installed, adjusted, or removed.
[E01B 23/08] Temporary tracks for use while repairing tramways -- The CPC entry E01B 23/08 concerns temporary tracks employed during tramway repair work. It covers easily dismountable or movable rail sections that are installed to provide a short-term way for vehicles while the permanent track is being serviced, falling under the hierarchy of Fixed Constructions → Building → Permanent Way.
[E01B 23/10] Shiftable tracks for heavy loads, e.g. carrying excavators -- E01B 23/10 covers modular track systems that can be shifted or reconfigured to carry heavy loads such as excavators, intended for temporary or easily dismountable railways. The invention typically involves robust rail sections, fastening methods and support structures that allow rapid assembly, relocation and removal while maintaining load-bearing capacity. It falls under fixed constructions, building and permanent way for movable tracks.
[E01B 23/12] Sleepers -- Sleepers are the transverse members that support rails in track structures, designed for rapidly assembled or removable railway sections such as temporary or portable lines. In this subgroup the emphasis is on sleepers suitable for easily dismountable or movable tracks, often using lightweight or modular materials to facilitate quick installation and removal.
[E01B 23/14] Fastening or joining means -- The code E01B 23/14 covers fastening or joining devices used to secure the components of temporary or easily movable railway tracks. It includes clamps, brackets, pins and other means that connect rails, sleepers and support structures for rapid assembly and disassembly. This entry falls under fixed constructions, building, permanent way, specifically for dismountable tracks.
[E01B 23/16] Switches -- E01B 23/16 covers railway switches designed for easily dismountable or movable track systems such as temporary railways used in construction, mining or event venues. The invention relates to the arrangement of points, frogs and related components that allow rapid assembly, re-routing and removal of track sections while maintaining safe vehicle guidance.
[E01B 25/00] Tracks for special kinds of railways -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Special kinds such as rack railways, mono rails, cable-drawn, stationary aerial rope, supporting rails for suspended vehicles, tracks for guiding vehicles, magnetic suspension.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Slaughterhouse conveying track -> A22B7/001 - Overhead rail system for handicapped persons -> A61G7/1042 - Electric propulsion -> B60L13/00 - Suspended load carriers movable by hand or gravity -> B65G9/008 - Wheel tracks -> E01C9/02
[E01B 25/02] Tracks for rack railways -- E01B 25/02 covers the design, construction and installation of permanent rack-railway tracks, including the layout of the rack bar, supporting structures, fastening systems and alignment of the rails to accommodate gear-driven locomotives on steep gradients. It falls under fixed constructions for buildings and permanent ways, specifically for special railway types.
[E01B 25/04] Rack rails -- E01B 25/04 pertains to rack rails, the toothed rails mounted alongside or within the running rails to engage a gear on locomotives for traction on steep or special railway lines, commonly employed on mountain or industrial tracks as part of permanent way construction.
[E01B 25/06] Switches -- E01B 25/06 designates fixed railway constructions that provide switches for specialised track systems, such as mine, industrial or narrow-gauge lines. The entry covers the design, structure and installation of turnouts, points and related devices that enable trains to change routes on permanent ways that differ from standard main-line railways.
[E01B 25/08] Tracks for mono-rails with centre of gravity of vehicle above the load-bearing rail -- The subclass E01B 25/08 relates to permanent way structures for monorail systems in which the vehicle's centre of gravity is located above the load-bearing rail. It covers the design, layout and supporting elements of single-rail tracks used for transport or material-handling applications.
[E01B 25/10] Mono-rails -- E01B 25/10 pertains to monorail systems, a type of fixed-construction railway where vehicles run on a single, often elevated, guideway. These permanent-way tracks are classified under building - permanent way - tracks for special kinds of railways, covering design, support structures and related infrastructure.
[E01B 25/12] Switches -- E01B 25/12 covers switches for permanent-way tracks of special railway types, specifying the design, arrangement and components of turnouts and related devices that guide train movement on fixed constructions.
[E01B 25/14] Tracks for cable-drawn railway vehicles -- E01B 25/14 refers to tracks built specifically for cable-drawn railway vehicles, such as funiculars or cable cars, which are a form of permanent way used in special railway systems. These tracks are fixed constructions integrated within the building category of railway infrastructure.
[E01B 25/15] Switches -- E01B 25/15 relates to railway switches, the movable track components that guide wheels from one line to another in fixed-construction railway systems. It covers the design, arrangement and integration of turnouts and related hardware within permanent-way infrastructure for special kinds of railways.
[E01B 25/16] Tracks for aerial rope railways with a stationary rope -- E01B 25/16 covers the permanent railway structures that support aerial rope railways in which the rope remains fixed, such as the track or guideway components of cable-car or gondola systems. It includes the design and construction of the fixed rails, pylons, and supporting framework that keep the stationary rope aligned for vehicle movement.
[E01B 25/18] Ropes -- E01B 25/18 covers ropes employed in permanent-way structures for special railway tracks, including cables, ropes, and other tensile members used for hauling, anchoring, or supporting track components such as bridges, inclines, and cable-driven systems. This subclass addresses the design, material, and installation of such rope elements within fixed railway constructions.
[E01B 25/20] Switches -- E01B 25/20 covers railway switches, also called turnouts, used in permanent-way constructions for special types of railways. The classification pertains to fixed track elements that guide vehicles from one line to another within building-related railway infrastructure. It falls under the hierarchy of Fixed Constructions → Building → Permanent Way → Tracks for special kinds of railways.
[E01B 25/22] Tracks for railways with the vehicle suspended from rigid supporting rails -- The E01B 25/22 subclass covers permanent-way structures where the vehicle hangs from solid, rigid rails rather than riding on top of them, such as monorail or suspended-track systems. It includes design, installation and supporting elements for these specialised railway tracks.
[E01B 25/24] Supporting rails -- Entry E01B 25/24 in the Cooperative Patent Classification pertains to supporting rails used on special-railway tracks. It covers devices such as chairs, brackets, sleepers and other structural elements that hold or sustain the running rails in permanent ways.
[E01B 25/26] Switches -- E01B 25/26 covers switches that form part of fixed constructions for permanent way in special railway systems, detailing the devices that guide rolling stock from one track to another. It is classified under Fixed Constructions - Building - Permanent Way - Tracks for special kinds of railways.
[E01B 25/28] Rail tracks for guiding vehicles when running on road or similar surface -- E01B 25/28 covers rail tracks that guide vehicles running on road or similar surfaces, such as tramways, guided buses and rubber-tyred transit systems with embedded rails. The code classifies permanent-way elements that are integrated into the roadway rather than a separate railway corridor.
[E01B 25/30] Tracks for magnetic suspension or levitation vehicles -- Tracks specially designed to carry magnetic-suspension or magnetic-levitation vehicles, including the guideways, support structures and alignment systems that enable vehicles to ride without physical wheels. The entry covers both road-like and rail-like configurations used for maglev and similar high-speed transport concepts.
[E01B 25/305] Rails or supporting constructions -- E01B 25/305 covers the design and arrangement of rails and their supporting structures used in special railway track systems. It includes the geometry, fastening and integration of rails with foundations or other permanent way components for non-standard railways.
[E01B 25/32] Stators, guide rails or slide rails -- The class E01B 25/32 concerns stators, guide rails and slide rails employed in permanent-way constructions for special railway systems. It covers devices that provide lateral guidance, support or sliding surfaces for rolling stock on non-standard or uniquely engineered tracks.
[E01B 25/34] Switches -- This CPC entry covers railway switches, the fixed track components that guide rolling stock from one line to another, including turnouts, points and related moving parts. It applies to permanent way constructions for special railway systems and addresses the design, arrangement and integration of these switching devices within the track infrastructure.
[E01B 26/00] Tracks or track components not covered by any one of the preceding groups -- E01B 26/00 concerns track or track components that do not fall within any of the previously defined subgroups of permanent way. It covers unconventional or specialized elements such as unusual fastenings, supports, or accessories used in railway, tram or similar fixed constructions. The class is applied to patents describing novel track components that differ from standard rails, ties or sleepers.
[E01B 26/005] Means for fixing posts, barriers, fences or the like to rails -- E01B 26/005 covers devices that attach posts, barriers, fences or similar elements to railway rails, providing a secure and durable connection for safety or operational purposes. It falls under Fixed Constructions → Building → Permanent Way → Tracks or track components not otherwise classified.
[E01B 27/00] Making, maintaining, renewing, or taking-up the ballastway or the track -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Placing, redistributing, levelling, loosening the ballast; removing or taking-up ballast; renewing or cleaning the ballast in situ, with or without taking-up or lifting the track; compacting the ballast with or without introducing additional fresh material under the sleepers.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - General body details of railway vehicles, e.g. working platform -> B61D - Removing undesirable matter from the permanent way -> E01H8/00  **Glossary:** - Packing sleepers: Locally compacting the ballast in the vicinity of the sleepers
[E01B 27/02] Placing the ballast -- The E01B 27/02 entry covers methods and equipment for laying ballast on railway tracks, including the distribution, leveling and compacting of the stone material that forms the track foundation. It belongs to the Fixed Constructions-Building-Permanent Way group and addresses the construction, maintenance and renewal of the ballastway that supports the rails and sleepers.
[E01B 27/021] by devices moving underneath the track, e.g. ballast sleighs -- E01B 27/021 pertains to the construction, maintenance or renewal of permanent railway tracks using equipment that moves beneath the rails, such as ballast sleighs that transport and place ballast without disturbing the superstructure.
[E01B 27/022] by devices moving on the track with or without spreading or levelling -- E01B 27/022 covers the use of machines that travel on railway tracks to build, maintain or renew the ballastway or track, including devices that spread or level ballast while moving.
[E01B 27/023] Spreading, levelling or redistributing ballast already placed -- This subclass deals with methods and tools for spreading, leveling, or redistributing ballast that has already been laid on a railway track, encompassing techniques used during construction, maintenance, or renewal of the ballastway. It includes equipment for re-profiling ballast and processes to achieve uniform support under sleepers.
[E01B 27/025] by means of non-driven tools -- E01B 27/025 covers the construction, upkeep, renewal or installation of ballastways and track using non-driven (hand-operated or manually powered) tools. It falls under Fixed Constructions → Building → Permanent Way and specifies work performed without motor-driven equipment. This classification is used for methods such as manual tamping, hand-compacting, or using simple levers to place or maintain track components.
[E01B 27/026] by means of driven tools, e.g. rotating brooms or digging devices -- This subclass covers the construction, maintenance, renewal or removal of ballastway or track by employing powered, driven tools that rotate or dig, such as rotating brooms, digging devices or similar equipment to remove, spread, level or compact the ballast or rail components.
[E01B 27/027] Loosening ballasting material -- E01B 27/027 addresses methods and apparatus for loosening ballast material on railway tracks, aiding maintenance, renewal and adjustment of the permanent way. The entry pertains to fixed construction activities that improve drainage, track stability and overall ballast performance.
[E01B 27/028] Compacting ballasting material; Performing ballast cores or seats for sleepers -- E01B 27/028 covers methods for compacting ballast material and forming ballast cores or seats for sleepers in permanent railway track construction. It details processes used to shape and densify the ballast layer during the building, maintenance, renewal, or taking-up of the track foundation.
[E01B 27/04] Removing the ballast -- The subclass E01B 27/04 covers methods and apparatus for removing ballast from railway track structures. It includes techniques for excavating, lifting, and transporting the stone or aggregate that supports the sleepers during track renewal, repair, or de-ballasting operations. This entry falls within the broader categories of fixed constructions, building, and permanent way.
[E01B 27/06] Renewing or cleaning the ballast -- The classification E01B 27/06 covers processes for renewing or cleaning the ballast that supports railway tracks, including removal of fouling, re-conditioning, and replacement of ballast material. It falls under fixed constructions for building permanent ways and addresses maintenance activities that restore track stability and drainage.
[E01B 27/08] the track having been taken-up -- E01B 27/08 covers the removal or dismantling of permanent railway track after it has been taken up, including disassembly of rails, sleepers, fastenings and ballast. It relates to methods for making, maintaining, renewing or decommissioning the ballastway and track structure.
[E01B 27/10] without taking-up track -- E01B 27/10 covers techniques for constructing, maintaining or renewing permanent way where the track is not removed or displaced. It includes procedures such as ballast cleaning, tamping or track renewal performed while the rails remain in place, allowing continued service.
[E01B 27/102] Removing unwanted material without removing the ballast -- The CPC subclass E01B 27/102 covers techniques for cleaning a permanent-way or ballastway by extracting unwanted material-such as debris, vegetation, or fouling-without taking out the ballast itself, enabling maintenance or renewal of the track while the ballast remains in place.
[E01B 27/105] the track having been lifted -- E01B 27/105 relates to methods and devices for lifting an already-installed railway track, enabling maintenance, renewal or repositioning of the track and its ballast. It covers jacking systems, temporary support structures and procedures used to raise the track during work on permanent way constructions.
[E01B 27/107] along between the tracks only -- The code E01B 27/107 relates to work on the ballastway or track that is carried out solely in the space between parallel tracks, encompassing actions such as making, maintaining, renewing or taking-up the permanent way in that narrow zone. It is used for methods and equipment that address track elements without affecting the adjoining track structures.
[E01B 27/11] combined with concurrent renewal of track components -- The E01B 27/11 class covers methods for carrying out ballastway or track work while simultaneously renewing track components such as rails, sleepers and fastenings. It includes procedures that coordinate replacement, repair or renewal of these elements with other permanent-way operations.
[E01B 27/12] Packing sleepers, with or without concurrent work on the track -- E01B 27/12 covers methods and apparatus for packing railway sleepers, whether or not the work is carried out simultaneously with other track-working operations. It includes the placement of ballast or other packing material around sleepers to secure them in the permanent way and may address related steps such as adjusting, compacting or renewing the ballast during the process.
[E01B 27/13] Packing sleepers, with or without concurrent work on the track -- E01B 27/13 covers methods for packing railway sleepers into the ballast, either as a separate operation or together with other track work such as alignment, renewal or renewal activities. It relates to the construction, maintenance and renewal of permanent-way structures in railway building.
[E01B 27/14] Manual tools or hand-held power tools therefor -- E01B 27/14 covers manual implements and hand-held power tools specifically designed for tasks such as laying, maintaining, renewing or taking up railway ballast and track components in the construction of permanent ways.
[E01B 27/16] Sleeper-tamping machines -- Sleeper-tamping machines are railway maintenance devices that compact ballast beneath track sleepers to secure proper alignment and stability of the permanent way, and they can also be employed to reposition or replace sleepers during track renewal work.
[E01B 27/17] combined with means for lifting, levelling or slewing the track -- The subclass E01B 27/17 covers permanent track constructions that incorporate devices for lifting, levelling or swivelling the rails, enabling adjustment of track geometry during installation, maintenance or renewal of the ballastway.
[E01B 27/18] by introducing additional fresh material under the sleepers, e.g. by the measured-shovel method, by the blowing method -- This subclass covers methods of replenishing ballast under railway sleepers by introducing fresh material, such as the measured shovel or blowing techniques. It belongs to the permanent way group of fixed construction building and relates to making, maintaining, renewing, or taking up the track ballastway.
[E01B 27/20] Compacting the material of the track-carrying ballastway, e.g. by vibrating the track, by surface vibrators -- E01B 27/20 covers techniques for densifying the ballast layer that supports railway tracks, such as applying mechanical vibration to the track structure or using surface vibrators. The methods aim to improve track stability and load distribution during construction, maintenance or renewal of permanent ways.
[E01B 29/00] Laying, rebuilding, or taking-up tracks -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Transporting, laying, removing or renewing rails, sleepers or lengths of assembled track;  Lifting or levelling of tracks; installing or removing fastening means or other track components, e.g. rail clips, sole-plates;  Undetachably joining or fastening track components in or on the track, e.g. by welding; Sealing rail joints.  **Glossary:** - Ribbon rails: Rails assembled into strings
[E01B 29/005] Making of concrete parts of the track  in situ -- This subclass relates to the on-site formation of concrete track elements such as sleepers, ties and track slabs, covering the casting, placement and curing of these parts while a railway line is being built, rebuilt or taken up. It falls under fixed constructions, building, permanent way, and the broader category of laying, rebuilding or taking-up tracks.
[E01B 29/02] Transporting, laying, removing, or renewing lengths of assembled track, assembled switches, or assembled crossings -- E01B 29/02 covers the handling of pre-assembled railway track components, including the transport, placement, removal and renewal of lengths of track, switches and crossings as part of permanent way construction.
[E01B 29/04] Lifting or levelling of tracks -- E01B 29/04 relates to methods and equipment used to raise, lower, or level railway tracks during construction, reconstruction, or maintenance. It includes lifting devices, jacks, hydraulic systems, and procedures for adjusting track geometry to achieve proper alignment and grade. The code falls under fixed constructions, building, permanent way.
[E01B 29/05] Transporting, laying, removing, or renewing both rails and sleepers -- The code E01B 29/05 relates to the handling of track components as a unit, covering methods and apparatus for transporting, laying, removing, or renewing both rails and their supporting sleepers during the construction, reconstruction, or maintenance of permanent railway ways.
[E01B 29/06] Transporting, laying, removing or renewing sleepers -- The entry E01B 29/06 covers techniques, machines and processes for transporting, laying, removing or renewing railway sleepers (ties) on permanent ways, including handling of pre-assembled track sections and rapid replacement of worn elements. It is part of the Fixed Constructions - Building - Permanent Way hierarchy, focusing on the construction and maintenance of track infrastructure.
[E01B 29/09] under, or from under, installed rails -- The code E01B 29/09 covers techniques or apparatus for working from beneath or through the space under installed railway rails. It includes methods of accessing, inspecting, cleaning, lubricating, or removing rails without dismantling the track structure, typically for maintenance or repair of permanent way systems.
[E01B 29/10] for inserting or removing sleepers -- This subclass covers techniques, tools and machines employed to insert new railway sleepers or remove existing ones during the laying, rebuilding or de-construction of permanent way tracks, including devices that lift, position, drive in or extract sleepers as part of track construction or maintenance.
[E01B 29/11] Removal involving destruction of the sleeper, e.g. the sectioning thereof -- E01B 29/11 addresses techniques for taking out railway sleepers by destroying them, for example by cutting or sectioning the timber or concrete elements. It applies to processes used when rebuilding or dismantling permanent way tracks.
[E01B 29/13] for moving sleepers in a direction parallel to the rails, e.g. for spacing or aligning them -- E01B 29/13 covers methods for moving railway sleepers along the track direction, such as adjusting their spacing or aligning them while laying, rebuilding, or taking up permanent way. The technique involves shifting sleepers parallel to the rails without removing them.
[E01B 29/14] for lifting sleepers up to the rails -- E01B 29/14 covers techniques and equipment for raising railway sleepers into position on the rails during track laying, rebuilding or removal. It belongs to the fixed-constructions hierarchy for permanent-way construction.
[E01B 29/16] Transporting, laying, removing, or replacing rails -- E01B 29/16 covers methods and equipment for moving, installing, removing or replacing railway rails as part of fixed construction of permanent ways in building projects.
[E01B 29/17] Lengths of rails assembled into strings, e.g. welded together -- The code E01B 29/17 covers rail strings formed by joining individual lengths of track, typically by welding, to create continuous sections for installation or replacement on permanent railway lines. It includes the preparation, alignment and securing of rails into these longer units for use in track laying, rebuilding or take-up operations.
[E01B 29/20] Moving rails placed on installed sleepers in the plane track -- E01B 29/20 relates to techniques for moving rails that are already placed on installed sleepers in a straight-track (plane) configuration, encompassing relocation, adjustment or replacement of the rails while preserving track geometry. It addresses equipment and procedures used to shift or realign the rails without dismantling the sleepers.
[E01B 29/22] Raising rails from sleepers, e.g. for inserting sole-plates -- This CPC subclass covers methods for lifting railway rails from their sleepers, typically to install or replace sole plates, including the use of jacks, hydraulic devices, or other mechanisms that raise the rail without removing it entirely. It falls under fixed constructions, building, permanent way, laying, rebuilding, or taking-up tracks.
[E01B 29/24] Fixing or removing detachable fastening means or accessories thereof -- E01B 29/24 pertains to the processes and equipment used to fix or remove detachable fastening components and their accessories in permanent way construction, such as rail track fastening systems.
[E01B 29/26] the fastening means being spikes -- E01B 29/26 covers fastening devices that are spikes used to attach rails to sleepers or other support elements in railway permanent-way construction, including their design, dimensions and mounting methods for laying, rebuilding or taking-up tracks.
[E01B 29/28] the fastening means being of screw-and-nut type -- E01B 29/28 covers screw-and-nut type fastening elements used in the permanent way of railway construction, such as attaching rails, sleepers or other track components. The subclass falls under Fixed Constructions → Building → Permanent Way → Laying, rebuilding or taking-up tracks.
[E01B 29/29] for horizontally-arranged fastening elements, e.g. fish-bolts -- The CPC subclass E01B 29/29 covers fastening devices that are arranged horizontally along railway track structures, such as fish-bolts used to secure rails, sleepers or other components. It applies to permanent-way construction and maintenance tasks involving laying, rebuilding or removing tracks. These elements are designed to provide secure, longitudinal attachment in fixed railway constructions.
[E01B 29/32] Installing or removing track components, not covered by the preceding groups, e.g. sole-plates, rail anchors -- E01B 29/32 pertains to the installation or removal of track components that are not listed in the preceding groups, such as sole-plates and rail anchors, within the broader field of permanent way construction for railways.
[E01B 29/40] Means or arrangements for temporarily supporting laid tracks, or rails or sleepers in the track -- E01B 29/40 covers devices and arrangements used to temporarily support laid railway tracks, rails or sleepers during construction, reconstruction or removal work.
