These means provide short-term stability for the track structure while permanent fixtures are being installed or repaired, and fall under the Fixed Constructions - Building - Permanent Way hierarchy.
[E01B 29/42] Undetachably joining or fastening track components in or on the track, e.g. by welding, by gluing -- E01B 29/42 covers permanent fastening of railway track elements, such as joining rails, sleepers or fasteners by means that cannot be detached, including welding, adhesive bonding or similar processes that create an undetachable connection. This subcategory falls under Fixed Constructions → Building → Permanent Way → Laying, rebuilding or taking-up tracks.
[E01B 29/44] Methods for effecting joining of rails in the track, e.g. taking account of ambient temperature -- E01B 29/44 covers methods for joining railway rails in the permanent way, emphasizing techniques that account for ambient temperature to achieve proper fit, alignment and lasting strength during laying, rebuilding or track removal. It falls under fixed constructions for building permanent railway tracks.
[E01B 29/46] Devices for holding, positioning, or urging together the rail ends -- This subclass groups devices employed in track laying, rebuilding, or removal that secure rail ends by holding, aligning, or urging them together, such as clamps, brackets, jigs and other urging mechanisms used while the rails are fastened or welded.
[E01B 3/00] Transverse or longitudinal sleepers -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Sleepers and other means for supporting rails, e.g. concrete slabs, regardless of the substructure, the sleepers being parallel or orthogonal to the rails.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Sleeper shoes for ballastless track -> E01B1/005 - Sleepers for switches or crossings -> E01B7/22 - Sleepers for shiftable tracks for heavy loads -> E01B23/12 - Manufacturing of concrete sleepers -> B28B
[E01B 3/02] made from wood -- E01B 3/02 classifies wooden sleepers used in permanent railway tracks, covering both transverse (crosswise) and longitudinal (along-track) forms. These are fixed constructions that form part of the building of a permanent way, i.e., the structural support for rails.
[E01B 3/04] Means for preventing cleaving -- E01B 3/04 covers devices, structures or methods installed on or with transverse or longitudinal railway sleepers to stop or reduce cleaving of the sleeper material. Typical solutions include protective coverings, fastening systems, reinforcement inserts or sealing compounds that keep the sleeper from splitting under load or environmental stress. The subclass falls under fixed constructions for permanent railway ways.
[E01B 3/06] Anti-cracking dogs -- E01B 3/06 pertains to anti-cracking dogs, which are devices fitted to transverse or longitudinal sleepers to prevent the sleeper material from cracking or splitting under load. These devices, often metal or polymer clamps, secure the sleeper faces together and help distribute stresses along the permanent way.
[E01B 3/08] Straps  or bands for hooping or encircling sleepers -- E01B 3/08 pertains to straps, bands or similar devices used to hoop or encircle railway sleepers, securing them together or to the track structure. It is classified under fixed constructions for permanent way, specifically for transverse or longitudinal sleepers.
[E01B 3/10] Composite sleepers -- Composite sleepers are railway track support components made from a blend of materials such as concrete, polymers, steel or wood, used as transverse or longitudinal elements in permanent way construction. They combine the strength and stability of traditional sleepers with reduced weight, better vibration damping and resistance to corrosion, extending service life while simplifying installation.
[E01B 31/00] Working rails, sleepers, baseplates, or the like, in or on the line -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Working such as reconditioning, repairing or treating track components in or on the line and machines specially designed therefor.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Working the ballast -> E01B27/00 - Laying, rebuilding or taking up tracks or track components -> E01B29/00 - Undetachably joining or fastening track components -> E01B29/42
[E01B 31/02] Working rail or other metal track components on the spot -- This subclass deals with the on-site installation, adjustment or repair of rails, fasteners, baseplates and other metal components of permanent railway tracks. It includes techniques for positioning, fixing and aligning working rails directly on the line, as well as related equipment.
[E01B 31/04] Sectioning or slitting, e.g. by sawing, shearing, flame-cutting -- The classification E01B 31/04 covers techniques for sectioning or slitting railway components such as rails, sleepers and baseplates that are in or on the track line. It includes methods such as sawing, shearing or flame-cutting to cut or separate these fixed constructions.
[E01B 31/06] Making holes, e.g. by drilling, punching, flame-cutting -- The CPC class E01B 31/06 covers techniques for creating holes in railway track elements such as rails, sleepers or baseplates, using processes like drilling, punching or flame-cutting. It is applied when preparing these components for bolts, pins or other fasteners during permanent-way construction.
[E01B 31/08] Bending, e.g. for straightening rails or rail joints -- The classification E01B 31/08 concerns techniques and equipment for bending rails, primarily used to straighten individual rails or rail joints during maintenance of permanent way systems. It includes processes that adjust rail curvature to restore proper alignment on tracks.
[E01B 31/12] Removing metal from rails, rail joints, or baseplates, e.g. for deburring welds, reconditioning worn rails -- E01B 31/12 deals with processes and tools for removing metal from railway rails, rail joints or baseplates, such as deburring welds and reconditioning worn rails. It falls under fixed constructions for building permanent ways, focusing on the maintenance and repair of track components.
[E01B 31/13] by milling -- The code E01B 31/13 classifies permanent-way work in which rails, sleepers, baseplates or similar components are shaped by milling, covering the manufacturing of these fixed constructions for railway lines.
[E01B 31/15] by planing or filing -- This subclass covers the preparation or finishing of permanent-way components such as rails, sleepers or baseplates by planing or filing. The methods involve mechanically removing material to achieve the required profile or smoothness before or during installation on the line.
[E01B 31/17] by grinding -- This CPC subclass covers techniques for shaping or refurbishing rails, sleepers, baseplates or similar components of a permanent railway line by grinding. The process removes material to restore correct geometry, improve surface finish, or eliminate defects. It applies to construction or maintenance work performed on or in the track structure.
[E01B 31/175] using grinding belts -- Using grinding belts refers to the process of employing abrasive belt devices to machine, re-profile, or clean railheads, sleepers and other permanent-way components. The technique removes surface irregularities, restores correct geometry and improves wheel-rail interaction, thereby extending the service life of the track.
[E01B 31/18] Reconditioning or repairing worn or damaged parts on the spot, e.g. applying inlays, building-up rails by welding -- The CPC subclass E01B 31/18 covers methods for restoring or repairing worn or damaged railway components directly on the line, such as applying inlays or rebuilding rails by welding. It lies within permanent way construction and addresses on-site reconditioning of rails, sleepers, baseplates and similar parts.
[E01B 3/12] Longitudinal sleepers -- Longitudinal sleepers are railway ties that run parallel to the track, forming a continuous support beam beneath the rails in permanent way constructions. They are typically made of concrete, steel or composite material and are used to improve track stability, distribute loads, and allow smoother riding on high-speed or heavily loaded lines.
[E01B 31/20] Working or treating non-metal sleepers in or on the line, e.g. marking, creosoting -- The subclass E01B 31/20 covers methods for working or treating non-metal railway sleepers that are placed on or in the track, including operations such as marking, impregnation with creosote or other preservatives, and similar surface treatments to protect or identify the sleepers.
[E01B 31/22] Cutting or grinding wooden sleepers, e.g. for forming rail seats -- This subclass deals with methods and apparatus for cutting or grinding wooden railway sleepers to form rail seats, including material removal, profiling and finishing operations used in the manufacture of permanent way components.
[E01B 31/23] Sectioning -- E01B 31/23 - Sectioning refers to methods and devices for dividing railway track components such as rails, sleepers or baseplates into separate sections for installation, replacement or maintenance, specifically in permanent way constructions where these elements are worked on or in the line.
[E01B 31/24] Forming, treating, reconditioning, or cleaning holes in sleepers -- The E01B 31/24 subclass covers processes for creating, treating, repairing, or cleaning the holes that receive fasteners in railway sleepers. It includes methods such as drilling, boring, deburring, chemical cleaning, and surface conditioning to ensure proper fit and durability of the sleeper attachments. This work is part of the broader permanent-way construction of rail infrastructure.
[E01B 31/26] Inserting or removing inserts or fillings for holes in sleepers, e.g. plugs, sleeves -- The classification E01B 31/26 covers techniques and apparatus for inserting or removing inserts, plugs, sleeves or other fillings placed in holes of railway sleepers, enabling repair, adjustment or reinforcement of the track structure. It applies to permanent-way construction where sleepers, rails and related components are fixed in place.
[E01B 31/28] Applying or removing anti-splitting or like reinforcing means -- E01B 31/28 covers methods and devices for fitting or taking off anti-splitting or similar reinforcing elements on rails, sleepers, baseplates or related components of permanent railway tracks. It addresses reinforcement of track members to prevent fracture during service or construction.
[E01B 3/14] Slabs -- E01B 3/14 covers slabs employed as transverse or longitudinal sleepers within permanent-way fixed constructions, such as railway tracks, where the slab forms the bearing surface for rails and distributes loads to the underlying foundation. This category includes concrete, steel or composite sleepers used in building permanent track structures.
[E01B 3/16] made from steel -- The subclass E01B 3/16 classifies steel sleepers that serve as transverse or longitudinal supports in permanent railway tracks. These fixed-construction components are used for building the track foundation and are made primarily from steel.
[E01B 3/18] Composite sleepers -- Composite sleepers are railway ties made from a combination of materials such as plastic, rubber, fiber-reinforced polymers or wood-plastic composites, intended to replace traditional timber or concrete sleepers. They provide resistance to rot, corrosion and mechanical wear while reducing weight and maintenance requirements. The classification covers both transverse and longitudinal designs used in permanent way constructions.
[E01B 3/20] Sleeper construction for special purposes, e.g. with openings for ballast working -- This subclass concerns railway sleepers that are specially configured, for example by incorporating openings, slots or other modifications to aid ballast placement, drainage or maintenance. It covers permanent-way sleepers designed for particular functions beyond standard load-bearing roles, such as facilitating ballast working or other specialised railway track tasks.
[E01B 3/22] Longitudinal sleepers -- E01B 3/22 refers to longitudinal sleepers, the structural members positioned parallel to the track direction that support rails in permanent way constructions such as railways and tramways. These sleepers distribute loads along the track and are commonly made of wood, concrete or steel.
[E01B 3/24] Slabs -- E01B 3/24 covers slabs that form the transverse or longitudinal sleepers of permanent way structures. The code is used for fixed constructions in building railway tracks, where concrete or other material slabs serve as the supporting elements for rails.
[E01B 3/26] combined with inserts of wood artificial stone or other material -- The code E01B 3/26 covers permanent-way sleepers that are built with inserts of wood, artificial stone or other materials incorporated into the main sleeper structure. It applies to fixed construction elements used in railway tracks where such composite materials are combined to form the sleeper.
[E01B 3/28] made from concrete or from natural or artificial stone -- E01B 3/28 covers transverse or longitudinal railway sleepers that are manufactured from concrete or from natural or artificial stone, intended for use in permanent way fixed constructions such as bridges, viaducts, or other building elements of the track infrastructure.
[E01B 3/30] Hollow sleepers -- Hollow sleepers are permanent way components that consist of a longitudinal or transverse support element for rails, formed with a cavity or hollow interior to reduce weight while maintaining strength. They are typically made of concrete, steel or composite materials and are used in railway track construction to provide stable track gauge and distribute loads.
[E01B 33/00] Machines or devices for shifting tracks, with or without lifting, e.g. for aligning track, for shifting excavator track -- This subclass covers equipment that moves railway or construction tracks laterally, with or without lifting, such as mechanisms for aligning track sections or repositioning excavator tracks, and includes devices that shift tracks without permanently altering them.
[E01B 33/02] for slewing, i.e. transversely shifting, in steps -- E01B 33/02 covers mechanisms that shift a track transversely in stepped movements, enabling slewing of permanent-way or construction tracks for alignment or repositioning, and may include devices that lift or simply slide the track without continuous motion.
[E01B 33/04] Manual tools -- E01B 33/04 pertains to manual tools employed in permanent-way construction for shifting or aligning tracks, such as devices used to move railway or excavator tracks without power assistance. This subclass falls under Fixed Constructions → Building → Permanent Way.
[E01B 33/06] for slewing in a continuous operation, e.g. for tracks which carry excavators -- E01B 33/06 relates to devices that enable continuous slewing of track systems, allowing the tracks to rotate while staying engaged with the ground. It is applied to constructions such as excavator tracks that need ongoing directional adjustment.
[E01B 33/08] Boom track-slewing machines -- E01B 33/08 covers machines that rotate or shift a boom's track, often with lifting capability, to align or reposition permanent way sections such as railway or tram tracks. These devices, known as boom track-slewing machines, are used in construction and maintenance of fixed track systems.
[E01B 33/10] Bridge track-slewing machines -- E01B 33/10 covers machines designed to rotate or shift railway tracks on bridges, allowing alignment or repositioning of track sections without lifting the bridge. These devices typically swivel the track assembly or move it laterally to facilitate maintenance or construction.
[E01B 33/12] Combined boom and bridge track-slewing machines -- E01B 33/12 covers machines that integrate a movable boom with a track-mounted bridge that can slew to lift, shift, or align railway or construction tracks. These devices combine the reach of a crane with a traveling platform, enabling precise repositioning of track sections during building or maintenance of permanent ways.
[E01B 33/18] Details not peculiar to a particular type of machine -- E01B 33/18 pertains to any machine or device that moves or shifts permanent-way tracks, including tools for aligning railway tracks or relocating excavator tracks, and covers features that apply broadly rather than to a specific type of equipment.
[E01B 3/32] with armouring or reinforcement -- The subclass E01B 3/32 includes railway sleepers-whether transverse cross-ties or longitudinal bars-designed with added armouring or reinforcement to increase their strength, durability and resistance to loading. It applies to permanent-way components that incorporate metal or composite reinforcement within the sleeper structure.
[E01B 33/21] Arrangement or construction of rollers moving the rails -- This subclass covers the design and layout of roller systems that move rails, used in equipment that shifts or aligns track. It specifies how rollers are arranged, mounted and integrated into machines for shifting tracks, such as those that adjust excavator or permanent-way rails.
[E01B 3/34] with pre-tensioned armouring or reinforcement -- E01B 3/34 covers railway sleepers-either transverse or longitudinal-that incorporate pre-tensioned armouring or reinforcement to increase strength and durability, fitting within the Fixed Constructions > Building > Permanent Way hierarchy.
[E01B 3/36] Composite sleepers -- Composite sleepers are railway ties made from a combination of materials such as wood, plastic, steel or concrete, designed to improve durability, reduce maintenance and lower vibration. They are used in permanent way construction as an alternative to conventional solid sleepers, offering better resistance to weather, rot and mechanical stress.
[E01B 3/38] Longitudinal sleepers -- Longitudinal sleepers are railway track components that run parallel to the rail direction, providing continuous support to the rails and transferring loads to the ballast or subgrade. They are used in certain permanent-way constructions to improve stability and reduce rail movement.
[E01B 3/40] Slabs -- E01B 3/40 pertains to permanent railway structures, specifically the use of concrete or other material slabs as transverse or longitudinal sleepers that form part of the track formation. It falls under the broader categories of Fixed Constructions, Building, and Permanent Way. The classification covers design, installation and related aspects of slab sleepers supporting rails.
[E01B 3/42] combined with inserts of wood or other material -- E01B 3/42 pertains to permanent way constructions in which transverse or longitudinal sleepers are combined with inserts of wood or other material, typically to enhance durability, elasticity or load distribution. The classification falls under Fixed Constructions → Building → Permanent Way.
[E01B 3/44] made from other materials only if the material is essential -- E01B 3/44 relates to permanent-way sleepers that are constructed from non-standard materials, where the chosen material is essential to the sleeper's performance. The classification applies when the material provides a functional property - such as specific strength, elasticity, or resistance - that cannot be achieved with conventional wood, steel or concrete, and the design would not function if a different material were used.
[E01B 3/46] made from different materials -- E01B 3/46 covers railway sleepers or ties that are formed from two or more distinct materials, such as wood combined with metal, concrete, or polymer components, and the techniques for arranging or bonding those materials in a single sleeper.
[E01B 3/48] Distance keepers or tie-rods for sleepers -- Distance keepers or tie-rods for sleepers are components that hold railway sleepers at a fixed spacing, preventing lateral movement and ensuring track geometry. They are used in permanent-way constructions where transverse or longitudinal sleepers are installed, helping maintain alignment and stability of the track structure.
[E01B 35/00] Applications of measuring apparatus or devices for track-building purposes -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Measuring for track building, maintaining or renewing purposes.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Apparatus on locomotives or cars to indicate or record bad track sections -> B61K9/00 - Devices along the track for guiding or ensuring the safety of railway traffic -> B61L
[E01B 35/02] for spacing, for cross levelling -- E01B 35/02 covers devices and methods employed in railway track construction to set the correct spacing between rails and to achieve cross-level alignment. It falls under the Fixed Constructions → Building → Permanent Way hierarchy and relates to measuring apparatus used for track-building purposes.
[E01B 35/04] Wheeled apparatus -- E01B 35/04 includes wheeled tools and machines used to measure, align, or inspect railway track geometry-such as gauges, levelers and profiling devices-during the construction or maintenance of permanent way.
[E01B 35/06] for measuring irregularities in longitudinal direction -- The subclass E01B 35/06 covers devices and methods used to measure longitudinal irregularities of railway tracks, such as variations in alignment, level or geometry, during the construction and maintenance of permanent way. It is applied to track-building apparatus that monitors these longitudinal deviations.
[E01B 35/08] for levelling -- E01B 35/08 relates to devices and methods for leveling railway tracks during construction or maintenance, covering instruments that measure and adjust the vertical alignment of rails and sleepers to achieve the required geometry. It is a subclass of permanent way, building, and measuring apparatus for track-building purposes.
[E01B 35/10] for aligning -- E01B 35/10 covers devices and methods used to align rails or track components during the construction or maintenance of permanent ways, ensuring correct geometry and continuity of the railway line. These solutions often involve measuring apparatus to verify alignment tolerances.
[E01B 35/12] for measuring movement of the track or of the components thereof under rolling loads, e.g. depression of sleepers, increase of gauge -- The E01B 35/12 subclass covers devices and methods used to measure the movement of railway track or its components when subjected to rolling loads, such as the depression of sleepers or changes in gauge. It applies to fixed construction and permanent-way monitoring systems that assess track deformation during operation.
[E01B 37/00] Making, maintaining, renewing, or taking-up the ballastway or the track, not provided for in a single one of groups -- E01B 37/00 covers general techniques and equipment for constructing, repairing, renewing or removing railway ballastways and tracks when the work is not specifically addressed in narrower subclasses. It includes overall procedures for laying, cleaning, stabilising, or extracting ballast and track components, as well as related maintenance practices that fall outside more detailed categories. This subclass captures catch-all operations in permanent rail ways.
[E01B 5/00] Rails -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Rails for carrying vehicles with their centre of gravity above or suspended from the rail; guard rails, guide rails to prevent derailing; distance keeping means.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Tie-rods for sleepers -> E01B3/00 - Rail parts for switches or crossings -> E01B7/00 - Rail anchors preventing shifting of the track -> E01B13/02 - Tie-rods for tramway rails -> E01B21/04 - Laying, re-building or taking-up the rails -> E01B29/00 - Working rails in or on the line -> E01B31/02 - Manufacture of rails -> B21B
[E01B 5/02] Rails -- E01B 5/02 covers rails, the longitudinal steel members that form the running surface of railway tracks. It falls under fixed constructions for building permanent ways, dealing with the design, material, profile and fastening of rails used in rail transport systems. This subclass includes innovations related to rail geometry, wear resistance and integration with sleepers and ballast.
[E01B 5/04] Grooved rails -- Grooved rails are railway rails designed with a longitudinal channel to receive the flanged wheels of trams, light-rail or similar vehicles, allowing the track to be embedded in roadways or sidewalks. This feature enables shared use of the surface while maintaining a permanent way.
[E01B 5/06] Reversible or invertible rails
[E01B 5/08] Composite rails -- Composite rails are railway track sections made from two or more different materials, typically a steel core combined with a polymer, concrete or other composite layer to achieve reduced weight, improved corrosion resistance and lower maintenance. They are used in permanent way construction where durability and performance are required.
[E01B 5/10] Composite grooved rails -- E01B 5/10 covers composite grooved rails, which are permanent way components formed from composite materials and provided with longitudinal grooves to receive fastening elements. These rails are used in fixed railway constructions to improve durability and reduce weight.
[E01B 5/12] Rails with a foot serving as a sleeper -- E01B 5/12 covers rails that include an integrated foot or base which serves as a sleeper, forming a single-piece track component. The design combines the rail and its supporting element for permanent ways in building and fixed-construction applications.
[E01B 5/14] Rails for special parts of the track, e.g. for curves -- E01B 5/14 relates to rails that are specifically designed for special sections of a railway track, such as those used in curves, turnouts or other non-standard track geometries, and includes the corresponding rail profiles, fittings and accessories adapted to these parts.
[E01B 5/16] Distance keepers -- E01B 5/16 relates to distance-keeping devices used on rail systems to automatically maintain a preset spacing between rolling stock or between a vehicle and a fixed point on the track, including sensors, control units and mechanical or electronic mechanisms that monitor and adjust the separation.
[E01B 5/18] Guard rails -- Guard rails are protective barrier systems installed alongside railway tracks to prevent derailments, protect equipment, and enhance safety for personnel and rolling stock. They are fixed metal or composite structures anchored to the track formation or adjacent structures, designed to absorb impact and guide derailed wheels.
[E01B 7/00] Switches -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Mechanical installation enabling trains to be guided from one track to another at a railway junction; crossing of tracks.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Guard rails -> E01B5/18 - Turntables -> B61J1/00 - Lubricating of rails -> B61K3/00 - Operating mechanisms -> B61L
[E01B 7/02] Tongues -- Tongues are the movable rail elements of a railway switch that guide wheels from one track to another, forming part of the point mechanism used in permanent-way constructions for buildings and other fixed installations.
[E01B 7/04] Constructions with tongues turning about a vertical pivot at the end -- E01B 7/04 covers fixed constructions in building permanent ways where a tongue or wing of a switch pivots vertically at its end. The classification is used for railway or tramway turnouts whose moving part rotates around a vertical axis to guide wheels. It falls under the broader categories of fixed constructions, building and permanent way.
[E01B 7/06] Constructions with flexible tongues or flexible fishplates -- E01B 7/06 relates to permanent-way constructions that employ flexible tongues or flexible fishplates to join rails, chiefly in switches and turnouts. The flexible elements allow controlled movement while keeping the track aligned, reducing stress and wear at the connection points.
[E01B 7/08] Other constructions of tongues, e.g. tilting about an axis parallel to the rail, movable tongue blocks or rails -- The subclass E01B 7/08 covers constructions of switch tongues that differ from simple fixed blocks, including tongues that tilt about an axis parallel to the rail, movable tongue blocks, and rail arrangements that allow the tongue to be displaced during operation. These devices are used in permanent-way installations where the switch must be adjustable rather than fixed.
[E01B 7/10] Frogs -- Frogs are the V-shaped rail components used in railway switches (turnouts) where two tracks intersect, guiding wheels from one line to another and maintaining the correct gauge at the crossing. They form part of the permanent way within fixed construction of rail infrastructure, ensuring smooth and safe wheel transition across the rail junction.
[E01B 7/12] Fixed frogs made of one part or composite -- The code E01B 7/12 covers frogs, the track crossing elements in permanent way switches, that are manufactured as a single piece or from a composite material. These fixed frogs form part of the permanent railway construction.
[E01B 7/14] with movable parts -- The class covers railway track switches (points) whose components can move to direct a train from one track to another, including movable rails, point blades and the mechanisms that operate them such as levers, hydraulic or electric actuators. It relates to permanent way constructions where the switch elements are designed to be repositioned during operation.
[E01B 7/16] Jump-over frogs -- E01B 7/16 pertains to jump-over frogs, a type of fixed construction installed in railway switches to guide wheels across the gap when a train moves from one rail to another. These devices are part of permanent way building and provide a smooth, safe transition for rolling stock crossing a switch.
[E01B 7/18] Combinations of switches and crossings -- This subclass covers railway track elements that combine a switch (point) and a crossing (frog) into a single unit, used in permanent-way infrastructure of railways, including integrated turnout designs, modular assemblies, and pre-fabricated components that provide both diverging and intersecting track paths.
[E01B 7/20] Safety means for switches, e.g. switch point protectors, auxiliary or guiding rail members -- The CPC subclass E01B 7/20 covers safety devices installed on railway switches, such as point protectors, auxiliary rail members and guiding rail components. These elements are designed to protect the switch points from damage, prevent derailments and ensure reliable operation of permanent-way switch assemblies.
[E01B 7/22] Special sleepers for switches or crossings -- Special sleepers for switches or crossings are railway track components designed to support and precisely align points and crossing assemblies, often featuring tailored shapes, profiles or materials to accommodate the complex geometry and forces at these junctions. They enhance stability, reduce wear and simplify maintenance within the permanent way of fixed constructions.
[E01B 7/24] Heating of switches -- The E01B 7/24 subclass concerns methods and apparatus for heating railway switches (points) and related components. It includes integrated heaters, electric or fluid-based warming systems, insulation, and control units designed to prevent ice, snow or frost from impairing switch movement, thereby ensuring reliable operation of permanent-way track infrastructure.
[E01B 7/26] Lubricating of switches -- E01B 7/26 relates to the lubrication of railway switches, covering the selection and application of oils, greases or other lubricants and the associated maintenance methods required to keep fixed track turnouts operating reliably.
[E01B 7/28] Crossings -- Crossings are fixed constructions where permanent ways such as railways, roads or tramlines intersect, including level crossings, over-passes, under-passes and related junction structures that allow one way to cross another safely. The entry covers the design, layout and supporting elements of these intersecting passages.
[E01B 7/30] Jump-over crossings -- E01B 7/30 defines jump-over crossings, a type of permanent way structure where one railway or roadway passes above another without intersecting at grade. The entry falls under fixed constructions, building, permanent way and switches, and covers the design and implementation of elevated crossing elements that enable uninterrupted traffic flow.
[E01B 9/00] Fastening rails on sleepers, or the like -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fastening means and other track components, e.g. tie-plates, sole-plates, pads or the like, used for attaching rails on their support.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Fastening switches or crossings on special sleepers -> E01B7/22 - Rail anchors -> E01B13/02 - Apparatus for fixing or removing fastening means or track components -> E01B29/24 , E01B29/32 - Fastening rails to bridges -> E01D19/12
[E01B 9/02] Fastening rails, tie-plates, or chairs directly on sleepers or foundations -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Means for fastening rails directly on sleepers or foundations, even if the rails rest on sole-plates or pads;  Means for fastening tie plates or chairs directly on sleepers or foundations.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Tie plates per se -> E01B9/40 - Fastening the rail on the tie-plate -> E01B9/44 - Rail chairs per se -> E01B9/54 - Fastening the rail in the chair -> E01B9/58 - Special rail fastenings -> E01B9/60 , E01B9/62 , E01B9/64 , E01B9/66
[E01B 9/04] Fastening on wooden or concrete sleepers or on masonry without clamp members -- The E01B 9/04 class covers methods and devices for attaching railway rails directly to wooden or concrete sleepers, or to masonry structures, without using separate clamp members. It includes fasteners such as screws, bolts, nails, adhesives or welded attachments that secure the rail to the sleeper surface. This subclass is part of the broader categories for permanent way construction and building of fixed rail systems.
[E01B 9/06] Railways spikes -- This subclass covers railway spikes used to fasten rails to sleepers, including their design, installation methods and attachment techniques in permanent-way track construction. It is part of the CPC hierarchy under Fixed Constructions, Building, Permanent Way.
[E01B 9/08] Elastic spikes -- Elastic spikes are fastening devices that secure rails to sleepers in permanent railway track construction. They use an elastic element that expands or deforms to grip the rail, providing a simple, adjustable and reusable rail-sleeper connection without bolts or welding.
[E01B 9/10] Screws or bolts for sleepers -- E01B 9/10 pertains to screws, bolts and similar mechanical fasteners engineered for securing railway sleepers to rails, including their design, material selection and manufacturing features. It is situated under Fixed Constructions → Building → Permanent Way → Fastening rails on sleepers.
[E01B 9/12] Retaining or locking devices for spikes or screws -- E01B 9/12 covers retaining or locking devices for spikes, screws or similar fasteners that attach rails to sleepers in permanent railway track construction. It includes clamps, clips, and other locking mechanisms designed to keep the fasteners secure under load.
[E01B 9/14] Plugs, sleeves, thread linings, or other inserts for holes in sleepers -- The code covers devices inserted into the drilled holes of railway sleepers, such as plugs, sleeves, thread linings or other inserts, which seal, protect or provide a mounting point for fasteners in permanent way construction. These components help prevent water ingress, reduce wear and improve fastening reliability on permanent way tracks.
[E01B 9/16] for wooden sleepers -- E01B 9/16 relates to methods, devices or arrangements for fastening rails to wooden sleepers in permanent-way railway construction. It is positioned under Fixed Constructions, Building, Permanent Way and addresses the securing of rails on sleepers made of wood.
[E01B 9/18] for concrete sleepers
[E01B 9/20] by keys -- E01B 9/20 covers fastening railway rails to sleepers by means of keys, where a key element is driven into a keyed slot or groove to lock the rail in place. It includes metal, plastic or composite keys, the tools for inserting them and combinations with clips or other securing devices.
[E01B 9/22] Fastening on steel sleepers without clamp members -- Fastening on steel sleepers without clamp members refers to railway track-fastening methods that attach rails directly to steel sleepers using bolts, screws, welds or similar devices, eliminating the need for separate clamp components. This falls under Fixed Constructions → Building → Permanent Way in the CPC system and addresses permanent, structural rail-sleeper connections.
[E01B 9/24] by keys -- E01B 9/24 covers methods and devices for fastening rails to sleepers using keys, such as keyed clips or lock keys that secure the rail head to the sleeper. It falls under the CPC hierarchy of Fixed Constructions → Building → Permanent Way, addressing rail-holding solutions that rely on keyed elements for alignment and vibration damping.
[E01B 9/26] Welded fastenings -- E01B 9/26 covers welded fastenings used to attach rails to sleepers in permanent railway track structures. The classification falls under fixed constructions, building, permanent way, focusing on rail-sleeper fastening or similar components.
[E01B 9/28] Fastening on wooden or concrete sleepers or on masonry with clamp members -- E01B 9/28 covers devices that clamp railway rails to wooden, concrete or masonry sleepers using clamp members. The classification pertains to permanent-way construction where rails are attached to sleepers without traditional bolts, employing specially shaped clamps that secure the rail to the sleeper surface.
[E01B 9/30] by resilient steel clips -- This subclass covers methods and devices for attaching rails to sleepers using resilient steel clips that provide elasticity and vibration damping, typically for railway track structures. It falls under the CPC group for permanent way constructions.
[E01B 9/303] the clip being a shaped bar -- E01B 9/303 covers the use of a shaped-bar clip to fasten rails to sleepers or similar members in permanent way construction, falling under the fixed constructions category for building railways.
[E01B 9/306] the clip being a shaped plate -- E01B 9/306 covers rail-fastening clips that consist of a shaped plate designed to secure rails to sleepers or similar supports in permanent way constructions. The plate may be formed to grip the rail foot and clamp onto the sleeper, providing a simple, rigid attachment method.
[E01B 9/32] Fastening on steel sleepers with clamp members -- The code E01B 9/32 relates to railway track construction methods that secure rails to steel sleepers using clamp-type fasteners, allowing the rail to be held firmly without welding or bolts. This classification addresses the design and application of clamp members that attach the rail directly to permanent steel sleepers.
[E01B 9/34] by resilient steel clips -- This CPC subgroup covers methods and devices for securing railway rails to sleepers with resilient steel clips that provide elastic clamping and vibration damping. It includes designs where the clips are placed between the rail foot and the sleeper, often incorporating spring or elastomer elements to maintain a consistent fastening force.
[E01B 9/36] Metal sole-plates for rails which rails are directly fastened to sleepers -- E01B 9/36 covers metal sole-plates that are placed under rails and fastened directly to sleepers, forming a fixed rail-to-sleeper connection in permanent way construction. The plates provide a durable bearing surface and aid in rail stability.
[E01B 9/38] Indirect fastening of rails by using tie-plates or chairs -- The E01B 9/38 class covers methods of securing railway rails to sleepers by means of intermediate components such as tie-plates, chairs or similar fittings rather than direct attachment. It includes designs where the rail is fastened indirectly through these plates or chairs, which transfer load to the sleeper and facilitate alignment, replacement or adjustment of the track. This classification falls under Fixed Constructions, Building, Permanent Way.
[E01B 9/40] Tie-plates for flat-bottom rails -- E01B 9/40 covers tie-plates designed for flat-bottom railway rails, which are metal plates that sit between the rail foot and the sleeper to distribute load and secure the rail to the sleeper, preventing movement and ensuring stable track alignment.
[E01B 9/42] of two or more parts -- This subclass deals with fastening rail sections to railway sleepers using systems composed of two or more separate components, such as brackets, clamps, bolts, or interlocking parts that together secure the rail. It includes designs where the fastening elements are assembled separately before being installed on the sleeper.
[E01B 9/44] Fastening the rail on the tie-plate -- The CPC subclass E01B 9/44 covers methods and devices for securing a rail to the tie-plate that sits on a sleeper, including bolts, clips, screws and other fastening systems used in permanent railway track construction. It falls under the hierarchy of Fixed Constructions → Building → Permanent Way and deals specifically with the attachment of rails to the supporting plates.
[E01B 9/46] by clamps -- The subclass E01B 9/46 covers methods of fastening rails to sleepers or similar supports by means of clamps. It includes clamp designs that grip the rail base and securely attach it to the sleeper, providing a permanent rail-way fix without the use of nails, bolts or welding.
[E01B 9/48] by resilient steel clips -- The code E01B 9/48 refers to fastening railway rails to sleepers using resilient steel clips, a technique employed in permanent-way construction. It covers the design, installation and function of such clips that provide elastic support while securing the rail to wooden, concrete or composite sleepers. This classification falls under fixed constructions, building and permanent way categories.
[E01B 9/483] the clip being a shaped bar -- E01B 9/483 covers the use of a shaped-bar clip to fasten railway rails to sleepers or similar supports. It belongs to the Fixed Constructions - Building - Permanent Way group, addressing methods of securing rails in place.
[E01B 9/486] the clip being a shaped plate -- E01B 9/486 covers a clip formed as a shaped plate used to fasten rails to sleepers or similar supports in permanent way construction. The plate is designed to grip the rail and hold it securely to the sleeper, providing a simple, fixed-construction fastening method.
[E01B 9/50] by keys -- E01B 9/50 designates rail-to-sleeper connections that employ metal keys or similar keyed elements to lock the rail in place. The key is driven into a slot or recess in the sleeper, securing the rail without requiring bolts or clips. This method is used for permanent way constructions such as railway tracks.
[E01B 9/52] by resilient keys -- E01B 9/52 relates to fastening railway rails to sleepers by using resilient keys that compress or flex to hold the rail securely while allowing limited movement, a sub-category of permanent way constructions within fixed-construction building technologies.
[E01B 9/54] Rail chairs -- Rail chairs are metal or cast components that hold and position railway rails on sleepers or tie plates, ensuring proper gauge and load distribution. They are fixed to sleepers by bolts, welding or casting, allowing secure fastening of rails in permanent way track structures. This entry covers the design, installation and variations of such fastening devices.
[E01B 9/56] for rails with two or more treads -- This subclass deals with methods and devices for fastening railway rails that have two or more treads to sleepers or similar supports, including clamps, bolts and other securing arrangements specifically adapted to multi-tread rail profiles.
[E01B 9/58] Fastening the rail in the chair -- This CPC subclass covers methods and devices for attaching a railway rail to its supporting chair, including clamps, bolts, clips and other fastening assemblies that secure the rail within the chair on sleepers or similar supports. It addresses the design and installation of such fixings in permanent way constructions.
[E01B 9/60] Rail fastenings making use of clamps or braces supporting the side of the rail -- The code E01B 9/60 refers to rail-fastening systems that secure rails to sleepers using clamps or side-supporting braces. It falls under Fixed Constructions > Building > Permanent Way and covers devices that grip the rail from its side rather than relying on spikes or bolts. This includes various mechanical clamps, brackets and brace assemblies designed to hold the rail in place.
[E01B 9/62] Rail fastenings incorporating resilient supports -- E01B 9/62 covers rail fastenings that incorporate resilient supports such as pads, springs or elastomeric elements to provide elasticity between the rail and its supporting structure. The devices are intended to secure rails to sleepers, bridges or similar permanent ways while reducing vibration and stress transmission. The classification falls under fixed constructions, building, permanent way.
[E01B 9/64] Rail fastenings gripping or encircling the sleeper -- E01B 9/64 covers rail fastening systems that grip or encircle the sleeper, securing rails to the supporting structure in permanent way constructions such as bridges, tunnels and other fixed railway installations. The subclass includes devices that wrap around or lock onto the sleeper to maintain rail alignment and load transfer.
[E01B 9/66] Rail fastenings allowing the adjustment of the position of the rails, so far as not included in the preceding groups -- E01B 9/66 pertains to rail-fastening devices that enable the position of a rail to be adjusted after installation, used in permanent track constructions and not covered by earlier groups. It includes mechanisms for fine-tuning rail alignment while the rail is fastened to sleepers or similar supports.
[E01B 9/68] Pads or the like, e.g. of wood, rubber, placed under the rail, tie-plate, or chair -- E01B 9/68 pertains to pads or similar elements, such as wooden or rubber inserts, placed between a rail and its supporting component like a tie-plate or chair. These devices help distribute load, reduce vibration and wear, and improve the stability of permanently fixed railway track.
[E01B 9/681] characterised by the material -- The subclass E01B 9/681 includes fastening systems for attaching railway rails to sleepers that are distinguished by the material from which they are made, such as steel, aluminium, polymer or composite. It covers devices and methods where the material composition is the primary feature used to classify the fastening solution.
[E01B 9/683] layered or composite -- The CPC class E01B 9/683 covers fastening systems for rail tracks in which the connection between rail and sleeper is formed from layered or composite constructions. It includes designs that employ multiple material strata-such as metal, polymer or wood laminates-to improve strength, corrosion resistance or vibration damping in permanent railway ways.
[E01B 9/685] characterised by their shape -- This CPC subclass covers fasteners that secure rails to sleepers or similar supports, where the inventive aspect is defined by the component's shape, such as specially profiled clamps, brackets or clips designed to enhance grip, ease of installation or durability.
[E01B 9/686] with textured surface -- This subclass covers methods and devices for fastening railway rails to sleepers that incorporate a textured surface on the fastener or the rail. The texture is intended to improve grip, reduce vibration, or enhance corrosion resistance in permanent-way construction. It falls under the broader categories of fixed constructions, building, and permanent way.
[E01B 9/688] with internal cavities -- E01B 9/688 covers fastening rail or track components to sleepers using devices that incorporate internal cavities, such as hollow bolts, plugs or brackets, to reduce weight, allow material flow or facilitate installation in permanent way constructions. This subclass falls under fixed constructions for building permanent railway infrastructure.

[E01C 11/106] Joints with only prefabricated packing; Packings therefor -- This class includes methods and materials for creating pavement joints-such as those in roads, sports grounds or similar surfaces-where the joint is filled solely with a prefabricated packing element, as well as the prefabricated packings themselves, covering their design, installation and types used to seal or cushion the joint.
[E01C 11/12] Packing of metal and plastic or elastic materials -- E01C 11/12 covers the packing of metal, plastic or elastic materials in pavements and other constructed surfaces. [E01C 11/123] Joints with only metal and  in situ  prepared packing or filling -- E01C 11/123 covers pavement joints that are formed solely from metal components together with a packing or filling material that is prepared and placed on site. It applies to fixed constructions such as roadways, sports fields and similar surfaces where the joint is sealed with in-situ grout, sand, bitumen or comparable filler, without the use of pre-formed non-metallic inserts.
[E01C 11/126] Joints with only metal and prefabricated packing or filling -- E01C 11/126 relates to joints in road, sports-ground or similar pavings that consist only of metal parts combined with a prefabricated packing or filling material, specifying the way the joint is constructed in fixed-construction surfaces.
[E01C 11/14] Dowel assembly -- A dowel assembly comprises metal rods, sleeves or similar connectors inserted in drilled holes of adjacent concrete paving units to transmit loads and restrict longitudinal movement. It is employed in road, sports-ground and other fixed paving constructions to align and lock slabs, enhancing stability under traffic and environmental stresses.

[E01C 11/225] Paving specially adapted for through-the-surfacing drainage, e.g. perforated, porous; Preformed paving elements comprising, or adapted to form, passageways for carrying off drainage  E01C9/004 ,  E01C9/008 ,  E01C9/08 ,  E01C9/10 ,  E01C11/228 ,  E01C13/00  take precedence -- Paving specially designed to allow water to drain through the surface, including perforated or porous materials and pre-formed units that incorporate passageways for drainage. The classification covers pavements that integrate drainage features directly into the paving element and is subordinate to related groups such as E01C9/004 and E01C11/228.
[E01C 11/226] Coherent pavings -- Coherent pavings are monolithic surface structures formed by a homogeneous material that hardens into a continuous, self-supporting slab, such as concrete, polymer or resin-bound layers, used for roads, tracks, or sports fields. The classification covers methods of preparing, laying and curing these integral pavings and the materials employed.
[E01C 11/227] Gutters; Channels  E01C11/223  takes precedence; permanently installed flushing means  E01H3/04 ; Roof drainage discharge ducts set in sidewalks -- The CPC entry E01C 11/227 covers gutters and channels that are permanently installed as part of fixed constructions such as pavings for roads, sports grounds or similar surfaces. It falls under the hierarchy "Fixed constructions - Building - Construction of, or surfaces for, roads, sports grounds, or the like - Details of pavings." The broader subclass E01C 11/223 takes precedence, and related drainage elements are found in E01H 3/04.
[E01C 11/228] Gutters for porous pavings -- The subclass E01C 11/228 covers gutters specifically intended for porous or permeable pavings, providing drainage channels that capture surface water while permitting infiltration through the paving material. It includes designs integrated into or adjacent to such pavings to manage runoff and prevent water accumulation.
[E01C 11/229] Shallow gutters, i.e. gutters forming a minor pothole -- The subclass E01C 11/229 covers shallow gutters that are formed as minor depressions or pothole-like cuts in pavement surfaces. These small, shallow channels are integrated into road, sports-ground or other fixed-construction pavings to provide localized drainage or water collection without constituting full-depth gutters.
[E01C 11/24] Methods or arrangements for preventing slipperiness or protecting against influences of the weather -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Spreading machines for wintry services, if they are not specially adapted for treating wintry roads but could also be used for spreading other materials -> E01C19/20 - Thawing materials in general -> C09K3/18 - Improving gripping of ice bound or other slippery traffic surfaces by spreading gritting or thawing materials on the surface of the road -> E01H10/00
[E01C 11/245] for preventing ice formation or for loosening ice, e.g. special additives to the paving material, resilient coatings  E01C11/26  takes precedence; built-in sanding or sprinkling devices  E01H10/00 -- This subclass covers pavements and surfaces for roads, sports grounds and similar areas where measures are employed to prevent ice formation or to loosen ice. It includes the use of special additives, resilient coatings or other treatments applied to the paving material; built-in sanding or sprinkling devices are classified elsewhere.
[E01C 11/26] Permanently installed heating or blowing devices -- The subclass E01C 11/26 covers permanently installed heating or blowing devices that are integrated into pavings such as roads, sports grounds or similar surfaces, used to melt ice, prevent frost or control surface temperature. It includes systems like embedded heating coils, hot-air ducts or radiant panels that are fixed in place as part of the construction.
[E01C 11/265] Embedded electrical heating elements  in floors  F24D13/00 ; Mounting thereof -- E01C 11/265 covers the integration of electric heating elements within floor pavements, including the methods and means for installing such heating systems in building or similar surfaces. It addresses the design, placement and mounting of the embedded heating components to provide radiant warmth for indoor floors or similar structures.
[E01C 13/00] Pavings or foundations specially adapted for playgrounds or sports grounds; -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Pavings for playgrounds or sports grounds (made of prefabricated single units or in situ) and their foundations, also including:  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - General layout of sport grounds -> A63C19/00 - Golf courses -> A63C19/003 - Mechanical ski trails -> A63C19/10 - Race tracks -> A63K1/00
[E01C 13/003] Construction of, or surfacings for, rinks or tracks for roller skating, skate-boarding or the like  layout of roller-skating rinks  A63C19/10 -- The CPC entry E01C 13/003 falls under Fixed Constructions → Building → Construction of, or surfacings for, roads, sports grounds or the like, and covers the design and installation of pavings or foundations specifically adapted for playgrounds or sports areas. It details the construction or surfacing of rinks and tracks intended for roller-skating, skate-boarding and similar activities.
[E01C 13/02] Foundations, e.g. with drainage or heating arrangements -- The code E01C 13/02 covers foundations that incorporate drainage or heating systems, intended for fixed constructions such as buildings, road surfaces or playgrounds and sports grounds. It addresses integrated foundation designs that manage water flow or provide thermal regulation.
[E01C 13/04] Pavings made of prefabricated single units -- The class E01C 13/04 refers to prefabricated single-unit pavings, such as interlocking blocks or mats. These modular elements are used to form roads, playgrounds, sports fields or other surface constructions where a quickly assembled, uniform surface is required.
[E01C 13/045] the prefabricated single units consisting of or including bitumen, rubber or plastics -- E01C 13/045 classifies prefabricated single units that incorporate bitumen, rubber or plastics and are used as pavings or foundations specifically adapted for playgrounds, sports grounds and similar fixed constructions.
[E01C 13/06] Pavings made ,
[E01C 13/065] at least one  in situ  layer consisting of or including bitumen, rubber or plastics -- E01C 13/065 covers the construction of pavings or foundations specially adapted for playgrounds or sports grounds that include at least one in-situ layer formed from bitumen, rubber or plastics. The code applies to surface layers applied directly on site as part of the overall ground structure.
[E01C 13/08] Surfaces simulating grass -- The subclass covers engineered surfaces that imitate natural grass, typically installed in playgrounds, sports fields or similar recreational areas. It includes synthetic-turf systems, rubber-filled mats and other layered constructions that replicate the appearance, feel and performance of grass for safety, durability and low maintenance.
[E01C 13/083] Construction of grass-grown sports grounds; Drainage, irrigation or heating arrangements therefor -- The code E01C 13/083 covers the design and construction of natural-grass sports fields, including the installation of drainage systems, irrigation networks, and heating installations to maintain playability and turf health. It addresses fixed constructions related to building and preparing surfaces for sportsgrounds.
[E01C 13/10] for artificial surfaces for outdoor or indoor practice of snow or ice sports -- This CPC subclass covers the design and construction of artificial playing surfaces intended for snow or ice sport activities, whether installed outdoors or within indoor facilities. It includes specialised paving, flooring, or foundation systems that replicate natural snow or ice conditions for training or competition.
[E01C 13/102] Civil engineering aspects of the construction of ice rinks or sledge runs made from frozen-liquid, semi-liquid or frozen-pasty substances, e.g. portable basins -- This CPC subclass covers civil engineering methods and structures for building ice rinks or sled runs using frozen liquids, semi-liquids or frozen-pasty substances, including portable basins and related foundations, and falls under fixed constructions, building and surfaces for sports grounds, focusing on specialized pavings.
[E01C 13/105] of artificially refrigerated rinks or runs, e.g. cooled rink floors or swimming pools or tennis courts convertible into rinks -- E01C 13/105 relates to the construction of permanently installed surfaces that incorporate artificial-refrigerated systems to produce ice rinks or convertible sports areas. It includes cooled floor installations for rinks, swimming pools or tennis courts that can be transformed into ice surfaces.
[E01C 13/107] Non-frozen surfacings for ice, skating or curling rinks or for sledge runs; Rinks or runs with such surfacings -- This subclass deals with artificial, non-frozen floorings designed for ice-type activities such as skating, curling or sled runs, including the construction of rinks or tracks that use such surfaces. It falls under fixed constructions for sports grounds.
[E01C 13/12] for snow sports
[E01C 15/00] Pavings specially adapted for footpaths, sidewalks or cycle tracks -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Specially adapted paving elements or systems for footpaths, sidewalks or cycle tracks, such as:  a walkway or cycle track in the countryside, see for example US4176982:  a set of paving elements for constructing an elevated walkway, see for example WO2008025201:  or a tactile walk path, see for example US2009162143:
[E01C 17/00] Pavement lights, i.e. translucent constructions forming part of the surface -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Lamp constructions incorporated in pavings or paving elements, e.g. paving block or tile.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Blocks specially designed for marking roads -> E01F9/50 - Illuminated advertising or display means on roads -> G09F19/22
[E01C 19/00] Machine, tools, or auxiliary devices for constructing or repairing the surfacing of roads or like structures -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Devices or methods for preparing and/or applying paving material, and/or consolidating and/or finishing the road surface.  Devices for guiding or controlling the machines along a predetermined path.  Removable forms or shutterings for road-building purposes.  Devices or arrangements for forming individual paving elements, e.g. kerbs or by sectioning a freshly laid slab, in situ.  Apparatus for laying individual preformed surfacing elements.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Apparatus or process for surface stabilisation for road building -> E01C21/00 - Devices for setting-out or checking the configuration of new surfacing -> E01C23/01 - Producing hydraulic cement concrete in general -> B28C5/00 - B28C9/00 - Stationarily-arranged melting boiler for tar or asphalt -> C10C3/12 - Smoothing tools for mass made in situ for indoor paving -> E04F21/24
[E01C 19/002] Apparatus for preparing and placing the materials and for consolidating or finishing the paving -- E01C 19/002 covers machines, tools or auxiliary devices used to mix, spread, compact or finish paving materials such as asphalt, concrete or bitumen on roads, sports fields and similar surfaces, enabling the preparation, placement and consolidation of the surfacing layer. It is applied in construction and repair of paved structures.
[E01C 19/004] Devices for guiding or controlling the machines along a predetermined path  for graders or bulldozers  E02F3/841 ; for other soil-shifting machines  E02F9/2045 -- The entry covers guidance and control devices that keep graders, bulldozers and other soil-shifting machines on a predetermined travel line while they construct or repair road, sport-ground or similar surfacing. It includes mechanical or electronic systems that steer, limit or monitor the machine's path to ensure accurate, straight or curving lines.
[E01C 19/006] by laser or ultrasound -- This subclass includes equipment that uses laser or ultrasonic techniques to lay, compact, bond, or otherwise treat road or similar surface layers during construction or repair. Devices may emit laser beams or ultrasonic waves to cut, weld, detect faults, or achieve precise shaping of pavement materials. It falls under the broader group for machines, tools, or auxiliary devices for constructing or repairing surfacing of roads, sports grounds, and comparable structures.
[E01C 19/008] by reference lines placed along the road, e.g. wires co-operating with feeler elements  E01C19/006  takes precedence -- This CPC entry covers machines, tools or auxiliary devices that employ reference lines placed alongside a road, such as wires, working together with feeler elements to guide or control the surfacing or repair process. It falls under fixed constructions for building road surfaces.
[E01C 19/02] for preparing the materials -- E01C 19/02 refers to machines, tools or auxiliary devices employed to prepare the materials used in constructing or repairing the surfacing of roads, sports grounds and similar structures. The classification falls under Fixed Constructions > Building > Construction of, or surfaces for, roads, sports grounds, or the like.
[E01C 19/025] for preparing hydraulic-cement-bound mixtures of which at least one ingredient has previously been deposited on the surface, e.g.  in situ  mixing of concrete -- This CPC subclass covers machines, tools or auxiliary devices that prepare hydraulic-cement mixtures directly on a surface where at least one ingredient has already been deposited, such as equipment for in-situ mixing of concrete during road or sports-ground surfacing.
[E01C 19/05] Crushing, pulverising or disintegrating apparatus -- E01C 19/05 covers machines, tools or auxiliary devices used to crush, pulverise or otherwise disintegrate materials in the construction or repair of road, sports-ground or similar surfaces, including equipment that breaks down aggregates, asphalt, concrete or other paving substances.
[E01C 19/08] Apparatus for transporting and melting asphalt, bitumen, tar, or the like -- E01C 19/08 covers machines that move and heat asphalt, bitumen, tar or similar materials while they are being laid for road or other surface construction. The apparatus typically combines a transport system with a heating unit to melt the material for continuous paving.
[E01C 19/10] Apparatus or plants for premixing or precoating aggregate or fillers with non-hydraulic binders, e.g. with bitumen, with resins, -- E01C 19/10 covers machines, tools or auxiliary devices used to premix or precoat aggregates, mineral fillers or similar materials with non-hydraulic binding agents such as bitumen or synthetic resins before they are laid as road or sports-ground surfacing. The equipment is intended for preparing the mixture that will be applied in the construction of fixed surfaces.
[E01C 19/1004] Reconditioning or reprocessing bituminous mixtures, e.g. salvaged paving, fresh patching mixtures grown unserviceable; Recycling salvaged bituminous mixtures; Apparatus for the in-plant recycling thereof  E01C19/1036  takes precedence -- This class covers machines, tools or auxiliary devices used to recondition, reprocess or recycle bituminous mixtures such as reclaimed paving material or degraded fresh patches, including apparatus for in-plant recycling of these materials. The focus is on equipment that restores or repurposes bituminous road-surfacing compounds.
[E01C 19/1009] Plant wherein type or construction of the mixer are irrelevant  E01C19/1004  takes precedence -- E01C 19/1009 refers to plant used for constructing or repairing road surfaces where the type or construction of the mixer is not a distinguishing factor. This subclass falls under machines, tools or auxiliary devices for surfacing roads and similar structures and is subordinate to E01C 19/1004.
[E01C 19/1013] Plant characterised by the mode of operation or the construction of the mixing apparatus; Mixing apparatus;  E01C19/1004 ,  E01C19/1054  take precedence -- The code E01C 19/1013 covers machines, tools or auxiliary devices used in the construction or repair of road, sports-ground or similar surfaces where the plant is defined by how the mixing apparatus works or is built, including the mixing apparatus itself and related sub-classifications such as E01C19/1004 and E01C19/1054.
[E01C 19/1018] Coating the solid ingredients by immersion in a bath of binder -- The entry E01C 19/1018 covers machines, tools or auxiliary devices that coat solid ingredients such as aggregates, stones or blocks by immersing them in a bath of binder. It relates to equipment used in the construction or repair of road, sports-ground or similar surfacing where a binder-coated layer is applied.
[E01C 19/1022] Coating the solid ingredients by passing same through a shower or cloud of binder  E01C19/21 ,  E01C19/4813  take precedence
[E01C 19/1027] Mixing in a rotary receptacle -- The subclass E01C 19/1027 covers machines, tools or auxiliary devices that mix material in a rotating container for the construction or repair of road, sports-ground or similar surfacing. It includes rotary mixers, whether vehicle-mounted or portable, that combine aggregates, binders, additives or other constituents before the material is laid or compacted.
[E01C 19/1031] the mixture being discharged continuously  E01C19/1036  takes precedence
[E01C 19/1036] for in-plant recycling or for reprocessing, e.g. adapted to receive and reprocess an addition of salvaged material, adapted to reheat and remix cooled-down batches -- E01C 19/1036 covers machines, tools or auxiliary devices for constructing or repairing road surfaces and similar structures that incorporate in-plant recycling, enabling salvaged material to be received, reprocessed, reheated and remixed with cooled-down batches.
[E01C 19/104] Mixing by means of movable members in a non-rotating mixing enclosure, e.g. stirrers  E01C19/08  takes precedence -- E01C 19/104 pertains to equipment used in the construction or repair of road surfaces and similar structures that mixes materials inside a non-rotating mixing enclosure by means of moving elements such as stirrers. The devices are classified under the broader group for mixing by movable members, and the more general subclass E01C19/08 has priority over this specific entry.
[E01C 19/1045] the mixture being discharged continuously -- This subclass covers machines, tools, or auxiliary devices that feed and lay a construction mixture in a continuous stream onto a road or sports-ground surface, such as continuously discharged asphalt or concrete. It includes systems that control the flow and placement of the material during surfacing.
[E01C 19/105] Mixing or coating by a combination of methods covered by  E01C19/1018  -  E01C19/104 , excluding  E01C19/1036 -- This CPC subclass covers machines, tools or auxiliary devices used for mixing or coating road or similar surfacing by employing a combination of the methods defined in sub-groups E01C19/1018 to E01C19/104, but not the method of E01C19/1036. It falls under the fixed constructions building group for road and sports-ground surfacing.
[E01C 19/1054] Preparing mixtures of which at least part of the solid ingredients has previously been deposited on the surface, excluding penetrating or surface dressing, e.g.  in situ  mixing, mixing involving lifting of spread or windrowed aggregate  preparing and placing such bituminous mixtures  E01C19/463 ; recycling in place or on the road  E01C23/065 -- The entry covers machines, tools or auxiliary devices used to prepare mixtures whose solid components have already been laid on a surface, such as in-situ mixing, lifting spread or windrowed aggregate, and the subsequent placing of bituminous mixes. It also includes equipment for recycling material directly on the road.
[E01C 19/1059] Controlling the operations; Devices solely for supplying or proportioning the ingredients
[E01C 19/1063] Controlling the operations  E01C19/1068  takes precedence -- This subclass covers systems that monitor, regulate, or automate the functioning of equipment used to lay or repair pavements, runways, sports fields and similar surfaces, such as control units, sensors, feedback loops and remote operation devices. It falls under fixed constructions, building, construction of surfaces, and is subordinate to subclass 1068 which takes precedence.
[E01C 19/1068] Supplying or proportioning the ingredients -- The code refers to machines, tools or auxiliary devices that deliver, mix or measure the raw materials used in road-surface construction or repair, such as asphalt, concrete or other surfacing mixes, allowing precise proportioning of the ingredients during the laying or patching process.
[E01C 19/1072] the solid ingredients -- E01C 19/1072 covers machines, tools and auxiliary devices that handle solid ingredients such as aggregates, cement or other particulate materials used in constructing or repairing road surfaces, sports fields and similar structures.
[E01C 19/1077] the liquid ingredients -- The entry E01C 19/1077 covers liquid ingredients such as binders, emulsions, adhesives, bitumen or other fluid materials that are mixed or applied in the construction, repair or resurfacing of roads, sports grounds and similar fixed surfaces. It pertains to the substances used as part of machines, tools or auxiliary devices for these surfacing operations.
[E01C 19/12] for distributing granular or liquid materials -- The CPC subclass E01C 19/12 relates to machines, tools or auxiliary devices that distribute granular or liquid materials when constructing or repairing road surfaces, sports-ground surfaces or similar fixed constructions. It is part of the hierarchy for building and surface construction.
[E01C 19/15] for laying-down uncoated stone or similar materials, or for striking-off or spreading same without compacting, e.g. for crushed rock base courses, sand cushions for paving -- E01C 19/15 covers machines, tools or auxiliary devices for laying down uncoated stone, crushed rock, sand or similar materials in road, sports-ground or similar surfacing without compacting, such as equipment for spreading or striking-off base courses.
[E01C 19/16] for applying or spreading liquid materials, e.g. bitumen slurries -- E01C 19/16 covers machines, tools, or auxiliary devices used to apply or spread liquid materials such as bitumen slurries during the construction or repair of road surfaces and similar structures.
[E01C 19/17] Application by spraying -- E01C 19/17 covers machines, tools or auxiliary equipment used to apply spray mixtures to road surfaces or similar structures for construction, resurfacing or repair, such as paving-asphalts, sealants or protective coatings applied by spraying.
[E01C 19/172] Apparatus with discharge by surpressure in tank -- This subclass covers machines or auxiliary devices used in road, sports-ground or similar surfacing that store material in a tank and release it by applying surpressure, enabling controlled discharge of asphalt, concrete or other surfacing compounds during construction or repair.
[E01C 19/174] Apparatus with pump-discharge -- The entry E01C 19/174 covers machines, tools or auxiliary devices used in the construction or repair of road, sports-ground or similar surfacing that incorporate a pump to discharge material such as asphalt, concrete or other paving compounds. It is classified under fixed constructions, building, and the construction of surfaces for roads, sports grounds or the like.
[E01C 19/176] Spraying or throwing elements, e.g. nozzles; Arrangement thereof or supporting structures therefor, e.g. spray-bars
[E01C 19/178] Elements or attachments for spreading-out or smoothing-down the applied material, e.g. brushes -- E01C 19/178 covers elements or attachments used to spread or smooth applied material in road or sports-ground surfacing, such as brushes and similar devices that are part of machines or tools for constructing or repairing these surfaces.
[E01C 19/18] Devices for distributing road-metals mixed with binders, e.g. cement, bitumen, without consolidating or ironing effect -- Devices for spreading metal aggregate mixed with cement, bitumen or similar binders over road or sports-ground surfaces, applying the material without compacting or ironing it, are covered by E01C 19/18. They belong to the Fixed Constructions group, specifically tools for constructing or repairing surfacing of roads and like structures.
[E01C 19/182] solely for depositing -- E01C 19/182 covers machines, tools or auxiliary devices that are used only to deposit material when constructing or repairing surfacing for roads, sports grounds or similar fixed structures. The equipment is specialised for laying aggregates, binders or other surfacing substances and does not perform any other construction function.
[E01C 19/185] for both depositing and spreading-out or striking-off the deposited mixture  laying-down the materials and consolidating them, or finishing the surface,  E01C19/48
[E01C 19/187] solely for spreading-out or striking-off deposited mixtures, e.g. spread-out screws, strike-off boards -- E01C 19/187 designates machines, tools or auxiliary devices used exclusively to level or spread freshly laid mixtures on surfaces such as roads or sports grounds, including devices like spread-out screws and strike-off boards that distribute or smooth the material.
[E01C 19/20] Apparatus for distributing, e.g. spreading, granular or pulverulent materials, e.g. sand, gravel, salt, dry binders -- This class includes devices that spread or sprinkle granular or powdery materials-such as sand, gravel, salt or dry binders-onto roadways, sports fields or similar surfaces during construction or repair. The apparatus may be vehicle-mounted or stationary and comprises spreaders, distributors and related control mechanisms.
[E01C 19/2005] without driven loosening, discharging or spreading  E01C19/205  takes precedence -- The E01C 19/2005 class defines machines, tools or auxiliary devices used in constructing or repairing road surfaces, sports grounds and similar structures that operate without driven loosening, discharging or spreading of material. It groups equipment that applies or compacts surfacing layers without mechanical dispersion.
[E01C 19/201] with driven loosening, discharging or spreading parts, e.g. power-driven, drive derived from road-wheels  E01C19/205  takes precedence -- E01C 19/201 covers machines, tools or auxiliary devices used in building or repairing road surfaces, sports grounds or similar structures that incorporate driven loosening, discharging or spreading mechanisms. The propulsion may be power-driven or derived from the wheels of the road-working vehicle. This subclass is superseded by E01C 19/205.
[E01C 19/2015] solely reciprocating, swinging to-and-fro, jolting or vibrating, e.g. oscilating stirrers, shaking spreading shoots -- The code E01C 19/2015 covers devices used in fixing constructions such as roads or sports grounds that operate by a purely reciprocating, swinging to-and-fro, jolting or vibrating motion. Typical examples are oscillating stirrers and shaking spreading shoots employed for surfacing work.
[E01C 19/202] solely rotating, e.g. discharging and spreading drums -- This class covers equipment used in road or sports-ground surfacing where material is applied by drums that rotate only, without additional moving parts, such as spreading or discharging drums. It falls under the fixed-construction hierarchy for building and surface construction.
[E01C 19/2025] Throwers with substantially horizontal axis, e.g. drums or brushes rotated to fling the material at the surface -- E01C 19/2025 covers machines, tools or auxiliary devices used for constructing or repairing road, sports-ground or similar surfaces that throw material onto the surface with a substantially horizontal axis, such as rotating drums or brushes that fling the material onto the work area.
[E01C 19/203] Centrifugal spreaders with substantially vertical axis -- This subclass includes centrifugal spreaders whose rotating drum is oriented on a substantially vertical axis for distributing aggregate, sand, or other surfacing material during construction or repair of roads, sports grounds and similar surfaces. The devices are typically mounted on vehicles or trailers and serve as machine, tool or auxiliary equipment for surfacing work.
[E01C 19/2035] both rotating parts and reciprocating, oscillating, jolting or vibrating parts
[E01C 19/204] Throwers or centrifugal spreaders, e.g. spinner spreader with oscillating stirrer -- E01C 19/204 classifies machines that disperse paving material by centrifugal action, including spinner spreaders equipped with oscillating stirrers. These devices are used to lay or repair surfaces of roads, sports fields and similar fixed constructions. The classification covers both the throwing mechanism and any auxiliary stirring components.
[E01C 19/2045] spreading by means of an endless belt or like conveyor -- This subclass covers equipment that spreads surfacing material such as gravel, asphalt or soil using an endless belt or a similar conveyor system, employed in the construction or repair of roads, sports fields and comparable surfaces.
