[E01C 19/205] the material being spread by means of a gaseous current -- This subgroup covers machines, tools or auxiliary devices that lay or spread construction material onto roads, sports grounds or similar surfaces by propelling it with a stream of gas, such as air-blown applicators or pneumatic spreaders. The focus is on the method of delivery rather than the material itself.
[E01C 19/21] for simultaneously but separately applying liquid material and granular or pulverulent material, e.g. bitumen and grit, with or without spreading -- The E01C 19/21 class contains machines, tools or auxiliary devices that apply a liquid binder and a separate granular or pulverulent component, such as bitumen and grit, to road or sports-ground surfaces at the same time, optionally spreading the materials.
[E01C 19/22] for consolidating or finishing laid-down unset materials -- E01C 19/22 covers equipment used to consolidate or finish freshly placed, unset paving materials such as asphalt, concrete or similar surfacing mixtures. The devices may include rollers, compactors, trowels or other tools that smooth, compact or otherwise treat the material before it sets, facilitating uniform surface formation on roads, sports grounds and related structures.
[E01C 19/23] Rollers therefor -- Rollers designated for use in constructing or repairing surface layers of roads, sports grounds, and similar structures are classified under E01C 19/23. They belong to the broader group of machines, tools, or auxiliary devices that facilitate the surfacing of fixed constructions such as highways and athletic fields.
[E01C 19/231] with both elastically-deformable and rigid rolling elements, e.g. pneumatic-tyred compactor with selectively operative or inoperative steel-faced roll  with auxiliary elastically-deformable rolling elements for working only restricted zones  E01C19/27 ; rigid-roll rollers with conventional pneumatic-tyred tractors or traction-units  E01C19/25 ,  E01C19/26 ,  E01C19/28 -- This CPC subclass covers machines and auxiliary devices used to construct or repair road, sports-ground or similar surfacing that combine elastic and rigid rollers. Examples include pneumatic-tyred compactors equipped with selectively active steel-faced rolls plus auxiliary soft rollers for limited zones, as well as rigid-roll rollers driven by conventional pneumatic-tyred tractors.
[E01C 19/233] the rigid rolling elements being vibrated or subjected to impacts  with vibrated elastically-deformable rolling elements  E01C19/287 -- This subclass covers machines, tools or auxiliary devices used in road or similar surface construction that employ rigid rolling elements that are vibrated or subjected to impacts together with elastically-deformable rolling elements, the combination being used to compact, shape or finish the surfacing.
[E01C 19/235] Rolling apparatus designed to roll following a path other than essentially linear, e.g. epicycloidal -- E01C 19/235 covers rolling devices that can follow non-linear paths such as epicycloidal trajectories, used in the construction or repair of road surfaces and similar structures. The classification falls under fixed constructions, building, and construction of roads, sports grounds, or the like, and pertains to machines, tools, or auxiliary devices for surfacing work.
[E01C 19/236] Construction of the rolling elements, e.g. surface configuration, rolling surface formed by endless track -- This class covers machines, tools or auxiliary devices used to build or repair surfacing for roads, sports grounds or similar structures, focusing on the construction of rolling elements such as the surface configuration and rolling surface formed by an endless track. It addresses the design and fabrication of these moving components.
[E01C 19/238] Wetting, cleaning or heating rolling elements, e.g. oiling, wiping, scraping -- The code E01C 19/238 covers devices that treat the rollers or other rotating elements of road- or sports-ground surfacing equipment, providing wetting, cleaning, heating, oiling, wiping or scraping functions to maintain traction, remove debris and control temperature during construction or repair work.
[E01C 19/24] hand propelled -- E01C 19/24 covers hand-propelled machines, tools or auxiliary devices used to construct or repair the surfacing of roads, sports grounds or similar fixed structures. These devices are operated manually without motor assistance and are intended for tasks such as laying, compacting or treating pavement materials.
[E01C 19/25] propelled by animals or vehicles -- Entry E01C 19/25 in the Cooperative Patent Classification covers machines, tools or auxiliary devices for constructing or repairing road surfaces, sports grounds or similar structures that are moved or powered by animals or vehicles rather than by self-propulsion.
[E01C 19/255] by vehicles placed on the rolling device; provided with means for facilitating rapid road transport, e.g. retractable transport wheels -- This subclass covers road-surfacing machines that are mounted on a rolling carriage and equipped with mechanisms such as retractable wheels to allow fast relocation of the device. It pertains to equipment used in the construction or repair of road or similar surfaces, where the vehicle can be placed on the rolling device for transport.
[E01C 19/26] self-propelled or fitted to road vehicles -- E01C 19/26 covers machines, tools or auxiliary devices that are either self-propelled or can be mounted on road vehicles for constructing or repairing road and similar surface structures. The category includes equipment such as paving machines, compactors and surface-preparation tools that operate directly on the road or are carried by a vehicle to perform surfacing work.
[E01C 19/262] pedestrian-controlled, e.g. with safety arrangements for operator -- E01C 19/262 covers pedestrian-controlled machines, tools or auxiliary devices used to construct or repair road, sports-ground or similar surfacing, equipped with safety features that protect the operator while the equipment is manually guided. It falls under the fixed-construction branch for building surface structures.
[E01C 19/264] with attachments for work other than rolling, e.g. grading, scarifying  rollers fitted to road-construction or earth-moving machinery  E01C19/266
[E01C 19/266] fitted to vehicles, road-construction or earth-moving machinery, e.g. auxiliary roll readily movable to operative position  E01C19/268 ,  E01C19/4806 ,  E01C19/488 ,  E01C19/52 ,  E01C21/00 ,  E01C23/065  take precedence ; provided with means for facilitating transport; Means for transporting rollers; Arrangements or attachments for converting vehicles into rollers, e.g. rolling sleeves for wheels -- The E01C 19/266 subclass covers auxiliary rolling devices that can be quickly positioned on road-construction or earth-moving machines, such as movable auxiliary rolls and transport-facilitating means for rollers. It includes arrangements that convert vehicles into rollers, for example rolling sleeves fitted to wheels, and related transport attachments.
[E01C 19/268] designed for rolling surfaces not situated in the plane of the riding surface of the apparatus, e.g. stepped-down surfaces, sloping edge of surfacing  trench rollers  E02D3/032 ; slope rollers  E02D3/039 -- E01C 19/268 covers machines, tools or auxiliary devices used in the construction or repair of road or similar surfaces that roll over non-planar riding surfaces, such as stepped-down areas, sloping edges, trench rollers or slope rollers.
[E01C 19/27] with elastically-deformable rolling elements, e.g. pneumatic tyres -- E01C 19/27 covers machines, tools or auxiliary devices used to construct or repair road, track or sports-ground surfacing that employ elastically-deformable rolling elements such as pneumatic tyres, enabling compacting, levelling or moving materials without damaging the finished surface. This subclass falls under fixed constructions for building and surface work.
[E01C 19/28] Vibrated rollers or rollers subjected to impacts, e.g. hammering blows -- The class covers vibrated rollers and rollers that receive impact blows such as hammering, used to compact or finish road, sports-ground or similar surfacing during construction or repair. These machines apply rapid vertical or oscillatory forces to achieve dense, smooth surfaces and belong to the fixed-construction equipment group.
[E01C 19/281] propelled by hand or by vehicles, e.g. towed  E01C19/285  -  E01C19/288  take precedence -- E01C 19/281 covers machines, tools or auxiliary devices used to construct or repair road or similar surface layers that are propelled manually or by vehicles, such as tow-behind equipment. It includes hand-driven and vehicle-towed apparatus designed specifically for surfacing tasks on roads, sports grounds and related structures.
[E01C 19/282] self-propelled, e.g. with an own traction-unit  E01C19/285  -  E01C19/288  take precedence -- This CPC subclass covers machines, tools or auxiliary devices that are self-propelled, having their own traction unit, for constructing or repairing road surfaces, sports grounds and similar structures. It includes equipment that moves under its own power to lay, compact or treat surfacing materials. The code is E01C 19/282 within the fixed-construction hierarchy.
[E01C 19/283] pedestrian-controlled, e.g. with safety arrangements for operator -- E01C 19/283 covers machines, tools or auxiliary devices used to construct or repair road, sports-ground or similar surfacing that are operated by a pedestrian, typically featuring safety arrangements to protect the operator while working.
[E01C 19/285] with attachments for work other than rolling, e.g. dozer blades, shoes for conversion into plate vibrator; fitted to vehicles, road-construction or earth-moving machinery  E01C19/29 ,  E01C19/4806 ,  E01C19/488 ,  E01C19/52 ,  E01C21/00 ,  E01C23/065  take precedence ; vibrated or the like auxiliary rolls, e.g. for rolling road edges; provided with means for facilitating transport
[E01C 19/286] Vibration or impact-imparting means; Arrangement, mounting or adjustment thereof; Construction or mounting of the rolling elements, transmission or drive thereto, e.g. to vibrator mounted inside the roll  E01C19/287  and  E01C19/288  take precedence -- E01C 19/286 covers devices that impart vibration or impact to assist in constructing or repairing road, sports-ground or similar surfacing, focusing on the arrangement, mounting or adjustment of such means and on the construction or mounting of the rolling elements, transmission or drive to them, for example a vibrator mounted inside a roll. It is subordinated to the more specific groups E01C 19/287 and E01C 19/288.
[E01C 19/287] with vibrated elastically-deformable or elastomer-faced rolling elements or with such elements subjected to impacts, e.g. multi-roll vibratory apparatus with an endless elastomer belt passed around the rolls  rollers with both non-vibrated, elastically-deformable and vibrated rigid rolling elements  E01C19/233 -- The entry E01C 19/287 covers machines, tools or auxiliary devices for surfacing roads or similar structures that employ vibrating or impact-loaded elastically-deformable or elastomer-faced rolling elements, such as multi-roll vibratory apparatus with an endless elastomer belt or rollers that combine non-vibrated, elastically-deformable and vibrated rigid elements.
[E01C 19/288] adapted for monitoring characteristics of the material being compacted, e.g. indicating resonant frequency, measuring degree of compaction, by measuring values, detectable on the roller; using detected values to control operation of the roller, e.g. automatic adjustment of vibration responsive to such measurements -- E01C 19/288 relates to machines, tools or auxiliary devices used in road or similar surface construction that monitor material properties during compaction-such as resonant frequency or degree of compaction-and use the measured values to automatically adjust the roller's operation, for example by varying vibration in response to the detected conditions.
[E01C 19/29] Rolling apparatus adapted to apply a rolling pressure less than its weight, e.g. roller finishers travelling on formrail -- E01C 19/29 covers rolling machines that are designed to exert a rolling pressure lower than the weight of the apparatus itself, such as roller finishers that travel on a form-rail while compacting or finishing surfaces. These devices are used in the construction or repair of road surfaces, sports grounds and similar structures.
[E01C 19/30] Tamping or vibrating apparatus other than rollers -- E01C 19/30 covers machines, tools or auxiliary devices that compact or vibrate road, sports-ground or similar surfaces during construction or repair, excluding conventional rollers. The apparatus employs tamping or vibrating actions to achieve a uniform, stable surface.
[E01C 19/32] Hand-held hand-actuated rammers or tampers -- Hand-operated rammers or tampers that are portable and manually driven for compacting or shaping materials during the construction, repair or maintenance of road surfaces, sports grounds and similar surfacing structures. They are used to improve density and smoothness of granular or soft layers without external power sources.
[E01C 19/34] Power-driven rammers or tampers -- E01C 19/34 covers power-driven rammers or tampers used as machines, tools or auxiliary equipment for constructing or repairing road surfaces and similar structures. These devices provide mechanized impact to compact or level subgrade materials, facilitating fast and efficient surfacing work. They fall under the fixed constructions subsection of building and road construction technologies.
[E01C 19/35] Hand-held or hand-guided tools -- E01C 19/35 covers manually operated devices used in the construction, maintenance or repair of road surfaces, pavements, sports fields and similar structures. This includes hand-held tools such as rakes, shovels, tamping rods, rollers and other portable implements that shape, compact or finish surfacing materials.
[E01C 19/36] with direct-acting explosion chambers -- The classification E01C 19/36 covers machines, tools or auxiliary devices employed in constructing or repairing road, sports-ground or similar surfacing where the equipment incorporates direct-acting explosion chambers, such as pneumatic or explosive units that act directly on the surface to compact, break up or shape the material.
[E01C 19/38] with means specifically for generating vibrations -- E01C 19/38 pertains to machines, tools or auxiliary devices employed in the construction or repair of road, sports-ground or similar surfaces that incorporate a mechanism for generating vibrations. The vibration function is used to compact or settle materials such as asphalt, concrete or aggregate, improving density and surface quality. Examples include vibratory rollers, plate compactors and vibrating tampers.
[E01C 19/40] adapted to impart a smooth finish to the paving, e.g. tamping or vibrating finishers -- E01C 19/40 covers machines, tools or auxiliary devices used in road or sports-ground surfacing that are designed to produce a smooth finish on the pavement, such as tamping rollers or vibrating finishers. These devices are employed during construction or repair to level and compact surface layers.
[E01C 19/402] the tools being hand-guided -- E01C 19/402 refers to hand-guided machines, tools or auxiliary devices employed in constructing or repairing the surfacing of roads, sports grounds and similar structures. It lies within the Fixed Constructions hierarchy under Building and the sub-category for construction of or surfaces for roads, sports grounds or the like.
[E01C 19/405] with spreading-out, levelling or smoothing means other than the tamping or vibrating means for compacting or smoothing, e.g. with screws for spreading-out the previously dumped material, with non-vibratory lengthwise reciprocated smoothing beam  E01C19/407  takes precedence -- This subclass covers machines, tools or auxiliary devices used in road or similar surface construction that spread, level or smooth material by means other than tamping or vibration. Examples include screw-type spreaders that distribute previously dumped material and non-vibratory lengthwise reciprocating smoothing beams.
[E01C 19/407] with elements or parts partly or fully immersed in or penetrating into the material to act thereon, e.g. immersed vibrators or vibrating parts, kneading tampers, spaders -- E01C 19/407 covers machines, tools or auxiliary devices used to construct or repair road surfaces and similar structures that have elements or parts partly or fully immersed in or penetrating the material, for example immersed vibrators, vibrating parts, kneading tampers or spaders.
[E01C 19/41] Apparatus having both rolling tools and ramming, tamping, or vibrating tools -- This classification covers machines that combine rolling tools with ramming, tamping or vibrating mechanisms to compact and finish road or sports-ground surfaces. Such equipment is used in the construction or repair of pavement, allowing simultaneous rolling and compaction in one tool.
[E01C 19/42] Machines for imparting a smooth finish to freshly-laid paving courses other than by rolling, tamping or vibrating -- E01C 19/42 covers machines, tools and auxiliary devices that smooth freshly-laid paving surfaces without employing rolling, tamping or vibration. The equipment is used on roads, sports grounds and similar constructions to impart a uniform, smooth finish after the surfacing material has been placed.
[E01C 19/43] Machines or arrangements for roughening or patterning freshly-laid paving courses, e.g. indenting rollers -- Machines or arrangements that roughen or pattern freshly-laid paving surfaces, such as indenting rollers, belong to this class. They are used during construction of roads, sports grounds or similar surfaces to create texture for better traction, drainage or aesthetic effect.
[E01C 19/44] Hand-actuated tools other than rollers, tampers, or vibrators, specially adapted for imparting a required finish to freshly-laid paving courses -- This subgroup covers manually operated hand tools, excluding rollers, tampers and vibrators, that are specially adapted to impart a required finish to freshly laid paving surfaces such as roads, sports grounds or similar constructions. The tools are used to achieve a specific texture, smoothness or level in the newly placed course.
[E01C 19/45] Portable apparatus for preparing, or for preparing and applying to the road, compound liquid binders, e.g. emulsified bitumen, fluxed asphalt -- This classification covers a mobile machine that mixes and lays liquid road binders such as emulsified bitumen or fluxed asphalt directly onto pavement. The apparatus combines preparation and application functions, enabling on-site treatment of road surfaces. It is intended for use in construction and repair of road surfacing.
[E01C 19/46] for preparing and placing the materials -- E01C 19/46 covers machines, tools or auxiliary devices used to prepare and place materials when constructing or repairing road surfaces, sports grounds or similar fixed structures. It falls under the hierarchy of Fixed Constructions → Building → Construction of, or surfaces for, roads, sports grounds, or the like. This subclass is part of the Cooperative Patent Classification shared by the EPO and USPTO.
[E01C 19/463] Bituminous mixtures of which at least part of the solid ingredients has previously been deposited on the surface, e.g. with lifting of spread or windrowed aggregate -- E01C 19/463 covers bituminous mixtures in which at least part of the solid ingredients has been previously deposited on the surface, such as lifted spread or windrowed aggregate, and are then combined with binder for surfacing roads, sports grounds, or similar structures. This relates to machines, tools or auxiliary devices used to construct or repair these surfaces.
[E01C 19/466] Solid materials, e.g. crushing same -- The CPC class E01C 19/466 falls under Fixed Constructions → Building → Construction of, or surfaces for, roads, sports grounds, or the like, and covers machines, tools or auxiliary devices used to construct or repair road surfaces and similar structures, specifically those that handle solid materials such as crushing, grinding or processing them for surfacing work.
[E01C 19/47] Hydraulic cement concrete mixers combined with distributing means specially adapted for road building -- This CPC entry E01C 19/47 covers hydraulic cement concrete mixers that incorporate a distribution system specially adapted for road building, enabling mixing and placement of concrete directly onto pavements and similar surfaces. It belongs to the fixed constructions building group for machines and tools used in surfacing roads, sports grounds and alike.
[E01C 19/475] Distributing means therefor, e.g. boom-and-bucket arrangements -- The code E01C 19/475 covers devices that spread or distribute material during the construction or repair of road surfaces and similar structures, such as boom-and-bucket arrangements that lay down asphalt, concrete or other surfacing substances. These mechanisms are part of the broader class of machines, tools, or auxiliary devices used for surfacing roads, sports grounds and related constructions.
[E01C 19/48] for laying-down the materials and consolidating them, or finishing the surface -- E01C 19/48 covers machines, tools or auxiliary devices used to lay down, compact and finish materials when constructing or repairing road surfaces, sports grounds or similar surfacing structures.
[E01C 19/4806] with solely rollers for consolidating or finishing  E01C19/4886  takes precedence; combined with other consolidating or finishing means  E01C19/488 -- This subclass covers machines, tools or auxiliary devices used in road or similar surface construction that employ only rollers to consolidate or finish the material, without any additional consolidating or finishing mechanisms. It applies when rollers are the sole means of compacting the surfacing layer, and any combination with other means falls under a different subclass.
[E01C 19/4813] the materials being simultaneously but separately applied binders and granular or pulverulent material  such application  per se   E01C19/21 -- The entry E01C 19/4813 covers machines, tools or auxiliary devices that lay down binders and granular or pulverulent material at the same time but in separate streams for constructing or repairing road or similar surfacing. It relates to equipment that applies the two components simultaneously without mixing them beforehand, enabling precise placement of each material during the surfacing process.
[E01C 19/482] the materials being uncoated stone or similar granular materials, e.g. sand  laying-down uncoated stone or similar materials  per se   E01C19/15 ; distributing granular or pulverung materials  per se   E01C19/20 -- E01C 19/482 pertains to machines, tools or auxiliary devices that lay or distribute uncoated stone, sand or comparable granular materials when constructing or repairing road, track or similar surfaces. It includes equipment for directly placing the granules and for spreading or pulverising them as standalone operations.
[E01C 19/4826] the materials being aggregate mixed with binders  distributing  per se   E01C19/18
[E01C 19/4833] with tamping or vibrating means for consolidating or finishing, e.g. immersed vibrators, with or without non-vibratory or non-percussive pressing or smoothing means  E01C19/4806 ,  E01C19/4886  take precedence; combined with rollers  E01C19/488 -- E01C 19/4833 covers machines, tools or auxiliary devices used in building or repairing road or similar surface constructions that employ tamping or vibrating mechanisms-such as immersed vibrators-to consolidate or finish the surfacing, optionally together with non-vibratory pressing or smoothing elements and combined with rollers.
[E01C 19/484] Rail- or like-borne apparatus, e.g. sliding on side forms, rolling on form rails, kerbs or like trackways -- This class covers devices that travel on rails or similar guides to assist the placement or compaction of formwork, kerbs or other trackways during road or sports-ground surfacing work. Such apparatus may slide along side forms or roll on form rails, providing controlled movement for constructing or repairing surfaces.
[E01C 19/4846] with non-vibratory or non-percussive pressing or smoothing means, e.g. trailing smoothing pans, plates pressing the materials processed by immersed vibrators; with supplemental elements penetrating the paving to work the material thereof, e.g. rodding means -- This subclass relates to machines, tools or auxiliary devices for constructing or repairing road or similar surfaces that use non-vibratory, non-percussive pressing or smoothing actions, such as trailing smoothing pans or pressing plates, and may incorporate supplemental penetrating elements such as rodding means to work the material.
[E01C 19/4853] Apparatus designed for railless operation, e.g. crawler-mounted, provided with portable trackway arrangements -- An apparatus intended for railless operation, such as a crawler-mounted machine, equipped with portable trackway arrangements for constructing or repairing the surfacing of roads, sports grounds or similar structures. It falls under the Cooperative Patent Classification E01C 19/4853 within the fixed-construction building hierarchy.
[E01C 19/486] with non-vibratory or non-percussive pressing or smoothing means; with supplemental elements penetrating the paving to work the material thereof -- The entry covers machines, tools or auxiliary devices for building or repairing road, sports-ground or similar surfacing that operate by non-vibratory, non-percussive pressing or smoothing, and that employ additional elements that penetrate the pavement to work the material.
[E01C 19/4866] with solely non-vibratory or non-percussive pressing or smoothing means for consolidating or finishing  E01C19/4886  takes precedence; combined with rollers  E01C19/488 -- This subclass covers equipment used for constructing or repairing road or similar surfaces that employ only non-vibratory, non-percussive pressing or smoothing actions to consolidate or finish the material, and may be used together with rollers.
[E01C 19/4873] Apparatus designed for railless operation -- The CPC entry E01C 19/4873 covers machines, tools or auxiliary devices used for constructing or repairing road surfaces or similar structures that operate without rails, such as portable or self-propelled equipment designed for surfacing work.
[E01C 19/488] with rollers for consolidating or finishing combined with tamping, vibrating, pressing or smoothing consolidation or finishing means  E01C19/4886  takes precedence -- Machines, tools or auxiliary devices used in constructing or repairing road, sports-ground or similar surfacing that integrate rollers with tamping, vibrating, pressing or smoothing elements to consolidate or finish the surface in a combined operation.
[E01C 19/4886] for forming in a continuous operation kerbs, gutters, berms, safety kerbs, median barriers or like structures  in situ , e.g. by slip-forming, by extrusion  removable forms for kerbs, gutters or similar structures  E01C19/506 ; making conduits  in situ   E02D29/10
[E01C 19/4893] Apparatus designed for railless operation -- This subclass includes machines, tools and auxiliary devices for constructing or repairing road-type surfaces that operate without guiding rails, such as self-propelled rollers, tampers or paving units that can move freely over asphalt, concrete or similar surfacing material.
[E01C 19/50] Removable forms or shutterings for road-building purposes -- Removable forms or shutterings for road-building are temporary molds and framing systems that shape and support concrete or other surfacing materials during construction of roads, parking lots or similar surfaces, and are designed to be dismantled and reused once the material has set.
[E01C 19/502] Removable forms or shutterings, e.g. side forms; Removable supporting or anchoring means therefor, e.g. stakes  for forming individual elements  E01C19/508 ; for grooves or like channels  E01C23/021 -- The code covers temporary shaping devices used in constructing or repairing surfacing for roads, sports grounds and similar structures, such as removable side forms, shutters and supporting or anchoring means like stakes that can be taken away after the material sets, and also tools for forming grooves or similar channels.
[E01C 19/504] adapted to, or provided with, means to maintain reinforcing or load transfer elements in a required position  removable supports for positioning reinforcing or load transfer elements in general  E01C23/045 -- This CPC subclass covers machines, tools or auxiliary devices used in constructing or repairing road and similar surfacing where they are equipped with mechanisms to hold reinforcing or load-transfer elements in the correct position, typically using removable supports for positioning such elements.
[E01C 19/506] for kerbs, gutters or similar structures, e.g. raised portions, median barriers -- The CPC subclass E01C 19/506 covers machines, tools and auxiliary devices specifically designed for constructing, repairing or maintaining kerbs, gutters and comparable fixed road elements such as raised portions and median barriers. It includes equipment that forms or restores these surfacing components as part of road or similar infrastructure construction.
[E01C 19/508] Devices or arrangements for forming individual paving elements  in situ , e.g. by sectioning a freshly-laid slab  permanent forms therefor  E01C9/001 ; imprinting freshly-laid paving to simulate stones  E01C19/43 ; cutting sett paving  E01C23/0906 -- The code E01C 19/508 covers machines, tools and auxiliary devices used on site to shape individual paving units as the surface is laid. It includes equipment for sectioning freshly poured slabs, permanent formwork, imprinting patterns that mimic stone, and cutting set paving.
[E01C 19/52] Apparatus for laying individual preformed surfacing elements, e.g. kerbstones -- A machine or auxiliary device designed to place single preformed surfacing units, such as kerbstones, directly onto road, sports-ground or similar surfaces during construction or repair. It automates the laying process, improving speed and accuracy for fixed-construction projects.
[E01C 19/522] Apparatus for laying the elements by rolling or unfolding, e.g. for temporary pavings -- The subclass covers machines, tools or auxiliary devices that place paving elements by rolling them out or unfolding them, such as mats, plates or boards used for temporary road, sports-ground or similar surfaces. It includes equipment that spreads, flattens or positions these elements during construction or repair.
[E01C 19/524] using suction devices -- The code E01C 19/524 covers machines, tools or auxiliary devices that employ suction mechanisms to aid in the construction or repair of road surfaces, sports grounds or similar paved structures. It falls under the fixed-construction branch for building and surfacing work, specifying the use of suction to lift, position or compact material during the paving process.
[E01C 19/526] hand operated  E01C19/524  takes precedence
[E01C 19/528] with wheels -- The class comprises wheeled machines, tools or auxiliary devices used to construct or repair the surfacing of roads, sports grounds and similar structures. They are designed to travel on the work surface while performing tasks such as laying, compacting or finishing the pavement.
[E01C 2013/006] Transportable sport surfaces for multipurpose stadiums -- E01C 2013/006 classifies inventions relating to transportable sport surfaces designed for use in multipurpose stadiums. It includes modular or removable turf, artificial playing fields, and other floor assemblies that can be assembled, disassembled, moved and re-installed to accommodate different sports or events, as well as the associated methods and devices for handling and securing these surfaces.
[E01C 2013/086] Combination of synthetic and natural grass -- The E01C 2013/086 subclass includes inventions that integrate synthetic turf with natural grass, covering hybrid playing surfaces, landscaping systems and related installation or maintenance methods that combine both material types to improve durability, performance or aesthetic qualities.
[E01C 2019/1081] Details not otherwise provided for -- E01C 2019/1081 is a CPC entry in section E, class 01, subclass C, which deals with soil-working and earth-moving apparatus used in agriculture, forestry, or construction. It classifies machines, tools and devices for excavating, moving, grading, or otherwise shaping earth, with the specific details of this particular entry not otherwise provided.
[E01C 2019/1086] Mixing containers having concentric drums -- E01C 2019/1086 covers mixing containers that incorporate a pair of concentric drums, where an inner drum rotates within a stationary outer drum or vice versa to achieve thorough blending of liquids or granules. These devices are commonly used in agricultural or farm settings for preparing feed, fertilizers, or chemicals.
[E01C 2019/109] Mixing containers having a counter flow drum, i.e. the flow of material is opposite to the gas flow -- A mixing container fitted with a drum in which the solid material circulates opposite to the direction of the gas stream, creating a counter-flow that enhances agitation while drawing gas through the material. This arrangement promotes uniform blending, improves heat or moisture removal, and reduces segregation of bulk solids or powders in processing equipment.
[E01C 2019/1095] Mixing containers having a parallel flow drum, i.e. the flow of material is parallel to the gas flow -- E01C 2019/1095 covers mixing containers that incorporate a drum in which the material travels while a gas stream flows parallel to it, allowing simultaneous agitation and gas-material contact. The parallel-flow arrangement enhances uniform mixing, heat or mass transfer, and can be used in agricultural, food-processing or industrial applications.
[E01C 2019/2055] Details not otherwise provided for -- E01C 2019/2055 is a CPC catch-all for land-working and earth-moving inventions where the particular technical feature does not fit any more specific E01C subclass. It is used for patents on soil-working machines or methods that lack a defined entry elsewhere, allowing them to be classified without detailed subclass assignment.
[E01C 2019/206] Hand operated spreading devices -- E01C 2019/206 covers manually powered spreading devices used in agriculture, horticulture or landscaping to distribute solid or granular materials such as seeds, fertilizers, or granules over soil or planting beds. The entry includes hand-operated spreaders with paddles, plates, disks or similar mechanisms that spread the material by manual actuation.
[E01C 2019/2065] Sensing the quantity of the material actually being distributed -- The code identifies systems or methods that detect and quantify the amount of a material, such as seed, fertilizer or pesticide, actually being released during distribution by agricultural equipment. It covers sensors, measuring devices and related control logic that monitor flow or discharge in real time to ensure accurate application. This helps adjust rates and improve efficiency of field operations.
[E01C 2019/207] Feeding the distribution means -- E01C 2019/207 covers agricultural equipment and methods for supplying material-such as seeds, fertiliser or chemical agents-to the distribution system of a soil-working implement. It includes devices that feed, regulate or control the flow of the material to the planting or spreading components of the machine. The classification is used for patents describing mechanisms that ensure consistent delivery during field operations.
[E01C 2019/2075] with longitudinal conveyor belt -- E01C 2019/2075 covers agricultural or horticultural machines that incorporate a longitudinal conveyor belt for moving harvested material along the length of the machine, such as combine harvesters or forage harvesters. The belt runs parallel to the main axis, transporting crop from the cutting or threshing area toward a discharge or collection point.
[E01C 2019/208] with longitudinal auger -- E01C 2019/208 covers earth-working machines that incorporate a longitudinal auger, a screw-type device running parallel to the machine's direction of travel for drilling, loosening or conveying soil or other materials. The auger is integral to the equipment's structure and operates while the machine moves forward, typically used in trenching, planting or soil-mixing applications.
[E01C 2019/2085] with transverse conveyor belt -- E01C 2019/2085 pertains to earth-working machines that incorporate a transverse conveyor belt, i.e., a belt running across the width of the equipment to move excavated material sideways from the digging area to a discharge point, enabling continuous loading or transport within the same machine.
[E01C 2019/209] with transverse auger -- E01C 2019/209 covers soil-working implements that incorporate a transverse auger, a screw-type device positioned across the width of the tool to convey, mix or loosen soil as the implement moves forward. It is applied to agricultural or forestry machinery where the auger is oriented perpendicular to the direction of travel.
[E01C 2019/2095] by tipping -- E01C 2019/2095 pertains to agricultural or horticultural soil-working where material is moved by tipping, covering devices such as tipper wagons, tipping blades or other implements that lift and dump soil in a single motion.
[E01C 21/00] Apparatus or processes for surface stabilisation for road building or like purposes, e.g. mixing local aggregate with binder -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Recycling in place or on the road involving soil stabilisation -> E01C23/065 - Stabilising soil under existing surfacing -> E01C23/10
[E01C 21/02] Fusing, calcining, or burning soil -- E01C 21/02 covers methods of treating soil by fusing, calcining or burning to improve its properties for use in road construction and similar surface-stabilisation applications. The processes involve heating the soil, often with binders, to create a hardened, durable layer suitable for pavement or sports-ground foundations.
[E01C 2201/00] Paving elements -- E01C 2201/00 classifies individual paving elements such as blocks, slabs, bricks, tiles, interlocking modules and related accessories that are installed to form fixed surfaces for roads, sidewalks, sports grounds and similar constructions. The code covers both prefabricated units and assembled systems used in permanent pavement structures.
[E01C 2201/02] having fixed spacing features -- The subgroup E01C 2201/02 covers paving elements that incorporate permanently fixed spacing features such as regularly spaced ribs, grooves, openings or other geometric gaps built into the unit during manufacture. It applies to prefabricated blocks, tiles or mats where the spacing is predetermined and integral to the element.
[E01C 2201/04] consisting of natural stones and a binder -- The code designates paving elements that are formed from natural stones combined with a binding material such as cement, lime or other binders, creating a composite unit for use in roadways, sports fields or similar surfaces. It falls under the broader group for fixed constructions in building and surface construction.
[E01C 2201/06] Sets of paving elements -- E01C 2201/06 covers sets of paving elements such as pre-manufactured blocks, tiles or slabs that are sold or installed together as a unit for constructing road, sports-ground or similar surfaces. The classification includes combinations designed to provide a continuous paved surface and addresses the arrangement, interlocking or jointing of the elements.
[E01C 2201/065] specially for coverings around manhole-shafts or the like -- E01C 2201/065 relates to specially designed paving elements intended to protect and cover the area around manhole shafts, utility openings or similar recesses in roadways, sports fields or other constructed surfaces. These elements are integrated into the pavement or surfacing system to provide a durable, slip-resistant covering that safeguards the opening while maintaining the functional and aesthetic qualities of the surrounding surface.
[E01C 2201/08] having direction indicating means -- E01C 2201/08 refers to paving elements that incorporate direction-indicating means, such as arrows or markings to guide traffic or users. This subclass covers surfaces for roads, sports grounds, or similar constructions where the paving includes built-in orientation features.
[E01C 2201/10] having build-in shock absorbing devices -- This subgroup includes paving elements that incorporate built-in shock-absorbing devices such as elastic inserts, resilient mats or other structures designed to lessen impact forces on foot or vehicle traffic. It applies to fixed constructions for roads, sports grounds or similar surfaces where the paving component itself provides cushioning, improving safety and comfort.
[E01C 2201/12] vertically interlocking -- This CPC subgroup covers paving elements that lock together by a vertical interlocking system, where the sides of blocks or tiles include protrusions or recesses that engage vertically to create a stable surface for roads, walkways, or sports fields.
[E01C 2201/14] Puzzle-like connections -- E01C 2201/14 refers to interlocking or puzzle-like connections used in paving elements for roads, sports grounds and similar surfaces. The classification covers modular blocks, plates or tiles designed to fit together without mortar, creating a continuous, stable surface through mechanical inter-locking. This enables quick assembly, easy replacement and improved load distribution in fixed construction applications.
[E01C 2201/16] Elements joined together -- E01C 2201/16 classifies paving elements that are assembled from separate parts that are joined together, such as interlocking blocks, modular tiles or composite panels used in road, sports-ground or similar surfaces. It covers the design, connection methods and materials that enable the individual elements to function as a continuous surface.
[E01C 2201/162] with breaking lines -- A paving element that includes breaking lines-pre-formed joints, grooves or weakened zones designed to control cracking or facilitate removal-is classified under E01C 2201/162. This subgroup falls within fixed constructions for roads, sports grounds and similar surfaces, specifying the use of intentional breaking lines in the construction of the paving material.
[E01C 2201/165] with spots of glue -- E01C 2201/165 classifies paving elements that are manufactured with localized applications of adhesive, such as glue spots, to facilitate bonding or anchoring during construction of roads, sports grounds or similar surfaces. This subclass falls under fixed constructions for building and pertains to specialized surface-treatment techniques.
[E01C 2201/167] by reinforcement or mesh -- This subclass covers paving elements that incorporate reinforcement or mesh, such as concrete slabs or blocks strengthened with steel or polymer mesh to improve load-bearing capacity and reduce cracking. The reinforcement can be integral to the element or added during manufacture.
[E01C 2201/18] Elements representing text -- Elements representing text are paving components that bear printed or molded characters, symbols, or numbers on road, sports-ground or similar surfaces, used to convey information, markings or instructions as part of a fixed construction.
[E01C 2201/20] Drainage details -- E01C 2201/20 covers drainage details for paved surfaces, including the design and integration of channels, gutters, perforated plates and other components that collect and remove water from roads, sidewalks, sports fields and similar constructions. It addresses the arrangement, dimensions and materials of these drainage elements within fixed construction.
[E01C 2201/202] Horizontal drainage channels -- The code E01C 2201/202 refers to horizontal drainage channels used in the construction of paved surfaces such as roads and sports fields. It includes channels that collect and convey surface water away from the pavement, typically integrated into the sub-base or surface layer. These elements are designed to improve drainage and prevent water accumulation on the finished surface.
[E01C 2201/205] channels on the top -- The CPC entry E01C 2201/205 covers paving elements that have one or more channels formed on their upper surface. These channels are intended to guide or drain liquids, cables or other lines in roadways, sports fields or similar built-up surfaces, helping control runoff and protect the underlying structure.
[E01C 2201/207] channels on the bottom -- The code designates paving elements that have channels machined or formed on their underside. These channels provide pathways for water drainage, runoff control or the routing of utilities beneath the surface, improving durability and serviceability of roads, tracks or sports fields.
[E01C 23/00] Auxiliary devices or arrangements for constructing, repairing, reconditioning, or taking-up road or like surfaces -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Apparatus or methods focussed on work carried out on existing surfaces, like roughening, cutting, reconditioning or marking out.  Devices for setting-out or checking the configuration of new surfacing; Applications of apparatus for measuring, indicating, or recording the surface configuration of existing surfacing.  Devices for making, treating or filling grooves in not-yet-hardened paving; Devices for introducing inserts or removable insert-supports in not-yet-hardened paving.  Arrangements for curing paving; Devices for applying curing means; Devices for laying prefabricated underlay; Protecting paving under construction or while curing.  Devices for laying reinforcing elements or dowel bars; Removable support for reinforcing elements; Devices for making essentially horizontal ducts in paving.  Devices for installing or removing forms.  Devices for heating or drying foundation, paving, or materials thereon.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Apparatus for reconditioning of salvaged non-hydraulic compositions for in plant recycling of salvaged bituminous mixtures -> E01C19/10 - Marking out playing courts -> A63C19/06 - Removing matter not bonded to the road surface -> E01H1/00 - Roughening or detaching ice -> E01H5/12
[E01C 23/01] Devices or auxiliary means for setting-out or checking the configuration of new surfacing, e.g. templates, screed supports -- E01C 23/01 covers devices and auxiliary means used to set out or verify the geometry of newly laid road or similar surface layers, such as templates, screed supports and other tools that help ensure correct thickness, alignment and profile during construction.
[E01C 2301/00] Machine characteristics, parts or accessories not otherwise provided for
[E01C 2301/02] Feeding devices for pavers -- This CPC subclass E01C 2301/02 relates to feeding devices for pavers used in fixed construction of roads, sports grounds and similar surfaces. It includes hoppers, conveyors, distribution units and other mechanisms that supply paving material to paving machines, enabling continuous laying of asphalt or concrete layers.
[E01C 2301/04] Independent shuttles -- E01C 2301/04 covers independent shuttle devices employed in fixed-construction projects such as the building of roads, sports grounds and similar surfaces, focusing on machine parts or accessories not otherwise classified. These shuttles are self-propelled or manually guided units that transport material or equipment along a predetermined path during the construction of such surfaces.
[E01C 2301/06] Windrow pickup -- The CPC subclass E01C 2301/06 addresses windrow-pickup devices employed in machines that build or surface roads, sports grounds and similar fixed constructions. It falls under machine characteristics, parts or accessories not otherwise provided for, covering mechanisms that collect and convey material from a windrow.
[E01C 2301/08] Pushing devices for lorries -- This CPC subclass covers machines or accessories designed to push lorries, such as tractors or pushers, during the construction of fixed structures like roads, sports fields or similar surfaces. It includes devices that apply force to move loaded or empty vehicles along a work site, facilitating positioning and transport of materials.
[E01C 2301/10] Heated screeds -- Heated screeds are machines or accessories that apply heat to a screed layer-typically bituminous, concrete or other paving material-while simultaneously leveling and compacting it, improving flow and adhesion during the construction of roads, sports grounds or similar surfaces.
[E01C 2301/14] Extendable screeds -- An extendable screed is a machine accessory designed for leveling and finishing surfaces in road, sports-ground or similar construction. It consists of a screed bar that can be telescopically extended to cover a wider width, allowing uniform compaction and smoothing of the surface without requiring multiple passes.
[E01C 2301/16] Laterally slidable screeds -- Laterally slidable screeds are adjustable finishing tools fitted to paving or compacting machines that can shift sideways to widen the compacted surface, allowing uniform surfacing of roads, sports grounds and similar constructions. The entry groups machine characteristics, parts or accessories that are not otherwise provided for in fixed building constructions.
[E01C 2301/18] the whole machine being laterally slidable -- This subclass covers construction machines used for building or surfacing roads, sports grounds and similar surfaces where the entire apparatus is designed to move laterally, allowing side-to-side positioning while operating. It falls under machine characteristics, parts or accessories not otherwise provided for.
[E01C 2301/20] Screed or paver accessories for paving joint or edge treatment -- E01C 2301/20 covers accessories used with screeds or pavers to treat the joints or edges of paved surfaces, such as joint fillers, edge formers or sealing devices, employed in the construction of roads, sports grounds and similar pavements.
[E01C 2301/30] Cabin details -- The entry E01C 2301/30 covers the specific design and features of cabins used in fixed constructions such as buildings, road or sports-ground surfaces, focusing on interior elements, fittings and accessories that are not classified elsewhere. It includes cabin components, layout details and ancillary parts that form part of the overall structure.
[E01C 2301/40] Working platform or walkway -- E01C 2301/40 covers machine characteristics, parts or accessories that relate specifically to working platforms or walkways used in the construction of fixed surfaces such as roads, sports grounds and similar installations.
[E01C 2301/50] Methods or devices for preventing dust by spraying or sucking -- This subclass covers devices and methods used in road, sports-ground and similar surface construction to control dust, typically by applying water spray or suction systems integrated with the equipment. It includes any spray or dust-extraction apparatus not otherwise classified, such as water-spraying nozzles, dust-collecting hoods or vacuum attachments fitted to compactors, rollers or paving machines.
[E01C 23/02] Devices for making, treating or filling grooves or like channels in not-yet-hardened paving, e.g. for joints or markings -- This classification covers auxiliary equipment used during construction of road or similar surfaces to create, treat or fill grooves, channels or joints in fresh paving material before it hardens. It includes devices that form markings, expansion joints or drainage features in the pavement.
[E01C 23/021] Removable, e.g. reusable, forms for grooves or like channels  E01C23/028  takes precedence; groove-forming members of groove-making apparatus  E01C23/025 ,  E01C23/026 ; Installing same prior to placing the paving  into laid paving  E01C23/026 -- The entry covers auxiliary devices for road or similar surface construction that are removable or reusable forms used to create grooves or channels in the paving before it is laid, allowing the forms to be taken away after the surface is set.
[E01C 23/023] Removable joint-body supports  E01C23/045  take precedence ; Installing joint or like bodies, e.g. waterstops, prior to placing the paving  into laid paving  E01C23/026  takes precedence -- The code E01C 23/023 covers auxiliary devices used in the construction or repair of road and similar surfaces, specifically removable supports for joint bodies such as waterstops that are installed before the paving is laid. These supports facilitate the placement and alignment of joints and can be removed after the pavement has cured.
[E01C 23/025] Making or working grooves or like channels in laid paving, e.g. smoothing groove edges  E01C23/026 ,  E01C23/028  take precedence -- E01C 23/025 covers methods and tools for forming or finishing grooves, slots or channels in already laid paving, such as smoothing the edges of existing grooves to improve drainage or surface texture. It is used in road or similar surface construction for repair, reconditioning or maintenance.
[E01C 23/026] Introducing preformed inserts into or filling grooves or like channels in laid paving, with or without concurrent making or working of groove or channel, e.g. filling groove with semi-plastic material  E01C23/028  takes precedence -- This classification covers methods and devices for inserting pre-formed inserts into or filling grooves or similar channels in an already laid paving surface, optionally while the groove is being formed, such as by applying a semi-plastic material. It relates to auxiliary constructions for road, sports-ground or similar surfaces.
[E01C 23/028] Positioning or producing markings, e.g. by forcing marking materials into the surface, by scoring -- E01C 23/028 pertains to devices or methods for applying markings on road or similar surfaces, such as embedding pigment or other material into the pavement or scoring the surface to produce lines, symbols or other indicia. The classification covers techniques used in the construction, repair or maintenance of highways, sports fields and related areas to create visible markings.
[E01C 23/03] Arrangements for curing paving -- The entry E01C 23/03 covers auxiliary devices or arrangements used to cure newly laid paving, such as concrete or asphalt surfaces, after they have been constructed or repaired. It includes systems for controlling temperature, moisture, or chemical processes that accelerate setting and improve the strength and durability of the pavement.
[E01C 23/04] Devices for laying reinforcing elements or dowel bars -- E01C 23/04 pertains to auxiliary devices that assist in positioning reinforcing elements such as dowel bars while constructing, repairing, or reconditioning road surfaces or similar pavements. These tools facilitate accurate placement of steel or other reinforcement to enhance the structural integrity of the finished surface.
[E01C 23/045] Removable, e.g. reusable supports for positioning reinforcing or load transfer elements, adapted or not to also hold joint bodies -- This subclass covers removable, possibly reusable supports used to position reinforcement or load-transfer components in road or similar surface construction, and may also be designed to hold joint bodies. It applies to temporary or permanent devices that aid in placement of rebar, ties, or other structural elements during pavement or surface work.
[E01C 23/05] Devices for installing or removing forms -- E01C 23/05 covers equipment used to set up or take down formwork and similar temporary structures that shape or support road, sports-ground or other surface constructions, such as molds, panels, braces, hydraulic lifts or mechanical removers designed for efficient installation and removal.
[E01C 23/06] Devices or arrangements for working the finished surface -- E01C 23/06 covers auxiliary equipment or methods used to treat, maintain, or finish the surface of roads, pavements, sports fields or similar constructions after they have been laid. It includes tools for smoothing, compacting, cleaning, or applying protective layers to the completed surface.
[E01C 23/065] Recycling in place or on the road, i.e. hot or cold reprocessing of paving  in situ  or on the traffic surface, with or without adding virgin material or lifting of salvaged material; Repairs or resurfacing involving at least partial reprocessing of the existing paving  in situ  mixing of hydraulic cement concrete  E01C19/025 ; apparatus for in-plant recycling  E01C19/1004 ,  E01C19/1036 -- This CPC subclass covers methods and equipment for recycling existing road or similar surfaces directly on the trafficway, either by heating or cold processing, with or without addition of new material, and includes partial re-processing repair, in-situ mixing of hydraulic cement concrete and plant-based recycling apparatus.
[E01C 23/07] Apparatus combining measurement of the surface configuration of paving with application of material in proportion to the measured irregularities -- This CPC entry covers equipment that simultaneously measures the profile of a paved surface and applies corrective material in amounts proportional to the detected irregularities, allowing on-the-fly leveling or resurfacing of roads, runways, sports fields and similar surfaces. It is classified under E01C 23/07 for auxiliary devices used in the construction, repair, or reconditioning of such surfaces.
[E01C 23/08] for roughening or patterning -- The CPC subclass E01C 23/08 covers auxiliary devices or arrangements employed to roughen or create patterns on road, sports-ground or similar surfaces during construction, repair, reconditioning or removal. It falls within the hierarchy of Fixed Constructions → Building → Construction of, or surfaces for, roads, sports grounds, or the like.
[E01C 23/081] by thermal or cryogenic treatment, excluding heating to facilitate mechanical working -- E01C 23/081 covers auxiliary equipment or methods used to construct, repair, recondition or remove road and similar surfaces by applying thermal or cryogenic treatment, but excludes heating that is intended to facilitate mechanical working.
[E01C 23/082] using non-powered tools -- This CPC subclass covers auxiliary devices or arrangements for constructing, repairing, reconditioning, or taking-up road or similar surfaces when non-powered tools are employed. It falls under Fixed Constructions, Building, Construction of, or surfaces for, roads, sports grounds, or the like, with code E01C 23/082.
[E01C 23/0825] rotary, e.g. gang discs -- Auxiliary equipment for road-surface work that employs rotary tools, such as gang discs, to construct, repair, recondition or remove pavement. The devices rotate to grind, smooth or shape the surface, facilitating efficient maintenance of roads, sports grounds and similar areas. This entry falls under the fixed-construction category for building and road-surface construction.
[E01C 23/085] using power-driven tools, e.g. vibratory tools -- The CPC entry E01C 23/085 covers auxiliary devices or arrangements for constructing, repairing, reconditioning or taking-up road or similar surfaces that employ power-driven tools, such as vibratory equipment, to aid the work. It pertains to the use of mechanised tools to compact, vibrate or otherwise treat the surface during construction or maintenance.
[E01C 23/0855] moved rectilinearly, e.g. scabblers -- Auxiliary equipment that travels in a straight line to prepare, scarify or clean road-type surfaces, such as scabblers that move rectilinearly while removing or roughening the pavement.
[E01C 23/088] Rotary tools, e.g. milling drums -- Rotary tools such as milling drums are auxiliary devices used in the construction, repair, reconditioning, or removal of road and similar surfaces; they rotate to grind, level, or finish the pavement, improving smoothness and durability.
[E01C 23/0885] with vertical or steeply inclined rotary axis -- E01C 23/0885 covers auxiliary devices or arrangements used in constructing, repairing, reconditioning or taking-up road, sports-ground or similar surfaces where the rotary axis of the working tool is vertical or steeply inclined, such as vertically-axis rollers, compactors or paving machines designed for these conditions.
[E01C 23/09] for forming cuts, grooves, or recesses, e.g. for making joints or channels for markings, for cutting-out sections to be removed -- The entry E01C 23/09 covers auxiliary devices or arrangements used to create cuts, grooves or recesses in road or similar surfaces, such as those needed for forming joints, channels for markings or sections that are to be removed. It applies to equipment that shapes or removes portions of the surface during construction, repair or re-conditioning.
[E01C 23/0906] for forming, opening-out, cleaning, drying or heating cuts, grooves, recesses or, excluding forming, cracks, e.g. cleaning by sand-blasting or air-jet  E01C23/096 ,  E01C23/0993  take precedence, save for  E01C23/0946 ; for trimming paving edges -- The E01C 23/0906 subclass covers auxiliary tools and arrangements used in building or maintaining road-type surfaces that form, open, clean, dry or heat cuts, grooves or recesses-such as sand-blasting or air-jet cleaning-and for trimming paving edges, while excluding devices that create cracks. It falls under the broader group for constructing, repairing or reconditioning road and similar surfaces.
[E01C 23/0913] with non-powered tools, e.g. trailed router blade -- This subclass refers to auxiliary equipment used in the construction, repair or maintenance of road or similar surfaces where the work is performed with non-powered hand tools, exemplified by a trailed router blade. It falls under the broader group of fixed constructions for building road surfaces.
[E01C 23/092] rotary, e.g. edge trimmer disc -- E01C 23/092 covers rotary auxiliary devices, for example edge-trimmer discs, used to finish or recondition the edges of road or similar surfaces. These tools are employed in the construction, repair or maintenance of pavements, sports fields and other built-up surfaces.
[E01C 23/0926] with power-driven tools, e.g. vibrated, percussive cutters -- The entry covers auxiliary devices or arrangements that employ power-driven tools such as vibrated or percussive cutters for constructing, repairing, reconditioning, or removing road or similar surfaces. It relates specifically to mechanised cutting actions used in fixed construction of highways, sports grounds, and comparable trackways.
[E01C 23/0933] rotary, e.g. circular-saw joint cutters -- The subclass E01C 23/0933 pertains to rotary cutting tools, for example circular-saw joint cutters, used as auxiliary devices in the construction, repair, reconditioning or removal of road, sports-ground or similar surfaces within fixed-construction projects. These tools cut joints in paving or surfacing elements.
[E01C 23/094] about vertical or inclined axes, e.g. for core sampling  E01C23/0946  takes precedence -- Auxiliary devices or arrangements used in the construction, repair, reconditioning or removal of road or similar surfaces that operate on a vertical or inclined axis, such as tools for taking core samples from the pavement. This sub-group falls under the broader category of fixed constructions for roads and sports grounds.
[E01C 23/0946] specially for forming recesses to receive marking materials or markers, e.g. cutters for milling traffic line channels, core drills for button-setting  other tools  E01C23/0993 -- This CPC subclass falls under fixed constructions for building road or sports-ground surfaces and concerns auxiliary devices used in constructing, repairing or taking-up such surfaces. It specifically covers tools that form recesses to receive marking materials or markers, including cutters for milling traffic-line channels, core drills for button-setting and similar equipment.
[E01C 23/0953] by thermal effect, e.g. cutting by melting, cleaning drying or heating with hot jets -- Auxiliary devices or arrangements that employ thermal effects-such as melting-cutting, cleaning, drying or heating with hot jets-to construct, repair, recondition or remove road, sports-ground or similar surfaces. This entry is part of the fixed-construction hierarchy for building and surface construction.
[E01C 23/096] for forming, opening-out, cleaning, drying or heating and filling  E01C23/0993  takes precedence -- The entry E01C 23/096 covers auxiliary devices or arrangements used in the construction, repair, reconditioning, or removal of road or similar surfaces that perform functions such as forming, opening-out, cleaning, drying, heating or filling. It includes tools, machines or systems specifically designed to prepare the surface before or during the laying of pavement or other surfacing material. This subclass is subordinate to the broader category for auxiliary devices in road and sports-ground construction.
[E01C 23/0966] for filling or priming, with or without working the surface of the filling or applying particulate material thereto, e.g. for filling the joints of stone-sett paving  E01C23/096 ,  E01C23/0993  take precedence -- The entry E01C 23/0966 covers auxiliary means for filling or priming road-type surfaces, including methods that work the surface or simply apply particulate material, such as filling the joints of stone-set paving. It pertains to devices or arrangements used during construction, repair, reconditioning or removal of such surfaces.
[E01C 23/0973] with liquid or semi-liquid materials, e.g. crack sealants -- E01C 23/0973 covers auxiliary devices or arrangements used in constructing, repairing, reconditioning or removing road or similar surfaces that apply liquid or semi-liquid materials such as crack sealants.
[E01C 23/098] and working the surface of the filling or applying particulate material thereto, e.g. smoothing, gritting -- This subclass covers equipment or processes that treat the surface of a road or similar fill by working it and applying particulate material, such as smoothing or gritting, to improve texture, traction or durability.
[E01C 23/0986] with preformed elements, e.g. joint strips -- This subgroup covers auxiliary devices or arrangements used in the construction, repair, reconditioning or removal of road or similar surfaces that employ pre-formed components such as joint strips. It relates to fixed constructions in building and infrastructure, specifically to methods that incorporate ready-made elements to form or maintain surface joints.
[E01C 23/0993] for forming or installing surface markings or signals in the paving, e.g. grooving for striping or for producing rumble strips, forming marker-receiving recesses  E01C23/0946  takes precedence -- E01C 23/0993 covers auxiliary methods and devices used to form or install markings and signals directly in paved surfaces, such as grooves for lane striping, rumble-strip grooves, or recesses that receive road markers.
[E01C 23/10] for raising or levelling sunken paving -- The code E01C 23/10 covers auxiliary equipment or methods used to raise or level sunken paving during construction, repair, reconditioning, or removal of road or similar surfaces. It includes devices that lift depressed sections of pavement to restore a uniform surface.
[E01C 23/12] for taking-up, tearing-up, or breaking-up paving, -- This CPC subclass covers auxiliary equipment and arrangements used to take up, tear up, or break up existing paving and similar road or sports-ground surfaces. It includes devices that facilitate removal, demolition or disassembly of constructed surfaces for repair, reconstruction or reclamation purposes.
[E01C 23/121] with non-powered tools, e.g. rippers  rollers with ripping attachments  E01C19/264 ,  E01C19/27 ,  E01C19/285 ; roughening or patterning using non powered tools  E01C23/082 -- E01C 23/121 covers auxiliary devices and arrangements that assist in constructing, repairing, reconditioning or removing road or similar surfaces when the work is performed with non-powered tools. It includes rippers, rollers fitted with ripping attachments and equipment for roughening or patterning the surface manually.
[E01C 23/122] with power-driven tools, e.g. oscillated hammer apparatus  hand-held breakers: percussion tools  B25D -- Auxiliary devices for constructing, repairing or removing road or similar surfaces that use power-driven percussion tools such as hand-held oscillating hammers or breakers. The entry covers arrangements that incorporate these powered hammer apparatuses to break, cut or shape pavement materials during construction or maintenance.
[E01C 23/124] moved rectilinearly, e.g. road-breaker apparatus with reciprocating tools, with drop-hammers -- This subclass covers auxiliary equipment that moves in a straight line to apply impact forces for constructing, repairing, or reconditioning road or similar surfaces, typically using reciprocating tools or drop-hammers. Examples include road-breaker machines that advance rectilinearly while repeatedly striking the pavement to break or level it.
[E01C 23/125] with direct-acting explosion chambers, i.e. Diesel breakers -- The subclass E01C 23/125 covers auxiliary equipment for constructing, repairing or resurfacing road or similar surfaces that employs diesel breakers with direct-acting explosion chambers. These devices generate controlled explosions to break or remove pavement material, aiding in the building or maintenance of such surfaces.
[E01C 23/127] rotary, e.g. rotary hammers -- The entry E01C 23/127 covers rotary tools such as rotary hammers that are employed as auxiliary devices for constructing, repairing, reconditioning or taking-up road or similar surfaces. It falls under the hierarchy of Fixed Constructions → Building → Construction of, or surfaces for, roads, sports grounds, or the like.
[E01C 23/128] with hydrojets  hydraulic cleaning  E01H1/101
[E01C 23/14] for heating or drying foundation, paving, or materials thereon, e.g. paint -- Auxiliary devices or arrangements used during the construction or repair of road, sports-ground or similar surfaces that provide heat or drying action to foundations, pavement layers or surface coatings such as paint, facilitating quicker setting, curing or removal of moisture.
[E01C 23/16] Devices for marking-out, applying, or forming traffic or like markings on finished paving -- The code covers devices used to mark, apply or form traffic or like markings on finished paving surfaces. It includes equipment for layout, paint or thermoplastic application, stencil or pattern formation, and tools that aid in painting, spraying or embossing road lines, symbols or other surface markings.
[E01C 23/163] for marking-out the location of the markings; Guiding or steering devices for marking apparatus, e.g. sights -- A device or arrangement belonging to the auxiliary tools for constructing or maintaining road or similar surfaces, designed to help position or steer marking equipment. It includes sights, guides or steering mechanisms that indicate where line or surface markings should be applied.
[E01C 23/166] Means for dispensing particulate material on to freshly applied markings or into the marking material after discharge thereof, e.g. reflective beads, grip-improving particles -- This subclass covers devices that apply particulate material-such as reflective beads or grip-enhancing particles-to freshly laid road or surface markings, either during the initial lay-down or by injecting the material into the marking after it has been deposited. It is part of the fixed-construction hierarchy for building and constructing road or similar surfaces.
[E01C 23/18] for applying prefabricated markings -- The entry covers auxiliary devices or arrangements used to apply prefabricated markings - such as road or surface markings, signs, or safety strips - to roads, sports grounds or similar surfaces during construction, repair or resurfacing.
[E01C 23/185] Tape- or sheet-shape markers or carriers -- Auxiliary devices used in road or similar surface construction that consist of tape- or sheet-shaped markers or carriers. These items are applied to delineate, guide or protect sections during building, repair, reconditioning or removal of pavements, sports grounds and related surfaces.
[E01C 23/20] for forming markings -- The code E01C 23/20 relates to auxiliary devices or arrangements used to form markings on road, pavement, sports-ground or similar surfaces, such as equipment for applying lane lines, road symbols or field markings during construction, repair or maintenance.
[E01C 23/203] the marking material being applied to the surface by contact with an application tool, e.g. paint roller -- E01C 23/203 pertains to auxiliary devices that apply marking material, such as paint or dye, to road, sports-ground or similar surfaces by direct contact with an application tool, for example a roller. It covers mechanisms used in the construction, repair or reconditioning of fixed surfaces to create visible markings.
[E01C 23/206] by applying fusible particulate material to the surface, e.g. by means of a gaseous stream -- This subclass covers methods and apparatus that apply fusible particulate material to road or similar surfaces, typically by a heated gaseous stream that melts the particles to form a binding coating for construction, repair, or resurfacing. It falls under fixed constructions for buildings and the construction of roads, sports grounds and the like.
[E01C 23/22] by spraying -- E01C 23/22 covers auxiliary equipment and methods that form or restore road or similar surfaces by spraying material such as binders, sealants, tackifiers or rejuvenators. It includes spray applicators, nozzles, pumps and control systems designed to deposit the coating uniformly, and the associated processes for surface preparation and curing.
[E01C 23/222] specially adapted for automatic spraying of interrupted, individual or variable markings -- This subclass covers devices attached to road-building equipment that are specially adapted to spray interrupted or variable markings automatically, such as lane delimiters, symbols or temporary lines. It pertains to auxiliary arrangements for constructing or maintaining road or similar surfaces.
[E01C 23/225] comprising means for mechanically working the applied material, e.g. smoothing brushes -- E01C 23/225 covers auxiliary devices used in constructing, repairing or reconditioning road or similar surfaces that incorporate mechanisms for mechanically working the applied material, such as smoothing brushes or other tools that finish or compact the surface. It is a sub-group of devices for road-surface construction and finishing.
[E01C 23/227] the marking material being supplied in aerosol containers -- The subclass covers marking materials that are supplied in aerosol containers for use in the construction, repair, reconditioning or maintenance of road, pavement, sports-ground or similar surfaces. The aerosol delivery enables quick, even application of paint or other marking compounds to define lanes, markings or work zones.
[E01C 23/24] by pouring -- E01C 23/24 covers auxiliary devices or arrangements used when constructing, repairing, reconditioning or taking up road, sports-ground or similar surfaces by pouring, such as equipment for mixing, transporting, distributing and shaping poured materials like concrete, mortar or asphalt. It falls under fixed constructions, building and the construction of surfaces for roads or sports grounds.
[E01C 23/243] specially adapted for automatic pouring of interrupted, individual or variable markings -- The code E01C 23/243 covers devices specially adapted to automatically pour interrupted or variable road markings such as lane lines, symbols or other surface patterns. These systems are used in road construction or maintenance to apply individual or varying markings without manual labor.
[E01C 23/246] comprising means for mechanically working the applied material, e.g. smoothing brushes -- E01C 23/246 covers auxiliary devices used in building or repairing road and similar surfaces that incorporate mechanical means to work the applied material, for example smoothing brushes that level or finish the surface. The class falls under Fixed Constructions - Building - Construction of, or surfaces for, roads, sports grounds, or the like.
[E01C 3/00] Structure of roads, playgrounds, sports grounds, airfields -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Arrangements of foundations; Use of special materials for building up the foundation layers of the road; Protecting foundations from destructive influences.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Prefabricated foundation elements for prefabricated paving elements -> E01C5/001 , E01C5/085 , E01C5/223 - Foundations specially adapted for playgrounds or sports grounds -> E01C13/02 - Draining the subbase of roads -> E01F5/00
[E01C 3/003] characterised by material or composition used, e.g. waste or recycled material  E01C3/006 ,  E01C3/04  and  E01C3/06  take precedence
[E01C 3/006] made of prefabricated single units  E01C3/06  takes precedence -- E01C 3/006 covers the construction of roads, airfields, playgrounds and sports surfaces using prefabricated single units such as modular blocks, panels or slabs that are installed as complete units.
[E01C 3/02] Concrete base for bituminous paving -- The E01C 3/02 subclass addresses the use of a concrete base beneath bituminous surfacing in the construction of roads, airfields, playgrounds and sports grounds. It covers the design, composition and thickness of the concrete sub-base required to support and distribute loads for asphalt pavements. This includes specifications for material properties, reinforcement and installation methods to ensure a stable, durable foundation.
[E01C 3/04] Foundations produced by soil stabilisation -- This subclass covers foundations that are formed by treating the soil to improve its engineering properties, such as mixing with cement, lime, bitumen, or other stabilising agents before or during placement. It includes methods and materials used to create stabilized ground for footings, slabs, or base layers of roads, airfields, playgrounds, and sports fields. The focus is on the preparation of the soil itself rather than conventional concrete or piled foundations.
[E01C 3/06] Methods or arrangements for protecting foundations from destructive influences of moisture, frost or vibration -- E01C 3/06 concerns techniques for shielding the foundations of fixed constructions such as roads, airfields, playgrounds or sports fields from damaging moisture, frost or vibration. It includes protective barriers, drainage systems, insulation, vibration-dampening devices and related arrangements that maintain structural integrity under environmental stresses.
