[E01C 5/00] Pavings made of prefabricated single units -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The covering of roads or sidewalks consisting of prefabricated single units:  Prefabricated foundation elements and individual couplings for these paving units.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Gratings -> E01C9/10 - Prefabricated paving units specially adapted for playgrounds or sports grounds -> E01C13/04 - Prefabricated pavings specially adapted for footpaths, sidewalks or cycle tracks and not being simple paving slabs (for example prefabricated elements for forming elevated sidewalks). -> E01C15/00
[E01C 5/001] on prefabricated supporting structures or prefabricated foundation elements except coverings made of layers of similar elements -- This class concerns the use of prefabricated supporting structures or foundation elements-such as blocks, piles, mats or other single-unit components-for constructing roads, sports grounds and similar paved surfaces, but it excludes coverings made from layered, identical elements.
[E01C 5/003] characterised by material or composition used for beds or joints; characterised by the way of laying -- This CPC subclass covers prefabricated single-unit pavings where the defining feature is the material or composition of the bedding or joint system, or the specific method of installation. It pertains to fixed constructions for roads, sports grounds, and similar applications.
[E01C 5/005] Individual couplings or spacer elements for joining the prefabricated units  E01C5/10  takes precedence -- This CPC subclass covers devices such as couplings or spacers that connect individual prefabricated paving units in fixed construction applications like road or sports ground surfaces. It is placed under E01C 5/005 within the hierarchy of pavings made of prefabricated single units and is overridden by the broader E01C5/10 group.
[E01C 5/006] Individual spacer elements -- Entry E01C 5/006 concerns individual spacer elements that are inserted between prefabricated single-unit paving blocks. These components keep a constant gap, aid load distribution and provide drainage in fixed constructions such as road or sports-ground surfaces.
[E01C 5/008] made of vegetable stems, e.g. straw, thatch  of wood  E01C5/14 ; made of textiles  of bituminous textile webs  E01C5/12 ; of linoleum  E01C5/20 -- E01C 5/008 concerns prefabricated single-unit pavings used on roads, sports grounds or similar surfaces that are made from non-concrete materials such as vegetable stems or straw, wood, textiles, bituminous textile webs or linoleum.
[E01C 5/02] made of natural stones, e.g. sett stones -- This subclass covers paving elements formed from natural stone such as sett or cobblestone blocks used in roads, sports grounds or similar surfaces. It falls under fixed constructions, building and the construction of surfaces for roads, sports grounds or the like.
[E01C 5/04] made of bricks -- E01C 5/04 covers pavings that consist of individual prefabricated bricks, used in the construction of roads, sports grounds, parking areas and similar surfaces. The code applies to fixed brick units placed in regular patterns to form durable, load-bearing surfaces.
[E01C 5/06] made of units with cement or like binders -- The E01C 5/06 entry covers pavements formed from prefabricated single units that are bonded together with cement, lime, or comparable binders. It applies to fixed constructions such as road surfaces, sports-ground surfacing, and other built-up flooring systems using these unitary, binder-held elements.
[E01C 5/065] characterised by their structure or component materials, e.g. concrete layers of different structure, special additives
[E01C 5/08] Reinforced units -- E01C 5/08 refers to reinforced prefabricated paving units, such as concrete blocks or pavers that incorporate steel or other reinforcement to increase strength and load-bearing capacity. These units are employed in the construction of roads, sports grounds, parking areas and similar surfaces where durable, modular surfacing is required.
[E01C 5/085] on prefabricated supporting structures or prefabricated foundation elements except coverings made of layers of similar elements -- This subclass concerns the use of prefabricated supporting structures or foundation elements in the construction of pavements, roads, sport grounds and similar surfaces, excluding layered covering systems made from repeated similar units.
[E01C 5/10] Prestressed reinforced units -- E01C 5/10 covers prefabricated paving elements that incorporate prestressed reinforcement, such as concrete blocks or tiles with tensioned steel to improve load-bearing capacity and durability, used in road or sport-ground surfaces.
[E01C 5/105] on prefabricated supporting structures or prefabricated foundation elements, except coverings made of layers of similar elements -- E01C 5/105 concerns the use of prefabricated supporting structures or foundation elements in the construction of fixed surfaces such as roads, pavements, sports grounds or similar installations, excluding coverings that consist of layered, similar units.
[E01C 5/12] made of units with bituminous binders -- The subclass E01C 5/12 concerns pavings made from prefabricated single units that are joined with a bituminous binder. It includes methods and products for constructing permanent road, sports-ground or similar surfaces using these bonded units.
[E01C 5/14] made of wooden units -- E01C 5/14 covers pavements formed from prefabricated wooden units, used in the construction of roads, sports fields and similar surfaces. The classification applies to fixed constructions where the flooring consists of individual wooden blocks or planks assembled on site.
[E01C 5/16] made of metallic units -- E01C 5/16 covers pavings constructed from prefabricated metallic units, such as steel or aluminum plates, bars, or lattice sections that are assembled to form road, sports-ground or similar surfaces. The classification applies to designs where the metallic elements are the primary structural component of the paving system.
[E01C 5/18] made of rubber units -- E01C 5/18 designates pavings formed from prefabricated rubber units, intended for fixed constructions such as roads, sports fields, or similar surfaces. The entry addresses the use of rubber blocks or tiles as the primary material for these surfaces.
[E01C 5/20] made of units of plastics,
[E01C 5/22] made of units composed of a mixture of materials covered by two or more of groups , -- E01C 5/22 covers pavings constructed from prefabricated single units that are made of a mixture of two or more material groups, such as stone, bitumen, plastic or similar composites, used for surfaces of roads, sports grounds or related fixed constructions.
[E01C 5/223] on prefabricated supporting or prefabricated foundation units, except coverings made of layers of similar elements -- This CPC subclass covers pavings that employ prefabricated supporting or foundation units, such as individual blocks or mats used to form a stable base, while excluding coverings composed of layered, similar elements. It falls under fixed constructions for roads, sports grounds and comparable surfaces.
[E01C 5/226] having an upper layer of rubber, with or without inserts of other materials; with rubber inserts -- E01C 5/226 designates prefabricated paving units whose surface is formed from a rubber layer, which may be solid or combined with inserts of other materials, including additional rubber inserts. The code applies to fixed construction of roads, sports fields and similar surfaces where such rubber-capped units are employed.
[E01C 7/00] Coherent pavings made -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Roads surfaces including road toppings, consisting of coherent paving made at the place of use, the road surfaces being made of road-metal with or without binders or by subjecting soil to stabilisation.  Repairing of concrete or bituminous roads.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Foundations for roads -> E01C3/00 - Individual units made from concrete cast in situ -> E01C9/001 - Coherent pavings made in situ specially adapted for playgrounds or sports grounds -> E01C13/06 - Coherent pavings made in situ specially adapted for footpaths, sidewalks or cycle tracks -> E01C15/00  **Glossary:** - Road-metal: Aggregate - Coherent paving: Paving made of a mass of cohering material
[E01C 7/02] made of road-metal without binders -- E01C 7/02 classifies coherent pavement structures that consist solely of compacted road-metal aggregates without any binding agents. These pavings are used for roads, sports grounds and similar surfaces and rely on mechanical interlocking of the aggregate to achieve stability.
[E01C 7/04] of broken stones, gravel, or like materials -- E01C 7/04 designates coherent pavings constructed from broken stones, gravel or comparable aggregates. It falls under fixed constructions for roads, sports grounds and similar surfaces, covering methods of laying and compacting these granular materials.
[E01C 7/06] by melting, burning, or vitrifying road-metal -- E01C 7/06 covers the production of coherent pavement or road surfaces by heating processes that melt, burn or vitrify the road-metal material, forming a solidified layer that hardens into a durable surface for roads, sports grounds or similar applications.
[E01C 7/08] made of road-metal and binders -- The CPC subclass E01C 7/08 covers coherent pavement structures that are formed from road-metal mixed with binders, creating a stable, self-supporting layer. It falls under fixed constructions for roads, sports grounds and similar surfaces.
[E01C 7/085] Aggregate or filler materials therefor; Coloured reflecting or luminescent additives therefor  thin surface layers or surface dressings  E01C7/35 -- The E01C 7/085 subclass covers the aggregate or filler substances used in fixed constructions such as roads, sports grounds and similar surfaces, as well as coloured, reflecting or luminescent additives incorporated in thin surface layers or dressings. It deals with the composition and application of these materials to modify the appearance, reflectivity or luminescence of the paved surface.
[E01C 7/10] of road-metal and cement or like binders -- E01C 7/10 covers coherent pavement structures in which road-metal aggregates are bound together with cement or similar binders, forming a solid, monolithic surface. This subclass includes the composition, preparation, and laying techniques for such road-metal-and-cement pavings used in roads, sports grounds, and related constructions.
[E01C 7/12] Mortar-bound paving -- E01C 7/12 covers mortar-bound paving, i.e., coherent pavings made by binding stones or other units with mortar, used for roads, sports grounds, or similar surfaces within the fixed constructions building subclass.
[E01C 7/14] Concrete paving -- E01C 7/14 covers concrete paving, including slabs, blocks or other prefabricated concrete units used to form road surfaces, sidewalks, parking areas, sports fields and similar fixed constructions. The subclass relates to the design, manufacture, installation and maintenance of concrete surfaces intended for vehicular or pedestrian traffic and associated load-bearing applications.
[E01C 7/142] Mixtures or their components, e.g. aggregate  E01C7/147 ,  E01C7/355  take precedence; different layers of different composition  E01C7/14 -- E01C 7/142 concerns the selection and use of specific mixtures or component materials such as aggregates in coherent pavings, including the application of separate layers that have different compositions, and relates to the construction of road, sports-ground or similar surfaces.
[E01C 7/145] Sliding coverings, underlayers or intermediate layers  isolating underlayers  E01C3/06 ; sliding layers between coverings of different materials  E01C7/34 ; Isolating or separating intermediate layers; Transmission of shearing force in horizontal intermediate planes, e.g. by protrusions, by inlays  anchoring new concrete wearing layers on old concrete paving  E01C7/147 -- The code E01C 7/145 pertains to fixed construction of pavings such as roads or sports grounds and specifically addresses sliding coverings, underlayers or intermediate layers that isolate or separate surfaces. It includes isolating underlayers, sliding layers between different materials, and mechanisms for transmitting shear forces in horizontal planes, for example by protrusions or inlays used to anchor new concrete wearing layers onto existing concrete paving.
[E01C 7/147] Repairing concrete pavings, e.g. joining cracked road sections by dowels, applying a new concrete covering  applying thin surface layers with other binder  E01C7/35 ; repairing pavings in general  E01C11/005 ; roughening by means of tools  E01C11/24 -- This classification covers repair of concrete pavings, including joining cracked road sections with dowels, applying a new concrete overlay or thin surface layers using alternative binders, as well as general paving repair and surface roughening with tools.
[E01C 7/16] Prestressed concrete paving -- Prestressed concrete paving refers to fixed paving structures built from concrete elements that have been prestressed with steel tendons or rods to improve load-bearing capacity and reduce cracking. These pavings are used for roads, sports grounds and similar surfaces, offering durability and reduced maintenance.
[E01C 7/18] of road-metal and bituminous binders -- This subclass covers the preparation and laying of road surfaces that use a mixture of mineral aggregates (road-metal) combined with bituminous binders, such as asphalt concrete and related surfacing layers. It includes methods for mixing, compacting and finishing these binders to form coherent pavings for roads, sports grounds and similar constructions.
[E01C 7/182] Aggregate or filler materials, except those according to  E01C7/26   E01C7/187 ,  E01C7/353  take precedence; different aggregates or fillers  E01C7/18 -- This CPC subclass concerns aggregate or filler materials used in fixed constructions such as roads, sports grounds or similar surfaces, but excludes those covered by sub-groups E01C7/26, E01C7/187 and E01C7/353, and it addresses situations where different aggregates or fillers are employed.
[E01C 7/185] Isolating, separating or connecting intermediate layers, e.g. adhesive layers; Transmission of shearing force in horizontal intermediate planes, e.g. by protrusions  E01C7/187  takes precedence; adhesive layers for materials  E01C7/35 -- The code E01C 7/185 classifies pavement technologies that isolate, separate or connect intermediate layers, such as adhesive layers, and that transmit shearing forces across horizontal planes often by protrusions. It falls under fixed constructions for roads and sport grounds and is superseded by the more specific sub-group E01C 7/187, while related adhesive-layer topics are covered in E01C 7/35.
[E01C 7/187] Repairing bituminous covers, e.g. regeneration of the covering material  in situ , application of a new bituminous topping  application of thin surface layers with other binders  E01C7/35 ; repairing pavings in general  E01C11/005 -- The CPC subclass E01C 7/187 covers in-situ repair of bituminous pavements, including regeneration of the existing cover, application of new bituminous toppings, and placement of thin surface layers bound with other binders. It is classified under fixed constructions, building, and the construction of roads, sports grounds, or similar surfaces.
[E01C 7/20] Binder incorporated in cold state, e.g. natural asphalt -- E01C 7/20 relates to methods or materials for forming coherent pavement where the binding agent is added while cold, such as using natural asphalt or other binders that do not require heating before placement. This sub-class addresses cold-mixed paving techniques for roads, sports grounds and similar surfaces.
[E01C 7/22] Binder incorporated in hot state, e.g. heated bitumen -- The code E01C 7/22 covers pavement constructions in which the binding material is introduced while hot, such as heated bitumen mixed with aggregates to form a hot-mix asphalt. This approach ensures the binder penetrates and adheres to the aggregate particles before cooling and solidifying, creating a cohesive, load-bearing surface for roads, sports fields, or similar applications.
[E01C 7/24] Binder incorporated as an emulsion or solution -- E01C 7/24 covers pavements where the binding material is applied as an emulsion or liquid solution, typically mixing bituminous or polymeric binders with aggregates to create a coherent surface for roads, sports grounds or similar constructions.
[E01C 7/26] mixed with other materials, e.g. cement, rubber, leather, fibre -- The CPC entry E01C 7/26 covers coherent paving structures that are formulated by mixing the primary paving material with other substances such as cement, rubber, leather or fibres to enhance durability, elasticity or other performance characteristics. It falls under fixed constructions for roads, sports grounds and similar surfaces where composite or reinforced pavements are specified.
[E01C 7/262] with fibrous material, e.g. asbestos; with animal or vegetal admixtures, e.g. leather, cork  E01C7/265  takes precedence -- E01C 7/262 covers the construction of fixed pavings for roads, sports grounds or similar surfaces that incorporate fibrous materials such as asbestos, or animal and vegetal admixtures like leather or cork. It specifies the use of these reinforcing or filler substances in the paving layer and takes precedence over the broader subclass E01C 7/265.
[E01C 7/265] with rubber or synthetic resin, e.g. with rubber aggregate, with synthetic resin binder  for thin overlays or surface dressings  E01C7/35 -- E01C 7/265 relates to fixed-construction surface treatments such as thin overlays or dressings in which the aggregate or binder contains rubber or a synthetic resin, often with rubber particles or a resin-based binder. It is used for improving or protecting pavements, roadways, sports fields and similar surfaces by applying a flexible, polymer-enhanced layer.
[E01C 7/267] with sulfur
[E01C 7/30] of road-metal and other binders, e.g. synthetic material -- E01C 7/30 covers coherent pavings formed from road-metal mixed with synthetic binders or other non-traditional materials, describing the composition and preparation of such surfaces for roads, sports grounds or similar applications. It pertains to the use of synthetic or polymeric binders to impart stability and durability to the pavement structure.
[E01C 7/32] of courses of different kind made -- E01C 7/32 covers the construction of coherent pavement surfaces in which the course (layer) composition varies, using different types of material or thicknesses within a single pavement structure. It applies to roads, sport grounds and similar surfaces where mixed or heterogeneous course designs are employed.
[E01C 7/325] Joining different layers, e.g. by adhesive layers; Intermediate layers, e.g. for the escape of water vapour, for spreading stresses  sliding layers  E01C7/34 -- The code E01C 7/325 covers methods and materials for joining different layers of a fixed construction surface, such as roads or sports grounds, using adhesive layers or intermediate membranes that permit water-vapour escape, spread stresses, or allow sliding between layers. It includes the design of such intermediate layers to improve durability and performance of the paving system.
[E01C 7/34] made of several courses which are not bound to each other -- This subgroup covers pavements that consist of several separate courses placed side by side without any binding or interlocking, such as stone or aggregate layers used in road or sports-ground surfaces.
[E01C 7/35] Toppings or surface dressings -- This subclass covers materials applied as a thin layer over existing pavements to improve surface properties, such as wearing courses, sealants, or decorative layers. It includes methods for installing toppings and dressings on roads, sports fields, and similar surfaces.
[E01C 7/351] with exclusively hydraulical binders; Aggregate, fillers or other additives for application on or in the surface of toppings with exclusively hydraulic binders -- The class E01C 7/351 covers pavement or topping constructions in which the binding material is exclusively a hydraulic binder such as cement. It includes the use of aggregates, fillers and other additives applied to the surface or incorporated into the topping, all intended for roads, sports grounds or similar flat surfaces.
[E01C 7/353] with exclusively bituminous binders; Aggregate, fillers or other additives for application on or in the surface of toppings with exclusively bituminous binders, e.g. for roughening or clearing -- The subclass E01C 7/353 covers coherent pavings that use only bituminous binders, together with aggregates, fillers or other additives applied to the surface or within the topping. It relates to materials used to roughen, clear or otherwise treat such bitumen-only pavements.
[E01C 7/355] with exclusively rubber as binder; Aggregate, fillers or other additives for application on or in the surface of toppings having exclusively rubber as binder -- This CPC subclass refers to pavement or surface constructions in which the topping layer is bound exclusively with rubber, and includes the use of aggregates, fillers or other additives mixed into that rubber binder. It is applicable to roads, sports grounds and similar fixed constructions.
[E01C 7/356] with exclusively synthetic resin as a binder; Aggregate, fillers or other additives for application on or in the surface of toppings having exclusively synthetic resin as binder -- This subclass covers coherent pavement structures whose binder is solely a synthetic resin, and it includes the aggregate, filler or other additive materials applied on or within the topping surface that also rely on an exclusively synthetic-resin binder.
[E01C 7/358] with a combination of two or more binders according to groups  E01C7/351  -  E01C7/356 -- E01C 7/358 covers the construction of fixed pavings on roads, sports grounds or similar surfaces that use a combination of two or more binders as defined in the preceding groups 351 to 356.
[E01C 7/36] by subjecting soil to stabilisation -- E01C 7/36 covers techniques for creating coherent pavements by stabilising the soil, such as mixing it with binders, additives or mechanical treatment before laying it for roads, sports fields or similar surfaces.
[E01C 9/00] Special pavings -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Paving elements poured in situ.  Coverings, the surface of which consists partly of prefabricated units and partly of sections made in situ.  Pavings for special parts of roads or with special functionality, incl.:  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Paving specially adapted for playgrounds or sports grounds -> E01C13/00 - Paving specially adapted for footpaths, sidewalks or cycle tracks -> E01C15/00 - Pavement lights -> E01C17/00 - Removable forms or shutterings for road-building purposes -> E01C19/50 - Bridge deck gratings -> E01D19/125 - Speed control bumps -> E01F9/529 - Manhole or like covers or frames -> E02D29/14 - Gully gratings -> E03F5/06
[E01C 9/001] Paving elements formed  in situ ; Permanent shutterings therefor  removable shutterings  E01C19/502 ; moulding machines therefor  E01C19/508 ; Inlays or reinforcements which divide the cast material in a great number of individual units  reinforcement girders which do not break the cohesion of the covering material  E01C11/16 ,  E01C11/185 -- This subclass covers pavements that are cast directly on site, including the permanent or removable formwork and the machines that shape the concrete. It also includes in-situ inlays or reinforcement systems that split the cast material into many units while keeping the covering layer continuous, such as girders that do not interrupt cohesion.
[E01C 9/002] Coverings, the surface of which consists partly of prefabricated units and partly of sections made  in situ -- E01C 9/002 pertains to coverings whose surface is made up of both prefabricated units and sections cast or installed in place, a typical solution for special pavings used in roads, sports grounds and similar fixed-construction surfaces.
[E01C 9/004] Pavings specially adapted for allowing vegetation  individual spacer elements  E01C5/006
[E01C 9/005] Coverings around trees forming part of the road  protecting plants in general  A01G13/00 -- E01C 9/005 relates to protective coverings incorporated into road or other special pavings that surround tree trunks or other plants as part of the surface. The subclass covers devices, guards or sleeves that shield vegetation from traffic, weather or mechanical damage while remaining integral to the roadway or sports-ground construction. It falls under the broader categories of fixed constructions, building, and construction of surfaces for roads, sports grounds or the like.
[E01C 9/007] Vehicle decelerating or arresting surfacings or surface arrangements, e.g. arrester beds  road surface configuration or surface arrangements for enforcing reduced speed, e.g. speed-control humps  E01F9/529 ; Escape roads, e.g. for steep descents, for sharp bends -- E01C 9/007 covers pavement and surface arrangements designed to decelerate or arrest vehicles, including arrester beds, speed-control humps and other road configurations that enforce reduced speed, as well as escape routes for steep descents or sharp bends. It falls under fixed constructions for roads, sports grounds and similar surfaces.
[E01C 9/008] Paving take-off areas for vertically starting aircraft  landing platforms for helicopters  E01F3/00 -- The subclass covers the design, construction and surfacing of dedicated areas for vertical take-off and landing of aircraft, such as helicopter pads and landing platforms. It includes special pavings and supporting structures prepared to sustain the loads and environmental conditions of rotary-wing operations.
[E01C 9/02] Wheel tracks -- E01C 9/02 covers wheel tracks, the impressions or depressions formed in soil, sand, gravel or other surface layers by the passage of wheels, rollers or similar equipment during road or sport-ground construction and maintenance. It includes methods for creating, preserving or removing such tracks as part of special paving work.
[E01C 9/04] Pavings for railroad level-crossings -- E01C 9/04 covers pavings specifically intended for railroad level-crossings. These special pavings provide a durable surface where roads meet railway tracks, allowing safe vehicle passage while accommodating train movements.
[E01C 9/06] Pavings adjacent tramways rails -- The subclass E01C 9/06 deals with pavements installed directly alongside tramway rails, covering their design, construction and material choices. It includes special paving solutions that accommodate the loads, drainage and integration requirements of surfaces positioned next to tram tracks.
[E01C 9/08] Temporary pavings -- E01C 9/08 covers temporary pavings used in the construction of roads, sports grounds, or similar surfaces. It includes removable or short-term paving systems such as matting, interlocking tiles, or roll-out surfaces that can be installed and taken away quickly. The class addresses methods and materials for creating temporary load-bearing surfaces during construction or events.
[E01C 9/083] made of metal, e.g. plates, network -- This subgroup of CPC E01C concerns special paving systems that are constructed from metal components such as plates or a network of metal elements, used for roads, sports fields, or similar surfaces. It includes designs where metal forms the primary structural material of the paving.
[E01C 9/086] made of concrete, wood, bitumen, rubber or synthetic material or a combination thereof -- The CPC subclass E01C 9/086 covers special paving elements that are fabricated from concrete, wood, bitumen, rubber, synthetic materials or any combination of these, and are employed in the construction of fixed surfaces such as roads, sports grounds or similar installations.
[E01C 9/10] Steel gratings -- E01C 9/10 covers steel gratings employed as structural flooring or paving components in fixed constructions such as roads, sports fields and similar surfaces. The class includes perforated or open-work steel panels designed to bear loads while permitting drainage or ventilation.
[E01D] CONSTRUCTION OF BRIDGES, OR VIADUCTS -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Spanning structures, e.g. railway bridges, for allowing traffic, e.g. pedestrians, automobiles, trains, to travel across naturally occurring obstructions, e.g. gorges, bodies of water, or over man-made obstacles, e.g. roads, canals. Each spanning structure including obstruction or obstacle traversing structure that provides a passageway for traffic and links or is held up by:  Spanning structures used for purposes other than carrying traffic, e.g. aqueducts, that are structurally similar to spanning structures for traffic and traverse the same types of obstructions and obstacles.  Specially adapted components for use in spanning structures, e.g. suspension cable clamps.  Accessories or ancillary devices specially adapted for use with spanning structures. e.g. drawbridge gates that are actuated when the span moves.  Methods specially adapted for constructing spanning structures, e.g. specially adapted bridge erecting or assembling techniques.  Methods specially adapted for restoring damaged, e.g. replacing dilapidated decking, or structurally augmenting, e.g. strengthening existing component to allow increased traffic load, existing spanning structures.  Methods specially adapted for demolishing or dismantling spanning structures.  Apparatus specially adapted for constructing, restoring, augmenting, or demolishing spanning structures.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Highways or trackways for toys that may include bridges -> A63H18/00 - Model railway bridges -> A63H19/34 - Arrangement of ship based onboard ramps or gangways -> B63B27/14 - Bridges extending between terminal buildings and aircraft for embarking or disembarking -> B64F1/305 - Loading ramps -> B65G69/28 - Supports that may include structure for spanning a road and supporting electric lines or communication cable going to overhead road signs or traffic signals -> E01F9/696 - Floating tunnels or submerged bridge-like tunnels -> E02D29/067 - Laying or reclaiming pipes; Repairing or joining pipes on or under water -> F16L1/00 - Supports for pipes, cables or protective tubing -> F16L3/00 - Floating power or information cables -> H01B7/12 - Overhead installations of electric lines or cables -> H02G7/00 - Installations of electric cables or lines in or on the ground or water -> H02G9/00 - Installations of electric cables or lines in or upon bodies of water that are supported by floats -> H02G9/12
[E01D 1/00] Bridges in general -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Bridges not covered by any of the other types of bridges  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Arch-type bridges -> E01D4/00 - Truss-type bridges -> E01D6/00 - Suspension or cable stay bridges -> E01D11/00 - Bridges characterised by a combination of structures -> E01D12/00 - Movable or portable bridges -> E01D15/00
[E01D 1/005] Bowstring bridges  truss-type bowstring bridges  E01D6/02 -- The entry E01D 1/005 classifies bowstring bridges, a truss-type bridge in which the upper chord forms an arch and the lower chord serves as a tie, used in fixed constructions and bridge building. It covers the design, fabrication and erection of these arch-truss structures.
[E01D 11/00] Suspension or cable-stayed bridges -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Bridges where the deck is supported by cables.
[E01D 11/02] Suspension bridges -- Code E01D 11/02 pertains to suspension bridges, a sub-category of fixed constructions for building bridges or viaducts. It covers the design, structural elements and construction methods of bridges that rely on suspended cables or chains, including towers, anchorage systems and deck support. The classification is used to identify patents related to these bridge types.
[E01D 11/04] Cable-stayed bridges -- E01D 11/04 covers the design and construction of cable-stayed bridges, where the bridge deck is supported by cables directly connected to one or more towers. It includes the structural elements, materials, and methods specific to cable-stayed bridge systems within the broader category of fixed bridge constructions.
[E01D 12/00] Bridges characterised by a combination of structures not covered as a whole by a single one of groups -- Bridges that integrate two or more distinct structural forms-such as arch, suspension, cable-stayed, beam or truss elements-so that the overall arrangement does not fall entirely within any single predefined bridge group. This category captures hybrid designs whose combined construction methods are not covered by a single existing subgroup.
[E01D 15/00] Movable or portable bridges -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Movable bridges for allowing passing of (waterborne) traffic, e.g. lift, swing, travelling or bascule bridges. Portable bridges being mainly temporary or military bridges.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Arrangement of ship-based outboard ramps or gangways -> B63B27/14 - Loading ramps in general -> B65G69/28
[E01D 15/005] Movable bridges in general  floating bridges with sections movable to allow passing of water-borne traffic  E01D15/145 ; lock gates  E02B7/20 ; Constructional elements peculiar to movable bridges -- The CPC subclass E01D 15/005 covers movable or portable bridges, including floating bridges whose sections can be shifted to permit water traffic, lock gate mechanisms, and other constructional elements specific to such structures. It falls under fixed constructions, building, construction of bridges or viaducts.
[E01D 15/02] Vertical lift bridges -- E01D 15/02 covers vertical lift bridges, a type of movable or portable bridge where the span is raised vertically on towers to allow ships to pass. These structures are part of the fixed constructions category within building and bridge construction and are classified under the Cooperative Patent Classification used by the EPO and USPTO.
[E01D 15/04] Swing bridges -- Swing bridges are a type of movable bridge that rotates horizontally around a central pivot to open a navigation channel for vessels and then returns to carry road or rail traffic. The design combines rotating mechanical components with fixed bridge elements, allowing rapid, controlled opening and closing while maintaining structural stability. This classification falls under fixed constructions, building, and construction of bridges or viaducts.
[E01D 15/06] Bascule bridges -- Bascule bridges are movable bridge structures that pivot upward around a horizontal axis using counterweights to balance the span, allowing ships to pass underneath. They belong to the class of fixed constructions for building bridges, specifically the subcategory of movable or portable bridges.
[E01D 15/08] Drawbridges -- The CPC entry E01D 15/08 pertains to drawbridges, a type of movable bridge that can be raised, lowered, swung or otherwise retracted to permit vessel passage beneath a fixed crossing. It encompasses designs, mechanisms and control systems for bascule, lift, swing or retractable bridge structures used in bridge construction.
[E01D 15/10] Travelling bridges -- Travelling bridges are movable or portable structures designed to be transported to a site and assembled for temporary river or gap crossing, often featuring sections that roll, fold, float or detach for rapid deployment and removal. This classification covers the design, construction, and operational features of such relocatable bridge systems.
[E01D 15/12] Portable or sectional bridges -- E01D 15/12 covers portable or sectional bridges, i.e., bridge structures that are fabricated in modular sections and can be transported, assembled, and disassembled for temporary or rapid deployment. The classification includes floating, folding, or otherwise movable bridge types designed for quick installation in locations where permanent bridges are impractical.
[E01D 15/122] Inflatable or unreelable bridges  inflatable or unreelable girders in general  E04C3/005 ; Bridges with main load-supporting structure consisting only of non-rigid elements, e.g. cables -- The CPC entry E01D 15/122 covers movable or portable bridges whose main load-supporting structure is made of non-rigid elements, such as inflatable or unreelable components and girders, including bridges that rely solely on flexible members like cables. It is placed under Fixed Constructions → Building → Construction of Bridges or Viaducts → Movable or Portable Bridges.
[E01D 15/124] Folding or telescopic bridges; Bridges built up from folding or telescopic sections  E01D15/122 ,  E01D15/127  take precedence; folding or telescopic girders in general  E04C3/005 -- The code E01D 15/124 covers bridges that are constructed from folding or telescopic sections, including fully telescoping or folding bridge structures and the mechanisms that allow them to retract or extend. It applies to movable or portable bridges where the deck or supporting girders are built from parts that can be folded together or telescoped one inside another for compact storage or rapid deployment.
[E01D 15/127] combined with ground-supported vehicles for the transport, handling or placing of such bridges or of sections thereof -- The subclass E01D 15/127 covers movable or portable bridge structures that are integrated with ground-supported vehicles for transporting, handling or installing the bridge or its sections. It includes systems where trucks, trailers or other land-based carriers are used to move and position modular bridge components during construction or deployment.
[E01D 15/133] built-up from readily separable standardised sections or elements, e.g. Bailey bridges -- This subclass covers movable or portable bridges that are assembled from readily separable, standardised sections or elements, such as prefabricated components, exemplified by Bailey bridges. It includes designs where the bridge is built up from these modular parts to allow rapid erection, adjustment, and disassembly.
[E01D 15/14] Floating bridges, e.g. pontoon bridges -- E01D 15/14 covers floating or pontoon bridges, where a series of boats or floating platforms support a roadway that can be assembled, disassembled or moved as needed. The classification applies to designs and construction methods for temporary or relocatable crossings over water, falling under the CPC hierarchy of fixed constructions, building, construction of bridges or viaducts, and movable or portable bridges.
[E01D 15/145] displaceable or with sections movable to allow passing of water-borne traffic  movable bridges actuated by floating bodies  E01D15/005  -  E01D15/10 -- E01D 15/145 covers movable or portable bridge structures whose sections can be displaced to permit passage of water-borne traffic, including bridges that are operated by floating bodies. The subclass focuses on designs that allow the bridge to be raised, shifted, or reconfigured to clear navigation channels.
[E01D 15/20] collapsible, expandable, inflatable or the like -- E01D 15/20 refers to movable bridges that can be collapsed, expanded, or inflated, allowing rapid deployment or temporary use. It includes designs such as fold-out, telescoping, or balloon-type bridge sections used for construction, military, or emergency crossing of gaps.
[E01D 15/22] designed as, or mounted on, vehicles -- The E01D 15/22 class covers bridges that are incorporated into or carried by vehicles, such as ferry-type spans, floating or rolling bridge sections, and other movable structures mounted on a vehicle chassis for rapid deployment and retrieval. It pertains to portable bridge solutions that travel with or are part of a vehicle rather than being fixed on site.
[E01D 15/24] Bridges or similar structures, based on land or on a fixed structure and designed to give access to ships or other floating structures -- This CPC class defines bridges or similar structures that are erected on land or on a fixed support and are intended to give access to ships or other floating installations. It includes movable or portable bridge types used for maritime access and falls under the hierarchy of fixed constructions, building, construction of bridges or viaducts.
[E01D 18/00] Bridges specially adapted for particular applications or functions not provided for elsewhere, e.g. aqueducts, bridges for supporting pipe-lines -- This subclass refers to bridges that are specially designed for particular functions not covered in other categories, such as aqueducts that convey water or bridge structures that carry pipelines and other utilities.
[E01D 19/00] Structural or constructional details of bridges -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Constructional details not necessarily related to types of bridges.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Road joints -> E01C11/04 - E01C11/10 - Protection of piers against colliding vehicles -> E01F15/00 - Foundations -> E02D - Sealing joints not restricted to joints for bridges; expansion joints for buildings in general -> E04B1/68
[E01D 19/005] Piers, trestles, bearings, expansion joints or parapets specially adapted for portable or sectional bridges -- This subclass deals with design and construction of support and connection elements specifically engineered for portable or modular bridges, including piers, trestles, bearing devices, expansion joints and parapet structures that enable rapid assembly and disassembly.
[E01D 19/02] Piers -- E01D 19/02 designates the structural and constructional details of bridge or viaduct piers, covering their types, foundations, materials and load-bearing features. It falls under Fixed Constructions → Building → Construction of Bridges, or Viaducts, and is used to classify patent documents describing pier design, fabrication and installation methods.
[E01D 19/04] Bearings -- E01D 19/04 covers bridge bearings, the devices that transfer loads from the bridge deck to the supporting pier or abutment while permitting limited movements such as thermal expansion, rotation, and settlement. These components include elastomeric, pot, rocker and other types designed to accommodate forces and deformations without damaging the structure. They are essential for maintaining the integrity and serviceability of bridges and viaducts.
[E01D 19/041] Elastomeric bearings  E01D19/048  takes precedence -- The subclass E01D 19/041 covers elastomeric bearings employed in bridge structures, detailing their design, material composition and functional characteristics. It is nested under fixed constructions, building and construction of bridges and supersedes the broader E01D 19/048 category.
[E01D 19/042] Mechanical bearings  E01D19/048  takes precedence
[E01D 19/043] Roller bearings -- The CPC subclass E01D 19/043 covers roller bearings used in bridge structures. It includes designs and arrangements of rollers that support and allow controlled movement of bridge components under load, facilitating thermal expansion, settlement, or vibration damping. These bearings are integral to the structural or constructional details of bridges and viaducts.
[E01D 19/045] Line and point rocker bearings -- E01D 19/045 covers line and point rocker bearings, which are movable support elements that allow bridge decks to rotate or translate under loads and thermal movements. These bearings consist of a curved interface that rocks about a line or a point, providing flexibility while transmitting loads from the superstructure to the substructure.
[E01D 19/046] Spherical bearings -- Spherical bearings are rotating supports that transmit loads between bridge components while permitting angular movement and rotation. They consist of a spherical inner element seated in a concave housing, allowing the superstructure to accommodate thermal expansion, settlement, and traffic-induced deflections. Used in bridge decks, piers and viaducts, they help maintain alignment and reduce stress.
[E01D 19/047] Pot bearings -- Pot bearings are elastomeric devices placed between a bridge's superstructure and substructure to accommodate rotations and translations while transferring loads. They consist of a steel pot filled with compressed rubber or polymer, allowing controlled movement and reducing stress. In bridge construction they are classified under E01D 19/047 for structural details of bridges.
[E01D 19/048] Bearings being adjustable once installed; Bearings used in incremental launching -- This CPC entry covers bridge bearings that can be adjusted after installation and those used in incremental launching construction methods, addressing load transfer and movement accommodation in bridge structures.
[E01D 19/06] Arrangement, construction or bridging of expansion joints -- This subclass covers the design and installation of expansion joints in bridges, focusing on how the joints are arranged, constructed and linked to allow controlled movement. It includes methods for joint slots, sealing, bridging elements and other structural details that accommodate thermal expansion, loads and settlement.
[E01D 19/062] Joints having intermediate beams -- E01D 19/062 classifies bridge joint structures that incorporate intermediate beams to transfer loads between bridge components. It addresses design, connection methods and reinforcement of such joints within bridge construction.
[E01D 19/065] Joints having sliding plates -- Sliding-plate joints are bridge connections that incorporate one or more metal plates capable of moving relative to each other, allowing controlled displacement to accommodate thermal expansion, settlement or load-induced deformation while keeping the bridge elements aligned.
[E01D 19/067] Flat continuous joints cast  in situ -- E01D 19/067 covers the design and implementation of flat continuous joints that are cast in situ as part of bridge or viaduct structures. It addresses the geometric and material characteristics of these joints, including their reinforcement, casting methods, and waterproofing, to ensure seamless load transfer and durability across bridge spans.
[E01D 19/08] Damp-proof or other insulating layers -- This subgroup covers the use of damp-proofing and other insulating layers in bridge structures, including the design, selection and application of membranes, coatings, boards or similar barrier materials on decks, piers, abutments and related components to prevent moisture penetration and to provide thermal or acoustic insulation.
[E01D 19/083] Waterproofing of bridge decks; Other insulations for bridges, e.g. thermal  insulation for buildings in general  E04B1/62 ; Bridge deck surfacings  road surfacings in general  E01C
[E01D 19/086] Drainage arrangements or devices  surface drainage of roads in general  E01C11/224 ; roof drainage  E04D13/04
[E01D 19/10] Railings -- E01D 19/10 designates railings installed on bridges or viaducts, covering their form, material, dimensions, attachment to the bridge structure and related safety features. It classifies these components within the broader category of fixed constructions for bridge building and structural details.
[E01D 19/103] Parapets, railings  E01D19/005  takes precedence; balustrades in general  E04F11/18 ; Guard barriers or road-bridges  in general  E01F15/00 -- The code E01D 19/103 covers the design and construction of parapets and railings that form part of a bridge's structural or constructional details. It includes the form, materials, attachment methods and safety features of these guard elements, with the broader subclass E01D 19/005 taking precedence.
[E01D 19/106] Movable inspection or maintenance platforms, e.g. travelling scaffolding or vehicles specially designed to provide access to the undersides of bridges  lifting devices for movable platforms on vehicles in general  B66F11/04 -- E01D 19/106 refers to movable inspection or maintenance platforms for bridges, including travelling scaffolding and specially designed vehicles that give access to bridge undersides, together with lifting devices used to raise and move those platforms.
[E01D 19/12] Grating or flooring for bridges -- E01D 19/12 covers grating and flooring components used in bridge construction, including deck surfaces and anti-slip members. It pertains to the structural or constructional details of bridges within the fixed constructions, building, construction of bridges hierarchy.
[E01D 19/125] Grating or flooring for bridges  waterproofing thereof  E01D19/083 ; floors in general  E04B5/00 -- The CPC entry E01D 19/125 covers grating or flooring installed on bridges, including the materials and designs used for bridge decks and their waterproofing, and links to broader floor classifications such as E04B5/00.
[E01D 19/14] Towers -- E01D 19/14 relates to towers that serve as vertical support elements in bridge or viaduct structures, covering their design, erection and load-bearing features such as piers, pylons and cable-stayed tower sections used in fixed constructions.
[E01D 19/16] Suspension cables -- This subclass defines the structural elements and engineering of suspension cables employed in bridge or viaduct construction, covering cable types, anchorage methods, tensioning arrangements and supporting hardware that form the primary load-bearing system of suspended spans.
[E01D 2/00] Bridges characterised by the cross-section of their bearing spanning structure -- Bridges characterised by the cross-section of their bearing spanning structure are covered under CPC code E01D 2/00. This subgroup includes designs where the shape of the main load-bearing members such as arches, trusses, or box girders determines the bridge type. It is used to classify bridge constructions based on the geometric profile of the structural elements that carry loads.
[E01D 2/02] of the I-girder type -- E01D 2/02 relates to fixed-construction bridges in which the primary load-bearing members are I-girder sections. The classification covers bridges whose spanning structure consists of I-shaped girders that support the deck, typical for steel or concrete bridges of short to medium span lengths.
[E01D 2/04] of the box-girder type -- It covers bridges whose main load-carrying element is a box girder, a hollow rectangular or trapezoidal cross-section that provides high torsional rigidity and strength, and is commonly used for medium to long spans.
[E01D 21/00] Methods or apparatus specially adapted for erecting or assembling bridges -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods or apparatus specially adapted for erecting or assembling bridges  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Assembling, placing or erecting temporary bridges -> E01D15/127 , E01D15/133
[E01D 2101/00] Material constitution of bridges -- E01D 2101/00 addresses the material constitution of bridges, detailing the types of structural components such as steel, concrete, timber, composite or alloy mixtures and their relevant physical and chemical properties used in bridge and viaduct construction.
[E01D 2101/10] Wood -- E01D 2101/10 designates the use of wood as the material constitution in bridge or viaduct construction. It falls under the hierarchy of Fixed Constructions → Building → Construction of Bridges, or Viaducts, and covers wooden structural elements, decks, and supports employed in such fixed civil works.
[E01D 2101/20] Concrete, stone or stone-like material -- Concrete, stone or stone-like material refers to the aggregate-based, cement-bound or naturally occurring substances used as the primary structural component in bridge and viaduct construction, providing compressive strength, durability and resistance to environmental factors. These materials are selected for their load-bearing capacity and long-term performance in fixed bridge works.
[E01D 2101/22] Masonry -- E01D 2101/22 pertains to the use of masonry as the material constitution of bridges and viaducts, covering stone, brick and similar aggregate units assembled into load-bearing structures. It applies to fixed bridge constructions within the building category of the Cooperative Patent Classification.
[E01D 2101/24] Concrete -- E01D 2101/24 designates concrete as the material constitution of bridges, covering its composition, properties, and manufacturing methods used in the construction of bridges and viaducts.
[E01D 2101/26] reinforced -- In this subclass, reinforced denotes bridges whose structural elements are strengthened by adding reinforcement such as steel bars, meshes or cords to concrete or other materials. The reinforcement improves tensile capacity, durability and load-bearing performance of the bridge components.
[E01D 2101/262] with steel fibres -- The subclass E01D 2101/262 covers bridges whose structural material incorporates steel fibres, such as fibre-reinforced concrete decks or elements. It is applied to bridge construction where the steel-fiber reinforcement is part of the material constitution, providing enhanced tensile strength and durability.
[E01D 2101/264] with glass fibres -- This CPC subclass concerns bridges whose structural material incorporates glass fibres, covering the use of glass-fiber-reinforced components in bridge construction. It falls under fixed constructions, building, construction of bridges, material constitution.
[E01D 2101/266] with fibres other than steel or glass -- The subgroup E01D 2101/266 comprises bridges whose material constitution incorporates fibre reinforcement other than steel or glass, such as carbon, basalt, aramid or natural fibres. It applies to designs where these alternative fibres are used as integral structural elements in bridge components.
[E01D 2101/268] Composite concrete-metal -- The classification E01D 2101/268 identifies bridge constructions that employ a combined concrete-metal material system, where concrete elements are reinforced, encased or bonded with steel or other metals to achieve enhanced strength, durability or load-distribution characteristics. It covers designs that integrate metal components such as plates, girders or reinforcements directly within the concrete structure of bridges or viaducts.
[E01D 2101/28] prestressed -- E01D 2101/28 covers the use of prestressed materials in bridge construction, such as prestressed concrete or steel elements that are tensioned to enhance load-bearing capacity. It pertains to the material constitution of bridges, describing how prestressing is applied to structural components.
[E01D 2101/285] Composite prestressed concrete-metal -- The CPC class E01D 2101/285 refers to composite prestressed concrete-metal structures used in bridge or viaduct construction, where concrete and steel components are bonded and pre-stressed to enhance load capacity and durability. It falls under the hierarchy of fixed constructions, building, and material constitution of bridges.
[E01D 2101/30] Metal -- The subclass E01D 2101/30 designates bridges whose primary structural material is metal, such as steel or iron, and covers all aspects where metal forms the main load-bearing elements. It resides in the hierarchy under fixed constructions, building, construction of bridges or viaducts, material constitution of bridges.
[E01D 2101/32] prestressed -- The CPC subclass E01D 2101/32 covers bridges that use prestressed concrete or steel as the material constitution, focusing on the structural design of fixed constructions such as bridges and viaducts. It falls under the hierarchy Fixed Constructions - Building - Construction of Bridges, or Viaducts - Material constitution of bridges.
[E01D 2101/34] non-ferrous, e.g. aluminium -- The code E01D 2101/34 designates bridge or viaduct structures whose primary material is non-ferrous, such as aluminium. It falls under fixed constructions for buildings, specifically the material constitution of bridges, and covers design, fabrication and assembly methods using these lightweight metals.
[E01D 2101/40] Plastics -- The CPC entry E01D 2101/40 covers the use of plastics in bridge construction, including polymer and composite materials that form structural or protective components of bridges and viaducts. It addresses the material constitution of bridge elements such as decks, decks, bearings and protective coatings made from plastic substances.
[E01D 21/06] by translational movement of the bridge or bridge sections -- E01D 21/06 covers methods or apparatus for constructing bridges in which the bridge or its sections are moved horizontally, using translational motion to position and assemble the structure. This includes systems that slide, roll or otherwise translate components into place during erection of bridges or viaducts.
[E01D 21/065] Incremental launching -- Incremental launching refers to a bridge-building method in which pre-fabricated bridge sections are assembled on one side of the structure and then progressively pushed, pulled or rolled forward along the bridge's alignment, allowing the span to be erected without extensive falsework or temporary supports. This technique is used for long, uniform spans and can accommodate varying grades and curvatures while minimizing site disruption.
[E01D 21/08] by rotational movement of the bridge or bridge sections -- E01D 21/08 covers methods or apparatus for constructing bridges or viaducts that employ a rotational movement of the entire bridge or its sections during erection or assembly. It falls under Fixed Constructions → Building → Construction of Bridges, or Viaducts, focusing on specialized rotating techniques.
[E01D 21/10] Cantilevered erection -- The CPC entry E01D 21/10 covers methods and apparatus specifically designed for erecting bridge structures by cantilevered construction, where sections are built out from a support without the need for temporary scaffolding or falsework. It includes techniques for sequentially adding span segments that balance the structure during assembly, enabling efficient construction of long-span bridges and viaducts.
[E01D 21/105] Balanced cantilevered erection -- Balanced cantilevered erection is a bridge-construction method in which segments are added alternately on opposite sides of a pier or support, keeping the structure in equilibrium during the build. This technique allows for the erection of long spans without the need for temporary falsework beneath the bridge, making it well suited to deep valleys, waterways or busy traffic corridors. It is classified under fixed constructions, building, construction of bridges, and methods or apparatus specially adapted for erecting or assembling bridges.
[E01D 22/00] Methods or apparatus for repairing or strengthening existing bridges -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Repairing, strengthening or dismantling brides.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Electrochemical desalination or re-alkalisation -> C04B41/00
[E01D 4/00] Arch-type bridges -- E01D 4/00 classifies arch-type bridges, a subcategory of construction of bridges or viaducts within fixed constructions. It includes the design, fabrication and erection of bridges that employ arch forms-whether masonry, concrete, steel or composite-together with associated structural elements and support systems.
[E01D 6/00] Truss-type bridges -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Bridge composed of connected elements (typically straight) which may be stressed from tension or compression.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Portable bridges, like Bailey bridges -> E01D15/133
[E01D 6/02] of bowstring type -- Truss-type bridges of the bowstring type are arch-shaped trusses in which the curved top chord carries compression and the straight bottom chord carries tension, forming a rigid, self-supporting structure. This design is common for medium-span road and railway bridges and viaducts, offering efficient material use and straightforward construction.
[E01F] ADDITIONAL WORK, SUCH AS EQUIPPING ROADS OR THE CONSTRUCTION OF PLATFORMS, HELICOPTER LANDING STAGES, SIGNS, SNOW FENCES, OR THE LIKE -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Additional equipment for roads or railways, including platforms, refuge islands, drainage means of the sub-base of roads or ballastway of railways, arrangements protecting against snow or landslides, arrangements against traffic noise.  Landing stages for helicopters.  Arrangements for facilitating the use of roads, including road signs, traffic signals, arrangement for enforcing caution, arrangements for obstructing or restricting traffic, safety arrangements for errant vehicles or against vehicular impact.
[E01F 1/00] Arrangement or construction of additional equipment for roads or railways, Landing stages for helicopters -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Platforms for e.g. railway or bus stations.  Refuge islands in traffic areas and kerbs specially adapted for such islands.  Filling station islands.  Portable or retractable platforms for traffic officers.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Kerbs informing road users -> E01F9/535 - Traffic lane delineators -> E01F9/588 - General arrangement of filling stations -> B60S5/02 - General arrangement of railway stations and platforms -> B61B1/02 - Horizontal loading or unloading platforms -> B65G69/22 - Traffic islands in roads -> E01C1/02 - Waiting shelters for bus stops -> E04H1/1211
[E01F 1/005] Portable or movable traffic-area platforms or islands, e.g. portable loading islands, retractable platforms for traffic-directing officer -- The CPC subclass E01F 1/005 covers portable or movable platforms and islands used in traffic areas. It includes devices such as temporary loading islands, retractable platforms for traffic-directing officers, and similar equipment that can be installed on roads, railways or helicopter landing stages.
[E01F 11/00] Embedding pads or other sensitive devices in paving or other road surfaces -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Road engineering aspects of embedding pads or other sensitive devices in paving or other road surfaces, e.g. traffic detectors, vehicle-operated pressure-sensitive actuators, devices for monitoring atmospheric or road conditions.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Pressure-sensitive elements as such -> G01L - Traffic control systems -> G08G
[E01F 13/00] Arrangements for obstructing or restricting traffic, e.g. gates, barricades -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Portable barriers, pedestrian barriers, barriers for channelling or controlling crowds  Removable barriers with permanently installed base members.  Barriers with flexible members, e.g. cords  Devices movable to allow or prevent passage, with obstructing members situated on, flush with, or below the traffic surface, e.g. with inflatable members on the surface  Devices with obstructing members moving in translatory motion, e.g. telescopic barriers posts, or with members moving by swinging them into a given position, e.g. swinging gates, tiltable sections of the road surface, tiltable posts.  Vehicle barriers adapted for allowing passage in one direction only  Arrangements for forcibly arresting or disabling vehicles, e.g. spiked mats.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Retractable speed humps -> E01F9/529 - Portable signs co-operating with permanent base -> E01F9/677 - Safety means for railroad crossing traffic, e.g. gates for level crossings -> B61L29/00 - Corrals -> E04H17/18 - Gates or doors for allowing passage through fences, barriers or the like -> E06B11/02
[E01F 13/02] free-standing -- E01F 13/02 free-standing covers constructions that are independent structures used to control or restrict traffic flow, such as stand-alone gates, barricades, or other obstacles that are not attached to other buildings or infrastructure. It applies to devices that impede vehicle or pedestrian movement and are installed as part of road or site safety equipment.
[E01F 13/022] Pedestrian barriers; Barriers for channelling or controlling crowds  E01F13/024 ,  E01F13/028  take precedence; corrals  E04H17/18 -- E01F 13/022 relates to pedestrian barriers and crowd-control devices such as gates, barricades and similar structures that obstruct or direct foot traffic, typically used in construction sites, road works or public areas to manage movement.
[E01F 13/024] Removable barriers with permanently installed base members, e.g. to provide occasional passage  E01F13/028 ,  E01F15/12  take precedence -- This CPC entry covers removable traffic barriers mounted on permanently fixed base members, allowing occasional passage while otherwise restricting vehicle flow. It applies to devices such as gates or portable barricades installed in roads, platforms, or similar structures. The subclassifications E01F13/028 and E01F15/12 take precedence.
[E01F 13/026] Detachable barrier bollards or parking posts  portable signs co-operating with permanent base  E01F9/677 -- E01F 13/026 covers detachable barrier bollards and parking posts that can be removed or repositioned, as well as portable signs designed to work with a permanent base. These devices are used in additional construction work such as road equipment, platform building, and traffic control.
[E01F 13/028] Flexible barrier members, e.g. cords; Means for rendering same conspicuous; Adapted supports, e.g. with storage reel -- The CPC subclass E01F 13/028 pertains to flexible barrier members such as cords used to obstruct or restrict traffic, including devices that make them conspicuous and adapted supports like storage reels. These elements are employed in road equipment, platform safety, helicopter landing stages, signage, snow fences and similar installations.
[E01F 13/04] movable to allow or prevent passage -- The subclass E01F 13/04 covers movable devices that control or restrict traffic flow, such as gates, barricades, temporary fences and similar constructions that can be opened or closed to allow or prevent passage on roads, platforms or other access ways.
[E01F 13/042] for controlling access to an individual parking space  E01F13/026 ,  E01F13/046 ,  E01F13/065 ,  E01F13/085  take precedence -- E01F 13/042 covers fixed traffic-obstructing devices installed in parking areas to control entry to a single space, such as gates, barriers or similar access-control structures. It falls under the broader group for additional work on roads and platforms, including equipment like signs and snow fences. The subclass is subordinate to E01F13/026, E01F13/046, E01F13/065 and E01F13/085.
[E01F 13/044] the barrier being formed by obstructing members situated on, flush with, or below the traffic surface, e.g. with inflatable members on the surface  E01F13/08 ,  E01F13/10  take precedence; retractable speed humps  E01F9/529 -- This CPC subclass concerns traffic barriers that are integral to or lie flush with the road surface, such as inflatable members or retractable speed humps, designed to obstruct or restrict vehicle movement.
[E01F 13/046] the obstructing members moving up in a translatory motion, e.g. telescopic barrier posts  for lane control  E01F9/594 -- The code relates to traffic-control devices whose blocking elements slide in a translatory motion, exemplified by telescopic barrier posts used to close or open lanes. It falls under fixed constructions for additional works such as road equipment and traffic-restriction arrangements.
[E01F 13/048] with obstructing members moving in a translatory motion, e.g. vertical lift barriers, sliding gates  E01F13/046 ,  E01F13/105  take precedence -- This CPC subclass covers fixed-construction building additions that impede or restrict traffic using obstructing members that move in a straight (translatory) path, such as vertically lifting barriers and sliding gates. It falls under the broader group for traffic-obstructing arrangements, with the related subclasses E01F 13/046 and E01F 13/105 taking precedence.
[E01F 13/06] by swinging into open position about a horizontal axis parallel to the road direction, i.e. swinging gates -- This subclass covers traffic-control devices that open by swinging into the open position about a horizontal axis that runs parallel to the road direction, such as swinging gates used to restrict or allow vehicle passage. It is part of the fixed-construction group for additional road-equipment and other traffic-obstructing arrangements.
[E01F 13/065] specially adapted for individual parking spaces -- E01F 13/065 covers traffic-control arrangements that are specially adapted to create individual parking spaces, such as dedicated gates, barriers or other devices that restrict or direct vehicle movement for single-car allocation. It falls under fixed constructions and additional work for obstructing or regulating traffic.
[E01F 13/08] by swinging into closed position about a transverse axis situated in the road surface, e.g. tiltable sections of the road surface, tiltable parking posts -- The subclass concerns traffic-obstruction devices that move into a closed position by rotating around a transverse axis embedded in the road surface, such as tiltable sections of pavement or parking posts that swing shut to block vehicles.
[E01F 13/085] specially adapted for individual parking spaces -- E01F 13/085 designates fixed constructions within the building of additional works-such as gates, barricades or traffic-restricting devices-that are specially adapted for individual parking spaces. It falls under the broader categories of fixed constructions, building, and additional work like equipping roads or constructing platforms.
[E01F 13/10] Vehicle barriers specially adapted for allowing passage in one direction only -- Vehicle barriers designed to permit traffic flow in only one direction, such as one-way gates, turnstiles or hinged barricades that close after a vehicle passes, are classified under E01F 13/10. The entry covers fixed construction elements used to obstruct or restrict vehicular movement while allowing controlled, single-direction passage, typically employed in road, platform or site access control.
[E01F 13/105] depressible by right-way traffic, e.g. pivotally; actuated by wrong-way traffic  type-disabling elements  E01F13/123
[E01F 13/12] for forcibly arresting or disabling vehicles, e.g. spiked mats -- The entry E01F 13/12 covers devices that forcibly arrest or disable moving vehicles, such as spiked or abrasive mats, road spikes, and similar mechanisms placed on surfaces to stop or impede automobile or aircraft travel. These arrangements are used for traffic control, security, or emergency stopping purposes.
[E01F 13/123] depressible or retractable below the traffic surface, e.g. one-way spike barriers, power-controlled prong barriers -- This subclass covers traffic-control installations that lie beneath the road surface and can be lowered or raised, such as one-way spike barriers and power-controlled prong barriers, used to obstruct or restrict vehicle movement. It falls under fixed constructions for building and additional work like equipping roads, where the devices are depressible or retractable below the traffic surface.
[E01F 13/126] Pitfall barriers, causing the vehicle to face a step-like obstruction -- E01F 13/126 refers to pitfall barriers that create a step-like obstruction to stop or deter vehicles, typically used as part of traffic-control structures such as gates, barricades or other fixed constructions installed on roads, platforms or landing areas. The code classifies these devices as additional work for restricting vehicle movement in the broader group of building and fixed-construction measures.
[E01F 15/00] Safety arrangements for slowing, redirecting or stopping errant vehicles, e.g. guard posts or bollards -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Individual devices arranged in spaced relationship, e.g. buffer bollards.  Continuous barriers extending along roads or between traffic lanes, including barriers essentially made of longitudinal beams or rigid strips, essentially made of cables, nettings or the like, essentially made of walls or wall-like elements.  Movable lane separating barriers, e.g. shiftable barriers.  Arrangements specially adapted for local protection, e.g. for bridge piers, for traffic islands.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Arrangements for forcibly arresting vehicles -> E01F13/12 - Guard barriers on road-bridges -> E01D19/103 - Fencing in general -> E04H17/00
[E01F 15/003] Individual devices arranged in spaced relationship, e.g. buffer bollards  spaced wall-type modules  E01F15/08 -- The class covers individual safety devices installed at spaced intervals, such as buffer bollards or wall-type modules, used to slow, redirect or stop errant vehicles. It belongs to the fixed-construction group for additional works like road equipment, platforms, landing stages, signs and snow fences.
[E01F 15/006] Lane control by movable lane separating barriers, e.g. shiftable barriers, retractable kerbs  E01F15/12  takes precedence; by crossable lane separators  E01F9/594 ; Apparatus or barriers specially adapted therefor, e.g. wheeled barriers  wall-type modules  E01F15/08 -- A CPC entry for movable lane-separating devices that control traffic flow, including shiftable barriers, retractable kerbs and related apparatus such as wheeled barriers or wall-type modules, used to slow, redirect or stop errant vehicles. It is classified under fixed constructions, building, additional work such as road equipment.
[E01F 15/02] Continuous barriers extending along roads or between traffic lanes -- E01F 15/02 covers continuous physical barriers installed along roadways or between traffic lanes to limit vehicle movement, provide crash protection and guide traffic. The class includes guardrails, crash barriers, crash cushions and similar structures designed to slow, redirect or stop errant vehicles.
[E01F 15/025] Combinations of at least two of the barrier member types covered by  E01F15/04  -  E01F15/08 , e.g. rolled steel section or plastic strip backed up by cable, safety kerb topped by rail barrier
[E01F 15/04] essentially made of longitudinal beams or rigid strips -- This subclass covers safety devices made essentially of longitudinal beams or rigid strips, such as guard posts, bollards and similar barriers that slow, redirect or stop errant vehicles on roads, platforms, helipad perimeters and related installations.
[E01F 15/0407] Metal rails  E01F15/0446  and  E01F15/0453  take precedence -- E01F 15/0407 covers metal rails employed as safety devices in fixed construction work such as guard posts, bollards, or other arrangements that slow, redirect or stop errant vehicles. The code falls under additional work for building, including roads, platforms, helicopter landing stages and related signage. More specific sub-entries E01F 15/0446 and E01F 15/0453 take precedence over this broader classification.
[E01F 15/0415] with pivoting members -- E01F 15/0415 classifies safety installations for slowing or redirecting errant vehicles that incorporate pivoting members, such as swing-type guard posts or movable bollards, used in additional work like road equipment, platforms, or signage.
[E01F 15/0423] Details of rails  E01F15/0415  takes precedence -- This class relates to the specific design features of rails that form part of vehicle-control devices such as guard posts, bollards or other safety barriers, including their shape, attachment methods, spacing and surface treatment.
[E01F 15/043] with multiple superimposed members; Rails provided with skirts -- This subclass covers fixed road-safety structures such as guard posts or bollards built from multiple superimposed members, where the rails are fitted with skirts that confine or guide errant vehicles. It applies to additional work like equipping roads, constructing platforms, landing stages, signs or snow fences.
[E01F 15/0438] Spacers between rails and posts, e.g. energy-absorbing means  E01F15/0415  takes precedence -- Under the fixed constructions building branch, this CPC class deals with auxiliary works like road equipment and safety measures. Code E01F 15/0438 refers to spacers positioned between rails and posts, including energy-absorbing means, used to cushion impacts in guard posts or bollards. It is a narrower subclass of E01F 15/0415.
[E01F 15/0446] Concrete rails  E01F15/0476  takes precedence; safety kerbs or walls  E01F15/08 -- E01F 15/0446 classifies concrete rails employed as safety devices for slowing, redirecting or stopping errant vehicles, such as guard posts, bollards or other fixed barriers. It falls under fixed constructions for additional road work, and is subordinate to the broader safety-kerb and wall classes E01F15/0476 and E01F15/08.
[E01F 15/0453] Rails of materials other than metal or concrete, e.g. wood, plastics; Rails of different materials, e.g. rubber-faced metal profiles, concrete-filled steel tubes  E01F15/0476  takes precedence; reinforced concrete rails  E01F15/0446 -- The entry classifies safety rails made from non-metallic or non-concrete materials, such as wood or plastics, as well as composite rails that combine different substances, for example rubber-faced metal profiles or concrete-filled steel tubes, used in devices that slow, redirect or stop errant vehicles like guard posts or bollards.
[E01F 15/0461] Supports, e.g. posts  roadside posts in general  E01F9/60 -- The CPC group E01F 15/0461 covers general roadside support structures, such as guard posts, bollards and other posts used as safety devices to slow, redirect or stop errant vehicles. It is classified under fixed constructions, building, and additional work like equipping roads, platforms or other installations.
[E01F 15/0469] Covers, e.g. of ground surface around the foot -- The E01F 15/0469 entry relates to protective covers applied to the ground surface surrounding the foot of a structure, such as a building or platform, to enhance safety and durability. It includes materials and methods for covering soil, gravel or paving in areas where vehicles, pedestrians or equipment may be present, often as part of additional works like guard posts, bollards or other traffic-control installations.
[E01F 15/0476] Foundations -- The code E01F 15/0476 covers foundations for fixed constructions such as buildings, platforms, helicopter-landing stages, road equipment, signs, snow fences and safety devices that slow, redirect or stop errant vehicles, including guard posts and bollards. It addresses the design, material and installation of the supporting structural base for these works.
[E01F 15/0484] Installing; Repairing; Adjusting -- E01F 15/0484 relates to the installation, repair and adjustment of safety devices that slow, redirect or stop stray vehicles, such as guard posts, bollards and similar traffic-control structures used in road work, platforms, helicopter landing stages, signs and snow fences.
[E01F 15/0492] Provisions for guiding in combination with rails, e.g. tyre-gutters -- The entry E01F 15/0492 covers devices that combine rail-like guides with additional elements such as tyre-gutters to direct and control vehicle movement. It includes safety installations that help slow, redirect or stop errant vehicles, for example guard posts, bollards, or similar guide-and-rail systems used on roads, platforms, helicopter landing stages and related infrastructure.
[E01F 15/06] essentially made of cables, nettings or the like
