[E01F 15/065] the barrier members being made of essentially non-metallic materials, e.g. natural or synthetic fibres or webbing -- E01F 15/065 classifies safety barriers whose structural members are formed mainly from non-metallic substances such as natural or synthetic fibers or webbing. These barriers are intended to slow, redirect or stop vehicles in fixed constructions like roads, platforms, helicopter landing stages, signs or snow fences. The code falls under the broader categories of fixed constructions and additional work in the CPC.
[E01F 15/08] essentially made of walls or wall-like elements -- E01F 15/08 classifies fixed building structures whose primary construction consists of walls or wall-like elements, such as guard posts, bollards and other safety installations designed to slow, redirect or stop errant vehicles. It falls under the broader categories of fixed constructions, building, and additional work like road equipment and platform construction.
[E01F 15/081] characterised by the use of a specific material -- E01F 15/081 covers safety devices installed in or alongside roads, platforms, helicopter landing areas, signs or snow fences that are intended to slow, redirect or stop vehicles, such as guard posts and bollards, where the invention is distinguished by the use of a particular material in the device's construction.
[E01F 15/083] using concrete -- E01F 15/083 covers the use of concrete to build fixed safety structures that slow, redirect or stop errant vehicles, such as guard posts, bollards and similar devices incorporated in additional works like road equipment, platforms, helicopter landing stages, signs or snow fences. It falls under the broader group for fixed constructions in building and additional work.
[E01F 15/085] using metal -- E01F 15/085 covers vehicle-control safety devices such as guard posts, bollards or similar metal structures that are installed in fixed constructions like roads, platforms or landing stages to slow, redirect or stop errant vehicles.
[E01F 15/086] using plastic, rubber or synthetic materials -- E01F 15/086 concerns safety devices that slow, redirect or stop errant vehicles and are made from plastic, rubber or other synthetic materials, including guard posts, bollards, snow fences and similar constructions. These devices are installed as part of additional works on roads, platforms, helicopter landing stages or other fixed building structures.
[E01F 15/088] Details of element connection -- E01F 15/088 relates to the specific ways in which components of vehicle-safety structures-such as guard posts, bollards, barriers or similar fixed constructions-are joined, including bolts, clamps, welding, pins or other connection devices that secure the elements together.
[E01F 15/10] portable, e.g. for temporary use -- E01F 15/10 covers portable safety devices that slow, redirect or stop vehicles, such as temporary guard posts, bollards or similar constructions, intended for use on construction sites, road works or other temporary settings. These arrangements are designed for quick installation and removal, providing short-term traffic control and protection.
[E01F 15/12] and having means for providing occasional passage, e.g. for emergency vehicles -- The entry covers fixed building structures such as guard posts or bollards that are intended to slow, redirect or stop errant vehicles but incorporate a mechanism that can be opened or removed to allow occasional passage, for example for emergency vehicles.
[E01F 15/14] specially adapted for local protection, e.g. for bridge piers, for traffic islands -- This subclass covers devices specially adapted to protect local structures such as bridge piers or traffic islands, including bollards, guard posts, or other installations designed to slow, redirect or stop errant vehicles.
[E01F 15/141] for column or post protection -- A protective system for structural columns or posts that prevents vehicle impact, such as bollards, guard posts, or crash barriers, designed to slow, redirect or stop errant vehicles and safeguard the column from damage.
[E01F 15/143] Protecting devices located at the ends of barriers  E01F15/145  takes precedence -- The CPC entry E01F 15/143 covers protecting devices mounted at the ends of barriers, such as guard posts, bollards, or other safety fittings that slow, redirect or stop errant vehicles. It is part of the Fixed Constructions → Building → Additional Work, Such as Equipping Roads or the Construction of Platforms, Helicopter Landing Stages, Signs, Snow Fences, or the Like → Safety Arrangements hierarchy, and is superseded by E01F 15/145.
[E01F 15/145] Means for vehicle stopping using impact energy absorbers -- E01F 15/145 covers devices integrated into fixed constructions such as guard posts, bollards or other road-side structures that stop moving vehicles by absorbing impact energy. The means may include deformable elements, hydraulic or pneumatic absorbers, or other energy-dissipating mechanisms designed to reduce vehicle speed or bring it to a halt upon collision.
[E01F 15/146] fixed arrangements -- E01F 15/146 classifies permanent safety devices installed in or near structures to control vehicular movement, including guard posts, bollards and similar fixed arrangements designed to slow, redirect or stop errant vehicles.
[E01F 15/148] mobile arrangements -- Mobile arrangements are portable safety devices such as movable guard posts, bollards, or barriers used to slow, redirect or stop errant vehicles during construction, road works, or on temporary platforms, including helicopter landing stages and similar sites. They are designed to be easily relocated as work zones change.

The element is a perforated or lattice-type surface that forms part of the barrier wall to mitigate air-transmitted noise.
[E01F 8/0058] with damping material, e.g. rockwool, sand -- The subclass defines arrangements that reduce air-transmitted noise from road or railway traffic by embedding damping materials such as rockwool or sand in structures like platforms, signs, snow fences or helicopter landing stages. It is coded E01F 8/0058 within the Fixed Constructions-Building-Additional work hierarchy.
[E01F 8/0064] Perforated plate or mesh, e.g. as wall facing -- The entry covers the use of perforated plates or mesh as wall facings to absorb or reflect air-transmitted noise from road or railway traffic, typically applied in fixed constructions such as sound barriers, platforms, helicopter landing stages, signs or snow fences. It falls under CPC group E01F 8/0064 for additional building work aimed at noise mitigation.
[E01F 8/007] with damping material -- E01F 8/007 covers fixed constructions in building projects where noise from road or railway traffic is mitigated through the use of damping material that absorbs or reflects sound, such as installations on platforms, helicopter landing stages, signs, snow fences or similar additional works.
[E01F 8/0076] Cellular, e.g. as wall facing -- E01F 8/0076 covers the use of cellular, such as honey-comb, panels as a wall facing in fixed-construction projects, especially for additional works like road or railway noise mitigation. The cellular facing is designed to absorb or reflect air-transmitted sound, improving the acoustic performance of barriers, platforms, helipad edges and similar structures.
[E01F 8/0082] with damping material -- E01F 8/0082 covers fixed construction elements that incorporate noise-control features, specifically arrangements that use damping material to absorb or reflect air-borne road or railway noise. It applies to additional works such as road equipment, platforms, helicopter landing stages, signs, snow fences and similar structures where acoustic mitigation is integrated.
[E01F 8/0088] Suspended, e.g. ropes or mesh -- This subclass covers suspended noise-control devices such as ropes, mesh or similar elements installed as part of additional road or railway infrastructure to absorb or reflect sound. They are used in fixed constructions like platforms, signs, snow fences and comparable structures.
[E01F 8/0094] constructions for generation of phase shifting -- The subclass E01F 8/0094 covers structures that intentionally create a phase shift in transmitted air-borne waves, typically to modify the acoustic field for noise-control purposes. Such constructions include diffusers, angled panels or other engineered surfaces that alter the phase relationship of reflected or transmitted sound to improve attenuation or dispersion. It falls under the broader category of additional road-related works aimed at absorbing or reflecting traffic noise.
[E01F 8/02] specially adapted for sustaining vegetation or for accommodating plants -- E01F 8/02 classifies fixed building works that are specially adapted to sustain vegetation or accommodate plants, such as green roofs, vegetated walls, planters and other plant-supporting elements integrated into noise-absorbing or reflecting structures for roads or railways.
[E01F 8/021] with integral support structure -- This subclass covers constructions that incorporate built-in support elements for noise-absorbing or reflecting devices used alongside roads, railways or similar infrastructure, such as barriers, screens or landing pads that are self-supported without separate foundations. It pertains to added works like platforms, signs, snow fences, etc., where the support is integral to the noise control element.

[E01F 8/028] Through-type, e.g. between adjacent kerbs -- The CPC entry E01F 8/028 covers through-type noise mitigation structures installed between adjacent kerbs, such as barriers that absorb or reflect air-transmitted road or railway sound. It falls under the fixed-construction group for building and additional works like road equipment, platforms, signs, snow fences and similar installations.
[E01F 9/00] Arrangements for facilitating the use of roads -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Traffic guidance, warning or control posts or bollards, delineator posts, milestones. Devices intended to be readily removable, e.g. for insertion into road-stud sockets.  Storing, transporting, placing or retrieving portable devices.  Arrangements for fastening signs or signals to safety barriers or the like.  Road surface markings, also including methods of forming, installing or applying markings in, on or to paving.  Kerbs or road edgings specially adapted for informing road users, e.g. illuminated.  Arrangements specially adapted for audible or vibrational signalling, e.g. rumble strips, or for enforcing reduced speed, e.g. speed bumps.  Traffic studs, marking blocks, also including methods of forming or installing same in or on the road surface. [E01F 9/30] Arrangements interacting with transmitters or receivers otherwise than by visible means, e.g. using radar reflectors or radio transmitters -- E01F 9/30 pertains to road-related constructions that employ non-visual communication with transmitters or receivers, such as radar reflectors, radio beacons or similar electronic signalling devices used to guide traffic, enhance safety, or support the operation of roads and related infrastructure.
[E01F 9/40] Arrangements responsive to adverse atmospheric conditions, e.g. to signal icy roads or to automatically illuminate in fog -- E01F 9/40 covers road-side or related installations that detect adverse weather and provide safety responses, such as devices that signal icy surfaces or automatically activate lighting in fog. These arrangements are intended to improve traffic safety under hazardous atmospheric conditions.
[E01F 9/50] Road surface markings -- E01F 9/50 covers road surface markings, the painted or otherwise applied signs and symbols on roadways that guide traffic, indicate lane boundaries, speed limits or other information to facilitate safe use of the road.

It falls under additional work for facilitating road use.
[E01F 9/512] Preformed road surface markings, e.g. of sheet material -- This CPC subclass E01F 9/512 covers preformed road surface markings made from sheet material, such as signs or lane markers, that are applied to roadways to provide visual guidance. It belongs to the fixed-construction group for additional work that facilitates the use of roads.
[E01F 9/518] formed -- E01F 9/518 pertains to formed arrangements that facilitate road use, such as built-up or molded road surfaces, formed shoulders, and other shaped structures that improve traffic flow and safety.
[E01F 9/524] Reflecting elements specially adapted for incorporation in or application to road surface markings -- The entry E01F 9/524 covers reflectors and other retro-reflective components that are designed to be incorporated directly into road-surface markings. These specially adapted elements, such as embedded glass beads or reflective paint, enhance the visibility of lane lines, signs, snow fences and similar roadway features, thereby facilitating safer and more efficient use of roads.
[E01F 9/529] specially adapted for signalling by sound or vibrations, e.g. rumble strips -- E01F 9/529 designates road-related constructions that generate sound or vibration to warn users, such as rumble strips or other tactile-acoustic surfaces installed on highways, bridges or landing pads to enhance safety.
[E01F 9/535] Kerbs or road edgings specially adapted for alerting road users -- Kerbs or road edgings specially adapted for alerting road users are devices installed along road edges that provide visual, tactile or auditory cues to improve safety, such as raised profiles, bright colors, reflective surfaces, or embedded lighting to warn drivers and pedestrians of the roadway boundary. These specialized kerbs are used in areas such as intersections, pedestrian zones and near hazards to enhance visibility and reduce accidents.
[E01F 9/541] Kerbs -- Kerbs are structural edges placed alongside roads or pavements to separate vehicular traffic from pedestrian zones, aid drainage, and define the roadway margin. In the CPC they are classified under additional work that facilitates road use, such as equipping roads, platforms, and related installations.
[E01F 9/547] illuminated -- The CPC subclass E01F 9/547 pertains to illuminated arrangements that facilitate the use of roads, encompassing lighting systems for road signs, helipad landing stages, snow fences and similar equipment that enhance visibility and safety.
[E01F 9/553] Low discrete bodies, e.g. marking blocks, studs or flexible vehicle-striking members -- The entry covers low, discrete components mounted on a road surface to improve safety or visibility, such as marking blocks, studs or flexible members that strike vehicles. These devices are installed as part of arrangements that facilitate road use, providing tactile or visual cues, impact protection or guidance for vehicles.
[E01F 9/559] illuminated -- The CPC subclass E01F 9/559 covers illuminated installations that form part of road-related infrastructure to aid the use of roads, such as lighting for signs, platforms, helicopter landing stages, snow fences or similar structures, providing visibility and safety for motorists and users.
[E01F 9/565] having deflectable or displaceable parts -- The E01F 9/565 entry covers fixed constructions that serve as additional works to improve road use, such as equipment, platforms, or protective structures, where the elements incorporated can be moved or flexed. It includes arrangements like deflectable or displaceable parts that adjust to traffic, weather or operational needs.
[E01F 9/571] displaceable vertically under load, e.g. in combination with rotation -- E01F 9/571 covers elements of road or platform constructions that can move vertically under load, often together with a rotation, allowing adjustable height or position for items such as signs, snow fences, helicopter landing stages or similar equipment.
[E01F 9/573] Self-righting, upright flexible or rockable markers, e.g. resilient flaps bending over  depressible road surface signs or flexible vehicle-striking members for audible warning  E01F9/602 -- E01F 9/573 covers self-righting, upright markers that are flexible or rockable, such as resilient flaps that bend over depressible road-surface signs or flexible members that strike vehicles to produce an audible warning. These devices are used in fixed constructions and additional road-related work to improve safety and visibility.
[E01F 9/576] Traffic lines -- E01F 9/576 relates to the use of traffic lines and other surface markings that guide vehicle movement and enhance safety on roads, tracks, helicopter landing stages and similar pathways, encompassing painted lines, lane delimiters and comparable visual cues.
[E01F 9/578] consisting of preformed elements, e.g. tapes, block-type elements specially designed or arranged to make up a traffic line  E01F9/582 ,  E01F9/588 ,  E01F9/594  take precedence; studs, blocks or like markers  per se   E01F9/553 -- This subclass concerns the use of pre-formed components such as tapes, block-type elements, studs or similar markers that are specially designed or arranged to form traffic lines and other road-facilitating arrangements. It covers the installation of these fixed-construction elements for road safety, signage, snow fences and related applications.
[E01F 9/582] illuminated -- E01F 9/582 covers illuminated devices or systems incorporated into road infrastructure to improve visibility and safety, such as streetlights, illuminated signs, or lighting for helicopter landing pads, snow fences and similar installations.
[E01F 9/588] Lane delineators for physically separating traffic lanes and discouraging but not preventing crossing -- The entry E01F 9/588 covers lane-delineator devices installed on roadways to create a physical separation between traffic lanes, discouraging vehicles from crossing while not completely preventing movement across the barrier, and is used in arrangements that facilitate road use.
[E01F 9/594] movable for reuse at different locations -- The subgroup E01F 9/594 includes movable constructions that can be relocated and reused to facilitate road use, such as temporary signs, barriers, snow fences, helicopter landing stages or similar equipment that can be installed, removed and moved to different locations.
[E01F 9/60] Upright bodies, e.g. marker posts or bollards -- E01F 9/60 is a CPC subclass for upright bodies such as marker posts or bollards employed in road-related installations. It falls under Fixed Constructions → Building → Additional work, covering items that facilitate the use of roads.
[E01F 9/602] Upright or projecting signs or signals permanently installed directly on or in the roadway and designed to be depressed by rolling or other loads and then to return to normal position, e.g. collapsible or rockable stop signs, flexible vehicle-striking members for audible warning  E01F9/565 ,  E01F13/10  take precedence -- This CPC entry covers upright or projecting traffic signs and signals that are permanently mounted directly on or within the roadway and are designed to be depressed by rolling or other loads and then automatically return to their normal position, such as collapsible or rockable stop signs and flexible vehicle-striking members that generate audible warnings. It falls under the hierarchy of fixed constructions, building, and additional work for facilitating road use.
[E01F 9/604] specially adapted for particular signalling purposes, e.g. for indicating curves, road works or pedestrian crossings -- E01F 9/604 falls under fixed constructions for building, covering additional road works such as signs, snow fences and platforms. It refers to arrangements that are specially adapted for specific signalling purposes, for example devices that highlight curves, road works or pedestrian crossings.
[E01F 9/608] for guiding, warning or controlling traffic, e.g. delineator posts or milestones -- This CPC entry covers devices installed along roads to guide, warn or control traffic, such as delineator posts, milestones and similar markers. It belongs to the fixed-construction group for additional road-related work, specifically arrangements that facilitate the use of roads.
[E01F 9/61] Special features of delineator posts, e.g. with parts cantilevered toward the roadway or fixed vertically on a tilted surface  E01F9/669 ,  E01F9/535 ,  E01F9/576  take precedence -- E01F9/61 pertains to special features of road delineator posts, such as cantilevered elements extending toward the travel lane or posts mounted vertically on a sloped surface. The subclass covers designs that improve visibility or stability of these markers as part of road-facilitating arrangements.
[E01F 9/612] for indicating freshly applied road surface markings, e.g. straddling lines -- E01F 9/612, a Cooperative Patent Classification entry, falls under Fixed Constructions → Building → Additional Work such as equipping roads and relates to arrangements that aid road use. It specifically covers devices or methods for indicating freshly applied road surface markings, for example temporary straddling lines placed on new pavement.
[E01F 9/615] illuminated -- E01F 9/615 covers illuminated devices or systems that are incorporated into road-related constructions such as signs, platforms, helipad stages or snow fences to improve visibility and safety. It includes lighting arrangements for guiding traffic, marking boundaries, or providing illumination for equipment on the road.
[E01F 9/617] Illuminated or wired-up posts, bollards, pillars or like upstanding bodies or structures for traffic guidance, warning or control  E01F9/559  takes precedence -- The subclass E01F 9/617 covers illuminated or electrically wired vertical bodies such as posts, bollards, pillars or similar structures that are installed on roads to provide traffic guidance, warning or control. It includes any lighting, wiring or signalling elements integrated into these upstanding devices and falls under arrangements for facilitating road use.
[E01F 9/619] with reflectors -- E01F 9/619 covers road-related installations that incorporate reflective devices to enhance visibility and safety, such as reflectors mounted on signs, barriers, snow fences, or other equipment used to facilitate road use.
[E01F 9/623] characterised by form or by structural features, e.g. for enabling displacement or deflection -- The code covers road-related structures whose shape or structural design provides functions such as allowing movement, flexure or displacement. It includes elements like flexible barriers, adjustable ramps or other forms that enable deflection under load.
[E01F 9/627] self-righting after deflection or displacement -- E01F 9/627 covers devices or structures that automatically return to their original position after being displaced, such as self-righting road signs, barriers, snow fences or helicopter landing platforms, ensuring continued functionality of ancillary road-related installations.
[E01F 9/629] Traffic guidance, warning or control posts, bollards, pillars or like upstanding bodies or structures -- This subclass covers upright structures such as traffic guidance, warning or control posts, bollards and pillars used to direct or protect vehicle and pedestrian movement on roads and related sites. These elements are installed as part of road arrangements to manage traffic flow, enhance safety and delineate areas.
[E01F 9/631] specially adapted for breaking, disengaging, collapsing or permanently deforming when deflected or displaced, e.g. by vehicle impact
[E01F 9/635] by shearing or tearing, e.g. having weakened zones -- Code E01F 9/635 covers road-related structures formed by shearing or tearing the material to create intentionally weakened zones, such as frangible sections that facilitate road use or maintenance. These features belong to additional works like snow fences, signs, or landing stages where controlled breakage assists the arrangement of the road system.
[E01F 9/638] by connection of stud-and-socket type, e.g. spring-loaded -- E01F 9/638 pertains to systems that aid road use through stud-and-socket, often spring-loaded, connections. These arrangements are used in additional road-related works such as equipping roads, building platforms or other fixed constructions. The code falls under the Fixed Constructions → Building → Additional Work hierarchy.
[E01F 9/642] friction-coupled -- E01F 9/642 covers friction-coupled devices used in additional road-related works. The subclass includes arrangements that rely on friction coupling to connect, guide or stabilize elements such as platforms, helicopter landing stages, signs or snow fences, thereby facilitating the use of roads.
[E01F 9/644] by connection of the "slide-shoe" type -- The entry E01F 9/644 covers arrangements that aid the use of roads by connecting slide-shoe type components, such as removable or adjustable fixtures that slide along a shoe to support signs, fences, or other road equipment. It falls under fixed constructions and additional work for equipping roads, platforms, helicopter landing stages, and similar structures.
[E01F 9/646] extensible, collapsible or pivotable -- E01F 9/646 covers road-related devices or structures that can be extended, folded or swivelled to provide temporary or adjustable support, protection or guidance, such as collapsible barriers, extendable platforms, pivoting signs or detachable snow fences used in road construction and maintenance.
[E01F 9/65] with rotatable, swingable or adjustable signs or signals -- This class pertains to road infrastructure where signs or signals are mounted on mechanisms that allow rotation, swinging or adjustment to change orientation or display. It includes devices such as rotating traffic signs, swingable warnings, adjustable signal arms used to manage traffic or convey information.
[E01F 9/654] in the form of three-dimensional [3D] bodies, e.g. cones -- E01F 9/654 covers three-dimensional objects such as cones that are installed as part of arrangements to facilitate road use, including traffic control, marking, and safety devices. These items belong to the additional-work category of fixed constructions for roads, platforms, helipad surfaces and similar infrastructure.
[E01F 9/658] characterised by means for fixing -- The CPC subclass E01F 9/658 covers constructions that improve road use by incorporating means for fixing equipment such as signs, snow fences, helicopter landing stages or platforms to the road structure. It falls under fixed constructions, building and additional work for equipping roads.
[E01F 9/662] mounted on vehicles, e.g. service vehicles -- The classification covers equipment that is installed on vehicles, such as service or maintenance trucks, to aid in road use. Examples include temporary signage, lighting, or snow-fence units that can be deployed while the vehicle is positioned or moving.
[E01F 9/669] for fastening to safety barriers or the like -- E01F 9/669 covers arrangements that attach or fasten components to roadside safety barriers and similar structures, facilitating the use of roads. It falls under Fixed Constructions - Building - Additional work such as equipping roads, including platforms, helicopter landing stages, signs, and snow fences.
[E01F 9/673] for holding sign posts or the like -- E01F 9/673 covers devices or structures designed to support or mount signposts, traffic signs, or similar elements along roads and related infrastructure. It includes brackets, frames, poles and mounting assemblies that facilitate the placement and visibility of signs in road construction, equipment, and auxiliary works.
[E01F 9/675] Glued to surface, e.g. road -- This subclass concerns constructions where a material is bonded directly to a road or similar surface by means of an adhesive. It includes techniques for applying glued surfacing, markings, protective layers, or other elements that become integral with the underlying pavement, facilitating road use and related structures.
[E01F 9/677] the sign posts being removable without tools, e.g. of stud-and-socket type -- The entry covers designs of road sign posts that can be removed or installed without special tools, typically using a stud-and-socket connection, facilitating quick replacement or reconfiguration of signage.
[E01F 9/681] the sign posts being fastened by removable means, e.g. screws or bolts -- This subclass covers road-related sign installations where the sign posts are attached by removable means such as screws or bolts, allowing the signs to be taken down or repositioned easily. It applies to traffic, guide or safety signs that are mounted on removable posts for temporary or adjustable use.
[E01F 9/685] Subsoil means, e.g. foundations -- E01F 9/685 concerns subsoil means such as foundations employed in fixed-construction projects, particularly where additional works like road equipment, platforms, helicopter landing stages, signs or snow fences are involved. It falls under arrangements for facilitating the use of roads within the building group.
[E01F 9/688] Free-standing bodies -- E01F 9/688 covers free-standing bodies installed as part of additional work for road use, such as standalone signs, snow fences, platform or heli-landing structures that assist traffic flow or safety. These elements are not attached to existing infrastructure but stand independently to facilitate road operation.
[E01F 9/692] Portable base members therefor -- The code E01F 9/692 covers portable base members used in road-related arrangements such as temporary platforms, landing stages, sign supports, or snow-fence foundations. These components are designed to be moved and positioned as needed to facilitate road usage and associated equipment.
[E01F 9/696] Overhead structures, e.g. gantries -- This subclass defines overhead structures built over or beside roads, such as gantries that support signs, lighting, or other equipment to improve traffic flow and safety. It is part of the fixed-construction category for additional road-related work.
[E01F 9/70] Storing, transporting, placing or retrieving portable devices -- The class covers systems and methods for storing, transporting, placing or retrieving portable devices that support road use, such as signage, barriers, temporary lighting, snow fences or similar equipment. It relates to additional road-related works like constructing platforms, helicopter landing stages or other fixed constructions that facilitate traffic flow.
[E01H] STREET CLEANING -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods and apparatus for cleaning streets, sports areas and the like.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Mowers convertible to apparatus for sweeping or cleaning lawns or other surfaces, e.g. to remove snow, or capable of sweeping or cleaning lawns or other surfaces -> A01D42/06 - Domestic, i.e. indoor cleaning -> A47L5/00 - A47L11/00
[E01H 1/00] Removing undesirable matter from roads or like surfaces, with or without moistening of the surface -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Apparatus for removing undesirable matter, e.g. by brushing, pneumatic, hydraulic or magnetic effect.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Moistening per se -> E01H3/00 - Removing snow -> E01H5/00 - Cleaning tramway rails -> E01H8/00 - Mower -> A01D34/00 - Sweeping apparatus particularly for lawns -> A01G20/30 , A01G20/40 - Removing undesirable matter from (indoor) floors and similar surfaces -> A47L - Obstruction-removers on vehicles -> B60R19/00 - Removing undesirable matter in combination with application of bitumen or the like -> E01C19/16 - Cleaning cuts, grooves in pavement; trimming pavement edges -> E01C23/09 - Removing undesirable matter in combination with application of traffic lines -> E01C23/16 - Removing oil or like floating materials from open water -> E02B15/04 - Built in cleaning device for tunnels or other underground cavities -> E21D11/003
[E01H 10/00] Improving gripping of ice-bound or other slippery traffic surfaces, e.g. using gritting or thawing materials -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Treatment of dispersed oil or similar pollution on roads -> E01H1/001 - Roughening ice by means of tools -> E01H5/12 - Applying de-icing agents to rails -> E01H8/10 - Thawing materials -> C09K3/18 - Improving gripping by constructional features -> E01C11/24 - Apparatus for distributing granular materials for road building purposes -> E01C19/20
[E01H 10/002] Roadside storage of gritting or solid thawing materials, e.g. grit or salt bins -- The code refers to installations positioned beside roads that store solid de-icing or gritting materials such as salt, grit or other thawing agents. These roadside bins enable quick distribution of the material onto icy or slippery traffic surfaces to improve vehicle traction.
[E01H 10/005] Permanently-installed devices for applying gritting or thawing materials, e.g. for spreading grit, for spraying de-icing liquids -- This class covers permanently installed systems that dispense solid gritting material or liquid de-icing agents onto traffic surfaces to improve traction, including automated spreaders, sprayers and related control mechanisms integrated into road infrastructure.
[E01H 10/007] Mobile apparatus specially adapted for preparing or applying liquid or semi-liquid thawing material or spreading granular material on wintry roads -- The code E01H 10/007 covers mobile machines designed to apply liquid or semi-liquid de-icing agents or to spread granular grit on icy or otherwise slippery road surfaces. These devices are used in street-cleaning operations to improve traction on wintry roads.
[E01H 1/001] Treatment of dispersed oil or similar pollution on roads, for instance devices for applying treating agents  hydraulic loosening or removal of liquids  E01H1/10 ; from surface of open water  E02B15/04 -- This CPC subclass deals with methods and apparatus for treating oil or similar pollutants spread on road surfaces, including devices that apply cleaning agents and hydraulic systems that loosen or extract the liquids. It also encompasses related techniques for removing such contaminants from open-water surfaces as referenced by related subclasses.
[E01H 1/003] Upkeep of road sides along the pavement, for instance cleaning devices particularly for side strips  control of undesirable vegetation  E01H11/00 ; mowers for embankments  A01D34/86 ; soil working  E02F ; trimming pavement edges  E01C23/09 -- E01H 1/003 relates to the upkeep of road sides along the pavement, encompassing cleaning devices for side strips, removal of unwanted vegetation, mowing of embankments, soil-working actions and trimming of pavement edges to keep the roadway margin free of debris and overgrowth.
[E01H 1/005] Mobile installations, particularly for upkeeping  in situ  road or railway furniture, for instance road barricades, traffic signs; Mobile installations particularly for upkeeping tunnel walls  built-in cleaning devices  E21D11/003 -- E01H 1/005 covers mobile units used to maintain road or railway furniture such as barricades, traffic signs, and tunnel walls, and may incorporate built-in cleaning devices for removing debris or contaminants on site.
[E01H 1/006] Specially adapted for removing excrements  hand implements  E01H1/12 -- The code E01H 1/006 covers street-cleaning tools that are specially adapted for the removal of animal or human excrement, typically handheld implements used to scrape, scoop or otherwise pick up waste from road or pavement surfaces. This falls within the broader categories of fixed constructions, building, and street cleaning in the Cooperative Patent Classification system.
[E01H 1/008] in situ  treatment of excrements -- The E01H 1/008 class addresses technologies for the in-situ treatment of excrements on streets, roads or similar surfaces, encompassing methods and apparatus that neutralise, disinfect or otherwise manage waste directly on the site, optionally using moisture, without removing the material for off-site processing.
[E01H 1/02] Brushing apparatus, -- E01H 1/02 covers brushing apparatus used to remove unwanted material from roads, pavements or similar surfaces, with or without moistening. The devices are typically mounted on fixed or mobile cleaning units and employ rotating or reciprocating brushes to sweep debris, sand, dust or liquids as part of street-cleaning operations.
[E01H 1/04] taking- up the sweepings, e.g. for collecting, for loading -- This subclass deals with devices that collect the material swept from roads or similar surfaces and transfer it to a container or conveyance system for loading or disposal, such as conveyors, suction pickups, or mechanical grabs that gather the sweepings after they have been removed.
[E01H 1/042] the loading means being an endless belt or an auger -- E01H 1/042 covers street-cleaning machines that collect debris using a continuous belt or an auger as the loading mechanism. It falls under fixed-construction building methods for removing unwanted material from roads, with or without moistening.
[E01H 1/045] the loading means being a rotating brush with horizontal axis  E01H1/042  takes precedence -- This subclass covers street-cleaning machines that use a loading mechanism consisting of a brush rotating about a horizontal axis to pick up debris from roads or similar surfaces, with or without water application. It falls under Fixed Constructions - Building - Street Cleaning, aimed at removing undesirable matter from paved ways.
[E01H 1/047] Collecting apparatus characterised by the hopper or by means for unloading the hopper -- E01H 1/047 covers street-cleaning devices that collect debris using a hopper or incorporate a mechanism for unloading that hopper, designed to remove unwanted material from roads or similar surfaces, with or without moistening.
[E01H 1/05] with driven brushes -- The subclass covers street-cleaning devices that use powered, rotating brushes to dislodge and remove debris from roads or similar surfaces, either dry or with the surface wetted. It applies to fixed-construction equipment designed specifically for cleaning paved ways.
[E01H 1/053] having vertical axes -- E01H 1/053 covers street-cleaning or road-cleaning apparatuses that remove debris using cleaning elements that rotate about vertical axes, such as vertically mounted rotating brushes or rollers, with or without water application. The code falls under Fixed Constructions → Building → Street Cleaning, describing devices that clean surfaces by vertical-axis motion.
[E01H 1/056] having horizontal axes -- This subclass covers street-cleaning equipment that removes debris from roads or similar surfaces using cleaning elements mounted on horizontal axes, such as rotating brushes or brooms, and may include optional moistening of the surface.
[E01H 1/08] Pneumatically dislodging or taking-up undesirable matter -- E01H 1/08 covers pneumatic techniques for street cleaning, using air blasts or suction to dislodge and collect unwanted material from road surfaces, with or without prior moistening of the pavement.
[E01H 1/0809] Loosening or dislodging by blowing  combined with suction  E01H1/0863 ; with steam  E01H1/10 ; removing snow by blowing  E01H5/106 ; cleaning rails by blowing  E01H8/105 ,  E01H8/125 ; removing fog by blowing  E01H13/00 ; cleaning joints in road construction by blowing  E01C23/0906 ; Drying by means of gas streams  by heat only  E01H1/08 ; combined with snow or ice melting  E01H5/10 ,  E01H5/106 ,  E01H5/108 -- E01H 1/0809 covers methods of loosening or dislodging unwanted material from roads or similar surfaces by using a blowing action, often together with suction, steam, or other agents such as heat or ice-melting substances. [E01H 1/10] Hydraulically loosening or dislodging undesirable matter -- The CPC subclass E01H 1/10 covers methods that use water pressure or similar hydraulic forces to loosen, break up, or dislodge unwanted debris, snow, ice, or other material from roads, pavements or comparable surfaces, either before or in combination with subsequent cleaning actions.

It is a subcategory of Fixed Constructions → Building → Street Cleaning, focusing on magnetic-effect removal of undesirable matter.

The methods focus on restoring beach conditions without transporting material offsite, and are superseded by the more specific subclass E01H 12/006.

[E01H 15/00] Removing undesirable matter, e.g. rubbish, from the land, not otherwise provided for -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Gatherers for removing stones, roots or the like from the soil -> A01B43/00
[E01H 2001/0881] Details for pneumatically dislodging or removing not related to the mouth-piece -- E01H 2001/0881 covers features of harvesting equipment that use pneumatic forces to dislodge or remove plant material-such as stalks, leaves or fruit-where the air-driven action is applied directly to the material rather than through a mouth-piece. It includes designs, mechanisms and operational details for air-pressure blow-off or suction systems that detach the crop during harvesting. This subclass refines the broader stalk-and-leaf-removal group with a focus on non-mouth-piece pneumatic techniques.
[E01H 2001/089] Suction-effect created through blowing, e.g. using the venturi effect -- The code E01H 2001/089 covers devices that generate a suction effect by directing a stream of fluid, typically air, to create a low-pressure zone such as with a venturi nozzle. It includes mechanisms that use the kinetic energy of the blowing flow to draw or transport material without moving parts, commonly applied in agricultural, horticultural and forestry equipment.
[E01H 2001/122] Details
[E01H 2001/1226] characterised by way of removing material -- The group E01H 2001/1226 contains inventions whose essential technical feature is the removal of material, such as cutting, grinding, shaving, or other ablation processes applied during soil working, earth moving or similar operations. It is used to classify devices or methods that achieve the intended function principally by subtracting material from the workpiece.
[E01H 2001/1233] by pressing a tool in the material to be removed -- The CPC subclass E01H 2001/1233 covers devices or methods that remove material by pressing a tool directly into the material to be removed, such as weeding or soil-working tools that engage and extract unwanted portions by pressure rather than cutting or pulling. It falls within the broader group for mechanical and hydraulic soil-working implements.
[E01H 2001/124] Glove-like means -- This CPC subgroup covers glove-like devices used in harvesting operations, such as protective or handling gloves that aid in picking, holding, or shielding crops, fruits, or vegetables during field work. It includes any hand-covering tool that functions as part of agricultural harvest equipment.
[E01H 2001/1246] Double or multiple closing means making horizontal sliding movement (not applied for grabs) -- This CPC subclass covers agricultural or horticultural containers, hoppers and similar equipment that use two or more sliding doors moving horizontally to close an opening, where the movement is not intended for gripping or grabbing. It is limited to double or multiple closing means that slide side-to-side to seal the entry.
[E01H 2001/1253] by using shoes to remove the material -- This CPC entry covers techniques in which a person employs shoes - such as boots, cleats, or specialized footwear - to detach, scrape, or lift a material, for example soil, debris, or plant matter, from a surface during agricultural or landscaping operations. It includes manual or mechanized processes where the shoe itself functions as the removal tool.
[E01H 2001/126] the implement being for single use but not glove-like -- E01H 2001/126 covers agricultural implements designed for single-use applications that are not glove-type devices, such as disposable seed-planters, sprayers or other one-time tools used in crop production. This subclass groups non-protective, consumable equipment intended to be discarded after a single operation.
[E01H 2001/1266] having lighting means -- E01H 2001/1266 relates to agricultural or horticultural machines or tools that incorporate lighting devices, such as LEDs or lamps, to promote plant growth, assist seedling development, or improve visibility during operation. The lighting means may be integrated into planters, seed drills, or other cultivation equipment.
[E01H 2001/1273] having a dog leash -- E01H 2001/1273 designates patents for dog leashes and related restraining devices, covering structures that connect a dog to a holder, length adjustment, breakaway safety, and ergonomic handles. It includes accessories such as retractable mechanisms, reflective elements, and attachment fittings for training or walking applications.
[E01H 2001/128] having means for storing bags or the like -- This subclass covers agricultural or horticultural machines that incorporate a system for holding bags, such as collection or storage compartments for harvested produce, bagged seeds, or waste. The feature may be integrated into the machine chassis or detachable and includes mechanisms for loading, securing, and unloading the bags.
[E01H 2001/1286] storing used bags -- The code refers to methods or apparatus for collecting and storing bags that have already been used in horticultural or agricultural operations, such as harvest or transport bags, to enable their reuse, recycling or safe disposal. It covers systems that keep the used bags in a designated container or area until further handling.
[E01H 2001/1293] Walking cane or the like, i.e. implements allowing handling while in an upright position -- The class covers handheld support devices that enable a user to remain upright while walking or standing, such as walking sticks, canes, crutches and similar aids. These implements are primarily intended to provide balance, reduce weight bearing on the legs, or assist mobility for persons who need support.
[E01H 2004/026] for making half pipes -- The CPC entry E01H 2004/026 falls under the E01H group dealing with earth moving and excavation works and specifically addresses processes or equipment for fabricating half-pipe elements, such as semicircular culvert sections, used in civil-engineering infrastructure. It includes methods of shaping, casting or assembling these curved conduit components.
[E01H 3/00] Applying liquids to roads or like surfaces, e.g. for dust control -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Mobile and fixed devices mainly for dust control.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Applying hot water for melting snow -> E01H5/10 - Weed destruction on roads -> E01H11/00 - Watering sportsgrounds -> A01G25/00 - Dust-laying agents -> C09K3/22
[E01H 3/02] Mobile apparatus, e.g. watering-vehicles -- The classification E01H 3/02 covers mobile equipment, such as watering-vehicles, that spray or distribute liquids onto roads, sidewalks or similar surfaces for cleaning, dust suppression or other maintenance purposes. The apparatus is mounted on a vehicle and is intended for use in street-cleaning operations.
[E01H 3/04] Fixed devices, e.g. permanently- installed flushing means -- This class covers permanently installed devices that deliver liquids onto roads or similar surfaces for cleaning, dust control or de-icing, such as built-in flushing or sprinkling mechanisms integrated into the pavement. It includes fixed spray nozzles, embedded piping and other permanent systems for applying water, chemicals or other fluids.
[E01H 4/00] Working on surfaces of snow or ice in order to make them suitable for traffic or sporting purposes, e.g. by compacting snow -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Working on surfaces of snow or ice in order to make them suitable for traffic or sporting purposes, e.g. by compacting snow.
[E01H 4/02] for sporting purposes, e.g. preparation of ski trails; , e.g. devices adapted for ski-trails -- The E01H 4/02 classification covers techniques and equipment used to work on snow or ice surfaces for sporting purposes, such as preparing ski trails. It includes methods for compacting snow, grooming, and other actions that make the surface suitable for activities like skiing or snowboarding. This falls under the broader categories of fixed constructions, building, and street cleaning within the Cooperative Patent Classification system.
[E01H 4/023] Maintaining surfaces for ice sports, e.g. of skating rinks -- This subclass covers techniques and apparatus for preparing and maintaining ice surfaces used in sports such as skating rinks, including actions like compacting snow or ice to create a suitable playing field. It falls under fixed constructions, building and street-cleaning categories.
[E01H 5/00] Removing snow or ice from roads or like surfaces -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Roughening by applying de-icing agents -> E01H10/00 - Obstruction removers on vehicles -> B60R19/00 - Removing snow from vehicles -> B60S - Stationary paving heating devices -> E01C11/26 - Removing snow from roofs -> E04D13/00
[E01H 5/02] Hand implements -- This classification covers manual tools used for clearing snow or ice from streets, sidewalks and similar surfaces. It includes handheld devices such as shovels, ice scrapers and brushes designed for street-cleaning tasks. The code E01H 5/02 is placed under fixed constructions, building and street cleaning.
[E01H 5/04] Apparatus propelled by animal or engine power -- The E01H 5/04 class covers self-propelled devices used for clearing snow or ice from roads and similar surfaces, powered either by animals such as horses or by internal-combustion or electric engines. Examples include motorised snowplows, horse-drawn snow shovels and ice-cutting machines.
[E01H 5/045] Means  per se  for conveying or discharging the dislodged material, e.g. rotary impellers, discharge chutes  dislodging apparatus provided therewith  E01H5/07 ,  E01H5/08 ,  E01H5/104 ,  E01H5/12 -- E01H 5/045 covers devices that transport or eject snow, ice or other material removed from road surfaces, such as rotary impellers, discharge chutes and similar conveying mechanisms integrated with the dislodging apparatus.
[E01H 5/06] dislodging essentially by non-driven elements, e.g. scraper blades -- This subclass covers systems that remove snow or ice from roads using passive, non-driven components such as fixed scraper blades rather than powered brushes or hydraulic jets. The technology is applied to street-cleaning or de-icing equipment that relies on the vehicle's motion or gravity to dislodge the accumulation.
[E01H 5/061] by scraper blades -- A method for removing snow or ice from road surfaces that employs scraper blades, classified under street-cleaning operations within the fixed-construction group for building works. This entry covers blade-based snow- and ice-removal techniques on paved ways.
[E01H 5/062] by scraper blades displaceable for shock-absorbing purposes -- This subclass (E01H 5/062) covers street-cleaning or road-maintenance devices that remove snow or ice using scraper blades that are designed to be displaceable for shock-absorbing purposes, allowing the blades to flex or move under impact while clearing surfaces.
[E01H 5/063] by snow-plough blades tiltable for shock-absorbing purposes -- E01H 5/063 covers fixed snow-removal devices used in street cleaning, specifically machines whose snow-plough blades can be tilted to provide shock-absorbing action while clearing snow or ice from roads and similar surfaces.
[E01H 5/065] characterised by the form of the snow-plough blade, e.g. flexible, or by snow-plough blade accessories  E01H5/061 ,  E01H5/063  take precedence -- This CPC subclass covers snow-ploughs distinguished by the form of their blade, such as flexible or otherwise shaped blades, and by the inclusion of special blade accessories. It falls within the street-cleaning group of fixed-construction building equipment.
[E01H 5/066] Snow-plough blade accessories, e.g. deflector plates, skid shoes -- E01H 5/066 pertains to accessories for snow-plough blades, including deflector plates and skid shoes, that are fitted to improve the removal of snow or ice from roads and similar surfaces.
[E01H 5/067] by side-wing snow-plough blades -- The E01H 5/067 entry covers snow-removal equipment that uses side-wing plough blades attached to a vehicle to clear snow or ice from roadways or similar surfaces. It classifies designs where the blades extend outward from the vehicle's central axis, allowing a wider clearing width and improved maneuverability in street-cleaning operations. This falls under the Fixed Constructions hierarchy for building-related street-cleaning methods.
[E01H 5/068] by backblades  scraper blades mounted on the rear of a tractor  E02F3/7622 -- The classification E01H 5/068 covers devices that remove snow or ice from roads or similar surfaces using backblades or scraper blades fixed to the rear of a tractor. It falls under fixed constructions, building, street cleaning, and specifically addresses rear-mounted snow-clearing equipment.
[E01H 5/07] and conveying dislodged material by driven or pneumatic means -- The CPC subclass E01H 5/07 pertains to street-cleaning equipment that removes snow or ice from roads and then transports the dislodged material using driven mechanisms such as belts, augers or pneumatic blowers. This includes any system that conveys the cleared debris away from the surface by mechanical or air-pressured means.
[E01H 5/073] by belt or chain conveyors  dislodging by means of elements secured to endless belts or chains  E01H5/08
[E01H 5/076] by rotary or pneumatic conveying means, e.g. impeller wheels  dislodging by rotary means  E01H5/09 -- E01H 5/076 covers equipment that removes snow or ice from roads by using rotary or pneumatic conveying devices such as impeller wheels that dislodge the material with a rotating action, often combined with a conveyor to transport the cleared snow or ice away from the surface. This method is applied in street-cleaning systems that rely on rotating mechanisms to break up and move frozen deposits.
[E01H 5/08] dislodging essentially by driven elements, -- E01H 5/08 covers methods and devices for removing snow or ice from road or similar surfaces by using driven elements such as rotating blades, flails or other mechanically powered tools that physically dislodge the accumulated material.
[E01H 5/09] the elements being rotary or moving along a closed circular path, e.g. rotary cutter, digging wheels -- E01H 5/09 covers components that rotate or travel on a closed circular path, such as rotary cutters or digging wheels, employed in street-cleaning or snow-and-ice removal machines. The classification groups any element that moves in a circular motion to perform the cleaning or de-icing function.
[E01H 5/092] Brushing elements -- E01H 5/092 covers brush-type components used in street-cleaning equipment designed to remove snow or ice from road surfaces, such as rotating or fixed brushes, brooms and agitator rods that contact the pavement to dislodge and convey frozen material. These elements are part of fixed construction systems for cleaning and de-icing public thoroughfares.
[E01H 5/094] about vertical or substantially vertical axises -- The CPC subclass E01H 5/094 covers equipment or methods for clearing snow or ice from roads or similar surfaces that operate using a vertical or substantially vertical axis, such as plows or rotary devices mounted on a vertically oriented shaft. It falls under Fixed Constructions → Building → Street Cleaning, focusing on the orientation of the working mechanism rather than the specific removal technique.
[E01H 5/096] about axes parallel or substantially parallel to the direction of clearing -- The CPC subclass E01H 5/096 refers to snow- or ice-removal devices whose operating axes are parallel or essentially parallel to the direction of travel during clearing. It includes mechanisms such as scrapers, brushes or rollers mounted so that their motion follows the forward path of the vehicle, improving efficiency on roads, sidewalks and similar surfaces.
[E01H 5/098] about horizontal or substantially horizontal axises perpendicular or substantially perpendicular to the direction of clearing -- E01H 5/098 covers devices for snow or ice removal that operate with horizontal or essentially horizontal shafts that are positioned perpendicular or nearly perpendicular to the direction of clearing, such as rotating brushes or rollers mounted on a vehicle to sweep or melt snow from road surfaces. This subclass falls under Fixed Constructions → Building → Street Cleaning in the Cooperative Patent Classification system.
[E01H 5/10] by application of heat ; -- E01H 5/10 covers techniques that melt or prevent snow and ice on roadways by applying heat, such as heated surfaces, hot fluids, or electrical heating elements, as part of fixed construction street-cleaning systems.
[E01H 5/102] Self-contained devices for melting dislodged snow or ice, e.g. built-in melting chambers, movable melting tanks  combined with removing devices  E01H5/104
[E01H 5/104] Removing devices for dislodging snow or ice; followed by melting the removed material  removing by suction only and subsequent melting  E01H5/106 -- This subclass covers equipment that removes snow or ice from roads or similar surfaces by suction and then melts the collected material, often using heated water or air, integrating removal and on-site melting within street-cleaning systems.
[E01H 5/106] Clearing snow or ice exclusively by means of rays or streams of gas or steam, or by suction with or without melting  E01H5/108  takes precedence; drying by means of gas streams, blowing- off dirt  E01H1/0809 ; sucking-off dirt  E01H1/0827 -- E01H 5/106 covers techniques for removing snow or ice from roads, streets and similar surfaces by directing rays, streams of gas or steam, or by suction, with or without simultaneous melting. The entry also includes related processes such as drying with gas streams and blowing off dirt, and it is superseded by the more specific E01H 5/108.
[E01H 5/108] Devices for melting snow or ice  in situ  and removing subsequently for the mud or melting water  melting  in situ   E01H5/10 ; by means of hot gases or steam  E01H5/106 ; removing water or mud  E01H1/108
[E01H 5/12] Apparatus or implements specially adapted for breaking, disintegrating, or loosening layers of ice or hard snow -- E01H 5/12 covers devices designed to break up or loosen compacted ice or hard snow on roadways and similar surfaces. It includes specialized tools such as ice cutters, snow-breaking plows, and mechanical agitators that fracture frozen layers to facilitate removal. These implements are used in street-cleaning operations to improve safety and traffic flow during winter conditions.
[E01H 6/00] Apparatus equipped with, or having provisions for equipping with, both elements removal of refuse or the like and elements for removal of snow or ice -- The E01H 6/00 class covers machinery that integrates both refuse-collection and snow- or ice-removal capabilities. It includes apparatus designed for street cleaning that can be fitted with or already contains equipment for picking up litter and for clearing snow or ice from public ways.

[E01H 8/02] Methods or apparatus for removing ice or snow from railway tracks, e.g. using snow-ploughs  -- This class covers devices and techniques for clearing ice and snow from railway tracks, such as snow-plows and related equipment, to keep the permanent way safe and operational.

[E01H 8/105] Pneumatically or hydraulically loosening, removing or dislodging undesirable matter, e.g. removing by blowing, flushing, suction; Application of melting liquids; Loosening or removing by means of heat, e.g. cleaning by plasma torches, drying by burners  E01H8/125  takes precedence; drying, blowing or liquid-applying devices incorporated in the permanent way  E01B19/00 ; heating or cooling rails during construction or maintenance  E01B31/18 ; wetting or lubricating rails  B61K3/00 ; plasma torches  H05H1/26 -- E01H 8/105 classifies methods and devices for cleaning the permanent way of railways by pneumatically or hydraulically loosening, removing or dislodging undesirable matter, such as blowing, flushing, suction, applying melting liquids, heat or plasma torches, and includes drying, blowing or liquid-applying devices integrated in the track structure.
