[E02F 9/2242] including an electronic controller -- E02F 9/2242 covers component parts of dredgers or soil-shifting machines that incorporate an electronic controller, such as programmable or sensor-based units used to regulate excavation, pump, or positioning functions within hydraulic-engineered fixed constructions.
[E02F 9/2246] Control of prime movers, e.g. depending on the hydraulic load of work tools -- This subclass deals with control systems for the prime movers of dredging or soil-shifting equipment, adjusting operation according to the hydraulic load of the work tools. It includes mechanisms that modulate power output to match varying demand without being limited to a specific type of dredger.
[E02F 9/225] Control of steering, e.g. for hydraulic motors driving the vehicle tracks  steering in general  B62D -- E02F 9/225 covers steering control arrangements for dredging or soil-shifting machines, particularly those using hydraulic motors to drive vehicle tracks. The code includes general steering mechanisms, linkages, valves and electronic controllers that direct track movement for precise navigation of the dredger.
[E02F 9/2253] Controlling the travelling speed of vehicles, e.g. adjusting travelling speed according to implement loads, control of hydrostatic transmission -- This CPC subclass pertains to mechanisms that regulate the travelling speed of dredging or soil-shifting vehicles, especially systems that adjust speed according to the load of the attached implement and control hydrostatic transmission. It includes speed-control devices, sensors and control algorithms used in hydraulic engineering dredging equipment.
[E02F 9/2257] Vehicle levelling or suspension systems  suspensions for vehicles in general  B60G -- The group includes suspension and levelling mechanisms used on dredging or soil-shifting vehicles, such as springs, hydraulic cylinders and related devices that support the machine's hull or chassis and keep it stable during operation.
[E02F 9/226] Safety arrangements, e.g. hydraulic driven fans, preventing cavitation, leakage, overheating -- This CPC subclass covers safety arrangements for component parts of dredgers and soil-shifting machines, such as hydraulic-driven fans that prevent cavitation, leakage or overheating. The provisions apply to a range of dredging equipment rather than a single specific type.
[E02F 9/2264] Arrangements or adaptations of elements for hydraulic drives -- E02F 9/2264 covers the design and configuration of hydraulic-drive components used in dredgers or soil-shifting machines, focusing on how elements are arranged or adapted to provide power transmission, control or fluid handling within the fixed construction of these hydraulic engineering systems.
[E02F 9/2267] Valves or distributors  position of valves arrangements on upper-structures  E02F9/0875
[E02F 9/2271] Actuators and supports therefor and protection therefor -- E02F 9/2271 covers the actuators, supporting structures and protective devices used on dredgers or other soil-shifting machines. It includes mechanisms that generate or transmit motion, brackets or mounts that hold components in place, and any shielding or guards that safeguard those parts during operation.
[E02F 9/2275] Hoses and supports therefor and protection therefor -- This subgroup concerns hoses employed in dredging and soil-shifting machines, together with the brackets, clamps or other supports that guide and hold them, and any protective devices that shield the hoses from abrasion, pressure surges or environmental damage. It includes all such components not limited to a particular dredger type.
[E02F 9/2278] Hydraulic circuits -- This class covers hydraulic circuit components used in dredging equipment and soil-shifting machines, where the parts are not specifically categorized elsewhere. It includes pumps, valves, pipelines, accumulators, and control devices that form the fluid-power system of such machines.
[E02F 9/2282] Systems using center bypass type changeover valves -- E02F 9/2282 classifies dredger and soil-shifting machine subsystems that employ center-bypass type changeover valves to redirect hydraulic fluid between circuits, allowing rapid switching of power flow or pump operation. The entry covers the design and arrangement of these valve systems as component parts of fixed dredging constructions, without limiting them to any specific machine variant.
[E02F 9/2285] Pilot-operated systems -- E02F 9/2285 relates to pilot-operated systems used as component parts of dredgers or soil-shifting machines. The classification covers hydraulic or pneumatic control mechanisms that use a pilot signal to regulate main power flow in dredging equipment, without being limited to specific machine types.
[E02F 9/2289] Closed circuit -- Closed circuit refers to a dredging system in which the excavated material is moved through a sealed loop of pipes or conduits, allowing the slurry to be processed and returned without exposure to the surrounding environment, and is used in component parts of dredgers or soil-shifting machines.
[E02F 9/2292] Systems with two or more pumps -- This code covers dredging or soil-shifting machines that incorporate two or more pumps operating together as a system. It includes arrangements where pumps are combined to increase capacity, provide redundancy, or enable different pressure stages, without being limited to a specific pump type.
[E02F 9/2296] Systems with a variable displacement pump -- The CPC subclass E02F 9/2296 covers hydraulic systems that incorporate a variable-displacement pump and are used as component parts of dredgers or other soil-shifting machines. It falls within the fixed-construction sector of hydraulic engineering, addressing designs where the pump's displacement can be adjusted to meet varying load or flow requirements.
[E02F 9/24] Safety devices -- E02F 9/24 covers safety devices incorporated in component parts of dredgers and other soil-shifting machines. It includes any protective mechanisms that prevent accidents, control hazardous movements or provide warning functions, without being limited to a specific type of dredger.

[E02F 9/267] Diagnosing or detecting failure of vehicles -- This CPC group relates to technologies for monitoring and diagnosing faults in dredging or soil-shifting vehicles and their components. It includes sensors, diagnostic software, and other devices that detect failures during operation to enable timely maintenance and reduce downtime. [E02F 9/28] Small metalwork for digging elements, e.g. teeth -- E02F 9/28 pertains to small metal components such as teeth attached to digging elements of dredgers or soil-shifting machines, covering parts that are not limited to the specific types listed in other groups. [E02F 9/2866] for rotating digging elements  for milling machines  B28D1/186 ; for mining machines  E21C35/18 -- The CPC entry E02F 9/2866 covers component parts of dredgers or soil-shifting machines that incorporate rotating digging elements, such as those used in milling machines (B28D 1/186) and mining machines (E21C 35/18), without being limited to any single subgroup of equipment.
[E02F 9/2875] Ripper tips -- Ripper tips are the replaceable cutting elements mounted on the rippers of dredgers or soil-shifting machines. They are designed to break up and dislodge sediment or earth during hydraulic excavation. The classification covers all varieties of these components regardless of specific machine type.
[E02F 9/2883] Wear elements for buckets or implements in general -- E02F 9/2883 covers wear elements such as liners, plates or pads fitted to buckets, scoops and other implements of dredgers or soil-shifting machines. These components protect the working surfaces from abrasion and extend equipment life. The class belongs to Fixed Constructions - Hydraulic Engineering - Dredging in the CPC hierarchy.
[E02F 9/2891] Tools for assembling or disassembling -- E02F 9/2891 covers tools used to put together or take apart component parts of dredging or soil-shifting equipment. These tools are not limited to a specific type of dredger and may include wrenches, hammers or specialized fixtures designed for assembling or disassembling machinery sections.
[E03] WATER SUPPLY -- E03 in the CPC under the Fixed Constructions group covers inventions related to water supply systems for buildings or other fixed structures. It includes designs for pipelines, pumps, storage, distribution and control of water, and related components used in residential, commercial or industrial installations.
[E03B] INSTALLATIONS OR METHODS FOR OBTAINING, COLLECTING, OR DISTRIBUTING WATER -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods and devices for collecting drinking water and conveying the water from a source to a tapping point.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Treatment of water for enhancing quality -> C02F - Drilling wells, obtaining fluids in general from wells -> E21B - Pipe-line systems in general -> F17D
[E03B 1/00] Methods or layout of installations for water supply -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Public and local water supply systems; greywater collecting and supply systems.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Prospecting or determining ground water flow -> G01V9/02
[E03B 1/02] for public or like main supply -- This subclass covers designs and methods for supplying water through public mains or comparable distribution networks, including layout of pipelines, pumping stations, storage facilities and control systems used to deliver water to communities or similar users.
[E03B 1/04] for domestic or like local supply -- E03B 1/04 covers the design and arrangement of water-supply systems intended for residential or similar local use, including the planning of pipe networks, storage tanks and distribution points that deliver water directly to houses, apartments or small communities. The classification addresses methods that optimize flow, pressure and accessibility for domestic consumption.
[E03B 1/041] Greywater supply systems -- E03B 1/041 covers the design and arrangement of systems that collect and distribute greywater - reclaimed household wastewater - for non-potable reuse such as irrigation or toilet flushing. It includes plumbing layouts, treatment integration and storage solutions that enable safe, efficient reuse within buildings or to external sites.
[E03B 1/042] Details thereof, e.g. valves or pumps -- Covers the specific components and sub-elements of water-supply installations, such as valves, pumps and other accessory devices that control or move water within the system.
[E03B 1/044] Switch valves in waste pipes -- E03B 1/044 covers switch valves installed in waste pipes as part of water supply systems. The code pertains to methods or layout of installations for obtaining, collecting, or distributing water, specifically the use of controllable valves that can isolate or redirect flow in sewage or drainage lines.
[E03B 1/048] Systems for collecting not used fresh water -- The code E03B 1/048 covers arrangements that capture rainwater, runoff or other fresh water that would otherwise be wasted, directing it into storage or reuse systems. It includes design and layout features for collectors, channels and tanks integrated into water-supply installations.
[E03B 11/00] Arrangements or adaptations of tanks for water supply -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Water storage tanks specially adapted for drinking water which are connected to the municipal or domestic water supply network.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Collecting and storage tanks for rain water -> E03B3/03 - Constructional features of tanks in general -> B65D88/00 - Storing bulk materials -> B65G3/00 - Storing fluids in natural or artificial cavities devices -> B65G5/00 - Assembling of bulk storage containers employing civil engineering techniques -> E04H7/00 - Constructional tower aspects -> E04H12/00
[E03B 11/02] for domestic or like local water supply -- This subgroup pertains to the design and modification of water-storage tanks used in domestic or similar local water-supply systems, addressing how tanks are arranged, integrated, or adapted to provide reliable water distribution within residential or small-scale installations.
[E03B 11/04] without air regulators -- The E03B 11/04 subclass covers fixed water-supply constructions in which tanks are arranged or modified to operate without air-regulating devices. It pertains to installations or methods for obtaining, collecting, or distributing water where the tank design excludes any air-regulator component.
[E03B 11/06] with air regulators -- The subclass E03B 11/06 covers arrangements or adaptations of water-supply tanks that incorporate air-regulating devices, such as pressure-actuated valves or venting mechanisms, to control the air pressure within the tank during water collection, storage, or distribution. It pertains to fixed construction installations for water supply where maintaining appropriate tank pressure is essential.
[E03B 11/08] Air regulators -- This subclass concerns devices that regulate the amount of air or its pressure in water-storage tanks. Air regulators include air valves, pressure-compensating devices and venting arrangements that keep tank pressure within set limits to ensure stable water flow. They are used in fixed water-supply installations to prevent vacuum formation and to control delivery pressure.
[E03B 11/10] for public or like main water supply -- E03B 11/10 relates to fixed constructions for water supply, focusing on the arrangement or adaptation of storage tanks that serve a public or like main water distribution system. It includes designs and modifications of tanks intended for municipal or community water provision.
[E03B 11/12] of high-level tanks -- E03B 11/12 pertains to the design and arrangement of high-level water storage tanks within water-supply installations. It includes supporting structures, positioning, and adaptations that facilitate the collection, storage, and distribution of water from elevated tanks.
[E03B 11/14] of underground tanks -- E03B 11/14 relates to the design, installation and modification of underground water-supply tanks, covering structural supports, lining, access openings, insulation and connections to distribution networks, and any adaptations that improve tank stability, maintenance or integration within a water-supply system.
[E03B 11/16] Adaptations of devices for putting pumping plants in and out of operation, e.g. automatic control devices -- The code addresses devices and control arrangements that switch water pumping plants on or off, including automatic control systems that are integrated with tanks or other fixed water-supply constructions. It covers adaptations that facilitate the operation of pumping stations within water distribution or collection installations.
[E03B 2001/045] using household water -- E03B 2001/045 classifies inventions that employ water drawn from a domestic supply for everyday household functions such as cleaning, cooking, personal hygiene, laundry, and small-scale garden irrigation. It includes systems, devices and methods that manage, distribute or treat water within a residence for these uses.
[E03B 2001/047] using rainwater -- E03B 2001/047 falls under CPC section E for construction and is part of subclass E03B, which deals with storm-water drainage and water-storage devices. The entry covers methods, apparatus and systems that collect, store, treat and reuse rainwater for purposes such as domestic supply, irrigation, industrial processes or groundwater recharge, including rain-water tanks, filtration units and distribution networks.
[E03B 2009/022] Hydrants with a tubular valve seat -- E03B 2009/022 covers fire-hydrant designs in which the shut-off mechanism incorporates a tubular valve seat, meaning the sealing surface for the valve stem is formed as a tube rather than a flat or recessed seat. This subclass groups hydrants that use that tubular configuration to improve sealing reliability, reduce wear, or simplify maintenance within water-distribution and fire-protection systems.
[E03B 2011/005] Tanks with two or more separate compartments divided by, e.g. a flexible membrane -- The subclass covers tanks that contain two or more separate chambers divided by a flexible membrane or similar partition, allowing distinct storage of different fluids or phases within a single container. It includes designs where the barrier can deform to accommodate volume changes while maintaining separation.
[E03B 3/00] Methods or installations for obtaining or collecting drinking water or tap water -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods and installations for collecting and confining water from different sources.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Condensation of vapours in general -> B01D5/00 - Constructional details of containers -> B65D88/00 - Mobile devices for obtaining water and/or treating it -> C02F9/00 - Barrages -> E02B7/00 - Dewatering soil for consolidation -> E02D3/00 - Rainwater deviating devices integrated in down-pipe -> E04D13/08 - Drilling of wells not specially adapted for obtaining water -> E21B43/00 - Dehumidification for air conditioning -> F24F3/00 - Condensers for collecting and removing condensate -> F28B9/08
[E03B 3/02] from rain-water -- E03B 3/02 covers fixed constructions for water supply that involve obtaining or collecting drinking water directly from rain. It includes installations and methods designed to capture, store and distribute rainwater for domestic use.
[E03B 3/03] Special vessels for collecting or storing rain-water for use in the household, e.g. water-butts -- A special vessel such as a water-butt designed to collect rainwater from roofs and store it for domestic use is classified under E03B 3/03. This falls within the broader categories of fixed constructions and water-supply installations or methods for obtaining, collecting, or distributing water. The entry covers household rain-water harvesting systems and the containers used for that purpose.
[E03B 3/04] from surface water -- This classification covers methods or installations that obtain drinking water directly from surface water sources such as rivers, lakes or reservoirs. It includes the capture, treatment and delivery systems used to supply tap water from these sources.
[E03B 3/06] from underground -- This subclass covers installations or methods that obtain drinking or tap water directly from underground sources such as wells, boreholes or aquifers. It includes the design of fixed structures like pump stations and shafts used to access and convey groundwater to the distribution network.
[E03B 3/08] Obtaining and confining water by means of wells -- The CPC class E03B 3/08 concerns methods and installations for obtaining and confining water by means of wells, including drilling, well construction and related equipment used to supply drinking or tap water.
[E03B 3/10] by means of pit wells -- This subclass covers methods and installations that obtain drinking- or tap-water by means of pit wells, shallow excavated wells whose bottom is open to the groundwater. It includes the design, construction and operation of such wells within water-supply systems.
[E03B 3/11] in combination with tubes, e.g. perforated, extending horizontally, or upwardly inclined, exterior to the pits
[E03B 3/12] by means of vertical pipe wells -- Methods or installations that obtain drinking or tap water by drilling a vertical well and inserting a pipe to convey groundwater to the surface, typically using casings, screens, and pumping equipment. The classification covers the design, construction, and operation of such vertical pipe wells for water supply purposes.
[E03B 3/14] by means of horizontal or oblique wells -- Methods and installations that obtain drinking-or tap water through horizontal or obliquely drilled wells, where the well bore extends laterally or at an angle from the surface to intersect aquifers, allowing water to flow into the system without vertical penetration.
[E03B 3/15] Keeping wells in good condition, e.g. by cleaning, repairing, regenerating -- The CPC subclass E03B 3/15 covers methods and installations for maintaining water-supply wells, including cleaning, repairing, and regenerating wells to keep them in good condition for the provision of drinking or tap water.
[E03B 3/16] Component parts of wells -- E03B 3/16 refers to the individual elements that constitute water wells, including casings, screens, pumps, sealing devices and related parts used in installations for obtaining or collecting drinking water. The code applies to component parts of fixed-construction water-supply systems.
[E03B 3/18] Well filters -- E03B 3/18 pertains to well filters, which are fixed constructions installed at water wells to remove impurities from drinking or tap water before it is collected or distributed. This classification falls under water-supply installations and methods for obtaining, collecting, or distributing water.
[E03B 3/20] of elements of special shape -- This subgroup relates to water-supply installations or methods that use elements with a special shape, such as uniquely formed pipes, tanks, or fittings, to obtain, collect or distribute drinking water. It addresses design features that deviate from standard geometric forms to meet functional or space-saving requirements.
[E03B 3/22] Selection of specified materials -- E03B 3/22 pertains to the choice of particular materials used in fixed water-supply constructions, covering installations or methods for obtaining, collecting or distributing drinking or tap water. It addresses how material selection influences the performance, durability and safety of such water-supply systems.
[E03B 3/24] formed of loose materials, e.g. gravel -- E03B 3/24 covers fixed water-supply constructions that are built from loose materials such as gravel, used in installations or methods for obtaining, collecting, or distributing drinking or tap water.
[E03B 3/26] with packed filtering material -- E03B 3/26 covers water-supply installations that obtain or collect drinking water by passing it through a packed filtering material such as sand, gravel or activated carbon. The classification is used for fixed-construction methods where the filter media is placed in a vessel or conduit to remove impurities before distribution.
[E03B 3/28] from humid air -- The subclass E03B 3/28 covers installations or methods that obtain potable water by extracting moisture directly from humid air, typically through condensation or dehumidification processes. It addresses devices such as atmospheric water generators that collect, treat and deliver the resulting water for drinking or tap use.
[E03B 3/30] from snow or ice -- The CPC subclass E03B 3/30 covers installations or processes that obtain drinking or tap water by harvesting and melting snow or ice. It includes systems for collecting, melting, treating and delivering the resulting water for domestic or municipal use.
[E03B 3/32] with artificial enrichment, e.g. by adding water from a pond or a river -- E03B 3/32 covers installations or methods that obtain or collect drinking water by artificially enriching the supply, for example by mixing water drawn from ponds, rivers or similar natural sources with the primary tap water. This subclass falls under fixed constructions related to water-supply systems.
[E03B 3/34] of underground water -- E03B 3/34 covers methods and installations used to obtain or collect drinking water from underground sources such as wells, boreholes, or aquifers, including the associated equipment and techniques for extracting, handling, and delivering the water to a distribution system.
[E03B 3/36] of surface water
[E03B 3/38] Curbs for springs -- E03B 3/38 concerns curbs installed at natural springs to protect and channel the water source, forming fixed constructions that aid in obtaining and collecting drinking water. These structures help prevent contamination and regulate flow at the spring outlet.
[E03B 3/40] Other devices for confining, e.g. trenches, drainage -- E03B 3/40 covers auxiliary devices that confine or guide water in water-supply installations, including trenches, drainage channels and similar structures used to direct or contain water flow.
[E03B 5/00] Use of pumping plants or installations -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Layout of permanent or temporary pumping installations specially adapted to provide drinking water.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Construction, conception and control of pumps in general -> F04
[E03B 5/02] arranged in buildings -- E03B 5/02 refers to water-pumping installations that are incorporated into the structure of a building, including pump rooms, suction or pressure systems and other equipment placed inside premises for supplying or circulating water.
[E03B 5/025] with surface tanks -- E03B 5/025 covers water-supply installations that incorporate surface storage tanks as part of the system. It applies to fixed constructions where pumping plants or similar equipment deliver water into tanks situated above ground for later distribution or use.
[E03B 5/04] arranged in wells -- A system or method that arranges pumping plants or related installations within wells to obtain, collect, or distribute water, forming part of fixed-construction water-supply infrastructure. This includes the configuration of equipment and components placed in wells for water extraction and delivery. It falls under the CPC subclass E03B 5/04.
[E03B 5/045] with surface tanks -- This subclass deals with water-supply systems that use pumping plants together with surface tanks, describing installations where pumped water is stored or regulated in open-top reservoirs above ground.
[E03B 5/06] Special equipment -- E03B 5/06 classifies special equipment used in water-supply pumping plants and related installations, including auxiliary devices, control mechanisms and ancillary machinery that support the collection, transport or distribution of water.
[E03B 7/00] Water main or service pipe systems -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Arrangement of devices within pipe systems for conveying drinking water; layout of drinking water supply systems.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Treatment of water -> C02F - Constructional details of valves -> F16K - Constructional details of hydrant valves -> F16K - Constructional details of component parts per se -> F16L , F16K , G01F - Constructional details of pipes -> F16L - Hot water supply systems -> F24H - Constructional details of flow meters -> G01F - Investigating fluid tightness of structures -> G01M3/00
[E03B 7/003] Arrangement for testing of watertightness of water supply conduits -- The subclass concerns devices or arrangements for testing the watertightness of water-supply conduits such as water mains or service pipe systems. It includes methods and apparatus used to detect leaks or verify seal integrity in fixed water-distribution installations.
[E03B 7/006] Arrangements or methods for cleaning or refurbishing water conduits -- E03B 7/006 covers arrangements or methods for cleaning or refurbishing water conduits in fixed water supply installations, such as water mains or service pipe systems. It includes techniques for removing deposits, repairing, or restoring pipes to ensure proper flow and hygiene.
[E03B 7/02] Public or like main pipe systems -- The classification E03B 7/02 covers fixed water-supply constructions involving public or comparable main pipe networks and service pipe systems that transport, collect or distribute water. It includes the design, installation and layout of municipal water mains and related distribution piping.
[E03B 7/04] Domestic or like local pipe systems -- The subclass E03B 7/04 covers domestic or similar local water-pipe systems, including the fixed constructions used to convey water from mains to buildings and within premises. It falls under water-supply installations for obtaining, collecting, and distributing water, specifically addressing the design and arrangement of service pipe networks for households or comparable sites.
[E03B 7/045] diverting initially cold water in warm water supply -- This subclass covers fixed water-supply constructions, specifically water-main or service-pipe systems, that include means to divert initially cold water into a warm water supply, allowing temperature regulation and improved energy efficiency.
[E03B 7/07] Arrangement of devices, e.g. filters, flow controls, measuring devices, siphons or valves, in the pipe systems -- The CPC entry E03B 7/07 relates to the design and layout of components such as filters, flow-control elements, measuring devices, siphons and valves within fixed water-main or service-pipe systems. It covers how these devices are arranged to obtain, collect or distribute water.
[E03B 7/071] Arrangement of safety devices in domestic pipe systems, e.g. devices for automatic shut-off -- E03B 7/071 pertains to the configuration of safety equipment within household water distribution piping, including the positioning of automatic shut-off valves and related devices to prevent damage from leaks or pipe failures. The classification addresses how these protective components are arranged in domestic main or service pipe systems.
[E03B 7/072] Arrangement of flowmeters -- E03B 7/072 covers the design and placement of flow-metering devices within water-main or service-pipe systems, including how meters are positioned, oriented, grouped or integrated into the pipe network to monitor water flow in supply installations.
[E03B 7/074] Arrangement of water treatment devices -- This subclass covers the layout and organization of devices used to treat water within a water main or service pipe system, including placement of filters, disinfectors, clarifiers, and other treatment units. It addresses how these components are arranged relative to each other and to the pipeline to achieve effective water purification and distribution.
[E03B 7/075] Arrangement of devices for control of pressure or flow rate -- This group covers the layout and positioning of components such as valves, regulators, and flow meters used to manage pressure and flow in water mains and service pipe networks. It includes the spatial organization of pressure-reducing devices, flow-control valves, and related control mechanisms within fixed water-supply constructions.
[E03B 7/077] Arrangement of backflow preventing devices -- E03B 7/077 covers the arrangement of backflow-preventing devices in water-main or service-pipe systems, detailing configurations that stop reverse flow to protect the water supply. It falls under the Fixed Constructions → Water Supply → Installations or methods for obtaining, collecting, or distributing water hierarchy.
[E03B 7/078] Combined units with different devices; Arrangement of different devices with respect to each other -- This subclass pertains to water main and service pipe systems where several functional components such as meters, valves, regulators, filters or other devices are combined into a single unit, and it defines the relative arrangement and integration of those devices within the assembly.
[E03B 7/08] Arrangement of draining devices, -- The subclass deals with the layout of drainage devices in fixed water-supply constructions such as water mains or service pipe systems, specifying how drains are positioned to remove water from the network.
[E03B 7/09] Component parts or accessories -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods for cleaning water supply systems; Devices for protecting pipes against frost damages.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Cleaning of metal surfaces -> C23F - Cleaning of metal surfaces -> C23G
[E03B 7/095] Component holders or housings, e.g. boundary boxes -- This CPC subclass covers devices that hold or enclose components of water main or service pipe systems, such as boundary boxes that protect joints, valves, or other fittings. It includes housings designed to secure, protect, and locate pipe accessories within fixed water supply installations.
[E03B 7/10] Devices preventing bursting of pipes by freezing -- This subclass concerns devices that intentionally freeze water in a pipe or water main to form an ice plug that prevents pressure surges and bursting. Such apparatus are used in water supply installations to protect piping during extreme temperature fluctuations.
[E03B 7/12] by preventing freezing -- E03B 7/12 covers water-main or service-pipe systems that incorporate measures to prevent freezing, such as heating elements, insulation, or circulating warm fluids. The subclass falls under fixed constructions for water supply, specifically installations or methods for obtaining, collecting, or distributing water, and addresses freeze-protection techniques for pipelines.
[E03B 7/14] Devices for thawing frozen pipes -- E03B 7/14 covers devices designed to thaw frozen water mains or service pipes, typically employing heat sources or circulation methods to restore flow. These devices are used in fixed water supply installations to prevent damage and maintain service.
[E03B 9/00] Methods or installations for drawing-off water -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fire hydrants; Outdoor taps; Other public drinking water tapping points.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Boundary boxes -> E03B7/095 - Decorative fountains -> B05B17/08 - Street caps or covers for manholes -> E02D29/00 - Domestic water-drawing devices -> E03C - Taps or valves per se -> F16K - Hydrant housings -> F16K27/00 - Preventing unauthorized access to valves -> F16K35/00
[E03B 9/02] Hydrants -- Hydrants are permanently installed fixtures on water-distribution systems that provide a point of access for drawing off water, primarily for fire-fighting, maintenance or emergency purposes. They are typically mounted above ground, connected to the mains, and equipped with valves and outlets that allow rapid connection of hoses and control of flow. This classification covers the design, placement and operation of such water-drawing installations.
[E03B 9/025] Taps specially designed for outdoor use, e.g. wall hydrants, sill cocks -- It covers taps designed for outdoor installation, including wall hydrants and sill cocks, used to draw off water from fixed constructions and suitable for exposure to weather.
[E03B 9/027] with features preventing frost damage -- This subclass relates to water-drawing installations that incorporate measures to avoid frost damage. It includes designs where heating elements, insulation, or frost-proof geometries prevent freezing of supply pipes or taps during low temperatures.
[E03B 9/04] Column hydrants -- E03B 9/04 covers column hydrants, which are fixed water-supply installations used to draw off water for firefighting or other purposes. These hydrants are mounted on a vertical pipe and provide a rapid connection point for hoses.
[E03B 9/06] Covers -- The CPC subclass E03B 9/06 pertains to covers used in water-drawing installations or methods, encompassing protective or sealing elements that shield water outlets, taps, wells and similar components from contamination, damage or accidental activation.
[E03B 9/08] Underground hydrants -- Underground hydrants are pressure-reducing devices installed below ground level that enable water to be drawn from a distribution mains for fire-fighting or other uses, providing a protected and space-saving supply point within fixed water-supply installations.
[E03B 9/10] Protective plates or covers -- The CPC subclass E03B 9/10 covers protective plates or covers employed in water-drawing installations, such as those used to shield pumps, intakes or other draw-off components from damage, debris, corrosion or wear. These elements form part of fixed water-supply constructions that safeguard equipment and maintain reliable water collection or distribution.
[E03B 9/12] Stand-pipes -- Stand-pipes are vertical tubular components that link a water distribution system to a source or storage tank, enabling water to be drawn off at a convenient height. They are installed on fixed constructions such as pumps, tanks or other water-supply infrastructure to facilitate collection and distribution.
[E03B 9/14] Draining devices for hydrants -- E03B 9/14 refers to draining devices for hydrants, covering apparatus such as valves, plugs or hoses that enable controlled removal or discharge of water from fire or water hydrants for maintenance, testing or de-pressurization. The class includes fittings that prevent water loss and allow safe drainage of the hydrant system.
[E03B 9/16] Devices for retaining foreign matter, e.g. sand -- The code E03B 9/16 pertains to devices incorporated in water-drawing installations that retain foreign matter such as sand, preventing solids from entering downstream parts of the system. It covers filters, screens, grit chambers and similar retaining elements used in fixed water-supply constructions for collecting or distributing water.
[E03B 9/18] Cleaning tools for hydrants -- This CPC entry covers devices and apparatus used to clean water-supply hydrants, such as brushes, scrapers and flushing tools, employed in the maintenance of hydrant systems for drawing-off water.
[E03B 9/20] Pillar fountains or like apparatus for dispensing drinking water -- E03B 9/20 covers pillar fountains and similar devices that dispense drinking water, including the structural elements that support the water source and the mechanisms that control flow. These installations are classified under fixed constructions for water supply, focusing on methods and apparatuses for drawing off and distributing potable water in public or private settings.
[E03C] DOMESTIC PLUMBING INSTALLATIONS FOR FRESH WATER OR WASTE WATER -- In this subclass non-limiting references (in the sense of paragraph 39 of the Guide to the IPC) may still be displayed in the scheme.
[E03C 1/00] Domestic plumbing installations for fresh water or waste water -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Water dispensing devices for bathroom and kitchen; constructional features of wash basins and sinks; draining devices for wash basins and sinks  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Wash-stands without connection to either water supply or waste pipe -> A47K1/00 - Plugs for wash basins -> A47K1/14 - Constructional features of shower bases and bath tubs -> A47K3/00 - Bathing devices for therapeutic purposes; constructional features of nozzles for whirlpools -> A61H33/00 - Mechanical filtering devices -> B01D - Disintegrating in general -> B02C - Nozzles and liquid spraying devices in general -> B05B - Roses; Shower heads -> B05B1/18 - Fountains in general -> B05B17/08 - Cleaning of pipes in general -> B08B - Beverages on draught with tap -> B67D1/00 - Local water supply pipe systems -> E03B - De-clogging devices specially adapted for toilets -> E03D9/00 - Devices in the toilet bowl producing upwardly directed sprays -> E03D9/08 - Building parts characterised by incorporated canalisations -> E04B1/0023 - Fixing elements in general -> F16B - Valves, taps and stopcocks in general -> F16K - Water conduits and pipes in general -> F16L - Lighting devices -> F21V - Domestic hot water supply systems -> F24D - Constructional features of different sensor types used for control of taps -> G01 - Control of flow rate in general -> G05D7/00 - Control of temperature in general -> G05D23/00
[E03C 1/01] for combinations of baths, showers, sinks, wash-basins, closets, urinals, or the like -- A subclass for domestic plumbing that deals with arrangements where two or more fixtures-such as baths, showers, sinks, wash-basins, closets or urinals-are combined in a single unit or layout for supplying fresh water or removing waste water.
[E03C 1/02] Plumbing installations for fresh water -- E03C 1/02 covers the design, layout and components of domestic plumbing systems that deliver fresh water to buildings, including pipe networks, valves, fittings and distribution manifolds. It addresses the arrangement of supply lines from the point of entry to fixtures such as sinks, showers and appliances, ensuring reliable and sanitary water flow.
[E03C 1/021] Devices for positioning or connecting of water supply lines  connection to the tap body  E03C1/0403
[E03C 1/023] with flow distribution, e.g. diverters -- This code covers fixed domestic plumbing installations that manage the distribution of fresh or waste water, specifically devices such as diverters that route flow to different outlets.
[E03C 1/025] Water supply lines as such, e.g. shower hoses -- This subclass covers the individual water-supply lines themselves, such as flexible hoses used for showers, including their design, material and connection methods within domestic fresh-water or wastewater plumbing systems. It does not include fixtures or fittings but the conduit that transports the water.
[E03C 1/04] Water-basin installations specially adapted to wash-basins or baths -- E03C 1/04 covers water-basin installations that are specifically designed for use with wash-basins or bathtubs, including supply and drainage components integrated into residential or similar settings. The classification addresses fixtures, piping, valves, and related hardware that provide fresh water to, and remove waste water from, these basins.
[E03C 1/0401] Fixing a tap to the sanitary appliance or to an associated mounting surface, e.g. a countertop -- E03C 1/0401 relates to the mechanical fixing of a tap to a sanitary appliance or its mounting surface, such as a countertop, within domestic fresh-water or waste-water plumbing installations. The code is part of the Fixed Constructions hierarchy for water supply, addressing how faucet fixtures are secured to the appliance or supporting surface.
[E03C 1/0402] with mounting from only one side -- This subclass covers domestic water-supply or waste-water fixtures that are attached to a supporting surface on a single side only, such as wall-mounted taps, valves or fittings designed for one-sided installation. It applies to fixed constructions where the mounting hardware is located solely on one face of the device.
[E03C 1/0403] Connecting the supply lines to the tap body -- The CPC entry E03C 1/0403 covers the means of attaching supply pipes to the body of a tap in domestic fresh-water or waste-water plumbing installations. It falls under the fixed-construction group for water-supply systems, specifically addressing the connection of the supply lines to the tap.
[E03C 1/0404] Constructional or functional features of the spout -- E03C 1/0404 covers the design and functional characteristics of spouts used in domestic water-supply and waste-water plumbing systems. It includes features such as shape, flow-control mechanisms, mounting arrangements, materials and any integrated devices that affect how water is delivered or discharged.
[E03C 1/0405] enabling multiple spray patterns -- The CPC subclass E03C 1/0405 covers domestic plumbing fittings that provide several selectable spray patterns, such as showerheads, faucets or sprayers whose flow can be switched between mist, jet, rain or other modes for fresh-water or waste-water systems. It addresses the design features that enable multiple spray options within a single fixture.
[E03C 1/0407] Spouts for unpressurized outflow of water -- The code E03C 1/0407 covers spouts that allow water to flow out without pressure, such as taps, faucet heads, or decorative spillways used in domestic fresh-water or waste-water plumbing systems. It applies to fixtures that discharge water by gravity rather than by pump or pressurised supply. This entry falls under the hierarchy of fixed constructions, water supply, and domestic plumbing installations.
[E03C 1/0408] Water installations especially for showers  E03C1/06  takes precedence; shower heads  B05B1/18 -- The entry E03C 1/0408 classifies water installations specifically designed for showers within domestic plumbing systems for fresh or waste water. It falls under Fixed Constructions → Water Supply → Domestic Plumbing Installations and is subordinated to the broader class E03C 1/06, with related shower-head technology referenced in B05B 1/18.
[E03C 1/0409] Shower handles -- The subclass E03C 1/0409 covers the various types of handles used to control water flow in residential showers, including lever, knob, rotary and thermostatic devices that adjust temperature and pressure. It applies to fixed-construction plumbing installations for fresh water or wastewater in domestic settings.
[E03C 1/041] having provisions against scalding, e.g. temperature limiting devices, external covers -- E03C 1/041 covers domestic plumbing installations that include measures to prevent scalding, such as temperature-limiting devices, thermostatic mixing valves, or protective external covers. These provisions are designed to control water temperature and safeguard users from hot-water injuries.
[E03C 1/0411] Taps specially designed for dispensing boiling water -- Taps specially designed for dispensing boiling water are domestic plumbing fixtures that safely deliver water at near-boiling temperatures for uses such as tea preparation or cooking. They incorporate heat-resistant materials, insulated handles and often include automatic shut-off or temperature-control mechanisms to prevent scalding. This subclass falls under fixed constructions for fresh-water or waste-water plumbing installations.
[E03C 1/0412] Constructional or functional features of the faucet handle  valve actuating members as such  F16K31/60 -- E03C 1/0412 covers the design and functional aspects of faucet handles and other valve-actuating components used in domestic plumbing for fresh water or waste water. It is part of the Fixed Constructions - Water Supply hierarchy, addressing how these parts are constructed or operate in residential installations.
[E03C 1/042] Arrangements on taps for wash-basins or baths for connecting to the wall -- E03C 1/042 covers the design of tap assemblies for wash-basins or baths that are mounted to connect directly to a wall, including the associated plumbing fittings and mounting configurations for supplying fresh water or handling waste water.
[E03C 1/044] having a heating or cooling apparatus in the supply line -- The entry E03C 1/044 designates domestic plumbing installations that incorporate a heating or cooling apparatus directly in the fresh-water supply line. It applies to fixed constructions for water supply in residential settings where temperature control is built into the piping system.
[E03C 1/046] Adding soap, disinfectant, or the like in the supply line -- E03C 1/046 covers methods and devices for introducing soap, disinfectant or similar agents into a domestic water supply line, typically as part of a mixing or dosing unit integrated into fresh-water or waste-water plumbing installations. It addresses the design of fixtures that automatically add the chemicals during normal flow.
[E03C 1/0465] by mounting an independent soap dispenser to outlet of tap -- The entry E03C 1/0465 covers a domestic plumbing arrangement in which an independent soap dispenser is mounted directly onto the tap outlet, allowing soap to be dispensed at the point of water flow in fresh-water or waste-water installations.
[E03C 1/048] supplying water through two or more openings around or along one side of the water-basin -- This subclass covers domestic plumbing arrangements where fresh water is delivered to a basin via multiple outlets located on the same side or around its perimeter, such as twin faucets or side-by-side spouts for sinks, bathtubs or similar fixtures.
[E03C 1/05] Arrangements of devices on wash-basins, baths, sinks, or the like for remote control of taps -- It deals with the layout of remote-control mechanisms such as foot, sensor or lever actuators integrated with basins, bathtubs, sinks or similar fixtures, enabling tap operation without direct hand contact.
[E03C 1/052] Mechanical devices not being part of the tap, e.g. foot pedals -- The subclass E03C 1/052 covers mechanical actuators that operate domestic water-supply or waste-water fixtures but are not built into the tap itself, such as foot-pedals, levers or other separate devices that open or close valves. These devices are used in fixed plumbing installations for fresh or waste water and provide alternative means of controlling flow.
[E03C 1/055] Electrical control devices, e.g. with push buttons, control panels or the like -- This subclass covers devices such as push-button switches, control panels and similar electrical control units used to operate domestic water supply or drainage systems. It includes circuitry that monitors or regulates flow, pressure or valve actuation in fixed plumbing installations.
[E03C 1/057] touchless, i.e. using sensors -- This subclass covers plumbing fixtures or systems that operate without manual actuation by employing sensors to detect presence and trigger water flow. It includes touchless faucets, taps, showers, and related control devices for fresh or waste water in residential settings. It is used to classify inventions that provide hands-free operation in domestic water supply.
[E03C 1/06] Devices for suspending or supporting the supply pipe or supply hose of a shower-bath -- The E03C 1/06 class defines devices used to suspend or support the supply pipe or hose that feeds a shower-bath, covering brackets, straps, clamps and other fittings that secure the line to walls, ceilings or other structural elements in domestic fresh-water or waste-water plumbing installations.
[E03C 1/063] with reciprocating mechanisms  reciprocating mechanisms for massage, other than showers  A61H2201/1669 -- E03C 1/063 classifies domestic water-supply or waste-water plumbing installations that incorporate reciprocating mechanisms for massage purposes, such as bathtub jets or spa jets, but does not include shower systems. It falls under the broader categories of fixed constructions, water supply, and domestic plumbing for fresh or waste water.
[E03C 1/066] allowing height adjustment of shower head
[E03C 1/08] Jet regulators , e.g. anti-splash devices -- The code E03C 1/08 refers to jet regulators, also known as anti-splash devices, used in domestic plumbing installations for fresh water or wastewater. These devices control water flow from fixtures to reduce splashing and improve safety. They fall under the fixed constructions category of water supply.
[E03C 1/084] Jet regulators with aerating means -- Jet regulators with aerating means are devices that control water flow while introducing air into the stream, creating a smoother, less turbulent discharge. They are used in domestic plumbing installations for fresh-water or waste-water systems to improve flow characteristics and reduce noise.
[E03C 1/086] Jet regulators or jet guides, easily mountable on the outlet of taps -- The entry E03C 1/086 covers jet regulators or jet guides that are designed to be easily mounted on the outlet of taps in domestic fresh-water or waste-water plumbing. These devices shape or restrict the water flow to produce a desired jet pattern and are classified under fixed constructions for water supply.
[E03C 1/10] Devices for preventing contamination of drinking-water pipes, e.g. means for aerating self-closing flushing valves -- Devices that safeguard drinking-water pipes from contamination, such as aerating mechanisms attached to self-closing flushing valves, fall under CPC code E03C 1/10. They are part of the fixed-construction water-supply group covering domestic plumbing installations for fresh or waste water.
[E03C 1/102] using an air gap device -- This subgroup deals with installations that incorporate an air-gap device to separate the potable water line from any downstream outlet, preventing backflow of waste water. It applies to domestic fresh-water and waste-water plumbing where an air-gap is required for hygiene and pressure control.
[E03C 1/104] using a single check valve -- This subclass covers domestic plumbing systems where a single check valve is employed to prevent reverse flow in fresh-water or waste-water supply lines. It applies to fixed construction installations that rely on one valve for backflow protection.
[E03C 1/106] using two or more check valves -- The E03C 1/106 entry covers domestic plumbing installations that incorporate two or more check valves to prevent backflow in fresh-water or waste-water systems. It classifies fixed constructions for water supply where multiple check valves are used together for flow control.
[E03C 1/108] having an aerating valve -- E03C 1/108 pertains to domestic plumbing installations that include an aerating valve, a device that mixes air with water as it flows through taps or showerheads to produce a smoother, less splashing stream and often to reduce water consumption in residential fresh-water and waste-water supply systems.
[E03C 1/12] Plumbing installations for waste water -- E03C 1/12 covers the design and layout of fixed domestic plumbing systems that convey waste water from sinks, showers, toilets and similar fixtures to the building's drainage network. It includes piping, fittings, traps and connectors used for the collection and removal of sewage and grey water within residential structures.
[E03C 1/122] Pipe-line systems for waste water in building -- A pipe-line system for waste water in a building is covered, detailing the design, arrangement and components of drainage networks that convey sewage or grey water from fixtures to the municipal or on-site treatment facilities. It falls within domestic plumbing installations for fresh water or waste water under the Fixed Constructions - Water Supply hierarchy.
[E03C 1/1222] Arrangements of devices in domestic waste water pipe-line systems -- E03C 1/1222 covers the layout and interconnection of components in residential wastewater piping, including the positioning of traps, vents, and cleanouts within a domestic sewage system. The subclass deals with how these devices are arranged to ensure proper flow, sealing and maintenance access.
[E03C 1/1225] of air admittance valves  structural details of air admittance valves  F16K24/06 -- E03C 1/1225 covers the design and construction of air admittance valves used in domestic plumbing systems for supplying fresh water or disposing waste water. It includes the structural features of the valves such as seals, housings and venting mechanisms, and relates to the broader class of fixed water-supply installations.
[E03C 1/1227] of pumps for facilitating drawing off -- E03C 1/1227 covers pumps incorporated in fixed domestic plumbing installations that assist in drawing off fresh-water or waste-water, such as those used in sinks, bathtubs or drainage systems. It falls under the water-supply hierarchy for domestic plumbing constructions.
[E03C 1/126] Installations for disinfecting or deodorising waste-water plumbing installations -- This subclass covers domestic plumbing systems that incorporate devices or processes to disinfect or deodorise waste-water after it leaves fixtures, such as built-in treatment units, chemical dosing mechanisms or ventilation components designed to eliminate odors and pathogens in residential drainage networks.
[E03C 1/14] Wash-basins connected to the waste-pipe -- This subclass covers wash-basins that are directly linked to a waste-pipe as part of domestic plumbing for fresh water or wastewater. It includes designs, fittings and installation methods that ensure the basin's drainage into the waste system.
[E03C 1/16] Wash-fountains connected to the waste-pipe -- E03C 1/16 covers fixed domestic plumbing installations where wash-fountains, such as hand-washing basins, are directly connected to a waste-pipe for draining used water, forming part of household fresh-water or waste-water systems. This subclass is nested under fixed constructions for water supply, focusing on the integration of the basin's drainage into the building's wastewater network.
[E03C 1/18] Sinks, whether or not connected to the waste-pipe -- E03C 1/18 covers sinks as components of domestic plumbing installations, including designs that may or may not be connected to a waste pipe. This classification falls under fixed constructions and water supply, addressing fixtures used for fresh-water or waste-water handling in residential settings.
[E03C 1/181] Anti-splash devices in sinks, e.g. splash guards -- The CPC subclass E03C 1/181 concerns anti-splash devices mounted in sinks, such as splash guards, for domestic fresh-water or waste-water plumbing installations. It is nested within the fixed constructions hierarchy under water supply and domestic plumbing installations.
[E03C 1/182] connected to the waste-pipe -- This subclass addresses elements of a domestic water-supply or drainage system that are directly attached to a waste-pipe, including fittings, connectors and accessories that join the pipe to fixtures or other pipe sections. It covers the design of couplings, seals and adaptations used to integrate the waste-pipe within indoor plumbing installations.
[E03C 1/184] adapted for attachment of washing machines or heating or cooling devices -- E03C 1/184 pertains to fixed domestic plumbing constructions for fresh-water or waste-water supply that are specially adapted to receive washing machines or to incorporate heating or cooling devices, providing attachment points or integrated fittings within the water-distribution system.
[E03C 1/186] Separate partition walls, lids, protecting borders, or the like, for sinks -- E03C 1/186 pertains to separate partition walls, lids and protective borders used with sinks in domestic fresh-water or waste-water plumbing installations. The code classifies components that divide or shield sink compartments, such as removable panels, covers or edging, within the broader category of fixed constructions for water supply.
[E03C 1/20] Connecting baths or bidets to the wastepipe -- The sub-group covers the connection of baths or bidets to a building's waste-pipe, detailing the arrangement, sealing and pipe geometry required to discharge used water safely into the drainage system. It is part of domestic plumbing installations for fresh water or waste water within the fixed-construction, water-supply hierarchy.
[E03C 1/22] Outlet devices mounted in basins, baths, or sinks
[E03C 1/23] with mechanical closure mechanisms -- The subclass E03C 1/23 covers domestic plumbing installations for fresh water or waste-water systems that incorporate mechanical closure mechanisms, such as valves, shut-off devices or other movable parts used to stop or regulate flow. It pertains to fixed constructions in residential water-supply plumbing.
[E03C 1/2302] the actuation force being transmitted to the plug via rigid elements -- E03C 1/2302 relates to domestic plumbing fixtures in which the force that moves a plug or valve is transferred through rigid components such as levers, rods, screws or other solid elements, rather than via flexible hoses or membranes. It covers designs where the actuation mechanism directly transmits mechanical force to the plug to control fresh-water or waste-water flow.
[E03C 1/2304] the actuation force being transmitted to the plug via flexible elements, e.g. chain, Bowden cable -- It covers domestic plumbing fixtures where the force to open or close a plug is conveyed through flexible links such as chains or Bowden cables, allowing remote actuation of valves or plugs in water supply or wastewater systems. This arrangement is classified in the E03C 1/2304 subgroup of the Cooperative Patent Classification for fixed constructions in water supply.
[E03C 1/2306] the plug being operated by hand contact -- A hand-operated plug for domestic water or waste-water piping, used in fixed plumbing installations to control flow. It is a manually actuated valve component that seals or releases the line when the user presses or pulls it by hand. This type of plug is commonly found in sinks, bathtubs and other household fixtures.
[E03C 1/2308] actuated by rotating the wash-basin -- This subclass covers domestic plumbing installations where the wash-basin itself is rotated to trigger water flow or other functions, such as tilt-activated or swing-type basins used for fresh-water or waste-water supply.
[E03C 1/232] combined with overflow devices -- The CPC entry E03C 1/232 covers combined overflow devices that are incorporated into domestic plumbing installations for fresh water or waste water. It falls under the fixed-construction category of water-supply systems, specifying components that provide simultaneous supply and overflow protection.
[E03C 1/24] Overflow devices for basins or baths -- This CPC subclass covers devices installed in sinks, bathtubs or similar basins to prevent overfilling by providing a channel for excess water to drain away. Such overflow mechanisms are integral to domestic plumbing systems for fresh or waste water, ensuring safety and protecting fixtures.
[E03C 1/242] automatically actuating supply or draining valves -- E03C 1/242 covers domestic plumbing installations that use valves which open or close automatically in response to pressure, flow or level changes, providing self-acting control of water supply or drainage without manual operation.
[E03C 1/244] Separate devices to be placed on the outlet opening -- E03C 1/244 covers separate devices that are installed on outlet openings of domestic fresh-water or waste-water plumbing systems, such as aerators, flow restrictors, filters or similar accessories that modify or control the discharge from taps, showers or other fixtures.
[E03C 1/26] Object-catching inserts or similar devices for waste pipes or outlets -- E03C 1/26 covers devices inserted into domestic fresh-water or waste-water pipes to catch objects, such as mesh screens, traps or removable inserts placed at pipe outlets to prevent debris from entering the drainage system.
[E03C 1/262] combined with outlet stoppers -- The code E03C 1/262 covers domestic plumbing installations where a water outlet such as a sink or bathtub is combined with an integrated stopper that seals the drain. It applies to fixed constructions for fresh-water or waste-water supply in residential settings.
[E03C 1/264] Separate sieves or similar object-catching inserts -- The code E03C 1/264 covers separate sieve devices or comparable inserts used in domestic water-supply or waste-water plumbing to catch debris and objects before they pass through a pipe or fixture, helping to protect downstream components and maintain system cleanliness.
[E03C 1/266] Arrangement of disintegrating apparatus in waste pipes or outlets -- This subclass covers the placement of devices that break down solid material within domestic waste or outlet pipes, such as macerators, grinding or shredding units, to facilitate flow and prevent clogging in fresh-water or wastewater plumbing systems.
[E03C 1/2665] Disintegrating apparatus specially adapted for installation in waste pipes or outlets  disintegrating apparatus  per se   B02C
[E03C 1/28] Odour seals -- Odour seals are devices incorporated in fixed domestic plumbing installations for fresh water or waste water to block the passage of gases and prevent unpleasant smells from escaping. They are usually flexible or compressible elements positioned at pipe joints, trap connections, or fittings to maintain a sealed barrier while allowing water flow.
[E03C 1/281] using other sealants than water -- The subclass covers domestic plumbing installations for fresh or waste water where the joining or sealing of pipes, fittings or fixtures is achieved with sealants other than water, such as adhesives, polymeric compounds or expanding foams. It includes any method or device that uses these alternative sealants to prevent leaks or improve durability in fixed water-supply or drainage systems.
[E03C 1/282] combined with additional object-catching devices -- This subclass covers systems where a domestic water supply or waste-water plumbing installation incorporates an additional object-catching device such as a screen, filter or trap that is integrated with the main pipe or fixture. It applies to fixed constructions that add protective or collection elements to keep debris out of the water system.
[E03C 1/284] having U-shaped trap -- This group covers domestic plumbing installations that incorporate a U-shaped trap to retain water and block sewer gases in fresh-water or waste-water supply lines. It is nested under Fixed Constructions → Water Supply → Domestic Plumbing Installations for Fresh Water or Waste Water.
[E03C 1/286] having hand-operated valves against return of waste water -- The entry E03C 1/286 belongs to the Cooperative Patent Classification for fixed constructions in water supply, specifically domestic plumbing installations handling fresh or waste water. It covers systems that incorporate hand-operated valves designed to prevent waste water from flowing back into the supply line, ensuring one-way flow and protecting the potable water network.
[E03C 1/288] having non-return valves against return of waste water -- The CPC entry E03C 1/288 classifies domestic fresh-water and waste-water plumbing installations that incorporate non-return valves to stop wastewater from flowing back into the system. It falls under Fixed Constructions → Water Supply → Domestic plumbing installations for fresh water or waste water.
[E03C 1/29] having housing containing dividing wall, e.g. tubular -- The entry refers to domestic plumbing fittings for fresh or waste water that incorporate a housing split by a dividing wall, such as a tubular housing that separates water-flow channels. It falls under the fixed-construction, water-supply group for residential installations.
[E03C 1/292] having elastic housing -- The code E03C 1/292 refers to domestic fresh-water or waste-water plumbing installations that use components with an elastic housing, such as flexible couplings, seals or protective covers that provide a resilient enclosure for pipes, fittings or fixtures. This subclass groups designs where the elastic material improves sealing, vibration damping or ease of assembly in household water supply systems.
[E03C 1/294] with provisions against loss of water lock -- E03C 1/294 covers domestic plumbing installations for fresh water or waste water that incorporate features to prevent loss of water lock, such as back-flow preventers, check valves, or other mechanisms that stop unintended water discharge or reuse. This subclass focuses on designs that maintain system integrity by avoiding water-loss incidents.
[E03C 1/295] using air-supply valves -- E03C 1/295 covers domestic plumbing installations that incorporate air-supply valves to regulate or assist the flow of fresh or waste water. These valves introduce pressurised air into the pipeline to enhance flushing, reduce water hammer, or control pressure in residential supply and drainage systems.
[E03C 1/296] using water-supply valves -- This subclass addresses devices or methods that employ water-supply valves within domestic plumbing systems for delivering fresh water or handling wastewater. It includes designs, control mechanisms, and installation techniques of valves used to regulate flow, shut off supply, or direct water in residential settings.
[E03C 1/298] consisting only of non-return valve -- This subgroup defines domestic plumbing fittings that consist exclusively of a non-return (check) valve, used to prevent backflow in fresh-water or waste-water pipework. It applies to residential water-supply or drainage installations where the only component is a check valve, falling under the fixed-construction category for domestic water supply.
[E03C 1/30] Devices to facilitate removing of obstructions in waste-pipes or sinks -- The entry covers tools and mechanisms designed to clear blockages from household waste pipes and sinks, such as augers, suction devices or chemical dispensers, used in fixed domestic plumbing installations for fresh water or wastewater.
[E03C 1/302] using devices moved through the pipes -- E03C 1/302 covers domestic plumbing systems for fresh water or wastewater where devices are propelled through the pipe line. It includes methods and apparatus such as cleaning pigs, inspection cameras, and other tools that travel within the pipe to remove obstructions, perform maintenance, or gather data, without the need for disassembly of the installation.
[E03C 1/304] using fluid under pressure -- E03C 1/304 covers domestic plumbing installations that rely on fluid under pressure to convey fresh water or waste water within fixed constructions. It includes the use of pumps, pressure-boosting devices, and regulated supply or discharge arrangements in homes and similar buildings.
[E03C 1/306] by means of a tube connected to the water mains -- This subgroup covers domestic plumbing arrangements that obtain fresh water or discharge waste water through a tube that is directly linked to the municipal water mains, including the connecting fittings, valves and control devices required for such a mains-connected supply.
[E03C 1/308] by means of a pumping device -- This CPC subclass covers domestic plumbing systems that use a pumping device to supply fresh water or remove waste water within a building. It falls under fixed constructions for water supply, specifically domestic installations where a pump provides pressure or circulation for the plumbing network.
[E03C 1/32] Holders or supports for basins -- The subclass E03C 1/32 covers devices that hold or support basins in domestic plumbing systems for fresh-water or waste-water use, including brackets, frames, mounts and other fixtures that secure sinks, washbasins or similar vessels to walls, floors or cabinets.
[E03C 1/322] connected to the wall only -- This subclass covers domestic plumbing installations for fresh water or waste water where the fixtures are attached only to the wall, without floor or ceiling support. It includes fixed water-supply or drainage components designed to be wall-mounted.
[E03C 1/324] adjustable -- E03C 1/324 relates to adjustable elements in domestic plumbing installations for fresh water or waste water, covering fittings, valves or devices whose position, length or flow rate can be varied to suit different conditions or user requirements. This subclass falls under fixed constructions, water-supply systems in residential settings.
[E03C 1/326] resting on the floor -- The code E03C 1/326 covers fixtures that rest on the floor in domestic plumbing installations for fresh water or waste water, such as floor-mounted sinks, washbasins and similar units. It falls under the CPC hierarchy of Fixed Constructions, Water Supply, Domestic Plumbing Installations for Fresh Water or Waste Water.
[E03C 1/328] adjustable -- The CPC code E03C 1/328 covers adjustable components used in fixed domestic plumbing installations that convey fresh water or waste water, such as valves, fittings or connectors whose length, angle or position can be varied to suit site conditions. It falls under the water-supply hierarchy for household systems.
[E03C 1/33] Fastening sinks or basins in an apertured support -- This subclass defines methods and devices for securing sinks or basins to a support that includes openings, such as brackets, frames or panels, within domestic plumbing installations for fresh water or waste water. It covers the use of clamps, bolts, brackets or other fastening elements that attach the basin to the apertured support.
[E03C 1/335] the fastening means comprising a screw -- E03C 1/335 covers fastening means that incorporate a screw for securing components of domestic fresh-water or wastewater plumbing installations. It includes screws used to attach pipes, fixtures or fittings within fixed water-supply constructions.
[E03C 2001/005] Installations allowing recovery of heat from waste water for warming up fresh water -- This CPC entry refers to equipment that extracts thermal energy from waste-water streams and transfers it to incoming fresh water, typically using heat-exchanger units installed in sewage, grey-water or industrial effluent lines to pre-heat the potable supply. It falls under the water-treatment subclass E03C and specifically addresses heat-recovery installations for warming fresh water.
[E03C 2001/026] with flow restricting devices -- E03C 2001/026 is a CPC sub-group for building installations where flow-restricting devices are integral to the system, such as valves, orifices, restriction screens or similar components that limit or regulate the movement of liquids or gases in water-supply, drainage, sewage or sanitation networks. It covers designs that embed these control elements within pipes, fittings, or fixtures to achieve desired flow characteristics.
[E03C 2001/028] Alignment aids for plumbing installations -- E03C 2001/028 covers devices and methods that help align pipes, fittings or fixtures during plumbing installation, such as guides, brackets, templates or positioning tools that ensure correct spatial arrangement and reduce installation errors.
[E03C 2001/0414] allowing different orientations of the spout or the outlet nozzle -- E03C 2001/0414 covers fluid-dispensing devices in which the spout or outlet nozzle is designed to be positioned in multiple orientations relative to the container, allowing the user to aim the flow in different directions for convenient use. This feature is typical of spray bottles, pour-spouts and similar apparatus where adjustable direction enhances functionality.
[E03C 2001/0415] having an extendable water outlet -- This CPC subgroup concerns water-supply fittings and pipe systems that feature an outlet which can be extended outward, such as telescoping nozzles or retractable spouts, enabling flexible direction of water flow in plumbing, irrigation or related applications.
[E03C 2001/0416] using a socket for mounting of faucet -- The CPC subclass E03C 2001/0416 covers faucet-mounting techniques that use a socket as the attachment element, whereby the socket receives the faucet body or components to secure the fixture to a sink, basin or countertop. It includes designs and methods for socket-shaped openings, inserts or sleeves that facilitate installation, alignment and sealing of the faucet.
[E03C 2001/0417] having space-saving features, e.g. retractable, demountable -- The entry covers building components that incorporate space-saving designs such as retractable or demountable features, typically applied to roofs, walls or other structural elements to allow compact storage or easy removal.
[E03C 2001/0418] having temperature indicating means -- The CPC subclass E03C 2001/0418 covers heating, ventilation or air-conditioning installations that incorporate a temperature-indicating device, such as a thermometer, gauge or digital display, to show the temperature of the conditioned space, fluid or surface as part of the system.
[E03C 2001/082] Outlets connected by an universal joint -- This CPC subgroup concerns air-distribution or fluid-distribution outlets that are joined by a universal joint, allowing relative angular movement between the connected parts. It covers flexible or articulated connections used in HVAC or similar systems to accommodate misalignment, vibration or thermal expansion.
[E03C 2001/1206] Pipes with specific features for influencing flow characteristics -- E03C 2001/1206 covers pipe designs that incorporate specific structural or surface features intended to modify the behavior of flowing media. This includes internal contours, coatings, inserts or other modifications that influence turbulence, pressure loss, mixing or flow rate, and is used to classify inventions that improve hydraulic performance or fluid handling efficiency.
[E03C 2001/1213] Constructional features of bidet bowls;  adapters for using other sanitary appliances as bidet -- The subclass E03C 2001/1213 covers inventions concerning the design and structural features of bidet bowls, including elements that enhance ergonomics, hygiene or installation. It also includes adapters that enable other sanitary fixtures, such as toilets or urinals, to be used as bidets.
[E03C 2001/2311] the actuation force being magnetic or electromagnetic -- E03C 2001/2311 pertains to garden or agricultural mowers and similar cutting devices where the moving or cutting parts are driven by magnetic or electromagnetic forces, such as electric-motor or solenoid actuation of the cutting head.
[E03C 2001/2313] the actuation force being pneumatic or hydraulic -- This CPC entry groups building-construction inventions whose actuation force is supplied by pneumatic or hydraulic means, such as doors, windows, panels or other movable building components that are opened, closed or adjusted using air or fluid pressure.
[E03C 2001/2315] the actuation force created by a turning movement of a handle -- E03C 2001/2315 is a CPC subgroup that covers the force generated when a handle is turned to actuate a mechanism, such as in tools, valves, or other devices that rely on a rotating handle to produce a usable actuation force. It falls under the broader class for machines and mechanisms that handle or move material.
[E03C 2001/2317] transmission of actuation force involves a spring -- This CPC group covers pipe, conduit or duct arrangements in which the actuation force is conveyed by a spring, such as spring-loaded valves, dampers or other devices where a spring transmits the force to the moving element.
[E03C 2001/2406] Overflow alarm devices -- E03C 2001/2406 covers devices that monitor liquid level in tanks, basins or pipelines and issue a visual or audible warning when the level exceeds a preset limit, helping to prevent spillage or damage. These overflow alarm devices are used in water-supply, wastewater and drainage systems to provide early indication of excess flow.
[E03C 2001/2413] Covers for temporarily closing off overflow outlets -- E03C 2001/2413 refers to devices that temporarily seal overflow outlets in drainage, sewer or water-treatment systems, allowing controlled flow during maintenance or emergencies. The covers can be removable, hinged or inflatable, and are designed to prevent unwanted discharge while enabling quick re-opening.
[E03C 2001/321] Siphon or angle valve covers -- The code E03C 2001/321 designates protective covers for siphons or angle valves used in water-handling systems such as drainage, irrigation or municipal water supply. These covers shield the valve body from debris, corrosion and accidental damage while allowing access for maintenance.
[E03C 2201/00] Details, devices or methods not otherwise provided for -- This CPC subclass E03C 2201/00 covers any additional details, devices or methods related to fixed water-supply constructions for domestic fresh-water or waste-water plumbing that are not specifically covered by other subclasses. It serves as a catch-all for unlisted features within that technical field.
[E03C 2201/30] Diverter valves in faucets or taps -- E03C 2201/30 classifies diverter valves built into faucets or taps used in domestic water-supply or waste-water installations that are fixed rather than portable. The code captures any detail, device or method for directing flow that is not covered by other specific subclasses.
