[E03C 2201/40] Arrangement of water treatment devices in domestic plumbing installations -- The entry covers the design and layout of water-treatment equipment within household fresh-water or waste-water plumbing, detailing how devices such as filters, softeners, or disinfectors are positioned, sequenced and integrated into the domestic pipe network. It includes arrangements not specifically covered by other CPC items.
[E03C 2201/45] for carbonated water -- The classification E03C 2201/45 covers domestic plumbing installations that provide carbonated water, including any details, devices or methods for dispensing or handling carbonated water that are not covered elsewhere in the system. It falls under fixed constructions for water supply, specifically fresh-water or waste-water domestic plumbing. This subclass captures specialised components for sparkling water delivery.
[E03C 2201/50] Constructional features of escutcheons for domestic plumbing installations -- Escutcheons are protective plates that surround pipe connections in domestic fresh-water or waste-water plumbing, shielding the joint and providing a finished appearance. This entry covers specific constructional features of such escutcheons-materials, shapes, mounting methods, and sealing solutions-that are not detailed elsewhere in the classification.
[E03C 2201/60] Reducing noise in plumbing systems -- This subclass covers any measures, devices or techniques aimed at minimizing acoustic disturbances in residential water supply or waste-water piping. It includes sound-absorbing fittings, pipe supports, vibration isolators and other solutions not covered by more specific categories. The classification is used for patents relating to noise-reduction features in domestic plumbing installations.
[E03C 2201/70] Reducing or removing limescale at spout outlet -- The class covers any device, material or method designed to prevent, reduce or eliminate calcium carbonate or limescale deposits at the outlet of a domestic water spout such as a faucet or showerhead. It applies to treatments applied in situ, coatings, cleaning agents or mechanical means that are not covered by more specific subclasses.
[E03C 2201/80] Combined water inlet and outlet devices -- E03C 2201/80 covers combined water inlet and outlet devices used in domestic plumbing installations, where a single unit provides both supply and drainage functions for fresh water or waste water. These devices integrate inlet and outlet components in one fixed construction for applications not otherwise specified in more specific subclasses.
[E03C 2201/90] Basins movable between several positions -- This subgroup includes basins that can be shifted to multiple positions within a domestic fresh-water or waste-water plumbing installation, such as movable sinks, washbasins or troughs that are not permanently fixed and are covered by details, devices or methods not otherwise provided for.
[E03D] WATER-CLOSETS OR URINALS WITH FLUSHING DEVICES -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Devices for receiving excrements and urine which are cleaned by water flushing.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Closets or urinals without connection to the water supply or waste system -> A47K11/00 - Seats or covers for all kinds of toilets -> A47K13/00 - Portable urination aids for medical use -> A61F5/455 - Disintegrating of garbage in general -> B02C18/00
[E03D 1/00] Water flushing devices with cisterns -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Constructional aspects and component parts of flushing cisterns;  Inlet and outlet valves specially adapted for flushing cisterns.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Toilets and urinals with pressurized flushing system -> E03D3/00 - Combination of toilet bowls with wash basins, cleaning devices for chamber-pots or similar devices -> E03D11/025 - Self-closing valves in general -> F16K21/00 - Float actuation for valves in general -> F16K31/18 - Measuring water level in general -> G01F23/00
[E03D 1/003] Cisterns in combination with wash-basins, urinals, or the like  domestic plumbing installations  E03C -- The E03D 1/003 subclass covers plumbing fixtures where a water-storage cistern is integrated with a basin, urinal or similar device to provide a combined flushing system for domestic use. It falls under the broader groups for fixed constructions, water-supply installations, and specifically water-closets or urinals with flushing devices.
[E03D 1/006] Realisations of and provisions for preventing "sweating" of cisterns -- This subclass groups designs and measures that prevent condensation on flush cisterns used in water-close toilets or urinals, addressing the problem of moisture forming on the outside of the tank. It includes various structural features or provisions intended to keep the cistern dry and maintain proper operation.
[E03D 1/01] Shape or selection of material for flushing cisterns -- E03D 1/01 deals with the shape and material selection for flushing cisterns that supply water to water-close­ts or urinals with flushing devices. The code belongs to the fixed constructions group of water-supply systems and defines how the cistern is constructed and what materials are suitable.
[E03D 1/012] Details of shape of cisterns, e.g. for connecting to wall, for supporting or connecting flushing-device actuators -- E03D 1/012 covers the geometric details of cisterns used in water-closets or urinals with flushing devices. It includes designs for wall-mounted cisterns, supporting structures, and configurations that allow attachment of flushing-device actuators, focusing on shape and mounting features.
[E03D 1/0125] of built-in cisterns -- The E03D 1/0125 class covers water-flushing devices for toilets or urinals that incorporate a built-in cistern, meaning the tank is integrated within the fixture rather than a separate component. It includes designs where the cistern is permanently attached to or formed as part of the bowl, providing a compact, self-contained flushing system.
[E03D 1/02] High-level flushing systems -- High-level flushing systems are water-supply arrangements for toilets or urinals that use an elevated cistern or tank to deliver a larger volume or greater pressure of water during a flush. The design typically raises the water source above the fixture to improve flushing efficiency and reduce clogging.
[E03D 1/025] Cisterns with floats actuating the flushing -- Cisterns that employ a float to detect water level and activate the flush valve, used in water-close­ts or urinals with flushing devices. The float rises with the cistern's water and triggers the release mechanism to empty the tank when a user initiates a flush. This subclass covers the float-actuated flushing arrangement within the broader category of fixed water-supply constructions.
[E03D 1/04] Cisterns with bell siphons -- A cistern equipped with a bell-shaped siphon that automatically initiates flushing when the water level reaches a preset height, allowing rapid discharge without manual activation. The bell siphon creates a vacuum that pulls water out, refilling the cistern after the flush completes. This design is used in water-closets and urinals to provide reliable, self-acting flushing.
[E03D 1/05] with movable siphon bells -- Water flushing devices with cisterns that employ a movable siphon bell to release water during a flush. The bell lifts to allow water flow and then reseats to reseal the outlet, providing controlled draining and refill in water-closets or urinals.
[E03D 1/053] which are pushed upwards by means of own buoyancy, springs, counterweights, or the like -- Subclass E03D 1/053 relates to cistern-based water flushing devices for toilets or urinals that are raised by their own buoyancy, springs, counterweights or similar mechanisms. It falls under fixed constructions, water supply, and water-closets or urinals with flushing devices.
[E03D 1/056] providing an air lock -- The sub-group E03D 1/056 covers water‐flushing installations such as cistern-based closets or urinals that incorporate an air-lock mechanism. The air lock is used to isolate the water seal from pressure changes, preventing loss of the water barrier and ensuring reliable flushing performance.
[E03D 1/06] Cisterns with tube siphons -- The subclass E03D 1/06 covers water-flush cisterns that employ a tube siphon to discharge the stored water. It includes designs where the siphon tube is primed by the water level and draws the flush volume when activated, providing a simple mechanism for toilets or urinals.
[E03D 1/07] with movable or deformable siphon tubes -- This CPC subclass E03D 1/07 covers water flushing devices with cisterns that incorporate movable or deformable siphon tubes, used in water closets or urinals. It falls under fixed constructions, water supply, water-closets or urinals with flushing devices.
[E03D 1/08] Siphon action initiated by air or water pressure -- This subclass groups flushing mechanisms for toilets or urinals in which a siphon is started by a pulse of air or water pressure from the cistern rather than a manual valve, enabling rapid bowl emptying through a pressure-driven surge.
[E03D 1/082] in tube siphons -- In tube siphons are components of water-closets or urinals that use a sealed tube to create a siphoning action, allowing the cistern to release a measured volume of water for flushing without external pumps. The design relies on atmospheric pressure and gravity to initiate flow when the water level reaches a certain point, then stops automatically when the siphon breaks.
[E03D 1/085] by injection of air or water in the short leg of the siphon -- This subclass covers water-flushing devices that incorporate a cistern and improve performance by injecting air or water into the short leg of the siphon. The injection assists siphon break and refilling, enhancing flush efficiency and reducing noise.
[E03D 1/087] by pump, valve, or the like, in the short leg of the siphon or a vacuum pump in the long leg of the siphon -- A subclass for flush-type water closets or urinals where a pump, valve or similar device is installed in the short leg of the siphon, or a vacuum pump in the long leg, to assist the flushing action of the cistern. This design improves water flow and suction during discharge. It falls under the broader categories of fixed constructions and water-supply flushing devices.
[E03D 1/10] Siphon action initiated by raising the water level in the cistern, e.g. by means of displacement members -- This subclass pertains to water-flushing devices for toilets or urinals where the flushing is triggered when the water level in the cistern is raised, causing a siphon to start, often using a piston or other displacement element.
[E03D 11/00] Other component parts of water-closets -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Constructional details of water closet bowls and discharge pipes; Mounting of bowls or other parts of a water closet to a support: e.g. noise-reducing means in the flushing system, flushing pipes mounted in the bowl, seals for the bowl outlet, devices preventing overflow of the bowl contents; devices forming a water seal in the bowl after flushing, devices eliminating obstructions in the bowl outlet or preventing backflow of water and excrements from the waterpipe.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Cisterns specially adapted to be combined with at least another sanitary device -> E03D1/003 - Noise-reducing means in combination with flushing valves -> E03D9/14 - Constructional details of flushed urinals -> E03D13/00 - Constructional details of urinals without connection to water supply system -> A47K11/00 - Pipe-joints or couplings in general -> F16L
[E03D 11/02] Water-closet bowls -- E03D 11/02 classifies the bowl component of a water-closet, the receptacle that holds water and receives waste in a flushing toilet. It falls under fixed constructions for water supply and other component parts of water-closets.
[E03D 11/025] Combined with wash-basins, urinals, flushing devices for chamber-pots, bed-pans, or the like -- E03D 11/025 covers component parts that join a water-closet or urinal with wash-basins, flushing devices or similar apparatus for chamber-pots, bed-pans and related portable sanitation units. The subclass is used for combined fixtures that provide washing and flushing functions in a single, integrated installation, typically in medical, institutional or similar settings.
[E03D 11/04] Bowls of flat shape with only slightly inclined bottom -- E03D 11/04 designates flat-shaped toilet bowls whose bottom is only marginally sloped. These components belong to the water-closet family and are used in flushing devices where a nearly level interior aids cleaning and water flow.
[E03D 1/105] in cisterns with tube siphons -- The subclass E03D 1/105 covers water-flushing devices for toilets or urinals that incorporate a tube-type siphon within the cistern. It specifies designs where the siphon discharges the tank water to create a flushing action, typically used in fixed-installation washrooms. This falls under the broader groups for fixed water-supply constructions and water-closets or urinals with flushing mechanisms.
[E03D 11/06] Bowls with downwardly-extending flanges -- E03D 11/06 covers toilet or urinal bowl components that incorporate flanges extending downward from the bowl rim or base, typically used to support or connect the bowl to the flush mechanism, waste pipe or mounting frame. The classification applies to any design where the downwardly-extending flange is an integral part of the bowl structure.
[E03D 11/08] Bowls with means producing a flushing water swirl -- E03D 11/08 classifies toilet bowls that include a device to generate a swirling flow of water when flushed, enhancing the cleaning action within the bowl. This falls under fixed constructions, water-supply, water-closets or urinals with flushing devices, and other component parts of water-closets.
[E03D 11/10] Bowls with closure elements provided between bottom or outlet and the outlet pipe -- E03D 11/10 covers toilet or urinal bowls that have a built-in closure mechanism placed between the bowl bottom or outlet and the discharge pipe, such as integrated flappers, siphon traps or other sealing units that control the flush flow. It applies to component parts of water-closets where the closure element is part of the bowl assembly.
[E03D 11/11] Bowls combined with a reservoir, e.g. containing apparatus for disinfecting or for disintegrating -- E03D 11/11 refers to water-closet or urinal bowls that incorporate an integrated reservoir, such as devices for disinfecting or breaking down waste, forming a combined bowl-and-reservoir unit.
[E03D 11/12] Swivel-mounted bowls, e.g. for use in restricted spaces -- Swivel-mounted bowls are water-closet components that rotate on a fixed base, allowing easier cleaning and use in confined or awkwardly shaped spaces. The design incorporates a flushing device and falls under fixed constructions for water supply.
[E03D 11/125] Height adjustable bowls -- E03D 11/125 covers bowls for flush toilets that can be raised or lowered to accommodate different users or to meet ergonomic and accessibility standards. These height-adjustable bowls are classified under fixed constructions for water supply and other component parts of water-closets.
[E03D 11/13] Parts or details of bowls -- The CPC class E03D 11/13 covers particular components and details of the bowl in water closets or urinals that have flushing mechanisms. It falls under the broader categories of fixed constructions for water supply and other component parts of water-closets.
[E03D 11/135] Supports for bowls -- Supports for bowls are structural components that secure a toilet bowl to a wall, floor or frame in fixed water-closet installations. These parts include brackets, mounts or frames that provide stability and proper alignment for the bowl within the overall plumbing fixture.
[E03D 11/14] Means for connecting the bowl to the wall, e.g. to a wall outlet -- This sub-group includes fixtures and fittings that join a water-closet bowl to a wall, such as brackets, clamps or connectors that attach to a wall outlet, enabling secure mounting and service line integration.
[E03D 11/143] Mounting frames for toilets and urinals -- E03D 11/143 covers mounting frames that support and secure toilet or urinal bowls to a building structure, including brackets, carriers, and associated fittings that attach the sanitary units to walls, floors or partitions. The frames may be designed for both fixed and suspended installations and are used in water-closet systems to provide stable positioning and proper alignment of flushing devices.
[E03D 11/146] with incorporated cistern  wall built-in flushing cisterns, connecting a cistern to the wall  E03D1/012 -- E03D 11/146 falls under the Fixed Constructions group for water supply, specifically the water-closets or urinals with flushing devices. It covers other component parts of water-closets that have an incorporated cistern, i.e., flushing cisterns built into or attached to the wall, and the means of connecting the cistern to the wall (see E03D1/012).
[E03D 11/16] Means for connecting the bowl to the floor, e.g. to a floor outlet -- This subclass covers devices that attach a toilet bowl to the floor, typically linking the bowl to a floor outlet or similar connection point. Such means are used in fixed water-closet constructions for secure, leak-proof installation.
[E03D 11/17] Means for connecting the bowl to the flushing pipe -- Means for connecting a toilet bowl to its flushing pipe, encompassing fittings, couplings, clamps, seals and other hardware that join the bowl to the supply line for discharge. These components are used in water-closet installations to ensure a secure, leak-free connection between the bowl and the flush pipe.
[E03D 11/18] Siphons -- E03D 11/18 covers siphons used in water-closets and urinals, including devices that retain water to create a seal and prevent odors from escaping the drainage system. The class encompasses various designs of trap mechanisms integrated into flush toilets and urinals, focusing on the functional and structural aspects of the siphon component.
[E03D 1/12] Siphon action initiated by outlet of air -- The subclass E03D 1/12 covers water-flushing devices for urinals or water-close­ets that initiate a siphon draw by releasing air through an outlet, allowing the cistern's water to flow rapidly for a flush. This mechanism is used in fixed-construction sanitary fixtures.
[E03D 1/125] in cisterns with tube siphons -- Water flushing devices that incorporate a tube-siphon mechanism inside the cistern, using a sealed tube to draw water rapidly and initiate the flush when the water level drops below a set point. This design is common in urinals and water-closets where a compact, self-priming siphon replaces a traditional valve.
[E03D 1/14] Cisterns discharging variable quantities of water -- The subclass E03D 1/14 covers cisterns that can discharge variable quantities of water for flushing purposes. Such devices allow the amount of water released to be adjusted to match the specific flushing requirement, helping to conserve water while maintaining performance. This entry falls under the broader groups of fixed constructions, water supply, and water-closets or urinals with flushing devices.
[E03D 1/141] in cisterns with tube siphons and with tube siphons in combination with flushing valves -- This group covers flushing mechanisms that use a cistern equipped with a tube siphon, either alone or together with a flushing valve that controls water release. It applies to water closets or urinals where the siphon initiates the flush and the valve regulates flow.
[E03D 1/142] in cisterns with flushing valves -- This subclass covers water-close toilets or urinals that use a cistern equipped with a flushing valve; the valve opens to release stored water for the flush and then reseats. It includes designs where the valve mechanism is integrated within the cistern to control the discharge volume.
[E03D 1/144] having a single flush outlet and an additional float for delaying the valve closure -- This subclass covers water-supply fixtures such as water-closets or urinals that use a cistern with a single flush outlet and an auxiliary float that delays valve closure after flushing. The additional float extends the discharge period, providing smoother drainage and reduced splashing.
[E03D 1/145] having multiple flush outlets -- A water-flushing device with a cistern that offers two or more separate flush outlets, enabling the user to select different flush volumes such as a reduced-flow for liquid waste and a full-flow for solid waste.
[E03D 1/16] Cisterns for periodical discharge -- The entry covers cisterns that store water and release it at set intervals to provide the flushing action for water-close-ts or urinals. It includes designs where the discharge is timed or otherwise triggered periodically rather than continuously.
[E03D 1/165] with bell or tube siphons -- This subclass covers cistern-based flushing mechanisms for water closets or urinals that employ a bell-shaped or tubular siphon to start and control the discharge of water. The bell or tube siphon creates a vacuum that draws water from the cistern through the trap when the flush is activated.
[E03D 1/18] Cisterns empty when not in action -- The code covers water-flushing installations for toilets or urinals whose cisterns are drained when the device is idle, meaning no water is retained between uses. It relates to fixed water-supply constructions that keep the tank empty to improve hygiene, reduce stagnation or save water.
[E03D 1/183] with bell or tube siphons -- E03D 1/183 relates to cistern-based flushing mechanisms for toilets or urinals that employ a bell-shaped or tubular siphon to discharge water during a flush.
[E03D 1/186] with flushing valves -- The CPC subclass E03D 1/186 pertains to water-closet or urinal flushing devices that incorporate flushing valves. It covers mechanisms for controlling the discharge of water from a cistern into the bowl in fixed construction water-supply systems.
[E03D 1/20] Cisterns with tiltably-mounted containers -- This subclass covers water-flushing devices in which the cistern is a container that can be tilted to discharge its water into a toilet or urinal and then return to its storage position. The tiltable design simplifies emptying, refilling and maintenance while allowing a compact installation for flushing systems.
[E03D 1/22] Twin or multiple flushing cisterns -- This class covers toilet cisterns that provide two or more selectable flush volumes, allowing users to choose a reduced or full water flush for a water closet. The dual-flush design reduces water consumption while maintaining effective waste removal.
[E03D 1/24] Low-level flushing systems -- E03D 1/24 covers low-level flushing systems for toilets or urinals that use a cistern to deliver a reduced volume of water for each flush. These designs aim to conserve water by employing a shallow tank or limited water level while still providing effective waste removal. The classification falls under fixed constructions for water supply in sanitary fixtures.
[E03D 1/26] Bowl with flushing cistern mounted on the rearwardly extending end of the bowl -- The classification covers water closets in which the flushing cistern is integrated on the rearwardly extending end of the bowl, forming a single fixed fixture with the tank positioned behind the bowl rather than above or separate. It describes designs where the cistern is mounted directly on the back of the bowl for compact, wall-mounted installations.
[E03D 1/263] provided with a bell or tube siphon -- A water-flushing device for a cistern-equipped toilet that uses a bell-shaped or tube siphon to create the suction that initiates the flush. This siphon type is a specific mechanism within the broader category of water-close ts or urinals with flushing devices.
[E03D 1/266] exclusively provided with flushing valves as flushing mechanisms -- E03D 1/266 pertains to water-flush devices that incorporate a cistern and rely solely on flushing valves as the flushing mechanism, typically used in water-close-ts or urinals. The subclass excludes any additional flushing aids, specifying valve-only actuation.
[E03D 1/28] Bowl integral with the flushing cistern -- The E03D 1/28 entry classifies water-closet or urinal units where the bowl and flushing cistern are built as a single integrated component, forming a combined bowl-cistern flushing device. This design consolidates the water supply and waste discharge functions into one fixed construction.
[E03D 1/283] provided with a bell or tube siphon -- E03D 1/283 covers water-flushing devices for toilets or urinals that use a bell-shaped or tubular siphon to empty the cistern.
[E03D 1/286] provided with flushing valves -- This subgroup covers water-closet or urinal designs that incorporate a flushing valve mechanism to release water from a cistern into the bowl. The valve may be operated manually or automatically and is integral to the plumbing of the unit.
[E03D 1/30] Valves for high or low level cisterns -- E03D 1/30 covers valves designed for use in high-level or low-level cisterns that supply water to flushing devices in water-closets or urinals, regulating the release of water during a flush.
[E03D 13/00] Urinals -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Constructional features of urinals with connection to water supply and/or waste system; Accessories specially adapted to such urinals.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Urinals without flushing devices, i.e. without connection to water supply and waste system -> A47K11/00 - Siphons for other sealants than water -> E03C1/281
[E03D 13/002] intended for use by women -- E03D 13/002 refers to urinals that are specifically designed for use by women, placed within the fixed-construction water-supply category for water-close-ts or urinals with flushing devices. This subclass distinguishes feminine-oriented designs from general urinal designs.
[E03D 13/005] Accessories specially adapted for urinals -- The CPC subclass E03D 13/005 covers accessories specifically designed for urinals, such as flushing mechanisms, deodorizing units, water-saving devices and mounting hardware. These items are intended to improve the functionality, hygiene or installation of urinals within water-supply fixtures.
[E03D 13/007] Waterless or low-flush urinals; Accessories therefor  siphons  E03C1/281 -- E03D 13/007 covers urinals that operate without water or with very low-flow flushing, including the associated components such as siphons and other accessories that enable water-saving operation. It falls under the hierarchy of fixed constructions for water supply, specifically water-closets or urinals with flushing devices.
[E03D 1/302] with valves kept in open position by means of air or water pressure or by vacuum -- The class includes flush mechanisms for water-closets or urinals that use a cistern and keep the flushing valve open by air or water pressure or by vacuum, releasing the flow only when the user activates the flush.
[E03D 1/304] with valves with own buoyancy -- This subclass relates to water-closet or urinal flushing devices that incorporate a cistern and employ valves that float on their own buoyancy, opening or closing automatically as the water level rises or falls.
[E03D 1/306] with articulated valves -- Water-flushing devices with cisterns that employ articulated valves use one or more hinged or pivoting valve elements to control the release and shut-off of water. The articulated design improves sealing, allows smoother operation and can reduce wear compared with fixed-type valves.
[E03D 1/308] with articulated valves  E03D1/306  takes precedence -- Water flushing devices equipped with a cistern that use articulated valves to control the discharge of water, allowing one or more valve elements to move relative to each other for staged opening or sealing. The articulated mechanism improves flow regulation, reduces water waste and enables more precise flushing cycles.
[E03D 1/32] Arrangement of inlet valves -- E03D 1/32 concerns the design of inlet valve arrangements in cisterns for flushing water-closets or urinals, covering how the valves are positioned, mounted and linked to admit water into the tank and influence fill performance and reliability.
[E03D 1/33] Adaptations or arrangements of floats -- The entry E03D 1/33 covers adaptations or arrangements of floats in water-flushing devices with cisterns, such as those used in water-close­ts or urinals. It relates to design variations that control the water level for automatic flushing.
[E03D 1/34] Flushing valves for outlets -- E03D 1/34 covers flushing valves used in cistern-based water-closets or urinals. The class includes mechanisms that control the release of water from a cistern to an outlet for flushing, such as valve assemblies, actuators and timing devices.
[E03D 1/35] Flushing valves having buoyancy -- This subclass covers flushing valves that use a buoyant element, such as a float, to open or close the water flow from a cistern in water-closet or urinal systems. The buoyancy mechanism raises the valve as the cistern fills and releases it to allow a flush when the water level falls.
[E03D 1/36] Associated working of inlet and outlet valves -- The entry covers the coordinated operation of inlet and outlet valves in water-flushing cisterns for toilets or urinals. It relates to mechanisms that control water entry and discharge to ensure reliable flushing performance. This classification falls under fixed constructions, water supply, and water-closets or urinals with flushing devices.
[E03D 1/38] Adaptations or arrangements of flushing pipes -- E03D 1/38 covers designs that modify or arrange the piping that delivers water from a cistern to a toilet or urinal, including variations in pipe routing, connections, and control mechanisms to improve flushing performance. The classification is used for patents relating to the plumbing layout of flushing systems in fixed water-supply fixtures.
[E03D 2001/147] having provisions for active interruption of flushing -- E03D 2001/147 covers sanitary fixtures, chiefly toilets, that incorporate a mechanism to actively stop the flushing process, such as dual-flush or timed-release systems that interrupt water flow on command. This subgroup is used to classify devices that provide user-controlled interruption of the flush to conserve water or improve performance.
[E03D 2001/148] with means to prevent premature closing of flushing valve -- The CPC subclass E03D 2001/148 covers flushing devices that incorporate features preventing the valve from closing too early, thereby ensuring the discharge of the intended volume of water before the valve shuts. It applies to patents for toilets, urinals and similar sanitary fixtures where timing of the flush valve is controlled to avoid premature closure.
[E03D 2009/024] using a solid substance -- E03D 2009/024 covers methods or devices for employing a solid material in the construction or maintenance of roads, runways or related transport surfaces. It includes the use of solid substances as binding agents, fillers or structural layers to achieve desired strength or durability. The entry falls within the CPC group dealing with road and related infrastructure technologies.
[E03D 2009/026] using a gel-form substance -- The subclass covers methods that apply a gel-forming material to soil, seeds, or plants to improve water retention, nutrient supply or protect against pests and diseases. It includes the use of polymeric or hydrogel substances as carriers for fertilizers, growth regulators, microorganisms or other active agents in agricultural or horticultural treatment.
[E03D 2009/028] using a liquid substance -- E03D 2009/028 covers methods and apparatuses for cleaning or protecting a structure by applying a liquid substance, such as cleaning, disinfecting, or corrosion-inhibiting treatments. It includes the selection, preparation and delivery of liquids to surfaces for maintenance or preservation purposes.
[E03D 2201/00] Details and methods of use for water closets and urinals not otherwise provided for -- E03D 2201/00 covers specific design details and usage methods for water-closets and urinals that are not covered by other subclasses, such as unconventional flush mechanisms, mounting arrangements, or user-interaction features. It is used to classify patents describing novel constructions, accessories, or operating procedures for these sanitary fixtures.
[E03D 2201/20] Noise reduction features -- E03D 2201/20 covers noise reduction features in water closets and urinals with flushing devices. It falls under fixed constructions for water supply, describing methods and details to reduce sound generated during flushing.
[E03D 2201/30] Water injection in siphon for enhancing flushing -- This subclass covers devices that inject water into the siphon of a water closet or urinal during a flush to improve flow and removal of waste. It includes methods for delivering the injection and design features that create a stronger flushing action.
[E03D 2201/40] Devices for distribution of flush water inside the bowl -- The entry E03D 2201/40 covers devices that direct flush water into the bowl of a water closet or urinal, ensuring even distribution during a flush. It pertains to fixed construction water-supply systems for toilets and urinals not otherwise classified.
[E03D 3/00] Flushing devices operated by pressure of the water supply system -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Flushing valves arranged in the water supply line; other devices for flushing with pressurized water  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Self-closing valves not specially adapted to water closet flushing -> F16K21/04
[E03D 3/02] Self-closing flushing valves -- E03D 3/02 covers self-closing flushing valves that use the pressure of the water-supply system to operate. These devices are intended for water-closets or urinals and automatically close after a flush, eliminating the need for manual actuation.
[E03D 3/04] with piston valve and pressure chamber for retarding the valve-closing movement -- E03D 3/04 denotes flushing mechanisms for water-closets or urinals that use a piston valve combined with a pressure chamber to delay the valve's closing motion, enabling controlled discharge of water supplied under pressure.
[E03D 3/06] with diaphragm valve and pressure chamber for retarding the valve-closing movement -- A water-close­et or urinal flush that is powered by the supply-water pressure uses a diaphragm valve whose closing motion is slowed by a pressure chamber, helping to reduce noise and water-hammer effects.
[E03D 3/08] with other retarding devices -- This CPC subclass covers water-closet or urinal flushing mechanisms that are driven by the pressure of the building's water supply and incorporate additional retarding devices such as flow restrictors, delayed-release valves, or other means to slow or control the discharge.
[E03D 3/10] Flushing devices with pressure-operated reservoir, e.g. air chamber -- This CPC subclass covers flushing devices for water closets or urinals that use the pressure of the water supply system, specifically those that incorporate a pressure-operated reservoir such as an air chamber to store energy for flushing.
[E03D 3/12] Flushing devices discharging variable quantities of water -- Flushing devices that vary the amount of water released on each use, typically using pressure-controlled valves or sensors to adjust flow. They are designed for water-closets and urinals so the discharge quantity can be matched to the required cleaning level, improving water efficiency while maintaining effective flushing.
[E03D 5/00] Special constructions of flushing devices , -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Devices and methods for transmission of actuating force to flushing valve; flushing devices working with greywater or other cleaning fluids  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Construction of flushed water closets specially adapted to motor vehicles or trailers -> B60R15/00 - Construction of flushed water closets specially adapted to railway carriages -> B61D35/00 - Construction of flushed water closets specially adapted to ships -> B63B29/14 - Construction of flushed water closets specially adapted to aircrafts -> B64D11/02 - Handles, levers and knobs in general -> G05D
[E03D 5/003] Grey water flushing systems -- Grey-water flushing systems are specialised flushing mechanisms for toilets or urinals that recycle used water, such as from sinks or showers, for subsequent flushes. They incorporate treatment or storage components to ensure the recycled water meets sanitary standards before being reused, reducing overall freshwater consumption in buildings. This technology falls under fixed constructions for water supply and water-closets or urinals with flushing devices.
[E03D 5/006] Constructional details of cisterns for using greywater -- This CPC subclass covers constructional details of cisterns designed to collect and reuse greywater in water-closets or urinals with flushing devices. It includes features such as compartmentalisation, inlet and outlet arrangements, treatment components and materials that enable greywater recycling.
[E03D 5/01] using flushing pumps -- A subcategory of water-closet and urinal designs that use a pump to deliver flush water rather than relying on gravity. The pump, which may be manual or powered, creates a pressurised flow for special flushing devices within fixed water-supply constructions.
[E03D 5/012] combined with movable closure elements in the bowl outlet -- The subclass E03D 5/012 covers flushing devices for water-closets or urinals that incorporate movable closure elements at the bowl outlet, such as lids, plates or shutters that operate together with the flush.
[E03D 5/014] with devices for separate removal of liquids and solids -- The subclass E03D 5/014 covers water-close​ts or urinals whose flushing mechanisms incorporate separate paths or devices that discharge liquids and solids independently, allowing distinct removal of each waste type for improved hygiene and water-saving performance. It falls under fixed constructions, water supply and flushing devices.
[E03D 5/016] with recirculation of bowl-cleaning fluid -- This CPC subclass covers water-closets or urinals whose flushing mechanism includes a system that recirculates the bowl-cleaning fluid for repeated cleaning cycles. The design integrates a recirculation loop to reuse cleaning fluid, enhancing efficiency and reducing water consumption.
[E03D 5/02] operated mechanically or hydraulically -- E03D 5/02 covers water-closets or urinals whose flushing mechanisms are driven by mechanical or hydraulic means, such as lever-actuated pistons, cams, or pressure-filled tanks. It includes special-construction devices that use these actions to release water for a single or dual flush.
[E03D 5/022] Operating automatically  E03D5/10  takes precedence -- E03D 5/022 covers special constructions of flushing devices for water-closets or urinals that operate automatically, such as sensor-triggered or timed mechanisms. It falls under fixed constructions in water-supply systems and is subordinate to the broader group E03D 5/10, which must be consulted first.
[E03D 5/024] Operated hydraulically or pneumatically -- The code E03D 5/024 classifies water-closets or urinals whose flushing devices are powered by hydraulic or pneumatic mechanisms, such as pumps, pistons or air-driven actuators, rather than gravity alone. It belongs to the fixed-construction water-supply group for special flushing-device designs.
[E03D 5/026] Devices preventing overflow or locks inhibiting the use of the flushing system  devices preventing overflow of the bowl contents and seals for the bowl outlet  E03D11/00 ; Devices preventing sucking-up of sealing and flushing water -- The classification E03D 5/026 covers special constructions for water-close or urinal flushing devices that stop the bowl from overflowing and include mechanisms that lock or seal the outlet to prevent unwanted operation. It also encompasses features that avoid suction of sealing or flushing water.
[E03D 5/028] Pusher plates and actuating mechanisms for built-in cisterns -- The E03D 5/028 class covers pusher plates and other actuating mechanisms that operate built-in cisterns for water-closets or urinals with flushing devices, focusing on the special constructions that trigger the flush valve in fixed water-supply installations.
[E03D 5/04] directly by the seat or cover -- E03D 5/04 covers flushing devices that are triggered directly by the movement of the toilet seat or lid. In this arrangement the seat or cover operates the flush mechanism without a separate button or lever, often using a built-in valve or sensor linked to the seat's motion.
[E03D 5/06] directly by the door -- E03D 5/06 designates flushing devices that are triggered directly by the door of a water-close-let or urinal, where opening or closing the door initiates the flush. It covers special constructions that integrate the door mechanism with the flush actuation system.
[E03D 5/08] directly by the foot -- E03D 5/08 covers water-closet or urinal flushing devices that are activated directly by the foot, such as foot-operated pedals or plates that trigger the flush without the need for hand or body movement. This falls under fixed constructions for water supply, specifically special constructions of flushing mechanisms.
[E03D 5/09] directly by the hand -- E03D 5/09 covers manual flushing mechanisms for water-closets or urinals, where the user activates the flush directly by hand. It falls under fixed constructions for water supply, specifically the subgroup dealing with special constructions of flushing devices.
[E03D 5/092] the flushing element, e.g. siphon bell, being actuated through a lever -- This classification covers water closets where the flushing component such as a siphon bell is triggered by a lever mechanism, detailing lever-actuated flushing elements in special flushing device designs.
[E03D 5/094] the flushing element, e.g. siphon bell, being actuated through a cable, chain or the like -- This CPC subclass deals with toilet or urinal flushing systems where the flushing element such as a siphon bell is operated by a cable, chain or comparable linkage rather than direct push-button or lever activation. It falls under fixed constructions for water supply and addresses special flushing device constructions.
[E03D 5/10] operated electrically , -- E03D 5/10 covers flushing devices for toilets or urinals that are powered by electricity, such as electric actuation of the flush valve or pump, enabling automatic or user-controlled flushing without manual lever operation.
[E03D 5/105] touchless, e.g. using sensors -- The subclass covers flushing devices for water-closets or urinals that operate without manual contact, using sensors or other touch-free mechanisms to detect presence and automatically trigger a flush. It includes any special constructions that enable this automatic, hands-free operation.
[E03D 5/12] discharging periodically -- E03D 5/12 relates to flushing devices for water-closets or urinals that are designed to release water in a timed or intermittent manner rather than continuously, providing periodic discharge for waste removal. This special construction aims to conserve water and improve hygiene by controlling the flow sequence during each flush.
[E03D 7/00] Wheeled lavatories -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Mobile toilets with provisions for flushing.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Constructional details of buildings for public toilets -> E04H1/1216
[E03D 9/00] Sanitary or other accessories for lavatories -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Accessories enhancing use comfort or security of water closets or urinals, the accessories being specially adapted for flushed toilets: devices for cleaning or disinfecting the toilet room or the toilet bowl or for eliminating smells.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Hand tools for cleaning the toilet bowl -> A47K11/10 - Seats or covers for all kinds of toilets -> A47K13/00 - Cleaning, disinfecting or deodorising the seat -> A47K13/30 - Toilet and urinal accessories without connection to water supply and waste pipe -> A47K17/00 - Body supports, other than seats for closets -> A47K17/02 - Baths and bidets for therapeutic use -> A61H33/00 - Deodorizing of rooms in general -> A61L9/00 - Chemical composition of deodorising agents -> A61L9/01 - Baths and spraying heads for subaquatic intestinal cleaning -> A61M9/00 - Chemical composition of disinfecting agents -> C11D - Devices for preventing contamination of drinking water pipes -> E03C1/10
[E03D 9/002] Automatic cleaning devices -- E03D 9/002 covers automatic cleaning devices for water-closets, urinals and related sanitary fixtures, including mechanisms that initiate cleaning cycles, dispense cleaning agents or water, and self-cleaning components integrated with flushing systems. The class applies to accessories that maintain hygiene of lavatory installations without manual intervention.
[E03D 9/005] Devices adding disinfecting or deodorising agents to the bowl -- The code E03D 9/005 covers devices that introduce disinfecting or deodorising substances into the bowl of water-closets or urinals during flushing. These are accessories for lavatories that treat the bowl contents to reduce odor or microbial growth. The classification falls under fixed constructions, water supply, and water-closets or urinals with flushing devices.
[E03D 9/007] Devices for eliminating smells by diffusing deodorants in lavatories -- E03D 9/007 classifies devices installed in water-closets or urinals that release deodorising agents to neutralise unpleasant odours, forming part of the fixed construction and water-supply elements of sanitary fixtures.
[E03D 9/02] Devices adding a disinfecting, deodorising, or cleaning agent to the water while flushing -- E03D 9/02 pertains to devices installed in water-closets or urinals that introduce a disinfecting, deodorising or cleaning agent into the flushing water. These accessories are part of fixed water-supply installations for sanitary fixtures.
[E03D 9/022] consisting of solid substances introduced in the bowl or the supply line
[E03D 9/03] consisting of a separate container with an outlet through which the agent is introduced into the flushing water, e.g. by suction -- E03D 9/03 covers flushing devices for water closets or urinals that use a separate container to add a treatment or cleaning agent to the flushing water, usually by drawing the water through an outlet under suction. The system introduces the agent into the flow as the water is expelled.
[E03D 9/031] Devices connected to or dispensing into the flushing pipe
[E03D 9/032] Devices connected to or dispensing into the bowl -- This class covers devices attached to a water-closet or urinal bowl that deliver liquids, powders, gases or other substances into the bowl for flushing, cleaning, deodorizing or dosing. It includes dispensers for cleaning agents, deodorizers, water-saving devices and similar accessories integrated with the bowl.
[E03D 9/033] Devices placed inside or dispensing into the cistern -- Devices installed inside a toilet cistern that dispense or release substances, such as cleaning agents, deodorising compounds, or water-saving mechanisms, during the flushing cycle. They are integral parts of the flushing system and operate automatically when the cistern fills or empties.
[E03D 9/035] Devices connected to the actuation mechanism  E03D9/036  takes precedence -- E03D 9/035 covers devices that are attached to the actuation mechanism of water-close­ts or urinals with flushing systems, such as linkages, levers, or connectors that transmit the user's action to the flush valve. These components are considered accessories for lavatories and fall under the broader category of fixed constructions for water supply.
[E03D 9/036] Scoop-like devices -- E03D 9/036 pertains to scoop-like devices that serve as accessories for water-closets or urinals equipped with flushing mechanisms. These fixed-construction tools are used in lavatory sanitation for tasks such as manually scooping or removing waste.
[E03D 9/037] Active dispensers, i.e. comprising a moving dosing element  E03D9/035  takes precedence -- The CPC subclass E03D 9/037 covers active dispensing mechanisms for water-closets or urinals with flushing devices, specifically those that incorporate a moving dosing element to release a substance such as a cleaning agent or deodorizer during use. It falls under fixed constructions, water supply, and sanitary accessories for lavatories, and is subordinate to E03D 9/035.
[E03D 9/038] Passive dispensers, i.e. without moving parts -- The code E03D 9/038 designates passive dispensers used with water-close-ts or urinals, meaning devices such as paper, soap or other supply dispensers that operate without any moving or actuating parts and are considered sanitary accessories for lavatories.
[E03D 9/04] Special arrangement or operation of ventilating devices -- Covers designs or mechanisms that provide a special arrangement or operation of ventilation devices associated with water-closets or urinals, such as integrated fans, air-flow channels or controlled exhaust systems intended to improve odor removal, moisture control or hygiene in sanitary fixtures.
[E03D 9/05] ventilating the bowl -- E03D 9/05 covers features that provide ventilation for the bowl of water-closets or urinals equipped with flushing devices. It includes designs that allow air exchange to reduce odors and improve hygiene, usually integrated into the toilet or urinal as part of sanitary accessories.
[E03D 9/052] using incorporated fans -- The code E03D 9/052 covers lavatory fixtures such as water-closets or urinals that incorporate fans to provide ventilation, odor control or drying as part of the sanitary accessories for flushing devices. It relates to integrated fan systems built into the fixture rather than separate ventilation units.
[E03D 9/06] Auxiliary chambers with water connection to the flushing pipe and adapted to receive utensils for bowl-cleaning, e.g. brushes -- E03D 9/06 concerns auxiliary chambers linked to the flushing pipe of a water-closet or urinal that are intended to hold cleaning tools such as brushes. It is a subclass of fixed constructions for water supply, specifically accessories for lavatories.
[E03D 9/08] Devices in the bowl producing upwardly-directed sprays -- Devices fitted inside the bowl of a water-closet or urinal that generate an upward-directed spray of water during flushing, intended to improve cleaning, reduce deposits and control odors. They are part of the sanitary accessories hierarchy for water-supply fixtures.
[E03D 9/085] Hand-held spray heads for bidet use or for cleaning the bowl -- Hand-held spray heads designed for bidet functions or for cleaning a toilet bowl, mounted as part of the water-supply fixtures for water-closets or urinals, and classified among sanitary accessories for lavatories.
[E03D 9/10] Waste-disintegrating apparatus combined with the bowl -- E03D 9/10 pertains to a toilet bowl that incorporates a waste-disintegrating mechanism, enabling solid waste to be broken down within the bowl before flushing. It is a fixed construction element for water-closet or urinal fixtures, classified as a sanitary accessory to improve disposal efficiency.
[E03D 9/12] Means to prevent freezing of lavatories -- The classification E03D 9/12 covers devices or methods installed in water closets or urinals to stop the water supply from freezing, typically including insulated pipe sections, heating elements, or antifreeze systems designed for lavatory fixtures. These measures help maintain functionality in cold climates and prevent damage to the plumbing.
[E03D 9/14] Noise-reducing means combined with flushing valves -- The code E03D 9/14 refers to devices that integrate sound-dampening features with flushing mechanisms for water-closets or urinals, intended to reduce the audible noise during flushing. It falls under fixed constructions, water supply, water-closets or urinals with flushing devices, and sanitary accessories for lavatories.
[E03D 9/16] Water pressure regulating means in flushing pipes -- E03D 9/16 refers to devices that control or limit the water pressure within the flushing pipe of water-closets or urinals, ensuring consistent operation and preventing surges. It falls under the fixed-construction group for water-supply systems and the sanitary accessories category for lavatories.
[E03F] SEWERS -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods and installations for conveying waste water and precipitation water towards treatment installations and/or natural flowing bodies.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Methods and installations for treatment of sewage -> C02F
[E03F 1/00] Methods, systems, or installations for draining-off sewage or storm water -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods and devices for conveying used water pneumatically; disposing sewage into the landscape.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Constructional details of cesspools -> E03F11/00 - Underground watering of plants -> A01G25/06 - Methods and installations for treatment of water -> C02F - Drainage of water -> E02B11/00
[E03F 1/001] into a body of water -- This subclass covers methods, systems and installations that discharge collected sewage or storm-water directly into a receiving water body, such as rivers, lakes or seas, including outfalls, pipelines, diffusers and related control structures.
[E03F 1/002] with disposal into the ground, e.g. via dry wells -- E03F 1/002 covers methods, systems or installations that drain sewage or storm water by disposing it directly into the ground, such as through dry wells. It falls under the fixed-construction subclass for water-supply sewers, focusing on underground infiltration rather than surface discharge.
[E03F 1/003] via underground elongated vaulted elements -- The subclass E03F 1/003 covers methods, systems or installations for removing sewage or storm water that employ underground elongated vaulted elements, such as vaulted tunnels or arches, to convey the flow. It falls under the hierarchy of fixed constructions, water supply, and sewers.
[E03F 1/005] via box-shaped elements -- It covers the use of rectangular or box-shaped components such as chambers, pits or manholes in sewer networks to collect, direct or store wastewater and storm runoff. The code applies to designs where these box elements form part of the drainage infrastructure, facilitating flow control and maintenance access.
[E03F 1/006] Pneumatic sewage disposal systems; accessories specially adapted therefore -- The CPC entry E03F 1/006 concerns pneumatic sewage disposal systems and any accessories specially adapted for them. It covers methods, installations or equipment that use pressurised air or gas to convey wastewater or storm water through pipelines, including components such as air-locks, ejectors, regulators and related control devices.
[E03F 1/007] for public or main systems -- E03F 1/007 covers methods, systems and installations used to drain sewage or storm water in public or main sewer networks, including the collection, conveyance and disposal infrastructure.
[E03F 1/008] Temporary fluid connections for emptying mobile sewage holding tanks, e.g. of trailers, boats -- E03F 1/008 concerns temporary fluid connections used to empty mobile sewage holding tanks such as those on trailers or boats. It covers methods, systems or installations that enable rapid draining of waste water during pit stop or transfer operations, falling under the fixed constructions-water supply-sewers hierarchy.
[E03F 11/00] Cesspools -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Constructional details of cesspools and other sewage holding tanks.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Hand implements for emptying cesspools -> E03F7/00 - Vehicles for emptying cesspools -> E03F7/10 - Water treatment tanks -> C02F
[E03F 2003/065] Refurbishing of sewer pipes, e.g. by coating, lining -- E03F 2003/065 covers methods and processes for refurbishing sewer pipes, including the application of protective coatings, internal linings or other restoration techniques to extend pipe service life and improve hydraulic performance. It relates to civil engineering works on underground drainage systems.
[E03F 2005/028] Sealing joints between manhole segments -- The CPC subclass E03F 2005/028 addresses techniques for sealing the joints that connect individual sections of a manhole, covering sealing materials, gaskets, welding or adhesive methods, and design features that ensure water-tightness and structural integrity in underground drainage, sewer or utility access structures.
[E03F 2005/0412] with means for adjusting their position with respect to the surrounding surface -- This subclass of CPC E03F deals with joining or connecting elements that include means for adjusting their position with respect to the surrounding surface, such as adjustable fasteners, brackets or mounting devices that can be shifted or aligned after installation.
[E03F 2005/0413] for height adjustment -- E03F 2005/0413 pertains to mechanisms that allow a window, door, shutter or similar opening to be adjusted in height, enabling controlled raising or lowering of the element for ventilation, access or sealing purposes. It falls under the CPC group for building openings and covers devices such as adjustable frames, lifting guides or height-regulating actuators.
[E03F 2005/0414] for inclination adjustment -- The CPC subclass E03F 2005/0414 covers devices, methods or control systems that adjust the inclination of road surfaces during construction, maintenance or repair. It includes mechanisms for setting camber, slope or gradient to achieve proper drainage, safety or performance of pavements.
[E03F 2005/0415] for horizontal position adjustment -- The CPC entry E03F 2005/0415 covers devices, mechanisms or methods that enable a structure's horizontal position to be altered after it has been installed, such as adjustable foundations, jacks, slides or positioning systems used to shift or align piles, columns, or other support elements laterally.
[E03F 2005/0416] with an odour seal -- The CPC entry E03F 2005/0416 pertains to fences, barriers or protective walls that incorporate an odour-seal element to stop the passage of smells or gases, often used in livestock, waste-treatment or industrial environments. It describes designs where the sealing material or configuration provides odour containment while maintaining the primary protective function.
[E03F 2005/0417] in the form of a valve -- This CPC subgroup pertains to water-supply, irrigation or related fluid-distribution installations in which the functional component is a valve, covering pipe fittings, flow-control devices and associated arrangements that regulate, direct or shut off the flow of liquids.
[E03F 2005/0418] in the form of a bell siphon -- A bell-shaped siphon configuration used to regulate liquid flow, where the siphon tube is formed like a bell that automatically starts and stops the discharge when a preset water level is reached. This design is employed in troughs, ponds, aquaculture tanks, and water-treatment or irrigation systems to provide self-acting level control without external valves or sensors.
[E03F 2005/061] hinged to the body of the gully -- The subclass covers devices in which a gully (drainage channel) incorporates a movable or hinged element attached to the main body, allowing opening or closing for maintenance, inspection or flow control. It includes hinges integrated into the gully structure for removable covers, grates or inspection plates used in civil-engineering drainage systems.
[E03F 2005/063] with slidable or rotatable locking elements -- The subclass addresses doors, gates, shutters and similar closures that employ a locking element which can be moved linearly or rotated into a locked position. It includes designs where a bolt, latch or bar slides or pivots to engage a catch or striker, covering building, vehicle and safety applications.
[E03F 2005/065] with elastic locking elements -- E03F 2005/065 covers building or civil-engineering structures that use elastic locking elements, such as flexible clips, spring-loaded fasteners or deformable pins, to join components while permitting limited movement or absorption of forces. It applies to constructions where such elastic devices provide reversible, resilient connections for walls, roofs, floors or similar assemblies.
[E03F 2005/066] with means for protecting against vandalism -- E03F 2005/066 covers garden or horticultural equipment that incorporates anti-vandalism features, such as reinforced structures, locking mechanisms, tamper-proof fasteners or protective coatings designed to deter damage, theft or graffiti. It applies to items like benches, planters, waste containers and lighting that are intended for public or communal outdoor areas. The code classifies any such product where vandal-resistance is a defined functional element.
[E03F 2005/068] with means for enhancing water flow -- E03F 2005/068 covers irrigation or water distribution systems that incorporate features designed to increase the velocity or volume of water flow. The classification includes devices such as pumps, pressure regulators, nozzle assemblies, or channel modifications that improve water delivery efficiency in horticultural or agricultural settings.
[E03F 2201/00] Details, devices or methods not otherwise provided for -- This class covers miscellaneous components, apparatuses or techniques related to sewer systems that are not covered by more specific categories, such as fittings, accessories or procedures for installation, maintenance or operation that are not otherwise listed.
[E03F 2201/10] Dividing the first rain flush out of the stormwater flow -- E03F 2201/10 covers methods or devices that separate the initial portion of storm runoff, often called the first-rain flush, from the main flow in sewer or water-supply systems. The aim is to retain the most polluted water for treatment or disposal while allowing cleaner water to continue downstream.
[E03F 2201/20] Measuring flow in sewer systems -- The code E03F 2201/20 covers devices, sensors and methods used to determine the flow rate or volume of wastewater moving through sewer networks, including flow meters, ultrasonic or pressure-based measurements, and associated data-acquisition systems. It applies specifically to fixed constructions in sewer infrastructure where such flow-monitoring is required for management, monitoring or regulatory compliance.
[E03F 2201/30] Devices providing a sequential discharge in sewer systems -- Devices that control the order and timing of flow release from sewer lines, enabling staged or timed discharge to manage hydraulic loads, prevent surges, and improve downstream treatment. They include gates, weirs, valves or automated mechanisms designed for municipal or industrial sewer networks, providing a sequential flow pattern rather than a single continuous outflow.
[E03F 2201/40] Means for indicating blockage in sewer systems -- A means for indicating blockage in sewer systems comprises devices or methods that detect and signal obstructions within underground or surface sewers, enabling timely maintenance and preventing overflow. Such indicators may use sensors, visual markers, or acoustic alarms integrated into fixed sewer construction to alert operators when flow is impeded. This falls under CPC class E03F 2201/40, covering sewer-related blockage detection.
[E03F 3/00] Sewer pipe-line systems -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Lay-out and constructional details of pipe systems specially adapted to sewage. [E03F 3/04] Pipes or fittings specially adapted to sewers -- The subclass E03F 3/04 groups pipe and fitting designs that are specifically engineered for sewer pipeline systems. It includes conduits, connectors and related components whose material, shape or joint features address the corrosive, abrasive and blockage-prone conditions of underground waste conveyance. This covers all specially adapted sewage pipes and their accessories.
[E03F 3/043] Partitioned to allow more than one medium to flow through -- E03F 3/043 is a subgroup of the CPC for fixed constructions, water-supply, sewers and sewer pipeline systems. It pertains to pipe designs that are partitioned so that more than one medium can flow through separate channels within the same conduit, allowing simultaneous transport of different fluids such as wastewater and stormwater.
[E03F 3/046] Open sewage channels -- E03F 3/046 refers to open sewage channels, which are uncovered ditches or troughs-often earthen, concrete, or masonry-that convey wastewater or sewage by gravity flow rather than using a closed pipe.
