[E03F 3/06] Methods of, or installations for, laying sewer pipes -- This subclass covers the techniques and equipment used to install sewer pipelines, including procedures for placing, aligning and joining pipes within underground networks. It encompasses trenching methods, laying mechanisms, supports and other installations that facilitate efficient and reliable sewer pipe installation.
[E03F 5/00] Sewerage structures -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Component parts of sewer systems other than conveying pipes.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Inspection of sewers -> E03F7/12 - Mechanical filtering in general -> B01D21/00 , B01D29/00 , B01D33/00 - Large underground containers -> B65D88/00 - Details of pavings and gutters -> E01C11/22 - Making sewers in situ -> E02D29/10 - Manhole shafts and covers not specially adapted to sewage -> E02D29/14 - Digging of trenches -> E02F5/10 - Methods of laying pipes underground -> E21B7/00 - Devices for covering leaks in pipes -> F16L55/16 - Control of flow in general , e.g. throttles -> G05D7/00
[E03F 5/02] Manhole shafts or other inspection chambers -- E03F 5/02 classifies manhole shafts and other inspection chambers that provide access to underground sewerage structures within water-supply networks, enabling maintenance, inspection and cleaning of pipelines. It is placed in the Fixed Constructions - Water Supply - Sewers - Sewerage structures hierarchy.
[E03F 5/021] Connection of sewer pipes to manhole shaft -- The class covers means for joining sewer pipes to a manhole shaft, including couplings, joints, sleeves and sealing arrangements that provide a watertight transition and allow access for inspection or maintenance. It addresses the structural and hydraulic requirements of pipe-to-manhole connections in sewerage systems.
[E03F 5/022] Partitioned to allow more than one medium to flow through -- E03F 5/022 relates to sewerage structures that are internally partitioned so that two or more different media can flow concurrently within the same conduit. Typical applications include separating storm water from sewage or isolating industrial effluents using walls, baffles or separate channels within a pipe.
[E03F 5/024] made of plastic material -- E03F 5/024 covers sewerage structures that are constructed from plastic material, including pipelines, chambers and fittings used in fixed water-supply and sewage installations. It specifies that the components are molded or extruded plastics rather than metal, concrete or other traditional materials. This subclass helps identify patents dealing with plastic-based designs for permanent sewer infrastructure.
[E03F 5/025] provided with a liner -- A sewerage structure that incorporates a protective liner on its interior surfaces, such as concrete, polymer or composite layers, to prevent corrosion, leakage or infiltration. The classification covers designs, materials and methods for installing such liners in pipes, tunnels or chambers.
[E03F 5/027] The bottom made of prefabricated segments -- This subclass covers sewerage structures whose invert or bottom is formed from prefabricated segments, such as modular or cast-in-place sections that are assembled in the field to create the floor of a sewer conduit. It includes designs, fastening methods and sealing techniques for joining the segments to provide a continuous, load-bearing bottom.
[E03F 5/04] Gullies with or without odour seals or sediment traps -- Gullies are low-lying inlet points in a sewerage system that allow wastewater to enter the main pipe, often located at floor level or in pits. They may be equipped with odour-seal covers to prevent gas escape, and some designs incorporate sediment traps to collect solids before the flow proceeds downstream.
[E03F 5/0401] Gullies for use in roads or pavements -- E03F 5/0401 covers gullies that are installed in roadways or pavement surfaces to collect and convey surface water into sewerage networks. These fixed construction elements form part of sewerage structures, providing entry points for drainage in streets, parking areas and similar paved zones.
[E03F 5/0402] provided with flushing means for cleaning or emptying -- E03F 5/0402 classifies sewerage structures that are equipped with flushing means for cleaning or emptying the conduit, covering fixed constructions in water-supply and sewage systems designed to clear debris or discharge fluid.
[E03F 5/0403] with a sediment trap -- This subclass covers sewerage structures that incorporate a sediment trap to capture solid particles before wastewater proceeds further in the system, improving flow efficiency and reducing blockages in fixed water-supply and sewer constructions. It falls under the hierarchy of Fixed Constructions → Water Supply → Sewers → Sewerage structures, coded E03F 5/0403.
[E03F 5/0404] with a permanent or temporary filtering device; Filtering devices specially adapted therefor -- E03F 5/0404 covers sewerage structures that incorporate a permanent or temporary filtering device, as well as filtering devices specifically designed for use in those structures. The classification addresses methods and apparatuses for removing solids or contaminants from wastewater within fixed sewer constructions.
[E03F 5/0405] with an odour seal -- This subclass covers sewerage structures that incorporate an odour seal to prevent the escape of unpleasant gases from the system. It applies to fixed constructions in water supply and sewer networks where sealing mechanisms are integrated into manholes, vents, or pipe connections.
[E03F 5/0406] the odour seal being easily accessible for cleaning -- This CPC entry E03F 5/0406, within the fixed constructions-water supply-sewers hierarchy, covers sewerage structures whose odour seal is designed to be readily accessible for cleaning, facilitating maintenance and preventing odor escape.
[E03F 5/0407] Floor drains for indoor use -- E03F 5/0407 covers floor drainage devices intended for indoor installations, such as those used in bathrooms, kitchens, or utility rooms. These floor drains are part of fixed construction systems that collect and convey wastewater into the building's sewerage network, providing a means to prevent water accumulation on interior surfaces.
[E03F 5/0408] specially adapted for showers -- The CPC subclass E03F 5/0408 covers sewerage structures that are specially adapted for showers, detailing design features such as shower-specific drainage slopes, waterproofing measures, trap arrangements and venting systems integrated into fixed construction. It pertains to the broader categories of water-supply and sewer infrastructure.
[E03F 5/0409] Devices for preventing seepage around the floor drain -- This CPC entry covers devices designed to stop water or liquids from leaking through or around floor drains in sewerage structures, such as sealing mechanisms, gaskets, or removable covers that provide a watertight barrier. It includes solutions integrated into the floor-drain assembly to prevent seepage into surrounding areas.
[E03F 5/041] Accessories therefor -- The subclass E03F 5/041 covers accessories used in sewerage structures, including fittings, joints, seals, access chambers, inspection hatches, cleaning and maintenance devices, and other ancillary components that support the installation, operation or upkeep of fixed water-supply and sewage systems.
[E03F 5/0411] Devices for temporarily blocking inflow into a gully -- This CPC subclass covers devices that temporarily block the inflow of water or other liquids into a gully, typically used during construction, repair or maintenance of sewerage structures to prevent flooding or contamination. The devices may be removable or adjustable and are intended for short-term use in fixed-construction water-supply and sewer systems.
[E03F 5/042] Arrangements of means against overflow of water, backing-up from the drain -- The subclass addresses designs that stop water from overflowing or backing up in sewerage systems, including devices, configurations and control methods that prevent flood or back-flow in drainage pipes. It falls under fixed constructions for water supply within sewage structures.
[E03F 5/046] adapted to be used with kerbs -- E03F 5/046 refers to sewerage structures that are specifically designed to be installed with or alongside road kerbs, such as curbside manholes, inlet boxes or drainage channels that fit into the kerb profile. The classification covers adaptations that allow the sewer components to be integrated with kerbs for efficient water collection and conveyance.
[E03F 5/06] Gully gratings -- Gully gratings are fixed construction elements installed in sewer openings to permit the passage of water while blocking litter, debris and animals. They form part of the broader water-supply and sewerage structure categories and are specified under the Cooperative Patent Classification code E03F 5/06.
[E03F 5/08] Ventilation of sewers -- The E03F 5/08 entry covers methods and devices for ventilating sewerage structures, ensuring adequate airflow to remove foul gases and regulate pressure within underground pipelines. It includes passive and forced ventilation systems such as vent shafts, fans, and aerodynamic designs integrated into fixed sewer constructions.
[E03F 5/10] Collecting-tanks -- E03F 5/10 designates the portion of the CPC that covers collecting-tanks used in sewerage structures. It falls under the hierarchy of fixed constructions for water supply and sewers, specifying tanks that receive and temporarily hold wastewater before it is conveyed to treatment or disposal facilities.
[E03F 5/101] Dedicated additional structures, interposed or parallel to the sewer system -- E03F 5/101 covers dedicated additional structures that are placed alongside or inserted into the sewer network, such as manholes, junction boxes, or ancillary chambers that serve specific functions separate from the main flow. These structures are installed in parallel or interposed within the sewer system to facilitate maintenance, monitoring, or distribution.
[E03F 5/102] using already existing parts of the sewer system for runoff-regulation -- E03F 5/102 covers methods that employ already installed elements of a sewer network, such as pipes, chambers or manholes, to manage and regulate surface-water runoff. It includes retrofitting or adapting existing sewerage structures for storm-water control without constructing new dedicated facilities.
[E03F 5/103] Naturals or landscape retention bodies, e.g. ponds -- It covers natural or landscaped water-retention structures such as ponds that are incorporated into water-supply or sewerage systems to temporarily store water. These bodies are used for flood control, water-quality improvement, or aesthetic integration within fixed constructions.
[E03F 5/105] Accessories, e.g. flow regulators or cleaning devices -- The CPC subclass E03F 5/105 pertains to accessories for sewerage structures within fixed water-supply constructions, including devices that regulate flow and tools used for cleaning or maintaining sewers. It covers items such as flow regulators, cleaning brushes, and related equipment integrated into stationary sewer systems.
[E03F 5/106] Passive flow control devices, i.e. not moving during flow regulation -- E03F 5/106 covers passive flow control devices used in sewerage structures, such as fixed weirs, baffles and overflow plates that regulate flow without moving parts. These devices are installed in sewers to maintain hydraulic conditions, prevent surges and distribute wastewater evenly.
[E03F 5/107] Active flow control devices, i.e. moving during flow regulation -- This subclass covers devices that move or adjust while in operation to regulate the flow of sewage, such as motor-driven gates, pressure-actuated valves or adjustable weirs installed in sewerage structures. It focuses on active flow-control mechanisms used in fixed water-supply and sewer constructions.
[E03F 5/108] Cleaning devices providing a flushing surge -- E03F 5/108 covers cleaning devices that generate a flushing surge to remove deposits and blockages from sewerage structures such as mains, manholes and pipelines. The devices typically use high-pressure water or surge tanks to provide a rapid flow that dislodges sediments.
[E03F 5/12] Emergency outlets -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Sewer channels or stormwater holding tanks with means for allowing surplus water to be discharged in a controlled way.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Devices for separating unwanted substances from flowing sewage which are integrated in the sewer system -> E03F5/14
[E03F 5/125] providing screening of overflowing water -- E03F 5/125 covers methods or devices for screening overflow water in sewerage structures, part of fixed constructions for water supply and sewers. It relates to preventing debris or excess water from entering the main sewer system.
[E03F 5/14] Devices for separating liquid or solid substances from sewage, e.g. sand or sludge traps, rakes or grates -- E03F 5/14 covers equipment used in sewerage structures to separate liquids or solids from sewage, such as sand and sludge traps, rakes and grates. These devices are designed to remove debris and sediments to protect downstream processes.
[E03F 5/16] Devices for separating oil, water or grease from sewage in drains leading to the main sewer -- E03F 5/16 relates to devices installed in drainage pipes that separate oil, water or grease from sewage before it reaches the main sewer. These fixed-construction systems are used in water-supply and sewerage structures to prevent contamination, blockages and maintenance problems in the sewer network.
[E03F 5/18] Tanks for disinfecting, neutralising, or cooling sewage -- The code E03F 5/18 designates fixed construction components within sewerage infrastructure that are tanks used to treat sewage by disinfection, neutralisation, or cooling. These tanks provide a controlled environment for chemical or thermal processes that reduce pathogens or adjust pH before the sewage proceeds to further treatment steps.
[E03F 5/20] Siphon pipes or inverted siphons -- E03F 5/20 covers the design and installation of siphon pipes or inverted siphons used in sewerage structures to convey wastewater across obstacles such as roads or low-lying terrain, ensuring a continuous flow without pumps. The classification addresses the geometry, materials and hydraulic principles of these fixed constructions within water-supply and sewer networks.
[E03F 5/22] Adaptations of pumping plants for lifting sewage -- E03F 5/22 covers modifications to sewage pumping stations that improve the lifting of wastewater, such as enhanced inlet devices, variable-speed drives, or pressure-boosting arrangements, aimed at optimizing flow in sewerage networks.
[E03F 5/24] Installations for neutralising explosions in sewers -- The code E03F 5/24 covers devices or systems installed in sewer networks to detect and mitigate explosive gases, using mechanisms such as venting, flame arrestors or chemical neutralisation to prevent pipe rupture. These installations are part of fixed construction for water supply and sewerage structures.
[E03F 5/26] Installations for stirring-up sewage -- Installations for stirring-up sewage are fixed devices incorporated in sewerage structures to keep solid waste in suspension and promote uniform flow. They typically use mechanical or hydraulic agitation to prevent sediment buildup, improve transport efficiency, and reduce blockages in underground pipelines.
[E03F 7/00] Other installations or implements for operating sewer systems, e.g. for preventing or indicating stoppage -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Devices for ensuring functioning of a sewer system.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Devices for refurbishing sewers -> E03F3/06 - Devices for cleaning sewers -> E03F9/00 - Refuse vehicles -> B65F3/00
[E03F 7/02] Shut-off devices -- E03F 7/02 covers shut-off devices used in sewer installations to stop or isolate the flow of wastewater. It includes valves, gates, plugs or other mechanisms that can be actuated manually or automatically for maintenance, emergency blockage prevention or flow indication. The subclass falls under fixed constructions for water supply and sewer systems.
[E03F 7/04] Valves for preventing return flow -- E03F 7/04 covers valves specifically designed to stop reverse flow in sewer networks, ensuring that water or waste does not travel back toward upstream sections. These devices are installed as part of fixed sewer constructions and may include mechanisms that automatically block back-pressure or allow controlled release when needed.
[E03F 7/06] Devices for restraining rats or other animals -- E03F 7/06 classifies devices installed in sewer infrastructure to restrain rats or other animals, including traps, barriers, repellents or other mechanisms fitted in pipes, manholes or related fittings to protect the system from animal damage. It falls under Fixed Constructions-Water Supply-Sewers-Other installations for operating sewer systems.
[E03F 7/08] Hand implements for emptying sewers or cesspools -- The E03F 7/08 class covers hand-operated tools designed to remove waste, water or debris from sewers, drainage channels or cesspools, such as shovels, scoops or suction devices. These implements are used manually to clear blockages or empty accumulated material in underground or surface sewer systems.
[E03F 7/10] Wheeled apparatus for emptying sewers or cesspools -- E03F 7/10 covers wheeled machines designed to empty sewers or cesspools, such as portable suction units, pumps or carts that can be moved along the pipe network to extract liquids and debris. These devices are used in sewer maintenance to clear blockages, monitor flow and prevent overflow.
[E03F 7/103] with a tank featuring one or more partition walls -- This subgroup covers sewer-system installations that incorporate a tank equipped with one or more internal partition walls, allowing separation of flow sections, staged processing, or improved detection and isolation of blockages within the sewer network.
[E03F 7/106] Accessories, e.g. hose support -- E03F 7/106 covers accessories for sewer system equipment such as hose supports used in fixed water-supply and sewer installations. It includes devices that aid operation, maintenance or monitoring of sewer lines, for example brackets or holders that keep hoses in place.
[E03F 7/12] Installations enabling inspection personnel to drive along sewer canals -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Devices enabling inspection of interior of sewer canals, whether or not manned and whether or not moved through the canal.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Devices for repairing or refurbishing sewer canals -> E03F3/06 - Devices for cleaning the interior of sewer canals -> E03F9/00
[E03F 9/00] Arrangements or fixed installations for cleaning sewer pipes, e.g. by flushing -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Devices for cleaning the interior of a sewer canal in order to improve flow through the canal.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Sediment traps, rakes, screens, or the like, arranged in sewer lines -> E03F5/14 - Cleaning of inner side of hollow articles -> B08B9/02 - Devices for cleaning or clearing domestic waste pipes -> E03C1/30 - Devices for clearing obstructions in water closets -> E03D9/00
[E03F 9/002] Cleaning sewer pipes by mechanical means  combined with flushing  E03F9/00 -- The classification covers fixed installations designed to clean sewer pipes by using mechanical devices in conjunction with flushing. It pertains to combined methods where physical agitation or cutting tools are employed while water is introduced to wash away debris.
[E03F 9/005] Apparatus for simultaneously pushing and rotating a cleaning device carried by the leading end of a cable or an assembly of rods -- The E03F 9/005 class covers devices that advance and rotate a cleaning tool attached to the front of a cable or rod string for cleaning sewer pipes. Such apparatus combine forward-propulsion with a twisting motion to enhance scouring efficiency while the tool is pulled through the conduit. It falls under fixed sewer constructions in the cooperative patent classification.
[E03F 9/007] Devices providing a flushing surge -- Devices that generate a short, high-pressure water surge to dislodge debris and clean the interior of sewer pipes. The surge is produced by a pump, accumulator or similar mechanism and is released into the pipe network to flush out blockages efficiently. This technology is used in fixed installations for routine sewer maintenance.
[E04] BUILDING -- E04 covers the construction of buildings, encompassing foundation work, structural frames, walls, floors, roofs and other fixed components. It also includes the methods, techniques and materials used to erect residential, commercial and industrial structures.
[E04B] GENERAL BUILDING CONSTRUCTIONS -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Building constructions, i.e. construction of edifices, their structural or non structural walls, their roof structures, their floor structures, suspended or false ceilings;  Insulation of buildings or other protection of buildings.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Railway vehicles -> B61 - Motor vehicles -> B62D - Ships or other waterborne vessels -> B63 - Aircrafts; Cosmonautics -> B64 - Bridges -> E01D - Foundations -> E02D - Structural elements for building construction -> E04C - Roof coverings -> E04D - Finishing work on buildings, e.g. lining, flooring -> E04F - Scaffolding;Shuttering -> E04G - Structures specially adapted for buildings for special purposes, general layout of buildings, e.g. modular co-ordination; Masts; Tents;Swimming pools -> E04H - Small buildings for limited occupation, e.g. kiosks, waiting shelters for bus stops -> E04H1/12 - Buildings or shelters adapted to withstand or provide protection against abnormal external influences, e.g. war-like action, earthquakes, hurricanes -> E04H9/00 - Border constructions of opening in walls, floors or ceilings -> E06B1/00 - Devices for fastening or securing general constructional elements or machine parts together -> F16B - Electromagnetic shielding -> H05K9/0001
[E04B 1/00] Constructions in general -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Building constructions in general, i.e. construction of edifices; Structures of buildings which are not restricted either to walls, or floors or ceilings or roofs.  Special structures and methods of construction of buildings, e.g. arched, movable, collapsible, box-like structures; Connections for building structures, e.g. connections of slab-shaped or elongated members; Insulation or other protection of buildings, e.g. against water-damage, heat, sound, fire.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Partitions per se -> E04B2/00 - Floor structures per se -> E04B5/00 - Roof structures per se -> E04B7/00 - Suspended or false ceilings per se -> E04B9/00 - Foundations per se -> E02D - Roof coverings per se -> E04D - Scaffolds, shutterings -> E04G - Structures specially adapted for buildings for special purposes;General layout of buildings, e.g. modular co-ordination -> E04H
[E04B 1/0007] Base structures; Cellars -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Cellars, basements, also retrofitted under existing buildings;  Crawl spaces.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Foundations per se -> E02D - Drainage and exterior waterproofing of foundation -> E02D31/00
[E04B 1/0015] Cellars constructed from prefabricated units -- This subgroup covers underground storage or service spaces built using prefabricated structural units such as modular walls, floors or vaults that are assembled on site to form a cellar. The focus is on the use of factory-produced components to expedite construction, improve quality control and permit standardized designs within general building constructions.
[E04B 1/0023] Building characterised by incorporated canalisations  E04B1/34869  takes precedence -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Building with passages for fluids integrally formed with structural elements of the building.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Box-like or cell-like units for special technical purposes -> E04B1/34869
[E04B 1/003] Balconies; Decks -- Entry E04B 1/003 covers the design and construction of balconies and decks attached to buildings, including their structural support, access means, guardrails, and weather protection. It addresses both residential and commercial applications and includes variations such as cantilevered or projecting platforms. This subclass falls under fixed building constructions within the general building category.
[E04B 1/0038] Anchoring devices specially adapted therefor with means for preventing cold bridging -- Anchoring devices designed for building constructions that incorporate built-in thermal breaks to stop heat-conductive paths, commonly known as cold bridges, fall under CPC code E04B 1/0038. These specialised fasteners are used to securely attach structural elements while maintaining thermal insulation performance.
[E04B 1/0046] Loggias -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Loggias, wintergardens, enclosed balconies, verandas.
[E04B 1/02] Structures consisting primarily of load-supporting, block-shaped, or slab-shaped elements -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Structures consisting primarily of large load-supporting panels or large load-supporting panel-shaped blocks.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Arched, vaulted or folded structures -> E04B1/32 - Extraordinary structures -> E04B1/34 - Structures characterised by movebale, separable or collapsible parts -> E04B1/343 - Structures covering a large free area -> E04B1/346 - Structures composed of e.g. box-like or cell-like units -> E04B1/348 - Extraordinary methods of construction -> E04B1/35 - Bearings or like supports allowing movement -> E04B1/36 - Masonry walls -> E04B2/02
[E04B 1/04] the elements consisting of concrete, e.g. reinforced concrete, or other stone-like material -- E04B 1/04 classifies building components that are made from concrete, reinforced concrete or other stone-like materials, such as walls, floors, columns and prefabricated units. It falls under the broader groups of fixed constructions and general building constructions, covering structural elements where the material itself provides the load-bearing function.
[E04B 1/043] Connections specially adapted therefor -- The classification covers structural joints and connecting elements specifically designed for joining building components such as prefabricated sections, modular walls or roof elements where the connection devices are integral to the construction method. It includes bolts, clips, brackets and other specially adapted connectors used in general building constructions.
[E04B 1/046] using reinforcement loops protruding from the elements -- This subclass covers building methods that employ reinforcement loops extending outward from structural elements such as walls, slabs or frames. The protruding loops are used to anchor, tie or transmit forces between components, often in masonry, concrete or timber construction. It pertains to general building constructions where such loops improve structural continuity and connection strength.
[E04B 1/06] the elements being prestressed -- E04B 1/06 covers building components that are subjected to prestressing, including beams, slabs, columns and other structural members in which tensile forces are introduced before service loads to improve strength and reduce cracking. The classification applies to methods and devices for applying prestress to such elements in fixed construction.
[E04B 1/08] the elements consisting of metal -- E04B 1/08 covers metal components used in fixed building constructions, such as structural members, connections, fittings and other metallic elements that form part of general building works. It falls under the hierarchy of Fixed Constructions → Building → General Building Constructions.
[E04B 1/10] the elements consisting of wood -- This subclass covers building elements that are primarily made of wood, such as timber frames, floors, walls, roofs and other structural or finishing components used in construction. It is part of the CPC group for general building constructions within the fixed constructions section.
[E04B 1/12] the elements consisting of other material -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Structures with elements consisting of specified materials other than concrete, stone-like material, metal or wood.
[E04B 1/14] the elements being composed of two or more materials -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Structures with elements consisting of the combination of two or more specified structural materials.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Structures consisting primarily of reinforced concrete block-shaped or slab-shaped elements -> E04B1/04
[E04B 1/16] Structures made from masses, e.g. of concrete, cast or similarly formed -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Arched, vaulted or folded structures -> E04B1/32 - Extraordinary structures -> E04B1/34 - Structures characterised by moveable, separable or collapsible parts -> E04B1/343 - Structures covering a large free area -> E04B1/346 - Structures composed of e.g. box-like or cell-like units -> E04B1/348 - Extraordinary methods of construction -> E04B1/35 - Bearings or like supports allowing movement -> E04B1/36
[E04B 1/161] with vertical and horizontal slabs, both being partially cast  in situ -- E04B 1/161 covers building methods where both the vertical walls and horizontal floor or roof slabs are partially cast in situ, combining on-site concrete work with prefabricated components. It pertains to fixed construction techniques within general building constructions, describing structures whose slab portions are formed by a mixture of casting and assembly.
[E04B 1/162] with a central core, used as form, in order to form a continuous concrete shell -- This classification covers techniques where a central core serves as a form around which concrete is cast to create a continuous shell structure, commonly employed in thin-shell or curved building components. It includes the use of removable or permanent cores that define the interior geometry of the shell during construction.
[E04B 1/163] with vertical and horizontal slabs, only the vertical slabs being partially cast  in situ   E04B2/86  takes precedence -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Walls made by casting in-situ in permanent forms -> E04B2/86
[E04B 1/164] with vertical and horizontal slabs, only the horizontal slabs being partially cast  in situ -- E04B 1/164 refers to fixed building constructions in which vertical slabs are fully fabricated off-site while the horizontal slabs are only partially cast in situ, combining prefabricated and on-site concrete elements in the same structural system.
[E04B 1/165] with elongated load-supporting parts, cast  in situ   E04B1/161 ,  E04B1/163  and  E04B1/164  take precedence -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Structures made from masses with vertical and horizontal slabs, both being partially cast in situ -> E04B1/161 - Structures made from masses with vertical and horizontal slabs, only the vertical slabs being partially cast in situ -> E04B1/163 - Structures made from masses with vertical and horizontal slabs, only the horizontal slabs being partially cast in situ -> E04B1/164
[E04B 1/166] with curved surfaces, at least partially cast  in situ  in order to make a continuous concrete shell structure  E04B1/169  takes precedence -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Structures made from masses cast with inflatable flexible permanent forms -> E04B1/169
[E04B 1/167] with permanent forms made of particular materials, e.g. layered products -- This subclass concerns building methods that employ permanent formwork made from particular materials, such as layered or composite products that remain in place as part of the structure. It includes techniques where the formwork is not removed after casting but becomes an integral component of the finished construction.
[E04B 1/168] flexible -- The subclass E04B 1/168 covers building constructions that are flexible, meaning they can be deformed, moved, or assembled and disassembled without permanent alteration of the structure. It includes temporary, portable, or adaptable elements such as tents, collapsible walls, and movable partitions used in general building projects.
[E04B 1/169] inflatable -- E04B 1/169 classifies constructions that rely on air pressure or gas to achieve their shape and structural integrity, such as inflatable tents, domes, shelters and other temporary building elements. The entry covers the design, fabrication, installation and use of these flexible, lightweight structures for general building applications.
[E04B 1/18] Structures comprising elongated load-supporting parts, e.g. columns, girders, skeletons -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Structures comprising elongated load-supporting parts, e.g. columns, beams, girders, skeletons.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Arched, vaulted or folded structures -> E04B1/32 - Extraordinary structures -> E04B1/34 - Structures characterised by moveable, separable or collapsible parts -> E04B1/343 - Structures covering a large free area -> E04B1/346 - Structures composed of e.g. box-like or cell-like units -> E04B1/348 - Extraordinary methods of construction -> E04B1/35 - Bearings or like supports allowing movement -> E04B1/36 - Elongated load-supporting parts as elements, columns, beams, girders, trusses, truss-like structures -> E04C3/00
[E04B 1/185] Connections not covered by  E04B1/21  and  E04B1/2403 , e.g. connections between structural parts of different material -- A subclass of fixed-construction building topics covering j­oints and other connections that are not classified elsewhere, for example where structural parts of different materials are joined together. It falls under E04B 1/185 within the general building-construction hierarchy.
[E04B 1/19] Three-dimensional [3D] framework structures -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Space frames, i.e. truss-like lightweight structures constructed from interconnected struts in a geometric pattern spanning a large area, with either short struts connected at the nodes or continuous struts folded at the nodes, wherein the frames are not habitable or accessible upon construction.
[E04B 1/1903] Connecting nodes specially adapted therefor -- The classification E04B 1/1903 covers specially adapted means for joining fixed building nodes, such as devices that connect beams, columns, panels or other structural elements in permanent construction. It deals with connection methods designed to secure these nodes while maintaining the integrity of the overall building framework.
[E04B 1/1906] with central spherical, semispherical or polyhedral connecting element -- The entry E04B 1/1906 covers fixed building constructions in which parts are joined through a central element that is spherical, semispherical or polyhedral. It pertains to general building structures where such rounded or angular connectors are used to link components together.
[E04B 1/1909] with central cylindrical connecting element -- The subclass E04B 1/1909 covers fixed building constructions in which the various structural parts are joined by a central cylindrical element that acts as a connector. This type of design is used in modular or prefabricated building components, where the cylindrical hub links arms, panels or other members to form the overall frame or support structure.
[E04B 1/1912] with central cubical connecting element -- A fixed construction for buildings in which a central cubical component serves as a hub that connects multiple structural members, such as beams, columns or panels, to form the overall framework. This arrangement is used in modular and prefabricated structures where the cube provides a standardized join for rapid assembly.
[E04B 1/20] the supporting parts consisting of concrete, e.g. reinforced concrete, or other stonelike material -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Structures with posts and beams made from concrete.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Space frames -> E04B1/19
[E04B 1/21] Connections specially adapted therefor -- The E04B 1/21 subclass covers specially adapted connections used in fixed building constructions, such as joints, fastenings, couplings and other means of linking structural elements. It includes design features that facilitate assembly, stability or transmission of loads between components.
[E04B 1/215] comprising metallic plates or parts -- A construction in which the main structural or enclosing elements are formed from metal plates or components, such as steel panels, metal cladding, or prefabricated metal frames used in general building structures. This category covers methods and assemblies where metallic parts constitute the primary load-bearing or protective elements.
[E04B 1/22] with parts being prestressed -- This subclass refers to building structures in which components are prestressed, meaning they are intentionally tensioned before loading to enhance strength and limit deformation. It applies to general fixed constructions in the building field, covering methods and devices that incorporate prestressed parts.
[E04B 1/24] the supporting parts consisting of metal -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Structures with posts and beams made of metal  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Space frames -> E04B1/19
