[E04B 2/44] using elements having specially-designed means for stabilising the position -- **Glossary:** - Spacers for cavity walls: spacers between parts of brick or block shaped elements for building cavity walls, at least one of the parts having cavities, e.g. spacers integrated in a block consisting of two parallel, non-massive, slab shaped parts kept at a distance by the spacer
[E04B 2/46] by interlocking of projections or inserts with indentations, e.g. of tongues, grooves, dovetails -- E04B 2/46 classifies wall components that are assembled by interlocking projections such as tongues, grooves or dovetail joints, allowing the parts to fit together without separate fasteners. This technique is applied to partitions and other building walls to provide structural stability and simplify construction.
[E04B 2/48] by filling material with or without reinforcements in small channels in, or in grooves between, the elements -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Walls made from layers of building elements of brick or block shape, the walls having cavities between, as well as in the elements, wherein the joints, e.g. small channels or grooves between the building elements and/or between the successive layers are filled with material, with or without additional reinforcements in the joints, small channels or grooves.
[E04B 2/50] using elements having a general shape differing from that of a parallelepiped -- This subclass deals with construction of walls or partitions where the constituent elements are not rectangular prisms but have other general shapes such as curved, wedge-shaped or irregular cross-sections. It covers methods and devices for assembling such elements in fixed building structures.
[E04B 2/52] the walls being characterised by fillings in some of the cavities forming load-bearing pillars or beams -- The group concerns fixed building walls in which selected cavities are filled to form integral load-bearing pillars or beams, combining partition and structural roles. It applies to general building constructions where walls incorporate such reinforced cavities.
[E04B 2/54] the walls being characterised by fillings in all cavities in order to form a wall construction -- E04B 2/54 covers wall and partition constructions in which every cavity within the wall is filled, creating a solid wall structure. The classification applies to building methods that use such filled-cavity walls for structural, insulating, or fire-resistance purposes.
[E04B 2/56] walls of framework or pillarwork -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Removable non-load-bearing partitions; Partitions with a free upper edge -> E04B2/74 - Curtain walls -> E04B2/88 - Pillars -> E04C3/30
[E04B 2/562] with fillings between the load-bearing elongated members -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Nogging of half-timbered walls.
[E04B 2/58] with elongated members of metal -- The subclass E04B 2/58 pertains to building walls or partitions that incorporate elongated metal members such as studs, tracks, or sections serving as the primary structural components of the wall. It addresses constructions where the metal elements form the framework that supports panels, finishes or insulation within the partition. This category falls under fixed constructions for general building walls.
[E04B 2/60] characterised by special cross-section of the elongated members -- The subclass E04B 2/60 covers building walls or partitions whose elongated structural members-such as studs, joists or mullions-are formed with a special cross-section, for example T-shapes, I-profiles, hollow, lattice or other non-rectangular sections that provide particular strength, rigidity, insulation or integration advantages.
[E04B 2/62] the members being formed of two or more elements in side-by-side relationship -- This CPC entry covers wall members in general building constructions that are made from two or more elements arranged side-by-side, forming a fixed partition or structural wall component.
[E04B 2/64] with elongated members of concrete -- E04B 2/64 covers building walls or partitions that are formed from elongated concrete members, such as precast or cast-in-place panels, studs, or lintels. It relates to fixed construction techniques where the concrete elements run vertically or horizontally to create structural or non-structural walls within a building.
[E04B 2/66] characterised by special cross-section of the elongated members -- E04B 2/66 covers walls or partitions in building construction that use elongated members-such as studs or beams-whose cross-sectional shape is specially designed rather than standard rectangular or circular forms, providing particular structural or functional advantages.
[E04B 2/68] made by filling-up wall cavities -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Walls having cavities in, but not between, the elements, the walls being characterised by fillings in some of the cavities forming load-bearing pillars or beams -> E04B2/24 - Walls having cavities between, but not in, the elements, the walls being characterised by fillings in some of the cavities forming load-bearing pillars or beams -> E04B2/38 - Walls having cavities between, as well as in, the elements, the walls being characterised by fillings in some of the cavities forming load-bearing pillars or beams -> E04B2/52
[E04B 2/70] with elongated members of wood -- This subclass deals with walls or partitions in buildings that are formed from elongated wooden members such as studs, joists, planks or similar continuous wood components, arranged vertically or horizontally to create the separating structure.
[E04B 2/701] with integrated supporting and obturation function -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Walls made from horizontally laid logs or beams, e.g. log cabins.
[E04B 2/702] with longitudinal horizontal elements  E04B2/704 ,  E04B2/705  take precedence -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Walls incorporating load-bearing elongated members of wood, with longitudinal horizontal elements shorter than the length of a wall -> E04B2/704 - Walls incorporating load-bearing elongated members of wood, with longitudinal horizontal elements placed between columns -> E04B2/705
[E04B 2/703] with longitudinal vertical elements -- E04B 2/703 covers wall or partition constructions that incorporate longitudinal vertical elements such as studs, pilasters or similar vertical members integrated along the length of the wall. The classification pertains to building components where these vertical elements provide structural support, reinforcement or aesthetic detailing within fixed building walls.
[E04B 2/704] with longitudinal horizontal elements shorter than the length of a wall -- This subclass covers walls or partitions in buildings that incorporate longitudinal horizontal elements whose length is shorter than the wall itself, such as short studs, lintels or horizontal panels used for reinforcement, modular construction or decorative purposes.
[E04B 2/705] with longitudinal horizontal elements placed between columns -- The code E04B 2/705 refers to wall or partition constructions in buildings that incorporate longitudinal horizontal members placed between structural columns, providing added support or rigidity to the fixed-construction wall assembly.
[E04B 2/706] with supporting function -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Walls comprising structural wooden studs or posts with non structural closure elements en-between, e.g. structural stud walls and shear bracing therefor.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Half-timbering -> E04B2/562
[E04B 2/707] obturation by means of panels -- E04B 2/707 relates to building walls or partitions that are closed or sealed using panels. It includes systems such as removable panels, shutters, doors or other panel-type closures that provide obturation for openings in fixed constructions. The code is used to classify patents dealing with the design, mounting or operation of such panel-based wall closures.
[E04B 2/708] obturation by means of longitudinal elements with a convex external surface -- The entry covers wall or partition sealing techniques that use elongated members with a convex outer surface to form an obturation layer. These longitudinal elements are fitted into or onto the wall to close gaps, improve insulation, or provide water- or air-tightness in building constructions.
[E04B 2/709] obturation by means of longitudinal elements with a plane external surface -- The code E04B 2/709 relates to walls or partitions in building construction that are sealed using longitudinal elements such as bars, slats or strips whose outer face is flat, creating a plane surface that closes gaps and provides an airtight or weather-tight barrier. This method is used in fixed construction systems to obtain obturation of openings.
[E04B 2/72] walls of elements of relatively thin form -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Non-load-bearing thin walls without studs or posts, with profiled or folded panels or trays.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Load-bearing] walls of framework or pillarwork; Walls incorporating load-bearing elongated members -> E04B2/56 - Non-load-bearing walls of elements of relatively thin form with joint fillings acting as framework or pillars -> E04B2/68 - Removable non-load-bearing partitions; Partitions with a free upper edge -> E04B2/74 - Non-structural stud walls -> E04B2/7453 - Curtain walls -> E04B2/88 - Building elements of relatively thin form for the construction of parts of buildings, e.g. sheet materials, slabs, or panels -> E04C2/00
[E04B 2/721] connections specially adapted therefor -- The subclass E04B 2/721 pertains to connections specially adapted for joining walls or partitions in building construction, encompassing brackets, clips, fasteners, interlocking profiles and adhesive systems that provide permanent or removable links between wall elements.
[E04B 2/723] constituted of gypsum elements -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Non-load-bearing thin walls with folded plasterboard or gypsum or plaster projected onto a lattice.
[E04B 2/74] Removable non-load-bearing partitions -- **Definition:** This place covers:  E.g., modular coordination.
[E04B 2/7401] assembled using panels without a frame or supporting posts, with or without upper or lower edge locating rails -- This CPC subclass covers building wall constructions that are built by joining prefabricated panels directly, without any supporting framework or vertical posts, and may include locating rails at the top or bottom. It falls under Fixed Constructions → Building → General Building Constructions → Walls, e.g. partitions, for buildings. Code E04B 2/7401
[E04B 2/7403] with special measures for sound or thermal insulation including fire protection -- The code E04B 2/7403 covers wall or partition constructions that incorporate special measures to provide sound or thermal insulation together with fire-protective features. It relates to building components designed to limit noise transmission, retain heat, and resist fire spread, typically used in residential, commercial or industrial structures.
[E04B 2/7405] with free upper edge, e.g. for use as office space dividers -- The classification E04B 2/7405 covers interior building partitions whose upper edge is left free, meaning the top is not attached to the ceiling or structural elements, allowing the wall to serve as a movable or temporary divider, often used in office environments.
[E04B 2/7407] assembled using frames with infill panels or coverings only; made-up of panels and a support structure incorporating posts  E04B2/78  and  E04B2/80  take precedence -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Removable non-load-bearing partitions or partitions with a free upper edge with framework or posts of metal characterised by special cross-section of the frame-members -> E04B2/78 - Removable non-load-bearing partitions or partitions with a free upper edge with framework or posts of wood -> E04B2/80
[E04B 2/7409] special measures for sound or thermal insulation, including fire protection -- E04B 2/7409 covers special measures applied to walls and partitions to provide sound or thermal insulation, and may also include fire-protective elements. It is used for building-construction technologies that enhance acoustic, temperature-control, or fire-resistance performance of interior or exterior wall structures.
[E04B 2/7411] Details for fire protection -- E04B 2/7411 pertains to fire-protection details applied to walls and partitions in buildings, covering elements such as fire-resistant linings, fire-stopping sealants, barriers, and other measures that prevent or limit the spread of fire through structural divisions. It includes designs, materials and construction methods that enhance a wall's fire safety performance.
[E04B 2/7412] Posts or frame members specially adapted for reduced sound or heat transmission -- E04B 2/7412 covers building posts, studs or other framing members that are specially designed to limit the passage of sound or heat through walls. These components incorporate materials, shapes or internal cavities that enhance acoustic and thermal insulation in fixed construction walls.
[E04B 2/7414] Posts or frame members with projections for holding sound or heat insulating fillings -- The code E04B 2/7414 refers to wall components such as posts or frame members that include built-in projections or recesses designed to retain insulating material for sound or thermal control, typically used in partition or building wall constructions.
[E04B 2/7416] with free upper edge, e.g. for use as office space dividers -- A subclass of fixed building constructions covering walls or partitions that terminate with a free upper edge, enabling the element to serve as a removable or movable divider, often used for creating temporary office spaces within a building.
[E04B 2/7422] with separate framed panels without intermediary support posts -- The code E04B 2/7422 classifies wall constructions that use individual framed panels as the structural element, eliminating the need for intermediate support posts. It falls within the hierarchy of fixed constructions, building, general building constructions, specifically walls or partitions for buildings. This entry covers systems where each panel provides its own load-bearing capacity without additional vertical framing.
[E04B 2/7424] Glazing details -- Glazing details refer to the design, arrangement and installation of glass components in building walls or partitions, covering aspects such as frame types, sealing methods, thermal and acoustic performance, and safety glazing requirements. This subclass classifies patents that address specific features of windows, facades, curtain walls or other transparent or translucent elements incorporated into fixed construction walls.
[E04B 2/7425] Details of connection of panels  E04B2/7427  takes precedence -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Partitions with a free upper edge with separate framed panels without intermediary support posts and with adjustable angular connection of panels -> E04B2/7427
[E04B 2/7427] with adjustable angular connection of panels -- This subclass relates to wall or partition panels that are joined using an adjustable angular connection, allowing the panels to be set at varying angles relative to each other. It is part of the fixed-construction group for general building walls and partitions.
[E04B 2/7429] using flexible hinges -- E04B 2/7429 covers building wall or partition systems that incorporate flexible hinges to allow movement, adjusting to deformation or thermal expansion while maintaining structural integrity. It falls under general building constructions for fixed constructions.
[E04B 2/7431] using hinges having two parallel rotation axes -- The code E04B 2/7431 covers wall or partition constructions in buildings that incorporate hinges with two parallel rotation axes, enabling double-leaf or folding panels to pivot in a coordinated manner for opening, closing or adjusting the partition.
[E04B 2/7433] with panels and support posts -- A wall or partition for a building constructed from prefabricated panels that are connected to vertical support posts, forming a fixed, load-bearing or non-load-bearing element within the building's structural framework. This falls under the Fixed Constructions → Building → General Building Constructions hierarchy of the Cooperative Patent Classification.
[E04B 2/7435] Glazing details -- E04B 2/7435 covers the design and construction features of glazing incorporated into walls or partitions of buildings. It includes detailed elements such as window frames, seals, mullions and related components that affect light transmission, insulation and structural integration. The classification helps identify patents dealing with specific methods, materials and configurations of glazed wall assemblies.
[E04B 2/7437] with panels hooked onto posts  E04B2/7438  takes precedence -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Non-load-bearing partition with free upper edge and panels hooked onto posts, e.g. with additional securing elements to prevent its unhooking.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Partitions with a free upper edge with panels with support posts and with adjustable angular connection of panels to posts -> E04B2/7438
[E04B 2/7438] with adjustable angular connection of panels to posts -- This subclass relates to wall or partition systems in which panels are joined to vertical posts by an adjustable angular connection, allowing the panels to be set at different angles for flexible layout or alignment. The feature is used in modular or prefabricated building constructions where the angle between panel and post can be varied during installation.
[E04B 2/744] using angularly-spaced longitudinal grooves of the posts -- The subclass E04B 2/744 relates to wall or partition construction in which the vertical posts are provided with longitudinal grooves that are spaced at specific angular intervals around the post. These angularly-spaced grooves facilitate the insertion of fittings, connectors or panels and improve alignment and load distribution in fixed building structures.
[E04B 2/7442] hinged connections  E04B2/7444  takes precedence -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Partitions with a free upper edge with panels with support posts and with panels hooked onto the rim or in a groove of circular posts -> E04B2/7444
[E04B 2/7444] panels hooked onto the rim or in a groove of circular posts -- This subclass covers wall panels that are attached by hooking them onto the outer rim or into a groove cut in circular supporting posts. The design enables quick assembly of partition walls and other interior constructions, typically using prefabricated panel sections that lock onto the posts without additional fasteners.
[E04B 2/7448] with separate framed panels without intermediary posts, extending from floor to ceiling -- A wall or partition built from separate framed panels that extend continuously from floor to ceiling, without any intermediary posts, is classified under this entry. It covers fixed, non-load-bearing wall constructions used in general building structures.
[E04B 2/745] Glazing details -- E04B 2/745 covers design and construction details of glazing for walls and partitions in buildings, including installation methods, sealing, framing, and ancillary components that integrate glass elements into fixed structures. The classification concerns fixed construction aspects of windows, panes, and related fittings within general building constructions.
[E04B 2/7451] with false tongue joints -- E04B 2/7451 covers wall or partition constructions that employ false-tongue joints, a method where a short tongue fits into a corresponding groove to interlock fixed building elements without mechanical fasteners. The classification applies to fixed-construction walls in general building structures.
[E04B 2/7453] with panels and support posts, extending from floor to ceiling -- E04B 2/7453 classifies walls or partitions built from panels that are supported by vertical posts extending continuously from the floor to the ceiling. The code applies to fixed building constructions where the panel-post system forms a permanent, load-bearing or non-load-bearing partition within a structure.
[E04B 2/7455] Glazing details -- The code E04B 2/7455 addresses the design and construction of glazing incorporated into walls or partitions of buildings. It covers details such as the arrangement, framing, sealing and finishing of glass units and related accessories used in fixed or operable glazing systems.
[E04B 2/7457] with wallboards attached to the outer faces of the posts, parallel to the partition  E04B2/7459  takes precedence -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Removable non-load-bearing partitions with panels and support posts, extending from floor to ceiling, the posts being telescoping to compensate for floor or ceiling irregularities -> E04B2/7459
[E04B 2/7459] with telescoping posts to compensate for floor or ceiling irregularities -- A wall or partition built with telescoping posts that can be length-adjusted to accommodate uneven floors or ceilings, enabling the structure to remain plumb despite vertical irregularities. This subclass falls under fixed constructions, building, general building constructions, walls/partitions.
[E04B 2/76] with framework or posts of metal -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Connections of horizontal and vertical metallic frame members.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Details of connections, thresholds or skirtings -> E04B2/82
[E04B 2/761] L-connections -- E04B 2/761 covers L-shaped connections used in the construction of walls and partitions within buildings. It pertains to fixed building components that join two wall elements at right angles, facilitating structural stability and alignment. The subclass falls under general building constructions and fixed constructions.
[E04B 2/762] Cross connections -- E04B 2/762 concerns cross connections installed in walls or partitions of buildings, covering the arrangement, sealing and joining of two separate pipe, duct or cable lines within a fixed construction to enable fluid, gas or signal transfer while preserving structural integrity.
[E04B 2/763] with one continuous profile, the perpendicular one passing continuously through the first one -- E04B 2/763 classifies building walls or partitions that are formed from a single continuous longitudinal profile with a transverse profile that passes continuously through it, creating a unified structural element for fixed constructions. This subclass applies to wall systems where the perpendicular profile is integrated along the entire length of the primary profile.
[E04B 2/764] with two interrupted profiles -- The CPC class E04B 2/764 relates to fixed building constructions, specifically walls or partitions that use two interrupted profiles. It falls under the hierarchy of BUILDING - GENERAL BUILDING CONSTRUCTIONS - Walls, e.g. partitions, for buildings. This code is applied to patents describing wall systems with a pair of discontinuous structural sections.
[E04B 2/765] with one continuous profile, the perpendicular one being interrupted -- The code E04B 2/765 covers wall or partition constructions in which a single continuous longitudinal profile runs along the wall while the transverse (perpendicular) profiling is broken or interrupted, typically meaning the main vertical element is uninterrupted but the horizontal or side members are not continuous. This classification is used for fixed building wall systems that employ such mixed continuous-interrupted profiling.
[E04B 2/766] T-connections -- E04B 2/766 designates T-shaped connections used to join wall elements such as partitions, frames or panels in building construction. The code applies to fixed constructions where a member is attached perpendicularly to the centre of another, forming a T configuration, and includes methods, devices and joining details for such connections.
[E04B 2/767] Connections between wall studs and upper or lower locating rails -- The code covers methods for joining wall studs to the horizontal locating rails that define the top or bottom of a partition. It includes mechanical fasteners, adhesives, interlocking profiles, and other securing techniques used in interior wall construction to create stable, load-bearing or non-load-bearing partitions.
[E04B 2/768] allowing vertical movement of upper rail with respect to the stud, e.g. by using slots in the rail or stud -- E04B 2/768 covers building wall constructions where the upper rail of a partition can be moved vertically relative to the stud, typically by providing slots or other guiding features in the rail or stud to allow adjustment during installation or later modification.
[E04B 2/78] characterised by special cross-section of the frame members -- E04B 2/78 covers walls and partitions in building construction where the frame members have a distinctive cross-section, such as I-shapes, channels or other specially profiled sections designed to improve strength or ease of assembly.
[E04B 2/7809] of single or multiple tubular form -- The CPC entry E04B 2/7809 covers walls, such as partitions, that are formed from single or multiple tubular elements, typically using hollow sections or pipe-like components to create structural or non-structural dividing walls in buildings. This includes methods and devices for assembling and connecting the tubes to achieve a continuous wall surface.
[E04B 2/7818] of substantially rectangular form -- A subclass of the Cooperative Patent Classification for fixed building constructions, covering walls or partitions that are designed with a substantially rectangular shape. It pertains to general building walls where the overall form is primarily rectangular, facilitating standard layout and modular installation.
[E04B 2/7827] with all four sides of the profiles having substantially similar undercut grooves or connecting projections -- The CPC subclass E04B 2/7827 relates to fixed building constructions, specifically general walls or partitions, where the profile used for the panels has substantially similar undercut grooves or connecting projections on all four sides, allowing uniform interlocking and attachment of the wall components.
[E04B 2/7836] with only two sides of the profiles having substantially similar undercut grooves or connecting projections -- The CPC subclass E04B 2/7836 pertains to wall or partition sections used in building construction where the profile's undercut grooves or connecting projections are substantially similar on only two of the profile's sides. It addresses designs that simplify joining by limiting the grooved geometry to a pair of opposing faces.
[E04B 2/7845] of curved cross-section, e.g. circular -- This subclass covers walls or partitions in building construction that have a curved cross-section, such as circular or other rounded profiles. It includes designs where the wall shape is intentionally arched or tubular to provide aesthetic, structural or space-saving benefits in residential, commercial or industrial settings.
[E04B 2/7854] of open profile -- E04B 2/7854 classifies wall or partition elements in building construction that incorporate an open cross-sectional profile, such as frames or lattices, rather than solid panels. It is used for designs where the wall consists of spaced structural members allowing passage of light, air or services. The code falls under Fixed Constructions - Building - General Building Constructions - Walls, e.g. partitions, for buildings.
[E04B 2/7863] of substantially I - section with web perpendicular to plane of partition -- E04B 2/7863 classifies fixed wall or partition constructions that use a substantially I-section profile, where the web of the section is oriented perpendicular to the plane of the partition. The code applies to building elements that rely on this geometric arrangement for structural or decorative purposes.
[E04B 2/7872] of substantially H - section with flanges perpendicular to plane of partition -- The CPC subclass E04B 2/7872 covers wall or partition elements made from a substantially H-section profile whose flanges are oriented perpendicular to the plane of the partition, a fixed-construction feature used in general building walls and partitions.
[E04B 2/7881] of substantially S - or Z - section; having a shape or cross-section adapted for gripping or overlapping panels by means of at least partially complementary shaped parallel elements -- This subclass covers wall or partition sections that are essentially S- or Z-shaped and formed to grip or overlap adjacent panels using at least partially complementary parallel elements. The design provides a fixed construction method for joining building panels with interlocking cross-sections.
[E04B 2/789] of substantially U- or C- section -- This subclass concerns walls or partitions whose cross-section is essentially U- or C-shaped, such as channel-type sections used in building frameworks. It covers constructions where the wall profile provides structural strength or allows integration of services within the section.
[E04B 2/80] with framework or posts of wood -- E04B 2/80 covers walls or partitions in building construction that are formed using a wooden framework or vertical posts. The category includes any fixed construction where the primary structural element is wood, such as timber stud walls, wooden post-and-beam partitions, and similar assemblies.
[E04B 2/82] characterised by the manner in which edges are connected to the building -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Means for connecting partition walls or panels to the ceilings -> E04B9/008
[E04B 2/821] Connections between two opposed surfaces (i.e. floor and ceiling) by means of a device offering a restraining force acting in the plane of the partition -- This class covers devices that join two opposite surfaces, for example a floor and a ceiling, by applying a restraining force within the plane of the partition. It includes mechanisms that secure the two surfaces together while allowing planar movement or load transfer, typically used in building partitions and fixed constructions.
[E04B 2/822] Elastic connections, e.g. inflated joints -- The E04B 2/822 entry covers elastic connections for building walls, such as inflated joints that provide flexible sealing between partitions. It addresses structures that can expand, contract or adjust to movement, helping maintain airtight or watertight joints in general building constructions.
[E04B 2/824] restrained elastically at one surface and inelastically at the opposing surface -- The CPC subclass E04B 2/824 covers wall or partition structures where one surface is restrained elastically and the opposite surface is restrained inelastically, creating a combined load-sharing behavior. This applies to fixed construction elements in general building construction to enhance strength, impact resistance, or thermal performance.
[E04B 2/825] the connection between the floor and the ceiling being achieved without any restraining forces acting in the plane of the partition -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Removable non-load-bearing partitions with panels and support posts, extending from floor to ceiling, the posts being telescoping to compensate for floor or ceiling irregularities -> E04B2/7459 - Connections between wall studs and upper or lower locating rails allowing vertical movement of upper rail with respect to the stud, e.g. by using slots in the rail or stud -> E04B2/768
[E04B 2/827] Partitions constituted of sliding panels -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Non-load-bearing partitions constituted of sliding panels, where the sliding occurs in a direction parallel or perpendicular to the plane of the partition
[E04B 2/828] Connections between partitions and structural walls -- The classification covers methods and devices for linking interior partitions to the main structural walls of a building, including mechanical fasteners, adhesives, brackets, and anchoring systems that secure partition walls to load-bearing elements. It addresses both permanent and removable connections used in general building construction.
[E04B 2/84] Walls made by casting, pouring, or tamping -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Walls built-up from layers of building elements -> E04B2/02 - Load-bearing walls of framework or pillarwork ; Walls incorporating load-bearing elongated members -> E04B2/56 - Reusable forms for walls made by casting, pouring, or tamping in situ -> E04G11/06 - Working of concrete or similar masses able to be cast or poured in situ -> E04G21/02
[E04B 2/842] by projecting or otherwise applying hardenable masses to the exterior of a form leaf -- E04B 2/842 covers methods of constructing building walls where a hardenable material is projected or otherwise applied to the exterior surface of a form leaf to create the wall. This includes spraying, casting or trowelling concrete, plaster or similar binders onto the form leaf, forming a solid wall as the material hardens.
[E04B 2/845] the form leaf comprising a wire netting, lattice or the like -- The class E04B 2/845 covers wall or partition members that are formed as a leaf of wire netting, lattice or similar mesh structures, used in building construction. It includes prefabricated panels where the leaf provides a supporting or separating function within fixed constructions.
[E04B 2/847] the form leaf comprising an insulating foam panel -- E04B 2/847 covers a form leaf made from an insulating foam panel that is used as a permanent or removable wall component in building construction. It falls under fixed constructions, specifically general building walls and partitions, and relates to the integration of foam-based panels as structural or insulating elements.
[E04B 2/86] made in permanent forms -- **Glossary:** In this place, the following terms or expressions are used with the meaning indicated: A "tie" holds the two opposite leaves of the form together. A "spacer" keeps the two opposite leaves at a predetermined distance from each other. A single element can but must not have both functions: i.e. a tie can be a spacer or not, a spacer can be a tie or not.
[E04B 2/8605] without spacers -- E04B 2/8605 covers walls or partitions in fixed building constructions that are assembled without any spacers, meaning the structural elements are joined directly to each other rather than being separated by intermediate material. This subclass addresses solid, non-cavity wall designs used in general building construction.
[E04B 2/8611] with spacers being embedded in at least one form leaf -- A subclass of the CPC for fixed building constructions that deals with walls or partitions in which spacers are integrated into at least one form leaf during manufacture. It covers methods and devices that embed these spacers to maintain spacing, alignment or insulation within the formed wall panels.
[E04B 2/8617] with spacers being embedded in both form leaves -- The subclass E04B 2/8617 relates to wall or partition constructions in which spacers are embedded in both form leaves of the moulded element. It falls under fixed constructions, building, general building constructions and addresses techniques for creating stable, insulated or ventilated panels by integrating the spacer material within the two sides of the formwork during casting.
[E04B 2/8623] with spacers and at least one form leaf being monolithic -- This subclass covers wall or partition construction that uses spacer elements while at least one form leaf is poured as a single continuous (monolithic) component. The spacers are integrated to maintain spacing and alignment, and the monolithic leaf provides a solid, uninterrupted surface, often enhancing strength or fire resistance.
[E04B 2/8629] with both form leaves and spacers being monolithic -- The code covers wall or partition elements in which the form leaves that shape the wall and the spacers that maintain the cavity are produced as a single, monolithic component. This design provides continuous strength and eliminates joints between separate formwork and spacer parts.
[E04B 2/8635] with ties attached to the inner faces of the forms  E04B2/8611  and  E04B2/8623  take precedence -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Walls made by casting, pouring, or tamping in situ in permanent forms with spacers being embedded in at least one form leaf -> E04B2/8611 - Walls made by casting, pouring, or tamping in situ in permanent forms with spacers and at least one form leaf being monolithic -> E04B2/8623
[E04B 2/8641] using dovetail-type connections -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Connections with dovetail or "T"-shaped grooves in the inner face of the form or the spacer
[E04B 2/8647] with ties going through the forms -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Ties going through the form, e.g. nails or screws going through the forms into the spacers.
[E04B 2/8652] with ties located in the joints of the forms -- This subclass concerns walls or partitions for buildings in which the reinforcing ties are positioned within the joints of the formwork, providing internal connection and stability to the constructed wall elements.
[E04B 2/8658] using wire netting, a lattice or the like as form leaves -- The CPC subclass E04B 2/8658, under Fixed Constructions → Building → General Building Constructions → Walls, partitions for buildings, relates to the use of wire netting, lattice or similar mesh as the form-leaves in wall construction. It covers techniques where the mesh acts as a temporary-or-permanent shaping element for masonry, concrete or other wall materials.
[E04B 2/8664] using flexible material as form leaves  E04B2/8658  takes precedence -- The subclass concerns wall or partition construction that uses flexible material as form leaves, allowing the cast building material to assume the desired shape before the form is removed. It includes methods for placing, supporting and releasing such flexible formwork in building structures.
[E04B 2/88] Curtain walls -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Fixing of glass panes -> E06B3/54
[E04B 2/885] comprising a supporting structure for flush mounted glazing panels -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Connectors for fixing the flush mounted glazing to the supporting structure -> E06B3/54
[E04B 2/90] comprising panels directly attached to the structure -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Panels directly attached to the structure, the panel comprising a frame or not  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Curtain walls comprising a supporting structure for flush mounted glazing panels -> E04B2/885
[E04B 2/92] Sandwich-type panels -- This subclass covers sandwich-type panels used as walls or partitions in buildings. It includes prefabricated panels with at least two outer layers separated by a core material that provides insulation, strength, or fire resistance. These panels are employed in fixed construction for rapid assembly and improved thermal performance.
[E04B 2/94] Concrete panels -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Curtain walls with sandwich-type panels -> E04B2/92
[E04B 2/96] comprising panels attached to the structure through mullions or transoms -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Panels attached with panel clamping bars to the mullions or the transoms.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Curtain walls comprising a supporting structure for flush mounted glazing panels -> E04B2/885 - Glass roofs -> E04D3/06 - Flush mounted glazing, the panels being attached without clamping bars to mullions or transoms -> E06B3/54
[E04B 2/962] with angles or corners in the curtain wall -- E04B 2/962 covers curtain-wall constructions that incorporate angled or corner sections, such as panels, mullions or frames designed to form the meeting point of two wall planes. The code applies to building envelope systems where the geometry includes a change in direction, enabling the creation of building corners, glazed or opaque transitions, and complex façade shapes. It is used to classify patents related to the design, fabrication or installation of these angled curtain-wall components.
[E04B 2/965] Connections of mullions and transoms -- E04B 2/965 covers devices and methods for joining vertical mullions with horizontal transoms in walls or partitions of buildings. It includes mechanical, adhesive, welded or other fastening techniques that secure the framing members, ensure proper alignment and provide structural stability to the assembled construction.
[E04B 2/967] Details of the cross-section of the mullions or transoms  E04B2/965  takes precedence -- The subclass E04B 2/967 concerns the geometry and profile of mullions or transoms within wall systems, describing their cross-sectional shape, dimensions, and any structural or decorative features. It refines the broader category E04B 2/965, which addresses general wall components, by focusing specifically on the detailed design of these vertical or horizontal framing elements.
[E04B 5/00] Floors -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Structural floors for buildings; Building floors construction in general and with regard to insulation; Connections specially adapted to floors.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Producing slab shaped elements of concrete in moulds -> B28B - Decks for ships -> B63B3/48 - Flat foundations, floors resting on the ground -> E02D27/00 - Girders for floors -> E04C3/00 - Flooring as finishing work, insulation of flooring, sectional false floors, e.g. for computers -> E04F15/00 - Decks, e.g. garden decks -> E04F15/00 - Disposable shutterings, forms for floors -> E04G
[E04B 5/02] Load-carrying floor structures formed substantially of prefabricated units -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Decks, e.g. garden decks -> E04F15/00 - Sectional false floors -> E04F15/024
[E04B 5/023] Separate connecting devices for prefabricated floor-slabs -- This subclass covers devices that are installed separately to join prefabricated floor-slabs, such as mechanical connectors, plates, bolts or other fastening elements, enabling the slabs to be linked after manufacturing. It relates to building construction methods for floors within the broader category of fixed constructions.
[E04B 5/026] with beams or slabs of plastic -- E04B 5/026 covers floor constructions in which the supporting elements or slabs are made of plastic, including beams, joists or flat panels of polymer material used as the primary structural component of a floor system. It falls under Fixed Constructions → Building → General Building Constructions → Floors.
[E04B 5/04] with beams of concrete or other stone-like material, e.g. asbestos cement -- A floor construction that includes beams formed from concrete, asbestos-cement or other stone-like materials, used as fixed structural components within a building. This falls under the CPC hierarchy of Fixed Constructions → Building → General Building Constructions → Floors, code E04B 5/04.
[E04B 5/043] having elongated hollow cores -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Load-carrying floor structures consisting of elongated hollow cores slabs, e.g. placed against one another.  **Glossary:** - Hollow core slab: Hollow core slab is a precast slab of prestressed concrete, having tubular voids extending the full length of the slab
[E04B 5/046] with beams placed with distance from another -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Load-carrying floor structures formed of prefabricated beams placed with distance from one another, the space between the beams being filled or covered by prefabricated elements and eventually completed by non-structural layers of concrete or mortar..  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Load carrying floor structures formed substantially of prefabricated units, and assembled of block shaped elements -> E04B5/08 - Load-carrying floor structures partly cast in situ with prefabricated stiffening ribs -> E04B5/23 - E04B5/29
[E04B 5/06] placed against one another -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Load-carrying floor structures consisting of elongated slabs placed against one another -> E04B5/04 - Load-carrying floor structures consisting of elongated hollow cores slabs placed against one another -> E04B5/043
[E04B 5/08] assembled of block-shaped elements, e.g. hollow stones -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Load carrying floor structures assembled of prefabricated block-shaped elements, e.g. joined together by mortar, tensioning or connecting elements.
[E04B 5/10] with metal beams or girders, e.g. with steel lattice girders -- E04B 5/10 covers floor structures that incorporate metal beams or girders, including steel lattice girders, as the primary supporting elements. It falls under the hierarchy of Fixed Constructions - Building - General Building Constructions - Floors.
[E04B 5/12] with wooden beams -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Also means for supporting beams.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Load-carrying floor structures formed substantially of prefabricated units with beams or girders laid in two directions -> E04B5/14
[E04B 5/14] with beams or girders laid in two directions -- E04B 5/14 classifies floor constructions in which the supporting beams or girders are placed in two orthogonal directions, forming a grid that distributes loads across the surface. It applies to building floors built with intersecting longitudinal and transverse members, enabling greater stiffness and load-bearing capacity.
[E04B 5/16] Load-carrying floor structures wholly or partly cast or similarly formed -- The CPC code E04B 5/16 classifies load-carrying floor structures that are wholly or partly cast, poured or otherwise formed in place as part of a building's fixed construction. It applies to floors that serve as integral structural elements within general building constructions.
[E04B 5/17] Floor structures partly formed -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Floor structures partly formed in situ, i.e. in which at least some parts of the structure are prefabricated, e.g. precast girders or prefabricated filling members between the beams.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Floor structures cast in situ with hollow cylindrical or spherical filling elements -> E04B5/32 - Floor structures cast in situ with form units as part of the floor; with slab shaped form units acting as reinforcement -> E04B5/36 , E04B5/38
[E04B 5/18] with stiffening ribs or other beam-like formations wholly cast between filling members -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Floor structures partly formed in situ, with prefabricated filling members and with stiffening ribs wholly cast between or in the prefabricated filling members; Prefabricated filling elements suitable for casting stiffening ribs between or in them.
[E04B 5/19] the filling members acting as self-supporting permanent forms -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Floor structures partly formed in situ, with prefabricated self-supporting filling members and with stiffening ribs wholly cast between or in the prefabricated self-supporting filling members, e.g. elongated self-supporting filling members resting on the walls.
[E04B 5/21] Cross-ribbed floors -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Floor structures partly formed in situ, with prefabricated filling members and with stiffening ribs wholly cast between or in the prefabricated filling members, and extending in two different and intersecting directions. Prefabricated filling elements suitable for casting cross-ribbed floors.
[E04B 5/23] with stiffening ribs or other beam-like formations wholly or partly prefabricated -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Floor structures partly formed in situ, with prefabricated or partly prefabricated beam-like formations, e.g. prefabricated beams of concrete, metal or wood.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - With all load-carrying parts substantially consisting of prefabricated units -> E04B5/02
[E04B 5/26] with filling members between the beams -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Floor structures partly formed in situ, with prefabricated or partly prefabricated beam-like formations and with prefabricated filling members between the beams;  Prefabricated elements for use as filling elements between prefabricated or partly prefabricated beams.
[E04B 5/261] Monolithic filling members -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Floor structures partly formed in situ, with prefabricated or partly prefabricated beam-like formations and with prefabricated monolithic filling members between the beams;  Prefabricated monolithic elements for use as filling elements between prefabricated or partly prefabricated beams.  **Glossary:** - Monolithic: In one piece
[E04B 5/263] with a flat lower surface  E04B5/265  takes precedence -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Load-carrying floor structures partly cast in situ with prefabricated or partly prefabricated beams and one or more hollow cores between the beams -> E04B5/265
[E04B 5/265] with one or more hollow cores -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Load-carrying floor structures partly cast in situ with prefabricated or partly prefabricated beams and one or more hollow cores between the beams
[E04B 5/266] Filling members covering the undersurface of the beams -- E04B 5/266 falls under fixed floor constructions in building work and concerns filling members that are positioned on the undersurface of beams. These components serve to create a level surface, distribute loads, or provide support for subsequent floor layers.
[E04B 5/268] End filling members to be placed between the floor and the wall, e.g. thermal breaks -- E04B 5/268 relates to end-filling members that are installed between a floor slab and the adjoining wall, serving to fill the joint and often providing a thermal break to reduce heat transfer. These components seal the connection while allowing differential movement and improving energy efficiency.
[E04B 5/28] Cross-ribbed floors -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Floor structures partly formed in situ, with prefabricated or partly prefabricated beam-like formations and with prefabricated filling members between the beams;  Prefabricated elements for use as filling elements between prefabricated or partly prefabricated beams in a cross-ribbed floor.
[E04B 5/29] the prefabricated parts of the beams consisting wholly of metal -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Beams, as independent entities, consisting of steel and concrete -> E04C3/293 - Beams, as independent entities, consisting of concrete combined with a girder-like steel structure extending laterally outside the element -> E04C3/294
[E04B 5/32] Floor structures wholly cast -- This subclass covers floor systems that are formed by casting the entire structural element in place, such as concrete slabs poured on site without pre-formed components. It includes monolithic cast floors, in-situ concrete plates and similar wholly cast floor constructions used in building.
[E04B 5/326] with hollow filling elements -- E04B 5/326 classifies floor constructions that incorporate hollow filling elements such as void-forming blocks, hollow bricks or prefabricated hollow panels. These elements reduce weight, provide insulation or allow services to run within the floor. The code is used for patents describing methods or structures where the floor incorporates such hollow components.
[E04B 5/328] the filling elements being spherical -- The CPC code E04B 5/328 designates floor structures in which the filler material consists of spherical elements, such as balls or globes, used to occupy voids, provide cushioning or reduce weight in the floor assembly. It applies to any building floor where the primary filling components are spherical in shape.
[E04B 5/36] with form units as part of the floor -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Floor structures wholly cast in situ with form units as part of the floor, the form units being either made in one piece or made of different elements, e.g. non-self-supporting form units.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Sectional false floors -> E04F15/024  **Glossary:** - Floor form unit: A form, either made of one element or of a series of elements, that allows the floor to be cast over it, in situ, without the need of additional forms.
[E04B 5/38] with slab-shaped form units acting simultaneously as reinforcement -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Floor structures wholly cast in situ with slab-shaped substantially self-supporting form units acting simultaneously as reinforcement;  Form slabs with reinforcements of metal or other material extending laterally outside the form slab, and that are suitable to be embedded in the concrete of the floor slab to be cast in-situ, e.g. "Predalles" concrete slabs or semi-finished reinforced concrete slabs.
[E04B 5/40] with metal form-slabs -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Floor structures wholly cast in situ with metal form-slabs, e.g. with corrugated or non-corrugated metal form-slabs.
[E04B 5/43] Floor structures of extraordinary design -- Floor structures of extraordinary design cover non-standard or novel floor systems that depart from conventional construction methods, incorporating unusual geometry, materials, or load-bearing concepts for aesthetic, functional or specialized purposes. These designs are typically employed when typical slab or joist arrangements are insufficient or when a distinctive architectural effect is required.
[E04B 5/44] Floors composed of stones, mortar, and reinforcing elements -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Floors composed of stones, e.g. hollow bricks, mortar and reinforcing elements.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Special adaptation of floors for transmission of light, e.g. by inserts of glass -> E04B5/46
[E04B 5/46] Special adaptation of floors for transmission of light, e.g. by inserts of glass -- E04B 5/46 covers floor constructions that are modified to allow light to pass through, such as sections of glass, translucent panels or other light-transmitting inserts integrated into a floor surface. The class is used for inventions that provide illumination, visual continuity or daylighting through floor areas within buildings.
[E04B 5/48] Special adaptations of floors for incorporating ducts, e.g. for heating or ventilating -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Special adaptations of floors for incorporating ducts, e.g. for heating or ventilating, in the structural body of the floor.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Sectional false floors -> E04F15/024
[E04B 7/00] Roofs -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Roofs, i.e. roof structures; Roof construction with regard to insulation, e.g. self-supporting insulated roof-slabs.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Structures for roofs, in particular for flat roofs, as well as for floors -> E04B5/00 - Ceilings, i.e. suspended ceilings -> E04B9/00 - Greenhouses -> A01G9/14 - Large containers having floating covers -> B65D88/34 - Roof trusses per se, truss-like structures -> E04C3/02 - Roof coverings -> E04D
[E04B 7/02] with plane sloping surfaces, e.g. saddle roofs -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Roofs formed in bays, e.g. saw-tooth roofs -> E04B7/12
[E04B 7/022] consisting of a plurality of parallel similar trusses or portal frames -- E04B 7/022 classifies roof structures that are built from a series of parallel, similar trusses or portal frames, used in fixed building constructions. It covers arrangements where multiple identical or alike frames support the roof, providing a uniform structural system across the span.
[E04B 7/024] the trusses or frames supporting load-bearing purlins, e.g. braced purlins  battens, supports for battens  E04D12/00
[E04B 7/026] consisting of prefabricated modules, e.g. box-like or cell-like units  E04B7/24  takes precedence -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Roof consisting of prefabricated modules, e.g. box-like or cell-like units, the prefabricated modules arriving in situ ready to be put in place as they are.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Roofs consisting of self-supporting slabs, the slabs being collapsible or retractable, e.g. for transport -> E04B7/24
[E04B 7/028] consisting of structures of pyramidal or conical shape -- E04B 7/028 covers fixed roof constructions that are formed as pyramidal or conical shapes, applied in building structures. It falls under the hierarchy of Fixed Constructions → Building → General Building Constructions → Roofs, and is used to classify patents for roof designs featuring a tapering, pointed geometry.
[E04B 7/04] supported by horizontal beams or the equivalent resting on the walls -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Details of connections between the roof structure and the supporting walls, for roofs in which the roof structure is supported by the walls, e.g. roof trusses supported by horizontal beams resting on the walls.
[E04B 7/045] with connectors made of sheet metal for connecting the roof structure to the supporting wall  connectors made of sheet metal for wooden elongated load-supporting elements in general  E04B1/2608 -- E04B 7/045 covers roof constructions that use sheet-metal connectors to join the roof framework to the supporting wall, focusing on the attachment of wooden elongated load-bearing members. The entry details these metal fasteners as a means of securing the roof structure in general building construction.
[E04B 7/06] Constructions of roof intersections or hipped ends -- **Glossary:** - Hipped end: The ridge end or node defined by the junction of three or more triangular sloping faces in a hip roof
[E04B 7/063] Hipped ends -- The code E04B 7/063 covers hipped ends, which are roof sections where the end walls slope downward from the ridge to the eaves, forming a hip instead of a vertical gable. This design provides uniform water runoff and greater structural stability for roofs with a hipped shape.
[E04B 7/08] Vaulted roofs -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Vaulted roofs, i.e. roofs having a general curved shape, in one or more directions, e.g. an arched shape, an hyperbolic-parabolic shape, a dome shape, a curved and folded shape.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Suspended roofs -> E04B7/14 - Inflatable tents or canopies, in general -> E04H15/20
[E04B 7/10] Shell structures, e.g. of hyperbolic-parabolic shape -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Curved roof structures made by shell structures, e.g. roof structures of hyperbolic-parabolic shape made of a thin-shell concrete membrane, or by a plurality of thin-shell units connected together;  Curved roof structures made by grid-like formations acting as shell structures, i.e. thin and substantially self-supporting, that is without the need of intermediate supports;  Curved roof structures made by a folded structure, e.g. globally curved roof structures made by concertina-like folded metal sheets.
[E04B 7/102] Shell structures -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Curved roof structures made by shell structures, e.g. roof structures of hyperbolic-parabolic shape made of a thin-shell concrete membrane, or by a plurality of thin-shell units connected together.
[E04B 7/105] Grid-like structures -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Curved roof structures made by grid-like formations acting as shell structures, i.e. thin and substantially self-supporting, that is without the need of intermediate supports.
[E04B 7/107] Folded structures -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Curved roof structures made by a folded structure, e.g. globally curved roof structures made by concertina-like folded metal sheets.
[E04B 7/12] formed in bays, e.g. sawtooth roofs -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Roofs formed in bays, e.g. massive saw-tooth roofs for factory buildings.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Curved roof structures made by a folded structure, e.g. globally curved roof structures made by concertina-like folded metal sheets -> E04B7/107
[E04B 7/14] Suspended roofs -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Suspended roofs, i.e. permanent roofs suspended from a structure, e.g. a roof for a stadium consisting of a membrane suspended by cables, or a roof for a building consisting of a membrane being anchored along its borders on the walls of the building.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Suspended tents or canopies in general -> E04H15/04
[E04B 7/16] Roof structures with movable roof parts -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Skylights or domes having movable parts -> E04D13/035
[E04B 7/163] characterised by a pivoting movement of the movable roof parts  E04B7/166  takes precedence -- E04B 7/163 is a CPC subclass for roofs whose movable sections operate by a pivoting movement, such as hinged or louvered elements that swing about an axis. It falls under Fixed Constructions → Building → General Building Constructions → Roofs and is superseded by the more specific subclass E04B 7/166.
[E04B 7/166] characterised by a translation movement of the movable roof part, with or without additional movements -- The CPC entry E04B 7/166 refers to roofs in which the moving portion operates by a translation, i.e., a sliding motion, and may also incorporate other movements such as rotation or tilt. It is used for classifying fixed-construction building roofs that open by sliding rather than hinging.
[E04B 7/18] Special structures in or on roofs, e.g. dormer windows -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Special structures on roofs, in connection with roof coverings -> E04D13/00 - Skylights and domes -> E04D13/03 - Chimneys -> E04F17/00  **Glossary:** - Dormer window: Roof window set in a structure that is protruding from the surface of a sloping roof; the window itself is substantially vertical
[E04B 7/20] Roofs consisting of self-supporting slabs, e.g. able to be loaded -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Roofs consisting of self-supporting slabs, e.g. a saddle roof in which each plane sloping surface is made by a series of adjacent self-supporting slabs resting only on the walls of the building and on the ridge beam of the roof.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Waterproof roofing slabs comprising two or more layers, e.g. for insulation -> E04D3/35
[E04B 7/205] the slabs having non-structural supports for roofing materials  for slaps having insulating properties  E04B7/225 -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Self-supporting insulating slabs for roofs, the slabs having non-structural supports for roofing materials -> E04B7/225 - Roof battens -> E04D12/004  **Glossary:** - Non-structural supports for roofing materials: Supports like battens, purlins
[E04B 7/22] the slabs having insulating properties, e.g. laminated with layers of insulating material -- This code covers roof slabs that incorporate insulation, such as laminated panels with layers of insulating material, used in fixed building constructions.
[E04B 7/225] the slabs having non-structural supports for roofing materials -- **Glossary:** - Non-structural supports for roofing materials: Supports like battens, purlins
[E04B 7/24] the slabs being collapsible , e.g. for transport -- This subclass of roofs covers roof slabs that can be folded, disassembled or otherwise collapsed for easier transport and later re-assembly on site. It applies to temporary, modular or relocatable building structures where the slab elements are designed to be reduced in size or shape for moving.
[E04B 9/00] Ceilings -- **Definition:** This place covers:  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Ceilings used as forms for making floors -> E04B5/00 - Coverings or linings for ceilings, directly attached to the lower face of the floor slab -> E04F13/00 - Plastering, finishing work -> E04F21/00
[E04B 9/001] characterised by provisions for heat or sound insulation -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Suspended ceilings or false ceilings having provisions for heat or sound insulation.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Sound absorbing open-work ceilings; Ceilings with acoustic baffles, e.g. sound absorbing panels vertically suspended from the ceiling -> E04B9/34
[E04B 9/003] with movable parts, e.g. pivoting panels, access doors -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Access doors mechanically connected to loft ladders -> E04F11/04
[E04B 9/005] composed of imitation beams or beam coverings -- E04B 9/005 covers ceiling systems that use imitation beams or beam coverings, where non-structural elements are arranged to resemble real beams for decorative or aesthetic purposes.
[E04B 9/006] with means for hanging lighting fixtures or other appliances to the framework of the ceiling -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Suspended or false ceilings with means for hanging lighting fixtures or other appliances to the framework of the suspended or false ceiling, e.g. to the T-bars of a suspended ceiling.
[E04B 9/008] with means for connecting partition walls or panels to the ceilings -- E04B 9/008 covers ceiling constructions that include devices or fittings for attaching partition walls or panels directly to the ceiling structure. It pertains to fixed building elements where the means of connection are integrated into the ceiling to support interior partitions.
[E04B 9/02] having means for ventilation or vapour discharge -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Suspended or false ceilings having means for ventilation or vapour discharge.
[E04B 9/023] comprising drainage gutters for condensed water or grease -- The subclass E04B 9/023 relates to fixed ceiling constructions that incorporate drainage gutters intended to collect and channel condensed water or grease. It covers built-in gutter systems forming part of the ceiling structure, enabling the removal of liquids from the interior space.
[E04B 9/04] comprising slabs, sheets or the like -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Suspended or false ceilings comprising slabs, sheets or the like, e.g. suspended ceiling comprising inverted tee-bars and panels.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Open-work ceilings, e.g. grids -> E04B9/34 - E04B9/36
[E04B 9/0407] being stiff and curved -- The code E04B 9/0407 covers ceiling structures that are stiff and curved, such as vaulted, arched or rib-reinforced panels. It relates to fixed-construction ceiling elements designed to bear loads while providing a curved surface for architectural or functional purposes.
[E04B 9/0414] being foldable, curvable or rollable -- E04B 9/0414 classifies ceiling elements that can be folded, curved or rolled for easy transport, storage or installation. It includes retractable or collapsible ceiling panels, flexible membranes and modular ceiling systems designed to be reshaped or compacted without permanent alteration, covering both temporary and permanent building applications.
[E04B 9/0421] comprising ducts -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Ceiling heating, cooling and ventilating systems in general -> F24D , F24F
[E04B 9/0428] having a closed frame around the periphery -- The CPC subclass E04B 9/0428 covers ceiling constructions that incorporate a continuous closed frame surrounding the entire perimeter of the ceiling surface. The frame encloses the edge of the ceiling, providing structural support, sealing, and often an aesthetic trim. This feature is typical of suspended or fixed ceiling systems where the peripheral border is fully bounded.
[E04B 9/0435] having connection means at the edges -- E04B 9/0435 relates to ceiling elements that incorporate means for joining at their perimeters, such as clips, brackets, interlocking profiles or other fastening devices that connect the ceiling to adjacent structural parts. This subclass addresses the provision of edge connections used to secure or integrate the ceiling within a building's fixed construction.
[E04B 9/0442] having a honeycomb core -- E04B 9/0442 covers ceiling constructions that incorporate a honeycomb core, a lightweight cellular structure used to provide rigidity and insulation. The honeycomb core is positioned between facing sheets to create a sandwich panel suitable for fixed building ceilings. This subcategory falls under general building constructions for fixed ceiling systems.
[E04B 9/045] being laminated -- The subclass E04B 9/045 concerns ceiling constructions that are formed by laminating one or more layers of material to create a unified panel or surface. It includes methods, structures and devices for producing laminated ceilings used in fixed building constructions. This classification falls under general building constructions and specifically addresses laminated ceiling systems.
[E04B 9/0457] having closed internal cavities -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Ceiling panels having a honeycomb core structure -> E04B9/0442
[E04B 9/0464] having irregularities on the faces, e.g. holes, grooves -- The code E04B 9/0464 covers ceiling constructions that incorporate surface irregularities such as holes, grooves or other depressions, providing aesthetic texture or functional openings. It falls under Fixed Constructions → Building → General Building Constructions → Ceilings.
[E04B 9/0471] having a trapezoidal cross section -- A ceiling element that is formed with a trapezoidal cross-section, used in fixed building constructions. The shape provides structural or aesthetic benefits such as improved load distribution or visual effect, and falls under the general building constructions category of the Cooperative Patent Classification system.
[E04B 9/0478] of the tray type -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Ceiling panels consisting of a tray made of a single element, and having 1 to 4 upstanding borders on their sides.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Ceiling panels having a frame -> E04B9/0428
[E04B 9/0485] containing a filling element -- The classification E04B 9/0485 pertains to ceiling constructions that incorporate a filling element, such as panels or modules containing insulating, acoustic or decorative inserts. It falls under fixed building constructions for general building work, specifically ceilings that are built with a built-in filler component.
[E04B 9/06] characterised by constructional features of the supporting construction -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Suspended or false ceilings characterised by constructional features of the supporting construction, e.g. by the cross section or the material of T-bar members.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Constructional features of the means for suspending the supporting construction -> E04B9/18 , E04B9/20
[E04B 9/061] supporting construction for curved ceilings -- E04B 9/061 refers to structures that support or reinforce curved ceiling systems in buildings, such as ribs, beams, or frames that maintain the shape and load distribution of non-flat ceilings. It falls under fixed constructions for ceilings within general building construction.
[E04B 9/064] comprising extruded supporting beams -- E04B 9/064 covers ceiling constructions that incorporate extruded supporting beams as part of the fixed building structure. The classification pertains to ceilings in which the load-bearing members are formed by extrusion processes, providing integrated support within the ceiling assembly. This falls under the hierarchy of building, general building constructions, and fixed constructions.
[E04B 9/065] comprising supporting beams having a folded cross-section -- E04B 9/065 is a subclass of the Cooperative Patent Classification covering building constructions, specifically ceilings that incorporate supporting beams with a folded cross-section. This design provides increased strength and stability while allowing efficient use of material in fixed ceiling structures.
[E04B 9/067] with inverted T-shaped cross-section -- This CPC subclass covers ceiling structures that incorporate a profile having an inverted T-shaped cross-section, typically used to form fixed building constructions such as suspended or supported ceiling panels. It falls under the hierarchy Fixed Constructions → Building → General Building Constructions → Ceilings and is identified by code E04B 9/067.
[E04B 9/068] with double web -- E04B 9/068 designates ceiling structures that incorporate a double-web system, meaning two parallel load-bearing panels or plates linked together to form a composite slab. The double-web arrangement is used to increase stiffness, distribute loads and permit longer spans in fixed building constructions.
[E04B 9/08] having the capability of expansion -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Suspended or false ceilings comprising a supporting construction having the capability of expansion.
[E04B 9/10] Connections between parallel members of the supporting construction -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Connections between parallel members of the supporting construction, e.g. connections side to side of T-bars or connections tip to tip with an additional joining element  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Suspended or false ceilings comprising a supporting construction having the capability of expansion. -> E04B9/08
[E04B 9/12] Connections between non-parallel members of the supporting construction -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Suspended or false ceilings comprising a supporting construction having the capability of expansion. -> E04B9/08
[E04B 9/122] one member passing through the other member, both members laying at least partly in the same plane -- The classification E04B 9/122 covers ceiling constructions where one structural member passes through another, with both members positioned at least partially in the same plane. This arrangement typically involves intersecting joists, beams or bars that are aligned to share a common plane while one member penetrates the other, facilitating joint strength or routing of services. It applies to fixed building components in general ceiling assemblies.
[E04B 9/125] both members being continuous members -- E04B 9/125 refers to ceiling constructions where the two primary supporting members run continuously across the span without intermediate joints, creating an uninterrupted structural line for the ceiling. This design is used to enhance load distribution and stiffness in general building ceiling systems.
[E04B 9/127] one member being discontinuous and abutting against the other member -- E04B 9/127 relates to ceiling constructions where one structural member is discontinuous and is placed directly abutting another member, such as a separate panel or strip joined to a solid ceiling component. The classification addresses the method of joining or supporting these non-continuous elements without overlapping joints.
[E04B 9/14] all the members being discontinuous and laying at least partly in the same plane -- E04B 9/14 covers ceiling constructions whose structural members are separated rather than continuous, yet positioned so that they lie partly in the same plane. The classification applies to systems such as tiled, panelled or modular suspended ceilings where individual elements are discrete but collectively form a planar surface.
[E04B 9/16] the members lying in different planes -- This CPC subclass refers to ceiling constructions where the structural members such as beams, ribs or trusses are arranged on different planes rather than a single flat layer. It includes designs where elements are staggered or offset vertically to form stepped, double-layer or multi-level ceiling systems. Used for describing non-planar ceiling frameworks in building construction.
[E04B 9/18] Means for suspending the supporting construction -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Means for suspending the supporting construction of the suspended ceiling from the lower face of the floor slab.
[E04B 9/183] having a lower side adapted to be connected to a channel of the supporting construction -- E04B 9/183 covers ceiling components whose underside is shaped to engage with a channel of the supporting structure, allowing the element to be fastened or inserted into the channel for stable installation. This design facilitates easy mounting and alignment of suspended or fixed ceilings.
[E04B 9/20] adjustable -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Adjustable means for suspending the supporting construction of the suspended ceiling from the lower face of the floor slab, particularly means adjustable in length.
[E04B 9/205] by means of a resilient clip -- A ceiling construction that is secured to the supporting structure using a resilient clip, which provides flexible attachment to accommodate movement, vibration or thermal expansion while maintaining the ceiling's position. This method falls under fixed building constructions for general ceiling installations.
[E04B 9/22] Connection of slabs, sheets or the like to the supporting construction -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Connections of substantially horizontal slabs, sheets or the like to the supporting construction of a suspended or false ceiling.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Open-work ceilings, e.g. grids -> E04B9/34
[E04B 9/225] with the slabs, panels, sheets or the like hanging at a distance below the supporting construction -- This subclass covers ceiling constructions where slabs, panels, sheets or similar elements are suspended below a supporting structure, creating a space between the ceiling surface and the support. It includes systems that use hangers, wires or other means to achieve the hanging arrangement.
[E04B 9/24] with the slabs, sheets or the like positioned on , or held against horizontal flanges of the supporting construction -- This CPC subclass covers ceiling structures in which slabs, sheets or similar elements are laid on, or fastened to, the horizontal flanges of the supporting framework. It falls under fixed building constructions, specifically general building construction ceilings.
[E04B 9/241] with the slabs,  panels, sheets or the like positioned on the upperside of the horizontal flanges of the supporting construction -- This subgroup concerns ceiling constructions where the covering elements such as slabs, panels or sheets are mounted on the upper side of the horizontal flanges of the supporting framework. It applies to fixed ceiling systems in which the load-bearing members carry the finishing material from above, falling under the broader categories of fixed constructions, building and general building constructions.
[E04B 9/242] with separate retaining elements -- This group covers ceiling systems in which the supporting or retaining components-such as beams, joists, trusses or brackets-are provided as separate elements independent of the ceiling covering, allowing the ceiling to be suspended or fixed by these distinct members.
