[E04B 9/244] comprising sealing means between the supporting construction and the slabs, panels, sheets or the like -- The code E04B 9/244 covers ceiling constructions that incorporate sealing elements between the supporting framework and the overlying slabs, panels, sheets or similar components, ensuring a watertight or airtight joint. It applies to general building ceilings where the seal prevents the passage of fluids, gases or fire through the interface. This classification falls under Fixed Constructions - Building - General Building Constructions - Ceilings.
[E04B 9/245] by means of screws, bolts or clamping strips held against the underside of the supporting construction  E04B9/26  takes precedence
[E04B 9/247] by means of sliding or pivoting locking elements, held against the underside of the supporting construction -- This CPC subclass refers to ceiling systems installed using sliding or pivoting locking components that are secured to the underside of the supporting structure. It covers methods and devices that lock ceiling panels in place without permanent fasteners, allowing easy assembly or removal.
[E04B 9/248] by means of permanent magnetic force held against the underside of the supporting construction -- E04B 9/248 covers ceiling installation methods that rely on permanent magnetic forces to hold panels or components against the underside of the supporting beam or slab. This approach replaces conventional fasteners, enabling reversible or adjustable mounting in fixed building constructions.
[E04B 9/26] by means of snap action of elastically deformable elements -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Connections of substantially horizontal slabs, sheets or the like to the supporting construction of a suspended or false ceiling by means of snap action of elastically deformable elements held against the underside of the supporting construction.
[E04B 9/28] with the slabs, sheets or the like having grooves engaging with horizontal flanges of the supporting construction or accessory means connected thereto -- This subclass covers fixed ceiling constructions where slabs, sheets or similar elements are formed with grooves that engage horizontal flanges of the supporting framework or attached accessory components, defining a specific method of joining ceiling panels in general building construction.
[E04B 9/30] characterised by edge details of the ceiling -- E04B 9/30 designates ceiling constructions in which the defining characteristic is the treatment of the perimeter, such as mouldings, trims or other edge details that shape the visual or functional profile of the ceiling. It is classified under fixed constructions in building, within the general building constructions category.
[E04B 9/303] for flexible tensioned membranes -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Suspended or false ceilings comprising a flexible tensioned membrane secured to the supporting construction and/or to the adjacent walls.
[E04B 9/306] Tensioning hand tools therefor -- E04B 9/306 covers hand-operated devices used to apply or adjust tension in ceiling constructions, such as tools for tightening tension rods, cables or support systems that hold plasterboard or other ceiling elements in place. These tools enable precise tensioning during installation or maintenance of fixed building ceilings.
[E04B 9/32] Translucent ceilings, i.e. permitting both the transmission and diffusion of light -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Suspended or false ceilings comprising a flexible tensioned membrane secured to the supporting construction and/or to the adjacent walls -> E04B9/303 - Grid-like or open-work ceilings, e.g. lattice type ceilings -> E04B9/34 - Lighting -> F21
[E04B 9/34] open-work ceilings, e.g. lattice type  -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Details of ceiling edges -> E04B9/30
[E04B 9/345] consisting of non-parallel slats, e.g. grids -- **Glossary:** - Slat: A long, thin, flat piece of lumber, metal or plastic.
[E04B 9/36] consisting of parallel slats -- In the CPC system, code E04B 9/36 covers ceiling constructions formed from a series of parallel slats. The slats are fixed to a supporting framework and used in building interiors for acoustic, decorative, or structural purposes. It falls under the hierarchy of fixed constructions, building, general building constructions, ceilings.
[E04B 9/363] the principal plane of the slats being horizontal -- The code E04B 9/363 classifies ceiling constructions that incorporate slatted components whose principal plane is installed horizontally. It relates to fixed building ceilings where the slats run parallel to the floor, typically for acoustic or decorative purposes. This falls within the broader group of general building constructions for ceilings.
[E04B 9/366] the principal plane of the slats being vertical -- A ceiling structure in which the slats are arranged so that the main surface of each slat stands vertically, rather than horizontally, creating a vertical-plane lattice often used for acoustic control or decorative effect. This configuration falls under the CPC subclass for general building constructions relating to ceilings.
[E04C] STRUCTURAL ELEMENTS -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Structural elongated elements for buildings, prefabricated or not, as individual structural elements per se, e.g. pillars, columns, beams, girders, trusses;  Two-dimensional prefabricated building elements for the construction of parts of buildings, like sheets, slabs or panels, mainly for walls or partitions;  Apparatus for handling smaller elements or hardenable material for the realization of two-dimensional concrete or stone-like prefabricated elements;  Bricks or block shaped elements, but only if having special adaptations, like e.g. serving for locating conduits, or being transparent, or being built-up from parts of different materials;  Concrete reinforcements, of metal or other materials, e.g. rebars, stirrups, reinforcing grids or cages, reinforcing fibres, connectors and spacers for reinforcements  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Compositions of concrete, mortars or like building materials -> C04B - Structural elements for bridges -> E01D - Building foundations -> E02D - Structural elements specially designed for insulation or other protection -> E04B - Building construction in general -> E04B1/00 - Load-bearing walls made from panels -> E04B1/02 - E04B1/14 - Load- and non-load-bearing walls made up of layers of building elements, e.g. brick walls; bricks or block shaped building elements for walls -> E04B2/02 - E04B2/54 - Other load-bearing walls -> E04B2/56 - E04B2/709 and E04B2/84 - E04B2/8664 - Other non-load-bearing walls; Curtain walls -> E04B2/72 - E04B2/8664 ; E04B2/88 - E04B2/967 - Floor structures -> E04B5/00 - Roof structures -> E04B7/00 - Suspended or false ceilings -> E04B9/00 - Roof coverings -> E04D - Finishing work on building, e.g. lining, covering, flooring -> E04F - Panels for lining or finishing -> E04F13/00 - Elements used as building aids, e.g. scaffolding, forms -> E04G - Buildings or like structures for particular purposes, e.g. small temporary buildings, waiting shelters, telephone cabinets -> E04H - Structural elements for mining -> E21 - Structural elements for tunnels -> E21D - Structural elements with broader range of application than for building engineering -> F16
[E04C 1/00] Building elements of block or other shape for the construction of parts of buildings -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Bricks or block shaped elements for the construction of parts of buildings, but only if having special adaptations, like e.g. serving for locating conduits, or being made-up of different materials, e.g. stones with insulating inserts, or being made-up of transparent materials, e.g. glass bricks  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Building elements of relatively thin form, e.g. panels -> E04C2/00 - E04C2/546 - Structural elongated elements designed for load-supporting, e.g. beams, column or pillars -> E04C3/00 - E04C3/46
[E04C 1/24] Elements for building-up floors, ceilings, roofs, arches, or beams  E04C1/39  -  E04C1/42  take precedence; flooring  E04F15/00 -- E04C 1/24 classifies building elements of block or other shaped material used to construct structural parts of buildings such as floors, ceilings, roofs, arches or beams, with specific sub-groups E04C 1/39 to 1/42 taking precedence for flooring. It falls under the Cooperative Patent Classification hierarchy of Fixed Constructions → Building → Structural Elements.
[E04C 1/34] designed for use as filling elements -- These are prefabricated building components shaped as blocks or other forms that are intended to fill voids or spaces within structural elements of a building, serving functions such as reinforcement, insulation or partitioning. The design focuses on their use as filling elements in walls, floors or other parts of a construction.
[E04C 1/36] between joists or girders -- The E04C 1/36 subclass covers structural components that are installed in the space between joists or girders in a building framework. It includes floor panels, bridging members, or any intermediate elements that span or fill the gap between these primary load-bearing members to provide support, rigidity, or utility integration.
[E04C 1/38] in ribbed or cross-ribbed floors, ceilings, or roofs consisting of reinforced concrete -- E04C 1/38 relates to reinforced-concrete building elements that form ribbed or cross-ribbed floors, ceilings or roofs. The subclass covers fixed construction components shaped as blocks or similar forms, designed to provide structural strength and rigidity in the horizontal members of buildings.
[E04C 1/39] characterised by special adaptations, e.g. serving for locating conduits, for forming soffits, cornices, or shelves, for fixing wall-plates or door-frames, for claustra -- **Glossary:** - Claustra: Open-work wall
[E04C 1/392] for ventilating, heating or cooling -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Building elements of block or other shape having openings or adaptations of relatively large section for ventilating, heating or cooling.
[E04C 1/395] for claustra, fences, planting walls, e.g. sound-absorbing  pots for vertical horticulture  A01G9/022 -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Pots for vertical horticulture -> A01G9/022
[E04C 1/397] serving for locating conduits  E04C1/392  takes precedence -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Building elements of block or other shape having openings or adaptations of relatively small section for locating conduits
[E04C 1/40] built-up from parts of different materials, e.g. composed of layers of different materials or stones with filling material or with insulating inserts -- E04C 1/40 classifies building elements that are constructed from different materials, such as layers of stones combined with filler or insulating inserts, forming composite parts used in the structural construction of buildings.
[E04C 1/41] composed of insulating material and load-bearing concrete, stone or stone-like material -- E04C 1/41 classifies building blocks or shaped structural elements that integrate an insulating layer with a load-bearing core of concrete, stone or stone-like material, such as insulated concrete forms or composite wall units used in constructing walls, floors or other parts of a building.
[E04C 1/42] of glass or other transparent material -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Slab-like translucent elements made of glass bricks -> E04C2/546
[E04C 2/00] Building elements of relatively thin form for the construction of parts of buildings, e.g. sheet materials, slabs, or panels -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Two-dimensional prefabricated building elements for the construction of parts of buildings, i.e. building elements in which one dimension is small in comparison to the other two, like e.g. sheets, slabs or panels, mainly used for walls or partitions  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Building elements of relatively thin form made in situ -> E04B - Load-carrying floor structures, e.g. load-carrying prefabricated floor slabs -> E04B5/02 , E04B5/16 - Roof or like slab shaped covering elements -> E04D3/00
[E04C 2002/001] Mechanical features of panels -- E04C 2002/001 groups inventions dealing with the mechanical characteristics of panels used in construction, such as hinges, brackets, locking or sealing devices, reinforcement ribs and mounting arrangements that allow the panel to be positioned, adjusted, secured or removed within a building structure.
[E04C 2002/002] Panels with integrated lifting means, e.g. with hoisting lugs -- A construction panel that includes built-in lifting devices, such as hoisting lugs or other integral fittings, enabling the panel to be raised, lowered or moved by crane, winch or other handling equipment without separate attachments. The code applies to panel products designed for easy vertical handling during erection or maintenance.
[E04C 2002/004] Panels with profiled edges, e.g. stepped, serrated -- E04C 2002/004 covers building panels whose edges are shaped with a profile such as steps or serrations to provide interlocking, improved sealing, or decorative effects. The classification applies to panels used in facades, cladding, roofing or interior partitions where the edge geometry is a functional or aesthetic feature.
[E04C 2002/005] Appearance of panels -- E04C 2002/005 concerns the visual characteristics of building panels, covering surface treatments, colours, textures and patterns applied to panel faces. It pertains to the appearance of panels used in construction, including decorative finishes and aesthetic features that affect the panel's look.
[E04C 2002/007] Panels with the appearance of a brick wall -- E04C 2002/007 refers to building panels that are manufactured to resemble a brick wall, used for external or interior cladding or façade applications. The panels may be made of metal, plastic, concrete or composite materials but are shaped or textured to give the visual effect of traditional masonry bricks, providing a decorative and often weather-resistant surface.
[E04C 2002/008] Panels with the appearance of a natural stone wall -- This subclass covers building panels designed to resemble natural stone walls, used for external or internal cladding and decorative facades. It includes prefabricated stone-like panels made of concrete, polymer composites, metal or other materials that simulate the appearance and texture of real stone.
[E04C 2002/045] with two parallel leaves connected by tie anchors -- E04C 2002/045 covers a double-leaf door or similar opening where the two parallel panels are joined by tie anchors that lock the leaves together and keep them aligned during operation, often used for hinged or sliding doors that open as a coordinated unit.
[E04C 2002/046] Flat anchors -- Flat anchors are surface-mounted anchoring devices used to secure structures directly to the ground without a protruding shank, typically consisting of a flat plate or strip that is embedded or bonded into the earth. They are employed in civil-engineering applications such as retaining walls, foundations, or slope stabilisation where a low-profile connection is required. This CPC subgroup groups inventions relating to the design, material, installation and attachment methods of such flat-type ground anchors.
[E04C 2002/047] Pin or rod shaped anchors -- The CPC subclass E04C 2002/047 covers pin- or rod-shaped anchors employed in civil-engineering foundations and structural fastening. It includes metal or composite rods inserted into drilled or blasted holes to secure loads to soil or rock, as used in footings, retaining walls, bridge piers and similar constructions. The classification groups devices designed for shear and tensile resistance in anchoring applications.
[E04C 2002/048] Bent wire anchors -- Bent wire anchors are metal fasteners formed by bending wire into a shape that can be driven into soil, concrete or masonry to secure foundations, retaining walls, or other structural elements. They provide a simple, low-cost method for anchoring and stabilising loads in civil-engineering and construction applications.
[E04C 2002/3411] Dimpled spacer sheets -- E04C 2002/3411 covers dimpled spacer sheets used in building façades and wall assemblies to create a ventilated air gap between the structural wall and the outer cladding. The dimples provide spacing and channels for moisture drainage and airflow, improving thermal insulation and durability of the envelope.
[E04C 2002/3416] with cylindrical dimples -- This CPC subgroup covers building wall or façade elements that incorporate cylindrical dimples on their surface. The dimples may be formed by molding or other processes to provide texture, improve adhesion, or enhance acoustic performance. It applies to panels, cladding, or decorative wall finishes featuring such cylindrical indentations.
[E04C 2002/3422] with polygonal dimples -- E04C 2002/3422 of the Cooperative Patent Classification refers to roof-covering elements, such as tiles or panels, that are formed with polygonal dimples on their surface, a feature used to enhance drainage, mechanical interlock, or visual texture.
[E04C 2002/3427] with conical dimples -- E04C 2002/3427 pertains to roof structures that incorporate conical dimples on the surface. The dimples are typically formed to improve water runoff, enhance ventilation, or provide decorative texture. This subclass covers the design and manufacturing of such dimpled roofing elements.
[E04C 2002/3433] with dimples extending from both sides of the spacer sheet -- E04C 2002/3433 pertains to building elements, specifically to spacer sheets used in sandwich panels or insulation systems that have dimples formed on both faces to improve adhesion, airflow, or mechanical interlocking. The dimples are typically molded or stamped and serve to maintain uniform spacing and enhance structural stability.
[E04C 2002/3438] with saddle-shaped dimples, e.g. eggcrate type spacer sheets -- The CPC entry E04C 2002/3438 covers building or construction elements that incorporate saddle-shaped dimples, such as eggcrate-type spacer sheets. These perforated or dimpled panels provide air gaps for improved insulation, acoustic damping or ventilation in walls, ceilings or flooring systems.
[E04C 2002/3444] Corrugated sheets -- CPC E04C 2002/3444 classifies corrugated sheets, covering their design, manufacture and use as building elements such as roofing, cladding or flooring. It includes metal, plastic, cardboard or composite sheets formed with a wave-like profile for strength and drainage. The code is used to index patents dealing with production methods, material compositions and applications of these corrugated panels.
[E04C 2002/345] with triangular corrugations -- The subgroup covers roof-covering elements, particularly metal sheets or panels, that are formed with triangular-shaped corrugations to increase stiffness and facilitate water runoff. It includes manufacturing, shaping and installing such corrugated roofing components.
[E04C 2002/3455] with trapezoidal corrugations -- The CPC entry E04C 2002/3455 designates roof-covering or building-covering elements that incorporate a series of trapezoidal corrugations in their profile. It covers panels, sheets, tiles or similar components whose structural or aesthetic shape is defined by a cross-section shaped like a trapezoid, typically used to increase stiffness and water runoff in roofing systems.
[E04C 2002/3461] with rectangular corrugations -- The code E04C 2002/3461 defines structural elements such as foundations, basements or retaining walls that incorporate rectangular corrugations. These corrugations are shaped as parallel rectangular ribs or channels to increase strength, stability or drainage. The classification is used for patents describing such corrugated concrete or masonry components.
[E04C 2002/3466] with sinusoidal corrugations -- E04C 2002/3466 relates to building elements whose surfaces have sinusoidal corrugations - regularly spaced wave-like ridges that increase stiffness, aid drainage or provide an aesthetic texture. It applies to roofs, walls, cladding and similar architectural components.
[E04C 2002/3472] with multiple layers of profiled spacer sheets -- E04C 2002/3472 is a CPC subclass for building elements that use several layers of profiled spacer sheets to form a composite wall or panel. It covers patents describing multi-layered spacer systems that provide thermal insulation, structural spacing or ventilation channels between facing surfaces.
[E04C 2002/3477] spaced apart by tubular elements parallel to the sheets -- E04C 2002/3477 refers to wall or façade panels made of flat sheets that are kept apart by tubular elements running parallel to the sheets. The tubular spacers create a regularly spaced cavity, improving ventilation, insulation or decorative appearance while maintaining structural integrity.
[E04C 2002/3483] spaced apart by spacers stamped from the sheets -- E04C 2002/3483 covers roof or building sheet constructions in which the sheets are separated by spacers that are stamped out of the same sheet material, providing a fixed gap without adding separate components. Such spacers create ventilation channels, drainage paths or thermal insulation between the sheets.
[E04C 2002/3488] spaced apart by frame like structures -- E04C 2002/3488 covers door, window or skylight arrangements where the opening members are separated by a frame-like structure that provides spacing and support, such as mullions, transoms, or intermediate frames.
[E04C 2002/3494] Apparatus for making profiled spacer sheets -- This CPC subgroup covers devices that manufacture spacer sheets with a defined profile, such as for glazing or insulating panels. The apparatus generally includes feeding, shaping, cutting and collecting stages and may incorporate bonding or sealing functions to produce continuous or discrete profiled sheets.
[E04C 2003/023] Lintels -- E04C is the CPC group for building elements, and sub-group 2003/023 specifically covers lintels. A lintel is a horizontal load-bearing member placed over door, window or other wall openings to support the structure above, commonly made of wood, steel, concrete or composite materials.
[E04C 2003/026] Braces -- Braces are structural elements that reinforce fences, walls, foot-bridges, parapets and similar constructions by providing diagonal or tension support, often made from metal, timber or composite materials. The subclass includes designs, attachment methods and configurations intended to increase the rigidity and stability of such barriers.
[E04C 2003/0404] beams, girders, or joists characterised by cross-sectional aspects -- E04C 2003/0404 covers structural members such as beams, girders or joists that are defined primarily by the shape of their cross-section. It includes designs where the profile, dimensions or geometry of the section provide particular strength, stiffness or functional properties. The classification is used for patents focusing on the sectional configuration rather than material or connection details.
[E04C 2003/0408] characterised by assembly or the cross-section -- This CPC subclass includes roof structures whose defining feature is the way the elements are assembled or the shape of their cross-section, such as modular panels, trusses, or curved sections. It covers patents describing the construction method or the geometric profile of the roof components.
[E04C 2003/0413] being built up from several parts -- E04C 2003/0413 covers building structures that are assembled from multiple separate components, such as prefabricated, modular, or kit-based elements. It includes methods and arrangements where the final construction results from joining distinct parts rather than casting or forming a monolithic unit. This subclass is used for patents describing phased or component-based erection of buildings.
[E04C 2003/0417] demountable -- E04C 2003/0417 relates to demountable roof, ceiling or wall covering elements that can be removed and reassembled, such as prefabricated panels, detachable tiles and modular roofing systems designed for easy maintenance or reuse. The code applies where the covering is not permanently fixed.
[E04C 2003/0421] comprising one single unitary part -- E04C2003/0421 relates to roof structures that are formed as a single, continuous unit without separate components. It covers designs where the entire roof is manufactured or installed as one piece, such as monolithic metal sheets or integrated membrane systems. This classification is used to identify patents describing unitary roof constructions.
[E04C 2003/0426] characterised by material distribution in cross section -- E04C 2003/0426 covers construction elements whose performance or function is defined by the way material is distributed across the component's cross-section, including layered, gradient or otherwise varying material arrangements. The classification applies to building or structural parts where this cross-sectional material profile is a distinguishing technical feature.
[E04C 2003/043] the hollow cross-section comprising at least one enclosed cavity -- E04C 2003/043 covers building elements whose cross-section is hollow, containing at least one sealed cavity, typically used for thermal or acoustic insulation in walls, ceilings or floors. It includes structural members such as beams, panels or cladding designed with internal voids to improve performance.
[E04C 2003/0434] the open cross-section free of enclosed cavities -- E04C 2003/0434 classifies inventions in which a structural element-such as a wall, floor, roof or similar component-has an open cross-section that does not contain any enclosed cavities, meaning the interior space is unsealed and freely accessible, typically for ventilation, passage or lightweight construction.
[E04C 2003/0439] the cross-section comprising open parts and hollow parts -- The CPC entry E04C 2003/0439 refers to designs of a cross-section that combines open portions - areas through which air can pass - with hollow portions - enclosed cavities that may guide or contain flow. It is applied to ventilation or air-distribution components of buildings such as ducts, grills, or diffusers where mixed open and sealed sections are integral to the function.
[E04C 2003/0443] characterised by substantial shape of the cross-section -- E04C 2003/0443 covers building or roofing elements whose primary inventive aspect is the overall geometry of the cross-section, such as beams, arches, panels or similar profiles, rather than surface details. It is applied to patents where the substantial shape of the section defines the function or advantage.
[E04C 2003/0447] circular- or oval-shaped -- This CPC subgroup covers agricultural or horticultural structures whose overall plan is circular or oval, such as barns, sheds, greenhouses, animal housing, storage silos, and processing units designed with a round or oval footprint. It is used to classify patents that focus on the shape of the building rather than its internal functions.
[E04C 2003/0452] H- or I-shaped -- E04C 2003/0452 covers roofing or building components that have an H- or I-shaped cross-section, such as metal panels, beams or profiled sheets used for roofs, ceilings or walls, where the shape provides structural strength and facilitates installation.
[E04C 2003/0456] hollow flanged, i.e. "dogbone" metal beams -- The CPC subclass E04C 2003/0456 covers hollow, flanged metal beams shaped like a "dog-bone", used as lightweight structural members in roofs, ceilings or walls. These beams provide high strength with reduced material by having a hollow core and widened ends that facilitate fastening and load transfer.
[E04C 2003/046] L- or T-shaped -- The CPC entry E04C 2003/046 covers roofing, gutter or branch-work components whose cross-section is L- or T-shaped, such as brackets, flashing, or junction pieces designed to join or support elements at right angles. It pertains to the structural form of these building-assembly parts rather than to a specific material or function.
[E04C 2003/0465] square- or rectangular-shaped -- E04C 2003/0465 relates to building foundations, footings or other substructural elements that are square or rectangular in shape, defining the geometry of the supporting structure for construction applications.
[E04C 2003/0469] triangular-shaped -- The CPC entry E04C 2003/0469 designates roof elements that are triangular in shape, such as triangular plates, panels, or ridges used to form or reinforce sloping or gable roofs. It covers the geometry, mounting and material aspects of these triangular components in building construction.
[E04C 2003/0473] U- or C-shaped -- The subclass E04C 2003/0473 concerns building foundation components whose cross-section is U-shaped or C-shaped, such as channel, bracket or trough elements used to support, reinforce or connect footings, piles, ground beams or other sub-structure parts. It includes designs that exploit the shape's strength and ease of installation for load-bearing or anchoring functions.
[E04C 2003/0478] X-shaped -- E04C 2003/0478 relates to X-shaped structural elements used in building and civil-engineering works, covering members or assemblies formed by intersecting parts that create an X configuration for reinforcement, bracing, or load-distribution purposes in walls, frames, or other constructions.
[E04C 2003/0482] Z- or S-shaped -- This subclass pertains to building elements that incorporate a Z- or S-shaped profile, such as joists, studs, or panels whose cross-section follows a Z or S form. It covers the design, manufacture and use of these shaped structural members in walls, roofs, partitions or other construction components.
[E04C 2003/0486] Truss like structures composed of separate truss elements -- This subclass covers constructions in which a truss system is built from discrete, individually formed truss members that are assembled to create a larger framework. It includes modular or prefabricated truss elements joined on-site to form roofs, bridges, or other load-bearing structures, emphasizing the use of separate components rather than monolithic trusses.
[E04C 2003/0491] the truss elements being located in one single surface or in several parallel surfaces -- E04C 2003/0491 covers structural truss systems in which the individual members are arranged either on a single plane or on multiple planes that run parallel to one another, such as flat or layered truss walls, floors or roofs. The classification is used for patents describing the geometry, assembly or reinforcement of these planar or stacked truss configurations.
[E04C 2003/0495] the truss elements being located in several non-parallel surfaces -- This subgroup of the CPC covers roof structures in which the truss members are positioned on several non-parallel surfaces, such as intersecting or differently sloped planes, enabling the roof to accommodate complex geometries or varying slopes.
[E04C 2/02] characterised by specified materials -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Slab-like translucent elements -> E04C2/54
[E04C 2/04] of concrete or other stone-like material -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Building sheets, slabs or panels composed of materials covered by two or more of groups E04C2/04 , E04C2/08 , E04C2/10 , or of materials covered by one of these groups with a material not specified in one of these groups -> E04C2/26
[E04C 2/041] composed of a number of smaller elements, e.g. bricks, also combined with a slab of hardenable material -- E04C 2/041 covers building elements that are made up of many small units such as bricks and may be combined with a hardening slab, forming thin structural components like panels used in construction.
[E04C 2/042] Apparatus for handling the smaller elements or the hardenable material; bricklaying machines for prefabricated panels  bricklaying machines in general  E04G21/22 -- E04C 2/042 covers equipment used to handle small building components or hardening materials, including machines that lay bricks and assemble prefabricated panels. It encompasses general bricklaying machines and devices that aid in positioning and fixing thin structural elements during construction.
[E04C 2/043] of plaster  E04C2/049  takes precedence
[E04C 2/044] of concrete  E04C2/049  takes precedence
[E04C 2/049] completely or partially of insulating material, e.g. cellular concrete or foamed plaster -- This subclass covers building elements such as sheets, slabs or panels that are made wholly or partly from insulating materials like cellular concrete or foamed plaster, intended for thin structural components in construction. It falls under fixed constructions, building, structural elements for relatively thin form components.
[E04C 2/06] reinforced -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Building sheets, slabs or panels made of concrete or other stone-like materials and reinforcing elements, e.g. slabs of concrete with reinforcing rods
[E04C 2/08] of metal, e.g. sheet metal -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Building sheets, slabs or panels composed of materials covered by two or more of groups E04C2/04 , E04C2/08 , E04C2/10 , or of materials covered by one of these groups with a material not specified in one of these groups -> E04C2/26
[E04C 2/10] of wood, fibres, chips, vegetable stems, or the like -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Building sheets, slabs or panels made of hydraulic cement and mineral fibres -> E04C2/04 - Building sheets, slabs or panels made of foamed concrete or other stone-like products -> E04C2/049 - Building sheets, slabs or panels composed of materials covered by two or more of groups E04C2/04 , E04C2/08 , E04C2/10 , or of materials covered by one of these groups with a material not specified in one of these groups -> E04C2/26
[E04C 2/12] of solid wood -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Building sheets, slabs or panels substantially made of solid wood, with or without reinforcements  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Building sheets, slabs or panels made of fibres, chips, vegetable stems or the like -> E04C2/16 - Building sheets, slabs or panels laminated and composed of materials covered by two or more of groups E04C2/12 , E04C2/16 , E04C2/20 -> E04C2/24
[E04C 2/14] reinforced -- E04C 2/14 deals with reinforced thin building components such as sheets, slabs or panels that form part of a structure's fixed construction. The classification is applied to inventions that strengthen these elements by reinforcement methods, for example with fibers, metal ribs or other supportive means.
[E04C 2/16] of fibres, chips, vegetable stems, or the like -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Building sheets, slabs or panels composed of organic or inorganic fibres, organic or inorganic chips, vegetable stems, or the like, with or without additional glue or adhesive  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Building sheets, slabs or panels made of hydraulic cement and mineral fibres -> E04C2/04
[E04C 2/18] with binding wires, reinforcing bars, or the like -- E04C 2/18 relates to thin building components such as sheets, slabs or panels that are strengthened or held together by binding wires, reinforcing bars or similar devices to improve their load-bearing capacity.
[E04C 2/20] of plastics -- This subclass pertains to thin building components fabricated from plastics, including sheets, panels and slabs employed as structural elements in construction. It falls under the broader categories of fixed constructions, building, and structural elements.
[E04C 2/205] of foamed plastics, or of plastics and foamed plastics, optionally reinforced -- E04C 2/205 covers thin building components such as sheets, slabs or panels that are made from foamed plastics, or from a combination of plastics and foamed plastics, and may include reinforcement; these elements are used in the construction of parts of buildings.
[E04C 2/22] reinforced -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Building sheets, slabs or panels made of plastics and comprising reinforcing elements such as fibres, rods
[E04C 2/24] laminated and composed of materials covered by two or more of groups -- E04C 2/24 relates to thin building components - sheets, slabs or panels - that are formed by laminating or otherwise combining materials from two or more of the CPC material groups, intended for use as structural elements in fixed-construction buildings.
[E04C 2/243] one at least of the material being insulating -- E04C 2/243 covers thin building elements such as sheets, slabs or panels whose construction incorporates at least one insulating material. The classification applies to structural components used in walls, roofs or floors where the insulating property is an integral part of the element. It falls under the Fixed Constructions-Building-Structural Elements hierarchy of the CPC.
[E04C 2/246] combinations of materials fully covered by  E04C2/16  and  E04C2/20 -- E04C 2/246 relates to thin building elements such as sheets, slabs or panels that are formed from combinations of materials fully addressed in the sub-groups E04C 2/16 (single-material thin elements) and E04C 2/20 (heterogeneous thin elements), covering their design and construction within fixed building structures.
[E04C 2/26] composed of materials covered by two or more of groups -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Building sheets, slabs or panels, laminated or not, and composed of materials covered by two or more of groups E04C2/04 , E04C2/08 , E04C2/10 or of materials covered by one of these groups with a material not specified in one of the groups.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Building sheets, slabs or panels made of hydraulic cement and mineral fibres -> E04C2/04
[E04C 2/28] combinations of materials fully covered by groups -- E04C 2/28 covers building components of thin form-such as sheets, slabs or panels-where two or more materials are combined in a single element and the combination is completely described by the broader groups above. It falls under Fixed Constructions → Building → Structural Elements.
[E04C 2/284] at least one of the materials being insulating -- E04C 2/284 covers thin building elements such as sheets, slabs or panels that form parts of a structure, where at least one of the constituent materials serves an insulating function. This subclass falls under Fixed Constructions → Building → Structural Elements in the CPC hierarchy.
[E04C 2/288] composed of insulating material and concrete, stone or stone-like material -- E04C 2/288 designates thin building elements-such as sheets, slabs or panels-that combine an insulating material with concrete, stone or a stone-like substance. These composite components are used in structural construction to provide both load-bearing capacity and thermal insulation.
[E04C 2/2885] with the insulating material being completely surrounded by, or embedded in, a stone-like material, e.g. the insulating material being discontinuous -- This subclass concerns thin building components in which the thermal insulating material is fully enclosed by, or embedded within, a stone-like material such as concrete or masonry, often in a discontinuous form, providing insulation while preserving structural properties.
[E04C 2/292] composed of insulating material and sheet metal -- A thin building panel that combines a layer of insulating material bonded to a sheet-metal face, forming a lightweight structural element for walls, roofs or partitions. The insulation provides thermal performance while the metal skin offers protection, rigidity and a mountable surface. This construction is suited to prefabricated or retrofit applications where space and weight are limited.
[E04C 2/296] composed of insulating material and non-metallic or unspecified sheet-material -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Building sheets, slabs or panels composed of insulating material and concrete, stone or stone-like material -> E04C2/288
[E04C 2/30] characterised by the shape or structure -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Slab-like translucent elements -> E04C2/54
[E04C 2/32] formed of corrugated or otherwise indented sheet-like material -- E04C 2/32 covers thin building components such as panels, sheets or slabs that are made from sheet material that has been corrugated or otherwise indented to give it a ribbed or profiled shape. These elements provide structural rigidity while remaining lightweight and are used in walls, roofs, ceilings or façade systems.
[E04C 2/322] with parallel corrugations -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Sheet-like material with parallel corrugations, e.g. with parallel undulated corrugations
[E04C 2/324] with incisions or reliefs in the surface  E04C2/326  takes precedence -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Building elements of relatively thin form with corrugations, incisions or reliefs in more than one direction of the element -> E04C2/326
[E04C 2/326] with corrugations, incisions or reliefs in more than one direction of the element -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Sheet-like material with corrugations, incisions or reliefs in more than one direction of the element, e.g. egg-crate reliefs
[E04C 2/328] slightly bowed or folded panels not otherwise provided for -- E04C 2/328 pertains to thin building elements such as sheets, slabs or panels that are slightly bowed or folded and are not otherwise covered by the classification. It relates to fixed constructions where the panels have a gentle curvature or fold, used as structural components in buildings.
[E04C 2/34] composed of two or more spaced sheet-like parts -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Sheet-like elements composed of two or more spaced sheet-like parts, e.g. sandwich panels
[E04C 2/3405] spaced apart by profiled spacer sheets -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Sheet-like elements composed of two or more spaced sheet-like parts, spaced apart by profiled spacer sheets, like e.g. corrugated cardboard
[E04C 2/36] spaced apart by transversely-placed strip material, e.g. honeycomb panels -- This subclass covers thin building components formed from two or more sheet layers that are kept apart by strips placed across the width, producing a honeycomb-type core. The transverse strips provide spacing and reinforce the panel while keeping the overall structure lightweight.
[E04C 2/365] by honeycomb structures -- A subclass for thin building elements that use honeycomb structures, such as panels or slabs with a honeycomb-core, providing high strength-to-weight ratios for fixed constructions in buildings.
[E04C 2/38] with attached ribs, flanges, or the like, e.g. framed panels -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Building sheets, slabs or panels having attached ribs, flanges, or the like, e.g. framed panels and panels with upstanding edges around their borders  **Glossary:** - Framed panel: Panel having a frame around its borders
[E04C 2/382] with a frame of concrete or other stone-like substance -- This subclass covers thin building components such as sheets, slabs or panels that are supported by a frame made of concrete or a similar stone-like material. It falls under fixed constructions and structural elements of buildings.
[E04C 2/384] with a metal frame -- E04C 2/384 covers thin building components such as sheets, slabs, or panels that incorporate a metal framework to provide stiffness or support. The metal frame may be integrated during manufacture or added later, and is used in walls, ceilings, or partitions to improve load-bearing capacity while keeping the element lightweight.
[E04C 2/386] with a frame of unreconstituted or laminated wood -- The entry E04C 2/386 covers thin building components such as sheets, slabs or panels that are constructed with a frame made of solid (unreconstituted) wood or laminated wood, used as structural elements in fixed building constructions.
[E04C 2/388] with a frame of other materials, e.g. fibres, plastics -- E04C 2/388 covers thin building components such as sheets, slabs or panels that are reinforced with a frame made from alternative materials like fibres or plastics. The classification falls under fixed constructions, building and structural elements and pertains to composite framed elements used in construction.
[E04C 2/40] composed of a number of smaller components rigidly or movably connected together, e.g. interlocking, hingedly connected -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Building sheets, slabs or panels, composed of a number of smaller components rigidly or movably connected together, e.g. foldable garage doors  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Building sheets, slabs or panels, composed of a number of smaller components rigidly connected together, of concrete or other stone-like material, of asbestos cement, of cement and other mineral fibres -> E04C2/041
[E04C 2/405] composed of two or more hingedly connected parts -- The CPC entry E04C 2/405 covers thin structural building elements such as sheets, slabs or panels that consist of two or more parts joined by a hinge, allowing them to pivot relative to each other. These components are used where flexible or movable connections are required in construction.
[E04C 2/42] Gratings -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Reinforcing grids or mats of metal -> E04C5/04 - Non-metallic reinforcing mats -> E04C5/07 - Grids for ceilings -> E04B9/345 - Built-in gratings -> E04F19/10 - Gratings or grilles made from a sheet -> F16S1/00 - Gratings in general -> F16S3/00
[E04C 2/421] made of bar-like elements, e.g. bars discontinuous in one direction -- This subclass covers thin building components such as sheets, slabs or panels that are formed from bar-like elements, where the bars may be spaced or discontinuous in one direction, providing a relatively thin structural element for construction.
[E04C 2/422] with continuous bars connecting at crossing points of the grid pattern -- E04C 2/422 classifies thin construction components such as sheets, slabs or panels that incorporate a grid pattern in which continuous bars join at the crossing points. This sub-category falls under Fixed Constructions - Building - Structural Elements and covers designs where the intersecting members of the grid are integral bars rather than discrete connections.
[E04C 2/423] with notches -- E04C 2/423 covers thin building components such as sheets, slabs or panels that incorporate notches, typically used to facilitate joining, alignment or mounting within a structural system. The notches may be cut, punched or formed to allow interlocking or attachment to other building elements.
[E04C 2/425] made of perforated bars -- A building element of relatively thin form that consists of a network of perforated bars, such as grates, lattices, or screened panels used for walls, floors, roofs or decorative cladding. It applies to any construction component where the primary structure is made from bar patterns that provide strength while allowing passage of light, air, or fluids.
[E04C 2/426] with continuous bars that remain unconnected at crossing points of the grid pattern, e.g. with undulating bars -- The code E04C 2/426 covers thin building elements such as sheets, slabs or panels that incorporate a grid of continuous bars whose crossing points are not joined, so the bars remain separate where they intersect. This includes designs where the bars undulate or curve, creating an undulating-grid pattern.
[E04C 2/427] Expanded metal or other monolithic gratings -- E04C 2/427 covers thin, load-bearing building components made from expanded metal or similar monolithic gratings, such as perforated sheets, slabs or panels used in floors, walls, roofs or ventilated façades. These elements are fabricated as a single piece of metal that has been stretched or cut to form a network of openings, providing strength while reducing weight and allowing air or light passage.
[E04C 2/428] Separate connecting means, e.g. connecting gratings to underlying structure -- E04C 2/428 relates to separate connecting means that fasten thin building components such as gratings, panels or sheets to an underlying structural framework. It includes brackets, clips, fasteners or other devices that provide a detachable or permanent link between the element and its support.
[E04C 2/44] characterised by the purpose -- E04C 2/44 covers thin building components such as sheets, slabs or panels that are defined primarily by their intended use rather than by material or shape. The subclass groups fixed construction elements whose classification is based on the purpose they serve in the building, for example fire-resistant cladding or acoustic insulation panels.
[E04C 2/46] specially adapted for making walls  E04C2/52 ,  E04C2/54  take precedence; structure of slab-shaped elements  E04B1/02 ; walls of elements of relatively thin form  E04B2/72 -- E04C 2/46 concerns building components of relatively thin form that are specially adapted for constructing walls, such as slab-shaped or panel elements used as wall modules, focusing on their design and arrangement within fixed constructions.
[E04C 2/48] as high as or higher than the room, i.e. having provisions concerning the connection with at least two floors  E04C2/52  and  E04C2/54  take precedence -- E04C 2/48 concerns thin structural building components that are erected to a height at least equal to the room height and include means for linking the component to two or more storeys. When more detailed provisions are given in the sub-classes E04C 2/52 or E04C 2/54 they override this broader definition.
[E04C 2/50] Self-supporting slabs specially adapted for making floors ceilings, or roofs, e.g. able to be loaded  E04C2/52 ,  E04C2/54  take precedence; structures of slab-shaped elements  E04B1/02 ; floor structures  E04B5/00 ; roofs consisting of self-supporting slabs  E04B7/20 ; ceilings  E04B9/00 ; roof coverings  E04D ; floor coverings  E04F15/00 -- The code E04C 2/50 covers self-supporting slabs that are specially designed for use as floors, ceilings or roofs in building construction. These relatively thin, slab-shaped elements form structural components that can bear loads without additional support, distinguishing them from other panel or sheet constructions.
[E04C 2/52] with special adaptations for auxiliary purposes, e.g. serving for locating conduits -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Slab-like translucent elements -> E04C2/54
[E04C 2/521] serving for locating conduits; for ventilating, heating or cooling -- A thin building element such as a sheet, slab or panel that is designed to incorporate means for locating electrical or utility conduits and to provide ventilation, heating or cooling within a fixed construction. This subclass covers structural components used for integrating service pathways and climate-control functions in buildings.
[E04C 2/523] for ventilating -- The code E04C 2/523 designates thin building components intended to provide ventilation, such as perforated panels, grilles, or vented slabs that are integrated into walls, ceilings or roofs to allow air flow while maintaining the structural integrity of the construction.
[E04C 2/525] for heating or cooling  solar heat collectors  F24S10/00 ; heat storage  F28D20/00
[E04C 2/526] with adaptations not otherwise provided for, for connecting, transport; for making impervious or hermetic, e.g. sealings -- E04C 2/526 concerns thin building components such as sheets, slabs or panels that incorporate special adaptations for joining, handling or transport and for providing airtight or watertight seals. The subclass captures features not otherwise covered in the broader categories of fixed constructions.
[E04C 2/528] Impervious or hermetic panels not otherwise provided for -- This subclass covers panels that are sealed against moisture or gas ingress and are not covered by other more specific subclasses, such as waterproof or airtight wall, roof, or floor panels made from sheet, slab, or panel materials. They are used where a continuous impervious surface is required.
[E04C 2/54] Slab-like translucent elements -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Translucent or transparent slab-like elements
[E04C 2/543] Hollow multi-walled panels with integrated webs -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Translucent hollow slab-like elements having a cross-section which is internally subdivided by walls, e.g. a translucent polyurethane roofing slab which cross section is internally subdivided by, in use, horizontal and vertical walls
[E04C 2/546] made of glass bricks -- E04C 2/546 refers to fixed building constructions that use glass bricks as thin-form structural elements such as panels or slabs. The subclass groups patents dealing with the design, manufacture, or installation of glass-brick elements in walls, façades, or other building parts where a lightweight, transparent material is required.
[E04C 3/00] Structural elongated elements designed for load-supporting -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Structural elongated elements designed for permanent load-supporting in buildings, e.g. building beams and columns  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Structural elongated elements for bridges -> E01D - Piles for foundations -> E02D5/00 - Non-load supporting elongated members for partition walls -> E04B2/74 - Non-load supporting elongated elements for suspended ceilings -> E04B9/06 - Structural elongated elements designed as building aids -> E04G - Structural elongated elements for masts, towers, poles -> E04H12/00 - Structural elongated elements for tents -> E04H15/00 - Structural elongated elements for mining, tunnels -> E21C , E21D
[E04C 3/005] Girders or columns that are rollable, collapsible or otherwise adjustable in length or height  girders as supporting members for forms  E04G11/54 -- **Glossary:** - Girders or columns that are rollable: Girders or columns that are coilable or windable
[E04C 3/02] Joists -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Arched girders or portal frames -> E04C3/38 - Roof structures having braced purlins -> E04B7/024  **Glossary:** - Joist: Horizontal supporting member that runs from wall to wall, wall to beam, or beam to beam to support a ceiling, a roof or a floor. - Girder: Support beam used in construction, e.g. an I-shaped girder, a box-shaped girder, a truss- or lattice-girder. - Truss: Elongated structure comprising one or more triangular units constructed with straight members whose ends are connected at joints referred as nodes. - Lintel: Load-bearing elongated element located in use over door or window openings. - Transom: A bar of wood or stone across the top of a door or window opening - Brace: A structural elongated element serving as connecting/reinforcing element between structural elements, e.g. braces between wooden joists in a floor structure
[E04C 3/04] of metal -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Joists, girders or trusses built-up from parts of different material -> E04C3/29
[E04C 3/06] with substantially solid, i.e. unapertured, web -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Honeycomb metallic beams, i.e. castellated beams; metallic beams with apertured solid web -> E04C3/083 - Metallic joists, girders and trusses with non-parallel upper and lower edges -> E04C3/11  **Glossary:** - web: The "vertical" element of the beam connected to the flange(s); the beam can either be an I-, T-,C- or a box beam; in case of an H or U-beam the web is considered to be the "horizontal" element (in analogy with I- and C-beams.)
[E04C 3/065] with special adaptations for the passage of cables or conduits through the web -- This subclass covers load-bearing elongated building elements such as beams, lintels or wall studs that are provided with openings, slots, ducts or integral conduits allowing cables or other utilities to pass through the web without compromising structural integrity.
[E04C 3/07] at least partly of bent or otherwise deformed strip- or sheet-like material -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Metallic girders or beams having a non-apertured web and made at least partially of deformed strip- or sheet-like material, e.g. by cold rolling
[E04C 3/08] with apertured web, e.g. with a web consisting of bar-like components -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Metallic joists, girders and trusses with non-parallel upper and lower edges -> E04C3/11 - Metallic arched girders or trusses, portal frames -> E04C3/40
[E04C 3/083] Honeycomb girders; Girders with apertured solid web -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Honeycomb metallic girders, i.e. castellated metallic girders; Metallic girders with apertured solid web, i.e. metallic girders in which apertures have been made in the solid web.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Metallic girders or beams made of bent or otherwise deformed strip- or sheet-like material, and having an apertured web -> E04C3/09
[E04C 3/086] of the castellated type -- E04C 3/086 classifies structural elongated load-supporting elements that are castellated, meaning they incorporate regular openings or indented sections resembling a castle battlement to reduce weight while preserving strength. These elements are used in fixed building constructions such as beams, girders or trusses.
[E04C 3/09] at least partly of bent or otherwise deformed strip- or sheet-like material -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Metallic girders or beams made of at least partially deformed strip- or sheet-like material, and having an apertured web, e.g. made by an expanded metal sheet or made by cutting holes in the solid web.
[E04C 3/10] prestressed -- E04C 3/10 covers prestressed structural elongated elements used in building construction, such as beams, columns, and lintels that are subjected to tensile forces before service loads are applied. The classification includes the design, materials, and methods for applying prestress to improve load-bearing capacity and limit deflection in load-supporting components.
[E04C 3/11] with non-parallel upper and lower edges, e.g. roof trusses -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Metallic arched girders, portal frames -> E04C3/40
[E04C 3/12] of wood, e.g. with reinforcements, with tensioning members -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Joists, girders or trusses made of wood and metal -> E04C3/292
[E04C 3/122] Laminated -- Laminated structural elongated elements are load-bearing components made by bonding together layers of material such as timber, metal or composite sheets. The lamination improves strength, stiffness and resistance to warping, allowing the elements to be used in beams, girders or bridge decks where high load capacity and dimensional stability are required. This classification covers methods of producing and applications of such laminated members in building structures.
[E04C 3/125] End caps therefor -- End caps for structural elongated load-supporting elements are components that close, protect, reinforce or connect the free ends of members such as columns, beams, posts or tubular sections. They are used in fixed constructions to prevent damage, improve load transfer, seal the element against moisture or debris, and to provide a finished appearance.
[E04C 3/127] with hollow cross section -- E04C 3/127 covers structural elongated building elements that provide load support and feature a hollow cross-section, such as tubular steel beams, hollow concrete columns, or other hollow-section members. The hollow shape reduces weight while maintaining strength and is used in frames, roofs, and other structural applications.
[E04C 3/14] with substantially solid, i.e. unapertured, web -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Joists, girders or trusses with non-parallel upper and lower edges, e.g. roof trusses -> E04C3/17 - Joists, girders or trusses made of wood with metal or other reinforcements or tensioning members -> E04C3/18
[E04C 3/145] with special adaptations for the passage of cables or conduits through the web, e.g. reinforcements -- This class covers elongated, load-bearing building elements like beams or lintels that are modified with openings, channels or reinforcements to let electrical cables, piping or other conduits pass through the web while maintaining structural integrity.
[E04C 3/16] with apertured web, e.g. trusses -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Joists, girders, or trusses with non-parallel upper and lower edges, e.g. roof trusses -> E04C3/17 - Wooden arched girders, portal frames -> E04C3/42
[E04C 3/17] with non-parallel upper and lower edges, e.g. roof trusses -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Wooden arched girders, portal frames -> E04C3/42
[E04C 3/18] with metal reinforcements or tensioning members -- E04C 3/18 covers building structural elongated elements such as beams, columns or similar members that incorporate metal reinforcements or tensioning components to enhance load-supporting capacity. It falls under the Fixed Constructions branch of the Cooperative Patent Classification, within the Building → Structural Elements hierarchy.
[E04C 3/185] Synthetic reinforcements -- E04C 3/185 covers the use of synthetic reinforcement materials in elongated load-bearing structural elements of buildings. This includes polymers, carbon fibre, glass fibre or other non-metallic composites incorporated into beams, columns or similar members to increase strength, stiffness or resistance to environmental degradation.
[E04C 3/20] of concrete or other stone-like material, e.g. with reinforcements or tensioning members -- The CPC subclass E04C 3/20 covers structural elongated building elements such as beams, lintels or joists that are made of concrete, stone-like materials, or composites incorporating reinforcement or tensioning members to bear loads. It is situated under fixed constructions, building, structural elements, and specifically those designed for load-supporting functions.
[E04C 3/205] with apertured web, e.g. frameworks, trusses  E04C3/26  takes precedence -- E04C 3/205 covers structural elongated building elements designed to support loads that incorporate an apertured web, such as framework members and trusses. It is a subcategory of fixed construction structural elements where the web is perforated or lattice-like to reduce weight while maintaining strength. This classification follows the broader group for load-supporting building components.
[E04C 3/22] built-up by elements jointed in line -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Concrete beams built-up by elements jointed in-line, e.g. made up of stacked elements
[E04C 3/26] prestressed -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Joists, girders or trusses made of concrete or other stone-like material, and built-up by elements jointed in line -> E04C3/22
[E04C 3/28] of materials not covered by groups -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Joists, girders or trusses made of specified materials other than metal, wood. concrete or stone-like materials, e.g. joists made of glass or plastics
[E04C 3/285] of glass
[E04C 3/29] built-up from parts of different material, -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Joists, girders or trusses built-up from parts of specified different materials, e.g. built-up from laminated parts of different materials, or from assembled parts of different materials  **Glossary:** - Composite structure: Composite structure refers to the structure of the assembly of the different parts, not to the material
[E04C 3/291] with apertured web -- This subclass covers elongated structural members for load-supporting purposes whose web contains openings, such as perforated beams, girders or lintels. The apertured web reduces weight, permits passage of services, or provides other functional benefits while retaining the element's primary structural role.
[E04C 3/292] the materials being wood and metal -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Wooden joists, girders or trusses with metal reinforcements or tensioning members -> E04C3/18
[E04C 3/293] the materials being steel and concrete -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Joists, girders or trusses made of concrete with internal reinforcements or tensioning members -> E04C3/20 - Metallic beams forming part of a floor structure -> E04B5/29
[E04C 3/294] of concrete combined with a girder-like structure extending laterally outside the element -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Girders or trusses built-up from a concrete element and a steel girder-like structure extending laterally outside the concrete element  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Light weight girders with concrete precast parts, used as reinforcement -> E04C5/065 - Metallic beams forming part of a floor structure, and extending below the floor -> E04B5/29
[E04C 3/30] Columns -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Structural elongated elements not designed for end loading -> E04C3/02 - E04C3/294 - Elongated supporting members for partition walls -> E04B2/74 - Structural elongated elements designed as building aids -> E04G - Posts, masts as independent structures -> E04H12/00 - Structural elongated elements for tents -> E04H15/00 - Poles for tents -> E04H15/60
[E04C 3/32] of metal -- E04C 3/32 covers metal structural members that are elongated and used as load-supporting components in fixed building constructions. It includes beams, columns, girders and similar metal elements designed to bear loads within the building framework.
[E04C 3/34] of concrete other stone-like material, with or without permanent form elements, with or without internal or external reinforcement, e.g. metal coverings -- E04C 3/34 relates to elongated structural elements of concrete or similar stone-like material used in buildings for load-bearing purposes. The elements may be cast with permanent formwork and can incorporate internal reinforcement such as steel rebars or external metal coverings. The classification covers any such concrete or stone-like components designed as load-supporting members.
[E04C 3/36] of materials not covered by groups -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Columns, pillars or struts made of specified materials other than metal or concrete or stone-like materials, e.g. wood, glass, plastics
[E04C 3/38] Arched girders or portal frames -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Straight girders able to be bent -> E04C3/02 - Inflatable girders or portal frames -> E04H15/20
[E04C 3/40] of metal
[E04C 3/42] of wood, e.g. units for rafter roofs -- This subclass covers structural elongated elements made of wood that serve load-supporting functions in buildings, notably pre-manufactured wooden units used for rafter roofs and similar roof-framing components. It includes any wooden members specifically designed to form the principal support for roof structures.
[E04C 3/44] of concrete or other stone-like material, e.g. with reinforcements or tensioning members -- This subclass covers elongated structural members such as beams, lintels or columns that are formed from concrete, stone or similar stone-like material and may include reinforcement or tensioning components to increase load-bearing capacity.
[E04C 3/46] of materials not covered by groups -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Arched girders or portal frames made of specified materials other than metal, wood, concrete or stone-like material, e.g. of glass, plastics
[E04C 5/00] Reinforcing elements, e.g. for concrete -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Reinforcing elements, metallic and non-metallic, in particular for concrete;  Auxiliary elements for reinforcements, e.g. connectors, spacers;  Arrangements of reinforcing elements, e.g. stirrup baskets  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Methods or devices for making metallic reinforcing materials -> B21D
[E04C 5/01] Reinforcing elements of metal, e.g. with non-structural coatings -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Members specially adapted to be used in prestressed constructions -> E04C5/08
[E04C 5/012] Discrete reinforcing elements, e.g. fibres -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Discrete reinforcing elements of metal, i.e. suitable to be mixed with the concrete before pouring, e.g. metal fibres
[E04C 5/015] Anti-corrosion coatings or treating compositions, e.g. containing waterglass or based on another metal  coating of discrete reinforcing elements  C04B20/10 -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Reinforcing elements of metal including anti-corrosion coatings or treating compositions or in-situ anti-corrosion treatments, e.g. cathodic protection  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Coating of discrete metallic reinforcing elements, e.g. coating of metallic fibres -> C04B20/10
[E04C 5/017] Anti-corrosion coatings or treating compositions containing cement -- E04C 5/017 covers anti-corrosion coatings or treating compositions that incorporate cement, intended for protecting reinforcing elements such as rebar within concrete structures. The class includes formulations that form a protective barrier by combining cementitious matrices with corrosion-inhibiting agents, applied during construction or as a maintenance treatment to extend the service life of structural elements.
[E04C 5/02] of low bending resistance, i.e. of essentially one-dimensional [1D] or two-dimensional [2D] extent -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Anchoring devices specially adapted for balconies -> E04B1/0038
[E04C 5/03] with indentations, projections, ribs, or the like, for augmenting the adherence to the concrete -- E04C 5/03 concerns fixed building constructions that incorporate reinforcing elements such as indentations, projections or ribs into concrete to improve its bond strength. This subclass falls under structural elements and focuses on designs that augment adherence to the concrete.
[E04C 5/04] Mats -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Metallic reinforcing mats  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Metallic reinforcing mats combined with reinforcing elements protruding out of the plane of the mat -> E04C5/0627 - Three-dimensional metallic reinforcing mats -> E04C5/0636 - Bases for plaster -> E04F13/04
[E04C 5/06] of high bending resistance, i.e. of essentially three-dimensional [3D] extent, e.g. lattice girders -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Anchorage devices specially adapted for balconies -> E04B1/0038 - Supporting devices for connector reinforcing rods for concrete walls, e.g. reinforcement connection boxes -> E04G21/125
[E04C 5/0604] Prismatic or cylindrical reinforcement cages composed of longitudinal bars and open or closed stirrup rods  E04C5/0631  takes precedence -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Reinforcing mats combined with separate prefabricated reinforcement cages or girders -> E04C5/0631  **Glossary:** - open stirrup rod: Rebar bent in an open loop - closed stirrup rod: Rebar bent in a closed loop
[E04C 5/0609] Closed cages composed of two or more coacting cage parts, e.g. transversally hinged or nested parts -- This subclass pertains to closed reinforcement cages for concrete that are built from two or more interacting parts, such as sections joined by transverse hinges or nested together. It covers structural elements where the cage consists of multiple cooperating components forming a sealed assembly.
[E04C 5/0613] Closed cages made of one single bent reinforcement mat -- E04C 5/0613 covers concrete reinforcement cages that consist of a single bent reinforcement mat formed into a closed shape, providing a prefabricated, self-contained reinforcing element for fixed building structures. It applies to structural elements where the entire cage is made from one continuous piece of mesh rather than assembled from separate parts.
[E04C 5/0618] Closed cages with spiral- or coil-shaped stirrup rod -- This class covers concrete reinforcement systems in which the cage is fully closed and the stirrups are formed as continuous spiral or coil rods, providing confinement and improved load distribution within reinforced concrete elements.
[E04C 5/0622] Open cages, e.g. connecting stirrup baskets  E04C5/0609  takes precedence -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Closed cages composed of two or more co-acting cage parts -> E04C5/0609  **Glossary:** - stirrup basket: a substantially U-shaped channel like basket made of a series of parallel open stirrups positioned one after the other and linked by at least one longitudinal rebar
[E04C 5/0627] Three-dimensional reinforcements composed of a prefabricated reinforcing mat combined with reinforcing elements protruding out of the plane of the mat  E04C5/0645  takes precedence -- This entry covers three-dimensional reinforcements where a prefabricated reinforcing mat is combined with elements that protrude out of the mat's plane, used to strengthen concrete structural components. It falls under fixed constructions, building structural elements, and takes precedence over subclass E04C 5/0645.
[E04C 5/0631] Reinforcing mats combined with separate prefabricated reinforcement cages or girders  E04C5/064  takes precedence -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Three-dimensional reinforcing mats composed of two parallel mats connected by separate connecting parts -> E04C5/064  **Glossary:** - girder: a light-weight metallic girder that can also be used as concrete reinforcement
[E04C 5/0636] Three-dimensional reinforcing mats composed of reinforcing elements laying in two or more parallel planes and connected by separate reinforcing parts  E04C5/0645  takes precedence -- **Glossary:** - separate reinforcing parts: parts that are not connected to the reinforcing elements before being connected to them
[E04C 5/064] the reinforcing elements in each plane being formed by, or forming a, mat of longitunal and transverse bars -- E04C 5/064 covers concrete reinforcement where, in each plane, the reinforcing elements are arranged as a mat composed of longitudinal bars intersected by transverse bars, creating a grid-like network that distributes loads and improves tensile strength throughout the structural element.
[E04C 5/0645] Shear reinforcements, e.g. shearheads for floor slabs -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Metallic reinforcements specially adapted for shear reinforcement, e.g. shearheads for floor slabs, shear baskets, shear spiral reinforcements, shear studs,
[E04C 5/065] Light-weight girders, e.g. with precast parts -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Light-weight girders, e.g. with precast concrete, like the pre-fabricated light-weight girders for floor construction
[E04C 5/0653] with precast parts -- E04C 5/0653 relates to reinforcing elements for concrete structures that are supplied or installed as precast components, covering the design, manufacture and integration of such precast reinforcement within fixed building constructions.
[E04C 5/0656] with lost formwork -- The code E04C 5/0656 covers reinforcing components used in concrete structures where the formwork is intentionally left to become part of the finished element after the concrete hardens. This "lost formwork" may be metal, plastic or other material that provides temporary shaping or support and remains embedded, eliminating the need for removal.
[E04C 5/07] Reinforcing elements of material other than metal, e.g. of glass, of plastics, or not exclusively made of metal -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Metal elements with non-structural coatings -> E04C5/015 , E04C5/017 - Anchoring devices specially adapted for balconies -> E04B1/0038
[E04C 5/073] Discrete reinforcing elements, e.g. fibres -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Non entirely metallic discrete reinforcing elements, i.e. suitable to be mixed with the concrete before pouring, e.g. glass or carbon fibres
[E04C 5/076] Specially adapted packagings therefor, e.g. for dosing -- This subclass covers specially designed packaging for reinforcing elements used in concrete construction, such as pre-formed packs or containers that enable accurate dosing and handling of the reinforcement on site.
[E04C 5/08] Members specially adapted to be used in prestressed constructions -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Members specially adapted to be used in prestressed constructions, e.g. prestressing tendons and cables, ducts, anchoring devices.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Tensioning of moulded or shaped articles, at the factory -> B28B23/00 ; B28B23/04 - Tools or methods for in situ tensioning -> E04G21/12
[E04C 5/085] Tensile members made of fiber reinforced plastics -- E04C 5/085 covers tensile members fabricated from fiber-reinforced plastics used as reinforcing elements in fixed building constructions such as concrete structures. The class includes rods, bars, strands and similar components that provide tensile strength while being made of composite material rather than steel.
[E04C 5/10] Ducts -- E04C 5/10 Ducts relates to fixed construction elements that serve as reinforcing components in concrete structures, such as channels incorporated in walls or slabs to convey services, allow passage, or provide reinforcement. It falls under the hierarchy Fixed Constructions → Building → Structural Elements → Reinforcing elements, e.g. for concrete.
[E04C 5/12] Anchoring devices -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Tools or methods for tensioning in situ -> E04G21/12
[E04C 5/122] the tensile members are anchored by wedge-action -- E04C 5/122 relates to building structural elements in which tensile reinforcement, such as steel bars, is secured to concrete by a wedge-action anchoring means, transferring loads through the wedge mechanism to the surrounding material.
[E04C 5/125] the tensile members are profiled to ensure the anchorage, e.g. when provided with screw-thread, bulges, corrugations -- E04C 5/125 addresses reinforced concrete structures in which tensile members such as rods or bars are given a profiled surface - for example screw-threads, bulges or corrugations - to improve anchorage and load transfer. It belongs to the fixed-construction hierarchy under building, structural elements and reinforcing elements.
[E04C 5/127] The tensile members being made of fiber reinforced plastics -- E04C 5/127 classifies fixed building structural elements that reinforce concrete where the tensile members, such as bars, rods, meshes or straps, are made from fiber-reinforced plastics instead of metal. It covers design, manufacture and use of these FRP tension components that provide strength and durability in reinforced concrete construction.
[E04C 5/16] Auxiliary parts for reinforcements, e.g. connectors, spacers, stirrups -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Auxiliary parts for reinforcements, e.g. connectors and spacers  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Tools for connecting reinforcing elements -> E04G21/12 - Supporting devices for connector reinforcing rods for concrete walls, e.g. reinforcement connection boxes -> E04G21/125
