[E04C 5/161] Protective caps for the ends of reinforcing bars -- Covers devices that are placed on the exposed ends of steel reinforcing bars in concrete structures to shield them from corrosion, mechanical damage or accidental contact. The caps may be made of polymer, metal or composite material and are designed to fit over the bar tip, providing a smooth, insulated surface that prolongs the durability of the reinforcement.
[E04C 5/162] Connectors or means for connecting parts for reinforcements  E04C5/168  takes precedence -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Machines for joining reinforcing bars -> E04G21/122
[E04C 5/163] the reinforcements running in one single direction -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Connectors connecting reinforcements running in one single direction, either coaxially or only parallel to one another
[E04C 5/165] Coaxial connection by means of sleeves -- E04C 5/165 describes a coaxial connection method for reinforcing elements in concrete where sleeves are placed over adjacent bars to align them and create a continuous load path. The sleeve-based joint enhances structural integrity by providing a secure, aligned link between reinforcement members.
[E04C 5/166] the reinforcements running in different directions -- E04C 5/166 covers concrete reinforcement schemes where bars or meshes are placed in multiple orientations across a structural element, such as intersecting longitudinal and transverse reinforcement or three-dimensional grids, to improve strength, crack control, and load distribution within the element. This subclass falls under fixed constructions, building structural elements, specifically reinforcing elements for concrete.
[E04C 5/167] Connection by means of clips or other resilient elements -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Connection by means of clips or other resilient material of reinforcements running in different directions
[E04C 5/168] Spacers connecting parts for reinforcements and spacing the reinforcements from the form -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Spacers connecting parts for reinforcement together, and spacing the reinforcements from the form
[E04C 5/18] of metal or substantially of metal -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Metallic or substantially metallic spacers spacing the reinforcements from the form or spacing the reinforcements from one another, e.g. spacers between two reinforcing grids (upper and lower) in a floor structure  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Spacers connecting parts for reinforcements and spacing the reinforcements from the form -> E04C5/168
[E04C 5/20] of material other than metal or with only additional metal parts, e.g. concrete or plastics spacers with metal binding wires -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Spacers of material other than metal or with only additional metal parts, e.g. concrete or plastics spacers with metal binding wires, spacing the reinforcements from the form or spacing the reinforcements from one another, e.g. spacers between two reinforcing grids (upper and lower) in a floor structure  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Spacers connecting parts for reinforcements and spacing the reinforcements from the form -> E04C5/168
[E04C 5/201] Spacer blocks with embedded separate holding wire or clips -- Spacer blocks designed for concrete construction that incorporate a separate holding wire or clips within the block to secure reinforcement bars. The embedded wire or clips maintain proper spacing and alignment of the reinforcement during casting, improving structural integrity and simplifying assembly of reinforced elements. This type of block is classified under fixed constructions for building reinforcement components.
[E04C 5/203] Circular and spherical spacers -- E04C 5/203 designates circular and spherical spacers employed in building structures to keep reinforcing elements, such as rebars, at a fixed distance within concrete or similar materials. These spacers are typically molded or manufactured from metal, plastic or composite materials and serve to improve load distribution and reduce cracking. The classification falls under Fixed Constructions, Structural Elements, Reinforcing elements.
[E04C 5/205] Ladder or strip spacers -- E04C 5/205 pertains to ladder or strip spacers employed as reinforcing elements in fixed building constructions, especially for concrete. These devices maintain a uniform spacing of reinforcement bars or support formwork, thereby enhancing structural stability.
[E04C 5/206] Spacers having means to adapt the spacing distance -- E04C 5/206 covers spacers designed for reinforcing concrete elements that incorporate adjustable mechanisms to set the desired gap between structural components. These devices allow the spacing distance to be varied during installation, ensuring proper alignment and load distribution in fixed constructions such as walls, floors, or foundations.
[E04C 5/208] Spacers especially adapted for cylindrical reinforcing cages -- E04C 5/208 covers spacers designed specifically for cylindrical reinforcing cages used in concrete construction. These devices keep the cage centered and maintain a uniform concrete cover, improving placement and strength of the reinforcement.
[E04D] ROOF COVERINGS -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Roof coverings including any similar kind of watertight covering against rain, snow, hail, or the like for other parts of buildings; sky-lights for flat or sloped roofs; roof drainage and gutters; roof-working tools  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Coverings of outer walls by plaster of other porous material -> E04F13/00
[E04D 1/00] Roof covering by making use of tiles, slates, shingles, or other small roofing elements -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Small roof covering elements, like grooved or vaulted tiles, slates, shingles ( E04D1/02 - E04D1/22 ); Special small roof covering elements characterized by their structure or their purpose ( E04D1/24 - E04D1/30 ); Fastenings for small roof covering elements and devices for sealing the spaces between small roof covering elements ( E04D1/34 - E04D1/365 ).  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Roofing supports and under-layers -> E04D12/00
[E04D 1/02] Grooved or vaulted roofing elements -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Small grooved or vaulted roof covering elements, like Spanish, Roman or Barrel tiles  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Roofing elements comprising two or more layers, e.g. for insulation -> E04D1/28 - Special roof-covering elements, e.g. ridge tiles, gutter tiles, gable tiles, ventilation tiles -> E04D1/30
[E04D 1/025] Diamond shaped and installed in diagonal lines -- This group covers roof coverings made of small elements formed in a diamond shape and laid in diagonal lines. It includes tiles, slates or shingles arranged to create a herringbone or basket-weave appearance typical of traditional tiled roofs.
[E04D 1/04] of ceramics, glass or concrete, with or without reinforcement -- The subclass E04D 1/04 covers roof coverings that are produced from ceramic, glass or concrete materials, either plain or reinforced. It includes tiles, slates, shingles or similar small elements made of these substances used to protect and finish a building's roof.
[E04D 1/045] Vaulted roofing elements laid alternately side-up and side-down, e.g. monks and nuns tiles -- This subclass relates to roof coverings made of small tiles that are installed alternately side-up and side-down, creating a vaulted or undulating surface often referred to as monk and nun tiles. The alternating arrangement gives the roof a distinctive pattern and aids water runoff on pitched structures.
[E04D 1/06] of metal -- Roof covering made of metal, using tiles, slates, shingles or other small metal elements to form a fixed roof structure. It includes metal sheets, profiled panels and interlocking pieces designed for durability and weather protection.
[E04D 1/08] of plastics -- This group concerns roof covering components that are made primarily of plastics, including tiles, slates, shingles or similar small elements used in fixed roof construction. It addresses the design, manufacture and application of plastic roofing members as alternatives to traditional mineral or metal materials.
[E04D 1/085] of wood -- The code E04D 1/085 covers roof coverings that are made of wood, such as wooden tiles, shingles, or slates. It falls under the broader category of fixed constructions for building roofs that use small individual elements. This classification addresses the use of timber as the primary material for these roof covering components.
[E04D 1/10] of specified materials , or of combinations of materials, not covered by any one of groups -- E04D 1/10 covers roof covering methods that employ particular materials or mixes of materials that are not listed in the other groups of the roof covering section. It includes any small roofing elements such as tiles, slates or shingles made from uncommon or combined substances, fitting under the fixed construction category of building roof coverings.
[E04D 11/00] Roof covering, as far as not restricted to features covered by only one of groups -- E04D 11/00 classifies roof coverings in general, covering inventions related to the overall structure, material or method of a roof that are not limited to a single, more specific feature group. It includes broad concepts such as protective layers, insulation, weatherproofing and mounting systems where the inventive aspect spans multiple sub-categories of roof covering technology.
[E04D 11/002] consisting of two or more layers, at least one of the layers permitting turfing of the roof -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Turfing tiles -> E04D2001/308
[E04D 11/005] Supports for elevated load-supporting roof coverings -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Supports, e.g. height-adjustable spacers, for load-supporting roof coverings on roofs, e.g. for elevated terrace floors on flat roofs.
[E04D 11/007] Height-adjustable spacers -- Height-adjustable spacers are components placed between roofing elements to vary the distance between them, accommodating irregular substrates and providing controlled ventilation or drainage. They are used in roof covering assemblies where the spacing cannot be fixed by a single rigid element, allowing the roof to maintain proper performance despite dimensional changes. This class falls under the CPC E04D 11/007 for roof coverings not limited to a single specific feature.
[E04D 11/02] Build-up roofs, i.e. consisting of two or more layers bonded together -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Gravelling of flat roofs -> E04D7/005 - Venting or ventilation of roof coverings -> E04D13/17
[E04D 1/12] Roofing elements shaped as plain tiles or shingles, i.e. with flat outer surface -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Small roof covering elements having a flat outer surface, like plain tiles, shingles or slates  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Roofing elements comprising two or more layers, e.g. for insulation -> E04D1/28 - Special roof-covering elements, e.g. ridge tiles, gutter tiles, gable tiles, ventilation tiles -> E04D1/30
[E04D 1/125] Diamond shaped elements specially adapted for being installed in diagonal lines -- E04D 1/125 covers roof covering components shaped like diamonds that are designed to be laid in diagonal rows. These small elements, such as tiles, slates or shingles, interlock or overlap to form a patterned, sloped surface, providing weather protection and aesthetic variation for building roofs.
[E04D 1/14] of slate material, with or without fastening means -- The subclass E04D 1/14 covers roof coverings made of slate, whether the slate is fastened with mechanical means, adhesive, or left unsecured, and is situated within the hierarchy of fixed constructions, building, roof coverings.
[E04D 1/16] of ceramics, glass or concrete, with or without reinforcement -- The E04D 1/16 subclass defines roof-covering components produced from ceramics, glass or concrete, whether or not they are reinforced, and used as tiles, slates, shingles or similar small elements in fixed building constructions.
[E04D 1/18] of metal -- This subclass covers roof coverings made from metal elements such as tiles, sheets, or shingles, and the methods for fixing these metal components to a building's roof structure. It includes all types of metal roofing materials and their installation techniques.
[E04D 1/20] of plastics -- E04D 1/20 covers roof covering elements made of plastics, such as plastic tiles, slates, shingles or other small components used to form a protective roof surface. It falls under the fixed-construction hierarchy for buildings, specifically the roof-coverings subclass that deals with tiled or shingled systems.
[E04D 12/00] Non-structural supports for roofing materials, e.g. battens, boards -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Built-up roofs -> E04D11/02 - Roof covering insulation -> E04D13/16 - Roof constructions consisting of a plurality of parallel similar trusses supporting load-bearing purlins -> E04B7/024 - Self-supporting slabs with non-structural supports for roofing materials -> E04B7/205 ; E04B7/225  **Glossary:** - Roof batten or roof purlin: In an inclined roof, an horizontal profile, e.g. wooden or metallic, for directly supporting the roof covering elements consisting of shingles, tiles, roofing slabs or stiff sheets; the roof battens or roof purlins are supported by the roof rafters, extending in the direction of the roof slope, or on the roof surface, e.g. for a roof made of concrete slabs.
[E04D 12/002] Sheets of flexible material, e.g. roofing tile underlay -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Sheets of flexible material, both water/vapour permeable or not, used as roof underlay and not as roof covering  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Roof covering by making use of flexible material -> E04D5/00
[E04D 12/004] Battens -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Battens for supporting roof covering elements as defined in groups E04D1/00 - E04D3/40 , i.e. small roof covering elements, roofing slabs or stiff sheets.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Roof constructions consisting of a plurality of parallel similar trusses supporting load-bearing purlins -> E04B7/024 - Self-supporting slabs with non-structural supports for roofing materials -> E04B7/205 ; E04B7/225
[E04D 12/006] Batten-supporting means -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Batten-supporting means located between the roof structure, e.g. rafters, and the battens
[E04D 12/008] Ridge-batten brackets -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Specially adapted brackets to support the ridge-batten
[E04D 1/205] of wood -- E04D 1/205 refers to roof coverings whose primary material is wood, encompassing wooden tiles, shingles, shakes and other small wooden elements used to protect a building's roof.
[E04D 1/22] of specified materials , or of combinations of materials, not covered by any one of groups -- E04D 1/22 groups roof-covering constructions that use particular materials or mixtures of materials which are not specifically covered by any other subgroup. It applies to fixed roofing elements such as tiles, slates, shingles or similar small components when the material composition is unique or combined beyond the defined groups.
[E04D 1/23] with tapered thickness -- This subclass covers roof covering elements such as tiles, slates or shingles whose thickness varies across the section, being thicker at one edge and progressively thinner toward the opposite edge. The tapered profile facilitates water runoff, interlocking or overlapping placement, and may improve load distribution.
[E04D 1/24] Roofing elements with cavities, e.g. hollow tiles -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Small roof covering elements having internal cavities in the thickness of the roof covering elements, e.g. hollow tiles  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Roofing elements comprising two or more layers, e.g. for insulation -> E04D1/28
[E04D 1/26] Strip-shaped roofing elements appearing as a row of shingles -- E04D 1/26 covers strip-shaped roofing elements that are laid out in continuous rows resembling shingles. These components are small, linear pieces used to form roof coverings such as tiles, slates or other similar elements on building roofs.
[E04D 1/265] the roofing elements being rigid, e.g. made of metal, wood or concrete -- This CPC subclass refers to roof coverings that consist of rigid elements such as metal sheets, wooden planks or concrete tiles, distinguishing them from flexible or overlapping materials. It is nested under Fixed Constructions, Building and Roof Coverings and specifically addresses roofing made from solid, hard components.
[E04D 1/28] Roofing elements comprising two or more layers, e.g. for insulation -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Roofing elements comprising two or more layers, the layers having a thickness and not being simple coating layers
[E04D 1/29] Means for connecting or fastening adjacent roofing elements -- This subclass covers devices that join adjacent roof tiles, slates, shingles or other small roofing elements, such as clips, fasteners, interlocking profiles or bonding systems, to create a continuous, fixed roof covering. It relates to the means for connecting or fastening neighboring roofing components within building construction.
[E04D 1/2907] by interfitted sections -- This subclass covers roof covering constructions where the individual tiles, slates, shingles or similar small elements are shaped to interfit with each other, forming a continuous covering by inter-fitted sections. It applies to fixed building roof coverings.
[E04D 1/2914] having fastening means or anchors at juncture of adjacent roofing elements -- The entry covers roof covering systems in which fastening devices or anchors are incorporated at the junction where adjacent tiles, slates, shingles or similar small roofing elements meet, providing secure attachment and preventing displacement of the elements.
[E04D 1/2916] the fastening means taking hold directly on adjacent elements of the same row -- E04D 1/2916 is a CPC subgroup under Fixed Constructions → Building → Roof Coverings that deals with roof covering made of tiles, slates, shingles or similar small elements. It specifically covers fastening means that grip directly onto adjacent elements of the same row, securing them without separate connectors.
[E04D 1/2918] the fastening means taking hold directly on adjacent elements of succeeding rows -- This subclass covers roof-covering fasteners that secure a tile, shingle or similar element by gripping the adjacent element of the next higher row, eliminating the need for separate clips or anchors. The fastening mechanism contacts the neighboring piece directly to hold the rows together.
[E04D 1/2921] having resilient detents adapted to be biased to a position to resist separation -- The classification covers roof coverings made of tiles, slates, shingles or similar elements that incorporate resilient detents designed to stay engaged and prevent individual pieces from separating. These detents are pre-loaded to a biasing position, providing a self-locking mechanism.
[E04D 1/2928] having slits receiving marginal edge of adjacent section -- The CPC entry E04D 1/2928 falls under Fixed Constructions → Building → Roof Coverings and deals with roof covering elements such as tiles, slates or shingles that incorporate slits designed to receive the marginal edge of an adjacent piece, providing an interlocking joint between sections.
[E04D 1/2935] folded, rolled, or indented  in situ -- This subclass covers roof coverings formed directly on the roof by folding, rolling or indenting tiles, slates, shingles or similar small elements during installation. The method creates a continuous surface without prefabricated shapes, allowing the material to be shaped in place to achieve the desired profile.
[E04D 1/2942] having folded sections receiving interfitted part of adjacent section -- The entry covers roof covering elements that incorporate folded edges designed to receive and lock the adjoining piece, creating an inter-fitted joint between sections. This folding mechanism provides a secure, water-tight connection for tiles, slates or similar small roofing components.
[E04D 1/2949] having joints with fluid-handling feature, e.g. a fluid channel for draining -- The subclass E04D 1/2949 covers roof coverings made from tiles, slates, shingles or similar small elements that incorporate joints equipped with a fluid-handling feature such as a channel for draining water. It is situated under Fixed Constructions → Building → Roof Coverings in the CPC hierarchy.
[E04D 1/2956] having tongues and grooves -- This subclass covers roof covering elements such as tiles, slates or shingles that incorporate tongue-and-groove features to interlock adjacent pieces, providing improved sealing and alignment. The design enables a continuous surface and simplifies installation.
[E04D 1/2963] having laminated layers overlapping adjacent sections -- The entry refers to roof-covering constructions that use laminated layers which overlap adjacent sections, such as tiled, slated or shingled roofs, to enhance weather resistance and durability.
[E04D 1/2984] with spacing or space-forming features, e.g. braces, fillers or drains -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Means for connecting or fastening adjacent roofing elements, with the additional purpose of forming a space in between said roofing elements.
[E04D 1/30] Special roof-covering elements, e.g. ridge tiles, gutter tiles, gable tiles, ventilation tiles -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Special small roof covering elements, characterized by their special purpose, like ridge tiles, turfing tiles, gutter tiles, gable tiles, ventilation tiles, special tiles allowing walking on the roof
[E04D 13/00] Special arrangements or devices in connection with roof coverings -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Provisions against atmospheric, e.g. wind vortex suppression, vegetal or animal influences on roofs ( E04D13/002 - E04D13/008 ); Sky-lights, domes and roof covering aspects thereof ( E04D13/02 - E04D13/0358 ); Roof drainage, roof gutters, down pipes ( E04D13/04 - E04D13/08 ); Special devices or arrangements for roofs, e.g. snow-traps, junctions of roof sheathings to chimneys, roof trimming strips, roof edge strips, fascias ( E04D13/14 - E04D13/1585 ); Insulating devices for the roof covering ( E04D13/16 - E04D13/1693 ); Ventilation of roof coverings ( E04D13/17 - E04D13/178 )  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Internal channels, e.g. drainage channels, chimneys, air-ducts -> E04F17/00
[E04D 13/002] Provisions for preventing vegetational growth, e.g. fungi, algae or moss -- The CPC entry E04D 13/002 covers measures applied to roof coverings that inhibit the development of vegetational growth such as fungi, algae or moss. It includes protective coatings, treatments, or structural features designed to keep roofs clean and prevent biological deterioration. This falls under Fixed Constructions → Building → Roof Coverings → Special arrangements or devices.
[E04D 13/004] Protection against birds, mice or the like  scaring devices for birds in general  A01M29/00 -- E04D 13/004 classifies devices attached to roof coverings that deter birds, mice or similar pests, including scare devices and other protective arrangements integrated with the roof structure. The subclass falls under fixed construction roof covering accessories aimed at preventing animal damage.
[E04D 13/006] Provisions for detecting water leakage -- The E04D 13/006 entry covers devices, systems or design features built into roof coverings that detect water ingress, such as moisture sensors, leakage-indicating layers or signaling mechanisms that alert occupants or maintenance personnel. It includes methods for early detection and monitoring of water penetration in roofs.
[E04D 13/008] Provisions for reducing rain noise -- E04D 13/008 covers devices or arrangements incorporated in roof coverings that dampen or limit the sound produced by rain striking the roof. It includes acoustic insulation, noise-absorbing panels, and other measures designed to reduce rain noise for improved interior comfort.
[E04D 13/02] Roof-covering aspects of dormer windows -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Sealing between the structure of dormer windows and the roof covering  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Roof covering aspects of sky-lights -> E04D13/03 - E04D13/0358 - Junctions of roof sheathings to chimneys or other parts extending above the roof -> E04D13/14  **Glossary:** - Dormer window: Roof window set in a structure that is protruding from the surface of a sloping roof; the window itself is substantially vertical
[E04D 13/03] Sky-lights -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Roof sky-lights of flat, domed or vaulted shape, their structures, their mechanisms, their connecting means to the roof structure, their ventilating means, their safety means and other means or accessories therefor  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Junctions of roof sheathings to chimneys or other parts extending above the roof -> E04D13/14  **Glossary:** - Sky-light: Window, roof lantern or oculus placed on the roof of a building and which is horizontal or parallel to the surface of the roof - Dome: Roof window having a domed shape, i.e. being curved along any two perpendicular directions - Vaulted shape: Having the shape of a vault, i.e. being curved along one direction only, like a cylinder
[E04D 13/0305] Supports or connecting means for sky-lights of flat or domed shape -- This subclass covers devices that support or connect flat or domed skylights to a roof structure. It includes brackets, frames, or other fittings that secure the skylight and transfer loads to the surrounding roof covering. The classification is used for patent documents describing such roof-integrated skylight mounting solutions.
[E04D 13/031] characterised by a frame for connection to an inclined roof -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Sky-lights or domes having a frame mainly installed in the plane of a sloping roof.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Sky-lights or domes having a curb frame installed in the plane of a sloping roof -> E04D13/0315
[E04D 13/0315] characterised by a curb frame -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Sky-lights or domes having a curb frame installed either on a sloping roof or on a flat roof  **Glossary:** - Curb frame: Raised frame, i.e. a frame that raises from the surface of the roof
[E04D 13/032] Supports or connecting means for sky-lights of vaulted shape -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Supports or connecting means for all kinds of elongated sky-lights, i.e. comprising sky-lights of vaulted shape, but also saddle-roof like elongated sky-lights
[E04D 13/0325] provided with ventilating means  roof ventilation  F24F7/02 -- This subclass covers roof coverings that incorporate ventilating means to promote airflow and control moisture, including vents, louvers or other devices built into the roof structure. It addresses special arrangements that provide passive or forced roof ventilation for buildings.
[E04D 13/033] provided with means for controlling the light-transmission or the heat-reflection, (e.g. shields, reflectors, cleaning devices) -- The subclass E04D 13/033 covers roof-covering systems equipped with features that regulate light transmission or heat reflection, such as shields, reflectors and cleaning devices, to improve energy efficiency or interior comfort. It includes any arrangement that actively controls illumination or thermal gain through the roof covering.
[E04D 13/0335] Skylight guards, security devices protecting skylights or preventing objects or persons from falling through skylight openings -- Skylight guards are security devices fitted to roof openings that prevent objects or people from falling through and protect the skylight from unauthorized entry. They are classified under special arrangements or devices for roof coverings in the cooperative patent system.
[E04D 13/035] characterised by having movable parts -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Sky-lights or domes having movable parts for opening and/or closing, e.g. for ventilation or for preventing the spread of fire
[E04D 13/0351] the parts pivoting about a fixed axis -- The code covers roof-covering components that rotate around a stationary hinge, such as vent openings, louvered panels or other movable sections that swing on a fixed axis to open, close or adjust. It includes the mechanisms and devices that enable this pivoting action.
[E04D 13/0352] the parts being of domed or pyramidal shape -- E04D 13/0352 covers special arrangements or devices for roof coverings in which the components are shaped like domes or pyramids. It applies to fixed construction elements of buildings such as vent caps, skylights or decorative roof parts having that geometric form.
[E04D 13/0354] the parts being flat
[E04D 13/0355] The parts being of vaulted shape -- This CPC subclass concerns roof covering systems that incorporate specially arranged or device-like components whose shape is vaulted. It falls under fixed constructions for buildings, specifically roof coverings, and addresses the geometry of parts that form an arched or curved profile.
[E04D 13/0357] the parts pivoting about an axis supported on a hinged frame or arms -- This CPC subclass covers roof covering devices where components rotate around an axis mounted on a hinged frame or arms, such as operable vents, louvers or adjustable panels that swing open or closed for ventilation or sunlight control. It falls under fixed constructions, building, roof coverings, special arrangements.
[E04D 13/0358] the parts moving, in their own plane, e.g. rolling or sliding, or moving in parallel planes with or without an additional movement, e.g. both pivoting and rolling or sliding -- The entry covers mechanisms for roof covering components that move within a single plane or along parallel planes, such as rolling, sliding, or combinations of pivoting with rolling or sliding. It pertains to special devices that enable these movements in roof systems.
[E04D 13/04] Roof drainage -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Drainage in or on the field of the roof, drainage fittings in flat roofs, balconies or the like ( E04D13/04 - E04D13/0481 ) .  Roof gutters, down pipes and accessories therefor ( E04D13/064 - E04D13/08 )
[E04D 13/0404] Drainage on the roof surface -- This subclass covers devices, arrangements or methods for removing water from a roof surface, including gutters, downspouts, channels, integrated trays or other structures that collect rainwater or meltwater and direct it away to prevent accumulation and damage.
[E04D 13/0409] Drainage outlets, e.g. gullies -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Drainage devices located in or on the roof surface or its perimeter for receiving water from the roof surface before it reaches the ground, the devices having limited extension and being only located at specific locations in or on the roof surface or its perimeter, e.g. roof gullies
[E04D 13/0431] with horizontal evacuation over the border of the roof -- E04D 13/0431 covers roof covering arrangements that incorporate a horizontal evacuation system extending over the roof edge, providing a walkway or platform for safe egress of personnel or equipment. Such devices are intended to allow movement beyond the roof perimeter, often for fire rescue, maintenance or rescue operations.
[E04D 13/0445] Drainage channels -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Gutters in or on the roof surface for draining water, e.g. inclined valley gutters
[E04D 13/0459] Drainage borders, e.g. dripping edges, gravel stops or dispersers -- The entry covers devices attached to roof coverings that direct or control water runoff, such as dripping edges, gravel stops or dispersers. These components are installed at the perimeters of roofs to manage drainage, prevent water infiltration and distribute rainwater away from the structure.
[E04D 13/0477] Underroof drainage layers  layers permitting turfing of the roof  E04D11/002 -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Drainage layers under the outer surface of the roof, e.g. drainage layer under the tiles of a terrazzo style flat roof  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - roofing tiles flexible underlays -> E04D12/002
[E04D 13/0481] Drainage guiding provisions, e.g. deflectors or stimulation by inclined surfaces -- E04D 13/0481 covers special devices incorporated into roof coverings that direct water away from the roof, such as deflectors, angled surfaces or other guiding elements that stimulate runoff and improve drainage. These arrangements are designed to control the flow of rainwater and prevent accumulation on the roof.
[E04D 13/064] Gutters -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Roof gutters around the borders of sloping roofs  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Drainage channels on the roof surface, e.g. inclined valley gutters -> E04D13/0445
[E04D 13/0641] Gutter ends -- E04D 13/0641 designates the part of a roof covering system that terminates a gutter, covering caps, outlets or connections that guide water from the gutter to downspouts or other drainage. The code applies to designs that form the end portion of a gutter and any associated sealing or attachment features.
[E04D 13/0643] Gutter corners -- E04D 13/0643 covers gutter corners, which are fittings that join two sections of a roof gutter at a right-angle to channel rainwater around the eaves or corners of a building. They are used to maintain effective drainage and protect roof edges from water damage.
[E04D 13/0645] Connections between gutter and down pipe -- This subclass covers specific ways to join a roof gutter to a downpipe, including fittings, clamps, seals, and other devices that ensure a secure, watertight connection. It addresses arrangements that manage water flow from the gutter to the pipe and prevent leakage.
[E04D 13/0646] made of concrete or stone-like material -- This CPC subclass concerns special arrangements or devices for roof coverings that are made from concrete or stone-like materials, such as integral concrete roof panels, stone-sleeve tiles, or prefabricated concrete components designed to protect a building's roof.
[E04D 13/0648] comprising provisions for heat insulation, e.g. an insulation layer -- The entry pertains to roof-covering assemblies that incorporate built-in heat-insulating features, such as an integrated insulation layer positioned within the roof structure. It covers designs where insulation is provided as part of the covering to improve the thermal performance of the building.
[E04D 13/068] Means for fastening gutter parts together -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Connections between gutter and down pipe -> E04D13/0645
[E04D 13/0685] in combination with hanging means -- The CPC subclass E04D 13/0685 covers roof-covering systems that incorporate hanging means as part of a special arrangement or device. It pertains to fixed building constructions where the roofing elements are supported, suspended, or attached using such hanging components.
[E04D 13/072] Hanging means -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Supports connecting gutter to roof  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Supports connecting gutter to roof in combination with means for fastening gutter parts together -> E04D13/0685
[E04D 13/0722] extending mainly under the gutter -- The E04D 13/0722 classification covers devices that extend primarily beneath a gutter to improve the performance of roof-covering systems. These extensions may be used to direct water away from the roof edge, provide additional support, or accommodate special arrangements that enhance drainage or protect the underlying structure.
[E04D 13/0725] situated above or inside the gutter -- E04D 13/0725 covers special arrangements or devices for roof coverings that are positioned above or within the gutter, such as flashings, diverters, or internal gutter systems designed to direct rainwater away from the roof structure.
[E04D 13/0727] situated mainly at the rear side of the gutter -- The classification covers components mounted on the inner or back side of a roof gutter, such as rear-facing extensions, drainage deflectors or protective plates that influence water flow after it leaves the gutter. These devices are part of special arrangements for roof coverings.
[E04D 13/076] Devices or arrangements for removing snow, ice or debris from gutters or for preventing accumulation thereof -- This subclass covers devices or systems integrated with gutters that clear or prevent the build-up of snow, ice or other debris on roof coverings. It includes mechanisms such as heated strips, brushes, or automated cleaning apparatus designed to keep gutters free of accumulation.
[E04D 13/0762] De-icing devices or snow melters -- **Definition:** This place covers:  De-icing devices and snow melters for gutters  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - De-icing devices for roof gullies -> E04D2013/0418 - Devices for mechanically removing snow from gutters -> E04D13/076 - De-icing devices for down pipes -> E04D2013/088 - Snow melters for roofs -> E04D13/103
[E04D 13/0765] Cleaning tools -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Cleaning tools for roof gutters  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Devices for mechanically removing snow from gutters -> E04D13/076
[E04D 13/0767] Strainers at connection between gutter and down pipe -- This subclass covers devices that filter debris at the junction where a rain-water gutter meets a down-pipe, preventing blockages in the drainage system. It includes built-in or add-on strainers and related arrangements designed specifically for roof-covering drainage.
[E04D 13/08] Down pipes -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Down pipes for roof gutters, and clamping means therefor
[E04D 13/10] Snow traps -- E04D 13/10 covers snow traps, which are devices installed on roofs to capture and retain falling snow, preventing its accumulation on the roof surface or shedding onto lower areas. These systems are used to manage snow loads, protect structural elements, and enhance safety.
[E04D 13/103] De-icing devices or snow melters  for drainage outlets  E04D13/0409 , for gutters  E04D13/0762 , for down pipes  E04D13/08 -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Snow melters and de-icing devices on the roof surface  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - De-icing devices and snow melters for roof gullies -> E04D2013/0418 - De-icing devices and snow melters for gutters -> E04D13/0762 - De-icing devices and snow melters for down pipes -> E04D2013/088
[E04D 13/106] Snow removing devices  for gutters  E04D13/076
[E04D 13/12] Devices or arrangements allowing walking on the roof or in the gutter -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Permanent installation on flat and inclined roofs allowing walking  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Terraces on flat roofs; elevated terraces on flat roofs -> E04D11/00 ; E04D11/005
[E04D 13/14] Junctions of roof sheathings to chimneys or other parts extending above the roof -- The subclass E04D 13/14 covers designs for joining roof sheathing to chimneys, vents, skylights or any other structures that project above a roof surface. It includes methods and devices that secure the roof membrane at these intersections to prevent water ingress and ensure structural continuity.
[E04D 13/1407] for flat roofs -- This CPC subclass relates to special arrangements or devices used on flat roofs, falling under fixed constructions for roof coverings. It covers elements such as drainage systems, insulation layers, protective membranes or other components specifically designed for flat-roof construction.
[E04D 13/1415] Junctions to walls extending above the perimeter of the roof  trimming strips, edge strips or fascias retaining the roof sheathing  E04D13/155 -- The entry E04D 13/1415 covers devices and arrangements that join a roof to a wall where the junction projects above the roof edge, such as trimming or edge strips and fascias that secure and conceal the roof sheathing at the perimeter.
[E04D 13/143] with ventilating means -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Junctions of roof sheathings to chimneys or other parts extending above the roof provided with ventilating means
