[E04D 13/1471] using a hinge mechanism for adaptation to the inclined roof -- The entry covers roof-covering systems that incorporate a hinge mechanism allowing the covering to be adjusted to the angle of an inclined roof. It pertains to special arrangements or devices that enable flexible attachment or movement of roofing panels in response to roof slope.
[E04D 13/1473] specially adapted to the cross-section of the parts extending above the roof -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Preformed junctions specially adapted for inclined roofs and to the cross-section of the parts extending above the inclined roof  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Deformable junction strips which are shaped in situ -> E04D13/147
[E04D 13/1475] wherein the parts extending above the roof have a generally rectangular cross-section -- This CPC subclass covers roof covering arrangements in which any parts that extend above the roof, such as parapets or ridge elements, possess a generally rectangular cross-section. It is situated within Fixed Constructions, Building, Roof Coverings, and the group for special arrangements or devices in connection with roof coverings.
[E04D 13/1476] wherein the parts extending above the roof have a generally circular cross-section -- This CPC subclass concerns roof covering constructions where parts that extend above the roof are formed with a generally circular cross-section. It is classified under fixed constructions for buildings, specifically special arrangements or devices related to roof coverings.
[E04D 13/1478] Junctions to walls extending above the roof -- This subclass covers devices or arrangements for connecting walls that project above the roofline to the roof covering. It includes methods for sealing, fastening or integrating the junction to prevent water ingress and to accommodate architectural protrusions while maintaining the roof's integrity.
[E04D 13/15] Trimming strips -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Trimming strips for roofs, roof edge strips, elements for covering the overhang at the eave side, e.g. soffits, or the verge of saddle roofs, roof fascias  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Wall copings -> E04D3/405 - Junctions of roof sheathings to chimneys or other parts, e.g. walls, extending above the roof -> E04D13/14  **Glossary:** - Fascias: In a sloped roof, vertical covering part of the cornice, i.e. of the roof eaves overhang - Soffits: In a sloped roof, horizontal covering part of the underside of the cornice, i.e. of the roof eaves overhang - Edge strip: Finishing strip around the border of a roof
[E04D 13/151] Expansion joints for roofs -- The entry covers devices installed in roof coverings that form an intentional gap to accommodate differential movement of structural elements, helping to prevent cracks, leaks, or stress in roofing materials. These expansion joints are used in various fixed roof constructions and are designed to maintain weather-tightness while allowing thermal or structural shifts.
[E04D 13/152] with ventilating means -- E04D 13/152 covers roof covering systems that incorporate integrated ventilation means, such as vents, air gaps or channels built into shingles, tiles or other fixed roofing elements to provide airflow and assist moisture removal.
[E04D 13/155] retaining the roof sheathing -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Edge strips or fascias retaining the roof sheathing  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Junctions of roof sheathings to walls, extending above the perimeter of a flat roof -> E04D13/1415 - Junctions of roof sheathings to walls, extending above an inclined roof -> E04D13/1478
[E04D 13/158] covering the overhang at the eave side, e.g. soffits, or the verge of saddle roofs -- **Glossary:** - verge of saddle roof: the edge of a saddle (gable) roof at the gable wall
[E04D 13/1585] covering the verge of saddle roofs  gable tiles  E04D1/30 -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Gable tiles -> E04D1/30
[E04D 13/16] Insulating devices or arrangements in so far as the roof covering is concerned, -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Insulation of the roof covering integrated in the roof covering structure, e.g. the insulation being positioned above, between or below the roof purlins; insulation for roofs made of corrugated sheets; insulation applied above the roof covering, e.g. inverted roofs; insulation of pre-existing roofs; insulation of roof coverings, the insulation having provisions for roof drainage.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Roofing slabs or stiff sheets comprising two or more layers, at least one of the layers being of insulating material -> E04D3/351 - E04D3/355 - Self-supporting roof slabs having insulating properties -> E04B7/22 , E04B7/225
[E04D 13/1606] Insulation of the roof covering characterised by its integration in the roof structure  self-supporting insulating slabs  E04B7/22 ,  E04B7/225 -- This class covers roof covering systems where the thermal insulation is built into the roof structure, typically using self-supporting insulating slabs that form part of the roof deck. It includes arrangements in which the insulation is a permanent element of the roofing assembly rather than an after-fit addition.
[E04D 13/1612] the roof structure comprising a supporting framework of roof purlins or rafters -- This CPC subclass (E04D 13/1612) covers roof constructions where the roof's load-bearing framework consists of purlins or rafters that support the covering material. It falls under the hierarchy Fixed Constructions → Building → Roof Coverings → Special arrangements or devices in connection with roof coverings.
[E04D 13/1618] with means for fixing the insulating material between the roof covering and the upper surface of the roof purlins or rafters -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Roof insulating arrangements in which the insulating material is fixed between the roof covering and the upper surface of the roof purlins or rafters, with or without special fixing means  **Glossary:** - Purlins: Battens
[E04D 13/1625] with means for supporting the insulating material between the purlins or rafters  insulating layers disposed between two longitudinal supporting elements  E04B1/7654 -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Roof insulating arrangements in which the insulating material is supported between the roof purlins or rafters, with or without special fixing means  **Glossary:** - Purlins: Battens
[E04D 13/1631] the means deriving from the nature or the shape of the insulating material itself -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Roof insulating arrangements in which the insulating material is supported between the roof purlins or rafters, by means deriving from the nature or the shape of the insulating material itself, e.g. expanding insulation.
[E04D 13/1637] the roof purlins or rafters being mainly insulated from the interior, e.g. the insulating material being fixed under or suspended from the supporting framework -- This CPC subclass covers roof structures where the purlins or rafters are principally insulated from the interior side, with the insulating material attached beneath or suspended from the supporting framework. It relates to special arrangements for roof coverings.
[E04D 13/1643] the roof structure being formed by load bearing corrugated sheets, e.g. profiled sheet metal roofs -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Roof insulating arrangements in which the insulating material is supported by load bearing corrugated sheets, e.g. profiled sheet metal roofs
[E04D 13/165] Double skin roofs -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Insulation for roof coverings comprising a double skin, i.e. two superposed water tight layers
[E04D 13/1656] Closure strips or elements filling the spaces between the corrugated sheets and a supporting wall -- Closure strips or filler elements are components installed between corrugated roof sheets and the adjoining supporting wall to seal gaps, protect against weather penetration and improve thermal performance. They are used in fixed roof constructions where the sheeting meets a wall.
[E04D 13/1662] Inverted roofs or exteriorly insulated roofs -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Inverted roofs or exteriorly insulated roofs, e.g. in which the insulation is exposed to the weather
[E04D 13/1668] the insulating material being masses or granules applied  in situ -- E04D 13/1668 covers roof-covering arrangements where the insulation is supplied as loose masses or granules that are placed and formed directly on site. The classification includes methods and devices for in-situ application of such particulate insulating material to improve thermal performance of roofs.
[E04D 13/1675] on saddle-roofs or inclined roof surfaces -- E04D 13/1675 covers special arrangements or devices that are mounted on or integrated into saddle-roofs or other inclined roof surfaces, such as systems for drainage, ventilation, or mounting of equipment.
[E04D 13/1681] Insulating of pre-existing roofs with or without ventilating arrangements -- This CPC subclass covers methods and devices for adding insulation to existing roof structures, optionally combined with ventilation systems to improve thermal performance and moisture control. It includes retrofitting measures that do not require removal of the original covering and may involve external or internal insulation layers.
[E04D 13/1687] the insulating material having provisions for roof drainage -- This subclass covers roof covering systems in which the insulating material incorporates built-in drainage provisions, such as integrated weep holes, channels or sloped surfaces that direct water away from the roof. It applies to fixed building constructions.
[E04D 13/1693] the upper surface of the insulating material forming an inclined surface -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Roof insulating arrangements in which the upper surface of the insulating material forms an inclined surface, e.g. sloped insulating elements on a flat roof
[E04D 13/17] Ventilation of roof coverings not otherwise provided for -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Ventilation of the spaces between the roof covering and the roof structure  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Roof ventilation, i.e. ventilation of spaces or rooms located below the roof supporting structure -> F24F7/02
[E04D 13/172] Roof insulating material with provisions for or being arranged for permitting ventilation of the roof covering -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Ventilation of roof coverings provided through roof insulating material having provisions for or being arranged for permitting ventilation of the roof covering, e.g. insulation so arranged that it provides ventilation gaps
[E04D 13/174] on the ridge of the roof -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Ventilation of roof coverings provided through ventilating means located on the ridge of the roof
[E04D 13/176] formed by flexible material suitable to be rolled up -- A roofing element made of a flexible sheet that can be unrolled to cover a roof and rolled up for storage, such as a tarpaulin, canvas or other pliable material designed to provide temporary protection or to form a retractable roof system.
[E04D 13/178] on the eaves of the roof -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Ventilation of roof coverings provided through ventilating means located on the eaves of the roof
[E04D 1/34] Fastenings for attaching roof-covering elements to the supporting elements -- Fastenings for attaching roof-covering elements to the supporting structure are devices that secure tiles, slates, shingles or similar small roofing pieces to roof decks, rafters or battens. They include nails, screws, clips, hooks, adhesive strips or specialized brackets designed to hold the coverings in place while allowing for thermal expansion, wind loads and weather resistance. These fastenings ensure a durable, weather-tight roof assembly.
[E04D 1/3402] for ridge or roofhip tiles -- The CPC subclass E04D 1/3402 covers roof coverings that consist of ridge or roof-hip tiles, which are small masonry or concrete elements used to finish the upper edges of a pitched roof. This entry falls under the hierarchy of fixed constructions, building, roof coverings, and specifically addresses tiles, slates, shingles or similar components employed at roof ridges and hips.
[E04D 1/3405] for eaves, valley, or verge tiles -- E04D 1/3405 covers roof-covering elements specifically designed for eaves, valleys or verges, such as tiles, slates or shingles that seal and protect these roof edges and intersections. The entry is part of the Fixed Constructions-Building-Roof Coverings hierarchy in the Cooperative Patent Classification used by the EPO and USPTO.
[E04D 1/36] Devices for sealing the spaces or joints between roof-covering elements -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Devices for sealing the spaces or joints between small roof covering elements, like sealing-strips  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Channels at the intersection of roof surfaces -> E04D13/0445 - Sealing joints not restricted to roof-covering elements -> E04B1/68
[E04D 1/365] Sealing strips between lateral sides of roof-covering elements -- The subclass concerns the application of sealing strips positioned between the lateral sides of individual roof-covering elements such as tiles, slates or shingles to improve weather tightness and prevent water infiltration.
[E04D 15/00] Apparatus or tools for roof working -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Apparatus or tools for roof working like e.g. apparatus or tools for removing roof material, for cleaning the roof, for building roof coverings comprising tiles, shingles or the like, for building roof coverings comprising slabs, sheets or flexible material for handling, during construction of the roof covering, roofing or sealing material in roll or bulk form  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Devices for removing snow, ice or debris from gutters -> E04D13/076 - De-icing devices for gutters -> E04D13/0762 - Cleaning tools for gutters -> E04D13/0765 - Scaffolds for working on roofs -> E04G3/26
[E04D 15/003] for removing roof material  for floor coverings  E04G23/006
[E04D 15/006] for cleaning the roof  for cleaning gutters  E04D13/0765 ; for removing snow from roofs  E04D13/106
[E04D 15/02] for roof coverings comprising tiles, shingles, or like roofing elements -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Apparatus or tools for removing roof material -> E04D15/003
[E04D 15/025] Templates, gauges or spacers for installing roof tiles or roof laths  templates for rafters  E04G21/1891 -- The classification covers devices such as templates, gauges and spacers used to position and align roof tiles, laths or rafters during installation, ensuring accurate spacing and alignment. It falls under fixed constructions, building, roof coverings and apparatus or tools for roof working.
[E04D 15/04] for roof coverings comprising slabs, sheets or flexible material -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Apparatus or tools for handling, removing or applying slabs, sheets or flexible materials to roofs  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Apparatus for handling roofing or sealing material in roll form -> E04D15/06
[E04D 15/06] for handling roofing or sealing material in roll form -- E04D 15/06 covers devices designed to handle roofing or sealing materials supplied in roll form, such as rollers, unrollers or feed mechanisms used during installation of roof coverings. These tools facilitate the positioning, unwinding and application of rolled sheet material on roofs.
[E04D 15/07] for handling roofing or sealing material in bulk form -- E04D 15/07 classifies devices used in roof construction for handling roofing or sealing materials supplied in bulk. It covers apparatus such as conveyors, mixers, dispensers and spreading tools that move, distribute or apply large quantities of compounds like tar, bitumen or sealants during roofing work.
[E04D 2001/005] the roofing elements having a granulated surface -- This CPC subclass covers roofing elements that have a granule-coated surface, such as tiles or slates with granules applied to improve weather resistance and aesthetics. It also includes manufacturing methods and material choices where granules are bonded to the outer face of the roofing component.
[E04D 2001/301] at roof edges, e.g. intersections with walls -- E04D 2001/301 relates to the treatment of roof edges where they intersect walls, covering flashing, sealing and finishing measures that ensure weather-tightness and structural continuity at these junctions.
[E04D 2001/302] Gable tiles -- E04D 2001/302 designates gable tiles, which are roofing elements placed on the triangular upper part of a building façade. The code covers various types of tiles - ceramic, metal or composite - intended to protect and finish the gable section of a roof.
[E04D 2001/303] Eave tiles -- E04D 2001/303 pertains to eave tiles, the roofing components that line the eaves of a building to shield the roof edge and add a finished appearance. It covers the design, material composition, attachment techniques and integration of these tiles with overall roof structures.
[E04D 2001/304] at roof intersections, e.g. valley tiles, ridge tiles -- E04D 2001/304 relates to roof-intersection components that manage water flow, such as valley tiles and ridge tiles. It covers the design, arrangement and fitting of these elements at the meeting points of roof planes to direct runoff and protect the structure from weather ingress.
[E04D 2001/305] Ridge or hip tiles -- E04D covers roofing tiles, and the subcategory 2001/305 specifically refers to ridge or hip tiles that seal the roof's ridges and hips where roof planes meet. These tiles protect the joint from weather, often overlap and may be made of clay, concrete or metal, providing both waterproofing and a finished appearance.
[E04D 2001/306] Under-ridge tiles -- E04D 2001/306 covers under-ridge tiles, which are roofing tiles installed beneath the ridge line to provide continuous covering and weather protection. These tiles are positioned lower than the ridge tile and typically overlap the ridge course, helping to seal roof joints and improve water runoff. The classification is used for patents relating to the design, material, or installation methods of such tiles.
[E04D 2001/307] for passages in the roof surface -- The CPC subgroup E04D 2001/307 refers to roof constructions that incorporate openings or passages through the roof surface. It covers features such as skylights, roof hatches, ventilation ducts, chimneys, and any passages that provide access or allow flow of light, air, or services through the roof. This classification is used for inventions that specifically relate to the design, sealing, or support of such roof openings.
[E04D 2001/308] for special purposes not otherwise provided for, e.g. turfing tiles, step tiles -- The CPC subclass E04D 2001/308 pertains to floor-covering elements designed for special applications not otherwise classified, such as tiles used for turfing or as step tiles. It falls within the broader group for floor-covering components.
[E04D 2001/309] Ventilation tiles -- E04D 2001/309 covers ventilation tiles, which are building components integrated into roofs or walls to allow controlled air exchange while protecting against weather. These tiles are typically made of ceramic, metal or polymer and may include adjustable openings or filters to regulate airflow for ventilation or cooling.
[E04D 2001/3408] characterised by the fastener type or material -- This CPC subgroup covers patents for doors, shutters or similar building elements where the distinguishing feature is the kind of fastener used - such as screws, rivets, adhesives - or the material of the fastener, like steel, polymer or composite, that influences assembly or performance.
[E04D 2001/3411] Metal wires or rods -- The CPC code E04D 2001/3411 covers metal wires or rods employed in civil-engineering works, such as reinforcement, tensioning, anchoring or stabilising elements in structures, foundations and underground constructions. It groups inventions that use metallic wire or rod members to provide strength, rigidity or load-transfer functions in building or civil-engineering applications.
[E04D 2001/3414] Metal strips or sheet metal -- This CPC entry covers inventions that involve metal strips or sheet metal employed as building elements, such as roofing, cladding, flooring or similar coverings, including their manufacture, shaping, joining, fixing or supporting methods. It is used to classify patents dealing with metal sheet components and related construction techniques.
[E04D 2001/3417] Synthetic, composite or molded material -- E04D 2001/3417 covers building elements made from synthetic, composite or molded materials, such as prefabricated panels, blocks or units for walls, floors and roofs. The classification is used for inventions that involve the design, manufacture or application of these engineered construction components.
[E04D 2001/342] Flexible filamentous ties, e.g. to be twisted or forming nets -- E04D 2001/342 relates to flexible filamentous ties, such as twisted strands or net-like systems, employed to bind, support or reinforce building components, particularly in timber or panel construction. The ties may be made of metal, polymer or composite filaments and are designed to be adaptable to various shapes and loads.
[E04D 2001/3423] Nails, rivets, staples or straps piercing or perforating the roof covering material -- The code E04D 2001/3423 designates fastening devices such as nails, rivets, staples or straps that are used to pierce or perforate roofing covering material. It covers the elements and methods employed to secure roof coverings to the underlying structure, ensuring attachment and weather resistance.
[E04D 2001/3426] Threaded elements, e.g. screws or bolts -- The CPC subclass E04D 2001/3426 covers threaded components such as screws, bolts, studs and similar fasteners used in construction or civil engineering applications. It includes specifications for their geometry, material composition, thread form, and methods of securing or joining structural elements.
[E04D 2001/3429] Press-studs elements or hook and loop elements -- Press-studs elements or hook-and-loop elements are fastening components that use interlocking mechanical connections, such as snap-type studs that engage by pressure or Velcro-type surfaces where hooks engage loops to create a releasable bond. The classification covers the design, structure, and application of these elements in garments, upholstery, and other assemblies where temporary, adjustable attachment is required. It also includes methods for producing and securing these fasteners.
[E04D 2001/3432] Strips covering the gap between the roof covering elements -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Strips fastening the roof-covering elements to an underlying support element, with the additional purpose of covering the gap between roof covering elements.
[E04D 2001/3435] Adhesive, hardenable or heat-sensitive material -- The CPC entry E04D 2001/3435 covers adhesive compositions that harden or become activated by heat, intended for joining building elements such as floor, wall or roof coverings. It includes both chemically curable and thermally-sensitive binders and the methods for applying them to attach covering materials in construction.
[E04D 2001/3438] Fasteners comprising several coacting elements -- E04D 2001/3438 covers fasteners formed from two or more interacting components that cooperate to join or secure structures, such as bolts with nuts, screws with threaded inserts, clips with locking elements, and similar multi-part fastening assemblies.
[E04D 2001/3441] Series of easily separable fasteners -- E04D 2001/3441 covers a series of easily separable fasteners, such as snap-type, hook-and-loop, magnetic, and pressure-release connectors, designed for rapid attachment and detachment without tools. The classification includes fasteners that can be engaged and disengaged repeatedly, often used in clothing, upholstery, and industrial assemblies where quick access or reconfiguration is required.
[E04D 2001/3444] characterised by the roof covering or structure with integral or premounted fastening means -- E04D 2001/3444 defines roof covering or roof structure that includes integral or premounted fastening means, covering any system where the fastening components are built into the covering or installed as a ready-to-use unit, facilitating attachment to the roof deck.
[E04D 2001/3447] the fastening means being integral or premounted to the roof covering -- E04D 2001/3447 covers roof covering systems whose fastening elements are built into or pre-installed on the panels, such as integrated clips, rivets, or anchoring ribs that secure the covering without separate fasteners. The classification applies to methods and products where the attachment means are part of the roofing material itself, simplifying installation and improving sealing.
[E04D 2001/345] the fastening means being integral or premounted to the roof structure -- E04D 2001/345 covers roof covering elements whose fastening means are built into the panel or supplied already attached to the roof structure, enabling quick and secure installation without separate mounting hardware. The classification includes systems where clips, brackets or locking devices are integral to the covering or pre-installed on the supporting framework.
[E04D 2001/3452] characterised by the location of the fastening means -- The CPC subgroup E04D 2001/3452 covers roof or building-covering inventions that are distinguished by the position of the fastening means, such as screws, nails or clips, relative to the covering material or supporting structure. It includes features that specify fastening at edges, in overlap zones, concealed within layers or otherwise located to improve sealing, load distribution or ease of installation. This classification isolates designs where the fastening location is a defining technical element.
[E04D 2001/3455] on the internal surface of the roof covering elements -- The entry covers inventions that relate to the interior side of roof-covering elements such as tiles, slates, panels or membranes and to any treatment, coating, layer or functional feature applied there. It includes measures for moisture protection, insulation, fire resistance, acoustic improvement or other performance enhancements on the surface that faces the building interior.
[E04D 2001/3458] on the upper or lower transverse edges of the roof covering elements -- The CPC entry E04D 2001/3458 pertains to roof covering elements such as tiles or slates and specifically to features located on their upper or lower transverse edges. It covers design aspects, sealing, fastening or protective measures applied to those edges to improve weather tightness, stability or installation of the roof covering.
[E04D 2001/3461] on the lateral edges of the roof covering elements -- The CPC subclass E04D 2001/3461 deals with features located on the lateral edges of roof-covering elements, such as the sealing, flashing or joining arrangements applied to the side boundaries of tiles, shingles, panels or similar coverings to prevent water ingress and improve edge integration.
[E04D 2001/3464] on the lateral corners of the roof covering elements for diagonally tiled roofs -- The entry deals with devices or features fitted to the side corners of roof covering elements on roofs that are tiled diagonally, such as corner plates, caps or sealing elements that protect the joint, improve waterproofing and stabilize the tiles where they meet at the lateral edges.
[E04D 2001/3467] through apertures, holes or slots -- The CPC subclass E04D 2001/3467 covers roof components that incorporate through apertures, holes or slots, such as ventilated or light-transmitting openings integrated into roofing materials. It includes designs where these cut-outs serve functions like airflow, illumination, access or drainage within the roof structure.
[E04D 2001/347] characterised by the fastening pattern -- E04D 2001/347 relates to doors, windows, shutters, gates or similar enclosures where the particular arrangement or pattern of fastening elements - such as hinges, latches, bolts or interlocking parts - is the distinguishing technical feature of the invention. It covers designs in which the fastening pattern contributes to the function, sealing, security or aesthetic of the product.
[E04D 2001/3473] fastening single roof elements to the roof structure with or without indirect clamping of neighbouring roof covering elements -- The E04D 2001/3473 subclass addresses techniques for attaching individual roof components directly to the underlying roof structure, such as by screws, nails, clips or adhesive, and may also include arrangements that indirectly clamp adjacent covering elements to secure them together. It covers both the fastening hardware and the method of installation that allows single elements to be fixed without relying solely on the pressure from neighboring pieces. This subclass is used for roofing systems where each element can be independently secured.
[E04D 2001/3476] the fastening means taking hold directly on adjacent elements of the same row and fastening them simultaneously to the roof structure -- E04D 2001/3476 covers roof-covering fasteners that grip two adjacent elements of the same row at once and attach them directly to the roof structure, allowing simultaneous securing of the pair with a single device such as a clip or screw. This reduces installation steps and improves alignment of the joined roofing components.
[E04D 2001/3482] the fastening means taking hold directly on elements of succeeding rows and fastening them simultaneously to the structure -- This CPC subclass covers fastening devices that engage directly with components of consecutive rows of building elements-such as bricks, blocks or tiles-and secure them to the structure in a single operation. It includes mechanisms that clamp or lock multiple layers simultaneously, improving the speed and stability of masonry assembly.
[E04D 2001/3488] characterised by the type of roof covering elements being fastened -- E04D 2001/3488 relates to roof constructions in which the covering elements-such as tiles, slates, metal sheets or similar panels-are secured to the roof structure by fastening means. The classification covers various methods of attachment, including nails, screws, clips, adhesives or other mechanical devices used to hold the covering in place.
[E04D 2001/3491] made of flexible material, e.g. shingles -- E04D covers roofing and roof-covering elements. Code 2001/3491 designates roof covering components made of flexible material, such as shingles, that are typically installed in overlapping rows to provide weather protection.
[E04D 2001/3494] made of rigid material having a flat external surface -- E04D 2001/3494 covers doors, windows, shutters or similar barriers that are constructed from a rigid material and feature a flat external surface, such as solid wood, metal or composite panels without any protruding or decorative surface shaping. This classification is used to identify building elements where the outer face is planar and unstructured.
[E04D 2001/3497] Spanish type tiles -- E04D covers tiles, bricks and similar building elements. The subgroup 2001/3497 designates Spanish-type tiles, characterized by a curved profile that allows overlapping installation, typically used for roofing or flooring and often glazed or terracotta. These tiles are defined by their shape and method of laying rather than material.
[E04D 2003/065] Corner- or point-supported glazing -- E04D 2003/065 covers glazing that is held in place primarily by supports located at its corners or at discrete points rather than along its edges. The classification includes windows, façade panels or skylights where the glass is suspended or clamped at corner fittings, bolts, brackets or other point fixtures, allowing minimal framing. It applies to constructions where the support arrangement reduces the visible frame or provides structural simplicity.
[E04D 2003/0806] the supporting section of the glazing bar consisting of one single extruded or rolled metal part -- This CPC entry defines the supporting section of a glazing bar that is made as a single extruded or rolled metal part, used to hold the glass in window or façade systems.
[E04D 2003/0812] the supporting section of the glazing bar consisting of one single bent or otherwise deformed sheetmetal -- E04D 2003/0812 pertains to the supporting section of a glazing bar that is made from a single piece of sheet metal which is bent or otherwise deformed to provide structural support for the glass. The feature is used in window and façade systems to simplify manufacturing and improve rigidity.
[E04D 2003/0818] the supporting section of the glazing bar consisting of several parts, e.g. compound sections -- The CPC entry E04D 2003/0818 covers the supporting portion of a glazing bar that is built from multiple elements, for example compound sections, used to reinforce or adjust the window frame that holds the glass. It pertains to window construction features that provide structural support within the glazing assembly.
[E04D 2003/0825] the metal section covered by parts of other material -- This subgroup deals with metal structural sections that are encased, clad or otherwise covered by another material such as concrete, polymer, composite or stone, primarily to enhance corrosion resistance, durability, fire protection or appearance in building and civil-engineering applications.
[E04D 2003/0831] Glazing gaskets of particular shape -- E04D 2003/0831 pertains to glazing gaskets that have a distinct shape or contour, designed to improve sealing, weather-proofing or acoustic performance in windows, doors or similar openings. The entry covers specially configured gasket profiles, such as angled, ribbed or interlocking forms, used in the assembly of glazing units. It falls within the CPC group for window and door accessories.
[E04D 2003/0837] Sections comprising intermediate parts of insulating material -- E04D 2003/0837 covers building sections made of insulating material that is placed between structural elements, such as walls, roofs or floors, to provide thermal or acoustic isolation. The code applies to constructions where the insulating layer forms an intermediate part rather than the primary load-bearing component. It is used to classify designs that integrate insulation within the building envelope for energy-efficiency or sound-proofing purposes.
[E04D 2003/0843] Clamping of the sheets or glass panes to the glazing bars by means of covering strips -- E04D 2003/0843 relates to the clamping of glass sheets or panes to glazing bars by using covering strips that hold the panes in place, providing both mechanical fixation and sealing. It covers devices or methods that attach the strips to the bars to secure the glass within windows, doors or similar building enclosures.
[E04D 2003/085] locked by snap action -- This CPC subclass covers door or window locking devices that operate by a snap-action mechanism, where a spring-loaded component snaps into or out of engagement to secure or release the closure. It is used for quick, reliable locking without manual turning of a latch.
[E04D 2003/0856] locked by screws, bolts or pins -- This CPC subgroup classifies building components such as doors, windows, shutters or gates that are secured by mechanical fasteners like screws, bolts or pins. The entry covers designs where the closing or locking action relies on these fasteners rather than hinges or latches, and is used to identify patents that describe such locking arrangements.
[E04D 2003/0862] by means of separate clips or hooks -- The CPC entry E04D 2003/0862 falls under the subclass for stairs, escalators and related access devices and covers constructions that are attached by means of separate clips or hooks, such as stair-treads, protective panels or hand-rail fittings that are secured without permanent fixing, using detachable clipping or hooking mechanisms.
[E04D 2003/0868] Mutual connections and details of glazing bars -- The CPC entry E04D 2003/0868 pertains to the design and engineering of glazing bars in windows or curtain walls, focusing on how the bars are mutually connected and the specific geometric or structural details of those connections. It covers the arrangement, joining methods, and auxiliary features that define the performance and aesthetic of the glazing framework.
[E04D 2003/0875] on the ridge of the roof or on intersecting roof parts -- E04D 2003/0875 covers roof-related elements that are positioned on the ridge line or where two roof planes meet, such as ridge caps, ridge vents, flashing or other sealing and protective devices designed to join intersecting roof parts and prevent water ingress. This subclass groups inventions that specifically address the treatment, reinforcement or weather-proofing of roof ridges and intersecting sections.
[E04D 2003/0881] on the eaves of the roof -- The entry covers any structures or accessories that are fitted directly on the roof eaves, including eave brackets, flashing, gutter and down-spout systems, rain-water collection devices, ventilation openings and decorative or protective trim elements that are mounted on or extend from the eaves.
[E04D 2003/0887] Glazing bars for coverings consisting of more than one sheet or glass pane -- E04D 2003/0887 covers glazing bars designed for windows or doors that are formed from two or more glass sheets or panes bonded together. The classification includes any structural or decorative bars that hold or separate the multiple panes within the same covering.
[E04D 2003/0893] Glazing bars comprising means for draining condensation water or infiltrated rainwater -- The classification E04D 2003/0893 refers to glazing bars-mullions, transoms or similar members in windows, doors or skylights-that incorporate built-in channels, ducts or other devices for collecting and draining condensation or infiltrated rainwater away from the glass surface. It covers any structural means integrated in the bars to conduct water to a drain or outlet, improving water management while maintaining the framing function.
[E04D 2003/285] with specially profiled marginal portions for connecting purposes -- This subclass includes building components such as bricks, blocks or panels that feature specially shaped marginal portions to interlock or connect with adjacent units, enabling secure assembly and structural continuity in walls, partitions or similar constructions.
[E04D 2003/3612] Separate fastening elements fixed to the roof structure and comprising plastic parts for avoiding thermal bridges -- The CPC subgroup E04D 2003/3612 covers fastening devices that are mounted on a roof structure and consist of separate plastic components designed to prevent thermal bridges. These elements provide secure attachment while minimizing heat loss through the junction between roof and building envelope.
[E04D 2003/3615] Separate fastening elements fixed to the roof structure and consisting of parts permitting relative movement to each other, e.g. for thermal expansion -- A roof-mounted fastening system in which distinct elements are attached to the roof structure but are designed to move relative to one another, allowing for thermal expansion or other dimensional changes. The elements are separate, fixed components that accommodate movement while maintaining the roof's integrity.
[E04D 2003/3617] with locking or snapping edges located in the thickness of the slab or sheet -- This CPC subclass covers building-wall panels, slabs or sheets that incorporate locking or snap-fit edges hidden within the thickness of the material, enabling the elements to interlock without external fasteners. It relates to constructions where the joining mechanism is integral to the panel's cross-section.
[E04D 2013/034] Daylight conveying tubular skylights -- E04D 2013/034 relates to daylight conveying tubular skylights, which are roof-mounted light tubes that capture natural light and channel it through a hollow conduit to illuminate interior rooms. The classification covers the design of the tube, diffuser, reflective surfaces and mounting arrangements used to improve light transmission and distribution.
[E04D 2013/0345] with skylight shafts extending from roof to ceiling -- E04D 2013/0345 is part of the CPC group covering openings in buildings and specifies the use of skylight shafts that extend continuously from the roof to the interior ceiling, allowing daylight to be directed deep into a space. This element is relevant for designs that aim to improve natural lighting and visual connection between exterior and interior areas.
[E04D 2013/0413] Strainers for drainage outlets -- E04D 2013/0413 designates strainers installed at drainage outlets to prevent solid particles from entering pipe networks. The devices usually consist of a removable mesh or perforated element that filters debris in sanitary, storm-water or industrial drainage systems.
[E04D 2013/0418] with de-icing devices or snow melters -- The CPC subclass E04D 2013/0418 pertains to heating or de-icing installations integrated with road surfaces, sidewalks or other traffic areas that incorporate de-icing devices or snow-melting systems, including methods that automatically apply heat, chemicals or other means to prevent ice buildup.
[E04D 2013/0422] for draining water above the roof level, e.g. gullies with overflow ports -- This CPC subclass (E04D 2013/0422) relates to devices or arrangements for draining water that collects above the roof level, such as roof gullies, channels or pipes equipped with overflow ports to safely discharge excess water. It covers the design of drainage systems that prevent water accumulation on the roof and direct it away from the building.
[E04D 2013/0427] with means for controlling the flow in the outlet -- E04D 2013/0427 relates to openings in building walls, roofs or other structures that incorporate devices for regulating the airflow at the outlet, such as louvers, dampers, flaps or valves. It covers ventilation or exhaust openings where the flow can be adjusted, restricted or stopped automatically or manually. This subclass is used for patents describing means that control the rate or direction of air or fluid exiting a building opening.
[E04D 2013/0436] with sealing means -- The CPC class E04D covers drainage and waterproofing of roofs, and the subgroup 2013/0436 specifies roof structures that incorporate sealing means to prevent water ingress. It applies to flat or low-slope roofs where membranes, gaskets, or other sealing elements are integral to the waterproofing system. This classification is used by both the EPO and USPTO for patent documents detailing such sealed roofing solutions.
[E04D 2013/044] on multiple levels -- The code classifies inventions relating to roofs arranged on more than one level, such as stepped, tiered or double-deck roofs where a roof also serves as a floor for an upper level. It covers structural features and methods for constructing or using multi-level roofing systems.
[E04D 2013/045] on inclined roofs -- E04D 2013/045 pertains to inventions relating to the placement, mounting or securing of devices, systems or structures on roofs that are sloped rather than horizontal. It includes methods and apparatuses for attaching equipment such as solar collectors, ventilation units, antennas or other installations to inclined roof surfaces, addressing aspects like fixing means, weather protection and load distribution. This subclass falls under the broader CPC group for roof constructions and accessories.
[E04D 2013/0454] at the intersection of roof surfaces, e.g. roof valleys -- The code covers architectural elements located where two roof planes converge, such as roof valleys that guide water away from the structure. It includes design and construction details for the shape, sealing, and drainage of these intersecting roof surfaces.
[E04D 2013/0463] Dispersers -- The CPC entry E04D 2013/0463 covers dispersers-chemical agents added to concrete, mortar, grout or similar building materials to improve the distribution of solid particles, lower viscosity and enhance workability. It includes patents on compositions, formulations and methods for using such dispersing additives in construction mixtures.
[E04D 2013/0468] Drip edges -- E04D 2013/0468 covers drip edges, which are roof or wall flashings that project outward at the lower edge of a roof covering to guide rainwater away from the underlying structure and prevent water infiltration. The element is typically metal or similar material and is used in residential and commercial building construction.
[E04D 2013/0472] Gravel stops -- E04D covers drainage structures such as channels, culverts and sewers. The sub-group 2013/0472 - Gravel stops - refers to devices placed at the outlet of a drainage line to retain or filter gravel, preventing it from escaping while allowing water to flow. These stops are used in road, railway or canal works to protect downstream structures and maintain proper drainage.
[E04D 2013/0486] Deflectors -- The CPC entry E04D 2013/0486 - Deflectors - covers devices incorporated in roofing structures that redirect or shield the roof from wind, rain, snow or debris, such as angled plates, baffles or aerodynamic elements placed on or near the roof surface to improve protection and performance.
[E04D 2013/049] of condensation water or infiltrated rainwater -- The code E04D 2013/049 covers features of building structures that manage water originating from condensation or rain that has penetrated the envelope, such as drainage channels, internal gutters, or waterproofing systems designed to collect and direct this moisture away from interior spaces.
[E04D 2013/0495] to storage tanks -- E04D 2013/0495 is a CPC subgroup that deals with earth-moving work related to the installation of storage tanks. It includes methods and equipment for constructing, placing, and securing tanks in the ground, covering foundation preparation, back-filling, and protective measures.
[E04D 2013/0806] Details of lower end of down pipes, e.g. connection to water disposal system -- E04D 2013/0806 pertains to the design and features of the lower portion of downpipes, including the means by which they connect to water disposal or drainage systems. It covers elements such as fittings, junctions, and sealing arrangements that manage the discharge of rainwater from building roofs into sewers or other drainage infrastructure.
[E04D 2013/0813] Water diverters -- E04D 2013/0813 covers water diverters, devices designed to redirect or control the flow of water from a source such as a pipe, roof or drainage system to a desired outlet, preventing accumulation or damage. Typical applications include plumbing fixtures, rain-water management and building drainage solutions that channel water away from vulnerable areas.
[E04D 2013/082] Down pipe branches -- The CPC group E04D covers roof drainage systems, and subclass 2013/082 specifically addresses the design and arrangement of down-pipe branches. It includes the configuration, connection and branching of downspouts or rainwater pipes that collect and convey water from a roof to drainage or storage facilities. This entry is used to classify inventions related to the geometry, fittings and methods of installing such pipe branches.
[E04D 2013/0826] Passages of down pipes through walls or floors -- This subclass deals with arrangements that guide a down pipe, such as rainwater or waste, through a building wall or floor, including the sealing, flashing, and structural provisions required to maintain waterproofing and integrity. It includes various methods of forming openings, sleeves, or brackets that accommodate the pipe while preventing leakage.
[E04D 2013/0833] Elbow pieces -- The CPC class E04D 2013/0833 defines elbow pieces as angular fittings that join two sections of a conduit, pipe or duct to change the direction of flow, commonly at 90° or other angles. These components are used in building-service installations such as ventilation, water, gas or hydraulic systems and are typically made of metal, plastic or composite material.
[E04D 2013/084] Means for fixing down pipes to structure -- The CPC subclass E04D 2013/084 covers devices for attaching down-pipes to a building or other structure. It includes brackets, clamps, straps or other fastening elements that secure the pipe to walls, roofs or foundations, ensuring a stable, watertight connection for rain-water drainage.
[E04D 2013/0846] Interconnecting down pipe parts -- The CPC entry E04D 2013/0846 covers devices and methods for joining sections of down-pipes in building drainage systems. It includes connectors, couplings, brackets or other fittings that interconnect pipe parts to provide a continuous vertical conduit for water runoff. This classification is used for patents relating to the assembly or improvement of down-pipe joints.
[E04D 2013/0853] Valves for controlling the rain water flow -- E04D 2013/0853 covers valves that regulate rain-water discharge in drainage or storm-water installations. It includes inlet, outlet and flow-control valves used in gutters, culverts, catch basins and similar structures to adjust or stop water movement, often featuring manual or automatic actuation.
[E04D 2013/086] Filters -- E04D 2013/086 covers filters employed in building construction and civil engineering. It includes devices that separate solids from liquids or gases, such as water, air, oil, and pollutant filters integrated into drainage, ventilation, or structural components. The classification applies to filter design, materials, and installation within civil works.
[E04D 2013/0866] Access opening for cleaning;  Devices for avoiding cumulation of debris -- E04D 2013/0866 falls under the building-engineering subclass for drainage and waste systems and concerns access openings that allow cleaning of conveyances such as channels or pipes. It also includes devices engineered to stop the accumulation of debris, ensuring unobstructed flow and easier maintenance.
[E04D 2013/0873] Rain water reservoirs integrated in down pipes -- E04D covers building installations for water supply, drainage and waste-water management, and the subgroup 2013/0873 specifically relates to rain-water reservoirs that are built into down-pipes. The code designates devices that combine collection, storage and conveyance of rainwater within the vertical pipe, allowing compact on-site retention and reuse.
[E04D 2013/088] De-icing devices or snow melters -- E04D 2013/088 comprises de-icing devices or snow melters designed to remove ice and snow from surfaces such as roads, runways, sidewalks, bridges or other civil-engineering structures, employing heating elements, chemical agents, air flow or mechanical action to melt, prevent accumulation or facilitate removal.
[E04D 2013/0886] Devices for preventing climbing on down pipes -- E04D 2013/0886 refers to devices fitted to drainage or down-pipes to deter climbing, such as caps, grates or anti-climb fittings that restrict access while still permitting water flow.
[E04D 2013/0893] incorporated in building structure -- The CPC entry E04D 2013/0893 covers building components that have other devices or systems built into the structural parts of a construction, such as integrated wiring, plumbing, heating, ventilation, solar panels or fire-safety equipment that form part of walls, floors or ceilings. It is used for patents where the functional element is permanently incorporated rather than merely attached.
[E04D 2013/1422] Parapet building elements for retaining the roof flashing -- The CPC entry E04D 2013/1422 covers parapet building elements that are designed to retain or support roof flashing. It relates to components of a parapet wall that hold the flashing in place, helping to direct water away from the roof and improve weather-tightness of the building envelope.
[E04D 2015/042] Fixing to the roof supporting structure -- The CPC subclass E04D 2015/042 covers methods, devices and arrangements for attaching elements directly to the supporting structure of a roof, such as brackets, fasteners, anchors and fixing systems used in building construction. It includes solutions for both new-build and retrofitted roofs, addressing load transfer, stability and protection against weather infiltration.
[E04D 2015/045] by nailing -- E04D 2015/045 covers roof-covering installations where the fixing is achieved by nailing. It pertains to methods or apparatuses that attach roofing sheets, slates, tiles or panels to a supporting structure using nails as the primary securing element.
[E04D 2015/047] by screwing -- The CPC subclass E04D 2015/047 refers to roof-related processes or structures that are assembled or secured by screwing. It covers methods of fixing roofing elements, such as panels, sheets or coverings, using screws rather than nails, adhesives or mechanical interlocks. This includes the use of pre-drilled holes, screw-driven fasteners and related tools for reliable roof installation.
[E04D 3/00] Roof covering by making use of flat or curved slabs or stiff sheets -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Roof covering by making use of tiles, slates shingles, or other small roofing elements -> E04D1/00 - Strip-shaped roofing elements appearing as a row of shingles -> E04D1/26 - Roof covering by making use of flexible material, e.g. roofing membranes -> E04D5/00 - Built-up roofs -> E04D11/02 - Roofing supports and under-layers -> E04D12/00 - Sunshades; Awnings -> E04F10/00 - Outer covering for walls not suitable for roof covering -> E04F13/007 - Coverings for buildings of particular purpose, e.g. bus shelters -> E04H - Roof slab elements comprising solar collectors -> F24S20/67 - Roof slab elements comprising solar cells -> H02S20/23
[E04D 3/02] of plane slabs, slates, or sheets, or in which the cross-section is unimportant -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Roofing elements consisting of plane slabs, slates, or sheets not having ribs, corrugations or profiles protruding neither from the plane nor from the margins of the roofing element.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Roofing slabs or stiff sheets comprising two or more layers, e.g. for insulation -> E04D3/35
[E04D 3/04] of concrete or ceramics -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Plane slabs of asbestos cement -> E04D3/18
[E04D 3/06] of glass or other translucent material -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Roof covering for buildings by making use of plane slabs or sheets of glass or other translucent material.  Fixing means specially adapted therefor.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Sky-lights -> E04D13/03 - Dormer-windows -> E04B7/18 - Fixing glass panes by means applicable to windows -> E06B3/54
[E04D 3/08] with metal glazing bars -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - constructions of roof intersections or hipped ends -> E04B7/06 , E04B7/063
[E04D 3/14] with glazing bars of other material, e.g. of glass -- The code E04D 3/14 covers roof coverings made from flat or curved slabs or stiff sheets that use glazing bars of a material other than the sheet, such as glass, to frame transparent sections. It is situated in the Fixed Constructions - Building - Roof Coverings hierarchy and specifies alternative bar materials for glazing.
[E04D 3/16] of metal -- E04D 3/16 refers to metal roof coverings made from flat or curved slabs or stiff sheets, applied in fixed building constructions as a subgroup of roof covering technologies.
[E04D 3/18] of specified materials, or of combinations of materials, not covered by any of groups -- The E04D 3/18 subclass covers roof coverings produced by laying flat or curved slabs or stiff sheets of particular materials, or blends of materials, that are not addressed in any other group within the roof-covering category of the Cooperative Patent Classification. This entry falls under Fixed Constructions → Building → Roof Coverings.
[E04D 3/24] with special cross-section, e.g. with corrugations on both sides, with ribs, flanges, or the like -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Roofing elements consisting of curved slabs or stiff sheets or of plane slabs or stiff sheets having ribs, corrugations, flanges or profiles protruding from the surface or the margins of the roofing element.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Roofing slabs or stiff sheets comprising two or more layers, e.g. for insulation -> E04D3/35
[E04D 3/26] of concrete or ceramics -- This subclass covers roof coverings that are formed by fixed constructions using flat or curved slabs or stiff sheets made of concrete or ceramic materials, applied as the protective outer layer of a building roof.
[E04D 3/28] of glass -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Roof covering for buildings by making use of curved slabs or sheets or of plane slabs or sheets having ribs, corrugations, etc., the slabs or sheets being made of glass or other translucent material.
[E04D 3/30] of metal -- This CPC subclass covers roof-covering elements that are made of metal flat or curved slabs or stiff sheets used in fixed-construction roofs. It includes metal panels, sheets and plates that form the protective covering of a building.
[E04D 3/32] of plastics, fibrous materials, or asbestos cement -- The subclass E04D 3/32 covers roof-covering systems that are formed from flat or curved slabs or stiff sheets made of plastics, fibrous materials or asbestos-cement, applied as fixed constructions on buildings. It specifies the material type for these rigid roofing elements.
[E04D 3/34] of specified materials, or of combinations of materials, not covered by any one of groups
[E04D 3/35] Roofing slabs or stiff sheets comprising two or more layers, e.g. for insulation -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Waterproof roofing slabs or stiff sheets either flat or corrugated comprising two or more layers, e.g. for insulation, and used as roof covering.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Connecting, fastening of double roof covering -> E04D3/3608 - Insulating devices or arrangements in the roof covering -> E04D13/16 - Multi-layered self-supporting roof slabs, and not generally used as roof covering -> E04B7/20 - E04B7/24
[E04D 3/351] at least one of the layers being composed of insulating material, e.g. fibre or foam material -- E04D 3/351 classifies roof coverings formed from flat or curved slabs or stiff sheets in which at least one component layer is made of an insulating material, such as fibre or foam, to improve thermal performance.
[E04D 3/352] at least one insulating layer being located between non-insulating layers, e.g. double skin slabs or sheets -- E04D 3/352 covers roof covering structures that employ flat or curved slabs or stiff sheets where an insulating layer is placed between two non-insulating layers, such as double-skin panels. The arrangement provides thermal insulation while maintaining a composite roof construction.
[E04D 3/354] more than one of the layers being composed of insulating material -- E04D 3/354 designates roof coverings formed from flat or curved slabs or stiff sheets in which more than one of the constituent layers is made of insulating material, covering constructions that use multiple insulating layers within the roof structure.
[E04D 3/355] the insulating layers of adjacent slabs having cooperating edges -- The entry covers roof-covering constructions that use flat or curved slabs or stiff sheets, specifically focusing on the insulating material placed between adjacent slabs where their edges interlock or otherwise cooperate to form a continuous thermal barrier.
[E04D 3/357] comprising hollow cavities -- E04D 3/357 pertains to roof coverings made of flat or curved slabs or stiff sheets that incorporate hollow cavities, which can reduce weight or improve insulation. This subgroup is applied to patents describing roofing elements that use internal voids as a functional feature.
[E04D 3/358] with at least one of the layers being offset with respect to another layer -- This CPC subclass covers roof covering structures that consist of multiple flat or curved slabs or stiff sheets where at least one layer is deliberately staggered or offset relative to an adjacent layer. The offset arrangement improves drainage, mechanical interlock, or thermal performance by preventing continuous joints across the roof surface.
[E04D 3/36] Connecting -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Connecting, in the sense of connecting means to connect adjacent roofing slabs or stiff sheets together along their margins, directly or indirectly, e.g. through a bridging element.  Fastening, in the sense of fastening means to fasten roofing slabs or stiff sheets to the underlying roof structure.
[E04D 3/3601] of roof covering supported by the roof structure with interposition of a insulating layer  E04D3/3607 ,  E04D3/3608 ,  E04D3/361  -  E04D3/369  take precedence -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Fastening means comprising spacer means adapted to the shape of the profiled roof covering -> E04D3/3607 - Fastening means for double roof covering or over-roofing -> E04D3/3608 - Connecting means between adjacent roofing slabs -> E04D3/361 - E04D3/369
[E04D 3/3602] The fastening means comprising elongated profiles installed in or on the insulation layer -- This CPC subclass deals with roof covering systems where the fasteners are elongated profile elements placed within or on the insulation layer to secure flat or curved slabs or stiff sheets. It covers methods and devices for attaching the roofing material to the building structure using those installed profiles.
[E04D 3/3603] the fastening means being screws or nails -- The entry E04D 3/3603 covers roof covering systems that use flat or curved slabs or stiff sheets fastened to the structure with screws or nails. It falls within the Fixed Constructions branch of the building classification, specifically under roof coverings that are attached by mechanical fasteners.
[E04D 3/3605] of roof covering supported directly by the roof structure  E04D3/3607 ,  E04D3/3608 ,  E04D3/361  -  E04D3/369  take precedence -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Fastening means comprising spacer means adapted to the shape of the profiled roof covering -> E04D3/3607 - Fastening means for double roof covering or over-roofing -> E04D3/3608 - Connecting means between adjacent roofing slabs -> E04D3/361 - E04D3/369
[E04D 3/3606] the fastening means being screws or nails -- E04D 3/3606 covers roof coverings formed from flat or curved slabs or stiff sheets and specifies the fastening means used, namely screws or nails, to secure the material to the building structure. It falls under the fixed constructions hierarchy for roof coverings within the Cooperative Patent Classification system.
[E04D 3/3607] the fastening means comprising spacer means adapted to the shape of the profiled roof covering -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fastening means for a profiled roof-covering, comprising spacer means in between the roof covering and the underlying roof structure, the spacer means being adapted to the shape of the profiled roof covering.
[E04D 3/3608] for double roof covering or overroofing -- The entry E04D 3/3608 covers double roof covering or overroofing, where an additional layer of flat or curved slabs or stiff sheets is applied over an existing roof to enhance protection, insulation or durability. It falls under the hierarchy of Fixed Constructions, Building, Roof Coverings, and Roof covering by making use of flat or curved slabs or stiff sheets.
[E04D 3/361] by specially-profiled marginal portions of the slabs or sheets -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Connecting adjacent roofing slabs or stiff sheets together by specially-profiled marginal portions of the slabs or sheets, e.g. standing seams, optionally with additional fastening of the border of the slabs or sheets to the underlying roof structure.  **Glossary:** - Standing seam: A seam between two adjacent roofing slabs or sheets, consisting of two adjacent and upwardly oriented marginal portions of the two slabs or sheets
[E04D 3/362] by locking the edge of one slab or sheet within the profiled marginal portion of the adjacent slab or sheet, e.g. using separate connecting elements -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Connecting adjacent roofing slabs or stiff sheets together by specially-profiled marginal portions of the slabs or sheets and by locking the specially-profiled marginal portion of one slab or sheet within the profiled marginal portion of the adjacent slab or sheet, with or without separate connecting means.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Connecting adjacent roofing slabs or stiff sheets together by specially profiled marginal portions of the slabs or sheets by folding of the edges -> E04D3/364  **Glossary:** - Locking the marginal portions of the slabs or sheets: Completely limiting movement of the marginal portions of the slabs or sheets in a direction perpendicular to the edge of the slabs or sheets
[E04D 3/363] with snap action -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Connecting adjacent roofing slabs or stiff sheets together by specially-profiled marginal portions of the slabs or sheets with snap action, the snap action occurring directly between the two adjacent specially-profiled marginal portions or between at least one specially-profiled marginal portion and a fastening element to the underlying roof structure.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - By closing the space between the slabs or sheets by gutters, bulges, or bridging elements, e.g. strips -> E04D3/366
[E04D 3/364] by folding of the edges -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Connecting adjacent roofing slabs or stiff sheets together by specially-profiled marginal portions of the slabs or sheets, by folding of the edges of the roofing slabs or stiff sheets, e.g. rolled seams, after the roofing slabs or stiff sheets have been mounted on the roof.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - By forcing together the marginal portions of adjacent slabs or sheets, e.g. crimping, clamping -> E04D3/368
[E04D 3/365] by simple overlapping of the marginal portions with use of separate connecting elements, e.g. hooks or bolts for corrugated sheets -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Connecting of adjacent roofing slabs or stiff sheets by simple overlapping of the marginal portions, with or without the use of separate connecting elements, e.g. roof-tile like connections and connections allowing movement in a direction perpendicular to the joint.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Connecting by means of standing seams -> E04D1/361 - E04D1/364  **Glossary:** - Standing seam: A seam between two adjacent roofing slabs or sheets, consisting of two adjacent and upwardly oriented marginal portions of the two slabs or sheets
[E04D 3/366] by closing the space between the slabs or sheets by gutters, bulges, or bridging elements, e.g. strips -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Connecting of adjacent roofing slabs or stiff sheets by closing the space between the slabs or sheets by structural or non-structural gutters, bulges, or bridging elements, not forcing together the marginal portions of the slabs or sheets, e.g. by caps protecting the connection against the weather, by bridging elements having also a structural function, by non-structural strip like bridging elements inserted into grooves in the thickness of the roofing slabs, by gutters draining the water at the joint between the slabs.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Bridging elements forcing, e.g. crimping or clamping, the marginal portions of adjacent slabs or sheets -> E04D3/368  **Glossary:** - Bulge: separate bridging element
[E04D 3/368] by forcing together the marginal portions of adjacent slabs or sheets -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Connecting adjacent roofing slab or sheets by forcing together, e.g. crimping or clamping, the marginal portions of the adjacent slabs or sheets.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Connecting by folding of the edges of the roofing slabs or stiff sheets, e.g. rolled seams -> E04D3/364 - Connecting by means of bridging elements not forcing together the marginal portions of adjacent slabs or sheets -> E04D3/366
[E04D 3/369] by welding of the marginal portions of adjacent slabs or sheets -- E04D 3/369 falls under fixed constructions for building roof coverings, specifically covering roofs by joining flat or curved slabs or stiff sheets through welding of the marginal portions of adjacent slabs or sheets. This method creates a continuous, sealed roof surface by fusing the sheet edges.
[E04D 3/38] Devices for sealing spaces or joints between roof-covering elements -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Roof-covering elements connecting means; roof-covering elements fastening means -> E04D3/36 - Bridging devices connecting adjacent roofing slabs or sheets and sealing the spaces or joints in between -> E04D3/366 - Bridging devices forcing together the marginal portions of adjacent roofing slabs or sheets and sealing the spaces or joints in between -> E04D3/368 - Sealing joints not restricted to roof-covering elements -> E04B1/68
[E04D 3/40] Slabs or sheets locally modified for auxiliary purposes, e.g. for resting on walls, for serving as guttering -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Ridge sealing -> E04D1/36 - Gutters -> E04D13/064 - Trimming strips; edge strips; fascias -> E04D13/15 - Ventilation of roof coverings, on the ridge of the roof -> E04D13/174 - Solar collectors on roofs -> F24S20/67 - Photovoltaic devices on roofs -> H02S20/23
[E04D 3/405] Wall copings -- Wall copings are protective caps or trim fitted to the top of walls within roof-covering systems that employ flat or curved slabs or stiff sheets. They shield the wall from weather and direct water away, typically made of concrete, metal or stone.
[E04D 5/00] Roof covering by making use of flexible material, e.g. supplied in roll form -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Roof covering by making use of flexible material of substantial dimensions, like flexible membranes, flexible sheets, flexible foils supplied in roll form  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Roof covering using stiff sheets -> E04D3/00 - Gravelling of flat roofs; roof covering exclusively consisting of sealing masses applied in situ -> E04D7/00 - Granulated roof covering -> E04D7/005 - Built-up roofs -> E04D11/02
