[E04D 5/12] specially modified, e.g. perforated, with granulated surface, with attached pads -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Roof covering by making use of specially modified flexible foils. sheets or membranes, like flexible foils, sheets or membranes having features that are not continuous in both length and/or width directions, e.g. perforated membranes, or having a surface that is not smooth, e.g. a granulated surface, or having attached pads
[E04D 5/14] Fastening means therefor -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fastening means for roof coverings comprising flexible materials
[E04D 5/141] characterised by the location of the fastening means -- E04D 5/141 refers to flexible roof coverings, such as rolled membranes, where the defining feature is the position of the fastening elements used to secure the material. It includes arrangements where fasteners are located at the edges, along seams, or at specific points on the surface, distinguishing the installation method from other flexible-cover solutions. This subclass falls under fixed constructions, building, roof coverings made from flexible material.
[E04D 5/142] along the edge of the flexible material -- E04D 5/142 concerns the handling or treatment of the edges of flexible roofing membranes, such as those rolled out for installation. It includes edge sealing, overlapping, fastening or protective edging methods that secure the material to the roof structure and prevent water ingress.
[E04D 5/143] in the field of the flexible material -- E04D 5/143 covers roof covering systems that employ a flexible material, such as sheets or membranes supplied in roll form, which is shaped or attached to form a protective covering for a building roof. The classification addresses the use of these pliable elements in constructing or renovating roof surfaces.
[E04D 5/144] Mechanical fastening means -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Mechanical fastening means for flexible roof covering material, like nails, screws, discs, clips, strips perforating or not the flexible material  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Mechanincal fasteners per se, e.g. nails, bolts, screws, discs, clips, if not specially adapted or indicated as fastening means for flexible roof covering material -> F16B
[E04D 5/145] Discrete fastening means, e.g. discs or clips -- E04D 5/145 designates individual fastening devices such as discs or clips that attach flexible, roll-form roof coverings to a building's structure, providing localized points of attachment, sealing and support for membrane roofing systems.
[E04D 5/146] Linear fastening means, e.g. strips -- E04D 5/146 pertains to linear fastening devices, such as strips, used to secure flexible roof-covering materials that are often supplied in rolls. These fasteners provide continuous attachment along the length of the membrane, ensuring a weather-tight seal to the underlying structure.
[E04D 5/147] not perforating the flexible material -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Fastening by gluing -> E04D5/148 - Fastening by welding -> E04D5/149
[E04D 5/148] fastening by gluing -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fastening of flexible roof covering material by making use of glue or adhesive
[E04D 5/149] fastening by welding -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fastening of flexible roof covering material by welding
[E04D 7/00] Roof covering sealing masses applied -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Roof coverings where the roof covering layer is formed by sealing masses, e.g. asphalt, bitumen, applied in situ;  Gravelling of roofs by applying loss gravel or granules in situ
[E04D 7/005] characterised by loose or embedded gravel or granules as an outer protection of the roof covering -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Gravelling of roofs by applying loose gravel or granules in situ, as an outer protection of the roof covering  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Flexible roof covering materials, e.g. membranes or sheets, having a pre-made granulated surface -> E04C5/12
[E04D 9/00] Roof covering by using straw, thatch, or like materials -- E04D 9/00 classifies roof coverings that are made from straw, thatch or comparable natural fibre materials. It applies to fixed building constructions where such organic coverings are used as the principal weather-protective layer on roofs.
[E04F] FINISHING WORK ON BUILDINGS, e.g. STAIRS, FLOORS -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Finishing work on buildings, like:  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Kitchen equipment -> A47J - Sanitary equipment; Accessories therefor, e.g. toilet accessories -> A47K - Tapestry work -> B44C - Floor structures -> E04B5/00 - Roof structures -> E04B7/00 - Suspended ceilings -> E04B9/00 - Roof coverings -> E04D - Repairing -> E04G - Windows, doors -> E06B - Ladders -> E06C
[E04F 10/00] Sunshades, -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Sunshades, e.g. Florentine blinds or jalousies;  Screens on the outside of buildings which provide a shading function;  Outside shading elements mounted to the building;  Awnings; Baldachins  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Awnings for tents -> E04H15/58 - Venetian-type lamellar screens -> E06B9/26 - Operating means -> E06B9/68
[E04F 10/005] Rigidly-arranged sunshade roofs with coherent surfaces -- **Definition:** This place covers:  This class contains shading elements for which the surface that provides the shading function is of a rigid inflexible material, e.g. plexiglass, metal.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Gutters for roofs -> E04D13/064
[E04F 10/02] of flexible canopy materials, e.g. canvas -- **Definition:** This place covers:  This class contains shading elements for which the surface that provides the shading function is of a flexible material, e.g. canvas
[E04F 10/04] with material fixed on sections of a collapsible frame -- E04F 10/04 covers sunshade systems in which the shading material is permanently fixed to individual sections of a foldable or collapsible frame, forming part of the building's finishing work. The classification applies to devices where the frame can be collapsed but the material remains attached to its segments.
[E04F 10/06] comprising a roller-blind with means for holding the end away from a building -- E04F 10/06 covers sunshades that integrate a roller-blind designed so that the blind's lower edge is kept separated from the building façade, typically using a support or frame that holds the blind away to allow airflow and reduce heat transfer.
[E04F 10/0603] with telescopic arms -- E04F 10/0603 covers sunshades that incorporate telescopic arms, allowing adjustable projection and positioning. The subclass falls under fixed constructions and building finishing work such as stairs and floors, and relates to devices that provide shade while being extendable or retractable.
[E04F 10/0607] with guiding-sections for supporting the movable end of the blind -- E04F 10/0607 covers sunshade or blind assemblies in which the movable end of the blind is supported by guiding sections that keep the shade aligned and prevent sagging as it is raised or lowered. The invention relates to finishing work on buildings, such as stair or floor installations, where the blind's travel is controlled by these guide elements.
[E04F 10/0611] with articulated arms supporting the movable end of the blind for deployment of the blind -- This CPC subclass covers building-finishing sunshade devices in which articulated arms support the movable end of a blind during deployment, enabling the blind to be extended or retracted as a shading element on a structure.
[E04F 10/0614] whereby the pivot axis of the articulation is parallel to the roller -- A sunshade that moves by rolling is designed so the pivot axis of its articulating panels runs parallel to the roller itself, enabling the shade to open or close while maintaining a fixed rotational line. This arrangement is classified under the CPC entry E04F 10/0614 within the building-finishing work category for fixed constructions such as stairs or floors.
[E04F 10/0618] whereby the pivot axis of the articulation is perpendicular to the roller -- E04F 10/0618 pertains to sunshade devices used in building finishing work where the articulation's pivot axis is oriented at a right angle to the roller that guides its movement, allowing the shade to open or close by rotating around a perpendicular axle. This configuration is common in architectural shading systems integrated into stairs, floors or other interior surfaces.
[E04F 10/0622] whereby the plane defined by three consecutive articulation points of an articulated arm is not parallel to the rotation axis of the roller, e.g. to create a saddle type roof construction or to prevent contact between the cloth and the arm sections -- This subclass covers sunshade constructions where an articulated arm is arranged so that the plane defined by three consecutive pivot points is not parallel to the roller's rotation axis, a geometry used to form saddle-type roof shapes or to keep the covering material clear of the arm sections.
[E04F 10/0625] whereby the roller is installed on the articulated arms -- This CPC entry E04F 10/0625 classifies sunshade devices where the roller is mounted on articulated arms, allowing the shade to be positioned and retracted as needed on a building façade. It falls under finishing work on buildings within the fixed-construction group.
[E04F 10/0629] whereby the arm is of the lazy tong type -- The CPC subclass E04F 10/0629 relates to sunshades that are part of finishing work on buildings. It specifically covers designs where the supporting arm operates as a lazy-tong, a folding, hinged mechanism that extends and retracts to deploy the shade.

[E04F 10/0637] with mechanisms for adjusting the inclination of the blind
[E04F 10/064] whereby the mechanism changes the inclination of the blind during unrolling -- The code E04F 10/064 relates to sunshades that incorporate a mechanism which alters the tilt of the blind as it is unrolled. It falls under fixed constructions, building finishing work, specifically devices that adjust shading inclination during deployment.
[E04F 10/0644] with mechanisms for unrolling or balancing the blind -- E04F 10/0644 is a CPC subclass for sunshades incorporated in building finishing work that include mechanisms for unrolling or balancing the blind, such as systems that automatically extend, retract, or self-adjust the shading element. It applies to devices used on stairs, floors and other interior or exterior surfaces where adjustable blinds are integrated into the construction.
[E04F 10/0648] acting on the roller tube -- E04F 10/0648 covers devices or methods for manipulating the roller tube of a sunshade or similar fixed construction element, including manual cranks, motor drives, or other actuation mechanisms. The classification applies to the operation of the tube that winds or unwinds the shade within building finishing work such as stairs, floors, or roof coverings.
[E04F 10/0651] acting on the arms -- The entry E04F 10/0651 pertains to sunshades whose operation is carried out by movable arms, covering mechanisms that adjust, extend, retract or otherwise control the shade elements attached to the arms in building finishing work. It includes devices that automate or manually actuate these arms to modulate sunlight and heat.
[E04F 10/0655] acting on the movable end, e.g. front bar -- The code refers to sunshade systems whose shading element is adjusted by acting on the movable end, such as a front bar that pivots, slides or lifts to open or close the shade. It covers mechanisms that control that end manually, pneumatically, electrically or hydraulically to vary the amount of light and heat entering a building.
[E04F 10/0659] Control systems therefor -- The entry E04F 10/0659 covers control systems for sunshades used in building finishing work, such as stairs or floors. It falls under the Cooperative Patent Classification hierarchy for fixed constructions, specifically building finishing, and addresses mechanisms that regulate the deployment, adjustment, or retraction of shading devices.
[E04F 10/0662] with arrangements for fastening the blind to the building -- This CPC subclass covers sunshades that incorporate a system for fastening the blind directly to the building structure, describing methods and devices for attaching the blind to walls, roofs or other fixed elements. It falls under fixed constructions, building, finishing work on buildings, specifically sunshades.
[E04F 10/0666] Accessories -- E04F 10/0666 covers accessories for sunshades used in building finishing work. The classification includes components such as brackets, louvers, control mechanisms or decorative elements that attach to or integrate with sunshade systems to adjust light, heat, or aesthetics. It is situated under fixed constructions, building and finishing work on buildings.
[E04F 10/067] acting as intermediate support of the flexible canopy -- E04F 10/067 covers means that serve as intermediate supports for flexible canopies, such as sunshades, awnings or similar roofing fabrics, installed on or attached to a building's structure. The classification includes brackets, rods, tensioning devices or other elements that hold the canopy between the primary mounting points and the fabric to maintain shape and stability. It is used in the finishing work of buildings where adjustable or movable shading is required.
[E04F 10/0674] acting as separate supporting bar -- E04F 10/0674 covers sunshade devices that are implemented as separate supporting bars attached to a building's structure. It falls under the CPC group for finishing work on buildings such as stairs and floors, specifically dealing with fixed constructions that provide shading.
[E04F 10/0677] acting as centre bearing -- The CPC entry E04F 10/0677 covers sunshade components that are incorporated into building finishes such as stairs or floors and function as centre bearings, supporting structural loads while providing shading. It falls under the hierarchy of fixed constructions, building, and finishing work on buildings.
[E04F 10/0681] Support posts for the movable end of the blind -- E04F 10/0681 classifies structural support posts that anchor the moving end of a blind or sunshade, used in building finishing work such as stair or floor installations. These posts provide a fixed point for the blind's movable portion, ensuring stability and proper operation of the shading device.
[E04F 10/0685] Covers or housings for the rolled-up blind -- E04F 10/0685 classifies protective covers or housings that enclose a rolled-up blind, such as shelters, casings or protective shells that shield the blind when retracted. The element is considered part of building finishing work, specifically sunshade systems, and relates to fixed constructions used in structures.
[E04F 10/0688] with the housing taking up the articulated arms -- This subclass covers sunshade systems in which the housing that contains the drive mechanism or other components is built into the articulated arms themselves, removing the need for a separate central housing. The design is applied to finishing work on buildings such as staircases or floors, allowing the shade to be deployed directly from the arm structure.
[E04F 10/0692] Front bars -- Front bars are structural elements fixed to the front of sunshade systems on building façades, providing support, stability or decorative framing for the shading device. They are typically horizontal or vertical members that help define the shade's exposed edge and can assist in directing or limiting sunlight entering the interior.
[E04F 10/0696] with means to attach an auxiliary screen -- E04F 10/0696 classifies sunshade structures that incorporate a system for fastening an auxiliary screen, such as an additional fabric or panel, to the primary shading element. The entry covers mechanisms that enable the auxiliary screen to be attached, detached, or adjusted in conjunction with the main sunshade.
[E04F 10/08] of a plurality of similar rigid parts, e.g. slabs, lamellae -- **Definition:** This place covers:  This class contains shading elements for which the surface that provides  the shading function is of a series of rigid parts. e.g. lamellae  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Rigidly-arranged sunshade roofs with coherent surfaces -> E04F10/005

[E04F 11/00] Stairways, ramps, or like structures -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fixed stairways and movable stairways, e.g. loft ladders, and their components, e.g. treads and risers; Ramps; Balustrades; Handrails  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Mounting or marking-off stairways -> E04F21/26 - Appliances for aiding patients or disabled persons to walk -> A61H3/00 - Stairways of temporary use during building construction -> E04G27/00 - Means for facilitating access to swimming pools -> E04H4/144 - Ladders permanently attached to fixed structures -> E06C9/00
[E04F 11/002] Ramps -- E04F 11/002 classifies ramps used in buildings as part of the finishing work on structures, alongside stairways and similar constructions. It covers the design, construction, and integration of inclined passageways that provide access between different levels, including wheelchair-friendly and service ramps. This subclass falls under the broader categories of fixed constructions and building finishing works.
[E04F 11/02] Stairways -- E04F 11/02 covers the design and construction of stairways, ramps and similar circulation structures that are part of building finishing work. It includes details of geometry, materials, handrails and integration with floors or stairs, focusing on the completed built element rather than the underlying structural system.
[E04F 11/0201] Space-saving stairways, e.g. having half steps -- E04F 11/0201 refers to compact stairways designed to minimise floor space, such as staircases with half-height or alternating tread steps, used in building interiors where space is limited.
[E04F 11/022] characterised by the supporting structure -- This subclass covers stairways, ramps and similar structures where the defining feature is the type of supporting structure used, such as cantilevered, suspended or load-bearing frameworks, applied in building finishing works.
[E04F 11/0223] Cantilevered stairways  E04F11/032  takes precedence -- The CPC subclass E04F 11/0223 covers cantilevered stairways, which are stairs supported at only one end or by a wall without intermediate columns, commonly used in building interiors or exteriors. This falls under finishing work on buildings, specifically stairways, ramps, or similar structures, and is overridden by the more specific subclass E04F11/032.
[E04F 11/0226] Stairways having treads supported by balusters -- E04F 11/0226 designates stairways whose treads are supported by balusters rather than a solid stringer, such as open or cantilevered staircases used in buildings. The classification covers the geometry, support arrangement and related structural features of these stair constructions.
[E04F 11/025] Stairways having stringers -- E04F 11/025 classifies fixed stairways that incorporate stringers - the inclined support members that run along the sides or centre of the flight and bear the treads and risers. The code is used for designs of stair constructions within buildings, including related ramp-like structures.
[E04F 11/0255] having adjustable gradient  E04F11/1041  takes precedence -- The code E04F 11/0255 classifies stairways, ramps and similar constructions whose gradient can be varied, such as adjustable-height steps, movable inclines or mechanically controlled slopes. It falls under the broader group for finishing work on buildings and specifically under "having adjustable gradient," which is subordinate to the higher-level entry E04F 11/1041.
[E04F 11/028] having a central stringer -- This classification covers stairways or ramps that incorporate a central stringer, the main supporting beam that runs the length of the flight. It applies to building finishing work where the central stringer is a defining structural element.
[E04F 11/032] Spiral stairways supported by a central column -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Only spiral stairways supported by a central column, where "by a central column" is to be read strictly
[E04F 11/035] Stairways consisting of a plurality of assembled modular parts without further support -- E04F 11/035 covers stairways, ramps or similar structures that are built from a series of prefabricated modular components that are joined together to form the whole stair, without requiring additional supporting elements such as columns or frames. This subclass falls within the finishing work on buildings section of the Cooperative Patent Classification.
[E04F 11/038] each modular part having a load-bearing balustrade part -- This classification covers modular building components that incorporate a load-bearing balustrade, such as stairs or ramps where the balustrade also serves structural support. It falls under finishing work on buildings, specifically stairways, ramps, or similar structures.
[E04F 11/04] Movable stairways, e.g. of loft ladders which may or may not be concealable or extensible -- The CPC entry E04F 11/04 covers inventions relating to movable stairways, such as loft ladders, that can be concealed, folded, extended or otherwise reconfigured to provide access between building levels. It falls under Fixed Constructions - Building - Finishing Work on Buildings, specifically the stairways, ramps or similar structures subclass.
[E04F 11/06] collapsible, e.g. folding, telescopic -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Ship ladders -> B63B - Stairs for aircraft -> B64F - Escalators or moving walkways -> B66B
[E04F 11/062] folding -- The CPC subclass E04F 11/062 refers to foldable stairways, ramps or similar structures that can be collapsed or folded for compact storage, transport, or installation. It covers designs where the walking element is hinged or otherwise articulated to allow the entire unit to be folded flat or reduced in size, typically used in temporary or space-saving construction.
[E04F 11/064] with stringers overlapping when folded -- The code E04F 11/064 classifies fixed building constructions that include stairways, ramps or similar structures whose stringers are designed to overlap when the unit is folded, a feature typical of prefabricated or collapsible stair assemblies used in finishing work on buildings.
[E04F 11/066] folding like scissors or accordions -- E04F 11/066 relates to stairways, ramps or similar structures that are designed to fold or collapse using scissors-type or accordion mechanisms, enabling them to be retracted or extended as part of building finishing work for space-saving or accessibility purposes.
[E04F 11/068] telescopic -- E04F 11/068 relates to telescopic stairways, ramps or similar structures that can be extended or retracted to vary their length or height, enabling compact storage or adaptable access as part of finishing work on buildings.
[E04F 11/09] Tread-and-riser units -- E04F 11/09 concerns tread-and-riser units, which are prefabricated components that combine a stair tread and its adjoining riser into a single, fixed element. These units are used in the construction of stairways, ramps or similar fixed structures and form part of the building finishing work.
[E04F 11/104] Treads -- Treads are the horizontal surfaces of stairways, ramps or similar structures that support the foot when ascending or descending. The classification covers their design, materials, dimensions and construction details as part of building finishing work.
[E04F 11/1041] having means to adjust the height, the depth and/or the slope of the stair steps  E04F11/1042  -  E04F11/116  take precedence -- The subclass E04F 11/1041 covers stairways, ramps or similar structures that include mechanisms to vary the riser height, tread depth or overall slope after installation, allowing the steps to be adjusted for accessibility, layout changes or user preference. It falls under Fixed Constructions → Building → Finishing Work on Buildings, specifically Stairways, ramps or like structures, and precedes the broader groups E04F 11/1042-E04F 11/116.

[E04F 11/17] Surfaces -- E04F 11/17 covers the design, treatment and finishing of surfaces on stairways, ramps and similar structures, including the choice of materials, anti-slip features, tread and riser coverings and protective layers, with emphasis on safety, durability and performance.

It falls under fixed-construction finishing work for stairways and ramps.
[E04F 11/1804] Details of anchoring to the wall -- The CPC entry E04F 11/1804 covers the specifics of how stairways, ramps or similar built-in structures are fastened to walls, including the design of anchors, brackets, bolts and other fixing devices, as well as the criteria for load transfer and stability of the attachment. It addresses both the mechanical details and the materials used to ensure secure, enduring connections in building finishing work.
[E04F 11/1808] Handrail members; Connections between handrail members -- The entry addresses the components that form handrails and the means of joining those components in stairways, ramps and similar building features. It includes brackets, couplings, bolts, welds and other devices that connect individual handrail members to each other or to supporting structures, ensuring a continuous and safe handrail system.
[E04F 11/181] Balustrades -- Balustrades are fixed railing systems installed on stairways, ramps, balconies or similar structures to provide safety and support. They are considered part of finishing work on buildings, falling under the fixed constructions subgroup in the Cooperative Patent Classification. This entry, E04F 11/181, specifically classifies patents relating to the design, construction or integration of balustrades.
[E04F 11/1812] Details of anchoring to the wall or floor -- E04F 11/1812 covers the specific methods and components used to anchor stairways, ramps or similar structures to walls or floors, including bolts, brackets, and fixation systems that secure the construction to the building substrate.
[E04F 11/1814] Covers for the base portions of the balustrade posts -- E04F 11/1814 is a CPC subclass that covers protective or decorative covers for the lower portions of balustrade posts in stairways, ramps or similar structures. It sits within the hierarchy Fixed Constructions - Building - Finishing Work on Buildings and is used by the EPO and USPTO to classify patents dealing with such base-post coverings.
[E04F 11/1817] Connections therefor  E04F11/1812  and  E04F11/1836  take precedence -- This subclass covers the methods and devices for joining stairways, ramps, and similar building elements to other parts of a structure, including bolts, welds, brackets, and other fastening means. More specific groups E04F11/1812 and E04F11/1836 take precedence over this entry.
[E04F 11/1834] with adjustable angle, e.g. pivotal connections -- The CPC class E04F 11/1834 relates to stairways, ramps or similar structures whose angle can be varied, typically by means of a pivotal or other adjustable connection. [E04F 11/1836] Handrails of balustrades; Connections between handrail members -- E04F 11/1836 pertains to handrails that form part of balustrades and the various means of joining handrail members, such as brackets, fittings, welding, or other connection techniques. [E04F 11/184] of variable curvature; flexible -- E04F 11/184 covers stairways, ramps or similar structures whose curvature can vary and that are designed to be flexible rather than rigid. [E04F 13/073] for particular building parts, e.g. corners or columns -- E04F 13/073 relates to coverings or linings applied to specific building elements, such as corners or columns, as part of finishing work on structures. It falls under the hierarchy of fixed constructions, building, and finishing work on buildings.
[E04F 13/0733] for corners -- This CPC subclass covers methods or materials for forming corners in coverings or linings applied to walls or ceilings during building finishing work. It is located under fixed constructions, building, finishing work on buildings, and deals with corner joints, profiles or treatments that ensure a neat and durable transition between intersecting surfaces.
[E04F 13/0736] for columns -- E04F 13/0736 covers finishing measures applied to structural columns, such as wall or ceiling linings, claddings, or other surface treatments used to protect, decorate, or integrate columns within a building's interior. It falls under the broader group of finishing work on buildings, specifically dealing with coverings or linings for walls, ceilings and related elements.
[E04F 13/074] for accommodating service installations or utility lines, e.g. heating conduits, electrical lines, lighting devices or service outlets -- E04F 13/074 classifies building-finishing work that provides coverings or linings for walls or ceilings specifically designed to house service installations or utility lines, such as heating conduits, electrical cables, lighting fixtures and service outlets. It falls under the broader category of fixed constructions and building finishing works.
[E04F 13/075] for insulation or surface protection, e.g. against noise or impact -- This CPC subclass covers methods and devices that apply insulating or protective layers to wall or ceiling coverings or linings, aimed at reducing sound transmission, impact damage, or similar hazards. It includes materials and procedures for acoustic, thermal, or impact-resistant treatment of interior surfaces.
[E04F 13/076] characterised by the joints between neighbouring elements, e.g. with joint fillings or with tongue and groove connections -- E04F 13/076 covers finishing work on buildings where wall or ceiling coverings are joined, detailing the characteristics of the joints between neighboring elements such as joint fillings or tongue-and-groove connections.
[E04F 13/077] composed of several layers, e.g. sandwich panels -- The classification covers building coverings or linings that consist of multiple bonded layers, such as sandwich panels used for walls or ceilings. These multi-layer constructions provide combined structural, insulating or decorative functions in fixed building elements.
[E04F 13/078] Stretched foil- or web-like elements attached with edge gripping devices -- This classification covers stretched foil or web-like elements that are attached to building surfaces using edge gripping devices. It applies to finishing work such as wall or ceiling coverings where the material is tensioned and secured at the edges.
[E04F 13/08] composed of a plurality of similar covering or lining elements, -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Elements of webs or fabrics -> E04F13/002 - Specially adapted, structured or shaped covering or lining elements -> E04F13/072 - Borders, skirtings -> E04F19/02 - Fixing strips -> E04F19/06 - Separate provisional spacers between adjacent tiles -> E04F21/0092 - Implements or means for setting tiles -> E04F21/18
[E04F 13/0801] Separate fastening elements  E04F13/088 ,  E04F13/0885  take precedence -- E04F 13/0801 relates to separate fastening elements employed in finishing work on buildings, specifically for attaching coverings or linings such as wall or ceiling panels. The code addresses individual components like brackets, clips or hooks that secure these finishes to the underlying structure, and it is superseded by the more detailed subclasses E04F13/088 and E04F13/0885.
[E04F 13/0803] with load-supporting elongated furring elements between wall and covering elements -- The code E04F 13/0803 designates finishing work on buildings that involves applying coverings or linings to walls or ceilings, where elongated furring members are installed between the structural wall and the finish material to provide load-supporting reinforcement. This classification covers systems that use such furring elements to create a cavity or support layer for the exterior or interior surface.
[E04F 13/0805] with additional fastening elements between furring elements and the wall -- The E04F 13/0805 entry covers building-finishing methods that attach wall or ceiling coverings to furring elements using extra fastening devices positioned between the furrings and the underlying wall, providing enhanced mechanical stability and alignment of the lining material.
[E04F 13/0807] adjustable perpendicular to the wall -- The code E04F 13/0807 pertains to building-finishing elements that can be positioned at a right angle to a wall, such as adjustable panels, battens or linings used for walls or ceilings. It covers systems that allow the angle to be altered after installation to achieve a perpendicular orientation.
[E04F 13/0808] adjustable in several directions one of which is perpendicular to the wall -- E04F 13/0808 covers building finishing elements, such as wall or ceiling coverings or linings, that can be adjusted in multiple directions, including a movement perpendicular to the wall. The provision allows for flexible positioning of the material to accommodate design or functional requirements during construction or renovation. This subclass falls under fixed constructions, building, and finishing work on buildings.
[E04F 13/081] with additional fastening elements between furring elements and covering elements -- The subclass E04F 13/081 covers building-finishing methods that use additional fastening devices positioned between furring members and the covering or lining material, such as walls or ceilings. It addresses the attachment of coverings with extra connectors to improve stability or performance.
[E04F 13/0812] fixed by means of spring action -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Separate fastening elements for coverings composed of a plurality of similar covering elements, with load-supporting elongated furring elements between wall and covering elements, and with additional fastening elements between furring elements and covering elements, (any of the elements being) fixed by means of spring action, e.g. effecting a clamping, the spring element being integral to or separate from the other elements. e.g. : (spring 8)
[E04F 13/0814] fixed by means of clamping action -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Separate fastening elements for coverings composed of a plurality of similar covering elements, with load-supporting elongated furring elements between wall and covering elements, and with additional fastening elements between furring elements and covering elements, (any of the elements being) fixed by means of clamping action. e.g. : (Clamping effected by elements 12-15)
[E04F 13/0816] the additional fastening elements extending into the back side of the covering elements  E04F13/083  takes precedence -- E04F 13/0816 classifies fastening elements that project into the rear side of wall or ceiling covering or lining components used in building finishing work such as stairs or floors; these additional fasteners secure the covering to the underlying structure. The subclass is superseded by the more specific E04F 13/083. This entry falls under Fixed constructions → Building → Finishing work on buildings → Coverings or linings.
[E04F 13/0817] extending completely through the covering elements -- This subclass includes building components that pass entirely through wall or ceiling coverings, such as staircases, ladders, ducts or shafts that extend from one side of the covering to the other. The element is installed so that it fully bridges the covering material without interruption.
[E04F 13/0819] inserted into grooves in the back side of the covering elements -- E04F 13/0819 covers building-finishing work where covering or lining elements-such as wall or ceiling panels-are fitted by inserting them into grooves cut on the back side of the element. The classification applies to methods and devices that secure these coverings in place by the groove-insertion technique.
[E04F 13/0821] the additional fastening elements located in-between two adjacent covering elements -- E04F 13/0821 concerns extra fastening components that are positioned between two adjoining covering or lining panels in building interiors, such as walls or ceilings, to improve joint stability or sealing. It belongs to the subclass for finishing work on buildings, specifically coverings or linings.
[E04F 13/0823] piercing the side faces of the covering elements -- The CPC subclass E04F 13/0823 covers techniques for piercing the side faces of covering or lining elements employed in building finishing work such as wall or ceiling panels. It falls under fixed constructions → building → finishing work on buildings → coverings or linings, and relates to modifications that create openings in the lateral surfaces of these elements.
[E04F 13/0825] engaging side holes preformed into the covering elements -- The subclass deals with wall or ceiling covering or lining elements that are manufactured with side holes intended for engagement, such as inter-locking, mounting or routing of services. It applies to finishing work on buildings where these pre-formed openings facilitate the attachment or connection of the panels.
[E04F 13/0826] engaging side grooves running along the whole length of the covering elements -- The entry covers building interior finishings in which wall or ceiling covering elements feature continuous side grooves along their full length that engage with adjacent pieces, providing alignment, interlocking and a seamless joint for the lining system.
[E04F 13/0828] engaging the outer surface of the covering elements, e.g. at the corners
[E04F 13/083] Hooking means on the back side of the covering elements -- A hooking means provided on the back side of covering or lining elements, such as wall or ceiling panels, enables secure attachment to the supporting structure during building finishing. This feature is used in fixed-construction applications to simplify installation and improve stability of the finished surface.
[E04F 13/0832] without load-supporting elongated furring elements between wall and covering elements -- This subgroup covers finishing work on buildings where coverings or linings are applied to walls or ceilings without load-supporting elongated furring elements between the wall substrate and the covering. It includes methods of direct attachment, such as adhesive or mechanical fixing, that omit intermediate structural furring.
[E04F 13/0833] not adjustable  E04F13/0857  takes precedence -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Separate fastening elements for coverings composed of a plurality of similar covering elements, without load- supporting elongated furring elements between wall and covering elements, the fastening element being not adjustable, e.g. no adjustment or compensation means for tolerances, inaccuracy, or by means of screws, teeth, wedges.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Supporting consoles, e.g. adjustable only in a direction parallel to the wall -> E04F13/0857
[E04F 13/0835] the fastening elements extending into the back side of the covering elements  E04F13/0851  takes precedence -- E04F 13/0835 relates to building-finishing work, specifically coverings or linings for walls or ceilings, where the fastening elements project through the back side of the covering components to secure them in place; this subclass takes precedence over the later E04F 13/0851.
[E04F 13/0837] extending completely through the covering elements -- The E04F 13/0837 subclass covers constructions in which a component passes entirely through a covering or lining element, such as walls, ceilings or floors, creating an opening that penetrates the material from one side to the other. It applies to finishing-work features like stair openings, ducts or service penetrations that extend completely through the covering.
[E04F 13/0839] the fastening elements situated at the corners of the covering elements, not extending through the covering -- The CPC class E04F 13/0839 covers corner fastening devices that secure the edges of wall or ceiling covering panels without passing through the material, such as clips, brackets or interlocking elements applied only to the panel surfaces. These fasteners hold adjacent coverings together at their corners while preserving the integrity and finish of the covering surface.

[E04F 13/0842] specially adapted for thin sheet-like materials, e.g. sheet-metal or plastics -- The code E04F 13/0842 covers building finishing work involving coverings or linings for walls or ceilings that are specially adapted for thin sheet-like materials such as sheet metal or plastics. It falls under the hierarchy of Fixed Constructions → Building → Finishing Work on Buildings, focusing on the use of lightweight, flexible sheets in interior surface treatments.
[E04F 13/0844] with means piercing the side faces of the covering elements -- E04F 13/0844 refers to building-finishing work involving wall or ceiling coverings where the fixing elements penetrate the side faces of the covering panels. It includes methods and devices such as side-driven nails, screws or clamps that secure the elements by piercing their edges rather than their front or back surfaces.
[E04F 13/0846] the fastening elements engaging holes or grooves in the side faces of the covering elements  E04F13/0835  and  E04F13/0839  take precedence -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Separate fastening elements for coverings composed of a plurality of similar covering elements, without load-supporting elongated furring elements between wall and covering elements, not adjustable, and with fastening elements engaging holes or grooves in the lateral edge side of the covering elements e.g.:  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - The fastening elements extending into the back side of the covering elements -> E04F13/0835 - The fastening elements situated at the corners of the covering elements, and not extending through the covering -> E04F13/0839

It falls under the hierarchy of fixed constructions, building, finishing work on buildings.
[E04F 13/085] with means piercing the side faces of the covering elements -- The CPC subclass E04F 13/085 covers building finishing work that uses devices or methods to pierce the side faces of wall or ceiling covering elements, such as panels, tiles or boards, to provide fastenings, openings or connections. It falls under fixed constructions, building, and finishing work on buildings, specifically dealing with coverings or linings.
[E04F 13/0851] Hooking means on the back side of the covering elements -- E04F 13/0851 covers devices attached to the rear side of wall or ceiling coverings, such as panels or tiles, that provide a hooking or fastening function for securing the elements in a building. The classification is used for inventions that integrate hooks, catches or similar means into the back of finish materials to simplify installation or improve attachment to supporting structures.
[E04F 13/0853] adjustable perpendicular to the wall -- E04F 13/0853 covers building-finishing elements that can be adjusted while being installed perpendicular to a wall, such as adjustable panels, moldings or linings used as coverings for walls or ceilings in stairs, floors or other interior surfaces. It belongs to the fixed constructions branch of the CPC.
[E04F 13/0855] adjustable in several directions, one of which is perpendicular to the wall -- E04F 13/0855 pertains to adjustable coverings or linings used in building finishing work that can be positioned in several directions, including a setting perpendicular to the wall. [E04F 13/0857] Supporting consoles, e.g. adjustable only in a direction parallel to the wall  E04F13/0853  takes precedence -- The CPC entry E04F 13/0857 falls under fixed constructions for building finishing work, specifically coverings or linings for walls or ceilings. It covers supporting consoles that can be adjusted only in a direction parallel to the wall, such as brackets for shelves or equipment mounted on interior surfaces.
[E04F 13/0858] fixed by means of spring action -- The E04F 13/0858 classification pertains to building finishing elements such as wall or ceiling coverings that are secured in place by a spring-action mechanism. It falls under fixed constructions for finishing work on buildings, including stairs and floors, where the spring provides the retaining force.
[E04F 13/086] fixed by means of clamping action -- The code designates building-finishing elements such as wall or ceiling panels that are secured in place by a clamping mechanism rather than by adhesives, nails or screws. It covers structures where the covering or lining is held by a pressure-oriented clamp that locks the element to its supporting frame, providing a detachable yet stable installation.
[E04F 13/0862] composed of a number of elements which are identical or not, e.g. carried by a common web, support plate or grid -- The code E04F 13/0862 covers building interior coverings or linings that consist of multiple elements, whether identical or varied, which are joined to a common web, support plate or grid. It pertains to systems such as modular wall panels, ceiling tiles or floor boards that are assembled on a shared structural backing.
[E04F 13/0864] composed of superposed elements which overlap each other and of which the flat outer surface includes an acute angle with the surface to cover -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Coverings or linings composed of superposed elements which overlap each other and of which the flat outer surface includes an acute angle with the surface to cover, where the expression "acute angle" is to be read strictly  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Case of absence of an acute angle -> E04F13/10 - E04F13/18

[E04F 13/0885] specially adapted for being adhesively fixed to the wall; Fastening means therefor; Fixing by means of plastics materials hardening after application -- The code refers to building finishing elements such as wall or ceiling coverings that are designed to be adhesively bonded to the substrate. The code is used to classify patents related to these joint solutions in construction.

[E04F 13/23] adjustable -- E04F 13/23 is a subclass of the Cooperative Patent Classification for building finishing work, specifically coverings or linings for walls or ceilings, nested under Fixed Constructions → Building → Finishing Work on Buildings (e.g., stairs, floors). The entry "adjustable" pertains to elements that can be repositioned, reshaped or otherwise varied in situ to modify the building's interior surface or protective layer.
[E04F 13/24] Hidden fastening means on the rear of the covering or lining elements -- The code E04F 13/24 applies to hidden fastening methods applied to the rear side of wall or ceiling coverings and linings, such as those used in stairs or floor finishing. [E04F 13/25] adjustable -- E04F 13/25 covers adjustable covering or lining elements used in building interiors, such as panels, boards or tiles that can be repositioned, replaced or reconfigured to change the appearance or function of walls or ceilings. [E04F 13/26] Edge engaging fastening means, e.g. clamps, clips or border profiles -- E04F 13/26 refers to edge-engaging fastening devices used in building finishing work, specifically for coverings or linings of walls and ceilings such as stairs or floors. [E04F 13/28] adjustable -- E04F 13/28 relates to adjustable coverings or linings for walls or ceilings used in finishing work on buildings, such as stair or floor surfaces. [E04F 13/30] Magnetic fastening means -- E04F 13/30 covers magnetic fastening means employed in building finishing work, specifically for attaching coverings or linings to walls, ceilings or stairs and floors. It includes devices that use magnetic attraction to secure panels, tiles or other surface elements without mechanical fasteners.

It pertains to finished building floors that incorporate the joint filler as part of the floor component's structure.

[E04F 15/02022] with means for aligning the outer surfaces of the flooring elements  E04F15/02038  takes precedence -- E04F 15/02022 is a CPC subclass within the Fixed Constructions group covering finishing work on buildings, specifically flooring. It relates to flooring elements that include provisions for aligning their outer surfaces, such as guide ribs or edge locks, to ensure a uniform surface. The subclass supersedes E04F 15/02038.
[E04F 15/02027] Means for spacing the flooring from an adjoining wall -- Means for spacing the flooring from an adjoining wall are devices or arrangements that create a controlled gap between the finished floor surface and the nearby vertical surface. The gap accommodates movement, provides ventilation, and helps manage moisture or thermal expansion in building finishing work.
[E04F 15/02033] Joints with beveled or recessed upper edges -- The code E04F 15/02033 covers floor joint designs where the upper edges are shaped with a bevel or a recess, allowing for smooth transitions and drainage between panels. It falls under the finishing work on buildings subclass for flooring within the Cooperative Patent Classification system used by the EPO and USPTO.
[E04F 15/02038] characterised by tongue and groove connections between neighbouring flooring elements -- The entry E04F 15/02038 falls under Fixed Constructions → Building → Finishing Work on Buildings → Flooring and identifies flooring systems that are characterised by tongue-and-groove joints linking adjacent floor elements. This connection method provides a snug, interlocking fit that helps align and secure the boards without additional fasteners.
[E04F 15/02044] Separate elements for fastening to an underlayer  E04F15/02133 ,  E04F15/0215  take precedence -- The CPC subclass E04F 15/02044 falls under Fixed Constructions ► Building ► Finishing work on buildings, e.g. stairs, floors ► Flooring and covers separate elements that are fastened to an underlayer, such as individual tiles or panels. It is superseded by the more specific subclasses E04F 15/02133 and E04F 15/0215, which take precedence.
[E04F 15/02133] fixed directly to an underlayer by means of magnets, hook and loop-type or similar fasteners, not necessarily involving the side faces of the flooring elements -- This subclass covers flooring that is attached directly to the substrate using magnetic, hook-and-loop or comparable fastening systems, eliminating the need for interlocking side edges. It pertains to installations where the fixing elements are applied without relying on the side faces of the flooring panels.
[E04F 15/02138] by hook and loop-type fasteners -- The CPC subclass E04F 15/02138 covers flooring installations that use hook-and-loop-type fasteners, such as Velcro strips, to attach floor panels or coverings without adhesives or mechanical fastening. It falls under Fixed Constructions → Building → Finishing work on buildings → Flooring, describing a method of securing floor elements by a detachable, reusable fastening system.
[E04F 15/02144] by magnets -- E04F 15/02144 falls within the Cooperative Patent Classification for building construction, specifically the sub-group dealing with finishing work on buildings such as flooring. The entry covers inventions that employ magnets to secure, position, or attach floor elements, offering a magnetic method or device for installing or fixing flooring components. This classification helps locate patents that use magnetic forces in floor-covering applications.
[E04F 15/0215] specially adapted for being adhesively fixed to an underlayer; Fastening means therefor; Fixing by means of plastics materials hardening after application -- E04F 15/0215 covers flooring components specifically designed to be adhesively fixed to an underlayer, the associated fastening means, and the use of plastic materials that harden after application to secure the floor.

[E04F 15/02166] wherein the grooves are filled with inserts -- This subclass concerns flooring systems in which the surface grooves are occupied by separate insert pieces. The classification falls under building finishing work for stairs and floors within the Fixed Constructions group of the CPC.

[E04F 15/126] Terrazzo floors -- Terrazzo floors are a type of building finish formed by embedding chips of marble, quartz, granite, glass, or other aggregates in a cementitious or polymer binder, then grinding and polishing the surface to a smooth, high-gloss appearance.
