[E04F 17/123] Cleaning or disinfecting thereof -- E04F 17/123 covers techniques and devices for cleaning or disinfecting vertical duct systems installed in buildings, a subcategory of finishing work on structures such as stairs and floors. The classification addresses maintenance procedures to remove contaminants and prevent microbial growth within ductwork.
[E04F 17/126] with water flushing -- The entry E04F 17/126 covers vertical ducts incorporated in buildings-such as risers, shafts or channels used for utilities or services-that are equipped with a water-flushing system to clean, clear or maintain the duct interior. It applies to finishing-work installations where periodic water flow is used to remove debris or blockages.
[E04F 19/00] Other details of constructional parts for finishing work on buildings -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Borders, finishing strips, light-coves, built-in cup-boards, niches, covers of holes enabling access to installations, built-in gratings, chimney mantel pieces  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Borders for protecting edges of plaster -> E04F13/06 - Climbing irons -> E06C9/04
[E04F 19/005] Chimney mantel pieces -- E04F 19/005 refers to chimney mantel pieces, the decorative or protective surrounds that frame a chimney as part of building finishing work. It is classified under fixed constructions, building, finishing work on buildings, and other details of constructional parts for finishing work.
[E04F 19/02] Borders -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Borders for protecting edges of plaster -> E04F13/06
[E04F 19/022] for use at vertical intersections of walls  E04F19/028  takes precedence -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Borders adapted for cushioning impacts at protruding corners -> E04F19/028
[E04F 19/024] for outside use, e.g. imitating brickwork -- E04F 19/024 covers exterior finishing components applied to buildings that imitate the appearance of brickwork. It includes decorative panels, claddings or mouldings designed for outside use to reproduce brick patterns without using actual bricks.
[E04F 19/026] specially adapted for cushioning impacts -- E04F 19/026 classifies constructional parts used in building finishing work, such as stairs or floor elements, that are specially adapted to cushion impacts. It covers designs or materials that absorb shock to protect users or structural components during use. This falls under the broader categories of fixed constructions, building, and finishing work on buildings.
[E04F 19/028] used at protruding corners -- E04F 19/028 covers the use of constructional elements or materials applied at protruding corners during finishing work on buildings, such as stair or floor finishes. The code is part of the Fixed Constructions group and relates to detailing of corner treatments in building interiors.
[E04F 19/04] for use between floor or ceiling and wall, e.g. skirtings -- E04F 19/04 covers components installed at the junction of a floor or ceiling and a wall, primarily decorative or protective trim such as skirtings. It falls within the building-finishing work segment of the Cooperative Patent Classification, addressing other details of constructional parts used for finishing interiors.
[E04F 19/0436] between ceiling and wall -- The CPC subclass E04F 19/0436 covers details of constructional parts used in finishing work on buildings that pertain to the junction or gap between a ceiling and a wall. It is situated within the hierarchy of fixed constructions, building, and finishing work on buildings and addresses how this interface is treated or detailed in construction.
[E04F 19/045] Hygienic or watertight plinths -- The CPC code E04F 19/045 designates hygienic or watertight plinths employed as fixed construction elements that form the base for building finishes, offering moisture resistance and sanitary conditions for stairs, floors and other finishing work.
[E04F 19/0459] characterised by the fixing method -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Borders or finishing strips for use between floor or ceiling and wall, e.g. skirtings characterised by the fixing method (group not used, see subgroups) e.g. by hooking in a direction perpendicular ( E04F19/0463 ) or parallel ( E04F19/0468 ) to the wall, by means of magnets or hook and loop-type fasterners ( E04F19/0472 ), by means of glue ( E04F19/0477 ):
[E04F 19/0463] Plinths fixed by snap-action in a direction perpendicular to the wall -- The code E04F 19/0463 denotes plinths that are attached to a wall with a snap-action mechanism that secures them in a direction perpendicular to the wall. It falls under the Cooperative Patent Classification hierarchy for fixed constructions, building, finishing work on buildings, and other details of constructional parts for finishing work.
[E04F 19/0468] Plinths fixed by hooking in a direction parallel to the wall -- The entry covers finishing-work techniques for attaching plinths to a building by hooking them in a direction parallel to the wall, a method used in fixed construction of interior or exterior surfaces. It falls under the broader categories of building, finishing work on buildings, and other details of constructional parts for such finishing work.
[E04F 19/0472] Plinths fixed by means of magnets, hook and loop-type or similar fasteners -- The entry covers plinths that are secured to a building using magnetic, hook-and-loop or comparable fasteners rather than traditional mechanical fixing. It falls under the finishing work category for buildings, specifically detailing alternative attachment methods for constructional parts.
[E04F 19/0477] Plinths fixed by means of adhesive -- E04F 19/0477 classifies the use of adhesives to secure plinths as part of finishing work on buildings, covering methods and materials for bonding these lower wall elements in fixed construction assemblies.
[E04F 19/0481] Skirtings or crown mouldings with a separate cladding strip -- This CPC subclass covers decorative interior trim such as skirtings or crown mouldings that incorporate a separate cladding strip to create a distinct layer or visual effect, and applies to finishing work on buildings where the moulding and its cladding are produced or installed as separate components.
[E04F 19/0486] Corner filling pieces -- E04F 19/0486 - Corner filling pieces refers to small components employed to seal or finish the intersection of two adjoining surfaces in building interiors, such as the junction of walls, floors, or stair treads. These pieces may be made of wood, metal, polymer or composite and are used to improve aesthetics, protect the joint and facilitate a smooth transition between finishes.
[E04F 19/049] Plinths specially adapted for joining wall coverings to floor surfaces -- E04F 19/049 refers to specially designed plinths that join wall coverings directly to floor surfaces, providing a seamless transition between wall and floor in building finishing work. These fixed construction elements are used where a plinth must support or anchor wall finishes while integrating with the floor.
[E04F 19/0495] Plinths fixed around wall openings or around corners of walls -- The code E04F 19/0495 covers fixed plinth elements that are installed around wall openings such as doors and windows or around wall corners, forming part of the finishing work on a building's interior or exterior structure.
[E04F 19/06] specially designed for securing panels -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - For use at vertical intersections of walls -> E04F19/022 - Specially adapted for cushioning impacts -> E04F19/026 - For use between floor or ceiling and wall -> E04F19/04
[E04F 19/061] used to finish off an edge or corner of a wall or floor covering area  in connection with floor fabrics  A47G27/045 ; sealings between walls and baths  A47K3/008 -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Borders or finishing strips, specially designed for securing panels, used to finish off an edge or corner of a wall or floor covering area e.g. floor transition pieces  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - In connection with floor fabrics -> A47G27/045 - Sealings between walls and baths -> A47K3/008
[E04F 19/062] used between similar elements  separate provisional spacers  E04F21/0092 -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Borders or finishing strips, specially designed for securing panels, used between similar elements, e.g., a bridger strip for a gap created by two panels, e.g.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Separate provisional spacers -> E04F21/0092
[E04F 19/063] for simultaneously securing panels having different thicknesses -- E04F 19/063 relates to finishing work on buildings and covers devices or methods for simultaneously attaching building panels that have differing thicknesses, enabling uniform installation in fixed constructions such as walls, ceilings or floors.
[E04F 19/064] in corners -- E04F 19/064 covers details of constructional parts used in finishing work on buildings that are applied to corners, such as corner fittings, trims or treatments on stairs, floors and other interior surfaces.
[E04F 19/065] Finishing profiles with a T-shaped cross-section or the like -- The CPC entry E04F 19/065 covers building-finish components that have a T-shaped or similar cross-section, such as trim, mouldings or edge pieces used to complete interior or exterior surfaces. It falls under Fixed Constructions → Building → Finishing work on buildings → Other details of constructional parts for finishing work.
[E04F 19/066] fixed onto a base profile by means of a separate connector -- It covers building-finishing components that are secured to a base profile by a separate connector, such as stair treads, floor panels or other fixed parts, focusing on the attachment method rather than the component itself. This subclass is used for describing how these elements are fixed in place during construction.
[E04F 19/067] with means preventing a tipping movement -- E04F 19/067 covers components used in the finishing of buildings, such as stairs or floor elements, that include provisions to prevent tipping movements, thereby enhancing stability and safety.
[E04F 19/068] with means allowing a tipping movement -- In the CPC class for fixed constructions in buildings, code E04F 19/068 covers finishing work on buildings that includes components equipped with a tipping movement, such as fold-down stairs, tilt-up floors or other elements that can be pivoted for access or storage.
[E04F 19/08] Built-in cupboards -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Inspection openings in bathtub aprons -> A47K3/1615 - Removable wall partitions -> E04B2/74 - Movable ceiling parts -> E04B9/003 - Cleanout doors of chimneys -> F23J - Covers for electrical distribution boxes -> H02G3/14
[E04F 19/083] Covers with fixing means providing for snap locking -- The entry covers building finishing components such as panels or covers that incorporate integrated fixing means which snap-locking into place for fast, detachable installation. It is classified in the CPC under E04F 19/083 for snap-locking covers used in stairs, floors and other interior finishes.
[E04F 19/086] Covers with magnetic fixing means -- Covers with magnetic fixing means are building components such as panels or plates that are attached to interior or exterior surfaces using magnets instead of screws or adhesives. They provide removable protection or finishing for stairs, floors, walls or other structural parts, allowing easy installation, adjustment or replacement without damaging the underlying construction.
[E04F 19/10] Built-in gratings, e.g. foot-scrapers -- E04F 19/10 designates built-in gratings that are incorporated into a building's interior or exterior finishing work, such as foot-scrapers or floor drains that are formed as part of the structure.
[E04F 2011/005] collapsible, e.g. folding, telescopic -- A CPC subgroup for building and civil-engineering elements that can be reduced in size by folding, collapsing or telescoping, enabling easier transport, storage or installation. It includes collapsible retaining walls, foldable sheet piles, telescopic foundation members and similar modular structures designed for rapid deployment.
[E04F 2011/007] characterised by the supporting structure -- The CPC subgroup E04F 2011/007 covers inventions whose distinguishing feature is the supporting structure of a building element, such as frames, brackets, columns or other load-bearing components that provide stability, reinforcement or mounting for the item being built. It is used for patents that focus on how the supporting framework is configured or integrated.
[E04F 2011/0203] Miscellaneous features of stairways not otherwise provided for -- E04F 2011/0203 is a CPC sub-group for stairways that covers any ancillary or safety elements not specifically listed in other stairway subclasses. This includes unusual handrails, guardrails, anti-slip treatments, lighting, signage, or decorative components that do not fall under the more detailed feature codes. The entry groups together such "miscellaneous" stairway features for patent classification.
[E04F 2011/0205] Stairways characterised by the use of specific materials for the supporting structure of the treads -- This subclass covers stairways where the supporting structure of the treads is made from specific materials such as metal, reinforced concrete, wood or composite elements, focusing on the choice of material for the load-bearing components of the stair.
[E04F 2011/0207] mainly of wood -- E04F 2011/0207 is a sub-group of the roofing class that covers roofs or roof components whose principal material is wood, including structures where wood forms the main load-bearing or covering element, regardless of any additional supporting materials.
[E04F 2011/0209] mainly of metal -- E04F 2011/0209 covers doors, windows, shutters, gates, locks or other fastenings whose components are primarily made of metal, specifying that metal is the main material used in their construction. This includes metal frames, panels, hinges, latches and related parts designed for structural or functional purposes.
[E04F 2011/021] mainly of stone or stone like materials, e.g. concrete;  mainly of glass -- This CPC subgroup relates to foundations or other sub-structural building elements whose principal material is stone, stone-like substances such as concrete, or glass. It covers the design, construction and treatment of such elements, including walls, retaining structures and similar components.
[E04F 2011/0212] mainly of concrete -- The CPC code E04F 2011/0212 covers inventions concerning building elements whose principal material is concrete, such as walls, floors, pavements and other structural components made predominantly of concrete. It is part of group E04F, which deals with materials used in construction.
[E04F 2011/0214] cast  in situ -- E04F 2011/0214 refers to technologies for cast-in-situ construction, covering processes, devices and compositions used to form structural elements such as walls, slabs or columns by pouring concrete, mortar or similar materials directly at the building site.
[E04F 2011/0216] mainly of glass -- E04F 2011/0216 refers to building components whose principal material is glass, such as glazing panels, glass doors, windows and other transparent or translucent parts used in construction. The code is applied to patents concerning the design, manufacture, installation or improvement of elements that are mainly of glass.
[E04F 2011/0218] mainly of organic plastics with or without reinforcements or filling materials -- E04F 2011/0218 covers building components such as walls, panels or roofing that are made primarily of organic polymer plastics, optionally combined with reinforcing fibers or filler particles to enhance strength, durability or other functional properties.
[E04F 2011/1046] Miscellaneous features of treads not otherwise provided for -- E04F 2011/1046 covers tread designs that include additional or unusual elements such as sipes, micro-grooves, patterned blocks or other surface modifications that are not specifically listed in other CPC subclasses. It is used for patents describing novel or supplemental tread features that improve traction, wear or noise without fitting into existing categories.
[E04F 2011/1047] with means for eliminating squeak -- E04F 2011/1047 classifies doors that incorporate features designed to prevent or reduce squeaking, such as lubricated hinges, noise-damping devices, self-lubricating materials or other mechanisms that eliminate audible friction during operation. This subclass is used to index patents describing such squeak-eliminating means in door constructions.
[E04F 2011/1048] with lighting means -- The CPC entry E04F 2011/1048 covers building heating, ventilation or air-conditioning installations that incorporate lighting devices. It applies where the same unit provides both climate control and illumination, for example ceiling-mounted HVAC fixtures with integrated lamps. Use in patents describing such combined systems.
[E04F 2011/1806] Balcony guard with additional child safety means -- The code E04F 2011/1806 denotes balcony guards that incorporate extra child-safety provisions, such as locking devices, higher or enclosed sections, or protective attachments designed to prevent children from slipping through or climbing over the barrier. It is a subcategory of balcony guard designs (E04F 2011) focused on enhanced safety features for young users.
[E04F 2011/1819] between balustrade posts and horizontal or sloping balustrade members -- The CPC entry E04F 2011/1819 covers the area between balustrade posts and the horizontal or sloping members that connect them, including the design of joints, brackets, spacers or other elements that secure or adjust the relationship of the posts to the rail or guard components.
[E04F 2011/1821] between balustrade posts and handrails -- The CPC entry E04F 2011/1821 covers the area located between balustrade posts and handrails, including design features that define the gap, its dimensions and any protective or decorative elements situated there. It relates to the structural and safety aspects of the space separating the vertical posts from the horizontal rail in staircases, balconies or similar constructions.
[E04F 2011/1823] between balustrade filling members, e.g. balusters or panels, and horizontal or sloping balustrade members -- E04F 2011/1823 covers the means for joining filler elements such as balusters, panels or similar members to the horizontal or sloping parts of a balustrade. The classification includes connections, brackets, fastening systems and other devices that secure these intermediate components to the rail or the inclined sections of the railing.
[E04F 2011/1825] between balustrade filling members, e.g. balusters or panels, and handrails -- This CPC subclass covers devices or elements that occupy the space between a handrail and the infill members of a balustrade, such as balusters, panels or similar components. It includes designs, mounting methods and accessories that provide continuity, safety or aesthetic integration between the railing and its fillers.
[E04F 2011/1827] between balusters and handrails -- The entry refers to a connecting element situated between the balusters and the handrail of a stair or balcony railing, providing structural continuity, safety and aesthetic finishing. It may be a spacer, infill panel or other device that links the vertical balusters to the horizontal handrail while meeting the functional and design requirements of the railing system.
[E04F 2011/1829] between filling panels and handrails -- The CPC entry E04F 2011/1829 pertains to building components positioned between infill panels and handrails, typically used to provide a protective or decorative intermediate element in stair, balcony or railing assemblies. It falls under the E04F subclass covering openings, doors and related hardware, specifying this particular arrangement of panel and rail elements.
[E04F 2011/1831] between balustrade filling members, e.g. panels, and balustrade posts -- E04F 2011/1831 covers the elements that join or secure panel-like infill members of a balustrade to its vertical posts, such as brackets, clips, fasteners or other linking devices that create a continuous or modular barrier.
[E04F 2011/1868] Miscellaneous features of handrails not otherwise provided for -- In CPC section E04F, which deals with handrails and guards for buildings and structures, the subgroup 2011/1868 is reserved for miscellaneous handrail characteristics that are not covered by other more specific codes. It includes features such as decorative elements, adjustable height, integrated lighting, special gripping surfaces or other auxiliary functions that fall outside the standard categories.
[E04F 2011/187] lengthwise adjustable, e.g. telescopic -- E04F 2011/187 covers building or vehicle panels, shutters, blinds or similar coverings that can be adjusted lengthwise, for example by telescoping mechanisms that extend or retract to vary the opening or coverage, providing variable shading, ventilation or protection.
[E04F 2011/1872] illuminated -- E04F 2011/1872 is a CPC group under the main group E04F for building components that covers inventions relating to illuminated elements of a building or structure. It includes lighting devices, light-emitting panels or integrated illumination systems designed to enhance visibility, aesthetics or safety of façades, roofs or other architectural components.
[E04F 2011/1874] Noise reduction -- The CPC entry E04F 2011/1874 covers measures and devices that reduce acoustic emissions in sanitary and building-service installations, such as noise-dampening pipe fittings, silencers, acoustic insulation for drainage or ventilation systems, and related methods for minimizing sound generated by water, air or waste flows. It is part of the E04F group for sanitary engineering within the Construction, Building and Civil Engineering section.
[E04F 2011/1876] Movable elements, e.g. against sunlight -- The code belongs to CPC section E (building) subclass E04F (buildings, doors, windows). The group 2011/1876 covers movable components used to regulate or block sunlight, such as external shutters, blinds, louvers, operable skylights, or adjustable screens that can be opened, closed or tilted. These elements are intended to control illumination and heat gain.
[E04F 2011/1878] Heat exchanger -- The CPC subclass E04F 2011/1878 covers heat-exchanger units used in building heating, ventilation and air-conditioning systems, encompassing devices that transfer thermal energy between two or more fluid streams. It includes plate, shell-and-tube, finned-tube, and other configurations designed for heating, cooling or recovering heat.
[E04F 2011/188] with flower pots -- The subclass E04F 2011/188 covers plant-growing or ornamental structures that incorporate one or more flower pots as integral parts of the device or system. It applies to arrangements where the pots are built-in, interchangeable or positioned to enhance the function of the horticultural or landscaping apparatus. This includes planters, raised beds or display units designed specifically to hold flower containers.
[E04F 2011/1882] with traction cable inside post or rail -- E04F 2011/1882 relates to ropeway or cable-transport installations where the traction cable is routed inside a supporting post or rail instead of being exposed externally. The cable is enclosed within the structural element to protect it from the environment, improve safety or aesthetics, and is commonly applied in ski lifts, aerial tramways and similar systems.
[E04F 2011/1885] Handrails or balusters characterized by the use of specific materials -- E04F 2011/1885 relates to handrails or balusters whose defining feature is the specific material from which they are made, such as glass, metal alloys, wood or composites, where the material itself contributes essential functional or aesthetic properties. The subclass includes inventions in which the choice of material is critical to performance, durability, transparency, corrosion resistance or other technical effects, rather than merely the shape or construction of the rail.
[E04F 2011/1887] mainly of wood -- E04F 2011/1887 designates building structures where wood is the principal material, covering foundations, subsoil works, and related external components constructed mainly of timber. It includes methods, joining techniques, and protective measures specific to wooden structural elements in civil engineering.
[E04F 2011/1889] mainly of metal -- E04F 2011/1889 classifies prefabricated building components or modular building elements whose construction is predominantly metal, such as steel or aluminium frames, panels, or modules used in the erection of structures. It applies to inventions where metal forms the principal material of the prefabricated element.
[E04F 2011/1891] mainly of stone or stone like materials, e.g. concrete;  mainly of glass -- This subclass covers constructions whose primary material is stone, stone-like substances such as concrete, or glass. It includes walls, facades, partitions and other structural elements that are built mainly from these materials, focusing on their use in building and civil engineering applications.
[E04F 2011/1893] mainly of concrete -- The CPC subclass E04F 2011/1893 covers foundation and supporting structures that are primarily made of concrete, including concrete piles, footings, rafts and similar elements used to distribute loads from buildings to the ground. It groups inventions where concrete is the main constituent material of the foundation.
[E04F 2011/1895] mainly of glass -- The CPC subgroup E04F 2011/1895 covers building components whose primary material is glass, including windows, skylights, glass doors, glazed facades and other structural elements made mainly of glass.
[E04F 2011/1897] mainly of organic plastics with or without reinforcements or filling materials -- The CPC subclass E04F 2011/1897 covers windows, doors, shutters, skylights and similar building components whose primary material is an organic plastic, either pure or combined with reinforcing fibres, mineral or other filler substances, without limiting the specific type of plastic used.
[E04F 2013/061] for arched or curved edges -- E04F 2013/061 classifies walls, retaining structures, flood barriers or similar enclosures whose edges are deliberately arched or curved, covering designs that use a curved profile for structural, hydraulic or aesthetic purposes.
[E04F 2013/063] for corners -- E04F 2013/063 covers inventions related to doors, windows, shutters, skylights or similar building openings that are installed at a corner of a structure, including the mechanisms, seals or mounting features required for angular placement.
[E04F 2013/065] for lower edges of outer insulation layers -- The CPC subclass E04F 2013/065 covers measures that treat the lower edges of outer insulation layers on building envelopes, such as sealing, fastening or protecting the bottom margin of external thermal-insulation panels on walls or roofs to prevent moisture ingress, heat loss or damage. It includes accessories, profiles or methods designed specifically for that edge region.
[E04F 2013/066] for expansion joints between two plaster layers -- This CPC subgroup covers the provision of expansion joints that separate two adjacent plaster layers, allowing movement and preventing cracking while enhancing the durability and performance of wall or ceiling finishes.
[E04F 2015/0205] with load-supporting elongated furring elements between the flooring elements and the underlayer -- The code designates floor structures that incorporate elongated furring elements positioned between the floor covering and the underlying layer to bear loads. These furring strips are typically spaced and sized to support the flooring panels, provide ventilation or level uneven surfaces, and transfer weight directly to the sub-structure.
[E04F 2015/02055] with additional supporting elements between furring elements and underlayer -- The code denotes a building-assembly feature where extra supporting elements are inserted between the furring strips and the underlying layer, such as a sheathing or substrate. This arrangement provides additional structural reinforcement, spacing, or integration of services within walls, ceilings or roof panels. It is classified under CPC group E04F, which covers doors, windows, shutters and related accessories.
[E04F 2015/02061] adjustable perpendicular to the underlayer -- E04F 2015/02061 classifies building or roofing components whose mounting lets the outer layer be positioned at right angles to the underlayer, allowing the outer panel or cladding to be adjusted perpendicularly for alignment, ventilation or aesthetic purposes. It covers mechanisms that enable such perpendicular adjustment of the outer sheet relative to its supporting substrate.
[E04F 2015/02066] with additional fastening elements between furring elements and flooring elements -- The CPC entry E04F 2015/02066 relates to wall or floor covering systems that incorporate additional fastening elements positioned between furring members and the flooring panels. It covers designs where extra fasteners improve the mechanical attachment of the floor covering to the underlying structure.
[E04F 2015/02072] the additional fastening elements extending into the back side of the flooring elements  E04F2015/021  takes precedence -- The subclass E04F 2015/02072 relates to floor covering elements that incorporate additional fastening parts which extend through the rear side of the panels to engage a supporting structure, enhancing the connection and stability of the flooring system. This subclass is superseded by the broader subclass E04F 2015/021, which takes precedence.
[E04F 2015/02077] the additional fastening elements located in-between two adjacent flooring elements -- The entry covers flooring systems that incorporate extra fastening components placed between neighboring floor panels to improve joint stability. It pertains to designs where these intermediate elements lock or bind adjacent boards, enhancing resistance to movement, separation or vibration.
[E04F 2015/02083] Piercing the side faces of the flooring elements -- This CPC subgroup covers processes and tools for forming holes or openings by piercing the side faces of floor panels or tiles, typically to accommodate wiring, drainage, or fastening elements. It includes techniques that penetrate the lateral surface rather than the top surface, and may involve mechanical, pneumatic or laser methods.
[E04F 2015/02088] Engaging side holes preformed into the flooring elements -- The entry concerns flooring components that have side holes formed during manufacture to receive fasteners or projections of neighboring panels, enabling a secure interlock and easier alignment during installation. Such pre-shaped holes facilitate mechanical engagement without additional machining, improving assembly speed and stability.
[E04F 2015/02094] Engaging side grooves running along the whole length of the flooring elements -- The entry refers to flooring components that feature side grooves extending continuously along their entire length. These grooves facilitate interlocking or alignment of the elements during installation, improving stability and ease of assembly. It is classified under the Cooperative Patent Classification group for floor coverings and related structures.
[E04F 2015/021] fixed by means of hooking means on the back side of the flooring elements -- The CPC subclass E04F 2015/021 refers to floor covering elements that are secured in place by hooking mechanisms attached to the reverse side of the panels. It covers installations where the flooring pieces interlock with each other or with a substrate using hooks, clips or similar devices that engage from the back rather than from the surface. This method enables a floating or snap-fit floor system without adhesives.
[E04F 2015/02105] without load-supporting elongated furring elements between the flooring elements and the underlayer -- The entry relates to floor constructions in which the flooring panels are installed directly on the supporting underlayer without the use of load-supporting elongated furring elements between them. It covers designs that eliminate intermediate strip joists, allowing the flooring elements to rest directly on the substructure. This classification is used for patents describing such furring-free floor assemblies.
[E04F 2015/02111] not adjustable -- E04F 2015/02111 covers lift, escalator or moving-walk designs in which key characteristics such as travel distance, speed, capacity or door dimensions are fixed at manufacture and cannot be altered or tuned by the user. The entry is applied to non-adjustable installations where no mechanisms for post-assembly modification are provided.
[E04F 2015/02116] with fastening elements extending into the back side of the flooring elements -- The code refers to flooring panels that incorporate fastening components that protrude into the underside of the panels to join them together, enabling mechanical coupling without adhesives. It belongs to CPC group E04F covering floor coverings and related structures. This subclass deals specifically with systems where the fasteners are integral to the panel back side.
[E04F 2015/02122] with fastening elements engaging holes or grooves in the side faces of the flooring elements  E04F2015/02116  takes precedence -- This classification sub-group defines flooring components whose fastening elements lock into holes or grooves cut in the side faces of the pieces, providing a mechanical connection without surface adhesives. The group is nested under E04F2015/02116, which has priority over it.
[E04F 2015/02127] adjustable perpendicular to the underlayer  E04F15/02464  takes precedence -- The subclass covers building elements whose movable part is set at a right angle to the supporting layer, such as shutters, blinds or other fittings whose blades or panels can be adjusted perpendicular to the underlying frame, and it overrides the more specific feature defined in E04F15/02464.
[E04F 2019/0404] characterised by the material -- E04F 2019/0404 is a CPC subgroup for building elements or structures where the material itself is a defining feature of the invention, such as using a specific type of wood, metal, composite or other substance to achieve particular performance or aesthetic characteristics. This code is applied when the material choice distinguishes the disclosed solution from prior art.
[E04F 2019/0409] of wood -- This subclass gathers patent documents that relate to items, structures or components in which wood is the principal material, such as timber building elements, wooden frames, flooring, joinery and other wood-based constructions. It is used to classify inventions where the functional or structural purpose is achieved primarily through the use of wood.
[E04F 2019/0413] of metal
[E04F 2019/0418] of stone or stone like material, e.g. ceramics, concrete;  of glass -- E04F 2019/0418 covers building elements or structural parts that are made from stone or stone-like materials such as ceramics, concrete, and also from glass, focusing on their use in construction, façades, and interior or exterior architectural components.
[E04F 2019/0422] of organic plastics with or without reinforcements or filling materials -- The code E04F 2019/0422 relates to building components that are made from organic plastics, whether or not they contain reinforcement fibres or filler substances. It covers the manufacture, shaping, and use of such plastic parts-such as panels, profiles or structural elements-in construction applications. This entry groups technologies that employ polymer-based materials, alone or combined with additives, for building purposes.
[E04F 2019/0427] with a integrally formed hinge -- E04F 2019/0427 covers foundations, footings, walls or supporting panels that are manufactured with an integral hinge, allowing the element to pivot or fold without separate hardware. The classification is used for structures where the hinge is formed as part of the casting or molding process.
[E04F 2019/0431] of two or more materials -- E04F 2019/0431 classifies inventions concerning building elements or systems that are made from two or more materials, such as composite walls, multi-material panels, or hybrid insulation structures. It is used to capture designs that deliberately combine distinct substances to achieve improved performance, durability, or thermal properties in construction.
[E04F 2019/044] with conduits -- The subclass defines thermal-insulation components that incorporate ducts or channels for pipes, cables or other services, enabling the conduits to be integrated within the insulating layer of walls, roofs or floors. It includes designs where the conduit is formed as part of the insulation material or fitted into it.
[E04F 2019/0445] Ventilating plinths -- E04F 2019/0445 covers ventilating plinths, which are floor or foundation elements designed to provide airflow beneath walls or floors to manage moisture, temperature and condensation in buildings. The classification falls within the construction and building structures group, focusing on components that improve building durability and indoor climate through passive ventilation.
[E04F 2019/0454] with decorative effects -- The CPC subclass E04F 2019/0454 covers windows, doors, shutters and similar openings that incorporate decorative features such as ornamental patterns, coloured glazing, surface treatments or other embellishments intended to enhance appearance. It applies to inventions where the primary function is an opening element but the design includes aesthetic effects.

It falls under the CPC hierarchy for fixed constructions, building finishing work such as stairs or floors. The code addresses composite material panels used in interior or exterior construction.
[E04F 2201/049] wherein the elements are made of organic plastics with or without reinforcements or filling materials -- The subclass E04F 2201/049 covers finishing work on buildings in which sheets, plates or panels are joined and the elements are made of organic plastics, optionally reinforced or filled with other materials. It applies to construction of stairs, floors and similar components using plastic-based components.
[E04F 2201/05] Separate connectors or inserts, e.g. pegs, pins, keys or strips -- E04F 2201/05 covers the use of separate connectors or inserts-such as pegs, pins, keys or strips-for joining sheets, plates or panels in building finishing work, including stairs and floors. These elements are applied to secure parts without integral fastening, facilitating assembly and maintenance of fixed constructions.
[E04F 2201/0505] Pegs or pins -- The code covers the use of pegs or pins for mechanically fastening sheets, plates or panels in building finishing work such as stairs and flooring. It applies to fixed-construction methods where these fasteners join components without adhesives or welding.
[E04F 2201/0511] Strips or bars, e.g. nailing strips -- The CPC entry E04F 2201/0511 covers strips or bars-typically nailing strips-used to join sheets, plates or panels in building finishing work such as stairs and floors. It falls under Fixed Constructions, Building, Finishing Work on Buildings, and specifically addresses methods of joining building components with these fastening elements.
[E04F 2201/0517] U- or C-shaped brackets and clamps -- The code E04F 2201/0517 covers U- or C-shaped metal brackets and clamps used to join sheets, plates or panels in building finishing work such as stairs and floors.
[E04F 2201/0523] Separate tongues -- E04F 2201/0523 refers to a finishing-work technique for buildings in which sheets, plates or panels are joined by using separate tongue pieces that interlock with corresponding slots, allowing a secure, often removable connection without adhesives.
[E04F 2201/0529] the interlocking key acting as a dovetail-type key -- The code E04F 2201/0529 relates to building-finishing work where sheets, plates or panels are joined using an interlocking key that functions like a dovetail. It covers the design and implementation of such dovetail-type keys to securely lock components together in fixed constructions.
[E04F 2201/0535] adapted for snap locking -- E04F 2201/0535 designates building-finishing components where sheets, plates or panels are joined using snap-locking systems, allowing rapid assembly without extra fasteners. It is part of fixed constructions for interior finishes such as stairs and floors.
[E04F 2201/0541] adapted to be moved along the joint edge -- The CPC subclass E04F 2201/0541 pertains to building finishing work that joins sheets, plates or panels by means of a joint that can be moved along its edge, allowing sliding or adjustable connections often used in stairs, floors or similar structures.
[E04F 2201/0547] adapted to be moved perpendicular to the joint edge -- The CPC subclass E04F 2201/0547 covers building-finishing elements where sheets, plates or panels are joined and the assembly is designed to be moved in a direction perpendicular to the joint edge, such as sliding or swinging panels used in walls, floors or stairs.
[E04F 2201/0552] adapted to be rotated around an axis parallel to the joint edge -- E04F 2201/0552 is a CPC subclass under fixed constructions, building, and finishing work on buildings such as stairs and floors, covering methods or devices for joining sheets, plates or panels where the joint is designed to rotate about an axis parallel to the joint edge.
[E04F 2201/0558] adapted to be rotated around an axis perpendicular to the joint edge -- The entry refers to a joining method or component for sheets, plates or panels in building finishing work-such as stairs or floor construction-that is designed to rotate around an axis perpendicular to the joint edge, facilitating adjustment or movement while maintaining the connection.
[E04F 2201/0564] depending on the use of specific materials -- E04F 2201/0564 covers finishing work on buildings, such as stairs or floors, that involves joining sheets, plates or panels, where the classification is determined by the specific material used, for example wood, metal, composite or other.
[E04F 2201/057] of wood -- E04F 2201/057 deals with techniques for joining wood sheets, plates or panels in the finishing of buildings such as stairs and floors. It includes adhesives, mechanical fasteners and other methods used to secure wood components in fixed constructions.
[E04F 2201/0576] of metal
[E04F 2201/0582] of fibres or chips, e.g. bonded with synthetic resins -- E04F 2201/0582 covers methods of joining sheets, plates or panels using fibres or chips that are bonded together with synthetic resins, such as the production of fibre-board or plywood for interior finishes. This subclass falls within the finishing work on buildings hierarchy, where the composite material is created for structural or decorative purposes.
[E04F 2201/0588] of organic plastics with or without reinforcements or filling materials -- This CPC subclass pertains to the joining of sheet, plate or panel components made from organic plastics, whether they contain reinforcing fibers or filler substances, in the context of building finishing work such as stairs or floors.
[E04F 2201/0594] Hinge-like connectors -- The code E04F 2201/0594 covers hinge-type connectors that join sheets, plates or panels in building finishing work, such as stairs, floors or other fixed constructions. These devices allow relative rotation between the connected elements while maintaining a secure attachment.
[E04F 2201/06] Magnets -- The subclass E04F 2201/06 concerns the use of magnetic devices for joining sheets, plates or panels in building-finishing work such as stairs, floors or other interior structures. It includes permanent magnets, electromagnets and magnetic fasteners employed to hold or align building components during construction or refurbishment.
[E04F 2201/07] with connections using a special adhesive material
[E04F 2201/08] hook and loop-type fastener or similar fixing means -- The subclass E04F 2201/08 covers hook-and-loop type fasteners and comparable fixing devices used to join sheets, plates or panels in building finishing work such as stairs and floors. It classifies these attachment methods under fixed constructions for buildings.
[E04F 2201/09] Puzzle-type connections for interlocking male and female panel edge-parts -- This CPC subclass E04F 2201/09 covers puzzle-type connections where male and female edges of panels interlock to join sheets or plates without additional fasteners, used in building finishing work such as stairs or floors.
[E04F 2201/091] with the edge-parts forming part of the panel body -- A panel whose edge parts are integrally formed as part of the panel body, rather than being added separately, is covered by this subclass. It applies to building-finishing work where sheets, plates or panels are joined and the edges are manufactured as a continuous portion of the panel itself. This classification falls under fixed constructions, specifically finishing work on buildings.
[E04F 2201/093] with the edge-parts being separate elements not being part of the panel body -- This subclass relates to building-finishing work where sheets, plates or panels are joined and the edging is supplied by separate elements that are not integral to the panel itself, such as detachable trims or profiles used on stairs, floors or other interior surfaces.
[E04F 2201/095] with both connection parts, i.e. male and female connection parts alternating on one edge -- The code covers joining sheets, plates or panels in building finishes such as stairs or floors where the edge carries alternating male and female connection parts. The alternating connectors are arranged on the same edge to allow interlocking of the components.
[E04F 2201/096] with only one type of connection parts, i.e. with male or female on one edge -- E04F 2201/096 pertains to joining building sheets, plates or panels using a single type of connection element, such as a male or female fitting applied to one edge of the material.
[E04F 2201/098] wherein the interlocking male and female edge-parts have a dovetail, mushroom or similar shape -- E04F 2201/098 covers building finishing work that joins sheets, plates or panels using interlocking edge parts, where the male and female portions are shaped as a dovetail, mushroom or comparable form to achieve a secure, self-locking connection. This subclass falls under fixed constructions and the broader category of finishing work on buildings such as stairs and floors.
[E04F 2203/00] Specially structured or shaped covering, lining or flooring elements not otherwise provided for -- This CPC subclass (E04F 2203/00) covers building-finishing components such as coverings, linings or floor elements that have a distinctive shape or structure and are not addressed in other more specific groups. It includes specially formed panels, tiles, mats or similar pieces designed for decorative, protective or functional purposes where standard flat or conventional elements are unsuitable.
[E04F 2203/02] having particular shapes, other than square or rectangular, e.g. triangular, hexagonal, circular, irregular -- E04F 2203/02 classifies specially shaped covering, lining or flooring elements used in building finishing work that are not square or rectangular, such as triangular, hexagonal, circular or irregular profiles. It falls under the broader categories of fixed constructions, building and finishing work on buildings.
[E04F 2203/023] having triangular shapes -- E04F 2203/023 covers covering, lining or flooring elements that are specially shaped into triangles for building-finishing applications. It includes triangular stair treads, decorative floor tiles, or ceiling panels that are not otherwise classified, emphasizing the geometric form as the defining feature.
[E04F 2203/026] having hexagonal shapes -- E04F 2203/026 classifies building-finishing components such as coverings, linings or flooring elements that are specially shaped, specifically those formed with hexagonal geometry. It is placed under fixed constructions, building, finishing work on buildings.
[E04F 2203/04] comprising a plurality of internal elongated cavities arranged in substantially parallel rows -- The subclass covers specially shaped covering or flooring components that incorporate a plurality of internal elongated cavities arranged in substantially parallel rows, providing a structured surface for building interiors.
[E04F 2203/06] comprising two layers fixedly secured to one another, in offset relationship in order to form a rebate -- A finishing component for buildings where two layers are permanently joined with an offset between them to create a rebate, or stepped groove, typically used in elements such as stairs or floor coverings to provide a recessed profile. This construction is fixedly secured so the layers act as a single unit while the offset forms the desired rebate shape.
[E04F 2203/065] in offset relationship longitudinally as well as transversely -- The code covers floor or covering components that are installed so that each element is shifted relative to adjacent ones both along the run and across its width, creating an offset pattern for structural or aesthetic purposes. It is used for designs where the covering elements are not uniformly aligned but are deliberately displaced in both directions to improve load distribution or visual effect.
[E04F 2203/08] with a plurality of grooves or slits in the back side, to increase the flexibility or bendability of the elements -- The CPC subclass E04F 2203/08 covers specially shaped covering, lining or flooring components that have multiple grooves or slits on their back side to enhance flexibility or bendability. It falls under the hierarchy of fixed constructions, building, finishing work on buildings such as stairs and floors.
[E04F 2290/00] Specially adapted covering, lining or flooring elements not otherwise provided for -- E04F 2290/00 covers specially adapted covering, lining or flooring components that are not otherwise classified, intended for use in finishing work on buildings such as stairs, floors and similar fixed constructions. It applies to unique or custom-made elements that do not fit existing categories within the building-finishing hierarchy.
[E04F 2290/02] for accommodating service installations or utility lines, e.g. heating conduits, electrical lines, lighting devices or service outlets -- E04F 2290/02 covers specially adapted floor, lining or covering components that are not otherwise classified, designed to incorporate service installations or utility lines such as heating conduits, electrical wiring, lighting fixtures or service outlets. It falls under the finishing work on buildings hierarchy.
[E04F 2290/023] for heating -- E04F 2290/023 covers heating functions built into specially adapted covering, lining or flooring elements not otherwise specified, such as radiant floor heating or heated tiles incorporated into building finishes.
[E04F 2290/026] for lighting -- The CPC subclass E04F 2290/026 covers building finishing elements that incorporate lighting, such as floor panels, stair treads, or wall linings designed to provide illumination. It relates to specially adapted coverings, linings or flooring where the lighting function is integrated into the material.
[E04F 2290/04] for insulation or surface protection, e.g. against noise, impact or fire -- E04F 2290/04 covers specially adapted covering, lining or flooring elements that are provided for insulation or surface protection of building interiors, such as solutions that reduce noise transmission, absorb impact or provide fire resistance, and that are not covered by other more specific subclasses.
[E04F 2290/041] against noise -- The CPC entry E04F 2290/041 refers to building-finishing components specially adapted to reduce noise, such as acoustic panels, sound-absorbing linings or flooring systems that are not covered by other, more specific subclasses. It falls under the hierarchy of fixed constructions, building, and finishing work on buildings.
[E04F 2290/042] with a facing or top layer for sound insulation -- E04F 2290/042 refers to specially adapted covering elements with a facing or top layer for sound insulation. This code falls under finishing work on buildings. It is a specific type of flooring element.
[E04F 2290/043] with a bottom layer for sound insulation -- The CPC entry E04F 2290/043 refers to specially adapted covering, lining or flooring elements for buildings that incorporate a bottom layer intended to provide sound-insulating properties, falling under the hierarchy of Fixed Constructions, Building and Finishing Work on Buildings.
[E04F 2290/044] against impact -- E04F 2290/044 covers building-finishing components such as coverings, linings or floor elements that are specially adapted to resist impact. It includes protective panels, resilient floor coverings or impact-absorbing liners not covered by other codes.
[E04F 2290/045] against fire -- The entry E04F 2290/045 concerns fire-protective finishing elements for buildings, including specially adapted coverings, linings or flooring that are not covered elsewhere. It addresses components such as fire-resistant stair treads, floor panels or wall linings designed to limit the spread of fire within a structure.
[E04F 2290/046] with a facing or top layer for fire insulation -- E04F 2290/046 designates building-finishing components such as coverings, linings or floor elements that incorporate an additional facing or top layer specifically intended to provide fire insulation. It falls under the CPC group for specially adapted covering, lining or flooring elements not otherwise provided for.
[E04F 2290/047] with a bottom layer for fire insulation -- This CPC entry covers specially adapted covering, lining or flooring components used in building finishing work that incorporate a bottom layer designed to provide fire insulation. It falls under the E04F 2290/047 subclass of Fixed Constructions for Buildings.
[E04F 2290/048] against static electricity -- E04F 2290/048 covers building interior surfaces such as floors, stairs or linings that are specially designed to prevent or dissipate static electricity. It includes anti-static or conductive floor coverings, treatments, or integrated grounding elements intended to reduce static charge accumulation in occupied spaces.
[E04G] SCAFFOLDING -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Moulds for making units for prefabricated buildings or prefabricated stair units -> B28B7/22 - Forms or shutterings of the construction, permanent or lost forms. -> E04B , E04C  **Glossary:** - Scaffold: Temporary platform or support
[E04G 1/00] Workmen's or safety scaffolds -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Workmen's or safety scaffolds i.e. temporary platforms or supports, supported mainly by the ground.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Component parts or accessories for scaffolds -> E04G5/00 - Stagings, scaffolding, shores or struts usable both on slipways and in dry docks -> B63C5/02 - Lifting devices for movable platforms or cabins, e.g. on vehicles, permitting workmen to place themselves in any desired position for carrying out required operations -> B66F11/04 - Ladders in general, accessories specific therefor -> E06C - Ladders with platforms -> E06C1/39 - Platforms on, or for use on, ladders -> E06C7/16 - Trestles (per se). Work benches, portable stands or supports for positioning portable tools or work to be operated on thereby of trestle type -> F16M11/00 , B25H1/08
[E04G 1/02] composed essentially of members elongated in one dimension [1D] only, e.g. poles, lattice masts, with or without end portions of special form, connected together by any means -- This subclass covers scaffolding structures that are made principally from members elongated in one dimension, such as poles or lattice masts, which may have specially shaped end portions and are joined together by any type of connector.
[E04G 1/04] the members being exclusively poles, rods, beams, or other members of similar form and simple cross-section -- This subclass covers scaffolding systems in which the supporting members consist solely of poles, rods, beams or similar elements having a simple, uniform cross-section. It pertains to workmen's or safety scaffolds where no complex profiles or plates are used.
[E04G 1/06] comprising members with rod-like or tubular portions fitting together end to end, with or without separate connecting pieces -- This CPC subgroup covers workmen's or safety scaffolds built from rod-like or tubular members that are joined end to end, either directly or by using separate connecting pieces, forming a fixed construction scaffold system.
[E04G 1/08] secured together by bolts or the like penetrating the members -- The entry covers workmen's or safety scaffolds that are assembled as fixed constructions in which the individual members are joined by bolts, screws or comparable fasteners that pass through the components. It specifies that the scaffold's stability relies on these penetrating connections rather than on ties or clamps.
[E04G 1/10] Timber-work constructions -- E04G 1/10 covers timber-work constructions used as workmen's or safety scaffolds. The code applies to temporary wooden frameworks that support workers and materials on building sites, including designs, joining methods, load-bearing specifications and safety features specific to timber scaffolding.
[E04G 11/00] Forms, shutterings, or falsework for making walls, floors, ceilings, or roofs -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Forms, shutterings, or falsework for making walls, floors, ceilings, or roofs, re-usable and to be used in situ.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Forming or shuttering elements for general use -> E04G9/00 - Falsework, forms, or shutterings for particular parts of buildings, e.g. stairs, steps, cornices, balconies, i.e. formworks showing features specific for the casting of particular parts of buildings, e.g. stairs, steps, cornices, balconies -> E04G13/00 - Forms or shutterings for making i.e. formworks showing features specific for the casting of openings, cavities, slits, or channels -> E04G15/00 - Props, struts or chocks for the construction -> E04G25/00 - Moulds for making units for prefabricated buildings, i.e. moulds for the prefabrication of units for prefabricated buildings in a prefabrication site rather than in situ -> B28B7/22 - Structures made from masses, e.g. of concrete, cast or similarly formed in situ with or without making use of additional elements, such as permanent forms made of particular materials, e.g. layered products, rather than re-usable forms -> E04B1/167 - Walls made by casting, pouring, or tamping in situ made in permanent forms rather than in re-usable forms -> E04B2/86 - Floor structures wholly cast in situ with or without form units or reinforcements, i.e. floors with lost forms rather than re-usable forms -> E04B5/32
[E04G 11/02] for rooms as a whole , whole storeys, or whole buildings -- E04G 11/02 covers scaffolding, formwork or falsework that is erected to support an entire room, a whole storey, or an entire building while walls, floors, ceilings or roofs are being constructed. The subclass is used for temporary structures that hold concrete or other building materials in place during the casting process and falls under the Fixed Constructions → Building → Scaffolding hierarchy.
[E04G 11/04] for structures of spherical, spheroid or similar shape, or for cupola structures of circular or polygonal horizontal or vertical section -- E04G 11/04 covers forms, shutterings or falsework used to construct scaffolding for walls, floors, ceilings or roofs that are spherical, spheroid or similarly curved, including cupola structures with circular or polygonal horizontal or vertical sections. It applies to temporary or permanent support systems designed to shape and hold non-rectilinear building elements during construction.
[E04G 11/045] Inflatable forms -- E04G 11/045 covers inflatable forms used in construction as temporary molds for walls, floors, ceilings or roofs. These are flexible, air-filled shape systems that replace traditional rigid formwork, allowing rapid erection and removal in building and scaffolding projects.
[E04G 11/06] for walls, e.g. curved -- E04G 11/06 covers formwork, shuttering or falsework used to construct walls, including systems designed for shaping curved walls. The class falls under fixed constructions and building scaffolding and pertains to equipment that supports concrete or masonry while it sets.
[E04G 11/062] Forms for curved walls  E04G11/20  takes precedence -- This subclass covers temporary scaffolding and falsework used to shape and support curved wall sections during construction, including specially formed forms or shutters that create non-straight wall profiles. It specifies devices for curved-wall construction and overrides the broader curved-wall-forms category E04G 11/20.
[E04G 11/065] with mechanical means to modify the curvature -- E04G 11/065 covers scaffolding, shuttering or falsework used in building walls, floors, ceilings or roofs that incorporates mechanical devices to alter the curvature of the form during construction, enabling the creation of curved structural elements. It falls under Fixed Constructions → Building → Scaffolding.
[E04G 11/08] Forms, which are completely dismantled after setting of the concrete and re-built for next pouring -- E04G 11/08 refers to temporary formwork that is completely taken apart once the concrete has set and then re-erected for later pours, used in constructing walls, floors, ceilings or roofs within fixed building and scaffolding projects.
[E04G 11/082] Retractable forms for the inside face of at least three walls  for rooms as a whole  E04G11/02 -- This CPC subclass E04G 11/082 covers retractable interior formwork that lines the inside faces of three or more walls of an entire room, allowing the form to be removed as a single unit after the concrete has set. It falls under scaffolding for building construction, facilitating the creation of walls, floors, ceilings or roofs.
[E04G 11/085] End form panels for walls  for floors  E04G11/365 -- E04G 11/085 covers end form panels employed in temporary formwork for constructing walls, floors, ceilings or roofs. These panels are positioned at the termination of formwork systems to provide a clean edge and support casting concrete. The subclass falls under scaffolding and fixed constructions in the Cooperative Patent Classification.
[E04G 11/087] Fill-in form panels in the plane of two adjacent forms -- This classification covers fill-in form panels that are installed within the plane between two adjacent formwork elements, creating a continuous surface for casting walls, floors, ceilings or roofs. They are used to streamline formwork assembly and improve concrete finish quality.
[E04G 11/10] of elements without beams -- E04G 11/10 covers scaffolding systems, forms, shutterings or falsework used to construct walls, floors, ceilings or roofs that are formed from components that do not include beams. The classification applies to fixed construction methods where the supporting elements rely on alternative structural members such as frames, panels or modular units. It falls under the broader categories of fixed constructions, building and scaffolding.
[E04G 11/12] of elements and beams -- E04G 11/12 covers forms, shutterings or falsework specifically designed for the arrangement of structural elements and beams in the construction of walls, floors, ceilings or roofs. It addresses temporary support structures that position and hold these components during casting or assembly.
[E04G 11/14] with beams arranged in alignment with and between the elements -- The subclass E04G 11/14 concerns scaffolding, formwork or falsework for walls, floors, ceilings or roofs in which the supporting beams are positioned in line with and span between the structural elements they brace. It is a narrow category within fixed constructions - building - scaffolding.
[E04G 11/16] with beams placed within the wall -- E04G 11/16 covers scaffolding, formwork or falsework systems used in wall, floor, ceiling or roof construction where the supporting beams are positioned inside the wall structure rather than on its surface, providing internal reinforcement during casting or erection.
[E04G 11/18] for double walls -- E04G 11/18 addresses scaffolding or falsework intended for building double-wall structures, where two parallel walls are erected simultaneously with a cavity between them. The subclass includes temporary forms that support concrete or other building materials while the double walls set, facilitating construction of walls, floors, ceilings or roofs.
[E04G 1/12] comprising members of special, e.g. composite, cross-section or with lugs or the like or lateral apertures for supporting or attaching other members -- This subclass pertains to scaffolding whose members have specially shaped or composite cross-sections, or incorporate lugs, slots or other lateral apertures that enable other components to be supported or attached directly to them. It covers designs that integrate these features into the scaffold members to facilitate modular connection and support.
[E04G 11/20] Movable forms -- The CPC entry E04G 11/20 designates movable formwork - portable molds, shuttering or falsework that can be shifted on-site to shape walls, floors, ceilings or roofs during building construction. It is classified under Fixed Constructions, Building, Scaffolding.
[E04G 11/22] Sliding forms raised continuously or step-by-step and being in contact with the poured concrete during raising -- E04G 11/22 covers systems for raising sliding concrete forms that stay in contact with the fresh concrete, allowing continuous or step-by-step elevation while walls, floors, ceilings or roofs are cast. These moving forms are part of scaffolding or falsework and rise with the concrete as it sets, streamlining fixed-construction building.
[E04G 11/24] Construction of lifting jacks or climbing rods for sliding forms -- The entry covers devices such as lifting jacks and climbing rods that are integrated into sliding formwork systems, allowing form panels to be raised vertically as concrete cures. These components are used in building scaffolding and falsework to construct walls, floors, ceilings or roofs efficiently.
[E04G 11/26] the sheathing of which consists of ribbons, endless aprons or the like, guided by driven rollers -- E04G 11/26 covers sheathing made of continuous ribbons, endless aprons or similar strips that are guided into place by driven rollers during the construction of forms, shutterings or falsework for walls, floors, ceilings and roofs. The technology is used in fixed-construction scaffolding systems to provide flexible, continuously supplied form surfaces.
[E04G 11/28] Climbing forms, i.e. forms which are not in contact with the poured concrete during lifting from layer to layer -- The E04G 11/28 subclass refers to climbing formwork systems used in building construction, where the forms are lifted away from fresh concrete as each layer sets, remaining out of contact during the lift. It covers equipment that enables continuous vertical construction of walls, floors, ceilings or roofs without the forms staying attached to the poured concrete.
[E04G 11/30] which are lifted from layer to layer by turning, tilting, or similar moving upwards about a horizontal axis -- E04G 11/30 covers scaffolding, forms, shutterings or falsework used to construct walls, floors, ceilings or roofs that are raised from one level to the next by rotating, tilting or otherwise moving upward about a horizontal axis, allowing successive layers to be lifted without dismantling the entire structure.
[E04G 11/32] Tiltable forms or tilting tables for making walls as a whole or in parts -- E04G 11/32 covers tiltable formwork or tilting tables used to cast walls either in a single piece or in sections. The device can be inclined or rotated to facilitate concrete placement and removal, supporting the construction of walls, floors, ceilings or roofs as part of temporary formwork systems.
[E04G 11/34] Horizontally-travelling moulds for making walls blockwise or section-wise -- The code E04G 11/34 covers horizontally-travelling moulds used on scaffolding or falsework to cast wall sections or blocks in place, enabling the sequential formation of walls, floors, ceilings or roofs. These moulds move along a fixed track, allowing repetitive, blockwise construction of structural elements.
[E04G 11/36] for floors, ceilings, or roofs of plane or curved surfaces -- This CPC class covers forms, shutterings, or falsework used to construct floors, ceilings or roofs that are either flat or curved surfaces. It falls under the scaffolding sub-category of building fixed constructions.
[E04G 11/365] Stop-end shutterings  end panels for walls  E04G11/085 -- E04G 11/365 covers stop-end shutterings, also known as end panels, that are applied to the ends of formwork for walls. These components seal the open ends of temporary wall molds during concrete casting, providing a smooth finishing surface and preventing concrete leakage.
[E04G 11/38] for plane ceilings of concrete -- The CPC subclass E04G 11/38 relates to formwork, shuttering or falsework specifically designed for casting flat concrete ceilings. It is part of the fixed-construction group covering scaffolding and form systems used in building walls, floors, ceilings or roofs.
[E04G 1/14] Comprising essentially pre-assembled two-dimensional [2D] frame-like elements, e.g. of rods in L- or H-shape, with or without bracing -- This classification covers workmen or safety scaffolds built from essentially pre-assembled two-dimensional frame-like components such as L- or H-shaped rods, which may be used with or without additional bracing.
[E04G 11/40] for coffered or ribbed ceilings -- E04G 11/40 covers temporary forms, shuttering or falsework specifically designed for constructing coffered or ribbed ceilings in building projects. These devices support the material until the ceiling structure hardens.
[E04G 11/42] with beams of metal or prefabricated concrete -- This subclass covers the use of metal beams or prefabricated concrete components in forms, shutterings, or falsework that support the construction of walls, floors, ceilings or roofs. It specifies the structural members that are made of metal or pre-cast concrete rather than timber or other materials.
[E04G 11/44] with supporting beams for the shuttering used simultaneously as permanent reinforcement of the ribs -- This subclass covers temporary or permanent formwork, shuttering or falsework for walls, floors, ceilings or roofs that incorporate supporting beams which remain in place to act as permanent reinforcement of the ribs of the structure.
[E04G 11/46] of hat-like or trough-like shape encasing a rib or the section between two ribs or encasing one rib and its adjacent flat floor or ceiling section -- E04G 11/46 covers scaffolding or formwork elements that have a hat-like or trough-like cross-section and are designed to surround a rib, the gap between two ribs, or a rib together with the adjacent flat floor or ceiling area, providing support for casting walls, floors, ceilings or roofs.
[E04G 11/48] Supporting structures for shutterings or frames for floors or roofs -- The subclass E04G 11/48 covers temporary supporting structures that hold formwork, shuttering or falsework in place while floors or roofs are poured and set. It includes scaffolding, frames, brackets, beams and other devices that sustain moulds or panels until the concrete hardens.
[E04G 11/483] Supporting heads  E04G11/486  takes precedence -- Supporting heads are structural elements used in scaffolding and falsework systems to bear and distribute loads at the top of vertical members, ensuring stable support for formwork, shutters, or falsework during construction of walls, floors, ceilings, or roofs. They typically consist of brackets, caps, or plates that connect to scaffolding standards and provide a secure bearing surface for placed loads.
[E04G 11/486] Dropheads supporting the concrete after removal of the shuttering; Connecting means on beams specially adapted for dropheads -- The entry covers devices that temporarily support concrete elements after the formwork is stripped, known as dropheads, together with specially designed beam connectors that receive those dropheads to maintain the structure during construction.
[E04G 1/15] essentially comprising special means for supporting or forming platforms -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Scaffolds essentially comprising special means for supporting or forming platforms i.e. scaffolds with specific fastening features on the scaffold frame or on the board for supporting or forming platforms, e.g. hooks and corresponding holes.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Scaffold boards or planks i.e. plain scaffold boards of planks without specific fastening features, e.g. hooks or corresponding holes. -> E04G5/08 - Clips or connections for securing boards, i.e. clips which are part of neither the plank or board not the scaffold frame -> E04G7/28 - Ladders with platforms -> E06C1/39 - Platforms on, or for use on, ladders -> E06C7/16
[E04G 11/50] Girders, beams, or the like as supporting members for forms -- E04G 11/50 covers the use of girders, beams and similar structural members that serve as primary supports for formwork, shuttering or falsework in the erection of walls, floors, ceilings or roofs, detailing how these components are integrated into temporary construction frameworks.
[E04G 1/151] Platforms made of wood, with or without reinforcements  E04G1/152  takes precedence
[E04G 1/152] Platforms made of metal or with metal-supporting frame -- E04G 1/152 covers platforms that are part of workmen or safety scaffolds and are made primarily of metal or supported by a metal frame, providing a fixed, elevated working surface for construction and maintenance tasks.
[E04G 11/52] of several units arranged one after another -- E04G 11/52 covers scaffold or falsework made from a series of identical units connected in a line to create continuous formwork for walls, floors, ceilings or roofs. The design enables rapid extension of the structure by adding successive modules.
[E04G 1/153] Platforms made of plastics, with or without reinforcement  E04G1/152  takes precedence
[E04G 1/154] Non-detachably fixed and secured connections between platform and scaffold -- This subclass covers safety scaffolds whose platforms are permanently joined to the supporting structure, using fixed joints, welds, bolts or similar fasteners that cannot be detached during use. It addresses construction of non-detachably fixed and secured connections between the working platform and the scaffold framework.
[E04G 11/54] of extensible type, with or without adjustable supporting shoes, fishplates, or the like -- E04G 11/54 classifies extensible scaffolding components used as formwork for walls, floors, ceilings or roofs, including systems that can be lengthened and may feature adjustable supporting shoes, fishplates or similar devices to secure and adapt the structure during construction.
[E04G 11/56] of telescopic type -- The CPC entry E04G 11/56 covers telescopic forms, shutterings or falsework used in building construction, where the elements can extend or retract to adjust length. These telescopic devices are employed to create temporary molds for walls, floors, ceilings or roofs, allowing quick adaptation to varying span dimensions.The CPC entry E04G 11/56 covers telescopic forms, shutterings or falsework used in building construction, where the elements can extend or retract to adjust length. These telescopic devices are employed to create temporary molds for walls, floors, ceilings or roofs, allowing quick adaptation to varying span dimensions.
[E04G 1/17] Comprising essentially pre-assembled three-dimensional [3D] elements, e.g. cubic elements -- E04G 1/17 covers scaffolding systems built from essentially pre-assembled three-dimensional elements such as cubic modules. The classification applies to workmen or safety scaffolds that are supplied as ready-made 3D units which can be rapidly assembled on site to form a stable framework.
[E04G 1/18] adjustable in height -- Adjustable-in-height scaffolding refers to temporary work platforms whose vertical size can be modified to suit different tasks or building levels, enhancing flexibility and safety for workers during construction, maintenance or repair activities. The design typically includes movable standards, ledgers and trays that lock at various heights.
[E04G 1/20] Scaffolds comprising upright members and provision for supporting cross-members or platforms at different positions therealong -- E04G 1/20 covers scaffolding systems that consist of vertical uprights together with means for attaching cross-members or platforms at multiple heights along those uprights. The classification applies to workmen's or safety scaffolds where the supporting elements can be positioned at different levels to create adjustable working platforms. It is used for patents describing such configurable scaffold constructions.
[E04G 1/22] Scaffolds having a platform on an extensible substructure, e.g. of telescopic type or with lazy-tongs mechanism -- This CPC entry covers scaffolding systems where the working platform is mounted on a substructure that can be extended, such as telescopic frames or mechanisms that operate like lazy-tongs, allowing the platform height to be adjusted while the overall scaffold remains a fixed construction. It falls under the hierarchy of building scaffolds for worker safety.
[E04G 1/24] comprising essentially special base constructions -- E04G 1/24 covers workmen or safety scaffolds that are fixed constructions and incorporate essentially special base structures designed to improve stability or adapt to uneven ground. It falls under the building category of scaffolding in the CPC system and relates to permanent or semi-permanent scaffold bases rather than temporary ties.
[E04G 1/28] designed to provide support only at a low height -- E04G 1/28 classifies workmen's or safety scaffolds that are intended only to provide support at a low height. These low-rise scaffolding systems are designed for temporary use during construction or maintenance and do not incorporate additional functions such as access platforms or protective enclosures. The code falls under the hierarchy Fixed Constructions → Building → Scaffolding.
[E04G 1/30] Ladder scaffolds -- Ladder scaffolds are workmen's or safety scaffolds formed by a series of ladder-like rungs attached to a supporting frame, providing vertical access at various heights on construction sites. This type is classified under E04G 1/30 in the Cooperative Patent Classification, a subclass of fixed construction scaffolding within the building category.
