[E04G 13/00] Falsework, forms, or shutterings for particular parts of buildings, e.g. stairs, steps, cornices, balconies -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Falsework, forms, or shutterings for particular parts of buildings, e.g. stairs, steps, cornices, balconies, re-usable and to be used in situ.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Stop-end shutterings for floors, ceilings, or roofs of plane or curved surfaces -> E04G11/365 - Moulds for making units for prefabricated buildings in a prefabrication site rather than in situ; Moulds for making prefabricated stair units in a prefabrication site rather than in situ -> B28B7/225 - Removable forms or shutterings for road-building purposes -> E01C19/50 - Flat foundations with lost forms rather than re-usable forms -> E02D27/01 - Columns; Pillars; Struts of concrete other stone-like material, with or without permanent form elements, with or without internal or external reinforcement, e.g. metal coverings using permanent forms rather than in re-usable forms -> E04C3/34 - Stairways; Layouts thereof, which are using permanent forms rather than in re-usable forms -> E04F11/02
[E04G 13/02] for columns or like pillars -- This subclass deals with falsework, forms or shoring that are specifically used to construct columns or similar pillar elements within building scaffolding systems, providing temporary support and shaping for these vertical structural components.
[E04G 13/021] for circular columns -- The CPC entry E04G 13/021 covers temporary falsework, formwork or shuttering that is specifically designed for constructing circular columns in building projects. It relates to scaffolding elements used to shape and support concrete columns with a circular cross-section during casting.
[E04G 13/023] with means for modifying the sectional dimensions  E04G13/021  takes precedence -- E04G 13/023 designates falsework, forms or shoring used for building elements such as stairs, steps, cornices or balconies that incorporate mechanisms to change their cross-sectional size. The adjustment features allow the dimensions of the support or mold to be varied during construction. This sub-group follows the broader E04G 13/021 category.
[E04G 13/025] with stiff clamping means bracing the back-side of the form without penetrating the forming surface -- E04G 13/025 covers falsework, forms or shutterings for building elements such as stairs, cornices or balconies that are secured by stiff-clamping devices which brace the back side of the form without any penetration of the forming surface. This solution provides support while keeping the mould surface intact.
[E04G 13/026] the forming surface-element being the same for different sections of the column -- This CPC entry covers falsework or formwork used in building construction where the same forming surface element is employed for multiple sections of a column, providing a uniform shape and support across the column's length. It applies to scaffolding systems such as those for stairs, cornices or balconies.
[E04G 13/028] for columns' capital -- The code designates temporary formwork or falsework used specifically on the capital of a column during construction, such as molds, scaffolds or shoring that support and shape the uppermost part of a column while concrete sets or other building elements are installed.
[E04G 13/04] for lintels, beams, or transoms to be encased separately -- The CPC subclass E04G 13/04 covers falsework, forms or shuttering systems designed to cast or encase lintels, beams or transoms as separate elements in building construction, providing temporary support and shaping for these load-bearing members before they are removed.
[E04G 13/06] for stairs, steps, cornices, balconies, or other parts corbelled out of the wall
[E04G 13/062] for stairs or steps -- This subclass refers to temporary structures, forms or shutterings specifically designed for constructing stairs or steps in a building. It is part of the scaffolding group within the broader fixed-construction category for building works.
[E04G 13/064] for chimney crowns -- The CPC subclass E04G 13/064 relates to temporary falsework, forms or shutterings specifically designed for constructing chimney crowns on buildings, covering the supporting structures used while the crown material sets.
[E04G 13/066] for overhangs -- E04G 13/066 covers falsework, forms or shutterings specifically designed for overhangs in building construction, such as the temporary structures used to shape or support stair treads, cornices, balconies or other projecting elements. It falls under the hierarchy Fixed Constructions > Building > Scaffolding. This classification is used in both the EPO and USPTO patent databases.
[E04G 13/068] for window or door sills -- This subclass addresses the temporary supporting structures, forms or shuttering specifically designed for constructing window or door sills in building projects. It falls under the fixed-construction category within building scaffolding in the CPC system.
[E04G 1/32] Other free-standing supports, e.g. using trestles -- E04G 1/32 classifies free-standing scaffold supports that are not covered by more specific categories, such as trestles and other independent frames used to hold platforms or work surfaces. It falls under the broader group of workmen's or safety scaffolds within the fixed-construction and building sections of the CPC.
[E04G 1/34] Scaffold constructions able to be folded in prismatic or flat parts or able to be turned down -- This entry covers scaffolding constructions that can be folded into prismatic or flat components or turned down for compact collapse, facilitating easy transport, storage and rapid assembly of temporary work platforms.
[E04G 1/36] Scaffolds for particular parts of buildings or buildings of particular shape, e.g. for stairs, cupolas, domes -- E04G 1/36 covers scaffolding systems specially designed for distinct sections of a structure or for buildings with unique shapes such as stairways, cupolas or domes, providing tailored access and support where conventional scaffolds are unsuitable.
[E04G 1/362] specially adapted for tanks, silos or similar vessels -- This CPC subclass E04G 1/362 covers scaffolding systems that are specially adapted for use with tanks, silos, or similar vessels. It pertains to fixed construction scaffolds designed to provide safe access and support around large cylindrical storage structures in industrial settings.
[E04G 1/365] specially adapted for staircases or stairs -- This CPC entry covers workmen or safety scaffold systems that are specially adapted for use on staircases or stairs, providing a stable platform that follows the incline of steps and allows safe access and material transport. It falls under Fixed Constructions - Building - Scaffolding.
[E04G 1/367] specially adapted for elevator shafts -- E04G 1/367 covers scaffolding systems specially adapted for installation within elevator shafts, providing safe access and support for work in confined vertical passages. The subclass includes designs that accommodate the shaft's dimensions, allow secure positioning, and integrate safety features for workers operating in lift shafts.
[E04G 1/38] Scaffolds partly supported by the building -- This subclass covers scaffolding systems that are attached to the building itself for part of their load-bearing, combining independent supports with brackets, clamps or ties fixed to walls, floors or roofs. Such scaffolds rely on the structure for stability while retaining some free-standing elements.
[E04G 15/00] Forms or shutterings for making openings, cavities, slits, or channels -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Forms or shutterings for making openings, cavities, slits, or channels, to be used in situ.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Forms forming part of shuttering for walls, e.g. double walls E04G11/18 -> E04G11/06 - Forms forming part of shuttering for double walls -> E04G11/18 - Means in or on the elements for connecting same to handling apparatus, i.e. anchors in or on building elements specially adapted for handling these elements -> E04G21/142 - Templates or adjusting tools for anchoring elements or elements to be incorporated in the structure -> E04G21/185 - Arrangements specially adapted for the production of shaped articles with elements wholly or partly embedded in the moulding material, to be used in the prefabrication process in prefabrication plants; Core or mandrels to be used in the prefabrication process in prefabrication plants -> B28B23/00 , B28B23/005 , B28B23/0056 , B28B7/28 - Installations of electric cables or lines in or on buildings, distribution boxes -> H02G3/08
[E04G 15/02] for windows, doors, or the like -- A CPC subclass that covers scaffolding formwork or shuttering used to create openings such as windows, doors or similar apertures in walls or structures. It includes temporary or permanent frames that define the shape, size and location of these cavities during construction.
[E04G 15/04] Cores for anchor holes or the like -- It covers removable or permanent inserts placed in concrete or masonry to form the opening for anchor bolts, rods or similar fasteners, ensuring the cavity remains clean and correctly sized while the surrounding material cures. These cores may be made of metal, plastic or composite and are used in building scaffolding and other fixed constructions.
[E04G 15/06] for cavities or channels in walls of floors, e.g. for making chimneys -- E04G 15/06 classifies forms or shutterings used to create cavities or channels in walls or floors, such as those required for chimney passages. These fixed construction components are part of scaffolding systems employed during building work to shape openings, slots or ducts in structural elements.
[E04G 15/061] Non-reusable forms  positioning means without forming function  E04G21/185 ; electric distribution boxes  H02G3/08 -- This subclass covers formwork or shuttering components that are installed once and not intended for repeated use, including the fixtures and positioning devices that hold them in place but do not participate in the shaping of concrete, and may incorporate electric distribution boxes for power supply. It pertains to scaffolding applications where the forms are disposable and integrated with electrical infrastructure.
[E04G 15/063] Re-usable forms -- E04G 15/063 covers reusable forms used in construction scaffolding to create openings, cavities, slits or channels. These forms are designed to be assembled, removed and employed repeatedly, offering a durable alternative to single-use shuttering in building projects.
[E04G 15/065] with mechanical means to modify the section  E04G15/068  takes precedence -- E04G 15/065 covers scaffolding or formwork used to create openings, cavities, slits or channels that incorporate mechanical devices for adjusting the shape or dimensions of the section, and it overrides the broader subclass E04G 15/068. This subclass falls under the hierarchy of Fixed Constructions → Building → Scaffolding → Forms or shutterings for making openings, cavities, slits, or channels.
[E04G 15/066] with fluid means to modify the section  E04G15/068  takes precedence -- The CPC subclass E04G 15/066 covers scaffolding or fixed construction forms and shutterings used to create openings, cavities, slits or channels in building work, wherein fluid mechanisms are employed to adjust the shape or dimension of the section. This subclass is subordinate to E04G 15/068, which has priority over it.
[E04G 15/068] for channels open towards the surface -- The code E04G 15/068 covers scaffolding forms or shutterings used to create channels that open directly to the surface, such as ducts, pipelines or passageways formed during building construction. It falls under fixed constructions, building, scaffolding, and the broader group for making openings, cavities, slits or channels. This classification is applied to technologies that shape and support open-to-surface channel structures on site.
[E04G 17/00] Connecting or other auxiliary members for forms, falsework structures, or shutterings -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Connecting or other auxiliary members for forms, falsework structure, or shutterings, for re-usable forms to be used in situ.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Forms for rooms as a whole, by which walls and floors are cast simultaneously, whole storeys, or whole buildings -> E04G11/02 - Forms for vertical ducts -> E04G11/06 - Forms for columns -> E04G13/02 - Struts fro the construction -> E04G25/00 - Walls made by casting, pouring, or tamping in situ made in permanent forms with ties going through the forms. i.e. ties for lost forms -> E04B2/8647
[E04G 17/001] Corner fastening or connecting means for forming or stiffening elements  forms for rooms as a whole  E04G11/02 ; forms for vertical ducts  E04G11/06 ; forms for columns  E04G13/02 -- The code E04G 17/001 relates to corner fasteners and other joining devices that connect or stiffen elements of formwork, falsework or shuttering. It includes connectors used for whole-room form systems, for vertical duct forms and for column forms, covering the means of fastening the corners of these temporary building structures.
[E04G 17/002] Workplatforms, railings; Arrangements for pouring concrete, attached to the form -- E04G 17/02 relates to work platforms, guardrails and associated devices that are integrated with formwork for concrete casting. It covers structures that support workers or equipment, and the means of attaching these platforms or railings to the forms, enabling safe access and concrete placement during construction.
[E04G 17/004] Strips for creating a chamfered edge -- Strips designed to produce a chamfered edge on formwork, falsework or shoring members. They are attached to scaffolding components to shape concrete edges or openings, improving finish and facilitating removal.
[E04G 17/005] Strips for covering joints between form sections, e.g. to avoid burring or spilling of laitance -- The CPC subclass E04G 17/005 covers thin strips or profiles used to seal the joints between adjacent formwork sections in concrete construction. These joint coverings are applied to prevent material leakage, edge burring, or the spilling of laitance, thereby ensuring a smoother surface and improved form integrity.
[E04G 17/007] Plugs to close openings in forms' surface -- The CPC entry E04G 17/007 covers plugs designed to seal openings in the surface of concrete forms, such as those used in scaffolding, falsework or shuttering. These plugs act as temporary closure members to prevent material leakage and improve surface finish during casting.
[E04G 17/02] Connecting or fastening means for non-metallic forming or stiffening elements -- The entry covers devices that join or secure non-metallic components used in scaffolding, such as clamps, brackets, ties or other fastening means that connect auxiliary members of formwork, falsework or shuttering structures. These solutions provide mechanical linkage without employing metal parts.
[E04G 17/04] Connecting or fastening means for metallic forming or stiffening elements -- The CPC entry E04G 17/04 covers devices that join or secure metal components used in scaffolding, formwork, falsework or shuttering systems. It includes bolts, brackets, clamps, welds and other fastening elements that connect metallic studs, members or stiffening pieces to create a stable, fixed construction framework. This classification is part of the building-scaffolding subgroup within fixed constructions.
[E04G 17/042] being tensioned by threaded elements  E04G17/047  takes precedence -- E04G 17/042 covers auxiliary members that connect or support scaffolding, formwork, falsework or shuttering and are tensioned by threaded elements such as bolts, screws or turnbuckles, allowing adjustment of tension. These components secure the temporary structure, and the more specific subclass E04G 17/047 supersedes it.
[E04G 17/045] being tensioned by wedge-shaped elements  E04G17/047  takes precedence -- E04G 17/045 refers to auxiliary members used in scaffolding, formwork or falsework that are secured by applying tension with wedge-shaped elements. The wedges create a tight fit that holds the components in place during construction. This method provides adjustable, removable tension for temporary building structures.
[E04G 17/047] simultaneously tying two facing forms -- This subclass deals with devices or procedures that fasten two opposite formwork panels together in a single action, securing alignment and rigidity while concrete is placed. It includes clamps, ties or locking mechanisms that simultaneously bind the facing forms, speeding erection and later release.
[E04G 17/06] Tying means -- E04G 17/06 covers tying means for scaffolding and related temporary structures, including ropes, wires, straps, clamps or other devices used to secure auxiliary members, formwork or falsework components together. These elements provide the mechanical connection that holds the construction in place during erection, adjustment or dismantling.
[E04G 17/064] Spacers placed on the bottom of the mould -- A spacer attached to the underside of a mould serves as a temporary support that maintains a uniform gap between the mould and the work surface during concrete placement, allowing proper concrete flow and preventing premature contact. This element is considered an auxiliary component for scaffolding and falsework structures, facilitating accurate formwork positioning and easier removal after curing.
[E04G 17/0642] Devices for extracting or inserting wall ties or parts thereof -- The entry E04G 17/0642 covers tools and mechanisms designed to extract or insert wall ties, or components of wall ties, in scaffolding, falsework or shuttering systems. It pertains to auxiliary devices that facilitate the removal, placement or adjustment of these fastening elements within fixed construction frameworks.
[E04G 17/0644] Plug means for tie-holes -- E04G 17/0644 relates to plugs that fill tie-holes in scaffolding, falsework or shuttering members. These removable devices seal openings created for ties when the ties are not installed, preserving the strength and stability of the temporary structure. They are used as auxiliary components in fixed-construction building applications.
[E04G 17/065] Tying means, the tensional elements of which are threaded to enable their fastening or tensioning -- A tying means used in scaffolding or falsework that incorporates threads on its tensile elements so they can be fastened or tensioned by screwing. The threaded design allows precise adjustment of tension and secure attachment to other components. It is classified under E04G 17/065 for auxiliary members connecting or supporting temporary structures.
[E04G 17/0651] One-piece elements -- The subclass covers single-piece components used to connect or support formwork, falsework, or shuttering in scaffolding systems, serving as auxiliary members without separate joints. These one-piece elements simplify assembly and provide structural continuity for fixed-construction applications.
[E04G 17/0652] fully recoverable -- The CPC entry E04G 17/0652 classifies scaffolding components that serve as connecting or auxiliary members for formwork, falsework or shuttering and are designed to be fully recoverable for reuse after the temporary structure is dismantled.
[E04G 17/0654] remaining completely or partially embedded in the cast material -- This subclass covers auxiliary members of scaffolding or falsework that are left in place within the concrete after it has set, either fully or partly embedded, rather than being removed. Such members become part of the finished structure, providing reinforcement or functional integration.
[E04G 17/0655] the element consisting of several parts -- E04G 17/0655 covers composite connector elements made of multiple joined parts that link or support components of scaffolding, formwork, falsework, or shuttering. These multi-part members provide adjustable or modular connections between structural members, facilitating assembly and disassembly of temporary construction frameworks.
[E04G 17/0657] fully recoverable -- E04G 17/0657 covers scaffolding components that are designed to be fully recoverable, meaning the connecting or auxiliary members used in forms, falsework or shuttering can be completely removed and reused after the structure is completed. These elements are intended for temporary installation and later disassembly without damage.
[E04G 17/0658] remaining completely or partially embedded in the cast material -- The code E04G 17/0658 designates scaffold or other auxiliary components that are left in place during concrete casting and remain wholly or partly embedded in the hardened material. It applies to connecting members for forms, falsework or shuttering that are intentionally retained in the finished structure rather than removed after the cast solidifies.
[E04G 17/07] Tying means, the tensional elements of which are fastened or tensioned by means of wedge-shaped members -- E04G 17/07 relates to tying means used in scaffolding, falsework or shuttering where the tension elements are secured or tensioned by wedge-shaped members, providing a way to connect auxiliary structural components in fixed construction.
[E04G 17/0707] One-piece elements -- A one-piece element is a single, integral component used to join or support auxiliary members of forms, falsework or shuttering in scaffolding systems, such as caps, brackets or similar connectors that provide a fixed construction link without separate fastening parts.
[E04G 17/0714] fully recoverable -- The CPC code E04G 17/0714 designates fully recoverable auxiliary members for scaffolding, falsework or shuttering in building construction. It includes connecting components that can be completely removed and reused after the formwork or falsework is taken down.
[E04G 17/0721] remaining completely or partially embedded in the cast material -- This subclass covers scaffolding or falsework members that are left in the concrete after it cures, either wholly or partly embedded in the cast material. It includes connectors, anchors, or other auxiliary parts that remain in place to provide structural support or serve as permanent fixtures.
[E04G 17/0728] the element consisting of several parts -- The entry covers a scaffolding component made up of two or more separate parts that together form a single element used to link or support forms, falsework or shuttering. It applies to fixed-construction building scaffolds where the multi-part piece functions as a connector, brace or other auxiliary member within the overall scaffolding system.
[E04G 17/0735] fully recoverable -- Fully recoverable members are auxiliary components used to connect or support formwork, falsework, or shuttering that can be completely removed and reused after the concrete has set, minimizing waste and allowing rapid disassembly of temporary structures.
[E04G 17/0742] remaining completely or partially embedded in the cast material -- This subclass covers scaffold or other auxiliary members used in forms, falsework, or shuttering that stay wholly or partly embedded in the poured material after it hardens, such as permanent ties, brackets or supports left in place within concrete or similar cast structures.
[E04G 17/075] Tying means, the tensional elements of which are fastened or tensioned by other means -- E04G 17/075 covers tying devices for scaffolding and other auxiliary members used in forms, falsework, or shuttering where the tension members are secured or tensioned by separate mechanisms. These tying means include clamps, brackets or adjustable fasteners that apply and maintain tension without relying on the tie itself for adjustment.
[E04G 17/0751] One-piece elements -- This CPC subclass covers one-piece auxiliary members that connect or support forms, falsework or shuttering in scaffolding systems. It includes monolithic brackets, links, connectors or similar elements that are produced as a single unit rather than assembled from separate parts, providing a permanent or semi-permanent joining function.
[E04G 17/0752] fully recoverable -- E04G 17/0752 covers scaffold or falsework connectors and auxiliary members that are designed to be completely removed and reused after the building work is finished. These fully recoverable components include detachable ties, clamps, brackets and other temporary joining devices that can be disassembled without damaging the structure.
[E04G 17/0754] remaining completely or partially embedded in the cast material -- This subcategory addresses scaffold or auxiliary connecting members that are left in place within the cast material after formwork is removed, whether they remain fully or only partially embedded. It relates to fixed-construction methods where such components become a permanent part of the building structure.
[E04G 17/0755] the element consisting of several parts -- E04G 17/0755 relates to a modular element composed of several parts that serves as a connecting or auxiliary member in scaffolding, falsework or shuttering structures, covering assemblies such as prefabricated ties, brackets or links that join structural components to form a stable framework.
[E04G 17/0757] fully recoverable -- Fully recoverable members are auxiliary components used in formwork, falsework or shuttering that can be removed and reused after the concrete has set. They are typically fabricated from steel or aluminum and provide temporary support while being designed for easy disassembly without damage, allowing repeated use in scaffolding and building construction.
[E04G 17/0758] remaining completely or partially embedded in the cast material -- This subclass concerns scaffolding or auxiliary members that are left in place within the cast material, such as connectors, brackets or ties that remain wholly or partially embedded after the formwork is removed. It applies to fixed construction elements used in building scaffolding and falsework that become a permanent part of concrete or other cast structures.
[E04G 17/12] with arms engaging the forms -- A subclass of the CPC for building scaffolding that covers auxiliary members used to connect or support formwork, false-work or shuttering systems, specifically those equipped with arms that engage the forms. This code groups devices such as brackets, clamps or other linking parts that hold the form panels in place during casting or construction.
[E04G 17/14] Bracing or strutting arrangements for formwalls -- This CPC entry covers the design and implementation of bracing or strutting systems that support formwalls in formwork, falsework or shuttering structures. It addresses auxiliary members that stabilize or reinforce the walls of temporary molds used in concrete construction, ensuring rigidity and safety during the casting process.
[E04G 17/16] Members, e.g. consoles, for attachment to the wall to support girders, beams, or the like carrying forms or moulds for floors, lintels, or transoms -- The code E04G 17/16 covers wall-mounted members such as consoles or brackets that support girders, beams or similar elements carrying formwork, falsework or shuttering for floors, lintels or transoms in building construction.
[E04G 17/18] Devices for suspending or anchoring form elements to girders placed in ceilings, e.g. hangers -- The entry covers devices that attach or hang formwork components to ceiling-mounted girders, such as hangers or similar anchoring hardware. These are used in scaffolding and falsework to secure form elements, shutters or auxiliary members that support concrete pours.
[E04G 19/00] Auxiliary treatment of forms, e.g. dismantling -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Load-engaging elements or devices attached to lifting or lowering gear of cranes or adapted for connection therewith for transmitting lifting forces to articles or groups of articles by mechanical means -> B66C1/10 - Lubricating compositions -> C10M
[E04G 19/003] Arrangements for stabilising the forms or for moving the forms from one place to another  lifting of articles in general  B66C1/00 -- E04G 19/003 pertains to devices and methods for stabilising scaffolding forms and for transporting them within a construction site, including general lifting arrangements. It includes mechanisms that secure the forms during use and facilitates their relocation, such as jacks, braces, or movable supports.
[E04G 19/006] Cleaning devices -- Cleaning devices for scaffolding are tools or systems designed to remove dust, debris, liquids or other contaminants from scaffold components during or after construction, facilitating safe dismantling or reuse. They may include brushes, water jets, vacuum units or integrated cleaning mechanisms attached to the scaffold structure.
[E04G 2001/155] Platforms with an access hatch for getting through from one level to another -- E04G 2001/155 covers platforms that have an access hatch enabling movement from one level to another, such as raised work platforms, staging or access floors equipped with a hatch for safe vertical passage. The code is part of the Cooperative Patent Classification for building and construction elements that facilitate level-to-level access.
[E04G 2001/156] Stackable platforms -- Stackable platforms are modular, load-bearing platforms that can be placed one on top of another to create adjustable height or extend floor area. They are used in construction, industrial settings, and temporary work sites for safe access, storage, or work platforms, allowing rapid assembly and reconfiguration.
[E04G 2001/157] Extensible platforms, e.g. telescopic platforms -- E04G 2001/157 classifies adjustable or extensible work platforms such as telescopic or fold-out platforms used in construction, maintenance, or access tasks. It covers designs that allow length or height to be varied on-site, including mechanisms, supports and safety features that enable rapid extension and retraction.
[E04G 2001/158] Platforms supported by spigots which engage through holes in the platform -- E04G 2001/158 covers platforms that are held up by spigots inserted into openings drilled in the platform surface, allowing the platform to be mounted on a supporting frame or structure. The spigots engage the holes to secure the platform in a fixed position, often used for agricultural or horticultural equipment installations where a stable, elevated working surface is required.
[E04G 2001/242] Scaffolds movable on wheels or tracks -- E04G 2001/242 refers to scaffolding systems that can be moved along wheels or tracks, allowing the structure to be repositioned easily during construction or maintenance work. The classification covers designs, attachment mechanisms and supporting elements that enable mobility while maintaining stability and load-bearing capacity. It is part of the CPC section for building and civil engineering, specifically the group for temporary works and scaffolds.
[E04G 2001/244] mechanically operated -- The CPC entry E04G 2001/244 pertains to building elements such as doors, windows or shutters that are opened, closed or adjusted by mechanical means, including levers, gears, cams, springs or hand-cranked devices. It covers the design, structure and operation of these mechanically powered closing or locking mechanisms.
[E04G 2001/246] human powered -- E04G 2001/246 is a CPC subgroup for inventions that rely on human effort as the source of power, covering manually operated devices such as human-powered ventilation, pumps, or other mechanical systems that use muscle work instead of electric or combustion energy.
[E04G 2001/248] Inclined struts -- E04G 2001/248 covers inclined struts used as angled structural members in roofs, walls or other constructions to support loads and provide bracing. The subclass includes rafters, sloping supports and similar elements, regardless of material, that are positioned at a non-vertical angle to transfer forces.
[E04G 2001/302] with ladders supporting the platform -- This CPC subclass covers scaffolding platforms where one or more ladders are integrated or attached to support the platform, providing access for workers. It includes designs where the ladder serves as the main means of supporting the working surface, typical in construction and maintenance scaffolds.
[E04G 2001/305] The ladders being vertical and perpendicular to the platform -- This CPC subgroup deals with structures where ladders are mounted vertically and at a right angle to a platform, providing safe access between levels on ships, offshore installations or similar platforms. It includes designs, attachment means and safety features for such perpendicular ladders.
[E04G 2001/307] The ladders being self-supporting folding ladders -- E04G 2001/307 covers self-supporting folding ladders, i.e. ladders that can be folded for storage and used without needing an external frame or wall for stability. These ladders incorporate hinges or joints that allow them to be compactly collapsed yet provide a rigid, load-bearing structure when opened.
[E04G 2003/283] mobile horizontally -- E04G 2003/283 relates to foundation or support structures that are capable of horizontal movement, such as sliding, rolling or otherwise mobile bearings and adjustable supports used to accommodate shifts in the ground or loads in bridges, buildings and other civil engineering works.
[E04G 2003/286] mobile vertically -- E04G 2003/286 classifies vertically mobile building components such as retractable panels, liftable walls or adjustable floors that can be moved up and down within a structure. The entry covers mechanisms that enable vertical translation of these elements for space management or access.
[E04G 2005/008] Hoisting devices specially adapted as part of a scaffold system -- E04G 2005/008 covers hoisting devices that are expressly incorporated into a scaffold system, such as winches, hoists, or pulley assemblies designed to lift building materials, tools or personnel while remaining integral to the scaffold's structure and operation.
[E04G 2005/068] Connections of consoles at an angle -- E04G 2005/068 covers the way supporting structures such as consoles are joined to other components at an angle, including methods, devices and arrangements that provide angular connections in building frameworks such as brackets, hinges and angled joints.
[E04G 2005/125] Roof for scaffolds, e.g. extending to the adjacent building -- E04G 2005/125 covers roof structures designed for scaffolding systems, especially those that can be extended or attached to an adjacent building to provide weather protection. It includes modular or adjustable roof panels that integrate with the scaffold framework and connect to the building surface.
[E04G 2005/148] latticed or netted -- E04G 2005/148 pertains to the use of latticed or netted constructions in building elements, encompassing frameworks, mesh, or grid structures employed for walls, roofs, formwork, reinforcement, or protective screens in civil engineering and architectural applications.
[E04G 2007/005] Adaptors to adapt the connection means of one manufacturer's scaffold system to the one of another manufacturer -- E04G 2007/005 covers auxiliary parts that modify a scaffold's joining elements so they can connect with components from a different manufacturer's system. The adaptors enable mixed-manufacturer scaffold assemblies by providing compatible couplings, brackets or sleeves that bridge differing connection standards. This facilitates flexible erection and reuse of scaffold structures across brands.
[E04G 2007/285] specially adapted for toe boards -- E04G 2007/285 covers building components that have been specially designed or modified to incorporate toe boards, the low barriers installed at the edge of platforms, scaffolds or floors to prevent tools, materials or workers from falling. The classification is applied to patents describing protective edge devices integrated into construction elements.
[E04G 2009/023] with edge protection -- E04G 2009/023 pertains to floor covering elements that incorporate edge protection, such as panels or boards with reinforced or sealed borders. The protection is intended to guard the perimeter against damage, wear, impact or moisture infiltration.
[E04G 2009/025] by a flange of the board's frame -- This CPC subclass covers mounting or securing building boards, panels or cladding by means of a flange that is an integral part of the board's frame, allowing the board to be attached directly to a supporting structure without additional fasteners. The method is typical for prefabricated wall or floor elements where the frame-flange provides a load-bearing connection.
[E04G 2009/026] specific for corners -- E04G 2009/026 relates to building components that are designed specifically for corners, such as protective corner trims, corner reinforcement elements or decorative corner mouldings used in the construction of walls, facades or other structural members. It covers the shape, attachment means and functional features intended to protect, strengthen or finish the intersection of two building surfaces.
[E04G 2009/028] with reinforcing ribs on the underside -- A roofing or building sheet that incorporates reinforcing ribs on its underside to increase stiffness, support loads and improve handling; the ribs are integral to metal, plastic or composite panels used as roof-covering elements.
[E04G 2011/067] Integrated forms comprising shuttering skin, bracing or strutting arrangements, workplatforms and railings -- The code covers integrated formwork systems used in concrete construction where the shuttering skin is combined with built-in bracing or strutting, along with attached work platforms and railings, providing a single, self-supporting unit for shaping and accessing poured concrete.
[E04G 2011/505] with nailable or screwable inserts -- E04G 2011/505 covers walls or partitions that include built-in inserts designed to receive nails or screws, allowing fixtures, panels or other components to be attached directly to the structure. The classification is used for building elements where such fastener-ready openings are integral to the wall design.
[E04G 2017/008] Pin and hole connection type -- E04G 2017/008 covers mechanical connections in construction where a pin is inserted into a corresponding hole to join components, typically used in timber or metal framing, allowing for easy assembly and disassembly. The classification addresses the geometry, materials, and fastening methods of such pin-and-hole joints.
[E04G 2017/0646] made of a flat strip, e.g. of metal -- In CPC E04G 2017/0646 the invention relates to roofing elements that consist of a flat strip, typically metal, such as panels, battens or sheets used to form a roof structure. The code covers constructions where the roofing material is a flat metal strip, often designed to interlock or overlap for weatherproofing.
[E04G 2017/0648] The strip being twisted -- E04G 2017/0648 covers methods and devices for twisting a strip of material to create a helical or spiraled element used in construction or masonry. The classification includes apparatus that bends, rotates or winds the strip to provide reinforcement, decorative profiling or joining of building components.
[E04G 2021/049] concrete mixing nozzles specially adapted for conveying devices -- The CPC subgroup E04G 2021/049 relates to concrete-mixing nozzles that are specifically designed to be integrated with conveying devices, such as pumps or conveyors, for delivering concrete to the point of use. It covers nozzle constructions that incorporate mixing elements within the nozzle itself, enabling on-site blending of cement, aggregates and water as the concrete is conveyed for applications like shotcrete, tunnelling or prefabricated element production.
[E04G 2021/127] Circular prestressing of, e.g. columns, tanks, domes -- E04G 2021/127 covers techniques for applying circular prestressing to structural members such as columns, tanks and domes. The subclass includes methods that use circumferentially arranged tendons, cables or reinforcement to generate compressive stresses, improving load capacity, crack resistance and overall durability of the element.
[E04G 2021/128] Prestressing each strand of a cable one by one to the same tension -- This subclass covers a method of tensioning a multi-strand cable in which each strand is prestressed sequentially to achieve the same target force, rather than loading the whole bundle together. The technique is applied in construction of prestressed concrete members, bridges, and other tensioned structures where uniform strand tension improves performance and safety.
[E04G 2021/208] on a vertical joint -- E04G 2021/208 relates to doors, windows, skylights, shutters or similar building elements that are installed on a vertical joint, i.e., the meeting line between two vertical structural parts such as wall panels or framing members. It covers the design, sealing, fastening or other provisions that enable the element to be mounted, connected or weather-tight at that vertical junction. This subclass is used for inventions that specifically address the challenges of fitting building apertures on vertical joints.
[E04G 2021/248] Tarpaulins specially adapted therefor -- E04G 2021/248 covers tarpaulins that are designed or modified for a specific purpose, such as protecting structures, machinery or goods during transport, storage or construction. The classification includes tarps with special reinforcement, fastening systems, weather-resistant coatings or shapes tailored to particular applications.
[E04G 2021/3257] specific for floors made of prefabricated hollow plates -- E04G 2021/3257 covers floor systems that employ prefabricated hollow plates, detailing their design, production, connection methods and installation in building structures. The subclass addresses the use of lightweight, cavity-filled panels to create spanning or load-bearing floors, including variations in material, reinforcement and prefabrication processes.
[E04G 2023/0248] of elements made of wood -- This CPC entry classifies patents concerning building components whose primary material is wood, such as structural members, panels, claddings or other elements used in construction or building extensions. It falls within the E04G group for elements of building construction, capturing innovations in wooden building parts.
[E04G 2023/0251] by using fiber reinforced plastic elements -- The subclass E04G 2023/0251 pertains to the use of fiber-reinforced plastic elements in construction, covering the design, manufacture or installation of building components such as beams, panels, columns or reinforcement that are made from or include FRP materials.
[E04G 2023/0255] whereby the fiber reinforced plastic elements are stressed -- E04G 2023/0255 covers inventions in which fiber-reinforced plastic components within a construction are subjected to mechanical stress, including methods for applying, measuring or monitoring that stress and related apparatuses. The entry is used for patents dealing with load-bearing or stress-testing of composite structural elements in buildings, bridges, roads or other civil-engineering works.
[E04G 2023/0259] Devices specifically adapted to stress the fiber reinforced plastic elements -- E04G 2023/0259 covers devices designed to apply load, tension, compression, bending or other mechanical stresses to fiber-reinforced plastic components for testing, shaping, joining or quality-control purposes. Examples include testing rigs, fixtures and apparatuses that specifically engage such reinforced plastic elements to evaluate their performance or to facilitate manufacturing processes.
[E04G 2023/0262] Devices specifically adapted for anchoring the fiber reinforced plastic elements, e.g. to avoid peeling off -- The subclass includes devices that secure fiber-reinforced plastic elements to a supporting structure so that the plastic does not delaminate or peel away. It covers mechanical anchors, brackets, interlocking fittings and similar means that provide a reliable, load-bearing connection for such composites.
[E04G 2023/085] crowbars specially adapted for wrecking wooden buildings -- The CPC entry E04G 2023/085 falls under the group for demolition tools and equipment used in construction. It specifically covers crowbars that have been specially designed or modified for the purpose of tearing down wooden buildings, focusing on features that enhance leverage, grip or cutting ability for timber structures.
[E04G 2023/086] of tanks, reservoirs or the like -- E04G2023/086 covers designs, construction and arrangement of tanks, reservoirs or similar liquid-storage units used for water supply, irrigation, flood control or related purposes. The subclass includes features such as shape, lining, access openings, foundations and integration with adjoining hydraulic infrastructure.
[E04G 2023/087] of chimneys, smoke stacks or the like -- The CPC subclass E04G 2023/087 concerns methods, apparatuses and procedures for dismantling, removing or demolishing chimneys, smokestacks and similar vertical exhaust structures, usually as part of site-clearance, land-reclamation or building-demolition activities, including related safety and handling techniques.
[E04G 2023/088] of window or door frames -- E04G 2023/088 covers inventions concerning the design, manufacture, assembly, or improvement of window or door frames for building construction, including material choices, profiling, fastening, sealing, reinforcement, and related safety or functional features. It addresses structural and functional aspects of frame components.
[E04G 2025/003] Supports therefor, e.g. tripods -- E04G 2025/003 concerns supporting devices for construction, civil-engineering or related works, specifically tripods and similar structures that provide a stable base for scaffolding, formwork, measurement or equipment. The subclass includes designs, adjustments and mechanisms that enable the tripod to bear loads and be positioned on various terrains. It is used to classify inventions that improve the stability, portability or load-carrying capacity of such supports.
[E04G 2025/006] Heads therefor, e.g. pivotable -- E04G 2025/006 covers the design of pivotable or otherwise movable heads attached to roof structures such as dormer tops, vent openings, shutters or awnings, where the head can be turned, folded or rotated to open, close or adjust the roof element. It includes mechanisms that allow the head to pivot about an axis to regulate access, ventilation or weather protection.
[E04G 2025/042] with devices to avoid accidental disengagement of the telescopic elements, e.g. during transport -- The entry covers mechanisms that lock or retain telescopic sections of doors, windows, shutters or similar extendable components so they cannot unintentionally separate, especially during handling or transport. It includes devices such as catches, latches or locking elements that keep the telescopic parts engaged until deliberately released.
[E04G 2025/045] which telescoping action effected by a lever -- The subclass covers mechanisms in which a lever drives a telescoping movement to open, close, or adjust a door, window, shutter or similar closure, using nested sections that extend or retract under lever action. It includes lever-actuated telescopic guides, rods and linkages that provide linear displacement for the opening or sealing function.
[E04G 2025/047] which telescoping action effected by a piston -- The code covers ventilating structures in roofs, ceilings or walls where a piston drives a telescoping movement of a flap or vent, allowing an adjustable opening for airflow. The mechanism uses the piston to extend or retract the component, providing controlled ventilation.
