[E04G 21/005] Mortar boards -- E04G 21/005 designates mortar boards, portable flat trays used on scaffolding to hold, mix and transport mortar or similar building materials, facilitating preparation and handling of construction elements at height.
[E04G 21/02] Conveying or working-up concrete or similar masses able to be heaped or cast -- The subclass E04G 21/02 covers techniques and apparatus for moving, shaping, or otherwise processing concrete and comparable bulk masses that are heaped or cast, including conveyors, mixers, vibrators and similar equipment used in handling such building materials during construction.
[E04G 21/025] Buckets specially adapted for use with concrete -- The subclass E04G 21/025 covers buckets that are specially designed for handling concrete, featuring reinforced walls, tapered shapes or built-in mixing elements to facilitate the transport, placement and working-up of concrete on construction sites. It falls under fixed constructions, building and scaffolding.
[E04G 21/04] Devices for both conveying and distributing -- E04G 21/04 covers apparatus used on scaffolding that simultaneously transports building materials and distributes them to specific work locations, such as integrated conveyor-feed systems that move items along the scaffold and release them where needed for assembly or maintenance.
[E04G 21/0409] with conveyor belts  truck mixer with conveyor belts  B28C5/4255 -- This subclass applies to scaffolding that includes conveyor belts, for example a truck-mounted mixer equipped with belt conveyors to transport building material. It relates to fixed construction equipment used in preparing, conveying or working-up building elements.
[E04G 21/0418] with distribution hose -- E04G 21/0418 pertains to scaffolding systems that incorporate a distribution hose for supplying fluids, powders or other building materials along the scaffold. The hose is integrated into the structure to convey materials directly to work zones, supporting preparation, conveyance or processing of building elements.
[E04G 21/0427] on a static support, e.g. crane -- The code E04G 21/0427 covers techniques for preparing, conveying or processing building materials while they rest on a stationary support such as a crane, commonly used in scaffolding and other fixed-construction operations. It includes devices and methods that handle loads on non-moving platforms during construction.
[E04G 21/0436] on a mobile support, e.g. truck -- This subclass covers scaffolding or other building work performed on a mobile support such as a truck, allowing the structure to be moved while maintaining a fixed construction arrangement. It includes methods and devices for preparing, transporting, or handling building materials and elements on such movable platforms.
[E04G 21/0445] with booms -- E04G 21/0445 covers scaffolding systems that incorporate boom extensions for handling or positioning building materials and components during construction. The booms may be telescopic, hinged, or articulated to reach elevated work areas and facilitate the conveyance, placement, or processing of structural elements. This subclass pertains to fixed constructions where such boom-equipped scaffolds are employed.
[E04G 21/0454] with boom vibration damper mechanisms -- The entry covers scaffolding systems that incorporate boom vibration-damper mechanisms to lessen oscillations while moving or positioning building materials. It pertains to fixed-construction scaffolding used for preparing, conveying, or working up building elements, improving stability and safety during operations.
[E04G 21/0463] with boom control mechanisms, e.g. to automate concrete distribution -- This subclass refers to scaffolding that includes boom-control mechanisms to automate concrete distribution, allowing precise and efficient placement of concrete during construction. It is grouped under fixed constructions, building, scaffolding, and the broader category of preparing, conveying, or working-up building materials and elements.
[E04G 21/0472] Details of connection of the hose to the formwork, e.g. inlets -- The entry classified under E04G 21/0472 concerns the specific design and detailing of hose connections to formwork, such as inlet fittings, attachment methods, sealing arrangements and related components used when conveying concrete or other building materials to the formwork.
[E04G 21/0481] Concrete chutes -- Concrete chutes are rigid channels or troughs installed on construction sites to direct fresh concrete from mixers to placement points, often integrated with scaffolding to convey the material vertically or horizontally. They enable controlled delivery of concrete, reducing manual handling during the preparation and conveying of building elements.
[E04G 21/06] Solidifying concrete, e.g. by application of vacuum before hardening -- E04G 21/06 relates to methods for solidifying concrete, for example by applying a vacuum to the material before it hardens. It is situated in the CPC hierarchy under Fixed Constructions, Building, Scaffolding, covering processes for preparing, conveying or working-up building materials and elements.
[E04G 21/061] by applying vacuum or vacuum combined with vibration -- E04G 21/061 covers techniques for preparing, moving or treating building components in scaffolding by employing a vacuum, optionally together with vibration, to aid in the removal of air, compacting, or positioning of materials.
[E04G 21/063] making use of vibrating or jolting tools  E04G21/08  takes precedence -- The code E04G 21/063 designates methods that employ vibrating or jolting tools in scaffolding work for preparing, conveying or processing building materials or elements. It falls under fixed constructions, building, scaffolding, and is subordinate to the broader group E04G21/08.
[E04G 21/065] acting upon the shuttering -- The subclass E04G 21/065 covers methods and devices for treating, handling, or manipulating the shuttering (formwork) used in construction, such as applying release agents, cleaning, repairing, coating, or otherwise preparing the formwork before or after concrete placement. It includes actions that facilitate removal, improve surface quality, or extend the service life of the shuttering during building or scaffolding operations.
[E04G 21/066] acting upon the surface of the concrete, whether or not provided with parts penetrating the concrete -- This subclass covers methods and devices for treating the surface of concrete, such as cleaning, coating, sealing, smoothing or other finishing operations, and includes tools that may or may not have parts that penetrate the concrete.
[E04G 21/068] acting upon the reinforcement -- E04G 21/068 covers operations that treat reinforcement during the erection or maintenance of scaffold structures. It includes techniques for positioning, securing, tensioning or otherwise manipulating reinforcing elements such as bars, rods or meshes to improve the strength or stability of the scaffold.
[E04G 21/08] Internal vibrators -- E04G 21/08 covers internal vibrators used in scaffolding and other fixed building constructions, focusing on devices that apply vibratory energy to compact, settle, or consolidate building materials or components during preparation, conveyance, or processing. This subclass falls under the broader category of preparing, conveying, or working-up building materials or building elements.
[E04G 21/10] Devices for levelling, e.g. templates or boards -- Devices for levelling, such as templates or boards, are tools used in building and scaffolding work to ensure that components are positioned at a uniform height or angle. They provide a reference surface that helps workers align building materials accurately during preparation, conveyance, or assembly.
[E04G 21/12] Mounting of reinforcing inserts -- The E04G 21/12 subclass covers methods and apparatus for attaching reinforcing inserts to scaffolding structures. It includes steps such as positioning, securing, and fixing the inserts to improve the strength or stability of the scaffold during construction or maintenance work.
[E04G 21/121] Construction of stressing jacks -- This class covers the design, erection, and integration of hydraulic or mechanical stressing jacks that apply tension to scaffolding or structural elements, including preparation of the supporting components and procedures for positioning and securing the jacks during construction. It pertains to fixed construction activities within building scaffolding.
[E04G 21/122] Machines for joining reinforcing bars -- This subclass covers equipment designed to connect reinforcement bars, such as mechanical couplers, automated tying devices, or welding machines, used in construction of scaffolding and other fixed structures. It includes systems that fasten, clamp, or otherwise join steel rebars to form continuous reinforcement.
[E04G 21/123] Wire twisting tools  bundling articles by twisting, e.g. wire  B65B13/28 ; bundling rods, sticks, or like elongated objects with, e.g. wire  B65B27/10 ; tying devices for hops, vines or trees with, e.g. wire  A01G17/08
[E04G 21/125] Reinforcement continuity box -- The code refers to a protective enclosure that maintains the continuity of reinforcement between scaffold components, ensuring secure load transfer and structural integrity. It applies to fixed construction elements used in the preparation, conveyance, or working-up of building materials for scaffolding.
[E04G 21/126] for cable loops -- This CPC subclass covers scaffolding systems that incorporate cable loops used to support, guide or secure building components during construction. It relates to methods and apparatus for preparing, conveying or working-up building materials using cable loops on fixed constructions.
[E04G 21/14] Conveying or assembling building elements -- The code E04G 21/14 classifies methods and apparatus for conveying, positioning and assembling building elements during construction, including the handling of prefabricated components, structural members and scaffolding parts. It covers processes that move and join these elements to form a building's fixed construction.
[E04G 21/142] Means in or on the elements for connecting same to handling apparatus  E04B1/38  takes precedence -- E04G 21/142 covers devices incorporated into or attached to building or scaffolding components that enable those components to be linked to handling or transport equipment. It falls under Fixed Constructions → Building → Scaffolding and relates to the preparation, conveyance, or working-up of building materials. The broader subclass E04B1/38 takes precedence.
[E04G 21/145] specific for hollow plates -- This subgroup deals with techniques, tools, or processes specially adapted for handling hollow plates in scaffolding work, such as their preparation, transport, positioning, or integration into temporary structures. It falls under the broader categories of fixed constructions, building, and scaffolding.
[E04G 21/147] specific for prefabricated masonry wall elements -- The CPC entry E04G 21/147 covers prefabricated masonry wall elements, detailing the preparation, transport and assembly of these building components, including the handling and installation of such elements on fixed construction scaffolding.
[E04G 21/16] Tools or apparatus -- The E04G 21/16 entry covers tools and apparatus used in scaffolding to prepare, convey or work up building materials and elements for fixed-construction projects such as buildings. It pertains to equipment that facilitates handling, positioning or processing components while erecting or maintaining scaffolds.
[E04G 21/161] Handling units comprising at least considerable parts of two sides of a room or like enclosed space -- E04G 21/161 relates to handling units that incorporate substantial portions of two opposite walls or sides of a room or similar enclosure, such as prefabricated wall panels, partitions, or floor sections, and addresses their transport, positioning, and installation within the building process.
[E04G 21/162] Handles to carry construction blocks  load carriers  B65G7/12 -- This subclass covers devices attached to building blocks or masonry units that serve as handholds for manual transport, such as built-in handles, grips or straps integrated into the block to facilitate lifting and placement during construction. It falls under fixed constructions, building, scaffolding, relating to preparation and handling of building elements.
[E04G 21/163] Jacks specially adapted for working-up building elements  jacks in general  B66F3/00 -- E04G 21/163 covers jacks specially adapted for handling and working-up building elements, such as lifting or positioning components during construction or maintenance of scaffolding and other fixed structures. It falls under the Cooperative Patent Classification hierarchy of Fixed Constructions, Building, Scaffolding and relates to the broader group of general jacks (B66F3/00).
[E04G 21/164] specially adapted for working-up chimneys -- E04G 21/164 covers scaffolding or ancillary devices that are specifically designed to facilitate the cleaning, repair or inspection of chimneys. The classification includes ladders, platforms, brackets or other temporary structures that can be positioned around or within a chimney to allow safe access and efficient working-up of the chimney interior.
[E04G 21/165] specially adapted for  in situ  prefabricated building elements to be tilted up in position -- The code E04G 21/165 covers equipment or procedures that are specially adapted for installing prefabricated building components on site by tilting them up into their final position. It relates to scaffolding and other fixed constructions used to support and raise these elements during erection.
[E04G 21/166] Landings, receiving platforms -- The CPC subclass E04G 21/166 covers landings and receiving platforms incorporated into scaffolding systems for construction. It relates to fixed constructions that provide safe access and material-handling areas on building sites, facilitating the movement of workers and building elements.
[E04G 21/167] specially adapted for working-up plates, panels or slab shaped building elements -- E04G 21/167 covers devices or tools specially adapted for processing plates, panels or slab-shaped building elements in scaffolding work. It falls under the fixed constructions category, specifically for preparing, conveying, or working-up building materials. The classification is part of the Cooperative Patent Classification used by the EPO and USPTO.
[E04G 21/168] used for tilting, e.g. from horizontal to vertical position or  vice versa -- This CPC subclass covers devices or methods for tilting scaffolding components from a horizontal to a vertical position or vice versa, facilitating repositioning of scaffold sections during construction. It is located under fixed constructions, building, scaffolding, and the preparation, conveying or working-up of building materials.
[E04G 21/169] specially adapted for working-up reinforcement mats -- E04G 21/169 covers equipment or tools that are specially adapted for processing reinforcement mats in scaffolding construction, including the preparation, handling and working-up of the mats before they are installed in building structures.
[E04G 21/18] Adjusting tools -- E04G 21/18 covers adjusting tools used in scaffolding work, such as devices for aligning, leveling or tightening scaffold components during the preparation, conveyance or finishing of building materials and elements. These tools enable precise positioning and secure assembly of fixed construction structures.
[E04G 21/1808] Holders for bricklayers' lines, bricklayers' bars; Sloping braces  cord-line chalkers  B44D3/38 -- E04G 21/1808 refers to scaffolding accessories that support and guide masonry work, specifically holders for bricklayers' lines or bars, sloping braces, and cord-line chalkers. These devices are used on fixed construction scaffolds to keep bricklaying lines taut and to provide adjustable support for positioning bricks.
[E04G 21/1816] Adjustable positioning means between line-holder bars and construction -- The entry E04G 21/1816 covers devices that provide adjustable positioning between line-holder bars and the scaffolding structure, allowing the bars to be moved, locked or released to align and support building materials during construction. It relates to scaffolding components that enable flexible placement and easy adjustment of line-holders for tasks such as guiding, conveying or working-up materials.
[E04G 21/1825] Line-holders without supporting bars -- E04G 21/1825 covers scaffolding components that secure or guide tension lines such as ropes or cables without incorporating any supporting bars. These line-holders are used to stabilize or position scaffold members during construction, transport, or assembly of building elements.
[E04G 21/1833] Line-holders for setting-out builders' work, e.g. foundations -- Devices that secure alignment lines or strings while setting out foundations and other building work, ensuring precise placement of structural elements. They are part of scaffolding tools used in fixed construction tasks.
[E04G 21/1841] Means for positioning building parts or elements  strutting means for wall parts  E04G21/26
[E04G 21/185] for anchoring elements or elements to be incorporated in the structure -- E04G 21/185 pertains to methods and devices for anchoring structural or ancillary components to scaffolding or other temporary building structures, including fasteners, brackets and fittings that become incorporated in the assembled construction. It falls under the cooperative patent classification hierarchy of fixed constructions, building and scaffolding, covering the preparation, conveyance or working-up of building materials or elements.
[E04G 21/1858] Templates for window or door openings, e.g. in a masonry wall  E04G15/02  takes precedence -- The classification concerns devices that serve as reusable patterns or guides for cutting or forming openings for windows and doors in masonry walls, helping ensure correct dimensions and alignment during construction. It falls under fixed constructions and scaffold-related processes, and is superseded by the broader subclass E04G15/02.
[E04G 21/1866] for making arches -- The CPC subclass E04G 21/1866 covers processes used in the preparation, transport or handling of building materials that are specifically aimed at constructing arches. It falls under the hierarchy of fixed constructions, building, scaffolding, and includes techniques for assembling, supporting or shaping arch elements on-site.
[E04G 21/1875] for making curved walls  E04G11/06  takes precedence
[E04G 21/1883] Spacers, e.g. to have a constant spacing between courses of masonry -- The entry refers to spacers employed in masonry construction to keep a constant gap between courses of bricks, blocks or other building units, ensuring uniform alignment and load distribution. Such spacers may be made of plastic, metal, wood or other materials and are positioned between elements during erection.
[E04G 21/1891] Cut-marking templates for rafters; Templates used for assembling building frameworks -- This classification covers template devices that are placed on rafters to produce cut-marks and other templates that guide the assembly of building frameworks, ensuring precise shaping and alignment of structural members during construction.
[E04G 21/20] for applying mortar -- E04G 21/20 pertains to tools, apparatus or processes used on scaffolding for the application of mortar to building components. It includes mechanisms that convey, spread or place mortar during construction or repair, facilitating efficient work on walls, floors or facades.
[E04G 21/201] Trowels -- Trowels are hand-held tools with flat, usually metal blades used to spread, level, shape and smooth building materials such as mortar, plaster, concrete or grout. In scaffolding work they allow precise application of these substances on walls, floors or other elements to achieve uniform surfaces and proper bonding. They are essential for preparing and finishing construction components in building projects.
[E04G 21/202] Hoses specially adapted therefor -- E04G 21/202 pertains to hoses specially adapted for use in fixed-construction scaffolding, including those employed to transport water, cement slurry, or other building materials during the preparation, conveyance, or processing of scaffolding components.
[E04G 21/203] Prepared mortar strips, pouches or the like to be applied between masonry courses -- The E04G 21/203 entry covers pre-formed mortar strips, pouches or similar units that are placed between masonry courses to supply mortar. It relates to fixed-construction building scaffolding and methods for preparing, conveying or working up building materials, enabling faster, more uniform mortar application during brick or block laying.
[E04G 21/204] Mortar sledges -- Mortar sledges are hand-operated tools with a flat, often metal-topped base and a handle, used to spread, shape and smooth mortar or similar plastering mixes onto masonry surfaces. They enable even application of binding material on bricks, blocks or stone during construction or repair work, supporting the preparation and working-up of building elements in scaffolding and fixed-construction tasks.
[E04G 21/205] with closable mortar opening -- The subclass E04G 21/205 covers scaffolding systems that incorporate a mortar opening which can be closed, such as doors, shutters or hinged panels that seal the gap used for mortar placement. This feature allows protection of the work area, control of material flow and improved safety while the scaffolding remains in use.
[E04G 21/206] Devices to apply thin-bed mortar joints -- Devices for applying thin-bed mortar joints to building elements, such as trowels, sprayers or similar equipment that spread or press a shallow layer of mortar onto walls, panels or other surfaces during construction or repair. This classification addresses tools designed specifically for the precise placement of thin-layered mortar in fixed construction and scaffolding work.
[E04G 21/207] Static templates for applying a predefined layer of mortar -- This CPC subclass covers static templates used to apply a predetermined mortar layer on construction surfaces, facilitating consistent thickness and shape, typically employed in masonry or prefabricated element assembly.
[E04G 21/22] for setting building elements with mortar, e.g. bricklaying machines -- The E04G 21/22 subclass covers equipment that places building elements using mortar, such as bricklaying machines. It pertains to fixed-construction processes involving the preparation, conveyance, or working-up of building materials within scaffolding systems.
[E04G 21/24] Safety or protective measures preventing damage to building parts or finishing work during construction -- This subclass covers methods, devices, or materials that protect structural components or finishing work from damage during building or repair, such as protective covers, shields, temporary supports and other safety measures applied while construction proceeds.
[E04G 21/241] for temporarily closing door or window openings -- E04G 21/241 relates to means for temporarily closing door or window openings, such as removable panels, shutters or sealing devices used on scaffolds or temporary structures. It covers solutions that allow quick access while protecting the interior from weather, debris or loss of heat during construction or maintenance work. This subclass falls under Fixed Constructions → Building → Scaffolding → preparing, conveying, or working-up building materials or elements.
[E04G 21/242] for temporarily covering the whole worksite, e.g. building, trench -- The code E04G 21/242 relates to systems or methods used to temporarily enclose an entire work area, such as a building under construction or an open trench, by covering it with protective materials like tarpaulins, sheets or shelters. It addresses the provision of a full-site barrier that shields the site from weather, debris or contamination while work is underway, and falls under the scaffold-related group for preparing and handling building elements.
[E04G 21/243] for creating a temporary partition in a closed room -- The CPC code E04G 21/243 covers methods or devices for installing a temporary partition inside a closed room as part of fixed construction or scaffolding work. It falls under the hierarchy of Fixed Constructions - Building - Scaffolding - Preparing, conveying, or working-up building materials or elements.
[E04G 21/245] for temporarily protecting wall crowns -- This subclass covers temporary protection of wall crowns, the uppermost part of a wall, during building-work. It includes scaffolding accessories, covers, panels or enclosures that shield the crown from weather, impact or falling debris while construction or maintenance is underway.
[E04G 21/246] specially adapted for curing concrete  in situ , e.g. by covering it with protective sheets -- The E04G 21/246 classification covers devices or methods specially adapted for curing concrete in situ, such as protective sheets or coverings that retain moisture and control temperature during setting. It is placed under Fixed Constructions → Building → Scaffolding → Preparing, conveying, or working-up building materials or building elements.
[E04G 21/247] specific for protecting windows or doors leaves -- E04G 21/247 covers devices, materials or procedures used to protect the leaves of windows or doors during construction, maintenance or repair, such as temporary shields, covers or sealing systems that prevent damage, weather ingress or contamination while work proceeds. It falls under fixed constructions, building scaffolding, and the broader preparation, conveying or working-up of building elements.
[E04G 21/26] Strutting means for wall parts -- E04G 21/26 covers devices that support or brace wall sections during construction, such as temporary frames, props or bracing systems that hold walls in place while work proceeds. It is classified under scaffolding within the fixed-construction group for preparing, conveying or working-up building materials and elements.
[E04G 21/28] against unfavourable weather influence -- The E04G 21/28 classification covers scaffolding and other fixed constructions that are designed to protect building work from adverse weather conditions, such as wind, rain, snow or temperature extremes. It includes protective coverings, enclosures, shields and other measures aimed at preventing weather-related damage or disruption during construction.
[E04G 21/30] against mechanical damage or dirt, e.g. guard covers of stairs -- E04G 21/30 covers protective measures that prevent mechanical damage or dirt on building elements, such as guard covers for stairs, and falls under fixed constructions - building - scaffolding and the handling of building materials.
[E04G 21/32] Safety or protective measures for persons during the construction of buildings -- E04G 21/32 covers safety or protective measures for workers while constructing buildings, specifically relating to scaffolding and the preparation, conveyance, or handling of building materials and elements. It addresses devices, procedures and systems designed to protect personnel from hazards during construction activities.
[E04G 21/3204] against falling down -- E04G 21/3204 covers safety provisions that prevent collapse or falling of scaffolding structures during construction or maintenance. It includes devices, fittings or methods designed to secure scaffolds and protect workers and materials from accidental descent. This subclass falls under Fixed Constructions → Building → Scaffolding in the Cooperative Patent Classification.
[E04G 21/3209] Temporary tunnels specially adapted against falling objects -- E04G 21/3209 refers to temporary tunnels that are specifically designed to guard against falling objects during construction activities. These structures are used in building and scaffolding work to provide a protective passage for workers and materials where debris or tools might drop.
[E04G 21/3214] Means for working on roofs  on flat roofs  E04G21/3223 -- This classification covers devices, apparatuses, or systems designed to enable safe access, support, or work on flat roof surfaces, including ladders, platforms, walkways, or safety-harness attachment points. It falls under scaffolding for preparing, conveying, or working up building elements on roofs.
[E04G 21/3219] Means supported by the building wall, e.g. security consoles  E04G5/06  takes precedence -- This subclass covers devices that are mounted on or supported by a building wall, such as security consoles, control panels or other equipment that relies on the wall for support, and applies to installations where the wall serves as the primary structural support.
[E04G 21/3223] Means supported by building floors or flat roofs, e.g. safety railings -- The entry E04G 21/3223 covers devices that are mounted on building floors or flat roof surfaces to provide protective barriers, such as safety railings and guardrails. It falls within the CPC section for fixed constructions, specifically building scaffolding and the preparation, conveying, or working up of building materials. This classification is used by both the EPO and the USPTO.
[E04G 21/3228] Folding railings for flat roof edge, e.g. to hide the railings from view when not in use -- Folding railings designed for the edge of a flat roof that can be collapsed or hidden when not required, providing a temporary safety barrier that blends with the roof surface when stored. This solution combines a protective railing with a compact, retractable design for buildings with flat roof construction.
[E04G 21/3233] without permanent provision in the floor or roof -- E04G 21/3233 covers scaffolding or other temporary support structures used during construction that are installed without any permanent anchoring or integration into the floor or roof. It relates to methods of preparing, conveying, or working up building materials where the support is removable and does not become a fixed part of the building.
[E04G 21/3238] using counterweights -- The entry covers methods and devices that employ counterweights to control or balance scaffolding elements during the preparation, transport, or assembly of building materials, improving stability and easing the raising or lowering of fixed construction components.
[E04G 21/3242] using clamps -- E04G 21/3242 covers the use of clamps in scaffolding work, specifically for preparing, moving, or handling building materials and components, as part of fixed construction processes. It details methods where clamps secure, position, or support elements during erection, adjustment, or dismantling of scaffolding structures.
[E04G 21/3247] Storey high safety barrier hung from the facade and sliding up from level to level as work progresses
[E04G 21/3252] Protections, e.g. cover, caps, against impaling on starter reinforcement bars -- The classification pertains to protective devices such as caps or covers fitted to starter reinforcement bars in scaffolding to prevent impalement injuries during the preparation, handling, or installation of building materials and elements.
[E04G 21/3261] Safety-nets; Safety mattresses; Arrangements on buildings for connecting safety-lines  jumping-sheets, jumping-mattresses  A62B1/22 -- This CPC subclass covers safety nets, safety mattresses and related arrangements mounted on buildings for connecting safety lines, jumping sheets or jumping mattresses to protect workers during work at height.
[E04G 21/3266] Safety nets -- Safety nets are protective netting installed on scaffolding systems to catch falling workers or objects, reducing injury risk during construction, maintenance or demolition activities. They are designed to be attached to or integrated within scaffold structures, often removable for reuse, and may be made of synthetic or metal fibers to withstand impact loads.
[E04G 21/3271] Safety mattresses -- E04G 21/3271 covers safety mattresses employed on scaffolding to protect workers from falls or impact, typically placed on platform edges or the ground as temporary cushioning for building-material handling and construction activities.
[E04G 21/3276] Arrangements on buildings for connecting safety-lines  fastening devices for devices for lowering persons from buildings  A62B1/04 -- This class covers equipment and arrangements installed on buildings or scaffolding that provide attachment points for safety-lines and fastening devices used to lower persons for rescue, maintenance or work at height. It includes anchors, harness points and related systems that secure individuals while they are lowered from the structure.
[E04G 21/328] fastened to the roof covering or insulation -- The CPC entry E04G 21/328 covers fasteners that are attached to a roof covering or to insulation, used in the preparation, conveyance or working-up of building materials within scaffolding structures. It resides under Fixed Constructions → Building → Scaffolding.
[E04G 21/3285] fastened to standing seams of the roof covering -- The code covers techniques and devices that are fastened to the standing seams of a roof covering, used to securely attach items such as solar panels, ventilation units or lighting fixtures while preserving the roof's integrity. It is classified under fixed constructions, building, scaffolding.
[E04G 21/329] with measures for dampening the fall -- E04G 21/329 covers scaffolding systems that incorporate devices or arrangements to reduce the impact of a fall, such as safety nets, shock-absorbing pads or fall-arrest mechanisms. The classification applies to fixed construction installations where these dampening measures are integrated into the preparation, conveyance or handling of building elements.
[E04G 21/3295] Guide tracks for safety lines  arrangements for bypassing safety track supports without life line disconnection  A62B35/0087 -- A guide-track system designed for attaching safety lines on scaffolding, enabling workers to move along the structure while staying protected. The entry also covers configurations that allow the safety track to be bypassed around supports without having to disconnect the lifeline, maintaining continuous fall protection during construction or maintenance tasks.

[E04G 23/02] Repairing, e.g. filling cracks -- E04G 23/02 pertains to methods and devices for repairing scaffolding on existing buildings, including techniques such as filling cracks, reinforcing or replacing damaged components, and other remedial measures that restore the safety and functionality of the temporary structure.
[E04G 23/0203] Arrangements for filling cracks or cavities in building constructions -- The entry covers methods and devices for repairing cracks or cavities in existing building structures, including sealing, grout injection, patching and other filling techniques applied during maintenance or repair of fixed construction elements.
[E04G 23/0207] in hollow structures, e.g. cavity walls -- The CPC subclass E04G 23/0207 relates to methods and tools used when working inside hollow building elements, such as cavity walls, during maintenance or repair of existing structures. It falls under fixed constructions, building, scaffolding, and working measures on existing buildings.
[E04G 23/0211] using injection -- E04G 23/0211 relates to measures for working on existing buildings that involve injecting a material such as grout, resin or mortar into structural or scaffold elements. The injection is used to fill voids, strengthen joints, improve load-bearing capacity or repair defects while the scaffolding remains in place.
[E04G 23/0214] using covering strips -- The CPC entry E04G 23/0214 falls under Fixed Constructions > Building > Scaffolding and concerns working measures on existing structures. It specifically covers the use of covering strips on scaffolding components to protect surfaces during construction or maintenance activities.
[E04G 23/0218] Increasing or restoring the load-bearing capacity of building construction elements  E04G23/0203  and  E04G23/0266  take precedence -- E04G 23/0218 is a CPC subclass within Fixed Constructions > Building > Scaffolding that covers measures applied to existing building structures to increase or restore the load-bearing capacity of construction elements such as beams, columns or supporting frames. It is subordinate to the broader categories E04G23/0203 and E04G23/0266, which have precedence over this entry.
[E04G 23/0222] Replacing or adding wall ties -- The code E04G 23/0222 covers procedures for replacing or installing additional wall ties in existing buildings, usually performed from scaffolding. It addresses the work needed to reinforce masonry walls by removing old ties and fitting new ones to maintain structural integrity.
[E04G 23/0225] of circular building elements, e.g. by circular bracing -- E04G 23/0225 covers methods for securing or reinforcing existing scaffold structures using circular components such as rings or circular braces. [E04G 23/0233] of vaulted or arched building elements -- E04G 23/0233 covers methods and devices for measuring, supporting, or accessing vaulted or arched elements in existing structures, falling under the scaffolding subgroup of the Fixed Constructions-Building hierarchy in the CPC. [E04G 25/00] Shores or struts -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Shores, struts or chocks for the construction industry.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Supporting structures for shutterings or frames for floors or roofs -> E04G11/48 - Strutting means for wall parts during construction e.g. for holding in position prefabricated walls -> E04G21/26 - Strutting of existing buildings -> E04G23/04 - Bordering or stiffening the sides of ditches or trenches or narrow shafts for foundations -> E02D17/08 - Props for mines -> E21D15/00
[E04G 25/02] non-telescopic -- E04G 25/02 covers non-telescopic shores or struts used in fixed construction scaffolding. These are rigid, fixed-length members that provide lateral support or load bearing for scaffolding frameworks without an adjustable or sliding mechanism. They are intended for permanent or semi-permanent installations in building work.
[E04G 25/04] telescopic -- E04G 25/04 classifies telescopic scaffolding, a type of temporary support structure consisting of extendable poles that can be lengthened or shortened to suit different working heights. It is a subcategory of fixed constructions under building, scaffolding, and specifically shores or struts.
[E04G 25/06] with parts held together by positive means -- This subgroup covers scaffolding structures such as shores or struts in which the individual components are joined by positive mechanical connections such as bolts, pins, clamps or other fastening devices, rather than by friction or tension. It relates to fixed construction scaffolding used in building.
[E04G 25/061] by pins -- The E04G 25/061 subclass covers scaffolding systems in which the shores or struts are secured by pins, providing a fixed-construction method of connecting the structural members. It is part of the broader group for building scaffolding within the Cooperative Patent Classification system used by the EPO and USPTO.
[E04G 25/063] with safety devices to avoid the accidental loss or unlocking of the pin, e.g. chains attaching the pin to the prop -- This CPC subclass covers fixed-construction scaffolding struts that incorporate safety devices to prevent the pin from being unintentionally lost or released, for example by attaching a chain to the pin.
[E04G 25/065] by a threaded nut -- This subclass covers scaffolding or shoring systems that are secured or adjusted using a threaded nut, allowing precise height or tension control. It falls under fixed constructions for building scaffolds and struts, where the nut serves as the primary fastening element.
[E04G 25/066] by a wedge -- The subclass E04G 25/066 covers scaffolding shores or struts that are positioned, tightened or locked by means of a wedge, enabling rapid adjustment and secure temporary support in building construction.
[E04G 25/068] by a cam -- A scaffolding shore or strut that is actuated by a cam mechanism to set, lock or release its position, providing adjustable support for temporary structures. The cam allows precise control of the strut's length or angle, facilitating rapid erection and dismantling while maintaining safety and stability.
[E04G 25/08] with parts held relatively to each other by friction or gripping -- E04G 25/08 covers scaffolding systems whose members are connected mainly by friction or gripping rather than bolts, clamps or welds. The design relies on tight fits, interlocking surfaces or pressed joints to hold the struts, ledgers and braces together, providing a fixed-construction scaffold that can be assembled and disassembled without permanent fasteners.
[E04G 27/00] Temporary arrangements for giving access from one level to another for men or vehicles, e.g. steps, ramps -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Temporary arrangements for giving access from one level to another for men or vehicles, e.g. steps, ramps, as building implement or building aid.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Steps or ladders specially adapted for scaffolds -> E04G5/10 - Gangways for ships -> B63 - Lifts -> B66 - Ramps -> E04F11/002
[E04G 3/00] Scaffolds essentially supported by building constructions, e.g. adjustable in height -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Workmen's or safety scaffolds (i.e. temporary platforms or supports) supported mainly by the building construction.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Scaffolds primarily resting on the ground -> E04G1/00 - Component parts or accessories for scaffolds -> E04G5/00 - Means for fastening, supporting, or bracing scaffolds on or against building constructions -> E04G5/04 - Scaffold consoles or brackets -> E04G5/06 - Safety or protective measures against falling down for persons during the construction of buildings, i.e. with the function of preventing or catching a fall and without the function of supporting the persons performing their normal work -> E04G21/3204 - Stagings, scaffolding, shores or struts usable both on slipways and in dry docks -> B63C5/02 - Lifting devices for movable platforms or cabins, e.g. on vehicles, permitting workmen to place themselves in any desired position for carrying out required operations -> B66F11/04 - Movable inspection or maintenance platforms, e.g. travelling scaffolding or vehicles specially designed to provide access to the undersides of bridges -> E01D19/106 - Devices or arrangements allowing walking on the roof or in the gutter -> E04D13/12
[E04G 3/18] supported by cantilevers or other provisions mounted in openings in the building, e.g. window openings -- E04G 3/18 addresses scaffolding that is supported by cantilevers or other provisions mounted in openings of a building, such as window openings. It applies to fixed-construction scaffolds that rely on the building itself for support rather than being free-standing.
[E04G 3/20] supported by walls -- E04G 3/20 relates to scaffolding that is principally supported by the walls of a building, such as temporary platforms or structures whose height can be adjusted for construction, repair or maintenance work. It is a subcategory of scaffolding within the fixed-construction hierarchy.
[E04G 3/22] supported by roofs or ceilings -- Scaffolds that receive their support from a building's roof or ceiling rather than from ground-based or standalone structures fall under CPC class E04G 3/22. This subclass covers fixed-construction scaffolding integrated with the overhead elements of a building, such as adjustable platforms anchored to roof or ceiling components.
[E04G 3/24] specially adapted for particular parts of buildings or for buildings of particular shape, e.g. chimney stacks or pylons -- E04G 3/24 covers scaffolding systems that are specially designed to fit specific sections of a building or structures of unusual shape, such as chimney stacks or pylons, allowing adjustment in height and integration with the fixed construction.
[E04G 3/243] following the outside contour of a building  E04G3/26  takes precedence -- This subclass covers scaffolding systems arranged to follow the external contour of a building, conforming to its shape and façade. The scaffold is designed to be adjustable in height and to support work on irregular or curved surfaces.
[E04G 3/246] following the inside contour of a building -- Scaffolding that conforms to the interior shape of a building, such as adjustable platforms that follow walls, ceilings, or other internal surfaces, enabling work along the inside contour of the structure.
[E04G 3/26] specially adapted for working on roofs -- E04G 3/26 covers scaffolding that is integrated with a building's permanent construction and designed specifically for roof work, such as platforms, walkways or adjustable supports that enable safe access and material handling on rooftops. It includes systems that can be installed, adjusted or removed while the building is standing, providing a stable working surface directly on or above the roof structure.
[E04G 3/265] with means to adapt it to a variable pitch of the roof -- The E04G 3/265 subclass covers scaffolding that is permanently attached to building structures and includes mechanisms allowing the scaffold to be adjusted for roofs with variable pitch. It pertains to fixed-construction scaffolds designed to follow the slope of a roof while providing safe worker access.
[E04G 3/28] Mobile scaffolds -- Mobile scaffolds are portable framework structures used in construction that can be moved and adjusted in height, allowing workers to access various levels of a building site. They fall under the CPC hierarchy of fixed constructions, building, scaffolding, code E04G 3/28.
[E04G 3/30] suspended by flexible supporting elements, e.g. cables -- The E04G 3/30 subclass covers scaffolding that is hung from flexible supports such as cables, ropes or chains, rather than resting on rigid points. It applies to temporary work platforms whose main load is carried by these suspended elements and are usually attached to fixed building structures.
[E04G 3/305] specially adapted for tanks, silos or similar vessels  E04G3/24  takes precedence
[E04G 3/32] Hoisting devices -- E04G 3/32 covers hoisting devices used in scaffolding that are supported by building constructions, such as adjustable-height systems. It includes winches, lifts, pulleys and other equipment employed to raise or lower loads on scaffolds.
[E04G 3/325] Safety devices for stabilising the mobile platform, e.g. to avoid it swinging in the wind -- The CPC entry E04G 3/325 falls under Fixed Constructions → Building → Scaffolding and covers safety devices that stabilise a mobile scaffolding platform, preventing it from swinging or swaying, particularly in wind. It includes mechanisms such as braces, dampers, counterweights or anchoring systems designed to keep the platform steady during use.
[E04G 3/34] characterised by supporting structures provided on the roofs -- This subclass covers scaffolding that is an integral part of a building and includes supporting structures installed on the roof, such as roof-mounted platforms or adjustable-height frameworks that rely on the building's fixed construction.
[E04G 5/00] Component parts or accessories for scaffolds -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Accessories for scaffolds, in particular scaffolds' feet, fastening means to building construction, consoles or brackets, boards or planks, steps or ladders, canopies, railings and struts or stiffening rods, devices which are specifically designed to be attached to the scaffold and to store buildlng material or tools.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Scaffolds essentially comprising special means for supporting or forming platforms i.e. scaffolds with specific fastening features on the scaffold frame or on the board for supporting or forming platforms (e.g. hooks and corresponding holes, etc.) -> E04G1/15 - Consoles or brackets essentially supported by building constructions and supporting persons performing their normal work -> E04G3/00 - Connections between parts of the scaffold -> E04G7/00 - Clips or connections for securing boards, i.e. clips which are part of neither the plank or board nor the scaffold frame -> E04G7/28 - Safety or protective measures preventing damage to building parts or finishing work during construction -> E04G21/24 - Safety or protective measures against falling down for persons during the construction of buildings, i.e. with the function of preventing or catching a fall and without the function of supporting the persons performing their normal work -> E04G21/3204 - Temporary arrangements for giving access from one level to another for men or vehicles, e.g. steps, ramps -> E04G27/00 - Ladders in general -> E06C - Fastenings in general -> F16B
[E04G 5/001] Safety or protective measures against falling down relating to scaffoldings  E04G5/14  takes precedence -- E04G 5/001 covers safety and protective devices designed to prevent falls from scaffolds, including guardrails, toe boards, safety nets, and other accessories attached to scaffold components. It falls under the Fixed Constructions - Building - Scaffolding branch of the CPC.
[E04G 5/002] Protections or covers for scaffolding connections -- The class covers devices that shield or enclose the joints and linking elements of scaffold structures, such as caps, guards, sleeves or covers fitted over bolts, pins, clamps or other connectors. These protections prevent damage from weather, debris or accidental contact and may be removable or permanently attached.
[E04G 5/003] Devices for storing material on the scaffold -- The CPC subclass E04G 5/003 defines devices that are attached to scaffolding to store or hold building materials, such as bins, shelves, trays or other containers, allowing convenient access and organization of supplies while workers operate at height.
[E04G 5/004] Storage and transport racks for scaffolding components -- This subclass covers devices designed to hold, organize, and move scaffold parts such as racks, frames, or carriers used on-site or in transit. It falls under the E04G 5/004 code within the CPC for fixed constructions, building, scaffolding, and component accessories.
[E04G 5/005] Devices for cleaning or repairing scaffold tubes -- E04G 5/005 covers tools and apparatus used to clean or repair the tubular members of scaffolding systems. It includes devices such as brushes, scrubbers, deburring instruments and repair kits designed to maintain or restore scaffold tubes, classified under component parts or accessories for scaffolds.
[E04G 5/006] Scaffold with cantilevered sections, e.g. to accommodate overhangs or recesses in the facade -- This subclass covers scaffolds that incorporate cantilevered sections extending beyond the main framework, allowing work on building facades with overhangs or recesses. It falls under Fixed Constructions > Building > Scaffolding and is indexed as E04G 5/006.
[E04G 5/007] Devices and methods for erecting scaffolds, e.g. automatic scaffold erectors -- This subgroup covers apparatus and procedures for assembling scaffolding systems, particularly mechanised or automatic erectors that raise, position and secure scaffold components. It includes devices that reduce manual labour and improve speed and safety during scaffold erection.
[E04G 5/02] Scaffold feet, e.g. with arrangements for adjustment -- E04G 5/02 covers the lower elements of scaffolding systems that support the structure on the ground, specifically scaffold feet that may include mechanisms for height or angle adjustment. These components attach to the legs or frames of scaffolds and can be moved or calibrated to accommodate uneven surfaces, improve stability, and facilitate assembly and disassembly.
[E04G 5/025] Foot load distribution plates -- Foot load distribution plates are components used beneath scaffold footings to spread applied loads over a larger area, enhancing stability and protecting the underlying ground or surface. They are typically made of steel or durable polymers and are installed as part of scaffold erection to prevent settlement and improve safety.
[E04G 5/04] Means for fastening, supporting, or bracing scaffolds on or against building constructions -- The entry covers devices and components that fasten, support or brace scaffolding to a building structure, such as clamps, brackets, ties, anchors and other accessories that secure scaffold frames to walls, floors or other structural elements.
[E04G 5/041] for fastening scaffolds on roof frameworks or on roofs -- E04G 5/041 concerns devices and methods for attaching scaffolding to roof frameworks or directly to roofs. It covers component parts or accessories that secure scaffolds in place on roof surfaces to enable safe construction or maintenance work.
[E04G 5/043] for fastening scaffolds on gutters -- The CPC subclass E04G 5/043 covers devices for fastening scaffolds to gutter structures, such as brackets, clamps or connectors that secure scaffold members to the roof gutter edge. It belongs to the hierarchy of fixed constructions - building - scaffolding - component parts or accessories for scaffolds.
[E04G 5/045] for fastening scaffoldings on profiles, e.g. I or H profiles -- E04G 5/045 covers devices and components used to attach scaffolding to structural profiles such as I or H sections. It includes clamps, brackets and other fastening accessories that secure the scaffold to these beams. The class falls under fixed constructions building scaffolding.
[E04G 5/046] for fastening scaffoldings on walls -- E04G 5/046 covers devices and methods used to attach scaffold components to walls, such as brackets, clamps, cleats, ties, or other fastening elements that secure the scaffold structure to a vertical surface, providing stability and load transfer for construction work.
[E04G 5/048] with hooks to be attached on the wall's crown -- A subclass of the CPC for building fixed constructions, specifically scaffold components or accessories equipped with hooks for securing them to the wall's crown. It covers devices that attach to the top of a wall to support or reinforce scaffold structures.
[E04G 5/06] Consoles -- Consoles are horizontal structural members that connect vertical scaffold uprights to form a stable framework for platforms or working surfaces. They distribute loads across the scaffold, provide lateral support, and often serve as attachment points for guardrails, ladders, or other accessories. This component is essential for safe load-bearing and stability in fixed-construction scaffolding systems.
[E04G 5/061] specially adapted for attachment to scaffolds -- A subclass of scaffold component parts covering devices specifically designed to be fitted onto scaffolding, such as brackets, clamps, hooks or other connectors that enable accessories, panels or equipment to be securely attached to the scaffold structure. This group includes any specially adapted fittings that facilitate mounting, fastening or linking items directly to scaffold members.
[E04G 5/062] specially adapted for attachment to building walls -- E04G 5/062 classifies scaffold components and accessories that are specially designed to be attached directly to building walls, such as wall-mounted brackets, anchors, clamps and similar fittings that secure scaffold elements to vertical surfaces. This subclass falls under the broader categories of fixed constructions, building, and scaffolding.
[E04G 5/064] specially adapted for attachment to building floors -- E04G 5/064 is a CPC sub-class within Fixed Constructions → Building → Scaffolding that covers component parts or accessories for scaffolds that are specially adapted for attachment to building floors, enabling direct connection of scaffold elements to floor structures for stability and ease of erection.
[E04G 5/065] specially adapted for attachment to ladders  platforms on, or for use on, ladders  E06C7/16 -- E04G 5/065 covers scaffold components or accessories that are specially designed to attach to ladders, including platforms that can be mounted on or used with ladders. These parts facilitate safe access and work on elevated surfaces by integrating ladder and scaffold functions.
[E04G 5/067] specially adapted for attachment to posts  E04G5/061  takes precedence -- The E04G 5/067 subclass covers component parts or accessories for scaffolds that are specially adapted for attachment to posts, such as brackets, clamps or fittings designed to secure scaffold elements directly to vertical supports. It falls under Fixed Constructions > Building > Scaffolding, and is superseded by the broader E04G 5/061 group for scaffold attachment components.
[E04G 5/08] Scaffold boards or planks -- Scaffold boards or planks are flat, load-bearing components used to create the working platforms on scaffolding structures, typically made of wood, metal or composite material. They are classified under the component-parts category of fixed-construction building scaffolding, facilitating safe access and material handling at height.
[E04G 5/10] Steps or ladders specially adapted for scaffolds -- E04G 5/10 pertains to steps or ladders that are specially adapted for use on scaffolding, serving as component parts or accessories that provide safe access between scaffold levels. These devices are designed to integrate with scaffold structures rather than function as standalone climbing equipment.
[E04G 5/12] Canopies -- E04G 5/12 classifies canopies that form part of scaffold systems, designed to shield workers, materials or equipment from weather or sunlight. The code applies to detachable or permanent overhead coverings attached to scaffolding frames as component accessories.
[E04G 5/14] Railings -- E04G 5/14 covers railings that are component parts or accessories for scaffolding systems. The code falls under the fixed constructions hierarchy, specifically building scaffolds and their ancillary elements.
[E04G 5/141] with an access door or the like therefor -- The CPC entry E04G 5/141 covers components or accessories for fixed-construction building scaffolds that incorporate an access door or a similar opening, enabling entry to the scaffold's interior or to attached work platforms.
[E04G 5/142] extensible or telescopic -- E04G 5/142 covers component parts or accessories for scaffolding that are designed to extend or retract, such as telescopic poles, adjustable brackets, or other movable elements that allow the scaffold's height or width to be changed. These items facilitate quick adaptation of scaffold dimensions without the need for separate fixed sections.
[E04G 5/144] specific for the lateral, i.e. short side of a scaffold -- E04G 5/144 covers components or accessories that are designed specifically for the lateral, or short, side of a scaffold. It includes braces, ties, frames or other devices that reinforce, connect or adjust the short side of scaffolding structures, ensuring stability and load distribution.
[E04G 5/145] Toe boards therefor -- E04G 5/145 designates toe boards for scaffolding, which are accessory components installed at the edges of scaffold platforms to prevent tools, debris or personnel from falling off. These devices are part of fixed construction scaffolding systems used in building work.
[E04G 5/147] specially adapted for mounting prior to the mounting of the platform
[E04G 5/16] Struts or stiffening rods, e.g. diagonal rods -- E04G 5/16 pertains to scaffold components that serve as struts or stiffening rods, including diagonal members used to reinforce and rigidify scaffold frames. It falls under the building-scaffolding sub-category of fixed constructions.
[E04G 5/165] Lintel for scaffoldings -- A lintel for scaffolding is a horizontal support element used to bridge openings or gaps in a scaffold structure, providing a stable load-bearing surface that distributes weight across the frame and enhances overall rigidity. It is typically positioned above doorways, windows, or other voids to maintain safety and structural integrity while work is carried out above.
[E04G 7/00] Connections between parts of the scaffold -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Connection between parts of the scaffold, e.g. with separate coupling elements e.g. ropes, cables, chains, clamps or with non-detachable coupling elements.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Scaffolds essentially comprising special means for supporting or forming platforms i.e. scaffolds with specific fastening features on the scaffold frame or on the board for supporting or forming platforms, e.g. hooks and corresponding holes. -> E04G1/15 - Mobile scaffolds; Scaffolds with mobile platforms suspended by flexible supporting elements, e.g. cables. -> E04G3/30 - Accessories for scaffolds, in particular scaffolds' feet, fastening means to building construction, consoles or brackets, boards or planks, steps or ladders, canopies, railings and struts or stiffening rods. -> E04G5/00 - Scaffold boards or planks i.e. plain scaffold boards of planks without details of specific fastening features, e.g. hooks or corresponding holes. -> E04G5/08 - Connections for (permanent) building structures in general -> E04B1/38 - Connections in general -> F16B - Connections of rods or tubes in general -> F16B7/00 - Means for fastening cables or ropes to one another or to other objects -> F16G11/00
[E04G 7/02] with separate coupling elements -- This subclass of the CPC covers building scaffolding in which the connections between parts are made using separate coupling elements such as brackets, pins or clamps rather than integral joints. It addresses modular assemblies that can be easily joined and detached for construction or maintenance purposes.
[E04G 7/04] Flexible elements, with or without brackets, e.g. ropes, cables, chains -- E04G 7/04 addresses the use of flexible elements for joining scaffold components, including ropes, cables, chains and similar links, with or without brackets, to provide adjustable or detachable connections within fixed-construction building scaffolding.
[E04G 7/06] Stiff scaffolding clamps for connecting scaffold members of common shape -- E04G 7/06 covers rigid clamps designed to join scaffold components that share the same cross-sectional shape. The clamps provide a stiff, secure connection for fixed-construction scaffolding systems, facilitating quick assembly while maintaining structural stability. It falls under the scaffold connections subgroup within the building-fixed constructions field.
[E04G 7/08] Clamps for parallelly-arranged members -- E04G 7/08 covers clamps designed to join scaffold components that run side-by-side, securing parallel members such as tubes or braces to maintain alignment and rigidity within a scaffold framework.
[E04G 7/10] Self-tightening clamps, e.g. stirrups -- This subclass covers self-tightening clamp devices such as stirrups designed to join scaffold components and maintain tension without manual adjustment. They are used in fixed construction scaffolding to secure connections between parts, improving stability and ease of assembly.
[E04G 7/12] Clamps or clips for crossing members -- E04G 7/12 covers devices that join intersecting scaffold components, such as clamps or clips used to secure crossing poles, tubes or bars. These fittings enable quick assembly and disassembly while maintaining structural stability and are typically metal, removable connectors for vertical, horizontal or diagonal scaffold members.
[E04G 7/14] for clamping the members independently -- E04G 7/14 covers scaffold connection devices that clamp individual members independently, enabling each component to be secured or adjusted without affecting adjacent parts. The solutions typically involve separate clamping mechanisms such as bolts, brackets or specialized fasteners that lock rods, ledgers or transoms in place, providing reliable, modular fixation for fixed-construction scaffolding.
[E04G 7/16] of which the clamping parts for the different members are rotatable with respect to one another -- This subclass relates to scaffolding connections where the clamp components that join different scaffold elements can rotate relative to each other, allowing adjustment of angle or alignment while maintaining secure attachment. It is used for modular scaffold systems where bolted or hinged clamps can be turned to fit various configurations.
[E04G 7/18] for clamping the members against one another or against a separate cushioning piece between them -- The subclass E04G 7/18 covers scaffold connections that secure members by clamping them together or against an intervening cushioning piece. It includes devices such as clamps, brackets or ties that lock structural elements directly or via a pad, providing a stable, fixed joint within scaffold systems.
[E04G 7/20] for ends of members only, e.g. for connecting members in end-to-end relation -- E04G 7/20 covers scaffold connections that join members only at their ends, such as end-to-end couplings, pins, clamps or other fasteners that align and secure adjacent tubes or poles. The classification is used for patents describing devices that provide a direct, terminal linkage between scaffold elements.
[E04G 7/22] for scaffold members in end-to-side relation -- E04G 7/22 belongs to the Fixed Constructions - Building - Scaffolding subgroup and relates to the way scaffold components are joined. It specifically covers connections where one scaffold member is attached to the side of another member at its end, forming an end-to-side joint. This classification is used for patents describing such scaffold joint configurations.
[E04G 7/24] Couplings involving arrangements covered by more than one of the subgroups -- This subclass includes scaffold couplings that incorporate features from more than one defined sub-group, such as joints that combine different attachment methods or mechanical arrangements within a single connection. It captures inventions where the coupling serves multiple functional roles across the scaffold structure.
[E04G 7/26] for use with specially-shaped scaffold members -- E04G 7/26 classifies devices, fittings or methods that join scaffold components having a special or non-standard shape, covering connectors designed specifically for uniquely shaped scaffold members within the fixed-construction building scaffold hierarchy.
[E04G 7/28] Clips or connections for securing boards -- E04G 7/28 covers devices such as clips, fasteners or other mechanical connections that secure the boards of a scaffold to its frame or to adjacent boards. It is part of the Fixed Constructions group, under Building → Scaffolding → Connections between parts of the scaffold.
[E04G 7/30] Scaffolding bars or members with non-detachably fixed coupling elements -- This CPC subclass covers scaffolding bars or members that are joined by coupling elements that are permanently fixed, meaning they cannot be detached without damaging the parts. It falls under Fixed Constructions ► Building ► Scaffolding ► Connections between parts of the scaffold, code E04G 7/30.
[E04G 7/301] for connecting bars or members which are parallel or in end-to-end relation -- E04G 7/301 concerns devices or methods for joining scaffold bars or members that run parallel or are placed end-to-end, creating continuous or rigid connections. It is used to secure the structural integrity of fixed scaffold constructions.
[E04G 7/302] for connecting crossing or intersecting bars or members -- The CPC entry E04G 7/302 covers methods or devices for joining scaffold components where bars or members cross or intersect, such as clips, brackets, pins or welds that secure intersecting elements. It falls under Fixed Constructions → Building → Scaffolding → Connections between parts of the scaffold.
[E04G 7/303] the added coupling elements are only fixed at one of the bars or members to connect -- This subclass covers scaffold connections in which a coupling element, such as a clamp, bracket, hook or similar device, is secured to only one scaffold bar or member and used to attach another component. It addresses temporary or adjustable joining methods where the added element is fixed at a single point rather than spanning multiple members.
[E04G 7/304] with tying means for connecting the bars or members -- This subclass covers scaffold connections that use tying means such as ropes, straps, cables or similar devices to join bars or members, providing flexible or adjustable links instead of rigid couplings. It applies to temporary building structures where the ties secure components without permanent fastening.
[E04G 7/305] without tying means for connecting the bars or members -- This subclass includes scaffold systems where the bars or members are joined without using tying devices such as rope, wire or strap; the connection is achieved by clamps, couplings, bolts, welds or other permanent or mechanical fittings that hold elements together.
[E04G 7/306] the added coupling elements are fixed at several bars or members to connect -- E04G 7/306 covers scaffold connections where additional coupling elements are permanently attached to multiple bars or members to join them together, creating fixed links that prevent relative movement. The classification applies to building-related scaffolding systems in which the added components serve as integral, non-adjustable connectors.
[E04G 7/307] with tying means for connecting the bars or members -- This subclass covers fixed scaffolding in which the bars or members are joined by tying means such as ropes, straps, cables or similar flexible connectors, rather than by rigid mechanical fasteners. It applies to scaffold connections that rely on tying to secure the components together.
[E04G 7/308] without tying means for connecting the bars or members -- E04G 7/308 refers to scaffolding connections without tying means. It is a subclass of scaffolding connections. This code is used for connections between parts of the scaffold.
[E04G 7/32] with coupling elements using wedges -- The E04G 7/32 subclass covers scaffold connections that employ wedge-type coupling elements. It pertains to fixed-construction scaffolding where the parts are joined by wedges that lock the components together, providing a secure, non-adjustable joint. This method is used to achieve stable, rigid scaffold assemblies.
[E04G 7/34] with coupling elements using positive engagement, e.g. hooks or pins -- The subclass E04G 7/34 pertains to scaffold connections that employ coupling elements which engage positively, for example hooks, pins or similar devices that lock the components together. It is used for describing fixed-construction scaffold systems where parts are fastened without relying on clamping or friction alone.
[E04G 9/00] Falsework, forms, or shutterings for shaping of building parts -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Forming or shuttering elements for general use, re-usable and to be used in situ.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Forms, shutterings, or falsework for making walls, floors, ceilings, or roofs, i.e. formworks showing features specific for the casting of walls, floors, ceiling -> E04G11/00 - Falsework, forms, or shutterings for particular parts of buildings, e.g. stairs, steps, cornices, balconies, i.e. formworks showing features specific for the casting of particular parts of buildings, e.g. stairs, steps, cornices, balconies. -> E04G13/00 - Forms or shutterings for making (i.e. formworks showing features specific for the casting of openings, cavities, slits, or channels) cf. . -> E04G15/00 - Moulds; Cores; Mandrels to be used for prefabrication of elements in prefabrication plants rather than in-situ. -> B28B7/00
[E04G 9/02] Forming boards or similar elements -- E04G 9/02 relates to forming boards and comparable elements that serve as falsework, forms or shutterings in building construction. These components provide temporary support or shaping for concrete and other building parts during erection or casting.
[E04G 9/021] the form surface being of cardboard -- E04G 9/021 classifies temporary formwork in scaffolding where the surface that shapes the building part is made of cardboard. It covers the use of cardboard panels as the facing of falsework, forms or shutters to impart surface texture and geometry during construction. This entry is part of the Cooperative Patent Classification used by the EPO and USPTO.
[E04G 9/04] the form surface being of wood -- E04G 9/04 relates to scaffolding falsework, forms or shutterings that have a wooden contact surface, used to shape concrete or other building components during construction. It is classified under Fixed Constructions - Building - Scaffolding - Falsework, forms or shutterings for shaping of building parts.
[E04G 9/05] the form surface being of plastics -- E04G 9/05 classifies temporary building forms whose contact surface is made of plastic materials. It applies to falsework, formwork or shuttering used to shape concrete or other building components, where the form's facing is constructed from plastics to provide a smooth, reusable surface.
[E04G 9/06] the form surface being of metal -- E04G 9/06 covers falsework, forms or shuttering used in construction where the mold surface that contacts the concrete is made of metal. Metal form surfaces provide a smooth finish, durability and resistance to wear, enabling precise shaping of building components such as walls, slabs or arches. This subclass is part of the Fixed Constructions - Building - Scaffoldings hierarchy.
[E04G 9/065] the form surface being of wire mesh -- This CPC entry relates to falsework, forms or shutterings used in building construction where the surface that shapes the poured material is composed of wire mesh. It falls under the hierarchy Fixed Constructions → Building → Scaffolding, and specifies the use of mesh as the form face for shaping building parts.
[E04G 9/08] Forming boards or similar elements, which are collapsible, foldable, or able to be rolled up -- This class includes temporary formwork components such as boards or panels that can be collapsed, folded or rolled for easy transport and storage in scaffolding, falsework and shuttering. They are used to shape concrete or other building parts and are designed to be reusable and compact.
[E04G 9/083] which are foldable -- A foldable falsework, form or shoring used in building construction to shape concrete or other building parts, designed to be collapsed for easier handling, transport and storage. This temporary structure belongs to the fixed-construction group under scaffolding and is classified under E04G 9/083.
[E04G 9/086] which are specially adapted to be degradable in time, e.g. by moisture or water -- E04G 9/086 covers temporary falsework, forms or shutterings used in building construction that are specifically designed to degrade over time, such as by moisture, water or other natural agents. The classification applies to biodegradable or water-soluble formwork intended to break down after serving its purpose.
[E04G 9/10] with additional peculiarities such as surface shaping, insulating or heating, permeability to water or air -- E04G 9/10 covers falsework, forms or shutterings used to shape building parts that incorporate special features such as surface profiling, integrated insulation or heating, or controlled permeability to water or air, enabling the finished element to have tailored texture, thermal performance or airflow characteristics.
[E04H] BUILDINGS OR LIKE STRUCTURES FOR PARTICULAR PURPOSES -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Structures specially adapted for buildings for special purposes; general layout of buildings as a whole, e.g. modular co-ordination; details which are peculiar to different types of the buildings for special purposes specified in the groups.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Foundations -> E02D
[E04H 1/00] Buildings or groups of buildings for dwelling or office purposes -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Buildings made of building modules;  Dwelling houses in general, i.e. apartment houses arranged in two or more levels;  Office buildings;  Small buildings for limited occupation.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Buildings or groups of buildings for public or similar purposes; Institutions, e.g. infirmaries or prisons -> E04H3/00
[E04H 1/005] Modulation co-ordination -- E04H 1/005 - Modulation co-ordination concerns methods and devices that synchronize the operation of building services such as heating, ventilation, lighting or shading to achieve a desired indoor environment in residential or office complexes. It covers control strategies, sensors and algorithms that adjust multiple subsystems in concert, improving energy efficiency and occupant comfort.
[E04H 1/02] Dwelling houses -- The CPC subclass E04H 1/02 pertains to dwelling houses, i.e., residential structures intended for people to live in such as single-family homes, apartments and similar accommodations. It addresses the design, construction and components of these residential buildings within the broader group of fixed constructions for dwelling or office purposes.
[E04H 1/04] Apartment houses arranged in two or more levels -- This CPC entry addresses multi-storey residential structures in which apartments are arranged on two or more levels, sharing common structural elements typical of apartment blocks or condominiums. It falls under fixed constructions, building, buildings or like structures for particular purposes, specifically those intended for dwelling or office use.
[E04H 1/06] Office buildings -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Small buildings or other erections for limited occupation, erected in the open air or arranged in buildings, e.g. kiosks, waiting shelters for bus stops or for filling stations, roofs for railway platforms, watchmen's huts or dressing cubicles -> E04H1/12
[E04H 1/12] Small buildings or other erections for limited occupation, erected in the open air or arranged in buildings, e.g. kiosks, waiting shelters for bus stops or for filling stations, roofs for railway platforms, watchmen's huts or dressing cubicles -- E04H 1/12 covers small structures intended for limited or occasional occupancy, such as kiosks, bus-stop shelters, fuel-station canopies, railway platform roofs, guard huts and changing cubicles, which are erected in the open air or incorporated within larger buildings.
[E04H 1/1205] Small buildings erected in the open air  E04H1/1272  takes precedence -- E04H 1/1205 covers small buildings erected in the open air, such as kiosks, garden sheds, temporary pavilions and similar lightweight structures that are permanently fixed but not enclosed within a larger building, intended for dwelling, office or ancillary purposes. This subclass falls under the hierarchy of fixed constructions for residential or office use.
[E04H 1/1211] Waiting shelters for bus stops -- E04H 1/1211 pertains to waiting shelters at bus stops, fixed constructions that protect passengers from weather while they await public transport. These structures are classified under buildings or groups of buildings intended for dwelling or office purposes, specifically for transport-related use.
