[E04H 1/1216] Public W.C.s -- Public water closets installed in residential or office buildings for general public use, typically comprising a toilet bowl, flushing mechanism, and associated plumbing, designed to serve occupants and visitors in a convenient, hygienic manner. This classification covers the architectural integration and functional provisions of such sanitary facilities within multi-unit dwellings or commercial premises.
[E04H 1/1222] Sales kiosks -- Sales kiosks are small, fixed retail structures, typically freestanding or attached to larger buildings, designed for the sale of goods or services in public or commercial areas. They provide a compact, often modular space for transactions, inventory display and customer interaction, and are classified under building constructions for dwelling or office purposes.
[E04H 1/1227] Traffic control kiosks -- E04H 1/1227 designates traffic control kiosks, small permanent or semi-permanent structures that house equipment for managing road, pedestrian or vehicle flow, such as signal controllers, information displays or ticket machines, and are installed as part of building complexes for dwelling or office purposes.
[E04H 1/1233] Shelters for filling stations -- Shelters for filling stations are structures such as canopies, huts or small garages that cover and protect fuel dispensing equipment, customers and staff at petrol or service stations. They may be freestanding or attached to a building and are designed to provide weather protection while allowing safe access to the pumps.
[E04H 1/1238] Shelters for engines, e.g. electrical meter housings -- A shelter that encloses a small engine or similar device, such as a housing for an electrical meter, is classified under E04H 1/1238. This entry covers protective enclosures integrated into fixed building structures, designed to shield the enclosed equipment from weather, vandalism, or unauthorized access while maintaining functionality. It is used for devices that require a dedicated, secure housing within residential or commercial premises.
[E04H 1/1244] Dressing or beach cabins -- E04H 1/1244 classifies small, fixed shelters such as dressing or beach cabins that provide changing or recreational spaces at seaside or similar leisure sites; these lightweight, often portable structures are intended for personal use and fall under buildings or groups of buildings designed for dwelling or office-type purposes.
[E04H 1/125] Small buildings, arranged in other buildings  E04H1/1272  takes precedence -- A subclass for small ancillary structures that are incorporated within larger buildings, such as stairwells, utility rooms, annexes or similar facilities used for dwelling or office purposes, describing their arrangement inside the host building. It falls under fixed constructions for buildings or groups of buildings and is superseded by the more specific subclass E04H1/1272.
[E04H 1/1255] Voting booths -- E04H 1/1255 pertains to voting booths, which are enclosed structures-permanent or portable-designed to give voters privacy and security while casting ballots. The class includes designs for modular, fixed or temporary booths, along with related access, support and shielding features used in elections.
[E04H 1/1261] Cubicles for fire-protection -- The CPC subclass E04H 1/1261 covers fire-protective cubicles incorporated in residential or office buildings, such as individual offices, workstations, or sleeping compartments that are designed to contain or resist fire spread. It includes structural arrangements, fire-rated partitions, and related protective elements that form a self-contained fire zone within a larger building.
[E04H 1/1266] Cubicles for dressing; Toilets -- E04H 1/1266 classifies the fixed-construction elements that provide personal hygiene and changing facilities within buildings, such as partitioned dressing cubicles and toilet compartments. It covers the design, arrangement and integration of these sanitary spaces as part of dwelling or office structures.
[E04H 1/1272] Exhibition stands -- Exhibition stands are modular or custom-built structures intended to showcase goods, services or information at trade fairs, museums and similar venues. Classified under fixed constructions, they are treated as buildings or groups of buildings serving commercial or office functions rather than residential use. The code E04H 1/1272 designates these specialized display installations.
[E04H 1/1277] Shelters for decontamination -- E04H 1/1277 covers shelters specifically designed for decontamination, such as facilities used to cleanse personnel, equipment or materials contaminated by hazardous substances. These structures are often temporary or semi-permanent and include containment, ventilation and washing systems to isolate and neutralize contaminants.
[E04H 1/14] Telephone cabinets -- Telephone cabinets are small prefabricated enclosures installed on or near buildings to house telecommunications equipment, offering weather-proof protection and a secure point for connecting telephone lines and network components in residential or office settings.
[E04H 12/00] Towers -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Access covers or locks for openings in masts or poles;  Structures made of specified materials, like e.g. plastic or composite layer of resin and longitudinally arranged glass fibres;  Structures made of wood and truss-like structures made of wood;  Structures made of metal, truss-like structures made of metal, structures with truss-like sections combined with tubular-like sections, details of flanges for tubular masts;  Structures made of concrete, with or without internal or external reinforcements, pre-stressed structures;  Movable structures or having movable sections, e.g. rotatable, telescopic, with identical elements or with hinged sections;  Side support means for towers, masts or poles, e.g. using guy ropes, struts;  Sockets or holders for poles or posts;  Cross arms;  Winding-towers for mines;  Chimney stacks, e.g. free-standing or similar ducts;  Water-towers;  Flag poles;  Arrangements for erecting and lowering towers, masts, poles, chimney stacks or the like.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Cooling-towers -> E04H5/12 - Derricks for oil drilling -> E21B15/00
[E04H 12/003] Access covers or locks therefor -- E04H 12/003 covers access provisions for tower structures, including protective covers, doors or similar enclosures and the locking mechanisms used to secure those openings. This relates to fixed constructions in buildings designed for specific tower purposes.
[E04H 12/02] Structures made of specified materials -- E04H 12/02 covers towers that are constructed from particular materials such as steel, concrete, wood or composites and relates to their design, fabrication and assembly for specific purposes. It falls within the hierarchy of fixed constructions, building, buildings or like structures for particular purposes, towers.
[E04H 12/04] of wood
[E04H 12/06] Truss-like structures -- E04H 12/06 denotes truss-like structures used in towers, classified under fixed constructions and buildings or like structures for particular purposes. It covers designs that employ a framework of interconnected members to form a rigid, load-bearing lattice, typically applied to communication, observation, or utility towers.
[E04H 12/08] of metal
[E04H 12/085] Details of flanges for tubular masts -- E04H 12/085 pertains to the specific design and detailing of flanges used on tubular masts of tower structures. It falls under the fixed-construction hierarchy for buildings or similar structures built for particular purposes, focusing on how flange geometry, attachment methods and reinforcement are configured to secure mast sections.
[E04H 12/10] Truss-like structures -- The CPC entry E04H 12/10 pertains to truss-like structures used in towers and similar fixed constructions. It covers frameworks composed of intersecting members that form triangular units to provide load-bearing support in tower-type buildings or specialized structures. The classification is applied to the design, analysis and construction of such truss configurations within the broader category of fixed building constructions.
[E04H 12/12] of concrete or other stone-like material, with or without internal or external reinforcements, e.g. with metal coverings, with permanent form elements -- E04H 12/12 covers towers constructed from concrete or other stone-like materials, including those that incorporate internal or external reinforcements such as metal coverings. The class also embraces designs that employ permanent form elements as part of the tower's structural system.
[E04H 12/14] Truss-like structures -- E04H 12/14 classifies truss-like structures used in towers, i.e., frameworks of interconnecting members forming triangular patterns that provide strength and stability for fixed building constructions. It covers the design, assembly and reinforcement of such skeletal frameworks employed to support loads in tall or specialized tower structures.
[E04H 12/16] Prestressed structures -- E04H 12/16 classifies towers that incorporate prestressed structural elements, such as concrete or steel members that are tensioned during fabrication to enhance load capacity and reduce deformation. It applies to fixed constructions used for communication, observation, or similar functions where high strength and stability are required.
[E04H 12/18] movable or with movable sections, e.g. rotatable or telescopic -- This subclass covers towers whose structures include movable or adjustable sections, such as rotatable, telescopic, or otherwise shiftable components that allow changes in height, orientation, or configuration. It applies to towers designed for purposes like communication, observation, or support where the ability to modify the structure's shape or position is integral to their function.
[E04H 12/182] telescopic  locking of telescopic systems in general  F16B7/10 -- The code E04H 12/182 covers mechanisms that lock telescopic sections of tower structures in place, ensuring stability after extension. It pertains to fixed constructions used in buildings or specialized towers where parts are deployed in a telescoping fashion and then secured against movement.
[E04H 12/185] with identical elements -- E04H 12/185 pertains to towers whose construction employs identical or repeated elements, such as modular sections, prefabricated components or uniform structural members. The subclass covers designs where the tower is assembled from these repeated parts to achieve a specific functional purpose.
[E04H 12/187] with hinged sections -- E04H 12/187 relates to towers constructed with hinged sections that can pivot, fold or be disassembled for transport, erection, or to improve wind resistance. The class applies to fixed constructions such as communication, observation or utility towers where the hinged design enables easier assembly, adjustment or removal.
[E04H 12/20] Side-supporting means therefor, e.g. using guy ropes or struts -- The CPC subclass E04H 12/20 pertains to side-supporting means for towers, such as guy ropes, cables or struts that brace the structure against lateral forces. It is classified under fixed constructions → building → buildings or like structures for particular purposes → towers.
[E04H 12/22] Sockets or holders for poles or posts -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Sockets or holders for poles or posts driven or screwed into the ground;  Sockets or holders for poles or posts to be placed on the ground, e.g. filed with, water, sand or the like;  Mounting poles or posts to a holder, e.g. on a flat base, in a socket;  Clamping poles or posts on a stub;  Means for adjusting the orientation of the post or pole;  Holders used for protection, repair or reinforcement of the post or pole.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - For sports articles, e.g. floor sockets -> A63B71/023 - For road signs -> E01F9/673
[E04H 12/2207] not used
[E04H 12/2215] driven into the ground  ground anchors  E02D5/80 -- E04H 12/2215 covers ground-anchored fixing methods in which anchors are driven into the soil to stabilize towers or other fixed constructions. The classification includes the design, installation and use of driven ground anchors as part of the supporting system for structures requiring high load resistance.
[E04H 12/2223] by screwing -- The subclass E04H 12/2223 covers towers whose structural elements are assembled by screwing, that is, using threaded fasteners to join components instead of welding, bolting or adhesives. It applies to fixed constructions of buildings or similar structures built for specific purposes where the primary joining method is screw fixation.
[E04H 12/223] with movable anchoring elements; with separately driven anchor rods -- The E04H 12/223 subclass covers towers that incorporate movable anchoring elements, such as anchor rods that can be driven separately from the main structure. It includes designs where the anchoring system can be adjusted or repositioned after installation to improve stability or adapt to site conditions.
[E04H 12/2238] to be placed on the ground  Christmas tree stands  A47G33/12 -- The code E04H 12/2238 covers fixed-construction devices that are placed on the ground to support Christmas trees, essentially ground-mounted stands for holding seasonal trees. It falls under the broader class of towers within buildings or like structures for particular purposes and cross-references the decorative-object class A47G33/12.
[E04H 12/2246] filled with water, sand or the like -- The classification covers towers that achieve stability by being filled with a dense medium such as water, sand, or similar materials. These filled towers are fixed constructions used in various building applications where added weight improves structural integrity or functionality.
[E04H 12/2253] Mounting poles or posts to the holder  E04H12/2284  takes precedence; break-away mountings  E01F9/631 -- The CPC subclass E04H 12/2253 covers the mounting of poles or posts to their supporting holder in tower-type constructions, describing the arrangement and attachment of the pole to the holder structure. It is subordinate to the more specific subclass E04H 12/2284, and related break-away mounting concepts are classified under E01F 9/631.
[E04H 12/2261] on a flat base  E04H12/08 ,  E04H12/10  take precedence -- E04H12/2261 covers towers that are erected on a flat foundation, classified under fixed constructions for buildings or like structures for particular purposes. This subgroup follows the more specific categories E04H12/08 and E04H12/10, which take precedence in the hierarchy.
[E04H 12/2269] in a socket -- The CPC code E04H 12/2269 classifies towers that are installed "in a socket," meaning they are mounted into a socket-shaped opening or recess in a foundation or supporting structure rather than being freestanding or anchored by piles. It applies to fixed constructions such as utility, communication or observation towers designed for specific purposes where the socket provides a stable connection to the ground or supporting element.
[E04H 12/2276] Clamping poles or posts on a stub  E04H12/2269  takes precedence -- The classification covers devices or methods for securing poles or posts to a short projecting stub as part of tower construction, typically used to fasten vertical elements to a base or support segment. It pertains to fixed-construction tower installations where the clamping arrangement provides stable anchorage.
[E04H 12/2284] Means for adjusting the orientation of the post or pole -- The subclass E04H 12/2284 relates to devices that allow a tower or pole to be turned or tilted, including rotating bases, swivels, tilt-adjustment mechanisms or other fittings that enable the orientation of the post or pole to be changed for alignment, load distribution or environmental adaptation.
[E04H 12/2292] Holders used for protection, repair or reinforcement of the post or pole  E04H12/2276  takes precedence -- E04H 12/2292 covers holders designed to protect, repair or reinforce the post or pole of a tower, such as brackets, clamps or sleeves that secure structural elements against degradation or damage. This subclass follows the broader group E04H12/2276, which deals with general reinforcement of tower posts.
[E04H 12/24] Cross arms -- Cross arms are horizontal structural members mounted on towers to support loads such as antennas, signal equipment, lighting or other devices. They extend outward from the tower shaft, providing a stable platform for mounting and helping distribute forces across the tower structure. Used primarily in telecommunications and broadcasting towers.
[E04H 12/26] Winding towers for mines -- The subclass E04H 12/26 classifies winding towers used in mines, which are fixed tower structures that house hoisting drums, cables and related machinery for raising and lowering cages or skips in underground shafts. It covers the tower framework and associated equipment designed specifically for mining hoist operations.
[E04H 12/28] Chimney stacks, e.g. free-standing, or similar ducts -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Chimneys as part of buildings -> E04F17/02
[E04H 12/30] Water-towers -- E04H 12/30 covers water-towers, which are elevated cylindrical or rectangular tanks supported by a framework to store water at height for gravity-driven distribution, commonly used for municipal supply, fire-fighting reserves, or industrial processes. The classification includes the structural elements, supporting columns, and access provisions for maintenance.
[E04H 12/32] Flagpoles -- E04H 12/32 pertains to flagpoles, the vertical structures used to display flags on towers, buildings or other fixed installations. The class includes designs, mounting methods and supporting mechanisms for erecting and operating flagpoles in various settings.
[E04H 12/34] Arrangements for erecting or lowering towers, masts, poles, chimney stacks, or the like -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Arrangements for casting in situ concrete towers or the like;  Arrangements for stacking tower sections on top of each other;  Arrangements for lifting tower sections for placing additional sections under them;  Arrangements for tilting up whole structures or sections thereof;  Arrangements for setting poles in the ground;  Arrangements for demolition towers or the like.
[E04H 12/341] Arrangements for casting  in situ  concrete towers or the like -- E04H 12/341 deals with systems and procedures for casting concrete towers or similar structures directly on site, including the formwork, reinforcement arrangement, and concrete delivery methods required to build in-situ concrete towers.
[E04H 12/342] Arrangements for stacking tower sections on top of each other  E04H12/344  takes precedence -- A classification for construction techniques that describe how separate tower sections are positioned and secured in a stacked arrangement, typically used when assembling tall structures. This grouping is overtaken by the more specific subclass E04H12/344, which details refined methods for aligning and joining tower sections.
[E04H 12/344] Arrangements for lifting tower sections for placing additional sections under them -- E04H 12/344 covers devices and methods used to raise existing tower sections so that further sections can be positioned beneath them, typically involving hoisting mechanisms, support frames or temporary platforms that enable safe and precise addition of height to towers.
[E04H 12/345] Arrangements for tilting up whole structures or sections thereof  E04H12/347  takes precedence -- The code E04H 12/345 covers designs that enable whole towers or parts of towers to be tilted upward during installation or operation, such as mechanisms that pivot or swing structural sections into position. This arrangement is superseded by the more specific class E04H 12/347.
[E04H 12/347] Arrangements for setting poles in the ground -- The CPC subclass E04H 12/347 covers methods and devices for installing poles or similar vertical supports into the ground, including the arrangement of foundations, anchoring, and positioning techniques used in towers and similar structures. It applies to fixed constructions where the poles are set for buildings or other specific purposes.
[E04H 12/348] Arrangements for demolition towers or the like -- The CPC entry E04H 12/348 covers designs and structural solutions for temporary demolition towers or similar fixtures used to support equipment and personnel during the dismantling of buildings or other fixed constructions. It includes the layout of access platforms, anchoring systems, and safety features specific to demolition operations.
[E04H 13/00] Monuments -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Accessories for grave sites, e.g. liners, covers, vault lowering devices;  Funeral monuments, grave sites curbing or markers not making part of vaults;  Ventilation systems for tombs and burial vaults;  Columbaria, mausoleum with frontal access to vaults;  Memorials for cremation ashes.
[E04H 13/001] Accessories for grave sites, e.g. liners, covers, vault lowering devices  E04H13/003  takes precedence -- The E04H 13/001 class covers accessories used at grave sites, including liners, covers and devices for lowering vaults, classified under fixed constructions, buildings or like structures for particular purposes and monuments. It is subordinate to E04H 13/003, which has precedence.
[E04H 13/003] Funeral monuments, grave sites curbing or markers not making part of vaults  E04H13/008  takes precedence -- A funeral monument or gravestone element that is a permanent, fixed structure but does not form part of an underground vault, such as a decorative marker, curbing or other surface memorial placed at a burial site. This falls under the CPC class for buildings or like structures for particular purposes, specifically monuments.
[E04H 13/005] Ventilation systems therefor -- E04H 13/005 pertains to ventilation systems integrated into monuments and similar fixed constructions. It covers the design, installation and operation of air circulation, heating, cooling and exhaust equipment specifically for historic or commemorative structures.
[E04H 13/006] Columbaria, mausoleum with frontal access to vaults -- E04H 13/006 covers monuments such as columbaria and mausoleums that provide a frontal entrance directly into individual vaults or niches, allowing visitors to access the burial spaces from the front rather than through a side or rear opening. This subclass falls under fixed constructions for particular purposes within the broader building and monument categories.
[E04H 13/008] Memorials for cremation ashes  E04H13/006  takes precedence
[E04H 14/00] Buildings for combinations of different purposes not covered by any single one of main groups -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Hotels; Motels; Coffee houses; Restaurants; Shops; Department stores -> E04H3/02 - Structures arranged in or forming part of buildings -> E04H9/06
[E04H 15/00] Tents or canopies, in general -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Hunting, fishing huts or the like;  Bathing or beach cabins;  Tents used as toys;  Tents or tent-like constructions composed partially of rigid panels;  Tents combined or specially associated with other devices, e.g. of suspended type, partially supported by vehicles;  Heating, lighting aspects for tents. Ventilating of tents and of tent roofs;  Tents having plural sectional covers, e.g. pavilions, vaulted tents, marquees, circus tents, plural tents, e.g. modular;  Inflatable tents;  Cone shaped tents;  Centre-pole supported tents, e.g. umbrella type tents;  Convertible tents, e.g. from one type to another type tent, from tent to canopy, from tent cover into diverse articles;  Parts, components, construction details, accessories, interior equipment, specially adapted for tents, e.g. guy-line equipment, skirts, thresholds;  Supporting means for tents, e.g. frames;  Covers, floors, closures, awnings sunshades;  Poles;  Pegs, stakes or the like;  Tent or canopy cover fastenings, e.g. the fixing members being a beading, the fixing members being locked by an additional locking member or the longitudinal recesses being made from two clamping members.
[E04H 15/001] Hunting, fishing huts or the like -- The class includes small shelters intended for hunting or fishing, such as blinds, portable cabins, or temporary huts that provide protection and concealment for users in natural settings. These structures may be fixed or movable and are used as dedicated accommodations for the activity.
[E04H 15/003] Bathing or beach cabins -- E04H 15/003 covers small portable structures intended for bathing, beach or seaside use such as bathing cabins or beach huts. These are classified as tents or canopies designed for recreational water-related activities and are part of the building structures for particular purposes category.
[E04H 15/005] Wind breakers, i.e. fabric stretched between posts anchored in the ground -- E04H 15/005 relates to wind-break devices formed by fabric stretched between posts that are fixed in the ground, serving as protective canopies or tents to shield an area from wind. The classification falls under fixed constructions, buildings or like structures for particular purposes, specifically tents or canopies.
[E04H 15/006] Tents used as toys -- E04H 15/006 covers small, portable tents or canopies that are intended primarily as play items rather than functional shelters, such as toy tents for children that mimic larger camping structures. The classification places these novelty or recreational constructions within the broader group of fixed building structures designed for specific purposes.
[E04H 15/008] Tents or tent-like constructions composed partially of rigid panels  E04H15/06  takes precedence; trailer-type vehicles or caravans with foldable or readily disassembled living accommodation comprising flexible elements  B60P3/341 -- This subclass covers tents or tent-like structures that incorporate rigid panels as part of their enclosure, rather than being wholly flexible. It falls under Fixed Constructions → Building → Buildings or like structures for particular purposes → Tents or canopies, in general. The broader subclass E04H15/06 is the parent, and the related vehicle-mounted version is classified under B60P3/341.
[E04H 15/02] Tents combined or specially associated with other devices -- This CPC subclass covers tents that are integrated with or fitted to other equipment, such as lighting, heating, or support structures, enabling combined functionality for temporary shelters. It includes designs where the tent is permanently attached to devices like solar panels, ventilation systems, or portable power units.
[E04H 15/04] suspended type, e.g. from trees or from cantilever supports -- The entry covers tents or canopies that are hung rather than anchored on the ground, being supported from above by ropes, cables or frames such as trees or cantilevered structures. It includes designs where the shelter's load is carried by suspension elements.
[E04H 15/06] Tents at least partially supported by vehicles -- These tents are designed to be anchored to or partly supported by a vehicle, using the vehicle's chassis, roof or frame as a structural element. The arrangement enables quick setup and added stability for camping, emergency or field uses where the vehicle provides portable support.
[E04H 15/08] Trailer awnings or the like -- E04H 15/08 relates to awnings that are attached to trailers, caravans or comparable mobile structures, providing shelter or shade when the vehicle is stationary. It includes retractable, fixed or semi-fixed coverings that can be deployed and removed as needed.
[E04H 15/10] Heating, lighting or ventilating -- The subclass E04H 15/10 covers heating, lighting and ventilating systems intended for temporary structures such as tents or canopies, including the equipment, distribution networks and control devices used to provide warmth, illumination or air movement within those shelters.
[E04H 15/12] Heating -- The code E04H 15/12 covers heating provisions for temporary structures such as tents or canopies. It includes devices, systems and methods for providing thermal comfort, temperature control or climate regulation within these portable enclosures. The classification is used for patents dealing with heating solutions specifically designed for such structures.
[E04H 15/14] Ventilating -- Ventilating refers to the provision of openings, ducts, vents or other means that allow air exchange in tents or canopies, helping to remove heat, moisture and odors while protecting the interior from precipitation. It includes fixed or adjustable apertures, mesh panels, flaps or self-inflating systems designed to maintain a comfortable interior climate for occupants or stored goods.
[E04H 15/16] of tent roofs -- E04H 15/16 is a CPC subclass that falls under Fixed Constructions → Building → Buildings or like structures for particular purposes → Tents or canopies, in general, and pertains specifically to the design and features of tent roofs, including their shape, supporting structure, material composition and opening or closing mechanisms.
[E04H 15/18] Tents having plural sectional covers, e.g. pavilions, vaulted tents, marquees, circus tents -- E04H 15/18 refers to tents composed of multiple sectional covers, including pavilions, vaulted tents, marquees and circus tents. These structures are classified under fixed constructions for particular purposes and describe modular or assembled roofing systems used for temporary or semi-permanent shelter.
[E04H 15/20] inflatable, e.g. shaped, strengthened or supported by fluid pressure -- E04H 15/20 covers inflatable structures such as tents or canopies that are shaped, reinforced or supported by fluid pressure. These are fixed constructions used as buildings or similar structures for particular purposes, where inflation provides the primary structural stability.
[E04H 15/22] supported by air pressure inside the tent -- E04H 15/22 covers tents, canopies or similar temporary structures whose shape is maintained by internal air pressure rather than rigid frames. The classification includes inflatable or air-supported shelters where the pressure of the contained air provides the supporting force for the covering material. It applies to designs, methods and apparatus that create, control or utilize this air-pressure support.
[E04H 15/24] cone shaped, e.g. teepees -- A cone-shaped temporary structure such as a teepee, designed for shelter and assembled from a framework covered with fabric or similar material, is classified under E04H 15/24. This entry covers tents or canopies that have a circular base and taper to a point at the top, providing a simple, portable enclosure.
[E04H 15/26] Centre-pole supported tents -- Centre-pole supported tents are temporary shelters whose roof or canopy is held up by a single central mast, often combined with peripheral tension lines or ribs. This design provides a simple, quickly erected structure used for events, camping, or emergency accommodation.
[E04H 15/28] Umbrella type tents -- E04H 15/28 refers to umbrella-type tents, portable shelters whose central pole supports a fabric canopy that can be opened and closed like an umbrella. These structures are used for temporary covering of people, equipment or activities and fall under the broader group of fixed constructions for particular purposes.
[E04H 15/30] convertible, e.g. from one type tent to another type tent, from tent to canopy or from tent cover into diverse articles -- The code E04H 15/30 concerns tents or canopies that can be reconfigured, for example changing from one tent type to another, converting a tent into a canopy, or adapting the tent cover into different articles. It falls under fixed constructions for particular building purposes.
[E04H 15/32] Parts, components, construction details, accessories, interior equipment, specially adapted for tents, e.g. guy-line equipment, skirts, thresholds -- E04H 15/32 classifies parts, components, construction details and accessories that are specially adapted for tents or canopies, including interior equipment such as guy-line gear, skirts and thresholds. It falls under Fixed Constructions → Building → Buildings or like structures for particular purposes → Tents or canopies, in general, and is used in CPC filings by the EPO and USPTO.
[E04H 15/322] Stretching devices -- E04H 15/322 covers mechanisms designed to apply tension to tents, canopies or similar temporary structures, improving their shape and stability. The devices may include rods, winches, pulleys or other apparatus that stretch the covering material uniformly.
[E04H 15/324] Beds constituted by the tent supporting means  beds  per se ,  A47C -- E04H 15/324 covers beds that are integrated into or formed by the structural elements of tents or canopies, such as built-in sleeping platforms or folding beds that form part of the tent's supporting framework. The classification is used for designs where the bedding is a permanent or semi-permanent component of the tent structure, rather than a separate piece of furniture.
[E04H 15/34] Supporting means, e.g. frames -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Tent frames of arch-shaped type, expansible, e.g. extensible in a fan type manner, flexible and foldable;  External type frames, i.e. outside cover, also with flexible supporting means;  Collapsible, e.g. breakdown type, having connecting nodes, telescoping and foldable, foldable, i.e. having pivoted or hinged means, of lazy-tong type or characterised by connecting arches with lazy-tongues, of parallelogram type.
[E04H 15/36] arch-shaped type -- The arch-shaped type refers to tent or canopy constructions formed by one or more curved arch elements that provide a self-supporting roof without external bracing. These arches may be made of metal, wood or fabric-reinforced ribs and are used for events, storage or temporary shelters where quick erection and clear interior space are desirable.
[E04H 15/38] expansible, e.g. extensible -- A structure classified under E04H 15/38 is a tent, canopy or similar building that can be enlarged or contracted, such as by unfolding, sliding, inflating or telescoping components, to adjust its size or shape for varying occupancy or environmental conditions. This expansible feature allows the shelter to be compact for transport and then extended to provide a larger covered area when needed.
[E04H 15/40] flexible -- E04H 15/40 pertains to flexible tents or canopies, covering structures whose shape can be altered or collapsed by bending, folding, or stretching of the material rather than by rigid components. It includes designs that allow easy erection, dismantling, or adaptation to different dimensions or configurations. This subgroup falls under building constructions intended for temporary or portable use.
[E04H 15/405] flexible and foldable -- The E04H 15/405 subclass covers tents, canopies and similar temporary shelters that are designed to be flexible or foldable, allowing them to be collapsed, transported and re-erected easily. It falls under fixed constructions for particular purposes within the broader building category.
[E04H 15/42] external type, e.g. frame outside cover -- The subclass E04H 15/42 covers tents or canopies whose supporting structure is situated outside the covering material, meaning the frame lies around the periphery rather than inside the fabric. This external-type arrangement is typical of structures where the cover can be easily installed, removed or folded while the frame remains stationary. It applies to general-purpose temporary shelters and similar constructions.
[E04H 15/425] Flexible supporting means -- Flexible supporting means are adjustable or collapsible components used to hold up tents or canopies, such as telescopic poles, bendable frames, or deployable struts. They enable quick erection, height variation and easy transport while providing structural stability for temporary shelters in recreational, military or emergency settings.
[E04H 15/44] collapsible, e.g. breakdown type -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - External type, e.g. frame outside of cover -> E04H15/42
[E04H 15/46] telescoping and foldable -- E04H 15/46 concerns telescoping and foldable tents or canopies, describing structures that can be extended, collapsed or reconfigured without dismantling for easy transport and rapid deployment, typically used as temporary shelters, exhibition booths or mobile shade.E04H 15/46 concerns telescoping and foldable tents or canopies, describing structures that can be extended, collapsed or reconfigured without dismantling for easy transport and rapid deployment, typically used as temporary shelters, exhibition booths or mobile shade.
[E04H 15/48] foldable, i.e. having pivoted or hinged means -- The E04H 15/48 classification covers tents or canopies that are designed to be foldable, employing pivoted or hinged mechanisms to allow the structure to collapse for compact storage and to be quickly unfolded for use. It applies to fixed-construction shelters intended for general or specific purposes where quick assembly and disassembly are required.
[E04H 15/50] lazy-tongs type -- The code E04H 15/50 covers tent or canopy constructions that use a lazy-tongs (scissor-type) frame to support a covering, allowing the structure to be extended, folded or collapsed easily for temporary or portable use. It falls under building structures for particular purposes, specifically tents or canopies in general.
[E04H 15/505] characterised by connecting arches with lazy-tongues -- A tent or canopy design in which the structural arches are joined together using interlocking lazy-tongue joints, allowing the arches to be assembled, disassembled and securely held without additional fasteners. This method provides a simple, robust connection for temporary or portable shelter structures.
[E04H 15/52] parallelogram type -- The code E04H 15/52 designates tents or canopies built in a parallelogram shape, where the supporting frame forms a four-sided figure with opposite sides parallel and equal, creating a planar roof and walls that can be erected or collapsed as a single unit.
[E04H 15/54] Covers of tents or canopies -- E04H 15/54 covers the protective envelope of tents or canopies, including the fabric or roof panels and the fastening systems that attach them to the supporting structure. It addresses material selection, shape, and methods of securing the cover to provide weather protection.
[E04H 15/56] Floors -- E04H 15/56 classifies flooring arrangements for tents, canopies and similar temporary structures, covering the design, materials and installation of floor surfaces that support occupants or equipment within these movable buildings. It includes load-bearing layers, covering panels, anchoring methods and adaptations for varying ground conditions.
[E04H 15/58] Closures -- E04H 15/58 covers the design and implementation of closures for tents and canopies, including doors, flaps, roll-up sections, zippers and other means of sealing openings to control access, ventilation or weather protection. The subclass addresses the structure, mounting and operation of such closure elements within temporary shelter systems.
[E04H 15/60] Poles -- E04H 15/60 classifies poles that serve as structural members in tents or canopies, forming part of the fixed construction of temporary or specialised buildings. It includes vertical, inclined or articulated support elements that create the framework for such shelters.
[E04H 15/62] Pegs, stakes or the like -- E04H 15/62 classifies pegs, stakes and similar fasteners that are used to anchor or support tents, canopies and related temporary structures. The entry falls under the Fixed Constructions hierarchy, specifically in the Building category for structures built for particular purposes. It covers devices that secure the fabric or frame of such coverings to the ground or other supports.
[E04H 15/64] Tent or canopy cover fastenings -- This classification covers mechanisms used to attach a cover to a tent or canopy, such as cords, buckles, straps, clips and other devices that secure the roof material to the supporting frame. It applies to temporary shelters and similar structures.
[E04H 15/642] with covers held by elongated fixing members locking in longitudinal recesses of a frame -- This subclass relates to tents or canopy structures whose outer covering is attached by elongated fixing members that lock into longitudinal recesses or slots cut into the supporting frame, providing a secure, lock-in method for holding the cover in place.
[E04H 15/644] the fixing members being a beading -- The subclass E04H 15/644 covers tents, canopies or similar structures in which the components that secure the covering are beadings, i.e., strip-like or bead-type fasteners that hold the fabric to the frame. It focuses on the method of fixation rather than on the overall shape or support system of the shelter.
[E04H 15/646] the fixing members being locked by an additional locking member -- A CPC subclass for fixed constructions covering tents or canopies where the primary fastening elements are secured by an extra locking component, such as a bolt, clamp or latch, that prevents the members from disengaging. This detail applies specifically to structures built for particular purposes and classified under E04H 15/646.
[E04H 15/648] the longitudinal recesses being made from two clamping members -- The CPC subclass E04H 15/648 covers tents or canopies in which the longitudinal recesses of the fabric or covering are created by using two clamping members that press together to form the groove. This technique is applied to fixed-construction building structures designed for specific purposes.
[E04H 17/00] Fencing, e.g. fences, enclosures, corrals -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Anti-climbing devices, e.g. fixed or rotary spikes;  Wire fencing;  Fences constructed of rigid elements;  Fences using prefabricated panel-like elements;  Posts for fences and anchoring means therefore;  Devices for erecting and removing fences  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Features peculiar to electrified fences -> A01K3/00 , H05C - Means for allowing passage through fences, barriers, or the like -> E06B11/00
[E04H 17/003] Anti-climbing devices, e.g. fixed spikes -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.
[E04H 17/004] Unstable, e.g. rotatory or resiliently mounted fence panels, spikes -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.
[E04H 17/006] Caps or covers for posts -- E04H 17/006 relates to caps or covers that are fitted onto structural posts such as fence, enclosure or corral posts. These components protect the posts from weather, corrosion, impact or other damage and are used in fixed constructions for specific purposes. The entry falls under the hierarchy of building and fixed constructions.
[E04H 17/009] Footing elements for fence posts or fence sections -- The code E04H 17/009 pertains to footing elements that form the base or support for fence posts or fence sections, such as concrete pads, ground-spike anchors or other foundation components designed to stabilise and anchor fencing installations. It falls within the hierarchy of Fixed Constructions → Building → Buildings or like structures for particular purposes → Fencing, e.g. fences, enclosures, corrals.
[E04H 17/013] in combination with gates -- E04H 17/013 covers fencing, enclosures or corrals that are designed to incorporate gates, detailing how gates are integrated with the surrounding barrier structure for access control in specialized building or agricultural settings.
[E04H 17/017] Fencing provided with electric elements or components, e.g. fencing member or component, such as fence strands, post, panel or rail, provided with electric elements or components -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Electrified wire fencing for any use or application.
[E04H 17/02] Wire fencing, e.g. made of wire mesh -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Wire mesh fencing.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Fences constructed of rigid elements, e.g. with additional wire fillings or with posts -> E04H17/14
[E04H 17/04] characterised by the use of specially adapted wire, e.g. barbed wire -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Wire fencing including barbed wire, wire mesh, toothed strips, or the like for any use or application.
[E04H 17/045] Barbed wire or toothed strip -- E04H 17/045 covers barbed wire and toothed strips employed as fencing elements in enclosures, corrals or other protective barriers. It falls under fixed constructions used to secure or delineate areas, often for agricultural or security purposes.
[E04H 17/05] Wire mesh or wire fabric -- E04H 17/05 covers wire mesh and wire fabric used as components of fencing, enclosures, or corrals in fixed building structures. It includes woven, welded, or welded-mesh panels intended for containment or barrier functions in agricultural, industrial, or residential settings. The classification addresses the material form rather than specific installation methods.
[E04H 17/055] Pickets for wire fencing -- The E04H 17/055 subclass concerns pickets for wire fencing, covering the design, shape, material and attachment methods of individual fence posts used in enclosures such as fences, corrals and similar structures.
[E04H 17/06] Parts for wire fences -- The entry E04H 17/06 covers components used in the construction of wire fencing, including posts, wires, ties, gates, fasteners and other accessories that provide support, attachment or protection for the fence. It falls within the broader group of fixed constructions for buildings or like structures intended for specific purposes such as enclosures or corrals.
[E04H 17/063] Fence liners; Vegetation prevention barriers -- The E04H 17/063 class covers liners and barriers attached to fences or enclosures that inhibit plant growth, such as sheet or mesh inserts that prevent vegetation from penetrating or damaging fence structures. It includes materials and designs specifically intended to keep grasses, weeds, vines or other flora from establishing along fence lines.
[E04H 17/066] Slats for chain link fences -- E04H 17/066 relates to slats designed for incorporation into chain-link fences, providing added privacy, security or structural support. The classification falls under fixed constructions, specifically buildings or like structures for particular purposes, within the fencing subgroup.
[E04H 17/08] Anchoring means therefor, e.g. specially-shaped parts entering the ground -- E04H 17/08 covers anchoring means for fences and similar enclosures, specifically shaped parts that are driven or inserted into the ground to secure the structure. The provision applies to the fixation of fencing, corrals or other barrier systems.
[E04H 17/10] characterised by the way of connecting wire to posts -- The CPC subclass E04H 17/10 covers fences, enclosures or corrals where the distinguishing feature is the method of fastening the connecting wire to the supporting posts, detailing specific attachment techniques, hardware or configurations used to secure the wire in place.
[E04H 17/12] the wire being placed in slots, grooves, or the like -- E04H 17/12 covers fence, enclosure or corral structures where the wire is installed in pre-formed slots, grooves or similar channels of the supporting members, securing the wire to form a continuous barrier for livestock, security or pest control.
[E04H 17/124] connecting by one or more clamps, clips, screws, wedges or ties -- The entry defines how fence sections or enclosure components are joined using removable mechanical fasteners such as clamps, clips, screws, wedges or tying devices, allowing assembly, adjustment or disassembly of fences, corrals and similar structures within the CPC E04H 17/124 category.
[E04H 17/127] Stretcher-type wire fencing; Tensioning devices for wire fencing -- The E04H 17/127 subclass covers wire fencing systems that are installed by a stretcher mechanism and includes devices that apply and maintain tension on the wire. These solutions are used for permanent enclosures such as fences, corrals and other fixed barriers in building-related applications.
[E04H 17/131] the wire being tensioned by one or more springs -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.
[E04H 17/133] the wire being tensioned by one or more winders -- The code refers to fence or enclosure systems whose backing wire is kept under tension by one or more winding devices. It covers the mechanisms, such as hand- or motor-driven winders, used to tighten and maintain the tautness of fencing wire during installation or adjustment. This classification applies to constructions like corrals, security fences and animal enclosures where precise wire tension is required.

[E04H 17/14] Fences constructed of rigid elements, e.g. with additional wire fillings or with posts -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fences made of building blocks or of woven slats;  Post and rail fences with or without vertical cross-members and connections between rail and post for such fences;  Picket fences with pickets attached to the side or going through the horizontal members connections between the horizontal members and post for such fences;  Fences using prefabricated panel-like elements, e.g. panels or wired panels with or without frame, with cross members, fitted in grooves of posts;  Corrals;  Posts for frames and anchoring means therefore;  Connections for attaching additional wire to frames, posts or railings
[E04H 17/1404] using building blocks, e.g. from concrete or stone -- E04H 17/1404 covers methods that employ prefabricated building blocks such as concrete or stone to construct fences, enclosures or corrals. The entry is situated in the hierarchy Fixed Constructions - Building - Buildings or like structures for particular purposes - Fencing.
[E04H 17/1408] using woven slats -- E04H 17/1408 covers fences, enclosures or corrals in which the barrier is formed from woven slats, typically interlaced or plaited strips of material such as wood, metal or synthetic fibers. The classification applies to fixed constructions where the slats create a continuous, flexible screening surface.
[E04H 17/1413] Post-and-rail fences, e.g. without vertical cross-members -- E04H 17/1413 covers post-and-rail fences in which vertical posts are connected by horizontal rails and omit extra vertical cross-members, a design commonly used for enclosures, corrals and similar barrier structures.
[E04H 17/1417] with vertical cross-members -- E04H 17/1417 classifies fences, enclosures or corrals that incorporate vertical cross-members to reinforce or shape the structure. The code applies to fixed constructions used for particular purposes where the vertical elements provide additional stability or functionality.
[E04H 17/1426] Picket fences -- Picket fences are lightweight, decorative fence structures consisting of evenly spaced vertical boards attached to horizontal rails, commonly made of wood, metal or composite material. They are installed as garden or residential boundaries, providing a visual barrier while allowing visibility and airflow. Within the CPC they fall under fixed constructions for building purposes, specifically the fencing subclass for enclosures and corrals.
[E04H 17/143] with separate pickets attached to the side of the horizontal members -- This subclass covers fences whose vertical pickets are separate components attached to the sides of the horizontal members rather than being integral with them. It applies to fixed-construction enclosures such as fences, corrals and similar structures designed for specific purposes.
[E04H 17/1439] with separate pickets going through the horizontal members -- This subclass covers fence designs where individual pickets are inserted through horizontal rails rather than being attached by nails, screws or welding, allowing modular assembly and adjustable spacing. It applies to fences, enclosures and corrals that use separate pickets passing through the horizontal members.
[E04H 17/1445] Fences with pivotable slats for reduced wind resistance or for adjusting visibility -- E04H 17/1445 pertains to fences that incorporate pivotable slats intended to reduce wind resistance or to adjust visibility. These designs are used in enclosures where aerodynamic performance or selective sight lines are required.
[E04H 17/1447] Details of connections between rails and posts -- The subclass E04H 17/1447 addresses the design and engineering of the joints that link fence rails to vertical posts, covering fasteners, brackets, welding, and other attachment methods used in fences, enclosures and similar fixed structures.
[E04H 17/1448] Adjustable, angled or hinged connections -- The CPC class E04H 17/1448 pertains to fencing, enclosures and corrals and specifically covers adjustable, angled or hinged connections that join fence components. These connections allow the geometry of the fence to be varied or moved after installation, facilitating flexible positioning, opening, closing or re-configuration of the structure.

[E04H 17/1456] the ends of the rails being located in vertical channels extending along a substantial portion of the length of the post -- This subclass covers fence posts whose rail ends are fitted into vertical channels that run along most of the post's length, providing a continuous guide or support for the rails. [E04H 17/1486] the rails being positioned between the uprights of double posts -- This classification addresses fence structures that use double vertical posts whose uprights are joined by horizontal rails positioned between them, providing a stable enclosure for purposes such as corrals, animal pens, or security barriers.

[E04H 17/16] using prefabricated panel-like elements, e.g. wired frames -- E04H 17/16 covers the use of prefabricated panel-like components, such as wired frames, in the construction of fences, enclosures, corrals and similar fixed building structures. [E04H 17/1602] Using wooden, plastic or composite-material panel-like elements -- This subclass addresses fences, enclosures or corrals that are built from panel-like elements of wood, plastic or composite materials, including how the panels are formed, joined and fixed in place. [E04H 17/166] with cross-members -- E04H 17/166 pertains to fences, enclosures or corrals that include cross-members joining the vertical posts, providing additional rigidity or support. [E04H 17/21] with hollow cross sections -- E04H 17/21 classifies fences, enclosures or corrals whose supporting members have hollow cross sections, such as tubes or channels, rather than solid bars. [E04H 17/26] Devices for erecting or removing fences -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Devices for post and wire handling, e.g. for erecting, for removing posts or for stretching or winding wire or wire mesh;  Hand tools for winding fences, e.g. tying or splicing tools.

[E04H 17/263] for erecting posts  pile driving  E02D7/00 -- This subclass deals with devices and methods for installing fence posts by driving piles into the ground, covering tools, techniques and apparatus used to position and secure posts in fences, enclosures or corrals.
[E04H 17/265] for removing posts -- E04H 17/265 addresses technologies for detaching or extracting fence posts from fixed fencing, enclosures or corrals, including tools, mechanisms or methods that enable removal, replacement or relocation of posts during maintenance, dismantling or re-configuration of such structures.
[E04H 17/266] for stretching or winding wire or wire mesh  stretcher-type wire fencing or tensioning devices for wire fencing  E04H17/127 -- The E04H 17/266 subclass covers devices used in fence construction to stretch, wind or tension wire or wire mesh, including stretcher-type wire fencing and other tensioning equipment. [E04H 17/268] Hand tools for wiring fences, e.g. tying or splicing tools  E04H17/266  takes precedence -- E04H 17/268 designates hand tools employed in wiring fences, including devices for tying or splicing fence wire. [E04H 2001/1283] Small buildings of the ISO containers type -- E04H 2001/1283 covers small buildings that are constructed from standard ISO shipping containers. It includes prefabricated structures such as housing units, offices, clinics or storage facilities that use one or more container modules, either as standalone units or combined to form larger, modular buildings.
[E04H 2001/1288] Sauna cabins -- E04H 2001/1288 falls under the CPC subclass for heating and climate control of buildings and designates inventions relating to sauna cabins, including the structural enclosure, heating elements, steam generation, insulation, and associated control systems for creating and maintaining sauna environments.
[E04H 2001/1294] Booths for smoking -- E04H 2001/1294 covers enclosed structures within a building that are designed to allow individuals to smoke while preventing smoke from spreading to other areas. These booths often include ventilation or sealing features and are installed in public venues, restaurants, or workplaces.
[E04H 2003/142] with vertically movable or tiltable playing field or sections -- E04H 2003/142 covers sports or playground equipment whose playing surface or parts of it can be raised, lowered or tilted, allowing the field to move vertically or change angle during use. This includes devices such as adjustable balance beams, tilting platforms or other play structures with movable sections that alter the playing area's height or inclination.
[E04H 2003/145] with reconfigurable seating arrangement -- Covers buildings or interior spaces that incorporate seats capable of being moved, folded, or otherwise rearranged to change the layout or capacity of the area. Typical applications include multipurpose halls, auditoria, stadiums and other venues where the seating configuration is adapted for different events or uses.
[E04H 2003/147] using horizontally movable sections of tribunes -- E04H 2003/147 concerns stadium or arena structures and the use of tribune sections that can be moved laterally to modify seating capacity or layout. The horizontally movable elements allow quick reconfiguration of the venue for different events or access requirements.
[E04H 2004/0068] made of plastic shells or plastic elements including at least parts of walls and floors -- The CPC subclass E04H 2004/0068 covers buildings, structures or parts thereof that are constructed primarily from plastic shells or plastic elements, where the plastic components form at least portions of the walls and floors. It includes prefabricated or modular units made of polymeric materials used as structural or finishing components in construction.
[E04H 2004/146] Fixing of liners -- Covers the means and processes for attaching liners-such as waterproof membranes, vapor barriers, insulation panels or other protective sheets-to roof or ceiling structures. Includes mechanical fasteners, adhesives, anchoring systems, welding or other techniques used to secure the liner in place.
[E04H 2004/147] the liner edge being held in a slot -- The CPC entry E04H 2004/147 relates to doors, windows, shutters or similar building closures where the edge of a liner-such as a door leaf, panel or sash-is positioned and retained in a surrounding slot. This arrangement secures the liner, provides guidance during movement, and often creates a weather-tight seal between the liner and the surrounding frame.
[E04H 2005/005] Buildings for data processing centers -- E04H 2005/005 covers the design and construction of structures that house computer and data-processing facilities, including server rooms, control rooms and supporting infrastructure such as cooling, power and fire-suppression systems. The classification addresses layout, structural requirements, environmental control and security features specific to high-density electronic equipment.
[E04H 2007/225] Silos with retaining wall type wall elements, e.g. trench silos -- E04H 2007/225 covers agricultural or industrial storage structures in which the silo is formed by a retaining-wall system, such as a trench or pit lined with wall panels that hold earth or other bulk material. The classification includes designs where the wall elements provide structural support and containment for the stored product.
[E04H 2012/006] Structures with truss-like sections combined with tubular-like sections
[E04H 2015/201] with inflatable tubular framework, with or without tent cover -- The CPC subclass E04H 2015/201 groups inventions that incorporate an inflatable tubular framework as the supporting skeleton of a structure, which may be combined with a tent-like cover or function without any cover. This applies to temporary shelters, inflatable buildings and similar devices where the load-bearing elements are air-filled tubes.
[E04H 2015/202] with inflatable panels, without inflatable tubular framework -- E04H 2015/202 classifies temporary or portable structures whose primary load-bearing elements are inflatable panels rather than an inflatable tubular framework. The panels are inflated on site to form walls, roofs or floors, providing rapid-assembly shelter for construction sites, disaster relief or recreational use.
[E04H 2015/203] supported by a non-inflatable structure or framework -- E04H 2015/203 classifies roof constructions that are held up by a permanent, non-inflatable framework such as trusses, beams, columns or other rigid support structures, rather than by air-filled or inflatable devices. It covers designs where the supporting skeleton provides the primary load-bearing function for the roof.
[E04H 2015/204] made from contiguous inflatable tubes -- A roofing or building covering made of a series of joined inflatable tubes that form a continuous, flexible membrane, typically used for temporary or permanent shelter structures where a lightweight, expandable surface is required. This includes constructions where the tubes are contiguous, providing a seamless inflated surface for protection against weather.
[E04H 2015/205] made from two sheets with intermediate spacer means -- E04H 2015/205 covers building panels formed from two outer sheets that are separated by an intermediate spacer structure, creating a sandwich-type element for walls, roofs or façades. The spacer may be a solid, cellular or honeycomb material that provides thermal, acoustic or mechanical performance while the outer sheets protect the core and give the panel structural integrity.
[E04H 2015/206] Details of inflation devices, e.g. valves, connections to fluid pressure source -- The CPC subclass E04H 2015/206 covers the specific details of inflation devices used in inflatable building elements, including the design and configuration of valves, fittings and other connections that link the structure to a fluid-pressure source. It addresses the mechanisms that introduce, control or maintain air or other gases within such architectural components.
[E04H 2015/207] Tents specially designed for insulation -- The CPC subclass E04H 2015/207 refers to tents specifically designed to provide thermal insulation, covering structures, materials and features that retain heat or protect against cold environments. It includes portable shelters such as cold-weather, insulated or heated tents used in outdoor, military or emergency applications.
[E04H 2015/208] with inflatable mattresses -- The CPC code E04H 2015/208 belongs to the group for roof constructions and coverings. It specifically covers roofs or roof coverings that incorporate inflatable mattresses, for purposes such as impact protection, insulation or temporary sleeping surfaces. This subclass is applied to inventions where the roof structure includes built-in inflatable mattress elements.
[E04H 2015/209] Structures with a netting or similar for anchorage -- The CPC entry E04H 2015/209 covers structures that incorporate a netting, mesh or comparable anchoring material to secure the construction or retain external loads, such as soil, vegetation, or tension forces. It includes designs where the netting is integral to fences, retaining walls, erosion-control systems or similar installations that rely on anchored net-like elements for stability.
[E04H 2015/326] Frame members connected by elastic cord -- E04H 2015/326 covers structures whose main frame components are joined by elastic cords, allowing the members to flex or retract relative to one another. The classification applies to floors, walkways, platforms or similar assemblies that use stretchable cords to provide shock absorption, collapsibility or adaptable shape.
[E04H 2015/328] Frames kept erected solely by guy lines -- E04H 2015/328 covers frames, towers or similar structures that are held upright only by guy lines, without other supports or foundations. The classification applies to temporary or permanent erectings such as communication masts, lighting towers and construction scaffolding that rely solely on tensioned wires for stability.
[E04H 3/00] Buildings or groups of buildings for public or similar purposes -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Buildings for hotels, restaurants, shops, department stores, museums, hospitals, mobile medical centres;  Public institutions like schools, prisons;  Buildings like e.g. stadiums, arenas or halls for meetings, entertainment and sport; Tribunes, grandstands and terraces for spectators, i.e. telescopic grandstands as well as foldable retractable or tiltable tribunes;  Other sporting buildings, like e.g. buildings housing swimming pools;  Buildings for theatres, concert halls, studios;  Stages, i.e. revolving stages or stages with shiftable or portable platforms;  Constructional features of auditoriums.
[E04H 3/02] Hotels -- E04H 3/02 designates hotels, buildings or groups of buildings constructed to supply temporary accommodation, meals and related services to paying guests. The class includes facilities from small inns to large resort complexes, generally intended for public use in urban, tourist or recreational settings.
[E04H 3/04] Restaurants or shops having arrangements for self-service -- The code E04H 3/04 relates to the architectural and structural design of public-use buildings that incorporate self-service concepts, such as restaurants and retail outlets where customers select and handle goods themselves. It covers layouts, counters, displays and related facilities that enable autonomous ordering, payment and consumption within such establishments.
[E04H 3/06] Museums -- E04H 3/06 concerns museums, buildings or groups of buildings constructed or adapted to exhibit, preserve and interpret cultural, artistic, scientific or historic collections for public use. The class includes purpose-built museum structures, converted facilities and related spaces such as galleries, storage areas and visitor services.
[E04H 3/08] Hospitals, infirmaries, or the like -- E04H 3/08 covers the design and construction of facilities dedicated to health care, such as hospitals and infirmaries, including their associated structures and layouts. The entry addresses architectural features specific to medical use, from patient wards to support areas, within the broader category of public-purpose buildings.
[E04H 3/10] for meetings, entertainments, or sports -- This subgroup refers to fixed constructions that are built or arranged specifically to accommodate gatherings such as meetings, entertainment events, or sporting activities, including venues like conference halls, theatres, stadiums and similar public-purpose buildings.
[E04H 3/12] Tribunes, grandstands or terraces for spectators -- Tribunes, grandstands or terraces for spectators are fixed constructions designed to provide elevated seating or standing areas for audiences at public venues. They are integral parts of buildings or groups of structures intended for the viewing of events such as sports, performances or ceremonies. This category covers the architectural features that accommodate large numbers of spectators in a permanent, purpose-specific setting.
[E04H 3/123] Telescopic grandstands -- E04H 3/123 refers to telescopic grandstands, which are retractable or fold-away seating systems that can be extended for use or collapsed to save space. These structures are designed for public venues such as stadiums, arenas or exhibition halls, allowing variable audience capacity and rapid reconfiguration of the occupied area.
[E04H 3/126] Foldable, retractable or tiltable tribunes  E04H3/123  takes precedence -- E04H 3/126 covers the design, construction and arrangement of movable spectator seating that can be folded, retracted or tilted, such as temporary or convertible tribunes used in stadiums, arenas and other public venues. The class addresses mechanisms and structural features that enable rapid transformation or storage of the seating units.
[E04H 3/14] Gymnasiums -- The code E04H 3/14 designates gymnasiums, which are public-purpose buildings intended for sports, physical education and related activities. It covers structures such as standard gym halls, indoor courts and associated support spaces like changing rooms, storage areas and equipment rooms, all constructed as fixed facilities for communal use.
[E04H 3/16] for swimming -- E04H 3/16 covers fixed constructions classified as buildings or groups of buildings intended for public or similar purposes that are designed specifically for swimming. This includes swimming pools, aquatic centres and related facilities integrated into larger public structures.
[E04H 3/165] having movable parts -- The entry covers public or similar buildings that incorporate movable components such as doors, shutters, hinged roofs or collapsible walls, allowing parts of the structure to be opened, closed, or reconfigured. It applies to fixed constructions whose functional flexibility is achieved through these moving elements.
[E04H 3/22] Theatres -- E04H 3/22 pertains to the design and construction of theatres, covering structural and functional aspects such as auditorium layout, stage configuration, seating arrangements, acoustics, lighting, access routes, and safety features for public performance venues. It falls under the broader categories of fixed constructions, building, and buildings or like structures for particular public purposes.
[E04H 3/24] Constructional features of stages -- E04H 3/24 covers the design and structural elements of temporary stages used in public or similar venues, such as platforms, support frames, decking, and access stairs. It falls under fixed construction of buildings or building groups intended for particular purposes, specifically addressing how stages are built, anchored, and integrated into larger architectural settings.
[E04H 3/26] Revolving stages -- A revolving stage is a rotating platform incorporated into a theatre or similar public building that allows rapid scene changes or varied audience perspectives. It typically consists of a circular deck mounted on a turntable mechanism powered by a motor and supported by a stable frame within the structure.
[E04H 3/28] Shiftable or portable platforms -- E04H 3/28 covers shiftable or portable platforms such as temporary stages, modular walkways and lift-on-lift-off work platforms that can be moved or removed for use in public or specialized building complexes.
[E04H 3/30] Constructional features of auditoriums -- E04H 3/30 pertains to the design and structural elements of auditoriums in public-type buildings, covering features such as seating configurations, acoustic treatments, stage layout, lighting, ventilation and other constructional aspects that influence audience comfort and performance conditions.
[E04H 4/00] Swimming or splash baths or pools -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Devices for producing waves in swimming pools;  Floating swimming pools;  Easy movable and transportable swimming pools, i.e. with inflatable parts or made of shell type elements or of panes;  Safety devices for swimming pools, i.e. floors adjustable in height;  Swimming pool coverings made of rigid elements or of flexible materials; Devices or arrangements for circulating water, i.e. devices for removal of polluted water, cleaning baths or for water treatment;  Devices and accessories peculiar to swimming pools, i.e. coping elements, liner fixing means, step units, lighting means;  Devices and apparatuses specially adapted for cleaning, i.e. hand-held strainers, powered cleaners, suction cleaners, self-propelled cleaners.
[E04H 4/0006] Devices for producing waves in swimming pools  generating current of water in swimming pools  A63B69/125 -- This subclass covers mechanisms that create artificial waves or water currents in swimming pools, such as wave generators, water jets, or circulating systems, used for recreation, training or therapeutic purposes. It is classified under fixed constructions for buildings or like structures for particular purposes.
[E04H 4/0012] Floating swimming pools -- E04H 4/0012 covers floating swimming pools, which are buoyant pool structures designed to rest on water surfaces rather than being fixed to land. These pools may be anchored, modular, or self-contained and are used for recreation, training, or therapeutic purposes in lakes, rivers, or coastal areas.
[E04H 4/0018] Easily movable or transportable swimming pools -- This class covers swimming pools that are designed to be easily moved or transported, such as prefabricated or modular pool structures that can be assembled, disassembled, or relocated without permanent installation. It includes portable pool kits, inflatable or frame-based designs intended for temporary or seasonal use.
[E04H 4/0025] with inflatable parts -- The subclass E04H 4/0025 covers swimming or splash-bath installations that incorporate inflatable components, such as air-filled walls, chambers, or accessories, to form or modify the pool's shape, depth zones or safety barriers. These inflatable parts are employed to create adaptable or temporary pool configurations within fixed construction settings.
[E04H 4/0031] with shell type elements -- E04H 4/0031 refers to swimming or splash pools that incorporate shell-type elements as part of their structure. The classification covers fixed constructions where the pool walls, floor or covering are formed from shell-shaped components, such as pre-cast concrete or fiberglass shells, designed for a specific purpose.
[E04H 4/0037] Mono-shell type -- E04H 4/0037 classifies patents for swimming or splash-bath pools of the mono-shell type, where the basin is formed as a single, continuous shell, often of reinforced concrete or similar material, providing a seamless interior surface. The entry is used for inventions concerning the design, construction, or prefabrication of such single-shell pools.
[E04H 4/0043] mainly made of panels  E04H4/0031  takes precedence -- This subclass relates to swimming or splash baths and pools that are principally built from panels, with the panel components forming the main structural elements of the fixed construction. It is nested under fixed constructions for particular purposes and is subordinate to E04H4/0031, which takes precedence.
[E04H 4/005] characterised by the framework for supporting the panels -- E04H 4/005 covers fixed swimming-pool or splash-bath constructions whose distinctive feature is a supporting framework that holds the panels forming the walls, floor or roof. The subclass applies to buildings or structures designed for water-recreation purposes where the panel-supporting skeleton is a key design element.
[E04H 4/0056] with a framework combined with a water retaining bag -- E04H 4/0056 pertains to swimming or splash-bath structures in which a supporting framework is combined with a flexible water-retaining bag, allowing the pool to be assembled from a rigid skeleton and a portable container that holds the water. This subclass falls under fixed constructions designed for particular purposes.
[E04H 4/0062] characterised by stacked planks or staves -- E04H 4/0062 covers swimming or splash-bath pools whose walls, floor or liner are formed by stacked wooden planks or staves, creating a fixed wooden construction for holding water. This design is used for pools built as permanent, purpose-specific structures.
[E04H 4/0075] made of concrete  E04H4/0012  takes precedence -- The subclass E04H 4/0075 relates to swimming or splash baths and pools that are constructed from concrete. It falls under the broader categories of fixed constructions, buildings, and buildings or like structures for particular purposes, specifying the material used for the pool's structure.
[E04H 4/0081] with walls and floor cast  in situ -- This subclass covers swimming or splash pools whose walls and floor are formed by casting the concrete in situ, creating a monolithic structure that is integral to the building. It applies to fixed-construction pools where the entire enclosure is poured on site rather than assembled from prefabricated components.
[E04H 4/0087] with walls prefabricated and floor cast  in situ -- The code E04H 4/0087 covers swimming or splash-bath pools whose structural walls are supplied as prefabricated sections while the floor is formed by casting concrete in place. It relates to fixed-construction pool designs that combine factory-made wall panels with an on-site poured slab, enabling rapid assembly and customized dimensions.
[E04H 4/0093] with walls and floor prefabricated -- This CPC subclass E04H 4/0093 relates to swimming or splash baths or pools whose walls and floor are manufactured as prefabricated components and then assembled on site. It covers fixed construction installations where the enclosure and base are produced off-site and installed as a unit.
[E04H 4/06] Safety devices -- The CPC subclass E04H 4/06 pertains to safety devices incorporated in swimming or splash baths and pools built as fixed constructions. It covers items such as protective covers, barriers, ladders, steps, anti-entrapment systems and other equipment designed to prevent accidents, injury or drowning in these water facilities.
[E04H 4/065] Floors adjustable in height -- Adjustable-height floors for swimming pools are mechanisms that allow the pool floor to be raised or lowered to change water depth, facilitating entry for users of different ages or abilities and enabling conversion between pool and shallow play area. They are commonly used in residential and public splash pools to improve safety and accessibility.
[E04H 4/08] Coverings consisting of rigid elements -- E04H 4/08 covers rigid-element coverings used in swimming or splash baths or pools, such as tiled decks, concrete slabs, metal or plastic panels that form the pool surface or its surrounding structure. It is a subclass of fixed constructions for buildings or like structures designed for particular purposes.
[E04H 4/082] composed of flexibly or hingedly-connected slat-like elements, which may or may not be wound-up on a fixed axis  E04H4/088  takes precedence -- The CPC subclass E04H 4/082 relates to swimming pools or splash baths whose walls or floors are built from flexible or hinged slat-like elements that may be rolled or folded around a fixed axis.
