This construction enables the pool to be collapsed, adjusted or stored more easily and refines the broader class E04H 4/088.
[E04H 4/084] with lifting mechanism -- The subclass E04H 4/084 defines swimming or splash-bath installations that include a lifting mechanism, such as a device for raising or lowering the pool basin, platform, or water-containing element to adjust depth, facilitate entry, or provide access for maintenance.
[E04H 4/086] Sliding covers  E04H4/084  and  E04H4/088  take precedence -- E04H 4/086 relates to sliding covers used on swimming or splash baths and pools, specifying the design and mounting of movable elements that can be opened or closed to protect the water surface. The classification falls under fixed constructions for buildings or like structures intended for particular purposes, with related groups E04H 4/084 and E04H 4/088 taking precedence.
[E04H 4/088] Covers made in concertina folds -- Covers made in concertina folds are accordion-style protective or concealing panels designed for swimming pools, splash baths or similar water structures. They can be folded compactly when not needed and unfolded to shield the pool surface, helping retain water temperature, prevent debris entry and improve safety. This classification applies to such folding cover systems installed on fixed aquatic constructions.
[E04H 4/10] Coverings of flexible material -- E04H 4/10 covers flexible material coverings used in swimming pools, splash baths and similar water installations. It includes liners, membranes and sheets that form the pool interior surface to retain water and provide a smooth, watertight barrier. The classification applies to flexible sheet, plastic, rubber or fabric liners installed as the primary enclosure of fixed pool structures.
[E04H 4/101] wound-up on a fixed axis -- This CPC subgroup covers swimming pools or splash baths that are formed as a wound or coiled structure around a stationary axis, allowing the pool to be compacted, transported or stored and then expanded for use. It includes designs where the tank or basin is wrapped or rolled on a fixed shaft.
[E04H 4/103] with inflatable chambers -- E04H 4/103 covers swimming or splash pools that incorporate inflatable chambers as part of their structure. The classification falls under fixed constructions for buildings or like structures intended for specific purposes, detailing designs where air-filled elements provide buoyancy, shape or safety.
[E04H 4/105] supported by air pressure under the cover -- E04H 4/105 covers swimming-pool or splash-bath installations whose covers are held up by air pressure, such as inflatable or air-supported lids that maintain a sealed enclosure over the water. The feature is applied to fixed-construction pools to improve safety, temperature control or contamination prevention.
[E04H 4/106] supported by separate buoyant bodies -- The CPC entry E04H 4/106 covers swimming or splash-bath pools that are constructed as floating structures, being supported by separate buoyant bodies rather than fixed foundations. It pertains to pool designs where the buoyancy elements provide the primary support, allowing the pool to be positioned on water or other surfaces without permanent anchoring.
[E04H 4/108] supported by frames -- The CPC entry E04H 4/108 titled supported by frames falls under fixed constructions, building, buildings or like structures for particular purposes, swimming or splash baths or pools. It covers swimming pools that are constructed with supporting frames, such as portable or modular pool structures.
[E04H 4/12] Devices or arrangements for circulating water -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Treatment of water for swimming pools;  Devices for removal of polluted water, i.e. circumferential gutters;  Bottom drains;  Recirculating pumps for swimming pool water, arm-mounted debris traps; floating skimmers and skimmers integrated in the pool wall;  Devices for distributing chemical products in the water of swimming pools and systems for heating the water content of swimming pools.
[E04H 4/1209] Treatment of water for swimming pools  filters  B01D -- E04H 4/1209 covers the treatment and conditioning of water used in swimming pools and splash baths, including filtration, clarification, disinfection, and chemical dosing methods that fall under the broader B01D category of separating solids from liquids. It addresses equipment and processes designed to maintain pool water quality and safety.
[E04H 4/1218] Devices for removal of polluted water; Circumferential gutters -- Devices mounted around the perimeter of swimming pools or splash baths that collect and convey contaminated water away from the basin for treatment or disposal. They typically consist of a gutter or channel that encircles the pool, allowing polluted water to be removed continuously or intermittently, often integrating filtration or drainage mechanisms to maintain water quality.
[E04H 4/1227] Circumferential gutters -- E04H 4/1227 covers circumferential gutters installed around the edge of swimming pools or splash baths to capture overflow water and direct it away from the pool area, often integrated into the pool wall or surrounding deck for drainage and safety.
[E04H 4/1236] Bottom drains -- E04H 4/1236 pertains to bottom drains installed in swimming or splash baths and pools, covering the design and placement of drainage openings in the basin floor to remove water efficiently. These components are integral to pool construction for hygiene and water circulation.
[E04H 4/1245] Recirculating pumps for swimming pool water -- This subclass covers devices that continuously move and filter water in a swimming pool, including pumps integrated in recirculation systems for maintaining water quality and temperature. It falls under fixed constructions, building, buildings or like structures for particular purposes, swimming or splash baths or pools.
[E04H 4/1254] Arm-mounted debris traps or flow diverters -- A device mounted on a pool's structural arm, such as a sidewall or coping, that captures floating debris or redirects water flow to improve circulation and cleanliness in swimming or splash-pool installations. It includes nets, grates, or deflectors fixed to the arm to act as a trap or flow diverter.
[E04H 4/1263] Floating skimmers -- Floating skimmers are devices that stay on the surface of a swimming pool or splash bath, using a porous or basket element to gather floating debris while water circulates. They are typically anchored or self-propelled to move across the pool and feed the collected material to a filtration system.
[E04H 4/1272] Skimmers integrated in the pool wall -- Skimmers that are built directly into the wall of a swimming pool, forming a permanent, fixed component for removing debris and surface water, are classified under E04H 4/1272 for integrated pool-wall skimming devices. This entry falls within the hierarchy of fixed constructions for buildings, specifically swimming or splash baths or pools.
[E04H 4/1281] Devices for distributing chemical products in the water of swimming pools -- This classification covers fixed installations in swimming pools that dispense chemical products such as disinfectants, pH adjusters, or algaecides to treat the water. It includes devices built into the pool structure that automatically inject, dissolve, or circulate these agents to maintain uniform water quality.
[E04H 4/129] Systems for heating the water content of swimming pools  solar collectors using pools  F24S10/17 ,  F24S10/10 ; by solar radiation  F24S20/02 -- E04H 4/129 covers installations that heat the water in swimming pools using solar energy, typically by employing solar collectors in which the pool water itself circulates as the heat-transfer fluid. It relates to building structures for pools and cross-references heating subclasses F24S10/17, F24S10/10 and solar-radiation heating F24S20/02.
[E04H 4/14] Parts, details or accessories not otherwise provided for -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Devices and accessories peculiar to swimming pools, i.e. coping elements; Liner fixing means;  Means for facilitating access, e.g. step units or slides;  Dividing means for swimming pool, e.g. panels, bulkheads;  Lighting means.
[E04H 4/141] Coping elements for swimming pools -- Coping elements for swimming pools are the finishing trims that seal the junction between the pool wall and the surrounding deck, providing waterproofing, structural reinforcement and a clean visual transition. They are used in fixed constructions of buildings dedicated to swimming or splash-bath facilities.
[E04H 4/142] with fixing means for sealing foil -- This subclass covers swimming or splash baths where a waterproof foil or liner is secured by fixing means such as clips, rails or adhesives to achieve a sealed enclosure.
[E04H 4/143] Swimming lane markers with or without wave suppressors -- The classification E04H 4/143 refers to devices installed in swimming pools or splash baths that define individual swimming lanes, guiding swimmers and separating lanes. These lane markers may be floating or fixed and can incorporate wave-suppressing features to reduce surface turbulence and improve performance.
[E04H 4/144] Means for facilitating access, e.g. step units or slides  invalid-lifting devices for swimming pools  A61G7/1005 -- This subcategory addresses devices that facilitate entry into swimming or splash pools, including step units, ladders, slides and lifting mechanisms intended for disabled users. It falls under fixed constructions for particular-purpose buildings, specifically swimming-pool structures.
[E04H 4/145] Dividing means for swimming pools, e.g. panels, bulkheads -- This CPC subclass (E04H 4/145) covers fixed constructions for swimming pools that provide division of the water space, such as panels, bulkheads or other separating structures. It relates to building or like structures designed for specific purposes, specifically swimming or splash baths.
[E04H 4/148] Lighting means -- The CPC subclass E04H 4/148 covers lighting devices installed in swimming pools, splash baths or similar water recreation facilities. It includes fixtures, lamps and illumination systems designed to operate safely in wet environments, provide adequate visibility, and may incorporate waterproof or corrosion-resistant features. This subclass falls under the hierarchy of fixed constructions for buildings intended for specific purposes.
[E04H 4/16] specially adapted for cleaning -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Devices and apparatuses specially adapted for cleaning the swimming pool, e.g. hand-held strainers, powered cleaners, suction cleaners, self-propelled cleaners, cleaners wherein the propulsion results from an intermittent interruption of the waterflow through the cleaner;  Connection between the cleaners and the water circulation system;  Cleaning whips;  Pool nozzles.
[E04H 4/1609] Hand-held strainers, non-powered brushes or scrubbers
[E04H 4/1618] Hand-held powered cleaners -- The subclass E04H 4/1618 covers hand-held powered cleaners intended for the interior of swimming pools, splash baths or similar water installations. It includes portable electric or battery-driven vacuum units, brush tools and other manually operated but motorised devices used to remove debris, sediment or algae from pool surfaces.
[E04H 4/1627] Powered gutter cleaners -- Powered gutter cleaners are automated devices designed to remove water, debris and contaminants from the gutters of swimming pools or splash baths, using mechanical or electrical energy to maintain clean drainage pathways and prevent blockages in these specialized pool structures.
[E04H 4/1636] Suction cleaners -- Suction cleaners classified under E04H 4/1636 are devices that remove debris from the water of swimming or splash pools by creating a flow that draws contaminants into a filtration system. They are typically installed in or near the pool structure and may be integrated with the pool's circulation or drainage network to maintain water cleanliness. The classification covers all fixed or portable suction cleaning apparatuses used specifically for pool environments.
[E04H 4/1645] Connections to the pool water circulation system -- E04H 4/1645 covers the means of linking a swimming-pool's water circulation system, including inlet and outlet pipes, pumps, filters, valves and related fittings that connect the pool basin to the circulation loop.
[E04H 4/1654] Self-propelled cleaners -- Self-propelled cleaners for swimming pools and splash baths are automated devices that move across the water or pool surfaces using built-in propulsion to collect debris and scrub walls or floors. The classification includes robotic vacuums, scrubbing bots and other autonomous cleaning systems designed for permanent or temporary pool installations.
[E04H 4/1663] the propulsion resulting from an intermittent interruption of the waterflow through the cleaner -- E04H 4/1663 is a Cooperative Patent Classification entry under Fixed Constructions → Building → Buildings or like structures for particular purposes → Swimming or splash baths or pools. It pertains to propulsion mechanisms that create thrust by intermittently interrupting the water flow through a pool cleaner.
[E04H 4/1672] Connections to the pool water circulation system -- The subclass E04H 4/1672 covers the components and means that link a swimming-pool or splash-bath to its water-circulation system, including inlet and outlet fittings, piping, valves, manifolds, pumps, and control devices that manage water flow, filtration, heating or aeration within the pool structure.
[E04H 4/1681] Cleaning whips -- The CPC subclass E04H 4/1681 relates to cleaning whips, which are flexible brush-type tools used to scrub and remove debris from the surfaces of swimming pools, splash baths or similar water-filled constructions. It covers the design, configuration and operation of such whips for maintaining pool hygiene and water quality.
[E04H 4/169] Pool nozzles -- The E04H 4/169 subclass covers the design and implementation of nozzles, jets and related dispensing devices used in swimming, splash-bath or other recreational pools. It includes components that direct water flow for propulsion, aeration, spray, cleaning or heating functions within the pool system.
[E04H 5/00] Buildings or groups of buildings for industrial or agricultural purposes -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Buildings for industrial purposes, e.g. for power-plants, factories, data processing centers;  Buildings forming pars of cooling plants;  Transformer houses;  Substations or switchgear houses;  Pits for inspection or services and coverings therefore;  Buildings for agricultural purposes;  Cooling towers.
[E04H 5/02] Buildings or groups of buildings for industrial purposes, e.g. for power-plants or factories -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Buildings forming part of cooling plants -> E04H5/10
[E04H 5/04] Transformer houses -- Transformer houses are purpose-built structures that enclose electrical transformers and related equipment, providing protection from weather, vandalism and accidental contact while allowing safe operation and maintenance. They are typically situated near power distribution networks or industrial facilities and designed to accommodate cooling, ventilation and access requirements specific to high-voltage equipment.
[E04H 5/06] Pits or building structures for inspection or services -- E04H 5/06 relates to pits and other built-in structures that give access for inspection, maintenance or servicing of utilities, equipment or infrastructure in industrial or agricultural settings. It includes manholes, access chambers, inspection shafts and service tunnels incorporated in or adjacent to larger buildings.
[E04H 5/08] Buildings or groups of buildings for agricultural purposes -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Silos -> E04H7/22
[E04H 5/10] Buildings forming part of cooling plants -- E04H 5/10 covers buildings that are integral components of cooling plants, such as structures housing cooling towers, condensers, heat exchangers or other equipment used for industrial or agricultural temperature control. It includes any permanent or semi-permanent constructions that form part of the plant's infrastructure.
[E04H 5/12] Cooling towers -- Cooling towers are fixed building structures designed to dissipate excess heat from industrial or agricultural processes by exposing water to air flow, facilitating heat transfer to the atmosphere. They are commonly employed in power generation, manufacturing, and large-scale farming to regulate temperature and conserve water.
[E04H 6/00] Buildings for parking cars, rolling-stock, aircraft, vessels or like vehicles, e.g. garages -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Garages for vehicles on two wheels;  Small garages, for one or two cars, like e.g. carports, small garages of the wheeled, hinged, foldable, telescopic, swinging or otherwise movable type, small garages using tiltable floors or ramps;  Garages for many vehicles with mechanical means for shifting or lifting vehicles or without mechanical means for shifting or lifting vehicles, like e.g. with means for transport in vertical direction only or independently in vertical and horizontal directions, either the cars rolling freely from the transfer means or using different transfer means or by the use of movable platforms for horizontal transport or characterised by the use of turntables, rotary elevators or the like on which the cars are not permanently parked;  Devices or arrangements peculiar to garages, not covered elsewhere, e.g. securing devices, safety devices, automatically operated car-parks, devices for collecting oil or further fluids under parked cars.  **Glossary:** - rolling-stock: railway vehicles, including locomotives, freight carriages and passenger coaches.
[E04H 6/005] Garages for vehicles on two wheels -- A garage designed specifically for storing two-wheel vehicles such as motorcycles, scooters or bicycles, typically providing covered protection, secure access and space optimized for narrow, elongated storage units. It falls under the building category for vehicle parking structures within the Cooperative Patent Classification system.
[E04H 6/02] Small garages, e.g. for one or two cars -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Attached to, or supported by, the vehicle -> B62D
[E04H 6/025] in the form of an overhead canopy, e.g. carports -- The subclass E04H 6/025 designates overhead canopies such as carports that are integrated into fixed building structures for parking vehicles, including cars, rolling-stock, aircraft or vessels. It falls under the hierarchy of buildings or like structures intended for particular parking purposes.
[E04H 6/04] wheeled, hinged, foldable, telescopic, swinging or otherwise movable -- The CPC subclass E04H 6/04 covers movable or adjustable elements of fixed-construction parking facilities, such as wheeled, hinged, foldable, telescopic, swinging or otherwise shiftable parts used in garages or similar structures for cars, trains, aircraft or vessels. It addresses designs that allow sections of the building to be repositioned, collapsed or extended to facilitate vehicle storage or access.
[E04H 6/06] with means for shifting or lifting vehicles -- E04H 6/06 covers buildings designed for parking cars, trains, aircraft or vessels that include mechanisms to shift or lift the vehicles within the structure. It falls under the fixed constructions group for specialized parking facilities such as garages equipped with lifts or automated storage systems.
[E04H 6/065] using tiltable floors or ramps  in garages for many vehicles  E04H6/26
[E04H 6/08] Garages for many vehicles -- The code E04H 6/08 relates to multi-vehicle garages, structures designed to house several cars, trucks, railway vehicles, aircraft or ships. It covers parking facilities such as large garages, hangars and similar enclosures built specifically for storing multiple vehicles in one building.
[E04H 6/10] without mechanical means for shifting or lifting vehicles, e.g. with helically-arranged fixed ramps, with movable ramps -- It covers structures designed for parking vehicles where the vehicles are moved by gravity or simple ramps rather than lifts or conveyors, including fixed helical ramps or movable ramps that guide cars, trains, aircraft or boats into storage positions.
[E04H 6/12] with mechanical means for shifting or lifting vehicles -- This subclass covers parking structures that incorporate mechanical devices for moving or raising vehicles, such as car lifts, hydraulic elevators, conveyors, or automated parking systems. The focus is on the means that shift or lift cars, trains, aircraft or vessels within the building to optimise space or facilitate loading.
[E04H 6/14] with endless conveyor chains having load-carrying parts moving vertically, e.g. paternoster lifts -- The CPC subclass E04H 6/14 covers structures such as garages or other vehicle-parking buildings that incorporate endless conveyor chains with load-carrying elements moving vertically, for example paternoster-type lifts that transport cars or other items between levels.
[E04H 6/16] Garages shaped as a wheel or drum rotatable about a horizontal axis -- A garage that is formed as a wheel-or-drum structure capable of rotating about a horizontal axis, allowing vehicles such as cars, rolling-stock, aircraft or vessels to be parked and retrieved as the entire enclosure turns. This design falls under the fixed-construction category for buildings intended for vehicle parking.
[E04H 6/18] with means for transport in vertical direction only or independently in vertical and horizontal directions -- This subclass covers parking structures that incorporate internal systems for moving vehicles either only in a vertical direction, such as car elevators, or independently in both vertical and horizontal directions, like combined lift-and-turn mechanisms, allowing multi-level storage without driver operation.
[E04H 6/181] the cars rolling freely from the transfer means  E04H6/282  takes precedence -- A building or structure designed specifically for parking vehicles, such as a garage, where cars can roll out directly from a transfer system without additional handling. The subclass E04H 6/181 covers the free-rolling of cars from such transfer means, but it is overridden by the more specific subclass E04H 6/282. This falls under the broader group of fixed constructions for particular purposes.
[E04H 6/182] using car-gripping transfer means  E04H6/285  takes precedence
[E04H 6/183] without transverse movement of the car after leaving the transfer means -- E04H 6/183 relates to parking structures where a vehicle leaves a transfer system such as a lift, conveyor or ramp and proceeds directly forward without any sideways movement. The design keeps the car on a straight path to its parking position, simplifying layout and reducing mechanical complexity.
[E04H 6/185] using comb-type transfer means  E04H6/287  takes precedence
[E04H 6/186] without transverse movement of the car after leaving the transfer means -- The CPC class E04H 6/186 covers fixed constructions such as garages or other parking structures where a vehicle is positioned and remains stationary in the lateral direction after it leaves the transfer system. It applies to buildings designed for parking cars, rolling-stock, aircraft, vessels or similar vehicles without transverse movement of the vehicle.
[E04H 6/187] using vertical parking loops -- E04H 6/187 covers vertical parking loops, a type of building structure designed for parking cars, rolling-stock, aircraft, vessels or similar vehicles, falling under the fixed constructions category for buildings or like structures for particular purposes such as garages.
[E04H 6/188] using only vertical transport means -- E04H 6/188 classifies buildings intended for vehicle parking-such as garages, aircraft hangars, train depots or ship bays-where access and movement are provided solely by vertical transport means like elevators or lifts, with no ramps or horizontal driveways. This subclass covers the design and construction features needed for vertical-only vehicle handling.
[E04H 6/20] characterised by use of conveyor chains or rotatable rollers for horizontal transport -- E04H 6/20 classifies structures built for parking cars, rolling-stock, aircraft, vessels or similar vehicles that incorporate conveyor chains or rotatable rollers to transport the vehicles horizontally within the building.
[E04H 6/22] characterised by use of movable platforms for horizontal transport -- E04H 6/22 covers parking structures or other vehicle-storage buildings that employ movable horizontal platforms to convey cars, trains, aircraft or vessels inside the facility. The platforms may be conveyor belts, rollers or sliding pallets that move the vehicle from the entry point to a storage position without driver steering. This class captures automated or robotic parking garages that rely on such horizontal transport mechanisms.
[E04H 6/225] without transverse movement of the parking palette after leaving the transfer means -- This CPC subclass deals with fixed building constructions that house vehicle-parking systems where the pallet carrying a car moves only longitudinally and does not shift sideways after being released from the transfer mechanism. It applies to garages and similar parking structures.
[E04H 6/24] characterised by use of dollies for horizontal transport -- E04H 6/24 refers to buildings designed for parking cars, rolling-stock, aircraft, vessels or similar vehicles, such as garages, where the structure's operation is characterised by the use of dollies or rollers to transport the vehicles horizontally within the building. This subclass addresses the specific means of moving parked items rather than the overall architecture of the storage facility.
[E04H 6/245] without transverse movement of the wheeled parking platform after leaving the transfer means -- E04H 6/245 covers parking systems for vehicles where the wheeled loading platform, after being released from a transfer device such as a conveyor or lift, proceeds straight ahead without any side-to-side (transverse) movement. The design ensures the platform follows a fixed linear path, simplifying guidance and alignment in car-parking garages or similar structures.
[E04H 6/26] characterised by use of tiltable floors or floor sections -- This subgroup covers structures such as garages or parking facilities in which the floor or individual floor sections can be tilted or rotated to move vehicles, facilitating storage of cars, rolling-stock, aircraft or vessels within a building designed for that purpose.
[E04H 6/28] characterised by use of turntables or rotary rings for horizontal transport -- E04H 6/28 relates to parking structures such as garages that include turntables or rotary rings to move vehicles horizontally within the building, enabling storage or access without steering.
[E04H 6/282] turntables, rotary elevators or the like on which the cars are not permanently parked -- E04H 6/282 covers rotating platforms such as turntables and rotary elevators used in parking structures where vehicles are only temporarily positioned. These devices enable vehicles to be turned or lifted for access or storage without being permanently stored on the platform. The classification applies to facilities for cars, trains, aircraft or vessels where the turning or lifting mechanism is integral to the building.
[E04H 6/285] using car-gripping transfer means -- This subclass covers methods that use car-gripping transfer mechanisms, such as lifts, conveyors or clamps, to move vehicles within fixed parking structures. It includes the automation of placing, retrieving or relocating cars in garages, rolling-stock depots, aircraft or vessel parking facilities.
[E04H 6/287] using comb-type transfer means -- The classification E04H 6/287 relates to parking structures for cars, trains, aircraft or ships that employ comb-type transfer means, i.e., mechanisms with parallel teeth that move vehicles between levels or positions. It covers designs where such comb devices shift, lift or guide vehicles within garages or similar facilities.
[E04H 6/30] with means for transport in horizontal direction only -- This subclass covers parking structures, such as garages, that include equipment to move vehicles only in a horizontal direction, for example conveyor belts, rollers or car-shuttle tracks, without vertical lifting components. The classification is used for designs where cars, trains or aircraft are positioned or retrieved by horizontal transport means inside the building.
[E04H 6/302] the cars rolling freely from the transfer means -- The entry covers parking structures that use a transfer system such as a ramp, conveyor or roller to move vehicles into a storage position and then allow the cars to roll out freely under their own weight onto the floor. It relates to designs of garages or similar buildings where the release of the vehicle from the transfer means is unassisted.
[E04H 6/305] using car-gripping transfer means -- The classification E04H 6/305 covers mechanisms that grip a car and transfer it within a parking facility, such as lifts, elevators or automated platforms that securely hold a vehicle's chassis or wheels for vertical or horizontal movement. It includes any system where the vehicle is physically clasped or locked by a device to facilitate storage, retrieval or relocation inside garages or similar structures.
[E04H 6/307] using comb-type transfer means -- This CPC subclass covers structures designed for parking cars, rolling-stock, aircraft, vessels or similar vehicles, such as garages, that employ comb-type transfer mechanisms to move the vehicles within the building. It relates to the use of these specialized conveyor or elevator systems to shift items between levels or positions in the parking facility.
[E04H 6/32] characterised by use of conveyor chains or rotatable rollers -- The entry covers parking structures for cars, trains, aircraft, ships or similar vehicles that employ conveyor chains or rotatable rollers to move the vehicles into storage positions, such as automated garages, rolling-stock depots, aircraft hangars or vessel bays.
[E04H 6/34] characterised by use of movable platforms -- E04H 6/34 categorises garages and similar parking structures for cars, rolling-stock, aircraft or vessels that are distinguished by the inclusion of movable platforms used to position or store the vehicles inside the building.
[E04H 6/36] characterised by use of freely-movable dollies -- A building or structure designed primarily for parking cars, rolling-stock, aircraft, vessels or similar vehicles-such as a garage-where the layout includes freely-movable dollies to shift the vehicles inside the facility. It belongs to the fixed-construction group for buildings intended for particular purposes.
[E04H 6/38] characterised by use of tiltable floors or floor sections -- This CPC subclass designates garage-type buildings for parking cars, trains, aircraft or vessels that incorporate floors or floor sections capable of tilting to move or reposition vehicles within the structure. The classification applies to fixed constructions where the tiltable floor mechanism is an inherent feature of the building.
[E04H 6/40] characterised by use of turntables or rotary rings -- The E04H 6/40 class covers structures built for parking vehicles such as cars, trains, aircraft or boats that incorporate turntables or rotary rings to move the vehicles into or within the space. This feature enables efficient positioning and storage in garages, hangars or similar facilities designed for particular transport modes.
[E04H 6/42] Devices or arrangements peculiar to garages, not covered elsewhere, e.g. securing devices, safety devices -- E04H 6/42 relates to devices and arrangements that are specific to garages and not covered elsewhere, including securing mechanisms, safety devices and other specialized features used for parking cars, rolling-stock, aircraft, vessels or similar vehicles.
[E04H 6/422] Automatically operated car-parks -- E04H 6/422 covers building structures that incorporate automated parking systems, such as mechanically or robotically controlled garages for cars, rolling-stock, aircraft or vessels. The classification refers to designs where vehicles are stored, retrieved or moved without manual handling, often using lifts, conveyors, shuttles or computer-controlled platforms integrated into the building's architecture.
[E04H 6/424] Positioning devices -- E04H 6/424 covers devices that guide or align vehicles within parking structures such as garages, aircraft hangars, or vessel storage bays. The classification includes mechanical, electronic or automated systems that position cars, rolling-stock, aircraft or similar vehicles precisely during parking or storage to optimise space usage and safety.
[E04H 6/426] Parking guides -- E04H 6/426 pertains to parking guides, which are devices, markings or systems that help drivers correctly position vehicles within specialized parking structures such as garages, aircraft hangars, train stations or vessel bays. The classification falls under fixed constructions, building, buildings or like structures for particular purposes, specifically buildings for parking cars, rolling-stock, aircraft, vessels or similar vehicles.
[E04H 6/428] Devices for collecting oil or other fluids under parked cars  servicing, maintaining, repairing or refitting of vehicles  B60S5/00
[E04H 6/44] for storing aircraft -- E04H 6/44 designates structures intended specifically for storing aircraft, such as hangars and related aviation storage facilities. It falls under Fixed Constructions, covering buildings or similar structures built for particular purposes, including parking and sheltering vehicles, with this subclass focused on aircraft-storage functions.
[E04H 7/00] Construction or assembling of bulk storage containers employing civil engineering techniques -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Containers for fluids or gases mainly made of metal, with vertical axis, roof constructions therefore, with horizontal axis or ball shaped or mainly made of reinforced or prestressed concrete;  Containers for fluent solids, e.g. silos, bunkers, supports therefore, e.g. with or without perforated walls mainly made of reinforced concrete, of metal or of wood.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Tower aspects -> E04H12/00 - Storage aspects, e.g. floating roofs, sealing means, filling or discharging means -> B65D , B65G , F17B , F17C - Foundations -> E02D27/38
[E04H 7/02] Containers for fluids or gases -- E04H 7/02 covers the construction or assembly of fixed, large-scale containers designed to store fluids or gases, such as tanks, silos or reservoirs, using civil-engineering methods. It falls within the hierarchy of fixed constructions, building structures, and buildings or like structures for particular purposes, focusing on bulk storage solutions.
[E04H 7/04] mainly of metal -- E04H 7/04 covers bulk storage containers such as silos, tanks or similar structures that are built mainly of metal using civil-engineering construction techniques and are intended for particular purposes.
[E04H 7/06] with vertical axis -- This CPC subclass covers the design and construction of bulk storage structures such as silos, tanks, or grain bins where the container is oriented with its main axis vertical. It falls under fixed constructions in buildings or like structures for particular purposes, focusing on civil-engineering techniques for erecting these vertical-axis storage units.
[E04H 7/065] roof constructions -- E04H 7/065 covers the design, erection and related methods for roofs on bulk storage containers such as silos, tanks and similar structures. It addresses roof types, support systems, sealing and integration with civil-engineering construction techniques. The entry is used for patents concerning roof construction in specialized storage buildings.
[E04H 7/14] ball-shaped -- The code E04H 7/14 covers the construction or assembly of spherical bulk-storage containers such as ball-shaped silos, tanks or reservoirs, applied in civil-engineering projects where a round shape provides structural or functional advantages. It falls under fixed constructions for specialized building purposes.
[E04H 7/16] with horizontal axis -- E04H 7/16 classifies the construction or assembly of large bulk-storage containers, such as silos or tanks, that are built using civil-engineering techniques and are oriented with a horizontal axis. It falls under fixed constructions for buildings or like structures designed for specific purposes.
[E04H 7/18] mainly of concrete, e.g. reinforced concrete, or other stone-like material -- This subclass covers the construction or assembly of bulk storage containers-such as silos, tanks, or warehouses-where the primary material is concrete, reinforced concrete, or other stone-like substances, using civil-engineering methods. It falls under fixed constructions for buildings or like structures intended for specific purposes.
[E04H 7/20] Prestressed constructions -- E04H 7/20 covers the design and erection of bulk storage structures that employ prestressing techniques, where steel tendons or cables are tensioned before or after the concrete is placed to improve strength, reduce cracking and allow thinner walls. It includes methods for tanks, silos and similar civil-engineered containers.
[E04H 7/22] Containers for fluent solids, e.g. silos, bunkers -- E04H 7/22 covers the design and construction of large storage vessels for flowable solid materials such as grain silos, sand bunkers, or similar bulk containers. The classification includes civil-engineering methods used to build or assemble these structures for specific storage purposes.
[E04H 7/24] Constructions, with or without perforated walls, depending on the use of specified materials -- This subgroup deals with bulk storage structures-silos, tanks or similar containers-where the decision to use solid or perforated walls is based on the material's requirements, such as ventilation, pressure relief or filtration. The design may include either type of wall, selected to suit the intended use of the stored contents.
[E04H 7/26] mainly of concrete, e.g. reinforced concrete or other stone-like materials -- This class relates to fixed building structures designed for specific uses, namely bulk-storage containers such as silos, tanks or bins that are constructed or assembled with civil-engineering techniques using primarily concrete, reinforced concrete or other stone-like materials.
[E04H 7/28] composed of special building elements -- E04H 7/28 denotes constructions where the building is made from special prefabricated or custom elements, such as modular panels, frames or containers, that are assembled using civil engineering techniques for large storage facilities. The class includes both standardised and uniquely designed components that form the structural system of the building.
[E04H 7/30] mainly of metal -- E04H 7/30 covers the construction or assembly of bulk storage containers such as silos, tanks or similar structures that are primarily made of metal, employing civil-engineering techniques for foundations, anchoring or supporting the metal vessel.
[E04H 7/32] mainly of wood -- E04H 7/32 covers the construction or assembly of large storage containers such as silos, bins or tanks that are built using civil-engineering methods and are primarily made of wood. The class falls under fixed constructions for buildings or similar structures intended for particular storage purposes.
[E04H 9/00] Buildings, groups of buildings or shelters adapted to withstand or provide protection against abnormal external influences, e.g. war-like action, earthquake or extreme climate -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Buildings, or groups of buildings, or shelters adapted to withstand or provide protection against abnormal external influences, e.g. war-like action, earthquake, extreme climate.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Foundation aspects -> E02D
[E04H 9/02] withstanding earthquake or sinking of ground -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Earthquake withstanding structures, having e.g. steel columns and beams or concrete columns;  Earthquake withstanding shelters and protection cells arranged inside of buildings;  Preventing constructional measures against earthquake damage in existing buildings;  Bearing, supporting or connecting construction for withstanding earthquake, damping devices having e.g. laminated structures of alternating elastomeric and rigid layers or structures comprising rolling elements like balls, pins.
[E04H 9/021] Bearing, supporting or connecting constructions specially adapted for such buildings -- The subclass E04H 9/021 covers bearings, supports and connection elements that are specially designed for buildings, groups of buildings or shelters built to endure abnormal external influences such as war, earthquakes or extreme climates. It includes specialized foundations, shock absorbers, bracing systems and joint devices that enhance the durability and protective performance of such fortified structures.
[E04H 9/0215] involving active or passive dynamic mass damping systems -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Protection of buildings by reducing the movement of buildings, e.g. caused by earthquakes, using mass-spring system, or systems where the mass is actively moved in opposite phase to the building movement, or tanks with liquid mass, with or without baffles for impeding liquid movement.
[E04H 9/022] and comprising laminated structures of alternating elastomeric and rigid layers -- This subclass covers fixed constructions such as buildings or shelters designed to withstand abnormal external influences like warfare, earthquakes or extreme weather, and that incorporate laminated assemblies of alternating elastomeric and rigid layers.
[E04H 9/023] and comprising rolling elements, e.g. balls, pins -- A subclass of protective buildings and shelters that incorporates rolling elements such as balls or pins into their structure, for example to allow movement, absorb shock or distribute loads in constructions designed to resist war-like action, earthquakes or extreme climate conditions.
[E04H 9/0235] Anti-seismic devices with hydraulic or pneumatic damping -- The entry E04H 9/0235 classifies anti-seismic devices that employ hydraulic or pneumatic damping to protect buildings and shelters from abnormal external influences such as earthquakes, war-like actions or extreme climate. These systems are designed to absorb and dissipate seismic energy, enhancing structural resilience.
[E04H 9/0237] Structural braces with damping devices -- Structural braces equipped with damping devices are elements that increase a building's resistance to dynamic loads such as seismic activity, explosions, or severe weather. The damping mechanisms absorb and dissipate energy, reducing vibrations and deformation in the structure, thereby enhancing safety and durability.
[E04H 9/024] Structures with steel columns and beams -- E04H 9/024 covers fixed constructions that employ steel columns and beams as the primary load-bearing elements, used in buildings or shelters specifically designed to endure war-like actions, earthquakes, or extreme climatic conditions.
[E04H 9/025] Structures with concrete columns -- E04H 9/025 refers to fixed constructions-buildings, groups of buildings or shelters-where concrete columns serve as the main structural elements, providing strength and stability for structures intended to endure abnormal external influences such as warfare, earthquakes or extreme climate conditions.
[E04H 9/027] Preventive constructional measures against earthquake damage in existing buildings -- E04H 9/027 covers methods and structural additions applied to existing buildings to reduce the effects of seismic activity. It includes retrofitting techniques such as reinforcing walls, installing base isolators, adding shear walls, or other measures that improve a building's ability to withstand earthquakes. This entry falls under the fixed-construction category for structures designed to protect against abnormal external forces.
[E04H 9/028] Earthquake withstanding shelters -- E04H 9/028 classifies shelters and related structures that are specifically designed to resist seismic forces, maintaining integrity and safety during earthquakes. It falls under Fixed Constructions for buildings or groups of buildings adapted to abnormal external influences such as war-like action, extreme climate, or seismic events.
[E04H 9/029] arranged inside of buildings  E04H9/06  takes precedence -- E04H 9/029 covers interior arrangements in buildings that are intended to protect occupants or equipment from abnormal external influences such as war-like action, earthquakes or extreme climate. It is part of the fixed-construction group for shelters adapted for particular purposes and is subordinate to the broader class E04H 9/06.
[E04H 9/04] against air-raid or other war-like actions -- This subclass concerns buildings or shelters specially constructed to resist or protect against air-raid and other war-like actions, including structural reinforcement, blast-resistant features and protective systems that limit damage from military attacks.
[E04H 9/06] Structures arranged in or forming part of buildings -- E04H 9/06 relates to structures that are arranged in or form part of a building, including internal frameworks, partitions, walls, floors or other supporting elements that define the interior or exterior layout of the construction.
[E04H 9/08] Structures arranged underneath buildings, e.g. air-raid shelters -- The code E04H 9/08 refers to auxiliary structures installed beneath a building that serve as protective shelters, such as air-raid shelters, and are intended to shield occupants from abnormal external influences like warfare, earthquakes or extreme weather conditions.
[E04H 9/10] Independent shelters -- Independent shelters are standalone structures intended to protect occupants from abnormal external influences such as warfare, earthquakes or extreme weather. They fall under the fixed-construction category for buildings or similar structures designed for particular protective purposes.
[E04H 9/12] entirely underneath the level of the ground, e.g. air-raid galleries -- E04H 9/12 covers building structures that are fully below ground level, such as air-raid galleries, designed to protect occupants from abnormal external influences like warfare, earthquakes or extreme climate.
[E04H 9/14] against other dangerous influences, e.g. tornadoes, floods -- The CPC entry E04H 9/14 covers buildings, groups of buildings or shelters specifically engineered to resist hazardous external forces such as tornadoes, floods, war-like actions, earthquakes or extreme climate conditions, providing protection and continued functionality under those abnormal influences.
[E04H 9/145] Floods  protecting building openings  E06B9/00
[E04H 9/16] against adverse conditions, e.g. extreme climate, pests -- E04H 9/16 concerns fixed constructions-buildings, groups of buildings or shelters-engineered to resist abnormal external influences such as extreme climate, seismic events, hostile actions or pest attacks, ensuring protection and continued use under adverse conditions.
[E05] LOCKS -- In this class, the following terms are used with the meanings indicated : "Wing" is a general term for swingable, slidable, or otherwise movable doors or windows. This term also includes other movable structures such as drawers, lids of chests, car boots, or car bonnets, to which the operating, mounting, latching, or locking means covered by this class may be applied; "Frame" means any member to which a wing may be held by a fastening device. It does not include a framework forming part of the wing, but it may be another wing; "Lock" means primarily a device for releasing or securing any member, which requires a key or a permutation mechanism for release. In groups E05B1/00  -  E05B9/00 , E05B13/00  -  E05B17/00 , E05B39/00  -  E05B47/00 , E05B51/00 , E05B53/00 , E05B63/00 and E05B65/00 however, the term "lock" may include other fastening devices; "Bolt" means a sliding, pivoted, or otherwise movable member such as is normally carried by a door to hold it shut by engagement with a keeper on the frame. It may be operated by hand directly or through mechanism or by a key; it may be a latch (see (5) below); "Latch" means a bolt arranged to be moved to the releasing position against the force of a spring, or some other returning force, when a wing meets the frame on closing, so that it does not have to be operated by hand to secure the wing, but only to open it; "Hasp" means a member hinged to the frame or wing so that it can be moved towards the face of the wing or frame and secured thereto, e.g. by a turn-button, by a padlock and staple.
[E05B] LOCKS -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Locks for swingable, slidable, or otherwise movable wings, e.g. doors or windows, for restricting access, which are usually, but not necessarily always, unlocked by means of a key or equivalent means, e.g. combination means, electrical means, etc. It also covers handles or knobs for wings, including handles for wings which do not operate the locking mechanism. It additionally covers latching or locking means for other movable structures such as drawers, lids of chests, vehicle doors, vehicle lids for luggage or engine compartments, to which the locking means covered by this subclass may be applied. It further covers devices for locking clothing, locks specially adapted for bicycles, locks for locking portable objects against unauthorised removal and handcuffs.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Buckles; Similar fasteners for interconnecting straps or the like -> A44B11/00 - Devices for fastening or securing constructional elements or machine parts together -> F16B - Electronic locks -> G07C9/00  **Glossary:** - Bolt: means a sliding, pivoted, or otherwise movable member such as is normally carried by a door to hold it shut by engagement with a keeper on the frame. It may be operated by hand directly or through mechanism or by a key; it may be a latch (see below); - Frame: means any member to which a wing may be held by a fastening device. It does not include a framework forming part of the wing, but it may be another wing. - Hasp: means a member hinged to the frame or wing so that it can be moved towards the face of the wing or frame and secured thereto, e.g. by a turn-button, by a padlock and staple. - Latch: means a bolt arranged to be moved to the releasing position against the force of a spring, or some other returning force, when a wing meets the frame on closing, so that it does not have to be operated by hand to secure the wing, but only to open it. - Lock: means primarily a device for releasing or securing any member, which requires a key or a permutation mechanism for release. In groups E05B1/00 - E05B9/00 , E05B13/00 - E05B17/00 , E05B39/00 - E05B47/00 , E05B51/00 , E05B53/00 , E05B63/00 and E05B65/00 however, the term "lock" may include other fastening devices. - Wing: is a general term for swingable, slidable, or otherwise movable doors or windows. This term also includes other movable structures such as drawers, lids of chests, car boots, or car bonnets, to which the operating, mounting, latching, or locking means covered by this class may be applied.
[E05B 1/00] Parts of locks or the like mountable on or in wings -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Knobs, handles or push-buttons for wings. The handles can be movable or non-movable  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Handles completely let into the surface of the wing -> E05B5/00 - Handles pivoted about an axis parallel to the wing -> E05B7/00
[E05B 1/0007] Knobs  E05B1/0015 ,  E05B1/0053 ,  E05B1/0061 ,  E05B1/0069  take precedence -- The code E05B 1/0007 covers knobs that are part of a lock's fixed construction, specifically the components mounted on or within the lock wings that a user turns to operate the mechanism. It includes various designs of rotating or push-type knobs used to engage or release locking elements.
[E05B 1/0015] Knobs or handles which do not operate the bolt or lock, e.g. non-movable; Mounting thereof -- E05B 1/0015 relates to knobs or handles that are attached to a lock but do not actuate the bolt, such as fixed or decorative handles, and includes the methods for mounting these non-movable components on the lock body.
[E05B 1/003] Handles pivoted about an axis perpendicular to the wing  E05B3/00 ,  E05B5/00 ,  E05B13/106  take precedence
[E05B 1/0038] Sliding handles, e.g. push buttons  E05B13/105  takes precedence -- This CPC subclass (E05B 1/0038) lies within fixed constructions-locks-parts of locks or the like mountable on or in wings and covers sliding handles such as push-buttons used to operate a lock. The related subclass E05B13/105, which deals with actuating elements, takes precedence over this entry.
[E05B 1/0046] sliding parallel to the plane of the wing -- The subclass covers lock components that move by sliding in a direction parallel to the plane of a wing or wing-like element, where the sliding part is mounted on or within the wing and functions to operate or secure the locking mechanism.
[E05B 1/0053] Handles or handle attachments facilitating operation, e.g. by children or burdened persons  foot-operation  E05B53/001 -- This subgroup covers lock-handle accessories that make operation easier, such as enlarged grips, ergonomic shapes, child-proof or assistive devices, and mechanisms that permit foot-actuated opening. The elements are mounted on or in the lock wings and are intended to aid users who have difficulty using a standard handle.
[E05B 1/0061] Knobs or handles with protective cover, buffer or shock absorber -- E05B 1/0061 covers knobs or handles for locks that incorporate a protective cover, buffer or shock absorber to shield the operating element from damage or tampering. These components are designed to be mounted on or within lock wings as part of the lock assembly.
[E05B 1/0069] Sanitary doorknobs or handles, e.g. comprising a disinfectant -- A sanitary doorknob or handle is a lock component designed to limit microbial growth, often incorporating antimicrobial materials or a built-in disinfectant supply. These fittings are mounted on the door's wing or backing plate and serve the same functional purpose as conventional handles while providing hygienic benefits in public or clinical settings.
[E05B 1/0084] Handles or knobs with displays, signs, labels pictures, or the like  displays on locks  E05B17/226 ; displays on keys  E05B19/0088 -- The entry E05B 1/0084 covers handles or knobs on locks that incorporate displays, signs, labels, pictures or similar visual elements, as well as analogous display features on keys. It falls under the Fixed Constructions - Locks hierarchy, addressing parts of locks or related components that are mountable on or in wings.
[E05B 1/0092] Moving otherwise than only rectilinearly or only rotatively  E05B5/003  takes precedence -- The code E05B 1/0092 refers to parts of a lock mounted on or in the wings that move by a motion other than purely straight-line or purely rotary movement, such as camming or oscillating actions. These elements fall under Fixed Constructions > Locks > Parts of locks or the like mountable on or in wings, and the subclass E05B5/003 takes precedence over this entry.
[E05B 1/04] with inner rigid member and outer cover or covers -- E05B 1/04 covers lock components that consist of an inner rigid element surrounded by one or more outer covers, designed to be mounted on or within the wings of a lock assembly. These parts are used in fixed constructions for securing doors, windows or other openings.
[E05B 1/06] of sheet material -- The subclass E05B 1/06 covers components of locks that are made from sheet material, such as metal plates, covers, or shields that are mounted on or within the wings of a lock. These parts serve structural or protective functions and are typically fabricated from thin sheet metal rather than solid blocks.
[E05B 11/00] Devices preventing keys from being removed from the lock -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Using a pair of interlocked keys -> E05B35/086
[E05B 11/005] by key modifications or attachments, e.g. excentric centre of gravity -- This subclass covers locks that keep the key from being withdrawn by altering the key itself or adding attachments, for example by shifting the key's centre of gravity away from the normal axis. Such modifications create a mechanical retention that prevents key removal until a specific action releases the key.
[E05B 11/02] before the wing is locked -- E05B 11/02 relates to mechanisms that keep a key inserted in a lock until the locking wing is engaged, preventing key removal before the lock is secured. It is classified under fixed constructions, locks, and devices that ensure the key remains in place while the lock is being closed.
[E05B 11/04] before the wing is closed -- E05B 11/04 covers devices that stop a key from being withdrawn from a lock prior to the wing or latch being closed, ensuring the key remains engaged while the lock is being secured. This subclass falls under fixed constructions and locks, focusing on mechanisms that retain the key until the locking motion is completed.
[E05B 11/06] for catching skeleton or incorrect keys -- This subgroup of the Cooperative Patent Classification, under fixed constructions - locks - devices preventing keys from being removed, specifies mechanisms that catch skeleton or otherwise incorrect keys to retain them in the lock body.
[E05B 13/00] Devices preventing the key or the handle or both from being used -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Covers preventing access to the handle or key;  Devices for locking the handle;  Devices for disconnecting the handle;  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Electrically locking the handle -> E05B47/0657 - Electrically disconnecting the handle -> E05B47/0676 - Disconnecting the handle in a lock in which a sliding latch is used as a locking bolt -> E05B55/06
[E05B 13/001] Covers preventing access to handles or keys -- E05B 13/001 designates devices that stop a key, a handle, or both from being operated, thereby preventing access to the lock mechanism. It falls under the Fixed constructions → Locks hierarchy and relates to safeguards that render handles or keys unusable.
[E05B 13/002] locking the handle  E05B13/10 ,  E05B47/0657  take precedence -- E05B 13/002 denotes devices that lock a door or window handle, preventing its movement or use alone or together with a key, within the broader fixed-construction lock category. The subclass falls under E05B13/10 and is superseded by the more specific E05B47/0657.
[E05B 13/004] by locking the spindle, follower, or the like -- This subgroup covers lock mechanisms that secure a door or other opening by immobilising the spindle, follower or similar component, thereby preventing any rotation or movement of the latch. It includes devices that lock the shaft that transmits motion from a key or handle to the locking bolt.
[E05B 13/005] Disconnecting the handle  E05B13/101 ,  E05B47/0676 ,  E05B55/06  take precedence -- E05B13/005 falls under fixed constructions - locks and covers devices that prevent the key or handle, or both, from being used. The entry specifically addresses mechanisms that disconnect the handle, ensuring the lock cannot be operated via the handle, and takes precedence over related subclasses such as E05B13/101, E05B47/0676 and E05B55/06.
[E05B 13/007] Handles which are locked or blocked in the open position -- E05B 13/007 covers fixed-construction lock devices in which a handle is deliberately prevented from moving to the closed position, remaining locked or blocked while in the open position. It includes mechanisms that secure the handle itself, ensuring it cannot be turned or retracted until released.
[E05B 13/008] A single handle being used for actuation of several wings, e.g. simultaneously -- The code covers devices in which one handle is linked to several lock wings so that moving the handle actuates all wings at the same time, enabling simultaneous operation of multiple locking elements.
[E05B 13/04] shaped as fork-like implements grasping and fixing the key -- A fork-shaped device that grasps the key and locks it in place, preventing the key from being turned or removed. It is used in fixed-construction locks where both the key and handle may be immobilised.
[E05B 13/10] formed by a lock arranged in the handle -- This subclass covers devices where the locking mechanism is built into the handle, so the handle cannot be operated unless the lock is released. It includes constructions that combine the lock and handle in a single unit to prevent unauthorized use of either component.
[E05B 13/101] for disconnecting the handle  E05B13/103 ,  E05B13/105  take precedence -- E05B 13/101 designates mechanisms that disconnect a lock's handle to stop it from being turned, thereby preventing operation by the handle or key. It falls under fixed constructions > locks > devices preventing key or handle use. More detailed subclasses E05B 13/103 and E05B 13/105 provide further refinements.
[E05B 13/103] Combination lock -- A combination lock is a locking device that secures a door, drawer or other aperture without a key, using a sequence of rotating discs or tumblers that must be aligned to a preset combination before the lock can be opened. It belongs to the class of fixed constructions and locks that prevent the key or handle from being used.
[E05B 13/105] the handle being a pushbutton  E05B13/103  takes precedence -- E05B13/105 covers lock mechanisms where the operating handle is a pushbutton instead of a traditional lever or knob. This sub-category falls under fixed constructions for locks that block use of the key or handle, and it is subordinate to E05B13/103, which defines the broader concept of push-type handles.
[E05B 13/106] for handles pivoted about an axis perpendicular to the wing  E05B13/101 ,  E05B13/103 ,  E05B13/105  take precedence -- This subclass concerns lock devices in which the handle is mounted to pivot on an axis that runs perpendicular to the lock's wing, allowing the handle to swing outward or inward while the lock body remains fixed. Such arrangements are used in fixed constructions to prevent the key or handle from being operated except when the handle is rotated about that transverse axis.
[E05B 13/108] the lock coaxial with spindle -- A lock coaxial with the spindle has its locking mechanism aligned on the same axis as the spindle, so the key or handle operates through a central, coaxial arrangement within the spindle housing, preventing use unless the mechanism is correctly aligned.
[E05B 15/00] Other details of locks -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Other details and parts of locks according to the following structure:
[E05B 15/0006] Devices for aligning wing and frame; Anti-rattling devices  for vehicle doors  E05B83/367 -- The CPC subclass E05B 15/0006 covers mechanisms that align a vehicle's door wing with its frame and prevent rattling during operation. It includes devices such as guide tracks, latches, and dampers that secure the door in position, reduce vibration, and ensure smooth opening and closing.
[E05B 15/0013] Followers; Bearings therefor -- E05B 15/0013 pertains to followers and the bearings that support or guide them in fixed-construction lock mechanisms, covering elements that transmit motion or maintain alignment of moving parts within a lock. It is a subclass in the Cooperative Patent Classification under the E05B series for locks.
[E05B 15/002] Energy storage by movement of wing -- The CPC class E05B 15/002 covers lock mechanisms that store energy by moving a wing or flap as part of the locking action. It includes designs where the wing's displacement is used to charge a spring, elastic element or other storage device, providing the force needed to engage or release the lock. This subclass falls under fixed constructions and other details of locks.
[E05B 15/0026] Adaptors cooperating with and acting on keys inserted in an existing lock, e.g. for transformation of a key actuated lock to handle actuation -- The entry covers devices that are fitted to an existing lock and cooperate with the inserted key to modify how the lock is operated, such as adapters that convert a key-actuated lock so it can be actuated by another means.
[E05B 15/0033] Spindles for handles, e.g. square spindles -- The CPC entry E05B 15/0033 falls under Fixed Constructions → Locks → Other details of locks and covers spindles used in handle mechanisms, such as square-shaped spindles that transmit motion from the lock body to the operable handle. This classification is shared by the EPO and USPTO.
[E05B 15/004] Lost motion connections -- E05B 15/004 classifies lost-motion connections in lock mechanisms, covering devices that permit relative movement between parts to absorb shock, allow partial engagement or provide safety release. It falls under the fixed-construction section for locks and includes various designs such as springs, ratchets or flexible links that transmit force only after a predetermined motion.
[E05B 15/0046] Ratchet mechanisms -- E05B 15/0046 covers ratchet mechanisms incorporated in lock assemblies, describing devices that permit motion in one direction while restraining reverse motion, typically used to enable incremental turning, locking or release actions within fixed lock constructions.
[E05B 15/0053] means providing a stable, i.e. indexed, position of lock parts -- The subclass E05B 15/0053 covers mechanisms that hold lock components in a fixed, indexed position relative to each other, ensuring consistent alignment during operation. It pertains to devices that maintain a stable positioning of shafts, tumblers or other moving parts within a lock.
[E05B 15/006] Spring-biased ball or roller entering a notch -- This subclass covers mechanisms where a spring-loaded ball or roller is used to engage a recess or notch to lock or release a device, typical in detent or indexing mechanisms. It includes designs where the biasing spring forces the element into the notch to hold position until a releasing force is applied.
[E05B 15/0073] magnetically operated -- E05B 15/0073 designates fixed-construction locks whose actuation relies on magnetic forces, such as magnetic levers, solenoids or field-triggered mechanisms. The class groups devices where a magnetic stimulus replaces or augments conventional mechanical operation.
[E05B 15/008] friction type -- E05B 15/008 refers to locks that retain a door or panel by friction rather than a positive latch or bolt, such as friction bolts, friction catches or cam-type devices that hold a shaft or component in place through resistive pressure. This subclass falls under Fixed Constructions → Locks → Other Details of Locks.
[E05B 15/0086] Toggle levers  toggle fasteners  E05C19/14
[E05B 15/0093] Weight arrangements in locks; gravity activated lock parts  E05B65/5292 ,  E05B77/06  take precedence -- E05B 15/0093 relates to the use of weighted components and gravity-driven mechanisms within locks to achieve or modify locking actions, covering arrangements such as falling bolts, weighted levers, and other mass-based actuators. It is a subcategory of other details of locks under fixed constructions, and is superseded where more specific subclasses E05B65/5292 or E05B77/06 apply.
[E05B 15/02] Striking-plates -- E05B 15/02 classifies striking-plates, the metal components fixed to a door or frame that receive the bolt, latch or dead-lock when the lock is engaged. It covers their shapes, mounting methods and related details that affect the locking function. This falls under Fixed constructions → Locks → Other details of locks.
[E05B 15/0205] Striking-plates, keepers, staples  means for reducing friction  E05B17/007 ; electric strikers  E05B47/0046 ; staples for padlocks  E05B67/383 ; strikers for espagnolettes  E05C9/1808
[E05B 15/021] Strikers having multiple positions for the bolt -- E05B 15/021 covers lock strike mechanisms where the bolt can be placed in more than one predefined position, such as fully retracted, partially extended, or fully engaged, enabling different locking or release functions. These striker designs are part of fixed construction lock assemblies and are used to provide selectable security settings or convenience features.
[E05B 15/022] movable, resilient or yieldable  adjustable into different discrete positions  E05B15/024 ; yieldable striker for emergency opening  E05B65/1026 -- The CPC subclass E05B 15/022 deals with lock elements that are movable, resilient or yieldable and can be adjusted into separate, discrete positions, such as adjustable spring-loaded bolts or latch mechanisms. It also includes yieldable strikers intended for emergency opening, linking to related groups like E05B 15/024 and E05B 65/1026 under the broader category of fixed constructions-locks-other details of locks.
[E05B 15/0225] Resilient block type, e.g. made from rubber -- A resilient block, typically made of rubber or a similar elastic material, is used in lock assemblies to provide sealing, cushioning or impact-absorbing functions. It is positioned within the lock housing to protect moving parts and improve durability.
[E05B 15/024] adjustable -- The CPC entry E05B 15/024 pertains to adjustable aspects of locks, covering features that allow the lock's components-such as bolts, latch or strike plates-to be altered in position or size to suit different installations or security requirements. It is classified under Fixed Constructions, specifically the "Other details of locks" subgroup.
[E05B 15/0245] Movable elements held by friction, cooperating teeth, or the like -- The CPC class E05B 15/0245 falls under Fixed Constructions → Locks → Other Details of Locks and covers lock mechanisms in which movable parts are retained by friction, intermeshing teeth, or similar holding means. It includes designs where the locking and unlocking actions rely on frictional contact or engaging tooth profiles rather than springs or latches. This subclass groups patents describing such friction-or tooth-based securing elements.
[E05B 15/025] the striker being movable by a screw/nut -- E05B 15/025 pertains to fixed-construction locks in which the striker is adjusted or displaced by means of a screw or nut, allowing precise positioning or retraction of the striking element for alignment or variable locking conditions.
[E05B 15/0255] Deformable element -- E05B 15/0255 pertains to deformable elements used in fixed-construction locks, such as spring-loaded pins, flexible plates, or other components that change shape to permit or block movement. These parts adjust locking positions or provide tension within the locking mechanism.
[E05B 15/029] Closures, e.g. preventing dirt or paint from entering into the striker -- E05B 15/029 covers closures used on fixed lock constructions that shield the striker area from contaminants such as dirt or paint, typically by means of a cover, seal or protective plate that fits over or around the striker opening. This subclass falls under the broader groups for fixed constructions and locks.
[E05B 15/0295] specially adapted for forked or bifurcated bolts  for vehicles  E05B85/045
[E05B 15/04] Spring arrangements in locks -- This subclass covers the design and placement of springs that provide tension or return force in lock mechanisms, including spring types, mounting, adjustment and interaction with locking components. It includes springs used for bolt retraction, deadbolt engagement, latch return and similar functions.
[E05B 15/06] Lock wards -- E05B 15/06 covers lock wards, which are metal plates or other protective elements built into a lock housing to block the passage of tools and impede tampering. These wards are positioned within the lock cylinder or bolt mechanism to restrict the movement of picks and bolts, enhancing security of the locking device.
[E05B 15/08] Key guides -- E05B 15/08 covers key guides, the components that direct a key into the correct position within a lock's mechanism to engage the tumblers or pins. These guides are part of the detailed construction of locks, ensuring proper alignment and reliable operation of the locking system.
[E05B 15/10] Bolts of locks or night latches -- The E05B 15/10 subclass covers the bolt components of locks and night-latch devices, including sliding, dead, latch and spring bolts. It concerns the design, shape, material and actuation mechanisms of these bolts as they engage or retract to secure a door or gate.
[E05B 15/101] Spring-retracted bolts -- E05B 15/101 covers springs that automatically withdraw a bolt or latch after the locking action, ensuring the bolt returns to a retracted position without manual effort. The provision is applied in fixed constructions where rapid resetting of a lock is required, such as doors, gates or safes, and includes mechanisms that combine a spring with a bolt or latch to achieve self-retracting operation.
[E05B 15/102] Bolts having movable elements  E05B23/00  takes precedence -- E05B 15/102 covers bolts used in fixed constructions whose head or shaft includes a movable part such as a latch or sliding component that can be operated without removing the bolt. This subclass falls under other details of locks and is subordinate to the broader subclass E05B23/00, which takes precedence for related features.
[E05B 15/104] Movable key-operated elements ("Schluesselangriff") -- E05B 15/104 covers the movable components of a lock that are actuated by a key, commonly referred to as the key-attack mechanism. It includes levers, pins, tumblers and similar parts that translate the key's motion into unlocking action. This subclass falls under fixed constructions - locks - other details of locks.
[E05B 15/108] Bolts with multiple head  E05B65/0075  takes precedence -- E05B 15/108 covers bolts that have more than one head, used in lock mechanisms to provide multiple points of engagement or adjustment. These multiple-head bolts are classified under fixed constructions and other details of locks, and the subclass takes precedence over the related E05B65/0075.
[E05B 15/14] Tumblers -- E05B 15/14 covers the tumblers that constitute the internal locking elements of mechanical locks. These components move within the lock housing to align or misalign with a key-profile, thereby permitting or preventing rotation of the bolt.
[E05B 15/143] elastic, e.g. formed by a spring -- The code E05B 15/143 designates elastic elements, typically springs, incorporated in lock assemblies to provide biasing, tension or recoil forces. Such components help keep a lock in a ready position, assist in latching or releasing mechanisms, and contribute to the overall operation of fixed-construction locks.
[E05B 15/16] Use of special materials for parts of locks -- E05B 15/16 covers the use of special materials in lock components, such as corrosion-resistant alloys, hardened steels, or polymeric inserts, to improve durability, security or resistance to wear. The subclass falls under fixed constructions, locks, other details of locks.
[E05B 15/1607] Adhesive -- E05B 15/1607 covers the use of adhesives in lock assemblies, including glues, bonding agents or sealants applied to secure or seal lock components, fasten moving parts, or provide weather-proofing. The classification relates to fixed-construction locks where adhesive methods are employed for functional or protective purposes.
[E05B 15/1614] of hard materials, to prevent drilling  E05B67/04 ,  E05B67/38  take precedence -- E05B 15/1614 covers locks and other fixed constructions that employ hard-material components or coatings specifically intended to resist drilling attacks. It falls under the broader "Locks" group and addresses anti-drill protection measures, though more detailed subclasses such as E05B67/04 and E05B67/38 provide precedence for particular material or design approaches.
[E05B 15/1621] Hardened bolts, or bolts with hardened inserts -- E05B 15/1621 covers bolts used in lock mechanisms that have been heat-treated or contain hardened metal inserts to improve strength and wear resistance, ensuring reliable fastening and resistance to tampering. This subclass falls under fixed constructions and other details of locks.
[E05B 15/1635] of plastics materials  springs  E05B15/04
[E05B 17/00] Accessories in connection with locks -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Accessories in connection with locks according to the following structure:  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Preventing dirt into the striker -> E05B15/029 - Illuminating devices attached to key rings -> E05B17/103 - With means carried by the lock for interlocking with the keeper -> E05B63/12 - Additional locking for safes responsive to attack -> E05B65/0082 - Weather protection for vehicles -> E05B77/34 - Noise prevention, anti-rattling means in vehicles -> E05B77/36 - Cushion elements in vehicles -> E05B77/38 - Vehicle locks elements covered by a silencing coating -> E05B77/40 - Means for damping the movement of vehicle lock parts -> E05B77/42 - Burglar prevention in vehicles, e.g. by opening by unauthorised tool -> E05B77/44 - For assisting closing vehicle doors -> E05B81/20 - Making locksmith's goods of sheet metal, e.g. handles -> B21D53/38 - Drill jibs for work pieces -> B23B47/28 - Assembling in general -> B23P21/00 - Slotted or mortise work -> B27F5/12 - Using toothed wheels between lock cylinder and operating bars in espagnolette locks -> E05C9/023 - Templates for hinges -> E05D11/0009 - Anti-tampering devices for hinges -> E05D11/0018 - Stops or buffers preventing slamming of the wings -> E05F5/02 - Means for taking the weight of the wing -> E05F7/06
[E05B 17/0004] Lock assembling or manufacturing  making locksmith's goods of sheet metal  B21D53/38 ; assembling in general  B23P21/00 -- This CPC subclass E05B 17/0004 falls under Fixed Constructions - Locks - Accessories in connection with locks. It covers the assembling or manufacturing of locksmith's goods made of sheet metal, including general assembly processes. The entry links to related classes B21D53/38 and B23P21/00.
[E05B 17/0008] Lock parts made by extrusion process -- E05B 17/0008 designates lock components that are produced by an extrusion process, such as extruded bars, plates or other structural elements used in lock mechanisms. It belongs to the accessories for locks subgroup within the broader fixed-construction category.
[E05B 17/0012] for lock parts held in place before or during mounting on the wing -- E05B 17/0012 covers accessories that hold lock components in position while they are being mounted on a lock wing, such as jigs, clamps or other retaining fixtures that keep the parts fixed before or during installation.
[E05B 17/0016] Defrosting, e.g. heating devices -- E05B 17/0016 covers heating or defrosting devices incorporated in lock accessories, such as elements that melt ice or frost on lock mechanisms to maintain reliable operation in cold environments.
[E05B 17/002] Weather or dirt protection  E05B15/029 ,  E05B17/14  take precedence; for vehicle locks  E05B77/34
