[E05B 17/0025] Devices for forcing the wing firmly against its seat or to initiate the opening of the wing  E05B15/022 ,  E05B65/0817 ,  E05B81/20 ,  E05C19/14  take precedence -- E05B 17/0025 covers devices that press a lock's wing tightly against its seat or trigger the wing's release, used as accessories for fixed-construction locks. The subclass details mechanisms that ensure secure positioning of the wing or facilitate its opening, and it supersedes related entries such as E05B15/022 and E05B81/20.
[E05B 17/0029] motor-operated  for vehicles  E05B81/20
[E05B 17/0033] for opening only  E05B17/0029  takes precedence -- The subclass E05B 17/0033 covers accessories fitted to a lock that serve only to permit its opening, such as opening levers, pins or special tools that do not add security functions. It falls under the "Accessories in connection with locks" group of fixed constructions, and is subordinate to the broader subclass E05B 17/0029, which takes precedence.
[E05B 17/0037] Spring-operated -- E05B 17/0037 designates spring-operated accessories for locks, covering devices that use a spring to bias a bolt, latch or dead-lock into or out of engagement. These components are employed in door, gate or safe lock assemblies to provide automatic closing, re-locking or holding functions.
[E05B 17/0041] Damping means  for vehicles  E05B77/42 ; time locks with retarders  E05B43/00
[E05B 17/0045] Silencing devices; Noise reduction  resilient strikers  E05B15/022 ; for vehicles  E05B77/36 -- E05B 17/0045 classifies accessories for fixed lock constructions that reduce noise, such as silencing devices and resilient strikers, including variants used on vehicles. It falls under the broader groups for locks and their ancillary components.
[E05B 17/005] Preventing accidental lock-out, e.g. by obstruction in the striker  securing the bolt in the retracted position  E05B63/18 ; stops and buffers preventing the slamming of wings  E05F5/02 -- The entry covers devices that keep a lock from unintentionally locking out a user, such as mechanisms that block the striker to hold the bolt in a retracted position, and includes stops or buffers that prevent the wings of a lock from slamming shut.
[E05B 17/0054] Fraction or shear lines; Slip-clutches, resilient parts or the like for preventing damage when forced or slammed -- E05B 17/0054 covers lock accessories that incorporate fractional or shear lines and slip-clutch or resilient elements intended to absorb shock and prevent damage when a lock is forced or slammed.
[E05B 17/0058] with non-destructive disengagement  E05B17/045  takes precedence -- E05B 17/0058 classifies accessories for locks that permit the lock to be opened or released without causing damage to the locking mechanism, such as removable pins, releasable bolts or non-destructive unlocking tools. This subclass falls under the broader group of lock accessories and is superseded by the more general subclass E05B17/045.
[E05B 17/0062] with destructive disengagement -- This subclass covers accessories used with locks that enable the lock to be opened by intentionally destroying or damaging it, such as break-away mechanisms or forced-release devices. It falls under fixed constructions, specifically the lock category and accessories related to locks.
[E05B 17/0066] Handles with destructible elements -- This subgroup covers lock handles that incorporate components designed to break or be destroyed under forced manipulation, providing a tamper-evident or deterrent function. It includes handles with built-in sacrificial plates, breakable inserts, or other destructible elements intended to damage the handle or the locking mechanism when attacked.
[E05B 17/007] Devices for reducing friction between lock parts  E05B17/08  takes precedence -- Devices that lessen friction among components of a lock, such as lubricants, low-friction plates or surface-treatments, belong to the E05B 17/007 subclass. They are accessories used with lock mechanisms to improve smooth operation and wear resistance, and this subclass takes precedence over the broader E05B 17/08 category.
[E05B 17/0075] Insulating, e.g. for limiting heat transfer; Increasing fire-resistance of locks -- The code E05B 17/0075 designates accessories for locks that provide insulation to limit heat transfer. It also covers measures that improve the fire-resistant performance of lock mechanisms, such as heat-shielding or fire-proof housings.
[E05B 17/0079] Locks with audio features  E05B45/00  takes precedence -- E05B 17/0079 classifies lock devices that incorporate audio functions, such as sound-producing alarms, chimes, or voice alerts, as accessories related to lock mechanisms. This subclass falls under the broader categories of fixed constructions and locks, and is superseded by the more specific E05B45/00 classification.
[E05B 17/0083] Sound emitting devices, e.g. loudspeakers -- E05B 17/0083 designates sound-emitting accessories for locks, such as loudspeakers and alarms that produce audible signals to indicate lock status or to deter tampering. The classification covers any device integrated with or attached to a lock system that generates sound for security or warning purposes.
[E05B 17/0087] Sound receiving devices, e.g. microphones -- E05B 17/0087 covers sound-receiving devices such as microphones that are incorporated as accessories for lock mechanisms. These devices capture acoustic signals to trigger or monitor lock operation, but are not part of the primary locking hardware.
[E05B 17/0091] including protruding or recessed symbols in a surface for tactile indication/information, e.g. Braille symbols
[E05B 17/04] Devices for coupling the turning cylinder of a single or a double cylinder lock with the bolt operating member -- This subclass covers mechanisms that connect the turning cylinder of a single- or double-cylinder lock to the bolt-operating member, ensuring that rotation of the key or handle drives the bolt. It includes cams, levers, gear trains and other coupling devices used to translate cylinder motion into bolt movement.
[E05B 17/041] Coupling device with a shaft projecting axially rearwardly from the cylinder, e.g. affording a degree of universal motion to compensate for misalignment -- A coupling device attached to a lock cylinder features a shaft that projects axially toward the rear, allowing limited universal motion to offset misalignment between the cylinder and the connected mechanism. This design helps maintain reliable operation despite slight positional errors in the lock assembly.
[E05B 17/042] using toothed wheels or geared sectors  E05C9/023 ,  E05C9/041  take precedence
[E05B 17/044] Clutches, disengageable couplings -- E05B 17/044 covers mechanical clutches and disengageable couplings that form part of lock accessories, enabling the transmission or interruption of motion between lock components such as tumblers, bolts or actuators. These devices allow selective engagement of the locking mechanism, facilitating control or safety functions.
[E05B 17/045] for keeping the rotor disconnected from the bolt actuating member, when being turned, e.g. forcefully, without the proper key -- This classification covers a protective mechanism for a lock that keeps the rotor isolated from the bolt-actuating component when the lock is turned without the correct key, such as during forced entry. The device disengages the rotor to stop the bolt from moving, enhancing resistance to tampering.
[E05B 17/046] disengaged manually from inside -- E05B 17/046 refers to accessories for locks that can be manually disengaged from the inside, such as interior release levers or thumb-turns allowing quick unlocking without a key. It falls under the fixed constructions - locks - accessories in connection with locks category and covers devices designed for emergency or convenient internal access.
[E05B 17/047] with rotating output elements forming part of cylinder locks, e.g. locking cams of double cylinder locks -- The E05B 17/047 subclass covers lock assemblies that incorporate rotating output components as integral parts of cylinder mechanisms, such as the locking cams used in double-cylinder locks. These rotating elements transmit motion from the key to the bolt, providing the securing action within the lock's housing. This category falls under accessories for locks within the broader fixed-construction group.
[E05B 17/048] with sliding output elements forming part of cylinder locks, e.g. in the form of pins or cams -- The CPC entry E05B 17/048 describes accessories for fixed-construction cylinder locks that use sliding output elements such as pins or cams as part of the lock mechanism. It falls within the hierarchy of locks, accessories in connection with locks.
[E05B 17/049] sliding essentially in the axial direction of the cylinder lock -- E05B 17/049 covers accessories for cylinder locks that slide primarily along the cylinder's axis, such as bolts, rods or shims that move linearly to engage or release the lock mechanism. It is classified under fixed constructions, locks, accessories in connection with locks.
[E05B 17/06] Templates for marking the position of apertures in fittings of wings -- E05B 17/06 covers templates used to mark where holes should be drilled or cut in the fittings of lock wings. These devices assist installers in accurately positioning apertures during assembly or retrofitting of lock mechanisms. They are classified as accessories related to locks within the fixed-construction group.
[E05B 17/08] Lubricating devices -- Lubricating devices are tools or mechanisms used to apply oil, grease or other lubricants to lock components to reduce friction, prevent wear and improve operation. Examples include oilers, grease guns, spray cans and automated lubrication systems designed specifically for lock hardware.
[E05B 17/10] Illuminating devices on or for locks or keys; -- The subclass E05B 17/10 includes lighting arrangements that are built into or mounted on locks or keys to provide illumination for operation, key insertion, or visual identification, such as LEDs on lock faces, illuminated key slots, or light-emitting key blanks. It covers devices whose primary purpose is to make the lock or key visible in low-light conditions.
[E05B 17/103] on keys  E05B17/106  takes precedence; attached to key rings  A44B15/005 ; on key bags or wallets  A45C11/32
[E05B 17/106] fluorescent -- The CPC subclass E05B 17/106 covers accessories for locks that use fluorescent materials or illumination, such as glow-in-the-dark markings, fluorescent paints, or light-emitting components to identify or locate lock parts. It falls under fixed constructions, locks, accessories in connection with locks.
[E05B 17/12] Devices for removing keys stuck in the lock -- The E05B 17/12 class covers tools and mechanisms that extract a key that has become jammed in a lock. It includes key pullers, tweezers, specialized removal devices and other accessories used for maintenance or emergency unlocking of fixed-construction locks.
[E05B 17/14] Closures or guards for keyholes -- This class covers devices attached to locks that protect or cover the keyhole, such as keyhole plates, shields, or decorative guards that prevent tampering, dust, or unauthorized key insertion. It includes both functional and ornamental closures.
[E05B 17/142] with key-operated locks, e.g. padlocks -- E05B 17/142 covers accessories that are part of a lock assembly and are operated by a key, such as the key-driven mechanisms found in padlocks. It includes the design of key cylinders, tumblers and related components that enable the lock to be opened only with the correct key. This subclass falls under fixed constructions for locks, specifically the accessories that work with key-operated locking devices.
[E05B 17/145] with combination lock -- The E05B 17/145 subclass groups accessories designed for locks that incorporate a combination-lock mechanism. It falls under Fixed Constructions → Locks → Locks → Accessories in connection with locks, encompassing devices such as changeable combination dials, shackle adapters and related hardware used to convert or augment conventional locks with combination functionality.
[E05B 17/147] electrically-operated -- The code E05B 17/147 covers electrically-operated accessories related to locks, such as solenoid or motor-driven bolt mechanisms, electronic control units, and power-supply components that are integrated with or added to lock hardware. It falls under the broader category of fixed constructions and lock accessories.
[E05B 17/16] shaped as pins or key bits -- The classification covers lock accessories shaped like pins or key bits, such as components that function as or replace standard pin tumblers or key teeth within lock assemblies. These items are designed to integrate with fixed lock constructions.
[E05B 17/18] shaped as lids or slides -- The CPC code E05B 17/18 designates accessories for locks that are formed as lids or sliding elements, such as protective covers, sliding bolt shields or similar components that close or move over a lock mechanism. These parts are used to improve security, weather-proofing or concealment of the lock assembly.
[E05B 17/181] pivoting inwardly -- E05B 17/181 covers lock accessories where a locking element pivots inwardly, such as a latch or bolt that swings into a door or frame to secure it. The subclass falls under fixed constructions, specifically accessories for locks that move inward rather than slide or retract.
[E05B 17/183] pivoting outwardly -- E05B 17/183 pertains to accessories for locks that allow a lock component to pivot outwardly, enabling release or manual operation of the locking device. The classification falls under fixed constructions and concerns mechanisms integrated with the lock housing that swing outward as part of the accessory design.
[E05B 17/185] pivoting about an axis perpendicular to the lock face -- E05B 17/185 covers lock accessories that rotate about an axis perpendicular to the lock face, such as pivoting levers, rotating covers or hinged mechanisms that move the lock face outward or inward.
[E05B 17/186] sliding -- E05B 17/186 covers sliding accessories that are used with lock mechanisms. This includes sliding bolts, bars, or other movable elements that engage or disengage a lock by sliding motion, typically employed on doors, gates, or windows. The code pertains to the design and implementation of such sliding components within lock assemblies.
[E05B 17/188] flexible -- This subclass comprises flexible components that are used as accessories for locks, such as bendable shafts, cables, strips or other pliable elements that transmit motion or force between lock parts while allowing curvature or twisting.
[E05B 17/20] Means independent of the locking mechanism for preventing unauthorised opening, e.g. for securing the bolt in the fastening position -- E05B 17/20 covers ancillary devices that operate independently of a lock's internal mechanism to stop a bolt from being opened without permission, such as secondary restraints that keep the bolt in its engaged position. These means provide extra security for locks by preventing unauthorised opening.
[E05B 17/2003] Preventing opening by insertion of a tool, e.g. flexible, between door and jamb to withdraw the bolt -- E05B 17/2003 covers lock accessories that prevent a door from being opened by inserting a tool, such as a flexible blade, between the door and its jamb to pull the bolt back. The devices are designed to block or obstruct such insertion, enhancing resistance to forced entry.
[E05B 17/2007] Securing, deadlocking or "dogging" the bolt in the fastening position  E05B15/0046 ,  E05B15/0086 ,  E05B67/383  take precedence -- The CPC subclass E05B 17/2007 covers devices that lock or deadlock a bolt in its fastening position, often called dogging mechanisms, used as accessories for locks. It pertains to methods and structures that maintain the bolt immobile after closure, ensuring security until released. This subclass is subordinate to broader groups such as E05B15/0046, E05B15/0086 and E05B67/383, which have priority.
[E05B 17/2011] using balls or the like cooperating with notches  E05B63/121  takes precedence -- The code E05B 17/2011 covers lock accessories that use balls or similar elements to engage with notches for transmitting motion or holding positions. These components are employed in fixed lock constructions to provide detent or indexing functions. The related subclass E05B63/121, which addresses other ball-and-notch mechanisms, takes precedence over this entry.
[E05B 17/2015] with wedging action -- E05B 17/2015 pertains to accessories for locks that operate by a wedging action, where a movable element is forced into a tighter fit to secure a door, window or similar opening. The wedge increases friction or displacement of a bolt, latch or shackle to achieve holding.
[E05B 17/2019] elastic, i.e. the dog or detent being formed or carried by a spring -- The code E05B 17/2019 covers lock accessories that incorporate an elastic element, such as a spring, to form or carry a dog or detent. It pertains to mechanisms where a spring provides the required holding or releasing action within the lock assembly.
[E05B 17/2023] Loose pins, screws, or the like; Portable devices -- The subclass E05B 17/2023 covers detachable components used with locks, such as loose pins, screws and similar fasteners, as well as portable devices that serve as lock accessories. It includes items intended for adjustment, repair, temporary attachment or auxiliary functions in lock mechanisms.
[E05B 17/2026] automatic, i.e. actuated by a closed door position sensor -- This subclass concerns lock accessories that operate automatically when a closed-door position is detected, using a sensor to trigger the locking mechanism. It includes devices that sense the door's final closed state and engage or release the lock without manual actuation.
[E05B 17/203] not following the movement of the bolt -- The subclass E05B 17/203 pertains to lock accessories that stay fixed in place and do not move with the bolt when it is actuated, such as stationary plates, guides or indicators that are mounted independently of the bolt's travel.
[E05B 17/2034] moving pivotally or rotatively -- E05B 17/2034 pertains to lock accessories that move about a pivot or rotate, such as levers, cams, rotating bolts or other elements that change position to engage or release a locking mechanism. It falls under the Fixed Constructions - Locks category, specifically addressing components whose motion is pivotal or rotary in nature.
[E05B 17/2038] moving rectilinearly -- E05B 17/2038 covers lock accessories that operate by straight-line motion. It includes devices such as sliding bolts, retractable latch plates, linear actuators and guide mechanisms that move rectilinearly to engage or release a lock.
[E05B 17/2042] moving otherwise than only rectilinearly and only pivotally or rotatively -- This subclass pertains to accessories for locks that provide movement not limited to straight, pivoting or rotating actions, such as camming, oscillating or other complex motions that aid locking or unlocking functions.
[E05B 17/2046] performing both movements simultaneously, e.g. screwing -- The code E05B 17/2046 covers lock accessories that cause two motions to occur at the same time, such as a device that both rotates and translates when engaged, for example a mechanism that simultaneously screws and moves a bolt during operation. It falls under the broader category of fixed constructions and locks.
[E05B 17/2049] following the movement of the bolt -- E05B 17/2049 covers devices that detect, indicate or record the movement of a lock bolt. Such accessories may include sensors, visual or audible indicators, or mechanical linkages that show bolt position during operation. They are classified under fixed constructions, specifically accessories for locks.
[E05B 17/2053] moving pivotally or rotatively relating to the bolt -- The subclass E05B 17/2053 covers accessories for locks that enable the bolt to be moved by pivoting or rotating motions, such as levers, cams or linkage mechanisms that convert a turning action into bolt movement.
[E05B 17/2057] moving rectilinearly relating to the bolt -- This group covers lock accessories that cause the bolt to travel in a straight line, such as linear actuators, cams or levers that push or pull the bolt without rotation. It includes devices that translate motion directly into rectilinear movement of the locking bolt.
[E05B 17/2061] moving otherwise than only rectilinearly and only pivotally or rotatively relating to the bolt -- The CPC subclass E05B 17/2061 refers to lock accessories in which the bolt is driven by a motion that is not limited to straight-line, pivoting or purely rotary movement, such as cam, eccentric or combined actions that cause the bolt to move along a curved or irregular path. This category covers mechanisms that produce such alternative bolt movements within fixed lock constructions.
[E05B 17/2065] performing both movements simultaneously, e.g. screwing -- E05B 17/2065 covers devices that combine two motions - typically rotational and linear - at the same time, such as mechanisms that screw while moving in another direction, used as accessories for lock systems.
[E05B 17/2069] by means of an additional dogging movement of a sliding bolt -- E05B 17/2069 relates to lock accessories that provide an additional dogging movement for a sliding bolt. The device adds a secondary action that holds the bolt in a selected position, improving security or ease of operation.
[E05B 17/2073] pivotally or rotatively -- E05B 17/2073 covers accessories for locks that operate by a pivoting or rotating motion, such as hinges, levers, rotary handles or other devices that transmit a turning movement to the locking mechanism. These elements enable the lock to be opened, closed or adjusted through a swing or spin action rather than linear motion.
[E05B 17/2076] about an axis parallel to its sliding movement -- The CPC code E05B 17/2076 falls under Fixed Constructions → Locks → Locks → Accessories in connection with locks. It covers lock components in which an axis runs parallel to the direction of a sliding movement, such as guide rails, sliders or other parts that move linearly along that axis. This classification is used for patents describing the geometry and function of such sliding lock elements.
[E05B 17/208] by means of an additional dogging movement of a rotary bolt -- A subclass for lock accessories that add a secondary dogging action to a rotary bolt, allowing the bolt to be held in a set position by an extra movement of the bolt's rotation, typically employed to enhance security or provide a backup holding function.
[E05B 17/2084] Means to prevent forced opening by attack, tampering or jimmying  E05B15/1614  takes precedence; for ignition or steering locks  B60R25/023
[E05B 17/2088] Means to prevent disengagement of lock and keeper  E05B63/12  takes precedence -- The code E05B 17/2088 covers devices that stop a lock from separating from its keeper, such as retainers, stop plates or interlocking elements that hold the two parts together after engagement. These accessories are used in fixed-construction lock installations and are superseded by the broader subclass E05B63/12.
[E05B 17/2092] Means responsive to tampering or attack providing additional locking  for safes  E05B65/0082 -- E05B 17/2092 covers accessories for locks that activate extra security when a safe is tampered with or attacked, such as relocking bolts, internal locking plates or sensors that trigger additional locking mechanisms. The devices are designed to engage automatically upon forced entry attempts, providing a secondary barrier that enhances the safe's resistance to burglary or forced opening. This falls under the broader category of lock accessories in the CPC system.
[E05B 17/22] Means for operating or controlling lock or fastening device accessories, i.e. other than the fastening members, e.g. switches, indicators -- E05B 17/22 classifies devices that operate or control accessories for locks and fastening systems, such as switches, indicators or other control mechanisms, but does not include the fastening members themselves.
[E05B 17/223] preventing lock actuation after detection of drug use, e.g. alcohol consumption
[E05B 17/226] Displays on locks, e.g. LED or LCD screens  on handles  E05B1/0084 ; on keys  E05B19/0088 -- The subclass E05B 17/226 covers locks that incorporate visual display units such as LED or LCD screens, either on the lock handle or on the key, to present status information, prompts or other data to the user. It includes both handle-mounted and key-mounted displays as accessories for fixed lock constructions.
[E05B 19/00] Keys -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Keys for locks and accessories therefor and it has the following structure:  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Key modifications or attachments preventing removal from the lock -> E05B11/005 - Key rings -> A44B15/00 - Key cases; e.g. purses for keys or small cases for extendable/retractable storage of several keys or shanks (however for single keys/shanks E05B19/0082 , E05B19/043 , E05B19/046 ) -> A45C11/32 - Key holders; boards or cabinets for key storage -> A47G29/10 - Rescue tools with forcing action -> A62B3/005 - Making keys -> B21D53/42 - Milling grooves in keys -> B23C3/35
[E05B 19/0005] Key safes  E05B35/086  takes precedence -- The CPC subclass E05B 19/0005 pertains to key safes, which are fixed or portable containers designed to store and protect keys with a locking mechanism for controlled access. These devices fall under Fixed constructions → Locks → Keys and are superseded by the more specific subclass E05B35/086.
[E05B 19/0011] Key decoders  general measuring of contours and curvatures  G01B3/14 ,  G01B5/20 ,  G01B7/28 -- E05B 19/0011 covers apparatus and methods for decoding keys by measuring their contour and curvature, typically using optical or mechanical techniques to capture the key profile for lock analysis or duplication. The subclass links to general measuring of contours and curvatures in G01B3/14, G01B5/20 and G01B7/28, indicating relevance to precision metrology of key shapes.
[E05B 19/0017] Key profiles -- Key profiles refer to the specific geometric shapes and cuts of a key that correspond to the internal configuration of a lock's tumblers or wards. These profiles determine how the key engages and turns the locking mechanism, and they are defined to ensure proper fit, security level, and compatibility with the associated lock design.
[E05B 19/0023] characterized by variation of the contact surface between the key and the tumbler pins or plates -- This subclass includes lock keys whose design modifies the shape of the surface that engages the tumbler pins or plates, such as varied cuts, slopes, or stepped profiles, to achieve distinct interaction with the locking mechanism. The variation is intended to alter the way force is transmitted to the pins, improving security or allowing special functions.
[E05B 19/0029] characterized by varying cross-sections of different keys within a lock system  cross-sections of single keys  E05B19/0041 -- This CPC subclass covers lock systems in which the keys have differing cross-sectional shapes, including arrangements where a single key presents multiple cross-sections. It falls under the hierarchy of Fixed Constructions → Locks → Locks → Keys.
[E05B 19/0035] characterized by longitudinal bit variations -- The subclass E05B 19/0035 covers keys that incorporate longitudinal variations in the shape or height of their individual bits along the length of the key blade. Such variations provide distinct profiles that engage corresponding lock mechanisms to enhance security or functionality.
[E05B 19/0041] characterized by the cross-section of the key blade in a plane perpendicular to the longitudinal axis of the key -- This subgroup covers keys whose defining feature is the shape of the blade's cross-section taken in a plane perpendicular to the key's longitudinal axis, such as round, rectangular, or specially profiled sections that determine how the key engages the lock.
[E05B 19/0047] with substantially circular or star-shape cross-section -- E05B 19/0047 classifies keys for fixed-construction locks whose shank has a substantially circular or star-shaped cross-section. The entry covers the geometric profile of the key blade used to engage the lock mechanism, distinguishing it from other key shapes within the broader locks category.
[E05B 19/0052] Rectangular flat keys -- Rectangular flat keys are a form of key for fixed lock constructions in which the key blade has a rectangular cross-section and a flat profile. The flat shape engages matching rectangular slots or keyways in the lock to rotate the bolt or cam and allow the lock to open.
[E05B 19/0058] with key bits on at least one wide side surface of the key -- This subcategory refers to lock mechanisms where the key has its cutting bits located on at least one broad side surface rather than the usual flat or edge surfaces. It covers designs in which the key's side faces carry the profile that engages the tumbler. Used for certain high-security or specialized locks.
[E05B 19/0064] and with additional key bits on at least one narrow side edge of the key -- This subclass concerns keys that include one or more extra bits attached to at least one narrow side edge of the key, providing additional engagement surfaces for tumblers or other locking elements within a fixed-construction lock.
[E05B 19/007] with U- or V-shaped cross-section -- E05B 19/007 covers fixed-construction locks whose keyway or locking element has a U- or V-shaped cross-section, defining the shape of the lock's internal profile for guiding and securing the bolt. This subcategory falls under the broader groups for locks and keys within the Cooperative Patent Classification system.
[E05B 19/0076] Keys with a curved longitudinal axis -- The class E05B 19/0076 covers keys whose longitudinal axis is curved rather than straight, allowing them to engage lock mechanisms with offset or contoured tumblers. Such curved-axis keys are used in specialized lock designs where a conventional straight shank would not align properly.
[E05B 19/0082] Keys or shanks being removably stored in a larger object, e.g. a remote control or a key fob  general constructional details of electronic keys  G07C9/00944 -- The entry covers keys or key shanks that are housed within a larger device such as a remote control or key-fob, focusing on how these removable elements are integrated and secured. It includes the general constructional details of electronic keys, describing the mechanisms that allow the key portion to be taken out of, and reinserted into, the host unit.
[E05B 19/0088] Displays on keys, e.g. LED or LCD screens -- The entry covers keys that incorporate visual display elements such as LED or LCD screens to convey information, status or instructions to the user. It pertains to lock systems where the key itself provides a dynamic interface rather than relying solely on mechanical features. This classification falls under fixed constructions for locks, specifically the subcategory of keys.
[E05B 19/0094] protruding elements on the key preventing unauthorized insertion into the keyhole -- This class covers keys equipped with protruding elements such as pins, notches or bumps that block the key from being inserted into a lock unless the correct key shape aligns them. The features are designed to prevent unauthorized entry by making the keyhole inaccessible to mismatched keys. It falls under the fixed constructions hierarchy for locks and keys.
[E05B 19/04] Construction of the bow of the key -- E05B 19/04 relates to the design and construction of the bow, the part of a key that is grasped by the user. It includes the shape, size, curvature, slots and any ornamental or ergonomic features of the bow that facilitate handling and key identification.
[E05B 19/043] the shank being pivotably mounted on the bow, e.g. for storage -- This subclass covers keys in which the shank is attached to the bow by a pivot, allowing it to swing or fold onto the bow for compact storage or transport. It includes designs where the shank rotates relative to the bow, facilitating easy concealment or convenient carry.
[E05B 19/046] the shank being slidingly mounted on the bow, e.g. for storage -- This subclass addresses key designs in which the shank is mounted on the bow in a sliding manner, enabling the shank to be retracted into or along the bow for compact storage. It is applied to portable or space-saving keys where the working part can be folded or telescoped when not in use.
[E05B 19/14] Double keys -- E05B 19/14 pertains to locks that require two distinct key elements, either inserted together or one after the other, to release the bolt or latch. The dual-key arrangement enhances security by demanding the presence of both keys, and is commonly used in high-security cabinets, safes and dual-authorization systems.
[E05B 19/18] Keys adjustable before use -- E05B 19/18 covers keys that can be altered or trimmed before they are employed in a lock, allowing the user to adjust cutting dimensions, profile or tumblers to match a specific locking mechanism. The classification includes mechanisms such as adjustable blade sections, removable inserts or modular key blanks designed for customization prior to first use.
[E05B 19/20] Skeleton keys -- Skeleton keys are simple, universal keys designed to operate a range of lock types within a family by engaging the basic warding pattern rather than precise pin configurations. They lack specific cuts, allowing temporary access to multiple locks but offering limited security against unauthorized use.
[E05B 19/205] Lock decoders -- E05B 19/205, Lock decoders, designates devices that interpret or translate a lock's combination or electronic code to enable opening without the original key, often using mechanical or electronic methods to reproduce the correct unlocking sequence.
[E05B 19/22] Keys with devices for indicating whether the last operation was locking or unlocking -- This CPC entry covers keys that incorporate a mechanism indicating whether the most recent operation was a lock or an unlock, such as a visual or tactile indicator built into the key. It is used to classify devices that give users feedback on the lock status after the key is turned.
[E05B 19/24] Key distinguishing marks -- The CPC class E05B 19/24 covers distinguishing marks on keys, including cuts, notches, letters, numbers, symbols or shape variations that identify a key's intended lock or function. Such marks are applied to the blade or bow of a key to provide visual or tactile differentiation for security, key control or user convenience. It falls under the hierarchy of fixed constructions, locks, and keys.
[E05B 19/26] Use of special materials for keys -- E05B 19/26 classifies inventions concerned with applying special materials to keys, including the use of hardened alloys, polymer composites, coatings or treatments that improve strength, wear resistance, corrosion protection, or security features. It pertains to fixed-lock constructions and the design of key blanks or key portions made from or modified with these materials.
[E05B 2001/0023] being movable into a non-operating position, e.g. foldable towards the mounting plane -- The subclass E05B 2001/0023 covers structures that can be shifted from an active state to a stowed or non-operating position, typically by folding or collapsing toward the plane in which they are mounted. It applies to devices whose movable parts are designed to lie flat or become compact when not in use. This includes, for example, foldable panels, doors or protective covers that transition to a storage configuration.
[E05B 2001/0076] The handle having at least two operating positions, e.g. the bolt can be retracted by moving the handle either upwards or downwards -- E05B 2001/0076 classifies handles that offer at least two distinct operating positions, enabling a bolt to be retracted by moving the handle either upward or downward, and thus providing bidirectional actuation of a locking mechanism.
[E05B 2003/006] Fastening knobs or handles to the spindle by means of tilt-plates -- The CPC subclass E05B 2003/006 covers devices that attach knobs or handles to a spindle by employing tilt-plates, which are adjustable plates that pivot to secure the grip element in a fixed position on the rotating shaft. This method is used in machine tools and related equipment to provide a sturdy, interchangeable handle connection.
[E05B 2009/004] Shape of the lock housing -- E05B 2009/004 belongs to the CPC subgroup for lock mechanisms and covers the geometric form of the lock housing, including its external contour, cross-section and overall dimensions that affect installation, integration or operation in doors, windows or other locking assemblies. It is used to classify patents that focus on the shape rather than the internal locking components.
[E05B 2009/006] circular -- E05B 2009/006 refers to circular elements or features employed in earth-moving, foundation or ground-improvement operations, such as circular piles, shafts, trench sections or other round-shaped components used in construction and excavation.
[E05B 2009/008] circle segment -- E05B 2009/008 pertains to devices or methods for producing or using a circular segment, a portion of a circle defined by a chord and the arc between its endpoints. It includes tools, fixtures, and manufacturing processes that generate or handle such segments in construction, metalworking or other engineering contexts.
[E05B 2009/043] whereby the casing contains an inner and outer rotor which are simultaneously operated by a long key -- The code covers casings that incorporate concentric inner and outer rotors which are driven at the same time by a long key that engages both shafts. This arrangement is used in mechanisms where synchronized rotation of two rotor stages within a single housing is required.
[E05B 2009/046] Cylinder locks operated by knobs or handles -- E05B 2009/046 designates cylinder locks that are actuated by turning a knob or handle attached to the cylinder, where the rotation directly raises or lowers the locking bolts. The classification covers lock assemblies used in doors for residential, commercial or institutional applications that employ a cylindrical housing and a manually turned exterior lever or knob to control access.
[E05B 2009/047] Means for returning cylinder locks to their neutral position -- The CPC entry E05B 2009/047 covers devices that automatically reset a cylinder lock to its neutral, ready-to-receive position after it has been turned, such as springs, recoil levers or other mechanical return mechanisms designed to bring the lock core back into alignment for the next operation.
[E05B 2015/0066] axially operated -- E05B 2015/0066 classifies lifts, escalators or moving walkways that are driven by an axial mechanism, such as a screw, spindle or linear actuator, rather than by cable, hydraulic or belt systems. The axial drive provides direct vertical or inclined movement of the carriage or platform.
[E05B 2015/0215] Screw-type -- The CPC entry E05B 2015/0215 refers to screw-type ground-engaging members such as helical piles, screw anchors and similar devices that gain purchase in soil by a helical blade and are employed for foundations, retaining structures or soil stabilisation.
[E05B 2015/023] Keeper shape -- E05B 2015/023 covers the geometric design of keepers, the stationary components that receive a latch or bolt in doors, gates, shutters and similar closures. It includes specifications of shape, profile and dimensions that affect the engagement and sealing characteristics of the locking mechanism.
[E05B 2015/0235] Stud-like -- E05B 2015/0235 covers building elements that are shaped like studs and are used to provide support, attachment points or reinforcement in walls, ceilings or other structural components. The classification includes studs for fastening, bracing or mounting of fixtures within construction assemblies.
[E05B 2015/026] using eccentrics -- E05B 2015/026 covers the use of eccentric devices to generate oscillatory or reciprocating motion in elevator, escalator or moving walk drive systems. The classification addresses mechanisms where an off-centre mass converts rotary input into a timed displacement for lifting or transporting loads.
[E05B 2015/0265] pivoting or tilting -- E05B covers building construction elements such as doors, windows and shutters, and subclass 2015/0265 relates to mechanisms that allow a panel or similar element to rotate about a horizontal or vertical axis, providing pivoting or tilting for opening, closing, ventilation or angle adjustment.
[E05B 2015/027] auto-adjustable -- E05B 2015/027 pertains to building elements such as windows, doors, or skylights that automatically modify their position, opening or shading in response to external conditions or control signals. It covers mechanisms that sense temperature, light, wind or occupancy and adjust apertures without manual intervention, including motor-driven or smart-material systems designed to improve energy efficiency, comfort or safety.
[E05B 2015/0275] in two directions -- E05B 2015/0275 covers doors, shutters, gates or similar moving building elements that can be operated to open or close in two opposite directions, such as double-swinging doors or reversible shutters, grouping mechanisms that allow bidirectional movement.
[E05B 2015/028] in three directions -- This CPC subgroup defines opening or closing devices for doors, windows, shutters or similar building openings that can be moved in three distinct directions, such as a combination of swinging, sliding, tilting or rotating motions, providing versatile operation and positioning of the closure element.
[E05B 2015/0285] Portable striker -- E05B 2015/0285 pertains to a portable striker, a detachable or adjustable component mounted on a door frame that receives a latch or bolt to secure the door, allowing temporary or flexible installation without permanent fitting.
[E05B 2015/0403] Wound springs -- E05B 2015/0403 pertains to wound springs, i.e., springs formed by coiling metal wire or strip, used in building, construction, mining or related equipment to store and release energy, provide tension, or assist in lifting or moving parts. The classification covers various forms such as helical, spiral or torsion springs employed in devices like excavators, concrete mixers, rescue gear and other mechanical systems.
[E05B 2015/0406] wound in a cylindrical shape -- This CPC subgroup concerns construction elements where a material is wound around a cylindrical member, such as reinforcement or protective layers applied to piles, caissons or other cylindrical foundations. It includes methods for forming, securing and using such wound cylindrical structures in building substructures.
[E05B 2015/041] loaded perpendicular to cylinder axis -- CPC group E05B 2015/041 relates to moving or lifting devices where a load is applied perpendicular to the cylinder axis, commonly in hydraulic or pneumatic cylinders used in construction. The classification covers arrangements and mechanisms that transfer the force sideways from the cylinder to the workpiece.
[E05B 2015/0413] loaded by compression -- E05B covers doors, windows, shutters and similar building openings; the 2015 subgroup specifies devices that are loaded by compression, meaning the element is held closed or opened by a component such as a spring or pneumatic cylinder that exerts force when compressed. This classification is applied to mechanisms where compression provides the operating load.
[E05B 2015/0417] loaded by traction -- E05B 2015/0417 covers lifts, inclines, conveyors, escalators or moving walks in which the load is moved by traction, such as a rope, belt, cable or chain that pulls the carriage or platform, rather than by direct mechanical drive. This subclass groups apparatus that rely on pulling forces to transport goods or persons.
[E05B 2015/042] wound in a plane, e.g. spirally -- The CPC subclass E05B 2015/042 covers doors, windows or similar building panels that incorporate a cut or wound in an otherwise flat surface, such as a spiral-shaped wound, used for decorative, ventilation or light-passing purposes. It falls under the group for doors with special panel designs.
[E05B 2015/0424] of conical shape -- E05B 2015/0424 covers the use of conical shapes in foundations and other support structures, such as conical footings, piles or shafts that taper to a point for load distribution. It includes design, manufacturing and installation aspects of these conical elements.
[E05B 2015/0427] curved, e.g. toroidal -- E05B 2015/0427 covers building or civil-engineering structures that incorporate curved forms, such as toroidal shapes, into components like arches, shells or beams, where the curvature is a functional or structural feature of the element.
[E05B 2015/0431] Modifying spring characteristic or tension  E05B2015/0441  takes precedence -- E05B 2015/0431 covers inventions that adjust the spring force or tension of movable building components such as doors, windows or shutters. It includes mechanisms that change spring stiffness, preload or damping to improve operation or safety. When a more detailed subclass (E05B 2015/0441) is applicable, it overrides this general group.
[E05B 2015/0434] Automatically
[E05B 2015/0437] Attachments or mountings;  Mounting of springs -- E05B 2015/0437 relates to devices and methods for attaching or mounting components, specifically the mounting of springs in buildings or civil-engineering works. It includes arrangements that secure springs to structural elements such as shock absorbers, vibration dampers or other load-bearing parts.
[E05B 2015/0441] Tensioning after mounting -- The code E05B 2015/0441 relates to techniques for applying or adjusting tension to a component after it has been mounted in a structure, such as ropes, cables, belts or similar elements. It covers mechanisms, devices or methods that allow post-installation tightening to achieve the required load, spacing or alignment in constructions, vehicles or equipment.
[E05B 2015/0444] Springs additionally fulfilling an electric function -- Springs that also perform an electrical function, such as providing electrical conductivity, acting as contacts, or integrating circuitry while maintaining their mechanical spring action, are classified under this entry. The code covers designs where the spring element simultaneously serves as a part of an electric circuit.
[E05B 2015/0448] Units of springs;  Two or more springs working together -- This CPC subclass covers devices that comprise two or more springs arranged to operate together as a unit. It includes mechanical assemblies in which springs are combined in series, parallel, or other configurations to achieve a desired load-deflection characteristic, such as suspension systems, shock absorbers, or load-balancing mechanisms.
[E05B 2015/0451] Folded springs -- Folded springs are spring elements formed by bending a strip of metal or other elastic material into one or more folds that act as a flexural spring. They are employed in building components such as hinges, latches, or sealing devices to provide a controlled resisting force or to hold positions. The CPC entry E05B 2015/0451 groups patents dealing with such folded-spring designs used in construction and related assemblies.
[E05B 2015/0455] Torsion springs of bar type -- E05B 2015/0455 designates bar-type torsion springs, which are elongated bars that store energy by twisting about their longitudinal axis and are used to transmit or absorb torque in mechanical and vehicular systems. The entry falls within the CPC class for power transmission and regulating devices.
[E05B 2015/0458] Leaf springs;  Non-wound wire springs -- E05B 2015/0458 covers leaf springs and non-wound wire springs employed as elastic suspension elements, chiefly in vehicles or industrial equipment. The code includes the design, production and mounting of plate-type leaf springs and straight or coiled wire springs that are not pre-wound, together with their attachment methods and related damping features.
[E05B 2015/0462] Ring springs -- The CPC entry E05B 2015/0462 for ring springs covers springs made as a closed circular coil that provides radial or axial force, used for sealing, locking, or vibration damping in mechanical assemblies. It includes designs where the spring material is shaped into a ring and may be loaded by compression, tension, or torsion. Applications span automotive, aerospace, and consumer products.
[E05B 2015/0465] Cup- or dished-disc springs -- Cup- or dished-disc springs are radially symmetric metal elements shaped like shallow cups or discs that compress under load and rebound when the load is removed, providing cushioning, vibration isolation or force-distribution in devices such as automotive suspensions, machinery mounts and precision equipment. The classification E05B 2015/0465 groups inventions that focus on the geometry, manufacturing methods or specific applications of these cup-shaped disc springs.
[E05B 2015/0468] made of one piece with a lock part -- E05B 2015/0468 refers to a door, window, or panel element that is formed as a single, integral piece which includes an attached locking component, eliminating separate lock fittings and allowing the lock mechanism to be molded or fabricated together with the main body.
[E05B 2015/0472] Made of rubber, plastics or the like -- E05B 2015/0472 designates constructional elements such as doors, windows, shutters, gates or barriers whose parts are made of rubber, plastics or similar non-metallic materials, typically to provide flexibility, sealing or insulation.
[E05B 2015/0475] of block type -- The entry E05B 2015/0475 covers closures of the block type, i.e., doors, windows, shutters or similar members formed from a solid block of material that moves as a single unit to open or close an aperture, including any associated moving or locking mechanisms.
[E05B 2015/0479] of elastic string type -- The CPC entry E05B 2015/0479 covers building opening or sealing devices that employ an elastic-string type element, where a stretchable cord or string provides a flexible seal or closure for doors, windows or similar apertures. It pertains to constructions that use the elasticity of the string to maintain contact with the surrounding surface and accommodate movement or irregularities.
[E05B 2015/0482] Heavy springs which cannot be operated by hand -- E05B 2015/0482 covers heavy-duty springs designed for applications where the force needed to compress or extend them exceeds manual capability, typically used in industrial machinery, presses or shock-absorbing systems. The classification applies to springs that must be installed, adjusted or actuated with mechanical assistance rather than by hand.
[E05B 2015/0486] A single spring working on more than one element -- The classification concerns devices in which a single spring exerts force on two or more separate components, allowing coordinated motion or tension distribution. It applies to mechanisms where one spring simultaneously influences multiple elements, such as in articulated linkages, multi-point lifts, or compound fastenings. The code is used to index patents that feature this shared-spring arrangement.
[E05B 2015/0489] on more than one tumbler -- This subclass covers lock or latch mechanisms that require the coordinated movement of more than one tumbler, such as multi-tumbler pin, wafer or disc locks where the key must align several tumblers simultaneously to release the lock. It includes devices whose actuation depends on the combined position of multiple tumblers.
[E05B 2015/0493] Overcenter springs -- E05B 2015/0493 covers overcenter springs used in doors, windows or gates to provide a bistable force that assists opening or closing and holds the element in either the open or closed position. These springs are typically integrated into hinges, latches or locking devices to improve functionality and safety.
[E05B 2015/0496] Springs actuated by cams or the like -- This subgroup covers devices in which a spring is driven to store or release energy by the rotation of a cam or a similar mechanism. Typical applications include self-closing doors, windows and shutters that use cam-actuated springs to provide controlled opening or closing motion.
[E05B 2015/105] Two pivoting latch elements with opposite inclined surfaces mounted on one slidable main latch-piece -- The subgroup covers latch assemblies in which a single slidable main latch carries two pivoting latch elements that have opposite inclined faces. The opposing surfaces cooperate to lock or release the latch in two directions, providing a compact bidirectional fastening mechanism for doors, gates or panels.
[E05B 2015/107] Roller bolt, i.e. a slidable main latch-piece with a roller-, ball- or barrel-shaped rotating element -- E05B 2015/107 refers to a roller bolt, a type of latch where a main sliding piece carries a small rotating element such as a roller, ball or barrel that engages a strike to secure a door, window or panel. The bolt moves linearly while the rotating part locks or releases by turning, providing a smooth, low-friction fastening. This mechanism is classified under E05B, which covers devices for opening, closing and sealing openings.
[E05B 2015/146] Tumblers with parts movable to each other -- The CPC subclass E05B 2015/146 covers lock tumblers whose components are designed to move relative to one another, forming a mechanism that can be set, disengaged or reset to secure or release a lock. It includes arrangements such as pin, disc or lever tumblers where the interaction of movable parts creates the locking action.
[E05B 2015/1628] Free-rotating protecting covers or discs -- E05B 2015/1628 covers free-rotating protecting covers or discs that shield openings such as doors, windows, vents or similar apertures while allowing objects or air to pass. The covers are mounted so they can turn freely, often using a bearing or pivot, providing protection against debris, weather or unauthorized entry without hindering normal flow. This subclass includes designs where rotation is the primary means of protection.
[E05B 2015/1642] for the whole lock -- E05B 2015/1642 classifies lock mechanisms that are provided as a single, integral unit for the whole lock, rather than as separate components, and are intended for doors, gates or similar building openings. The entry includes designs where the housing, bolt and actuation mechanism are combined into one piece.
[E05B 2015/165] for bolts -- The CPC entry E05B 2015/165 covers devices, fittings and anchoring systems specifically designed to secure or support bolts in construction and civil-engineering applications. It includes bolt anchors, expansion bolts, anchor bolts and related accessories used for fastening structural elements to foundations, walls or other building components.
[E05B 2015/1657] for escutcheons -- E05B 2015/1657 refers to escutcheons, the protective or decorative plates that cover openings such as keyholes, switches or pipe penetrations in floor or wall covering systems. The entry classifies designs where the escutcheon is integrated with or applied to tiled, panelled or other constructed surfaces, ensuring a flush finish and protection against wear. It is used to catalogue hardware that secures and aesthetically finishes such openings in building interiors.
[E05B 2015/1664] for lock housing  E05B17/002 ,  E05B17/0041 ,  E05B17/0045  take precedence -- E05B 2015/1664 classifies inventions concerning the housing that encloses a lock mechanism, typically in doors or windows. It belongs to the broader E05B group for building hardware, and the more specific sub-classes E05B17/002, E05B17/0041 and E05B17/0045 provide detailed precedence for particular lock-housing designs.
[E05B 2015/1671] for strikers  E05B15/022  takes precedence
[E05B 2015/1678] for handle bearings -- E05B 2015/1678 belongs to the CPC class for hand tools and covers devices that provide bearings for handles to reduce friction and improve maneuverability. The subclass relates to mechanisms that support the handle on a rotating or pivoting axis, such as swivel or ball bearings integrated into tool handles.
[E05B 2015/1685] Sheet materials -- E05B 2015/1685 covers sheet materials used as building elements, including metal, plastic, composite or mineral sheets that form walls, roofs, floors or waterproofing layers. The subclass defines the physical form and typical applications of flat or slightly curved panels that are installed as part of a structure. It is part of the broader E05B group for building elements and foundations.
[E05B 2015/1692] Wires or straps -- E05B 2015/1692 covers the use of wires or straps as securing or supporting elements for doors, gates, shutters, blinds or similar building components. It relates to arrangements where flexible members bind or tension parts to improve closure, lock or safety functions.
[E05B 2017/0095] Means preventing wrong operation, e.g. preventing damaging contact between extended bolt and striker or bolt and frame -- A means that prevents incorrect operation of a mechanism, for example by avoiding damaging contact between an extended bolt and a striker or between a bolt and its frame, ensuring safe engagement and reducing wear.
[E05B 2017/043] Planetary gear -- E05B 2017/043 covers planetary gear arrangements in which a central sun gear meshes with multiple planet gears that rotate on a carrier and engage an outer ring gear, providing high torque density and compact size for power transmission.
[E05B 2017/2096] Preventing tampering by removal of lock parts which are vital for the function -- E05B 2017/2096 pertains to security measures that deter tampering by designing locks so that removal of essential components disables the device or renders it inoperative. The classification includes mechanisms where vital parts are concealed, breakable, or integrated such that their extraction compromises the lock's function.
[E05B 2027/0025] having means preventing rotation of the tumbler -- A lock mechanism that incorporates features to stop the tumbler or cylinder from rotating, typically using blocking pins, rollers, spring-loaded elements or other restraining devices that engage when an incorrect key is inserted, thereby preventing unauthorized turning and improving security.
[E05B 2035/009] Locks where a characteristic part of the user's body is used as a key -- This subclass includes lock mechanisms that are opened by a distinctive part of the user's body, such as a fingerprint, hand geometry, vein pattern or retinal scan, which serves as the key to grant access.
[E05B 2043/002] using mechanical clocks -- E05B 2043/002 designates inventions that use mechanical clocks as functional components in building-related equipment or processes. It includes arrangements where clock mechanisms drive, trigger or coordinate actions such as opening doors, regulating lifts, or timing construction operations.
[E05B 2043/007] using hydraulic or pneumatic retarders -- The E05B 2043/007 subclass covers lift-type transport systems that employ hydraulic or pneumatic retarders to provide controlled slowing or stopping of the moving carriage. It includes devices that use fluid pressure, alone or with other brakes, to achieve deceleration or holding of elevators, escalators or similar equipment.
[E05B 2045/0615] triggered by movement of the handle -- The CPC subclass E05B 2045/0615 relates to scaffolding or ladder accessories that are activated by the motion of a handle. It covers mechanisms where moving a handle initiates a function such as extending, locking, or adjusting a component of the scaffold or step system. The classification is used for inventions where handle movement serves as the trigger.
[E05B 2045/062] by traction on a chain -- This subclass covers transport or handling systems that move a load by using a chain as the driving element, such as chain-driven conveyors, chain hoists and other equipment where traction is provided through a chain.
[E05B 2045/0625] by pressure on a safety bar -- This subclass covers mechanisms that are operated by applying pressure to a safety bar, such as safety-bars on vehicle doors, windows or other closures that unlock or release when the bar is pressed.
[E05B 2045/063] by movement of the wing -- Covers mechanisms that regulate ventilation, air supply or heating by moving a wing-shaped element, such as hinged flaps, louvers or wing valves, to open or close an opening.
[E05B 2045/0635] by rotation of key or rotor -- The CPC code E05B 2045/0635 designates devices that are operated by rotating a key or a rotor, such as the control mechanisms that start, stop, or change the direction of elevators, escalators, moving walks or other material-handling installations. It covers systems where the user turns a keyed element or rotor to engage the drive or braking components.
[E05B 2045/064] by movement of the lock housing or part thereof -- E05B 2045/064 covers lock mechanisms that operate by moving the lock housing or a portion of it, rather than moving a bolt or latch alone. It includes designs where the entire lock body, a shell, or an internal component shifts to lock or unlock a door, window, or similar opening, using translation or rotation of the housing to engage the securing element.
[E05B 2045/0645] by movement of the keyhole cover -- An element of fire-protection equipment in which the closure mechanism is actuated by moving a keyhole cover, typically found on fire doors or shutters that are opened or closed by rotating or sliding the cover that houses the locking key.
[E05B 2045/065] Switch or sensor type used in alarm locks -- The CPC subclass E05B 2045/065 covers the specific kinds of switches or sensors employed in alarm-locked doors and windows, including magnetic, mechanical, infrared, pressure-activated and electronic detection devices that trigger the locking mechanism when an intrusion is sensed.
[E05B 2045/0655] Photo-diodes -- The CPC entry E05B 2045/0655 concerns photodiodes used as light-sensing components in building control systems, such as automatic regulation of illumination, daylight harvesting, or occupancy detection within HVAC or lighting installations.
[E05B 2045/066] Mercury switches -- The CPC subclass E05B 2045/066 pertains to mercury switches, which are devices that use liquid mercury to open or close an electrical circuit when the unit is tilted, vibrated or subjected to temperature changes, and are commonly used in safety, control and measurement applications such as thermostats, tilt sensors and alarm systems.
[E05B 2045/0665] Magnetic switches, e.g. reed- or hall-switch -- E05B 2045/0665 relates to magnetic switching devices that operate by detecting a magnetic field, including reed switches where contacts close within a sealed glass envelope and Hall-effect switches that generate a voltage when exposed to a magnetic flux, used for control, sensing or safety functions.
[E05B 2045/067] Switches triggered by inertia -- The code covers switches whose actuation relies on inertial forces, such as a moving weight, pendulum or ball that shifts when the device experiences acceleration, tilt or vibration, causing the switch to change state. These inertia-triggered switches are used in safety, motion-sensing and control applications for doors, windows, shutters and similar building fittings.
[E05B 2045/0675] Foam or rubber element with contacts which close a circuit due to deformation of the element -- This subclass covers devices in which a foam or rubber body contains electrical contacts that are forced together when the elastomer deforms, completing a circuit. The mechanism is used for pressure or impact sensing, safety switches, and similar applications where deformation of the material triggers an electrical response.
[E05B 2045/068] Piezoelectric sensors -- E05B 2045/068 relates to piezoelectric sensors used in building and construction applications, describing devices that convert mechanical stress, pressure or vibration into electrical signals for purposes such as structural health monitoring, fire detection and occupancy sensing.
[E05B 2045/0685] Optical fibres for transmitting the sensor signal -- The class covers the use of optical fibres as the transmission medium for sensor outputs, including fibre-optic cables embedded in structures to convey measurements such as temperature, strain, pressure or other physical parameters to a processing unit. It applies when the fibre itself carries the signal rather than power.
[E05B 2045/069] Timers therefor -- Timers for heating appliances such as fireplaces, stoves, furnaces or other fire-producing devices, used to start, stop, cycle or otherwise control operation automatically for safety, efficiency or convenience. This subclass covers devices that provide timing functions directly to the heating unit or its control system.
[E05B 2045/0695] Actuation of a lock triggering an alarm system, e.g. an alarm system of a building -- The code E05B 2045/0695 covers devices in which opening or closing a lock automatically activates an alarm system, for example a building security alarm that is triggered when the lock is actuated. This includes mechanisms that integrate lock operation with alarm signaling to provide immediate warning of unauthorized access.
[E05B 2047/0007] with two or more electromagnets -- This CPC subgroup belongs to E05B, which covers railway and road-rail vehicles, and specifies designs that employ two or more electromagnets, typically to provide magnetic levitation, propulsion, or braking forces for the vehicle or its track system.
[E05B 2047/0008] having different functions -- E05B 2047/0008 covers building-ventilation or climate-control devices that are engineered to perform multiple tasks, such as a fan combined with a heater, humidifier, or air-filter. The classification applies to apparatuses whose structure integrates distinct functions into a single unit for heating, cooling, ventilating, or humidifying purposes.
[E05B 2047/0013] more than one motor for the same function, e.g. for redundancy or increased power -- E05B 2047/0013 relates to lifting or transport devices in which two or more motors are employed to perform the same function, such as providing redundancy, boosting power output, or ensuring continuous operation if one motor fails. It covers arrangements where the motors are coordinated to drive a single load or movement.
[E05B 2047/0014] Constructional features of actuators or power transmissions therefor -- E05B 2047/0014 covers constructional features of actuators or power transmissions used in moving or steering devices such as rudders, sails, or other maritime equipment. It pertains to the design and arrangement of components that generate or transmit motion, including gears, hydraulic or pneumatic systems, and related mechanisms.
[E05B 2047/0015] Output elements of actuators -- The code E05B 2047/0015 designates the components of an actuator that transmit its motion or force to the driven mechanism in building-related lifting or moving equipment. It covers pistons, rods, levers, gear trains, hydraulic or pneumatic cylinders and similar output elements.
[E05B 2047/0016] with linearly reciprocating motion -- This CPC subgroup covers conveyors or transfer installations that move articles by a straight-line back-and-forth motion, i.e., linear reciprocating motion. It includes tables, platforms or other elements that oscillate linearly to feed, sort, compact or convey material, using pistons, cams or similar mechanisms rather than rotary or circular vibration.
[E05B 2047/0017] with rotary motion -- The E05B 2047/0017 code refers to door or window handles with rotary motion. It is part of the Cooperative Patent Classification system used by the EPO and USPTO. This code categorizes specific types of handles.
[E05B 2047/0018] Details of actuator transmissions -- E05B 2047/0018 pertains to the transmission elements that convey motion from an actuator to a door, window, skylight or similar building opening. It includes gear trains, belts, chains, cables, screw or rack-and-pinion drives and other mechanisms that link the power source to the moving part of the opening device. This subclass is used to classify patents describing how the actuation force is transferred within opening-closure systems.
[E05B 2047/002] Geared transmissions -- The classification E05B 2047/002 covers geared transmission arrangements used in moving-and-lifting devices, such as elevators, conveyors, and other mechanical handling equipment. It includes gear trains that transmit rotation or linear motion between shafts, incorporating elements like spur, helical, bevel or worm gears, and may address specific configurations for speed reduction, torque increase, or direction change within such systems.
[E05B 2047/0021] Geared sectors or fan-shaped gears -- E05B 2047/0021 concerns gear arrangements in which the teeth are formed on sector-shaped or fan-shaped blanks rather than on full-circular wheels. It includes designs where each gear segment covers a limited angular span and meshes with adjacent sectors, typical of compact, high-torque mechanisms such as wind-turbine gearboxes or specialized transmission units.
[E05B 2047/0022] Planetary gears -- E05B 2047/0022 covers planetary gear systems in which a central sun gear meshes with several planet gears that rotate about it, usually mounted on a carrier and engaging an outer ring gear. These arrangements provide torque conversion and speed variation in a compact, high-ratio transmission.
[E05B 2047/0023] Nuts or nut-like elements moving along a driven threaded axle -- The CPC class E05B 2047/0023 covers devices in which a nut or nut-like component travels along a threaded spindle that is driven to produce linear motion. It includes arrangements such as screw jacks, linear actuators, and similar machines where rotation of the axle converts to axial movement of the nut.
[E05B 2047/0024] Cams -- The CPC entry E05B 2047/0024 covers cams employed in lifting and handling equipment, describing cam profiles that drive a follower to control the movement of a load. This sub-group specifies cams having particular geometric or functional features such as adjustable radius, eccentric mounting, or integration with a transmission to produce a defined lifting action.
[E05B 2047/0025] in the form of grooves -- E05B 2047/0025 relates to stairs, ladders or similar walkways that incorporate grooved treads or risers, where the grooves are designed to improve traction, aid drainage or define a stepping pattern.
[E05B 2047/0026] Clutches, couplings or braking arrangements -- This CPC subgroup covers devices that join, separate or regulate the transmission of rotary motion between shafts, including clutches, mechanical couplings and braking arrangements used in pumps, compressors, gearboxes or similar machinery.
[E05B 2047/0027] using centrifugal action -- The classification E05B 2047/0027 covers methods of forming, compacting or treating concrete, mortar, or similar building materials by applying centrifugal force. It includes processes that use rotation to improve density, remove air, or shape elements such as columns, pipes or precast parts during construction.
[E05B 2047/0028] using electromagnetic means -- The subclass E05B 2047/0028 addresses methods and devices that use electromagnetic fields or forces to install, secure, align or manipulate building components, for example magnetic fastening, inductive heating for joining, or levitation-based positioning of panels during construction.
[E05B 2047/003] of the overload- slip- or friction type -- This CPC subgroup covers devices that protect structural members by allowing a controlled slip or friction action when an overload occurs, thereby limiting forces transmitted through connections, joints or supports. Such mechanisms are used in building and civil engineering to prevent damage from excessive loads.
[E05B 2047/0031] of the elastic type -- The CPC subclass E05B 2047/0031 covers elastic-type supporting or guiding devices for pipes, cables or similar members, employing springs, rubber or other compressible materials to provide flexible load bearing and vibration damping. It falls within the broader group for ground and supporting structures in building construction.
[E05B 2047/0032] using flywheel action -- E05B 2047/0032 classifies mechanisms that employ a flywheel to store kinetic energy for the opening or closing of doors, windows, shutters, gates or similar barriers, using the flywheel's inertia to smooth motion and reduce the force required from the operator. It falls under the broader CPC group for manually or automatically operated building access elements.
[E05B 2047/0033] using electro-rheological or magneto-rheological substances -- This CPC subgroup covers inventions that use electro-rheological or magneto-rheological fluids or similar smart materials to change a system's mechanical properties, such as variable damping, stiffness or positioning, often in actuators, shock absorbers, or other control devices.
[E05B 2047/0034] using materials with a volume which changes due to electrical voltage or current -- The CPC subclass E05B 2047/0034 covers devices or methods that employ materials whose dimensions expand or contract when an electrical voltage or current is applied, such as electro-active polymers or shape-memory alloys used for actuation, positioning or adaptive structural functions.
[E05B 2047/0035] Actuators being driven in a single direction only -- E05B 2047/0035 covers actuators that operate only in one direction, such as devices driven by hydraulic, pneumatic or electric power that move a component forward without a reverse stroke. It is a sub-group of building-related actuators used for functions like opening doors, windows or valves where motion is required in a single sense.
[E05B 2047/0036] Reversible actuators -- The CPC entry E05B 2047/0036 defines reversible actuators used in elevators, escalators, moving walks and related supporting structures. It covers devices that can produce controlled motion in both directions, such as hydraulic, pneumatic, electric or mechanical actuators, and includes the mechanisms, control systems and power sources that enable the bidirectional operation.
[E05B 2047/0037] Powered actuators with automatic return to the neutral position by non-powered means, e.g. springs -- The code covers devices that use a powered actuator to move a door, gate, shutter or similar element, but which automatically return to the neutral or closed position by a non-powered means such as a spring, gravity or a counterweight. It applies to systems where the power source initiates motion and the return is achieved without additional energy input.
[E05B 2047/0048] Circuits, feeding, monitoring -- The subclass covers electrical circuits that provide power to, and monitor the operation of, building installations such as lighting, heating, ventilation or solar-energy systems. It includes feeding arrangements, control devices, sensors and other circuitry used to manage and supervise these installations.
[E05B 2047/005] Opening, closing of the circuit -- E05B 2047/005 relates to devices that open or close an electrical circuit, including switches, contactors, relays and similar control mechanisms. It covers manual, automatic, thermal or magnetic actuation methods for making or breaking the electrical connection.
[E05B 2047/0051] by closing the wing -- E05B 2047/0051 relates to devices that close a wing of a building element such as a roof, vent or shade, moving the wing to a sealed position for protection or weatherproofing. It includes hinged, sliding or folding wings that can be locked, latched or otherwise secured when closed.
[E05B 2047/0052] opening the circuit by detection of current increase -- The CPC code E05B 2047/0052 relates to protective devices that interrupt an electrical circuit when they sense a rise in current above a predetermined level. It covers methods and apparatuses-such as overload relays, circuit-breakers, and electronic protection modules-that monitor current magnitude and automatically open contacts to prevent damage or hazards. This subclass is applied to safety systems that rely on current-increase detection to trigger circuit opening.
[E05B 2047/0053] by operating the handle -- E05B 2047/0053 covers roof or ceiling opening devices that are opened or closed by means of a manually operated handle, such as a lever, crank or rotating grip, enabling the user to lift, tilt or swing a hatch, skylight or vent.
[E05B 2047/0054] using microprocessor, printed circuits, or the like -- This subclass covers heat-exchange, heating, cooling or drying structures that are controlled, monitored or regulated by electronic means such as microprocessors, printed circuit boards or similar digital circuitry.
[E05B 2047/0055] by blowing -- E05B 2047/0055 classifies ventilation arrangements that move air by active blowing, using fans, blowers or similar devices to force fresh air into, or extract stale air from, a building or enclosure.
[E05B 2047/0056] by sensing the presence of a person, e.g. body heat sensor -- The code designates devices that detect a person's presence, for example by using body-heat or infrared sensors, to control the opening, closing or locking of doors, windows, shutters or similar access elements. It covers the sensing function itself, not the subsequent actuation mechanism.
[E05B 2047/0057] Feeding -- E05B 2047/0057 concerns feeding devices and methods that supply material, workpieces or objects to a machine or process, including mechanisms that regulate the flow, distribution or positioning of the feed stock, typically used in industrial, agricultural or construction equipment.
[E05B 2047/0058] by batteries -- E05B 2047/0058 covers building-related installations that obtain their energy from batteries, including battery-powered lighting, heating, ventilation, fire-protection and emergency systems, as well as the methods for connecting and managing such power sources within a structure.
[E05B 2047/0059] by transfer between frame and wing -- E05B 2047/0059 covers lifting devices in which load, power or motion is transferred from the main supporting frame to an extending wing or boom, typically using hydraulic lines, cables, gears or pneumatic links. It includes the mechanisms that convey the force or material between the stationary part of a crane or tower-type lifting apparatus and its movable arm.
[E05B 2047/0061] using induction -- This subclass covers methods that use induction heating to treat or cure building foundations, floor slabs or other sub-structure elements, for example to accelerate concrete setting, dry moisture or de-ice surfaces. It includes equipment and processes that generate an alternating magnetic field to heat embedded metal reinforcement or conductors within the structure.
[E05B 2047/0062] by generator -- E05B 2047/0062 pertains to combined heating, ventilation and air-conditioning systems in which the conditioned air is produced by a dedicated generator such as a boiler, heat pump, furnace or other heat-producing unit, rather than by recovering heat from the building or external sources.
