[E05D 15/0604] allowing an additional movement  E05D15/10  takes precedence; raising wings before sliding  E05D15/565 -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Suspension arrangements for wings sliding horizontally, allowing an additional movement such as pivoting of the wing.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Wings movable out of one plane into a second parallel plane -> E05D15/10
[E05D 15/0608] caused by track lay-out -- This subclass covers suspension systems for doors, windows or wing panels where the method of supporting the moving part is determined by the layout of the guiding track. It deals with how the track geometry influences the hinge or suspension action of the wing.
[E05D 15/0613] with multi-directional trolleys -- E05D 15/0613 covers suspension arrangements for doors, windows or wings that use multi-directional trolleys to guide the movement of the wing, allowing it to travel along several axes or change direction during opening and closing. The code applies to fixed construction hardware such as shutters or sliding panels that require complex guiding mechanisms.
[E05D 15/0617] of cantilever type -- E05D 15/0617 covers cantilever-type suspension arrangements for doors, windows or other wings, where the panel is supported at one end and extends outward without a second support. The classification includes devices that use a cantilever beam, bracket or similar structure to hold the wing in an open or closed position, often employed in hinge-less or pivoting applications.
[E05D 15/0621] Details, e.g. suspension or supporting guides  E05D15/0604 ,  E05D15/08  -  E05D15/14  take precedence -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Details of suspension arrangements for wings sliding horizontally such as suspension and guiding tracks, sliding blocks, rollers, trolleys, and height adjustment.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Suspension or supporting arrangements for wings with an additional movement besides horizontal sliding -> E05D15/0604 , E05D15/08 - E05D15/14
[E05D 15/0626] for wings suspended at the top -- E05D 15/0626 designates suspension arrangements for wing-type doors or windows where the wing is hung from a top mounting point, allowing it to swing or open without a conventional hinge. It falls under fixed constructions, locks and hinges or suspension devices for doors, windows or wings.
[E05D 15/063] on wheels with fixed axis -- E05D 15/063 covers suspension arrangements for doors, windows or wing sections that are mounted on wheels whose axle does not move, allowing the wing to swing or slide while the wheel remains fixed in place. Such devices are used where a fixed pivot simplifies operation and reduces wear.
[E05D 15/0634] with height adjustment -- E05D 15/0634 covers suspension arrangements for doors, windows or wings that include a height-adjustment feature, allowing the wing to be positioned at different vertical levels. It falls under the fixed constructions group of hinges and suspension devices.
[E05D 15/0639] by vertical bolts -- This subclass covers suspension arrangements for doors, windows or wing panels that are achieved using vertical bolts, which run parallel to the direction of gravity and secure the wing to a frame or supporting structure, allowing tilting, pivoting or other controlled movement. It is located under Fixed constructions → Locks → Hinges or suspension devices for doors, windows or wings → Suspension arrangements for wings.
[E05D 15/0643] on balls or floating rollers -- This class concerns suspension arrangements for doors, windows or similar wings that employ balls or floating rollers as the supporting element. The devices use spherical or freely rotating rollers to carry the wing's weight and enable smooth opening and closing.
[E05D 15/0647] on sliding blocks -- The subclass E05D 15/0647 covers suspension arrangements for doors, windows or wings that employ sliding blocks to support, guide or restrain the moving part. It pertains to mechanisms where the wing or panel rests on or moves along blocks that slide within a fixed frame, often used in lock or hinge systems for fixed constructions.
[E05D 15/0652] Tracks  E05D15/063  -  E05D15/0647  and  E05D15/0656  take precedence -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Track arrangements for horizontally sliding wings suspended at the top of the wing.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Details of the suspension arrangement of top-suspended horizontally sliding wings related to wheels, rollers, and sliding blocks -> E05D15/063 - E05D15/0647 - Details of the suspension arrangement of top-suspended horizontally sliding wings related to bottom wing guides -> E05D15/0656
[E05D 15/0656] Bottom guides -- E05D 15/0656 relates to bottom guides used in suspension arrangements for wings such as sliding doors or windows. The guides support and direct the lower edge of the moving wing, ensuring smooth travel and preventing sagging or impact with the floor.
[E05D 15/066] for wings supported at the bottom -- The CPC subclass E05D 15/066 covers suspension arrangements for wing doors in which the wing is supported at its lower edge. It falls under fixed constructions, locks, and hinges or suspension devices for doors, windows or wings, and specifically addresses bottom-supported wing mechanisms.
[E05D 15/0665] on wheels with fixed axis -- E05D 15/0665 covers suspension arrangements for doors, windows or wings that use wheels mounted on a fixed axle. The wheels guide the wing's movement, allowing it to swing open or closed while the axis remains stationary.
[E05D 15/0669] with height adjustment -- The subclass E05D 15/0669 concerns suspension arrangements for doors, windows or similar wings that incorporate a means for adjusting the height of the wing after installation. It falls within the fixed-construction and lock categories, specifically addressing hinges or other devices that allow the upward or downward positioning of the suspended element.
[E05D 15/0673] by vertical bolts -- It covers suspension arrangements for doors, windows or wings that are mounted or supported using vertical bolts, a particular method of attaching the hinge or suspension mechanism to a frame. This falls under the broader categories of fixed constructions, locks, and hinges or suspension devices for doors, windows or wings.
[E05D 15/0678] on balls or floating rollers -- The subclass E05D 15/0678 defines suspension arrangements for doors, windows or wings that employ balls or floating rollers to support and guide the moving element. These devices provide low-friction movement and are a specific type of hinge or suspension mechanism within the fixed constructions group.
[E05D 15/0682] on sliding blocks -- E05D 15/0682 refers to sliding blocks used in suspension arrangements for doors, windows or wing panels, forming part of the hinge or suspension device that guides or supports the movement of the opening along a track or rail.
[E05D 15/0686] Tracks  E05D15/0665  -  E05D15/0682  and  E05D15/0691  take precedence -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Track arrangements for horizontally sliding wings supported at the bottom of the wing.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Details of the support arrangement of bottom-supported horizontally sliding wings related to wheels, rollers, and sliding blocks -> E05D15/0665 - E05D15/0682 - Details of the support arrangement of bottom-supported horizontally sliding wings related to top wing guides -> E05D15/0691
[E05D 15/0691] Top guides -- E05D 15/0691 Top guides are upper guide elements that direct and stabilize the movement of doors, windows or wings in suspension arrangements. Positioned at the top of the opening, they keep the panel aligned and prevent sagging during operation, often forming part of a lock or hinge system in fixed constructions.
[E05D 15/08] consisting of two or more independent parts movable each in its own guides -- The CPC subclass E05D 15/08 covers suspension arrangements for wings in which the wing is formed from two or more separate parts, each part moving independently within its own guiding track. This design allows the sections to be adjusted or opened individually while remaining part of a fixed construction such as a door or window.

[E05D 15/18] consisting of two or more independent parts, movable each in its own guides -- This CPC subclass covers wing suspension systems built from two or more separate components, each movable in its own guide. [E05D 15/20] movable out of one plane into a second parallel plane -- The CPC subclass E05D 15/20 covers devices that support a door, window or wing so that it can be displaced from its original closed plane into a second plane that runs parallel to it, typically by a hinge, pivot or sliding suspension mechanism.

[E05D 15/242] Hinge connections between the parts -- E05D 15/242 classifies hinge connections that join the moving parts of suspension arrangements for doors, windows or wings. This subclass falls under the broader categories of fixed constructions, locks and hinges or suspension devices for doors, windows or wings.
[E05D 15/244] Upper part guiding means -- E05D 15/244 - Upper part guiding means - covers mechanisms that guide the upper edge of a suspended wing, such as a roof or door, ensuring proper alignment and smooth movement during opening or closing. These devices may include rollers, tracks or guide rails that cooperate with the suspension system to prevent lateral displacement.
[E05D 15/246] with additional guide rail for producing an additional movement -- E05D 15/246 covers suspension arrangements for doors, windows or wings that incorporate an extra guide rail to create an additional movement, extending the basic hinge or lock mechanism. [E05D 15/248] with lever arms for producing an additional movement -- The E05D 15/248 subclass covers hinges or suspension devices for doors, windows or wings that incorporate lever arms to create an extra movement beyond the basic swing. [E05D 15/26] for folding wings -- E05D 15/26 covers suspension arrangements for folding wings, encompassing mechanisms that support and guide wing-shaped elements such as shutters, doors or panels when they are folded. These devices belong to the fixed-construction group dealing with hinges, locks or other suspension systems for doors, windows or wings.
[E05D 15/262] folding vertically -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Suspension arrangements for wings folding vertically, i.e. panels/wings folding upwardly about horizontal fold-lines.  **Glossary:** - Wings folding vertically: wings folding upwardly, i.e. folding about a horizontal fold-line
[E05D 15/264] for bi-fold wings -- This subclass covers suspension mechanisms designed to support and guide bi-fold doors or windows. [E05D 15/425] specially adapted for overhead wings -- This CPC subclass covers suspension mechanisms designed specifically for overhead wings such as garage doors, skylights, or similar movable panels. [E05D 15/46] with two pairs of pivoted arms -- E05D 15/46 covers suspension mechanisms for wing doors or windows that employ two pairs of pivoted arms. [E05D 15/463] specially adapted for overhead wings -- The subclass E05D 15/463 covers suspension arrangements that are specially adapted for overhead wings, such as awnings, roof sections or other hinged or sliding wing elements that open upward. [E05D 15/466] specially adapted for windows -- This subclass covers suspension mechanisms specially adapted for windows, including devices that support, hold, or control the movement of window wings. [E05D 15/48] allowing alternative movements -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Arrangements for wings sliding horizontally -> E05D15/0604 - Arrangements for wings sliding vertically -> E05D15/22
[E05D 15/50] for opening at either of two opposite edges -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Arrangements of doors, windows, flaps or other wings that can, in use, be opened selectively at either of two opposite edges.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Hinges or pivots of special construction to allow easy separation or connection of the parts at the hinge axis (for easy mounting and de-mounting) -> E05D7/10 - Hinges allowing easy detachment from the wing or the frame (for easy mounting and de-mounting) -> E05D7/12
[E05D 15/502] by axial separation of the hinge parts at the hinge axis -- E05D 15/502 classifies suspension arrangements for doors, windows or wings that operate by separating the hinge parts along the hinge axis, creating an axial gap between the components to enable movement.
[E05D 15/505] by radial separation of the hinge parts at the hinge axis -- The code E05D 15/505 classifies hinge or suspension devices for doors, windows or wing panels that achieve movement by separating the hinge components radially from the hinge axis. This radial separation creates a gap at the axis, allowing the wing to swing or be adjusted while maintaining a fixed construction.
[E05D 15/507] by detachment of the hinge from the wing or the frame -- This subclass covers suspension arrangements for doors, windows or wing panels in which the hinge is detached from the moving wing or from the frame, allowing the wing to be removed, folded or swung away while support is provided by a separate attachment mechanism.
[E05D 15/52] for opening about a vertical as well as a horizontal axis -- E05D 15/52 covers mechanisms that allow a door, window or similar wing to be opened by rotating about a vertical axis and also about a horizontal axis. The devices combine swing and tilt motions, enabling the wing to swing outward or tilt inward for access or ventilation.
[E05D 15/5202] with non-horizontally extending checks
[E05D 15/5205] with horizontally-extending checks -- E05D 15/5205 classifies suspension arrangements for doors, windows or wing sections that include horizontally-extending checks to restrain the movement of the wing, typically used in lock or hinge assemblies to improve stability and control opening angles.
[E05D 15/5208] with means for transmitting movements between vertical and horizontal sliding bars, rods or cables -- E05D 15/5208 covers suspension arrangements for wings that incorporate mechanisms to transfer motion between vertical and horizontal sliding bars, rods or cables, allowing coordinated movement of door, window or wing sections.
[E05D 15/5211] Concealed suspension fittings -- E05D 15/5211 relates to concealed suspension fittings that attach doors, windows or other wings to a frame while remaining hidden from view. The fittings provide secure, adjustable support for the moving element without exposing visible hardware.
[E05D 15/5214] Corner supports -- This CPC subclass (E05D 15/5214) covers corner supports used in suspension arrangements for wings of doors, windows or other movable panels. It specifies devices that reinforce or stabilize the corner region of a hinged or suspended wing, ensuring proper alignment and load distribution during operation.
[E05D 15/5217] Tilt-lock devices -- Tilt-lock devices are mechanisms that secure a door, window or other wing in a tilted or partially open position, typically forming part of a suspension arrangement for the wing. They belong to the fixed-construction group of locks and are classified under the CPC code E05D 15/5217.
[E05D 15/522] with disconnecting means for the appropriate pivoting parts -- E05D 15/522 covers suspension arrangements for door, window or wing panels that incorporate a disconnecting mechanism to release the pivoting parts, allowing the wing to be separated from its hinge or suspension device when needed. It falls under fixed constructions, locks, hinges or suspension devices for doors, windows or wings.
[E05D 15/523] using movable rods -- The code E05D 15/523 covers suspension arrangements for doors, windows or wing panels that employ movable rods to support and adjust the element's position. Such systems use rods that can be shifted or re-aligned to change the angle or height of the suspended component, enabling controlled opening, closing or tilt functions.
[E05D 15/524] Actuating mechanisms -- E05D 15/524 designates actuating mechanisms that generate the opening or closing movement of wing doors or windows within suspension arrangements. It includes levers, cams, hydraulic, pneumatic or electric devices that drive the wing through the hinge or lock system.
[E05D 15/526] Safety devices -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Tilt-lock devices -> E05D15/5217
[E05D 15/54] for opening both inwards and outwards -- E05D 15/54 covers suspension arrangements for doors, windows or other wings that permit the element to swing in either direction, enabling it to open both inwardly and outwardly. The classification pertains to hinge or suspension devices designed for bidirectional movement of the wing.
[E05D 15/56] with successive different movements -- The subclass E05D 15/56 covers suspension arrangements for doors, windows or wings that provide a series of different movements in succession, such as opening, tilting, folding or rotating in a defined order. It specifies mechanisms that enable the wing to change position through multiple distinct actions rather than a single motion.
[E05D 15/565] for raising wings before sliding -- E05D 15/565 covers suspension mechanisms that lift a wing upward before it slides, enabling the wing to clear a frame or obstruction during opening. The arrangement is used for roof windows, skylights, awnings or similar hinged or sliding panels.
[E05D 15/58] with both swinging and sliding movements -- E05D 15/58 covers suspension systems for doors, windows or wing sections that allow the element to both swing and slide. The classification includes mechanisms that combine hinge action with a sliding track, enabling mixed motion for opening or closing. It falls under fixed constructions, locks, and hinges or suspension devices.
[E05D 15/581] the swinging axis laying in the sliding direction  E05D15/1015  takes precedence -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Suspension arrangements for wings performing successive swinging and sliding movements, wherein the swinging axis lies parallel to the sliding direction.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Windows with an intermediate tilt position -> E05D15/1015
[E05D 15/582] with horizontal swinging axis  E05D15/581  takes precedence -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Suspension arrangements for wings performing successive swinging and sliding movements, with a horizontal swinging axis.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Wings with the swinging axis laying in the sliding direction -> E05D15/581
[E05D 15/583] specially adapted for overhead wings -- The code E05D 15/583 covers suspension arrangements that are specially adapted for overhead wings, such as awnings, garage-door panels or other horizontal wing elements. It includes hinges and other devices designed to support, guide and operate these wings when they are raised, lowered or folded, providing secure and controlled movement in fixed constructions.
[E05D 2001/045] for telescopic hinges -- E05D 2001/045 covers hinges whose leafs can slide relative to each other, providing a telescopic movement. These hinges are used where an adjustable or extending opening is required, such as in folding doors, shutters, or furniture. The code falls under the CPC group for hinge constructions that permit linear displacement while maintaining a pivot connection.
[E05D 2003/025] having three knuckles -- The subclass covers devices that incorporate three knuckles as articulated joint elements, used to permit pivoting or transfer motion while supporting loads. It includes hinges, couplings, and similar mechanisms where three knuckles form the functional structure, typically applied in construction, handling equipment, or related tools.
[E05D 2003/027] the end knuckles being mutually connected -- E05D 2003/027 covers hinges whose end knuckles are joined together, forming a continuous or mutually linked knuckle assembly that allows rotation of a door, window or similar movable building element. This design detail pertains to the way the hinge's terminal knuckles are connected to each other rather than being separate.
[E05D 2003/163] Horizontal pivot-axis -- E05D 2003/163 classifies mechanisms that provide a horizontal pivot axis for moving parts such as doors, gates, covers or panels, allowing them to swing open or closed around a line parallel to the ground. The code covers the structural features of the pivot, its mounting and any related locking or sealing elements that enable horizontal movement.
[E05D 2003/166] Vertical pivot-axis -- The CPC subclass E05D 2003/166 covers connections and joint elements whose main pivot or rotation axis is oriented vertically. It includes devices such as vertical hinges, rotating brackets and other mechanisms that allow building components to swing or turn around a vertical line. Applications range from doors and shutters to rotating panels and roof sections.
[E05D 2005/0253] the panels having conical or stepped recesses -- This CPC subgroup concerns building panels such as doors, windows or façade elements that incorporate conical or stepped recesses, providing decorative texture, light-scattering effects or mounting features.
[E05D 2005/0261] connecting two or more glass panels -- E05D 2005/0261 covers arrangements for joining two or more glass panels, including sealing, bonding, clamping or mechanical fastening techniques used in windows, facades or glazing systems to create a continuous or laminated glass unit.
[E05D 2005/0269] the panels being coplanar -- The CPC subclass E05D 2005/0269 covers constructions in which building panels are arranged to lie in the same plane, i.e., coplanar, such as wall or façade systems with flat, continuous panel surfaces. It is used to classify patents describing methods or structures that ensure panels remain coplanar during installation or service.
[E05D 2005/0284] for embedding in concrete or masonry -- E05D 2005/0284 falls within the CPC group for anchoring or securing elements and specifies devices, materials or methods intended to be embedded in concrete or masonry, such as anchors, inserts, reinforcement bars, sensors or other fittings that become integral to the hardened structure.
[E05D 2005/0292] for passing through insulating layers -- A device, component or method that enables pipes, cables, ducts or other services to pass through a building's insulating layer while preserving the thermal barrier, such as penetration sleeves, sealing collars or connectors designed for the purpose.
[E05D 2005/067] gooseneck shaped -- The entry E05D 2005/067 designates features that are gooseneck shaped, i.e. a curved profile resembling a goose's neck, typically applied to hinges, brackets or supporting arms in doors, windows, shutters or similar building elements. It classifies inventions where this shape provides a specific motion or aesthetic function.
[E05D 2005/102] Pins -- E05D 2005/102 covers pins used in earth-moving and construction equipment, such as fastening or alignment pins that secure components like buckets, blades, frames or hydraulic cylinders to the machine structure. The classification groups inventions relating to the design, material, shape or mounting of such pins for improved strength, wear resistance or ease of assembly.
[E05D 2005/104] characterised by the materials -- E05D 2005/104 covers lift (elevator) inventions that are defined primarily by the specific materials employed in their construction, such as the car, guide rails, ropes, or housing components. The classification is used to capture material-based improvements or alternatives within lift technology.
[E05D 2005/106] with non-cylindrical portions -- This CPC subclass covers building components that incorporate shapes other than cylinders, such as rectangular, square or irregular cross-sections. It is used for patents on walls, beams, columns or other structural parts where the profile is non-cylindrical. It falls under the E05D main group for building elements.
[E05D 2005/108] with elastically deformable parts -- This subclass groups doors, windows, shutters, gates and similar building-opening devices that incorporate elastically deformable components such as flexible seals, spring-biased latches or rubber gaskets, enabling elastic movement or sealing. It is applied to inventions where the elastic element is an essential functional part of the closure or regulation mechanism.
[E05D 2005/122] externally threaded -- E05D 2005/122 covers doors, windows, shutters, gates or similar building elements that incorporate external threading, such as rods, bolts or screws visible on the outer surface and used for securing, adjusting or operating the component.
[E05D 2005/124] internally threaded -- The CPC code E05D 2005/124 falls under class E for fixed building structures, subclass 05 for building elements, and group D for doors, windows and similar openings; the "internally threaded" sub-category designates those components that incorporate a screw thread on the interior side for fastening, adjustment or locking purposes.
[E05D 2005/145] with elastically deformable parts -- This CPC subgroup covers windows, doors, roofs, shutters or similar building elements that incorporate elastically deformable components such as flexible seals, springs, or gaskets, which allow the element to flex, seal or return to position during operation.
[E05D 2007/0036] with axially fixed hinge pins -- This CPC subgroup refers to doors, windows or shutters whose hinge pins are fixed on the hinge axis, meaning the pins are integral and not removable, providing a permanent hinge connection. Such designs are employed where a robust, tamper-resistant hinge is required.
[E05D 2007/0063] Eccentric hinge pins -- E05D 2007/0063 relates to hinge pins whose axis is offset from the centre of the pin, creating an eccentric pivot. The eccentric arrangement permits adjustment of the relative position or angle between hinged members such as doors, windows or panels, enabling fine-tuning of clearances, alignment or closing force.
[E05D 2007/0072] with sliding sleeves -- E05D 2007/0072 covers doors, windows or similar openings that incorporate sliding sleeves, which act as movable guides or protective covers that travel along a fixed track to seal, insulate or secure the aperture during operation. The sleeves may be positioned on the interior or exterior of the frame and are designed to move in coordination with the opening element. This subclass groups mechanisms where the sliding sleeve is an essential functional component.
[E05D 2007/0081] with swinging or rolling sleeves -- E05D 2007/0081 covers closure devices such as doors, gates, windows or shutters that use sleeves which swing or roll to open, close or seal an opening. The subclass includes mechanisms where a rotating or rolling sleeve provides the moving element, for example rolling-sleeve shutters, swing-sleeve doors and similar arrangements.
[E05D 2007/0438] with bolts fixedly mounted on the hinge part -- E05D 2007/0438 classifies hinges or articulation mechanisms for doors, windows or lids that incorporate bolts permanently attached to the hinge part, providing a built-in locking or securing function without separate, removable bolts. This subclass addresses designs where the bolt is an integral component of the hinge assembly.
[E05D 2007/0446] with threaded bolts fixedly mounted on the hinge part -- E05D 2007/0446 falls within the CPC class for doors, windows, shutters and similar openings and specifies hinges that incorporate threaded bolts permanently fixed to the hinge part. The classification is used to identify hardware where the bolts are mounted on the hinge itself for secure attachment or adjustment of the moving leaf. It is employed by both the EPO and USPTO in patent documents.
[E05D 2007/0453] with threaded sleeves -- E05D 2007/0453 covers building elements such as doors, windows or panels that incorporate threaded sleeves for attaching fittings, hardware or reinforcement. The sleeves are machined or formed to receive screws, bolts or other threaded fasteners, allowing adjustable or removable connections within the construction. This subclass refines broader categories of hinged or sliding building parts by specifying the presence of threaded sleeve components.
[E05D 2007/0461] in angular arrangement to the wing or the frame -- The CPC entry E05D 2007/0461 classifies roof or covering elements that are mounted at an angle to a wing or supporting frame, such as sloped, cantilevered or otherwise angularly arranged panels. It is used for patents describing the geometric relationship of roofing components to adjoining structural members.
[E05D 2007/0469] in an axial direction -- E05D 2007/0469 covers doors, windows, shutters, blinds or similar building elements whose operative part moves linearly along its own axis, i.e., in an axial direction rather than rotating or swinging. The classification applies to mechanisms that translate the closure element inward or outward parallel to its longitudinal axis.
[E05D 2007/0476] Pocket hinges -- E05D 2007/0476 covers pocket hinges, which are hinge mechanisms that let a door, panel or window fold and be stored within a recessed pocket in a surrounding structure, providing space-saving opening and closing. These hinges are used in furniture, cabinets, doors and similar constructions where a concealed hinge is desired.
[E05D 2007/0484] in a radial direction -- E05D 2007/0484 classifies hoisting or lifting devices that operate or transmit force in a radial direction relative to the axis of the machine, such as mechanisms where motion is applied outward from or toward the center of rotation. This subclass is used for radial drive, radial positioning, or radial force-transmission features in lifts, winches, jacks and related equipment.
[E05D 2007/0492] in three directions -- E05D 2007/0492 relates to building elements that provide passage in three mutually different directions, such as a stair or ramp system where the travel path changes orientation twice, linking separate levels or zones within a structure. This subclass covers configurations that guide users along three distinct directional segments in a single integrated assembly.
[E05D 2007/1027] by axially moving free pins -- The subclass covers locking or fastening mechanisms that achieve engagement or release by the axial movement of one or more free pins, which slide within a housing to lock or unlock a member. It includes devices such as cam locks, drawer bolts, and safety latch systems where the pin is not fixed but moves longitudinally to provide holding positions.
[E05D 2007/1033] by axially moving free balls -- The CPC subgroup E05D 2007/1033 pertains to safety arrangements for lifts, escalators, moving walkways or transport shafts that function by allowing free balls to move axially, usually to detect or block the motion of a carriage, door or other moving element.
[E05D 2007/1038] by axially moving free sockets -- This subclass covers ladders or similar step structures in which the rungs or supporting elements are held in free sockets that can slide axially along the ladder stringer, allowing adjustment, assembly or removal without additional fasteners. The axial movement of the sockets provides a simple, modular means of securing the components.
[E05D 2007/1088] using hinge pins having different lengths -- E05D 2007/1088 pertains to door, window, or shutter fittings that use hinge pins of differing lengths to create staggered pivot points, allow adjustable clearance, or enable multi-position movement. The classification addresses the mechanical advantage and functional benefits of varying pin lengths within the same hinge assembly.
[E05D 2007/1094] Guiding devices therefor -- The subclass covers components that guide and stabilise moving cars, platforms, steps or belts in lifts, escalators, moving walks and similar transport installations, such as guide rails, rollers, sidewalls and related mechanisms that keep the moving element on its intended path.
[E05D 2007/126] in an axial direction -- E05D 2007/126 classifies bridge constructions where structural members are oriented or loaded in an axial direction, i.e., parallel to the longitudinal axis of the bridge. It covers longitudinal girders, decks, tension elements and similar components designed to bear loads along that axis.
[E05D 2007/128] in a radial direction -- E05D 2007/128 designates any component of a building opening that operates or is positioned in a radial direction, meaning the movement or orientation extends outward from a central axis, as with a window sash that pivots about a central point.
[E05D 2011/0036] near the hinge -- The CPC subclass E05D 2011/0036 covers features, treatments or components situated in the area of a door, window, shutter or gate that is adjacent to the hinge. It includes reinforcement, sealing, protective or decorative elements positioned near the hinge line to improve functionality, durability or appearance. This classification is used for inventions that specifically address the hinge-proximate region of such openings.
[E05D 2011/0045] on the hinge -- E05D 2011/0045 pertains to windows, doors, shutters or comparable closure elements that are mounted on a hinge, covering the hinge mechanism, pivot points and associated bearing structures that enable the element to swing relative to its frame.
[E05D 2011/0063] for screw-heads or bolt-heads -- This subgroup comprises features or devices intended to engage or be applied to the heads of screws or bolts, such as adapters, fastening elements, or tool attachments used in construction or building applications. They are classified under E05D for construction elements where the fastener head serves as the functional part of the assembly.
[E05D 2011/0072] for the gap between hinge parts -- This subgroup relates to a feature that provides a clearance or spacing between the leaves of a hinge, typically to accommodate movement, reduce friction, or house sealing elements. It is used in doors, windows or lids where a defined gap improves operation or enables sealing.
[E05D 2011/009] Impact absorbing hinges for vehicle doors -- E05D 2011/009 covers vehicle door hinges that incorporate mechanisms to absorb or reduce impact energy. The design includes elastic, hydraulic, or deformable elements that lessen forces transferred to the door and vehicle structure during collisions or sudden opening, enhancing passenger safety and reducing damage.
[E05D 2011/045] located in line with the hinge axis -- The CPC code E05D 201/045 relates to doors, gates or similar members whose latch, lock, handle or other functional element is placed directly on the line of the hinge axis, i.e., aligned with the axis about which the panel swings, so that the component operates without any lateral offset. This placement is intended to simplify the actuation mechanism and improve sealing or security.
[E05D 2011/085] the friction depending on the opening angle -- E05D 2011/085 covers door, window or shutter mechanisms in which the resistance to movement changes with the angle of opening, such as hinges or pivots that provide variable friction to control speed or hold position at specific angles. This classification applies to devices that deliberately modulate friction as the element is swung open or closed.
[E05D 2011/088] with automatic disengagement -- E05D 2011/088 covers door, gate or window locking devices that incorporate a mechanism which automatically releases or disengages the lock, for example in response to a power failure, fire alarm or other emergency condition.
[E05D 2011/1035] with circumferential and evenly distributed detents around the pivot-axis -- E05D 2011/1035 covers hand-held tools that feature a pivoting element equipped with circumferential, evenly spaced detents, providing indexed positions around the axis of rotation; examples include ratcheting wrenches, socket drivers, and similar devices where the detents give precise angular stops.
[E05D 2011/1092] the angle between the hinge parts being adjustable -- E05D 2011/1092 covers hinges whose opening angle can be altered after fitting, allowing the relative position of the hinged parts to be adjusted to suit different opening requirements. It applies to doors, windows, shutters and similar assemblies where the hinge includes a mechanism for setting or varying the angle between the connected members.
[E05D 2015/0695] Magnetic suspension or supporting means -- E05D 2015/0695 covers magnetic suspension or supporting means used in building construction and civil engineering. It includes systems that employ magnetic forces to levitate, stabilize or bear loads such as elevator cars, moving platforms, or structural components, allowing contact-free support and movement. The classification groups technologies that replace mechanical bearings with magnetic levitation for safety, wear reduction and precision control.
[E05D 2015/1018] with the track rotating around its axis -- The CPC entry E05D 2015/1018 covers conveyor tracks that rotate around their own longitudinal axis, such as cylindrical rollers, rotating drums or chain links that turn as the payload moves. This design enables continuous transport of goods while the track itself provides the motion rather than relying on external drive mechanisms.
[E05D 2015/1026] accessories, e.g. sliding or rolling guides, latches -- The CPC subclass E05D 2015/1026 covers accessories for opening or closing building elements such as doors, windows or shutters, including sliding or rolling guide rails, rollers and latch mechanisms that aid movement or secure closure.
[E05D 2015/1028] with only the wing moving transversely -- The code designates a building element-such as a door, window, shutter or awning-in which the movable wing is constrained to travel only sideways across the opening, sliding or shifting transversely without any pivoting or rotating action. This classification covers mechanisms that provide opening or closing purely by lateral movement of the wing.
[E05D 2015/1031] the wing supported on arms extending from the carriage -- E05D 2015/1031 pertains to agricultural or horticultural machines in which a functional wing-such as a cutter, harvester, or sprayer-is mounted on a carriage and held out by arms extending from that carriage, enabling the wing to be positioned relative to the ground or the crop.
[E05D 2015/1034] the carriage having means for preventing rotation of the wing -- The code E05D 2015/1034 covers a transport carriage that supports a wing-type element and includes mechanisms to restrict the wing's rotation while moving or positioning it. It is used for the safe handling of large aerodynamic or blade structures such as aircraft wings or wind-turbine blades during transport, assembly, or installation.
[E05D 2015/1036] the arms being movable in vertical, e.g. transverse, planes -- This CPC subclass covers doors, shutters, gates, louvres or similar building elements whose arms are designed to move in vertical or transverse directions, enabling opening, closing or positional adjustment. It includes mechanisms that allow the arms to pivot, slide, lift or swing within these planes.
[E05D 2015/1039] the wing sliding transversely on the carriage -- The CPC subclass E05D 2015/1039 covers containers whose wing or flap moves transversely relative to a carriage that supports it. It pertains to mechanisms that allow the wing to slide sideways for opening, closing or adjusting the opening, typically found in doors, hatches or dispensing lids.
[E05D 2015/1049] the carriage swinging or rotating in a transverse plane -- E05D 2015/1049 covers elevator or similar transport carriages whose cabin is allowed to swing side-to-side or rotate about a vertical axis while moving, providing a transverse-plane motion for passenger orientation, smoother boarding or specialized applications. This feature is classified under the broader group for elevators, escalators and moving walkways.
[E05D 2015/1052] transversely over-dimensioned track sections or carriage -- This subgroup covers railway track sections or vehicle bodies that are intentionally built wider across the track than standard dimensions, allowing them to support oversized loads, enhance stability or accommodate special functions. It includes design features that extend the transverse width of the track or carriage beyond the normal gauge.
[E05D 2015/1055] with slanted or curved track sections or cams -- E05D 2015/1055 covers elevators, escalators and moving walks that incorporate slanted or curved track sections or cams in their guide or drive mechanisms. The classification is used to identify designs where the moving carriage follows non-linear paths to achieve specific transport functions.
[E05D 2015/1057] the carriage swinging or rotating in those track sections -- E05D 2015/1057 covers arrangements in which a carriage, platform or vehicle body is designed to swing or rotate while moving on a track section, such as swivel track segments, turntables, or rotating bridges that allow the load to change direction without detaching from the rails. This includes mechanisms that permit the carriage to pivot around a vertical axis or to oscillate laterally within the track geometry.
[E05D 2015/106] transversely orientated track sections -- E05D 201/106 covers modular track sections that are positioned transverse to the direction of travel, typically as parts of road, railway or tramway structures. These sections are designed to be installed across the main carriageway to provide reinforcement, facilitate crossings, or accommodate utilities while maintaining a continuous surface.
[E05D 2015/1063] disconnecting the carriage from the track -- The entry addresses devices or methods for releasing a rail-carriage from its rails, enabling the carriage to be removed, repositioned or serviced without dismantling the whole track. It includes mechanisms such as quick-release couplings, retractable guide elements or specialised track sections that allow the carriage to be safely and efficiently disconnected from the running line.
[E05D 2015/1071] the track being directly linked to the fixed frame, e.g. slidingly -- The CPC subclass E05D 2015/1071 covers stair, ladder, escalator or moving-walkway systems in which the travel track is attached directly to a stationary frame and moves by sliding rather than by rotating or being carried on a separate guide. This includes designs where the tread or step slides along the fixed structure, providing a direct, non-detachable link between the moving element and the frame.
[E05D 2015/1073] rocking transversely -- The CPC code E05D 2015/1073 refers to lock mechanisms in which the locking element operates by a transverse rocking motion, such as a latch that swings sideways to engage or release a strike plate.
[E05D 2015/1076] swinging transversely, e.g. on arms -- E05D 2015/1076 covers mechanisms that cause a load or lifting arm to swing transversely, for example by pivoting the arms of a crane or hoist to move the load side-to-side during handling. It includes designs where the transverse motion is achieved by articulated arms, linkages or swinging fixtures.
[E05D 2015/1078] swinging or rotating in a horizontal plane -- The CPC code E05D 2015/1078 relates to building or construction elements whose functional parts swing or rotate in a horizontal plane, such as turntables, rotating platforms, swing arms or mechanisms that enable 360° movement about a vertical axis. This sub-group is used to classify structures or devices where the primary motion is horizontal rotation rather than vertical pivoting.
[E05D 2015/1084] the carriage being directly linked to the fixed frame, e.g. slidingly -- E05D 2015/1084 designates earth-moving or anchoring equipment in which the carriage is attached directly to the fixed frame and moves by sliding rather than by a rotating or hinged connection. The class is used for machines such as drilling rigs or anchoring devices where the carriage's motion is confined to a linear path along the frame.
[E05D 2015/1086] swingingly, e.g. on arms -- The CPC entry E05D 2015/1086 relates to apparatuses that achieve a swinging motion by means of arms, such as safety or fire-control devices whose functional elements are mounted on articulated arms that swing during operation. It covers the design and use of such swingingly-mounted components in building-environment systems.
[E05D 2015/1089] the carriage having means for preventing rotation of the wing -- The subclass concerns carriages for wind-energy or similar wing devices that incorporate elements to stop the wing from rotating about its axis. It includes designs with locks, guides, bearings or other restricting structures that hold the wing in a fixed orientation during operation.
[E05D 2015/1092] the carriage swinging or rotating in curved track sections -- The subclass concerns transport or support units that move on a curved track and are capable of swinging, rotating or otherwise pivoting relative to the track so that the carriage can align, turn or adapt its orientation while following the curve. It covers mechanisms used in material-handling conveyors, amusement-ride cars, mining trams and similar systems where the carriage's angular movement is integral to operation.
[E05D 2015/1094] disconnecting itself from the track -- This invention relates to a rail vehicle or carriage that can automatically detach from the rails, using sensors and a release mechanism to separate wheels or bogies in case of obstacle detection, emergency stop, or maintenance.
[E05D 2015/1097] with the carriage and track forming a telescopic element -- This CPC subgroup covers lifting devices in which the carriage and its guide track are combined to form a telescopic element, allowing the assembly to extend and retract as the carriage slides within the track. It applies to cranes, hoists, elevators and similar equipment where a telescoping carriage-track arrangement provides variable height or reach.
[E05D 2015/225] specially adapted for overhead wings -- The CPC entry E05D 2015/225 refers to fittings or structures that are specially adapted for overhead wings of agricultural or horticultural buildings, such as greenhouses, poultry houses, or similar enclosures. It includes devices for supporting, moving, adjusting, or securing the wing sections that extend above the main roof, often to provide ventilation, shading, or access.
[E05D 2015/268] the wings being successively folded -- E05D 2015/268 relates to the design of wing-type appendages on agricultural or forestry machines that are folded one after another rather than all at once. The classification covers mechanisms that allow a series of wing sections to be successively collapsed, reducing the overall width of the equipment for transport or storage while maintaining structural integrity when extended for operation.
[E05D 2015/482] for panic doors -- E05D 2015/482 relates to doors specifically designed for panic escape, featuring panic hardware that allows rapid opening in emergencies. The classification covers the construction, mounting and operating mechanisms of such doors used in public and commercial buildings.
[E05D 2015/485] Swinging or sliding movements -- E05D 2015/485 covers designs and mechanisms that allow doors, windows, shutters or similar openings to be moved by swinging, sliding or related actions, including hinges, tracks, rollers and control devices that facilitate such motion.
[E05D 2015/487] Tilting or swinging movements -- The CPC subclass E05D 2015/487 groups patents that disclose devices for tilting or swinging the movement of doors, windows, shutters or similar closure elements. It includes hinges, pivots, swing arms and other mechanisms that convert rotational or angular motion into opening or closing actions. Used in building and vehicle applications.
[E05D 2015/5263] acting parallel to the plane of the wing -- E05D 2015/5263 covers wing-mounted devices whose motion runs parallel to the wing's longitudinal plane, such as flaps, slats or ailerons that slide or rotate along the wing direction to modify lift, drag or control characteristics. The classification is used for patents describing the structure, actuation means and aerodynamic effects of such parallel-acting wing elements.
[E05D 2015/5266] acting perpendicular to the plane of the wing -- E05D 2015/5266 covers mechanisms, devices or methods that cause a movement or force perpendicular to the plane of an aircraft wing. It includes flaps, spoilers, leading-edge devices, winglets or any actuation that changes camber or lift by moving outward or upward relative to the wing surface.
[E05D 2015/585] with stationary hinge parts -- E05D 2015/585 covers doors, windows, shutters, gates and similar constructions that incorporate stationary hinge parts, meaning the hinge is fixed to one component while the other moves relative to it. It includes designs where the hinge remains non-movable during the opening or closing action, such as fixed-frame hinges for doors or windows. This subgroup specifies the structural features of the stationary hinge arrangement.
[E05D 2015/586] with travelling hinge parts -- E05D 2015/586 covers doors, gates or similar closures that incorporate travelling hinge parts, meaning hinge mechanisms that move along a defined path as the door opens or closes, such as pivot, swing or sliding hinges that travel with the door leaf. This subgroup is used for inventions where the hinge itself is part of the moving assembly rather than a fixed pivot point.
[E05D 2015/587] with axially separating hinge parts -- A door, window, shutter, gate or similar construction that uses a hinge in which the hinge leaves are positioned on opposite sides of the hinge axis, so the hinge parts are separated axially rather than overlapping one another. This arrangement provides a clear gap between the moving and fixed sections while allowing rotation about the axis.
[E05D 2015/588] with radially separating hinge parts -- This subclass includes hinges whose cooperating parts are arranged so that they are spaced apart in a radial direction from the hinge axis, allowing the hinged element to swing while keeping a clear gap. Such designs are used for doors, windows, lids and similar structures to provide smooth movement, avoid interference and often improve sealing.
[E05D 2700/00] Hinges or other suspension devices especially for doors or windows -- The CPC subclass E05D 2700/00 covers hinges and other suspension devices specifically for doors, windows or wing panels. It includes mechanisms that support and enable pivoting, swinging or folding motion, such as pivot, butt, concealed or spring hinges and related mounting hardware.
[E05D 2700/02] Hinges with one pivot axis and one bearing surface -- This classification covers hinges that operate on a single pivot axis and use a single bearing surface to support the movement, typically employed in doors, windows or similar components. It includes designs where the hinge provides rotation about one axis with one contact point, distinguishing them from multi-axis or multi-bearing hinges.
[E05D 2700/04] Hinges with one pivot axis and more than one bearing surface -- The subclass E05D 2700/04 classifies hinges that operate on a single pivot axis while providing two or more bearing surfaces to support and distribute the load of doors, windows or wings, improving stability and smooth movement. It is nested under fixed constructions, locks and hinges or suspension devices for doors, windows or wings.
[E05D 2700/10] Various door and window fittings, e.g. suspension devices for double hung windows or screens -- This group includes fittings used to suspend doors or windows, such as brackets, reels, chains and other devices that support double-hung windows, screens or similar openings. It covers hardware that holds or guides movable panels rather than the hinge itself.
[E05D 2700/12] Suspension devices for doors or windows movable in a direction perpendicular to their plane or pivotable about an axis being situated at a considerable distance from the edge of the wing by means of pivot arms -- E05D 2700/12 covers suspension devices that allow doors or windows to move out of their plane or to swing about an axis located far from the edge of the wing, using pivot arms to provide the motion. These mechanisms are a subclass of hinges or other suspension devices for doors, windows or wings within the CPC system.
[E05D 3/00] Hinges with pins -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Hinges characterized by the nature of the pivotal connection, i.e. the number and type of hinge pins and arms.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Spigots placed at opposite edges of the wing, e.g. trunnions -> E05D7/08
[E05D 3/02] with one pin -- E05D 3/02 relates to hinges that use a single pin as the sole pivot for connecting doors, windows or other movable wings to their frames. The pin provides the only rotating support, bearing the leaf's weight while allowing opening and closing. This subclass is nested under fixed constructions, locks and hinges or suspension devices.
[E05D 3/022] allowing an additional lateral movement, e.g. for sealing -- This subclass covers pin-type hinges that include a provision for lateral displacement of the hinged leaf relative to the frame, often to improve sealing or accommodate tolerances. The additional side movement may be achieved by floating pins, sliding sleeves or other mechanisms that permit a small sidewise shift while the hinge remains functional.
[E05D 3/04] engaging three or more parts, e.g. sleeves, movable relatively to one another for connecting two or more wings to another member -- The code E05D 3/04 classifies hinges that employ pins and involve three or more interlinked components, such as sleeves, which can move relative to each other. It covers devices that connect two or more doors, windows or wings to a common frame or support, allowing coordinated motion of the linked parts.
[E05D 3/06] with two or more pins -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Spigots placed at opposite edges of the wing, e.g. trunnions -> E05D7/08
[E05D 3/08] for swing-doors, i.e. openable by pushing from either side -- E05D 3/08 designates hinges with pins specifically for swing-doors that can be opened by pushing from either side, allowing the door to pivot in both directions. It falls under the hierarchy of fixed constructions, locks, and hinges or suspension devices for doors, windows or wings.
[E05D 3/10] with non-parallel pins -- E05D 3/10 designates hinges that employ pins whose axes are not parallel to one another. Such non-parallel pins are used to allow variable motion or offset positioning in door, window or wing assemblies. The subclass falls under fixed constructions, locks, and hinges or suspension devices for doors, windows or wings.
[E05D 3/12] with two parallel pins and one arm -- A hinge classified under E05D 3/12 comprises two parallel pins that serve as the pivot axes and a single arm attached to one of the pins, linking the moving leaf to the frame. This arrangement provides a simple, robust connection for doors, windows or wing panels while allowing controlled swing.
[E05D 3/122] Gear hinges -- Gear hinges are hinge mechanisms that use interlocking gear teeth between the moving parts to provide controlled rotation and positioning for doors, windows or other hinged members. The gear interface allows smooth adjustment and the ability to lock the hinge in selected angles, improving durability and precise movement.
[E05D 3/125] specially adapted for vehicles -- E05D 3/125 covers pin-type hinges that are specially adapted for use on vehicles, such as automobile, truck or railway doors and other moving panels. The classification includes designs that accommodate vehicle vibrations, space constraints and exposure to weather while providing reliable pivoting action.
[E05D 3/127] for vehicle doors -- The code E05D 3/127 covers hinges that incorporate pins to mount and swing doors on vehicles such as cars, trucks, or buses. It includes designs where the pin acts as the pivot axis and may be removable or lockable to secure the door in position.
[E05D 3/14] with four parallel pins and two arms -- The CPC subclass E05D 3/14 covers hinges that use four parallel pins and two arms to connect a movable wing to a fixed frame. Such hinges provide a sturdy pivot mechanism for doors, windows or other panels, allowing smooth opening and closing while distributing load across multiple pins.
[E05D 3/142] with at least one of the hinge parts having a cup-shaped fixing part, e.g. for attachment to cabinets or furniture  E05D11/1021  takes precedence -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Hinges with devices for preventing movement between relatively-moveable hinge parts, e.g. hold-closed springs -> E05D11/1021
[E05D 3/145] specially adapted for vehicles -- This subclass covers pin-type hinges that are specially adapted for use on vehicles, such as automobile doors, hatches or other movable panels. The designs address automotive requirements for compactness, durability under vibration and impact, and often integrate with latch or lock mechanisms.
[E05D 3/147] for vehicle doors -- This subclass concerns hinges that incorporate pins for mounting vehicle doors, covering the design and arrangement of the pin and hinge leaf that enable the door to open and close.
[E05D 3/16] with seven parallel pins and four arms -- E05D 3/16 refers to fixed-construction hinges that employ seven parallel pins and four arms to join doors, windows or wing sections, a detailed subcategory within the broader class of lock and hinge devices.
[E05D 3/18] with sliding pins or guides -- E05D 3/18 classifies hinges whose pivot pins are designed to slide or run in guides, allowing the hinge axis to be adjusted or to accommodate movement while maintaining alignment. This applies to fixed-construction door, window or wing hardware where the hinge pin is not fixed in place.
[E05D 3/183] with at least one of the hinge parts having a cup-shaped fixing part, e.g. for attachment to cabinets or furniture -- This CPC subgroup covers hinges with pins in which at least one hinge component includes a cup-shaped fixing element for mounting the hinge to cabinets, furniture or similar structures. It falls under fixed constructions, locks, hinges or suspension devices for doors, windows or wings.
[E05D 3/186] Scissors hinges, with two crossing levers and five parallel pins -- E05D 3/186 covers scissors hinges that incorporate two intersecting levers forming a scissor action and are secured by a set of five parallel pins. The configuration provides a strong, compact pivot for doors, windows or other hinged panels.
[E05D 5/00] Construction of single parts, e.g. the parts for attachment -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Features of hinge parts, namely hinge pins, sockets, sleeves and the parts for attachment of the hinge to doors, windows, flaps or other wings and/or for attachment to a fixed structure.
[E05D 5/02] Parts for attachment, e.g. flaps -- E05D 5/02 classifies components that are used to attach elements in fixed constructions, such as flaps, brackets or similar parts that secure doors, windows or wings to their frames. It falls under the hierarchy of fixed constructions - locks - hinges or suspension devices for doors, windows or wings - construction of single parts, e.g. the parts for attachment.
[E05D 5/0207] for attachment to vehicles  E05D5/043 ,  E05D5/062  take precedence -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Flat flaps for attachment to vehicles -> E05D5/043 - Bent flaps for attachment to vehicles -> E05D5/062
[E05D 5/0215] for attachment to profile members or the like -- This CPC subgroup covers fixed constructions for doors, windows or wings, specifically hinges or suspension devices where single parts are manufactured for attachment to profile members or similar elements. It includes brackets, clips or fittings designed to connect such devices to structural profiles.
[E05D 5/0223] with parts, e.g. screws, extending through the profile wall or engaging profile grooves -- E05D 5/0223 relates to building individual parts of lock, hinge or suspension devices for doors, windows or wings where the components are fixed by screws that pass through the profile wall or engage profile grooves, providing a secure attachment method.
[E05D 5/023] with parts extending through the profile wall -- The subclass E05D 5/023 covers fixed-construction fittings where the individual attachment parts, such as hinges, lock mechanisms or suspension devices for doors, windows or wings, pass completely through the profile wall, providing a secure, through-wall connection of the component.
[E05D 5/0238] with parts engaging profile grooves -- E05D 5/0238 classifies individual lock or hinge components that are designed to fit into matching profile grooves, such as latch bars, rollers or mounting brackets that engage a shaped channel for guided attachment and secure positioning of doors, windows or wings.
[E05D 5/0246] for attachment to glass panels -- This CPC subclass pertains to hardware that secures hinge or suspension mechanisms directly to glass panels. It includes brackets, clamps, fittings and other single-part attachments designed to be fixed onto glazing without additional framing. Used in doors, windows or movable wings where the hinge is mounted on transparent surfaces.
[E05D 5/0276] for attachment to cabinets or furniture, the hinge having two or more pins  E05D5/046 ,  E05D5/065 ,  E05D7/125  take precedence -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Flat flaps for attachment to cabinets or furniture -> E05D5/046 - Bent flaps for attachment to cabinets or furniture -> E05D5/065 - Attachment parts to allow easy detachment of the hinge from the wing or frame -> E05D7/125
[E05D 5/04] Flat flaps -- E05D 5/04 covers flat, typically rectangular or square, flaps that are attached to doors, windows or wings as single-part components of hinges, locks or suspension devices. These flat pieces serve as attachment surfaces or reinforcing plates, enabling secure mounting of the moving or locking elements.
[E05D 5/043] specially adapted for vehicles -- The subclass covers hinges, locks and other attachment parts that are specially adapted for use on vehicles such as cars, trucks or aircraft, focusing on designs that can be mounted to vehicle frames and operate under automotive conditions.
[E05D 5/046] specially adapted for cabinets or furniture -- The code E05D 5/046 classifies single-part hinges or suspension devices that are specially adapted for use on cabinets or other furniture. It falls under fixed constructions, specifically locks for doors, windows or wings, and concerns components designed to attach these parts to furniture structures.
[E05D 5/06] Bent flaps -- Bent flaps are single-part, bent-sheet components used in fixed door, window or wing constructions to provide a flexible sealing or locking element, often engaging with a latch, hinge or suspension device. Their shape allows them to fold or pivot for secure attachment while maintaining a simple, integral construction.
[E05D 5/062] specially adapted for vehicles -- E05D 5/062 covers lock or hinge components that are designed specifically for use on vehicles, such as car doors, windows or roof panels. The code pertains to the construction of individual attachment parts adapted to the automotive environment, including measures for vibration, space constraints and corrosion resistance.
[E05D 5/065] specially adapted for cabinets or furniture
[E05D 5/08] of cylindrical shape -- E05D 5/08 designates cylindrical-shaped single parts that form components of locks, hinges or suspension devices for doors, windows or wings. It includes shafts, rods, pins and similar elements that serve as attachment or suspension members in fixed constructions.
[E05D 5/10] Pins, sockets or sleeves -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Devices for selectively disconnecting pins or pivoting parts (in wings opening about a vertical as well as a horizontal axis) -> E05D15/522
[E05D 5/12] Securing pins in sockets, movably or not -- The E05D 5/12 subclass covers the design and construction of securing pins inserted into sockets, whether the pins are fixed or can move. It pertains to fixed construction elements of doors, windows or wings, specifically the components used for attaching or locking mechanisms.
[E05D 5/121] Screw-threaded pins -- E05D 5/121 covers screw-threaded pins that are manufactured as separate components for attaching parts of hinges or suspension devices in doors, windows or wings. [E05D 5/127] by forcing the pin into the socket  E05D5/125  takes precedence -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Non-removable snap-fitted pins -> E05D5/125 - Removable snap-fitted pins -> E05D7/1022 , E05D7/1055
[E05D 5/128] the pin having a recess or through-hole engaged by a securing member -- E05D 5/128 covers pins that incorporate a recess or a through-hole designed to receive a securing element, such as a screw or clip, to lock the pin in place. The feature is used in hinges or suspension devices for doors, windows or wings, forming part of the single components that attach the moving parts to the fixed construction.
[E05D 5/14] Construction of sockets or sleeves -- E05D 5/14 covers the design and manufacture of sockets or sleeves that receive bolts, pins or other fastening elements in fixed constructions such as locks, hinges or suspension devices for doors, windows and wings. The classification applies to single-part components serving as attachment elements, detailing their shape, material and integration within the overall assembly.
[E05D 5/16] to be secured without special attachment parts on the socket or sleeve -- This subclass covers hinges or suspension devices for doors, windows or wings that are fixed to a socket or sleeve without using separate attachment components such as brackets, pins or clips. The securing relies on the shape or integral features of the socket, allowing the part to be attached directly and simply.
[E05D 7/00] Hinges or pivots of special construction -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Hinges or pivots constructed to provide additional hinge-specific features relating especially to features facilitating mounting, de-mounting and setting-up of hinges, e.g. adjustability, easy separation of the hinge parts, easy detachment of the hinge from the wing or frame.  Also covered are hinges or pivots constructed for specific uses such as elongated hinges (e.g. piano-hinges), convertible right-hand or left-hand hinges, hinges placeable on opposite edges of the wing (e.g. trunnions), hinges for safes.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Arrangements for water-tight doors in ships -> B63B43/24 - Heavy bearings in sluice gates -> E02B7/40 - Self-closing hinges -> E05F1/06 , E05F1/12 , E05F3/20 - Hinges with means for raising wings before being turned -> E05F7/02 - Hinges for glasses -> G02C5/22 - Hinges for folding parts of portable electronic devices -> G06F1/1615 , G06F1/1633 , H04M1/02  **Glossary:** - Member(s): Wing(s) or Frame(s)
[E05D 7/0009] Adjustable hinges  E05D7/04  takes precedence -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Hinges featuring internal adjustability, e.g. at the hinge axis.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Hinges adjustable relative to the wing or the frame -> E05D7/04
[E05D 7/0018] at the hinge axis -- The code E05D 7/0018 refers to hinges or pivots of special construction where the description centers on the hinge axis itself, including its formation, placement or integration within the hinge assembly. It applies to doors, windows or other movable panels whose operation depends on the specific characteristics of that axis.
[E05D 7/0027] in an axial direction -- E05D 7/0027 covers hinges or pivots of special construction that operate in an axial direction, meaning the movement of the hinged element follows the axis of the shaft or rod rather than swinging outward. It applies to fixed-construction door, window or wing hardware where the pivoted part rotates about its own longitudinal axis.
[E05D 7/0045] in a radial direction -- E05D 7/0045 relates to hinges or pivots of special construction that operate in a radial direction, meaning the rotation occurs about an axis extending outward from the centre of the door or window wing. It falls under the CPC group for fixed constructions, locks and hinge devices.
[E05D 7/0054] by means of eccentric parts -- E05D 7/0054 covers hinges or pivots that operate through eccentric parts, meaning the moving element is offset from the axis of rotation to create a lever effect. This design allows doors, windows or other wings to swing with improved mechanical advantage or spacing, often used where a compact or concealed pivot is required.
[E05D 7/009] Elongate hinges, e.g. piano-hinges -- Elongate hinges, such as piano-hinges, are classified under E05D 7/009, a subgroup of hinges or suspension devices for doors, windows or wings that belong to the fixed-construction category. These hinges consist of a long, narrow strip that runs continuously along the length of the joint, providing a smooth, uniform pivot for large panels or covers.
[E05D 7/02] for use on the right-hand as well as the left-hand side -- E05D 7/02 covers hinges or pivots of special construction that can be installed on either the right-hand or left-hand side of doors, windows or similar openings. The design allows a single hardware piece to serve on both orientations, simplifying fitting and inventory.
[E05D 7/04] Hinges adjustable relative to the wing or the frame -- E05D 7/04 covers hinge mechanisms that can be adjusted after installation to change the relative position of a door, window or wing with respect to its frame. The adjustment may involve altering the angle, height or alignment to improve fit, sealing or operation, and is used in fixed constructions where precise positioning is required.
[E05D 7/0407] the hinges having two or more pins and being specially adapted for cabinets or furniture -- E05D 7/0407 covers hinges that use two or more pins and are specially designed for cabinets or furniture, falling under the fixed-construction category of lock and hinge devices. These multi-pin hinges provide added stability and are commonly employed in interior cabinetry.
[E05D 7/0415] with adjusting drive means -- This CPC subclass covers hinges or pivots of special construction that incorporate an adjusting drive mechanism to allow precise positioning or tension adjustment of the moving part, typically used in doors, windows or panels. The drive means can be manual or motorised and provides controlled movement.
[E05D 7/0423] Screw-and-nut mechanisms  E05D7/0407 ,  E05D7/043  take precedence -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Hinges with two or more pins, adapted for cabinets or furniture -> E05D7/0407 - Hinges adjustable relative to the wing or the frame by means of dowel attachments -> E05D7/043
[E05D 7/043] by means of dowel attachments -- E05D 7/043 classifies hinges or pivots of special construction that are joined by dowel attachments, where dowels are inserted into the moving and stationary members to secure the connection. This subclass falls under fixed constructions, locks, and hinges or suspension devices for doors, windows or wings.
[E05D 7/06] to allow tilting of the members -- **Glossary:** - Member(s): Wing(s) or frame(s)
[E05D 7/08] for use in suspensions comprising two spigots placed at opposite edges of the wing, especially at the top and the bottom, e.g. trunnions -- E05D 7/08 covers hinges or pivots specially constructed for suspending a door, window or wing by means of two spigots positioned at opposite edges, typically one at the top and one at the bottom, as seen in trunnion-type hinges. This subclass falls under fixed constructions and hinges or suspension devices for doors, windows or wings.
[E05D 7/081] the pivot axis of the wing being situated near one edge of the wing, especially at the top and bottom, e.g. trunnions -- This subclass covers hinges or pivots where the pivot axis is positioned near one edge of the door or wing, typically at the top and bottom, such as trunnion arrangements. It falls under fixed constructions, locks, hinges or suspension devices for doors, windows or wings, specifically hinges or pivots of special construction, code E05D 7/081.
[E05D 7/082] the pivot axis of the wing being situated at a considerable distance from the edges of the wing -- E05D 7/082 covers hinges or pivots whose rotation axis is set well away from the wing's outer edges, allowing the wing to swing with a longer lever arm. This arrangement is used in doors, windows or similar panels where the pivot is positioned at a considerable distance from the edge.
[E05D 7/083] with a fixed pivot axis -- E05D 7/083 pertains to hinges or pivots of special construction that operate with a fixed pivot axis, meaning the shaft about which the leaf swings does not move relative to the frame. These devices are used for doors, windows or wing panels where the hinge stays stationary while the attached part rotates.
[E05D 7/084] with a movable pivot axis -- E05D 7/084 designates hinges or pivots of special construction in which the pivot axis is movable, allowing the hinge line to be adjusted relative to the fixed element. This feature is used in doors, windows or other hinged wings to vary opening angles or alignment.
[E05D 7/085] with two or more pivot axes, e.g. used at the same time -- E05D 7/085 covers hinges or pivots that incorporate two or more pivot axes and can be operated at the same time, allowing multi-axis movement of doors, windows or similar panels, often used in specialized or heavy-duty applications.
[E05D 7/086] Braking devices structurally combined with hinges -- E05D 7/86 covers hinges or pivots that incorporate a braking mechanism to control movement, such as devices that limit opening or closing speed of doors, windows or comparable wings. The braking function is integrated into the hinge structure rather than added as a separate component, providing combined support and stopping action.
[E05D 7/10] to allow easy separation of the parts at the hinge axis -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Hinges for disconnecting pins or pivoting parts in wings opening selectively about either of two opposite edges -> E05D15/50
[E05D 7/1005] by axially moving free pins, balls or sockets -- The code E05D 7/1005 covers hinges or pivots of special construction in which the pivoting element-such as a free pin, ball or socket-moves axially to allow adjustment, accommodate mis-alignment or absorb thermal expansion in doors, windows or wing panels. It falls under the hierarchy of fixed constructions, locks, and hinges or suspension devices for doors, windows or wings.

[E05D 7/1016] requiring a specific angular position -- A hinge or pivot classified under E05D 7/1016 is designed to hold a door, window or wing at a predetermined angle, often using a stop, detent or self-locking mechanism that restricts movement to that specific angular position. This special construction ensures the element remains fixed in the set position during normal use.
[E05D 7/1022] with snap-fitted pins -- This subclass covers hinges or pivots for doors, windows or wings that incorporate snap-fitted pins as the securing element. The pins are inserted by a press-fit action and lock in place without additional fasteners, providing a quick, reusable connection within the special-construction hinge family.
[E05D 7/1044] in an axial direction  E05D7/1005  takes precedence -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Hinges allowing easy separation of the parts at the hinge axis by axially moving parts -> E05D7/1005

The devices include stop hinges, adjustable-angle pivots and other mechanisms that lock or limit movement to a specific opening angle.
[E05D 7/1055] with snap-fitted pins -- This CPC subclass covers hinges or pivots of special construction that use snap-fitted pins to lock components together without separate fasteners. It is situated within fixed constructions, locks, and hinges or suspension devices for doors, windows or wings.

[E05D 7/1066] requiring a specific angular position -- This subclass covers hinges or pivots that are constructed to maintain a door, window or wing at a predetermined angular position, often using built-in stops or adjustable mechanisms to limit movement to a set angle. Such devices are used where precise opening angles are required for security, sealing or functional reasons.
[E05D 7/1072] the pin having a non-circular cross-section -- This CPC subclass covers hinges or pivots in which the central pin is formed with a non-circular cross-section, such as square, rectangular or polygonal shapes. The specially shaped pin provides increased resistance to rotation, improved load distribution or enhanced alignment between the hinge leaves, distinguishing it from standard round-pin hinges.
[E05D 7/1077] with snap-fitted pins -- E05D 7/1077 covers hinges or pivots of special construction that use snap-fitted pins to secure the moving parts. The pins are designed to snap into place without tools, allowing rapid assembly of door, window or wing hinges while maintaining strength and alignment. This subclass falls under fixed constructions, locks, and hinges or suspension devices for doors, windows or wings.
[E05D 7/1083] facilitating simultaneous assembly of a plurality of hinges, e.g. for mounting heavy wings -- The entry relates to devices or methods that allow several hinges to be installed at the same time, a feature especially useful for attaching heavy doors, windows or wing panels. It covers constructions that enable simultaneous mounting of a plurality of hinges, simplifying and speeding up the assembly process.
[E05D 7/12] to allow easy detachment of the hinge from the wing or the frame -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Hinges detachable from wings or frames for use with wings opening about either of two opposite edges -> E05D15/507

[E05D 7/123] specially adapted for cabinets or furniture -- The classification covers hinges or pivot devices that are designed specifically for use in cabinets, furniture or similar fixed constructions.
