[E05D 7/125] the hinge having two or more pins -- The CPC subclass E05D 7/125 lies within fixed constructions, locks and hinges or suspension devices for doors, windows or wings, specifically hinges or pivots of special construction. It covers hinges that employ two or more pins to join the moving parts, distributing load across multiple pin axes for enhanced strength and stability.
[E05D 7/14] Hinges for safes -- E05D 7/14 covers hinges that are specially designed for safes, including heavy-duty and reinforced hinges that support safe doors and provide added security against forced entry. The subclass groups any hinge or pivot construction intended for the particular requirements of safe enclosures, such as high load capacity, tamper-resistant features, or integration with lock mechanisms.
[E05D 9/00] Flaps or sleeves specially designed for making from particular material, e.g. hoop-iron, sheet metal, plastics -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Hinge parts designed for making from a particular material.
[E05D 9/005] from plastics  E05D1/02  takes precedence -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Pinless hinges made from one (elastically deformable) piece -> E05D1/02
[E05F] DEVICES FOR MOVING WINGS INTO OPEN OR CLOSED POSITION -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Devices for moving wings, such as doors or windows, into open or closed position; checks for wings, such as doors or windows; wing fittings not otherwise provided for, concerned with the functioning of the wing.  Gravity-, spring-, or power-operated devices to provide a force input for opening or closing wings, such as door openers and door closers, self-closing hinges and hinges with wing-counterbalancing function.  Also covered are operating mechanisms for wings constructed to convert the force input from a user, motor, opener or closer into movement of a wing, such as cable-, cord-, chain-, or belt-drives, lifting arms, rack and pinion drives or screw and nut arrangements.  The subclass furthermore covers braking devices, stops and buffers for wings as well as controlling means, such as remote wireless control means, and safety devices such as obstruction detection devices.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Counterbalancing means for sliding or lifting wings -> E05D13/10  **Glossary:** - Braking device: Devices constructed to slow down the movement of wings - Closer: Gravity-, spring- or power-operated device constructed to provide a closing force to wings over a substantial part of their movement; includes devices for assisting users in wing-movement or for wing-counterbalancing; a closer can take the shape of a self-closing hinge - Opener: Gravity-, spring- or power-operated device constructed to provide an opening force to wings over a substantial part of their movement; includes devices for assisting users in wing-movement or for wing-counterbalancing - Operating mechanism for wings: Mechanism constructed to convert force inputs from a user, motor, opener or closer into movement of wings - Self-closing hinge: Gravity- or spring-operated hinge constructed to provide a closing force to wings over a substantial part of their movement; includes devices for wing-counterbalancing - Vehicle: Land vehicles, e.g. cars, trucks, buses or trains - Wing: Pivotable, slideable, or otherwise moveable part of doors, windows, flaps, covers or similar structures to prevent traffic through a passageway in building construction and outfitting, fences, domestic appliances, vehicles and furniture; the term also includes drawers, lids of chests, car boots/trunks or car bonnets/hoods
[E05F 1/00] Closers or openers for wings, not otherwise provided for in this subclass -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Gravity-, or spring-operated closers or openers for wings including those in the shape of hinges, i.e. self-closing hinges.
[E05F 1/002] controlled by automatically acting means  for powered-operated mechanisms  E05F15/70
[E05F 1/004] by thermostats, rain, wind or noise  E05F1/006  takes precedence -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Automatically acting control means triggered by emergency conditions such as fire -> E05F1/006
[E05F 1/006] by emergency conditions, e.g. fire  operating or controlling mechanisms for physical fire-barriers  A62C2/24 -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Operating or controlling mechanisms for physical fire-barriers -> A62C2/24
[E05F 1/008] by time control -- E05F 1/008 covers wing closures or openings that are triggered by a timing device rather than manual operation or external signals. The entry includes mechanisms such as clock-driven gears, delayed-release levers or programmable timers that open or close doors, shutters or similar wings after a preset interval.
[E05F 1/02] gravity-actuated -- Gravity-actuated devices that automatically move a wing, flap or similar element between open and closed positions, using the weight of the component or a counterweight to provide the driving force. These mechanisms are used where no separate motor or manual effort is required and are not covered by other more specific subclasses.
[E05F 1/025] with rectilinearly-moving counterweights -- This subclass covers devices that open or close hinged wing sections, such as doors or shutters, by employing counterweights that travel in a straight-line (rectilinear) path, rather than rotating or curving. It is used when the counterweight mechanism does not fall under any more specific subclass for wing-moving devices.
[E05F 1/04] for wings which lift during movement -- E05F 1/04 relates to mechanisms that cause a wing, such as a door or panel, to lift as it is moved into an open or closed position. It is classified under fixed constructions, locks, and devices for moving wings into open or closed position, covering wing lift devices not covered elsewhere.
[E05F 1/043] with cams, helical tracks  E05F1/061  takes precedence -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Gravity-actuated self-closing hinges with cams and helical tracks -> E05F1/061  **Glossary:** - Self-closing hinge: Gravity-operated hinge constructed to provide a closing force to wings over a substantial part of their movement; includes devices for wing-counterbalancing
[E05F 1/046] with rectilinearly-inclined tracks for sliding wings -- The subclass covers mechanisms that slide hinged or swing-type wings along straight tracks that are inclined at a constant angle, using a rectilinear path to open or close the wing section.
[E05F 1/06] Mechanisms in the shape of hinges or pivots, operated by the weight of the wing -- The CPC subclass E05F 1/06 covers mechanisms shaped as hinges or pivots that use the weight of a wing to move it between open and closed positions, such as self-acting closers or openers for doors, shutters or panels not otherwise classified elsewhere.
[E05F 1/061] with cams or helical tracks -- E05F 1/061 covers wing-closing or opening devices that employ cam profiles or helical track systems to convert rotational motion into the linear movement needed to swing a wing from open to closed position. Such mechanisms are used in automotive doors, aircraft panels and other fixed constructions where a simple, reliable actuation is required.
[E05F 1/063] with complementary, substantially identical and slidingly cooperating cam surfaces  E05F1/066  takes precedence -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Gravity-actuated self-closing hinges with helical grooves, slots, threads or the like -> E05F1/066  **Glossary:** - Self-closing hinge: Gravity-operated hinge constructed to provide a closing force to wings over a substantial part of their movement; includes devices for wing-counterbalancing
[E05F 1/065] Cam-and-wheel arrangements -- E05F 1/065 covers cam-and-wheel mechanisms that drive the opening or closing of wing-type doors, shutters or similar movable panels. The arrangement uses a rotating cam engaging a wheel or gear to translate rotary motion into a controlled swing of the wing, and is classified under devices for moving wings that are not provided for elsewhere.
[E05F 1/066] Helical grooves, slots, threads or the like -- E05F 1/066 covers mechanisms that use helical grooves, slots, threads or similar screw-type features to convert rotary motion into linear movement for closing or opening wing-like members such as doors or shutters. The feature provides controlled positioning through threaded interaction.
[E05F 1/068] with inclined pivot-axes -- The subclass E05F 1/068 covers wing-type closures-such as doors, shutters or panels-whose opening or closing motion is driven by pivot axes that are set at an angle to the wing's plane. It includes mechanisms where inclined pivots, often combined with links, cams or gears, produce the desired swing, and it is applied when other, more specific wing-opener subclasses do not apply.
[E05F 1/08] spring-actuated -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Spring-actuated counterbalancing of sliding or lifting wings -> E05D13/12
[E05F 1/10] for swinging wings -- E05F 1/10 covers devices that swing wings-such as doors, windows or shutters-between open and closed positions, employing mechanisms other than those listed in related subclasses. It includes simple levers, gear drives, tension springs or motorised linkages that cause a pivoting movement to lock or release the wing. This subclass falls under Fixed Constructions → Locks → Devices for moving wings into open or closed position.
[E05F 11/00] Man-operated mechanisms for operating wings, including those which also operate the fastening -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Operating mechanisms in wings, casings or frames constructed to convert the manual force input from a user into movement of a wing, such as belt-, cable-, cord-, or chain-drives, lifting arms, rack and pinion drives or screw and nut arrangements.
[E05F 1/1008] with a coil spring parallel with the pivot axis  E05F1/1207  takes precedence -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Spring-actuated self-closing hinges with a coil spring parallel with the pivot axis -> E05F1/1207  **Glossary:** - Self-closing hinge: Spring-operated hinge constructed to provide a closing force to wings over a substantial part of their movement; includes devices for wing-counterbalancing
[E05F 1/1016] with a canted-coil torsion spring -- E05F 1/1016 covers wing-closing or opening devices that employ a canted-coil torsion spring as the actuation source. The spring stores torsional energy in a helical coil tilted to the axis, releasing it to move the wing from open to closed (or vice-versa) when triggered, without falling under any other more specific subclass.
[E05F 11/02] for wings in general, e.g. fanlights -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Mechanisms specially designed for passing through a wall -> E05F11/36
[E05F 1/1025] with a compression or traction spring -- The entry E05F 1/1025 covers devices that close or open wing-type doors, shutters or panels by using a compression or traction spring. It falls under fixed constructions and locks, specifying spring-driven mechanisms that are not covered by any other more specific subclass.
[E05F 1/1033] with a torsion bar  E05F1/123  takes precedence -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Spring-actuated self-closing hinges with a torsion bar -> E05F1/123  **Glossary:** - Self-closing hinge: Spring-operated hinge constructed to provide a closing force to wings over a substantial part of their movement; includes devices for wing-counterbalancing
[E05F 11/04] with cords, chains or cables -- The subclass E05F 11/04 pertains to manually operated mechanisms that move hinged panels, doors or other wings between open and closed positions by means of cords, chains or cables, and may also operate associated fastening devices. It lies within the hierarchy of fixed constructions, locks, and devices for moving wings into open or closed position.
[E05F 1/1041] with a coil spring perpendicular to the pivot axis  E05F1/1246  takes precedence -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Spring-actuated self-closing hinges with a coil spring perpendicular to the pivot axis -> E05F1/1246  **Glossary:** - Self-closing hinge: Spring-operated hinge constructed to provide a closing force to wings over a substantial part of their movement; includes devices for wing-counterbalancing
[E05F 1/105] with a compression spring -- A device for moving a door or window wing to an open or closed position that uses a compression spring to store energy and release it for closing or opening. It is a type of wing closer or opener not covered by other subclasses and falls under the fixed-construction lock category.
[E05F 1/1058] for counterbalancing -- E05F 1/1058 covers devices that balance the movement of a wing, such as a door or panel, by using a counterweight or spring system to offset its weight. The counterbalancing mechanism assists in opening or closing the wing, reducing the effort required.
[E05F 11/06] in guide-channels -- E05F 11/06 covers man-operated mechanisms that move building or vehicle wings between open and closed positions by means of guide-channels. The devices may also incorporate the fastening of the wing and fall under the hierarchy of fixed constructions, locks and devices for moving wings.
[E05F 1/1066] with a traction spring -- E05F 1/1066 covers wing-closing or opening devices that use a traction spring to move a fixed door leaf between open and closed positions. The spring provides the pulling force that drives the wing, and the subclass applies to configurations not covered by more specific CPC entries.
[E05F 1/1075] for counterbalancing -- This CPC subclass E05F 1/1075 covers devices for moving wings such as doors, shutters or panels that incorporate a counterbalancing mechanism to offset the wing's weight, making opening or closing easier. It falls under Fixed Constructions - Locks - Devices for moving wings into open or closed position.
[E05F 11/08] with longitudinally-moving bars guided, e.g. by pivoted links, in or on the frame -- A man-operated device that moves a door, shutter or similar wing between open and closed positions using bars that travel longitudinally along the frame, the bars being guided by pivoted links. It falls under fixed constructions, locks and wing-moving mechanisms.
[E05F 1/1083] with a leaf or similar spring  E05F1/1284  takes precedence -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Spring-actuated self-closing hinges with a leaf or similar spring -> E05F1/1284  **Glossary:** - Self-closing hinge: Spring-operated hinge constructed to provide a closing force to wings over a substantial part of their movement; includes devices for wing-counterbalancing
[E05F 1/1091] with a gas spring  E05F1/1292  takes precedence -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Spring-actuated self-closing hinges with a gas spring -> E05F1/1292  **Glossary:** - Self-closing hinge: Spring-operated hinge constructed to provide a closing force to wings over a substantial part of their movement; includes devices for wing-counterbalancing
[E05F 11/10] Mechanisms by which a handle moves the bar -- E05F 11/10 covers mechanisms where a handle is directly linked to a bar that slides or pivots to open or close a wing such as a door or hatch in fixed constructions and lock systems. The handle's motion is transmitted through levers, cams or linkages to move the bar into the required position.
[E05F 11/12] Mechanisms by which the bar shifts the wing -- Man-operated mechanisms that use a bar to move a vehicle or aircraft wing from a closed to an open position or vice-versa. The bar acts as a lever or pushrod that transmits force from the operator's hand to the hinge, allowing the wing to be positioned and locked securely. This class covers devices where the shifting action is achieved primarily by the bar's linear or angular motion.
[E05F 11/14] directly, i.e. without links, shifting the wing, e.g. by rack and gear or pin and slot -- This CPC subclass covers man-operated mechanisms that shift a wing directly without intermediate links, such as rack-and-gear or pin-and-slot arrangements, used in doors, lids or shutters. It falls under fixed constructions, locks, devices for moving wings into open or closed position.
[E05F 11/145] by pin and slot -- E05F 11/145 covers man-operated devices that move a wing, panel or flap between open and closed positions by means of a pin inserted into a slot, providing a simple locking or positioning mechanism within fixed construction systems.
[E05F 11/16] shifting the wing by pivotally-connected members -- The CPC subclass E05F 11/16 concerns man-operated mechanisms that shift a wing by means of pivotally connected members, enabling the wing to move between open and closed positions. It is classified under fixed constructions, locks, and devices for moving wings into open or closed position.
[E05F 11/18] consisting of a lever, e.g. an angle lever, only -- E05F 11/18 covers manually operated devices that move wings-such as doors, hatches or panels-between open and closed positions using a lever mechanism, typically an angle lever, without additional power sources.
[E05F 1/12] Mechanisms in the shape of hinges or pivots, operated by springs
[E05F 11/20] consisting of a lever, e.g. an angle lever, and only one additional link -- E05F 11/20 covers manually operated mechanisms that move a wing such as a door, cover or panel using a lever, typically an angle lever, together with a single additional link to translate the lever motion into opening or closing movement. The configuration allows simple and compact actuation of the wing with minimal moving parts.
[E05F 1/1207] with a coil spring parallel with the pivot axis -- E05F 1/1207 covers wing-moving devices that employ a coil spring arranged parallel to the pivot axis to assist the opening or closing action of a door, panel or flap. The spring provides the stored energy needed to move the wing without additional actuators, and the subclass is used when no other more specific provision applies.
[E05F 1/1215] with a canted-coil torsion spring -- This subclass covers mechanical closers or openers for fixed wings such as doors or shutters that employ a canted-coil torsion spring to generate the required torque for moving the wing between open and closed positions, when no other specific spring type is defined.
[E05F 11/22] consisting of a lever, e.g. an angle lever, and two or more additional links in series -- The subclass E05F 11/22 covers manually operated mechanisms that move wings-such as doors, shutters or panels-between open and closed positions by using a lever, for example an angle lever, together with two or more additional links arranged in series to transmit the motion. It applies to devices where the operator's effort on the lever is transferred through the linked system to operate the wing.
[E05F 1/1223] with a compression or traction spring -- A device that automatically moves a wing-such as a door, flap or lid-between open and closed positions by means of a compression or traction spring. The spring stores energy when the wing is moved and releases it to close or open the wing without additional mechanisms.
[E05F 1/123] with a torsion bar -- The subclass E05F 1/123 covers devices that close or open a wing-such as a door, flap or panel-by means of a torsion bar that stores mechanical energy when the wing is moved and releases it to return the wing to a closed or open position, providing a simple, self-acting actuation without additional power sources.
[E05F 1/1238] specially adapted for vehicles -- E05F 1/1238 covers devices that move wing-like parts such as doors, hatches or panels between open and closed positions and are specially adapted for use on vehicles. It includes closers or openers for vehicle wings that are not otherwise classified in this subclass, falling under the hierarchy of fixed constructions, locks and devices for moving wings.
[E05F 11/24] shifting the wing by pivotally-connected members -- E05F 11/24 covers manually operated mechanisms that shift a wing-such as a door, shutter or panel-by means of members that are pivotally connected, allowing the wing to be moved between open and closed positions. This includes devices where the pivoting action is the primary means of wing displacement.
[E05F 1/1246] with a coil spring perpendicular to the pivot axis -- This subclass covers wing closing or opening mechanisms that employ a coil spring mounted so its axis is at right angles to the pivot axis of the wing, providing the force needed to move the wing. The spring is arranged perpendicular to the pivot, distinguishing it from other spring configurations.
[E05F 1/1253] with a compression spring -- E05F 1/1253 classifies wing-moving devices that employ a compression spring to drive a door, flap or similar element between open and closed positions, where the mechanism is not covered by any other subclass. It falls under Fixed Constructions → Locks → Devices for moving wings into open or closed position, specifically covering spring-powered closers or openers for wings.
[E05F 11/26] consisting of a lever, e.g. an angle lever, only -- A manually operated lever, such as an angle lever, used to move a wing-like element of a fixed construction between open and closed positions, typically for doors, shutters or similar panels. This device provides the mechanical means for the operator to swing or lift the wing without additional power sources.
[E05F 1/1261] for counterbalancing -- This subclass covers devices that assist the opening or closing of hinged or sliding wings by using a counterbalancing mechanism such as springs, weights, or hydraulic pistons to reduce the effort required. The counterbalance may be integrated with the lock or closure system and is applied where no other specific subclass applies.
[E05F 1/1269] with a traction spring -- E05F 1/1269 refers to devices for moving wing-type doors, panels or shutters that use a traction spring to provide the force needed to close or hold the wing in the closed position, falling within the broader fixed-construction lock category.
[E05F 1/1276] for counterbalancing -- The subclass E05F 1/1276 covers wing-type devices that employ a counterbalancing element-such as a weight, spring or fluid system-to assist the opening or closing motion. It is applied to fixed constructions where the wing's movement is not otherwise specified, providing a balanced force that reduces the effort required to move the wing.
[E05F 11/28] consisting of a lever, e.g. an angle lever, and one or more additional links -- E05F 11/28 classifies manually operated mechanisms that move wings such as doors or shutters by using a lever - often an angle lever - combined with one or more additional link components to achieve opening or closing. These arrangements are part of fixed construction lock systems.
[E05F 1/1284] with a leaf or similar spring -- This subgroup covers mechanisms that automatically close or open hinged sections such as doors or windows using a leaf-type spring or comparable spring element. The spring provides torque to move the wing between open and closed positions without other specialized devices. It falls under CPC E05F 1/1284.
[E05F 1/1292] with a gas spring -- A device for moving a wing, such as a door or shutter, that employs a gas-spring mechanism to assist opening or closing. The gas spring provides controlled force and damping, enabling smooth, self-holding motion without the need for additional actuators.
[E05F 11/30] consisting of links in rhomb-form -- A man-operated mechanism that moves a wing (such as a door, shutter or panel) by means of a rhomb-shaped linkage of links. The rhombic arrangement provides a smooth converting motion and mechanical advantage, allowing the wing to be opened or closed and sometimes to engage a fastening device.
[E05F 11/32] with rotary bars guided in the frame -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Screw and nut mechanisms -> E05F11/34
[E05F 11/34] with screw mechanisms -- This subclass covers manually operated devices that move doors, shutters or other hinged wings between open and closed positions by means of a screw mechanism, often integrating the fastening action, and is situated under fixed constructions, locks and devices for moving wings.
[E05F 11/36] specially designed for passing through a wall -- This subclass covers manually operated devices that move wing-like elements such as doors, shutters or panels between open and closed positions, limited to constructions specially designed to pass through a wall, for example wall-mounted hinged doors or curtain-type openings.

[E05F 1/14] with double-acting springs, e.g. for closing and opening or checking and closing -- E05F 1/14 pertains to fixed-construction devices that move a wing (such as a door or flap) between open and closed positions and are not covered by other subclasses. It specifically covers wing closers or openers that use double-acting springs to provide both opening and closing (or checking and closing) forces.

All groups listed in this Warning should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[E05F 11/53] for sliding windows, e.g. vehicle windows, to be opened or closed by horizontal movement -- This subclass defines mechanisms that slide a window horizontally to open or close it, such as side-windows on vehicles that move laterally along a guide. It covers man-operated devices that shift the wing from a closed to an open position by horizontal movement.
[E05F 11/535] for vehicle windows -- E05F 11/535 classifies man-operated mechanisms that move vehicle windows between open and closed positions, covering devices such as hand-cranked, lever-actuated or push-button systems that raise, lower, tilt or otherwise adjust windows in cars, trucks and similar vehicles.
[E05F 11/54] for doors -- The CPC subclass E05F 11/54 covers man-operated mechanisms that move doors-or wing panels-between open and closed positions, including devices that also actuate the fastening of the door. It is nested under Fixed Constructions, Locks, Devices for Moving Wings into Open or Closed Position.
[E05F 1/16] for sliding wings -- E05F 1/16 pertains to devices that slide wing-type elements-such as doors, shutters or panels-between open and closed positions, where the movement is achieved by a sliding motion rather than a pivot or hinge. It falls under the hierarchy of Fixed Constructions, Locks, and Devices for Moving Wings into Open or Closed Position, covering sliding wing mechanisms not classified elsewhere.
[E05F 13/00] Mechanisms operated by the movement or weight of a person or vehicle -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Operating mechanisms for wings constructed to convert the force input from the weight of a person or vehicle into movement of a wing.
[E05F 13/02] by devices, e.g. lever arms, affected by the movement of the user -- E05F 13/02 classifies locks that are operated by the motion or weight of the user, using devices such as lever arms to move a wing into its open or closed position. The mechanism relies on the user's movement rather than independent power sources.
[E05F 13/04] by platforms lowered by the weight of the user -- E05F 13/04 covers lock systems in which the wing or flap is moved to its closed position by a platform that descends under the weight of the user, using that weight as the operating force. The mechanism is a fixed construction that relies on the user's mass to lower the platform and engage the lock.
[E05F 15/00] Power-operated mechanisms for wings -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Operating mechanisms for wings constructed to convert the force input from a motor into movement of a wing.  The group furthermore covers safety devices for power-operated wings, such as obstruction detection means, and controlling means, such as remote wireless controls means.
[E05F 15/40] Safety devices, e.g. detection of obstructions or end positions -- E05F 15/40 relates to safety devices for power-operated mechanisms that move wings between open and closed positions, such as sensors that detect obstructions or confirm end-positions within fixed-construction locking systems.
[E05F 15/41] Detection by monitoring transmitted force or torque -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Detection by means of monitoring transmitted force or torque by transmission of mechanical forces, e.g. using rigid, movable members -> E05F15/48 - Detection by means of monitoring transmitted force or torque in pressure medium-operated mechanisms for wings -> E05F15/49
[E05F 15/42] Detection using safety edges -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Detection of obstructions using devices mounted on the edge of the wing or mounted adjacent to or in the closing path of the wing.
[E05F 15/43] responsive to disruption of energy beams, e.g. light or sound -- E05F 15/43 covers power-operated mechanisms that move wing-type elements such as shutters or doors to open or close positions, where the motion is triggered by the interruption of an energy beam like light or sound. The devices use sensors that detect the beam disruption to activate the moving system, typically in security, lighting or ventilation applications.
[E05F 15/431] specially adapted for vehicle windows or roofs -- E05F 15/431 relates to power-operated mechanisms that move vehicle windows or roof panels between open and closed positions. The entry covers devices specially adapted for automotive applications, integrating lock and actuation functions for wing-type openings such as sunroofs, side windows or roof sections.
[E05F 15/44] responsive to changes in electrical conductivity -- E05F 15/44 refers to power-operated mechanisms that move wings or similar devices between open and closed positions, where the actuation is triggered by a change in electrical conductivity. It falls under Fixed Constructions → Locks → Devices for moving wings into open or closed position, and is used for electrically responsive locking or unlocking of wing sections.
[E05F 15/443] specially adapted for vehicle windows or roofs -- E05F 15/443 covers power-operated mechanisms that move wings such as windows or roof sections on vehicles, designed specifically for automotive applications. The devices belong to the fixed-construction group for locks and wing-moving devices, enabling automatic opening and closing of vehicle glazing or roof panels.
[E05F 15/46] responsive to changes in electrical capacitance -- E05F 15/46 covers power-operated devices that open or close a wing-such as doors, shutters or vent panels-where the motion is controlled by detecting changes in electrical capacitance. The capacitance-based feedback typically triggers the actuator to adjust the wing position automatically in response to environmental or user-induced variations.
[E05F 15/47] responsive to changes in fluid pressure -- The code E05F 15/47 covers power-operated mechanisms that move doors, shutters or other wings between open and closed positions and that respond to changes in fluid pressure, such as hydraulic or pneumatic actuation.
[E05F 15/48] by transmission of mechanical forces, e.g. rigid or movable members -- E05F 15/48 classifies wing-locking devices that are powered mechanically and transmit force through rigid or movable members such as levers, cams, linkages or gear trains, rather than using hydraulic, pneumatic or electrical actuation. The category covers power-operated mechanisms that move a wing to an open or closed position by direct mechanical transmission. It is used for doors, shutters and similar fixed-construction elements.
[E05F 15/49] specially adapted for mechanisms operated by fluid pressure, e.g. detection by monitoring transmitted fluid pressure -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Safety edges for power-operated mechanisms for wings using detection by means of monitoring fluid pressure -> E05F15/47
[E05F 15/50] using fluid-pressure actuators -- The subclass E05F 15/50 covers mechanisms that employ hydraulic or pneumatic pressure to move wing-type elements-such as doors, shutters or flaps-between open and closed positions within fixed constructions, especially lock devices. It includes power-operated systems that use fluid-pressure actuators to effect the motion of the wing.
[E05F 15/51] for folding wings -- E05F 15/51 relates to power-operated mechanisms that fold wings, moving them from an open to a closed position for storage or transport. It includes hydraulic, pneumatic, electric or motor-driven devices designed to fold aircraft or similar wings into a compact configuration.
[E05F 15/53] for swinging wings -- The CPC class E05F 15/53 covers power-driven mechanisms that swing or rotate wings, such as those used on aircraft, roofs, or other structures, enabling opening or closing motion. It falls under devices for moving wings within the fixed-construction and lock categories.
[E05F 15/54] operated by linear actuators acting on a helical track coaxial with the swinging axis -- A power-operated device that moves aerodynamic wings between open and closed positions by means of linear actuators that travel along a helical track aligned with the wing's swing axis, falling under the fixed-construction locks category for wing-positioning mechanisms.
[E05F 15/56] for horizontally-sliding wings -- This CPC subclass covers power-operated devices that move wings horizontally to a sliding open or closed position, such as sliding windows or doors. It falls under fixed constructions, locks, devices for moving wings into open or closed position.
[E05F 15/565] for railway-cars -- This subclass covers power-operated mechanisms that move wing-type doors on railway cars between open and closed positions. It includes fixed-construction lock devices that control the door movement.
[E05F 15/57] for vertically-sliding wings -- This CPC entry covers power-operated mechanisms that move wings vertically in a sliding motion, such as those used in fixed constructions and lock systems to shift a wing from an open to a closed position or vice versa. It pertains to devices that translate rotational or linear motor power into vertical translation of the wing element.
[E05F 15/59] for overhead wings -- E05F 15/59 relates to power-operated mechanisms that move overhead wings between open and closed positions. It covers devices such as motors, actuators or hydraulic systems used to raise, lower, or swing roof or aircraft wings, enabling automated control of the wing's position.
[E05F 15/60] using electrical actuators -- E05F 15/60 relates to power-operated mechanisms that move wings, shutters or panels between open and closed positions by means of electrical actuators, typically used in building, vehicle or equipment constructions. It falls under fixed constructions-locks-devices for moving wings into open or closed position.
[E05F 15/603] using rotary electromotors -- E05F 15/603 classifies power-operated mechanisms that move wings, such as aircraft or device flaps, between open and closed positions using rotary electric motors; it is situated under fixed constructions, locks, and devices for moving wings into open or closed positions.
[E05F 15/605] for folding wings -- E05F 15/605 pertains to power-driven systems that fold wings from an open to a closed position, such as on aircraft or other vehicles. It includes hydraulic, electric or pneumatic mechanisms that move the wing sections and secure them for storage or transport.
[E05F 15/608] for revolving wings -- E05F 15/608 covers power-operated devices that rotate wings or similar hinged elements between open and closed positions. The mechanism uses a motor, hydraulic or pneumatic drive to swing the wing about a pivot, enabling automatic opening, closing or repositioning. Typical applications include rotating doors, roof panels, or aircraft wing sections that require controlled, powered movement.
[E05F 15/611] for swinging wings -- E05F 15/611 designates power-operated mechanisms that swing wing-type elements between open and closed positions, such as electric or hydraulic actuators used for shutters, doors or ventilation panels. The subclass covers devices that impart a swinging motion about a pivot or hinge to move the wing.
[E05F 15/614] operated by meshing gear wheels, one of which being mounted at the wing pivot axis -- The CPC entry E05F 15/614 covers power-operated mechanisms for moving a wing between open and closed positions that are driven by meshing gear wheels, with one of the gears fixed on the wing's pivot axis. It falls under fixed constructions, locks, and devices for moving wings.
[E05F 15/616] operated by push-pull mechanisms -- The CPC subclass E05F 15/616 covers power-operated devices that move a wing, such as a door or flap, between open and closed positions by means of a push-pull mechanism, falling under the hierarchy of fixed constructions, locks, and devices for moving wings into open or closed position.
[E05F 15/619] using flexible or rigid rack-and-pinion arrangements -- E05F 15/619 covers power-operated devices that move shutters, blinds or similar wing elements between open and closed positions by employing a rack-and-pinion system, whether the rack is flexible or rigid. The classification applies to mechanisms that translate rotary motion into linear movement to adjust the wing's position.
[E05F 15/622] using screw-and-nut mechanisms -- E05F 15/622 covers power-operated devices that move wings, shutters or panels between open and closed positions by means of screw-and-nut mechanisms. It falls under the fixed-construction group for locks and the sub-category of devices that shift wings, specifying the use of threaded conversion to generate the required motion.
[E05F 15/624] using friction wheels -- E05F 15/624 defines power-operated systems that move wings between open and closed positions by using friction wheels. The friction wheel transmits torque to a gear or shaft, allowing controlled rotation of the wing hinge. This subclass falls under fixed constructions, locks and devices for moving wings.
[E05F 15/627] operated by flexible elongated pulling elements, e.g. belts, chains or cables -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Using rotary electromotors to swing wings by push-pull mechanisms using flexible or rigid rack-and-pinion arrangements -> E05F15/619
[E05F 15/63] operated by swinging arms -- E05F 15/63 covers power-driven devices that move a wing, such as a garage door or a flap, by means of swinging arm mechanisms. The classification applies to systems where the wing's opening or closing motion is generated through an arm that pivots to transfer force from a motor or other power source.
[E05F 15/632] for horizontally-sliding wings -- A power-operated mechanism that moves a wing, shutter, door or similar element horizontally between open and closed positions by sliding it along a track or guide. It belongs to the fixed-construction group for devices that position wings, and is used where a horizontal slide motion is required for opening or sealing.
[E05F 15/635] operated by push-pull mechanisms, e.g. flexible or rigid rack-and-pinion arrangements -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Horizontally-sliding wings operated by swinging arms -> E05F15/649 - Horizontally-sliding wings operated by screw mechanisms -> E05F15/652 - Horizontally-sliding wings for railway-cars -> E05F15/655 , E05Y2900/51
[E05F 15/638] allowing or involving a secondary movement of the wing, e.g. rotational or transversal -- E05F 15/638 covers power-operated devices that give a wing a secondary movement, such as rotation or transversal shift, after it has been positioned open or closed. It falls under fixed constructions, then locks, then devices for moving wings into open or closed position, and finally power-operated mechanisms for wings.
[E05F 15/641] operated by friction wheels -- E05F 15/641 pertains to power-operated mechanisms that move building wings, such as doors or shutters, by means of friction wheels. The wheel rolls against a surface, converting rotary motion into the linear force required to open or close the wing.
[E05F 15/643] operated by flexible elongated pulling elements, e.g. belts, chains or cables -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Horizontally-sliding wings for railway-cars -> E05F15/655 , E05Y2900/51
[E05F 15/646] allowing or involving a secondary movement of the wing, e.g. rotational or transversal -- E05F 15/646 covers power-operated mechanisms for fixed-construction wings that provide a secondary movement, such as rotating or shifting the wing transversely, in addition to the primary opening or closing action. It applies to devices that enable the wing to move beyond simple pivoting, allowing extra adjustability for sealing, ventilation or access.
[E05F 15/649] operated by swinging arms -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Horizontally-sliding wings for railway-cars -> E05F15/655 , E05Y2900/51
[E05F 15/652] operated by screw-and-nut mechanisms -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Horizontally-sliding wings for railway-cars -> E05F15/655 , E05Y2900/51
[E05F 15/655] specially adapted for vehicle wings -- The CPC subclass E05F 15/655 covers power-operated mechanisms that are specially adapted for moving vehicle wings between open and closed positions. It includes actuators, motors, hydraulic or pneumatic devices and their integration with the vehicle's fixed construction and locking systems.
[E05F 15/657] enabling manual drive, e.g. in case of power failure -- The subclass concerns power-operated mechanisms for moving wings-such as doors, flaps or panels-into open or closed positions that incorporate a provision for manual operation when the power supply fails, allowing the wing to be driven by hand as a backup.
[E05F 15/659] Control circuits therefor -- E05F 15/659 covers the electrical control circuitry that operates power-driven devices moving wings-such as doors, shutters or blinds-between open and closed positions in fixed constructions. It includes wiring, switches, relays, sensors and other electronic components that manage the automated opening and closing functions.
[E05F 15/662] Motor units therefor, e.g. geared motors -- This class includes motor units that power mechanisms for moving wings of fixed-construction lock devices between open and closed positions, such as geared motors integrated into the wing-actuating system.
[E05F 15/665] for vertically-sliding wings -- A power-operated mechanism that moves a wing or shutter vertically to open or close it, typically employed in fixed constructions such as doors, windows or protective panels. The device slides the wing along a straight, up-or-down path, providing automated control for locking or unlocking the opening. It falls under the CPC class for mechanisms that position wings in open or closed positions.
[E05F 15/668] for overhead wings -- E05F 15/668 pertains to power-operated mechanisms that move overhead wings, such as shutters, awnings or hinged panels, between open and closed positions. It includes electric, hydraulic, pneumatic or other motor-driven devices that raise, lower, fold or otherwise manipulate the wing for ventilation, lighting or access.
[E05F 15/67] operated by flexible or rigid rack-and-pinion arrangements -- This CPC subclass E05F 15/67 covers power-operated mechanisms for moving building wings such as shutters or doors that employ flexible or rigid rack-and-pinion gear arrangements. It is part of the fixed constructions hierarchy under locks and devices for moving wings into open or closed positions.
[E05F 15/673] operated by screw-and-nut mechanisms -- E05F 15/673 covers power-operated devices that move wings, such as doors or flaps, between open and closed positions using screw-and-nut mechanisms to transmit motion and generate the required force. This subclass falls under the broader categories of fixed constructions, locks, and devices for moving wings. It specifies the mechanical principle of a threaded screw driving a nut to achieve the wing movement.
[E05F 15/676] operated by friction wheels -- E05F 15/676 covers power-operated devices that move wings-such as doors, lids or shutters-into open or closed positions by using friction wheels to transmit motion. The classification falls under fixed constructions, locks, and mechanisms for moving wings, and relates to systems where wheel friction provides the driving force.
[E05F 15/678] operated by swinging lever arms -- A power-operated mechanism that moves a wing, such as a door or hatch, between open and closed positions by means of swinging lever arms. It falls under fixed-construction locks that control wing movement, specifically the class for devices that use lever-actuated motion.
[E05F 15/681] operated by flexible elongated pulling elements, e.g. belts -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Flexible rack-and-pinion arrangements -> E05F15/67
[E05F 15/684] by chains -- E05F 15/684 relates to power-operated mechanisms that move wing sections, such as doors or shutters, by means of chain drives. The classification covers devices in fixed constructions where a chain transmits the driving force to open or close the wing.
[E05F 15/686] by cables or ropes -- E05F 15/686 covers power-driven systems that open or close wings such as shutters, doors or louvers by means of cables or ropes. The classification includes mechanisms where cables or ropes transmit the driving force from an actuator to the moving wing.
