[E05F 15/70] with automatic actuation -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Operating mechanisms actuated, i.e. started or controlled after starting, by automatic means.  **Glossary:** - Automatic: Independent of external or manual control, i.e. control by detection, sensing or measuring
[E05F 15/71] responsive to temperature changes, rain, wind or noise -- E05F 15/71 covers power-operated mechanisms that move aircraft or other wings between open and closed positions and are triggered automatically by environmental conditions such as temperature shifts, rain, wind or noise.
[E05F 15/72] responsive to emergency conditions, e.g. fire -- E05F 15/72 covers power-driven mechanisms that automatically open or close building wings, such as fire shutters or emergency ventilation panels, in response to fire or other emergency conditions.
[E05F 15/73] responsive to movement or presence of persons or objects -- E05F 15/73 covers power-operated mechanisms that move wings, flaps or similar structures between open and closed positions, activating automatically in response to the movement or presence of persons or objects. The classification applies to devices where the wing motion is triggered by detection of a human, animal, or other item, without manual control.
[E05F 15/74] using photoelectric cells -- E05F 15/74 covers power-driven systems for opening or closing wings that employ photoelectric cells to sense light or position and control the motion. The classification applies to automated doors, shutters or similar devices where a light-sensing element triggers or regulates the actuator.
[E05F 15/75] responsive to the weight or other physical contact of a person or object -- This subclass relates to power-operated mechanisms that move wings, shutters or similar devices between open and closed positions, where the operation is triggered by the weight of, or direct physical contact with, a person or object. It covers systems that automatically respond to that load without manual input.
[E05F 15/76] responsive to devices carried by persons or objects, e.g. magnets or reflectors -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Wireless control devices carried by persons or objects -> E05F15/77
[E05F 15/77] using wireless control -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Automatic wireless control of wings, e.g. programmable control transmitted wirelessly.
[E05F 15/78] using light beams -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Wireless control using light beams, e.g. infrared control.
[E05F 15/79] using time control -- This subclass relates to power-operated mechanisms that move wings, shutters or similar panels between open and closed positions and incorporate a timing device to control the operation. It covers systems where the movement is triggered, delayed or stopped according to a preset time interval, fitting within the hierarchy of fixed constructions, locks and devices for moving wings.
[E05F 17/00] Special devices for shifting a plurality of wings operated simultaneously -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Operating mechanisms for wings constructed to convert the force input from a user, motor, opener or closer into simultaneous movement of several wings.
[E05F 17/001] of prison cell doors -- This CPC subclass covers special mechanisms that shift a plurality of prison cell doors, or wings, at the same time, moving them between open and closed positions. It resides under the hierarchy of fixed constructions, locks, and devices for moving wings.
[E05F 17/002] for wings which lie one behind the other when closed -- This CPC subclass E05F 17/002 falls under fixed constructions, locks, devices for moving wings into open or closed position, and covers mechanisms that shift multiple wings arranged one behind another when closed. The devices enable simultaneous movement of a series of wings that are stacked in the closed position.
[E05F 17/004] for wings which abut when closed -- The code E05F 17/004 classifies mechanisms that move several wings at the same time, designed for wing sections that meet or abut when fully closed. These are special devices used in fixed construction locks to shift multiple flaps, shutters or blinds simultaneously from open to closed positions.
[E05F 2003/228] Arrangements where the end of the closer arm is sliding in a track -- The code designates mechanical arrangements for a door or gate closer in which the distal end of the lever or arm is guided to move along a channel or track. This sliding track controls the motion of the closer arm and can be used in self-closing devices for doors or gates. It falls under the CPC subgroup for building openings and their operation.
[E05F 2005/043] operated by centrifugal action at high closing speed -- E05F 2005/043 covers air-handling devices that use centrifugal action to move fluid, operating with a rapid closing motion. It typically includes high-speed centrifugal fans, blowers or impellers whose blades or shutters snap shut quickly to generate airflow for heating, ventilation or air-conditioning purposes.
[E05F 2005/046] hand operated -- E05F 2005/046 groups inventions where a building element is actuated manually by a hand-operated mechanism. It includes levers, cranks, push bars and similar devices that open, close, adjust or secure doors, windows, shutters, panels and other movable parts without motorized or hydraulic assistance.
[E05F 2011/387] using arrangements in the window glass, e.g. holes -- Group E05F2011/387 is impacted by reclassification into group E05F15/6894 . Groups E05F2011/387 and E05F15/6894 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[E05F 2015/432] with acoustical sensors -- E05F 2015/432 classifies ventilation or air-handling installations that incorporate acoustic sensors to detect sound levels, airflow noise, or other acoustic indicators for monitoring, control or safety functions. The code is used for systems where sound-based feedback is integral to the operation or regulation of the ventilation device.
[E05F 2015/433] using reflection from the obstruction -- The subclass covers arrangements that employ a reflective surface on or near an obstruction to redirect light or provide a view past the obstacle. It includes mirrors, prisms or reflective coatings mounted on walls, fences or other structures so that illumination or observation can be achieved by reflection rather than direct line of sight.
[E05F 2015/434] with cameras or optical sensors -- E05F 2015/434 refers to ship-control or steering systems that incorporate cameras or other optical sensors, covering visual-imaging, infrared or lidar devices used for navigation, monitoring or automatic control of vessels within the Cooperative Patent Classification.
[E05F 2015/435] by interruption of the beam -- E05F 2015/435 covers lighting devices that create illumination effects by periodically interrupting the light beam. This includes shutters, rotating disks, electro-optical modulators or other mechanisms that block and release the beam to produce flashing, stroboscopic or patterned lighting in stage and entertainment settings.
[E05F 2015/436] the beam being parallel to the wing edge -- This subclass covers aircraft wings that incorporate a reinforcing beam whose longitudinal axis runs parallel to the outer edge of the wing. The beam follows the wing-tip contour, providing structural support while maintaining a smooth aerodynamic surface. It is applied to designs where the beam is aligned with, rather than extending beyond, the wing edge.
[E05F 2015/437] the beam being perpendicular to the wing edge -- The CPC subgroup E05F 2015/437 covers wing structures in which a longitudinal or transverse beam is arranged at a right angle to the wing's edge, providing reinforcement or load-carrying support perpendicular to the leading or trailing edge. This classification is used for aircraft and similar flight-surface designs where such perpendicular beams improve stiffness or attach ancillary components.
[E05F 2015/447] using switches in serial arrangement -- E05F 2015/447 concerns arrangements in building-technology systems where two or more switches are connected in series to control a load. The serial configuration allows a function to operate only when all switches are closed, providing sequential activation, safety interlocks or energy-saving control in heating, ventilation, lighting or other building installations.
[E05F 2015/483] for detection during opening -- E05F 2015/483 covers devices or systems that detect when a door, window, shutter or similar opening element is being opened, such as sensors, monitoring or alarm mechanisms that signal the opening action for safety, security or automated control.
[E05F 2015/487] Fault detection of safety edges -- The CPC entry E05F 2015/487 covers methods, devices or systems that detect faults, failures or malfunctions of safety-edge components on doors, windows, skylights and similar movable closures. It includes sensors, diagnostic circuits or monitoring techniques that sense loss of operation, damage or improper performance of pressure-sensitive or optical edge devices used for protective or automatic closing functions.
[E05F 2015/631] the end of the arm sliding in a track;  Slider arms therefor -- This CPC subclass relates to devices in which the distal end of an arm is guided by a longitudinal groove or channel, allowing linear motion. It includes slider arms and associated hardware that slide within a track to transmit force or adjust position in construction, agricultural or similar machinery.
[E05F 2015/763] using acoustical sensors -- E05F 2015/763 covers inventions that employ acoustic sensors to monitor, detect or control building elements such as walls, floors, or other structural components. The classification applies to systems that use sound-based measurements for safety, maintenance, or performance assessment in construction-related applications.
[E05F 2015/765] using optical sensors -- E05F 2015/765 covers lighting or illumination devices that incorporate optical sensors to detect light intensity, presence, color or other optical cues and adjust illumination accordingly. It includes systems such as daylight-harvesting controls, occupancy-based dimming, sensor-driven color tuning and related feedback mechanisms that use photodiodes, cameras or similar optical detectors.
[E05F 2015/767] using cameras -- E05F 2015/767 covers the use of cameras for inspecting, monitoring or documenting building structures and construction sites. It includes visual or infrared imaging, remote video surveillance and recording to assess condition, detect defects or guide work, and applies to methods, devices or systems that employ cameras as a primary means of observation.
[E05F 2017/005] for sliding wings -- It relates to building elements that incorporate sliding-wing mechanisms, such as doors, windows or façade panels that move horizontally to open, close or adjust ventilation, and includes the tracks, guides and drive systems that enable the sliding motion.
[E05F 2017/007] with means for interlocking the wings -- Covers devices that incorporate a mechanism to lock wings together when they are folded or retracted, preventing relative movement and increasing structural stability during storage, transport or operation. The interlocking means are typically applied to aircraft, drones or similar apparatus with foldable wing sections.
[E05F 2017/008] for swinging wings -- The CPC subclass E05F 2017/008 covers building closures in which at least one wing swings about a vertical or horizontal axis, such as hinged shutters, doors, or ventilating panels that pivot to open and close. It includes mechanisms and arrangements that enable the swinging motion.
[E05F 3/00] Closers or openers with braking devices, e.g. checks -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Closers and openers, including self-closing hinges, with integrated braking devices such as rotary or piston fluid brakes, friction brakes or counteracting springs.  Construction of braking devices for such closers and openers.  Closers with integrated braking devices and additional arrangements, e.g. holding the wing open or assisting in opening the wing.
[E05F 3/02] with pneumatic piston brakes -- The subclass E05F 3/02 covers fixed-construction lock mechanisms that move a wing such as a door, shutter or gate and use a pneumatic piston to provide braking. The air-driven piston applies force to resist or stop the wing's motion, giving a controlled opening or closing function often used as a safety check.
[E05F 3/04] with liquid piston brakes -- This subgroup includes mechanisms that move doors, shutters or similar wings and use a liquid-piston brake to control the motion. The piston filled with fluid provides resistance that can stop, hold or slow the wing, acting as a check in closers or openers.
[E05F 3/06] in which a torsion spring rotates a member around an axis perpendicular to the axis of the piston -- E05F 3/06 covers closers or openers that use a torsion spring to rotate a component about an axis that is perpendicular to the piston's axis, typically incorporating a braking or checking mechanism to control the movement of wings or doors.
[E05F 3/08] in which a torsion spring rotates a member around an axis arranged in the direction of the axis of the piston -- E05F 3/08 relates to lock closers or openers that incorporate a braking device, specifically those in which a torsion spring drives a component that rotates about an axis aligned with the piston's axis to move a wing or door between open and closed positions.
[E05F 3/10] with a spring, other than a torsion spring, and a piston, the axes of which are the same or lie in the same direction -- The code E05F 3/10 covers closing or opening devices for wings-such as doors, shutters or gates-that use a non-torsion spring together with a piston whose axes are coincident or parallel, providing a controlled movement often combined with a braking or checking mechanism.
[E05F 3/102] with rack-and-pinion transmission between driving shaft and piston within the closer housing -- E05F 3/102 covers closers or openers for doors, windows or similar openings that employ a rack-and-pinion gear set to transmit motion from a driving shaft to a piston inside the housing, typically incorporating a braking or check device to control the movement.
[E05F 3/104] with cam-and-slide transmission between driving shaft and piston within the closer housing -- This subclass covers mechanisms that use a cam-and-slide transmission to link the driving shaft to a piston inside the closer housing of a fixed-constructions lock device. The arrangement provides controlled movement of the wing and includes braking or checking functions to hold the position.
[E05F 3/106] with crank-arm transmission between driving shaft and piston within the closer housing -- E05F 3/106 relates to door or window closers that employ a crank-arm mechanism to transmit motion from a driving shaft to a piston inside the closer housing, providing controlled opening or closing with integrated braking or checking features.
[E05F 3/108] with piston rod protruding from the closer housing; Telescoping closers -- E05F 3/108 covers lock devices that move a door or wing by means of a closer or opener equipped with a braking or checking mechanism, where the piston rod extends outward from the housing. The classification applies to telescoping closers that use the protruding rod to transmit force and control the speed of closing.
[E05F 3/12] Special devices controlling the circulation of the liquid, e.g. valve arrangement -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Hydraulic power-locks in closers -> E05F3/223
[E05F 3/14] with fluid brakes of the rotary type -- The subclass E05F 3/14 covers wing-moving lock devices that employ a rotary fluid brake, such as hydraulic or pneumatic cylinders, to provide braking torque and hold the wing in either the open or closed position. This arrangement enables controlled, gradual stopping and positioning of the lock, often used in doors, shutters or similar movable closures.
[E05F 3/16] with friction brakes -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Friction devices in hinges to hold relatively-moveable hinge parts and consequently wings in a user-chosen position -> E05D11/08  **Glossary:** - Brake: device constructed to slow down the movement of a wing
[E05F 3/18] with counteracting springs -- The subclass E05F 3/18 covers closers or openers for doors, windows or shutters that incorporate counteracting springs to control the movement of the wing. The springs work against the braking device to smooth the opening or closing action and prevent sudden motion. This is applied to fixed-construction lock systems where the wing is moved between open and closed positions.
[E05F 3/20] in hinges -- E05F 3/20 concerns hinges used in fixed constructions for moving wings such as doors or shutters, focusing on devices that enable opening and closing and may include braking or checking features. It is classified under the broader groups of locks and devices for moving wings into open or closed positions.
[E05F 3/22] Additional arrangements for closers, e.g. for holding the wing in opened or other position -- E05F 3/22 pertains to additional mechanisms for door, window or other wing closers that hold the wing in an opened or alternative position, such as latches, detents or braking devices. It falls under fixed constructions, locks, devices for moving wings into open or closed positions.
[E05F 3/221] Mechanical power-locks, e.g. for holding the wing open or for free-moving zones -- **Glossary:** - Mechanical power-lock: Mechanically operated lock for holding wing in position other than closed – includes spring operated
[E05F 3/222] electrically operated  E05F3/223  takes precedence
[E05F 3/223] Hydraulic power-locks, e.g. with electrically operated hydraulic valves -- Hydraulic power-locks are mechanisms that employ hydraulic pressure, often controlled by electric valves, to move and hold doors, windows or other movable panels in open or closed positions. The classification covers devices where hydraulic actuators provide the locking force and braking action, typically activated electrically.
[E05F 3/224] for assisting in opening the wing -- E05F 3/224 covers assist devices that help open a wing or door, typically used in conjunction with a closer or opener that may have a braking function. These mechanisms can include springs, pneumatic or hydraulic aids, or other means that reduce the effort required to move the wing to the open position.
[E05F 3/225] mounted at the bottom of wings, e.g. details related to seals, covers, connections to the wings, embedding in the floor -- This subclass covers components attached to the lower side of movable wing sections such as seals, covers, and fastening elements that join the wing to the floor or embed it, typically used in lock mechanisms with braking devices.
[E05F 3/226] with means to adjust the closed position of the wing -- This subclass covers closers or openers for shutters, blinds or similar wings that incorporate a mechanism to fine-tune the final closed position, allowing precise adjustment of the sealing gap.
[E05F 3/227] mounted at the top of wings, e.g. details related to closer housings, covers, end caps or rails therefor -- E05F 3/227 covers components that are mounted on the upper edge of door or shutter wings, such as housings, covers, end caps or rails that support closers or openers with braking or checking functions. These parts are part of fixed constructions for moving wings into open or closed positions.
[E05F 5/00] Braking devices, e.g. checks -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Construction of braking devices, stops and buffers for wings.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Construction of pneumatic or liquid braking devices -> E05F3/00 - Braking devices, buffers or end stops on drawers for tables, cabinets or like furniture -> A47B88/473 - Braking devices combined with devices for holding the wing -> E05C17/00 - Devices for limiting opening of wings -> E05C17/04
[E05F 5/003] for sliding wings  Fasteners specially adapted for holding sliding wings open  E05D13/04
[E05F 5/006] for hinges having a cup-shaped fixing part, e.g. for attachment to cabinets or furniture -- A braking device for a hinge whose fixing part is cup-shaped, such as those used to attach cabinet or furniture doors, limiting or stopping wing movement. It falls under fixed constructions, then locks, then devices for moving wings into open or closed positions.
[E05F 5/02] specially for preventing the slamming of wings -- The code E05F 5/02 falls under fixed constructions, locks, devices for moving wings into open or closed position, specifically braking devices such as checks, and covers mechanisms designed to prevent the sudden closing or slamming of wing elements on doors, windows, or similar structures.
[E05F 5/022] specially adapted for vehicles, e.g. for hoods or trunks -- E05F 5/022 covers braking devices or checks used to hold vehicle openings such as hoods, trunks or other wing-type doors in a fixed position, preventing unintended movement. The classification applies to specially adapted mechanisms that secure these components in open or closed states on cars, trucks and similar vehicles.
[E05F 5/025] specially adapted for vehicle doors -- The subclass E05F 5/025 covers braking or checking mechanisms specially adapted for vehicle doors, designed to control the movement of door wings into open or closed positions. These devices typically prevent unintended opening or closing and are often integrated with lock systems to enhance safety and security.
[E05F 5/027] with closing action -- The code E05F 5/027 covers devices that move a wing, door, window or similar element into the closed position by means of a braking or check mechanism. It includes mechanisms that arrest the motion to hold the element securely closed, such as checks, catches or stays used in fixed constructions.
[E05F 5/04] hand-operated -- A hand-operated braking device used to move a wing, flap or similar element between open and closed positions, typically employing a manually controlled lever, handle or crank to apply resistance and hold the element in place. It is applied in shutters, blinds, doors or other fixed constructions where a user must secure the wing without power.
[E05F 5/06] Buffers -- Buffers are shock-absorbing devices fitted to fixed constructions such as doors, gates or movable panels to reduce impact and control movement when they close or open. They typically consist of a spring, hydraulic or pneumatic element that cushions the final motion, preventing damage and providing a smooth, safe stopping action. This function places them within the broader group of locking mechanisms that control wing movement.
[E05F 5/08] with springs -- E05F 5/08 covers braking mechanisms for fixed-construction devices that move wings between open and closed positions, where the braking action is provided by springs. These spring-loaded checks are used to arrest or control the motion of the wing or flap.
[E05F 5/10] with piston brakes -- The subclass E05F 5/10 pertains to brake mechanisms that employ a piston to apply a holding force on a movable wing, flap or shutter in fixed constructions such as doors, gates or shutters. The piston-actuated brake locks the wing in its open or closed position, often used as a check to prevent accidental movement.
[E05F 5/12] specially for preventing the closing of a wing before another wing has been closed -- A device that blocks a wing from closing until a second wing has already been closed, typically used in doors, shutters or similar fixed constructions. It belongs to the braking-type checks within lock mechanisms that control the sequence of wing movement.
[E05F 7/00] Accessories for wings not provided for in other groups of this subclass -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Accessories or devices providing additional control to the wing, e.g. devices for alignment of the wing, rattling control, or devices to take the wing's weight arranged away from a hinge axis.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Accessories for wings, adapted for furniture -> A47B95/00 - Door lifters -> B66F , E04F21/00 - Knobs or handles -> E05B
[E05F 7/005] Aligning devices for wings -- A device that adjusts or guides the position of a wing-type door, shutter or panel so that it can be locked in either the open or closed state. It is used as an accessory for wing constructions when the wing's alignment is not covered by other more specific subclasses. The mechanism typically ensures precise registration of the moving wing relative to its frame.
[E05F 7/02] for raising wings before being turned -- E05F 7/02 covers accessories that lift or raise a wing - such as a door, flap or panel - before the wing is rotated to its open or closed position. These devices are part of fixed construction locks and are used where the wing movement requires an initial upward motion prior to turning.
[E05F 7/04] Arrangements affording protection against rattling -- The group E05F 7/04 includes devices fitted to movable wings, shutters or similar elements that prevent noise generated when the parts swing or slide, such as dampers, seals, cushioning or locking mechanisms that absorb impact and stop rattling. These accessories are not covered by other wing-related subclasses.
[E05F 7/06] Devices for taking the weight of the wing, arranged away from the hinge axis -- E05F 7/06 covers mechanisms that support the weight of a movable wing by transferring the load away from the hinge axis, typically using levers, braces or counterweights. These devices help balance the wing during opening or closing, reducing stress on the hinge and improving operation. They are classified under fixed constructions, locks and accessories for wings not covered in other groups.
[E05F 7/08] Means for transmitting movements between vertical and horizontal sliding bars, rods, or cables -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Means for transmitting movements between vertical and horizontal sliding bars, rods, or cables in wings opening about a vertical as well as a horizontal axis -> E05D15/5208
[E05F 9/00] Operating mechanisms for wings -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Hand-held rods constructed to operate wing bolts or fastenings and to operate by hand the wings per se.
[E05G] SAFES OR STRONG-ROOMS FOR VALUABLES -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Portable or fixed safes and strong-rooms for keeping valuables including details such as wall structure, alarms and repellent or fire-extinguishing material releasing devices for such safes and strong-rooms.  Also covered are bank protection devices, such as entrance control or trapping mechanisms, and safety transaction partitions such as barriers for bank teller windows.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Bank buildings in general -> E04H1/06 - Buildings resistant to earthquake or war-like action -> E04H9/00  **Glossary:** - Bank: A building or portion of a building devoted to the safekeeping or exchange of valuables between the "bank" and its customers - Bank protection device: A mechanism in or on a bank for protecting the valuables or repelling attacks by stealth or force
[E05G 1/00] Safes or strong-rooms for valuables -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Portable or fixed safes and strong-rooms for keeping valuables including details such as wall structure, alarms and repellent or fire-extinguishing material releasing devices for such safes and strong-rooms.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Savings boxes -> A45C1/12 - Floatable safes -> B63C7/30 - Containers for storage -> B65D - Bank buildings in general -> E04H1/06 - Buildings resistant to earthquake or war-like action -> E04H9/00
[E05G 1/005] Portable strong boxes, e.g. which may be fixed to a wall or the like  E05G1/08 ,  E05G1/14  take precedence; devices to prevent theft or loss of bags, trunks, baskets, or the like  A45C13/18 ; coin boxes for coin freed apparatus  G07F9/06 -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Portable safes and strong-boxes for keeping valuables, e.g. armoured carrying cases, cash cassettes and small safes which may be attached to a supporting structure.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Strong boxes with multiple, individually secured compartments -> E05G1/08 - Strong boxes with means for masking or destroying the valuables -> E05G1/14
[E05G 1/02] Details -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Details, such as wall or panel structure, doors and fasteners, of safes and strong-rooms for valuables.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Hinges for safes -> E05D7/14
[E05G 1/024] Wall or panel structure -- E05G 1/024 covers safes or strong-rooms whose protective enclosure is built as a wall or panel construction, specifying the arrangement, material composition and attachment of the panels that form the vault. The subclass includes designs where the security barrier consists of one or more panels constituting the safe's walls.
[E05G 1/026] Closures -- E05G 1/026 - Closures covers the means by which a safe or strong-room is sealed, including doors, lids, bolts, seals and related locking hardware that provide the closing function for valuable storage.
[E05G 1/04] Closure fasteners -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fasteners for doors or other closures of safes or strong-rooms for valuables. e.g. safe locking arrangements, bolts.
[E05G 1/06] having provision for multiple compartments -- E05G 1/06 covers safes or strong-rooms that are designed with multiple internal compartments, allowing separate storage areas within a single unit. This feature provides enhanced organization and security for different types of valuables.
[E05G 1/08] secured individually -- E05G 1/08 covers safes or strong-rooms in which each compartment or vault is provided with its own separate locking mechanism, allowing individual securing of stored valuables within a single fixed construction.
[E05G 1/10] with alarm, signal or indicator -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Strong boxes with means for masking or destroying the valuables -> E05G1/14
[E05G 1/12] with fluent-material releasing, generating or distributing means, e.g. fire-retardant or fire extinguishing means -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Safes and strong-rooms for valuables featuring arrangements for generating, releasing and/or distributing fluent materials such as for fire extinguishing or burglar repelling.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Strong boxes with means for masking or destroying the valuables -> E05G1/14 - Identifying, scaring or incapacitating burglars, thieves or intruders with smoke, gas, powder or liquid -> G08B15/02
[E05G 1/14] with means for masking or destroying the valuables, e.g. in case of theft -- The classification E05G 1/14 refers to safes or strong-rooms that incorporate anti-theft devices which mask, damage or destroy the stored valuables when an intrusion is detected, such as shredders, chemical neutralisers or incendiary mechanisms that activate in case of theft.
[E05G 2700/00] Safes or accessories thereof -- E05G 2700/00 covers safes and accessories designed for secure storage of valuables, including the construction of strong-rooms and vault doors. It also includes fitting of locks, hinges, seals and other devices that enhance protection against unauthorized access.
[E05G 2700/02] Strong boxes, wall safes -- Strong boxes and wall safes are portable or built-in containers designed to protect valuables against theft and fire, featuring reinforced doors, heavy locking mechanisms and often fire-resistive construction. They are classified under the fixed-construction category for safes or strong-rooms, covering both the units and their accessories.
[E05G 2700/04] Alarm devices, trapping mechanisms or similar devices for safes or the like -- The entry covers alarm and trapping mechanisms that are incorporated into safes or strong-room doors to detect or respond to forced entry. It includes devices that sound an alarm, lock the safe, retain the lock bolt or otherwise hinder theft when tampering is sensed.
[E05G 5/00] Bank protection devices -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Devices and arrangements for the protection of banks by control of ingress and egress to the bank as well as control of valuables in case of hold-up. The group includes entrance control systems, transaction security booths, trapping mechanisms and access sluices.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Safes or strong-rooms with fluent material releasing, generating or distributing means, e.g. repellent or fire extinguishing -> E05G1/12 - Safety transaction partitions such as movable pay-plates and bank drive-up windows -> E05G7/00
[E05G 5/003] Entrance control  E05G5/02  takes precedence -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Entrance control systems and access sluices for bank and valuables protection including security doors, turnstiles, cages and respective sensors and control systems.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Trapping or confining mechanisms such as transaction security booths -> E05G5/02
[E05G 5/006] Cashier's counters or receptables for holding valuables readily accessible for handling, with safeguard mechanisms for the valuables in case of hold-up -- E05G 5/006 relates to cashier counters or receptacles that hold cash and other valuables while keeping them readily accessible for handling, and incorporates safeguard mechanisms that protect the contents in case of a hold-up.
[E05G 5/02] Trapping or confining mechanisms, -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Trapping or confining mechanisms and transaction security booths for bank and valuables protection.
[E05G 7/00] Safety transaction partitions, e.g. movable pay-plates -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Devices and arrangements to secure safety during transactions of valuables, such as bank depositories and security devices for bank teller windows.
[E05G 7/001] Bank depositories  automatic safe-deposit box systems  E05G1/06 -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Bank depositories such as night safes and safe-deposit boxes.
[E05G 7/002] Security barriers for bank teller windows -- Security barriers for bank teller windows are protective installations, such as bullet-resistant glass or polymer shields, positioned between customers and tellers to prevent forced entry, robbery or assault. They form part of fixed construction safety devices designed for valuable-handling areas.
[E05G 7/004] movable  E05G7/005  takes precedence -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Movable security barriers for bank teller windows.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Pass-boxes for security barriers in bank teller windows, e.g. with stationary, rotatable or sliding bottoms -> E05G7/005
[E05G 7/005] Pass-boxes therefor, e.g. with stationary bottoms -- Pass-boxes are secure transfer compartments installed within safes or strong-rooms that allow items such as cash or documents to be passed in or out without opening the main enclosure. They typically have a fixed bottom and a movable front panel that can be opened independently, providing a controlled, audit-able transaction point. This element falls under safety transaction partitions in the fixed-construction lock category.
[E05G 7/007] with rotatable bottoms -- A safety transaction partition that includes a bottom portion capable of rotating, allowing a movable pay-plate or similar device to be positioned for secure cash handling within a safe or strong-room.
[E05G 7/008] with slidable bottoms, e.g. bank drive-up windows -- E05G 7/008 covers safes, strong-rooms or other secure transaction partitions that incorporate a slidable bottom element, such as the movable plate used in drive-up bank windows. The subclass falls under fixed constructions, locks and safes or strong-rooms for valuables, describing mechanisms that allow the lower panel to slide open for access.
[E05Y] INDEXING SCHEME ASSOCIATED WITH SUBCLASSES -- E05Y designates the indexing scheme that organizes the various CPC subclasses within the Fixed Constructions > Locks section. It defines the systematic cross-reference structure used to catalog and retrieve lock-related patent documents by linking each subclass to its hierarchical position and related technical topics.
[E05Y 2201/00] Constructional elements -- The subclass E05Y 2201/00 relates to the individual constructional elements of lock mechanisms, covering parts such as bolts, cylinders, levers and other structural components that together form a lock's functional assembly. It falls under the broader fixed-construction group for security devices.
[E05Y 2201/10] Covers -- The CPC subclass E05Y 2201/10 pertains to protective or decorative covers for lock mechanisms, encompassing casings, plates or enclosures that shield the lock from environmental influences or tampering.
[E05Y 2201/11] Covers -- E05Y 2201/11 designates constructional elements of fixed lock assemblies, covering structural parts such as housings, frames, or supports that contain or protect the locking mechanism, and provides an indexing scheme for related lock subclasses.
[E05Y 2201/20] Brakes -- The code E05Y 2201/20 covers brake mechanisms that are built into fixed lock constructions, such as devices that arrest or control movement of the locking element to enhance security or safety. It includes structural arrangements of cams, springs, pawls or other components that apply a retarding force to the lock's operation.
[E05Y 2201/21] Brakes -- E05Y 2201/21 classifies brake mechanisms that form part of lock hardware in fixed constructions. It includes devices such as spring-loaded, friction or hydraulic brakes designed to restrain or release a lock under load, used in doors, gates, safes and similar installations.
[E05Y 2201/212] Buffers -- Buffers are components installed in lock mechanisms to absorb kinetic energy, limit impact forces, and maintain proper clearances between moving parts. They typically consist of spring-loaded or elastomeric elements that protect gears and bolts during operation and reduce wear.
[E05Y 2201/214] Disengaging means -- Disengaging means are components in fixed-construction lock assemblies that allow the locking elements to be released, such as springs, levers, cams or other mechanisms that separate the bolt or latch from its engaged position. They are classified under constructional elements for locks.
[E05Y 2201/216] Clutches -- E05Y 2201/216 classifies clutch mechanisms as fixed construction elements within lock systems, covering devices that transmit, interrupt or modify rotational motion between components. It includes mechanical arrangements that engage or disengage power transmission, such as friction, gear or hydraulic clutches, used to control locking or unlocking actions.
[E05Y 2201/218] Holders -- E05Y 2201/218 refers to holders, components that secure or support parts within fixed constructions, particularly in lock mechanisms. The subclass covers various types of holders used to retain or position lock elements as part of constructional elements.
[E05Y 2201/22] Locks -- E05Y 2201/22 relates to fixed-construction locks that are built into doors, windows, cabinets or similar openings as integral parts of the structure. It covers lock mechanisms such as deadbolts, latch bolts and their actuating components that are permanently installed rather than removable devices.
[E05Y 2201/221] Touch latches -- E05Y 2201/221 designates touch latches, which are locking mechanisms that operate by a light manual pressure without the need for turning or pulling. They belong to the fixed constructions group for locks and are used in doors, cabinets, and other closures where quick, simple release is required.
[E05Y 2201/222] Stabilizers, e.g. anti-rattle devices -- The E05Y 2201/222 subclass covers stabilising components integrated in fixed lock constructions, such as anti-rattle devices that reduce vibration and movement of the lock mechanism. These elements are used to secure the positioning of parts within a lock housing and improve operational reliability.
[E05Y 2201/224] Stops -- The CPC subclass E05Y 2201/224 "Stops" covers devices that prevent the motion of doors, windows, panels or other moving parts, such as mechanical, hydraulic or magnetic stops used in lock assemblies. It includes any constructional element that arrests movement to secure a position, typically integrated into fixed constructions and lock mechanisms.
[E05Y 2201/23] Actuation thereof -- The CPC subclass E05Y 2201/23 covers the means used to operate a lock, that is, the mechanisms that cause the lock's moving parts to engage or disengage. It includes devices such as levers, cams, springs, pneumatic or electric actuators and other arrangements that transmit a manual or powered input into the lock's actuation motion.
[E05Y 2201/232] by automatically acting means -- The subclass E05Y 2201/232 covers lock constructions that are operated by automatically acting means, such as electronic, hydraulic, pneumatic, or mechanical devices that engage or disengage the locking mechanism without manual effort. It falls under Fixed Constructions → Locks and concerns constructional elements that provide self-activating locking functions.
[E05Y 2201/234] direction dependent -- The CPC subclass E05Y 2201/234 covers lock components whose operation varies with the direction of movement, such as a bolt that engages when turned clockwise but not counter-clockwise, or a latch that slides only in one direction. It addresses constructional elements whose functional behavior is dependent on the direction in which they are actuated.
[E05Y 2201/236] using force or torque -- E05Y 2201/236 covers fixed lock constructions where the mechanism is driven by applying force or torque, such as turning a bolt, cam or lever to secure or release the lock. It pertains to the way mechanical effort is used to actuate the locking element.
[E05Y 2201/238] reaction force or torque -- E05Y 2201/238 classifies lock elements that produce or resist reaction forces or torque, such as springs, cams, levers or other mechanical members used in fixed-construction locking arrangements to absorb, transmit or balance applied loads.
[E05Y 2201/24] using lost motion -- The code E05Y 2201/24 refers to lock mechanisms that employ a lost-motion feature, allowing a prescribed amount of movement before the locking action engages. This constructional element is used in fixed installations to provide controlled actuation, overload protection or safety release in various lock designs.
[E05Y 2201/242] using threshold speed -- E05Y 2201/242 covers fixed lock constructions that are actuated by a threshold speed, where the locking mechanism engages or releases only when a rotational or linear speed surpasses a predefined value, such as speed-activated deadbolts or safe locks.
[E05Y 2201/244] by manual operation -- This CPC subclass (E05Y 2201/244) pertains to fixed construction elements that serve as locks and are operated manually. It is nested under the broader categories of fixed constructions, then locks, within the indexing scheme for constructional elements.
[E05Y 2201/246] by auxiliary motors, magnets, springs or weights -- E05Y 2201/246 falls under Fixed Constructions > Locks and covers lock mechanisms that are operated by auxiliary power sources such as motors, electromagnets, springs or weighted masses, rather than solely by manual force. It includes devices where these elements provide the moving force to engage or release the locking element.
[E05Y 2201/248] Transmissions -- E05Y 2201/248 classifies the transmission elements used within fixed constructions such as locks, covering mechanisms that convey motion or force between components. It includes gear trains, belts, chains, cams, pulleys and similar devices that link a driving part to a driven part in stationary assemblies.
[E05Y 2201/25] Mechanical means for force or torque adjustment therefor -- The E05Y 2201/25 subclass covers devices in fixed lock constructions that provide mechanical adjustment of the applied force or torque, such as screw-type tensioners, spring-loaded cams, or other adjustable mechanisms that fine-tune the locking action. These elements allow users to calibrate the strength required to engage or release the lock.
[E05Y 2201/252] Type of friction -- E05Y 2201/252 classifies the type of friction employed in fixed lock constructions, covering the use of dry, lubricated, or self-lubricating surfaces to transmit force between moving parts. It is used to distinguish designs where friction is a functional element of the locking mechanism.
[E05Y 2201/254] Fluid or viscous friction -- E05Y 2201/254 covers the use of fluid or viscous friction in fixed locking mechanisms, such as damped bolts, hydraulic or oil-filled seals that resist movement by viscosity. It includes designs where a fluid layer provides resistance to opening or closing actions, enhancing security or controlling motion.
[E05Y 2201/256] with pistons or vanes -- This CPC subclass covers fixed lock constructions where the locking action is achieved by pistons or vanes moving within a housing. It falls under constructional elements of locks and indexes the specific mechanism of piston-based or vane-based actuators.
[E05Y 2201/258] Magnetic or electromagnetic friction -- E05Y 2201/258 is a CPC subclass for lock constructions that employ magnetic or electromagnetic friction. It covers devices where magnetic fields or electromagnets create resistive forces to control or retain lock components, often used for safety or security functions.
[E05Y 2201/26] Mechanical friction -- E05Y 2201/26 covers mechanical friction elements incorporated in fixed lock constructions, such as friction washers, detents or springs that generate resistance to movement and retain components in set positions. These components are used to ensure secure and controlled operation of locking mechanisms.
[E05Y 2201/262] Type of motion, e.g. braking -- This CPC subclass (E05Y 2201/262) concerns the type of motion of fixed constructions such as locks, particularly mechanisms that provide braking or control of movement. It sits within the hierarchy Fixed Constructions - Locks - Indexing scheme associated with subclasses - Constructional elements.
[E05Y 2201/264] linear -- E05Y 2201/264 covers linear constructional elements used in fixed constructions such as locks. The class pertains to straight-line components like rods, bars or beams that provide linear motion or alignment within lock mechanisms.
[E05Y 2201/266] rotary -- E05Y 2201/266 designates rotary mechanisms incorporated in fixed constructions such as locks, focusing on constructional elements that provide rotational movement to engage or disengage locking components. It falls under the hierarchy of Fixed Constructions → Locks → Constructional elements.
[E05Y 2201/40] Motors -- This CPC subclass E05Y 2201/40 falls under fixed constructions, locks, and constructional elements and relates to motors used in locking mechanisms. It includes any electric, hydraulic or pneumatic motor that provides the driving force for lock actuation. The code is used by the EPO and USPTO for indexing patent documents concerning motor-driven lock components.
[E05Y 2201/404] Function thereof -- E05Y 2201/404 classifies patents that discuss the functional aspects of constructional elements in fixed locks, detailing the purpose or operation of each component within the locking mechanism rather than its mere structural form.
[E05Y 2201/406] for a secondary movement of the wing -- The subclass E05Y 2201/406 covers fixed-construction lock devices that incorporate a secondary movement of a wing, such as a latch arm that pivots or swings after the primary actuation. It falls under constructional elements within the broader FIXED CONSTRUCTIONS hierarchy.
[E05Y 2201/408] for braking -- The CPC subgroup E05Y 2201/408 pertains to braking devices incorporated in fixed-construction locks. It covers mechanisms that retard or stop the motion of lock components, such as safes, vault doors or secure enclosures, using friction, hydraulic, pneumatic or other braking means. This classification is used to index patents dealing with the design of such braking systems within lock construction.
[E05Y 2201/41] for closing -- The CPC subclass E05Y 2201/41 covers lock mechanisms whose primary function is to close an opening, such as doors, windows, lids or panels. It includes devices that secure the closure by mechanical engagement, latching or similar actions within fixed constructions.
[E05Y 2201/412] for the final closing movement -- E05Y 2201/412 covers the final closing movement of a lock, referring to the mechanical action that drives the bolt, latch or other locking element into its fully engaged position. It falls under the Fixed Constructions group for locks and is used to index subclasses dealing with constructional elements of locking devices. This subclass is part of the CPC scheme shared by the EPO and USPTO.
[E05Y 2201/414] for the initial closing movement -- This subclass covers the initial closing movement of a lock, i.e., the act that first brings the bolt or latch toward the locked position. It includes mechanisms that start the closing sequence, such as manual lever pull, key turn, or automatic actuation, before the bolt fully engages.
[E05Y 2201/416] for counterbalancing -- The CPC subclass E05Y 2201/416 covers counterbalancing mechanisms incorporated in fixed constructions such as locks, where devices like springs, weights or hydraulic elements are used to offset forces and maintain stable operation. It is part of the constructional elements category within the broader fixed-constructions hierarchy.
[E05Y 2201/418] for holding -- A subclass of the CPC dealing with fixed constructions and locks, E05Y 2201/418 covers elements that are designed to hold or retain components within a lock assembly, such as retaining plates, pins, or brackets that secure moving parts in place. It pertains to constructional features that provide a holding function for lock mechanisms.
[E05Y 2201/42] for locking -- E05Y 2201/42 relates to fixed construction elements that provide a locking function for doors, windows, panels or other parts of a building. The subclass covers arrangements where the construction itself serves to secure the element in a closed position.
[E05Y 2201/422] for opening -- E05Y 2201/422 covers features of fixed constructions that provide an opening function, such as lock mechanisms integrated into doors, gates or panels. It belongs to the CPC hierarchy under Fixed Constructions, Locks and Constructional elements. The entry addresses methods or components that enable the structure to be opened.
[E05Y 2201/424] for the final opening movement -- The CPC subclass E05Y 2201/424 relates to fixed constructional elements of locks that provide the final opening movement, i.e., the last action that releases the locking bolt or latch to complete unlocking. It includes mechanisms that translate a previous actuation into the decisive motion that opens the secured entry.
[E05Y 2201/426] for the initial opening movement -- E05Y 2201/426 addresses the initial opening movement of fixed lock mechanisms, describing the first motion that releases the lock. It is classified under constructional elements for fixed constructions, specifically within the lock subgroup.
[E05Y 2201/428] for suspending or supporting -- E05Y 2201/428 is a CPC subclass for fixed construction elements of locks that provide suspension or support, covering brackets, plates, frames and similar parts that hold lock mechanisms in place. It is used to index patents dealing with structural support features of locking devices.
[E05Y 2201/43] Motors -- E05Y 2201/43 covers motors that are integrated into fixed-construction lock devices, falling under the Constructional elements subgroup of the indexing scheme for locks. It pertains to power-driven actuators used to open, close or secure such locking mechanisms.
[E05Y 2201/434] Electromotors -- This subclass covers electromotors incorporated in locking mechanisms, detailing motor-driven components that actuate bolt or latch movement. It falls under fixed constructions and locks, providing an indexing scheme for patents involving electric actuation of locks.
[E05Y 2201/438] Rotors -- The CPC subclass E05Y 2201/438 pertains to rotors used as constructional elements in fixed-construction lock mechanisms. It classifies devices in which a rotating component engages or transmits motion within a lock, such as cam or wheel rotors that move a bolt or latch. This entry falls under the broader categories of Fixed Constructions and Locks.
[E05Y 2201/442] Stators -- The E05Y 2201/442 subclass pertains to stators, the stationary parts of electromechanical devices such as motors, generators and actuators. It covers the design, mounting and integration of these fixed components within lock-type or other fixed constructions, focusing on their structural and functional attributes.
[E05Y 2201/446] Windings -- The subclass E05Y 2201/446 refers to the use of windings - typically coil or spring elements - as constructional parts of fixed-construction locks. It covers the arrangement of these windings around shafts or cores to provide tension, return-action or locking force within the lock mechanism.
[E05Y 2201/448] Fluid motors -- E05Y 2201/448 covers fluid-powered devices used to actuate lock mechanisms, such as hydraulic or pneumatic motors that convert fluid pressure into rotational or linear motion to move bolts or latch components. The subclass pertains to fixed constructions where the motor is integral to the lock assembly.
[E05Y 2201/454] Cylinders -- The CPC subclass E05Y 2201/454 covers cylinders used in locking mechanisms, referring to the rotating or sliding components that house the bolt or latch and receive the key or actuation device. These elements are integral to the construction of fixed locks, providing the primary means of securing doors, safes or other enclosures through mechanical movement.
[E05Y 2201/456] Pistons -- The CPC entry E05Y 2201/456 falls under Fixed Constructions → Locks → Constructional elements and covers pistons that are integral parts of lock mechanisms, used to transmit force or control movement within the locking device. It includes designs where the piston is a sealed, movable component that engages with other lock parts to lock or unlock a system.
[E05Y 2201/458] Valves -- E05Y 2201/458 pertains to valves incorporated in fixed constructions such as locks, describing devices that regulate fluid flow within lock mechanisms. It includes gate, plug, butterfly and similar valves used to control water or hydraulic pressure in permanent lock structures.
[E05Y 2201/46] Magnets -- E05Y 2201/46 relates to the incorporation of magnets as constructional elements in fixed-construction lock mechanisms. It classifies designs where magnetic components are used to secure, release or assist the operation of locks, fitting within the broader categories of fixed constructions and locks.
[E05Y 2201/462] Electromagnets -- This subclass covers the use of electromagnets as components within fixed lock constructions, where the magnetic device provides actuation or holding functions. It includes designs that integrate electromagnets into the lock's mechanical structure for electronic control or power-assist.
[E05Y 2201/47] Springs -- E05Y 2201/47 refers to springs employed in fixed constructions, specifically within lock mechanisms. It covers constructional elements that provide elastic or resilient force-such as coil, helical or leaf springs-used to return lock components to a default position or to store mechanical energy.
[E05Y 2201/474] Compression springs -- E05Y 2201/474 designates compression springs used as constructional elements in fixed constructions such as locks. The subclass covers springs that store energy by being compressed and release it to provide axial force in locking mechanisms.
[E05Y 2201/476] Disk springs -- E05Y 2201/476 refers to disk springs, a type of constructional element - used in locks and other fixed constructions.
[E05Y 2201/478] Gas springs -- E05Y 2201/478 designates gas springs as constructional elements within the fixed-construction locks subclass of the CPC. The code covers spring-loaded gas pistons used to provide controlled movement or force in locking mechanisms and related hardware. It is used for indexing patents concerning such devices in Europe and the United States.
[E05Y 2201/48] Leaf or leg springs -- Leaf or leg springs are flexible metal strips or cantilevered components used in lock mechanisms to store and release energy, providing controlled movement of lock parts. In the CPC they fall under fixed constructions for locks, classified under E05Y 2201/48 as a specific type of constructional element.
[E05Y 2201/482] Ribbon springs -- Ribbon springs are thin, flat metal strips formed into a serpentine shape that flex elastically to provide biasing or actuation within lock mechanisms and other fixed constructions. They are employed to return components to a default position, control movement, or absorb shock in devices such as latches, seals, and other lock assemblies.
[E05Y 2201/484] Torsion springs -- This subclass relates to torsion springs employed as constructional elements in fixed constructions such as locks. It covers springs that store mechanical energy by twisting, providing restoring torque for latch or fastening mechanisms. The code E05Y 2201/484 identifies this specific element within the CPC.
[E05Y 2201/486] Torsion rods -- E05Y 2201/486 classifies torsion rods, the spring-type components that store and release angular energy to return a lock's bolt or latch to its original position after actuation, commonly found in dead-bolt, padlock and other mechanical locking devices. This falls under the Fixed Constructions → Locks hierarchy of the Cooperative Patent Classification system.
[E05Y 2201/488] Traction springs -- Traction springs are mechanical elements used in locking mechanisms to exert a pulling or tension force, typically to draw a bolt or latch into a locked position and maintain it until released. They are installed within the lock body and work by storing energy when compressed or twisted, then releasing to move the locking component.
[E05Y 2201/49] Wrap springs -- Wrap springs are helical coils that encircle a core or shaft to store torsional energy and provide a restoring or holding force in lock mechanisms. In the CPC they are classified under fixed constructions for locks, specifically the constructional elements subcategory. The code E05Y 2201/49 identifies this element.
[E05Y 2201/496] Double acting springs -- E05Y 2201/496 covers double-acting springs used in fixed constructions such as locks, where the spring provides force in both compression and extension. These components are designed to store energy and release it in two opposite directions, enabling mechanisms like latch retraction and bolt advancement. The entry falls under the constructional elements category of the CPC hierarchy.
[E05Y 2201/499] Spring tensioners -- E05Y 2201/499 covers spring-tensioners used in lock assemblies and other fixed constructions. It includes devices in which a spring applies a pulling or compressive force to keep a bolt, latch or other locking element in a defined position, enhancing security and reliability in building and mechanical lock applications.
[E05Y 2201/50] Weights -- E05Y 2201/50 covers the use of weights as constructional elements in lock mechanisms, including any adjustable or fixed masses that provide tension, balance or positioning of moving parts such as bolts, levers or tumblers. It pertains to fixed-construction locks where the weight contributes to the functional operation or security of the device.
[E05Y 2201/502] Wing weights -- Wing weights are balanced masses attached to the movable parts of a lock or latch mechanism to counteract gravity and friction, ensuring smooth operation and consistent positioning when the element is opened or closed. They are typically molded or screwed onto the moving arm or bolt to maintain alignment and reduce wear.
[E05Y 2201/60] Suspension or transmission members -- The class deals with parts that connect and convey force between the fixed elements of a lock, including rods, bars, levers, cams, springs and other members that suspend or transmit motion or load within the locking assembly.
[E05Y 2201/604] Transmission members -- E05Y 2201/604 covers transmission members within fixed constructions of locks, referring to components that convey motion or force between lock parts such as levers, gears, cams, rods or chains.
[E05Y 2201/606] Accessories therefor -- The CPC code E05Y 2201/606 covers accessories designed for fixed lock constructions. It includes supplementary components that enhance or support the primary lock mechanism, such as protective plates, mounting hardware, or auxiliary fastening devices.
[E05Y 2201/608] Back-drive -- Back-drive in CPC E05Y 2201/608 covers lock constructions where the output shaft can drive the input, allowing the lock to be opened by applying torque to the follower rather than the primary actuator. This reverse-motion feature is used in safety devices and tamper-resistant mechanisms.
[E05Y 2201/61] Cooperation between suspension or transmission members -- The subclass E05Y 2201/61 covers designs in which suspension or transmission members work together, such as linked lock components that cooperate to convey motion or force. It is placed under fixed constructions, locks, and the indexing scheme for constructional elements.
[E05Y 2201/612] between carriers and rails -- E05Y 2201/612 covers components situated between the carrier and the rail in fixed-construction lock assemblies, defining the interfacing elements that guide or support movement within the locking mechanism.
[E05Y 2201/614] Anti-derailing means -- E05Y 2201/614 covers anti-derailing devices incorporated in lock assemblies to keep moving parts such as bolts, levers or dead-bolts securely within their guiding channels and prevent them from being displaced or withdrawn from the lock housing during operation or impact. These mechanisms typically use retaining tabs, shoulders or guided tracks to maintain alignment and ensure reliable locking performance.
[E05Y 2201/616] to ensure mutual engagement, e.g. counter-rollers -- The code E05Y 2201/616 classifies mechanisms that achieve mutual engagement of lock parts by means such as counter-rollers, keeping the elements firmly coupled within fixed constructions. It covers designs where the rollers press against each other or adjoining surfaces to prevent relative movement and ensure secure locking.
[E05Y 2201/618] Transmission ratio variation -- The CPC subclass E05Y 2201/618 covers lock constructions that incorporate a variable transmission ratio, such as adjustable gear trains or mechanisms that can change the speed-torque relationship between the actuation input and the locking output. It includes designs where the ratio can be modified to suit different operating conditions or security levels.
[E05Y 2201/62] Synchronisation of suspension or transmission members -- The code E05Y 2201/62 refers to devices that coordinate the motion of suspension or transmission parts so they operate in unison, typically by linking gears, belts, cams or similar mechanisms. Such synchronisation ensures consistent force distribution or timing within fixed constructions and locking systems.
[E05Y 2201/622] Suspension or transmission members elements -- A CPC subclass for fixed-construction lock devices that addresses the elements forming suspension or transmission members. It includes components such as rods, links, shafts or similar parts that convey motion or force within the lock's moving mechanism. This grouping helps index patents concerning the structural parts that support or transmit movement in lock assemblies.
[E05Y 2201/624] Arms -- The E05Y 2201/624 subclass pertains to the structural elements of locks, specifically the arms that transmit motion, retain position or engage the locking mechanism. It includes levers, dead-bolt arms, latch-type members and other projecting components that form part of a lock's constructional assembly.
[E05Y 2201/626] Levers -- This CPC subclass comprises levers that form part of lock mechanisms, including lever tumblers, lever bolts and other lever-type components that engage or transmit motion to operate the lock. It covers the structural elements of levers used to secure or release the locking device.
[E05Y 2201/628] Bearings -- E05Y 2201/628 defines bearings employed in lock mechanisms and other fixed constructions. The subclass groups inventions that incorporate such bearing elements within locks.
[E05Y 2201/63] Races -- The CPC subclass E05Y 2201/63 "Races" covers race-type mechanisms incorporated in lock constructions, where a rotating or sliding element transfers motion to engage or release a locking bolt or latch. It includes designs in which the race interacts with cams, pins or other components to produce the locking or unlocking action.
[E05Y 2201/632] Sleeves -- E05Y 2201/632 Sleeves covers tubular or cylindrical elements that form part of a lock's fixed construction, serving to guide, protect or house internal components such as pins, tumblers or bolts within the lock mechanism.
[E05Y 2201/636] Universal or ball joints -- Universal or ball joints are constructional elements used in fixed-construction lock assemblies to connect parts while permitting angular movement and misalignment. They consist of a spherical bearing that transmits torque or force between components, allowing a range of motion without compromising the integrity of the lock mechanism. This facilitates flexible installation and reliable operation in various locking devices.
[E05Y 2201/638] Cams -- Cams are rotating or reciprocating constructional elements used in fixed lock assemblies to convert uniform motion into a predetermined variable movement, enabling controlled engagement or release of the locking mechanism. The subclass E05Y 2201/638 covers the design, shape and positioning of such cams within lock structures.
[E05Y 2201/64] Carriers -- The subclass E05Y 2201/64 defines carriers that are integral parts of fixed-construction locks, serving to hold, guide or transmit motion to other lock components. It falls within the constructional-elements grouping of the indexing scheme for lock subclasses.
[E05Y 2201/642] Trackless carriers -- E05Y 2201/642 pertains to trackless carriers, which are movable supporting structures for conveying loads without fixed rails, such as wheeled or sled-type transport devices used in construction, material handling or similar applications. The subclass falls under fixed constructions and describes constructional elements that operate independently of tracks.
[E05Y 2201/644] Flexible elongated pulling elements -- E05Y 2201/644 designates flexible elongated pulling elements such as cables, cords, or straps that are integrated into lock assemblies to transmit pulling forces for actuating a lock. These components may be made of metal, polymer or composite material and are used to link the lock's operating mechanism with a remote handle or lever. The subclass covers their construction, arrangement and attachment within fixed lock constructions.
[E05Y 2201/646] continuous, e.g. closed loops -- The CPC subclass E05Y 2201/646 covers continuous, closed-loop elements used in fixed lock constructions, such as rings or cables that form an uninterrupted circuit. It is part of the "Locks" group under "Fixed constructions" and pertains to constructional components that operate as a single, unbroken loop.
[E05Y 2201/648] having teeth -- The subgroup E05Y 2201/648 designates lock constructions that incorporate tooth-like elements, such as gear-type projections or serrations used to engage, retain or release the locking mechanism. It falls under Fixed Constructions → Locks → Constructional elements in the CPC indexing scheme.
[E05Y 2201/652] Belts -- E05Y 2201/652 classifies belts that are part of fixed construction elements, especially those incorporated in lock mechanisms. It includes the design, arrangement and integration of belts used to transmit motion or force within such structures.
[E05Y 2201/654] Cables -- E05Y 2201/654 classifies cables incorporated in fixed constructions, especially those used in lock assemblies and other building components. It covers all types of cable elements that are integral to structural or security installations, including reinforcing, securing, or power-transmission cables embedded in the construction.
[E05Y 2201/656] Chains -- E05Y 2201/656 covers chains used as constructional elements in fixed constructions such as locks, where the chain functions to transmit motion, secure components or provide tension within the lock mechanism.
[E05Y 2201/658] Members cooperating with flexible elongated pulling elements -- This subclass covers lock mechanisms in which a flexible elongated pulling element such as a cable, strap or rope works together with a fixed component to actuate or release a locking element. It includes designs where the pulling element transmits force to a latch, bolt or similar member, allowing operation without rigid links, and is applied to doors, windows, containers and similar constructions.
[E05Y 2201/66] Deflectors -- Deflectors are structural parts incorporated in fixed constructions such as locks to guide, redirect or restrict the movement of a bolt, latch or other moving element, enhancing control or security of the mechanism. They are typically rigid plates, tabs or angled surfaces positioned to deflect motion away from a predefined path.
[E05Y 2201/662] Cable sheaths -- E05Y 2201/662 pertains to cable sheaths, protective coverings applied to electrical cables to guard against mechanical damage, moisture and environmental wear. It is classified under constructional elements within the fixed constructions-locks group of the CPC.
[E05Y 2201/664] Drums -- The E05Y 2201/664 subclass pertains to drums as constructional elements in fixed lock assemblies. It includes cylindrical components that rotate to transmit motion, position or retain pins or tumblers within a lock mechanism. These drums are integral to the operation of mechanical locking devices.
[E05Y 2201/666] Magazines -- A magazine is a removable or fixed container integrated into a lock that holds a set of key pins, tumblers, or other locking elements, allowing quick replacement or reconfiguration of the lock's internal components. It is commonly used in high-security or master-keyed systems to store multiple pins or cores for rapid servicing and key change. This element falls under the fixed-construction category for locks.
[E05Y 2201/668] Pulleys -- The subclass E05Y 2201/668 covers pulleys employed as constructional elements within fixed lock assemblies, focusing on wheel-or-sheave devices that transmit force or motion in lock mechanisms. [E05Y 2201/67] in tackles -- E05Y 2201/67 designates fixed-construction lock arrangements that incorporate tackle mechanisms, such as pulley or rope systems, for moving or securing lock components. [E05Y 2201/672] Tensioners, tension sensors -- E05Y 2201/672 covers devices that apply or monitor pulling forces in fixed constructions, such as tensioners and tension sensors used with locks and other building elements. [E05Y 2201/674] Friction wheels -- E05Y 2201/674 refers to friction wheels employed in lock mechanisms as part of fixed construction elements. The subclass covers wheels that transmit motion or force through surface friction, such as cams or rotating elements that engage with other lock components to secure or release the lock.
[E05Y 2201/676] Transmission of human force -- E05Y 2201/676 designates constructional elements that transmit human-applied force, such as levers, handles, or other mechanisms that convey manual effort to operate or secure a fixed construction like a lock. It falls under the broader categories of fixed constructions, locks, and indexing schemes for subclasses.
[E05Y 2201/678] Hand chains -- Hand chains are metal or synthetic links attached to a door and its frame, allowing a door to be partially opened while remaining secured.
