[E05Y 2201/68] Handles, cranks -- E05Y 2201/68 relates to the design and structure of handles and cranks employed on fixed-construction locks, covering features that allow manual turning or pulling and their integration with the locking mechanism.
[E05Y 2201/682] Pins -- Pins are small metal or polymer elements used in lock mechanisms to engage tumblers or other components, allowing the lock to be set, released or adjusted. In the CPC hierarchy they fall under Fixed Constructions → Locks → Constructional elements, and the code E05Y 2201/682 designates this specific type of lock component.
[E05Y 2201/684] Rails -- E05Y 2201/684 pertains to rails as fixed construction components, especially within locking mechanisms and related hardware. The code groups inventions that use rail-shaped elements to guide, support or secure moving parts in a variety of devices.
[E05Y 2201/686] Rods, links -- The CPC subclass E05Y 2201/686 groups rod-type components and links used as constructional elements in fixed lock mechanisms. It covers straight, bent or articulated metal or polymer rods that transmit motion, hold positions or connect lock parts such as bolts, cams and tumblers. This heading is part of the broader fixed constructions and locks hierarchy.
[E05Y 2201/688] Rollers -- E05Y 2201/688 refers to rollers employed in lock hardware as part of fixed constructions. The code covers roller-type cam, bolt or latch elements that rotate or roll to lock or release a mechanism, typically integrated within the lock body. It falls under the constructional elements category of the Cooperative Patent Classification.
[E05Y 2201/69] having inclined axes -- E05Y 2201/69 covers lock constructions whose functional parts - such as cams, levers, bolts or tumblers - are arranged with axes that are tilted relative to a reference plane, rather than being parallel or perpendicular. The inclined orientation is used to achieve specific locking actions, improve mechanical advantage, or accommodate space constraints in fixed-construction lock designs.
[E05Y 2201/692] having vertical axes -- The subclass E05Y 2201/692 within the CPC addresses fixed-construction lock mechanisms that operate around vertical axes. It includes designs where the main rotating or moving component, such as a vertical bolt or cam shaft, is oriented vertically for doors, windows or similar closures. This code is used to index patents describing lock hardware whose motion depends on a vertical axis.
[E05Y 2201/694] Scissor mechanisms -- Scissor mechanisms are articulated linkages of crossed arms that open and close in a synchronized motion, providing compact folding or expanding movement for fixed constructions such as doors, gates, and other locking assemblies. The subclass covers the structural arrangement of these linked members and their actuation methods.
[E05Y 2201/696] Screw mechanisms -- Screw mechanisms are devices that convert rotational movement into linear displacement using a threaded shaft and nut, commonly employed in lock assemblies to adjust tension, secure bolts, or engage cam levers. They may include lead screws, adjusters, or self-locking threads and are designed to provide precise, repeatable positioning within fixed construction elements.
[E05Y 2201/70] Nuts -- The CPC subclass E05Y 2201/70 covers nuts, which are threaded fasteners that mate with bolts or screws to lock components together in fixed constructions such as locks. It includes various shapes, materials and securing features designed to resist loosening under load, and falls under the broader categories of constructional elements, locks and fixed constructions.
[E05Y 2201/702] Spindles -- E05Y 2201/702 classifies spindles, the rotating shafts or rods that transmit motion within lock mechanisms. It falls under Fixed constructions → Locks → Constructional elements, grouping components that form the core mechanical structure of locking devices. This subclass is used for patents describing spindle design, materials, or integration in locks.
[E05Y 2201/704] Worm wheels -- E05Y 2201/704 designates worm wheels, the toothed components that mesh with a worm screw to transmit rotary motion at right angles. The class groups the constructional features of these gear elements, covering materials, geometry, mounting and related details within the broader categories of fixed constructions and locks. This entry is used for patent classification of inventions involving worm-wheel design, manufacture or application.
[E05Y 2201/706] Shafts -- The CPC subclass E05Y 2201/706 classifies shafts that form part of a lock's constructional elements. It includes rotating or sliding shafts that transmit motion, serve as cams, or provide a mounting axis for other lock components. This code is applied to designs where the shaft is a fundamental element of the locking mechanism.
[E05Y 2201/708] Sliders -- Sliders are movable members that slide relative to a fixed part to engage or release a locking function, typically used in doors, windows or other fixed constructions. In the CPC they fall under the constructional elements of locks, providing linear motion to position or secure a locking element. This subclass covers designs where the sliding action is an integral part of the lock's operation.
[E05Y 2201/71] Toothed gearing -- E05Y 2201/71 is the CPC subclass that deals with toothed gearing, describing the design and interaction of meshing gear teeth used in fixed constructions such as locks. It falls under the hierarchy of Fixed Constructions → Locks → Constructional elements and indexes related gear configurations.
[E05Y 2201/712] with incomplete toothing -- This subclass of the CPC Fixed Constructions - Locks scheme covers lock mechanisms that employ incomplete or partial tooth profiles on their engaging elements. It includes designs where the toothing is not continuous around the full circumference, such as staggered or spaced teeth used to control torque transfer or enhance security. The classification applies to both mechanical and electronic locks employing this specific tooth configuration.
[E05Y 2201/716] Pinions -- Pinions are toothed wheels that mesh with other gears to transmit rotary motion in lock mechanisms. In the CPC hierarchy they fall under Fixed Constructions → Locks → Constructional elements, classifying the gear components that drive locking bolts or cams. This entry (E05Y 2201/716) specifically covers the design and integration of such pinions in lock assemblies.
[E05Y 2201/718] Bevelled pinions -- Bevelled pinions are gear elements whose teeth are cut at an angle, allowing them to mesh with intersecting gear shafts in fixed lock constructions. In lock mechanisms they transmit rotary motion between non-parallel axes, often forming part of the indexing scheme that positions tumblers or levers. This subclass (E05Y 2201/718) groups such angled pinion components used in the constructional elements of locks.
[E05Y 2201/72] Planetary gearing -- The CPC subclass E05Y 2201/72 covers planetary gear arrangements employed in the constructional elements of fixed-construction locking devices. It includes gear sets where a central sun gear, one or more planet gears, and a surrounding ring gear transmit motion in a compact, high-torque configuration, typically used to drive bolt or latch mechanisms. This subclass groups patents that specifically integrate such gearing systems into lock hardware.
[E05Y 2201/722] Racks -- E05Y 2201/722 refers to racks, which are fixed construction elements used for supporting or storing items, often incorporated into lock systems or other secure structures. The subclass falls under Fixed Constructions > Locks > Constructional elements and defines racks that may be part of the mechanism or mounting framework.
[E05Y 2201/724] Flexible -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Flexible push-pull elements, e.g. flexible elements rigid in one direction.
[E05Y 2201/726] Ring gears -- E05Y 2201/726 covers ring gears, which are circular toothed components that transmit motion between concentric shafts or within a fixed housing. They are classified under fixed constructions and locks as constructional elements used for power transmission in gear assemblies.
[E05Y 2400/00] Electronic control -- E05Y 2400/00 covers electronic control systems for fixed lock constructions, including sensors, actuators, circuits and software that manage lock actuation, monitoring or access verification, integrating electronic devices with mechanical locking mechanisms.
[E05Y 2400/10] Electronic control -- Electronic control refers to lock systems that are operated by electronic means rather than purely mechanical action, encompassing devices such as keypads, card readers, biometric sensors, remote or wireless actuators, and micro-processor based controllers that manage locking and unlocking functions. This subclass covers the integration of electronic circuits, power supplies, and signal processing with the mechanical lock hardware to enable automated or remotely commanded access control.
[E05Y 2400/20] of brakes, disengaging means, holders or stops -- E05Y 2400/20 classifies electronic control of fixed constructions such as locks, specifically addressing brakes, disengaging means, holders or stops that are used to arrest or release motion in these systems.
[E05Y 2400/202] Force or torque control -- This subclass covers electronic control methods that regulate the force or torque applied in lock mechanisms, such as actuators that ensure proper engagement, release or holding strength. It is part of the fixed-construction group for locks and is indexed under the scheme for electronic control of force or torque.
[E05Y 2400/21] by controlling the viscosity -- The subclass E05Y 2400/21 covers lock mechanisms that use electronic control to adjust the viscosity of a fluid, such as hydraulic, magnetorheological or electro-rheological liquids, in order to engage or release the locking device. This approach enables variable resistance or movement of lock components based on real-time viscosity changes, providing adaptive security or convenience functions.
[E05Y 2400/30] of motors
[E05Y 2400/3013] during manual wing operation -- The entry E05Y 2400/3013 refers to electronically-controlled lock devices that operate while a wing is moved manually, such as mechanisms used to secure aircraft or turbine wings during manual deployment or adjustment. It classifies the integration of electronic control functions with manual wing-operation locking systems.
[E05Y 2400/3014] Back driving the transmission or motor -- E05Y 2400/3014 pertains to fixed-construction lock systems in which external forces cause a transmission or motor to rotate in reverse, such as when electronic control is lost or overridden, allowing the driven components to be back-driven for safety or functional reasons.
[E05Y 2400/3015] Power assistance -- E05Y 2400/3015 - Power assistance refers to fixed lock constructions equipped with electronic control that provide mechanical aid-typically electric, hydraulic or pneumatic-to assist the opening or closing of the lock, reducing the effort required from the user.
[E05Y 2400/3016] Overriding existing wing movement -- This subclass covers mechanisms that, via electronic control, can supersede a previously programmed or default motion of a door or window wing, enabling an alternative position or operation. It is used for safety or convenience in lock systems that adjust wing positions.
[E05Y 2400/3017] Safety means therefor -- E05Y 2400/3017 classifies safety features integrated into lock systems that are controlled electronically. It covers devices and methods that prevent unauthorized operation, such as sensors, monitoring circuits, or fail-safe mechanisms, and is placed under the fixed-construction, lock subgroup of the CPC.
[E05Y 2400/302] during electric motor braking -- The CPC subclass E05Y 2400/302 covers fixed constructions for locks that are actuated by an electric motor while the motor is in a braking phase, using electronic control to engage or release the locking mechanism during motor deceleration. This pertains to devices where the braking action of the motor directly triggers the lock operation.
[E05Y 2400/304] Voltage control -- E05Y 2400/304 designates voltage-control mechanisms within electronic control systems for fixed-construction locks, as defined in the CPC indexing scheme for lock subclasses. It covers methods and devices that regulate electrical voltage to operate or secure locking hardware.
[E05Y 2400/306] Temperature control -- E05Y 2400/306 designates temperature control mechanisms incorporated in fixed constructions such as locks, particularly those employing electronic control. It covers systems that regulate heating or cooling to maintain desired temperatures for security components or related devices.
[E05Y 2400/31] Force or torque control -- The CPC subclass E05Y 2400/31 covers electronic control systems that regulate the force or torque applied by lock mechanisms. It pertains to fixed-construction locks where the actuation or holding force is adjusted through electronic sensing or feedback, ensuring precise engagement or release. This subclass falls under the hierarchy of Fixed Constructions → Locks → Indexing Scheme Associated With Subclasses → Electronic control.
[E05Y 2400/315] Curve setting or adjusting -- E05Y 2400/315 covers methods and devices for adjusting the curvature of a lock's fixed construction, typically using electronic control mechanisms to set or fine-tune the lock's locking curve for precise engagement and release. It is used in lock designs where the curve must be programmable or remotely modified.
[E05Y 2400/32] Position control, detection or monitoring -- E05Y 2400/32 covers technologies that control, detect or monitor the position of locking components, typically using electronic or sensor-based methods to verify whether a lock is engaged, released or in an intermediate state. It applies to fixed construction systems where precise position feedback is required for safety or automation.
[E05Y 2400/322] by using absolute position sensors -- E05Y 2400/322 designates lock mechanisms that use electronic control with absolute position sensors to determine the exact position of moving parts, allowing precise actuation and status monitoring. It is a subclass of fixed constructions → locks → indexing scheme associated with subclasses.
[E05Y 2400/324] Switches -- E05Y 2400/324 classifies switches employed in fixed-construction lock mechanisms, specifically those that provide electronic control of locking functions. The entry covers devices that detect, initiate or terminate electrical signals to operate the lock's moving parts.
[E05Y 2400/326] of the angular type -- E05Y 2400/326 covers angular-type electronic control devices used in lock mechanisms that are part of fixed constructions. It falls under Fixed Constructions → Locks → Indexing scheme associated with subclasses → Electronic control and pertains to angular motion or positioning of electronic actuation elements in locking systems.
[E05Y 2400/328] of the linear type -- E05Y 2400/328 designates electronic control devices for locks that operate by linear motion, such as solenoid or linear-actuator mechanisms, used in fixed constructions to engage or release a locking element. This subclass groups patents that describe linear-type actuation for electronic lock systems.
[E05Y 2400/33] by using load sensors -- E05Y 2400/33 covers lock systems that incorporate load-sensing devices to monitor the force applied to the lock. By detecting pressure or strain, these electronic controls can trigger alarms, adjust locking torque, or provide tamper-resistance, enhancing security and operational reliability.
[E05Y 2400/332] Switches -- E05Y 2400/332 covers electronic switches employed in the control of fixed-construction locking devices. It pertains to the electronic control subsystem that activates or deactivates a lock's mechanism, allowing remote or automated operation of the lock. This subclass falls under fixed constructions, locks, indexing scheme associated with subclasses, and electronic control.
[E05Y 2400/334] by using pulse generators -- The subclass E05Y 2400/334 relates to fixed lock constructions whose operation is controlled electronically by pulse generators, defining how the generated pulses activate the locking or releasing mechanisms.
[E05Y 2400/336] of the angular type -- The CPC subclass E05Y 2400/336 groups angular-type locking mechanisms used in fixed constructions such as doors, windows and other building openings. It covers devices in which the locking element pivots about an axis, creating a latch or bolt that moves in an angular motion to engage or release the lock. The classification is applied to mechanical and electronic-controlled angular locks and their related components.
[E05Y 2400/337] Encoder wheels -- Encoder wheels are rotary devices that convert angular position or motion into electronic signals, commonly used in locking mechanisms and other fixed constructions to provide precise electronic control. In the CPC they appear under E05Y 2400/337, a subclass for electronic control components within the locks group.
[E05Y 2400/338] of the linear type -- E05Y 2400/338 covers lock mechanisms that are electronically controlled by a linear-type actuator, in which the unlocking or locking motion follows a straight-line path rather than a rotary one. The subclass includes devices such as solenoid-driven bolts or deadbolts and the associated electronic control circuitry.
[E05Y 2400/34] Pulse count limit setting -- This subclass covers electronic-controlled lock mechanisms that include a setting to limit the number of control pulses applied, allowing the device to stop operation after a predetermined count for safety or security purposes.
[E05Y 2400/342] Pulse count value setting or correcting -- This CPC subclass concerns electronic control of fixed-construction locks where the pulse count value is set or corrected, typically to adjust timing or actuation sequences. It includes methods and mechanisms for programming, updating, or calibrating pulse counters in electronic lock systems.
[E05Y 2400/35] related to specific positions -- The subclass E05Y 2400/35 covers electronic control features for fixed-construction locks that deal with the precise positioning of lock components. It includes systems that define, adjust or detect specific positions of locking elements through electronic means, enabling targeted actuation or monitoring within the lock assembly.
[E05Y 2400/354] End positions -- The subclass E05Y 2400/354 covers the defined final or neutral positions of lock components, describing how a lock's movable parts settle when engaged, released or powered off, especially in electronically controlled locking systems for fixed constructions.
[E05Y 2400/356] Intermediate positions -- A lock having electronically controlled intermediate positions provides a state between fully locked and fully unlocked, such as a hold-open or night-latch setting that permits limited or temporary access while maintaining security. The classification covers mechanisms that detect or set these partial-engagement positions through electronic actuation or sensors.
[E05Y 2400/358] in the proximity of end positions -- E05Y 2400/358 pertains to electronic control systems applied to locks that operate when moving parts approach their final positions, such as sensors or actuators that detect a bolt, latch or cam reaching fully closed, open or locked states to trigger or modify the locking action.
[E05Y 2400/36] Speed control, detection or monitoring -- E05Y 2400/36 relates to electronic systems that control, detect or monitor the speed of lock mechanisms or other fixed constructions. It includes sensors, controllers and feedback devices that adjust or report operating rates to ensure proper performance.
[E05Y 2400/37] by using acceleration sensors -- The CPC subclass E05Y 2400/37 covers lock devices that incorporate acceleration sensors to provide electronic control, such as detecting motion or vibration to trigger opening, closing or security functions. It falls under fixed constructions, locks, and indexing schemes linked to electronic control.
[E05Y 2400/40] Control units therefor -- E05Y 2400/40 denotes control units for electronic control of fixed constructions such as locks. It covers devices that manage, monitor or program the operation of locking mechanisms, including electronic or digital controllers integral to the lock's functioning.
[E05Y 2400/41] for multiple motors -- E05Y 2400/41 defines fixed-construction locks that employ electronic control using more than one motor. It covers lock mechanisms in which multiple actuating motors are coordinated to perform locking, unlocking or positioning functions, typically for enhanced speed, torque distribution or redundancy. This subclass sits under the broader categories of locks, indexing schemes for subclasses, and electronic control.
[E05Y 2400/415] for multiple wings -- The CPC subclass E05Y 2400/415 covers electronically controlled locking mechanisms that operate on multi-wing devices such as doors, cabinets or safes with two or more movable leaves. It pertains to systems where electronic actuation synchronises, locks or releases each wing individually.
[E05Y 2400/42] for multiple openings -- E05Y 2400/42 pertains to electronic control solutions for fixed-construction locks that enable multiple openings, such as systems allowing several doors, gates or compartments to be unlocked through a single electronic mechanism. This subclass addresses the integration of electronic actuation, sensing or programming features that manage more than one access point within the same locking arrangement.
[E05Y 2400/44] Sensors not directly associated with the wing movement -- E05Y 2400/44 covers sensors incorporated in lock or fixed-construction mechanisms that monitor variables such as temperature, pressure, vibration or position but are not directly linked to the movement of the lock's wing or rotating element. The classification groups these electronic-control sensors used for condition monitoring, diagnostics or safety functions rather than for actuating the wing itself.
[E05Y 2400/445] Switches -- Switches in the CPC class E05Y 2400/445 refer to devices that control the operation of locking mechanisms by establishing or breaking an electrical circuit. The entry covers electronic control components such as push-buttons, reed switches, magnetic, capacitive or photoelectric sensors that actuate lock bolts or latch releases. These switches are used in fixed constructions where the lock's opening and closing are governed by an electronic signal.
[E05Y 2400/446] Vehicle state sensors, e.g. parked or inclination -- E05Y 2400/446 covers sensors that monitor a vehicle's condition, such as whether it is parked, its inclination or other positional states, and provide that information to electronic control systems, particularly those related to lock mechanisms and security functions. It falls under fixed constructions and electronic control within the CPC.
[E05Y 2400/447] Moisture or submergence sensors -- This subclass deals with electronic sensors that detect moisture or submergence and are used to control lock mechanisms. It covers devices that trigger or modify locking actions when wet or underwater conditions are sensed, providing safety, security or anti-tamper functions.
[E05Y 2400/449] Pollutant or particulate sensors -- E05Y 2400/449 covers sensors that detect pollutants or particulate matter and are integrated into electronic control systems for fixed constructions such as locks. The subclass groups devices that monitor air quality, dust, gases or similar contaminants to trigger or modify lock operation.
[E05Y 2400/45] Control modes -- E05Y 2400/45 designates the electronic-control methods used in fixed lock constructions, covering the various control modes such as manual activation, remote signalling, timed operation, sensor-triggered or programmable control of locking mechanisms. It falls under the hierarchy of Fixed Constructions → Locks → Indexing scheme associated with subclasses → Electronic control in the Cooperative Patent Classification system.
[E05Y 2400/452] for saving energy, e.g. sleep or wake-up -- This subclass of the Cooperative Patent Classification (CPC) under Fixed Constructions - Locks, indexed by E05Y 2400/452, covers lock mechanisms that incorporate electronic control features to reduce power consumption, such as sleep modes or wake-up functions. It addresses energy-saving strategies for electronic locking devices, focusing on methods that enable the device to enter low-power states and resume operation on demand.
[E05Y 2400/454] for accommodating handicapped users -- The CPC subclass E05Y 2400/454 defines lock or fixed-construction systems that integrate electronic control features specifically designed to accommodate handicapped users, such as adaptive entry mechanisms, accessible keypads or automated opening functions. It groups technologies that provide easier or safer access for people with disabilities.
[E05Y 2400/456] for programming, e.g. learning or AI [artificial intelligence] -- The CPC subclass E05Y 2400/456 covers electronic control features that enable programming of lock mechanisms, including learning and artificial-intelligence techniques used to adapt or optimize fixed-construction locking systems. It addresses software-driven methods for improving lock performance and security.
[E05Y 2400/458] for generating service signals -- The CPC subclass E05Y 2400/458 falls under Fixed Constructions → Locks → Electronic Control and covers lock mechanisms that incorporate circuitry to generate service signals, such as status or diagnostic messages, to external devices or monitoring systems. [E05Y 2400/654] by electrical cables -- This subclass covers lock devices that are controlled or powered through electrical cables, including mechanisms where the cable supplies power or transmits signals to operate the locking function. [E05Y 2400/656] by travelling contacts -- A lock with fixed construction that uses electronic control through moving electrical contacts to transmit power or signals. [E05Y 2600/14] with position retaining means -- The subclass E05Y 2600/14 covers mounting or coupling arrangements for locks that incorporate position-retaining means, such as latches, pins, brackets or other devices that hold the lock in a predetermined orientation or location during operation. It is used to classify lock systems where the mechanism includes features that prevent unintended movement after placement.
[E05Y 2600/20] with specific transmission movement -- E05Y 2600/20 covers mounting or coupling arrangements in fixed lock constructions where movement is conveyed by a defined transmission mechanism such as gears, cams, levers or similar devices. The subclass addresses designs that provide a specific way of transmitting motion to achieve the locking function.
[E05Y 2600/30] Adjustment motion -- The subclass E05Y 2600/30 relates to mechanisms that provide adjustment motion for lock components, covering mounting or coupling arrangements that allow the position, alignment or tension of elements to be fine-tuned. It includes devices such as adjustable brackets, sliding or rotating links and other structures that enable controlled movement of the locking parts to achieve proper fit or operation.
[E05Y 2600/31] Linear motion -- E05Y 2600/31 covers linear-motion features of mounting or coupling arrangements in fixed lock constructions, such as bolts, latches or other lock elements that move along a straight path to engage or disengage. It classifies the design of components that provide this straight-line motion within a lock system.
[E05Y 2600/312] Horizontal motion -- The entry covers mounting or coupling arrangements that enable a lock component to move laterally, providing horizontal translation of the locking element such as sliding bolts, latch plates or other devices that shift side-to-side to engage or release a lock. It focuses on the mechanical configuration that produces this horizontal motion.
[E05Y 2600/314] Vertical motion -- E05Y 2600/314 refers to the vertical motion aspects of mounting or coupling arrangements for lock elements. It includes devices and mechanisms that raise, lower or translate lock components along a vertical axis, such as sliding bolts, liftable deadbolts or vertically-actuated fastening systems. The code is used to classify patents dealing with the vertical movement of fixed-construction lock hardware.
[E05Y 2600/32] Rotary motion -- The E05Y 2600/32 subclass covers lock-related devices that generate or transmit rotary motion within mounting or coupling arrangements for fixed-construction elements. It includes mechanisms such as cams, gears, shafts or other rotary actuators that drive locking or unlocking actions, as well as associated coupling structures.
[E05Y 2600/322] around a horizontal axis -- The code E05Y 2600/322 covers mounting or coupling arrangements for lock-related elements that rotate or swing about a horizontal axis, such as hinges, brackets or couplings that enable horizontal-axis movement of the locking component. It is used for fixed constructions where the primary motion of the lock or its accessory is centered on a horizontal line.
[E05Y 2600/324] around a vertical axis -- E05Y 2600/324 covers mounting or coupling arrangements for lock elements that rotate about a vertical axis, such as pivots, hinges or rotary mechanisms used in fixed constructions. It classifies devices where the locking hardware is oriented to turn around a vertical line to achieve opening or securing functions.
[E05Y 2600/33] Stepwise motion -- E05Y 2600/33 classifies stepwise motion mechanisms used in fixed constructions, specifically in locks, where the movement of a component occurs in discrete increments rather than continuously. This includes devices such as ratchets, pawls or indexed cam systems that advance or retract a lock element one step at a time.
[E05Y 2600/40] Mounting location -- The CPC subclass E05Y 2600/40 defines the mounting location for lock or fixed-construction components, describing where the elements are attached to a supporting structure such as a door, frame or panel. It addresses the spatial position, orientation and integration of the mounting arrangement within the lock system.
[E05Y 2600/41] Concealed -- E05Y 2600/41 covers mounting or coupling arrangements for lock elements that are concealed from view or access, such as hidden hinges, recessed fasteners, or cover plates that hide the fastening hardware. The focus is on designs that embed the lock components within surrounding structures to prevent detection or tampering.
[E05Y 2600/412] in the rabbet -- The CPC subclass E05Y 2600/412 covers mounting or coupling arrangements for lock components that are installed "in the rabbet," meaning the lock is positioned within a recessed groove or rebate cut into a fixed construction such as a door or panel. This subclass falls under Fixed constructions → Locks → Mounting or coupling arrangements for elements provided for in this subclass. It specifies how the lock is seated in the rabbet to achieve secure attachment.
[E05Y 2600/45] in or on the fixed frame -- This subclass addresses mounting or coupling arrangements for lock components that are situated directly on the fixed frame of a door, gate, window or similar structure. It includes designs where the lock or its parts are attached to the immovable frame rather than to the moving leaf, falling under fixed constructions of locks.
[E05Y 2600/452] in or on the floor or wall -- E05Y 2600/452 relates to mounting or coupling arrangements that secure elements directly to the floor or wall, such as fixtures, brackets, or locking devices installed on building surfaces. It is part of the Fixed Constructions > Locks hierarchy in the Cooperative Patent Classification used by the EPO and USPTO.
[E05Y 2600/454] in or on the motor -- The subclass covers locking or related elements that are mounted directly on a motor or integrated within the motor housing, focusing on the coupling arrangements that secure these components in fixed constructions. It is applied to inventions where the lock mechanism is positioned in or on the motor to transmit motion or provide attachment.
[E05Y 2600/456] in or on a suspension member -- A classification for mounting or coupling arrangements that are placed on or in a suspension member, such as a rod, cable, strap or chain, within lock assemblies. It covers devices that attach, support or link lock components to the suspended element.
[E05Y 2600/458] in or on a transmission member -- The subclass E05Y 2600/458 covers mounting or coupling arrangements that locate a lock or locking element directly on or in a transmission member such as a shaft, gear, belt or similar moving part, allowing the lock to act through the transmitting element. It is used for designs where the lock is integrated with the mechanical transmission rather than attached to a separate housing.
[E05Y 2600/46] in or on the wing -- E05Y 2600/46 pertains to mounting or coupling arrangements for components that are positioned in or on the wing of a vehicle, such as an aircraft or wind-turbine blade. It covers fixed-construction lock mechanisms that secure devices, sensors, or accessories directly on the wing surface.
[E05Y 2600/50] Mounting methods -- E05Y 2600/50 is a CPC subclass under Fixed Constructions → Locks that defines the ways elements described in the broader subclass are mounted or coupled together. It includes various mechanical arrangements such as brackets, fixtures, and linking mechanisms that secure lock components in place. This code is used to index patent documents dealing with specific mounting methods for lock hardware.
[E05Y 2600/502] Clamping -- The classification covers devices and methods that secure two or more parts by applying a compressive force, typically using a clamp or similar mechanism to hold fixed constructions such as lock components in place. It includes arrangements for mounting or coupling elements within a fixed system to provide a tight, non-permanent attachment.
[E05Y 2600/504] Expansion -- E05Y 2600/504 - Expansion refers to features that let components of a lock's mounting or coupling arrangement increase in size, often to accommodate thermal growth, adjust fit, or engage a broader range of positions. The subclass covers fixed-construction lock systems where such expansion mechanisms are integral to the mounting or coupling of the elements.
[E05Y 2600/506] Plastic deformation -- E05Y 2600/506 defines patent subject matter concerning the plastic deformation of components used in fixed-construction lock systems, specifically where such deformation is part of mounting or coupling arrangements for the elements covered by the broader subclass. It addresses ways in which lock parts are intentionally shaped or altered by plastic flow to achieve a secure or functional fit.
[E05Y 2600/508] Riveting -- E05Y 2600/508 deals with riveting as a mounting or coupling technique in fixed constructions, particularly within lock mechanisms. It includes devices and arrangements that use rivets to join components, creating a secure, permanent connection.
[E05Y 2600/51] Screwing or bolting -- E05Y 2600/51 covers mounting or coupling arrangements that are secured by screwing or bolting, specifically applied to fixed constructions such as locks. The subclass details how threaded fasteners are used to attach components within the broader category of fixed construction assemblies.
[E05Y 2600/52] Toolless -- E05Y 2600/52 covers lock mounting or coupling arrangements that can be installed, adjusted, or removed without the use of tools. It includes snap-fit, quick-release, and other toolless mechanisms that secure components in fixed constructions. The subclass addresses designs where the lock or related element is attached by manual force or simple motions rather than screws, bolts, or other tools.
[E05Y 2600/522] Axial stacking -- E05Y 2600/522 - Axial stacking refers to the arrangement of lock components or other fixed constructions stacked along a common longitudinal axis, allowing sequential placement or removal while maintaining alignment. This subclass covers coupling or mounting methods where elements are positioned one behind another rather than side-by-side.
[E05Y 2600/524] Friction -- This subclass deals with friction-based mounting or coupling devices used in lock assemblies, where friction between components holds parts together or permits controlled movement. It includes features such as friction pads, clutches, or surface treatments that generate resisting forces to secure or release locked elements without relying on mechanical interlocks.
[E05Y 2600/526] Gluing or cementing -- E05Y 2600/526 refers to the use of gluing or cementing techniques in the mounting or coupling of lock elements, part of the fixed constructions group for locks. It addresses how adhesive bonds attach components within lock assemblies.
[E05Y 2600/528] Hooking, e.g. using bayonets -- The entry covers devices that attach or secure components by means of a hook-type connection, such as bayonet fittings, enabling quick coupling or locking of parts within fixed constructions. It includes the structural features and methods used to engage and disengage the hooked elements.
[E05Y 2600/53] Snapping -- The code E05Y 2600/53 designates snapping mechanisms that join components by a snap-fit, using elastic deformation to secure parts without separate fasteners, typically in doors, panels or lock assemblies. It falls under mounting or coupling arrangements for elements in the locks subclass of fixed constructions.
[E05Y 2600/54] Welding -- The CPC entry E05Y 2600/54 falls under fixed constructions and locks, specifically covering welding techniques used to mount or couple components within those assemblies. It addresses the methods, equipment and joint designs for welding parts that form part of lock mechanisms or related fixed structures.
[E05Y 2600/56] Positioning, e.g. re-positioning, or pre-mounting -- The E05Y 2600/56 subclass concerns methods or devices for positioning, re-positioning or pre-mounting components of fixed constructions such as locks. It covers arrangements that aid in aligning or installing elements before final attachment, ensuring accurate placement within the overall mounting or coupling system.
[E05Y 2600/58] by using indicators or markings, e.g. scales -- This subclass pertains to fixed-construction lock or fastening arrangements that employ visual indicators, markings or scale markings to denote the position, adjustment or coupling of the components. The use of such markers simplifies assembly, alignment and inspection of the mounted elements.
[E05Y 2600/60] Mounting or coupling members -- The E05Y 2600/60 subclass defines mounting or coupling members that attach or join lock components to a supporting structure, such as brackets, plates, fittings or other devices that secure the lock mechanism in place. It covers the various ways these elements are linked or fixed together within lock assemblies.
[E05Y 2600/61] Threaded members -- The E05Y 2600/61 subclass covers threaded components such as screws, bolts, studs and other members with helical ridges that are employed in fixed-construction lock assemblies. It pertains to the mounting or coupling arrangements of these threaded parts within the lock mechanism, detailing how they secure, align or transmit force between lock elements.
[E05Y 2600/62] Bolts -- E05Y 2600/62 classifies bolts that serve as mounting or coupling elements in fixed-construction lock assemblies. It pertains to the way bolts are used to secure, connect, or adjust components within lock mechanisms, outlining their functional role in the overall construction.
[E05Y 2600/622] Dowels -- Dowels are cylindrical pins used to align, locate or join components in fixed-construction assemblies, often serving as simple, non-threaded coupling elements within lock mechanisms or other mounted parts. They provide a straightforward means of transmitting shear loads and maintaining positional accuracy between adjoining pieces.
[E05Y 2600/624] Nuts -- The CPC subclass E05Y 2600/624 covers nuts used as fastening components in mounting-or-coupling arrangements for lock hardware. It is situated within fixed constructions and addresses the design and integration of nuts that secure elements in lock mechanisms.
[E05Y 2600/626] Plates or brackets -- This sub-group covers plates, brackets or similar mounting elements that are used to attach or couple components of a lock assembly. It classifies inventions where such support parts are integral to the fixed construction of the locking device, specifying their shape, attachment method or functional arrangement.
[E05Y 2600/628] Profiles -- The entry E05Y 2600/628 covers the shape and cross-sectional profile of components used to mount or couple elements in lock mechanisms. It pertains to fixed constructions of locks, specifying how particular profiles enable secure attachment or alignment of parts within the overall assembly.
[E05Y 2600/63] Retainers -- E05Y 2600/63 Retainers covers devices that hold or support components in fixed-construction lock systems, providing mounting or coupling arrangements to secure elements such as bolts, shafts or plates.
[E05Y 2600/632] Screws -- E05Y 2600/632 designates screws used as mounting or coupling components in fixed-construction lock assemblies. The classification covers threaded fasteners that connect or secure elements of lock mechanisms.
[E05Y 2600/634] Spacers -- The code E05Y 2600/634 designates spacers that are part of mounting or coupling arrangements for lock elements within fixed-construction systems. These components maintain a defined gap or separation between parts, ensuring correct alignment and spacing in the assembled lock mechanism.
[E05Y 2600/636] Washers -- Washers are thin, typically disc-shaped elements inserted between mating parts of a lock to spread load, prevent loosening, and accommodate minor misalignments. In fixed-construction lock assemblies they serve as mounting or coupling components that secure bolts, pins or shafts and help maintain proper spacing and alignment. This subclass covers the specific use of washers within such lock-related arrangements.

[E05Y 2800/108] Lubrication -- E05Y 2800/108 - Lubrication covers the application of oils, greases, waxes or other lubricants to the moving parts of fixed lock constructions such as bolts, tumblers, hinges and cylinders to reduce friction, wear and corrosion, and may include related dispensers or maintenance accessories not otherwise classified.
[E05Y 2800/12] Sealing -- The E05Y 2800/12 subclass groups sealing solutions related to fixed-construction locks, including gaskets, O-rings, sealing compounds and other devices that prevent the ingress of liquids, dust or debris into the lock housing or its moving parts. It also covers methods of applying or integrating such seals in lock assemblies.
[E05Y 2800/122] Telescopic action -- Telescopic action refers to the linear extension or retraction of a component by nesting sections within one another, commonly used in lock mechanisms to adjust length, provide reach, or accommodate variable positions. [E05Y 2800/13] Sequential actions -- A sequential-action lock is a fixed construction where the opening or closing movements occur in a predetermined order, typically involving a series of cams, levers or tumblers that engage one after another. This subclass captures auxiliary features, accessories and procedural steps for such locks that are not covered by more specific categories, covering any detail of the staged mechanism used to achieve the intended locking function.

It includes arrangements where the fixed and moving sections are integrated into a single construction, and related accessories or auxiliary operations not covered elsewhere.
[E05Y 2800/162] the wings being coplanar when the movable wing is in the closed position -- E05Y 2800/162 classifies lock constructions of the "fixed constructions" group that include details, accessories or auxiliary operations not covered elsewhere, specifically where the movable wing aligns in the same plane as the fixed wing when the lock is closed, ensuring the wings are coplanar in the closed position.

[E05Y 2800/362] horizontal frame members -- E05Y 2800/362 covers components that form the horizontal members of a lock's frame, including any ancillary parts or operations related to those members that are not specified elsewhere. It is used for patents describing the design, attachment, or adjustment of horizontal frame sections in fixed-construction lock systems.
[E05Y 2800/364] vertical frame members -- E05Y 2800/364 covers vertical frame members that form the upright components of lock assemblies within fixed constructions. It includes any supporting or aligning members for the lock mechanism that are not otherwise classified in more specific subclasses.
[E05Y 2800/37] Length, width or depth adjustment -- A CPC subclass for fixed constructions, specifically locks, that covers any detail, accessory or auxiliary operation enabling adjustment of length, width or depth of the device, where such features are not otherwise listed. It falls under the hierarchy FIXED CONSTRUCTIONS → LOCKS → INDEXING SCHEME ASSOCIATED WITH SUBCLASSES.
[E05Y 2800/372] Telescopically -- The CPC entry E05Y 2800/372 falls under Fixed Constructions > Locks > Indexing scheme associated with subclasses and covers telescopic elements that form part of lock assemblies. It includes details, accessories and auxiliary operations involving a telescopic action that are not otherwise specified in the classification.
[E05Y 2800/40] Physical or chemical protection -- The subclass E05Y 2800/40 groups patents for auxiliary elements that give physical or chemical protection to locking mechanisms, such as seals, barriers or protective coatings not covered elsewhere. It covers details, accessories and operations that enhance security against intrusion, corrosion or environmental damage.
[E05Y 2800/402] against corrosion -- A technical solution for protecting fixed lock constructions and related accessories from corrosion, including any auxiliary measures not covered by other subclasses. This entry addresses protective coatings, materials or designs that mitigate corrosion effects in lock hardware.
[E05Y 2800/404] against component faults or failure -- The E05Y 2800/404 subclass groups measures that protect lock components from faults or failure, covering details, accessories and auxiliary operations that are not specified elsewhere. It applies to fixed-construction locking devices and includes protective designs, monitoring features and preventive maintenance techniques.
[E05Y 2800/406] against deformation -- E05Y 2800/406 classifies devices, accessories and auxiliary measures that protect lock components from deformation, such as supports, reinforcements or corrective mechanisms not covered by other subclasses. It falls under Fixed Constructions → Locks → Indexing scheme associated with subclasses and captures any detail aimed at preventing shape or dimensional change.
[E05Y 2800/407] plastic deformation -- E05Y 2800/407 - plastic deformation refers to methods and devices that deform lock components or related fixed constructions by applying permanent strain, such as shaping, hardening or adjusting metal parts, where the operation is not covered by other subclasses.
[E05Y 2800/409] against faulty mounting or coupling -- E05Y 2800/409 deals with measures, devices or methods that prevent or correct errors in mounting or coupling of lock components within fixed constructions. It covers auxiliary or accessory features that ensure proper alignment, secure attachment or reliable coupling of locks where more specific subclasses are not applicable.
[E05Y 2800/41] against finger injury -- E05Y 2800/41 covers safety devices and auxiliary features incorporated in lock mechanisms to prevent finger injuries, such as protective sleeves, retractable bolts, finger-guard panels or other measures that reduce the risk of accidental pinching or crushing during operation. This subclass is used for detailing those accessories not otherwise covered in the main lock categories.
[E05Y 2800/412] against friction -- Entry E05Y 2800/412 pertains to lock devices and related constructions where friction-reducing features are employed. It includes details, accessories and auxiliary operations such as low-friction surfaces, lubricating elements or special engagements that mitigate friction but are not otherwise classified.
[E05Y 2800/414] against high or low temperatures -- E05Y 2800/414 classifies details, accessories and auxiliary operations for locks that provide protection against extreme temperatures, covering devices or measures that mitigate the effects of high or low heat on lock functionality. This entry falls under the fixed constructions hierarchy, specifically the "locks" group and the indexing scheme for subclasses not otherwise provided for.
[E05Y 2800/416] against fire -- This subclass covers auxiliary devices, methods and materials that protect lock mechanisms from fire damage, such as fire-resistant housings, sealing arrangements, protective coatings and related operations that are not specified in other subclasses.
[E05Y 2800/42] against smoke or gas -- E05Y 2800/42 covers lock devices and related accessories that are specifically designed to prevent the passage of smoke or gas, such as fire-resistant or gas-tight locking systems. It includes any auxiliary features or details not covered by other subclasses, focusing on sealing functions in building security or safety applications.
[E05Y 2800/422] against vibration or noise -- This subclass covers devices or methods attached to or incorporated in locks that reduce or dampen vibration and acoustic emissions. It includes auxiliary components such as dampers, noise-absorbing materials, or vibration-isolating mounting used when the main lock structure does not specifically provide such features. Used for detailing accessories not otherwise classified.
[E05Y 2800/424] against unintended use, e.g. protection against vandalism or sabotage -- This subclass covers features integrated into fixed constructions with locks that deter unauthorized access, such as anti-vandalism or anti-sabotage measures, including protective devices, tamper-evident mechanisms, or other accessories not covered elsewhere.
[E05Y 2800/426] against unauthorised use, e.g. keys -- E05Y 2800/426 covers devices, methods or auxiliary features that prevent unauthorised use of locks, such as specialised keys or other mechanisms that restrict access. It includes supplemental elements designed to stop opening without permission.

It covers any structural or functional features that are not otherwise specified but serve to keep moisture or frost from impairing lock operation.

It includes details, accessories and auxiliary operations designed to reduce or prevent wear on components of locking mechanisms and other fixed constructions.

[E05Y 2900/544] Tailboards, tailgates or sideboards opening downwards -- In the CPC, code E05Y 2900/544 falls under Fixed Constructions → Locks → Indexing scheme associated with subclasses → Application of doors, windows, wings or fittings thereof. It covers tailboards, tailgates or sideboards that open downwards, such as hinged rear-panels on trucks or trailers.
[E05Y 2900/546] Tailboards, tailgates or sideboards opening upwards -- The entry E05Y 2900/546 covers tailboards, tailgates or sideboards that open upward, such as rear or side doors on trucks, trailers, railcars or similar vehicles. It includes the construction, mounting and locking features that enable the panel to lift vertically for loading or access.
[E05Y 2900/548] Trunk lids -- E05Y 2900/548 classifies trunk lids, covering the design, construction and locking or opening mechanisms of lids for vehicle trunks and similar enclosures. This falls under fixed constructions and locks, specifically the indexing scheme for applications of doors, windows, wings or related fittings.
[E05Y 2900/55] Windows -- E05Y 2900/55 covers windows as fixed building constructions, focusing on their integration with locking devices and the related indexing schemes used to categorize such fittings. The entry includes structures, seals, hinges and other mechanisms that enable the installation, operation or sealing of windows in doors, wings or similar apertures.
[E05Y 2999/00] Subject-matter not otherwise provided for in this subclass -- E05Y 2999/00 is a catch-all subclass for fixed-construction lock devices that are not covered by any other specific subclass within the LOCKS group of the Cooperative Patent Classification. It is used when the subject-matter cannot be classified elsewhere in the hierarchy.
[E06] DOORS, WINDOWS, SHUTTERS, OR ROLLER BLINDS IN GENERAL -- The E06 code covers general aspects of fixed constructions that incorporate openings such as doors, windows, shutters and roller blinds. It includes design, materials, installation methods and functional features applicable to these elements when they are integral parts of a building envelope. The classification is used for patents relating to any invention that improves or modifies such opening devices in a broad, non-specific context.
[E06B] FIXED OR MOVABLE CLOSURES FOR OPENINGS IN BUILDINGS, VEHICLES, FENCES OR LIKE ENCLOSURES IN GENERAL, e.g. DOORS, WINDOWS, BLINDS, GATES -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fixed and movable closures for openings in buildings; Windows and doors themselves, and items that are installed before them, like blinds, screens or shutters; Insulating glazing units; Closures in fences, e.g. gates; Turnstiles.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Shades of blinds for greenhouses -> A01G9/22 - Shop furniture -> A47F - Picture frames -> A47G - Curtains -> A47H - Making metal frames -> B21D53/74 - Clamps for holding while making frames -> B25B5/142 - Holes in frames for fittings -> B27F5/12 - Assembly apparatus -> B27M - Welding plastic frames -> B29C65/00 - Automobiles -> B60J - Vehicle door or window arrangements -> B60J1/00 , B60J10/00 - Train -> B61D - Lids for car boot or bonnets -> B62D25/10 - Ships -> B63B - Aeroplanes -> B64C - Laminated or coated glass -> C03C17/00 , C03C27/00 - Laminated glass -> C03C27/00 - Wash/drying machines -> D06F - Sliding partition walls -> E04B2/00 - Curtain walls -> E04B2/88 - Roof windows -> E04D - Sunshades, awings -> E04F10/00 - Awnings -> E04F10/00 - Decorative trim -> E04F19/00 - Locks -> E05B65/00 - Hinges -> E05D15/00 - Safes -> E05G - Refrigerators -> F25D - Testing of doors or windows -> G01M - Display boards -> G07F  **Glossary:** - Wing: swingable, slidable or otherwise movable member such as a door or window, for closing an opening - Wing frame: the peripheral edge or edges which define the outer border of the wing
[E06B 1/00] Border constructions of openings in walls, floors, or ceilings -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Constructions that are placed at the border of openings in walls, essentially between the wall structure and the door or window frame, e.g. fastening means or joints, and frames to be rigidly mounted in openings in walls.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Features of rigidly-mounted outer frames relating to the mounting of wing frames; Window sashes or door leaves -> E06B3/00 - Corber joints or edge joints -> E06B3/96 - Doors, windows, or like closures for special purposes; Border constructions therefor -> E06B5/00 - Trap-doors -> E06B5/01 - Hand held tools for joints -> B25B27/00 - Lintels -> E04C3/02 - Roof windows and skylights -> E04D13/00 - Mounting and marking devices for door leaves and frames -> E04F21/0007 , E04F21/003
[E06B 1/003] Cavity wall closers; Fastening door or window frames in cavity walls  lintels  E04C3/02 -- The entry covers methods and devices for securing door or window frames within cavity walls, including the use of lintels to support and fasten the framing members. It addresses how the frame is anchored to the surrounding wall structure, ensuring stability and proper sealing of the opening.
[E06B 1/006] of curvilinear outline -- This subgroup deals with border constructions for openings whose outlines are curved rather than straight, such as arched, rounded or serpentine frames applied to doors, windows, shutters, gates and similar fixtures.
[E06B 1/02] Base frames, i.e. template frames for openings in walls or the like, provided with means for securing a further rigidly-mounted frame -- E06B 1/02 covers base frames or template frames installed in walls, floors or ceilings that serve as a foundation for a second rigidly mounted frame such as a door, window or blind, providing anchorage and structural support for the final closure.
[E06B 1/04] Frames for doors, windows, or the like to be fixed in openings -- E06B 1/04 covers structural frames that are installed in openings of walls, floors or ceilings to support doors, windows, shutters, blinds or similar closures. It includes fixed or removable framing members that secure the opening perimeter and facilitate the attachment of the movable closure.
[E06B 1/045] with separate wing abutment strips, e.g. adjustable; Door stops  sealing arrangements  E06B7/16 -- This subclass relates to border constructions for openings in walls, floors or ceilings where the closure incorporates separate wing abutment strips, such as adjustable strips that serve as door stops or sealing arrangements.
[E06B 1/06] Wooden frames -- E06B 1/06 covers wooden frames that form the border of openings in walls, floors or ceilings, such as those used for doors, windows, shutters or blinds. It classifies the wooden perimeter structures that support fixed or movable closures for building or vehicle apertures.
[E06B 1/08] composed of several parts with respect to the cross-section of the frame itself -- This subgroup deals with frames for doors, windows, shutters or similar openings whose cross-section is formed by several joined parts rather than a single piece. It includes composite or multi-element profiles used in walls, floors or ceilings to define the border of the opening.
[E06B 1/10] adjustable with respect to the thickness of walls -- E06B 1/10 concerns border constructions for openings in walls, floors or ceilings that can be adjusted to suit different wall thicknesses. It includes frames, jambs or similar elements used with doors, windows, shutters, blinds or gates, enabling the closure to be fitted in variable building structures.
[E06B 11/00] Means for allowing passage through fences, barriers or the like, e.g. stiles -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Closures for allowing passage through fences; entry gates; turnstiles; safety gates for unloading docks  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Cattle grids -> A01K3/002 - Features peculiar to electrified wire fencing -> A01K3/005 - Road barriers and traffic gates -> E01F13/00 - Suspension of gates per se -> E05D - Turnstiles with registering means -> G07C9/10 - Coin-free facilities for turnstiles -> G07F17/14
[E06B 11/02] Gates -- E06B 11/02 covers gates, i.e., movable closures installed in fences, barriers or similar enclosures that provide passage for vehicles or pedestrians. It includes designs and constructions for swinging, sliding, folding, rolling or automated gates, as well as related operating mechanisms, sealing elements and mounting structures.
[E06B 11/021] Wire gates, e.g. traction gates; Fastenings therefor  insulators, poles, handles, or the like, in electric fences  H01B17/145 -- The subclass E06B 11/021 covers wire gates such as traction gates employed in electric fences, together with their fastenings, insulators, supporting poles and handles. It is situated within the broader group for fixed or movable closures that provide passage through fences, barriers or similar enclosures.
[E06B 11/022] characterised by the manner of movement -- E06B 11/022 covers doors, windows, shutters, blinds and similar closures whose design is defined by the manner in which they move, such as sliding, swinging, folding or rotating, and includes passage means through fences or barriers like stiles.
[E06B 11/023] where the gate opens within the plane of the gate -- E06B 11/023 covers gates that open without leaving the plane of the gate, such as doors or gates that swing, slide or fold within the same vertical plane of the barrier. This includes designs where the motion is confined to the gate's plane, allowing passage while the overall profile of the closure remains unchanged.
[E06B 11/025] vertically
[E06B 11/026] horizontally -- The subclass E06B 11/026 covers horizontally oriented passage elements in fixed or movable closures such as fences, gates or similar enclosures, including horizontal stiles, bars, rails or similar means that allow people or objects to pass through the barrier.
[E06B 11/027] where the gate rotates around a horizontal axis parallel to the plane of the gate -- Subclass E06B 11/027 covers gates that open by rotating around a horizontal axis that runs parallel to the gate plane, such as paddle or swing gates that lift or lower rather than swing sideways. The classification applies to means for allowing passage through fences, barriers or similar enclosures.
[E06B 11/028] with posts connected underground -- The subclass E06B 11/028 covers fence or barrier openings such as stiles where the vertical posts are linked below the ground surface, providing a passage while maintaining overall structural stability. This includes designs in which the posts are joined by underground connections rather than above-ground hardware, typical for rural gates, agrarian fences or similar fixed closures.
[E06B 11/04] characterised by the kind of suspension -- E06B 11/04 covers closures whose design is defined by the kind of suspension used, such as hinged, pivoted, sliding or rolling mechanisms for doors, windows, shutters or similar openings in buildings, vehicles or fences. The entry groups devices where the method of hanging or supporting the element determines its operation, including stiles and other means of allowing passage through barriers.
[E06B 11/045] exclusively for horizontally sliding gates -- E06B 11/045 pertains to gates that open by moving horizontally along a track rather than swinging or rolling, covering the structural elements, drive mechanisms and accessories that enable smooth lateral passage through fences, barriers or similar enclosures.
[E06B 11/08] Turnstiles -- E06B 11/08 designates turnstiles, mechanical barriers that control passage through fences, gates or similar enclosures by allowing only a single person to pass at a time, often using rotating arms or paddles. They are employed in venues such as stadiums, transit stations and secure facilities to regulate entry and exit.
[E06B 11/085] non-rotary or with a limited angle of rotation, e.g. 90° -- E06B 11/085 covers closures that do not rotate continuously but open only through a limited angle, typically up to 90°, such as hinged doors, gates or stiles used in buildings, vehicles or fences. The classification applies to designs where the movement is restricted to a small swing rather than full rotation.
[E06B 1/12] Metal frames -- E06B 1/12 covers metal frames that form the structural support for fixed or movable closures such as doors, windows, shutters, blinds or gates, forming the border of openings in walls, floors or ceilings. The subclass includes the design, manufacturing and assembly of metal profiles, joints and reinforcements that define the aperture's shape and provide attachment points for glazing, sealing or hardware. It is used to classify inventions dealing with metal framing in building and vehicle enclosures.
[E06B 1/14] of special cross-section -- E06B 1/14 covers border constructions for openings in walls, floors or ceilings that have a special cross-section shape, such as uniquely profiled frames for doors, windows, shutters or blinds, used in buildings, vehicles or similar enclosures.
[E06B 1/16] Hollow frames -- E06B 1/16 covers frames for doors, windows, shutters or similar openings that are hollow rather than solid, including designs where a cavity is formed by separate side members, reinforcing ribs or inserts. Such frames may be made of metal, wood or composite materials and are intended to reduce weight, provide space for insulation or services, and simplify manufacturing.
[E06B 1/18] composed of several parts with respect to the cross-section of the frame itself -- E06B 1/18 covers border constructions of openings in walls, floors or ceilings where the frame cross-section is made up of several distinct parts, such as combined profiles, reinforced sections or multi-piece frames. The entry applies to fixed or movable closures like doors, windows, shutters or roller blinds and focuses on the way the frame members are combined in the sectional design.
[E06B 1/20] adjustable with respect to the thickness of walls -- E06B 1/20 pertains to border constructions for doors, windows, shutters, roller blinds and similar openings that can be adjusted to accommodate different wall thicknesses, allowing the frame or sealing element to be moved or resized for a proper fit.
[E06B 1/22] with integral thresholds of special form -- This subclass refers to fixed or movable closures whose threshold is formed as an integral part of the door, window, shutter or similar opening, such as built-in steps, raised sills or specially shaped thresholds that are fabricated together with the frame. It excludes separate added thresholds and applies to any border construction where the threshold is a permanent, molded component of the closure.
[E06B 1/24] Frames of natural stone, concrete, or other stone-like material -- E06B 1/24 covers frames constructed from natural stone, concrete or other stone-like materials that form the border of openings in walls, floors or ceilings, such as those surrounding doors, windows, shutters or roller blinds in buildings, vehicles, fences or similar enclosures.
[E06B 1/26] Frames of plastics -- E06B 1/26 covers the use of plastic materials to form frames for doors, windows, shutters, roller blinds and similar fixed or movable closures in buildings, vehicles or other enclosures as part of border constructions around openings in walls, floors or ceilings. It addresses the design, manufacturing and installation of these plastic frames.
[E06B 1/28] Hollow frames -- E06B 1/28 pertains to hollow frames employed as border constructions for openings in walls, floors or ceilings, such as doors, windows, shutters and blinds. The classification covers frames that are not solid, featuring internal cavities to reduce weight, provide insulation or accommodate services.
[E06B 1/30] composed of several parts with respect to the cross-section of the frame itself -- E06B 1/30 pertains to door, window, shutter or similar closures whose frame is formed from several separate parts that define its cross-section. It addresses the way these components are combined to create the overall profile of the frame, including multi-piece or mixed-material configurations.
[E06B 1/32] Frames composed of parts made of different materials -- E06B 1/32 covers frames for doors, windows, shutters or similar closures whose structural members are built from two or more different materials, such as metal-wood hybrids or composite sections, defining a mixed-material construction for building openings.
[E06B 1/325] comprising insulation between two metal section members -- The subclass E06B 1/325 covers fixed or movable closures whose frame is formed by two metal sections with an insulating material placed between them, providing thermal separation for doors, windows, shutters, gates or similar border constructions in walls, floors or ceilings. It addresses designs that improve energy efficiency and reduce heat loss through the frame of the opening.
[E06B 1/34] Coverings, e.g. protecting against weather, for decorative purposes -- The subclass E06B 1/34 relates to protective or decorative coverings applied to doors, windows, shutters, blinds, gates or similar fixed or movable closures, such as weather-proof panels, cladding, shutters or films that shield the opening from the elements while adding an aesthetic finish. It addresses any covering used primarily for protection against weather or for decorative purposes on the border constructions of openings in walls, floors or ceilings.
[E06B 1/342] Reveal covering members disposed alongside of a window frame -- E06B 1/342 relates to reveal covering members that are positioned alongside a window frame to conceal or protect the exposed side surface of a window opening. These components form part of fixed constructions for doors, windows, shutters or blinds and serve to finish the border of openings in walls, floors or ceilings.
[E06B 1/345] Renovation window frames covering the existing old frames -- E06B 1/345 refers to retrofit window frames that are fitted over existing, already-installed frames as part of a renovation. The classification covers solutions for upgrading or replacing the visible part of a window while the original frame remains in place, typical in building refurbishment projects.
[E06B 1/347] Renovation door frames covering the existing old frames -- E06B 1/347 concerns retrofit door frames that are fitted over an existing opening, creating a new frame that encloses the old one during renovation. The approach upgrades or replaces doors without dismantling the original frame and is applicable to a range of building types.
[E06B 1/36] Frames uniquely adapted for windows -- The CPC subclass E06B 1/36 covers frames that are specially designed to support and secure windows, differing in shape, material, or attachment method from standard window frames. It includes constructions where the frame uniquely adapts to the window opening, providing structural integrity, weather sealing, or aesthetic integration within doors, windows, shutters or similar fixed or movable closures.
[E06B 1/363] Bay windows -- E06B 1/363 - Bay windows covers fixed or movable window constructions that project outward from a wall, forming a recessed or angular opening, often with multiple glazed panels and supporting frames. It pertains to the border constructions of such openings in walls, floors, or ceilings in buildings.
[E06B 1/366] Mullions or transoms therefor -- E06B 1/366 covers mullions and transoms, the vertical and horizontal members that divide a window or door opening into smaller panes. These elements are fixed constructions that form the border framework of openings in walls, floors, or ceilings.
[E06B 1/38] for shop, show, or like large windows -- E06B 1/38 classifies large, shop- or exhibition-type fixed windows that form the border of openings in walls, floors or ceilings, distinguishing them from ordinary residential glazing. It falls under the broader group for fixed constructions such as doors, windows, shutters and roller blinds used in buildings, vehicles or similar enclosures.
[E06B 1/40] Frames with parts removable to admit the glass -- The class covers frames for doors, windows, shutters or similar openings in which portions of the frame can be detached to receive or replace glass, such as removable mullions, sash sections or glazing beads. It applies to constructions that allow easy insertion, removal or servicing of glass panels in buildings, vehicles or other enclosures.
[E06B 1/52] Frames specially adapted for doors -- This CPC subclass covers door frames that are specially designed or modified to meet particular functional or architectural requirements, such as reinforced, insulated, fire-rated, adjustable or aesthetic adaptations. It falls under fixed constructions for doors, windows, shutters or roller blinds, specifically relating to border constructions of openings in walls, floors or ceilings.
[E06B 1/522] for overhead garage doors -- E06B 1/522 covers fixed or movable closures specifically designed as overhead garage doors, used to close openings in building walls or other structures, where the door moves vertically or rolls upward into a cavity.
[E06B 1/524] Mullions; Transoms -- E06B 1/524 covers mullions and transoms, the vertical and horizontal members that divide openings such as doors, windows, shutters or roller blinds into separate panels. The code applies to fixed or movable frames that form borders within walls, floors or ceilings, shaping the overall construction of openings in buildings, vehicles, fences or similar enclosures.
[E06B 1/526] for door wings that can be set up to open either left or right, outwards or inwards, e.g. provided with grooves for easily detachable hinges or latch plates  locks therefor  E05B63/04 ; hinges therefor  E05D7/02 -- E06B 1/526 classifies door wings that can be installed to swing either left or right and to open inward or outward, typically by means of grooves or fittings that allow hinges, latch plates or locks to be detached and repositioned easily.
[E06B 1/528] with ducts for cables or pipes -- The CPC subclass E06B 1/528 covers border constructions of openings in walls, floors or ceilings that incorporate ducts for routing cables, pipes or similar services. It falls under fixed constructions for doors, windows, shutters and roller blinds, specifying built-in conduits integrated into the framing of the opening.
[E06B 1/56] Fastening frames to the border of openings -- This subclass covers methods and devices for attaching door, window or similar frames to the surrounding border of openings in walls, floors or ceilings, ensuring a secure connection between the frame and the building structure. It includes mechanical fasteners, adhesives, anchoring systems and related sealing or reinforcement measures.
[E06B 1/58] by filling up the joints, e.g. by cementing -- The subclass E06B 1/58 covers border constructions of openings such as doors, windows, shutters or roller blinds, specifically the technique of filling the joints, for example by cementing, to create a sealed or fixed closure.
[E06B 1/60] by mechanical means, e.g. anchoring means -- This subgroup deals with securing doors, windows, shutters, blinds or similar closures to their surrounding structure using mechanical fastening devices such as anchors, bolts, brackets or clamps. It includes any anchoring means that attach the closure to walls, floors or ceilings without relying on adhesives or welds.
[E06B 1/6007] between similar contiguous frames  bay windows  E06B1/363 -- The E06B 1/6007 subclass concerns the border construction of openings such as bay windows formed between two or more adjacent, similar frames that share a common side. It addresses the structural relationship and detailing of contiguous fixed frames within walls, floors or ceilings, distinguishing this configuration from other door, window or shutter arrangements.
[E06B 1/6015] Anchoring means  E06B1/6007 ,  E06B1/6046  -  E06B1/6092  take precedence -- The entry covers devices or methods for fastening doors, windows, shutters, blinds, gates or similar closure elements to their surrounding structure, ensuring secure attachment to walls, floors or ceilings. It includes brackets, screws, welds, adhesives and other anchoring components that transmit loads from the closure to the building fabric. It is used when designing fixed or movable closures in buildings, vehicles or fences.
[E06B 1/6023] completely hidden between the frame and the border of the opening, at least part of the means being previously fixed to the wall  E06B1/6038  takes precedence
[E06B 1/603] adjustable -- E06B 1/603 refers to adjustable border constructions for openings in walls, floors or ceilings, such as doors, windows, shutters or blinds. The code covers devices whose dimensions or position can be varied after installation to accommodate changes in use, alignment or sealing requirements.
[E06B 1/6038] specially adapted for being embedded in the wall -- This CPC subclass covers border constructions for openings in walls, floors or ceilings that are specially adapted to be embedded in the surrounding structure, such as recessed or flush-mounted frames, trims or sealing elements that allow doors, windows, shutters or similar closures to sit within the wall surface rather than project outward.
[E06B 1/6046] Clamping means acting perpendicular to the wall opening; Fastening frames by tightening or drawing them against a surface parallel to the opening  E06B1/6007  takes precedence
[E06B 1/6053] the frame being moved perpendicularly towards the opening and held by means of snap action behind a protrusion on the border of the opening -- This subclass covers closures in which the frame is displaced straight toward the aperture and then locked by a snap-action that engages behind a protruding element on the surrounding border. It is used for doors, windows, shutters or similar fixed or movable openings.
[E06B 1/6061] with separate clamping means acting on opposite wall or associated surfaces -- It covers border constructions for openings where the closure-such as a door, window, shutter or blind-is secured by separate clamping means that act on opposite walls or related surfaces, providing sealing or locking without relying on hinges or conventional latches.
[E06B 1/6069] Separate spacer means acting exclusively in the plane of the opening; Shims; Wedges; Tightening of a complete frame inside a wall opening  E06B1/6084  takes precedence -- The code E06B 1/6069 covers separate spacer elements that operate only within the plane of an opening, such as shims, wedges or devices that tighten a complete frame inside a wall, floor or ceiling opening. It is used for fixed or movable closures like doors, windows, shutters or blinds, where the spacer functions to adjust fit or secure the frame without protruding beyond the opening.
[E06B 1/6076] of screw-type
[E06B 1/6084] Mounting frames elastically, e.g. with springs or other elastic material, between the wall and the frame  E06B1/6046  takes precedence -- The code E06B 1/6084 covers devices that attach door, window or shutter frames to a surrounding wall using elastic means such as springs or other flexible materials, allowing the frame to be mounted with a degree of give. This arrangement is used to absorb stresses, improve sealing or facilitate movement while the frame remains securely held in the opening. It falls under the broader class of fixed constructions for openings in buildings and similar structures.
[E06B 1/6092] Fastening door frames to the floor or ceiling; Jamb feet; Cross members uniting the jamb feet  E06B1/70  and  E06B3/9632  take precedence -- This group covers means for attaching door or window frames to building structures such as a floor or ceiling, including the design of jamb feet and cross members that unite them. It is part of fixed constructions for openings and follows the broader categories E06B1/70 and E06B3/9632.
[E06B 1/62] Tightening or covering joints between the border of openings and the frame -- E06B 1/62 covers devices or methods that tighten or seal the joint where a door, window, shutter or similar opening meets its surrounding frame, improving weatherproofing, acoustic insulation or structural stability. It includes gaskets, sealing strips, clips, adhesives or other elements applied to the border of the opening to prevent ingress of air, water or dust.
