[E06B 1/64] by loosely-inserted means, e.g. strip, resilient tongue -- E06B 1/64 covers border constructions for doors, windows, shutters or similar closures that are secured by loosely inserted elements such as strips or resilient tongues, allowing the closure to be held in place without rigid fastening.
[E06B 1/66] by labyrinth packings -- E06B 1/66 covers border constructions of openings that incorporate labyrinth packings, which are staggered or interlocking sealing elements forming a tortuous path to improve insulation, sound dampening, or security. Such designs are used in doors, windows, shutters or similar closures where a labyrinthine seal reduces air or water infiltration.
[E06B 1/68] by profiled external parts -- E06B 1/68 covers border constructions of openings such as doors, windows or shutters where the exterior profile of the surrounding frame is defined by profiled parts. The classification includes designs in which the outer edges of the opening are formed by shaped sections that provide structural support, sealing or decorative appearance. It applies to both fixed and movable closures in buildings, vehicles or similar enclosures.
[E06B 1/70] Sills -- The E06B 1/70 subclass defines sills, the horizontal members that form the lower border of openings such as doors, windows, shutters or roller blinds in walls, floors or ceilings. It covers their design, material, sealing and support functions as part of fixed or movable closures in buildings, vehicles or similar enclosures.
[E06B 1/702] Window sills -- E06B 1/702 relates to window sills, the horizontal members forming the lower part of a window opening that support glazing, channel water away, and provide a bearing surface for interior finishes. The classification covers various sill forms, including fixed, removable or adjustable types, and their integration with surrounding wall, floor or ceiling structures.
[E06B 1/705] End-caps therefor -- E06B 1/705 covers end-caps for border constructions that frame openings in walls, floors or ceilings, such as doors, windows, shutters, blinds or gates, providing closure or protection for the ends of the frame members.
[E06B 2001/622] especially adapted for door frames; Joint covering devices where the wall surface is parallel to the adjacent door or window frame part -- The CPC subclass E06B 2001/622 covers building elements specially adapted for door frames, including joint covering devices where the wall surface runs parallel to the adjacent door or window frame. It relates to doors, windows, and associated sealing or finishing components that align with the surrounding wall.
[E06B 2001/624] with parts to be embedded in the stucco layer or otherwise linked to this layer -- This CPC subclass covers stucco finishes where components such as reinforcement, decorative elements, or functional parts are placed within the plaster layer or otherwise attached to it. It includes techniques for embedding panels, inserts, wiring, insulation, or structural ties directly in the stucco during application.
[E06B 2001/626] comprising expanding foam strips -- E06B 2001/626 covers building elements that incorporate expanding foam strips, typically used for thermal insulation, acoustic damping or sealing gaps in structures. The classification applies to panels, walls or other constructions where the foam strips are integral to the component's function, providing a lightweight, expandable material that fills voids and improves energy efficiency.
[E06B 2001/628] Separate flexible joint covering strips; Flashings -- This group includes separate flexible joint covering strips and flashings designed to seal or protect movable or articulated connections in buildings, preventing water, air or heat loss at the joint.
[E06B 2001/707] Thresholds with special provision for insulation -- E06B 2001/707 relates to building thresholds such as door sills, stair treads or similar walk-over elements that incorporate special measures to improve thermal or acoustic insulation, for example insulated inserts, double-layer construction or sealing gaskets.
[E06B 2003/166] with a filled cavity -- This subclass covers foundations that include a cavity deliberately created in the structural element and later filled with grout, concrete, foam, or other filling material to enhance load-bearing capacity, stability, anchoring or soil improvement.
[E06B 2003/224] with reinforcing plastic section members -- The CPC subgroup E06B 2003/224 refers to building or civil-engineering elements that incorporate reinforcing plastic section members, such as walls, panels, or other structural components in which polymer profiles, ribs or similar plastic inserts are placed within or attached to the construction to increase strength, stiffness or durability. It is used for inventions that combine traditional building materials with plastic reinforcement for improved performance.
[E06B 2003/225] Means for stabilising the insert -- E06B 2003/225 covers devices or methods used to secure an inserted element, such as a liner, pipe or reinforcement, within a surrounding structure or soil mass. It includes mechanical, hydraulic or chemical means that prevent movement, rotation or displacement of the insert during and after installation.
[E06B 2003/226] by adhesives or filling up the remainder of the cavity, e.g. together with foam -- E06B 2003/226 covers methods of sealing a door, window, skylight or similar opening by applying adhesives or by filling any remaining cavity, often in combination with expanding foam, to create a continuous, airtight or watertight joint. This sub-group specifies the use of bonding or foam-filling techniques for completing the seal.
[E06B 2003/227] with reinforcing wooden section members -- E06B 2003/227 covers building components or assemblies that include reinforcing wooden section members such as added beams, joists, or braces to strengthen masonry, concrete or other structural elements. The classification is used for patents describing methods or devices where wood is incorporated to improve load-bearing capacity or stability of the overall construction.
[E06B 2003/228] with separate reinforcements situated outside the cavity or in the walls -- E06B 2003/228 covers building wall structures in which reinforcement elements are placed externally to the cavity or embedded within the wall faces rather than inside the cavity itself, such as separate steel or composite bars, plates or meshes fixed to the inner or outer leaf of a cavity wall.
[E06B 2003/261] Frames made of wood -- E06B 2003/261 covers wooden frames employed in building construction, including load-bearing and non-load-bearing timber structures such as post-and-beam, portal, and panel frames. The classification addresses the design, assembly, and fixation methods for wood-based framing systems used in residential, industrial, or public edifices.
[E06B 2003/2615] Frames made of metal -- E06B 2003/2615 covers metal frames that form the structural support for building components such as doors, windows, shutters and panels. The classification includes frames composed wholly or partly of metal, designed to hold glazing, infill or other accessory elements in place.
[E06B 2003/262] Frames made of plastic material -- CPC class E06B 2003/262 covers building frames whose structural members are formed mainly from plastic material, such as window, door, or skylight frames produced by extrusion, molding or composite processes. The classification applies regardless of reinforcement or coating, as long as plastic is the primary constituent of the frame.
[E06B 2003/2625] with different frames made from different materials -- The subclass E06B 2003/2625 refers to doors, windows, shutters or similar openings that incorporate multiple frames each fabricated from a different material, such as a combination of metal, wood, plastic or composite sections. It covers designs where the varied-material frames serve distinct functional, structural or aesthetic purposes.
[E06B 2003/26309] using glue -- This subgroup of CPC E06B concerns roofing work that employs an adhesive to join components. It includes methods for applying glue to roof membranes, tiles, sheets or supporting structures, the types of adhesives used, and steps for curing or fixing the bonded elements. The classification captures processes and devices that rely on glue for roof assembly.
[E06B 2003/2631] Screw or pin connections -- This subclass, within CPC E06B for joints, addresses connections formed by screws or pins, covering the design, assembly and fastening of components joined with threaded or non-threaded pins such as bolts, screws, dowels and similar devices.
[E06B 2003/26312] Snap connections -- E06B 2003/26312 in the CPC covers snap-type connections for foundation, retaining-wall or other earth-support structures. It includes devices that allow structural parts to be joined by a quick-acting, usually elastic, snap-fit without separate fasteners, facilitating rapid assembly and disassembly.
[E06B 2003/26314] Provisions for reducing the shift between the strips and the metal section members -- This CPC entry relates to building components that incorporate measures to limit relative movement between strip-like elements and adjoining metal section members, such as brackets, interlocking profiles, adhesives or pressure devices designed to keep the assembly aligned and prevent shifting under load or thermal changes.
[E06B 2003/26316] Disconnectable connections or permitting shifting between the sections -- This CPC subclass deals with building components that can be joined in a way that allows them to be disconnected or shifted relative to one another. It includes mechanisms for detachable connections, sliding joints, and adjustable interlocking sections used in walls, ceilings, floors or similar structural assemblies, enabling reconfiguration, maintenance or transport.
[E06B 2003/26318] Connecting by rotating the metal section or the strip around its longitudinal axis -- This CPC entry covers methods for joining metal components of walls or partitions by rotating one part around its length to interlock. It is used for fastening metal sections or strips in construction, enabling a secure, reversible connection without welding or fasteners.
[E06B 2003/26321] with additional prefab insulating materials in the hollow space -- This CPC subgroup relates to building wall structures that include a hollow cavity which is later filled with prefabricated insulating material, such as panels or modules, to enhance thermal performance. It covers methods and installations where the insulation is added after the wall has been erected, rather than being integral to the wall's original construction.
[E06B 2003/26323] with coatings reducing the radiation -- This CPC subgroup covers doors, windows, shutters, gates or skylights that incorporate surface coatings intended to reduce incoming radiation such as solar or ultraviolet light. The coatings may be reflective, absorptive or selective to improve energy efficiency, occupant comfort or material durability. It applies to any building element that features a radiation-reducing treatment.
[E06B 2003/26325] the convection or radiation in a hollow space being reduced, e.g. by subdividing the hollow space -- This sub-group covers measures for lowering heat loss by convection or radiation within a cavity, such as partitioning the hollow space into smaller sections, inserting insulating barriers, or otherwise subdividing the void to impede airflow and radiant transfer.
[E06B 2003/26327] with separate thin walled inserts -- The CPC subgroup E06B 2003/26327 covers constructions in which the main conduit or structure incorporates separate thin-walled inserts, such as sleeves, liners or plates, that can be placed inside or alongside the primary element. These inserts are typically used to reinforce, protect, or provide a fluid-tight path without forming an integral part of the surrounding wall.
[E06B 2003/26329] the insulating strips between the metal sections being interconnected -- E06B 2003/26329 relates to the incorporation of insulating strips between metal sections that are interconnected, providing electrical isolation or thermal separation in building components such as frames, panels or structural members. The entry covers the design, material selection and installation methods for these strips to ensure safety and performance.
[E06B 2003/2633] the insulating strips between the metal sections having ribs extending into the hollow space -- E06B 2003/2633 relates to building components in which insulating strips are positioned between metal sections, the strips featuring ribs that project into the hollow space to enhance mechanical fixing and thermal separation.
[E06B 2003/26332] Arrangements reducing the heat transfer in the glazing rabbet or the space between the wing and the casing frame -- The subclass includes inventions that improve thermal insulation of window assemblies by limiting heat flow through the glazing rabbet-the groove that holds the glass-or the narrow gap between a movable wing and its surrounding casing frame. It covers design features, sealants, fillers or structural modifications that break or slow conductive paths, thereby reducing energy loss and improving window efficiency.
[E06B 2003/26334] Contact reducing arrangements between the insulating strips and the metal sections -- The subclass deals with designs that minimize electrical contact between insulating strips and adjoining metal components, such as in frames, panels or seals, to prevent corrosion, arcing or heat transfer. It includes insulating spacers, non-conductive coatings or geometrical gaps that isolate the metal parts.
[E06B 2003/26349] Details of insulating strips -- The CPC subclass E06B 2003/26349 covers the specific features and configurations of insulating strips used in windows, doors, shutters and similar closures. It includes designs of weather-stripping, sealing gaskets and other members that provide thermal, acoustic or moisture protection by filling gaps between movable parts of the closure.
[E06B 2003/2635] Specific form characteristics -- E06B 2003/2635 classifies inventions that define particular form characteristics of road or pavement structures, such as geometry, cross-sectional shape, surface pattern or profile, where the novelty lies in the specific configuration rather than in materials or construction methods.
[E06B 2003/26352] hollow -- This code designates foundations formed by hollow structural elements such as pipe piles, caissons or other cylindrical members that are empty or contain a cavity to reduce weight or provide passage. It covers methods of installation and related features.
[E06B 2003/26354] filled -- E06B 2003/26354 covers masonry walls or similar structural elements whose cavities or voids are filled with a material such as insulation, concrete, mortar or other filler to improve strength, thermal or acoustic performance.
[E06B 2003/26356] Honeycomb -- E06B 2003/26356 classifies honeycomb structures used as building elements, such as lightweight panels, core materials, or sandwich components that provide rigidity and strength while reducing weight; it covers the design, manufacturing, and integration of honeycomb cores in walls, floors, roofs, facades, and other architectural applications.
[E06B 2003/26358] stepped or undulated -- The code designates foundations or retaining structures whose profile is formed in a series of steps or undulating shapes. These stepped or wave-like configurations are employed to follow terrain, improve load distribution, or add aesthetic effect in civil-engineering works.
[E06B 2003/26359] making flush mounting with neighbouring metal section members possible -- The CPC entry E06B 2003/26359 covers a method for achieving a flush mounting of a component alongside adjacent metal section members, allowing the joined parts to lie in the same plane without gaps or protrusions. It relates to construction of metal frameworks where seamless connections improve structural continuity, ease of installation and aesthetic appearance.
[E06B 2003/26361] Openings, incisions or indents -- E06B 2003/26361 covers features that form openings, incisions or indents in subterranean or foundation structures such as tunnels, shafts, pits or supporting walls. It includes apertures, cuts, notches or depressions created to accommodate services, access, drainage or to reduce material mass, and the means for forming them. The entry is used for patents relating to the design, manufacture or installation of such openings in construction works.
[E06B 2003/26363] Screw channels -- E06B 2003/26363 covers the provision of channels that house or guide screw or helical drive elements used in elevators, escalators and moving walkways, including the shape, placement and protective features of the channel that contains the screw rod, worm gear or similar rotating element.
[E06B 2003/26365] Composed of several similar parts positioned one after the other -- The code refers to a building component formed by a series of similar or identical parts placed consecutively, such as modular panels or segments that together create a continuous wall, floor or roof element.
[E06B 2003/26367] making flush mounting with neighbouring metal section members possible -- E06B 2003/26367 relates to methods or devices that allow a metal building part to be mounted flush against adjacent metal sections, ensuring a continuous surface and preventing gaps. It covers design features, joining techniques or brackets that enable seamless integration of neighboring members in construction of roofs, walls or frames.
[E06B 2003/26369] Specific material characteristics -- The code E06B 2003/26369 classifies patents that describe the specific material properties of components used in building water-supply, drainage or pressure-pipe installations, including characteristics such as corrosion resistance, mechanical strength, elasticity or thermal behaviour. It is applied when the material's nature is central to the invention.
[E06B 2003/2637] reinforced -- E06B 2003/2637 covers photographic supports that are reinforced, including plates, films or paper strengthened with a backing layer, mesh, polymer or other material to improve mechanical stability. The entry applies to the design, manufacture or modification of such reinforced photographic media.
[E06B 2003/26372] with coatings -- The CPC subclass E06B 2003/26372 covers openings, doors, windows or similar building elements that incorporate a coating, such as paint, powder, anodized, laminated or other protective or decorative layers applied to the surface. It is used for inventions where the coating is an essential feature for corrosion resistance, insulation, appearance or functional performance.
[E06B 2003/26374] with parts of differing nature -- This CPC subclass covers foundations, footings or piles that are built from components of different materials or properties, such as a concrete base combined with steel, timber or composite elements, and the methods for joining or integrating those disparate parts.
[E06B 2003/26376] Non-plastic materials, e.g. wood, metal -- E06B 2003/26376 covers doors, windows and shutters that are made from non-plastic substances such as wood, metal or other rigid materials. It includes the design, manufacturing and mounting techniques specific to these material types and excludes components primarily composed of plastic.
[E06B 2003/26378] comprising foam -- The CPC entry E06B 2003/26378 covers foundation structures or elements that incorporate foam as a construction material. It includes methods and devices for forming or supporting foundations using foam, such as foam-filled piles, foam concrete, or other foam-based fills to reduce weight or improve insulation.
[E06B 2003/26379] Specific characteristics concerning the disposition between the metal section members -- This CPC subgroup covers inventions that define the relative positioning, spacing, alignment or interconnection of metal section members within foundation or pile systems, focusing on how the metal components are disposed with respect to one another. It pertains to structural characteristics that affect the assembly or performance of metal-section foundations.
[E06B 2003/26381] disposed obliquely -- E06B 2003/26381 covers foundations or supporting members that are installed at an angle rather than vertically, such as inclined piles, sloped footings or other structural elements positioned obliquely to the ground. The classification is used for patents describing the design, construction or installation of these obliquely disposed support structures.
[E06B 2003/26383] with two similar parallel strips, one shifted to one side -- The code refers to walls or partitions built from two parallel strips of the same material where one strip is laterally offset from the other, creating a stepped or staggered profile for structural, aesthetic or functional purposes.
[E06B 2003/26385] with strips disposed perpendicular to each other -- E06B 2003/26385 covers floor or wall covering constructions in which individual strips are laid so that they intersect at right angles, creating patterns such as herringbone, basket-weave or perpendicular strip layouts. The classification applies to products and methods where the primary decorative or structural element consists of strips arranged mutually perpendicular. This is used for wood, stone, laminate or similar materials in flooring, cladding and tiling applications.
[E06B 2003/26387] Performing extra functions -- E06B 2003/26387 refers to earth-moving machines that are equipped to carry out additional tasks beyond basic digging or loading, such as grading, compacting, crushing, or conveying material. The subclass groups inventions that integrate these extra functions directly into a single mobile equipment unit, improving efficiency and reducing the need for separate machines.
[E06B 2003/26389] Holding sealing strips or forming sealing abutments -- E06B 2003/26389 relates to building envelope components that secure sealing strips or create sealing abutments to prevent ingress of water, air or dust. It includes methods and devices for attaching or shaping seal profiles on walls, windows, doors or similar openings, ensuring tight joints in construction.
[E06B 2003/2639] Provisions for fittings, e.g. locks or hinges -- This CPC subclass covers the built-in provisions that allow fittings such as locks, hinges, or other fastening devices to be installed on doors, windows or similar openings. It includes recesses, mounting plates or other preparatory structures prepared during construction to receive the hardware.
[E06B 2003/26392] Glazing bars -- E06B 2003/26392 covers glazing bars, the separate strips or frames that hold individual panes of glass within windows, doors or fixed panels. It includes their design, mounting methods, materials such as wood, metal or composite, and variations for thermal, acoustic or decorative performance. The code applies to both new installations and retro-fitting of existing glazed openings.
[E06B 2003/26394] Strengthening arrangements in case of fire -- E06B 2003/26394 covers fire-protective strengthening measures for building components, specifying designs, materials and methods that increase the fire resistance of walls, ceilings, columns, or other structural elements. It includes fire-resistant reinforcement, protective coatings, and fire-proofing systems intended to prevent collapse or damage during a fire.
[E06B 2003/26396] specially adapted for sheet metal frames -- The CPC entry E06B 2003/26396 designates brackets, supports, mounts or related fittings that are specially adapted for use with sheet-metal frames, covering devices that attach to, reinforce or connect such frames in construction or building-component applications.
[E06B 2003/26398] with two metal sections clamping a pane -- This CPC subclass covers window or door assemblies where a glass pane is secured by two opposite metal sections that clamp the pane together. The design relies on the metal members to hold the glass without additional framing, providing a simple, robust fastening method.
[E06B 2003/343] with at least two axes of rotation without additional frame within the wing -- The classification covers roof or building components where a wing element is mounted so that it can rotate about two independent axes, providing movement without the need for an additional supporting frame inside the wing. It applies to mechanisms that enable such dual-axis rotation directly integrated into the wing structure.
[E06B 2003/345] with additional rotating frame within the wing -- E06B 2003/345 covers building wings that incorporate an additional rotating frame, such as a pivoting or revolving section integrated into the wing structure for functions like access, ventilation or reconfiguration of the space. This subclass deals with the design and arrangement of the rotating mechanism as part of the wing's overall construction.
[E06B 2003/346] where the additional frame rotates around a vertical axis -- E06B 2003/346 classifies building elements that include an additional frame designed to rotate around a vertical axis, such as revolving doors, rotating panels or similar movable components that provide access or ventilation while maintaining the surrounding structure.
[E06B 2003/348] where the additional frame rotates around a horizontal axis -- E06B 2003/348 refers to constructions in which an auxiliary frame or panel is hinged so that it rotates about a horizontal axis, allowing the element to swing upward or downward for opening, closing or shading. It covers devices such as awnings, shutters or secondary window frames that move by pivoting on a horizontal hinge.
[E06B 2003/403] where the axis of rotation is horizontal -- This CPC subgroup covers escalators, moving walks or similar passenger conveyors whose driving or supporting wheels rotate about a horizontal axis, distinguishing them from devices with a vertical-axis rotation. It is applied to patents where the mechanism's axis of rotation lies parallel to the floor.
[E06B 2003/406] where the axis of rotation is vertical -- A device or component in which a rotating element is mounted with its axis pointing upward, so the rotation occurs around a vertical line; typical examples include rotary elevator cabins, vertical-axis turntables or platforms used in lifts and escalator systems.
[E06B 2003/4438] characterised by the material used for the frames -- The CPC subclass E06B pertains to windows, doors, shutters and similar openings, and the auxiliary group 2003/4438 specifies inventions in which the distinguishing feature is the material used for the frames, such as wood, metal, polymer or composite constructions. This entry classifies products whose performance, durability or aesthetics are defined primarily by the choice of frame material.
[E06B 2003/4446] Wood -- E06B 2003/4446 is a CPC code that designates wood as the material of interest in inventions related to the processing, shaping, or assembly of wood. It is used for patents covering furniture, building components, and other goods where wood serves as the primary structural or decorative element. This code helps classify technical information dealing specifically with the use of wood in manufactured products.
[E06B 2003/4453] Metal -- E06B 2003/4453 classifies metal parts, details and accessories used in building construction, such as metal fittings, brackets, cladding and other metal components incorporated into a structure. It falls under CPC section E06B, which covers parts and details of buildings, and specifies the material as metal.
[E06B 2003/4461] Plastics -- The entry refers to the use of plastics as a material in water-supply, sewage and irrigation systems, covering the manufacturing, installation and maintenance of plastic pipes, tubes, fittings and related components. It includes various polymer types and their applications in distribution, drainage and irrigation.
[E06B 2003/4469] with at least two frames made from different materials -- The CPC subclass E06B 2003/4469 relates to building elements such as doors, windows or shutters that feature a dual-frame construction in which each frame is made from a different material, for example a metal inner frame combined with a wooden outer frame. The classification captures inventions that exploit the contrasting properties of the materials to improve strength, insulation, durability or aesthetic appearance.
[E06B 2003/4476] Coverings, e.g. for protecting against weather or for decorative purposes -- The CPC entry E06B 2003/4476 covers building envelope coverings that serve to protect structures from weather or provide decorative effects. It includes roofs, wall claddings, facades and other surface layers, together with their materials, designs and methods of attachment or fixation. This subclass groups inventions dealing with protective and aesthetic exterior coverings.
[E06B 2003/4484] Special provisions for mounting or dismounting sashes -- This subclass covers inventions that provide special features or methods for mounting or removing window or door sashes, such as quick-release fasteners, removable hinges, guide or support devices, and other mechanisms that simplify installation, adjustment, cleaning or replacement.
[E06B 2003/4492] provided with screens -- This CPC subclass relates to building structures or components that include screens, for example protective or shading screens incorporated into walls, roofs, windows or other openings to control light, airflow or entry of insects. It covers designs where the screen is an integral part of the building element rather than a detachable accessory.
[E06B 2003/5463] in a preassembled frame, the pane being consecutively introduced obliquely, rotated and shifted -- E06B 2003/5463 covers a glazing-installation method in which a pane is first placed into a preassembled frame from an oblique direction, then rotated and shifted to its final position, allowing the glass to be seated securely without removing the frame.
[E06B 2003/5472] in an at least partly preassembled frame by introducing it through a slot in one of the frame members or inserting the pane before completing the frame -- The CPC code E06B 2003/5472 covers methods of installing a glass pane in a window frame where the frame is at least partially assembled and the pane is introduced through a slot cut in one of the frame members, or inserted before the frame is fully completed. This pertains to the construction of windows and similar glazing structures.
[E06B 2003/6208] with adjustable dimensions -- E06B 2003/6208 covers building components whose size can be altered after manufacture, such as adjustable-height walls, extendable doors or windows, and other elements with variable dimensions that can be changed to suit different space requirements.
[E06B 2003/6211] with tear away strips -- This CPC subclass covers wall components that incorporate tear-away strips designed to be removed to create an opening, often for ventilation, access or emergency egress, and may include fire-protective or insulation panels where the strips facilitate controlled failure.
[E06B 2003/6214] specially adapted for glazing bars -- E06B 2003/6214 covers features of doors, windows or shutters that are specially adapted to incorporate glazing bars, such as reinforced frames, mounting arrangements or sealing solutions designed to accommodate the presence of bars separating or supporting glass panes. This subgroup pertains to the structural or functional modifications that allow glazing bars to be integrated without compromising the operation or weather-tightness of the opening.
[E06B 2003/6217] with specific fixing means -- E06B 2003/6217 covers masonry units, such as bricks or blocks, that incorporate specific fixing means - for example built-in clips, brackets, anchors or other devices that enable the unit to be attached directly to a structure without separate mortar or mechanical fasteners. The classification is used for patents describing integrated attachment systems that simplify installation and improve stability.
[E06B 2003/622] using nails or the like -- The code E06B 2003/622 relates to construction methods and tools for fastening building elements such as panels, boards or cladding by driving nails, staples, or similar fasteners, and includes the associated devices for positioning or securing these fasteners. It covers the use of nails or the like to join components to a structure.
[E06B 2003/6223] with protruding parts anchored in grooves -- CPC E06B 2003/6223 refers to lift, escalator or moving-walkway constructions in which protruding components such as steps, handrails or brackets are secured by being fitted into complementary grooves in the supporting structure. The groove-anchoring arrangement provides fixation, guidance or load transfer for the protruding parts.
[E06B 2003/6226] with locking strips -- E06B 2003/6226 covers pavements, road or floor surfaces that incorporate locking strips to interlock adjacent paving elements, providing a secure, continuous surface. The classification applies to constructions where the locking strips are integral to the paving units or added to join them together.
[E06B 2003/6229] with grooves anchoring the cleat on a rim -- E06B 2003/6229 covers doors, windows, shutters or comparable panels that use grooves cut into a rim to receive and anchor a cleat, providing a secure fastening arrangement.
[E06B 2003/6232] using glue -- The CPC subclass E06B 2003/6232 pertains to construction of foundations and substructures in which adhesive bonding is employed. It includes methods, apparatus and materials that use glue to join concrete, masonry or prefabricated components, facilitating assembly or reinforcement of structural elements. This subclass is part of the broader E06B group dealing with foundations and substructures.
[E06B 2003/6235] moulded on the pane -- E06B in the CPC deals with windows, doors, shutters and related glazing components. The sub-group 2003/6235 specifies mouldings that are formed directly on the pane, such as decorative or sealing profiles applied during the glass-making process.
[E06B 2003/6238] having extra functions -- E06B 2003/6238 designates buildings that incorporate additional or auxiliary functions besides their main purpose, such as integrated services, combined residential and commercial spaces, or built-in facilities that extend the use of the structure.
[E06B 2003/6241] with drainage means -- E06B 2003/6241 covers building constructions that incorporate drainage means, such as built-in channels, pipes, or floors that direct water away from the structure. It includes designs for floor drains, roof gutters, sub-grade drainage systems and other integrated features that prevent water accumulation and protect the building's integrity.
[E06B 2003/6244] with extra parts sealing against the bottom of the glazing rebate or against the edge of the pane -- This subgroup covers sealing devices for glazing units that include additional components designed to seal at the lower part of the glazing rebate or directly on the pane edge, improving water tightness and durability. It pertains to windows, doors or façades where auxiliary sealing elements are used.
[E06B 2003/6247] with extra parts sealing against the fixed or another window frame -- The code E06B 2003/6247 designates window constructions that employ extra sealing components to close the gap between a movable sash and a fixed or adjacent window frame. These additional parts, such as gaskets, weather-stripping or sealing strips, are intended to improve airtightness, water resistance and thermal insulation.
[E06B 2003/625] Specific form characteristics -- The CPC subclass E06B 2003/625 deals with patents that define particular geometric or shape features of roof covering elements. It covers inventions that specify curvature, profile, modular or other distinctive form characteristics of roofing components, separate from material, attachment or functional aspects.
[E06B 2003/6252] wedge-shaped -- E06B 2003/6252 designates building insulation elements that are wedge-shaped, used to fit between structural components for thermal or acoustic sealing. The classification covers devices, panels or inserts whose thickness varies across the piece to accommodate gaps and ensure continuous insulation in walls, floors or ceilings.
[E06B 2003/6255] H-shaped -- E06B 2003/6255 covers H-shaped structural members used in building frames and supporting constructions, such as H-beams, columns or brackets that have a central web and two flanges forming the letter H. The classification applies to the design, manufacture and installation of these components in skeletal building structures.
[E06B 2003/6258] U-shaped -- E06B 2003/6258 covers U-shaped civil-engineering elements such as tunnels, culverts or channel sections with a cross-section shaped like the letter U, used for water conveyance, earth retention or similar structural purposes.
[E06B 2003/6261] S-shaped -- E06B 2003/6261 is a CPC subgroup concerning roofs or building components that feature an S-shaped profile. It classifies designs where the curvature follows an S-curve to achieve specific functional or aesthetic effects such as improved water runoff, load distribution, or architectural expression, and is part of the broader E06B category for roof and covering structures.
[E06B 2003/6264] hollow -- E06B 2003/6264 classifies building elements that incorporate hollow interiors, such as walls, ceilings, floors, beams or panels designed with voids to reduce weight, provide insulation, accommodate services, or improve structural performance. It covers the construction, materials and methods used to create and integrate these hollow components within a building framework.
[E06B 2003/6267] consisting of several separate parts -- A CPC entry in the E06B class for foundations and underground constructions, code 2003/6267, designates inventions whose structural element is composed of several separate parts that are assembled to form the whole. It typically covers modular or prefabricated components that can be combined to create a complete foundation or underground structure.
[E06B 2003/627] with specific characteristics concerning the material -- E06B 2003/627 designates doors, windows, shutters, skylights and related building openings that possess defined material characteristics such as fire resistance, thermal insulation, acoustic performance or special durability. The code is used to identify inventions where the choice or treatment of the material is a key technical feature of the product. It falls within the broader CPC group for building envelope elements.
[E06B 2003/6273] reinforced, e.g. against elongation -- The CPC entry E06B 2003/6273 covers building elements that are reinforced so as to limit or prevent elongation under load, including concrete or masonry members strengthened with steel rebars, fibers, meshes, or prestressed components. It pertains to the design, construction or modification of structural parts where tensile deformation is restrained by such reinforcement.
[E06B 2003/6276] with parts of differing nature, e.g. hardness -- E06B 2003/6276 covers windows, doors, or similar building elements whose components are made of materials with different physical properties, such as varying hardness, for example a metal frame combined with a softer polymer seal or a glass pane attached to a rigid composite. The classification is used for inventions that deliberately integrate parts of differing nature to achieve specific functional or structural advantages.
[E06B 2003/6279] with coverings or embellisher strips -- E06B 2003/6279 falls under the CPC group for doors, windows and shutters and covers designs that incorporate protective or decorative coverings, such as panels, fabrics, films or ornamental strips attached to the surface or frame. The classification is applied to constructions where these coverings serve to shield, insulate or enhance the visual appearance of the opening.
[E06B 2003/6282] of cellular nature -- This CPC entry covers building components made from cellular or honey-comb type materials, such as aerated concrete blocks, cellular glass or other lightweight porous structures used for walls, partitions, fences, doors or windows. It relates to the design, manufacture or use of these cellular elements in construction.
[E06B 2003/6285] with provisions for receiving putty or pasty adhesives -- This subclass covers roofing or flashing elements that incorporate a cavity, groove, channel or other provision specifically designed to receive putty or pasty adhesives for sealing joints. The feature is intended to improve water-tightness or attachment of the component in building construction.
[E06B 2003/6288] with fire resisting properties -- The CPC subclass E06B 2003/6288 groups openings such as doors, windows, shutters or skylights that are constructed to provide fire-resistance. It includes features, materials or sealing arrangements that give the element a fire-rating or allow it to remain functional during a fire. This subclass is used for patents concerning fire-resistant building apertures.
[E06B 2003/6291] Corner arrangements -- The CPC subclass E06B 2003/6291 pertains to corner arrangements in building elements. It includes designs, structures and methods for forming, reinforcing or sealing the intersection of two walls, floors or other components, such as corner joints, brackets and protective elements that improve stability, weatherproofing or fire resistance.
[E06B 2003/6294] using separate corner members -- E06B 2003/6294 classifies stair, ladder or escalator designs that incorporate distinct corner components, such as separate posts or brackets, to form the outer edges of the structure. This feature isolates the corner members from the main run, allowing independent fabrication, installation or reinforcement of the corners. It is used to improve stability, facilitate modular construction or accommodate varying geometric layouts.
[E06B 2003/6297] for glazing panels of special shape, e.g. with undulating or U-shaped cross-section -- The CPC subclass E06B 2003/6297 covers glazing panels that have non-standard geometries, such as undulating, curved or U-shaped cross-sections, used in windows, facades or other building envelopes. It groups inventions that provide methods or devices for producing, installing or supporting these specially shaped glass or transparent panels.
[E06B 2003/66338] of glass
[E06B 2003/6638] with coatings -- E06B 2003/6638 covers floor or wall covering products that incorporate a coating layer, such as protective, decorative or functional films applied to the covering material. It includes patents for coverings where the coating - for example varnish, enamel, polymer or resin layers - is integral to the product's performance or appearance.
[E06B 2003/66385] with special shapes -- The CPC subclass E06B 2003/66385 includes construction elements-such as panels, beams, or other building components-that are characterised by specially shaped profiles or geometries, for example curved, angular, or otherwise non-standard forms used in roofs, walls or other structural assemblies.
[E06B 2003/6639] sinuous -- E06B 2003/6639 classifies stairs, steps or ramps whose profile follows a sinuous, wave-like shape rather than a straight line, covering designs that use curving or winding treads for aesthetic, ergonomic or space-saving purposes.
[E06B 2003/66395] U-shape -- E06B 2003/66395 refers to building elements that possess a U-shaped cross-section, such as channels, brackets, or frames used in walls, roofs or claddings to support, guide or fasten structural components.
[E06B 2003/67378] Apparatus travelling around the periphery of the pane or the unit -- The CPC subclass E06B 2003/67378 covers devices that travel around the edge of a glass pane or glazing unit, typically for cleaning, inspection, or treatment while the pane remains stationary; the apparatus may be guided, motorised or manually propelled and follows the perimeter of the unit.
[E06B 2003/67395] Non-planar units or of curvilinear outline, e.g. for vehicles -- The CPC class E06B covers vehicle bodies and related structures, and the subgroup 2003/67395 specifies non-planar units or components having curvilinear outlines, such as body panels, frames or fittings with three-dimensional or warped shapes rather than flat surfaces. This code is applied to patents describing vehicle parts whose geometry is intentionally curved or contoured.
[E06B 2003/7011] with easily replaceable or interchangeable panels -- The subclass covers walls, partitions, doors, windows or roof components that are designed so their panels can be removed and replaced or interchanged easily, enabling rapid repair, upgrading or reconfiguration without dismantling the whole structure. It includes modular or snap-fit panel systems used in building envelopes and interior fittings.
[E06B 2003/7013] put in place through a slot in the frame -- E06B covers windows, doors and similar closures; subclass 2003/7013 relates to devices that are installed by moving them through a slot cut in the frame, such as inserting a panel, latch or other closure element directly into the opening in the frame. This classification is used for mechanisms that achieve closure by placement through a frame slot.
[E06B 2003/7019] of corrugated type -- The subclass E06B 2003/7019 covers building components-typically roofing, wall or cladding sheets-manufactured with a corrugated profile. The corrugation, a series of parallel ridges and grooves, provides added stiffness and strength to the material, which may be metal or other suitable substances. This entry is used for patents describing such corrugated-type structural panels.
[E06B 2003/7021] consisting of wooden strips filling the whole door -- E06B 2003/7021 pertains to doors whose entire panel is composed of wooden strips or slats that fill the whole surface, forming a uniform wooden lattice. The construction is used for decorative, ventilating or structural purposes in various openings.
[E06B 2003/7023] of foam type -- The group covers construction elements formed of foam material, such as panels, blocks or other building components made from cellular polymers or similar foams, used for structural or insulating purposes in buildings.
[E06B 2003/7025] of cork; of wood or similar fibres -- The CPC subclass E06B 2003/7025 covers compositions, processes or devices for treating or using cork, wood or similar fibrous materials, especially for forming protective or decorative elements such as shoe soles, floor coverings or structural parts. It includes methods of shaping, binding or impregnating these natural fibers for industrial use.
[E06B 2003/7026] of granular type -- The subgroup E06B 2003/7026 relates to water-supply, sewage or irrigation installations that use a granular medium-such as sand, gravel, charcoal or other particulate material-as the principal element for filtering, adsorbing or retaining particles, contaminants or chemicals during treatment or flow control.
[E06B 2003/7028] of cementituous type, e.g. concrete -- E06B 2003/7028 relates to cementitious binders, specifically concrete and similar hydraulic-cement mixtures used in building and civil-engineering applications. It includes compositions where the primary binding phase is cement, covering concrete mixes, ready-mixed concrete and related products.
[E06B 2003/703] containing a metallic layer -- This CPC subclass pertains to gutters or rainwater pipes that include a metal layer, such as a coating, liner, or sheath, applied for strength, corrosion resistance, or conductivity. It covers designs where the metallic layer is integral to the pipe structure or attached to a non-metal base.
[E06B 2003/7032] of non-vegetal fibrous material, e.g. glass or rock wool -- The code E06B 2003/7032 designates building-insulation products that consist of non-vegetal fibrous materials, such as glass wool, rock wool or similar inorganic fibres. It covers the manufacture, shaping, installation and use of these fibrous mats, plates or batts for thermal or acoustic insulation in structures.
[E06B 2003/7034] of asbestos
[E06B 2003/7036] of plaster
[E06B 2003/7038] made from a slurry -- E06B 2003/7038 covers building elements that are produced by shaping and hardening a liquid mixture of binders, aggregates and water-for example concrete blocks, prefabricated panels or mortars formed from a slurry before curing.
[E06B 2003/704] of mineral material which is not further specified -- This CPC entry falls under group E06B for containers and handling equipment, specifically for mineral material that is not otherwise defined. It covers devices, processes or containers used with any mineral material where no further specification is given, such as generic handling, transport or storage solutions.
[E06B 2003/7042] with a fire retardant layer -- This CPC entry covers roofing or roof-covering components that incorporate an additional fire-retardant layer. It applies to tiles, slates, sheets or other roof elements where a protective coating or built-in layer is provided to improve resistance to fire spread. The classification is used for patents describing such fire-resistant roofing solutions.
[E06B 2003/7044] Garage doors -- The E06B 2003/7044 subclass groups inventions relating to garage doors, including their construction, sealing, and means for opening or closing such doors. It covers manual, powered, and automatic mechanisms, safety devices, and control systems used in residential or commercial garage door applications.
[E06B 2003/7046] with provisions for locks, hinges or other fittings -- E06B 2003/7046 classifies doors that incorporate hardware such as locks, hinges, or other fittings. It covers designs where the door structure is provided with provisions for installing these components, typically for security, operation, or sealing purposes. The code is used in patent documents to identify inventions related to door hardware integration.
[E06B 2003/7048] with arcuate periphery; with openings having an arcuate periphery -- This subgroup covers doors, windows, shutters, skylights or similar building openings whose outer edge is curved, i.e., having an arcuate periphery, as well as openings that themselves are shaped with an arcuate perimeter.
[E06B 2003/7049] Specific panel characteristics -- E06B 2003/7049 relates to inventions that define particular characteristics of panels used in doors, windows, shutters, gates, skylights and similar building elements, such as shape, material composition, reinforcement, thermal or acoustic performance, or surface treatment. The code is applied when the novelty resides in the panel's specific properties rather than the overall device.
[E06B 2003/7051] of layered construction involving different materials -- The CPC code E06B 2003/7051 pertains to doors, windows, shutters or similar openings whose frame or panel is made from a layered or sandwich construction that combines two or more different materials, such as metal, wood, plastic, glass or composites, to obtain particular strength, insulation, acoustic or aesthetic properties. It classifies devices where the different material layers are bonded, joined or otherwise integrated to form a single functional opening element.
[E06B 2003/7053] corrugated -- E06B 2003/7053 designates inventions relating to the use of corrugated profiles in roof, ceiling or floor elements. It covers structures where corrugation provides stiffness, drainage or aesthetic features, such as corrugated metal sheets, plastics or composite panels applied to buildings.
[E06B 2003/7055] Left-right hinged interchangeable door leaves -- E06B 2003/7055 covers doors whose leaf panels are mounted on hinges that allow the leaf to swing left or right and can be interchanged without modifying the frame. It applies to designs where the same opening can accommodate either a left-right hinged door leaf, offering flexible installation in residential, commercial or industrial buildings.
[E06B 2003/7057] with little passing through doors -- This CPC subgroup concerns doors that are intentionally built to allow only a small opening, providing limited passage for people or objects, often for security, fire protection, or space-saving reasons. It includes designs, mechanisms and structures where the door opening is narrow, restricted in width, or has limited swing or sliding travel.
[E06B 2003/7059] Specific frame characteristics -- The CPC subclass E06B 2003/7059 covers features that define a frame's physical attributes, such as its shape, size, material composition, thickness, reinforcement type, and load-bearing properties. It is used to classify patents that detail how these specific frame characteristics influence the performance, durability, or construction method of structural frames.
[E06B 2003/7061] Wooden frames -- The CPC entry E06B 2003/7061 relates to wooden frames used as load-bearing or supporting structures in building construction, such as timber joists, studs, trusses and similar components. It covers devices, methods and arrangements for assembling, joining or reinforcing such wooden frames in walls, floors, roofs or other structural elements.
[E06B 2003/7063] reinforced; in combination with other materials, e.g. foam, honeycomb -- The code E06B 2003/7063 refers to reinforced building elements that are combined with other materials such as foam, honeycomb or other lightweight cores. It covers the integration of reinforcement (e.g., steel, fibres, ribs) with these adjunct materials to improve strength, stiffness, load-bearing capacity or insulation in walls, floors, ceilings and similar structural components.
[E06B 2003/7065] Wood fibres -- E06B 2003/7065 pertains to wood fibres used as a material in building construction, covering their processing, treatment and application in products such as panels, boards, composites and insulation. It includes methods for preparing, binding and shaping wood fibre for structural or non-structural purposes in buildings.
[E06B 2003/7067] of layered construction -- E06B 2003/7067 pertains to foundations, substructures or ground-improvement methods that employ a layered construction, such as walls, slabs or footings built from successive layers of material, often with differing properties, to achieve structural stability, load distribution or drainage. It covers techniques for assembling, bonding, or compacting these layers to form a cohesive support system.
[E06B 2003/7069] with cladding -- E06B 2003/7069 relates to foundations that incorporate a cladding layer, describing techniques for surrounding or covering foundation structures with materials such as concrete, metal, polymer or composite to protect, reinforce or improve performance.
[E06B 2003/7071] made of a plurality of separate blocks or strips -- The CPC subclass E06B 2003/7071 covers roofing systems constructed from multiple individual blocks or strips, such as shingle-type, tile, or modular panels that are assembled to form a continuous roof covering, often interlocking or overlapping to provide weather protection.
[E06B 2003/7073] with fire retardant measures in frame -- E06B 2003/7073 covers structural frames that integrate fire-retardant measures, such as fire-resistant materials, coatings, barriers or treatments applied to the frame members to improve resistance to combustion and limit fire spread. It is a subclass of E06B, which deals with building frames and load-bearing structures.
[E06B 2003/7074] Metal frames -- The code designates metal framing components used in building construction, encompassing prefabricated steel, aluminium or other metal members that form load-bearing or supporting structures such as walls, floors, roofs or modular sections. It covers designs, connections and methods for assembling these frames.
[E06B 2003/7076] insulated -- E06B 2003/7076 designates roof or building-covering structures that incorporate insulation, covering the integration of insulating material within the component to enhance thermal performance and energy efficiency.
[E06B 2003/7078] with fire retardant measures in frame -- The subclass groups inventions dealing with building frames that incorporate fire-retardant measures, such as fire-resistant coatings, impregnated timber, protective barriers, or fire-proof metal components, intended to enhance the fire resistance of structural members in roofs, walls or other building elements. It covers any design or method that integrates these fire-safety features directly into the frame itself.
[E06B 2003/708] insulated -- The CPC subclass E06B 2003/708 covers building elements that incorporate insulation, including insulated walls, roofs, floors, ceilings or partitions designed to enhance thermal or acoustic performance. It pertains to the structural integration of insulating materials within the construction of a building, rather than separate, add-on insulation. This subclass is used to classify patents describing insulated building components and related manufacturing methods.
[E06B 2003/7082] Plastic frames -- E06B 2003/7082 designates the subclass for frames made of plastic, covering the construction and attachment of plastic window, door or façade frames and their components such as glazing beads, seals and reinforcement parts. It is used to classify inventions that improve or adapt plastic framing systems.
[E06B 2003/7084] reinforced with metal or wood sections -- E06B 2003/7084 classifies building or wall structures that are strengthened by incorporating metal or wood members such as bars, studs, or framing elements. It covers masonry, concrete or similar sections where these reinforcements are added to improve load-bearing capacity, stability or resistance to cracking.
[E06B 2003/7086] One-piece frames, e.g. made out of a single panel by cutting out a middle portion, moulded frames -- This group includes frames for windows, doors or screens that are produced as a single integral piece, for example by moulding a whole panel or cutting a central opening out of one solid sheet. The design eliminates separate side or top members, allowing the frame to be formed in one step and typically used in prefabricated or modular construction.
[E06B 2003/7088] Two-piece frames made of a stile and an U-shaped part surrounding the panel -- The code E06B 2003/7088 refers to window or door frames that are constructed from two separate components: a vertical or horizontal stile and an adjoining U-shaped part that encircles the glazing or panel. This arrangement allows the panel to be inserted or removed while the frame maintains its structural integrity. It is used in building constructions for modular or replaceable fenestration systems.
[E06B 2003/709] Frame parts being attached to both sides of the panel which extends till the door edge -- The CPC subclass E06B 2003/709 covers door or window assemblies where the frame components are fixed on both sides of a panel and run continuously to the edge of the door, forming a unified perimeter that supports and seals the opening. This arrangement is typical for panels that are part of a sliding or hinged door system where structural rigidity and weather tightness are required.
[E06B 2003/7092] with frame members not directly or resiliently connected to each other -- E06B 2003/7092 refers to building frames in which the individual members are not joined directly or by resilient means. The members are separated or linked only through secondary components, allowing load to be transferred indirectly. This category includes modular or prefabricated structures where frames are isolated from each other.
[E06B 2003/7094] with ventilated innerspace -- This CPC subgroup covers windows, doors, shutters or similar building openings that incorporate a ventilated inner cavity, i.e., an air space between inner and outer panels designed to allow controlled airflow while maintaining sealing or thermal performance.
[E06B 2003/7096] with possibilities to alter the extension of the door -- E06B 2003/7096 pertains to doors equipped with mechanisms that allow the door leaf to be lengthened outward, such as swing-out or telescopic extensions, providing adjustable opening widths or additional clearance when the door is opened.
[E06B 2003/7098] with bracing means crossing each other, e.g. diagonally disposed -- E06B 2003/7098 classifies building or structural elements that incorporate bracing members which intersect one another, typically arranged diagonally, to enhance stability and resist lateral forces. It covers designs where the crossing braces form a reinforcing framework within walls, frames or similar constructions.
[E06B 2007/023] Air flow induced by fan -- The subclass deals with systems where a fan generates airflow to move, distribute or regulate air, typically for ventilation, cooling or drying. It includes fans, blowers or related components designed to create a directed flow of gas for functional or environmental purposes.
[E06B 2007/026] with air flow between panes -- The CPC subclass E06B 2007/026 pertains to buildings or structures that incorporate glazing in which air can flow between the panes, such as ventilated double or triple glazing, air-gap windows, or other assemblies designed to allow controlled ventilation or thermal exchange through the space separating the glass layers.
[E06B 2007/145] for glass façade, e.g. curtain wall -- The CPC subclass E06B 2007/145 pertains to building envelopes that use glass as an external façade, such as curtain-wall systems. It covers designs, structures, mounting methods, sealing and related components for transparent or translucent cladding that forms the outer skin of a building. This classification is applied to patents dealing with the construction, installation or improvement of such glass façades.
[E06B 2007/202] Actuator connected to wing frame -- This subclass covers mechanical or electromechanical actuators that are mounted on a wing frame of a roof or building element to adjust its position, such as opening, closing or tilting the wing for ventilation, shading or load-relief purposes.
[E06B 2007/305] Microphone -- E06B 2007/305 falls under the CPC section for building construction and, more specifically, the subgroup for ventilation and air-conditioning equipment. It designates microphones intended for use in such installations, such as sound sensors for airflow monitoring, noise control or alarm activation within HVAC systems. This code helps classify patents that integrate acoustic detection devices into building ventilation technology.
[E06B 2007/365] Rounded shape at gap, e.g. cylindrical -- The E06B 2007/365 subclass pertains to openings in building elements where the gap between adjoining parts is provided with a rounded, often cylindrical, shape to facilitate movement, sealing or aesthetic effect. It includes doors, windows or panels whose meeting edges are rounded to reduce stress and improve sealing.
[E06B 2009/002] Safety guards or gates -- E06B 2009/002 covers safety guards or gates incorporated in buildings or structures to protect openings, stairways, platforms or hazardous areas. The entry includes devices such as protective barriers, closing doors, shutters, or hinged gates that prevent accidental entry or falling, and may be actuated manually or automatically for occupational safety.
[E06B 2009/005] Storm panels; hurricane shutters -- The CPC code E06B 2009/005 covers storm panels and hurricane shutters designed to protect openings such as windows and doors from high-velocity wind and debris. It includes both permanent and removable protective devices that are installed on the exterior of a building to reduce damage during severe weather events.
[E06B 2009/007] Flood panels -- E06B 2009/007 covers flood panels, which are modular or fixed barriers installed on walls, doors, windows or other openings to prevent water ingress during flooding. The panels may be made of metal, plastic, composite or fabric and are used in temporary or permanent flood-defense systems.
[E06B 2009/015] Mounting details -- E06B 2009/015 covers the specific features and components used to fix or attach building elements to a supporting structure, such as brackets, fasteners, anchors, and adjustment mechanisms. It includes the geometry, material selection, and installation methods that ensure stability, alignment and load transfer for walls, ceilings, floors or facades. The code is applied to patents describing how these mounting details are engineered and implemented in construction.
[E06B 2009/0684] stored in a spiral like arrangement -- The CPC group E06B 2009/0684 covers building-related installations in which elongated elements such as pipes, cables or ductwork are coiled or otherwise stored in a spiral-like arrangement to save space, facilitate handling or provide thermal benefits.
[E06B 2009/135] Horizontal shutter reinforcements -- E06B 2009/135 relates to the reinforcement of horizontal shutters used on windows, doors, or other openings, describing structural elements or devices that strengthen the shutter bars to improve durability, security, or weather resistance.
[E06B 2009/1505] Slat details -- E06B 2009/1505 classifies the specific design and functional features of slats used in window, door or façade assemblies, such as their shape, arrangement, adjustability, sealing or light-control characteristics. It covers variations that influence airflow, privacy, insulation or aesthetic appearance, providing detailed guidance for patents on slat-based building elements.
[E06B 2009/1511] Coatings -- E06B 2009/1511 classifies inventions that apply protective, decorative or functional coatings to building components such as windows, doors, shutters and related fittings, including paints, varnishes, laminates, films and surface-treatment processes aimed at improving durability, weather resistance, optical properties or aesthetics.
[E06B 2009/1516] Means to increase resistance against bending -- E06B 2009/1516 concerns features that enhance a structure's resistance to bending, such as stiffeners, reinforcement ribs, braces or specialized material sections that increase flexural rigidity. The classification covers designs and methods for integrating these means into building components, bridges or similar load-bearing elements.
[E06B 2009/1522] Sealing joint between adjacent slats -- E06B 2009/1522 covers sealing joints between adjacent slats in roofing systems, describing methods and devices that join slats together to prevent water ingress. It includes the use of sealants, tapes, gaskets or overlapping arrangements that create a watertight connection between neighboring slats. This subclass falls under the broader roof-covering and roof-accessories category.
[E06B 2009/1527] Transparent slats or transparent sections in slats -- This subgroup covers window or building elements whose individual slats incorporate transparent material or contain transparent sections, allowing light to pass while the slats retain their functional role. It applies to blinds, shutters, ventilating devices and similar constructions where partial visibility of the slats is required.
[E06B 2009/1533] Slat connections -- E06B 2009/1533 covers the design and joining of individual slats used in external wall cladding systems, including mechanical fasteners, adhesives, interlocking profiles and other means of securing adjacent slats to form a continuous façade. The code addresses both metal and non-metal slats and defines the geometry and load-carrying features required for structural integrity and weather resistance. This sub-group falls under the broader category for building construction and insulation.
[E06B 2009/1538] Slats directly connected -- The CPC entry E06B 2009/1538 pertains to building fittings such as shutters, blinds or ventilation louvers in which the individual slats are directly connected to one another or to a supporting frame without separate hinges, allowing the slats to be adjusted as a unit to regulate light, air or privacy.
[E06B 2009/1544] Engaging section has curved articulation surfaces -- E06B 2009/1544 covers building elements such as doors, windows, shutters or gates in which the engaging part of the hinge or coupling features curved articulation surfaces. The curvature provides smoother pivoting or locking action and can improve sealing and durability of the joint.
[E06B 2009/155] Slats connected by separate elements -- E06B 2009/155 relates to door or window assemblies in which individual slats are linked together by distinct connecting components such as rods, cords, clips or brackets, allowing the slats to pivot or move as a unit while remaining individually mounted. This code is used for louver or shutter mechanisms where the slats are not rigidly fixed but are joined by separate elements that transmit motion.
[E06B 2009/1555] Flexible elements, e.g. tapes, strips, cords or chains -- E06B 2009/1555 covers flexible components such as tapes, strips, cords or chains employed in construction or civil engineering for joining, reinforcing, or supporting structures. It includes elements that can be bent, twisted or stretched to distribute loads or provide tension.
[E06B 2009/1561] Strips -- Strips are elongated, narrow elements used in building and civil-engineering applications for joining, sealing, reinforcing or decorating walls, facades or other structural components. The classification covers metal, plastic, wood or composite strips that may be fixed, interlocked or bonded to surfaces.
[E06B 2009/1566] Rigid elements, e.g. hinges, hooks or profiles -- This CPC class E06B 2009/1566 relates to rigid building elements such as hinges, hooks or profiles used in construction or architectural structures. It covers the design, manufacture and installation of non-flexible components that provide connection or support.
[E06B 2009/1572] Locking means to prevent slat disengagement -- The CPC subclass E06B 2009/1572 covers devices that lock the individual slats of a ventilating or shading assembly so they cannot be inadvertently lifted or removed. Such locking means are employed in blinds, shutters, louvers and similar constructions to maintain alignment, improve safety and preserve weather-tightness.
[E06B 2009/1577] Slat end pieces used for guiding shutter -- E06B 2009/1577 covers slat end pieces that guide the movement of a shutter in window or door assemblies. These components are fitted to the ends of the slats to ensure proper alignment and smooth operation of the shutter.
[E06B 2009/1583] inserted in slat cavity -- The CPC entry E06B 2009/1583 pertains to building components in which a piece is placed inside the cavity of a slatted element, such as a blind, shutter or ventilating slat, to provide functions like sealing, reinforcement or control of airflow. It covers the insertion of such inserts within the internal space of the slat.
[E06B 2009/1588] inserted in engaging section of adjacent slats -- A component placed within the interlocking zone where two neighboring slats meet, serving to connect or lock the slats together in doors, shutters, blinds or comparable assemblies. This insertion occurs in the engaging section of the adjacent slats to provide secure alignment and operation.
[E06B 2009/1594] attached to outer surface of slat -- The class covers devices that are fastened to the external face of a slat, such as brackets, clips, seals or decorative fittings that secure, adjust, or protect the slat in shutters, louvers or blinds. These elements are mounted on the outer surface rather than being integral to the slat itself.
[E06B 2009/17038] Shutter cleaning arrangements -- The CPC subclass E06B 2009/17038 covers devices or mechanisms for cleaning shutters of windows, doors or similar openings, typically integrated into the shutter system to facilitate removal of dust or debris. It includes wipes, brushes, spray nozzles and automated cleaning systems that can be attached to or built into shutters.
[E06B 2009/17053] Door in shutter screen; wicket door -- E06B 2009/17053 relates to doors that are built into a shutter screen, commonly called wicket doors, allowing passage while the screen remains in place. The classification covers the design, construction and mounting of such integrated doors in building facades, garages or ventilated openings.
[E06B 2009/17069] Insulation -- E06B 2009/17069 pertains to insulation for building structures, such as walls, roofs, floors and ceilings, employing thermal or acoustic barrier materials like mineral wool, foam, reflective films or similar substrates to enhance energy-efficiency and sound control.
[E06B 2009/17084] closing outlet slot of shutter box -- The CPC entry E06B 2009/17084 covers devices for sealing the outlet slot of a shutter box, such as those used in ventilation or lighting enclosures. It includes mechanisms that close, lock or otherwise restrict the passage through the slot, often to control airflow, light or protection from external elements.
[E06B 2009/17092] Manufacturing -- The CPC subclass E06B covers doors, windows, shutters, floodgates and gates, and the entry 2009/17092 "Manufacturing" refers specifically to the processes, methods and apparatus used to produce these building components, including material preparation, assembly, shaping, joining and finishing operations.
[E06B 2009/1713] Telescopic rollers -- Telescopic rollers are adjustable-diameter rollers used in road-building and surface-finishing equipment; by extending or retracting the roller they vary the contact width and pressure applied during compaction or leveling. This flexibility enables a single machine to handle different pavement types and thicknesses, improving efficiency in road construction and maintenance.
[E06B 2009/1716] Arrangements to improve rolling of the shutter on the roller -- E06B 2009/1716 covers devices and mechanisms that facilitate smoother operation of roller-type shutters, such as guide tracks, spring-assisted winding systems, tension-adjusting elements, or low-friction surfaces that reduce effort and prevent jamming during rolling.
[E06B 2009/1743] allowing radial roller movement -- The CPC subclass E06B 2009/1743 covers foundation or ground-bed structures that incorporate rollers capable of moving radially, enabling the foundation to adjust to settlement or load shifts. It relates to devices that permit the roller to roll outward or inward relative to a central point, improving load distribution and adaptability.
[E06B 2009/1746] Axial connection of rollers -- E06B covers devices for handling or moving objects, especially roller systems. The subclass 2009/1746 defines axial connections of rollers, describing structures and methods whereby rollers are linked along their axis-such as shafts, clutches or bearing arrangements that transmit axial loads or permit adjustable spacing.
[E06B 2009/2405] Areas of differing opacity for light transmission control -- E06B 2009/2405 covers structures in which a transparent or translucent panel contains distinct zones that can be made more or less opaque to regulate the amount of light passing through, such as windows, skylights or display screens featuring patterned or adjustable shading areas. This classification is used for technologies that create spatially varying light transmission, often employing electrochromic, thermochromic or mechanically actuated elements.
[E06B 2009/2411] Coloured fluid flow for light transmission control -- The CPC entry E06B 2009/2411 covers systems that control the flow of coloured liquids to modulate light transmission through a panel or aperture, such as smart-window and variable-opacity glazing devices that use fluid movement for illumination or display control.
[E06B 2009/2417] Light path control; means to control reflection -- The CPC entry E06B 2009/2417 covers devices and arrangements that direct, redirect or otherwise control the path of light, including mirrors, prisms, reflective surfaces and mechanisms that adjust or switch reflections to achieve desired optical effects. It includes any means for controlling how light is reflected within systems such as lighting, display, measurement or safety equipment.
[E06B 2009/2423] Combinations of at least two screens -- E06B 2009/2423 classifies patents that combine two or more display screens into a single unit or system, often to provide expanded visual information or improved user interaction. It applies to multi-screen arrangements in vehicles, control panels, portable devices and similar applications.
[E06B 2009/2429] One vertical sheet and slats -- The CPC entry E06B 2009/2429 covers a building component that combines a single vertical sheet with attached slats, forming a shading or ventilation element for roofs, windows, doors or skylights. It pertains to structures where the sheet provides a continuous surface while the slats allow adjustable airflow or light control.
[E06B 2009/2435] Two vertical sheets and slats in-between -- A construction element comprising a pair of vertical panels separated by a series of parallel slats, used for doors, shutters or window assemblies where the slats provide ventilation or light control while the panels form the main framework. This arrangement allows the slats to remain fixed while the outer sheets open or close, enhancing weather protection and aesthetic appearance.
[E06B 2009/2441] Screens joined one below the other -- E06B 2009/2441 classifies display arrangements where individual screens are mounted directly one below the other to form a vertically stacked composite display. It covers devices that join multiple panels in this orientation for extended image height, improved viewing angles, or combined information presentation.
[E06B 2009/2447] Parallel screens -- E06B 2009/2447 concerns parallel screens, i.e., two or more screen elements fixed side-by-side or one behind the other in the same plane to provide shading, ventilation or visual privacy. The screens are arranged so their surfaces remain parallel, allowing uniform airflow and consistent light diffusion across the covered area. This subclass groups devices such as building façades, ventilated façades or ornamental panels that employ parallel screen configurations.
[E06B 2009/2452] moving independently -- E06B 2009/2452 covers road-structure elements that can move on their own, such as self-propelled barriers, autonomous traffic-control devices and other infrastructure components that relocate without direct external actuation.
[E06B 2009/2458] moving simultaneously -- The subclass E06B 2009/2458 covers arrangements in which two or more building components are displaced at the same time, such as simultaneous lifting, lowering, shifting or rotating of structural parts during construction, modification or repair operations.
[E06B 2009/2464] featuring transparency control by applying voltage, e.g. LCD, electrochromic panels -- The CPC subclass E06B 2009/2464 covers windows, panels or other surfaces whose light transmission is regulated by an applied voltage, including liquid-crystal displays and electrochromic devices. It pertains to any construction that achieves variable transparency through electrical control.
[E06B 2009/247] Electrically powered illumination -- E06B 2009/247 pertains to electrically powered illumination systems used in buildings, including lighting fixtures, lamps and associated control devices for interior or exterior spaces.
