It falls within the CPC section for building construction and addresses the design, installation and operation of electric lighting equipment.
[E06B 2009/2476] Solar cells -- E06B 2009/2476 covers photovoltaic or solar-cell devices, including their semiconductor materials, cell structures, encapsulation, mounting and integration methods for converting sunlight into electricity. The classification also addresses manufacturing processes, performance testing and applications of solar cells in power generation systems.
[E06B 2009/2482] Special shape -- E06B 2009/2482 denotes building elements that possess a non-standard geometric form, such as arched, conical, wedge-shaped or otherwise specially configured shapes, employed to achieve particular functional, structural or aesthetic objectives within a construction.
[E06B 2009/2488] Curved perimeter -- E06B 2009/2488 covers patents dealing with the design, construction or application of curvilinear edges in road paving, surfacing and covering, such as curved perimeters of pavements, sidewalks, or other road-related surfaces.
[E06B 2009/2494] Trapezoidal or triangular -- E06B 2009/2494 concerns building openings, windows, doors, shutters or façade elements that are trapezoidal or triangular in shape, covering their design, manufacturing, installation and sealing as non-rectangular construction components.
[E06B 2009/2622] Gathered vertically; Roman, Austrian or festoon blinds -- The classification covers window coverings formed from fabric that is gathered into vertical folds, encompassing designs such as Roman, Austrian and festoon blinds. These blinds are made of a continuous panel that is pulled upward to create pleats, providing adjustable shading and a decorative, streamlined appearance. They are operated by cords, rods or other mechanisms that raise and lower the gathered fabric.
[E06B 2009/2625] Pleated screens, e.g. concertina- or accordion-like -- The CPC entry E06B 2009/2625 covers pleated screen structures such as concertina or accordion-type panels used in building construction for ventilation, shading, partitioning or decorative purposes. It includes designs where the screen material is folded to create flexible, expandable sections.
[E06B 2009/2627] Cellular screens, e.g. box or honeycomb-like -- The CPC subclass E06B 2009/2627 covers cellular screens such as box-type or honeycomb-type panels that serve as ventilating, shading or partitioning elements in building constructions. These structures are typically assembled from a network of hollow cells to provide lightweight strength, airflow or light diffusion, and are used in facades, walls, roofs or interior partitions.
[E06B 2009/2643] Screens between double windows -- E06B 2009/2643 relates to fenestration devices where a screen is installed in the space between two adjacent windows, forming a secondary barrier for ventilation, insect protection, or safety while preserving the primary window's function. This subclass covers the structural arrangements and mounting methods for such inter-window screens.
[E06B 2009/2646] Magnetic screen operator -- The CPC subclass E06B 2009/2646 covers magnetic screen operators, devices that use a magnetic field to separate ferrous particles or contaminants from bulk materials such as powders, granules or foods. These operators typically consist of a perforated or woven screen combined with magnets positioned to attract and retain magnetic debris while allowing the product to pass through. The classification applies to equipment designed for continuous or batch processing in industrial cleaning and quality-control applications.
[E06B 2009/285] Means for actuating a rod (being tilt rod or lift rod) -- E06B 2009/285 covers devices that provide the mechanical or powered action to move a tilt or lift rod used in building components such as windows, doors or shutters. It includes levers, cams, gears, hydraulic, pneumatic or electric actuators that translate manual input or control signals into rod displacement for opening, closing or adjusting the element.
[E06B 2009/3222] Cordless, i.e. user interface without cords -- The CPC subclass E06B 2009/3222 belongs to the group for user-interface devices and defines cordless interfaces, meaning the user interacts with the system without any physical connection. It covers wireless input methods such as remote controls, Bluetooth or infrared controllers, and other untethered human-machine interaction technologies.
[E06B 2009/3225] Arrangements to aid the winding of cords rollers -- This subclass covers devices that assist in winding hoisting cords in lift or escalator systems, such as rollers, pulleys and guides that control rope tension, alignment and routing during operation.
[E06B 2009/3227] Axially moving rollers -- The CPC class E06B 2009/3227 refers to conveyors that use rollers whose axes move longitudinally, typically for aligning, loading or unloading material on a belt or track. It covers mechanisms where the rollers translate along the direction of travel to adjust spacing, engage or disengage loads, or provide variable feed rates.
[E06B 2009/3265] Emergency release to prevent strangulation or excessive load -- The CPC subclass E06B 2009/3265 covers emergency-release mechanisms designed to stop a rope, cable or similar line from tightening to a dangerous degree, thereby preventing strangulation of a load or excessive tension that could cause failure. It applies to safety devices in lifting, hoisting and similar operations that automatically disengage or loosen the line when a predetermined load limit is reached.
[E06B 2009/402] adjustable without the use of tools or cutting instruments -- The CPC subclass E06B 2009/402 covers building components that can be repositioned, aligned or modified manually without the need for tools, fasteners or cutting. It includes features such as hinges, sliders or adjustable brackets that allow on-site adjustment of doors, windows, panels or other structural parts.
[E06B 2009/405] Two rollers -- E06B 2009/405 relates to saws that use two rollers to feed or support the workpiece during cutting, a arrangement common in panel or band saws for continuous processing of wood or similar sheet materials.
[E06B 2009/407] Telescopic roller -- The CPC entry E06B 2009/407 covers telescopic rollers, which are earth-moving compactors equipped with drums that can be elongated or retracted to adjust width or pressure during soil compaction. These machines are used on construction and road-building sites to improve efficiency and adapt to varying ground conditions.
[E06B 2009/425] Pull chain or cord attached to bottom edge of screen -- E06B 2009/425 covers devices in which a pull chain or cord is connected to the lower edge of a screen to raise, lower, or adjust it, typically found in window blinds, protective screens, or similar movable panels. The classification applies to mechanisms that control screen movement via manual cord actuation attached at the bottom.
[E06B 2009/445] by adhesive -- E06B 2009/445 covers wall, fence or panel structures that are assembled or bonded using adhesive materials instead of mechanical fasteners, welding or other joining methods. It includes the use of glue, resin or other bonding agents to attach components in construction or repair of barriers and enclosures.
[E06B 2009/524] Mesh details -- The code E06B 2009/524 in the CPC refers to the specific features and configurations of mesh used in stairs, ramps, walkways and related constructions. It includes descriptions of mesh geometry, perforation pattern, attachment methods and functional details such as slip resistance or reinforcement.
[E06B 2009/525] Patches for mesh repair or decoration -- E06B 2009/525 covers patches designed to repair or decorate mesh structures, such as reinforcement or protective meshes in building, infrastructure, or industrial applications. The patches may be made of similar or different materials and are applied to restore integrity or provide aesthetic enhancement.
[E06B 2009/527] Mounting of screens to window or door -- The CPC subclass E06B 2009/527 covers arrangements and devices for fixing screens-such as insect, privacy or sun-blocking screens-to windows or doors. It includes brackets, clips, rails, hinges or other mounting structures that secure a screen to the surrounding frame, allowing the screen to be attached, removed or adjusted as needed.
[E06B 2009/528] Screens extending between movable wing and fixed frame of window or door -- The code covers protective screens that bridge the gap between a movable wing, such as a sash or hinged panel, and the fixed frame of a window or door, allowing ventilation while keeping out insects or debris. It includes the design, attachment and movement mechanisms that let the screen operate in concert with the opening wing.
[E06B 2009/543] Horizontally moving screens -- The CPC class E06B covers the construction and operation of rotating or revolving kilns and similar devices. Subgroup 2009/543 refers specifically to screens that travel horizontally within the kiln to separate or convey material as the kiln rotates. These screens can be adjustable and are used for sorting, cooling, or feeding processes.
[E06B 2009/546] connected to end- or bottom-bar of roller shutter -- The CPC entry E06B 2009/546 covers features that are attached to or integrate with the end or bottom bar of a roller-type shutter, such as locking devices, sealing strips, guides or drive connections that engage the lower horizontal bar to secure, guide, or operate the shutter. It relates specifically to the mechanical or functional elements linking the shutter's lower edge to the supporting structure.
[E06B 2009/583] Cords or cables -- E06B 2009/583 covers cords or cables used in foundation work and underground construction, such as tensioning or anchoring elements that stabilize soil, support retaining structures, or assist in excavation. The code is part of the CPC group for earthworks - foundations.
[E06B 2009/585] Emergency release to prevent damage of shutter or guiding device -- This CPC subgroup covers devices that provide an emergency release for shutters or guiding mechanisms, allowing them to open or detach quickly to avoid damage during abnormal conditions such as fire, overload or impact. The release may be manual, automatic or triggered by a safety system.
[E06B 2009/586] with heating to prevent frost or condensate -- E06B 2009/586 refers to building elements in which heating is incorporated to avoid frost formation or condensation on surfaces such as windows, walls, roofs, or other architectural components. The classification covers systems that actively raise the temperature of the element or adjacent air to keep moisture from depositing as ice or water.
[E06B 2009/587] Mounting of guiding devices to supporting structure -- The CPC subclass E06B 2009/587 deals with the methods and arrangements for attaching guiding or rail systems-such as door tracks, window slides, or conveyor guides-to a supporting framework or building structure. It covers fixtures, brackets, fasteners and related elements that secure the guiding device to walls, frames, ceilings or other supporting elements.
[E06B 2009/588] Sealings for guides -- E06B 2009/588 covers sealing solutions for linear or roller guides, including shafts, rails and carriages, that protect the sliding surfaces from contaminants, dust, moisture and lubricant leakage. The entry includes various types of seals, wipers and labyrinth arrangements designed to maintain guide accuracy and extend component life.
[E06B 2009/6809] Control -- The CPC subclass E06B 2009/6809, labelled "Control", covers devices, systems and methods for monitoring, regulating or directing the operation of construction or building equipment, such as cranes, lifts, scaffolding or automated building elements, including sensors, actuators, feedback loops and control panels that manage movement, position or performance during building work.
[E06B 2009/6818] using sensors -- The CPC group E06B 2009/6818 covers inventions that employ sensors-such as strain gauges, accelerometers, temperature or humidity detectors-to monitor, measure, or control parameters of building structures or construction processes. It includes sensor-based systems for structural health monitoring, environmental regulation, safety management, and automated operation of construction equipment.
[E06B 2009/6827] sensing light -- E06B 2009/6827 covers lock or security devices that incorporate a light-sensing element to detect illumination levels and use the signal to control locking, unlocking or alarm actions. It includes methods for employing photodetectors in access-control systems and related safety mechanisms.
[E06B 2009/6836] sensing obstacle -- The CPC subclass E06B 2009/6836 covers systems and methods that detect or sense obstacles in the environment, such as for vehicle navigation, robotics or safety applications. It includes any sensor-based techniques-ultrasonic, radar, lidar, infrared or vision-that provide information on the presence, distance or characteristics of objects to enable collision avoidance or path planning.
[E06B 2009/6845] sensing position -- E06B 2009/6845 covers devices or methods that detect the position of movable building elements such as doors, windows, shutters or other openings. It includes sensors, limit switches, position detectors and associated control circuitry used to monitor or indicate whether the element is open, closed, partially open or otherwise positioned.
[E06B 2009/6854] sensing torque -- E06B 2009/6854 covers torque-sensing devices used in building construction, including sensors and systems that detect or measure the turning force applied to fasteners, joints or structural elements to ensure proper tightening and load monitoring. It encompasses electronic, mechanical or hydraulic torque transducers integrated into tools or building components.
[E06B 2009/6863] sensing wind speed -- E06B 2009/6863 covers devices and systems for sensing wind speed, including anemometers, wind-speed sensors integrated into structures, and associated signal-processing or display units. It also includes methods for measuring, converting or transmitting wind-speed data for monitoring or control purposes.
[E06B 2009/6872] using counters to determine shutter position -- The subclass relates to mechanisms that employ electronic or mechanical counters to sense and indicate the angular or linear position of a shutter, such as for windows, doors or blinds. It includes systems that track the number of movements to infer the current opening state and may integrate the data with control or monitoring units.
[E06B 2009/6881] Mechanical counters -- E06B 2009/6881 relates to devices that count discrete events by mechanical means, such as gear-driven or lever-operated counters used in odometers, tally meters, and similar instruments. The classification includes counter mechanisms, display elements, and methods of resetting or advancing the count.
[E06B 2009/689] using end switches -- E06B 2009/689 relates to the incorporation of end switches in fence or gate systems, where the switches are positioned at the terminal points to detect opening, closing or tampering. The technology is used to trigger alarms, control access or activate other safety mechanisms.
[E06B 2009/725] with epicyclic or planetary gear train -- The CPC subclass E06B 2009/725 groups patents for lifts, escalators, or other loading-unloading machines that incorporate an epicyclic or planetary gear train to transmit motion. It applies when the gear arrangement is a principal element of the drive or speed-changing mechanism in the device.
[E06B 2009/785] by belts, straps, bands, tapes, cords, tassels -- This CPC subgroup classifies building or wall constructions where components are joined, fastened or ornamented by belts, straps, bands, tapes, cords or tassels, such as textile or polymer ties used to hold panels, cladding or structural elements together.
[E06B 2009/801] Locking arrangements -- E06B 2009/801 covers devices and methods that secure openings such as doors, windows, shutters or hinged panels, including mechanical, electromechanical or magnetic locking mechanisms, latches, bolts and related actuation or control systems intended to prevent unauthorized entry or maintain a closed position.
[E06B 2009/802] located in or close to shutter box -- The CPC subgroup E06B 2009/802 covers doors, windows, shutters or related devices that have a component positioned in or close to the shutter box, such as mechanisms, controls or accessories integrated within the box or installed adjacent to it. This classification is used to identify designs where the functional element is directly associated with the shutter housing rather than being separate from it.
[E06B 2009/804] acting directly on the shutter -- E06B 2009/804 pertains to shutters that are operated by a direct action mechanism. The classification includes devices where the user or a motor applies force straight to the shutter leaf, such as manual levers, handles, push rods or directly coupled actuators, without intermediate gear trains or remote control systems.
[E06B 2009/805] located on or in the guides -- E06B 2009/805 covers lift or elevator components that are mounted on or within the guide rails or other guiding structures of the hoisting system. It includes arrangements where cables, ropes, rollers, brakes, sensors or similar devices are positioned on or inside the guides to ensure alignment, support and safe movement of the car.
[E06B 2009/807] Brakes preventing fast screen movement -- The CPC subclass E06B 2009/807 covers devices that limit or stop rapid movement of protective or decorative screens, such as insect screens, sunshades or fencing panels. It includes mechanical brakes, dampers, locking or friction devices that restrain sliding, rotating or folding screens to prevent sudden opening or closing, thereby enhancing safety or control.
[E06B 2009/808] Fluid brakes -- E06B 2009/808 designates fluid-actuated brake systems, covering hydraulic, pneumatic or hybrid brakes for road and rail vehicles. It includes master and wheel cylinders, pressure reservoirs, valves, and control mechanisms that transmit fluid pressure to apply braking force, as well as related safety and modulation features.
[E06B 2009/885] Braking mechanism activated by the bottom bar -- Entry E06B 2009/885 covers braking or locking devices that are engaged by a bar positioned at the bottom of a moving part, such as a drawer, door or panel, causing the mechanism to stop or hold the element in place. It is applied to sanitary or bathroom fixtures and related furniture where the bottom-mounted bar activates the brake to control motion.
[E06B 2009/905] using wrap spring clutches -- E06B 2009/905 relates to mechanical power transmission devices that employ a wrap-spring clutch to engage or disengage rotation. The clutch consists of a helical spring wrapped around a shaft; when the spring is tightened it grips the shaft to transmit torque and releases when loosened, providing controlled torque transmission, overload protection, or speed variation in gearboxes, conveyors and similar machinery.
[E06B 3/00] Window sashes, door leaves, or like elements for closing openings -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Wings, including the wing frames and insulating glazing units therefor ; corner joints or edge joints ; machines specifically designed for the assembly of insulating glazing units  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Doors, windows, or like closures for special purposes; Border constructions therefor -> E06B5/00 - Shutters or the like -> E06B9/00 - Glass panes per se, glass composition, coatings -> C03C27/00 , C03C17/00 , C08J7/00 , C23C14/00  **Glossary:** - IGU: insulating glazing unit, e.i.unit comprising two or more glass or like panes permanently secured together
[E06B 3/01] Removable or disappearing walls for hangars or other halls, e.g. for aircraft -- The subclass deals with walls that can be removed or made to disappear in large enclosures such as aircraft hangars or other halls, allowing the interior space to be opened up or reconfigured as needed. It covers systems where the wall can be taken down, folded, or otherwise concealed to provide a clear opening.
[E06B 3/02] Wings made completely of glass -- E06B 3/02 covers movable closure elements such as window sashes or door leaves that are fabricated entirely from glass. The entry includes glass wings used as doors, windows, shutters or similar fixtures for sealing openings in buildings, vehicles, fences or comparable enclosures.
[E06B 3/025] consisting of multiple glazing units  units  per se   E06B3/66
[E06B 3/04] Wing frames not characterised by the manner of movement -- E06B 3/04 covers wing frames for windows, doors or similar closures where the classification does not depend on how the wing moves. It includes any fixed-construction frame element used to close an opening, irrespective of whether the wing swings, slides or folds. The code is applied when the movement mechanism is not the defining feature.
[E06B 3/06] Single frames -- E06B 3/06 designates single-frame constructions used to close openings such as windows, doors and similar closures. The code applies to individual sashes, door leaves or comparable elements that form a solitary frame around an opening, whether in buildings, vehicles or other enclosures. It is part of the fixed constructions group for doors, windows, shutters and roller blinds.
[E06B 3/08] Constructions depending on the use of specified materials -- E06B 3/08 covers fixed or movable closures such as window sashes, door leaves and similar elements whose design is defined primarily by the specific material employed, for example wood, metal, glass or composite, and the way that material is used to achieve structural or functional requirements.
[E06B 3/10] of wood -- E06B 3/10 refers to wooden components that form fixed or movable closures for openings in buildings, vehicles, fences or similar enclosures, such as window sashes, door leaves and comparable elements used to shut doors, windows, shutters or blinds.
[E06B 3/105] reinforced -- Reinforced elements for closing building or vehicle openings, such as window sashes, door leaves or similar components, where the structural strength is increased by additional material, internal frames, bracing or other means to improve rigidity, resistance to warping or impact, and overall durability.
[E06B 3/12] of metal -- E06B 3/12 designates metal window sashes, door leaves or similar closing elements used to seal openings in buildings, vehicles, fences or comparable enclosures. The code is part of the fixed constructions group that includes doors, windows, shutters and roller blinds, specifying metal as the material of the closing members.
[E06B 3/14] of special cross-section -- This subgroup addresses window sashes, door leaves and comparable closure elements whose cross-section is not the usual rectangular profile, including channel-shaped, double-wall, profiled or otherwise specially shaped sections that provide particular structural, sealing or functional advantages.
[E06B 3/16] Hollow frames -- E06B 3/16 covers hollow frames used as sashes, door leaves or similar elements that close openings in buildings, vehicles or other enclosures. The subclass pertains to constructions where the frame members are internally empty or contain a cavity, typically to reduce weight, improve insulation or allow passage of services. It is applied to doors, windows, shutters, blinds and related movable or fixed closures.
[E06B 3/163] with a filled cavity -- E06B 3/163 covers window sashes, door leaves, or similar closing elements that incorporate a cavity filled with material such as insulation, glazing or a structural filler, rather than being hollow. The filled cavity is intended to improve thermal, acoustic or mechanical performance while retaining the element's overall shape and function in fixed or movable closures.
[E06B 3/18] of concrete or other stone-like material -- This subclass covers window sashes, door leaves and similar closing elements that are formed from concrete, stone or other stone-like materials. It falls under the CPC section for fixed constructions, specifically the group for doors, windows, shutters and roller blinds in general.
[E06B 3/20] of plastics -- E06B 3/20 refers to fixed or movable closure elements such as window sashes, door leaves and similar components that are made primarily of plastic materials, encompassing both solid-plastic and plastically reinforced constructions used in buildings, vehicles or similar enclosures.
[E06B 3/205] moulded or extruded around a core -- E06B 3/205 refers to window sashes, door leaves or similar closure elements that are formed by molding or extruding a material around a supporting core. The classification covers fixed or movable closures for openings in buildings, vehicles or other enclosures where the profile is produced by this method.
[E06B 3/22] Hollow frames -- E06B 3/22 covers hollow frames used as components of windows, doors and similar closures. The classification includes sashes, door leaves and other fixed-or-movable elements that have a hollow interior for structural strength, insulation or to accommodate hardware.
[E06B 3/221] with the frame member having local reinforcements in some parts of its cross-section or with a filled cavity -- E06B 3/221 relates to window, door or similar closure frames whose members include locally reinforced sections of the cross-section or contain a filled cavity, providing added strength, rigidity or improved thermal behaviour for fixed or movable openings in buildings and comparable structures.
[E06B 3/222] with internal prefabricated reinforcing section members inserted after manufacturing of the hollow frame -- This subclass deals with window sashes, door leaves or similar closing elements that have a hollow frame into which a prefabricated reinforcing section is inserted after the frame is manufactured, adding extra strength. It is part of the broader group for fixed or movable closures such as doors, windows, blinds and gates.
[E06B 3/223] the hollow frame members comprising several U-shaped parts assembled around a reinforcing core member -- The code E06B 3/223 designates hollow frame members made from two or more U-shaped sections that are assembled around a central reinforcing core, forming a lightweight yet stiff profile used for window sashes, door leaves and similar fixed or movable closures.
[E06B 3/24] specially adapted for double glazing -- The subclass E06B 3/24 covers window sashes, door leaves and similar closing elements that are specially designed for use with double-glazed glazing. It includes constructions adapted to hold two panes of glass with an insulating cavity, providing improved thermal and acoustic performance for fixed or movable openings.
[E06B 3/26] Compound frames, i.e. one frame within or behind another -- E06B 3/26 concerns compound frames, meaning a frame built inside or behind another frame for windows, doors or similar closures. The classification covers arrangements where an inner frame is positioned within an outer frame to provide additional sealing, strength, thermal insulation or decorative effect.
[E06B 3/2605] with frames permanently mounted behind or within each other, each provided with a pane or screen -- E06B 3/2605 covers window sashes, door leaves or similar closing elements whose frames are permanently mounted behind or within one another, with each frame carrying its own pane or screen. This subclass falls under fixed constructions for doors, windows, shutters or roller blinds in general.
[E06B 3/263] Frames with special provision for insulation -- E06B 3/263 pertains to frames for windows, doors, shutters or similar closures that include dedicated features for thermal or acoustic insulation, such as built-in insulated sections, cavities, insulating strips or other provisions that improve the enclosure's energy-saving performance.
[E06B 3/26301] with prefabricated insulating strips between two metal section members  with two metal frame members having interpenetrating or overlapping parts when mounted  E06B3/26336 ; combined with insulating elements formed  in situ   E06B3/2675 -- This subclass covers metal door or window frames in which two adjoining metal sections interlock by overlapping or interpenetrating parts, and the joint is insulated either with prefabricated insulating strips placed between the sections or with insulating material applied in situ.
[E06B 3/26303] with thin strips, e.g. defining a hollow space between the metal section members -- E06B 3/26303 concerns window sashes, door leaves or similar closing elements that use thin strips to define a hollow space between metal section members, providing reinforcement, insulation or a decorative cavity in fixed or movable closures for buildings, vehicles or comparable enclosures.
[E06B 3/26305] Connection details  deformation of portions of the metal frame members  E06B3/273 ; expansion of the extremities of the insulating elements  E06B3/277 -- E06B 3/26305 covers specific connection details for fixed or movable closures such as windows and doors, focusing on the deformation of metal frame portions and the expansion of the extremities of insulating elements. It includes methods to accommodate dimensional changes in the frame or seal while maintaining the integrity of the closure. This subclass falls under the broader group dealing with sashes, door leaves and similar closing elements.
[E06B 3/26307] Insulating strips and metal section members both having L-shaped ribs, the engagement being made by a movement transversal to the plane of the strips -- E06B 3/26307 pertains to constructions for windows, doors or similar closures in which an insulating strip and a metal section both feature L-shaped ribs that lock together by a transverse sliding motion across the plane of the strips. The arrangement provides a sealed, rigid joint for fixing sashes, leaves or comparable elements.
[E06B 3/2632] with arrangements reducing the heat transmission, other than an interruption in a metal section -- E06B 3/2632 covers fixed or movable closures such as window sashes, door leaves or similar elements that incorporate design features to lower heat transfer, for example insulated glazing, thermal-break constructions or low-emissivity coatings, provided the heat-reduction is achieved without creating an interruption in a metal section.
[E06B 3/26336] with two metal frame members having interpenetrating or overlapping parts when mounted  involving sheet metal section members  E06B3/2735 -- E06B 3/26336 classifies fixed or movable closures such as window sashes or door leaves that use two metal frame members whose parts interpenetrate or overlap when assembled, typically employing sheet-metal section members to form the framing structure. This applies to doors, windows, shutters and similar building or vehicle openings.
[E06B 3/26338] comprising short insulating elements disposed at intervals -- The entry covers window sashes, door leaves or similar closing members that incorporate short insulating inserts positioned at regular intervals along the frame. These elements are intended to improve the thermal or acoustic sealing of the opening while retaining the basic structure of a fixed or movable closure. It falls under the fixed-construction subclass for doors, windows, shutters and related closures.
[E06B 3/2634] without separate insulating elements, e.g. the heat transmission being reduced by a smaller cross-section -- The subclass E06B 3/2634 covers window sashes, door leaves and similar closure elements whose heat transmission is limited not by added insulating parts but by design features such as a reduced cross-section that lower thermal loss. It falls within the broader group of fixed or movable closures for openings in buildings, vehicles or fences.
[E06B 3/26341] comprising only one metal frame member combined with an insulating frame member -- E06B 3/26341 covers window sashes, door leaves or similar closure elements that are built from a single metal framing piece joined to an insulating frame member, forming a composite frame. The subclass applies to fixed or movable openings where the metal component is singular and paired with insulation.
[E06B 3/26343] with two or more separate insulating zones alternating with metal section members -- This CPC subclass covers fixed or movable closure elements such as window sashes or door leaves that incorporate two or more distinct insulating chambers separated by intervening metal sections, providing a composite construction with alternating insulation and metal members for improved thermal performance.
[E06B 3/26345] for wooden or plastic section members -- E06B 3/26345 classifies fixed or movable building closures such as window sashes and door leaves that are constructed from wooden or plastic sectional members, covering the design and assembly of these components within doors, windows, shutters or similar openings.
[E06B 3/26347] specially adapted for sliding doors or windows -- E06B 3/26347 refers to window sashes, door leaves or similar closure elements that are specially adapted for sliding operation. The elements are designed to move horizontally along a track to open and close an opening, as used in sliding doors or windows.
[E06B 3/267] with insulating elements formed -- E06B 3/267 covers window sashes, door leaves or comparable closure elements that incorporate insulating material as an integral part of the frame or panel, such as double-glazed units, insulated panels or sealed thermal breaks, aimed at reducing heat transfer through the opening. This subclass falls under fixed constructions for doors, windows, shutters or roller blinds and specifies the inclusion of built-in insulating elements.
[E06B 3/2675] combined with prefabricated insulating elements -- E06B 3/2675 pertains to fixed or movable closures such as window sashes, door leaves or similar components that are integrated with prefabricated insulating elements, typically to improve thermal performance of the opening. It falls under the broader category of fixed constructions for doors, windows, shutters or roller blinds in buildings, vehicles or similar enclosures.
[E06B 3/273] with prefabricated insulating elements held in position by deformation of portions of the frame members -- The subgroup concerns doors, windows, shutters or similar fixed or movable closures whose prefabricated insulating inserts are secured by deforming or flexing portions of the frame members, which hold the insulating element in position without additional fasteners, thereby improving thermal performance.
[E06B 3/2735] comprising a sheet metal member, an edge or which is bent over the edge of the other metal member with interposition of an insulating element -- E06B 3/2735 covers fixed or movable closures for openings such as windows or doors that are made from a sheet-metal member whose edge is bent over the edge of another metal member with an insulating element placed between them, forming a composite sash or leaf that provides sealing and thermal insulation.
[E06B 3/277] with prefabricated insulating elements held in position by expansion of the insulating elements -- E06B 3/277 covers window sashes, door leaves and similar closure elements that incorporate prefabricated insulating components which are secured in position by the expansion of the insulating material itself. The classification applies to constructions where the insulating element expands to lock the closure element within the frame.
[E06B 3/28] with additional removable glass panes or the like, framed or unframed -- E06B 3/28 covers window sashes, door leaves or similar closing elements that are provided with additional removable glass panes or equivalents, whether the panes are fitted in a frame or installed without framing. This subclass addresses constructions where secondary glazing can be taken out for cleaning, replacement or ventilation.
[E06B 3/285] flexible transparent foils without a proper frame fixed and sealed at a distance from the existing glass pane -- The entry covers flexible, transparent foil panels that are installed separate from an existing glass pane, lacking a rigid frame and being sealed in place to create a secondary glazing layer. It relates to movable or fixed closures such as windows, providing an additional insulated barrier without traditional framing.
[E06B 3/30] Coverings, e.g. protecting against weather, for decorative purposes -- E06B 3/30 relates to coverings applied to window sashes, door leaves or similar closure elements primarily to protect against weather and also serve a decorative function. It includes protective or ornamental panels, skins, or films fitted to fixed or movable closures in buildings, vehicles or similar structures.
[E06B 3/301] consisting of prefabricated profiled members or glass -- E06B 3/301 classifies window sashes, door leaves and similar closure elements that are constructed from prefabricated profiled members or from glass. It applies to fixed or movable closures for openings in buildings, vehicles, fences or comparable enclosures where the closing component is produced as a pre-shaped profile or glass panel.
[E06B 3/302] Covering wooden frames with metal or plastic profiled members -- The code E06B 3/302 covers methods and devices for covering wooden window or door frames with profiled metal or plastic members, typically to improve durability, weather resistance, or aesthetics. It applies to fixed or movable closures in buildings and similar structures.
[E06B 3/303] Covering metal or plastic frames with wooden profiled members -- E06B 3/303 covers the practice of applying wooden profiled members to metal or plastic frames of window sashes, door leaves or similar closure elements, creating a wooden-covered frame for aesthetic or protective purposes. This subclass is part of the broader group for fixed or movable closures in buildings and vehicles.
[E06B 3/304] the frames consist of two metal section members with insulating zone in between -- E06B 3/304 covers window, door or similar frames that are formed from two metal section members spaced apart with an insulating zone in between, creating a thermal break. The construction is used in fixed or movable closures for openings in buildings or vehicles to improve energy efficiency and reduce heat transfer. This design is typical for sash, leaf or panel systems where metal strength is combined with insulation.
[E06B 3/305] Covering metal frames with plastic or metal profiled members -- E06B 3/305 classifies the practice of covering metal frames of windows, doors, or similar fixed or movable closures with a plastic or metal profiled member, either as a protective layer or for decorative purposes. The subclass addresses the attachment of these covering profiles to sashes, door leaves, or analogous elements in building or vehicle enclosures.
[E06B 3/306] Covering plastic frames with metal or plastic profiled members -- The subclass E06B 3/306 covers the practice of fitting metal or plastic profiled members over plastic window or door frames, creating a protective or decorative strip that enhances rigidity, weather-tightness and appearance. It applies to fixed or movable closures such as doors, windows, blinds or gates in buildings, vehicles and similar enclosures.
[E06B 3/307] Coverings with special provisions for insulation, e.g. foam filled -- E06B 3/307 covers window sashes, door leaves and similar fixed or movable closures that incorporate special insulation measures, such as foam-filled panels or other insulating fillings, to improve thermal performance of the opening.
[E06B 3/308] Wing frames covered on the outside by a rigidly-mounted outer frame -- This subgroup concerns wing or leaf elements such as windows or doors that are surrounded on their external side by a rigidly attached outer frame. The outer frame is permanently mounted and forms a protective, sealing or decorative enclosure around the moving or fixed wing.
[E06B 3/32] Arrangements of wings characterised by the manner of movement -- E06B 3/32 covers the design of window sashes, door leaves and similar closure elements where the classification is based on the manner in which the wings move, such as swinging, sliding, tilting or folding. It classifies arrangements of these movable parts according to their movement mechanism.
[E06B 3/325] Wings opening towards the outside -- E06B 3/325 classifies movable closure elements-commonly called wings-that swing outward from the frame to open, such as doors, windows or shutters that pivot away from the opening. It falls under the broader group for fixed constructions and specifically addresses designs where the wing opens toward the exterior of a building, vehicle or enclosure. This includes any mechanism that causes the leaf to move outward rather than inward.
[E06B 3/34] with only one kind of movement -- E06B 3/34 classifies window sashes, door leaves and comparable closure elements that operate with only one kind of movement, such as exclusively swinging, sliding, rotating or pivoting, without any combined motions.
[E06B 3/341] Tilt-and-turn wings -- E06B 3/341 Tilt-and-turn wings refers to window sashes, door leaves or similar closure elements that can be tilted inward for ventilation and swung outward for full opening, providing flexible access and sealing for doors, windows and other openings in buildings, vehicles or similar enclosures.
[E06B 3/36] with a single vertical axis of rotation at one side of the opening, or swinging through the opening -- The CPC subclass E06B 3/36 covers fixed or movable closures for building openings that operate by rotating around a single vertical axis located at one side of the opening, such as casement windows, hinged doors or similar devices that swing through the opening.
[E06B 3/362] Double winged doors or windows -- This class covers constructions where a door or window consists of two adjoining panels that swing outward or inward, often hinged, and can be opened together or separately. It includes the framing, hardware and sealing arrangements specific to double-winged closures for buildings, vehicles or similar enclosures.
[E06B 3/365] Astragals for double doors -- E06B 3/365 covers astragals used on double doors, which are vertical strips or bars that bridge the gap between the two door leaves to improve sealing, security and appearance. The classification includes designs, materials and attachment methods for these closing elements in residential or commercial door systems.
[E06B 3/367] specially adapted for furniture -- The subclass includes door leaves, window sashes and similar closure elements that are specially designed to be incorporated into furniture, such as cabinet doors, wardrobe panels or other built-in furnishing components.
[E06B 3/38] with a horizontal axis of rotation at the top or bottom of the opening -- This subclass includes window sashes, door leaves, or similar closure elements that rotate about a horizontal axis located at the top or bottom of the opening, such as awning-type windows and hopper windows.
[E06B 3/385] specially adapted for furniture -- The E06B 3/385 subclass covers window sashes, door leaves and comparable closing elements that are specially adapted for integration with furniture, such as built-in wardrobe doors, cabinet shutters or other closure members shaped or configured to function as part of a piece of furniture rather than as a freestanding opening.
[E06B 3/40] with a vertical or horizontal axis of rotation not at one side of the opening, e.g. turnover wings -- E06B 3/40 covers window sashes, door leaves and similar closure elements that rotate about a vertical or horizontal axis positioned away from the edge of the opening, such as turnover wings that swing inward or outward rather than hinge on a single side.
[E06B 3/42] Sliding wings -- This classification covers sliding components of doors, windows or similar closures, such as sashes that move horizontally or vertically along a track to open or close an opening. It includes designs where the wing slides rather than swings, used in building or vehicle openings.
[E06B 3/44] Vertically-sliding wings -- The code E06B 3/44 covers vertically-sliding wing elements used as sashes, leaves or similar parts that move up and down to close or open an opening, such as sliding doors, window panels or blind sections.
[E06B 3/4407] Single-hung, i.e. having a single vertical sliding panel -- E06B 3/4407 covers opening closures where only one vertical sash or panel moves, such as single-hung windows in which a single pane slides up or down while the other remains fixed. The classification applies to doors, windows, shutters, or similar elements with a single movable vertical component.
[E06B 3/4415] Double-hung, i.e. with two vertical sliding panels -- This CPC subclass (E06B 3/4415) covers double-hung windows, which have two vertically sliding sashes that can be moved independently, allowing both upper and lower panels to be opened. It falls under the hierarchy of fixed constructions for doors, windows, shutters and similar closures.
[E06B 3/4423] disappearing in a wall pocket; Pockets therefor -- E06B 3/4423 covers window sashes, door leaves or similar closing elements that are designed to disappear into a recessed pocket within a wall, providing a concealed or flush-mounted opening. The classification includes the pockets themselves and mechanisms that allow the closure to slide, fold or otherwise retreat completely out of sight when opened.
[E06B 3/443] specially adapted for furniture -- E06B 3/443 covers window sashes, door leaves or similar closure elements that are specially adapted to serve as parts of furniture, such as built-in cabinet doors, wardrobe panels or integrated shelving units. The classification applies to fixed or movable closures designed to blend seamlessly with interior furnishings.
[E06B 3/46] Horizontally-sliding wings -- E06B 3/46 pertains to window sashes, door leaves or similar closure elements that move by sliding horizontally across an opening, enabling doors, windows, shutters or comparable fittings to be opened and closed with a side-to-side motion.
[E06B 3/4609] for windows  E06B3/4654  -  E06B3/4681  take precedence
[E06B 3/4618] the sliding wing being arranged beside a fixed wing  E06B3/4627  takes precedence -- This subclass groups window or door assemblies in which a movable sliding wing is placed next to a stationary wing, allowing the sliding part to open the opening while the fixed part remains in place. It is used for combinations of sliding and fixed sashes in façades, doors or similar closures. A more specific subclass, E06B3/4627, takes precedence over this group.
[E06B 3/4627] with the sliding wing flush closing or moving a considerable distance towards the opening when closing -- E06B 3/4627 covers window sashes, door leaves or similar fixed closures in which the sliding wing closes flush with the surrounding frame and must travel a relatively long distance across the opening before sealing.
[E06B 3/4636] for doors  E06B3/4609  and  E06B3/4654  -  E06B3/4681  take precedence; sliding shower doors  A47K3/34
[E06B 3/4645] with the sliding wing flush closing or moving a considerable distance towards the opening when closing -- E06B 3/4645 covers window sashes, door leaves or similar fixed or movable closures whose sliding wing closes flush with the frame or travels a considerable distance toward the opening during closure. It applies to doors, windows, shutters or comparable building openings.
[E06B 3/4654] disappearing in pockets in the wall; Pockets therefor -- The code E06B 3/4654 covers constructions in which a window sash, door leaf or similar closing element can be withdrawn completely into a recess or pocket built into the surrounding wall, so the opening appears flush with the surface when the element is hidden. This includes the design of the pocket, the sealing and any guiding mechanisms that allow the element to disappear into the wall.
[E06B 3/4663] specially adapted for furniture -- E06B 3/4663 designates window sashes, door leaves or similar closure elements that are specially adapted for integration with furniture, such as built-in cabinets, wardrobes or other interior fittings, providing both the function of closing an opening and serving as a furniture component.
[E06B 3/4672] with the sliding wing flush closing or moving a considerable distance towards the opening when closing -- This subgroup refers to fixed or movable closure elements such as window sashes or door leaves that close by sliding a wing flush with the surrounding frame, moving a considerable distance toward the opening during closure. It includes designs where the panel travels largely across the opening before sealing.
[E06B 3/4681] made of glass panes without frames  E06B3/4663  takes precedence -- E06B 3/4681 covers window sashes, door leaves or similar closure elements that consist solely of glass panes and lack supporting frames, fitting within the broader class of fixed constructions for doors, windows, shutters and roller blinds. The preceding subclass E06B 3/4663, which addresses glass elements with frames, takes precedence over this entry.
[E06B 3/469] Arrangements at the overlapping vertical edges of the wings that engage when closing -- This subclass deals with the geometry and devices located on the overlapping-vertical edges where two movable panels such as doors, windows or blinds meet, providing interlocking or locking functions when the panels are closed. It includes latches, catches, seals or other engaging means positioned at the edge to ensure a secure closure.
[E06B 3/48] Wings connected at their edges, e.g. foldable wings -- The code E06B 3/48 covers window, door or similar closure members formed as adjoining wings that share a common edge and can be folded together. It includes designs where the wings pivot or hinge at their joint to collapse or open, providing space-saving or adjustable opening solutions.
[E06B 3/481] Wings foldable in a zig-zag manner or bi-fold wings -- The CPC subclass E06B 3/481 covers hinged or sliding panels that fold in a zig-zag or bi-fold pattern to close an opening. These wings are commonly employed in window sashes, door leaves or similar fixed or movable closures for buildings, vehicles or fences.
[E06B 3/482] specially adapted for furniture -- The CPC subclass E06B 3/482 covers window sashes, door leaves or similar closing elements that are specially designed or modified for integration into furniture such as cabinets, wardrobes or other built-in fixtures. It falls under the broader group of fixed constructions for doors, windows, shutters or roller blinds in general.
[E06B 3/483] folding upwardly  E06B3/482  takes precedence -- E06B 3/483 covers window sashes, door leaves or similar closing elements that fold upward when opened, rather than swinging or sliding. It is part of the fixed constructions group for doors, windows and shutters and specifies the upward-folding mechanism. This subclass is subordinate to E06B 3/482, which takes precedence.
[E06B 3/485] Sectional doors -- E06B 3/485 covers sectional doors used as movable closures for openings in buildings, vehicles or other enclosures. The classification includes doors made of hinged or interlocking panels that move vertically and/or horizontally to open and close, commonly found in garages, warehouses and industrial facilities. It falls under the hierarchy of fixed constructions - doors, windows, shutters or roller blinds in general.
[E06B 3/486] with hinges being at least partially integral part of the section panels -- E06B 3/486 covers doors, windows, blinds or similar closures in which the hinge is at least partially integral to the section panel, including designs where the hinge is built into the sash or leaf.
[E06B 3/487] sliding laterally -- The code E06B 3/487 covers window sashes, door leaves or similar closure elements that open or close by moving laterally across the opening rather than swinging, including systems where the panel slides horizontally on tracks or guides. This sub-group falls under fixed constructions for doors, windows, shutters and rollers, specifically addressing lateral sliding mechanisms for building and vehicle openings.
[E06B 3/488] with horizontal strengthening means; anti-sag devices -- This subclass concerns window sashes, door leaves or similar closing elements that incorporate horizontal strengthening means or anti-sag devices to prevent deformation, such as reinforcement bars, braces, or other rigidity-maintaining structures.
[E06B 3/50] with more than one kind of movement -- E06B 3/50 covers window sashes, door leaves or similar closure elements that operate by more than one type of movement, such as a combination of swinging, sliding, tilting or revolving actions, enabling varied ways to open or close an opening.
[E06B 3/5009] where the sliding and rotating movements are necessarily performed simultaneously  E06B3/5027 ,  E06B3/5036  take precedence -- E06B 3/5009 covers window sashes, door leaves or similar closure elements in which sliding and rotating motions must occur together, such as pivot-hinged sliding doors or tilt-and-turn windows. The subclass defines the combined kinematic action required for the closure to function, and it supersedes the more specific entries E06B 3/5027 and E06B 3/5036.
[E06B 3/5018] where the pivot axis slides during pivoting of the wing -- E06B 3/5018 relates to window, door or similar closure elements whose pivot axis moves along a sliding path while the wing pivots, for example swing-type casement windows where the hinge point translates during opening.
[E06B 3/5027] "Austral" windows -- E06B 3/5027 classifies a window type known as an "Austral" window, in which a fixed lower sash is combined with an operable upper sash that pivots or slides to provide ventilation while preserving a clear, unobstructed view. The design is applied to residential and commercial openings where a stable, weather-tight lower portion is required alongside adjustable airflow.
[E06B 3/5036] Louvre type windows, e.g. windows made of movable strips of glass -- E06B 3/5036 covers louvre-type windows, which consist of a series of movable glass strips that can be tilted to regulate light and ventilation. The classification falls under fixed constructions for doors, windows and similar closures in buildings or vehicles.
[E06B 3/5045] specially adapted for furniture  E06B3/52  takes precedence -- The CPC subclass E06B 3/5045 covers window sashes, door leaves and similar closing elements that are specially adapted for integration with furniture, such as built-in cabinet doors or fitted shutters that function as part of a piece of furniture rather than as standalone openings. It falls under the broader group for fixed constructions and movable closures for building or vehicle openings.
[E06B 3/5054] where the sliding and rotating movements are independent of each other -- The subclass classifies closures such as window sashes or door leaves in which the sliding action and the rotating (swinging) action are mechanically independent, allowing each movement to be performed without the other. This applies to designs where a pane can both slide and swing separately.
[E06B 3/5063] the vertical sliding wings having the possibility of an additional rotational movement -- E06B 3/5063 classifies window sashes, door leaves or similar closure elements that primarily move by sliding vertically but are also designed to permit an additional rotational movement, enabling combined up-and-down and swing actions in fixed or movable building openings.
[E06B 3/5072] the horizontal sliding wings having the possibility of an additional rotational movement -- This subgroup relates to window or door elements that move horizontally as sliding wings but also include a provision for an additional rotational movement, allowing the leaf to pivot while sliding. It applies to fixed constructions such as sash windows, door leaves and similar closures where combined sliding-and-rotating operation is provided.
[E06B 3/5081] at least two wings have a different kind of movement -- E06B 3/5081 covers closures for openings-such as windows or doors-where at least two wings or leaves move in different ways, for example one panel swings while another slides, rolls, or tilts, allowing combined or complementary actions in a single fixed construction.
[E06B 3/509] where one of the sliding and rotating movements necessarily precedes the other -- This classification code E06B 3/509 refers to window sashes or door leaves with sliding and rotating movements. The movements are sequential, with one necessarily preceding the other. It is a type of fixed or movable closure for openings in buildings or vehicles.
[E06B 3/52] Wings requiring lifting before opening -- This subclass covers hinged or pivoting panels that must be lifted upward before they can swing open, such as awning windows, lift-up doors and similar shutters. It includes mechanisms where the wing is raised to a horizontal or near-horizontal position prior to pivoting, a feature common in garage doors, ventilating windows and other building openings.
[E06B 3/54] Fixing of glass panes or like plates -- E06B 3/54 covers the means and procedures for securing glass panes, panels or similar flat plates in building openings such as windows, doors or skylights. It includes brackets, clips, adhesives, sealants and mounting frames designed to hold the glass in place while providing weather-tightness and structural support. The class addresses both retrofit and original-installation fixtures for fixed and operable closures.
[E06B 3/5409] Means for locally spacing the pane from the surrounding frame -- This subclass covers devices that create a localized gap between a glass pane and its surrounding frame, such as spacer strips, seals or adjustable fittings that maintain separation to improve thermal insulation, acoustic performance or to accommodate expansion.
[E06B 3/5418] Permanently fixing of an additional unframed pane  compound frames  E06B3/2605 -- This subclass covers the permanent attachment of an additional unframed glass pane to a window sash, door leaf or similar closing element, forming a compound frame in which the extra pane is fixed directly to the existing structure without a separate frame, often to create double- or multi-glazing.
[E06B 3/5427] the panes mounted flush with the surrounding frame or with the surrounding panes  E06B3/5436  takes precedence; supporting structures for curtain walls  E04B2/885 -- Entry E06B 3/5427 relates to fixed building closures such as windows and doors where the glazing panes are installed flush with the surrounding frame or adjacent panes, forming a seamless surface. This subclass is subordinate to E06B 3/5436 and is distinct from supporting structures for curtain walls classified under E04B2/885.
[E06B 3/5436] involving holes or indentations in the pane -- The CPC subclass E06B 3/5436 pertains to fixed or movable closures whose glazing pane contains holes or indentations, including decorative apertures, ventilation openings or recesses for mounting hardware. It applies to window sashes, door leaves and similar elements used to close openings in buildings, vehicles or comparable enclosures.
[E06B 3/5445] Support arms engaging the holes or indentations -- Support arms designed to engage holes or indentations in window sashes, door leaves, or similar closing elements, providing a stable mounting point for the component within fixed or movable closures such as doors, windows, shutters, or blinds.
[E06B 3/5454] inside U-shaped section members -- E06B 3/5454 covers the use of internal U-shaped section members that are incorporated into window sashes, door leaves or similar closure elements to provide structural support, reinforcement or sealing within the opening. These members are positioned inside the frame rather than externally and are intended for fixed or movable closures in buildings, vehicles or similar enclosures.
[E06B 3/5481] by means of discrete fixing elements, e.g. glazing clips, glaziers points -- This subgroup of the CPC covers window sashes, door leaves and similar closure elements that are attached to the surrounding frame using individual fixing devices such as glazing clips or glazier's points. The focus is on discrete, separate fasteners rather than continuous sealing or bonding methods.
[E06B 3/549] by clamping the pane between two subframes -- This subgroup covers window or door units in which the glass pane is secured by being clamped between two separate subframes that hold it in place. It pertains to fixed constructions such as sashes, door leaves, or similar closures where the pane is not bonded but mechanically restrained.
[E06B 3/56] by means of putty, cement, or adhesives only -- The classification covers fixed or movable closure elements such as window sashes and door leaves that are secured to the surrounding frame solely by putty, cement or other adhesive compounds, without the use of mechanical fasteners.
[E06B 3/58] by means of borders, cleats, or the like -- E06B 3/58 covers the method of attaching window sashes, door leaves or similar closure elements by using borders, cleats, brackets or comparable joining features, allowing the panels to be fixed or removable within doors, windows, shutters or other building and vehicle openings.
[E06B 3/5807] not adjustable -- E06B 3/5807 covers window sashes, door leaves and similar fixed closure elements that are installed without any adjustment mechanism. The subclass is applied to non-adjustable components of doors, windows, shutters or comparable closures in buildings, vehicles or fences.
[E06B 3/5814] together with putty or fixed by glue -- The subclass E06B 3/5814 covers window sashes, door leaves or similar closure elements that are installed in conjunction with putty or secured by an adhesive. It relates to fixed or movable closures for openings in buildings or vehicles where the element is bonded rather than mechanically fastened.
[E06B 3/5821] hooked on or in the frame member, fixed by clips or otherwise elastically fixed  E06B3/585  takes precedence -- E06B 3/5821 covers window sashes, door leaves or similar closure elements that are attached to the frame by being hooked on or into the frame member and secured with clips or other elastic retaining means. The class addresses constructions where the fixing relies on a resilient or snap-fit connection rather than rigid fastening, and it is superseded by the more specific subclass E06B 3/585.
[E06B 3/5828] on or with auxiliary pieces -- The code E06B 3/5828 covers auxiliary elements that are provided on or with window sashes, door leaves or similar fixed or movable closures, such as stays, hinges, locks, guides, seals or other supplementary pieces that support or enhance the primary closing member.
[E06B 3/5835] together with parts of the border in the same undercut groove in the frame  E06B3/5842  takes precedence -- E06B 3/5835 covers window sashes, door leaves or similar closing elements in which the border parts are positioned in the same undercut groove of the frame, allowing a flush or concealed joint. This arrangement is used to improve sealing, strength and aesthetic continuity. The broader subclass E06B 3/5842 supersedes it.
[E06B 3/5842] fixed by a tongue-and-groove or mortise-and-tenon connection substantially parallel to the pane -- The CPC subclass E06B 3/5842 covers fixed constructions where a window sash, door leaf or similar closing element is attached to the frame by a tongue-and-groove or mortise-and-tenon joint that runs essentially parallel to the pane. It relates to fixed or movable closures in buildings, vehicles or comparable enclosures.
[E06B 3/585] adjustable, e.g. for accommodating panes of various thickness, or with provisions for altering the clamping force on the pane  E06B3/5871  takes precedence -- The subclass E06B 3/585 relates to fixed or movable closure elements such as window sashes or door leaves that can be adjusted to accommodate panes of different thicknesses or to modify the clamping force on the pane. It covers mechanisms allowing the position or pressure of the glazing to be varied, and is superseded by the more specific subclass E06B 3/5871.
[E06B 3/5857] the fixing being adjustable, e.g. in one of several possible positions -- E06B 3/5857 covers window sashes, door leaves or similar closure elements whose fixing can be set to different positions, allowing the element to be adjusted within the opening. The adjustment enables selection among multiple preset locations for sealing or operation.
[E06B 3/5864] with adjusting screws, wedges or cams -- The CPC subclass E06B 3/5864 covers fixed or movable closures such as window sashes, door leaves or similar elements that incorporate adjusting screws, wedges or cams to fine-tune their position or sealing. It belongs to the broader group for doors, windows, shutters and roller blinds in general.
[E06B 3/5871] pivotally fixed on the frame; Borders with pivoting parts -- The code denotes window, door or similar closure components whose sash or leaf is attached to the surrounding frame by a pivot, allowing the border to swing open or closed while remaining fixed to the frame. Such pivoting borders are used in fixed constructions for buildings, vehicles or similar enclosures.
[E06B 3/5878] the borders being pre-assembled in a frame-like manner on the pane or on the frame before the pane is fitted to the frame  E06B3/645  takes precedence -- E06B 3/5878 relates to window or door units in which the border elements are pre-assembled in a frame-like configuration on the glazing or on the surrounding frame before the pane is installed, providing a built-in seal or trim. This sub-group is subordinate to E06B3/645.
[E06B 3/5885] Corner arrangements for borders; Devices for making rounded corners -- E06B 3/5885 relates to corner arrangements for the borders of fixed or movable closures such as window sashes and door leaves, and to devices that produce rounded corners on these elements.
[E06B 3/5892] Fixing of window panes in openings in door leaves -- E06B 3/5892 covers the means and methods for securing glass panes within the openings of door leaves, including fixtures, sealing devices and supporting structures that integrate the pane into the door panel. It applies to fixed constructions where the pane is fixed directly to the door rather than to a separate window frame.
[E06B 3/60] of clamping cleats of metal -- This subclass covers metal clamping cleats designed for use on window sashes, door leaves or similar closing elements. The cleats serve to secure the movable part to the frame or guide, ensuring firm positioning and resistance to disengagement. It falls under the broader group of fixed constructions and closures for openings in buildings and other structures.
[E06B 3/605] made of sheet metal -- E06B 3/605 covers fixed or movable closures for building, vehicle or fence openings such as doors, windows, shutters or blinds where the closing elements like sashes or leaves are constructed from sheet metal. The subclass groups metal-fabricated door leaves, window frames and similar components used in general-purpose constructions.
[E06B 3/62] of rubber-like elastic cleats -- E06B 3/62 classifies rubber-like elastic cleats used on window sashes, door leaves or similar closing elements, intended to provide flexible, resilient sealing or holding of fixed or movable closures such as doors, windows, shutters or rollers. The cleats are made of elastomeric material that can stretch and return to shape, improving fit and reducing drafts.
[E06B 3/6202] positioned between adjoining panes without separate glazing bar -- This subclass refers to fixed-or-movable closures in which adjacent glass panes are joined directly, without a separate glazing bar separating them. The design relies on alternative sealing or framing methods to hold the panes together, typically applied to window sashes, door leaves, or similar elements.
[E06B 3/6205] having on at least one lateral side no backing from a separate rigid glazing bead or other stiff part of the window frame -- The code E06B 3/6205 designates fixed or movable closure elements such as window sashes or door leaves that, on at least one side, lack a separate rigid glazing bead or other stiff frame part providing backing. The construction relies on the pane or surrounding structure itself for support rather than an additional solid member.
[E06B 3/64] Fixing of more than one pane to a frame -- E06B 3/64 covers methods and devices for attaching multiple glass panes to a window or door frame, including sealing, supporting, and securing arrangements that hold two or more panes as a unit. It relates to fixed constructions such as windows, doors, shutters, and roller blinds where the glazing is fixed to the frame.
[E06B 3/645] of units comprising two or more parallel panes to frames designed for single panes only, e.g. having too shallow a rabbet -- This subclass covers window or door units where the frame is dimensioned for a single glass pane but the glazing consists of two or more parallel panes, such as double- or triple-glazed units, which may result in insufficient depth of the rabbet. It includes designs addressing the accommodation of multi-pane units within single-pane frames.
[E06B 3/66] Units comprising two or more parallel glass or like panes permanently secured together -- E06B 3/66 classifies window, door or similar closure units that contain two or more parallel glass or comparable panes that are permanently bonded or otherwise fixed together, forming a single element that functions as a pane in a sash or leaf. This includes multi-layer glazing used for insulation or safety.
[E06B 3/6604] comprising false glazing bars or similar decorations between the panes  connectors therefor  E06B3/6675 -- The class covers window or door sashes that incorporate decorative false glazing bars placed between the glass panes, together with the means for securing those bars to the sash. It pertains to fixed constructions for openings, focusing on ornamental elements that mimic real glazing bars.
[E06B 3/6608] without separate spacing elements  E06B3/6707  and  E06B3/6715  take precedence -- The CPC subgroup E06B 3/6608 covers window sashes, door leaves or similar closure elements that are formed without separate spacing components such as seals or gaskets; the element itself provides the necessary sealing or insulation. More detailed sub-groups (E06B 3/6707 and E06B 3/6715) describe particular arrangements and therefore take precedence over this broader definition.
[E06B 3/6612] Evacuated glazing units -- The CPC entry E06B 3/6612 covers window sashes, door leaves or similar closure elements that incorporate evacuated glazing units, i.e., one or more glass panes separated by a vacuum or low-pressure space to improve thermal insulation, sound reduction or safety. It applies to fixed or movable closures for buildings, vehicles or similar enclosures where such high-performance glazing is fitted.
[E06B 3/6617] one of the panes being larger than another -- E06B 3/6617 covers window or door closures in which a multi-pane sash includes at least two panes of different dimensions, specifically where one pane is larger than the other. The classification applies to fixed or movable constructions where the size disparity is a defining feature of the assembly.
[E06B 3/6621] with special provisions for fitting in window frames or to adjacent units; Separate edge protecting strips  E06B3/66347  takes precedence -- E06B 3/6621 covers window sashes, door leaves or similar fixed-construction closing elements that include special provisions for fitting into window frames or adjacent units, such as separate edge-protecting strips. It falls under the broader class for doors, windows, shutters and roller blinds in general.
[E06B 3/6625] molded on the edges -- The subclass includes window sashes, door leaves and similar closure members whose peripheral edges are produced by a molding process. The molded edges may provide sealing, structural reinforcement or decorative profile and are applied to fixed or movable openings in buildings, vehicles or similar enclosures.
[E06B 3/663] Elements for spacing panes -- E06B 3/663 covers components that maintain a defined gap between glass panes in windows, doors or similar fixed or movable closures, such as spacers, seals or supporting strips. These elements ensure structural stability, thermal insulation and airtightness of the glazing.
[E06B 3/66304] Discrete spacing elements, e.g. for evacuated glazing units -- Discrete spacing elements are individual spacers positioned between glass panes to maintain a controlled gap in sealed glazing assemblies, such as evacuated (vacuum) units. They provide structural support, thermal insulation and pressure equalisation while allowing the enclosure to remain airtight and mechanically stable.
[E06B 3/66309] Section members positioned at the edges of the glazing unit -- E06B 3/66309 covers section members located at the periphery of a glazing unit, such as edge frames, seals or reinforcing pieces that secure and support the glass in windows, doors or similar closures. These elements are designed to hold the glazing in place and provide structural integrity and weather tightness.
[E06B 3/66314] of tubular shape  E06B3/66323  and  E06B3/66333  take precedence -- This subclass covers window sashes, door leaves or similar closing elements that are formed as tubes, such as hollow profiles or round sections. It falls under fixed constructions for doors and windows, and more detailed definitions in subclasses 3/66323 and 3/66333 have priority.
[E06B 3/66319] of rubber, plastics or similar materials -- E06B 3/66319 covers window sashes, door leaves and similar closure elements that are constructed primarily from rubber, plastics or comparable synthetic materials. The classification applies to fixed or movable closures for openings in buildings, vehicles or other enclosures where such non-metallic materials are used for the closing component.
[E06B 3/66323] comprising an interruption of the heat flow in a direction perpendicular to the unit -- E06B 3/66323 relates to door or window elements that incorporate a thermal break that interrupts heat flow across the profile, typically achieved by inserting low-conductivity material or an air gap perpendicular to the sash or leaf, improving insulation.
[E06B 3/66328] of rubber, plastics or similar materials  E06B3/66314 ,  E06B3/66323  and  E06B3/66342  take precedence; transparent materials  E06B3/66333
[E06B 3/66333] of unusual substances, e.g. wood or other fibrous materials, glass or other transparent materials -- E06B 3/66333 covers fixed or movable closure elements such as window sashes or door leaves that are made from atypical materials, including wood, other fibrous substances, glass or any other transparent material, used in doors, windows, shutters or similar building openings.
[E06B 3/66342] characterised by their sealed connection to the panes -- E06B 3/66342 covers window sashes, door leaves or similar closure elements that are joined to the glass pane by a sealed connection, preventing the ingress of air or water. The sealed joint may be created with a gasket, adhesive, welding or other sealing techniques.
[E06B 3/66347] with integral grooves or rabbets for holding the panes  E06B3/66366  takes precedence -- This subclass covers fixed window or door elements that incorporate built-in grooves or rabbets to secure glass panes, allowing the panes to be held without separate framing. It falls under the fixed constructions group for doors, windows, shutters and related closures and is superseded by subclass E06B 3/66366.
[E06B 3/66352] with separate sealing strips between the panes and the spacer -- This subclass covers fixed or movable glazing elements such as window sashes or door leaves that incorporate separate sealing strips positioned between the glass panes and the spacer, creating an individual sealed cavity for each pane to improve insulation, moisture protection and overall performance.
[E06B 3/66357] Soldered connections or the like -- The code E06B 3/66357 classifies inventions that involve soldered joints or similar bonding techniques for parts of window sashes, door leaves or comparable closure elements, addressing how metal portions are connected by melting and solidifying filler metal.
[E06B 3/66361] with special structural provisions for holding drying agents, e.g. packed in special containers -- E06B 3/66361 designates windows, doors, shutters or similar fixed or movable closures that incorporate built-in features for storing drying agents, such as specially packed desiccant containers, to control moisture within the closure assembly. This subclass covers any structural provision that holds these agents in place as part of the sash, leaf or related element.
[E06B 3/66366] specially adapted for units comprising more than two panes or for attaching intermediate sheets -- This subgroup covers window or door closure elements that are specially designed to hold glazing units with more than two panes or to attach intermediate glass sheets, such as triple-glazed panels, using spacers, bonding or framing arrangements that secure the additional sheets in place.
[E06B 3/66371] positioned entirely outside the gap between the panes -- This subgroup covers window sashes, door leaves or similar closure elements that are installed completely on the exterior side of the glazing gap, meaning the entire frame or leaf sits outside the space between the glass panes. It includes external shutters, cladding or protective panels that do not occupy the interior gap.
[E06B 3/66376] comprising lighting means -- The subclass E06B 3/66376 covers window sashes, door leaves and similar closure elements that incorporate lighting devices, such as built-in lamps or LEDs, to illuminate the opening or surrounding area. It falls under the broader class for fixed constructions and movable closures in buildings and vehicles.
[E06B 3/667] Connectors therefor -- Connectors for window sashes, door leaves or similar closure elements are devices that join, link or attach these components to frames, hinges, tracks or each other, enabling their movement, sealing or structural support. Typical examples include hinges, latches, pivots, brackets and fastening brackets designed for fixed or movable doors, windows, shutters or roller blinds.
[E06B 3/6675] for connection between the spacing elements and false glazing bars -- The CPC subclass E06B 3/6675 relates to arrangements that link the spacing elements of a window or door sash to secondary or false glazing bars, securing these components together in fixed or movable closures while preserving the overall assembly.
[E06B 3/67] characterised by additional arrangements or devices for heat or sound insulation -- E06B 3/67 pertains to window sashes, door leaves and similar closure elements that include extra arrangements or devices for heat or sound insulation, improving the thermal or acoustic performance of fixed or movable openings in buildings, vehicles or comparable enclosures.
