[E06B 3/6707] specially adapted for increased acoustical insulation  doors, windows for insulation against noise  E06B5/20 -- The code E06B 3/6707 classifies window sashes, door leaves and similar fixed or movable closures that are specially adapted to provide increased acoustic insulation, i.e., designs that reduce noise transmission through doors or windows. This falls under the broader group for fixed constructions such as doors, windows, shutters and roller blinds used in buildings, vehicles, fences or similar enclosures.
[E06B 3/6715] specially adapted for increased thermal insulation or for controlled passage of light  E06B3/66323  takes precedence; coating of glass pains  C03C17/00 ; devices or arrangements for controlling the intensity, colour, phase, polarisation or direction of light  G02F1/00 -- This subgroup of E06B covers window sashes, door leaves and similar fixed or movable closures that are specially designed to improve thermal insulation or to regulate the amount of light passing through, such as insulated glazing or light-filtering panels.
[E06B 3/6722] with adjustable passage of light -- The subgroup covers window sashes, door leaves or comparable closure elements that include means for varying the amount of light that passes through, such as adjustable louvers, slats, operable panes or variable-opacity glazing.
[E06B 3/673] Assembling the units -- E06B 3/673 deals with the methods and apparatus used to assemble window sashes, door leaves and similar closure units for buildings, vehicles, fences or other enclosures. It includes the joining of frame sections, glazing, hardware and sealing elements to produce a complete movable or fixed closure.
[E06B 3/67304] Preparing rigid spacer members before assembly -- The entry relates to methods for producing rigid spacer members that are shaped, treated or pre-assembled prior to being incorporated into window sashes, door leaves, or similar closure elements. It covers preparatory steps such as cutting, profiling, inserting insulating material or applying coatings to ensure the spacers maintain fixed gaps and structural integrity once the closure is assembled.
[E06B 3/67308] Making spacer frames, e.g. by bending or assembling straight sections  connectors  E06B3/667 -- This CPC subclass covers the manufacture of spacer frames for window sashes, door leaves or similar closing elements, including methods that bend straight sections or join them with connectors. It addresses processes that create the framing components that maintain spacing between panels or glass panes in fixed or movable closures.
[E06B 3/67313] by bending -- E06B 3/67313 pertains to window sashes, door leaves or similar closure members that are manufactured by bending the material rather than assembling separate parts. The subclass groups those fixed or movable closures for openings in buildings, vehicles or similar structures where the element is produced through a bending process.
[E06B 3/67317] Filling of hollow spacer elements with absorbants; Closing off the spacers thereafter -- The entry covers methods for inserting absorbent material into hollow spacer elements of window sashes, door leaves or similar closing components and then sealing the spacers to lock the material inside, providing moisture control, insulation or fire-protection benefits.
[E06B 3/67321] Covering spacer elements, e.g. with sealants -- The subclass E06B 3/67321 covers spacer components that are applied to window sashes, door leaves or similar closure elements and are provided with sealants to improve sealing, insulation or weather-tightness. It relates to fixed or movable closures such as doors, windows and blinds where the spacer is covered by a sealing material.
[E06B 3/67326] Assembling spacer elements with the panes -- The subclass covers techniques for installing spacer elements that separate glazing panes within window sashes, door leaves or similar closures. It includes methods and devices for positioning, fixing and securing spacers between panes during assembly to maintain a uniform gap and seal.
[E06B 3/6733] by applying, e.g. extruding, a ribbon of hardenable material on or between the panes -- The code E06B 3/6733 covers methods for producing window sashes, door leaves or similar closure elements by extruding a ribbon of curable material onto or between the glazing panes. The technique is used to create sealing, reinforcing or decorative layers during manufacture of fixed or movable openings.
[E06B 3/67334] by soldering; Preparing the panes therefor -- The subclass E06B 3/67334 covers methods of preparing panes for windows, doors or similar closures by soldering, i.e., joining glass pieces with a soldered seal or joint. It is part of the fixed-construction group for doors, windows, shutters and related closure elements.
[E06B 3/67339] Working the edges of already assembled units -- The code E06B 3/67339 covers processes applied to the perimeters of pre-assembled door, window or similar closure units, such as trimming, sealing, applying gaskets, glazing or mounting hardware to the edges after the main frame and leaf have been built. It includes finishing operations that secure the edges and prepare the unit for installation in a building or vehicle.
[E06B 3/67343] Filling or covering the edges with synthetic hardenable substances -- The CPC subclass E06B 3/67343 concerns the filling or covering of the edges of window sashes, door leaves or similar closure elements with synthetic hardenable substances such as resins, sealants or adhesives. This treatment is aimed at improving sealing, insulation, durability or structural integrity of fixed or movable openings.
[E06B 3/67347] by extrusion techniques -- This subclass covers the production of window sashes, door leaves and similar fixed or movable closure elements by extrusion processes, describing methods and apparatus for forming these components from extruded profiles of metal, plastic or composite material for use in doors, windows, shutters or comparable enclosures.
[E06B 3/67352] by forming techniques -- This subclass covers the manufacturing of window sashes, door leaves and similar closure elements by forming methods such as extrusion, roll-forming, stamping or molding. It relates to fixed or movable closures for openings in buildings, vehicles or fences, focusing on the technique used to shape the component rather than its material or function.
[E06B 3/67356] Covering the edges with bands or profiles -- E06B 3/67356 relates to fixing edge coverings on the peripheral parts of window sashes, door leaves or similar closing elements, using bands, profiles or comparable trim. The purpose is to protect, seal or enhance the appearance of the closure's margins.
[E06B 3/6736] Heat treatment  for soldering  E06B3/67334 -- This subclass covers thermal processes applied to window sashes, door leaves or similar closure elements to prepare them for soldering, including pre-heating, annealing or other heat-treatment steps during manufacture or repair of fixed or movable openings in buildings, vehicles or similar enclosures.
[E06B 3/67365] Transporting or handling panes, spacer frames or units during assembly  E06B3/67304  -  E06B3/67339  take precedence -- The code covers methods, devices or apparatus for moving or positioning glass panes, spacer frames or pre-assembled window units while assembling doors, windows or similar closures. It includes conveyors, lifts, clamps, jigs and robotic handlers used during factory or on-site installation.
[E06B 3/67369] Layout of the assembly streets -- E06B 3/67369 covers the spatial planning of workstations or assembly lines used to fabricate and fit window sashes, door leaves and similar closure elements. It addresses the layout of streets or aisles within factories or workshops to optimise material flow and worker access during construction of fixed or movable closures.
[E06B 3/67373] Rotating panes, spacer frames or units -- The classification E06B 3/67373 covers window or door elements that rotate within a frame, including panes that swing, pivot or turn, and the spacer frames or modular units that support such rotating glass or panel sections. It applies to constructions where the closure element is mounted to allow angular movement for ventilation, cleaning or aesthetic purposes.
[E06B 3/67382] Transport of panes or units without touching the bottom edge -- It refers to methods or devices for moving a window pane, glass unit or similar closure element by gripping or supporting it away from its lower edge, such as using suction cups, clamps or rollers, allowing the element to be lifted, slid or positioned without contacting the bottom edge, typically during installation, replacement or cleaning.
[E06B 3/67386] Presses; Clamping means holding the panes during assembly -- The CPC subclass E06B 3/67386 covers devices such as presses or clamping mechanisms that hold glass panes while they are being assembled into window sashes, door leaves or similar fixed or movable closures. It pertains to methods of securing panes during manufacturing of doors, windows, shutters or roller blinds.
[E06B 3/67391] Apparatus travelling around the periphery of the pane or the unit -- The code E06B 3/67391 designates devices that move along the outer edge of a window pane or glazed unit, such as tracks, rollers or carriers that guide opening or closing elements around the periphery. It falls under the class for fixed or movable closures such as doors, windows and shutters.
[E06B 3/677] Evacuating or filling the gap between the panes -- E06B 3/677 covers devices or processes for removing or inserting material into the space between the panes of a window or similar closure element. It includes vacuum or gas filling techniques, pressure equalisation and sealing methods used to improve thermal or acoustic performance.
[E06B 3/6775] Evacuating or filling the gap during assembly -- E06B 3/6775 covers methods and apparatus for removing air or introducing sealing material into the cavity that forms between a frame and a window sash, door leaf or similar closure element during its installation. It includes techniques such as suction, pressurising, or dispensing of putty, foam or other fillers to achieve a tight seal as the component is fitted.
[E06B 3/68] Window bars -- E06B 3/68 relates to window bars, which are protective grilles or metal fittings installed on windows to deter intrusion, prevent break-ins, and enhance safety. The bars may be fixed or removable and are used in residential, commercial or industrial buildings to secure openings.
[E06B 3/685] False glazing bars  E06B3/6604  takes precedence
[E06B 3/70] Door leaves -- E06B 3/70 designates the door leaf, the movable panel or section of a door that swings, slides or folds to close an opening in a building, vehicle, fence or similar enclosure. It is a sub-category of fixed constructions for doors, windows, shutters and other closures.
[E06B 3/7001] Coverings therefor; Door leaves imitating traditional raised panel doors, e.g. engraved or embossed surfaces, with trim strips applied to the surfaces -- The CPC subclass E06B 3/7001 concerns decorative coverings applied to door leaves that mimic traditional raised-panel doors, for example surfaces that are engraved, embossed or otherwise textured and finished with trim strips. It falls under fixed constructions for doors, windows and similar closures.
[E06B 3/7003] consisting of several adjacent similar elements, e.g. planks, without outer covering panels -- The subclass E06B 3/7003 covers closures such as window sashes or door leaves that are formed from a series of adjacent, similar elements like planks and have no outer covering panels. It applies to fixed or movable openings built from a continuous array of such components without additional cladding.
[E06B 3/7005] without a separate supporting frame -- E06B 3/7005 covers window sashes, door leaves and similar closure elements that are formed without a distinct supporting frame, meaning the frame is integrated into the element itself. It applies to fixed constructions such as doors, windows, shutters and roller blinds where the closing part is self-supporting.
[E06B 3/7007] Door leaves with curved, e.g. cylindrical or oval cross-section  E06B3/7001  takes precedence -- E06B 3/7007 classifies door leaves that have a curved cross-section, such as cylindrical or oval shapes, within the broader group of fixed constructions for doors, windows, shutters and similar closures. It is a subdivision of E06B 3/7001, which covers curved-section door leaves in general.
[E06B 3/7009] Door leaves with two similar parts placed one above the other, pivoting separately -- This CPC entry (E06B 3/7009) covers door leaves made of two similar parts placed one above the other, each able to pivot separately for opening or closing. It belongs to the broader group for fixed or movable closures such as doors, windows, shutters and blinds.
[E06B 3/7015] characterised by the filling between two external panels  fireproof doors  E06B5/16 ; noise insulating doors  E06B5/20 -- E06B 3/7015 designates fixed or movable closures such as doors and windows that use a filling material between two external panels, a construction employed for fire-proof or noise-insulating performance.
[E06B 3/7017] of grating type -- E06B 3/7017 classifies window sashes, door leaves and comparable closing elements that are constructed as gratings, meaning they consist of a framework of intersecting bars or strips allowing light and air to pass while providing a barrier. This falls under the broader category of fixed or movable closures for openings in buildings, vehicles, fences or similar enclosures.
[E06B 3/72] consisting of frame and panels -- This subclass covers fixed or movable closures for openings such as windows and doors where the closure is formed by a frame that holds one or more panels. It includes sashes, door leaves and similar elements whose construction consists essentially of a supporting frame combined with removable or integral panels.
[E06B 3/721] with panels on one lateral side of the frame only -- This subgroup concerns doors, windows, shutters or similar closures whose panels are provided on only one side of the surrounding frame. It includes constructions where the movable leaf or sash carries panels solely on the lateral side facing the interior or exterior, while the opposite side remains plain. It falls under fixed constructions for building or vehicle openings.
[E06B 3/723] at least one side of the frame consisting solely of a bent panel edge -- The code E06B 3/723 covers window or door frames in which at least one side of the frame is made exclusively from a panel whose edge has been bent to form the frame side, eliminating a separate moulding or profile. This design is applied to fixed or movable closures such as windows, doors, or similar openings, providing a simplified construction where the bent panel edge serves as the structural and sealing element.
[E06B 3/725] with separate hollow frames, e.g. foam-filled -- The subclass concerns doors, windows, shutters or similar closures that incorporate a separate hollow frame, for example a profile filled with foam, to improve insulation or structural performance. The hollow cavity is distinct from the surrounding framing material.
[E06B 3/726] of metal -- The subclass E06B 3/726 covers metal components used as closing elements for openings, such as window sashes, door leaves and similar fixed or movable parts. It applies to constructions where the closure element is made primarily of metal, including doors, windows, shutters and related fixtures in buildings, vehicles or other enclosures.
[E06B 3/728] of sheet metal -- The subclass E06B 3/728 covers window sashes, door leaves and similar fixed or movable closure elements that are made of sheet metal. It applies to constructions where the primary material for the closing part is sheet metal, regardless of the overall building or vehicle context.
[E06B 3/74] with wooden panels -- E06B 3/74 covers fixed or movable closures for building openings - such as window sashes, door leaves or similar elements - that incorporate wooden panels. It is a sub-category of the broader groups for fixed constructions and doors, windows, shutters or roller blinds in general.
[E06B 3/76] with metal panels -- E06B 3/76 covers window sashes, door leaves or similar closure elements that incorporate metal panels into their construction, such as frames, cladding or infill panels made of steel, aluminium or other metals, whether the closures are fixed or movable.
[E06B 3/78] with panels of plastics -- E06B 3/78 classifies window sashes, door leaves and similar closure elements that are constructed with panels of plastics. It falls under fixed constructions for doors, windows, shutters and other openings, covering designs where the primary material of the panel is a polymer or composite plastic.
[E06B 3/80] flexible -- E06B 3/80 covers flexible elements used as window sashes, door leaves or similar closing parts for openings, including bendable or deformable panels such as flexible glass, polymer sheets, fabrics or other material that can be shaped to seal doors, windows, shutters or roller blinds.
[E06B 3/805] folding sideways  doors of the bellows type  E06B3/94 -- This subclass covers doors that fold sideways in a bellows-type arrangement, with hinged panels that collapse like an accordion when opened, providing a compact closure for openings in buildings or vehicles. It is part of the fixed constructions category for doors, windows, shutters and similar closures.
[E06B 3/82] Flush doors, i.e. with completely flat surface -- E06B 3/82 covers flush doors, which have a completely flat surface and lack raised panels or mouldings, forming a smooth, uninterrupted façade as part of fixed or movable closures in buildings, vehicles or similar enclosures. These doors are typically integrated with the surrounding frame to provide a level, planar appearance.
[E06B 3/822] with an internal foursided frame -- This subclass covers window sashes, door leaves or similar closing elements that incorporate an internal frame formed by four sides surrounding the opening. The inner four-sided frame provides structural support and may hold glazing, seals or hardware within the closure assembly.
[E06B 3/825] with a wooden frame -- This subclass covers window sashes, door leaves or similar closing elements that are built with a wooden frame. It is part of the fixed constructions category for doors, windows, shutters and comparable closures where the frame material is wood.
[E06B 3/827] of metal without an internal frame, e.g. with exterior panels substantially of metal -- E06B 3/827 pertains to metal window sashes, door leaves or similar closure elements that are constructed without an internal frame, relying instead on exterior panels that are substantially metal. It falls under the hierarchy of fixed constructions for doors, windows, shutters or roller blinds in general.
[E06B 3/84] of plywood -- E06B 3/84 designates components such as window sashes, door-leaves or similar closure elements that are constructed primarily from plywood. It falls within the fixed constructions group for doors, windows, shutters and related closures in buildings and vehicles.
[E06B 3/86] of plastics -- E06B 3/86 covers window sashes, door leaves and similar closure elements that are made primarily of plastic materials. The code applies to fixed or movable openings in buildings, vehicles or other enclosures where the closing parts are constructed from polymers rather than metal or wood.
[E06B 3/88] Edge-protecting devices for door leaves -- This subclass of the CPC (E06B 3/88) covers devices that protect the edges of door leaves, such as protective strips, caps, guards or similar fittings intended to prevent damage, wear, injury or improve sealing of the door. The devices may be used on doors in buildings, vehicles, fences or other enclosures.
[E06B 3/90] Revolving doors -- E06B 3/90 covers revolving doors, which are rotating assemblies of door leaves that provide continuous access while minimizing air exchange and space usage. The classification includes structural components, mounting arrangements and operational mechanisms for such doors in buildings and other enclosures.
[E06B 3/903] consisting of arcuate wings revolving around a parallel axis situated outside the wing, e.g. a cylindrical wing revolving around its axis  E06B3/906  takes precedence -- E06B 3/903 covers fixed or movable building closures such as window sashes or door leaves that incorporate arcuate wing elements which rotate around an axis that runs parallel to and outside the wing, for example a cylindrical wing turning about its own shaft. The more specific subclass E06B 3/906 supersedes it.
[E06B 3/906] combining radially extending wings and separately movable arcuate wings -- This subgroup covers window, door or similar closure elements that integrate both radially extending wings and independently movable curved wings, allowing combined pivot and swing motions for opening or sealing an aperture. It applies to designs where straight-acting sections are joined with arcuate, hinged sections to achieve varied opening geometries.
[E06B 3/92] Doors or windows extensible when set in position -- E06B 3/92 covers doors or windows that can be extended - such as sliding, folding, or telescoping panels - after they have been positioned in an opening, allowing the opening to be widened or sealed more tightly. The classification includes mechanisms that permit the closure element to be lengthened or retracted while remaining mounted.
[E06B 3/921] with several parts mounted telescopically inside each other -- E06B 3/921 covers window sashes, door leaves or comparable closure elements that consist of several parts mounted telescopically inside each other, enabling the member to extend or collapse within a single opening.
[E06B 3/922] with several wings opening horizontally towards the same side of the opening and each closing a separate part of the opening  E06B3/921  and  E06B3/928  take precedence
[E06B 3/924] positioned in one plane when closed -- The subclass E06B 3/924 concerns window sashes, door leaves or similar closing elements that, when in the closed position, lie flat in a single plane. It includes constructions where the movable part is designed to be coplanar with the surrounding frame, such as flat-pane windows, flush doors or analogous closures.
[E06B 3/925] with several wings opening vertically towards the same side of the opening and each closing a separate part of the opening  E06B3/921  and  E06B3/928  take precedence -- The code E06B 3/925 classifies window sashes, door leaves or similar fixed-construction elements that have multiple wings opening vertically toward the same side of an opening, each wing closing a distinct portion of that opening; this subclass follows the broader groups E06B 3/921 and E06B 3/928.
[E06B 3/927] positioned in one plane when closed -- The code E06B 3/927 covers fixed or movable closure elements such as window sashes, door leaves or similar parts that, when closed, lie in a single plane with the surrounding frame or surface, creating a flush or planar sealing arrangement. This includes designs where the closing member is positioned to be coplanar with the surrounding construction, minimizing gaps or protrusion.
[E06B 3/928] Doors or windows of the lazy tongs type -- Lazy-tongs doors or windows consist of two hinged leaves that fold together and slide on a rail, allowing a compact opening that occupies little space when retracted. The leaves interlock and can be pushed or pulled to open, commonly used where interior clearance is limited. This type falls under the fixed constructions category for building closures.
[E06B 3/94] Doors of the bellows type -- E06B 3/94 classifies doors that are constructed as bellows, i.e. accordion-like flexible panels that fold or expand to close an opening. These doors are a type of fixed or movable closure for buildings, vehicles or similar enclosures and are used where a compact, flexible sealing solution is required. The code falls under the broader category of fixed constructions for doors, windows and shutters.
[E06B 3/96] Corner joints or edge joints for windows, doors, or the like frames or wings -- The entry E06B 3/96 covers the design and construction of corner or edge joints that connect the frame or wing members of windows, doors and similar closures. It includes methods for assembling, reinforcing or sealing the joints where frame sections meet at right-angles or adjoining edges.
[E06B 3/9604] Welded or soldered joints  E06B3/988 ,  E06B3/99  take precedence; welding of plastics  B29C65/00 , soldering or welding of metals  B23K -- The CPC subclass E06B 3/9604 covers the use of welded or soldered joints in window sashes, door leaves and similar fixed or movable closures. It relates to methods of joining metal or plastic elements of doors, windows and comparable constructions, and references more specific welding and soldering sub-classes such as E06B3/988, E06B3/99, B29C65/00 and B23K.
[E06B 3/9608] Mitre joints -- E06B 3/9608 Mitre joints covers the design, manufacture and assembly of mitre-type angled joints used in window sashes, door leaves and similar closure elements for buildings, vehicles or other enclosures. It specifies how two members are cut and fitted together at a right angle to form a corner, a common feature of fixed or movable doors, windows and shutters.
[E06B 3/9612] by filling  in situ  the hollow ends of the abutted frame members with a hardenable substance -- E06B 3/9612 covers the technique of filling the hollow ends of abutted frame members, such as window sashes or door leaves, with a hardenable substance while in place to create a sealed joint. This method is used in the manufacture of fixed or movable closures for openings in buildings, vehicles or similar enclosures.
[E06B 3/9616] characterised by the sealing at the junction of the frame members  E06B3/9632  takes precedence -- The subclass E06B 3/9616 covers window sashes, door leaves or similar closing elements that are characterised by a seal applied at the joint where frame members meet, ensuring a weather-tight or acoustic barrier. This feature is superseded by the more specific subclass E06B 3/9632.
[E06B 3/962] Mitre joints -- E06B 3/962 covers the use of mitre joints in the construction of window sashes, door leaves and similar fixed or movable closures. It classifies devices where two members are cut at complementary angles and fitted together to form a tight corner, commonly employed in frames for doors, windows, shutters and comparable enclosures.
[E06B 3/9624] with means specially adapted for aligning the frontal surfaces of adjacent frame member ends -- The subclass E06B 3/9624 covers window or door frames that incorporate special aligning devices for the frontal faces of adjoining frame members, such as pins, guides, sealing strips or other mechanisms that ensure precise positioning and smooth operation of sashes, leaves or similar closures.
[E06B 3/9628] Mitre joints concealed to look like butt joints -- The code E06B 3/9628 covers designs where the joint between two framing members such as window sashes or door leaves is a mitre joint that is hidden so that it appears as a simple butt joint, giving a seamless visual finish while retaining the strength of a mitre connection.
[E06B 3/9632] between a jamb and the threshold or sill of window or door frames -- The CPC entry E06B 3/9632 covers elements located between a jamb and the threshold or sill of window or door frames, such as sealing strips, guide rails, weather-proofing or insulating components that bridge that gap and improve the fixed closure's function.
[E06B 3/9636] for frame members having longitudinal screw receiving channels -- E06B 3/9636 covers frame members that incorporate longitudinal channels designed to receive screws, used in sashes, door leaves or similar closures for windows and doors. It falls under the fixed constructions group for doors, windows, shutters or roller blinds and specifies the fastening arrangement of the frame.
[E06B 3/964] using separate connection pieces, e.g. T-connection pieces -- The CPC subclass E06B 3/964 concerns fixed constructions such as window sashes or door leaves that are assembled by means of separate connection pieces, for example T-shaped connectors, to join the elements that close openings in buildings, vehicles or similar enclosures.
[E06B 3/9641] part of which remains visible  E06B3/9647  takes precedence -- E06B 3/9641 covers doors, windows or similar closure elements whose design leaves a portion of the component visible, such as transparent panels, cut-outs or open sections that remain exposed when the closure is installed. This group is overridden by the more specific subgroup E06B 3/9647.
[E06B 3/9642] Butt type joints with at least one frame member cut off square; T-shape joints  E06B3/9641 ,  E06B3/9644 ,  E06B3/9647 ,  E06B3/9649 ,  E06B3/968  take precedence -- The subclass E06B 3/9642 relates to fixed or movable closures such as windows, doors and shutters where the joining method uses butt-type joints with at least one frame member cut off square or employs T-shaped joints, covering the way sashes, door leaves or similar elements are connected to frames.
[E06B 3/9643] for frame members being in one line with each other -- This subclass covers fixed or movable closures whose frame members are positioned in a single straight line, such as window sashes, door leaves, or similar elements where side rails, muntins or profiles align collinearly. It is part of the broader group for fixed constructions under E06B 3/96.
[E06B 3/9644] L-shaped corner pieces having two articulated or flexible joined legs; Corner joints with variable angle -- E06B 3/9644 covers L-shaped corner pieces used in window sashes, door leaves or similar fixed or movable closures, where the two legs are joined by an articulation or flexible element that permits the angle between them to be varied. Such adjustable corner joints enable custom fitting of frames in buildings or vehicles.
[E06B 3/9645] Mitre joints  E06B3/9641 ,  E06B3/9644 ,  E06B3/9647 ,  E06B3/9649 ,  E06B3/968  take precedence -- The CPC entry E06B 3/9645 classifies mitre joints used in fixed or movable closures such as window sashes, door leaves, shutters and similar elements that seal building or vehicle openings. It covers the design and construction of angled joints, typically 45°, where two members meet to form a corner, and takes precedence over related sub-entries like E06B 3/9641, 3/9644, 3/9647, 3/9649 and 3/968.
[E06B 3/9646] using two similar connecting pieces each connected with one or the frame members and drawn together at the joint -- E06B 3/9646 describes a joining technique for window sashes, door leaves or similar closure elements in which two identical connecting pieces are each fastened to the adjoining frame members and then drawn together at the joint, creating a symmetrical, pull-together fastening for fixed or movable openings. This approach simplifies assembly and enhances the structural integrity of doors, windows, shutters or comparable closures.
[E06B 3/9647] the connecting piece being part of or otherwise linked to the window or door fittings -- This subclass covers window or door assemblies where a connecting element such as a hinge, pivot rod or linking bracket is formed as an integral part of, or is mechanically linked to, the surrounding fittings. It includes designs in which the connector is built into the frame, sash, leaf or the hardware that secures the closure.
[E06B 3/9648] Mitre joints -- The subclass E06B 3/9648 classifies the use of mitre joints for assembling window sashes, door leaves and similar fixed or movable closures. It includes methods of cutting and fitting two members at a 45-degree angle to form a tight corner, often reinforced with fasteners, adhesives or profiles.
[E06B 3/9649] Tie rods spanning the whole width or height of the frame; Straps encircling the frame -- Tie rods that extend across the entire width or height of a window, door or similar frame are used to reinforce the opening and maintain its shape under load. The same function can be achieved with straps that encircle the frame, providing uniform tension and resistance to deformation.
[E06B 3/968] characterised by the way the connecting pieces are fixed in or on the frame members -- This sub-group covers window sashes, door leaves and similar closure elements where the invention is defined by the specific manner in which the connecting pieces are attached to or embedded in the frame members, such as by clips, brackets, screws, welds or adhesives.
[E06B 3/9681] by press fit or adhesion -- The class E06B 3/9681 relates to fixed or movable building closures such as window sashes, door leaves and similar elements that are joined to their frames or each other by a press-fit or adhesive bond rather than hinges or fasteners. It falls under the broader hierarchy of fixed constructions, doors, windows, shutters and roller blinds in general.
[E06B 3/9682] Mitre joints -- E06B 3/9682 covers mitre joints used in the fabrication of window sashes, door leaves and similar fixed-or-movable closures. The code includes design and construction of joints where two members meet at an angle, typically 45°, providing a neat, strong connection in building openings.
[E06B 3/9684] by hooking protrusions on the connecting piece in openings of the frame member, e.g. by snap-locking  E06B3/972 ,  E06B3/976 ,  E06B3/98  take precedence -- This subgroup covers window sashes, door leaves or similar closure elements that are attached to a frame by hooking protrusions on the connecting piece, such as snap-locking mechanisms, and falls under the broader category of fixed or movable closures for openings in buildings, vehicles or similar enclosures.
[E06B 3/9685] Mitre joints -- E06B 3/9685 designates inventions that employ mitre joints in the construction of window sashes, door leaves, or comparable closing components. The classification belongs to the CPC group for fixed constructions, covering doors, windows, shutters and roller blinds in buildings, vehicles, fences and similar enclosures. It is used to index patents that describe how mitre-jointed members improve strength, alignment or sealing of opening closures.
[E06B 3/9687] with screws blocking the connecting piece inside or on the frame member  E06B3/972 ,  E06B3/976  take precedence -- This CPC subclass covers fixed or movable closures such as window sashes or door leaves in which the connecting piece is secured by screws that block it inside the frame or on the frame member. The feature is defined in code E06B 3/9687, but more specific subclasses E06B3/972 and E06B3/976 have priority.
[E06B 3/9688] Mitre joints -- E06B 3/9688 relates to mitre joints, the angled cuts that join two members at a corner, as applied to window sashes, door leaves and similar fixed or movable closures in buildings, vehicles or other enclosures.
[E06B 3/972] by increasing the cross-section of the connecting pieces, e.g. by expanding the connecting pieces with wedges -- E06B 3/972 concerns the design of window sashes, door leaves or similar closure elements in which the strength of the connecting pieces is increased by enlarging their cross-section, for example by inserting wedges to expand the members.
[E06B 3/9725] Mitre joints -- This subclass covers the use of mitre joints as the joining method for window sashes, door leaves, or similar fixed or movable closures in buildings, vehicles, fences or comparable enclosures. It includes designs where intersecting members are cut at complementary angles to create a tight, often decorative, joint.
[E06B 3/976] by deformation of the frame members -- The code E06B 3/976 covers window sashes, door leaves or similar closing elements whose frame members are shaped or adjusted by deforming the frame itself, rather than by adding separate hinges or actuators. It applies to fixed or movable closures for building, vehicle or fence openings that achieve movement through elastic or plastic deformation of the frame.
[E06B 3/9765] Mitre joints -- E06B 3/9765 covers mitre joints employed in the construction of window sashes, door leaves and similar fixed or movable closures, detailing the geometry and securing methods for joining angular members to form a tight, often beveled, corner connection. This subclass falls under the broader category of fixed constructions for doors, windows, shutters and related building openings.
[E06B 3/98] the connecting pieces being specially adapted for drawing the frame members towards each other -- The subclass E06B 3/98 covers devices in which the joining elements are specially shaped to draw the parts of a frame toward one another, enabling the closure of openings such as window sashes or door leaves. Examples include pull-bars, tie-rods, draw-latches and similar mechanisms that actively bring the frame members together.
[E06B 3/982] Mitre joints -- E06B 3/982 pertains to the use of mitre joints in the assembly of movable or fixed closures such as window sashes, door leaves and similar elements. The classification covers the design and construction of these joints where two members meet at an angle, typically 45° or 90°, to provide a crisp corner or seal in doors, windows, shutters or other building openings.
[E06B 3/984] specially adapted for frame members of wood or other material worked in a similar way -- E06B 3/984 classifies window sashes, door leaves and comparable closing components that are specially adapted for use with frame members made of wood or other material worked in a similar way, covering design features that accommodate the characteristics of such frames.
[E06B 3/9845] Mitre joints -- The code E06B 3/9845 classifies mitre joints used in window sashes, door leaves and similar closure elements, describing their design, manufacture and assembly for fixed or movable openings in buildings, vehicles or fences.
[E06B 3/988] specially adapted for sheet metal frame members with an open U-shaped cross-section -- The entry covers window sashes, door leaves and similar closure elements that are specifically designed to fit sheet-metal frame members having an open U-shaped cross-section. Such components are adapted to accommodate the shape and structural characteristics of the metal framing, typically used in lightweight or prefabricated constructions.
[E06B 3/9885] Mitre joints -- The code E06B 3/9885 designates mitre joints employed in fixed or movable closures such as window sashes, door leaves and similar elements that seal openings in buildings, vehicles or fences. It specifies the use of the angled joint to connect intersecting frame parts, ensuring a neat, secure fit.
[E06B 3/99] for continuous frame members crossing each other with out interruption -- E06B 3/99 pertains to doors, windows, shutters or similar closures in which continuous frame members intersect one another without any interruption or joint, forming the supporting structure of sashes, leaves or comparable elements. It covers designs where the crossing members are integral and uninterrupted.
[E06B 5/00] Doors, windows, or like closures for special purposes -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Doors windows or like closures and border construction therefore which can readily be recognised as having a special purpose or function, e.g. fireproof doors, bullet-proof windows.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Insulated compound frames specially adapted for sliding doors or windows -> E06B3/26347 - Flood barriers -> E06B9/00 - Elevator doors -> B66B13/30 - Closures in false ceilings -> E04B9/00 - Safety windows in connection with machinery -> F16P1/02 - Electric heating systems in doors and windows -> F24D13/026 - Pressure resistant sight glasses -> G02B7/005 - Door or windows for electrical apparatus -> H02B1/28 , H05K5/00
[E06B 5/003] Storm doors; Combination-screen-and-storm-doors -- The class covers door assemblies installed in front of an external door to protect against weather while allowing ventilation. It includes units that combine a protective solid panel with a mesh screen that can be opened or closed, commonly used as storm doors.
[E06B 5/006] for furniture  E06B3/00 ,  E06B9/115  take precedence -- This subclass covers doors, windows, shutters or similar closures that are integrated into furniture rather than into building structures. It applies to fixed or movable closures used as decorative or functional elements of cabinets, wardrobes, desks or other pieces of furniture, and it overrides the broader groups E06B3/00 and E06B9/115.
[E06B 5/01] Trap-doors -- The CPC entry E06B 5/01 covers trap-doors, which are hinged or sliding closures that can be opened to provide access to an opening below the normal floor level. They are used in buildings, vehicles or similar enclosures where a temporary or concealed passage is required for service, ventilation or emergency egress.
[E06B 5/02] for out-buildings or cellars -- The subclass E06B 5/02 covers doors, windows, shutters, blinds or similar closures that are specifically designed for out-buildings or cellars, such as garages, sheds, barns, basements and underground rooms. It includes any fixed or movable closure adapted to the particular conditions of these ancillary structures.
[E06B 5/025] Provisional closures, e.g. temporary security doors -- E06B 5/025 covers provisional closures such as temporary security doors used to protect openings during construction, maintenance or emergencies. These are non-permanent barriers that can be quickly installed and removed, providing short-term protection for doors, windows or other building apertures.
[E06B 5/04] with fixed wooden frames -- This subclass covers doors, windows, shutters and similar closures that are built with permanent wooden frames. It applies to fixed constructions where the frame is integral and not removable, used in buildings, vehicles, fences and comparable enclosures.
[E06B 5/06] with fixed metal frames -- E06B 5/06 pertains to doors, windows, shutters, roller blinds and similar closures that incorporate fixed metal frames, covering fixed constructions for openings in buildings, vehicles, fences or comparable enclosures. It falls under the broader category of fixed constructions and the sub-group for closures designed for special purposes.
[E06B 5/08] with fixed frames made of concrete, stone-like material or plastics -- The subclass E06B 5/08 covers doors, windows, shutters, blinds and similar fixed or movable closures whose frames are permanently made from concrete, stone-like material or plastics, intended for use in buildings, vehicles, fences or comparable enclosures. It is part of the broader group for fixed constructions and specialized closure devices.
[E06B 5/10] for protection against air-raid or other war-like action -- E06B 5/10 covers doors, windows, shutters, roller blinds and similar closures that are specially constructed to provide protection against air-raid or other war-like actions, such as reinforced or blast-resistant panels used in buildings, vehicles or other enclosures.
[E06B 5/103] safety foils therefor -- E06B 5/103 pertains to safety foils used on doors, windows, shutters, blinds and similar fixed or movable closures, providing protective layers that enhance resistance to impact, weathering, fire or unauthorized entry.
[E06B 5/106] Frames for bullet-proof windows  transparent bullet-proof laminates  F41H5/0407 -- E06B 5/106 covers the design and construction of frames that support bullet-proof glazing, including transparent ballistic laminates used in doors, windows or other fixed openings. The classification applies to protective enclosures where the frame must accommodate high-strength, transparent armor panels.
[E06B 5/11] against burglary -- E06B 5/11 covers doors, windows and similar closures that incorporate features intended to deter or prevent burglary, such as reinforced frames, special locking devices, burglar-resistant glazing and other protective measures.
[E06B 5/113] Arrangements at the edges of the wings, e.g. with door guards to prevent the insertion of prying tools  protection of the locking region by preventing insertion of a tool  E05B17/2003 ; means to prevent forced opening by attack, tampering or jimmying of lock  E05B17/2084 ; anti-tamper devices arranged on or near the hinges  E05D11/0018 -- E06B 5/113 relates to protective arrangements on the edges of door, window or similar wings, such as guards or barriers that prevent prying tools from reaching the locking region, thereby deterring forced opening, tampering or jimmying; it also includes anti-tamper devices positioned on or near hinges to enhance security.
[E06B 5/116] Arrangements preventing the removal of glazing panels -- The code E06B 5/116 covers devices or structures that inhibit the removal of glazing panels from doors, windows, or other fixed or movable closures. It includes locking mechanisms, interlocks, sealing systems or any arrangement that secures glass or transparent inserts against intentional or accidental disassembly.
[E06B 5/12] against air pressure, explosion, or gas -- This subclass covers doors, windows, shutters, blinds or similar closures that are specifically engineered to resist differential air pressure, blast loads or the passage of gases. It includes designs that provide sealing, reinforcement or pressure-equalising features for explosion-proof or pressure-resistant applications.
[E06B 5/125] Closures for relieving excess pressure inside the building -- E06B 5/125 refers to doors, windows, shutters, or similar closures that are designed to relieve excess pressure inside a building, such as pressure-relief doors, vents or blast-resistant openings that allow controlled air flow while maintaining security and weather protection.
[E06B 5/14] Gasproof doors or similar closures -- E06B 5/14 covers doors, windows or similar closures specifically designed to be gas-tight, preventing the passage of gases, vapours or fumes through the opening. It includes sealing mechanisms, gasketing and other features that enable the enclosure to maintain a controlled atmosphere in buildings, vehicles or other structures.
[E06B 5/16] Fireproof doors or similar closures -- The class includes doors, windows, shutters and comparable closures that are constructed to maintain their integrity during a fire, such as fire-rated doors, fire shutters, fire-proof gates and other fire-resistant openings for buildings, vehicles or similar enclosures. It covers any fixed or movable closure specially designed to limit flame spread and protect adjoining spaces.
[E06B 5/161] Profile members therefor -- E06B 5/161 concerns the profile members employed in doors, windows and comparable fixed or movable closures, including extruded, moulded or cast sections that provide framing, reinforcement or sealing for these openings.
[E06B 5/162] Fireproof doors having windows or other openings, e.g. for permitting ventilation or escape -- The subclass E06B 5/162 covers fire-resistant doors that include windows, vents or other openings designed to provide ventilation or a means of escape. It is part of the broader group for fixed or movable closures such as doors, windows and shutters used in buildings or vehicles.
[E06B 5/164] Sealing arrangements between the door or window and its frame, e.g. intumescent seals specially adapted therefor -- The code covers sealing arrangements that join a door, window or similar closure to its surrounding frame, including specialised seals such as intumescent devices that expand when heated. It applies to constructions where the seal provides fire resistance, weather-tightness or acoustic insulation.
[E06B 5/165] Fireproof windows  layered glass panes with expansion layers for fire protection  B32B17/10311 -- The E06B 5/165 subclass covers fire-resistant windows that use layered glass with interstitial expansion layers to keep the opening intact during a fire. It falls under fixed constructions for doors, windows and similar closures, emphasizing designs that provide fire protection through specialized glazing.
[E06B 5/167] Devices blocking doors or windows inside its frame in case of fire -- E06B 5/167 covers fire-protective devices that block a door, window or similar opening from the inside of its frame, preventing the passage of fire and smoke. It applies to fixed or movable closures designed specifically for fire-safety purposes in buildings or vehicles.
[E06B 5/168] Shape of edges of wing and/or its frame specially adapted therefor -- E06B 5/168 concerns the particular shaping of the edges of a wing-such as a door, window or blind leaf-and its adjoining frame when these profiles are specially adapted for functional, sealing or aesthetic purposes, covering designs that modify the contour or profile of the moving closure element.
[E06B 5/18] against harmful radiation -- E06B 5/18 covers doors, windows, shutters, blinds or similar fixed or movable closures that are specially designed to protect people, equipment or interiors from harmful radiation such as UV, infrared or ionising rays, typically by incorporating shielding materials, filters or reflective surfaces. The classification applies to building and vehicle openings where radiation attenuation is a primary function.
[E06B 5/20] for insulation against noise -- E06B 5/20 pertains to doors, windows, shutters or similar closures that are designed specifically to provide acoustic insulation, incorporating features such as sound-absorbing seals, insulated glazing or dampening materials to reduce noise transmission in buildings, vehicles or other enclosures. It falls under the subgroup for special-purpose fixed or movable closures within the broader category of fixed constructions.
[E06B 5/205] windows therefor -- E06B 5/205 covers windows intended for specific applications in buildings, vehicles, fences or similar enclosures. It includes designs, configurations and accessories for windows that serve special functions such as safety, insulation, fire resistance or aerodynamic requirements. The code is used to classify patents related to such purpose-specific window constructions.
[E06B 7/00] Special arrangements or measures in connection with doors or windows -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Special arrangements or measures in connection with doors or windows;  Accessories which are add-ons for doors or windows, e.g. sealing arrangements, door plates, finger guards, peep holes;  Ventilation arrangements which are part of the door or window construction  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Arrangements against burglary at the edges of the wings -> E06B5/113 - Screening or similar protective devices -> E06B9/00
[E06B 7/02] for providing ventilation, e.g. through double windows -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Airflow control members per se -> F24F13/08  **Glossary:** - Ventilation rose: Shutter with revoluble plate featuring apertures which can be aligned with apertures in window or static plate
[E06B 7/03] Ventilating devices for inserting under upwardly-sliding windows -- A ventilating device designed to be fitted beneath an upward-sliding window, enabling airflow while the window is closed. [E06B 7/04] with ventilation wings -- E06B 7/04 covers fixed or movable closures such as doors, windows, shutters or gates that incorporate ventilation wings, which are additional leafs or panels designed to admit airflow while the main closure remains closed. The code is used for special arrangements that combine the primary opening element with these ventilating extensions.
[E06B 7/06] with one ventilation wing only -- E06B 7/06 pertains to doors, windows, shutters or similar closures that incorporate a single ventilation wing as the sole means of providing airflow, describing the special arrangement where only one ventilating panel is attached to the fixed or movable closure.
[E06B 7/08] Louvre doors, windows or grilles -- It covers doors, windows or grilles made of angled slats that can be tilted to regulate light, airflow or privacy. These louvre constructions may be fixed or movable and are used in buildings, vehicles and similar enclosures as special arrangements for openings.
[E06B 7/082] with rigid or slidable lamellae -- The code E06B 7/082 covers doors, windows, shutters, blinds or similar closures that incorporate rigid or sliding lamellae, such as louvers, slatted shutters or adjustable slats used to regulate light, ventilation or privacy. It applies to any fixed or movable building or vehicle opening where the lamellae form part of the closure system.
[E06B 7/084] with rotatable lamellae -- The subclass covers doors, windows, shutters or similar openings that incorporate rotatable lamellae-adjustable slats or louvers-used to regulate light, ventilation or shading. It includes the provision of mechanisms that allow the lamellae to pivot within the frame of the closure.
[E06B 7/086] interconnected for concurrent movement -- E06B 7/086 covers arrangements where two or more doors, windows, shutters or similar closures are mechanically linked so that they move together, such as pivoting pairs that open or close simultaneously, or linked sliding elements that operate in unison. The classification includes devices that ensure coordinated operation for safety, convenience or space-saving purposes. It applies to any fixed or movable closure system where concurrent movement is achieved through interconnection.
[E06B 7/088] with facing protective grille or safety guard -- E06B 7/088 relates to fixed or movable closures such as doors, windows, shutters or roller blinds that are provided with a facing protective grille or safety guard. The grille or guard is mounted on the side of the closure that faces the interior or exterior to prevent injury or unauthorized entry. This subgroup falls under the broader category of fixed constructions for openings in buildings, vehicles or similar enclosures.
[E06B 7/09] mounted in movable wing, e.g. door
[E06B 7/092] operable in two or more distinct sets -- E06B 7/92 covers closures such as doors, windows, shutters or blinds that can be operated in two or more separate configurations, for example double-leaf doors, split-section windows or multi-position blinds that allow different opening modes. The subclass groups arrangements where the same closure can be used in distinct sets rather than a single fixed position.
[E06B 7/094] operable in sequence -- This subclass covers systems where doors, windows or similar closures are designed to operate one after another in a defined order, using mechanical interlocks, control circuits or timing devices to coordinate the sequence. It includes arrangements that provide safety, security or functional sequencing of multiple openings.
[E06B 7/096] operated or interconnected by gearing -- The subclass E06B 7/096 pertains to door, window, shutter or blind systems whose opening or closing action is achieved by gear mechanisms, including gear-linked arrangements that synchronize the movement of multiple closures. [E06B 7/098] with weather seal -- E06B 7/098 pertains to doors, windows, shutters or similar closures that incorporate a weather seal, i.e., a device or arrangement designed to prevent the passage of air, water, dust or other elements through the opening. [E06B 7/10] by special construction of the frame members -- This subgroup covers doors, windows, shutters or similar closures where the frame members are formed by a special construction, such as reinforced, insulated, hinged, sliding or otherwise uniquely shaped sections that provide particular structural, sealing or functional advantages.

[E06B 7/20] automatically withdrawn when the wing is opened -- E06B 7/20 covers mechanisms that release or withdraw a latch, lock, or other retaining device automatically as soon as a door, window or similar wing is opened, eliminating the need for manual disengagement and ensuring smooth, unobstructed operation.
[E06B 7/205] with sealing strip mounted on sill -- E06B 7/205 classifies arrangements for doors, windows, shutters or similar closures in which a sealing strip is attached to the sill to improve weather tightness. [E06B 7/21] with sealing strip movable in plane of wing -- E06B 7/21 classifies doors, windows, shutters or similar closures that incorporate a sealing strip which can move in the same plane as the wing, allowing the seal to adjust with the opening's position for improved weather-tightness or security. [E06B 7/215] with sealing strip being moved to a retracted position by elastic means, e.g. springs -- The entry covers doors, windows or similar closures where a sealing strip is drawn back to a retracted position by an elastic device such as a spring, providing automatic sealing or release during operation. [E06B 7/22] by means of elastic edgings, e.g. elastic rubber tubes -- E06B 7/22 relates to doors, windows, shutters or similar closures that incorporate elastic edgings, such as rubber tubes, to provide flexible sealing, cushioning or attachment, especially where a resilient seal is needed for fixed or movable openings in buildings, vehicles or comparable enclosures.
[E06B 7/23] Plastic, sponge rubber, or like strips or tubes -- The code E06B 7/23 covers the use of plastic, sponge-rubber or similar flexible strips or tubes as sealing, cushioning or insulating elements in doors, windows, shutters, blinds or comparable closures. It includes members that are applied to improve weather-tightness, noise reduction or impact resistance of fixed or movable building and vehicle openings.
[E06B 7/2301] without an integrally formed part for fixing the edging -- A subclass for doors, windows, shutters or similar closures where the edging or frame is attached by separate fixing elements rather than being cast or formed as a single integral piece. It covers designs that use bolts, clips, brackets or similar components to secure the perimeter, allowing retrofit or modular construction. This arrangement is distinct from profiles where the edging is molded in one continuous part.
[E06B 7/2303] hollow -- The subclass E06B 7/2303 covers doors, windows, shutters or similar closures that incorporate a hollow interior, typically to provide thermal or acoustic insulation, reduce weight or allow space for mounting hardware or services. It applies to fixed or movable constructions where the cavity is an integral part of the element's design rather than a separate insert. This classification groups such hollow-section closures within the broader category of fixed constructions.
[E06B 7/2305] with an integrally formed part for fixing the edging  E06B7/2316  takes precedence -- E06B 7/2305 covers doors, windows, shutters or similar closures that incorporate an integrally formed component specifically designed to secure the edging of the opening. It relates to special arrangements where the fixing element is part of the closure itself rather than a separate accessory, streamlining installation and improving sealing or structural integrity. This sub-class follows the broader category of fixed constructions for openings in buildings, vehicles or fences.
[E06B 7/2307] with a single sealing-line or -plane between the wing and the part co-operating with the wing -- The subclass covers fixed or movable building or vehicle openings in which the wing (door, window, shutter, etc.) meets the adjoining part along a single sealing-line or sealing-plane, providing a continuous seal between the two components.
[E06B 7/2309] with a hollow sealing part -- E06B 7/2309 pertains to doors, windows, shutters or comparable closures that incorporate a hollow sealing part, such as a cavity-filled strip or insert, to enhance insulation, sound attenuation or weather protection.
[E06B 7/231] with a solid sealing part -- E06B 7/231 covers doors, windows, shutters or similar closures that incorporate a solid sealing component to improve airtightness, water-tightness or thermal insulation. It includes any arrangement where a fixed or movable closure relies on a continuous, non-flexible seal, such as gasketed panels, fixed weather-stripping or integrated sealing frames, to achieve a hermetic or highly sealed joint.
[E06B 7/2312] with two or more sealing-lines or -planes between the wing and part co-operating with the wing -- The CPC entry E06B 7/2312 covers doors, windows, shutters or similar closures that incorporate two or more sealing lines or sealing planes between the moving wing and the adjoining stationary part. It relates to special arrangements that improve weather-tightness, sound insulation or security by providing multiple contact surfaces in the closure assembly.
[E06B 7/2314] characterised by the material -- E06B 7/2314 relates to doors, windows, shutters or roller blinds in which the distinguishing feature is the material employed, such as wood, metal, glass, polymer or composite, and includes any special arrangements or measures specifically associated with that material.
[E06B 7/2316] used as a seal between the floor and the wing -- The subgroup concerns sealing devices positioned between a floor and the wing of a door, window or similar closure to prevent draft, moisture or heat loss. It covers special arrangements for fixed or movable openings where a seal is fitted at the floor-wing junction. This is used to detail such measures in patent classifications for building closures.
[E06B 7/2318] by applying over- or under-pressure, e.g. inflatable  gasproof doors or windows  E06B5/12 ; for vehicles  B60J10/244 -- This subclass covers special arrangements for doors, windows, shutters or similar closures that use over- or under-pressure, such as inflatable or gas-proof devices, to seal an opening. It also includes pressure-based closures applied to vehicles.
[E06B 7/232] Resilient strips of hard material, e.g. metal -- E06B 7/232 covers the use of resilient strips made from hard materials such as metal on doors, windows, shutters or similar closures, providing a durable sealing or protective element that can absorb impact or maintain alignment. These strips are intended to improve airtightness, weather resistance or mechanical performance of fixed or movable building or vehicle openings.
[E06B 7/24] without separate sealing members, e.g. with labyrinth -- E06B 7/24 covers doors, windows, shutters, blinds or similar closures that achieve sealing without separate sealing members, using integrated features such as labyrinth passages, interlocking profiles or overlapping sections to create an airtight or watertight barrier.
[E06B 7/26] Rain or draught deflectors, e.g. under sliding wings -- E06B 7/26 covers devices that shield openings from rain or drafts, typically deflector strips or plates fitted beneath sliding wings, doors or windows. The arrangements are intended to prevent water ingress and air leakage in building or vehicle closures.
[E06B 7/28] Other arrangements on doors or windows, e.g. door-plates, windows adapted to carry plants, hooks for window cleaners -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Arrangements mounted on doors or windows; Finger plates; Peep-holes; Devices for speaking through; Windows in doors; Serving doors; Passing-through doors; Pet doors; Cupboards in doors; Finger plates  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Special glazing; emergency glazing; double glazing -> E06B3/66 - Edge protecting devices for door leaves -> E06B3/88 - Sporting equipment on doors -> A63B21/16
[E06B 7/285] Finger plates  escutcheons  E05B15/02
[E06B 7/30] Peep-holes -- E06B 7/30 pertains to peep-holes, small viewing apertures fitted in doors, windows or similar closures that enable observation from one side without opening the opening. The classification covers design, mounting and related measures for such surveillance or visual access features in buildings, vehicles or other enclosures.
[E06B 7/32] Serving doors -- E06B 7/32 covers serving doors, which are auxiliary or service openings that provide access for maintenance, cleaning or special functions within buildings, vehicles or similar enclosures. The code belongs to the fixed constructions group for doors, windows, shutters and related closures.
[E06B 7/34] Doors containing cupboards -- E06B 7/34 relates to doors that integrate cupboard or storage compartments within the door structure, typically used in buildings, vehicles or other enclosures. The classification includes designs where the door itself provides shelving or cabinets, allowing space-saving access and organization.
[E06B 7/36] Finger guards -- E06B 7/36 covers finger guards, safety devices fitted to doors, windows, shutters or similar openings to prevent accidental finger entrapment. It includes retractable shields, flexible strips, protective plates or other measures that block gaps while allowing normal operation.
[E06B 7/362] the gap between the door and the door frame at the hinge side being constructed in a way to remain too small or too wide to cause injury -- E06B 7/362 covers design measures that deliberately set the hinge-side clearance between a door and its frame either very narrow or unusually wide to prevent accidental contact or injury, such as reducing the risk of fingers being trapped or limiting forced entry. It relates to safety-oriented door-frame geometry within the broader fixed constructions group.
[E06B 7/367] by covering the gap between the door and the door frame at the hinge side -- This subclass addresses measures that seal the gap between a door leaf and its frame on the hinge side, including weather-stripping, seals or filler devices intended to block drafts, moisture or noise.
[E06B 9/00] Screening or protective devices for openings, with or without operating or securing mechanisms -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Screening or protective devices for wall or similar openings;  Operating or securing mechanisms for screening or protective devices;  Grilles fixed to walls, doors, or windows;  Grilles moving with doors or windows;  Walls formed as grilles;  Shutters, movable grilles, or other safety closing devices, e.g. against burglary;  Roller shutters;  Roll-type grilles;  Screens or other constructions affording protection against light, especially against sunshine;  Screens for privacy or appearance;  Lamellar or like blinds, e.g. Venetian blinds, roller blinds;  Screens for protection against insects, e.g. fly screens;  Operating, guiding or securing devices or arrangements for roll-type closures.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Wings for doors or windows, connected at their edges -> E06B3/48 - Doors or windows for protection against air-raid or other war-like action; for other protective purposes -> E06B5/10 - Curtains -> A47H - Gratings as building elements -> E04C2/24
[E06B 9/01] Grilles fixed to walls, doors, or windows -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Grilles attached to walls, doors or windows such that relative movement of grilles in relation to the supporting structure is not possible.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Emergency release of window grilles -> E05B65/1033 - Security bars for wings -> E05C19/003
[E06B 9/02] Shutters, movable grilles, or other safety closing devices, e.g. against burglary -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Louvre windows or grilles -> E06B7/08 - Lamellar blinds; Venetian blinds -> E06B9/26
[E06B 9/04] of wing type, e.g. revolving or sliding -- E06B 9/04 covers wing-type screening or protective devices for openings, such as revolving or sliding panels that can be opened, closed or angled to control light, airflow or access. These devices are used on doors, windows, shutters or similar closures in buildings, vehicles or other enclosures.
[E06B 9/06] collapsible or foldable, e.g. of the bellows or lazy-tongs type -- E06B 9/06 covers screening or protective devices for doors, windows, shutters or similar openings that are designed to collapse or fold, such as bellows-type or lazy-tongs mechanisms, allowing the closure to be compactly stored or easily retracted when not in use.
[E06B 9/0607] comprising a plurality of similar rigid closing elements movable to a storage position -- E06B 9/0607 refers to closures that use several identical rigid elements-such as slats, panels or shutters-that can be moved in unison into a compact storage position, typically for doors, windows, blinds or similar openings. The elements may be operated manually or by a mechanism and provide screening or protection when closed.
[E06B 9/0615] characterised by the closing elements -- The subclass covers screening or protective devices for building, vehicle or fence openings where the protection is provided by the closing element itself, such as shutters, blinds or screens that close directly without separate locking mechanisms.
[E06B 9/0623] Grilles -- E06B 9/0623 covers grilles that serve as screening or protective devices for openings in buildings, vehicles or similar enclosures. The grilles may be fixed or movable and can be incorporated into doors, windows, shutters, gates or other closures to prevent entry of objects or provide ventilation while restricting access.
[E06B 9/063] Bars or rods perpendicular to the closing direction -- The subclass E06B 9/063 covers protective or screening elements that consist of bars or rods arranged perpendicular to the direction in which a door, window, shutter or similar closure moves. These components are intended to block or filter entry while allowing the closure to operate normally, and are applied to fixed or movable openings in buildings, vehicles, fences or comparable enclosures.
[E06B 9/0638] Slats or panels -- The code covers slats or panels that form part of screening or protective devices for openings, such as doors, windows, shutters, blinds or gates. It includes both fixed and movable arrangements and may involve mechanisms for operating or securing the slats or panels.
[E06B 9/0646] characterised by the relative arrangement of the closing elements in the stored position -- The code E06B 9/0646 covers closures such as doors, windows, shutters or blinds whose distinguishing feature is the way the closing elements are arranged when stored, for example folded, stacked or rolled in a specific configuration. It applies to screening or protective devices for openings that are defined by this relative arrangement of the movable parts in their stored position.
[E06B 9/0653] stored side by side in the closing plane -- E06B 9/0653 covers screening or protective devices for building or vehicle openings that are stored side-by-side in the same plane as the closing element. It applies to systems where multiple panels, blinds or shutters are arranged adjacent to each other when closed, allowing compact storage within the door, window or gate frame.
[E06B 9/0661] Lazy tongue, pantograph or scissor-like systems in the plane of the opening -- The entry E06B 9/0661 covers screening or protective devices for openings that use lazy-tongue, pantograph or scissor-type mechanisms operating in the same plane as the opening, such as collapsible blinds, shutters or gating systems that fold or slide without protruding beyond the frame.
[E06B 9/0669] stored in a zig-zag arrangement -- A screening or protective device for an opening, such as a blind, shutter or similar closure, that folds or stores in a zig-zag, accordion-like arrangement when not in use. This design enables compact storage while allowing rapid deployment to cover the opening.
[E06B 9/0676] stored in a stacked configuration -- E06B 9/0676 covers screening or protective devices for openings-such as blinds, shutters or similar closures-that are stored in a stacked configuration when not in use, allowing them to be folded or piled. This sub-category falls within the broader group of fixed or movable closures for openings in buildings, vehicles, fences and similar enclosures.
[E06B 9/0692] comprising flexible sheets as closing screen -- E06B 9/0692 covers screening or protective devices for openings that use flexible sheets as a closing screen, applicable to doors, windows, shutters, roller blinds and similar closures in buildings, vehicles or fences. The classification addresses systems where the flexible sheet forms the primary barrier, with or without operating or securing mechanisms.
[E06B 9/08] Roll-type closures -- E06B 9/08 pertains to roll-type closures, such as roller blinds, rolling shutters or roll-up doors, that provide screening or protection for openings in buildings, vehicles, fences or similar enclosures. It covers devices that roll up or down, with or without operating and securing mechanisms, as a subclass of fixed or movable closures for doors, windows and other openings.
[E06B 9/11] Roller shutters -- E06B 9/11 designates roller shutters, movable panels that roll up or down to close openings such as doors, windows, vents or similar apertures, providing screening and protection against weather, noise or intrusion. The code falls under fixed constructions for doors, windows, shutters and related closures in buildings, vehicles or fences.
[E06B 9/115] specially adapted for furniture -- E06B 9/115 covers screening or protective devices for openings, such as doors, windows, blinds or shutters, that are specially adapted for integration into furniture, for example built-in blinds, protective panels or locking mechanisms incorporated into cabinet or other furniture structures.
[E06B 9/13] with closing members of one piece, e.g. of corrugated sheet metal -- E06B 9/13 refers to screening or protective devices for building or vehicle openings in which the closing element is formed as a single piece, for example a corrugated sheet-metal door, window or blind. The code falls under the fixed constructions group for doors, windows, shutters and similar closures.
[E06B 9/15] with closing members formed of slats or the like -- This sub-group covers screening or protective devices for openings where the closing element consists of slats, louvres or similar strip members, such as louvered shutters, slatted blinds or ventilating doors. It falls under fixed constructions for doors, windows, shutters and roller blinds, specifically closures formed of slats.
[E06B 9/165] with slats disappearing in each other -- E06B 9/165 classifies screening or protective devices for openings-such as doors, windows, shutters or blinds-where the slats are designed to slide into one another and effectively disappear when closed, providing an interlocking, concealed closure.
[E06B 9/17] Parts or details of roller shutters, e.g. suspension devices, shutter boxes, wicket doors, ventilation openings -- E06B 9/17 covers components of roller shutters such as the systems that suspend the shutter, the enclosing boxes for the rolled material, small doors or wicket openings within the shutter, and integrated ventilation openings. These parts relate to the mechanical and functional elements that allow the shutter to operate and provide access or airflow.
[E06B 9/17007] Shutter boxes; Details or component parts thereof -- E06B 9/17007 covers shutter boxes, the enclosing structures that house sliding or hinged shutters, and any individual parts or sub-components of those boxes such as frames, guides, seals, mounting brackets or locking mechanisms.
[E06B 9/17015] made of at most two pieces; Front opening details -- The code E06B 9/17015 covers screening or protective devices for openings such as doors, windows or shutters that are constructed from no more than two separate parts and includes specific front-opening details. It falls under fixed constructions for doors, windows, blinds and similar closures.
[E06B 9/17023] made of more than two pieces -- The subclass E06B 9/17023 covers screening or protective devices for openings such as doors, windows, blinds or gates that are built from more than two separate pieces. It resides in the CPC hierarchy under fixed constructions and movable closures for buildings, vehicles or fences, and includes devices with or without operating or securing mechanisms.
[E06B 9/1703] Fixing of the box; External plastering of the box -- E06B 9/1703 concerns the securing of a protective-screen or shutter box to a building opening and the subsequent external plastering or rendering of the box's outer surface, typically applied to doors, windows, blinds or similar closures.
[E06B 9/17046] Bottom bars -- The subclass E06B 9/17046 refers to bottom bars incorporated in doors, windows, shutters or roller blinds as part of fixed or movable closures. It covers the lower horizontal element that provides support, sealing or security for openings in buildings, vehicles, fences or similar enclosures.
[E06B 9/17061] Connection of the box to the guides -- The CPC entry E06B 9/17061 concerns the means of attaching a drive or motor box for screens, blinds or roller shutters to their guide rails or tracks. It includes brackets, clips, fasteners or other linkages that secure the box while allowing it to move along the guides. The provision is applied to fixed or movable closures in buildings, vehicles and comparable enclosures.
[E06B 9/17076] Sealing or antirattling arrangements -- E06B 9/17076 covers sealing or antirattling arrangements for fixed constructions such as doors, windows, shutters, blinds or gates. It includes devices that prevent air, water, insects or noise infiltration and that reduce vibration or rattling, for example weather-stripping, gaskets, spring-loaded seals and damping mechanisms. The arrangements may be integrated with the closure or added as retrofit components.
[E06B 9/171] Rollers therefor -- E06B 9/171 covers rollers used in screening or protective devices for openings such as doors, windows, shutters or roller blinds. The code applies to mechanisms that enable the closure or movement of these fixed or movable closures, including any associated operating or securing components.
[E06B 9/172] by clamping bars -- This subclass covers screening or protective devices for doors, windows, shutters or similar openings that are secured by clamping bars. The devices may be fixed or movable and include mechanisms that hold the protective element in place by pressure from bars. They are used in buildings, vehicles, fences or similar enclosures to control access, ventilation, or light while providing protection.

[E06B 9/304] with tilting bar and separate raising shaft -- E06B 9/304 classifies screening or protective devices for openings such as doors, windows or shutters that use a tilting bar to adjust the angle of a blind or screen combined with a separate raising shaft to lift or lower it. The arrangement allows independent control of tilt and vertical movement, typical of tilt-and-lift window blinds.

The bar is fixed in a inclined position and the cords run along it to enable smooth opening and closing of the screened opening.

It is used for doors, windows, gates and other building or vehicle openings.
[E06B 9/32] Operating, guiding, or securing devices therefor -- The classification covers mechanisms that operate, guide or secure screening or protective devices for openings such as doors, windows, shutters, blinds and gates.
