[E06B 9/322] Details of operating devices, e.g. pulleys, brakes, spring drums, drives -- E06B 9/322 classifies the components that make moving or securing mechanisms work in fixed constructions such as doors, windows, shutters, blinds and gates, covering items like pulleys, brakes, spring drums and drive units that control opening and closing.

[E06B 9/324] Cord-locks -- The subclass E06B 9/324 includes cord-lock mechanisms used to secure or release cords that operate blinds, screens, shutters or other protective closures for openings in buildings, vehicles and similar enclosures. [E06B 9/325] Immobilising devices preventing raising -- E06B 9/325 refers to mechanisms that secure a door, window, shutter or blind in its lowered position so that it cannot be raised or opened, typically by means of locks, latches or other restraining fittings incorporated into the closure.
[E06B 9/326] Details of cords, e.g. buckles, drawing knobs -- E06B 9/326 pertains to the hardware elements of cords used in screening or protective devices for doors, windows, shutters and similar closures, covering items such as buckles, drawing knobs and other fittings that enable cords to be pulled, adjusted or secured. The subclass details the design and construction of these cord accessories as part of fixed or movable closure systems.
[E06B 9/327] Guides for raisable lamellar blinds with horizontal lamellae -- E06B 9/327 in the Cooperative Patent Classification describes guides designed for raisable lamellar blinds that have horizontal lamellae, directing and supporting the slats as the blind is lifted within window or door openings. These devices are part of fixed or movable closures and provide controlled movement and alignment of the blind.
[E06B 9/34] roller-type; -- E06B 9/34 covers roller-type screening or protective devices fitted to doors, windows, shutters, gates or similar openings in buildings, vehicles or fences. The entry includes mechanisms that roll the screen or blind to open or close, with or without additional securing features.
[E06B 9/36] with vertical lamellae -- E06B 9/36 covers screening or protective devices for building or vehicle openings that use vertical lamellae, such as vertical slat blinds or shutters. These devices may be fixed or movable and can include mechanisms for opening, closing, or securing the lamellae.
[E06B 9/361] Transmissions located at the end of the supporting rail -- E06B 9/361 covers devices that transmit motion or force from a driving mechanism to a screen, blind or similar protective closure, with the transmission situated at the far end of the supporting rail. Typical examples include gear drives, cable-pulley systems or linkage arrangements that operate the closure from a remote position along the rail. This subclass falls under fixed constructions for doors, windows, shutters or roller blinds.
[E06B 9/362] Travellers; Lamellae suspension stems -- E06B 9/362 covers the travelers and suspension stems that support and guide lamellae in adjustable blinds or shading devices, allowing the slats to be raised, lowered or tilted for controlling light, ventilation and privacy.
[E06B 9/364] Operating mechanisms therein -- E06B 9/364 covers the means used to open, close, or adjust fixed or movable closures such as doors, windows, shutters, blinds or gates. It includes manual, motorised, hydraulic or pneumatic devices and linkages that are built into or attached to the closure, such as hinges, cranks, rollers, gear drives, electric actuators or locking mechanisms.
[E06B 9/365] Distance pieces therefor -- Distance pieces are spacers, gaskets or similar elements placed between a screening or protective device-such as a shutter, blind or door-and the surrounding frame to maintain a defined clearance, improve sealing or facilitate movement. They are used in fixed or movable closures for openings in buildings, vehicles or similar enclosures.
[E06B 9/367] Lamellae suspensions  E06B9/362  takes precedence ; Bottom weights; Bottom guides -- The code E06B 9/367 covers lamella-type screening or protective devices for openings that are suspended by bottom-weight or bottom-guide systems. It relates to the means of supporting and guiding the lower edge of the slats, ensuring smooth opening and closing in doors, windows, shutters or similar closures.
[E06B 9/368] Driving means other than pulling cords -- E06B 9/368 covers the power-or other actuation systems that move screens, blinds, shutters or similar protective devices for openings, when the movement is not achieved by pulling cords. It includes electric, hydraulic, pneumatic, spring or gear-driven mechanisms and any other means that drive the closure without manual cord operation.
[E06B 9/38] Other details -- E06B 9/38 covers ancillary or supplementary elements of screening and protective devices for openings such as doors, windows, shutters, blinds or gates. It includes any other details not covered elsewhere, for example seals, gaskets, reinforcement ribs, locking or fastening provisions, and miscellaneous fittings that aid the function or installation of the closure.
[E06B 9/382] Details of ladder-tapes or ladder chains, e.g. buckles for local shortening of tapes -- E06B 9/382 covers hardware for ladder-type screening or protective devices, such as buckles, clamps or other fittings that enable a portion of a ladder-tape or ladder chain to be tightened or shortened locally. These components are used in fixed or movable closures like doors, windows, shutters and similar openings to adjust tension or spacing.
[E06B 9/384] Details of interconnection or interaction of tapes and lamellae -- E06B 9/384 covers the specific ways that tapes, slats or lamellae are linked or interact within screening or protective devices such as blinds, shutters and similar fixed or movable closures for openings in buildings, vehicles or fences. It includes details of hinges, joints, couplings and other mechanisms that connect the individual elements.
[E06B 9/386] Details of lamellae -- E06B 9/386 covers the specific design features of individual lamellae that form part of screening or protective devices such as blinds, shutters or similar movable closures. It includes aspects such as shape, material, connection, angle adjustment and surface treatment that affect light control, ventilation or security of openings.
[E06B 9/388] Details of bottom or upper slats or their attachment -- E06B 9/388 covers the design and mounting of the lower or upper slats of screening or protective devices such as blinds, shutters or similar closures, including the shape, material, connection method and adjustment features.
[E06B 9/40] Roller blinds -- Roller blinds are screening or protective devices for openings that consist of a flexible material wound on a tube and can be raised or lowered to control light, privacy, and ventilation, commonly applied to windows, doors or other apertures in buildings and vehicles.
[E06B 9/42] Parts or details of roller blinds, e.g. suspension devices, blind boxes -- The subclass E06B 9/42 pertains to components of roller-type blinds, including the mechanisms that suspend the blind fabric and the protective or decorative housing (blind box) that contains the roller. It covers any detail or part that enables the blind to be lowered, raised, or secured within doors, windows or similar openings.
[E06B 9/44] Rollers therefor -- E06B 9/44 designates rollers employed in screening or protective devices for openings such as doors, windows, blinds or gates. The code covers the cylindrical or similar rolling elements that support and guide the movement of these fixed or movable closures.
[E06B 9/46] by clamping bars -- This subclass concerns screening or protective devices for openings in buildings, vehicles, fences or similar enclosures that are secured by clamping bars, such as grills, screens or shutters that are mounted without hinges. The focus is on the method of attachment by means of bars that clamp the device in place to block or protect the opening.
[E06B 9/48] by clasps or buttons -- The subclass E06B 9/48 covers screening or protective devices for doors, windows, shutters, gates or similar openings that are fastened or held closed by clasps, buttons or comparable fastening elements, with or without additional operating or securing mechanisms. It addresses the means of attachment rather than the structure of the opening itself.
[E06B 9/50] Bearings specially adapted therefor -- E06B 9/50 covers bearing devices that are specifically designed for use in fixed or movable closures such as doors, windows, shutters or roller blinds, providing support and smooth operation of the moving parts. These bearings may be integrated with sealing, guiding or locking mechanisms and are adapted to the particular loads and motion of the closure.
[E06B 9/52] Devices affording protection against insects, e.g. fly screens -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Screens for household or table -> A47G5/00
[E06B 9/521] Frameless fly screens; Tensioning means therefor  E06B9/54  takes precedence -- E06B 9/521 covers screening or protective devices for building or vehicle openings that are provided without a surrounding frame, such as frameless fly screens, and the means used to tension or secure them in place. It falls under the broader group for fixed constructions and movable closures for doors, windows, shutters and similar openings.
[E06B 9/522] Dimensionally adjustable fly screens  E06B9/54  and  E06B9/521  take precedence -- E06B 9/522 covers fly screens whose size can be altered to fit openings of varying dimensions, such as adjustable mesh panels or frames that can be expanded, contracted, or otherwise modified to seal doors, windows, or similar apertures while permitting ventilation. This subclass includes mechanisms that allow the screen to be fitted securely despite changes in width or height, providing flexible protection against insects without permanent alteration of the opening.
[E06B 9/54] Roller fly screens -- E06B 9/54 covers roller-type fly screens designed to protect openings such as windows, doors or shutters from insects while allowing ventilation. The device consists of a flexible mesh wound on a roller that can be pulled down and retracted, often with a manual or automatic mechanism for opening and securing. It is classified under fixed constructions for protective screening of building and vehicle openings.
[E06B 9/56] Operating, guiding or securing devices or arrangements for roll-type closures -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Suspension devices for wings -> E05D - Operating mechanisms for wings in general -> E05F
[E06B 9/58] Guiding devices -- The E06B 9/58 subclass covers guiding devices for fixed or movable closures such as doors, windows, shutters, blinds and gates. It includes mechanisms that keep the moving part aligned and on a defined path, for example rollers, tracks, hinges, guides and related components that control the direction of opening or closing.
[E06B 9/581] Means to prevent or induce disengagement of shutter from side rails -- Means designed to keep a shutter attached to its side rails or to allow it to be released, such as latches, catches, springs, locking bars or automatic release devices. These mechanisms may secure the shutter during normal operation or enable quick disengagement for cleaning, maintenance or emergency opening.
[E06B 9/582] Means to increase gliss, light, sound or thermal insulation -- The E06B 9/582 subclass covers devices and measures that improve the insulation of fixed or movable openings such as doors, windows, shutters or gates, aiming to reduce glare, light transmission, sound transmission or heat loss. It includes glazing, shading panels, acoustic seals, thermal breaks and other accessories that enhance visual, acoustic or thermal performance of the opening.
[E06B 9/60] Spring drums operated only by closure members -- E06B 9/60 classifies spring-drum mechanisms that are driven only by the motion of the closure members themselves, such as doors, windows, shutters or blinds, and are used as part of screening or protective devices for building or vehicle openings.
[E06B 9/62] Counterweighting arrangements -- E06B 9/62 covers counterweighting arrangements used in screening or protective devices for openings such as doors, windows, shutters or roller blinds. The code applies to mechanisms that balance the movement of these closures, often employing weights or springs to ease opening and closing. It falls under fixed constructions for doors, windows, shutters and similar enclosures.
[E06B 9/64] with lowerable roller -- E06B 9/64 covers screening or protective devices for openings that incorporate a lowerable roller, such as roller blinds, roller shutters or similar closures that can be lowered to cover a door, window, gate or other aperture. The classification includes the roller system itself and any associated mechanisms for operating, securing or fixing the lowered position.
[E06B 9/66] with a roller situated at the bottom -- This subclass covers screening or protective devices for openings in buildings, vehicles or similar enclosures-such as doors, windows, shutters or blinds-that incorporate a roller situated at the bottom to guide or assist opening and closing movement.
[E06B 9/68] Operating devices or mechanisms, e.g. with electric drive -- E06B 9/68 relates to mechanisms that operate or drive fixed or movable closures such as doors, windows, shutters or roller blinds, including electric drives and other powering means. The classification also covers screening or protective devices fitted to openings in buildings, vehicles or similar enclosures.
[E06B 9/70] comprising an electric motor positioned outside the roller -- The subclass E06B 9/70 covers roller-type shutters or blinds where the drive motor is mounted externally to the roller, rather than integrated within it. This arrangement is used for screening or protecting openings in buildings, vehicles or similar structures, and may include mechanisms for opening, closing or securing the closure.
[E06B 9/72] comprising an electric motor positioned inside the roller -- The code E06B 9/72 covers roller-type closures such as blinds or shutters that incorporate a self-contained electric motor within the roller assembly itself, providing drive power without external gear or motor housings. This arrangement is used for automated opening and closing of fixed or movable openings in buildings, vehicles, fences or similar enclosures.
[E06B 9/74] adapted for selective electrical or manual operation -- These are screening or protective devices for doors, windows, shutters, blinds or similar openings that can be operated selectively, either by an electrical system or by manual control, allowing partial or full opening as needed.
[E06B 9/76] using crank handles -- E06B 9/76 classifies screening or protective devices for doors, windows, shutters or similar openings that are opened or closed by crank handles, encompassing both fixed and movable closures used in buildings, vehicles, fences or comparable enclosures.
[E06B 9/78] for direct manual operation, e.g. by tassels, by handles -- The CPC entry E06B 9/78 covers screening or protective devices for building, vehicle or fence openings that are operated directly by hand, for example with tassels or handles. It belongs to the subclass for fixed constructions, specifically doors, windows, shutters or roller blinds in general.
[E06B 9/80] Safety measures against dropping or unauthorised opening -- E06B 9/80 covers safety devices for doors, windows, shutters, blinds or similar openings that prevent accidental dropping or unauthorized opening, including locks, latches, mechanical stops, sensors, and other anti-theft or child-safety mechanisms.
[E06B 9/82] automatic -- E06B 9/82 covers automatic screening or protective devices for openings such as doors, windows, blinds or gates, including mechanisms that open, close or secure the closure without manual effort. It pertains to fixed or movable closures in buildings, vehicles or similar enclosures.
[E06B 9/84] against dropping -- E06B 9/84 covers safety devices that prevent a door, window, shutter, gate or similar closure from unintentionally falling or dropping, typically using mechanical stops, latches, dampers or other securing mechanisms to arrest or limit downward movement. This subclass is part of the broader group for screening or protective devices on building and vehicle openings.
[E06B 9/86] against unauthorised opening -- E06B 9/86 covers protective elements that prevent unauthorized opening of doors, windows, shutters, roller blinds or similar closures in buildings, vehicles or fences. It includes locks, security bars, latching systems and other mechanisms that restrict access without using the normal operating means. The subclass falls under fixed constructions for screening or protective devices for openings.
[E06B 9/88] for limiting unrolling -- E06B 9/88 covers devices that limit the unrolling of flexible screens, blinds or protective curtains used in doors, windows or other openings. It applies to mechanisms that control the material's extension to prevent excessive deployment.
[E06B 9/90] for immobilising the closure member in various chosen positions -- E06B 9/90 covers devices that lock or hold a door, window, shutter, blind or similar closure in selected positions, providing screening or protection for openings in buildings, vehicles, fences or other enclosures. The classification includes mechanisms that immobilise the closure member either fully or partially, often as part of safety, security or weather-tightening features.
[E06B 9/92] Means allowing the closures to be shifted out of the plane of the opening -- A means for moving a door, window, shutter or similar closure out of the plane of its opening, so that the opening is cleared when the closure is retracted. This includes hinged, pivoting, folding or sliding mechanisms that shift the closure away from the opening to provide unobstructed access or view.
[E06C] LADDERS -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Ladders in general, ladders mounted on undercarriages or vehicles, component parts or accessories of ladders, and ladders in relation with buildings  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Step-tools -> A47C12/00 - Adaptation of ladders to use on ships -> B63B - Adaptation of ladders to use on aircrafts -> B64 - Staircases -> E04F11/00 - Scaffolding -> E04G - Ladders for special structures, e.g. ladders for swimming- pools -> E04H
[E06C 1/00] Ladders in general -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Ladders in general with rigid longitudinal members, e.g. ladders for resting against objects or ladders capable of standing by themselves, and ladders in general with non-rigid longitudinal members, e.g. rope or chain ladders.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Ladders mounted on undercarriages or vehicles -> E06C5/00 - Ladders permanently attached to fixed structures -> E06C9/00 - Hunting stands -> A01M31/00 - Ladders on boats, ships -> B63B27/14 - Staircases -> E04F11/00 - Loft ladders -> E04F11/04 - Swimming pool ladders -> E04H4/144
[E06C 1/005] Ladders in use as furniture or ladders in relation to furniture, e.g. in cupboards  step-stools, chairs or benches requiring conversion between the step and seat functions  A47C12/02 -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Ladders either fixedly or foldably attached to furniture and eventually storable into furniture  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Step-stools, chairs or benches requiring conversion between the step and seat functions -> A47C12/02
[E06C 1/02] with rigid longitudinal member or members -- The CPC subclass E06C 1/02 covers ladders that incorporate one or more rigid longitudinal members, such as side rails or a central support, to provide structural stability. It falls under fixed constructions for doors, windows, shutters or roller blinds, specifically within the broader category of general ladders.
[E06C 1/04] Ladders for resting against objects, e.g. walls poles, trees -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Ladders specially adapted for resting against objects, e.g. walls poles, trees
[E06C 1/06] in one piece -- E06C 1/06 designates ladders that are produced as a single, integral unit rather than assembled from separate parts, covering any one-piece ladder used in fixed constructions such as doors, windows, shutters or roller blinds.
[E06C 1/08] multi-part -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Ladders comprising a series of longitunal sections fitted substantially end to end and/or ladders being extensible in their length by way of sliding means only (e.g. telescopic ladders)  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Ladders with a strut which is formed as a ladder and can be secured in line with the ladder (i.e. ladders having a strut formed as a ladder, hinged with the ladder and securable in line with the ladder); Longitunally folded ladders that can be extended in line by unfolding -> E06C1/32
[E06C 1/10] Sections fitted end to end -- The E06C 1/10 subclass covers ladders that are constructed from separate sections joined together end-to-end, allowing the overall length to be varied by adding or removing modules. It includes designs where the sections are coupled by mechanical fasteners, hinges or telescoping links to form a continuous ladder for use in construction, maintenance or access tasks.
[E06C 1/12] extensible, e.g. telescopic -- E06C 1/12 covers ladders whose length can be varied, such as telescopic or extensible designs. These ladders consist of two or more sections that slide or lock together to provide a longer reach when needed and collapse for compact storage. The classification applies to any ladder mechanism that allows adjustable height within the fixed-construction group.
[E06C 1/125] with tubular longitudinal members nested within each other -- A telescopic ladder formed from tubular longitudinal members that are inserted one inside another, allowing the ladder to extend and collapse while maintaining a rigid, fixed construction for use as a door, window or general access apparatus.
[E06C 1/14] Ladders capable of standing by themselves -- This subclass covers ladders that are self-supporting and can remain upright without attachment to a wall, floor or other structure. It includes freestanding designs such as step ladders, extension ladders with stabilising legs and any configuration that enables the ladder to stand by itself.
[E06C 1/16] with struts which rest on the ground -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Non-hinged free-standing ladders, e.g. ladders with struts rigid with the ladder -> E06C1/24 - Ladders having platforms -> E06C1/39
[E06C 1/18] with supporting struts formed as ladders -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Ladders capable of standing by themselves and with telescopic supporting struts formed as ladders -> E06C1/22 - Ladders with a strut which is formed as a ladder and can be secured in line with the ladder -> E06C1/32
[E06C 1/20] with supporting struts formed as poles -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Ladders capable of standing by themselves and with supporting struts formed as poles as part of the ladder.
[E06C 1/22] with extensible, e.g. telescopic, ladder parts or struts -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Ladders capable of standing by themselves and with telescopic supporting struts formed as ladders.
[E06C 1/24] free-standing ladders -- E06C 1/24 refers to free-standing ladders, which are portable, self-supporting climbing devices not attached to walls or other structures. This subclass covers ladders that can be placed independently on the ground and used for access to heights in construction, maintenance, or domestic settings. It falls under the broader categories of fixed constructions and ladders in general.
[E06C 1/26] in one piece -- Ladders classified under E06C 1/26 are constructed as a single, integral unit rather than assembled from separate components. The entire structure, including side rails, rungs and any fittings, is formed in one piece, typically by casting, welding or molding, providing greater rigidity and eliminating joints.
[E06C 1/28] multi-part -- E06C 1/28 covers ladders that are built from two or more separate sections which can be joined together to form a single ladder, allowing the device to be extended, folded or disassembled for transport or storage. The classification applies to multi-part ladders used in fixed constructions such as doors, windows, shutters or roller blinds. It includes ladders with detachable or telescoping elements that can be re-configured to suit different heights or access needs.
[E06C 1/30] extensible, e.g. telescopic -- Extensible ladders, such as telescopic or fold-out types, designed for use in fixed constructions like doors, windows, shutters or roller blinds. The subclass covers adjustable-length ladders that can be extended or retracted for convenient reach and storage.
[E06C 1/32] Ladders with a strut which is formed as a ladder and can be secured in line with the ladder -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Ladders with a strut which is formed as a ladder and can be secured in line with the ladder, e.g. ladders having a strut formed as a ladder and hinged with the ladder, that can be secured in line with the ladder. This group also covers longitunally folded ladders that can be extended in line by unfolding.
[E06C 1/34] Ladders attached to structures, such as windows, cornices, poles, or the like -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Ladders specially adapted by using intrinsic means (i.e. the means being part of the ladder) for being attached to or to properly rest against structures, such as windows, cornices, poles or the like  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Ladders permanently fastened to buildings -> E06C9/00 - Climbing irons -> E06C9/04 - Ladders permanently fastened to buildings, and laterally displaceable -> E06C9/12
[E06C 1/345] specially adapted to be installed parallel to the roof surface -- The subclass E06C 1/345 covers ladders that are specially designed to be mounted parallel to a roof surface, allowing safe ascent and descent along sloping roofs. These roof-adapted ladders typically incorporate features such as angled rungs, anti-slip fixtures, and secure attachment points to accommodate the roof's pitch.
[E06C 1/36] Ladders suspendable by hooks or the like -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Ladders suspendable by hooks, e.g. ladders having a hook or grab at their top
[E06C 1/38] Special constructions of ladders, e.g. ladders with more or less than two longitudinal members, ladders with movable rungs or other treads, longitudinally-foldable ladders
[E06C 1/381] Ladders with rungs or treads attached only to one rigid longitudinal member -- The subclass E06C 1/381 covers ladders in which the rungs or treads are fixed only to a single rigid longitudinal member rather than to two parallel rails. Such designs are used in fixed constructions where a single supporting column provides the structural backbone for the steps.
[E06C 1/382] Tripod ladders -- The CPC class E06C 1/382 pertains to tripod ladders, a subset of ladders within the fixed constructions group covering doors, windows, shutters and roller blinds. Tripod ladders are three-legged, self-standing ladders used for temporary access to elevated work areas, typically in construction, maintenance or rescue operations.
[E06C 1/383] Foldable ladders in which the longitudinal members are brought together on folding -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Foldable ladders in which the longitudinal members are brought together on folding, e.g. ladders in which the rungs are hinged on the longitudinal members, or the hinges are foldable to allow the longitudinal members to be brought together  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Ladders in general capable of standing by themselves, with hinged struts -> E06C1/16 - Ladders with a strut which is formed as a ladder and can be secured in line with the ladder, e.g. ladders having a strut formed as a ladder and hinged with the ladder, that can be secured in line with the ladder -> E06C1/32 - Ladders permanently attached to fixed structures in which the longitudinal members are brought together on folding -> E06C9/085
[E06C 1/3835] having collapsible steps comprising a hinge in their central part -- E06C 1/3835 classifies ladders whose steps can be folded by means of a hinge located in the middle of each step. The design allows the ladder to collapse for compact storage or transport while maintaining structural integrity when extended.
[E06C 1/387] having tip-up steps -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Ladders having tip-up steps, e.g. ladders having rungs collapsible in-line with the ladder on folding
[E06C 1/39] Ladders having platforms -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Lifts on ladders, as accessories for ladders -> E06C7/12 - Platforms for use on ladders, as accessories -> E06C7/16
[E06C 1/393] Ladders having platforms foldable with the ladder -- E06C 1/393 relates to ladders that incorporate platforms which can be folded together with the ladder for compact storage while still providing a usable work surface. This subclass falls under fixed constructions, specifically ladders in general.
[E06C 1/397] characterised by having wheels, rollers, or runners -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Ladders being mounted on undercarriages or vehicles -> E06C5/00 - Ladders permanently fastened to buildings, and laterally displaceable, e.g. library ladders -> E06C9/12
[E06C 1/52] with non-rigid longitudinal members -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Ladders in general with non-rigid longitudinal members, e.g. lazy-tongs type, rope or chain ladders  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Ladders with non-rigid longitudinally members permanently attached to fixed structures and movably mounted, e.g. rope or chain ladders -> E06C9/14
[E06C 1/525] made of a continuous strip of material -- A ladder whose side rails and steps are formed from a single, unbroken strip of material, such as metal or plastic, rather than assembled from separate components. The continuous strip is shaped or bent to create the rungs and overall ladder profile, providing a simple, often lightweight construction. This design is covered by CPC code E06C 1/525.
[E06C 1/54] of the lazy-tongs type -- E06C 1/54 designates ladders of the lazy-tongs type, i.e. folding ladders whose rungs are linked by a pantograph or similar mechanism that lets the ladder collapse compactly while keeping uniform spacing when extended. These ladders are used where portability and space-saving are required.
[E06C 1/56] Rope or chain ladders -- E06C 1/56 classifies ladders constructed from rope or chain, including flexible or rigid configurations used for climbing or access. The entry covers design features, dimensions, and supporting structures specific to rope- and chain-type ladders within the broader category of fixed constructions for doors, windows, shutters and roller blinds.
[E06C 1/58] with both rigid and non-rigid longitudinal members -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Ladders in general having both rigid and non-rigid longitudinal members, e.g. ladders comprising sections in which the longitudinal members are rigid and sections in which the longitudinal members are not rigid
[E06C 5/00] Ladders characterised by being mounted on undercarriages or vehicles Securing ladders on vehicles -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Ladders mounted on undercarriages or ground vehicles and having rigid longitudinal members, or ladders mounted on undercarriages or ground vehicles and having non-rigid longitudinal members, and accessories specially adapted therefor.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Ladders having wheels, rollers, or runners -> E06C1/397 - Arrangements of steps or ladders facilitating access to or on a vehicle; retractable -> B60R3/00 ; B60R3/02 - Steps for railway vehicles -> B61D23/00 - Ladders on boats or ships -> B63B27/14
[E06C 5/02] with rigid longitudinal members -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Running boards for vehicles; for vehicle tops -> B60R3/002 ; B60R3/005
[E06C 5/04] capable of being elevated or extended -- E06C 5/04 refers to ladders mounted on vehicle undercarriages that can be raised or lengthened for access, such as rack-type or sliding ladders that extend outward or pivot upward when needed. The subclass addresses the means of elevating or extending the ladder while it remains secured to the vehicle.
[E06C 5/06] by piston and cylinder, or equivalent means, operated by a pressure medium -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Ladders having elevating assist, by piston and cylinder, or equivalent means, operated by a pressure medium
[E06C 5/08] derived directly from a pressure reservoir -- E06C 5/08 refers to vehicle-mounted ladders whose deployment or operation is obtained directly from a pressure reservoir, such as a hydraulic or pneumatic tank, without intermediate mechanisms. The subclass covers ladders that draw the necessary force straight from stored fluid pressure for raising, lowering, or securing the ladder on an undercarriage.
[E06C 5/10] charged by a pump or compressor driven by the motor of the vehicle or another motor on the vehicle -- This subclass covers ladders that are mounted on a vehicle's undercarriage and are raised or lowered by a pump or compressor powered by the vehicle's own motor or another motor on the vehicle, providing a pneumatic or hydraulic means of extending the ladder while it remains secured to the vehicle.
[E06C 5/12] derived directly from a pump or compressor -- E06C 5/12 relates to ladders that are mounted on, or secured to, the undercarriage or other parts of a vehicle, allowing the ladder to be deployed from the vehicle's structure. The subclass also covers arrangements where the ladder is derived directly from a pump or compressor, using the fluid-power system to raise, lower or lock the ladder.
[E06C 5/14] driven by the motor of the vehicle or another motor on the vehicle -- E06C 5/14 is a CPC subclass for fixed constructions involving ladders mounted on vehicle undercarriages, where the ladder movement is powered by the vehicle's motor or another motor mounted on the vehicle. It refines the broader groups for doors, windows, shutters, roller blinds and ladders secured to vehicles.
[E06C 5/16] using mechanical transmission only, with or without fluid or other non-mechanical couplings or clutches -- E06C 5/16 designates ladders that are mounted on the undercarriage or other part of a vehicle and are moved or secured using only mechanical transmission, optionally combined with fluid or other non-mechanical couplings or clutches. The code covers gear, chain, cable or pulley systems that raise, lower, or lock the ladder without external power sources.
[E06C 5/18] using power from the motor of the vehicle or another motor -- E06C 5/18 covers ladders mounted on vehicle undercarriages that are powered by the vehicle's motor or an auxiliary motor, allowing automatic extension or retraction for safe access and maintenance.
[E06C 5/20] using hand-power -- E06C 5/20 relates to hand-powered means for securing ladders that are mounted on undercarriages or other vehicles, enabling the ladder to be attached, released or adjusted manually. It falls within the fixed-construction group covering doors, windows, shutters, roller blinds and ladder systems integrated with such structures.
[E06C 5/22] by, or with the assistance of, springs -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Ladders mounted on vehicles, with rigid longitudinal members, and capable of being elevated or extended by piston and cylinder or equivalent means -> E06C5/06 - Ladders mounted on vehicles, with rigid longitudinal members, and capable of being elevated or extended using mechanical transmission -> E06C5/16
[E06C 5/24] Dismounting ladders from vehicles -- E06C 5/24 addresses the removal or detaching of ladders that are mounted on vehicle undercarriages, describing the mechanisms and procedures for safely dismounting such ladders after use.
[E06C 5/26] with non-rigid longitudinal members -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Ladders mounted on undercarriages or vehicles and having non-rigid longitudinal members
[E06C 5/28] of the lazy-tongs type -- E06C 5/28 covers ladders of the lazy-tongs type designed to be mounted on undercarriages or vehicles, providing a means to secure the ladder while attached to the transport platform. Such ladders are fixed constructions used for access in doors, windows, shutters or roller-blind systems.
[E06C 5/30] formed of links which reinforce themselves against each other -- E06C 5/30 covers ladders constructed from a series of interlinked sections that mutually reinforce one another, providing added strength and stability. These self-supporting link systems are typically used for ladders mounted on vehicle undercarriages or other mobile platforms, allowing the ladder to remain secure while the vehicle is in motion.
[E06C 5/32] Accessories -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Safety devices and accessories for use with or on ladders mounted on undercarriages or vehicles
[E06C 5/34] Indicating devices -- In the CPC class E06C 5/34, indicating devices refer to signals attached to ladders that are mounted on vehicle undercarriages, such as lights, flags, audible alarms or visual markers that warn of the ladder's presence, position or use while the vehicle is moving or stationary. These devices enhance safety by alerting personnel and other road users to the ladder's deployment.
[E06C 5/36] Safety devices against slipping or falling of ladders -- E06C 5/36 covers safety devices that prevent ladders mounted on undercarriages or vehicles from slipping or causing falls, including brackets, locks, anti-slip mechanisms and other means of securing the ladder to the vehicle.
[E06C 5/38] Devices for blocking the springs of the vehicle -- E06C 5/38 covers devices that lock or block the springs of a vehicle to secure ladders mounted on its undercarriage, preventing spring movement during transport or use. These mechanisms are part of fixed-construction arrangements for doors, windows, shutters or roller blinds that include ladders attached to vehicles.
[E06C 5/40] Devices for canting ladders laterally -- The E06C 5/40 subclass covers devices that allow a ladder mounted on a vehicle or undercarriage to be tilted or swung sideways for easier access or positioning. Such mechanisms secure the ladder during transport and enable controlled canting when the ladder is deployed, typically found on trucks, vans, or specialized service vehicles.
[E06C 5/42] Devices for altering inclination -- E06C 5/42 covers mechanisms that change the tilt or angle of ladders mounted on vehicle undercarriages or other fixed constructions, allowing the ladder to be inclined safely for access. It includes devices such as hinges, pivots, adjustable brackets or other apparatus that alter the ladder's inclination and secure it in the desired position.
[E06C 5/44] Other accessories on ladders, e.g. acoustical signalling devices, dismountable switchboards -- E06C 5/44 covers ancillary devices fitted to ladders that are mounted on vehicles or undercarriages, such as acoustic signalling units, removable switchboards and other accessories that improve safety or operational control. These items are considered part of the ladder assembly rather than the vehicle itself.
[E06C 7/00] Component parts, supporting parts, or accessories -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Component parts, supporting parts, or accessories for ladders in general, e.g. lifts or hoisting devices on ladders, holders for pails or other equipment on ladders, accessorial platforms on ladders, ladder heads and ladder feet.
[E06C 7/003] Indicating devices, e.g. user warnings or inclinators -- E06C 7/003 covers indicating devices used on fixed constructions such as doors, windows, shutters, roller blinds or ladders, including accessories that provide user warnings, position indicators or inclinators to signal tilt, opening status or safety conditions.
[E06C 7/006] Devices for preventing access -- This subclass covers devices that block, restrict or otherwise prevent entry to fixed openings such as doors, windows, shutters or ladders, including locks, barriers, grates, shutters and similar mechanisms used as component or supporting parts.
[E06C 7/02] Extending means -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Extending means for ladders mounted on undercarriages or vehicles -> E06C5/00
[E06C 7/04] Hand-operated extending means carried by the ladder -- E06C 7/04 relates to hand-operated extending devices that are integral to a ladder, enabling the user to manually lengthen or shorten the ladder by sliding or telescoping sections. The classification includes any built-in mechanisms that provide adjustable reach while the ladder remains in use.
[E06C 7/06] Securing devices or hooks for parts of extensible ladders -- This classification covers devices that lock, attach or otherwise secure the components of an extendable ladder, such as hooks, clamps, brackets or similar fittings that hold the ladder sections together or fasten them to a structure. It includes mechanisms intended to prevent accidental collapse and to aid safe deployment of the ladder.
[E06C 7/08] Special construction of longitudinal members, or rungs or other treads -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Constructive details of longitudinal members, rungs or other threads, e.g. rungs styles connections
[E06C 7/081] Rungs or other treads comprising anti-slip features -- The CPC subclass E06C 7/081 covers ladder components, specifically rungs or other treads that incorporate anti-slip features such as ribs, grooves, textured surfaces or coatings designed to improve traction and prevent slipping during climbing. It applies to any ladder where the tread itself provides enhanced grip.
[E06C 7/082] Connections between rungs or treads and longitudinal members -- This class deals with the means of joining ladder rungs or treads to the longitudinal members such as side rails, covering mechanical fasteners, welding, bonding, brackets or other connection elements used in fixed constructions like ladders, doors and shutters.
[E06C 7/083] Bracket type connection -- E06C 7/083 covers bracket-type connections used in ladder components, supporting parts or accessories for fixed constructions such as doors, windows, shutters or roller blinds. The code classifies those brackets that join ladder sections or attach ladders to other structural elements. It falls under the broader group for ladders within the fixed-construction hierarchy.
[E06C 7/084] Rungs comprising projecting tabs or flanges -- A ladder rung designed with projecting tabs or flanges that serve as component or supporting parts in fixed constructions, such as doors, windows, shutters or roller blinds, is classified under E06C 7/084. This entry covers the specific shape of the rung rather than the whole ladder.
[E06C 7/085] achieved by deforming the rung or the stile  E06C7/084  takes precedence -- This subgroup relates to ladder parts whose rung or stile is intentionally deformed to create a specific shape or function such as hooks, steps or grips, and it is treated as a subcategory of E06C7/084, which has priority over it.
[E06C 7/086] with a connecting piece inserted in a hollow rung  E06C7/088  takes precedence
[E06C 7/087] with a connecting piece installed around the rung -- This CPC subclass concerns ladders that are part of fixed constructions such as doors, windows, shutters or roller blinds, specifically the component parts that include a connecting piece mounted around a rung. It covers designs where the connector encircles the rung to join ladder sections or attach accessories.
[E06C 7/088] with tie rods parallel to the rungs -- E06C 7/088 classifies ladder components or accessories that incorporate tie rods running parallel to the rungs, used to reinforce or adjust the ladder structure. It is part of the fixed-construction group for doors, windows, shutters and roller blinds.
[E06C 7/10] Reinforcements for ladders -- E06C 7/10 covers devices or structural elements that reinforce ladders, such as braces, plates, ribs or other stiffening members, used as component or supporting parts in fixed constructions like doors, windows or shutters.
[E06C 7/12] Lifts or other hoisting devices on ladders -- The class defines devices attached to ladders that aid or automate the lifting of loads, such as powered or manual hoists, winches and similar mechanisms for raising or lowering persons or goods. It applies to component parts and accessories used with ladders in fixed construction contexts.
[E06C 7/14] Holders for pails or other equipment on or for ladders -- E06C 7/14 covers devices that are mounted on ladders to support pails, tools or other equipment, allowing secure transport or placement while the ladder is in use. These holders may be clips, brackets or trays designed to fit the ladder rungs or side rails and are considered accessory components of ladders.
[E06C 7/143] specially adapted to be inserted into the end of a hollow rung -- E06C 7/143 covers ladder components that are designed to fit into the open end of a hollow rung, serving as a filler, connector or reinforcement piece. The part is inserted into the rung to provide strength, stability or to accommodate accessories, and is classified under fixed constructions for doors, windows, shutters or roller blinds in general.
[E06C 7/146] made from wire  E06C7/143  takes precedence -- E06C 7/146 classifies ladder components or accessories that are manufactured from wire, fitting within the broader category of fixed constructions such as doors, windows, shutters and roller blinds, specifically under the sub-group for ladder parts and supporting elements. This code is subordinate to E06C 7/143, which defines the overall ladder component group.
[E06C 7/16] Platforms on, or for use on, ladders, e.g. liftable or lowerable platforms -- E06C 7/16 relates to platforms that are mounted on ladders and can be raised or lowered to create a working surface, including liftable or lowerable platforms used as extensions of the ladder.
[E06C 7/165] specially adapted to be fixed to only one rung -- E06C 7/165 classifies ladder components that are specifically designed to be mounted on a single rung, such as brackets, clamps or other accessories that attach to only one step. These parts provide support or enable fixed constructions related to ladders used in doors, windows, shutters or roller blinds. The entry covers any device adapted solely for attachment to one rung.
[E06C 7/18] Devices for preventing persons from falling -- This CPC subclass covers safety equipment that stops or arrests a person descending a ladder or similar vertical access, such as fall-arrest belts, harnesses, safety rails, or catching devices. It includes component parts and accessories that protect users from falling while working on or near ladders and other fixed constructions.
[E06C 7/181] Additional gripping devices, e.g. handrails -- E06C 7/181 covers supplementary gripping elements fitted to ladders, such as handrails or other devices that improve user safety and stability while climbing. These accessories are classified under fixed constructions for doors, windows, shutters and roller blinds, specifically as component or supporting parts for ladders.
[E06C 7/182] situated at the top of the ladder -- E06C 7/182 covers components that are placed at the upper end of a ladder, such as platforms, guardrails or foot-rests that support or protect a user when reaching the top. These parts are considered accessories for ladders used in fixed constructions such as doors, windows, shutters or roller blinds.
[E06C 7/183] situated along the ladder -- E06C 7/183 covers parts or accessories that are mounted on or integrated into a ladder, such as side rails, steps, platforms, safety devices or other support elements situated along the ladder.
[E06C 7/185] Devices providing a back support to a person on the ladder, e.g. cages -- The subclass E06C 7/185 covers devices that provide back support to a person on a ladder, such as cages, guard rails or harnesses that are attached to the ladder framework. These accessories are intended to reduce strain and improve safety while a user ascends, works on, or descends the ladder.
[E06C 7/186] Rail or rope for guiding a safety attachment, e.g. a fall arrest system -- E06C 7/186 covers rails or ropes that serve as guides for safety attachments such as fall-arrest devices on ladders and other fixed construction elements. These components are intended to direct the movement of a personal protective harness or rope during a climb, helping to prevent falls. The code classifies the guiding elements as accessory parts of ladders within the broader category of doors, windows, shutters and roller blinds.
[E06C 7/187] Guiding rail -- Guiding rails are linear elements incorporated into ladder systems to direct the movement of ladder sections, rungs or accessories, ensuring smooth and controlled operation. They serve as support or alignment components that help maintain proper positioning of the ladder during use or adjustment.
[E06C 7/188] Accessories for temporary stabilising a ladder, e.g. temporary attaching devices  ladder feet or struts  E06C7/42 , ladder heads  E06C7/48 -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Ladder feets or struts -> E06C7/42 - Ladder heads -> E06C7/48
[E06C 7/42] Ladder feet -- E06C 7/42 covers the lower attachments of ladders that rest on the floor, known as ladder feet. These components distribute the ladder's load, protect surfaces, and may be adjustable, rubber-coated or equipped with spikes for grip. They are classified under component parts and accessories of ladders in the fixed-construction group.
[E06C 7/423] Ladder stabilising struts -- E06C 7/423 designates ladder stabilising struts, which are component parts fitted to ladders to increase rigidity and prevent sway during use. These struts may be fixed or adjustable and serve as accessories that support the ladder's legs or side rails, enhancing safety in construction, maintenance and access tasks.
[E06C 7/426] Height adjustable supports for receiving both ladder feet -- Height-adjustable devices that support the feet of ladders, allowing vertical positioning to match varying floor or ground levels. They are used as accessories in fixed constructions such as doors, windows, shutters or roller blinds to provide stable ladder placement.
[E06C 7/44] Means for mounting ladders on uneven ground -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Means for mounting ladders on uneven ground, i.e. means for mounting ladders on a staircase
[E06C 7/46] Non-skid equipment -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Non-skid equipment for ladder feet, e.g. anchor in the ground
[E06C 7/48] Ladder heads -- E06C 7/48 covers ladder heads, the upper terminating sections of ladders that provide a platform, handrail or attachment point for connecting the ladder to a structure such as a wall, roof or balcony. These components are classified as accessories for ladders within the fixed-construction group covering doors, windows, shutters and rollers.
[E06C 7/482] Supports specially adapted for resting the ladder against or in a corner -- E06C 7/482 covers ladder-support devices that are designed to hold a ladder against a wall or in a corner, providing stability and preventing slip when the ladder rests on a fixed construction such as a door, window, shutter or roller blind. These accessories are classified under component or supporting parts for ladders.
[E06C 7/484] Supports specially adapted for use with a wall opening, e.g. windows -- This CPC subclass covers supports specially designed for use with a wall opening such as windows, including brackets, frames or other devices that hold or reinforce the opening. It falls under fixed constructions, doors, windows, shutters or roller blinds in general, ladders, component parts, supporting parts or accessories.
[E06C 7/486] Supports specially adapted to support a ladder on a gutter -- E06C 7/486 concerns specially-adapted supports that attach a ladder to a gutter, providing a stable climbing aid for accessing doors, windows, shutters or roller blinds. It falls under component parts and accessories for ladders in fixed-construction settings.
[E06C 7/488] Supports specially adapted to attach a ladder to a ridge of a roof -- E06C 7/488 covers supports specially designed to attach a ladder to the ridge of a roof, providing safe access for work on fixed building elements such as doors, windows, shutters or roller blinds. These components are ladder accessories that enable secure climbing on roof ridges.
[E06C 7/50] Joints or other connecting parts -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Joints or other connecting parts for fixing ladders to each other or to construction parts, e.g. ladderhooks to be fixed on a wall to suspend a ladder vertically or horizontally against a wall. Hinges are not considered here to be connections.
[E06C 7/505] Anchors being adapted to fix a ladder to a vertical structure, e.g. ladder hooks -- E06C 7/505 refers to anchors designed to secure a ladder to a vertical surface, such as hooks or other fixing devices that attach the ladder to walls, frames or other upright structures. These components are considered part of fixed constructions for doors, windows, shutters or roller blinds and are classified under ladders and their supporting parts.
[E06C 9/00] Ladders characterised by being permanently attached to fixed structures, e.g. fire escapes -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Ladders permanently attached to fixed structures, either rigidly mounted, e.g. in the form of climbing irons, or movably mounted, e.g. having rigid longitudinal members and being laterally displaceable, or having non-rigid longitudinal members like a rope or chain escape ladder.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Staircases -> E04F11/00 - Means for facilitating access to swimming pools -> E04H4/144
[E06C 9/02] rigidly mounted -- The CPC subclass E06C 9/02 covers ladders that are rigidly mounted on fixed structures, such as permanent fire-escape ladders attached to walls or other building elements. It falls under the broader group for doors, windows, shutters and roller blinds and specifically addresses fixed-construction ladders.
[E06C 9/04] in the form of climbing irons or the like -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Built-in climbing irons for poles -> A63B27/00 - Built-in climbing devices as construction element, e.g. staircases -> E04F11/00
[E06C 9/06] movably mounted -- E06C 9/06 designates ladders that are permanently fixed to structures such as fire-escape systems, allowing the ladder to be moved or repositioned along the attached framework for access. This sub-class falls under fixed constructions for doors, windows, shutters or roller blinds, specifically covering ladders that are integrated into the building and not freely portable.
[E06C 9/08] with rigid longitudinal members -- E06C 9/08 designates ladders that are permanently attached to a fixed construction such as a fire-escape and are built with rigid longitudinal members. The class falls under fixed constructions for doors, windows, shutters or roller blinds, specifically describing ladders integrated into the structure rather than portable units.
[E06C 9/085] ladders unfolded in the direction of the longitudinal members -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Ladders permanently attached to fixed structures in which the longitudinal members can be brought together, e.g. ladders in which the rungs are hinged on the longitudinal members
[E06C 9/10] forming part of a building, such as a balcony grid, window grid, or other window part -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Ladders forming part of a building with non-rigid longitudinal members -> E06C9/14
[E06C 9/12] laterally displaceable -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Ladders movably mounted on a fixed structure and laterally displaceable, e.g. library ladders
[E06C 9/14] with non-rigid longitudinal members, e.g. rope or chain ladders, ladders of the lazy-tongs type
[E21] EARTH DRILLING -- E21 Earth drilling refers to machines, tools and equipment designed to bore holes in the ground, encompassing drilling rigs, augers, rotary and percussion devices, and associated support systems. The classification covers technologies used for extracting minerals, installing foundations, constructing tunnels, placing utilities or exploiting geothermal resources, and any apparatus that creates or enlarges boreholes in earth materials.
[E21B] EARTH OR ROCK DRILLING -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Earth drilling, e.g. deep drilling; obtaining oil, gas, water, soluble or meltable materials or a slurry of minerals from wells. This subclass also includes drilling at shallow depths but over extended distances, such as drilling for laying a pipeline under surface obstacles.
[E21B 1/00] Methods or apparatus for drilling -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Machines and methods in which the drilling action is done principally by percussion.  The percussive drive is placed outside of the borehole, and the drilling can be for any depth, including drillholes and mining applications.  Illustrative examples of the subject matter classified in this place:  1.
[E21B 10/00] Drilling tools -- **Definition:** This place covers: Drill bits for any application in earth drilling, be it deep drilling for oil and gas or rock drilling for mining or for use in an underground space.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** This place does not cover: Drill bits specially adapted for deflecting the direction of boring E21B7/064 Drill bits with means for collecting substances E21B27/00  **Glossary:** In this place, the following terms or expressions are used with the meaning indicated: Arrangement of teeth or other cutting elements: placement or number of elements on a bit body Wear resisting parts: materials on the body of the bit adapted for withstanding wear caused by the flow of drilling fluid having cuttings entrained therein Chisel or button type inserts: Preformed cutting elements mounted on a distinct support:
[E21B 10/003] with cutting edges facing in opposite axial directions -- The code E21B 10/003 designates fixed-construction drilling tools used for earth or rock drilling whose cutting edges are arranged to face opposite axial directions, allowing simultaneous removal of material from both sides of the tool during operation. This configuration is employed to improve cutting efficiency and stability in fixed-type drill systems.
[E21B 10/006] providing a cutting edge which is self-renewable during drilling -- The class covers drill designs in which the cutting edge is automatically refreshed as it wears, allowing continuous cutting during earth or rock drilling. It includes mechanisms that replace or regenerate the edge while the tool remains in operation, improving durability and cutting efficiency.
[E21B 10/02] Core bits -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Core bits characterised by wear resisting parts -> E21B10/48
[E21B 10/04] with core destroying means -- E21B 10/04 covers drilling tools for fixed-construction earth or rock drilling that incorporate a core-destroying device, meaning the tool fragments or shatters the bore-core as it is produced rather than retrieving it intact. This subgroup falls under the hierarchy of Fixed Constructions → Earth Drilling → Earth or Rock Drilling → Drilling Tools.
[E21B 10/06] Roller core bits -- Roller core bits are fixed-construction drill bits used in earth or rock drilling to cut cylindrical cores. They employ a set of hardened rollers that roll along the bore surface, providing efficient low-friction removal of material in hard formations. Typically they are employed in mining, geotechnical and construction applications.
[E21B 10/08] Roller bits -- Roller bits are fixed-construction drill tools used for earth or rock drilling, typically mounted on a drill string to cut through hard formations. They consist of a set of rotating steel rollers or cones that crush and fracture the material, creating boreholes for mining, construction, and geotechnical applications.
[E21B 10/083] with longitudinal axis, e.g. wobbling or nutating roller bit  longitudinal axis roller reamers  E21B10/30 -- E21B 10/083 covers drilling tools whose cutting elements are arranged on a longitudinal axis, such as wobbling or nutating roller bits and longitudinal-axis roller reamers. These tools are used in earth or rock drilling and belong to the fixed-construction, earth-drilling subclass.
[E21B 10/086] with excentric movement -- E21B 10/086 covers drilling tools for earth or rock drilling whose cutting or working element is driven with an eccentric motion, meaning the tool's axis is offset from the rotation axis to impart a wobbling or tapping action. This movement improves chip breakage and penetration efficiency in fixed-construction drilling rigs.
[E21B 10/10] with roller axle supported at both ends -- E21B 10/10 covers drilling tools in earth-rock drilling where the roller axle is supported at both ends, enhancing stability and guiding the drill string during operation. This subclass falls under Fixed Constructions → Earth Drilling → Earth or Rock Drilling → Drilling tools.
[E21B 10/12] with discs cutters -- E21B 10/12 covers drilling tools for earth or rock that incorporate disc-shaped cutters. The disc cutters are arranged on a rotating bit or barrel and crush or cut the formation as the tool advances, commonly used in rotary or hammer drilling. These bits allow efficient removal of hard material in mining, construction, or geothermal drilling.
[E21B 10/14] combined with non-rolling cutters other than of leading-portion type -- E21B 10/14 pertains to fixed earth-drilling tools that incorporate non-rolling cutters which are not of the leading-portion type. Such tools use alternative cutter designs, like stationary or reciprocating cutters, to break rock without the rotating cutter elements typical of standard roller-cone bits. The classification applies to devices used in drilling operations where a mixed cutter arrangement is employed.
[E21B 10/16] characterised by tooth form or arrangement -- This subclass includes drilling bits and other earth-drilling tools where the defining feature is the geometry or pattern of the cutting teeth. It covers designs that vary the shape, spacing, or orientation of teeth to improve rock removal or durability. The classification applies to any rock-drilling tool whose performance is primarily defined by its tooth configuration.
[E21B 10/18] characterised by conduits or nozzles for drilling fluids -- The CPC subclass E21B 10/18 covers drilling tools for earth or rock drilling that are specifically designed with internal conduits or external nozzles to deliver drilling fluids to the borehole. These fluid-guiding features aid cooling, lubrication, and debris removal during the drilling process.
[E21B 1/02] Surface drives for drop hammers , e.g. with a cable -- This subclass of the Cooperative Patent Classification deals with surface-mounted drives that raise and release drop hammers used in earth or rock drilling, typically employing a cable or similar mechanism to lift the hammer and then let it fall.
[E21B 10/20] characterised by detachable or adjustable parts, e.g. legs or axles -- The E21B 10/20 subgroup pertains to earth-or-rock drilling tools whose legs, axles or similar structural elements can be detached or adjusted, enabling re-configuration for different ground conditions, easier transport, or assembly and disassembly of the equipment.
[E21B 10/22] characterised by bearing, lubrication or sealing details -- E21B 10/22 refers to drilling tools for earth or rock drilling that are defined by specific bearing, lubrication or sealing arrangements. The subclass groups inventions where the performance of the drill relies on these detailed mechanical or fluid-tight components, distinguishing them from generic drill-bit designs.
[E21B 10/23] with drilling fluid supply to the bearings -- The subclass E21B 10/23 classifies drilling tools that integrate a system for delivering drilling fluid directly to the tool's bearings, providing lubrication and cooling during operation. It applies to earth-or-rock drilling equipment where the fluid supply is routed to the bearing assemblies as part of the fixed construction.
[E21B 10/24] characterised by lubricating details -- E21B 10/24 pertains to drilling tools used in earth or rock drilling that incorporate lubricating elements such as oil passages, grease-filled bearings or self-lubricating surfaces to reduce friction during operation.
[E21B 10/246] with pumping means for feeding lubricant -- This subclass defines drilling tools for fixed earth or rock drilling installations that integrate a pump assembly to deliver lubricant directly to the cutting head during operation. It covers devices where the feeding of lubricant is achieved by built-in pumping means, improving drilling efficiency and tool lifespan.
[E21B 10/25] characterised by sealing details -- E21B 10/25 is a subclass of the EPC group for drilling tools used in earth or rock drilling, specifically covering designs that incorporate particular sealing details. These features aim to prevent fluid escape, maintain pressure integrity and enhance the reliability of the drill during operation.
[E21B 10/26] Drill bits with leading portion, i.e. drill bits with a pilot cutter -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Percussion drill bits with leading portion -> E21B10/40
[E21B 10/265] Bi-center drill bits, i.e. an integral bit and eccentric reamer used to simultaneously drill and underream the hole -- E21B 10/265 relates to bi-center drill bits that integrate a conventional drill bit with an eccentric reamer, enabling simultaneous drilling of a hole and underreaming to enlarge it. These tools are used in earth-rock drilling to produce enlarged or shaped boreholes in a single pass.
[E21B 10/28] with non-expansible roller cutters -- E21B 10/28 covers earth-drilling tools that use roller cutters which are fixed in size and cannot expand during operation. The classification applies to fixed-construction drill bits where the cutters are mounted on a non-expansible carrier, typically for hard rock or mineral drilling.
[E21B 10/30] Longitudinal axis roller reamers, e.g. reamer stabilisers -- Longitudinal axis roller reamers are drilling tools fitted on the main shaft of a drill string, using rollers to enlarge and guide the borehole while acting as reamer stabilisers during earth or rock drilling.
[E21B 10/32] with expansible cutting tools -- This subclass covers earth drilling tools that incorporate expansible cutting implements designed to enlarge or adjust during operation - improving rock engagement. It belongs to the drilling tools group within the earth or rock drilling section of the CPC.
[E21B 10/322] cutter shifted by fluid pressure  E21B10/345  takes precedence
[E21B 10/325] the cutter being shifted by a spring mechanism -- A drilling tool for earth or rock drilling in which the cutting element is mounted so that it can move relative to the tool body and is displaced by a spring mechanism, allowing the cutter to shift during operation to improve chip removal or adapt to varying rock conditions. This spring-biased cutter arrangement falls within the CPC subclass for fixed-construction earth-drilling tools.
[E21B 10/327] the cutter being pivoted about a longitudinal axis  E21B10/34  takes precedence -- E21B 10/327 covers drilling tools in which the cutting elements are mounted so they can pivot about their longitudinal axis, allowing the cutter to adjust its orientation during rotation. This design is used in fixed-construction earth or rock drilling bits to improve penetration and reduce wear.
[E21B 10/34] of roller-cutter type -- E21B 10/34 covers roller-cutter type drilling tools used in fixed constructions for earth or rock drilling. The tools employ rotating cutting rollers to fragment material in boreholes, typical for mining or civil-engineering drilling operations.
[E21B 10/345] cutter shifted by fluid pressure -- E21B 10/345 covers drilling tools in which the cutting element is displaced or actuated by hydraulic or pneumatic fluid pressure, allowing the cutter to shift during earth or rock drilling operations. This mechanism is used in fixed construction drilling equipment to improve penetration or adapt to varying material hardness.
[E21B 10/36] Percussion drill bits -- Percussion drill bits are drilling tools used in earth or rock drilling that break material by repeated hammering impacts rather than rotary cutting. They are mounted on drill rods and are suited for creating holes in hard formations where a percussive action provides efficient penetration.
[E21B 10/38] characterised by conduits or nozzles for drilling fluids -- E21B 10/38 classifies drilling tools for earth or rock that incorporate internal conduits or external nozzles to convey drilling fluid to the cutting zone. The fluid delivery system is used for cooling, lubricating, removing cuttings or controlling pressure during drilling operations. This subgroup falls under Fixed Constructions → Earth Drilling → Earth or Rock Drilling → Drilling tools.
[E21B 1/04] Devices for reversing the movement of the rod or cable at the surface -- The CPC entry E21B 1/04 covers mechanical devices that change the direction of motion of a drill rod or cable at the surface of a drilling installation. Such devices, for example reversing gearboxes, pulleys or clutch assemblies, enable the rod or cable to be driven upward when the power source is rotating in the opposite direction, facilitating controlled raising and lowering of the drill string during earth-or-rock drilling operations.
[E21B 10/40] with leading portion -- The E21B 10/40 subclass covers drilling tools that incorporate a leading portion extending ahead of the main tool body. This forward-projecting element, such as a pilot bit or guide section, is designed to initiate or guide the cutting action when drilling into earth or rock.
[E21B 10/42] Rotary drag type drill bits with teeth, blades or like cutting elements, e.g. fork-type bits, fish tail bits -- This subclass refers to rotary-drag drill bits employed in earth or rock drilling that use teeth, blades or similar cutting elements to fracture and remove material, including designs such as fork-type or fish-tail bits. The bits are mounted on a rotating shaft and rely on the drag action of the cutting elements to advance the bore.
[E21B 10/43] characterised by the arrangement of teeth or other cutting elements -- The subclass E21B 10/43 pertains to drilling tools used in fixed-construction earth-or-rock drilling whose defining feature is the specific arrangement of teeth or other cutting elements, such as their spacing, pattern or orientation, which influences cutting performance and tool wear.
[E21B 10/44] Bits with helical conveying portion, e.g. screw type bits -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Rotary drag type drill bits with teeth, blades or like cutting elements, e.g. fork-type bits or fish tail bits -> E21B10/42
[E21B 10/445] percussion type, e.g. for masonry -- E21B 10/445 classifies percussion-type drilling tools designed for masonry work, in which the tool breaks and penetrates material by delivering repeated blows to the bit. It falls under the hierarchy of fixed constructions, earth drilling, earth or rock drilling, and drilling tools.
[E21B 10/46] characterised by wear resisting parts, e.g. diamond inserts -- The E21B 10/46 subclass groups patents for drilling tools used in earth or rock drilling that incorporate wear-resisting elements such as diamond inserts. It covers designs where these hard materials are part of the cutting or bearing surfaces to extend tool life and improve performance. This classification is found under Fixed Constructions → Earth Drilling → Earth or Rock Drilling → Drilling tools.
[E21B 10/48] the bit being of core type -- E21B 10/48 designates drilling tools in fixed-construction earth or rock drilling equipment whose cutting element is a core-type bit, i.e., a cylindrical cutter that extracts a solid core of material while advancing the bore. The subclass covers the geometry, mounting and operation of such core bits.
[E21B 10/485] with inserts in form of chisels, blades or the like -- E21B 10/485 covers drilling tools for earth or rock drilling that incorporate inserts shaped as chisels, blades or similar cutting elements. These inserts may be fixed or replaceable and are intended to improve material removal and tool wear resistance in fixed-construction drill rigs.
[E21B 10/50] the bit being of roller type -- E21B 10/50 classifies drilling tools whose cutting element is a roller-type bit, used in earth or rock drilling operations. The roller bit rotates and rolls along the borehole surface to break material, providing efficient advance in fixed-construction drilling rigs. This subclass falls under the broader categories of earth drilling and drilling tools.
[E21B 10/52] with chisel- or button-type inserts -- The subclass E21B 10/52 covers drilling tools for earth or rock drilling that use chisel-type or button-type inserts, which are hardened cutting elements mounted on the bit to fracture material. These tools belong to the fixed-construction category of earth-drilling equipment.
[E21B 10/54] the bit being of the rotary drag type, e.g. fork-type bits -- E21B 10/54 classifies drill bits that employ a rotary-drag mechanism, specifically fork-type bits, for use in earth or rock drilling. The entry falls under Fixed Constructions → Earth Drilling → Earth or Rock Drilling → Drilling tools and defines this particular bit design within the Cooperative Patent Classification system.
[E21B 10/55] with preformed cutting elements -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Rotary drag type bits with blades having preformed cutting elements mounted on a distinct support, e.g. polycrystalline inserts.
[E21B 10/56] Button-type inserts -- Button-type inserts are replaceable wear elements mounted on the cutting face of fixed rock-drilling tools. They are typically made of hard materials such as carbide and are shaped as small buttons to provide concentrated cutting action and easy replacement when worn.
[E21B 10/567] with preformed cutting elements mounted on a distinct support, e.g. polycrystalline inserts -- A drilling tool in which the cutting bits are pre-shaped and affixed to a separate holder, such as polycrystalline inserts fixed on a distinct support, enabling interchangeable or replaceable cutting surfaces for earth or rock drilling applications.
[E21B 10/5671] with chip breaking arrangements -- The CPC subclass E21B 10/5671 covers drilling tools for earth or rock drilling that include chip-breaking arrangements, i.e. features that fragment or control the chips produced during cutting. It falls under the fixed-construction category of earth-drilling equipment.
[E21B 10/5673] having a non planar or non circular cutting face -- E21B 10/5673 covers drilling tools whose cutting face is not flat or round, such as blades or inserts shaped with irregular geometry to enhance rock breakage. It applies to equipment used in fixed-construction earth or rock drilling operations.
[E21B 10/5676] having a cutting face with different segments, e.g. mosaic-type inserts -- E21B 10/5676 classifies fixed-construction drill bits for earth or rock drilling whose cutting face is composed of separate segments, such as mosaic-type inserts. The segmented geometry allows different wear or cutting characteristics across the bit.
[E21B 10/573] characterised by support details, e.g. the substrate construction or the interface between the substrate and the cutting element -- This subclass covers drilling tools where the support structure, such as the substrate or the connection between the substrate and the cutting element, is specifically described. It includes designs that define the material, shape or mounting of the substrate and how it interfaces with the cutting insert, aimed at improving stability and cutting performance in earth or rock drilling.
[E21B 10/5735] Interface between the substrate and the cutting element -- E21B 10/5735 covers the interface between the substrate and the cutting element in fixed earth-drilling tools, describing how the cutting insert is joined, clamped or bonded to the tool body to transfer loads and control wear. It falls under the hierarchy of earth-drilling constructions for rock drilling.
[E21B 10/58] Chisel-type inserts -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Drill bits characterised by wear resisting parts, the bit being of core type with inserts in form of chisels, blades or the like -> E21B10/485 - Drill bits characterised by wear resisting parts, the bit being of roller type with chisel- or button-type inserts -> E21B10/52
[E21B 10/60] characterised by conduits or nozzles for drilling fluids -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Roller drill bits characterised by conduits or nozzles for drilling fluids -> E21B10/18 - Percussion drill bits characterised by conduits or nozzles for drilling fluids -> E21B10/38
[E21B 10/602] the bit being a rotary drag type bit with blades -- E21B 10/602 designates rotary-drag drill bits that incorporate cutting blades for fixed-construction earth or rock drilling. The classification covers bits in which the cutting action is produced by rotating the bit itself, with blades that shear the formation as the bit advances.
[E21B 10/605] the bit being a core-bit -- The CPC subclass E21B 10/605 pertains to drilling tools whose cutting element is a core-bit, used in fixed-construction earth-drilling equipment for extracting cylinders of rock or soil. It covers the design and features of such core-bits employed in earth or rock drilling operations.
[E21B 10/61] characterised by the nozzle structure -- E21B 10/61 is a subclass of the Cooperative Patent Classification for fixed-construction earth-drilling tools, specifically those whose performance is defined by a nozzle structure that directs fluid or abrasive jets onto the drill bit or work-piece. This code groups inventions where the nozzle geometry is a key technical feature.
[E21B 10/62] characterised by parts, e.g. cutting elements, which are detachable or adjustable -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Roller bits characterised by detachable or adjustable parts -> E21B10/20 - Augers with leading portion or with detachable parts -> E21B10/44 - Drill bits characterised by the whole or part thereof being insertable into or removable from the borehole without withdrawing the drilling pipe -> E21B10/64
[E21B 10/627] with plural detachable cutting elements -- E21B 10/627 covers fixed earth-drilling tools in which the cutting elements are provided in plural and can be detached and replaced individually, enabling rapid renewal of worn surfaces while maintaining the overall drill structure.
