This subclass applies to rock and earth drilling devices that employ multiple removable cutter inserts.
[E21B 10/633] independently detachable -- E21B 10/633 covers drilling tools whose components can be removed or replaced without dismantling the entire assembly, allowing the drill head, bits or auxiliary devices to be detached independently for maintenance, tool change or repair. This facilitates quick swapping of wear parts while the main drilling apparatus remains fixed in the borehole.
[E21B 10/64] characterised by the whole or part thereof being insertable into or removable from the borehole without withdrawing the drilling pipe -- The class covers drilling tools designed so that the whole tool or parts of it can be inserted into or removed from a borehole while the drill string stays in place, avoiding the need to withdraw the pipe. It includes releasable bits, reamers, measurement devices and similar components that can be run or retrieved through the pipe.
[E21B 10/66] the cutting element movable through the drilling pipe and laterally shiftable -- E21B 10/66 covers drilling tools for fixed-construction earth or rock drilling in which the cutting element can travel through the drill pipe and be laterally shifted. This allows the bit to be positioned off-center for steering the bore or engaging different rock layers without moving the whole pipe.
[E21B 11/00] Other drilling tools -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Other drilling tools different from the drill bits of E21B10/00
[E21B 11/005] Hand operated drilling tools -- E21B 11/005 covers manually powered devices used to drill into soil or rock, such as hand-held augers, brace-driven bits and similar non-motorized tools that create boreholes for construction, sampling or installation tasks. The classification groups these tools with other fixed-construction earth-drilling equipment.
[E21B 11/02] Boring rams -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Impact driven tools or devices for forming holes in ground or soil.
[E21B 11/04] Boring grabs -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Boring devices that both form the borehole and collect material for removal from the borehole.  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:
[E21B 11/06] with driven cutting chains or similarly driven tools -- This subclass concerns earth-or-rock drilling equipment in which the cutting action is provided by driven cutting chains or comparable powered tools. It includes chain-driven drill bits and similar mechanisms used on fixed construction drilling machines for rock or soil penetration.
[E21B 1/12] with a reciprocating impulse member -- E21B 1/12 relates to earth-or-rock drilling methods or apparatus that employ a reciprocating impulse member to generate the drilling action; the moving part repeatedly strikes or pushes the drill bit or tool, providing a pulsed force that advances the bore without continuous rotation.
[E21B 1/14] driven by a rotating mechanism -- E21B 1/14 covers drilling methods or apparatus in which the drill is powered by a rotating mechanism, such as a motor or gearbox, to transmit rotary motion to the drill bit. It applies to fixed-construction drilling rigs used for earth or rock drilling.
[E21B 1/16] with spring-mounted reciprocating masses, e.g. with air cushion -- E21B 1/16 covers methods or apparatus for earth or rock drilling that employ spring-mounted reciprocating masses, for example an air cushion, to produce impact forces. The spring-loaded system creates a periodic reciprocating motion that assists the drill bit in breaking material, enhancing efficiency and reducing tool wear.
[E21B 1/18] with elastic joining of the drive to the push-rod by double buffer springs -- E21B 1/18 relates to earth-drilling machines in which the drive is elastically linked to the push-rod by a pair of buffer springs, providing shock absorption and flexible power transmission during rock or soil drilling.
[E21B 1/20] formed as centrifugal hammers -- E21B 1/20 designates drilling apparatus that employ centrifugal hammers, where a rotating mass is formed to generate impact forces on the drill bit. The subclass covers methods and tools using this centrifugal-hammer principle for fixed-construction earth or rock drilling operations.
[E21B 12/00] Accessories for drilling tools -- E21B 12/00 covers auxiliary parts and devices that are fitted to or used with drilling tools for earth or rock drilling, including bits, collars, stabilisers, fluid delivery systems, and other accessories that enhance tool performance or handling. It encompasses all supporting components that are not the primary drill itself but are essential for operation and maintenance.
[E21B 12/02] Wear indicators -- E21B 12/02 designates wear indicators, devices attached to drilling tools that signal the condition of the bit or other components during earth or rock drilling. These accessories help operators monitor tool wear and schedule replacements, improving efficiency and safety. The class falls under Fixed Constructions > Earth Drilling > Earth or Rock Drilling > Accessories for drilling tools.
[E21B 12/04] Drill bit protectors -- Drill-bit protectors are accessories mounted on or surrounding a drill bit to shield it from wear, impact and abrasion during earth or rock drilling operations. They are designed to extend bit life, reduce breakage and improve safety by preventing direct contact between the cutting elements and the formation or surrounding equipment. This element falls under the Cooperative Patent Classification code E21B 12/04 for accessories to drilling tools.
[E21B 12/06] Mechanical cleaning devices -- The CPC code E21B 12/06 designates mechanical cleaning devices used as accessories for earth-or-rock drilling tools. It includes equipment such as brushes, scrapers, air- or water-jet systems and other mechanisms that remove cuttings, mud and debris from drill bits, rods and associated hardware during or after operation.
[E21B 1/22] driven by electromagnets -- This subclass groups drilling tools whose motion is produced by electromagnets, using magnetic attraction or repulsion to drive the drill rod or shaft. It applies to both rotary and percussive earth-or-rock drilling machines where the power source is an electromagnetic actuator rather than conventional mechanical or hydraulic drives.
[E21B 1/24] the impulse member being a piston driven directly by fluid pressure -- E21B 1/24 covers drilling devices in which the impulse-generating member is a piston that receives its striking force directly from fluid pressure, eliminating intermediate mechanical transmission. This subclass falls under fixed-construction earth-or-rock drilling methods and apparatus, specifying fluid-driven piston hammers for rock penetration.
[E21B 1/26] by liquid pressure -- E21B 1/26 covers fixed drilling installations that advance a drill bit in earth or rock by means of liquid pressure, such as hydraulic or mud drilling systems. The classification applies to methods and apparatus where fluid pressure is the primary driving force for the drilling operation.
[E21B 1/28] working with pulses -- E21B 1/28 covers drilling techniques that employ pulsed energy or pressure to advance a drill bit, such as hydraulic, pneumatic or mechanical pulse generation used to break rock, reduce torque and improve penetration. The subclass includes both rotary and percussive systems where the drilling action is driven by repetitive pulses rather than continuous force. It falls under fixed-construction earth-drilling methods and apparatus.
[E21B 1/30] by air, steam or gas pressure -- E21B 1/30 refers to fixed earth-drilling installations that create boreholes using the force of air, steam or other gases. The class covers drilling machines or systems in which pneumatic, steam-driven or gas-pressure mechanisms provide the cutting energy, typically for construction, mining or civil-engineering projects.
[E21B 1/32] working with pulses -- E21B 1/32 covers drilling methods that employ pulsed or impact forces to advance a drill bit, using intermittent energy spikes rather than continuous rotation. The classification includes percussive, hammer, and other impact-driven techniques for earth or rock drilling within fixed-construction machines.
[E21B 1/34] the impulse member being a piston of an internal-combustion engine -- This CPC subclass covers drilling installations in which the driving impulse is supplied by a piston of an internal-combustion engine, applied to fixed earth-drilling constructions for rock or soil.
[E21B 1/36] Tool-carrier piston type, i.e. in which the tool is connected to an impulse member -- E21B 1/36 covers piston-type tool carriers in fixed-construction earth or rock drilling rigs where the drill tool is linked to an impulse member that delivers rapid impact strokes to the bit. The classification applies to apparatus that use a reciprocating piston to generate the impulses needed for drilling.
[E21B 1/38] Hammer piston type, i.e. in which the tool bit or anvil is hit by an impulse member -- E21B 1/38 defines hammer-piston drilling devices in which a moving piston delivers an impulse to the tool bit or anvil, producing repeated blows that advance the drill. This mechanism is used in earth or rock drilling machines to break material by rapid impacts rather than continuous rotation.
[E21B 15/00] Other equipment or details for drilling -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Drilling derricks or masts having such dimensions that require considerable complication for assembling and disassembling them, so that they cannot be considered as "movable" or "portable" units:
[E21B 15/003] adapted to be moved on their substructure, e.g. with skidding means; adapted to drill a plurality of wells -- E21B 15/003 pertains to fixed drilling constructions that can be moved on their own substructure, for example by skidding, and are designed to drill a plurality of wells in earth or rock. The code applies to equipment or details that enable multi-well drilling operations.
[E21B 15/006] Means for anchoring the drilling machine to the ground -- E21B 15/006 covers devices that secure a drilling machine to the ground, such as anchors, stabilization frames or ground-spike assemblies, ensuring the rig remains fixed during operation. These anchoring means are used in earth or rock drilling equipment to prevent movement and improve drilling efficiency.
[E21B 15/02] specially adapted for underwater drilling -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Supports for the drilling machine specially adapted for directional drilling, e.g. slant hole rigs -> E21B15/04
[E21B 15/04] specially adapted for directional drilling, e.g. slant hole rigs -- E21B 15/04 covers fixed drilling installations that are specially modified for directional or slant-hole drilling. The classification includes rigs and related structures designed to deviate from vertical drilling paths, enabling the creation of angled boreholes in earth or rock. It falls under the broader group of other equipment or details for drilling.
[E21B 15/045] Hydraulic, pneumatic or electric circuits for their positioning -- E21B 15/045 covers the control systems that power and guide the positioning of fixed earth-drilling equipment, including hydraulic, pneumatic and electric circuits that drive the drill's movement, alignment and positioning mechanisms. It relates to the infrastructure that ensures precise placement and operation of rock-drilling tools in mining or construction settings.
[E21B 17/00] Drilling rods or pipes -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Pipes for use in wellbores, for drilling but also for subsequent operations, such as casings or risers. Couplings between pipes. Centralisers and other accessories for pipes.
[E21B 17/003] with electrically conducting or insulating means  E21B17/028  and  E21B17/023  take precedence -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Arrangements for connecting cables or wirelines to downhole devices -> E21B17/023 - Electrical or electro-magnetic connections -> E21B17/028
[E21B 17/006] Accessories for drilling pipes, e.g. cleaners  wear protectors  E21B17/10 ; handling drilling pipes  E21B19/00 ; thread protectors  B65D59/00 -- E21B 17/006 concerns auxiliary devices used with drilling rods or pipes in earth-or-rock drilling, including cleaning tools, wear-protective sleeves, mechanisms for handling and positioning the pipes, and thread-protectors that shield and preserve the pipe connections during transport, storage or operation.
[E21B 17/01] Risers -- E21B 17/01 covers risers, which are the vertical or inclined pipe sections that connect a drilling platform or vessel to the wellbore. They form part of the drilling rods or pipes subgroup and are designed to transmit drilling forces, manage pressure differentials, and provide a conduit for fluids and tools between the surface equipment and the underground drill string. This classification pertains to fixed-construction earth or rock drilling systems.
[E21B 17/012] with buoyancy elements  E21B17/015  takes precedence -- E21B 17/012 refers to drill rods or pipes used in earth-or-rock drilling that incorporate buoyancy elements to offset weight, making handling easier and reducing stress on the equipment. It resides within the Fixed Constructions → Earth Drilling → Earth or Rock Drilling → Drilling Rods or Pipes hierarchy, while the more specific subclass E21B 17/015 supersedes it.
[E21B 17/015] Non-vertical risers, e.g. articulated or catenary-type -- E21B 17/015 covers non-vertical risers used in drilling operations, including articulated or catenary-type risers that support drill strings at angles other than vertical. These components belong to fixed constructions for earth or rock drilling and are classified under drilling rods or pipes.
[E21B 17/017] Bend restrictors for limiting stress on risers -- This class covers devices fitted to drilling rods or pipes that restrict bending in order to reduce stress on risers during earth or rock drilling operations. Bend restrictors are commonly employed on fixed-construction drilling rigs to protect riser integrity by limiting angular deviation and mitigating mechanical loads.
[E21B 17/02] Couplings -- E21B 17/02 covers couplings designed for joining drilling rods or pipes in fixed earth-drilling constructions. The class pertains to equipment used in rock or soil drilling systems where rod or pipe sections must be securely connected to transmit torque and axial forces.
[E21B 17/021] Devices for subsurface connecting or disconnecting by rotation -- This CPC subclass covers rotating mechanisms that join or separate drilling rods or pipes below the surface during earth or rock drilling operations. It includes devices such as threaded couplings, rotary lock systems and comparable tools that are actuated by turning to make or break the connection.
[E21B 17/023] Arrangements for connecting cables or wirelines to downhole devices -- E21B 17/023 covers the specific methods and hardware used to attach electrical cables or wirelines to tools and equipment placed downhole in fixed-construction drilling rigs, such as those for earth or rock drilling and drilling rods or pipes. It addresses connectors, couplings, and related arrangements that ensure reliable signal or power transmission while the drill string is in operation.
[E21B 17/025] Side entry subs -- E21B 17/025 covers side-entry subs, which are sections of drilling rods or pipes designed to be attached to a main drill string from the side rather than the end, enabling directional changes or tool insertion in fixed-construction earth or rock drilling operations.
[E21B 17/026] Arrangements for fixing cables or wirelines to the outside of downhole devices -- The E21B 17/026 classification concerns devices used in fixed-construction earth-drilling systems for securing cables or wirelines to the exterior of downhole tools. It covers the mechanical or structural arrangements that attach transmission lines to drill rods, pipes or other downhole equipment during rock-or-earth drilling operations.
[E21B 17/028] Electrical or electro-magnetic connections -- E21B 17/028 covers the means for providing electrical or electromagnetic connections to drilling rods or pipes used in fixed earth-drilling constructions. It includes couplings, contacts and wiring systems that allow power or signal transmission along the drill string while maintaining structural integrity.
[E21B 17/0283] characterised by the coupling being contactless, e.g. inductive
[E21B 17/0285] characterised by electrically insulating elements -- E21B 17/0285 is a CPC subclass for earth-or-rock drilling equipment, specifically drilling rods or pipes that incorporate electrically insulating elements. The provision covers fixed construction designs where the insulating material is integrated to prevent electrical conduction during drilling operations.
[E21B 17/03] between drilling rod or pipe and drill motor , e.g. between drilling rod and hammer -- E21B 17/03 covers devices that join a drilling rod or pipe to a drill motor, such as couplings, adapters or connectors that transmit torque and axial force between the rod and the motor or hammer in earth-rock drilling applications.
[E21B 17/04] between rod and bit or between rod and rod -- E21B 17/04 covers devices that join a drilling rod to a drill bit or to another rod, including couplings, connectors and intermediate fittings that transmit torque and axial force in earth-or-rock drilling rigs.
[E21B 17/041] specially adapted for coiled tubing -- E21B 17/041 designates drilling rods or pipes specially adapted for use with coiled tubing, falling under the fixed constructions category of earth or rock drilling. These adapted components enable continuous coiled-tube operations in drilling systems.
[E21B 17/042] Threaded -- E21B 17/042 relates to the use of threads on drilling rods or pipes for earth or rock drilling. It addresses the design of threaded connections that join sections of a drill string, enabling transmission of torque and axial forces while maintaining alignment and sealing.
[E21B 17/0423] with plural threaded sections, e.g. with two-step threads
[E21B 17/0426] with a threaded cylindrical portion, e.g. for percussion rods -- E21B 17/0426 classifies drilling rods or pipes used in earth or rock drilling that feature a threaded cylindrical portion, such as those employed as percussion rods. This subclass falls under Fixed Constructions → Earth Drilling → Earth or Rock Drilling → Drilling rods or pipes.
[E21B 17/043] with locking means -- E21B 17/043 covers drilling rods or pipes used in earth or rock drilling that incorporate locking means to secure the components together during operation. The locking features may include mechanical catches, wedges, or other devices that prevent axial movement and ensure a rigid assembly of the drill string. This subclass falls under fixed constructions for earth-drilling equipment.
[E21B 17/046] with ribs, pins, or jaws, and complementary grooves or the like, e.g. bayonet catches -- E21B 17/046 classifies fixed earth-drilling constructions in which the drill rods or pipes are provided with ribs, pins, jaws or similar elements that engage complementary grooves, such as bayonet-type catches, to transmit torque and axial force.
[E21B 17/0465] characterised by radially inserted locking elements -- The E21B 17/0465 class covers fixed-construction earth-drilling rods or pipes that secure themselves by locking elements inserted radially into the tube wall. These radial locks provide a positive, repeatable connection for earth or rock drilling tools, improving torque transmission and preventing axial separation.
[E21B 17/05] Swivel joints -- E21B 17/05 classifies swivel joints designed for connecting drilling rods or pipes in earth or rock drilling applications. These components allow rotation and angular movement between sections while maintaining the integrity of the drillstring, facilitating the transmission of torque and fluid. The code falls under the Fixed Constructions → Earth Drilling → Earth or Rock Drilling hierarchy.
[E21B 17/06] Releasing-joints, e.g. safety joints -- The code E21B 17/06 covers releasing-joints, such as safety joints, that enable quick separation of drilling rods or pipes in earth-rock drilling. These devices disconnect the rod when overload or a fault occurs, protecting the drilling assembly and operators by preventing damage or injury.
[E21B 17/07] Telescoping joints for varying drill string lengths -- This CPC subclass deals with telescoping joints that permit drill strings to be lengthened or shortened while operating, providing adjustable drill-string configurations. The joints are applied to drilling rods or pipes used in earth or rock drilling and fall under code E21B 17/07 within the Fixed Constructions hierarchy.
[E21B 17/073] with axial rotation -- A subclass for fixed-construction earth-or-rock drilling equipment that specifies drilling rods or pipes which rotate about their longitudinal axis while advancing, providing axial rotation to the drill string during operation.
[E21B 17/076] between rod or pipe and drill bit -- E21B 17/076 relates to the connection between a drilling rod or pipe and the drill bit in earth-or-rock drilling machines. It covers devices, couplings, and methods that join the rod or pipe to the bit, ensuring secure transmission of torque and axial force during drilling operations. This subclass falls under Fixed Constructions → Earth Drilling → Earth or Rock Drilling → Drilling rods or pipes.
[E21B 17/08] Casing joints -- E21B 17/08 covers the joining of casing sections used in earth-or rock-drilling operations. It includes devices, couplings, threads and sealing arrangements that connect drilling rods or pipes to form a continuous well casing.
[E21B 17/085] Riser connections  connectors for wellhead  E21B33/038 -- The E21B 17/085 subclass relates to riser connections and connectors for wellheads used in earth-or-rock drilling, specifically within fixed construction drilling rods or pipes. It covers mechanical couplings, joints and sealing devices that join the riser to the wellhead and transmit drilling fluids or pressures, including various clamp, bolt, and latch systems employed on offshore or on-shore drilling rigs.
[E21B 17/0853] Connections between sections of riser provided with auxiliary lines, e.g. kill and choke lines -- The code E21B 17/0853 pertains to the coupling of riser sections that include built-in auxiliary lines such as kill and choke lines, used in earth-drilling rigs to convey control fluids and manage well pressure. It covers the design of connections that maintain the integrity of both the main riser pipe and the attached auxiliary conduits.
[E21B 17/10] Wear protectors -- E21B 17/10 refers to wear protectors mounted on drilling rods or pipes employed in earth-or-rock drilling. These devices shield the rod surfaces from abrasion and damage during drilling operations.
[E21B 17/1007] for the internal surface of a pipe, e.g. wear bushings for underwater well-heads -- This classification covers devices applied to the inner wall of a pipe, such as wear-resistant bushings used in underwater well-head assemblies, providing protection against abrasion and corrosion during drilling operations. It is positioned under Fixed Constructions, Earth Drilling, Earth or Rock Drilling and Drilling rods or pipes.
[E21B 17/1014] Flexible or expansible centering means, e.g. with pistons pressing against the wall of the well  E21B17/1042  takes precedence -- This subclass deals with devices that center a drill pipe within a borehole by expanding or flexing to press against the wall, often using pistons or similar mechanisms to maintain alignment during drilling operations. It falls under fixed constructions for earth or rock drilling, specifically drilling rods or pipes.
[E21B 17/1021] with articulated arms or arcuate springs -- This subcategory covers drilling rod or pipe assemblies that incorporate articulated arms or curved spring elements to allow flexible movement, improving alignment and load distribution during earth or rock drilling. It includes mechanisms where the rods are linked by hinged or spring-shaped joints that can bend or swing to accommodate irregular bore trajectories.
[E21B 17/1028] with arcuate springs only, e.g. baskets with outwardly bowed strips for cementing operations
[E21B 17/1035] for plural rods, pipes or lines, e.g. for control lines -- This subclass concerns devices or methods that handle several drilling rods, pipes or lines simultaneously, such as systems that guide, support or connect multiple rods or control lines during drilling operations. It includes arrangements for bundling, tensioning or managing plural conduits in earth or rock drilling.
[E21B 17/1042] Elastomer protector or centering means -- E21B 17/1042, titled Elastomer protector or centering means, is a CPC subclass under Fixed Constructions → Earth Drilling → Earth or Rock Drilling → Drilling rods or pipes. It covers elastomeric devices such as rubber sleeves, buffers or centring inserts that shield drill rods from wear and keep them properly aligned while the rod is advanced through the borehole.
[E21B 17/105] split type -- The split type refers to drilling rods or pipes that are manufactured in two longitudinal halves which are assembled and secured together, allowing easier repair, replacement of worn sections, and reduced weight compared to solid bars. This design is used in earth-rock drilling tools to improve handling and adaptability in fixed-construction drilling operations.
[E21B 17/1057] Centralising devices with rollers or with a relatively rotating sleeve  E21B17/1014  takes precedence
[E21B 17/1064] Pipes or rods with a relatively rotating sleeve -- E21B 17/1064 covers drilling rods or pipes equipped with a sleeve that can rotate relative to the main body. The rotating sleeve is used to transmit torque, enable steering or adjust the drilling direction while the rod remains stationary, and is applicable to fixed-construction earth or rock drilling systems.
[E21B 17/1071] specially adapted for pump rods, e.g. sucker rods -- This group includes drilling rods or pipes that are specially modified to work with pump rods such as sucker rods, featuring couplings, sleeves or reinforced sections that allow efficient transmission of mechanical motion and fluid. It is used mainly in oil-field or well-bore drilling where reciprocating pumps drive the rods.
[E21B 17/1078] Stabilisers or centralisers for casing, tubing or drill pipes  devices for off-center positioning  E21B17/10 ;  E21B17/1007  -  E21B17/1064  take precedence -- Stabilizers or centralizers designed to keep casing, tubing or drill pipes positioned off-center or centered within a borehole during earth or rock drilling operations. They are used in fixed-construction drilling equipment to control pipe alignment and improve drilling stability. This falls under CPC group E21B 17/1078 within the Earth drilling hierarchy.
[E21B 17/1085] Wear protectors; Blast joints; Hard facing -- A classification for protective elements applied to drilling rods or pipes used in earth or rock drilling, covering wear protectors, blast joints and hard-facing treatments that shield the components from abrasion, impact and corrosion during drilling operations.
[E21B 17/1092] Gauge section of drill bits -- The gauge section of drill bits is the component that defines the final diameter of a borehole, ensuring consistent hole size during earth-or-rock drilling. This CPC subclass covers the design, dimensions and construction of that gauge portion for fixed-construction drilling rods or pipes.
[E21B 17/12] Devices for placing or drawing out wear protectors -- Devices used to install, retrieve or replace wear-protective sleeves, pads or similar elements on drilling rods or pipes in earth-drilling operations, allowing the protective components to be positioned along the rod's length and removed for maintenance or replacement. These tools support efficient handling of wear protectors during rock or soil drilling.
[E21B 17/14] Casing shoes -- Casing shoes are hardened protective fittings mounted on the lower end of a drilling casing to guide the pipe through the borehole, protect it from abrasion and help keep it centered while drilling fluid passes. They are typically made of steel or composite material and are used in earth or rock drilling operations.
[E21B 17/16] Drill collars -- Drill collars are heavy, thick-walled tubular members placed in the lower part of a drilling string to transmit weight on the bit and provide rigidity against bending and torque. Typically forged from high-strength steel, they may be solid or contain a concentric bore for fluid flow and often feature replaceable wear sleeves for abrasive formations. They are essential components in rotary drilling operations for earth or rock penetration.

[E21B 17/20] Flexible or articulated drilling pipes -- E21B 17/20 designates flexible or articulated drilling pipes employed in earth or rock drilling, enabling the pipe string to bend or articulate around deviations in the borehole. These components belong to the drilling rods or pipes subgroup within fixed constructions.
[E21B 17/203] with plural fluid passages -- The subclass E21B 17/203 covers fixed earth-or-rock drilling rigs whose drill rods or pipes are equipped with multiple fluid passages for delivering drilling fluid, such as mud, coolant or lubricants, to the drill bit. It pertains to the design of drilling-rod or pipe assemblies that provide more than one channel for fluid flow within the same structural member.
[E21B 17/206] with conductors, e.g. electrical, optical -- E21B 17/206 covers drilling rods or pipes used in fixed-construction earth or rock drilling that incorporate conductive elements such as electrical wiring or optical fibres, allowing power or data transmission along the drill string.
[E21B 17/22] Rods or pipes with helical structure -- E21B 17/22 covers drilling rods or pipes that incorporate a helical shape or thread along their length, used in fixed earth-drilling installations to convey rotary motion or fluid. The helical structure can provide stiffness, torque transmission, or improve soil displacement.
[E21B 19/00] Handling rods, casings, tubes or the like outside the borehole, e.g. in the derrick -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Machines or methods performed in the surface for assembling pipes, hoisting pipes, pushing pipes into a borehole, storing pipes and similar. The application is typically for drilling, i.e. vertical, horizontal, deep drilling, mining or other earth drilling, but can also be for well treatment or other.
[E21B 19/002] specially adapted for underwater drilling  E21B19/09 ,  E21B19/143  take precedence -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Handling and supporting of tubulars in relation to underwater drilling operations other than feeding the drill string into the borehole or controlling the weight on bit. [E21B 19/004] supporting a riser from a drilling or production platform -- The CPC entry E21B 19/004 relates to structures that support a riser on a drilling or production platform, providing stability and alignment for the pipe or conduit that connects the subsea well to the vessel during drilling or production operations.
[E21B 19/006] including heave compensators -- The subclass E21B 19/006 covers devices and methods for handling drill rods, casings or tubes outside the borehole, such as in a derrick, and specifically includes mechanisms that incorporate heave compensators to offset vertical movement of the rig during earth-or-rock drilling operations.
[E21B 19/008] Winding units, specially adapted for drilling operations -- The code E21B 19/008 covers winding units such as drums, winches or cable reels that are specially adapted for use in fixed earth-drilling rigs to handle drill rods, casings or tubes outside the borehole, typically mounted on the derrick or hoisting system. These devices are designed to control the vertical movement and positioning of drill strings during drilling operations.

[E21B 19/04] Hooks -- The code E21B 19/04 covers hooks employed in earth-or-rock drilling systems for handling rods, casings, tubes or similar elements outside the borehole, such as on a derrick. It pertains to devices that secure, lift, or swing these components during drilling operations.
[E21B 19/06] Elevators, i.e. rod- or tube-gripping devices -- E21B 19/06 covers elevators used in fixed-construction earth-drilling systems to grip and handle rods, casings or tubes outside the borehole, typically from a derrick. These devices secure and lift drill string components during insertion, removal or positioning, facilitating the handling of drilling tools in rock or soil excavation operations.
[E21B 19/07] Slip-type elevators -- Slip-type elevators are devices mounted on derricks that grip and lift drill rods, casings or tubes by inserting a slip that engages the surface, enabling controlled raising and lowering of the load during drilling. They are used in earth or rock drilling to handle components outside the borehole.
[E21B 19/08] Apparatus for feeding the rods or cables -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Apparatus for pushing drilling tools into the borehole. [E21B 19/102] using rollers or spherical balls as load gripping elements -- E21B 19/102 covers devices for handling drill rods, casings or similar tubular components outside a borehole, where the gripping means consist of rollers or spherical balls.
