[E21B 19/12] Rope clamps -- Rope clamps are devices that grip a rope to secure or guide it when attaching to rods, casings or tubes during handling outside a borehole, especially on a derrick. They are used in earth-drilling operations to prevent slippage and to control the movement of drilling components.
[E21B 19/14] Racks, ramps, troughs or bins, for holding the lengths of rod singly or connected -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Storage of rods and handling of rods between storage and a position where the rods can be connected or disconnected.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Combined feeding from rack and connecting -> E21B19/20 - Handling reeled pipe or rod units -> E21B19/22
[E21B 19/143] specially adapted for underwater drilling -- This subclass covers drilling installations designed for subaqueous operations, where rods, casings or tubes are manipulated outside the borehole, typically from a derrick, and are specially adapted to withstand water pressure, corrosion and sealing requirements of underwater drilling.
[E21B 19/146] Carousel systems, i.e. rotating rack systems -- This subclass covers carousel or rotating rack systems used for handling drill rods, casings, tubes and similar components outside the borehole, typically mounted on a derrick. It falls under fixed constructions for earth drilling and specifies equipment that rotates to position and convey drilling elements during rock drilling operations.
[E21B 19/15] Racking of rods in horizontal position -- The CPC entry E21B 19/15 covers the horizontal racking of drill rods, casings or tubes as part of fixed drilling installations. It relates to handling and storing these elements outside the borehole, typically on a derrick or similar structure, within earth or rock drilling operations.
[E21B 19/155] Handling between horizontal and vertical position -- E21B 19/155 pertains to equipment or procedures for transferring drill rods, casings, tubes or similar members from a horizontal to a vertical alignment, typically in the derrick or handling area of a fixed earth-drilling installation. The classification covers devices that guide, rotate, lift or swing the elements as they change orientation outside the borehole.
[E21B 19/16] Connecting or disconnecting pipe couplings or joints -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Method or apparatus for connecting or disconnecting the rods.  Illustrative example of the subject matter classified in this place:  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Combined feeding from rack and connecting, e.g. automatically -> E21B19/20
[E21B 19/161] using a wrench or a spinner adapted to engage a circular section of pipe  E21B19/168  takes precedence -- E21B 19/161 covers methods of handling drill rods, casings or tubes outside the borehole by using a wrench or a spinner that engages the circular section of the pipe. It falls under fixed constructions for earth drilling and is superseded by the narrower subclass E21B 19/168.
[E21B 19/162] cathead actuated -- **Definition:** This place covers:  A powered winch system used to tension chains, cables and ropes for actuation of the wrench.  Illustrative example of the subject matter classified in this place:
[E21B 19/163] piston-cylinder actuated -- The CPC entry E21B 19/163 covers piston-cylinder actuated devices for handling rods, casings or tubes outside a borehole in fixed earth-drilling constructions. It concerns mechanisms that use a piston and cylinder to move or position these elements during the drilling or extraction process.
[E21B 19/164] motor actuated  E21B19/162  and  E21B19/163  take precedence -- E21B 19/164 covers motor-actuated mechanisms used to handle rods, casings, tubes or similar elements outside the borehole, such as in the derrick of a drilling rig, and it supersedes the earlier subclasses 19/162 and 19/163 within the fixed-construction earth-drilling hierarchy.
[E21B 19/165] Control or monitoring arrangements therefor -- E21B 19/165 relates to fixed earth-drilling installations and defines control or monitoring systems for handling rods, casings, tubes or similar components outside the borehole, such as on a derrick. The classification covers sensors, actuators, automated feedback loops and supervisory interfaces that ensure safe and coordinated movement of these elements during drilling operations.
[E21B 19/166] Arrangements of torque limiters or torque indicators -- The code E21B 19/166 classifies devices that incorporate torque limiters or torque indicators in handling equipment for rods, casings or tubes used in earth-or rock-drilling operations, typically positioned outside the borehole such as on derricks. These arrangements monitor or restrict twisting forces to protect drilling strings and improve safety.
[E21B 19/167] using a wrench adapted to engage a non circular section of pipe, e.g. a section with flats or splines -- The code E21B 19/167 refers to tools and methods for handling rods, casings or tubes in drilling operations that use a wrench specially shaped to grip a non-circular pipe section, such as flats or splines, enabling secure turning or extraction of irregularly profiled drill components.
[E21B 19/168] using a spinner with rollers or a belt adapted to engage a well pipe -- E21B 19/168 belongs to the CPC group for fixed constructions in earth drilling, specifically the handling of rods, casings or tubes outside the borehole. It covers devices that employ a spinner equipped with rollers or a belt designed to engage a well pipe for positioning, rotation or retrieval.
[E21B 19/18] Connecting or disconnecting drill bit and drilling pipe -- The code E21B 19/18 covers devices, couplings and procedures for attaching or detaching a drill bit from the drilling pipe in earth-or-rock drilling operations, typically performed outside the borehole such as in the derrick. It includes mechanisms that secure the bit to the pipe and those that allow quick release for bit changes. This class is part of the broader group dealing with handling rods, casings or tubes in fixed-construction drilling systems.
[E21B 19/20] Combined feeding from rack and connecting, e.g. automatically -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The combination of feeding from rack and connecting.  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:
[E21B 19/22] Handling reeled pipe or rod units, e.g. flexible drilling pipes -- The classification E21B 19/22 covers equipment and procedures for handling pipe or rod units that are supplied on reels, such as flexible drilling pipes, during earth-or-rock drilling. It relates to moving, supporting, unspooling and storing these lines outside the borehole, typically in a derrick or similar handling area.
[E21B 19/24] Guiding or centralising devices for drilling rods or pipes -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Devices located at the surface for guiding or centralising drill pipe as it is inserted into a well.
[E21B 21/00] Methods or apparatus for flushing boreholes, e.g. by use of exhaust air from motor -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Control of circulation and pressure of fluids in a borehole, typically drilling fluids, tools for filling a borehole, treatment of drill cuttings, various types of drilling fluids.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Freeing objects stuck in boreholes by flushing -> E21B31/03
[E21B 21/001] specially adapted for underwater drilling  dual gradient drilling  E21B21/082 -- The E21B 21/001 subclass covers fixed earth-drilling constructions that incorporate borehole-flushing techniques, such as using exhaust air from the drilling motor to clear the hole. It includes specialised adaptations for underwater drilling and dual-gradient drilling methods that manage pressure and fluid flow in submerged or high-pressure environments.
[E21B 21/002] Down-hole drilling fluid separation systems  containers comprising collecting means with a strainer  E21B27/005 ; subsoil filtering  E21B43/02 ; down-hole production separators  E21B43/38 -- E21B 21/002 covers down-hole drilling-fluid separation systems that use containers equipped with collection means and a strainer to remove cuttings and contaminants from the circulating fluid. It includes subsoil filtering arrangements and down-hole production separators for earth-or-rock drilling operations.
[E21B 21/003] Means for stopping loss of drilling fluid  plastering the borehole wall  E21B33/138 -- This classification (E21B 21/003) relates to earth-or-rock drilling equipment that stops the loss of drilling fluid by plastering or sealing the borehole wall, typically as part of a flushing system that uses exhaust air or other means to control fluid escape.
[E21B 21/01] Arrangements for handling drilling fluids or cuttings outside the borehole, e.g. mud boxes -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Handling drilling fluids or cuttings outside the borehole without modification to the composition of the drilling fluid.
[E21B 21/011] Dust eliminating or dust removing while drilling -- The E21B 21/011 subclass covers methods, devices or systems for removing or reducing dust generated during earth-or-rock drilling operations, such as flushing boreholes with air, water or other media. It includes techniques that use exhaust air from the drilling motor or similar exhaust flows to keep the drilling zone clear of particulate matter.
[E21B 21/012] using exhaust air from the drilling motor for blowing off the dust at the borehole entrance -- The entry covers methods or apparatus that employ the exhaust air produced by a drilling motor to clear dust from the entrance of a borehole during earth or rock drilling. By directing this airflow outward, the technique improves visibility, reduces material buildup and helps maintain efficient drilling performance.
[E21B 21/013] by liquids -- This subclass covers the use of liquids to flush boreholes in fixed earth-drilling installations. It includes techniques where water, drilling mud or other fluids are circulated through the borehole to remove cuttings, control pressure or clean the hole during drilling operations.
[E21B 21/0135] Liquid flushing installations -- E21B 21/0135 covers liquid flushing installations used in earth-or-rock drilling to clean, cool or remove cuttings from boreholes, often by channeling exhaust air from the drilling motor to assist the liquid flow. The systems are designed for continuous or intermittent flushing during drilling operations.
[E21B 21/014] Preventing exhaust air from the drill motor from blowing-off towards the working face
[E21B 21/015] Means engaging the bore entrance, e.g. hoods for collecting dust -- The entry E21B 21/015 covers devices that engage the entrance of a borehole to collect dust or debris, such as hoods or similar fittings. These means are used in earth-or-rock drilling operations where boreholes are flushed, often using exhaust air from the drilling motor.
[E21B 21/019] Arrangements for maintaining circulation of drilling fluid while connecting or disconnecting tubular joints -- The entry covers devices or procedures that keep drilling fluid circulating while pipe sections are joined or separated, maintaining pressure and preventing loss of circulation during earth-drilling operations. It falls under fixed constructions for borehole flushing, including methods such as using exhaust air to sustain fluid flow during connection or disconnection of tubular joints.
[E21B 21/02] Swivel joints in hose-lines -- E21B 21/02 relates to swivel joints incorporated in hose-lines used for flushing boreholes, for example by routing exhaust air from a drill motor. The joints allow the hose to rotate and bend while maintaining a continuous flow, improving the efficiency of cleaning or cooling the drilled hole.
[E21B 21/06] Arrangements for treating drilling fluids outside the borehole -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Treating drilling fluid outside the borehole in a way that modifies the composition of the drilling fluid. This includes drilling fluid exiting the well and new drilling fluid introduced into the well.
[E21B 21/062] by mixing components -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Treating drilling fluids and wellbore treatment fluids by mixing in new or additional components.
[E21B 21/063] by separating components -- E21B 21/063 concerns methods or devices for flushing boreholes during earth-or-rock drilling by separating components of the drilling assembly, such as routing exhaust air from the motor or other fluids through distinct passages to clean the hole and remove cuttings. This approach improves borehole hygiene and drilling efficiency by keeping flushing media separate from the main drilling flow.
[E21B 21/065] Separating solids from drilling fluids -- E21B 21/065 covers devices and processes that separate solid cuttings from the drilling fluid used in earth-or-rock drilling. The classification includes cyclonic separators, shaker screens, centrifuges and filter systems that recover solids while returning clean fluid to the borehole. It falls under methods for flushing boreholes within the fixed-construction category.
[E21B 21/066] with further treatment of the solids, e.g. for disposal -- E21B 21/066 covers techniques for flushing boreholes in earth or rock drilling where the extracted solids are subsequently treated, such as by disposal, recycling or further processing.
[E21B 21/067] Separating gases from drilling fluids -- E21B 21/067 covers techniques and apparatus for separating gases that become entrained in drilling fluids during borehole flushing, such as using exhaust air from the drill motor or other gas-removal methods. It applies to earth-drilling operations where the removal of gas from the circulating fluid improves borehole stability and drilling efficiency.
[E21B 21/068] using chemical treatment -- The subclass deals with techniques that apply chemical agents to clean or flush boreholes during earth or rock drilling, often by circulating the chemicals through the hole to dissolve cuttings or control pressure. It includes methods that combine the chemicals with exhaust air from the drilling motor to enhance debris removal.
[E21B 21/07] for treating dust-laden gaseous fluids -- E21B 21/07 relates to earth-drilling techniques for flushing boreholes, specifically by using exhaust air from the drill motor to treat dust-laden gaseous fluids. This subclass falls under Fixed Constructions → Earth Drilling → Earth or Rock Drilling and addresses methods or apparatus that clean the borehole environment during drilling operations.
[E21B 21/08] Controlling or monitoring pressure or flow of drilling fluid, e.g. automatic filling of boreholes, automatic control of bottom pressure -- The E21B 21/08 entry pertains to systems that control or monitor the pressure or flow of drilling fluid during earth-or-rock drilling, especially for flushing boreholes. It includes automatic methods for filling boreholes and regulating bottom pressure to optimise drilling performance.
[E21B 21/082] Dual gradient systems, i.e. using two hydrostatic gradients or drilling fluid densities -- A dual-gradient drilling system employs two distinct hydrostatic gradients or drilling-fluid densities within the borehole to create separate pressure zones. By varying fluid density, it stabilises the hole, controls formation pressures and enhances cut-tings transport, especially in earth-or-rock drilling and borehole-flushing operations.
[E21B 21/085] Underbalanced techniques, i.e. where borehole fluid pressure is below formation pressure -- E21B 21/085 covers underbalanced drilling methods where the fluid pressure in the borehole is intentionally kept below the surrounding formation pressure, allowing formation fluids to flow into the wellbore. This includes techniques and apparatus that flush the borehole, often using exhaust air from the motor, to maintain the lower pressure and improve drilling efficiency and rock removal.
[E21B 21/10] Valve arrangements in drilling-fluid circulation systems -- This CPC subclass deals with the design and configuration of valves that control drilling-fluid circulation in earth-drilling operations, especially for flushing boreholes by directing exhaust air or fluid. It covers methods and apparatus for regulating flow, pressure and cleaning within the drilling-fluid system.
[E21B 21/103] Down-hole by-pass valve arrangements, i.e. between the inside of the drill string and the annulus  valves specifically for maintaining circulation of drilling fluid while connecting or disconnecting tubular joints  E21B21/019 -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Valves specifically for maintaining circulation of drilling fluid while connecting or disconnecting tubular joints -> E21B21/019
[E21B 21/106] Valve arrangements outside the borehole, e.g. kelly valves  valves specifically for maintaining circulation of drilling fluid while connecting or disconnecting tubular joints  E21B21/019 -- The subclass E21B 21/106 covers external valve arrangements used in earth-drilling operations, such as kelly valves, that maintain circulation of drilling fluid while tubular joints are connected or disconnected. It focuses on devices that keep the borehole flushed during pipe handling.
[E21B 21/12] using drilling pipes with plural fluid passages, e.g. closed circulation systems -- E21B 21/12 refers to earth-drilling methods that use drilling pipes equipped with multiple fluid passages, such as closed-circulation systems, to flush boreholes by circulating fluid or exhaust air.
[E21B 21/14] using liquids and gases, e.g. foams -- This subclass pertains to methods and apparatus for flushing boreholes in earth-or-rock drilling by injecting liquids, gases or mixtures such as foams to remove cuttings, improve circulation or cool the drill bit. It includes systems that combine liquid and gas phases, for example aerated water or foam, to clean or condition the hole during drilling.
[E21B 21/16] using gaseous fluids -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Methods or apparatus for flushing boreholes using liquids and gases, e.g. foams -> E21B21/14
[E21B 21/18] Preventing exhaust air from the drill motor from blowing-off towards the working face -- The entry E21B 21/18 covers techniques for stopping the exhaust air from a drill motor from being blown toward the working face during earth or rock drilling. It falls under fixed-construction methods for flushing boreholes, aimed at improving safety and efficiency in drilling operations.
[E21B 2200/00] Special features related to earth drilling for obtaining oil, gas or water -- The CPC subclass E21B 2200/00 covers special features of earth-drilling equipment and methods used to extract oil, gas or water, such as unique drill bits, bore-hole stabilisation systems, mud-circulation enhancements, and hydraulic or pneumatic assistance. It falls within the hierarchy of Fixed Constructions → Earth Drilling → Earth or Rock Drilling, addressing innovations that improve efficiency, safety or depth capability in hydro-resource extraction.
[E21B 2200/01] Sealings characterised by their shape -- A sealing device used in earth-or-rock drilling equipment, designed specifically for oil, gas or water extraction, is classified here when its functional characteristics are defined by the shape of the seal. The code covers any form-shaped gasket, packer or barrier that provides a leak-tight connection between drill components or between the drill string and the borehole.
[E21B 2200/02] Down-hole chokes or valves for variably regulating fluid flow -- Down-hole chokes or valves for variably regulating fluid flow are adjustable flow-control devices installed within the borehole of an oil, gas or water well. They allow operators to modulate pressure and fluid rate during drilling or production, enhancing safety and efficiency. These components are part of fixed construction equipment used in earth or rock drilling.
[E21B 2200/03] Valves operated by gear mechanisms, e.g. rack and pinion mechanisms -- The code E21B 2200/03 covers valves that are driven by gear arrangements such as rack-and-pinion systems, used in fixed earth-drilling equipment for extracting oil, gas or water. It classifies the mechanical actuation of drilling-fluid control devices within the broader earth-or-rock drilling category.
[E21B 2200/04] Ball valves -- The E21B 2200/04 subclass covers ball valves designed for use in fixed-construction earth-drilling equipment, particularly systems that extract oil, gas or water. It specifies valves with sealing and flow-control characteristics suited to the high pressures and abrasive environments encountered in rock-based drilling operations.
[E21B 2200/05] Flapper valves -- E21B 2200/05 covers flapper valves used in drilling equipment for extracting oil, gas or water. These valves are designed to allow fluid flow in one direction and prevent backflow, facilitating pressure control in drill strings and wellbore systems. The classification is part of the fixed constructions group for earth or rock drilling.
[E21B 2200/06] Sleeve valves -- E21B 2200/06 Sleeve valves covers fixed-construction components placed in the drill string to open, close or throttle fluid passages during earth or rock drilling operations aimed at oil, gas or water extraction. The valves typically consist of a movable sleeve that slides within a housing to provide sealing, pressure control or flow reversal without removing the drill pipe.
[E21B 2200/08] Down-hole devices using materials which decompose under well-bore conditions -- This class covers down-hole tools and equipment that incorporate substances designed to break down or dissolve under the temperature, pressure or chemical environment of a well bore, such as degradable polymers or sacrificial components, facilitating removal or reducing long-term impact after drilling for oil, gas or water.
[E21B 2200/09] Detecting, eliminating, preventing liquid slugs in production pipes
[E21B 2200/20] Computer models or simulations, e.g. for reservoirs under production, drill bits -- E21B 2200/20 covers the use of computer models or simulations in earth-drilling operations, such as reservoir behaviour under production and the design or analysis of drill bits, focusing on special features that support oil, gas or water extraction.
[E21B 2200/22] Fuzzy logic, artificial intelligence, neural networks or the like -- E21B 2200/22 is a CPC subclass under Fixed Constructions → Earth Drilling → Earth or Rock Drilling, covering special features for oil, gas or water drilling that employ fuzzy logic, artificial intelligence, neural networks or similar computational techniques. It addresses intelligent control, optimisation and decision-making systems used in drilling equipment and processes.
[E21B 23/00] Apparatus for displacing, setting, locking, releasing or removing tools, packers or the like in boreholes or wells -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Actuators for installing tools used for any application in a borehole, wherein the way of installation is more relevant than the function of the tool as such.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Setting of casings, screens or liners -> E21B43/10
[E21B 23/001] Self-propelling systems or apparatus, e.g. for moving tools within the horizontal portion of a borehole -- E21B 23/001 covers self-propelling systems or apparatus used to move tools along the horizontal portion of a borehole, such as devices that advance, position, or retrieve equipment in drilling operations. These systems are employed in earth-drilling or rock-drilling contexts to transport tools, packers, or other downhole hardware without external pulling forces.
[E21B 23/004] Indexing systems for guiding relative movement between telescoping parts of downhole tools
[E21B 23/006] "J-slot" systems, i.e. lug and slot indexing mechanisms -- J-slot systems are lug-and-slot indexing mechanisms that lock drill-string components, tools or packers into a predetermined angular position within a borehole. The lug engages a matching slot to prevent rotation while allowing axial movement for setting or release, providing reliable positioning and load transfer during earth or rock drilling operations.
[E21B 23/01] for anchoring the tools or the like -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Anchoring of drives in the borehole -> E21B4/18 - Apparatus for locking the tools or the like in landing nipples or in recesses between adjacent sections of tubing -> E21B23/02 - Apparatus for setting the tools into, or removing the tools from, laterally offset landing nipples or pockets -> E21B23/03 - Apparatus operated by fluid means -> E21B23/04 - Apparatusfor setting packers -> E21B23/06
[E21B 23/02] for locking the tools or the like in landing nipples or in recesses between adjacent sections of tubing -- E21B 23/02 covers devices that lock tools, packers or similar items in landing nipples or in recesses formed between adjoining sections of tubing in boreholes or wells, facilitating secure placement during drilling or workover operations within the broader class of fixed-construction earth-drilling apparatus.
[E21B 23/03] for setting the tools into, or removing the tools from, laterally offset landing nipples or pockets -- E21B 23/03 refers to devices used in earth or rock drilling that set or retrieve tools, packers or similar equipment from laterally offset landing nipples or pockets within boreholes or wells. These mechanisms handle displacement, locking, release or removal of the tools to maintain well integrity.
[E21B 23/04] operated by fluid means, e.g. actuated by explosion -- The CPC subclass E21B 23/04 refers to apparatus for moving, setting, locking, releasing or retrieving tools, packers or similar devices in boreholes or wells during earth or rock drilling, where the operation is powered by fluid means such as an explosive charge.
[E21B 23/0411] specially adapted for anchoring tools or the like to the borehole wall or to well tube -- The code E21B 23/0411 refers to apparatus used in earth or rock drilling that is specially adapted to anchor tools, packers or similar items to the borehole wall or to a well tube, enabling secure positioning, locking, release or removal of the equipment.
[E21B 23/04115] using radial pistons -- This subgroup covers devices that use radially extending pistons to drive, set, lock, release or retrieve tools, packers or similar equipment within a borehole or well. It falls under fixed-construction apparatus for earth or rock drilling.
[E21B 23/0412] characterised by pressure chambers, e.g. vacuum chambers -- A drilling apparatus that employs pressure or vacuum chambers to displace, set, lock, release or remove tools, packers or similar items in a borehole or well, using sealed chambers that generate suction or positive pressure to manipulate the equipment.
[E21B 23/0413] using means for blocking fluid flow, e.g. drop balls or darts  using balls or the like for actuating downhole valves  E21B34/142 -- The entry E21B 23/0413 covers devices and methods that employ flow-blocking elements such as drop balls, darts or similar objects to actuate or control downhole valves, packers or other tools in earth-or-rock drilling operations, including mechanisms for releasing, setting, locking or removing those tools by means of the blocking balls.
[E21B 23/0414] using explosives -- This subclass covers the use of explosive energy to operate or assist apparatus that displaces, sets, locks, releases or retrieves tools, packers or similar devices in boreholes or wells. It includes methods where an explosive charge initiates motion or separation of the equipment. Code E21B 23/0414.
[E21B 23/0415] using particular fluids, e.g. electro-active liquids -- This subclass covers devices that employ specific fluids, such as electro-active liquids, to assist the displacement, setting, locking, releasing or removal of tools, packers or similar equipment in boreholes or wells. It falls under earth-drilling categories, focusing on fluid-mediated mechanisms for tool manipulation in drilling operations.
[E21B 23/0416] characterised by force amplification arrangements -- This subgroup covers devices used in earth or rock drilling that incorporate mechanisms to amplify the applied force when moving, setting, locking, releasing or retrieving tools, packers or similar equipment in a borehole or well. It falls under the fixed constructions category of drilling apparatus.
[E21B 23/0417] Down-hole non-explosive gas generating means, e.g. by chemical reaction -- Down-hole non-explosive gas-generating means are devices placed in a borehole that produce gas by a chemical reaction to create pressure or fluid flow for moving, setting, locking, releasing or removing tools, packers and similar equipment, without using explosives.
[E21B 23/0418] specially adapted for locking the tools in landing nipples or recesses -- The CPC subclass E21B 23/0418 covers devices that are specially adapted to lock tools within landing nipples or recesses during earth-or-rock drilling. It falls under the broader group of apparatus for displacing, setting, locking, releasing or removing tools, packers or similar items in boreholes or wells.
[E21B 23/0419] using down-hole motor and pump arrangements for generating hydraulic pressure -- E21B 23/0419 covers apparatus that uses a down-hole motor together with a pump to produce hydraulic pressure for displacing, setting, locking, releasing or removing tools, packers or similar devices within boreholes or wells, applied in fixed earth-drilling constructions.
[E21B 23/042] using a single piston or multiple mechanically interconnected pistons -- The subclass covers apparatus that uses a single piston or a group of mechanically linked pistons to generate the force needed for moving, setting, locking, releasing or retrieving tools and packers in a borehole or well. The pistons operate together as a unified mechanism to transmit displacement without separate hydraulic or pneumatic systems.
[E21B 23/0421] using multiple hydraulically interconnected pistons -- E21B 23/0421 classifies drilling apparatus that employs several pistons linked by hydraulic circuits to move, set, lock, release or retrieve tools and packers within a borehole or well. The design uses the coordinated action of the pistons to provide precise control of tool positioning during earth or rock drilling operations.
[E21B 23/0422] characterised by radial pistons  using radial pistons for anchoring  E21B23/04115 -- This CPC subclass (E21B 23/0422) covers devices for displacing, setting, locking, releasing or removing tools, packers or similar apparatus in boreholes or wells that employ radial pistons as the anchoring mechanism, a feature defined in the related group E21B 23/04115. It falls under Fixed Constructions → Earth Drilling → Earth or Rock Drilling.
[E21B 23/0423] using step motors -- E21B 23/0423 covers the use of step motors to power devices that displace, set, lock, release or retrieve tools, packers or similar equipment within boreholes or wells during earth or rock drilling. The step motor provides precise incremental motion for the tool-handling mechanisms.
[E21B 23/06] for setting packers -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Tools for setting packers, detachable from the packer.
[E21B 23/065] setting tool actuated by explosion or gas generating means -- E21B 23/065 classifies setting tools that are powered by an explosive charge or gas-generating reaction to drive a tool, packer or other downhole device into position within a borehole or well. These devices belong to fixed constructions for earth drilling and are used to lock, release or retrieve equipment in rock or soil.
[E21B 23/08] Introducing or running tools by fluid pressure, e.g. through-the-flow-line tool systems -- E21B 23/08 concerns the introduction and movement of tools in boreholes or wells by fluid pressure, such as through-the-flow-line tool systems, enabling insertion, setting, locking, releasing or removal without mechanical handling. This approach is used in earth-drilling and rock-drilling operations to manipulate packers, drill-stem equipment and similar components.
[E21B 23/10] Tools specially adapted therefor -- E21B 23/10 pertains to specialized tools used in fixed-construction earth or rock drilling systems for moving, setting, locking, releasing or retrieving other tools, packers and similar devices within boreholes or wells. These devices are designed specifically to facilitate the handling and operation of equipment in drilling and completion processes.
[E21B 23/12] Tool diverters -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Tool diverters, e.g. for through-the-flow line tool systems or for wire-line tools
[E21B 23/14] for displacing a cable or a cable-operated tool, e.g. for logging or perforating operations in deviated wells -- This subclass covers devices that move a cable or a cable-operated tool within a borehole, typically for logging or perforating functions in deviated wells. It includes mechanisms that displace, set, lock, release or retrieve such tools during Earth drilling operations.
[E21B 25/00] Apparatus for obtaining or removing undisturbed cores, e.g. core barrels or core extractors -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Tools for receiving, preserving, transporting, etc., a core.  Illustrative example of the subject matter classified in this place:  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Core bits -> E21B10/02
[E21B 25/005] Above ground means for handling the core, e.g. for extracting the core from the core barrel -- E21B 25/005 covers devices used on the surface to handle undisturbed drill cores, such as mechanisms that remove the core from the core barrel after extraction. These apparatus are employed in fixed-construction drilling rigs for geological sampling.
[E21B 25/02] the core receiver being insertable into, or removable from, the borehole without withdrawing the drilling pipe -- This subclass covers core receivers designed to be introduced into or extracted from a borehole while the drill string remains in place, enabling retrieval of undisturbed core samples without pulling out the drilling pipe. It applies to fixed-construction earth or rock drilling equipment.
[E21B 25/04] the core receiver having a core forming cutting edge or element, e.g. punch type core barrels -- E21B 25/04 classifies core receivers that incorporate a cutting edge or element, such as punch-type core barrels, used to form and extract undisturbed cores during earth or rock drilling.
[E21B 25/06] the core receiver having a flexible liner or inflatable retaining means -- The entry covers core receivers used in earth-or-rock drilling that incorporate a flexible liner or an inflatable mechanism to retain the core. It relates to apparatus designed to obtain or remove undisturbed core samples, such as core barrels or extractors, and falls under the fixed-construction category for drilling equipment.
[E21B 25/08] Coating, freezing, consolidating cores -- The E21B 25/08 subclass covers techniques for treating drill cores after extraction, such as applying protective coatings, freezing, or other consolidation methods that preserve the core's structure and prevent damage during handling and analysis. It pertains to equipment or processes used in earth-or-rock drilling to obtain undisturbed core samples.
[E21B 25/10] Formed core retaining or severing means -- It covers devices that retain a drill core while severing it from the surrounding formation, such as core barrels, splitters or other retaining mechanisms used in earth or rock drilling. These tools are part of fixed-construction equipment for obtaining undisturbed cores.
[E21B 25/12] of the sliding wedge type -- The E21B 25/12 subclass covers apparatus for obtaining or removing undisturbed geological cores that employ a sliding-wedge mechanism, such as core barrels or extractors where a wedge slides to grip, drive, or release the core rod during drilling. This design is used in earth-or-rock drilling equipment to retrieve intact core samples for analysis.
[E21B 25/14] mounted on pivot transverse to core axis -- E21B 25/14 pertains to drill-core extraction apparatus in which the core barrel or extractor is attached to a pivot that lies transverse to the core's axis, enabling the tool to swing away for retrieval of undisturbed cores during earth or rock drilling.
[E21B 25/16] for obtaining oriented cores -- This subclass covers apparatus that retrieve core samples while preserving their natural orientation, such as specially designed core barrels or extractors that keep grain or layering aligned. It is used in earth or rock drilling to obtain undisturbed oriented cores for geological analysis.
[E21B 25/18] the core receiver being specially adapted for operation under water -- E21B 25/18 pertains to core receivers that are specifically designed for underwater use, allowing the collection of undisturbed drill cores while the drilling assembly is submerged. The invention typically includes waterproof sealing, pressure-compensating features and corrosion-resistant materials to ensure reliable operation beneath water.
[E21B 27/00] Containers for collecting or depositing substances in boreholes or wells, e.g. bailers, for collecting mud or sand -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:
[E21B 27/005] Collecting means with a strainer -- E21B 27/005 covers devices used in earth-or-rock drilling that incorporate a strainer to filter material while collecting it from boreholes or wells. These collecting means, such as bailers equipped with screens, are intended to separate sand, mud or other solids from liquids during drilling operations.
[E21B 27/02] Dump bailers, i.e. containers for depositing substances, e.g. cement or acids -- E21B 27/02 designates dump bailers, which are containers used to deposit substances such as cement, acids or other fluids into boreholes or wells during earth or rock drilling operations. They are a type of collection or deposition device for borehole work.
[E21B 27/04] where the collecting or depositing means include helical conveying means -- The subclass E21B 27/04 covers fixed drilling equipment that includes containers such as bailers for collecting or depositing material in boreholes, where the material is moved by a helical conveying mechanism. It falls under the earth-or-rock drilling group for fixed constructions.
[E21B 28/00] Vibration generating arrangements for boreholes or wells, e.g. for stimulating production -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The vibration is normally generated by tools inside the borehole.  Illustrative example of the subject matter classified in this place:  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Vibration for fishing for or freeing objects -> E21B31/005 - Means for transmitting measuring-signals or control signals from the well to the surface, or from the surface to the well, e.g. for logging while drilling, using acoustic waves -> E21B47/14 - Generating seismic energy for geophysical measurements -> G01V1/02
[E21B 29/00] Cutting or destroying pipes, packers, plugs or wire lines, located in boreholes or wells, e.g. cutting of damaged pipes, of windows -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Cutting or deforming methods and apparatus for pipes, lines etc. inside the wellbore.
[E21B 29/002] Cutting, e.g. milling, a pipe with a cutter rotating along the circumference of the pipe -- The code E21B 29/002 covers methods and devices for cutting or milling a pipe, packer, plug or wire line that is positioned in a borehole or well, usually by a cutter that rotates around the pipe's circumference. It falls under Fixed Constructions - Earth Drilling - Earth or Rock Drilling.
[E21B 29/005] with a radially-expansible cutter rotating inside the pipe, e.g. for cutting an annular window -- The code E21B 29/005 refers to a pipe-cutting tool that creates an annular window by rotating a radially-expansive cutter inside the pipe, typically used to cut or remove damaged sections of pipe, packer or wire line in boreholes or wells.
[E21B 29/007] with a radially-retracting cutter rotating outside the pipe -- E21B 29/007 classifies drilling tools that cut or destroy pipes, packers, plugs or wire lines situated in boreholes by employing a cutter that rotates around the pipe and retracts radially outward to sever the component. Such radially-retracting cutters are used for removing damaged pipe sections or clearing obstructions without pulling the tool through the pipe.
[E21B 29/02] by explosives or by thermal or chemical means -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Freeing stuck objects by explosives -> E21B31/002
[E21B 29/04] Cutting of wire lines or the like -- The CPC subclass E21B 29/04 covers methods and tools for cutting wire lines, cables or similar items that have become stuck or need removal inside boreholes or wells. It falls under fixed constructions, earth drilling and earth-or-rock drilling, and also includes cutting damaged pipes, packers or plugs situated in the drilled hole.
[E21B 29/06] Cutting windows, e.g. directional window cutters for whipstock operations -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Cutting a pipe with a cutter rotating along the circumference of the pipe with a radially-expansible cutter rotating inside the pipe -> E21B29/005 - Cutting or deforming pipes to control fluid flow -> E21B29/08
[E21B 29/08] Cutting or deforming pipes to control fluid flow -- E21B 29/08 covers techniques for cutting, removing or deforming pipes, packers, plugs or wire lines positioned in boreholes or wells to control fluid flow, including the cutting of damaged pipe sections or creating flow windows.
[E21B 29/10] Reconditioning of well casings, e.g. straightening -- E21B 29/10 covers methods and devices for reconditioning well casings, such as straightening or repairing damaged pipe sections that remain in a borehole. It falls under the earth-drilling hierarchy, specifically dealing with cutting, destroying, or restoring pipes, packers, plugs, or wire lines within wells. This classification is used by the EPC and USPTO to catalogue technologies that restore the integrity of casing strings after deformation or damage.
[E21B 29/12] specially adapted for underwater installations -- E21B 29/12 covers fixed earth-drilling constructions that cut or destroy pipes, packers, plugs or wire lines situated in boreholes or wells, with designs particularly adapted for underwater installations. It falls under the hierarchy of Fixed Constructions, Earth Drilling, and Earth or Rock Drilling.
[E21B 3/00] Rotary drilling -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Machines and methods in which the drilling action is done principally by rotation.  Illustrative example of the subject matter classified in this place:
[E21B 3/02] Surface drives for rotary drilling -- Surface drives for rotary drilling are mechanisms mounted at the ground surface that transmit torque and axial force from a motor to the drill string, enabling the rotation of the bit for earth or rock drilling. They are fixed constructions used in rotary drilling operations to provide reliable power transfer and control during drilling.
[E21B 3/022] Top drives -- Top drives are motorized units mounted on the rotary table of a drilling rig that rotate the drill string directly, eliminating the traditional turntable. They supply high torque and enable faster, more efficient handling of drill pipe during rotary earth-or-rock drilling.
[E21B 3/025] with a to-and-fro rotation of the tool -- This subclass covers rotary drilling methods where the drill bit performs a reciprocating rotational motion, moving back and forth while rotating, used in fixed earth-drilling installations for rock or soil. It is part of the Earth drilling group under fixed constructions.
[E21B 3/03] with an intermittent unidirectional rotation of the tool -- E21B 3/03 covers rotary drilling methods in which the drill-tool rotates in one direction but the rotation is applied intermittently rather than continuously, allowing pauses or reversals between forward turns. This technique is used in earth or rock drilling to control torque, improve chip removal, or reduce wear on the drill-string. It falls under the broader categories of fixed constructions, earth drilling, and earth-or-rock drilling.
[E21B 3/035] with slipping or elastic transmission -- E21B 3/035 classifies rotary drilling machines for earth or rock that employ a slipping or elastic transmission between the drive shaft and the drill bit, allowing torque modulation and shock absorption. The code sits under Fixed Constructions → Earth Drilling → Earth or Rock Drilling and is used in the CPC system by the EPO and USPTO.
[E21B 3/04] Rotary tables -- This subgroup covers rotary tables used in earth or rock drilling, devices that support and rotate the drill string during rotary drilling operations, providing a platform for the drill rig and enabling transfer of torque.
[E21B 3/045] movably mounted on the drilling structure or platform  derricks adapted to be moved on their substructure  E21B15/003 -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Derricks adapted to be moved on their substructure -> E21B15/003
[E21B 3/06] Adaptation of rotary draw works to drive rotary tables -- E21B 3/06 covers the modification of rotary draw mechanisms so they can power rotary tables used in rotary drilling operations for earth or rock drilling. The classification pertains to adaptations that link the draw works' rotational output to the table that turns the drill string, enabling efficient torque transmission during drilling. This falls under the broader categories of fixed constructions for earth drilling, specifically rotary drilling systems.
[E21B 31/00] Fishing for or freeing objects in boreholes or wells -- This subclass deals with techniques and equipment used to retrieve or release objects that have become stuck in boreholes or wells, such as fishing tools, jarring devices, and other removal methods employed during drilling operations. It covers both temporary and permanent extraction of tools, debris, or other foreign items from the drilled hole.
[E21B 31/002] Destroying the objects to be fished, e.g. by explosive means -- This CPC entry covers techniques for eliminating objects that need to be retrieved from boreholes or wells, typically by breaking them up with explosives or similar destructive means. It falls within fixed-construction earth-drilling activities that involve fishing for or freeing such objects.
[E21B 31/005] using vibrating or oscillating means -- The code E21B 31/005 refers to fixed earth-or-rock drilling installations where vibrating or oscillating means are employed to fish for or free objects that have become lodged in boreholes or wells.
[E21B 31/007] fishing tools with means for attaching comprising fusing or sticking -- This CPC subclass covers tools for retrieving or releasing objects from boreholes or wells that incorporate an attachment method based on fusing or sticking, such as adhesive or welded connections. The devices are used in fixed-construction earth or rock drilling operations where fishing for or freeing items is required. It is part of the E21B 31/00 series for fishing tools in drilling applications.
[E21B 31/03] Freeing by flushing -- The classification E21B 31/03 covers techniques for releasing objects that have become stuck in boreholes or wells by flushing them with a fluid. It includes methods and apparatus that use water, mud, air, or other liquids or gases to create a flow that dislodges and removes the trapped items.
[E21B 31/035] controlling differential pipe sticking -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Processes for unsticking the pipe from the wellbore wall by, for example, applying force to pipe, lowering pressure in the wellbore or using treatment fluid.
[E21B 31/06] using magnetic means -- E21B 31/06 pertains to techniques and devices that use magnetic forces to retrieve or release objects trapped in boreholes or wells during earth-drilling operations, falling under the CPC subgroup for fishing for or freeing items in rock or earth drilling contexts.
[E21B 31/08] using junk baskets or the like -- E21B 31/08 pertains to methods that employ junk baskets or comparable devices to retrieve or free objects stuck in boreholes or wells during earth-or-rock drilling within fixed construction operations.
[E21B 31/107] using impact means for releasing stuck parts, e.g. jars -- This classification covers methods for releasing stuck parts in earth or rock drilling operations by applying impact forces, such as using jars or similar devices to deliver sudden blows to the tool string.
[E21B 31/1075] using explosives -- E21B 31/1075 refers to the use of explosives to fish for or release objects lodged in boreholes or wells during fixed-construction earth or rock drilling operations. It covers techniques that employ blasting to dislodge or retrieve tools, debris, or other items obstructing the drilling cavity.
[E21B 31/113] hydraulically-operated -- This CPC subclass covers hydraulic mechanisms used to retrieve or release objects stuck in boreholes or wells during earth or rock drilling operations. It includes devices that use fluid pressure to power tools such as grapples, jacks or retrieval buckets for fishing operations.
[E21B 31/1135] Jars with a hydraulic impedance mechanism, i.e. a restriction, for initially delaying escape of a restraining fluid -- The code E21B 31/1135 covers hydraulic-impedance jars used in earth-or-rock drilling operations, especially when fishing for or freeing objects in boreholes or wells. These jars incorporate a restriction that initially delays the release of the restraining fluid, providing controlled energy buildup before the jar fires to free stuck tools. This mechanism improves reliability and reduces the risk of sudden pressure spikes during retrieval operations.
[E21B 31/12] Grappling tools, e.g. tongs or grabs -- E21B 31/12 classifies grappling devices such as tongs or grabs that are used to fish for or release objects in boreholes or wells, forming part of the earth-drilling subgroup for earth or rock drilling. These tools are designed to grasp, retrieve or free tools, rock or debris during drilling operations.
[E21B 31/125] specially adapted for parted wire line or ropes -- The CPC subclass E21B 31/125 covers tools and devices that are specially adapted for use with a parting wire line or ropes when fishing for, retrieving, or freeing objects in boreholes or wells. It falls under Fixed Constructions → Earth Drilling → Earth or Rock Drilling → Fishing for or freeing objects in boreholes or wells. This includes specially shaped jaws, clamps, or other attachments designed to work with wire-line or rope systems.
[E21B 31/14] with means deflecting the direction of the tool, e.g. by use of knuckle joints -- E21B 31/14 covers devices for retrieving or releasing objects from boreholes or wells that incorporate a directional-deflection mechanism, such as knuckle joints, to steer the tool during the fishing operation. It falls under the fixed-construction earth-drilling segment of the CPC.
[E21B 31/16] combined with cutting or destroying means -- E21B 31/16 covers tools for fishing or freeing objects in boreholes or wells that incorporate a cutting or destroying element, allowing the retrieval or release of stuck items while also fragmenting or cutting surrounding material. It applies to fixed earth-drilling installations where combined fishing and cutting functions are integrated into the equipment.
[E21B 31/18] gripping externally, e.g. overshot -- This subclass covers devices that grip the outside of objects in boreholes, such as an overshot tool, used to retrieve or free stuck items during earth or rock drilling operations. It falls under fixed constructions for earth drilling, specifically fishing for or freeing objects in boreholes or wells.
[E21B 31/20] gripping internally, e.g. fishing spears -- E21B 31/20 belongs to the Fixed Constructions group for Earth drilling, specifically Earth or rock drilling methods that involve fishing for or freeing objects in boreholes or wells. The entry covers tools that grip objects from the inside of a hole, such as fishing spears used to retrieve or release items lodged in the borehole.
[E21B 33/00] Sealing or packing boreholes or wells -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Apparatus and methods for sealing, both at the surface and in the interior of a borehole
[E21B 33/02] Surface sealing or packing -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Apparatus for sealing a wellbore and placed outside the wellbore, be it at the surface or subsea, such as wellheads, casing or tubing hangers,  Christmas trees or blow-out preventers:
[E21B 33/03] Well heads -- E21B 33/03 Well heads is a subclass of fixed constructions for earth or rock drilling that deals with the sealing, packing and control devices installed at the surface of boreholes or wells, covering their design, construction and functional components.
[E21B 33/035] specially adapted for underwater installations -- E21B 33/035 is a CPC subclass for sealing or packing boreholes or wells that are specially adapted for underwater installations, such as subsea drilling or offshore oil- and gas wells. It encompasses devices, materials and methods that maintain a reliable seal under water pressure and marine conditions.
[E21B 33/0353] Horizontal or spool trees, i.e. without production valves in the vertical main bore -- The E21B 33/0353 entry covers horizontal or spool tree devices used in earth-drilling boreholes that lack production valves in the vertical main bore, focusing on components that aid in sealing or packing the well during drilling operations.
[E21B 33/0355] Control systems, e.g. hydraulic, pneumatic, electric, acoustic, for submerged well heads -- E21B 33/0355 covers control systems, such as hydraulic, pneumatic, electric or acoustic devices, that operate submerged well heads used for sealing or packing boreholes and wells in earth- or rock-drilling applications. It falls under the Fixed Constructions hierarchy for Earth Drilling.
[E21B 33/037] Protective housings therefor -- The class E21B 33/037 concerns protective housings employed in sealing or packing boreholes and wells during earth or rock drilling. It includes shields, liners, jackets or similar structures that safeguard the borehole wall and surrounding formation from collapse or contamination.
[E21B 33/0375] Corrosion protection means -- E21B 33/0375 is a CPC subclass within fixed constructions, earth drilling, earth or rock drilling and sealing or packing boreholes or wells. It addresses corrosion protection means applied to drilling equipment and well-bore components, such as protective coatings, cathodic protection systems and sacrificial anodes, to prevent metal deterioration.
[E21B 33/038] Connectors used on well heads, e.g. for connecting blow-out preventer and riser -- E21B 33/038 covers connectors mounted on well heads that join equipment such as a blow-out preventer to a riser, facilitating secure attachment and fluid sealing in drilling operations. These devices are part of fixed constructions for earth or rock drilling, specifically used to seal or pack boreholes and wells.
[E21B 33/0385] electrical connectors -- Under CPC code E21B 33/0385, electrical connectors are defined for use in fixed-construction earth or rock drilling systems that seal or pack boreholes and wells.
[E21B 33/0387] Hydraulic stab connectors -- Hydraulic stab connectors are devices that join drill-pipe or downhole components by inserting a male part into a female fitting and using hydraulic pressure to create a sealed, leak-tight connection, allowing quick assembly and reliable fluid transfer in boreholes or wells.
[E21B 33/04] Casing heads -- E21B 33/04 refers to casing heads, which are components installed at the top of a borehole or well to provide a secure connection between the casing string and surface equipment, facilitating sealing, pressure control and access for maintenance. They are used in fixed construction drilling of earth or rock, supporting sealing and packing operations.
[E21B 33/0407] with a suspended electrical cable -- The CPC subclass E21B 33/0407 covers methods and devices for sealing or packing boreholes or wells in earth or rock drilling that employ a suspended electrical cable to position or activate the sealing material. It pertains to fixed-construction drilling operations where the cable is used to deliver the sealant into the borehole.
[E21B 33/0415] rotating or floating support for tubing or casing hanger -- The E21B 33/0415 class defines rotating or floating support devices that hold tubing or a casing hanger in place while sealing or packing an earth-or-rock drilling borehole. These mechanisms enable controlled movement of the pipe elements during well construction or maintenance, ensuring a secure seal around the drill or production string.
[E21B 33/0422] a suspended tubing or casing being gripped by a slip or an internally serrated member -- This subclass deals with mechanisms that hold a tubing or casing suspended in a borehole by means of a slip or an internally serrated component that engages the outer surface to prevent movement. It is used in sealing or packing boreholes during earth or rock drilling.
[E21B 33/043] specially adapted for underwater well heads -- E21B 33/043 covers devices and methods for sealing or packing the head of a well that is installed underwater. It includes specially adapted constructions that protect the wellhead from water pressure and corrosion, ensuring reliable operation of offshore drilling installations. These solutions are part of the fixed construction group for earth and rock drilling.
[E21B 33/047] for plural tubing strings -- E21B 33/047 covers methods and devices for sealing or packing boreholes or wells during earth or rock drilling that employ multiple tubing strings. The classification addresses arrangements where several tubes are used together to provide a reliable barrier against fluid migration and to maintain well integrity during drilling operations. It falls under the broader categories of fixed constructions, earth drilling, and sealing of boreholes.
[E21B 33/05] Cementing-heads, e.g. having provision for introducing cementing plugs -- E21B 33/05 covers cementing heads used in earth-or-rock drilling, specifically designs that incorporate features for introducing cementing plugs into boreholes or wells. These devices form part of the sealing and packing equipment that secures the drilled cavity during cement placement.
[E21B 33/06] Blow-out preventers -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Rotating blow-out preventers -> E21B33/085
[E21B 33/061] Ram-type blow-out preventers, e.g. with pivoting rams -- Ram-type blow-out preventers with pivoting rams are devices used in fixed-construction earth-drilling operations to seal or pack boreholes and wells. They operate by closing hinged rams around the drill pipe to prevent uncontrolled fluid release, providing a reliable safety barrier during drilling and well completion. This subclass falls under the broader categories of earth (rock) drilling and sealing of boreholes.
[E21B 33/062] with sliding rams -- E21B 33/062 deals with methods and apparatus for sealing or packing boreholes or wells in earth or rock drilling using sliding rams that move to press sealing elements into the borehole wall. The sliding ram mechanism provides a controllable, reversible seal for temporary or permanent well completion.
[E21B 33/063] for shearing drill pipes  cutting of wireline  E21B29/04 -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Cutting of wireline -> E21B29/04

The subclass addresses devices, materials or methods that ensure reliable closure and pressure control in submerged drilling environments.
