It relates to fixed-construction earth-drilling techniques and describes how the pendulum's response is employed to determine the inclination or vertical alignment of the drill hole during inspection.
[E21B 47/024] of devices in the borehole -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Processes or apparatus for measuring the inclination or orientation of down-hole tools.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Determining slope or direction of the borehole -> E21B47/022
[E21B 47/026] of penetrated ground layers -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Processes or apparatus for measuring the inclination, i.e. dip, or orientation, i.e. strike, of a drilled stratum, e.g. by using a specially adapted compass, e.g. a Brunton compass.
[E21B 47/04] Measuring depth or liquid level -- The E21B 47/04 entry covers techniques and apparatus for determining how deep a borehole or well is and for measuring the level of liquids inside it, typically used during drilling and surveying of earth or rock holes. It includes devices such as depth rods, pressure gauges, electronic sensors and related methods that provide accurate depth or fluid-level data for construction, mining or geotechnical projects.
[E21B 47/047] Liquid level -- The classification E21B 47/047 covers techniques for measuring the liquid level in boreholes or wells during earth or rock drilling surveys. It includes methods and apparatus such as sounding, pressure, acoustic or electronic sensors used to determine fluid height, volume or interface within the drilled hole.
[E21B 47/053] using radioactive markers -- This subclass covers the use of radioactive tracers or markers to survey and characterize boreholes or wells during earth-or-rock drilling operations. It details methods for introducing, detecting, and interpreting radioactive substances to assess drill-hole geometry, integrity and surrounding formation properties.
[E21B 47/06] Measuring temperature or pressure -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Measurements of temperature or pressure when specific measurement techniques are disclosed, or if the temperature or pressure data is relevant for the execution of another operation.
[E21B 47/07] Temperature -- E21B 47/07 covers the determination or monitoring of temperature within boreholes or wells during earth or rock drilling operations, including instruments and techniques used to record thermal conditions for geological or engineering surveys. It is used for classification of patents relating to thermometric sensors, logging tools and data-acquisition systems employed in subsurface investigations.
[E21B 47/08] Measuring diameters or related dimensions at the borehole -- E21B 47/08 pertains to techniques and apparatus for measuring the diameter or related dimensions of a borehole during or after earth-drilling, using mechanical, optical, electronic or other sensors. It is applied in surveying boreholes or wells to obtain accurate size data for engineering, geological or production assessments.
[E21B 47/085] using radiant means, e.g. acoustic, radioactive or electromagnetic -- This CPC subclass covers methods for surveying boreholes or wells using radiant energy such as acoustic, radioactive or electromagnetic waves to detect formation properties. It is situated within fixed constructions for earth drilling.
[E21B 47/09] Locating or determining the position of objects in boreholes or wells -- E21B 47/09 covers techniques for locating or determining the position of objects within boreholes or wells, including tools, sensors, or material strings, using methods such as sonar, magnetic, optical or mechanical measurements. The classification applies to surveys performed during or after drilling to identify the spatial placement of equipment, samples or deposits inside the drilled cavity.
[E21B 47/092] by detecting magnetic anomalies -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Processes or apparatus for determining the whereabouts or position of objects in a borehole or well using magnetic attraction/repulsion force irregularities.
[E21B 47/095] by detecting an acoustic anomalies, e.g. using mud-pressure pulses -- E21B 47/095 relates to methods for surveying boreholes or wells by detecting acoustic anomalies, such as using mud-pressure pulses. It falls under fixed constructions, earth drilling, earth or rock drilling. This classification covers acoustic monitoring techniques for evaluating borehole conditions.
[E21B 47/098] using impression packers, e.g. to detect recesses or perforations -- E21B 47/098 falls under Fixed Constructions - Earth Drilling - Earth or Rock Drilling - Survey of boreholes or wells. It covers methods that employ impression packers to identify recesses, perforations or irregularities within a borehole or well. The technique is used to improve assessment of well integrity and guide subsequent drilling or completion operations.
[E21B 47/10] Locating fluid leaks, intrusions or movements -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Processes or apparatus for determining if the pipes are sufficiently fluid-tight to prevent leakage or ingress of liquid or gas.  Processes or apparatus for detecting unwanted fluids flowing from the formation into the borehole.  Processes or apparatus for detecting the presence and properties of production fluids in the borehole.  Processes or apparatus for determining well debit or flow rate.  Processes or apparatus for measuring or detecting flow of oil, water, or gas, or measuring properties (e.g. electrical) of fluids.
[E21B 47/103] using thermal measurements -- This subclass pertains to techniques that employ temperature measurements while surveying boreholes or wells, such as thermal logging to determine formation characteristics, fluid flow, or heat exchange. It falls under fixed constructions, earth drilling, earth or rock drilling.
[E21B 47/107] using acoustic means -- E21B 47/107 pertains to the application of acoustic techniques for surveying boreholes or wells during earth or rock drilling, such as acoustic logging or sonar methods used to assess formation properties and drill-hole integrity. It is nested under Fixed Constructions → Earth Drilling → Earth or Rock Drilling → Survey of Boreholes or Wells.
[E21B 47/11] using tracers -- E21B 47/11 covers techniques for employing chemical or physical tracers to analyse and monitor boreholes or wells during earth-or-rock drilling operations, aiding in the assessment of formation properties, fluid movement, and drilling performance. The classification falls under Fixed Constructions → Earth Drilling → Earth or Rock Drilling → Survey of Boreholes or Wells.
[E21B 47/111] using radioactivity -- The E21B 47/111 subclass covers techniques that employ radioactive sources or the detection of natural radioactivity to evaluate boreholes or wells during earth-or-rock drilling surveys. It includes methods such as gamma-ray logging, neutron activation and radiometric measurements used to determine formation composition, density and other geological parameters.
[E21B 47/113] using electrical indications -- The code E21B 47/113 applies to fixed earth-drilling constructions that employ electrical signals or measurements to survey or monitor boreholes and wells, such as detecting formation properties, tool position, or fluid presence during drilling.
[E21B 47/114] using light radiation -- E21B 47/114 covers techniques that employ light radiation, such as lasers or optical sensors, to examine, measure, or map the interior of boreholes and wells during or after drilling. The methods are used for obtaining geometric, structural, or compositional information of the drilled formation without physical contact.
[E21B 47/117] Detecting leaks, e.g. from tubing, by pressure testing -- E21B 47/117 pertains to techniques for detecting leaks in boreholes or wells, such as from tubing, by applying pressure-testing methods. It falls under the Fixed Constructions group for Earth drilling, specifically Earth or Rock drilling, and focuses on surveying boreholes or wells.
[E21B 47/12] Means for transmitting measuring-signals or control signals from the well to the surface, or from the surface to the well, e.g. for logging while drilling -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Processes or apparatus in which an information or control signal is sent to or from equipment located in the borehole over an information transmission link.
[E21B 47/125] using earth as an electrical conductor -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Processes or apparatus in which the transmission link is through the ground.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Means for transmitting measuring-signals or control signals from the well to the surface, or from the surface to the well, e.g. for logging while drilling, by electromagnetic energy -> E21B47/13
[E21B 47/13] by electromagnetic energy, e.g. radio frequency -- This classification covers methods for surveying boreholes or wells using electromagnetic energy, such as radio-frequency signals, to detect geological formations, fluid presence, or structural features. It includes non-contact probing techniques that generate and receive EM fields to map the interior of drilled holes.
[E21B 47/135] using light waves, e.g. infrared or ultraviolet waves -- The entry covers techniques for surveying boreholes or wells that employ light waves, such as infrared, ultraviolet or other optical radiation, to detect, map or analyse the geometry, composition or condition of the drilled formation. It includes the use of optical sensors, spectroscopic methods, or laser-based devices for non-contact inspection of underground openings.
[E21B 47/138] Devices entrained in the flow of well-bore fluid for transmitting data, control or actuation signals -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Devices or carriers that are flowable with or through fluids in the well for performing measurements or for transmitting data, control or actuating signals.
[E21B 47/14] using acoustic waves -- The subclass E21B 47/14 refers to techniques that employ acoustic waves for surveying boreholes or wells during fixed-construction earth or rock drilling. It includes methods and devices that generate, transmit, receive or analyze acoustic signals to determine geological or structural information about the drilled formation. This classification is used for acoustic logging, sonic imaging and related non-destructive evaluation of drilled holes.
[E21B 47/16] through the drill string or casing -- The CPC subclass E21B 47/16 covers techniques for surveying boreholes or wells by using instruments or sensors positioned inside the drill string or the casing, allowing measurements to be taken while drilling or when the hole is cased. It pertains to fixed-construction earth-drilling equipment used for geological, geophysical or engineering investigations.
[E21B 47/18] through the well fluid -- The class covers techniques and devices that perform surveys of a borehole or well by moving through the fluid that fills the hole, such as tools for logging, sampling, acoustic or electromagnetic measurement, or imaging while the well contains drilling or completion fluid.
[E21B 47/20] by modulation of mud waves, e.g. by continuous modulation -- This subclass pertains to borehole or well surveying methods that employ modulation of the drilling mud, for example by continuously varying mud flow to create detectable wave patterns. The mud-wave modulation is used to acquire data on borehole geometry or surrounding formations.
[E21B 47/22] by negative mud pulses using a pressure relieve valve between drill pipe and annulus -- This subclass covers methods of surveying boreholes or wells that employ negative mud pulse telemetry, where pressure pulses are generated by a valve that relieves pressure between the drill pipe and the annulus. The technique is used to transmit data from downhole tools to the surface without active mud injection.
[E21B 47/24] by positive mud pulses using a flow restricting valve within the drill pipe -- E21B 47/24 covers a borehole-survey method that creates positive mud pulses by operating a flow-restricting valve inside the drill pipe, allowing pressure-based telemetry of measurement data while drilling. The technique is used for monitoring and logging conditions in earth or rock drilling operations.
[E21B 47/26] Storing data down-hole, e.g. in a memory or on a record carrier -- The entry E21B 47/26 classifies devices that store information within a borehole or well, such as down-hole memory modules or record carriers, enabling data collection and retrieval directly at the drilling site.
[E21B 49/00] Testing the nature of borehole walls -- E21B 49/00 covers methods and devices for evaluating the condition of borehole walls during or after drilling, including measurements of stability, rock quality, moisture, and stress. Techniques such as visual inspection, acoustic logging, electrical resistivity, and pressure testing are included to assess wall integrity and guide drilling or support decisions.
[E21B 49/001] specially adapted for underwater installations -- E21B 49/001 relates to devices or methods for testing the nature of borehole walls that are specially adapted for underwater installations, such as offshore or subsea drilling operations. These tools are designed to operate reliably under submerged conditions where conventional testing equipment may not be suitable.
[E21B 49/003] by analysing drilling variables or conditions  E21B49/005  takes precedence; systems specially adapted for monitoring a plurality of drilling variables or conditions  E21B44/00 -- The entry E21B 49/003 covers methods for testing borehole-wall nature by analysing drilling variables or conditions, such as torque, penetration rate, and fluid pressure. It is subordinate to E21B 49/005, which has precedence, and relates to systems specially adapted for monitoring a plurality of drilling variables or conditions within the broader categories of fixed constructions, earth drilling and earth-or-rock drilling.
[E21B 49/005] Testing the nature of borehole walls or the formation by using drilling mud or cutting data -- E21B 49/005 concerns methods for testing the nature of borehole walls or the surrounding formation by analysing drilling-mud characteristics or cutting data obtained during earth or rock drilling. This falls under fixed constructions for earth drilling, specifically earth or rock drilling testing. It is used to classify patents related to borehole-wall evaluation techniques.
[E21B 49/006] Measuring wall stresses in the borehole -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Measuring wall stresses in the borehole through in-situ measurements only.
[E21B 49/008] by injection test; by analysing pressure variations in an injection or production test, e.g. for estimating the skin factor  measuring pressure  E21B47/06 -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Measuring pressure of boreholes or wells -> E21B47/06
[E21B 49/02] by mechanically taking samples of the soil -- E21B 49/02 covers methods for testing borehole wall conditions by mechanically extracting soil or rock samples from the drilled hole. The technique involves using sampling tools to retrieve material for analysis, providing direct information on the composition and properties of the surrounding earth. This subclass falls under Fixed Constructions → Earth Drilling → Earth or Rock Drilling → Testing the nature of borehole walls.
[E21B 49/025] of underwater soil, e.g. with grab devices -- This subgroup covers techniques for examining the material of borehole walls that are submerged, typically by employing grab samplers to retrieve or assess underwater soil.
[E21B 49/04] using explosives in boreholes -- E21B 49/04 pertains to techniques that place explosives in a borehole to assess the properties of the surrounding earth or rock, such as strength, fracture behavior or mineral composition, serving as a test of borehole-wall nature during drilling operations.
[E21B 49/06] using side-wall drilling tools or scrapers -- The classification E21B 49/06 relates to methods that employ side-wall drilling tools or scrapers to test borehole wall characteristics during earth or rock drilling operations. It falls under the testing the nature of borehole walls subgroup of fixed constructions for earth drilling and is used in patents concerning geological evaluation of drilled holes.
[E21B 49/08] Obtaining fluid samples or testing fluids, in boreholes or wells -- E21B 49/08 covers techniques and equipment for retrieving fluid samples and analysing the properties of fluids present in boreholes or wells during drilling operations. The entry includes procedures for in-situ testing of water, drilling mud, or formation fluids to assess composition, pressure, temperature and other parameters relevant to geological and engineering evaluation.
[E21B 49/081] with down-hole means for trapping a fluid sample  E21B49/10  takes precedence -- This CPC subclass covers earth-drilling tools that incorporate down-hole devices designed to capture fluid from the borehole for analysis, used to test the nature of borehole walls. It is a subset of E21B 49/10, which addresses testing borehole wall properties, and falls under the broader categories of fixed constructions for earth or rock drilling.
[E21B 49/0813] Sampling valve actuated by annulus pressure changes -- E21B 49/0813 covers sampling valves in drilling rigs that open or close in response to pressure changes in the annular space around the drill string, allowing retrieval of cuttings or fluid from the borehole wall without interrupting drilling. These devices are used in earth-rock drilling to obtain representative samples for analysis.
[E21B 49/0815] Sampling valve actuated by tubing pressure changes -- This classification covers a sampling valve for borehole testing that is triggered by changes in the pressure of the drilling tubing, allowing formation fluids or cuttings to be collected without manual intervention. The device is used in earth-rock drilling to assess borehole-wall properties.
[E21B 49/082] Wire-line fluid samplers  E21B49/083  takes precedence -- The subclass E21B 49/082 covers wire-line fluid samplers, devices mounted on a wire-line that retrieve fluid from a borehole for analysis of the surrounding wall material. These tools are employed in earth or rock drilling to test the nature of borehole walls and assess formation properties during drilling operations.
[E21B 49/083] Samplers adapted to be lowered into or retrieved from a landing nipple, e.g. for testing a well without removing the drill string -- E21B 49/083 defines samplers that can be lowered through or retrieved from a landing nipple to test bore-hole walls or well conditions without dismantling the drill string, falling under fixed-construction earth-drilling equipment.
[E21B 49/084] with means for conveying samples through pipe to surface -- E21B 49/084 covers drill rigs or earth-drilling installations that incorporate a system for transporting rock or soil samples from the borehole to the surface through a pipe. The classification applies to fixed-construction equipment used when testing borehole wall conditions, enabling continuous or periodic sample conveyance for analysis.
[E21B 49/086] Withdrawing samples at the surface -- The code E21B 49/086 covers techniques for removing material from a borehole to the surface for analysis of the surrounding earth or rock walls. It includes devices and procedures such as coring, side-wall sampling and other methods that retrieve intact samples for testing the nature of borehole walls.
[E21B 49/087] Well testing, e.g. testing for reservoir productivity or formation parameters -- E21B 49/087 covers methods for testing borehole walls and evaluating reservoir productivity or formation parameters, including pressure-transient analysis, flow-rate measurement and fluid sampling to assess the physical and hydraulic characteristics of the geological formation.
[E21B 49/0875] determining specific fluid parameters -- This CPC subgroup concerns methods for evaluating the properties of drilling fluids by measuring parameters such as viscosity, density and chemical composition to assess borehole wall conditions during earth or rock drilling. It is located under Fixed Constructions, Earth Drilling, Earth or Rock Drilling, Testing the nature of borehole walls.
[E21B 49/088] combined with sampling -- The E21B 49/088 subclass covers methods that test borehole wall characteristics while simultaneously retrieving material from the hole. It pertains to earth-drilling operations where sampling and wall-condition assessment are performed together, providing combined geological data in a single procedure.
[E21B 49/10] using side-wall fluid samplers or testers -- E21B 49/10 covers techniques that employ side-wall fluid samplers or testing tools to collect and analyse fluids from the walls of a borehole while drilling, enabling assessment of the geological formation's properties. The methods are used in fixed construction drilling operations to evaluate the nature of earth or rock surrounding the drilled cavity.
[E21B 6/00] Drives for drilling with combined rotary and percussive action -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Drives outside the borehole for combined percussion and rotary drilling.  Illustrative example of the subject matter classified in this place:
[E21B 6/02] the rotation being continuous -- This subclass concerns drives for earth-or-rock drilling machines that combine a continuously rotating motion with percussive impacts. It covers mechanisms that maintain constant rotation while delivering hammering forces to advance the drill bit, typical of fixed-construction rotary-percussive drill rigs.
[E21B 6/04] Separate drives for percussion and rotation -- E21B 6/04 pertains to earth-or-rock drilling machines that use distinct drive units for the rotary and percussive motions. By providing separate mechanisms for each action, the design allows independent control and optimization of drilling performance, fitting within fixed-construction drilling equipment that combines rotary and percussive action.
[E21B 6/06] the rotation being intermittent, e.g. obtained by ratchet device -- E21B 6/06 refers to drilling drives in which the rotary motion is applied intermittently rather than continuously, typically achieved with a ratchet mechanism that engages and disengages the drive to produce a stepwise rotation during drilling operations. This classification covers systems that combine rotary and percussive actions for earth or rock drilling where the rotation is not steady.
[E21B 6/08] Separate drives for percussion and rotation -- The subclass covers drill machines in which the rotary motion and the percussive impact are generated by separate drive units rather than a single combined mechanism, allowing independent control of each action. It includes designs where distinct motors, gear trains or shafts deliver rotation and hammering to the drill bit, typically used for earth or rock drilling where combined rotary-percussive action is required.
[E21B 7/00] Special methods or apparatus for drilling -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Drilling methods and apparatus characterised by any aspect that differs from those of conventional drilling, which is understood as drilling a vertical borehole with a roller or drag type bit, using a derrick or mast.
[E21B 7/001] Drilling a non circular hole  excavating trenches  E02F5/02 ; cutting machines for slitting  E21C25/00 -- E21B 7/001 covers special methods or apparatus for creating non-circular openings in the ground, such as drilling irregular-shaped holes or excavating trenches, and includes related cutting machines used for slitting. The classification groups techniques that deviate from standard circular drilling to achieve specific trench or slot profiles.
[E21B 7/002] Drilling with diversely driven shafts extending into the borehole  ⁠  E21B7/001  takes precedence -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Drilling devices with multiple shafts in the borehole that are independently driven or one shaft is driven relative to another.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Drilling a non-circular hole -> E21B7/001
[E21B 7/003] Drilling with mechanical conveying means -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Drilling devices with mechanical means for removing drill cuttings and soil from the borehole.
[E21B 7/005] with helical conveying means  E21B7/201  takes precedence; augers  E21B10/44 ; drilling rods or pipes with helical structure  E21B17/22
[E21B 7/006] combined with a bucket-type container -- E21B 7/006 covers drilling apparatus that incorporates a bucket-type container, enabling the simultaneous removal or collection of cut earth or rock as the drill advances. The bucket may be mounted on the drill frame or carriage, allowing material to be gathered without separate handling equipment, thereby streamlining the drilling and loading process.
[E21B 7/007] Drilling by use of explosives  underwater drilling using explosives  E21B7/1245 -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Underwater drilling using explosives -> E21B7/1245
[E21B 7/008] Drilling ice or a formation covered by ice -- E21B 7/008 covers equipment and techniques for penetrating ice layers or drilling through formations that are overlain by ice. The subclass includes specialized drill bits, heating mechanisms, and support systems designed to maintain borehole stability and reduce friction in frozen environments. It falls under the CPC group for special methods or apparatus for drilling within the fixed constructions sector.
[E21B 7/02] Drilling rigs characterised by means for land transport , e.g. skid mounting or wheel mounting -- E21B 7/02 refers to earth-or-rock drilling rigs that include built-in land-transport means such as skids, wheels or other chassis, enabling the whole drilling machine to be moved across terrain without dismantling. The mobility features are integral to the rig's design.
[E21B 7/021] With a rotary table, i.e. a fixed rotary drive for a relatively advancing tool -- The code E21B 7/021 covers fixed-construction earth-or-rock-drilling machines that use a rotary table as a permanent rotary drive for a tool that advances into the ground. It refers to special methods or apparatus where the table provides the rotating motion while the drill bit moves forward.
[E21B 7/022] Control of the drilling operation; Hydraulic or pneumatic means for activation or operation  control circuits for drilling masts  E21B15/045 -- E21B 7/022 covers systems that control the drilling process on earth-or-rock drilling machines, specifically using hydraulic or pneumatic power to operate the control circuits of drilling masts. It includes devices that activate or regulate drilling functions through fluid-driven mechanisms, enabling precise management of feed rate, rotation and other operational parameters.
[E21B 7/023] the mast being foldable or telescopically retractable -- E21B 7/023 covers drilling equipment whose mast can be folded or telescopically retracted, enabling compact storage or transport while maintaining the ability to extend for earth or rock drilling operations. This subclass falls under special methods or apparatus for drilling within the fixed-construction earth-drilling category.
[E21B 7/024] having means for adapting to inclined terrain; having means for stabilizing the vehicle while drilling -- The CPC class E21B 7/024 defines earth-drilling machines that incorporate features for operating on sloping ground and for stabilising the vehicle while drilling. It pertains to fixed-construction drilling equipment and specifies means such as adjustable legs, counter-weights or hydraulic supports that allow the machine to maintain alignment and safety on inclined terrain.
[E21B 7/025] Rock drills, i.e. jumbo drills -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Drilling holes into rock material or soil in mines, quarries and construction sites, e.g. blast holes or anchor bolt holes, using drilling machines with drill motor carriage displaceable on feed beam.
[E21B 7/026] having auxiliary platforms, e.g. for observation purposes -- The E21B 7/026 subclass concerns earth-or-rock drilling equipment that includes auxiliary platforms, such as observation decks, enabling operators or observers to stand or move around the drill while it is in use. These platforms may be fixed or retractable and are intended to improve safety, monitoring, or control of the drilling operation.
[E21B 7/027] Drills for drilling shallow holes, e.g. for taking soil samples or for drilling postholes -- E21B 7/027 classifies fixed-construction drills intended for shallow-hole applications such as taking soil samples or creating postholes. It falls under the hierarchy of earth drilling, specifically earth-or-rock drilling, and addresses special methods or apparatus for shallow-depth drilling.
[E21B 7/028] the drilling apparatus being detachable from the vehicle, e.g. hand portable drills -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Drilling tools portable, e.g. by hand, and detachable from portable drilling rig vehicles, e.g. from motorcars.
[E21B 7/04] Directional drilling -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Directional drilling in the general sense of producing a borehole in a direction other that vertical. The drilling direction can be essentially horizontal, such as for laying a pipe under an obstruction or in an urban environment.
[E21B 7/043] for underwater installations -- E21B 7/043 covers special methods or apparatus for drilling earth or rock in underwater installations, such as offshore foundations, subsea pipelines or underwater mining operations. It pertains to fixed-construction applications where drilling is performed beneath the water surface.
[E21B 7/046] horizontal drilling  drilling with mechanical conveying means  E21B7/003 -- A method and apparatus for horizontal drilling in which the cut material is removed by a mechanical conveying system rather than fluid transport. It falls under the CPC group for special methods or apparatus for earth or rock drilling, specifically within the fixed-construction earth-drilling subclass. This enables efficient drilling in shallow or shallow-angled formations while maintaining continuous removal of cuttings.
[E21B 7/06] Deflecting the direction of boreholes -- E21B 7/06 covers special methods or devices that change the trajectory of a drill during earth or rock drilling, allowing the borehole to be steered or deflected from its original path. The classification is used for apparatus such as bent-stem tools, steering heads, or directional-drilling mechanisms.
[E21B 7/061] the tool shaft advancing relative to a guide, e.g. a curved tube or a whipstock -- A method in earth-rock drilling where the drill shaft moves forward while being guided by a separate element such as a curved tube or a whipstock, allowing the shaft to advance along a curved or redirected path rather than a fixed straight line.
[E21B 7/062] the tool shaft rotating inside a non-rotating guide travelling with the shaft  E21B7/067  and  E21B7/068  take precedence -- E21B 7/062 covers earth or rock drilling where the drill shaft itself rotates while a stationary guide or sleeve moves along the shaft, supporting and steering the tool. This arrangement is used for special drilling methods or apparatus. The more specific sub-groups E21B 7/067 and E21B 7/068 provide further detail and take precedence over this broader group.
[E21B 7/064] specially adapted drill bits therefor -- This subclass concerns drill bits that have been specially designed or modified for use in fixed-construction earth-or-rock drilling machines. It includes bits with unique geometries, materials or features intended to enhance cutting efficiency, durability or adaptability to particular drilling conditions.
[E21B 7/065] using oriented fluid jets -- Code E21B 7/065 covers drilling techniques that employ fluid jets directed in a specific orientation to assist or replace mechanical cutting when penetrating earth or rock. The method uses high-pressure streams of water or other fluids aligned to the drill axis or to the borehole wall to fracture material, improve chip removal, or cool the cutting zone, and may be integrated with conventional drill rigs.
[E21B 7/067] with means for locking sections of a pipe or of a guide for a shaft in angular relation, e.g. adjustable bent sub -- The code E21B 7/067 covers drilling hardware that incorporates a locking device for connecting pipe sections or guiding shafts at a defined angle, such as an adjustable bent sub. This mechanism allows the drill string to be set in an angular relationship, facilitating directional changes or coupling of components in earth-or-rock drilling rigs.
[E21B 7/068] drilled by a down-hole drilling motor  down-hole drives  per se   E21B4/00 ,  E21B7/067  takes precedence -- This sub-group covers earth-or-rock drilling methods in which the cutting action is powered by a motor situated down the borehole, providing the drive directly at the drill bit. It includes apparatuses and techniques where the down-hole motor is the primary means of advancing the drill without surface-mounted drive mechanisms.
[E21B 7/10] Correction of deflected boreholes -- E21B 7/10 covers methods and tools used to correct boreholes that have become deflected during earth or rock drilling. It includes techniques for realigning the drill path, adjusting the drill string, and employing stabilising or steering devices to bring the hole back to its intended trajectory.
[E21B 7/12] Underwater drilling -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Underwater drilling using heave compensators -> E21B19/09
[E21B 7/122] with submersible vertically movable guide -- This class covers fixed drilling installations for earth or rock that incorporate a submersible guide capable of vertical movement during operation. The vertically movable guide is designed to work underwater or in flooded conditions, providing precise alignment and depth control for the drill bit.
[E21B 7/124] with underwater tool drive prime mover, e.g. portable drilling rigs for use on underwater floors -- The code E21B 7/124 refers to earth-or-rock drilling equipment that incorporates the prime mover and tool drive beneath the water surface, such as portable rigs used on underwater floors. It covers systems where the drilling power source is located in the submerged environment, allowing direct drilling without surface-based drive shafts.
[E21B 7/1245] using explosive means  anchors driven in by explosive charges  B63B21/28 -- E21B 7/1245 covers special methods or apparatus for earth or rock drilling that use explosive means, specifically the deployment of anchors driven into the ground by explosive charges. This subclass falls under the broader groups for fixed constructions and earth drilling, linking explosive anchoring techniques to drilling operations.
[E21B 7/128] from floating support with independent underwater anchored guide base -- The CPC subclass E21B 7/128 covers special drilling methods or apparatus where the drilling platform is a floating support that operates using a separate, underwater-anchored guide base to steer and stabilise the drill. This arrangement allows precise earth or rock drilling from vessels that remain buoyant while the guide base remains fixed on the seabed.
[E21B 7/132] from underwater buoyant support -- E21B 7/132 classifies special earth-drilling methods or apparatus that employ underwater buoyant support to stabilize or position drilling tools, allowing operations in submerged or marine environments. The entry covers devices such as buoyancy aids, floating platforms or submerged housings that assist rock-or-soil drilling by providing lift and steadiness while the drill functions beneath water.
[E21B 7/136] from non-buoyant support -- E21B 7/136 refers to drilling arrangements where the drill is mounted on a support that does not rely on buoyancy, such as a fixed platform, rig or land-based structure. It encompasses special methods or apparatus that secure the drill head without using floating devices, typically for earth or rock drilling from stable, non-buoyant bases.
[E21B 7/14] Drilling by use of heat, e.g. flame drilling -- E21B 7/14 covers special drilling methods that use heat-such as flame drilling-to melt, vaporise or otherwise soften earth or rock, allowing the drill to advance without traditional mechanical cutting. It falls under fixed constructions, earth drilling, earth or rock drilling, and special methods or apparatus for drilling.
[E21B 7/143] underwater -- The code E21B 7/143 covers drilling installations and devices that are specifically adapted for use under water, such as subsea or marine drilling rigs, floating drilling towers and related apparatus with sealing, buoyancy or pressure-compensation features that enable earth or rock drilling in a submerged environment.
[E21B 7/146] Thermal lances -- The CPC subclass E21B 7/146 covers thermal lances, devices that burn a mix of iron powder and oxygen to generate a high-temperature flame for cutting or drilling through rock and earth formations. It pertains to special methods and apparatus for earth or rock drilling that rely on the intense heat produced by the lance to melt or fracture the material. This subclass falls under Fixed constructions → Earth drilling → Earth or rock drilling.
[E21B 7/15] of electrically generated heat -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Drilling using, for example, lasers or electrical discharge between electrodes that generate shock waves.
[E21B 7/16] Applying separate balls or pellets by the pressure of the drill, so-called shot-drilling -- This subclass covers techniques where individual steel balls or pellets are propelled forward by the drill's own pressure to create a series of holes, known as shot-drilling, used for anchoring or stabilizing structures in rock or soil. It is applied in fixed-construction earth-drilling operations to quickly install reinforcement.
[E21B 7/18] Drilling by liquid or gas jets, with or without entrained pellets -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Drilling by use of heat, e.g. flame drilling -> E21B7/14
[E21B 7/185] underwater -- E21B 7/185 covers drilling equipment and techniques specifically adapted for use beneath water, including submersible rigs, underwater drill strings, and devices that allow drilling through a water column to reach seabed or submerged ground formations. The classification addresses the special requirements of pressure sealing, buoyancy control and corrosion resistance needed for effective underwater earth-or-rock drilling.
[E21B 7/20] Driving or forcing casings or pipes into boreholes, e.g. sinking -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods in which the drilling action takes place simultaneously with the installation of a casing or of the pipe to be installed, with the borehole being horizontal or not.
[E21B 7/201] with helical conveying means -- E21B 7/201 is a CPC subclass within Fixed Constructions → Earth Drilling → Earth or Rock Drilling that covers special drilling methods or apparatus using helical conveying means, such as screw-type conveyors or auger flights, to transport cuttings or tools along the drill string.
[E21B 7/203] using down-hole drives -- E21B 7/203 covers the use of down-hole drives in earth or rock drilling equipment, a special method or apparatus for drilling where the power source is placed near the drill bit for improved torque and efficiency.
[E21B 7/205] without earth removal  E21B7/30  takes precedence -- E21B 7/205, within the hierarchy Fixed Constructions → Earth Drilling → Earth or Rock Drilling → Special methods or apparatus for drilling, covers drilling techniques or equipment that operate without removing earth, such as core-tube or annular-drilling systems. This subclass is subordinate to E21B 7/30, which takes precedence over it.
[E21B 7/206] using down-hole drives -- E21B 7/206 covers the use of down-hole drives in earth or rock drilling. It falls under the CPC group for special methods or apparatus for drilling within fixed constructions, earth drilling and earth or rock drilling. This classification is used by the EPO and USPTO to index technologies that power the drill bit from a drive located in the borehole.
[E21B 7/208] using down-hole drives  E21B7/203  and  E21B7/206  take precedence -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Using down-hole drives in combination with helical conveying means -> E21B7/203 - Using down-hole drives without earth removal -> E21B7/206
[E21B 7/24] Drilling using vibrating or oscillating means, e.g. out-of-balance masses -- E21B 7/24 covers drill systems that advance a bit in earth or rock by generating vibration or oscillation, for example using out-of-balance masses or similar devices, rather than purely rotary or percussive force.
[E21B 7/26] Drilling without earth removal, e.g. with self-propelled burrowing devices -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Drilling or forming holes without cutting into the soil or earth.  Drilling or forming holes by driving a tool into the earth to displace and compact the soil.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Enlarging drilled holes, e.g. by counterboring, without earth removal -> E21B7/30
[E21B 7/265] Combined with earth removal -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The use of earth removal devices or processes separate from the drilling devices to remove earth or soil from the hole.
[E21B 7/267] Drilling devices with senders, e.g. radio-transmitters for position of drilling tool -- The class E21B 7/267 covers drilling installations that incorporate transmission units such as radio-frequency or other senders to convey the position, depth or orientation of the drill bit or tool while drilling fixed earth or rock structures. These devices enable real-time monitoring and control of the drilling process.
[E21B 7/28] Enlarging drilled holes, e.g. by counterboring -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Vertical or horizontal drilling, in which means are provided for enlarging a borehole made previously, which borehole may have been made by the same machine carrying out the enlargement, or by a different machine or in a previous operation.  Illustrative examples of the subject matter classified in this place:  1.
[E21B 7/30] without earth removal -- E21B 7/30 covers drilling devices that create holes without extracting the cut material, such as core-less or fluid-based drilling methods. It pertains to fixed construction equipment for earth or rock drilling where the drilled material is displaced or dissolved rather than removed.
[E21C] MINING OR QUARRYING -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods or apparatus specific to mining or quarrying.
[E21C 2100/00] Modeling, simulating or designing mining operations -- The class covers the use of computer models, simulations, and design tools to plan and optimise mining processes, including drill planning, equipment layout, and production scheduling for fixed-construction mining operations. It also includes virtual testing of excavation methods, resource-extraction scenarios, and analysis of safety or environmental impacts.
[E21C 2200/00] Mining robots -- E21C 2200/00 covers robotic equipment designed for use in mining and quarrying operations, including autonomous or remotely controlled machines that perform drilling, ore extraction, material handling or underground surveying. The classification applies to fixed-construction systems where robots replace or assist human labor in the earth drilling and mining processes.
[E21C 25/00] Cutting, Slitting, Dislodging -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Machines having cutting means for making a slit or a plurality of slits in the seam, so that the material in the seam can be removed in a subsequent step. Illustrative example:  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Dislodging machines with slitting means -> E21C27/02 , E21C27/10 , E21C27/18
[E21C 25/02] Machines slitting solely by one or more percussive tools moved through the seam -- The code covers machines that slit material only with one or more percussive tools that are driven through a seam, without any rotating or grinding action. It applies to fixed-construction equipment used in earth-drilling, mining or quarrying for cutting, slitting or dislodging rock or similar substrates.
[E21C 25/04] Cutting crowns or other tools -- The subclass E21C 25/04 pertains to cutting crowns or other tools employed in fixed-construction earth-drilling or mining operations. It includes methods and devices for shaping, removing or refurbishing tool crowns as part of cutting, slitting or dislodging processes.
[E21C 25/06] Machines slitting solely by one or more cutting rods or cutting drums which rotate, move through the seam, and may or may not reciprocate -- E21C 25/06 refers to machines used in mining or quarrying that perform slitting operations solely with one or more rotating cutting rods or drums that travel through the seam, and may or may not include a reciprocating motion.
[E21C 25/08] Mountings for the rods or drums -- E21C 25/08 covers devices that secure the rods or drums on drilling machines used for earth drilling in mining or quarrying. These mountings ensure proper alignment and transmission of torque to the cutting or slitting tools - they are part of fixed construction drilling equipment.
[E21C 25/10] Rods -- E21C 25/10 covers rods used as cutting, slitting or dislodging elements in earth-drilling, mining or quarrying operations, including drill rods, drive rods and other elongated components that transmit rotary or percussive force. The code applies to the design, material and configuration of these rods for fixed-construction drilling equipment.
[E21C 25/14] with equipment for cleaning the slit -- E21C 25/14 covers fixed-construction earth-drilling machines used in mining or quarrying that incorporate apparatus for cleaning the slit produced during cutting or slitting operations, typically removing debris and ensuring the opening remains clear for further processing.
[E21C 25/145] Cowls -- Cowls are protective housings or shrouds fitted around cutting or drilling tools in fixed construction mining equipment. They guide and contain material displaced during cutting, slitting or dislodging operations, improving safety and directing debris away from the work area.
[E21C 25/16] Machines slitting solely by one or more rotating saws, cutting discs, or wheels -- This CPC subclass E21C 25/16 covers machines that perform slitting operations using one or more rotating saws, cutting discs, or wheels, typically employed in earth drilling and mining to separate material. The machines are designed solely for cutting, slitting, or dislodging rock or ore, without additional processing functions.
[E21C 25/18] Saws -- E21C 25/18 covers saws employed in fixed earth-drilling installations for mining or quarrying, specifically tools that cut, slit or dislodge rock and other hard materials. The classification includes circular, band and reciprocating saws mounted on drilling rigs or stationary frames to shape or break down mineral seams.
[E21C 25/20] Machines slitting solely by one or more reciprocating sawing implements or reciprocating cutter chains -- E21C 25/20 pertains to fixed construction equipment for mining or quarrying that performs slitting exclusively with one or more reciprocating saw blades or reciprocating cutter chains, without using rotary or other cutting tools. These machines are designed to cut, slit or dislodge rock, ore or similar materials by a back-and-forth motion of the cutting implements.
[E21C 25/22] Machines slitting solely by one or more cutter chains moving unidirectionally along jibs -- E21C 25/22 covers machines designed to slit material using one or more cutter chains that travel in a single direction along a jib or similar support, without additional cutting actions. The chains provide continuous, linear cutting for preparing sections of rock, ore or other substrates in fixed earth-drilling or mining installations. This subclass is part of the broader Fixed Constructions → Earth Drilling → Mining or Quarrying → Cutting, Slitting, Dislodging hierarchy.
[E21C 25/24] with flat jibs only -- It designates stationary earth-drilling installations used in mining or quarrying where the supporting arms are flat jibs, meaning the jib sections lie in a single plane and lack rotating or angled sections. These flat-jib structures are employed for cutting, slitting or dislodging rock during drilling operations.
[E21C 25/26] with curved jibs only -- This CPC subgroup covers fixed earth-drilling constructions for mining or quarrying that use only curved jibs to position the drill or cutting tool. It applies to machines where the supporting arm is permanently curved rather than straight, enabling horizontal or inclined drilling operations.
[E21C 25/28] Chains or chain guides -- E21C 25/28 covers the use of chains or chain guides within fixed constructions employed for earth drilling and mining or quarrying operations, specifically for cutting, slitting, or dislodging material. The classification pertains to devices that transmit mechanical force via a chain or guide to the workpiece.
[E21C 25/30] Chain guides -- E21C 25/30 pertains to chain guides employed in fixed-construction earth-drilling machines for mining or quarrying, where they direct and support the chain mechanisms used to cut, slit or dislodge material. These guides ensure proper chain alignment and tension, improving operational efficiency and equipment longevity.
[E21C 25/32] specially adapted for curved jibs -- E21C 25/32 refers to fixed-construction equipment for earth-drilling, mining or quarrying in which the cutting or dislodging tool is mounted on a jib that is specially shaped and curved. The curved jib permits the tool to be positioned at an angle or around obstacles, improving reach and flexibility.
[E21C 25/34] Chains -- E21C 25/34 refers to chains employed in earth-drilling and mining operations for cutting, slitting or dislodging material, as well as for hoisting, anchoring or conveying loads within quarries and mines. The subclass includes various chain designs such as link, roller or endless chains that are integral to fixed-construction equipment.
[E21C 25/36] Couplings for links -- The code E21C 25/36 covers couplings designed to join links in fixed-construction drilling equipment used for earth-drilling, mining or quarrying operations. These components transmit torque and axial forces between drill rods, pipe sections or other drilling members, facilitating cutting, slitting or dislodging of material.
[E21C 25/365] allowing the bending into at least two planes  chains for conveyors which are allowed to bend into at least two planes  B65G17/385 -- E21C 25/365 covers conveyor chains that can bend in at least two planes, allowing flexible routing of material handling systems within mining, quarrying and earth-drilling installations. The design supports cutting, slitting or dislodging functions in fixed construction equipment.
[E21C 25/40] having links with integrally formed picks -- E21C 25/40 covers fixed-construction drilling tools for mining or quarrying in which the cutting elements are integral picks that are joined to the body by links formed as a single piece, providing a continuous, robust cutting assembly.
[E21C 25/50] with equipment for cleaning the slit -- This CPC subclass (E21C 25/50) covers fixed earth-drilling installations used in mining or quarrying where a slit is cut in the ground and the subsequent cleaning of that slit is performed with dedicated equipment. The cleaning equipment removes debris, water or loose material to maintain the slit's dimensions and ensure effective subsequent operations such as pipe insertion or reinforcement.
[E21C 25/52] Machines incorporating two or more of the slitting means according to groups -- E21C 25/52 covers machines that incorporate two or more slitting mechanisms as defined in the relevant groups, used for cutting or dislodging material in earth-drilling and mining operations.
[E21C 25/54] Slitting by unguided cutter cables or cutter chains, or by unguided tools drawn along the working face by cables or the like -- E21C 25/54 relates to earth-drilling and mining operations that create slits by pulling unguided cutter cables, cutter chains or similar tools along the working face, using the cables to draw the cutters without any guiding mechanism.
[E21C 25/56] Slitting by cutter cables or cutter chains or by tools drawn along the working face by cables or the like, in each case guided parallel to the face, e.g. by a conveyor or by a guide parallel to a conveyor -- The code E21C 25/56 covers methods of slitting a rock face in fixed-construction earth-drilling machines using cutter cables, cutter chains, or similar tools that are drawn along the working face. The tools are guided parallel to the face, often by a conveyor or a parallel guide, to create precise cuts.
[E21C 25/58] Machines slitting by drilling hole on hole -- E21C 25/58 classifies machines that perform slitting by first drilling a hole in the workpiece, enabling the material to be split along a predetermined line. These devices are used in earth-drilling, mining and quarrying operations for cutting, slitting or dislodging rock or other substrates.
[E21C 25/60] Slitting by jets of water or other liquid -- The code E21C 25/60 covers techniques that use a high-pressure jet of water or another liquid to slit or cut material during earth-drilling operations for fixed constructions such as shafts, tunnels or mine openings. It falls under the broader categories of mining or quarrying and cutting, slitting, dislodging.
[E21C 25/62] Machines for making slits approximately perpendicular to the seams either level with, or above or below the level of, the machine
[E21C 25/64] Slitting machines guided solely by hand and either carried by hand or mounted on supports -- E21C 25/64 designates slitting machines that are guided only by the operator's hand and are either hand-held or mounted on supports, used in earth-drilling, mining or quarrying for cutting and dislodging material.
[E21C 25/66] Machines for making slits with additional arrangements for drilling -- The subclass E21C 25/66 includes machines used in mining and quarrying that cut or slit rock or earth while also providing a drilling capability. Such equipment combines cutting or slitting tools with a drill to create precise openings or slots as part of fixed construction earth-drilling operations.
[E21C 25/68] Machines for making slits combined with equipment for removing, e.g. by loading, material won by other means
[E21C 27/00] Machines which completely free the mineral from the seam -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Machines that can break the mineral by any means that dislodge it completely from the seam.  **Glossary:** - Planer: A fixed-blade device for continuous longwall mining of narrow seams of friable coal; the machine is operated along the coal face, planing a narrow cut from the solid coal as it travels.
[E21C 27/01] specially adapted for removing overhanging coal -- E21C 27/01 classifies mining machines specially adapted for the removal of overhanging coal, such as cutters, shears or hydraulic tools designed to free coal that projects beyond the seam face. The entry covers equipment that can reach and extract these protruding sections while maintaining seam stability.
[E21C 27/02] solely by slitting -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Machines which completely free the mineral from the seam solely by slitting; Shearer loaders. While E21C25/00 is only for slitting, with the material remaining in place and needing to be removed by other means, in E21C27/02 the material is slit and removed in one go. The machines of this groups have e.g. drums with spiral-shaped cutters for slitting and extracting the cut material. Illustrative example:
[E21C 27/04] by a single chain guided on a frame with or without auxiliary slitting means -- E21C 27/04 covers fixed mining machines that completely free the mineral from the seam by using a single chain guided on a frame, optionally fitted with auxiliary slitting means to assist in cutting or separating the material.
[E21C 27/06] with a slewing frame -- E21C 27/06 designates mining machines used in earth-drilling operations that fully liberate the mineral from the seam and incorporate a slewing frame, allowing the working tool to rotate horizontally around a vertical axis for improved positioning.
[E21C 27/08] with additional means for cutting the mineral into blocks -- E21C 27/08 pertains to mining machines that not only extract the mineral from a seam but also incorporate auxiliary cutting tools to segment the ore into blocks for easier transport and processing. The additional cutting means are integrated into the device that completely frees the mineral, enhancing handling efficiency in underground mining operations.
[E21C 27/10] by both slitting and breaking-down
[E21C 27/12] breaking-down effected by acting on the vertical face of the mineral, e.g. by percussive tools -- E21C 27/12 covers machines that break mineral material by acting on the vertical face of the seam, typically using percussive tools. It is applied to equipment that frees the rock in mining or quarrying operations through downward striking impacts on the wall of the cut.
[E21C 27/122] with breaking-down members having a striking action -- E21C 27/122 covers earth-drilling machines used in mining or quarrying that free the mineral by means of breaking-down members which strike the workpiece, such as hammer-type drill heads that deliver impact forces to break the seam.
[E21C 27/124] with rotatable cutters provided with breaking-down members -- This subclass covers earth-drilling machines that free the ore by using rotatable cutters equipped with breaking-down members. The cutters rotate while the attached members crush or break the rock, allowing complete extraction of the mineral from the seam.
[E21C 27/126] with slitting and breaking-down members situated in parallel on an intermediary level -- A mining machine for completely freeing mineral from a seam that uses parallel slitting and breaking members located on an intermediate level, allowing the rock to be split and fragmented efficiently during earth-drilling operations.
[E21C 27/128] with breaking-down members functioning on the slitting level -- These fixed-construction earth-drilling machines for mining or quarrying completely free the mineral from its seam by using breaking-down members that operate at the slitting level, cutting or splitting the rock to release the ore.
[E21C 27/14] breaking-down effected by force or pressure applied to side of slit, e.g. by wedges -- The subclass E21C 27/14 covers techniques for splitting rock or mineral seams by applying force or pressure to the side of a slit, typically using wedges, to break the material apart. It pertains to machines that fully free the mineral from the seam in mining or quarrying.
[E21C 27/16] with means for both slitting and breaking-down -- The CPC subclass E21C 27/16 covers earth-drilling machines used in mining or quarrying that completely free the mineral from the seam and are equipped with tools that both slit and break down the material. It is situated under fixed constructions, earth drilling, mining or quarrying, and machines which completely free the mineral from the seam.
[E21C 27/18] by both slitting and planing -- A machine for earth-drilling in mining or quarrying that fully releases the mineral from the seam by first slitting or cutting the material and then planing it to separate and smooth the ore. This approach combines two actions-slitting and planing-in a single piece of equipment to achieve complete mineral liberation.
[E21C 27/20] Mineral freed by means not involving slitting -- E21C 27/20 covers machines that free mineral from a seam without using a slitting action, such as those employing breaking, crushing or other mechanical means to detach the ore while remaining within the fixed-construction, earth-drilling, mining or quarrying category.
[E21C 27/22] by rotary drills with breaking-down means, e.g. wedge-shaped drills -- The E21C 27/22 subclass covers rotary drilling tools that incorporate a breaking-down element, such as a wedge-shaped drill, to fracture and free mineral material from a seam during earth-drilling operations. It applies to machines that completely release the ore by combining rotation with mechanical breaking.
[E21C 27/24] by milling means acting on the full working face -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Mineral freed by milling means acting on the full working face not involving slitting; Continuous miners. llustrative example:
[E21C 27/26] by closely adjacent cutter chains acting on the full working face -- The E21C 27/26 subclass covers machines that free mineral from a seam by using cutter chains placed closely together across the entire working face. These machines apply the chains simultaneously to the full surface, enabling continuous, uniform cutting for earth-drilling in mining or quarrying operations.
[E21C 27/28] by percussive drills with breaking-down means, e.g. wedge-shaped tools -- E21C 27/28 designates machines for earth drilling in mining that free the mineral from the seam by percussive action combined with breaking-down means such as wedge-shaped tools, extending the hierarchy of fixed constructions, earth drilling and mining or quarrying equipment.
[E21C 27/30] by jaws, buckets or scoops that scoop-out the mineral -- E21C 27/30 covers machines that completely free mineral from a seam using jaws, buckets or scoops that physically scoop out the material. These devices are fixed constructions employed in earth drilling and mining or quarrying operations to remove ore without breaking it apart.
[E21C 27/32] by adjustable or non-adjustable planing means with or without loading arrangements -- E21C 27/32 covers mining machines that release ore from a seam by planing the surface with adjustable or fixed planing tools, and may include provisions for loading the material directly onto a transport system. This subclass falls under fixed-construction earth-drilling equipment used in mining or quarrying operations.
[E21C 27/34] Machine propelled along the working face by cable or chain -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Machine propelled along the working face by cable or chain; Coal ploughs. Illustrative example:
[E21C 27/35] Ram-ploughs -- Ram-ploughs are earth-drilling devices used in mining or quarrying that advance a plough blade into a mineral seam by means of a hydraulic or pneumatic ram, shearing and extracting the material as the plough moves forward. They belong to the class of machines that completely free the mineral from the seam.
[E21C 27/36] Machine self-propelled along the working face -- A machine that moves on its own along the working face of a mine, advancing while it extracts the mineral from the seam. It is designed for earth-drilling operations in mining or quarrying where the equipment is self-propelled and operates directly at the extraction point.
[E21C 27/38] Machine stationary while planing in an arc
[E21C 27/40] Machine and its planing tool making alternative step-wise movements along the working face -- A machine used in mining or quarrying that fully separates the mineral from the seam by moving its planing tool in alternating step-wise motions along the working face, typically advancing forward, retracting and repositioning to continue cutting. This arrangement allows controlled, repetitive removal of material while maintaining the integrity of the surrounding rock.
[E21C 27/42] combined with scraper or collector box -- This subclass covers earth-drilling machines that completely free mineral from the seam and incorporate a scraper or collector box to gather the broken material as it is extracted.
[E21C 27/44] Planing knives -- Planing knives are cutting elements mounted on the cutting heads of machines that completely free mineral from a seam. They shave or plane the seam face to maintain a uniform surface during earth-drilling or mining operations, and are designed to be durable and replaceable for different rock hardness.
[E21C 27/46] by percussed planing means -- The CPC subclass E21C 27/46 covers machines used in earth-drilling for mining or quarrying that free the mineral entirely from the seam by means of percussive planing actions, such as hammering or striking tools that cut and break rock in a controlled, planing motion. This sub-category falls under fixed-construction drilling equipment designed for complete mineral extraction.
[E21C 29/00] Propulsion of machines for slitting or completely freeing the mineral from the seam -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Propulsion of machines for slitting or completely freeing the mineral from the seam; Propulsion of machines along the seam. Illustrative example:  Propulsion of machines into the seam, as in highwall mining. Illustrative example:
[E21C 29/02] by means on the machine exerting a thrust against fixed supports -- E21C 29/02 covers mining or quarrying machines that advance by pushing on stationary supports, using the machine's own thrust to slide or free mineral seams. The thrust is generated on the equipment itself rather than by external power sources, enabling propulsion against fixed structures.
[E21C 29/04] by cable or chains -- E21C 29/04 covers mining equipment that uses cable or chain drives to propel machines that slit or completely free mineral seams; it falls under fixed-construction earth-drilling systems for mining or quarrying.
[E21C 29/06] anchored at one or both ends to the mine working face -- The code E21C 29/06 covers propulsion systems for mining equipment that are fixed to the mine working face by anchoring at one or both ends. It pertains to machines used to slit or fully release mineral seams, forming part of fixed constructions in earth-drilling and mining or quarrying operations.
[E21C 29/08] Anchoring arrangements -- Anchoring arrangements refer to devices or systems that secure drilling or mining machines to the ground, seam or surrounding rock to transmit propulsion forces and maintain stability during slitting or complete mineral liberation. They include bolts, clamps, brackets and other fastening structures designed for fixed constructions in earth-drilling, mining or quarrying operations.
[E21C 29/10] Cable or chain co-operating with a winch or the like on the machine -- The E21C 29/10 subclass covers mining or quarrying equipment where a cable or chain works together with a winch or similar device to propel the machine that slits or frees mineral material from a seam. It falls under Fixed Constructions → Earth Drilling → Mining or Quarrying → Propulsion of machines for mineral release. This classification relates to the power-transmission arrangement using flexible links and winches.
[E21C 29/12] Machines propelled by thrust or pull against a part alternately anchored to and released from a cable or chain -- The classification E21C 29/12 covers machines used in earth drilling or mining that are propelled by thrust or pull against a component that is alternately secured to and freed from a cable or chain, allowing the machine to advance through a mineral seam.
[E21C 29/14] by haulage cable or chain pulling the machine along the working face -- E21C 29/14 relates to fixed-construction mining where the propulsion of drilling or extraction machines along the working face is achieved by a haulage cable or chain that pulls the machine forward, rather than by wheels, tracks or conveyors. This applies to earth-drilling, mining or quarrying operations that free the mineral from the seam.
[E21C 29/145] Means for tensioning the haulage chains or cables -- This subclass covers devices that apply or maintain tension on the haulage chains or cables used to pull material in earth-drilling and mining operations. Such mechanisms ensure proper traction and safety by adjusting the length and force of the chain or cable, often using hydraulic, pneumatic, or mechanical means. They are employed in systems that free the mineral from the seam.
[E21C 29/16] Winches or other means for pulling cable or chain -- E21C 29/16 concerns winches and similar pulling devices such as hoists or motor-driven drums used in mining or quarrying equipment to draw cables or chains that free mineral material from a seam, supporting the propulsion of machines that slit or release the ore.
[E21C 29/18] Coupling and uncoupling machine to cable or chain -- The code E21C 29/18 covers devices that couple or uncouple a drilling or extraction machine to a pulling cable or chain, enabling the machine to be propelled along a mineral seam and later released. These mechanisms are used in mining and quarrying to start or stop the movement of fixed construction equipment.
[E21C 29/185] with coupling or uncoupling of the machine at will -- The subclass deals with mining or quarrying machines whose drive can be coupled to or uncoupled from the power source at the operator's discretion, allowing the equipment to be engaged or disengaged for slitting or completely freeing mineral seams as needed.
[E21C 29/20] with safety devices operating in the event of breakage of the cable or chain -- The CPC subclass E21C 29/20 covers safety mechanisms that activate when a cable or chain breaks in machines used for slitting or completely freeing mineral from a seam during earth-drilling mining or quarrying operations. It falls under the broader groups for fixed constructions and propulsion of such machines, specifying protective devices that prevent accidents caused by cable or chain failure.
[E21C 29/22] by wheels, endless tracks or the like -- E21C 29/22 covers mining equipment that uses wheels, continuous tracks or similar locomotion devices to move machines that slit or fully extract mineral seams. It applies to fixed-construction systems for earth drilling, mining or quarrying where propulsion is provided by such mobile mechanisms.
[E21C 29/24] Trucks carrying the machine while working -- The code designates trucks that not only transport a drilling or mining machine but also serve as the working platform for the machine during operation, enabling the equipment to move and function while mounted on the vehicle in fixed-construction, earth-drilling, and mining or quarrying applications.
[E21C 29/26] with means for adjustably positioning the machine on the truck -- E21C 29/26 covers mining or quarrying machines that are propelled to slit or fully free mineral seams and are mounted on a truck, featuring mechanisms that allow the machine's position on the vehicle to be adjusted for optimal operation. This subclass pertains to fixed-construction earth-drilling equipment with adjustable placement on the transport platform.
[E21C 29/28] adjusting the height of the whole machine -- The class E21C 29/28 covers mechanisms that raise or lower an entire earth-drilling or mining machine to set its operating height. It relates to fixed-construction equipment used for propulsion and slitting of mineral seams, enabling adjustment of the machine's vertical position for optimal cutting or extraction.
[E21C 31/00] Driving means incorporated in machines for slitting or completely freeing the mineral from the seam -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Drive units for energising any of the parts forming the machine. Illustrative example:
[E21C 31/02] for cutting or breaking-down devices -- E21C 31/02 covers cutting or breaking-down devices that are integral to the driving means of fixed-construction earth-drilling machines used in mining or quarrying to slit or completely free the mineral from the seam.
[E21C 31/025] by another slitting or extracting member -- This subclass covers fixed-construction earth-drilling, mining or quarrying machines whose built-in driving means employ a separate slitting or extracting member to free the mineral seam. It specifies the use of an additional cutting or extraction element integrated in the equipment.
[E21C 31/04] imparting both a rotary and reciprocating motion -- Entry E21C 31/04 covers mining and quarrying machines in which the cutting or freeing tool is driven by a mechanism that provides both rotary and reciprocating motion. The combined motion is integrated into the driving means of the cutter head, enhancing the ability to slit or fully release mineral from a seam.
[E21C 31/06] actuated by an endless cable or chain -- The CPC subclass E21C 31/06 covers earth-drilling equipment in which the mechanism that drives the cutting or freeing tool is powered by an endless cable or chain, rather than by direct hydraulic or motor drive. This applies to machines used in mining or quarrying where the driving means is incorporated to slit or release the mineral seam.
[E21C 31/08] for adjusting parts of the machines -- E21C 31/08 relates to adjustments of components in fixed-construction earth-drilling machines used for mining or quarrying, specifically mechanisms that modify or set driving means incorporated in the equipment for slitting or releasing the mineral seam. It covers devices such as selectors, speed-change gearings, and other adjustment assemblies that fine-tune the operation of the drilling system.
[E21C 31/10] for slewing parts of the machines -- E21C31/10 covers the slewing components of fixed drilling machines used in mining or quarrying, where the driving mechanism is built into the equipment to slit or fully release the mineral seam. It pertains to the rotating or pivoting parts that allow the drill or cutter to be oriented during operation.
[E21C 31/12] Component parts -- E21C 31/12 covers component parts of driving means that are built into machines used for slitting or fully freeing mineral seams during earth-drilling, mining or quarrying operations. It pertains to the mechanical elements integrated in fixed constructions that provide the power or motion needed to separate the ore from the surrounding rock.
[E21C 33/00] Trucks or other devices for transporting machines for slitting or completely freeing the mineral from the seam -- E21C 33/00 refers to trucks and comparable transport devices that convey machines employed to slit or completely free mineral from a seam within fixed-construction earth-drilling, mining or quarrying operations.
[E21C 33/02] with equipment for loading or unloading the machine on to or from the truck -- This subclass covers systems that load or unload mining or earth-drilling machines onto or from transport trucks, including hydraulic lifts, conveyors, ramps or other handling mechanisms integrated with the vehicle. It pertains to equipment that facilitates moving the machine between the work site and the transport vehicle.
[E21C 33/025] the unloaded machine remaining connected to the transport vehicle -- A mining or earth-drilling machine that has been emptied of material but stays physically linked to the transport vehicle for movement, positioning or power transfer is covered by this subclass, which falls under fixed constructions for trucks or other devices used to transport equipment that frees mineral from the seam.
[E21C 35/00] Details of, or accessories for, machines for slitting or completely freeing the mineral from the seam, not provided for in groups -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Specific details of mining machines not disclosed in E21C25/00 - E21C33/00 .
[E21C 35/02] Locking equipment for slewable parts -- E21C 35/02 designates locking devices for slewable components of mining or quarrying machines that cut or free mineral seams, covering accessories that secure rotating or swingable parts not otherwise classified. It falls under Fixed Constructions, Earth Drilling, Mining or Quarrying, and details of machines for slitting or completely freeing the mineral from the seam.
[E21C 35/04] Safety devices -- E21C 35/04 covers safety devices used in fixed constructions for earth-drilling, mining or quarrying operations, specifically accessories for machines that slit or completely free mineral from a seam and are not classified elsewhere. It pertains to equipment that protects workers or prevents accidents while the mineral is being liberated from the rock face.
[E21C 35/043] Protection against overload during transfer of machines for slitting or completely freeing the mineral from the seam -- Code E21C 35/043 covers protective devices that prevent overload when moving machines used to slit or fully liberate mineral seams in mining or quarrying operations. The classification includes any accessories or mechanisms designed to safeguard the equipment during transfer.
[E21C 35/046] Protection of cables or pipes for supplying power -- E21C 35/046 concerns protective measures for electrical cables or power-supplying pipes installed on machines that slit or free mineral seams in mining or quarrying. It includes accessories that shield these conduits from damage during earth-drilling and extraction operations.
[E21C 35/06] Equipment for positioning the whole machine in relation to its sub-structure -- E21C 35/06 relates to devices that adjust or align the whole mining or drilling machine with its underlying support structure, allowing precise positioning of equipment used for cutting or liberating mineral seams. These mechanisms are accessories not otherwise classified in existing groups.
[E21C 35/08] Guiding the machine -- This subclass covers devices that steer, align or otherwise guide a machine used for slitting or completely freeing mineral seams in mining or quarrying. It includes guides, rails, hydraulic or mechanical steering mechanisms and related accessories that control the travel path of the cutter or extraction unit where such features are not covered elsewhere.
[E21C 35/10] by feelers contacting the working face -- E21C 35/10 covers equipment for freeing mineral from a seam in earth-drilling or mining operations that uses feeler probes to sense and follow the working face, guiding cutting or slitting tools without relying on visual or mechanical references. The feelers detect the surface of the seam and transmit that information to control the machine's extraction action.
[E21C 35/12] along a conveyor for the cut material -- Equipment or accessories mounted on a conveyor to receive, transport, or otherwise handle mineral that has been cut or separated from the seam. It covers devices that move the cut material along the belt line in mining or quarrying operations.
[E21C 35/125] Means for inclining the conveyor -- This subclass covers devices that tilt or angle a conveyor system to facilitate the movement of extracted material, including adjustable ramps, bevelled rollers or articulating frames that change the conveyor's slope for improved feed control in mining or quarrying operations.
[E21C 35/14] Equipment for pressing the conveyor towards the working face
[E21C 35/16] by making use of the timbering, filling, or other supports -- E21C 35/16 concerns accessories or detail features for machines that slit or completely free mineral seams, specifically those that employ timbering, filling or other support structures to aid the extraction process and are not covered by other groups within the mining-or-quarrying subclass.
[E21C 35/18] Mining picks -- E21C 35/18 relates to mining picks, which are handheld or mechanically driven tools used to break and extract rock or ore from seams in underground or surface mining.
