The code covers various pick-head designs, shafts and mounting arrangements that are not classified elsewhere in the CPC.
[E21C 35/183] with inserts or layers of wear-resisting material -- The subclass covers fixed mining or quarrying structures whose slitting or seam-freeing machines are equipped with wear-resisting inserts or layered coverings. It pertains to details or accessories for such equipment that are not covered by other groups, focusing on durable material applications that extend service life.
[E21C 35/1831] Fixing methods or devices -- E21C 35/1831 concerns methods and devices for fixing equipment used in earth-drilling operations within mining or quarrying, specifically for machines that slit or completely free mineral seams and are not covered by other groups. It addresses accessories that secure or stabilize such machinery during the extraction process.
[E21C 35/1833] Multiple inserts -- E21C 35/1833 covers the use of multiple inserts as part of machines that slit or completely free mineral from a seam, where the inserts are not otherwise classified. It relates to fixed construction equipment used in earth-drilling, mining or quarrying operations for separating ore from the host rock.
[E21C 35/1835] Chemical composition or specific material -- E21C 35/1835 covers the chemical composition or specific material of accessories used in fixed-construction earth-drilling machines that slit or completely free mineral seams in mining or quarrying operations, where such details are not covered by other subclasses. It relates to the material make-up of components that aid the extraction process.
[E21C 35/1837] characterised by the shape -- E21C 35/1837 covers mining or quarrying equipment where the distinguishing feature is the shape of components used in machines that slit or completely free mineral seams, addressing details not covered in other groups. It pertains to accessories or construction details whose geometry is essential to the function of the slit-freeing device.
[E21C 35/184] Reversible picks, i.e. having two working ends, one operating, the other in reserve -- E21C 35/184 concerns reversible picks for earth-drilling or mining machines, featuring two working ends so that one can operate while the other remains in reserve, permitting continuous use without removing the tool.
[E21C 35/186] using a locking piston actuated hydraulically, e.g. by spraying-fluid pressure -- E21C 35/186 relates to machines for slitting or completely freeing mineral seams in earth-drilling and mining where a locking piston is driven hydraulically, for example by a spray of fluid pressure, to perform the release operation.
[E21C 35/187] with arrangement of fluid-spraying nozzles -- This CPC subclass covers accessories or details of mining machines that free mineral from seams by slitting, specifically those that incorporate a system of fluid-spraying nozzles to assist cutting or cleaning the seam. It is placed under fixed constructions, earth drilling, mining or quarrying in the CPC hierarchy.
[E21C 35/188] characterised by adaptations to use an extraction tool
[E21C 35/19] Means for fixing picks or holders -- E21C 35/19 concerns devices that attach or secure picks, bits or their holders to drilling or mining machines in fixed constructions. It includes clamps, brackets, fastening arrangements and related mechanisms designed to keep cutting tools firmly in place during earth-drilling, mining or quarrying operations, while excluding broader machine structures.
[E21C 35/191] for fixing holders -- Entry E21C 35/191 covers devices that secure or attach holders to machines used for slitting or fully liberating mineral seams during earth-drilling, mining or quarrying operations, where such accessories are not covered by other specific groups. It relates to fixed constructions that provide stable mounting of these holders.

[E21C 35/1933] the picks having a cylindrical shank -- E21C 35/1933 refers to picks with a cylindrical shank used as accessories for fixed earth-drilling or mining machines that slit or free mineral seams, covering designs not classified elsewhere in the mining or quarrying groups. This classification falls under Fixed Constructions → Earth Drilling → Mining or Quarrying, specifically detailing components that attach to machines for mineral extraction.
[E21C 35/1936] the picks having a square- or rectangular-section shank -- This CPC subclass E21C 35/1936 covers picks with a square or rectangular shank used in fixed constructions for earth drilling in mining or quarrying, specifically accessories for machines that free mineral from the seam not covered elsewhere.
[E21C 35/197] using sleeves, rings or the like, as main fixing elements -- E21C 35/197 addresses machines for slitting or completely freeing mineral seams in mining or quarrying where the primary fixing elements are sleeves, rings or similar components. It covers the design and use of these elements to secure tools or parts of the extraction equipment, a sub-category not covered by other groups.
[E21C 35/20] General features of equipment for removal of chippings, e.g. for loading on conveyor -- This subclass covers general aspects of devices that collect and convey cut rock or ore chips from drilling or cutting equipment, such as conveyors, chutes, or collection bins, used to transport the material away from the work face. It includes mechanisms for gathering, guiding and loading chippings onto transport systems, but not detailed specific machine types.
[E21C 35/22] Equipment for preventing the formation of, or for removal of, dust -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Picks with arrangement of fluid-spraying nozzles -> E21C35/187
[E21C 35/223] Equipment associated with mining machines for sucking dust-laden air from the cutting area, with or without cleaning of the air -- This sub-group concerns devices that extract dust-laden air from the cutting area of mining or earth-drilling machines, including suction units and optional air-cleaning systems, and is intended for accessories that free the mineral seam in fixed-construction mining operations.
[E21C 35/226] Control valves for the spraying liquid used in dust suppression systems -- E21C 35/226 refers to control valves that regulate the liquid spray in dust-suppression systems used in mining or quarrying. It is placed under fixed constructions for earth drilling, specifically for accessories of machines that free the mineral from the seam and are not covered by other groups.
[E21C 35/23] Distribution of spraying-fluids in rotating cutter-heads -- E21C 35/23 covers arrangements for delivering spraying fluids such as water, air or foam into the cutting zone of rotating cutter heads on drilling machines used in mining or quarrying. The fluid distribution is intended to cool the cutter, reduce friction, and suppress dust while the mineral seam is being cut or freed. It relates to accessories that are not covered by other CPC groups.
[E21C 35/24] Remote control specially adapted for machines for slitting or completely freeing the mineral -- A remote-control device specially adapted for use with machines that slit or completely free mineral seams in mining operations. It falls under the Fixed Constructions category of Earth Drilling, covering accessories that are not listed elsewhere for such mineral-extraction equipment.
[E21C 35/282] Autonomous machines; Autonomous operations -- The code E21C 35/282 covers autonomous or self-controlled equipment used in earth-drilling for mining or quarrying, including machines that operate without direct human control and related accessories that enable fully automatic seam access and mineral extraction. It addresses technologies such as driverless drill rigs, automated positioning systems, and sensors that allow the machine to perform drilling, cutting or freeing of ore without manual intervention.
[E21C 35/302] Measuring, signaling or indicating specially adapted for machines for slitting or completely freeing the mineral  measuring or indicating on tunneling machines  E21D9/003 -- This subclass covers devices that measure, signal or display information specifically designed for machines that slit or fully release mineral seams, such as tunneling equipment. It includes sensors, gauges, indicators and related accessories that monitor parameters like position, pressure or progress during mining or quarrying operations. The classification is part of the Fixed Constructions - Earth Drilling - Mining or Quarrying hierarchy.
[E21C 37/00] Other methods or devices for dislodging with or without loading -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Techniques for dislodging minerals for mining applications.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Breaking-down by means inserted in slits -> E21C27/14
[E21C 37/005] by projectiles -- The E21C 37/005 subclass covers earth-drilling or mining techniques that break rock or soil by means of projectiles, such as dropped or launched rods, balls, or explosive charges, either alone or together with loading devices.
[E21C 37/02] by wedges -- E21C 37/02 refers to wedge-type tools or mechanisms used to dislodge rock or earth in mining, quarrying, or earth-drilling operations of fixed constructions. The classification includes devices that apply a wedge action, with or without accompanying loading equipment, to break or separate material.
[E21C 37/04] by devices with parts pressed mechanically against the wall of a borehole -- E21C 37/04 concerns earth-drilling techniques used in fixed-construction mining or quarrying where the dislodging action is achieved by devices that press parts mechanically against the wall of a borehole to break or move material.
[E21C 37/06] by making use of hydraulic or pneumatic pressure in a borehole -- E21C 37/06 pertains to fixed earth-drilling or mining installations that use hydraulic or pneumatic pressure applied inside a borehole to loosen or dislodge material, with or without subsequent loading, as an alternative to conventional mechanical or explosive methods.
[E21C 37/08] Devices with pistons, plungers, or the like, pressed locally against the wall of the borehole -- This entry covers equipment used in underground drilling or mining that employ pistons or plungers to apply pressure directly against the borehole wall to dislodge material. Such devices are typically integrated into fixed construction rigs and may operate with or without additional loading. They fall under the broader category of other methods or devices for dislodging in earth drilling.
[E21C 37/10] Devices with expanding elastic casings -- This subclass covers tools used in earth-drilling and mining that employ elastic casings which expand to support or line a borehole. The devices may be inserted in a drilled hole and then inflated or otherwise enlarged to press against the surrounding rock, providing temporary or permanent reinforcement during extraction or loading operations.
[E21C 37/12] by injecting into the borehole a liquid, either initially at high pressure or subsequently subjected to high pressure, e.g. by pulses, by explosive cartridges acting on the liquid -- E21C 37/12 covers methods of dislodging earth in drilling operations by injecting a liquid into the borehole, either at high-pressure initially or through subsequent high-pressure pulses, including the use of explosive cartridges to drive the liquid.
[E21C 37/14] by compressed air -- E21C 37/14 covers Earth-drilling methods used in mining or quarrying that employ compressed air to help dislodge material, either alone or in combination with other loading techniques. It falls under fixed-construction drilling techniques for removing rock or soil.
[E21C 37/16] by fire-setting or by similar methods based on a heat effect -- E21C 37/16 covers techniques for dislodging rock in earth-drilling or mining operations that rely on fire-setting or other heat-based methods, wherein high temperature is applied to fracture or crack the material before removal. These methods may be used with or without subsequent loading.
[E21C 37/18] by electricity -- E21C 37/18 covers earth-drilling or quarrying machines that employ electrical energy to loosen or remove rock or soil, rather than hydraulic, pneumatic or manual means. It includes electrically powered drills, breakers and similar devices used in fixed-construction settings for mining or quarrying operations.
[E21C 37/20] by ultrasonic waves -- E21C 37/20 covers earth-drilling or mining devices that employ ultrasonic waves to loosen or dislodge material, either with or without loading. This subclass falls under the broader categories of fixed constructions, earth drilling, mining or quarrying, and other methods or devices for dislodging.
[E21C 37/22] Hand tools or hand-held power-operated tools specially adapted for dislodging minerals -- The CPC entry E21C 37/22 covers hand tools and hand-held power-operated instruments that are specifically designed to loosen or break mineral deposits in mining or quarrying operations. It includes any portable device, whether purely manual or powered, used for dislodging minerals without necessarily loading them.
[E21C 37/24] Pick hammers -- E21C 37/24 covers pick hammers used in mining or quarrying to dislodge material. These are manual or powered tools that apply concentrated impact to break rock or ore, often attached to a handle or frame for operator use. The classification includes designs for both loaded and unloaded operation in fixed construction settings.
[E21C 37/26] Chisels or other cutting tools not mentioned before -- E21C 37/26 covers chisels and other cutting tools that are not otherwise specified for use in fixed construction methods of earth drilling, mining or quarrying, particularly when they are employed to dislodge material with or without loading. It includes specialized implements such as custom-shaped or sized chisels designed for particular rock or soil conditions that fall outside the broader tool categories.
[E21C 39/00] Devices for testing -- E21C 39/00 covers devices installed on fixed drilling installations used to test the performance of the drill system, the borehole or the extracted material. These may include pressure- and flow-meters, torque monitors, vibration sensors, cut-tings samplers and other instruments that assess drilling parameters, hole stability or rock quality during mining or quarrying operations.
[E21C 41/00] Methods of mining or quarrying -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods disclosing sequences of steps or combinations of operations of mining.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Machines for underground mining -> E21C25/00 - E21C39/00 - Methods of hydraulic mining, hydraulic monitors -> E21C45/00 - Machines for surface mining -> E21C47/00 - Mining methods for obtaining peat -> E21C49/00 - Methods of underwater mining -> E21C50/00 - Obtaining minerals through a borehole drilled from the surface -> E21B43/28 - E21B43/29
[E21C 41/16] Methods of underground mining -- The CPC class E21C 41/16 covers methods of underground mining. It includes techniques such as shaft sinking, drift and stope development, sublevel stoping, block caving and related support, ventilation and ore-transport systems used to extract minerals below the surface.
[E21C 41/18] for brown or hard coal -- This CPC subclass E21C 41/18 concerns methods for mining brown or hard coal, falling within the broader category of fixed-construction earth-drilling techniques used in mining or quarrying operations.
[E21C 41/20] for rock salt or potash salt -- E21C 41/20 covers methods and equipment for extracting rock salt or potash salt, specifically addressing fixed-construction techniques used in earth-drilling operations for mining or quarrying these minerals.
[E21C 41/22] for ores, e.g. mining placers -- The E21C 41/22 subclass covers methods for extracting ore deposits that are not embedded in solid rock, such as placer mining of loose sediments, gravels, or sands where valuable minerals are concentrated. It includes techniques for locating, excavating, separating, and recovering these ores, often using water-based or gravity-based processes.
[E21C 41/24] for oil-bearing deposits -- E21C 41/24 covers methods for extracting oil-bearing deposits from the earth, including techniques that combine drilling and mining operations to recover petroleum or bitumen from solid formations. It addresses procedures for accessing, loosening and removing the oil-rich material in situ.
[E21C 41/26] Methods of surface mining -- E21C 41/26 covers methods of surface mining, describing techniques for extracting mineral deposits from the ground's surface such as open-pit, strip and quarrying operations. It falls within the hierarchy of fixed constructions, earth drilling and mining or quarrying, and addresses the overall procedures and equipment used to remove material without underground tunnelling.
[E21C 41/28] for brown or hard coal -- This subclass covers techniques and equipment used to extract brown or hard coal from underground or surface deposits, focusing on fixed construction methods such as drilling, shaft sinking, and coal-face development. It pertains to earth-drilling and mining operations under the broader categories of fixed constructions and methods of mining or quarrying.
[E21C 41/30] for ores, e.g. mining placers
[E21C 41/31] for oil-bearing deposits -- E21C 41/31 relates to methods of mining or quarrying that employ fixed-construction earth-drilling techniques specifically designed to access oil-bearing deposits. It covers the procedural aspects of locating, penetrating, and extracting hydrocarbons from geological formations within a mining context.
[E21C 41/32] Reclamation of surface-mined areas -- The code E21C 41/32 covers methods for reclaiming land after surface mining, encompassing techniques to reshape terrain, spread and compact overburden, restore topsoil, establish vegetation, and stabilize soils to mitigate erosion and enable productive post-mining use. It falls under Fixed Constructions → Earth Drilling → Mining or Quarrying → Methods of mining or quarrying.
[E21C 45/00] Methods of hydraulic mining -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods of hydraulic mining; Hydraulic monitors; Excavating alluvial or other mineral deposits by means of high-pressure water jets. Illustrative example:  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Slitting by jets of water -> E21C25/60
[E21C 45/02] Means for generating pulsating fluid jets -- The subclass E21C 45/02 covers apparatus that generate pulsating fluid jets for hydraulic mining operations. Such means are used in fixed drilling constructions to break rock or sediment by delivering rapid, intermittent streams of water or slurry, improving cutting efficiency and reducing energy consumption.
[E21C 45/04] by use of highly pressurised liquid -- This subclass covers hydraulic mining techniques that employ highly pressurised liquid to dislodge or transport earth material, typically using high-pressure water jets or pumps to break rock and convey slurry for extraction.
[E21C 45/06] by use of compressed gases -- The subclass E21C 45/06 covers techniques for hydraulic mining in fixed earth-drilling installations that employ compressed gases to dislodge or transport material. It applies to mining and quarrying operations where gas pressure is used instead of water or mechanical means to break rock or sediment within a defined structure. This method is a specialized variant of hydraulic excavation.
[E21C 45/08] Automatic or remote control of hydraulic monitors -- E21C 45/08 concerns the automatic or remote operation of hydraulic monitors employed in hydraulic mining, including control systems that adjust water pressure, flow direction or nozzle positioning without direct human handling, thereby improving safety and efficiency in fixed-construction mining operations.
[E21C 47/00] Machines for obtaining or the removal of materials in open-pit mines -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Machines for obtaining or the removal of materials in open-pit mines. These are fixed-construction conveyance systems that receive material directly from the excavator or loader and transport it across the pit or to processing facilities. The classification is applied to designs where the bridge and the win-i…
[E21C 47/06] Cableway conveyors used in co-operation with the winning apparatus -- E21C 47/06 concerns cable-driven conveyor systems that work in conjunction with the winning apparatus in open-pit mining operations. These conveyors transport extracted rock or ore along cableways from the point of removal to a processing or disposal area, forming part of the fixed construction equipment used for material extraction.
[E21C 47/08] Devices for cutting-out partings, e.g. layers of sand between seams of coal -- E21C 47/08 classifies devices that cut out partings such as sand layers separating coal seams, typically mounted on machines used in open-pit mining. These tools belong to the broader groups for fixed constructions, earth drilling, and mining or quarrying equipment.
[E21C 47/10] for quarrying stone, sand, gravel, or clay -- E21C 47/10 classifies machines that are fixed constructions for quarrying stone, sand, gravel or clay in open-pit mining operations, describing equipment designed to extract these materials from the earth.
[E21C 49/00] Obtaining peat -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods and machinery for draining and preparing the peat deposit before exploitation, as well as for the extraction of peat.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Treating peat -> C10F
[E21C 49/02] by excavating -- E21C 49/02 pertains to the extraction of peat through excavation methods, covering techniques and equipment that dig out peat layers for uses such as fuel, horticulture or land reclamation. It falls under the fixed-construction category of earth-drilling activities related to mining or quarrying, specifically addressing the removal of peat by digging rather than other extraction processes.
[E21C 49/04] by digging in the form of peat sods -- E21C 49/04 covers the method of obtaining peat by excavating it in the form of sods, i.e., cutting and removing surface layers of peat as solid blocks. This technique falls under fixed-construction earth-drilling activities within mining and quarrying operations.
[E21C 50/00] Obtaining minerals from underwater, not otherwise provided for -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods and machinery for obtaining minerals from sea bodies, such as ocean floors, or lake or river beds.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Suction dredgers or component parts thereof -> E02F3/88 - Equipment for conveying or separating excavated material -> E02F7/00 - Dippers -> G01N1/12
[E21C 50/02] dependent on the ship movements -- E21C 50/02 designates underwater mining techniques whose operation depends on the movement of the host vessel; the ship's forward motion, vibration, or water flow generated by propulsion is used to drive or assist drilling, suction, or collection of mineral deposits from the seabed. This subclass falls under fixed constructions for earth drilling and covers methods not otherwise specified.
[E21C 51/00] Apparatus for, or methods of, winning materials from extraterrestrial sources -- The CPC subclass E21C 51/00 covers equipment and techniques for extracting resources from bodies beyond Earth, such as asteroids, moons or planets. It includes drilling rigs, collection devices and processing methods designed for off-world mining operations, fitting within the broader categories of fixed constructions and earth-drilling mining technologies.
[E21D] SHAFTS -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods or apparatus for making or lining tunnels, galleries or large underground chambers, using underground mining methods only, i.e. not involving disturbance of the ground surface.  Techniques and tools for stabilising such underground constructions against the pressure of the surrounding soil or rock.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Soil conditioning or soil stabilising materials -> C09K17/00 - Underground spaces made by foundation engineering, i.e. involving disturbance of the ground surface. -> E02D - Deep drilling -> E21B - Drilling or cutting machines for mining or quarrying -> E21C - Safety devices, transport, rescue, ventilation or drainage -> E21F
[E21D 1/00] Shafts -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Vertical excavation of shafts from the surface and removal of cuttings:
[E21D 1/02] by hand -- The CPC subclass E21D 1/02 covers earth-drilling shafts that are erected or advanced manually, that is, using hand-operated tools rather than powered equipment. It applies to fixed-construction drill shafts where the excavation or placement is performed by human effort without mechanised assistance. This subclass falls under the broader categories of fixed constructions, earth drilling, and shafts.
[E21D 1/03] mechanically, -- E21D 1/03 covers shafts in fixed earth-drilling constructions that are driven mechanically, i.e., by gears, belts or other direct mechanical power transmission rather than hydraulic or pneumatic means. The classification applies to the design, arrangement and support of such mechanically powered shafts that transmit torque to the drill string.
[E21D 1/04] with grabs -- This subgroup covers shaft-driven fixed drilling installations that incorporate a grab mechanism for retrieving cuttings or equipment. It applies to earth-drilling shafts where a grab is integrated into the drilling head or guide system. Used for rock or soil extraction in mining, construction, or oil-field contexts.
[E21D 1/06] with shaft-boring cutters -- The CPC subclass E21D 1/06 covers earth-drilling machines in which the drill shaft itself is equipped with cutters that bore the shaft, allowing the shaft to be formed or enlarged during drilling. It applies to fixed-construction drilling rigs that integrate shaft-boring cutters.
[E21D 1/08] while moving the lining downwards -- E21D 1/08 covers methods and apparatus for advancing a shaft lining while it is being lowered during construction, including techniques for supporting, guiding, or installing the lining as it moves downwards in an earth-drilling shaft.
[E21D 1/10] Preparation of the ground -- E21D 1/10 covers techniques and equipment used to ready the terrain before constructing a shaft, including excavation, soil stabilization, dewatering, blasting, and removal of obstacles. The subclass addresses preparatory steps that ensure safe and stable shaft installation in various ground conditions.
[E21D 11/00] Lining tunnels, galleries or other underground cavities, e.g. large underground chambers -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Materials, methods and machines for lining the surface of an excavated tunnel:  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Linings specially for shafts -> E21D5/00 - Setting anchoring bolts -> E21D20/00 - Anchoring bolts -> E21D21/00
[E21D 11/003] Linings or provisions thereon, specially adapted for traffic tunnels, e.g. with built-in cleaning devices  E21D11/38  takes precedence -- This CPC subclass covers linings and associated features specially designed for traffic tunnels, such as integrated cleaning devices or other provisions that facilitate tunnel operation. It falls under the broader groups for fixed constructions, earth drilling, shafts, and lining of underground cavities.
[E21D 11/006] Lining anchored in the rock  setting anchoring-bolts  E21D20/00 ; anchoring-bolts  E21D21/00 -- The entry E21D 11/006 covers methods for installing linings in underground shafts, tunnels or chambers where the lining is secured directly to the surrounding rock by means of anchoring bolts, including the placement of the bolts and the subsequent attachment of the lining elements.
[E21D 11/02] Lining predominantly with wood -- This CPC subclass E21D 11/02 relates to the construction of underground shafts, tunnels, galleries or large chambers where the primary lining material is wood, covering designs, methods and installations that use timber as the main structural element for supporting and protecting such underground openings.
[E21D 11/03] using timber- setting machines -- E21D 11/03 relates to the installation of timber linings in shafts, tunnels, galleries or other underground cavities, specifically describing machines that drive, position and set timber elements for structural support during construction.
[E21D 11/04] Lining with building materials -- E21D 11/04 refers to the lining of shafts, tunnels, galleries or other underground cavities with building materials such as concrete, brick or stone. The entry covers techniques for constructing and reinforcing the interior surfaces of these voids to provide stability and protection. It is placed under the hierarchy of fixed constructions, earth drilling, shafts.
[E21D 1/105] by water level modification -- The subclass E21D 1/105 covers shafts for earth-drilling in which the water level in the borehole is deliberately altered to facilitate construction. It includes methods such as dewatering, water-table control, and temporary flooding to stabilize the shaft walls or assist extraction. This approach is applied to fixed construction projects where water-level modification is a key technical feature.
[E21D 11/05] using compressible insertions -- The E21D 11/05 subclass covers methods and devices for lining shafts, tunnels, galleries or other underground cavities by employing compressible insertions, such as inflatable or expandable elements that expand to fit the cavity and provide support or sealing. It includes the use of temporary or permanent compressible liners placed during construction or repair to improve stability, prevent water ingress or accommodate irregular shapes.
[E21D 11/06] with bricks -- E21D 11/06 classifies the use of bricks to line shafts, tunnels, galleries or other underground cavities, including large underground chambers. It addresses brick masonry techniques for constructing or reinforcing the interior surfaces of these subterranean structures.
[E21D 11/07] using brick- laying machines -- The code E21D 11/07 covers methods and apparatus for constructing tunnel or shaft linings where brick-laying machines are employed to place masonry units continuously, improving speed and uniformity of the lining. It falls under fixed constructions, earth drilling, shafts, lining tunnels, galleries or other underground cavities.
[E21D 11/08] with preformed concrete slabs -- E21D 11/08 covers earth-drilling shaft construction where the lining of tunnels, galleries or other underground cavities is formed with pre-cast concrete slabs, detailing methods and arrangements for installing these slab sections to create a stable underground structure.
[E21D 11/083] Methods or devices for joining adjacent concrete segments -- The subclass E21D 11/083 covers techniques and equipment for connecting adjacent concrete lining segments in shafts, tunnels, galleries or other underground cavities. It includes methods such as grouting, bolting, welding, mechanical clamps and related devices used to align, seal and secure concrete pieces during construction.
[E21D 11/086] Methods of making concrete lining segments -- E21D 11/086 concerns the manufacturing techniques for concrete segments that form the lining of shafts, tunnels, galleries or other underground cavities. It includes processes such as casting, curing, prefabrication and any treatment steps used to produce these fixed-construction concrete elements.
[E21D 11/10] with concrete cast -- E21D 11/10 covers the use of concrete that is cast in place to line shafts, tunnels, galleries or other underground cavities, forming a permanent structural lining for such excavations. It is a sub-category of fixed constructions for earth-drilling shafts, focusing on cast-in-place concrete applications.
[E21D 11/102] Removable shuttering; Bearing or supporting devices therefor -- The subclass E21D 11/102 covers removable shuttering and the bearing or supporting devices used to hold it in place when constructing shafts, tunnels, galleries or large underground chambers. It includes temporary formwork panels, frames, brackets and other equipment that can be taken away after the concrete or lining has set.
[E21D 11/105] Transport or application of concrete specially adapted for the lining of tunnels or galleries  in building  E04F21/08 ,  E04G21/02 ; Backfilling the space between main building element and the surrounding rock, e.g. with concrete -- E21D 11/105 covers methods for transporting and applying specially formulated concrete to line tunnels, galleries or other underground cavities, including backfilling the gap between the primary structure and surrounding rock. The code relates to fixed-construction earth-drilling shaft work and references related building classifications such as E04F21/08 and E04G21/02.
[E21D 11/107] Reinforcing elements therefor; Holders for the reinforcing elements -- E21D 11/107 pertains to reinforcing components and the devices that hold them in place for lining shafts created by earth-drilling, such as tunnels, galleries or large underground chambers. It includes elements like ribs, bolts, plates and the brackets, clamps or other holders used to secure these reinforcements.
[E21D 11/12] Temporary supports for use during building -- E21D 11/12 relates to temporary support devices employed while constructing shafts, tunnels or other underground cavities, providing stabilization of the lining until permanent systems are installed. The class includes props, frames, mats or other removable structures used during earth-drilling and fixed-construction projects.
[E21D 11/14] Lining predominantly with metal -- E21D 11/14 covers methods and structures for lining shafts, tunnels or underground chambers where metal forms the main lining material. It includes designs, installation techniques and supporting elements for metal liners used in earth-drilling constructions.
[E21D 11/15] Plate linings -- Plate linings are fixed constructions used to line shafts, tunnels, galleries or other underground cavities, providing structural support and protection against collapse or water ingress. The subclass covers the design, installation and material specifics of plate-type linings, such as steel, concrete or composite panels, employed in earth-drilling and underground shaft projects. It includes methods of securing the plates to the surrounding ground and to each other to form a continuous, load-bearing tunnel lining.
[E21D 11/152] Laggings made of grids or nettings -- The code E21D 11/152 covers laggings that consist of metal or polymer grids or nettings used to line and support the walls of shafts, tunnels, galleries or other underground cavities during earth-drilling operations. These grid or net structures provide temporary reinforcement to prevent collapse and to maintain the shape of the excavation.
[E21D 11/155] Laggings made of strips, slats, slabs or sheet piles  longitudinal struts for mine arches  E21D11/28 -- Laggings constructed from strips, slats, slabs or sheet piles that serve as longitudinal struts to support mine arches, used in the lining of shafts, tunnels, galleries and other underground cavities. This falls under Fixed Constructions → Earth Drilling → Shafts → Lining tunnels, galleries or other underground cavities.
[E21D 11/157] Laggings making use of fluid cushions, e.g. the fluid containing a hardenable material  chocks made of flexible containers  E21D15/483
[E21D 11/18] Arch members -- E21D 11/18 relates to arch‐shaped structural members employed in the lining of shafts, tunnels, galleries or other underground cavities created by earth drilling. These components provide load-bearing support and aid in maintaining the stability of the excavation, and are commonly made from steel, concrete or composite materials.
[E21D 11/183] Supporting means for arch members, not provided for in  E21D11/22 -- The code E21D 11/183 covers devices or arrangements that provide support for arch members within the lining of shafts or tunnels, where such support is not described in the preceding sub-group E21D 11/22. It includes supplemental brackets, temporary jigs, or other means that stabilize arches during construction or operation of underground cavities.
[E21D 11/186] Pre-stressing or dismantling devices therefor -- E21D 11/186 relates to devices for applying pre-stress to shaft-lining structures or for assisting their removal during dismantling, used in fixed earth-drilling constructions such as shafts, tunnels and underground chambers.
[E21D 1/12] by freezing -- E21D 1/12 covers the construction of vertical or inclined shafts in which the surrounding ground is temporarily frozen to increase stability and control water ingress during excavation. The freezing may be achieved by circulating a coolant through boreholes or injecting a frozen slurry, allowing safe removal of material in challenging soil or permafrost conditions.
[E21D 11/20] Special cross- sections, e.g. corrugated -- E21D 11/20 relates to shaft linings that use special cross-sectional shapes, such as corrugated or otherwise profiled sections, for reinforcing tunnels, galleries or other underground chambers. It covers designs where the lining's geometry deviates from simple circular or rectangular profiles to improve strength, flexibility or installation efficiency. This subclass falls under fixed constructions for earth-drilling shafts.
[E21D 11/22] Clamps or other yieldable means for interconnecting adjacent arch members either rigidly, or allowing arch member parts to slide when subjected to excessive pressure -- The entry covers devices such as clamps or other yieldable fittings that join adjacent arch members in tunnel or shaft linings, providing a rigid connection or permitting the arches to slide when subjected to excessive pressure. These elements help maintain structural stability while allowing controlled movement under high loads.
[E21D 11/24] Knuckle joints or links between arch members -- The code E21D 11/24 classifies knuckle joints or linking elements that connect arch components in underground linings such as tunnels, shafts or galleries. These devices provide flexible or hinged connections between curved or straight arch members, facilitating assembly and accommodating movements in large underground chambers.
[E21D 11/26] Shoes for connecting arch members to longitudinal struts -- E21D 11/26 covers specialized shoes that attach arch members of a tunnel or shaft lining to longitudinal struts, enabling secure load transfer and structural stability in underground cavity constructions. These components are used where arches and longitudinal supports must be fastened together.
[E21D 11/28] Longitudinal struts -- Longitudinal struts are structural members installed lengthwise within the lining of shafts, tunnels or underground chambers to support and reinforce the surrounding earth or rock. They transfer axial loads and help resist deformation, improving stability of the constructed cavity.
[E21D 11/30] Bases for lower arch members -- E21D 11/30 deals with bases that support the lower members of an arch within shafts used for earth-drilling, such as foundations for tunnel, gallery or other underground cavity linings. These components provide the structural support needed at the base of the arch ribs that transfer loads from the lining to the surrounding ground.
[E21D 11/34] Joints between vertical props and horizontal top bars -- The code E21D 11/34 designates technical solutions for joining vertical support props to horizontal top bars in the lining of shafts, galleries or other underground cavities. It covers joint configurations used in fixed-construction earth-drilling projects to secure the structural framework of tunnel linings.
[E21D 11/36] Linings or supports specially shaped for tunnels or galleries of irregular cross-section -- E21D 11/36 covers specially shaped linings or support structures designed for tunnels, galleries or other underground cavities that have irregular cross-sections, such as large chambers created during earth-drilling shaft construction. The classification applies to non-standard profiles used to reinforce or line these irregularly shaped voids.

The classification addresses how the intersecting portions are supported or finished to maintain stability and functionality.

[E21D 11/38] Waterproofing -- The E21D 11/38 subclass deals with waterproofing of fixed underground structures such as shafts, tunnels, galleries and large chambers created by earth-drilling. It includes the design, materials and techniques used to prevent water ingress, such as membranes, sealants, coatings and drainage systems applied to the lining of these cavities.
[E21D 11/381] Setting apparatus or devices -- The CPC class E21D 11/381 covers devices and apparatus for setting lining elements in shafts or tunnels during earth-drilling construction, used to install and secure structural supports in underground chambers.
[E21D 11/383] by applying waterproof flexible sheets; Means for fixing the sheets to the tunnel or cavity wall -- This group deals with methods of installing waterproof flexible membranes in underground structures, specifically techniques for attaching the sheets to the walls of shafts, tunnels, galleries or large chambers. It includes various fixing means such as mechanical fasteners, adhesives or anchor systems used to secure the sheets and ensure water tightness.
[E21D 11/385] Sealing means positioned between adjacent lining members  for joints in foundation structures  E02D29/16 ; for sewers  E03F3/04 ; for building structures in general  E04B1/68 -- E21D 11/385 covers sealing means that are installed between adjacent lining members to form joint seals in foundations, shafts, tunnels, large underground chambers and similar structures. The classification also applies to sealing solutions for sewers and general building structures, linking to related subclasses E02D29/16, E03F3/04 and E04B1/68.
[E21D 11/386] inflatable sealing means -- Inflatable sealing means are flexible devices that can be expanded by air or fluid to create a tight, temporary seal against the walls of shafts, tunnels, galleries or other underground cavities during earth-drilling operations. They conform to irregular surfaces, supporting excavation, groundwater control or construction-stage protection.
[E21D 11/388] having longitudinal cavities communicating with the exterior on the outer side of the lining elements -- A shaft or underground cavity whose lining incorporates longitudinal cavities that open to the exterior on the outer side of the lining elements, allowing fluid flow, ventilation or access through the lining. This feature is typical of fixed-construction earth-drilling applications such as large underground chambers.
[E21D 1/14] Freezing apparatus -- E21D 1/14 relates to freezing apparatus used in shaft drilling. It includes devices that circulate refrigerants or use other cooling methods to freeze the ground around a borehole, improving stability and preventing collapse during excavation. Such systems are employed in fixed construction earth-drilling operations.
[E21D 11/40] Devices or apparatus specially adapted for handling or placing units of linings for tunnels or galleries -- E21D 11/40 covers devices or apparatuses specifically designed to handle or position lining units when constructing tunnels, galleries, shafts or other underground cavities, facilitating the installation of structural linings in fixed construction projects.
[E21D 11/403] combined with the head machine -- E21D 11/403 covers techniques for constructing and sealing underground shafts, tunnels or chambers where the lining operation is integrated with the drilling head machine. It includes head-mounted equipment that simultaneously drills and installs lining elements, enabling continuous excavation and immediate support of the excavation to improve stability and reduce construction time.
[E21D 11/406] Placing endless lining elements, e.g. from reels  in combination with a tunneling shield  E21D9/0609 -- This class covers the method of installing continuous, prefabricated tunnel lining - such as pipe or segment rings supplied on reels - while a tunneling shield advances, allowing the lining to be unrolled or unwrapped and positioned directly behind the shield to form the final tunnel wall. It pertains to shaft construction and underground cavity lining within fixed-construction earth-drilling operations.

[E21D 15/00] Working-face supports -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Props: elongated supports to prop the roof of tunnels or galleries.
